title: A Good Udder, Glazed with Sugar, in an Orange Sauce (Ein gutes Euter, mit Zucker glacirt, in einer Pomeranzenſauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- two cow udders
- butter
- flour
- beef broth
- white wine
- sugar
- orange zest
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Take two cow udders, cook them soft, then remove them from the broth, remove the skin, and form them completely round.
2. Make a sauce from a piece of butter and a spoonful of flour, let it turn slightly yellow, add beef broth, a glass of white wine, a piece of sugar rubbed on orange peel, and the juice of the same, let the sauce cook well, strain it through a sieve onto the dish, place the udder on top, sprinkle it well with sugar.
3. Burn this sugar with a glowing iron so that the udder gets a glaze, and serve it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aal am Rost gebraten (Eel Roasted on the Grill)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- Warm butter
- Salt
- Maitre d'hôtel butter

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the eel and cut into desired pieces
2. Brush with warm butter on both sides and sprinkle with salt
3. Fry golden on both sides on the grill
4. Arrange in a bowl and serve with Maitre d'hôtel butter
5. Alternatively, the eel can be fried in the same manner on a flat pan

title: Aal mit Gratin (Eel with Gratin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- Breadcrumbs
- Hot fat
- Mushrooms
- Onions
- Butter
- Madeira sauce
- Maitre d'hôtel butter

## Directions

1. Salt the eel, cut lengthwise in 3 places, sprinkle with breadcrumbs
2. Fry golden on both sides in hot fat on a pan
3. Arrange in a bowl and cover with prepared mushrooms
4. Finely chop mushrooms and onions, roast in butter, pour on Madeira sauce and bring to a boil
5. Pour this sauce over the fried eel, cover with large pieces of Maitre d'hôtel butter
6. Place in the oven for 1/2 hour, then serve

title: Aal mit Mayonnaise und Aspic (Eel with Mayonnaise and Aspic)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- salt water
- aspic
- butter
- mayonnaise
- cold mustard sauce

## Directions

1. The eel is prepared like the white-prepared eel, then let it cool in its broth, arrange it neatly on a platter and garnish it nicely with chopped and broken aspic, colored butter and the like. The mayonnaise is served in a sauce boat and the fish is somewhat brushed with it. Some cold mustard sauce can also be served with it. You can also keep the eel whole and prepare and arrange it as indicated in the braised eel recipe.

title: Aal, blau zu sieden (Eel Boiled Blue)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- wine vinegar
- butter
- bay leaves
- cloves
- salt
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Strike an eel on the head with a piece of wood until it is dead
2. Open it, wash it out, and spray wine vinegar on it
3. Set wine vinegar with a small piece of butter, several bay leaves, whole cloves, salt, and pepper on the fire
4. When it boils, add the eel with its skin, cover it well, let it cook through completely
5. Serve it with a little fresh wine vinegar and decorated with green parsley
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aal, gebraten (Fried Eel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- sage leaves
- bacon
- butter
- lemon juice
- breadcrumbs
- anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Kill the eel by driving a nail through its head and nailing it to a table
2. Cut the skin off around the head and pull it off the entire eel
3. Hold it on both sides over glowing coals until you can pull off any remaining skin
4. Salt and lard it completely from above with sage leaves and bacon interspersed
5. Roll it up into a round shape, cut off the head, and place the eel on a sheet or casserole lid
6. Pour butter over it and place it in an oven or baking oven
7. Roast it, basting it frequently with butter and lemon juice during cooking
8. When roasted, coat it with glaze or sprinkle with breadcrumbs as desired to give it color
9. Serve it whole on a dish with anchovy sauce poured over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Abgekochte Äpfelscheiben (Cooked Apple Slices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Small round cutter
- Flour for dredging
- Eggs for coating
- Breadcrumbs for rolling
- Lard for frying
- Powdered sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Using a small round cutter as described in the Fried Apples recipe, remove the core house and peel the apples.

title: Abgerührte Zimttorten (Stirred Cinnamon Tarts)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarter pound butter
- egg yolks
- half a pound pounded almonds
- half a pound sifted sugar
- two lots pounded cinnamon
- three-quarter pound flour
- canned morello cherries, currants, etc.

## Directions

1. Stir the butter until it is quite foamy
2. Add egg yolks gradually
3. Add the pounded almonds, sifted sugar, and pounded cinnamon
4. Stir this mixture for a whole hour
5. Add three-quarter pound of flour
6. Brush a tart sheet with the rim
7. Make the bottom the thickness of half a knife back
8. Fill it with canned morello cherries, currants, etc.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Abgerührte Zimttorten (Stirred Cinnamon Tarts)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound butter
- egg yolks
- half pound ground almonds
- half pound sifted sugar
- two loth ground cinnamon
- three-quarters pound flour
- tart sheet
- preserved morello cherries
- currants

## Directions

1. Stir three-quarters pound butter until very foamy
2. Add egg yolks gradually
3. Add half pound ground almonds, half pound sifted sugar, and two loth ground cinnamon
4. Stir everything together for one hour
5. Mix in three-quarters pound flour
6. Brush a tart sheet and rim with the batter to the thickness of half a knife-back
7. Fill with preserved morello cherries, currants, etc.
8. Make a lattice from the remaining dough over the top
9. Bake slowly
10. Pour icing over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Abgescbmalgene Nudeln (Butter-Cooked Noodles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter flour
- 2 whole eggs
- salt
- water
- 8 decagrams hot butter
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 liter flour on a board, make a hollow in the middle
2. Put 1 teaspoon salt, 2 whole eggs and if necessary some cold water in it
3. Work this into a smooth dough as described in 'Feudelsuppe'
4. On a board dusted with flour, roll the small dough pieces knife-back thick
5. When they are dried, cut them into 3-finger-wide strips
6. When these are evenly piled on top of each other, cut them into knife-back-wide noodles
7. Put the noodles in boiling salt water and cook covered until they come to the surface
8. Pour them into a colander, rinse with cold water
9. When the water has drained well, put them in 8 decagrams very hot butter
10. Salt to taste and leave in, stirring often, until the noodles are hot
11. Arrange raised on a dish and serve
12. These noodles can also be served with grated Parmesan cheese if desired
13. The noodles can also be put directly from boiling water into the hot fat

title: Abgetriebene Butternockerl (Worked Butter Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs
- flour
- butter and sweet milk or cream
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Make dough with butter, eggs, and flour by working together
2. Set a flat pan or casserole with butter and sweet milk or cream on the heat
3. When this simmers, drop the dumplings in with a spoon
4. Apply heat above and below
5. Let the milk reduce until they become beautifully brown
6. Remove and place on a platter to serve
7. Sugar may be added to the milk if desired
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Abgetriebene Grieß-Strudeln (Whipped Semolina Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka Butter
- 5 whole eggs
- 1/4 liter milk cream
- 30 Deka semolina
- 1/2 liter flour for dough

## Directions

1. Whip 16 Deka butter until fluffy
2. Stir in 5 whole eggs, 1/4 liter milk cream into 30 Deka semolina
3. Let stand covered for 2 hours
4. Spread this filling on stretched dough made from 1/2 liter flour
5. Proceed as with lung strudel

title: Abgeſottener Lachs (Poached Salmon)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon piece, three or four pounds, preferably from the middle
- Water
- Half a measure of white wine
- Wine vinegar
- Onions
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Bay leaves
- Fine herbs
- Salt
- Root vegetables

## Directions

1. Place the salmon piece in a casserole with water, half a measure of white wine and wine vinegar so it is covered
2. Add onions, cloves, pepper, bay leaves, a little fine herbs, salt and root vegetables
3. Cook the piece for three-quarters of an hour
4. Arrange on a platter
5. Strain the sauce through a fine sieve and serve with it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aepfel-Gefrornes (Apple Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 pieces Maschanzger apples
- Water
- Three quarter pound clarified sugar
- Juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Peel 24 pieces of Maschanzger apples, cut them up and remove the seeds
2. Pound them fine in a mortar and let them cook with some water on a charcoal fire
3. Press through a hair sieve and mix the puree with three quarter pound clarified sugar
4. When the mixture is well cooled, press in the juice of 2 lemons, mix well together, and place in the freezing pan
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aepfelkoch (Apple Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled apples
- one-half pound apple pulp (after pushing through)
- one and one-half quarter pounds butter
- one-quarter pound blanched and finely ground almonds
- finely chopped lemon zest
- ten egg yolks
- the beaten whites of five eggs
- one-quarter pound sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Take peeled apples and stew them, then push them through a sieve
2. For every half pound of pushed-through apples, beat one and one-half quarter pounds butter until very fluffy in a bowl
3. Stir in one-quarter pound blanched and finely ground almonds, finely chopped lemon zest, then ten egg yolks, each stirred well
4. Stir in the pushed-through apples
5. Take the beaten whites of five eggs and one-quarter pound sifted sugar, and stir these two ingredients in gradually
6. Spread a sheet pan and rim with butter
7. Bake it slowly for one hour in a cake pan
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aepfeltorte (Apple Tart)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- sugar
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Peel and cut apples into pieces
2. Cook them with sugar, lemon peel, and some cinnamon into a jam that is neither thin nor watery
3. Let cool
4. Make a tart from puff pastry
5. Fill with the apple mass
6. Spread it finger-thick
7. Make a thin slitted lid over the top
8. Bake the tart slowly
9. Glaze it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aepfelwein (Apple Cider)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 120 pounds apple must
- 3 pounds raw finely crushed cream of tartar
- 15 pounds ripe sloes or 10 pounds dried sloes
- sugar if the must is not sweet enough

## Directions

1. Dissolve 3 pounds raw finely crushed cream of tartar in some must and add this solution to 120 pounds of apple must in a barrel
2. Once fermentation has begun, press 15 pounds of ripe sloes or crush and add 10 pounds of dried sloes to the barrel
3. After fermentation is complete, rack the wine into a clean barrel
4. The cream of tartar should prevent the cider-like taste, and the sloes should give it a similarity to sweet wine
5. If the must is not sweet enough, add sugar
6. The apples should be fresh, without rot and not overripe, nor taken unripe from the tree; they are better if they have lain in heaps in the open air for several days and nights
7. If desired, some quinces may be pressed along with the apples, and a portion of pitted plums may be added
8. Once fermentation is complete, treat the apple must with topping up and racking as with grape must
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Agneau Bouilli (Boiled Lamb)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow turnips
- carrot piece
- parsley piece
- celery piece
- salt water
- vinegar
- thyme sprigs
- spices (cloves, bay, etc.)
- lamb meat, cut into pieces
- green parsley, finely cut
- chervil
- tarragon
- butter
- sour cream
- broth

## Directions

1. In a casserole, add yellow turnips, a piece of carrot, a piece of parsley, a small piece of celery, all very finely cut like noodles, then pour in 1 liter of boiling salt water and 1/4 liter vinegar.
2. Let it cook with 5 sprigs of thyme and 5 grains each of the spices (which are tied together in an organza cloth).
3. When the root vegetables are almost tender, add the lamb meat cut into desired pieces and braise covered until tender.
4. Remove the meat with a slotted spoon, arrange it piled on a platter, sprinkle with finely cut green parsley, chervil and tarragon and garnish with the strained root vegetables.
5. Blend several ounces of butter with some sour cream, pour in 1-1/2 liters of hot broth (from the root vegetables) and serve in a sauceboat with the meat.

title: Agneau Frit (Fried Lamb)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb breast and shoulder, cut into pieces two fingers wide and one finger long
- salt

## Directions

1. Usually the front part of the lamb is taken, namely the breast and shoulder, cut it into pieces two fingers wide and one finger long, salt it.

title: Agneau Rôti (Roasted Lamb)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb leg or piece
- pepper
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Select a suitable leg or piece, bone it and let it rest for 1 hour.
2. After this time, sprinkle the meat with some pepper and place it on wood pieces or cooking spoons in a roasting pan so that the skin faces up.
3. Baste with hot fat and roast in the oven at strong heat for about 3/4 to 1 hour.
4. When the meat has a golden crust on top, cut it into desired pieces, reassemble beautifully on a deep platter and serve with salad, vegetables or orange slices.

title: Alettis-Zöttchen (Alettis Biscuits)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deka finely sifted sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 16 deka fine flour
- blanched, finely cut almonds
- finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Beat together 16 deka finely sifted sugar, 8 egg yolks, and 2 whole eggs for 1/2 hour
2. Quickly mix in 16 deka fine flour
3. Spread this mass on a butter-greased sheet
4. Sprinkle with blanched finely cut almonds
5. Dust with finely sifted sugar
6. Bake in the oven at moderate heat until golden
7. Cut the baked biscuit into 2 finger-wide and finger-long pieces

title: Allemande Sauce (Allemande Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping tablespoon flour
- 16 ounces butter
- salt
- nutmeg
- 1/4 liter boiling water
- 8 ounces crumbled butter
- lemon juice
- spices

## Directions

1. Rub together 1 heaping tablespoon flour with 16 ounces butter, salt, and nutmeg in a saucepan using a wooden spoon
2. Pour in 1/4 liter boiling water and stir quickly over heat with a wooden spoon
3. At the moment it binds, it forms a sauce, thickens, and must be removed from heat immediately
4. If it becomes too thick, add several spoonfuls of warm water
5. Then add 8 ounces crumbled butter and stir on the hot plate with a wooden spoon until the fresh butter has melted
6. It should spin from the spoon and become fluffy and light from the constant stirring
7. For asparagus, cauliflower, artichokes, and salsify, use the sauce as it is; for fish dishes, enhance the flavor with lemon juice, salt, and spices
8. If desired, you can stir in 3 egg yolks with some lemon juice into the sauce
9. Instead of lemon juice, vinegar can also be used

title: Allgemeiner fluten (General Short Pastry Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca flour
- 8 deca butter
- 1 whole egg
- salt
- 1 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. When 28 deca flour has been finely worked with 8 deca butter, make a depression in the middle
2. Add 1 whole egg, a little salt, and 1 teaspoon salt to it
3. Work into a dough and use as desired

title: Almond and Lemon Pudding (Mandelkoch von Limonien)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound ground almonds
- three-quarters pound sugar
- one large lemon
- six whole eggs
- ten egg yolks
- butter for the mold

## Directions

1. In a mixing bowl, take three-quarters pound ground almonds and the same amount of sugar
2. Grate a large, beautiful lemon on a grater well
3. Let the peel simmer, as well as a couple of eggs, strain it, press it out well, and mix it under the almonds and sugar
4. Stir everything well together
5. Take six whole eggs and ten egg yolks, beat them in gradually, and stir for a whole hour
6. Coat the mold with butter, fill in the stirred mixture, bake it for a whole hour slowly, and it is done
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Almond Cakes (Mandelcarten)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca butter cut into pieces
- 32 deca warm flour
- 32 deca sugar
- 32 deca almonds finely pounded and passed through a sieve with their skin
- 1 table spoon grated chocolate
- some cinnamon and nutmeg
- 1 whole egg and 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix butter with warm flour on a board, rubbing finely with hands
2. Add sugar, almonds, chocolate, cinnamon, and nutmeg, mixing well
3. Make a hollow in the center and add 1 whole egg and 2 egg yolks
4. Mix well with a fork and work into a dough, letting it rest covered in a cool place
5. After approximately half an hour, roll out the dough on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a knife back
6. Cut out in cake form and place at finger-width distance on a butter-greased baking sheet
7. Brush the cakes with beaten eggs, cover with halved blanched almonds, and bake at moderate heat in the oven
8. Remove from baking sheet with a thin, long knife and let cool on a sieve or board

title: Almond Horseradish
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dkg butter
- 1 dkg flour
- 1/2 milk
- salt
- sugar
- 3 dkg soaked, chopped almonds
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Heat 4 dkg butter in a pot
2. Stir in 1 dkg flour and deglaze with milk
3. Season with salt and sugar
4. Add 3 dkg softened, chopped almonds and cook thoroughly
5. Just before serving, add the cleaned grated horseradish

title: Almond Paste Confection (Süßware aus Hartzkartenmasse)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deca peeled almonds
- 1/8 liter water
- 32 deca finely sifted sugar
- Juice of one lemon
- 32 deca finely sifted sugar (second addition)
- 1 tablespoon finely cut citron
- 1 tablespoon finely cut candied orange peel
- 4 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 1 heaped tablespoon flour

## Directions

1. Pound almonds with water to a paste
2. Stir in first amount of sugar and lemon juice over heat until dough is dry and releases from the spoon
3. Pound dough finely again in mortar
4. Place on plate and let rest in cool place
5. Transfer to bowl and mix with second amount of sugar, citron, and candied orange peel
6. Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites and flour
7. Knead well on board
8. Roll out to knife-back thickness
9. Cut into desired figures
10. Place on dry baking sheet
11. Brush with beaten eggs
12. Dust with sugar
13. Bake golden in well-heated oven

title: Almond Slices (Weis Mandel Stangen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter
- 14 deca lemon sugar
- 1 whole egg and 3 egg yolks
- 14 deca blanched almonds finely pounded with 1 egg white
- 14 deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deca butter until fluffy
2. Gradually add 14 deca lemon sugar, 1 whole egg and 3 egg yolks
3. Mix in blanched almonds finely pounded with egg white and flour, working into a dough on a board
4. Let rest covered in a cool place for approximately 2 hours
5. Roll out dough to finger thickness and cut according to the size of the baking sheet
6. Roll up the dough on a noodle roller and unroll on a butter-greased baking sheet
7. Brush with beaten eggs and bake pale yellow at low heat in the oven
8. Cut the baked product into rows with a round cutter, layering them 2 by 2 with boiled jam between each

title: Almond Soufflé (Soufflé aux amandes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 drams flour
- 1 liter cold cream
- 14 drams butter, beaten
- 16 drams sugar
- 14 egg yolks
- 6 blanched, finely ground bitter almonds
- 9 egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Pour 1 liter cold cream slowly over 12 drams flour.
2. Stir over heat until it has thickened considerably.
3. Set aside and let cool.
4. Mix 14 drams beaten butter with 16 drams sugar thoroughly.
5. Stir in the 14 egg yolks one after another.
6. Mix this with the 6 blanched, finely ground bitter almonds and the cooled flour mixture.
7. Lightly fold in the snow from 9 egg whites.
8. Fill a buttered mold, dusted with flour.
9. If desired, insert biscuits, crushed macaroons, or small pastries.
10. Bake at low heat for 3/4 hour in the oven.

title: Almond Soufflé with Eggs (Soufflé aux amandes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces flour
- 0.5 liter cold cream
- 10 ounces blanched almonds
- 2 egg whites
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 ounces sugar
- 0.5 liter cream
- 6 egg whites, whipped
- baked biscuit

## Directions

1. Gradually stir flour into cold cream until smooth
2. Add blanched almonds finely pounded with 2 egg whites, then egg yolks, sugar, and cream one by one
3. Gently fold in the whipped egg whites
4. Fill a butter-greased, flour-lined mold, insert baked biscuit as desired
5. Cover and steam for 3/4 hour or bake in the oven

title: Almond Straws (Mandelsträßchen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca finely pounded, passed almonds
- 16 deca sugar
- 1 pinch of finely cut lemon zest
- cinnamon and cloves to equal measure
- 1 table spoon grated chocolate
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix almonds, sugar, lemon zest, cinnamon, cloves, and chocolate together well
2. Lightly fold in the snow from 2 egg whites
3. Roll out the mixture on a sugar-dusted board to the thickness of a small finger
4. Cut out with various cutters in lovely forms, layer 2 by 2 with a little boiled jam in the middle
5. Place on a baking sheet lined with wax or unlarded lard using a knife
6. Brush with beaten eggs, sprinkle with finely pounded sugar, and dry in the preheated oven

title: Almond Wreaths (Mandelkränze)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound marzipan dough
- 2 to 5 egg whites
- crushed cinnamon
- a little flour
- coarsely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Take half pound marzipan dough, 2 to 5 egg whites, crushed cinnamon, and a little flour
2. Knead the mixture into a dough
3. Scatter coarsely chopped almonds on the work table
4. Form finger-long sausages from the dough
5. Roll them in the chopped almonds
6. Join the two ends together to form a wreath
7. Place on paper
8. Slide into a well-heated oven and let bake until light brown
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Almut eingefänzent (Sauerkraut)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage heads
- salt
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Let the cabbage heads lie for 8 days or longer to ferment
2. Remove the first two leaves from each head and twist out the stem with a knife or corer
3. Slice the cabbage with a cabbage plane or knife onto a clean cloth
4. Line the bottom of the barrel or tub with cabbage leaves
5. Sprinkle with salt mixed with caraway seeds
6. Add hand-height pieces of cabbage, then salt and caraway again, and continue this process
7. When the barrel is half full, stamp down the cabbage very firmly with a wooden pestle or by foot
8. Ensure the cabbage forms an even surface without any indentations
9. Continue filling and stamping in this manner
10. When the barrel is full and salt and caraway are sprinkled on top, stamp again
11. Cover with cabbage leaves and place a fitting lid on top, weighting it down with stones
12. If using a special barrel, drill two holes one finger width below the rim and insert a tube so excess water can drain off
13. If using a screw barrel, the screw must be tightened daily
14. After three weeks, when the cabbage is already edible, skim off the foam and water, rinse the cabbage well, cover and weight it, and pour fresh water over so it stands hand-high above the cabbage
15. Each time cabbage is removed, take it evenly so no indentations form, then cover, weight, and pour fresh water over
16. Wash the cabbage, lid, and stones every 8 days
17. Cabbage can be stored 1-2 years

title: Alrune im Rudolf's Schnitte oder Torte (Alrune's Rudolf's Slices or Torte)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 Deca butter
- 16 egg yolks
- 32 Deca blanched almonds, very finely chopped with 2 egg whites
- 8 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 12 Deca flour
- marmalade as needed
- orange glaze
- candied cherries
- angelica, finely chopped
- green almonds
- black currants

## Directions

1. Beat 32 Deca butter and 16 egg yolks for 1 hour
2. Add 32 Deca blanched almonds very finely chopped with 2 egg whites, mix well
3. Lightly fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites from 8 eggs with 12 Deca flour at the same time
4. For a torte: place in a torte mold 2 finger widths high and bake carefully in the oven for 3/4 hour
5. For slices: spread the mixture knife-back width thick and bake until light yellow
6. Spread half immediately with marmalade, place the other half on top, press slightly, cover with orange glaze knife-back width thick and immediately cut out half-moon shapes with a round pastry cutter
7. Place a candied cherry on the center of each slice and decorate with finely chopped angelica, green almonds, black currants or as desired
8. The slices can also be glazed with any desired color so they appear shaded

title: Alte Hühner in schwarzer Sauce (Old Chicken in Black Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Old chicken blood
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Onion (cut into thin leaves)
- Bay leaf
- Thyme (bunches)
- Lemon peel
- Black bread (cut into thin leaves)
- Wine
- Soup broth

## Directions

1. The blood obtained when slaughtering the chicken is mixed with some vinegar and placed in a suitable dish.
2. To make the meat of an old chicken tender, after the entrails are removed and disposed of, let it lie in the feathers for 1-2 days.
3. After this time, pluck it, clean it, and cut it into neat pieces.
4. Into a casserole, which you have previously put fat, 1 tablespoon of thinly sliced onion, 1 bay leaf, some bunches of thyme and lemon peel, lay the cut and salted chicken and let it braise covered.
5. When all the liquid has evaporated, add thinly sliced black bread, pour over the meat with as much wine, vinegar, and broth that the liquid just covers it, and let it braise covered until tender.
6. When this is done, add the blood of the chicken, stir it well together, and let it boil a little.
7. Then remove the meat, place it in a bowl, and pour the strained, defatted sauce over it.

title: Alte Hühner würze zu machen (Preparation of Old Chicken)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Old chicken
- Cold water
- Salt

## Directions

1. From a slaughtered old chicken, immediately remove the entrails and place the chicken in a deep bowl.
2. Pour cold water over it and let it lie with the feathers at a cool place for about three days.
3. Change the water fresh every day.
4. After this time, remove it, pluck the feathers, clean, dress, and salt the chicken, and let it lie for some hours.
5. Roast or braise it like a poularde and serve it with a fine sauce or with cabbage.

title: Amourettes de Veau Marines (Marinated Veal Marrow)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal marrow
- Salt water
- Cold water
- Lemon juice
- Lard
- White pepper
- Green parsley
- Bread or egg batter
- Hot fat

## Directions

1. Since a veal loin does not provide much marrow and it does not keep well, it is necessary to order it at the right time
2. Carefully remove the marrow from the outer skin
3. Poach it for 1 minute in boiling salt water
4. Remove it and shock with cold water
5. Lay on a cloth and cut into finger-length pieces
6. Place in a bowl, season with lemon juice, necessary lard, white pepper and green parsley
7. Cover with butter-brushed paper and stir occasionally
8. Shortly before serving, dip each piece in bread or egg batter and fry in very hot fat
9. It is usually arranged mixed with fried brains

title: Ananas (Pineapple)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful pineapple
- pineapple skin
- 60 decilitres sugar
- cold sugar water

## Directions

1. Peel a beautiful pineapple and place the skin immediately in preserving jars
2. Cut the pineapple into slices knife-back thick or as desired and place also in preserving jars
3. Pour cold sugar water (of 60 decilitres sugar) over both the pineapple and the skin so that it covers them and only a small finger-height space remains empty in the jar
4. Bind and cook as the Muscatel pears (Nr. 1842) for 3-4 hours in steam

title: Ananas-Gefrornes (Pineapple Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pineapple
- 8 lemons
- Sugar
- Three quarter pound finely clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Rub the outside of the pineapple on sugar and scrape this onto a plate
2. Cut the pineapple itself into pieces and press the juice through a hair sieve well
3. Do the same with 8 lemons
4. After cooling, stir three quarter pound finely clarified sugar into the mixture
5. Pour into the freezing pan and process
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ananas-Marmelade (Pineapple Jam)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilo butter
- 1/4 liter cold water
- 1 kilo peeled pineapple, cut in slices
- 1/2 kilo finely pounded sugar
- 1 1/4 liters water

## Directions

1. Put into a stone mortar 1/2 kilo butter, 1/4 liter cold water and 1 kilo peeled, sliced pineapple
2. This is pounded very finely into a dough
3. Then with 1/2 kilo finely pounded sugar and 1 1/4 liters water in a saucepan placed on the fire and cooked under constant stirring for approximately 10 minutes
4. Then quickly passed through a sieve and cooked again for 10 minutes
5. Then fill this jam hot into jars
6. One must be very careful in this preparation, so that the pineapple does not lose its yellow color through too long cooking

title: Ananas-Marmelade (Pineapple Preserves)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram sugar
- 0.25 liter cold water
- 1 kilogram peeled pineapples, cut into slices
- 0.5 kilogram finely powdered sugar
- 1.5 liters water

## Directions

1. Put 0.5 kilogram of sugar, 0.25 liter of cold water, and 1 kilogram of peeled pineapples cut into slices into a stone mortar.
2. Pound this very finely into a dough, then place in a saucepan on heat with 0.5 kilogram of finely powdered sugar and 1.5 liters of water.
3. Cook while stirring constantly for approximately 10 minutes, then pass quickly through a sieve.

title: Ananas-Sulz (Pineapple Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple juice
- orange peel
- 2 lemons
- refined sugar
- isinglass

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as Orange Jelly (No. 1660), with the difference that orange peel is added to the sugar, juice from only 2 lemons is used, and pineapple juice is used instead of wine.

title: Ananas-Suppe mit Champagner (Pineapple Soup with Champagne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large peeled pineapple
- 8 deca crushed sugar
- 16 deca sugar
- 3/4 liter water
- 8 deca sugar
- 1 bottle Champagne
- 1/2 liter Rhine wine
- 16 deca sugar
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the flesh of a large peeled pineapple into small pieces, scatter these with 8 deca crushed sugar and set on ice.
2. Place the skin and remains in a bowl, mix 16 deca sugar with it and strain the crushed remains through a sieve to the cut pineapple.
3. Place it in a saucepan, let it simmer with 3/4 liter water with 8 deca sugar, strain it once more through a sieve and when it has cooled, pour in 1 bottle Champagne, 1/2 liter Rhine wine with 16 deca sugar and the juice of 1 lemon, mix everything together and then set it on ice in a bowl for several hours.

title: Anchovied Chicken with Crayfish and Lemons (Poulets Braises aux Anchois)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of onion
- green parsley
- 6 pieces of anchovy fillets
- 12 decagrams of fat
- 3 tablespoons of breadcrumbs
- juice of 1 lemon
- 2 chickens, cut into pieces
- 4¾ liters of beef stock

## Directions

1. Finely chop together 1 head of onion, green parsley, and 6 pieces of anchovy fillets
2. Place in a casserole containing 12 decagrams of fat
3. Toast with 3 tablespoons of breadcrumbs
4. Add 2 chickens cut into pieces and juice of 1 lemon
5. Cover and braise for some time
6. Pour 4¾ liters of beef stock over
7. Cover and braise until tender
8. Arrange chickens in a serving dish and pour the defatted sauce over

title: Anchovy Butter (Beurre d'Anchois)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 teaspoons cleaned, quickly washed anchovies
- 30 teaspoons butter

## Directions

1. Finely pound 10 teaspoons of cleaned, quickly washed anchovies with 30 teaspoons of butter in a mortar
2. Pass through a sieve and arrange neatly on a plate

title: Andere Weise einer Creme (Another Way of Making Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One Seidel good cream
- A piece of sugar
- Some whole vanilla
- 6 egg yolks
- Egg white
- One spoonful flour
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cook one Seidel good cream with a piece of sugar and some whole vanilla
2. Let it cool, beat 6 egg yolks and the egg white each separately very well
3. Stir one spoonful of flour into this, mix in the rest, set on a coal fire, and stir until sufficiently thick
4. Pour it into a mold, sprinkle it strongly with sugar, and brown it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Angler gebacken (Fried Monkfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 dkg Fisch (fish)
- Salt
- Schmalz (lard)
- Zitronenſcheiben (lemon slices)
- Sauerkraut or Kartoffelsalat (sauerkraut or potato salad) for serving

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the fish, clean it, wash it and place it in milk water.
2. Either remove and cut the fillets from the sides or divide the fish widthwise into cutlets.
3. Salt them and let them rest for 30 minutes.
4. Wipe them with a cloth, bread them and fry them in hot lard, then let them drain.
5. Serve on a long platter, topped with lemon slices, with sauerkraut or potato salad as a side dish.

title: Anis-Brot (Anise Bread)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 drams fine sifted sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 tablespoon anise
- 36 drams flour
- butter for greasing the baking form

## Directions

1. Stir 30 drams fine sifted sugar with 12 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs for 3/4 hour
2. Lightly mix in 1 tablespoon anise with 36 drams flour
3. Pour into a baking form greased with butter and dusted with flour
4. Bake at moderate heat for 1 to 1 1/4 hours
5. Turn out onto a sieve and let cool
6. Cut into slices the thickness of a knife back and dry these in the heated oven as desired
7. The anise can be omitted and vanilla sugar used instead of ordinary sugar, giving it the name Vanille-Brot

title: Anis-Schnitte (Anise Slices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 16 Deca finely chopped sugar
- 12 Deca potato flour
- 12 Deca regular flour
- finely frozen sugar mixed with flooded anise

## Directions

1. Beat egg whites from 8 eggs until stiff
2. Mix gradually with 16 Deca finely chopped sugar, 12 Deca potato flour and 12 Deca regular flour
3. Spread this mixture finger-high on wafers, sprinkle with finely frozen sugar mixed with flooded anise
4. Cut into strips of any desired size and bake on a baking sheet at low heat in the oven
5. When half baked, that is still soft, place on a warmed sponge form and dry completely in the very hot oven

title: Anisbiskuit (Anise Biscuit)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs' weight sugar
- 5 fresh eggs
- juice and zest of ½ lemon
- 4 eggs' weight flour
- 5 dkg melted butter
- anise seeds

## Directions

1. Beat 4 eggs' weight of sugar with 5 fresh eggs in a snow basin with a whisk for 20 minutes until thick
2. Add juice and zest of ½ lemon
3. Fold in 4 eggs' weight of flour, 5 dkg melted (not hot) butter, and some anise seeds using a wooden spoon
4. Fill into a buttered, floured biscuit mold
5. Bake slowly
6. The next day, after turning out, cut into thin slices
7. Optionally place slices on a baking sheet and bake on both sides in an open oven to keep longer

title: Anisbögen (Anise Arches)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 whole eggs
- 3 eggs' weight sugar
- 2 eggs' weight flour
- butter
- flour for dusting
- anise seeds

## Directions

1. Beat 3 whole eggs with 3 eggs' weight of sugar for 15 minutes
2. Mix in 2 eggs' weight of flour
3. Using two spoons, make small discs on a buttered, floured baking sheet
4. Sprinkle with anise seeds
5. Bake in a moderately hot oven until light brown
6. Carefully lift with a knife and immediately curl over a wooden spoon handle while still warm, as they cannot be bent once cooled

title: Anisbögen (Anise Arches)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 whole eggs
- 3 eggs' weight of sugar
- 2 eggs' weight of flour
- anise seeds

## Directions

1. Beat 3 whole eggs with 3 eggs' weight of sugar for 15 minutes
2. Mix in 2 eggs' weight of flour
3. Using two spoons, make small discs on buttered, floured baking sheet
4. Sprinkle with anise seeds
5. Bake in moderate heat until light brown
6. Carefully lift with a knife and immediately twist over a wooden spoon handle

title: Aniseis (Anise Glaze)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Orange Glaze (No. 1560)
- 3 tablespoons of anise

## Directions

1. To the Orange Glaze (No. 1560), 3 tablespoons of anise are stirred in.

title: Annolisibufferln (Italian)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca pignoli
- 2 egg whites
- 16 deca fine sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Pound 28 deca pignoli with 2 egg whites
2. Add 16 deca fine sifted sugar and pound everything together into a dough
3. Using a teaspoon or piping bag, place small mounds on a baking sheet lined with wax paper
4. Sprinkle with fine pounded sugar
5. Dry in a cool oven

title: Another Kind of Champagne Wine (Eine andere Art Champagner-Wein)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 pounds white sugar
- 8 pounds raisins
- 4 good lemons, cut into slices
- good white French wine

## Directions

1. Fill an upright standing barrel with the white sugar, raisins, and sliced lemons, and fill with good white French wine to within a few finger-widths of the top
2. Once the sugar has dissolved, stop the tap hole in the upper bottom, then roll the barrel vigorously for half an hour
3. Place the barrel upright in a warmth of 14 degrees Reaumur
4. Pierce the cork of the tap hole and insert a feather tube that is open at both top and bottom
5. When no strong pungent vapor comes from this any longer, fermentation is finished and the opening is sealed airtight
6. Place the barrel in the coolest cellar possible and let it stand for twelve weeks
7. Tap onto bottles by means of a pipe attached at the bottom of the barrel; the bottles must be as strong as Champagne bottles, wound around the stopper with wire, sealed with pitch, laid down, and drunk after 8 weeks
8. If desired, a large piece of sugar can be placed in each bottle, which will save many bottles that otherwise tend to burst frequently
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Another Kind of Strong Broth
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound kidney fat, cut into slices
- several large onions, cut into slices
- 4 pounds veal meat and bones
- 4 pounds beef
- one old hen
- 3 measures water
- bouillon
- celery
- parsnip
- yellow turnips

## Directions

1. Cut the kidney fat and onions into slices
2. Place 4 pounds veal meat and bones, 4 pounds beef, and one old hen in a casserole
3. Set it over charcoal fire and pour 3 measures water into it
4. Let it draw until it turns yellow
5. Fill it up with bouillon
6. Cut celery, parsnip, and yellow turnips into it
7. Skim it frequently and continue cooking until everything is quite soft
8. Skim off the fat
9. Strain the consommé through a fine napkin to preserve it for further use
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Anspeisung's Schlackwurst nach Braunschweiger Art (Braunschweig-Style Slackwurst)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two parts lean meat
- One part fat
- Ham
- Loin bacon
- Good meat without sinews
- Salt
- Black pepper, several handfuls

## Directions

1. Cut all meat into small pieces
2. Remove every sinew, even the smallest, with a knife
3. Chop very finely
4. Sprinkle several handfuls of salt and about one handful of ordinary black pepper over it
5. Chop again diligently and continuously until no peppercorn remains whole
6. Continue chopping until all pepper is completely minced
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Apfel im Schlafrock (Apple in Nightgown)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- marmalade or preserved fruit as desired
- butter dough
- eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the core casing of the apples by pressing a small corer into the side of the stem and inserting a narrow knife into the corer so that it reaches the core casing, twist slightly and pull it out with the casing
2. The apples must not be pierced through, but a knife-back-width thick bottom must remain
3. Peel the apples and fill the empty space created by the coring with desired marmalade or preserved fruit
4. Cut knife-back-width rolled out butter dough into squares that must correspond to the size of the apples
5. Brush all edges of each square with beaten egg, place an apple in the middle of each square with the open side facing up
6. Bend up the 4 corners and fasten them together above the middle of the apple
7. Place the apples in nightgown on a sheet dampened with water and brush the dough with beaten egg so that a knife-back-width border remains dry on each side
8. Place them in the hot oven and bake for 1.5 to 2.5 hours
9. After this time, remove them from the sheet with a very thin knife, arrange them on a plate and sprinkle them heavily with sugar

title: Apfel im Schlafrock (Apples in Pajamas)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg butter
- 21 dkg flour
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- salt
- 1 egg
- water
- 8-1 kg small Maschanzker apples
- 12-14 pieces cube sugar
- 1 scoop water

## Directions

1. Make a butter dough and roll out thin
2. Cut into squares and place a half or whole over-stewed apple in the middle
3. Brush corners with egg white and press crosswise over the apple
4. Bake on an unbuttered sheet at high heat until light brown
5. Sprinkle with sugar and serve cooled

title: Apfel Mariane in Brotcrustén (Charlotte in Bread Crusts)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Muscatel apples
- sugar
- 2 tablespoons marmalade
- black or wheat bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Peeled and sliced Muscatel apples are put into a saucepan with the necessary sugar and covered, stewed until soft, then passed through a sieve into another saucepan, stirred well with 2 tablespoons marmalade, brought to a boil and then set aside. Now cut from black or wheat bread a slice as thick as a finger's width, which is adapted to the size of the short saucepan to be used; furthermore 2 fingers wide and 2 fingers long strips are cut, which are dipped with the previously cut bread slice in hot butter. Then cover the bottom of a buttered short saucepan with the bread slice, line the side wall with the 2 finger wide bread strips so that one lies close beside the other. Then put the apple mass indicated above on top, cover it with a bread slice and then bake in the oven at low heat for 1 hour. Turn the mold onto a dish, sprinkle the baked food with finely pounded sugar, or cover it with dissolved marmalade, or decorate it as desired with white apple puree. The preparation of this dish requires great care, as it can easily fall apart through a small oversight when turning out.

title: Apfel Schmarn (Apple Scramble)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deka apples (10 pieces)
- 1/4 liter milk
- 5 whole eggs
- 1/4 liter milk
- 4 deka sugar
- 12 deka hot butter

## Directions

1. Slice the apples thinly and place them in a casserole
2. Pour 1/4 liter milk over them
3. Beat together 5 whole eggs, 1/4 liter milk, and 4 deka sugar
4. Pour this mixture over the apples
5. Place the mixture in a casserole containing 12 deka very hot butter
6. Let it steam in the oven for 15 to 20 minutes
7. If desired, mix lingonberries and raisins into the mixture before baking
8. When finished steaming, arrange the Schmarn on a plate using a pastry server
9. Sprinkle with vanilla sugar

title: Apfel à la Mannheim (Apples à la Mannheim)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dkg butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 3 bread rolls soaked in water
- 3 stiffly beaten egg whites
- 7 dkg swelled, grated almonds
- stewed cooled apples
- currants

## Directions

1. Cream together the butter, egg yolks, sugar, squeezed bread rolls, egg whites and almonds
2. Fill half the mass into a buttered baking pan
3. Spread the stewed cooled apples on top, scatter currants over them
4. Spread the remaining batter on top and bake in the oven at moderate heat
5. Cut into pieces, remove with a spatula, sprinkle with sugar and serve warm

title: Apfel-Charlotte (Apple Charlotte)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deka flour
- approximately 24 cooking apples
- sugar as needed
- marmalade as desired
- eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare a butter dough of finest quality from 28 Deka flour (No. 1035) and let it rest covered in a cool place
2. Meanwhile prepare the filling: peel about 24 cooking apples, remove the core casings, cut the apples into wedges and put them in a casserole, sweeten as desired and steam covered without water or other liquid until no liquid remains
3. Add some spoonfuls of desired marmalade, stir well together and let it come to a boil once, then set aside and let cool
4. After the butter dough has rested for 1 hour, roll it out on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a knife back, cut into two equal parts
5. Roll one part onto a strudel roller and unroll it without damage onto a sheet dampened with water
6. Brush the dough with the cold apple marmalade leaving a thumb-width border free on all 4 sides, brush this border with beaten egg
7. Roll the second part of the rolled out butter dough onto the strudel roller and unroll it over the apples so that the edges lie evenly together and the side that was on the board comes on top
8. Lightly press the edges together, make shallow cuts in the upper dough layer with a knife, brush the dough with beaten egg but leave the edges knife-back-width dry
9. Place these prepared apple charlottes in the fairly hot oven and bake for 1.5 to 3.5 hours
10. When the dough is half baked, remove the sheet, sprinkle the dough heavily with finely ground sugar and finish baking
11. When the apple charlotte has a brown color and the sugar looks glassy, loosen it from the sheet with a very thin knife, cut into desired pieces, arrange on a plate and serve warm or cold as desired

title: Apfel-Gefrorne mit Rum oder Arrak in Gläsern (Apple Frozen with Rum or Arrack in Glasses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Apple-Frozen, prepared and finely worked
- 1/4 liter Rum or Arrack
- Stiffly beaten snow from 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. After an Apple-Frozen has been prepared and finely worked, fold it well with 1/4 liter Rum or Arrack and the stiffly beaten snow from 3 egg whites.
2. Work it well and serve in glasses.

title: Apfel-Getränk (Apple Beverage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 washed, unsalted, sour apples
- 0.75 liter water
- Sugar
- Wine
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the apples into 4 pieces and remove the core.
2. Put them in a coffee pot and boil them with 0.75 liter of water until quite soft.
3. Strain them through a sieve or cloth and add sugar to taste, or also wine or lemon juice.

title: Apfel-Kochli (Apple Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- About 12 peeled apples
- Water and necessary sugar
- 8 Deka butter, beaten
- 16 Deka lemon sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- 8 Deka white bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut peeled apples into thin slices
2. Stew covered with some water and necessary sugar until soft
3. Place on a sieve, let juice drain, and pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
4. Mix 8 Deka beaten butter with 16 Deka lemon sugar
5. Add 8 egg yolks one after another and stir for ¼ hour
6. Add the cold apples and lightly mix in the snow from 8 egg whites simultaneously with 8 Deka white bread crumbs
7. Fill this mixture into a butter-brushed, flour-dusted cake mold
8. Bake for ¾ hour in the oven
9. Remove the rim and arrange the souffle on a cake plate in a bowl, sprinkling with sugar and serving

title: Apfel-Strudel (Apple Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Strudel dough made from 1/2 liter flour
- crème made from 12 deca butter and 16 deca vanilla sugar, beaten cold
- 6 egg yolks, stirred in gradually
- 6 egg whites, snow, lightly mixed in
- peeled apples, cut into thin slices
- selected raisins
- currants
- finely ground sugar
- cinnamon, as desired

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough from 1/2 liter flour and let it rest
2. Beat 12 deca butter with 16 deca vanilla sugar until cold
3. Gradually stir in 6 egg yolks
4. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
5. Stretch the dough until thin
6. Spread with the crème mixture
7. Layer with peeled apples cut into thin slices
8. Sprinkle with selected raisins, currants, and finely ground sugar, and cinnamon as desired
9. Treat further as for Ham Strudel (No. 1229)
10. Cut into desired pieces and arrange in a bowl
11. Serve dusted with sugar

title: Apfel-Strudel (Apple Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ liter flour
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- peeled apples
- raisins
- currants
- sugar
- cinnamon (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough from ½ liter flour for baking
2. After stretching it finely, sprinkle with breadcrumbs toasted in butter
3. Lay peeled apples cut into thin slices on top
4. Sprinkle with selected raisins, currants, and finely crushed sugar, and optionally with cinnamon
5. Treat further as described in the Ham Strudel recipe
6. Cut into desired pieces, arrange on a serving dish, and serve sprinkled with sugar

title: Apfel-Suppe (Apple Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 peeled Reinette or similar apples
- 1/4 liter Rhine wine or white wine
- 32 deca sugar
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 1/2 liter water
- 1/2 kilo crushed sugar
- 24 deca cleaned currants
- 24 deca raisins
- 1/2 liter water

## Directions

1. Cut one-third of 15 peeled apples into slices, add 1/4 liter Rhine wine or white wine, 32 deca sugar, the juice of 1 lemon and let simmer.
2. Cook the remaining, similarly thinly cut apples in 1/2 liter water and the juice of 1 lemon, strain through a sieve and stir this together with 1/2 kilo crushed sugar, the juice of 1 lemon and 1 1/2 liters good wine.
3. Clean and search through 24 deca currants and an equal amount of raisins, let them simmer slightly in 1/2 liter water, strain through a sieve and pour this with the apple slices into the soup bowl, then place on ice.
4. Serve this soup in the same manner as the Strawberry Soup.

title: Apfelcompot mit Zitris-Glut (Apple Compote with Citrus Glaze)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- About 10 apples (Pearmain or Casseler apples)
- Lemon juice
- Cold water
- 1/8 liter boiling water
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 10 deka sugar
- Wine, as desired
- 1 stick lemon peel
- Cinnamon, as desired
- Redcurrant or desired marmalade
- Gelée cut into strips

## Directions

1. Halve the apples and remove the seed cavity, then peel them
2. Rub the apples with lemon juice and immediately place them in cold water with lemon juice to prevent discoloration
3. In a saucepan, combine 1/8 liter boiling water with the juice of 1 lemon, 10 deka sugar, and wine as desired
4. Add the apples to this mixture and cook covered until fairly tender
5. Remove the apples and let them cool on a covered dish
6. Cook the remaining liquid uncovered with 1 stick of lemon peel and cinnamon as desired until the syrup begins to thicken
7. Strain the syrup through a cloth into a dish and let it cool
8. Arrange the apples on a compote dish in a crown shape or as desired
9. Pour the syrup over the apples and decorate with redcurrant or desired marmalade and gelée cut into strips

title: Apfelkren (Apple Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Horseradish
- Salt
- Sugar
- Vinegar
- Cold water

## Directions

1. Peel and grate the apples
2. Grate the horseradish in the same manner
3. Mix together with salt, sugar, vinegar, and cold water
4. Cover in a porcelain bowl and let stand until serving

title: Apfelkuchen (Apple Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 210 grams flour
- 140 grams butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 1-2 tablespoons milk
- 70 grams sugar
- 1 egg, beaten

## Directions

1. Make a crumbly dough from flour, butter, egg yolks, milk, and sugar
2. Let rest, then divide into 2 equal parts
3. Roll out both parts equally
4. Place one part on an unbutterd baking sheet
5. Spread the filling on top
6. Place second part on top and press edges together
7. Brush with beaten egg
8. Bake at high heat until light brown
9. Cut into pieces, sprinkle with sugar, and serve cooled

title: Apfelkuchen Fülle (Apple Cake Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kg apples
- 6-8 pieces cube sugar
- small amount of water
- 50 grams raisins

## Directions

1. Peel and core the apples, cut into thin slices
2. Stew gently with cube sugar and a small amount of water until soft
3. Mix in raisins
4. Use filling only when cooled and without liquid

title: Apfels-Charlotte mit Biscuit (Charlotte with Biscuit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Muscatel apples
- sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- butter
- finely pounded sugar
- baked biscuit
- dissolved marmalade

## Directions

1. From Muscatel apples the core is removed, the tips are cut, then cut in half, put into boiling sugar water into which the juice of 1 lemon has been dripped, covered and stewed until soft, then removed with a spoon and placed on a sieve. Then a short saucepan brushed with butter and dusted with finely pounded sugar is lined with baked biscuit, covering the bottom with a suitably sized slice and the side wall of the saucepan with 2 finger wide and 2 finger long strips, of which the shiny side comes outward. The bottom is now lined with the soft-stewed apples, covered with baked biscuit which is brushed with melted marmalade, then covered with apples and so continues until the saucepan is full. It is now placed in the oven and the apple charlotte is baked at low heat for 3/4 hour.

title: Apfelsteiflisch (Apple-Spiced Pickled Meat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece of meat from the brisket (beef tongue part), about 4 kilos
- about 5 tablespoons salt
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 piece of onion cut in leaf-like pieces
- some garlic
- a few bunches of mugwort
- wintergreen and basil if desired
- some rosemary
- 5 juniper berries
- various spices
- salt water
- about 3 tablespoons saltpeter (if using within 3 days)
- water
- 1/2 liter wine
- 1/4 liter vinegar
- soup or water
- 16 decagrams bacon
- yellow turnips and celery cut in leaf-like pieces
- 1 piece of black bread
- 1/2 liter cream
- capers
- green parsley

## Directions

1. Grind all spices very finely.
2. Rub the meat thoroughly with the spice mixture.
3. Place in an earthen vessel, cover with a wooden lid, and weight down with a stone.
4. Store in a cool place for 14 days.
5. If using within 3 days, add about 3 tablespoons of saltpeter.
6. After pickling, boil the meat in water and serve with any piquant sauce or French mustard.
7. Alternatively, braise it covered with 1/2 liter wine, 1/4 liter vinegar, and some soup or water (enough to come up over the meat), 16 decagrams of bacon, some yellow turnips and celery cut in leaf-like pieces.
8. When half-braised, add 1 piece of black bread.
9. When the meat is tender, remove it, skim fat from the sauce, pass through a sieve, return the meat to the sauce, and pour 1/2 liter of cream with some capers over it.
10. Let braise for another 1/2 hour.
11. When serving, garnish with green parsley and capers.

title: Apfelstrudel von Buttetertig (Apple Strudel of Puff Pastry)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry (No. 1045)
- Peeled apples, cut into thin slices
- Bread crumbs
- Raisins, pitted
- Powdered sugar, finely ground
- Beaten eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. Roll out the puff pastry (No. 1045), both 'finest' and 'other' kind, on a flour-dusted board knife-back-thick.
2. Cut it into strips which are 3 hand-widths wide, as required by the size of the board on which it will be baked.
3. Lay peeled, thinly sliced apples in the center of this pastry strip, one hand-width wide, along its length.
4. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and pitted raisins and dust heavily with finely ground powdered sugar.
5. Brush the edges of both narrow sides with beaten eggs and fold the empty pastry on both sides over the apples so that the pastry is one finger-width apart.
6. Grasp the strudel at both narrow sides with your hands and lift it as quickly as possible onto a board moistened with water.
7. Make several cuts with a knife along the length on both sides of the pastry, brush with beaten eggs, and if desired lay small pastry strips on top which are also brushed.
8. If you have more than one strudel, place them side by side at three-finger distance apart if the board width permits, lay a moistened wooden spoon or moistened paper between them, and bake at fairly strong heat in the oven for 3/4 to 1 hour.
9. After this time, loosen the baked strudel with a very thin knife, cut it on the board into desired pieces, arrange these elevated on a dish, and serve hot or cold as desired.

title: Apfelſpalten (Apple Slices in Batter)
body:
## Ingredients

- wine
- 2-3 tablespoons sugar
- 1-2 eggs
- 16-18 dkg flour
- apples
- hot lard

## Directions

1. Beat together the wine, 2-3 tablespoons sugar, 1-2 eggs, and 16-18 dkg flour until smooth.
2. Peel the apples, core them using an apple corer without separating them, and cut into slices.
3. Dip the apple slices in the batter.
4. Fry them in plenty of hot lard on both sides until light brown.
5. Drain and sprinkle with sugar.
6. Serve warm.

title: Appetitbrötchen (Savory Appetizer Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal leftovers or 30 dkg freshly braised veal shoulder
- 3 passed anchovies
- salt
- bread rolls, buttered

## Directions

1. Grind veal leftovers or veal shoulder twice and pass through a sieve
2. Mix with 3 passed anchovies and salt
3. Spread on buttered bread rolls
4. Serve

title: Appetitschniten (Savory Appetizer Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dkg ham, finely chopped
- 4 dkg tongue, finely chopped
- 4 dkg Veronese salami, finely chopped
- 3 dkg grated Emmental cheese
- 1 hard-boiled egg
- 1 anchovy, passed
- 6 dkg butter
- salt
- bread or roll slices
- parsley for decoration
- butter balls

## Directions

1. Chop ham, tongue, and salami very finely
2. Mix with grated Emmental, hard-boiled egg, and passed anchovy
3. Stir into 6 dkg butter and salt
4. Spread on bread or roll slices
5. Arrange slices in a wreath shape on a platter
6. Place butter balls in the center
7. Decorate with parsley

title: Apple Charlotte (Aepfel-Charlotte)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- several beautiful apples
- sugar
- lemon peel
- water
- Mundgemmel (a type of roll)
- melted butter
- apricot marmalade
- optional: raisins, orange peel, citron peel, cinnamon, or crushed vanilla

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, cut them into pieces, and cook with sugar, lemon peel, and a little water into a marmalade.
2. Allow the marmalade to cool.
3. Slice a Mundgemmel into thin slices and dip each piece into melted butter.
4. Lay them symmetrically one over the other in a pan, spreading the apple marmalade over them but leaving a hole in the middle.
5. Fill the hole in the middle with apricot marmalade.
6. Cover the top completely with bread slices dipped in butter.
7. Place in a well-heated oven and bake for three-quarters of an hour.
8. If desired, add large and small raisins, orange peel or citron peel, cinnamon, or some crushed vanilla.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Apple Frozen Dessert (Glace aux pommes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- About 10 pieces of Kaiserapfel, Pastanäpfel, Tyrolean, or Rosmarin apples
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 16 deca grams sugar
- 1/2 liter cold water
- 16 deca grams sugar
- 1/2 liter cold water
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Peel about 10 apples, cut them into wedges, and quickly rub them with lemon juice
2. Pound them very finely with 16 deca grams sugar in a stone mortar, adding 1/2 liter cold water gradually
3. Place this mass in a porcelain bowl
4. Dissolve 16 deca grams sugar in 1/2 liter cold water, mix this with the juice of 1 lemon into the apple mass, and let it sit covered for 1 hour
5. Pass it through a coarse sieve, weigh it to 22 degrees, and proceed as with the 'Ordinary Strawberry Frozen Dessert' (Recipe 1746)

title: Apple Horseradish
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 apples
- 6 tablespoons horseradish
- salt
- sugar
- vinegar
- cold water

## Directions

1. Peel and grate the apples
2. Grate the horseradish as well
3. Mix with salt, a little sugar, vinegar, and cold water
4. Let stand covered in a porcelain bowl until serving

title: Apple Horseradish (Apple Horseradish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large apples
- cold water
- lemon juice or vinegar
- dissolved alum (optional)
- 1 liter cold water
- 6 ounces sugar
- juice from ½ lemon
- 8 ounces grated rolls soaked in cold water and well pressed out
- salt
- 2 tablespoons grated horseradish
- 1 tablespoon oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut 6 large apples into quarters, peel them and place in 1 liter of cold water which has been soured with lemon juice or vinegar (or alternatively a pinch of dissolved alum can be added)
2. This souring keeps the apples white
3. Meanwhile, place 1 liter of cold water, 6 ounces sugar, and the juice of ½ lemon in a casserole
4. As soon as this sugar water boils, add the apples, cover them and let them stew until soft
5. Place the apples on a sieve and pass 8 ounces of grated rolls soaked in cold water and well pressed out into a deep bowl
6. When the apples are completely softened, add some salt, 2 tablespoons grated horseradish, 1 tablespoon oil, and vinegar as needed, and stir into a thick sauce
7. If it should be not sweet enough, add the necessary sugar

title: Apple Jelly (Apfels Saft-Gelee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 pieces of Maasanian or Tafeliner apples
- 2 liters water
- Sugar equal in weight to the juice
- A few drops of elderberry juice (optional, for coloring)

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the apples in half
2. Boil them quickly in a casserole with 2 liters of water until soft
3. Strain through a cloth
4. Add to this juice an equal amount of sugar as the weight of the juice and boil it again quickly until it becomes thick, removing the foam as it forms
5. Fill the jelly into glasses, bind these and boil for 3/4 hour in a steam bath
6. If desired, the jelly can be colored red with a few drops of elderberry juice before filling into glasses

title: Apple Pudding (Pouding aux pommes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams peeled, diced Mischantscher apples
- 0.5 ounce water
- 0.5 ounce coffee
- 16 decagrams sugar
- 14 decagrams whipped butter
- 16 decagrams finely sifted sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 6 decagrams white bread crumbs
- butter for the mold
- flour for the mold

## Directions

1. Place 16 decagrams peeled, diced apples in a saucepan
2. Add 0.5 ounce water, 0.5 ounce coffee, and 16 decagrams sugar
3. Stew until soft and set aside to cool
4. Beat 14 decagrams whipped butter with 16 decagrams finely sifted sugar well
5. Add 6 egg yolks one by one and beat
6. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites simultaneously with 6 decagrams white bread crumbs
7. Butter a mold and dust with flour
8. Spread a portion of the butter mixture in the mold
9. Layer with cooked apples, cover with more butter mixture
10. Continue alternating until all ingredients are used
11. Pour the juice from the stewed apples over the top
12. Bake at moderate heat for 1/2 hour in the oven

title: Apple Purée (Purée de pommes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 8 Muskät apples
- 12 tablespoons sugar
- 4 tablespoons white wine or juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Peel about 8 Muskät apples
2. Cut into pieces
3. Place in a casserole with 12 tablespoons sugar and 4 tablespoons white wine or lemon juice
4. Braise until soft
5. Pass through a sieve
6. Arrange heaped on a platter
7. Sprinkle with fine sugar and, if desired, immediately burn the sugar with a glowing iron

title: Apples en Balon (Aepfel en balon)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- preserves (such as gooseberries or others)
- good butter dough
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the apples as above.
2. Fill with preserves.
3. Wrap in a piece of good butter dough.
4. Arrange on a tart pan.
5. Bake slowly in the oven.
6. Glaze with sugar.
7. The same can also be made with pears.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Apples in Portuguese Style (Aepfel auf portugieſiſche Art)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Macheanz apples
- preserves or pistachio cream
- wine dough
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and hollow out the center.
2. Fill with preserves or pistachio cream.
3. Wrap in wine dough.
4. Bake very slowly.
5. Glaze with sugar.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Apples in Sauce
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled apples
- water
- wine
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut peeled apples into thin slices
2. Braise with some water, several spoonfuls of wine, and as much sugar as desired
3. Make a butter sauce
4. Stir it with the juice from the apples
5. Add the apples to it
6. Let simmer

title: Apricot Compote (Compot von Aprikoſen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots (early ones without removing kernels or skin; later ones with kernels and skin removed)
- water
- sugar (clarified)
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Leave the kernels in early apricots and do not peel them; for later ones, remove the kernels and skin once they are larger.
2. Blanch the apricots lightly in water.
3. Once they become soft, place them on a cloth to drain and cool.
4. Place them in clarified sugar.
5. Bring to the boil several times and skim off the foam.
6. Keep the syrup short cooked.
7. Arrange the apricots on a compote dish and pour the syrup over them.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Apricot Frozen Dessert (Glace aux abricots)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 to 20 pieces ripe apricots
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 28 deca grams sugar
- 1/4 liter cold water

## Directions

1. Pass 15 to 20 pieces of ripe apricots through a fine sieve into a deep bowl
2. Add the juice of 2 lemons and 28 deca grams sugar dissolved in 1/4 liter cold water to this mass
3. Crush the stones of the apricots somewhat and stir them into the mixture
4. Let this sit covered for 1 hour, then weigh it to 22 degrees, strain it through a fine sieve, and proceed further as with the 'Ordinary Strawberry Frozen Dessert' (Recipe 1746)

title: Apricot Pastries (Aprikoſen-Küchlein)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful ripe apricots
- crushed sugar
- fruit brandy (Franzbrantwein)

## Directions

1. Select beautiful ripe apricots, peel off the skin, and cut into two pieces.
2. Marinate with crushed sugar and fruit brandy.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aprikose Knödel von Erdäpfelteig (Apricot Dumplings with Potato Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- potato dough as for Abgeschmalzene Erdäpfelnudeln, Recipe 1209
- apricots

## Directions

1. Prepare potato dough as specified in Abgeschmalzene Erdäpfelnudeln (Recipe 1209)
2. Roll it out on a flour-dusted board very thin
3. Proceed as specified in Zwetschkenknödel von Strudelteig (Recipe 1223)

title: Aprikosen-Küchlein (Apricot Fritters)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful ripe apricots
- Crushed sugar
- Fruit brandy
- Wine batter

## Directions

1. Select beautiful ripe apricots, remove the skin, cut into two pieces, then marinate with crushed sugar and fruit brandy.
2. Remove them from the marinade, dry them off, and coat them in wine batter.
3. Bake them quickly, glaze them lastly with sugar, and serve them to the table.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aprikoſen-Gefrornes (Apricot Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots (Marillen), same quantity as for pear ice cream
- Three quarter pound clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Take as many apricots as for pear ice cream
2. When they are well pressed through, stir in three quarter pound clarified sugar until everything is well mixed
3. Pour into the freezing pan
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Artichoke Egg Dish
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 boiled potatoes, small variety
- 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped parsley
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Hard-boiled eggs, sliced
- Sorrel, diced finely
- 1/2 liter cream
- 3 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Peel 12 boiled small potatoes and slice them thinly.
2. Mix with 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped parsley and some salt.
3. Butter a casserole, dust with flour, and line the bottom with the sliced potatoes.
4. Sprinkle with hard-boiled eggs cut into slices and finely diced sorrel.
5. Beat 1/2 liter cream with 3 whole eggs and pour some of this mixture over the eggs and sorrel.

title: Artichokes (Artichaute)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt water
- lemon juice
- cold water
- broth
- butter sauce
- peas
- ham slices or cutlets

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the artichokes and remove the three lowest leaves
2. Cut off the tip with a strong scissors 2 fingers width
3. Place the artichokes in boiling salted water with lemon juice and leave until the leaves can be easily pulled from the crown
4. Transfer to cold water, hollow out the hairy middle with a teaspoon, place in a shallow casserole and cover with boiling well-strained broth
5. Before serving, fill the hot artichokes with hot braised peas, heap on a platter, pour butter sauce over them and garnish with various cutlets or ham slices fried in butter
6. Note: if too little lemon juice is added to the salt water, the artichokes will lose color

title: Artichokes with Butter Sauce (Artichauts, sauce au beurre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt water
- lemon juice
- butter sauce
- white wine or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare as mentioned above, but cut the leaves more, closer to the white flesh, and cook them until soft as above
2. Before serving, pour strong butter sauce over them, which has been cooked with lemon juice or good white wine

title: Artichokes with Mustard Sauce (Artichauts sauce remoulade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt water
- lemon juice
- cold mustard sauce
- parsley or watercress for garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the artichokes as previously described, cook them in salted water with lemon juice and let them cool in their broth
2. Before serving, arrange them so they stand easily and securely and the whole looks fan-like
3. Place cold mustard sauce in the center and decorate the platter with peeled gherkins, capers, marinated fish, olives, and if available, small fatty pieces
4. Sprinkle the artichokes with cold mustard sauce and cover with parsley or watercress
5. Serve as an appetizer or cold dish

title: Artischocken mit grünen Erbsen (Artichokes with Green Peas)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Green peas
- Butter
- Green parsley
- Veal, finely diced and slightly overbrowned
- Nutmeg
- Ground pepper
- Cream
- Artichokes
- Salt water
- Butter sauce
- Fried goose liver

## Directions

1. Braise green peas with butter and green parsley
2. Add to a light butter sauce with finely diced, slightly overbrowned veal, a little nutmeg, some ground pepper, and a spoonful of cream
3. Boil artichokes in salt water and carefully remove the insides
4. Fill the artichokes with the pea mixture
5. Place the filled artichokes in the remaining butter sauce and let them cook well together
6. Garnish with fried goose liver
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Artischocken zu erhalten (Artichokes Preserved)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- water
- artichokes

## Directions

1. Sprinkle salt on the bottom of a clean barrel, place the cut stems of the artichokes upright, reverse the following ones and position them with the tips between the tips of the lower ones, and continue until the barrel is full.
2. Have the cooper seal it after first boring a hole in the upper bottom.
3. Take a good portion of flowing water and salt, let it boil for quite a long time, and let it cool again, and pour it through the hole into the artichokes.
4. When the barrel is full, seal the hole firmly with a stopper, place it in the cellar, and shake it occasionally; this way they will keep for a very long time.
5. Alternatively, before placing them in the barrel, you may cook them in the manner of asparagus, but they must lie in the warm water for 2 hours and dry for the same length of time before being placed in the barrel.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Artischockensalat (Artichoke Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Oil
- Mixed spices
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the artichokes clean and cut off the tips
2. Cook them until soft
3. Remove the rough and fibrous parts
4. Arrange them on an oiled grate, drizzled with fine oil and sprinkled with mixed spices
5. Roast them slowly
6. Place them on a serving dish
7. Dress with vinegar and oil and serve cold at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Asparagus (Asperges)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt
- butter
- bread crumbs
- Maitre Sauce

## Directions

1. When cleaning asparagus, hold the stalk in your hand and use a knife to remove the thin, closely attached, scale-like leaves from the top downward
2. Lightly scrape the stem for over half its length from the top
3. Lay it in cold water
4. Tie the asparagus into bundles and cut the bottom even
5. Place it in boiling salted water and let it cook soft
6. Lay a napkin on a platter and arrange the asparagus on it
7. Serve hot butter with toasted bread crumbs in one sauce boat and Maitre Sauce in another
8. If placing the asparagus on an uncovered platter, pour the butter sauce or butter with bread crumbs over it
9. If two bundles are cooked, arrange them on the platter so the heads face each other and the stems lie outward at the edge of the platter

title: Asparagus or Salsify with Butter (Spargel oder Schwarzwurzeln mit Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bunch asparagus
- ½ kg salsify
- salt
- hot butter
- breadcrumbs
- vinegar
- oil
- Hollandaise sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the asparagus carefully toward the head, cut off the base and wash
2. Tie the asparagus plants loosely together
3. Place them in an asparagus pan or skillet in cold salted water
4. Cook for 20-30 minutes
5. They are done when they pierce easily with a knife
6. Lift them carefully with a skimmer onto a long platter
7. Pour over breadcrumbs browned in hot butter
8. Can also be cooled, served as a salad with vinegar and oil, or warm with Hollandaise sauce
9. Salsify is scraped, washed and quickly placed in milk water to keep white
10. Then cook like asparagus in salted water for 15-20 minutes
11. Serve with butter and breadcrumbs or as a salad

title: Asparagus with Mayonnaise (Mayonnaise d'Asperges)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Cook the asparagus until soft and let it cool
2. Pour mayonnaise over it or serve both separately

title: Asparagus with Morels (Asperges aux Morilles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- morels
- asparagus
- butter
- fresh parsley
- butter sauce
- milk cream
- asparagus water

## Directions

1. Cut off the stems of the morels
2. After washing the heads, cut them into rings
3. Braise these in butter with fresh parsley
4. Prepare a butter sauce and dilute it with milk cream and some asparagus water
5. Reduce it well
6. Pour this sauce over ordinarily cooked asparagus and serve with the morels

title: Asparagus with Morels (Asperges aux morilles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Morels
- Asparagus
- Butter
- Fresh parsley
- Cream
- Asparagus water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the morels; wash the heads, cut them into strips, and braise them in butter with fresh parsley.
2. Prepare a butter sauce with cream and some asparagus water and cook it well.
3. Pour this sauce over the boiled asparagus prepared in the usual way and serve with the morels.

title: Aspik (Aspic Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ calf's head
- 1 calf's foot
- 2 pork feet
- 3/4 kg pork rind
- 25 dkg hind beef shank
- 8 dkg beef liver
- 8 Heller cleaned, washed vegetables (leafy cut)
- 1 large onion
- 2 bay leaves
- 10 peppercorns and spice berries
- thyme
- 1 spoon salt
- 6 spoons vinegar
- 4 liters water

## Directions

1. Clean and wash all the meat and ingredients well several times.
2. Bring to a boil with cleaned, washed, leafy-cut vegetables, onion, bay leaves, peppercorns, spice berries, thyme, salt, and vinegar in an enameled pot with 4 liters of water.
3. Skim off foam.
4. Cook slowly for 3 hours, skimming frequently.
5. Season again to taste with salt and acid.
6. Cook slowly for another hour.
7. Strain through a sieve into a vessel.
8. Let stand until the next day.
9. Remove the fat from the top with a spoon the next day.
10. Place the vessel on the heat until the aspic is melted.
11. Pour into a larger pot; season with a little wine and lemon juice.
12. Add 6 firm snow and whisk the mass until very hot, almost boiling.
13. Let the pot stand on the edge of the stove until the aspic settles.
