14. Place an inverted stool on a chair, tie a hot-water-dipped, well-wrung linen cloth or napkin to the 4 feet; place the chair near the stove with a bowl underneath the cloth.
15. Remove the cloudy, dirty snow from the top of the aspic; pour the aspic spoonful by spoonful onto the cloth.
16. Do not stir the aspic.
17. If it is not clear, place another bowl under the cloth and let the aspic run through again.
18. It must be clear, golden yellow, and slightly sour.
19. Fill the still-warm aspic into molds rinsed with water.
20. Let it set in the molds.
21. To unmold, hold the form in hot water for a moment, or remove with a silver spoon and serve broken into pieces.
22. Garnish cold fish (such as pike-perch or pike) and goose liver with it; also give to the sick.
23. For red aspic, add a few drops of beet juice to the molds before filling.
24. It can be kept for weeks by pouring it into preserving jars, covering them well with parchment paper, letting it set in steam for 1 hour, and storing in a cool place. Otherwise it keeps only 2–3 days.

title: Assiette von Fisch (Fish Platter)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg Kipfler potatoes
- 10 h mixed greens (lamb's lettuce or chicory)
- 4 Kiel sprats
- 1 milk herring
- 1 smoked herring
- 8 anchovies
- 1 piece of eel
- 4 dkg salmon
- 2 lemons
- 2 hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Wash the sprats and anchovies; cut off heads.
2. Remove heads from herring and smoked herring; also remove the backbone, wash inside and out, and cut into pieces.
3. Boil the potatoes and arrange them in a salad.
4. On a glass platter, pile the potato salad in a mountain shape.
5. Around it arrange alternately the greens, hard-boiled egg slices and lemon slices (garnished with caviar if desired), cut eel, herring, and smoked herring.
6. Place the sprats and other small fish crosswise on top of the potato salad.
7. Top with a lemon basket, garnished with caviar if desired.
8. The potato salad may be omitted and the platter filled only with fish, though more fish will be needed.

title: Aueraahn (Capercaillie)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Bacon
- Onions
- Celery root
- Various vegetables, 5 grains each
- 5 crushed juniper berries
- 40 grams pork cutlets
- 1 large or 2 small goose livers
- 1/4 liter white wine
- Equal amount of broth
- Lard
- Salt
- Pepper
- 2 egg yolks
- 10 grams oil
- 1/2 liter melted aspic
- 1 tablespoon French mustard
- Lemon juice or vinegar
- Tongue
- Hard-boiled egg white
- Salad leaves
- Capers

## Directions

1. Roast 3 pieces onion, 1 piece yellow turnip, parsley and celery, all cut into leaves, with cleaned and plucked stomach, crop and liver of the capercaillie, cut into pieces
2. Add 1/2 quart wine, equal amount vinegar and water, 2 bay leaves, 3 sprigs thyme, 7 juniper berries, 5 grains of each spice
3. Boil for approximately 3/4 hours, cool and pour over the dressed capercaillie
4. Cover with bacon strips and buttered paper and let stand 3-4 days in a cool place, turning daily
5. On the second day, remove, cool, place in a casserole with the cooking liquid, cover with bacon strips and braise covered in the oven
6. After some time, add thinly sliced black bread and 5 nutmeg kernels, also some Madeira wine and truffle scraps if available
7. Braise covered until tender, requiring 2-3 hours total
8. Transfer to a platter, remove string, carve neatly and arrange
9. Skim fat from sauce, strain and reduce, add thinly sliced truffles and serve
10. Serve with a Palais-Spöbel, puff pastry or desired compotes

title: Auerhahn, gebraten (Roasted Capercaillie)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one capercaillie
- salt
- crushed rowan berries
- basil
- rosemary
- wine
- vinegar
- onions
- bay leaves
- lard for larding
- melted lard for basting
- lemon peel for garnish
- anchovy or caper sauce

## Directions

1. Pluck the capercaillie, wash it well, salt it thoroughly and rub it with crushed rowan berries, basil and rosemary
2. Let it lie for one hour, then make a marinade from wine, vinegar, onions and bay leaves and pour it over hot
3. If it is old, it must be marinated for two to three days and poured over with hot marinade twice daily; if it is young, marinate it for only one day
4. Then lard it beautifully with lard, roast it slowly on a spit, baste it frequently with melted lard
5. Make a quick anchovy or caper sauce from the drippings and the marinade
6. Arrange it on a dish, garnish it with lemon peel and serve the sauce in a bowl alongside
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aufgelaufenes Kipfelkoch (Risen Crescent Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kipfel or horns or other tender baked goods
- milk
- 4 loth butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- small raisins, cleaned and washed
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut 4 kipfel or horns or other tender baked goods into cubes and soak in milk
2. Beat 4 loth butter
3. Beat in 2 whole eggs and 4 egg yolks
4. Mix this with well-cleaned and washed small raisins, sugar and cinnamon
5. Butter a pan well
6. Pour everything in and bake with heat from above and below
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aufgelaufenes Reißkoch (Risen Rice Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maß milk
- 1/4 pound washed rice
- 1/4 pound lard
- 6 loth finely ground almonds
- 6 loth sifted sugar
- zest of 1 lemon
- 5 eggs
- 6 egg yolks
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook 1/2 maß milk in a pot
2. Add 1/4 pound washed rice and cook it in the milk
3. Let it simmer slowly until cooked
4. Transfer to a dish and let cool
5. Pound it in a mortar
6. Beat 1/4 pound lard in a bowl until fluffy
7. Add the rice, 6 loth finely ground almonds, 6 loth sifted sugar, lemon zest
8. Add 5 eggs and 6 egg yolks, each well beaten
9. Stir for another hour
10. Pour into a sealed ring mold
11. Sprinkle with sugar and bake slowly in a heated cake pan
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Auflauf (Sweet Custard Soufflé)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine wheat flour
- Milk
- Good butter
- Egg yolks
- A little sugar
- Egg whites, beaten to foam
- Cinnamon, vanilla, lemon peel, or orange peel (any one used sparingly)

## Directions

1. Stir fine wheat flour into milk over strong heat until a thick paste forms
2. Let this paste cool completely
3. Half an hour before serving, take it out again
4. Stir good butter, some egg yolks, and a little sugar cold together with the milk paste
5. When everything is well combined, fold in the foam from beaten egg whites
6. Season as desired with cinnamon, vanilla, lemon peel, or orange peel, each used individually and sparingly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Auflauf von Hirn (Calf Brains Soufflé)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One whole calf brain
- Lukewarm water
- One-quarter pound butter
- Equal amount finely pounded almonds
- 5 whole eggs
- 5 egg yolks
- Interior of one roll
- One Seidel cream
- One-quarter pound sugar
- One lemon, zest grated

## Directions

1. Place the whole calf brain in lukewarm water and clean the red veins away carefully.
2. Blanch it slightly, let it dry on a hair sieve, and cut it finely with a knife.
3. Beat one-quarter pound butter to foam and add an equal amount finely pounded almonds, 5 whole eggs, and 5 egg yolks.
4. Take the interior of one roll, soak it in one Seidel cream, and beat it together with the brain, one-quarter pound sugar, and grated lemon zest for a quarter hour vigorously.
5. Coat a rimmed dish with butter, fill the mass into it, and bake it slowly and carefully.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Auflauf von Kalbsmilchen oder Brieschen (Calf Sweetbreads Soufflé)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound white bread
- Water
- One-quarter pound butter
- 12 Loth butter
- 12 egg yolks
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- Egg whites from 8 eggs
- 6 to 8 calf sweetbreads
- Salt
- Butter
- Finely chopped shallots
- Parsley
- Lemon peel
- Fine flour
- Good bouillon or stock
- One glass wine
- Lemon juice
- Butter for greasing
- Roll crumbs

## Directions

1. Soak half pound white bread in water, press it dry, and cook it over fire with one-quarter pound butter.
2. Beat 12 Loth butter with 12 egg yolks to foam, add the bread with some nutmeg and salt, and stir vigorously for a quarter hour, then fold in the snow from 8 eggs.
3. Blanch 6 to 8 calf sweetbreads in salted water, peel them clean, and cut into small dice.
4. Cook them gently with a piece of butter, finely chopped shallots, parsley and lemon peel, dust with a spoonful of fine flour, and fill with a little good bouillon or stock and one glass wine, cooking it down briefly.
5. Add a squeeze of lemon juice and set aside to cool.
6. Coat a form with butter, sprinkle with roll crumbs, fill in half the mass, place the ragout on top, cover with the remaining half, and bake the soufflé slowly.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Auflauf von Krebsen nach bayerischer Art (Crayfish Soufflé Bavarian Style)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 beautiful crayfish
- 2 Seidel milk
- 16 egg yolks
- Three-quarters pound crayfish butter
- One-quarter pound pistachios
- One-quarter pound sugar
- One-quarter pound grated rolls
- Wine vinegar

## Directions

1. From 15 crayfish make one-quarter pound crayfish butter.
2. From the remaining half of the crayfish, remove the bitter part and pound them alive, then stir under 2 Seidel milk and strain through a hair sieve, ensuring no shell passes through.
3. Heat this strained milk with a little wine vinegar over the fire, stirring constantly, until it curdles into cheese, then pour it onto a hair sieve.
4. When the sour water has drained off, pound the cheese finely, beat it to foam with the crayfish butter, and gradually add 16 egg yolks.
5. Mix in the remaining ingredients and finally fold in the snow from the eggs.
6. Bake this mass in a rimmed form for one hour.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Auflauf von Sago (Sago Soufflé)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Three-quarters pound sago
- One measure milk
- One Seidel cream
- Some cinnamon
- One-quarter pound fresh butter
- Equal amount sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- Egg whites from 12 eggs
- Sugar for garnish

## Directions

1. Rinse the sago well, boil it several times with water, and cool it on a hair sieve with fresh water.
2. Bring one measure milk and one Seidel cream to a boil with some cinnamon, and add the sago to it so that it swells well and cooks; watch it carefully as it tends to stick to the bottom.
3. When the sago is well swollen and short, stir in one-quarter pound fresh butter and an equal amount of sugar, and let it cool.
4. In the same manner, stir in 12 egg yolks, beat the egg whites to snow, and fold them in slowly.
5. Bake in a rimmed dish.
6. If the soufflés are sprinkled with sugar beforehand, they will get a beautiful glaze.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Auflauf von Sago (Sago Soufflé)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound sago
- water
- mass milk
- seidel cream
- cinnamon
- quarter pound fresh butter
- equal amount sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Rinse the sago, cook it several times with water, and cool it on a hair sieve with fresh water
2. Heat a mass of milk and a seidel of cream with some cinnamon and add the sago to it so it swells and cooks well
3. Watch carefully as it easily sticks to the bottom
4. When well swollen and short, stir in quarter pound fresh butter and an equal amount sugar and let it cool
5. Stir in 12 egg yolks and the whites in the same way
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Auflauf von weißem Käse (White Cheese Soufflé)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds white cheese
- 1 pound almonds, of which one-quarter pound should be bitter
- Half pound sugar with 2 lemons zest
- Some pounded cinnamon
- 1 pound butter
- 21 eggs

## Directions

1. Pound the peeled almonds with 4 egg whites very finely, then similarly pound the cheese and butter as finely as possible.
2. Beat all this in a bowl with 18 egg yolks until very foamy.
3. Gradually mix in the almonds, sugar and cinnamon, and when all is done, slowly fold in the snow from the remaining eggs.
4. Coat a form or rimmed dish with butter, fill the mass into it, but only half full, and bake the soufflé in an hour and a half, not too hot.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Aufläufs-Strudel (Soufflé Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ liter flour
- ¼ liter flour (for filling)
- ½ liter milk (for filling)
- 12 deca butter
- 16 deca vanilla sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough as described in the Rice Strudel recipe with the difference that the following mass is used instead of rice filling
2. In a saucepan, put ¼ liter flour, gradually pour in ½ liter milk while stirring constantly
3. Stir this on the fire until it becomes thick, then pour into a deep bowl
4. Stir cold with 12 deca butter and 16 deca vanilla sugar
5. Gradually stir 6 egg yolks one after another into this now-cooled mass
6. Lightly fold in the snow (whipped egg whites) from 6 egg whites last

title: Augsburger Sausages (Augsburger)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-6 Augsburger sausages
- flour
- hot lard
- spinach or lettuce salad

## Directions

1. Halve the sausages lengthwise and remove the skin
2. Make diagonal cuts on top
3. Roll the sausages in flour on one side
4. Place in hot lard with the floured side down
5. Fry brown on both sides
6. Serve with spinach or lettuce salad

title: Augsburger-Wurst (Augsburg Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Six pounds beef from the leg, taken while still warm from the ox
- Six Seidel of water
- Six measures of salt
- Cleaned beef intestines

## Directions

1. Remove skin and all sinews from the warm beef leg
2. Beat it very fine with a wooden mallet
3. While beating, add six Seidel of water and salt with six measures of salt
4. Let stand overnight
5. The next day, salt it a bit more
6. Fill it firmly into cleaned beef intestines
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ausgebratene Gans (Braised Goose)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- salt and spices
- white mineral pitch (colophonium)
- onion and root vegetables
- bacon slices
- raw turnips or roasted chestnuts, or beans boiled in salt water and drained, or macaroni, braised sturgeon or rice (optional fillings)
- melted fat
- broth
- white wine

## Directions

1. To remove fine down from the goose, after plucking, rub it with finely powdered white mineral pitch (colophonium), then place in a deep dish, pour warm water from the side, turn the geese in it several times quickly, brush off the pitch and wash it off.
2. Open the goose, clean it well, rub inside and out with salt and marjoram as desired, truss it and roast slowly on a spit for 1-2 hours.
3. Alternatively, place a trussed goose in a pan, pour melted fat over it and roast in the oven slowly, basting frequently with its own juices.
4. Before trussing, if desired, the goose can be filled with raw turnips, roasted chestnuts, beans boiled in salt water and drained, macaroni, braised sturgeon or rice. The minced liver is always added to the filling in the goose; sew it closed and truss as directed in recipe 735.

title: Ausgebünstete Erdäpfel-Rubel (Steamed Potato Noodles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- potato noodles prepared as for Abgeschmalzene Erdäpfel-Rubel (No. 1209)
- 1 liter boiling milk
- 3/4 melted butter
- finely sifted sugar
- cinnamon (optional)
- 4 tablespoons maraschino (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare potato noodles and place them in 1 liter boiling milk instead of salt water
2. Cover and let cook until the milk is absorbed sufficiently
3. Place the noodles in a casserole with melted butter and steam in the oven until dry
4. Arrange heaped on a platter with a spoon, sprinkle with finely sifted sugar and optionally cinnamon, and serve
5. Before serving, optionally pour 4 tablespoons maraschino over them

title: Ausgebünstete imitierte Macaroni (Steamed Imitation Macaroni)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter flour
- 5 egg yolks
- a pinch of salt
- some milk cream
- 1 liter boiling milk
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 4 tablespoons sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Place 1/2 liter flour on a board, make a well in the center, add 5 egg yolks, a pinch of salt, and some milk cream
2. Mix with a spoon and work into a smooth, firm dough
3. Let rest covered for 1/2 hour
4. Cut the dough into walnut-sized pieces and roll them into pencil-thick noodles with your hands
5. Cook them in 1 liter boiling milk, stirring well together
6. Steam with 8 tablespoons butter and 4 tablespoons sifted sugar in the oven or on the stove
7. If using these noodles as a garnish for braised meat or stew, omit the sugar

title: Ausgezogenen Mehlstrudel (Drawn Flour Strudel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound flour
- 2 eggs and the white of 1 egg
- a nut-sized piece of melted butter
- salt
- lukewarm water
- melted butter for brushing
- sour cream
- fine bread crumbs
- 1 measure simmering cream

## Directions

1. Mix flour, eggs, melted butter, salt, and a little lukewarm water to make a dough as firm as butter dough
2. Knead the dough under the hands until very fine
3. Wrap in cloth to keep from drying and keep slightly warm
4. After half an hour, spread a clean cloth over a pillow or table
5. Roll out the dough slightly and have two people gently stretch it from both sides
6. Place on the cloth and stretch it all around as finely as possible without tearing
7. Let it dry slightly when thin enough
8. Brush with melted butter, then with sour cream
9. Sprinkle fine bread crumbs over it
10. Roll loosely together and place in a buttered pan shaped like a spiral
11. Pour about 1 measure of simmering cream around it
12. Bake in an oven or with heat above and below until lightly baked
13. Serve in the pan or removed onto a platter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Austern einzusalzen (Salted Oysters)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- whole cinnamon
- green fennel, crushed in summer

## Directions

1. Remove the oysters from their shells
2. Place them in large earthenware pots such that the bottom is sprinkled with some salt and pepper
3. Lay some bay leaves, whole cinnamon, and green fennel (crushed in summer) on top
4. Then lay a layer of oysters, and again a layer of the aforementioned spices, then oysters and so forth until the vessel is full
5. You can then eat them raw or use them cooked for various dishes
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Austern Hasteten (Croustade aux Huitres) (Oyster Pastry Case)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- About 24 oysters
- Own oyster liquid
- A few tablespoons full of sea water or white wine
- Butter
- Flour
- Butter dough, finger-thick
- 2 deca shredded butter
- Lemon juice
- Butter dough, finger-thick
- Beaten eggs
- Bread crumbs
- A small piece butter

## Directions

1. Remove about 24 oysters from the shell, place them with their own liquid and a few tablespoons full of sea water or white wine in a casserole and let warm covered.
2. When this is done, set them aside and let them cool in their juice.
3. Line any mold brushed with butter and dusted with flour with finger-thick butter dough, add the oysters, scatter with 2 deca shredded butter, sprinkle with lemon juice, cover with a finger-thick butter dough lid matching the mold, which after being cut several times with a knife is brushed with beaten eggs, then bake the pastry at quite strong heat in the oven.
4. Meanwhile, to the oyster liquid remaining in the casserole add a little bread crumbs, a small piece of butter and lemon juice, let it boil well and pour it in after the pastry is finished baking and the lid has been removed as was indicated for the potato pastry, and serve the pastry immediately.

title: Austernfisch, gebraten, mit fines herbes (Braised Sea Bass with Herbs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 80 dkg Fisch (fish)
- 6 Champignons (mushrooms)
- Butter
- ½ Ringelzwiebel (scallion)
- Sardellen (anchovies)
- Petersilie (parsley)
- 5 dkg Butter (butter)
- Salt
- Salzlkartoffeln (boiled potatoes) for serving

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the fish, clean it, wash it and place it in milk water, then remove and salt well.
2. Make small incisions either on top or on the side and insert either small pieces of mushrooms or anchovies into them.
3. Heat a piece of butter in a roasting pan, add the fish and half a scallion and roast it in the oven for 30-45 minutes, basting frequently with its own juice and adding more liquid as needed.
4. Finely chop the mushrooms and soften them with a small piece of finely chopped onion and parsley in 5 dkg butter.
5. Lift the fish onto a platter, coat with the fines herbes, strain the sauce and serve it separately.
6. Serve boiled potatoes as a side dish.

title: Austernpästechen (Oyster Pastries)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters with their sauce
- pastry cases
- feather for decoration
- Madeira or Tokay wine

## Directions

1. Place the required number of oysters with their sauce on gentle heat for a quarter hour
2. During this time, form the pastry cases and place one oyster in the middle of each
3. Draw a feather crosswise over the oyster to make it look like a rose
4. Bake the pastries quickly on a baking sheet in the oven
5. Drizzle a little Madeira or Tokay wine in the middle
6. Serve immediately warm
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Auſtern, gebratene (Roasted Oysters)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh oysters
- Pepper
- Lemon juice
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Open the oysters and remove the top shell
2. Rinse the shells clean so the water runs out
3. Place them on the grate with the bottom shell down
4. Season with pepper and lemon juice
5. Add a little sugar
6. Roast them and bring quickly to the table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Baked Eggs (Gebackene Eyer)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- butter
- chopped fresh parsley
- chopped anchovies
- a handful of bread crumbs
- several spoonfuls of cream
- salt
- pounded pepper
- beaten eggs
- half bread crumbs and half flour for coating
- lard for frying
- lemon juice
- anchovies
- fresh parsley
- chives
- pea water

## Directions

1. Take hard-boiled eggs and cut off a small piece from the bottom so they stand on the dish.
2. Cut off the top as a lid, remove the yolks, and pound them fine in a mortar.
3. Beat a piece of butter until light, add finely chopped fresh parsley, some finely chopped anchovies, a handful of bread crumbs, several spoonfuls of cream, salt and some pounded pepper, and stir everything well together.
4. Fill the eggs with this mixture, dip the filled eggs in beaten egg, coat them in half bread crumbs and half flour, then fry them in hot lard.
5. Make a small sauce of lemon juice, anchovies, fresh parsley and chives, which all go into a thin yellowish roux, and is extended with pea water.
6. Add several spoonfuls of cream and let the whole reduce to a thin sauce.
7. Place the eggs on a dish, pour the sauce over them, and they are ready.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Barley Cream Soup (Crème maigre d'orge)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 liter rolled barley
- 2 liters water
- 12 Deca butter
- 0.25 liter sour cream
- Vinegar
- Salt
- 1 clove

## Directions

1. Boil the rolled barley in 2 liters water with 12 Deca butter for about 2 hours slowly
2. Sour with 0.25 liter sour cream and vinegar as needed
3. Add salt and 1 clove, and spices if desired
4. Simmer for some time
5. Stir well and pass through a sieve
6. The soup should have a pleasant sour taste

title: Basavets-Pudding (Basavets Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 ounces vanilla sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites, beaten to snow
- 16 ounces flour

## Directions

1. Beat 10 ounces vanilla sugar with 8 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
2. Gently fold in the snow from 8 egg whites simultaneously with 16 ounces flour
3. Fill a buttered mold lined with flour and bake at low heat in the oven
4. When completely cooled, cut into knife-back-thick slices, slightly smaller in circumference than the casserole intended for cooking
5. Line the casserole with butter and flour, lay a biscuit slice on the bottom, cover finger-high with raw snow made from 6 eggs
6. Cover with another biscuit slice and continue this way until all is used
7. Finally cover with a biscuit slice
8. Cover and steam for 3/4 hour
9. Turn out onto a plate and serve with fruit juice, chocolate, or cream of choice

title: Bauernknödel ohne Ei (Peasant Quenelles without Egg)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pieces of rolls (32 Loffel), finely diced
- 16 Loffel fat (butter or pork lard)
- 1 teaspoon finely cut green parsley
- 1 1/2 liter flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 liter cold water

## Directions

1. Dice 8 pieces of rolls (32 Loffel) finely and roast in 16 Loffel fat (butter or pork lard) with 1 teaspoon finely cut green parsley; the rolls must brown quickly
2. Mix 1 1/2 liter flour and 1 teaspoon salt together in a deep bowl
3. Stir the flour with 1/2 liter cold water to a thin dough
4. Add the roasted rolls

title: Baumkuchen (Tree Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds butter
- 2 pounds fine flour
- 1.5 pounds sugar
- 30 eggs
- half a lot cinnamon and mace
- zest of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar for half an hour
2. Melt the butter and, when it is no longer hot, add it to the eggs and sugar
3. Stir until the mixture is quite foamy
4. Stir in the spices
5. Gradually add the flour
6. Slowly fold in the egg white (snow) from the aforementioned eggs
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Baumstamm (Chocolate Log Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 8 dkg sugar
- 5 dkg grated chocolate
- 3 dkg rum-moistened breadcrumbs
- 3 egg whites
- 8 dkg raw grated almonds
- 12-15 dkg butter for cream
- 5 dkg sugar for cream
- 4 dkg grated chocolate for cream
- 1 egg yolk for cream
- swollen chopped pistachios for topping

## Directions

1. Beat 3 egg yolks and 1 whole egg with 8 dkg sugar for 15 minutes
2. Stir in 5 dkg grated chocolate and 3 dkg rum-moistened breadcrumbs
3. Fold in 3 stiff egg whites together with 8 dkg raw grated almonds
4. Fill the mixture into a buttered and floured round log pan
5. Bake at moderate heat
6. Cut the cake lengthwise through the middle
7. Fill and brush the outside with cream
8. Mark the bark with a fork dipped in hot water by stroking from top to bottom
9. Sprinkle with swollen chopped pistachios

title: Baumwollen-Suppe (Cotton Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 tablespoons fine flour
- hot milk
- 5 to 6 eggs
- pea broth

## Directions

1. Stir 5 tablespoons of fine flour with hot milk to form a batter like a drip soup
2. Beat 5 to 6 eggs into it and stir well
3. Let it drop into boiling pea broth and stir constantly until it becomes thick
4. Season to taste when serving
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bavaroise (Tea with Eggs and Milk)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 seidel ordinary, somewhat strong tea
- 0.5 seidel milk
- 6 egg yolks
- cold milk
- Syrop Capilaire

## Directions

1. Mix 0.5 seidel ordinary, somewhat strong tea with 0.5 seidel milk
2. Beat 6 egg yolks with some cold milk
3. Stir the beaten egg yolks into the tea mixture
4. Sweeten sufficiently with Syrop Capilaire
5. Serve hot
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bayerische Kügel (Bavarian Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dl lukewarm milk
- 1 egg
- 1 egg yolk
- 8 dkg melted butter
- salt
- 4 dkg sugar
- 1½ dkg yeast
- 30 dkg warmed flour
- hot fat for frying
- powdered sugar
- jam

## Directions

1. Mix 2 dl lukewarm milk with 1 egg, 1 egg yolk, 8 dkg melted butter, salt, 4 dkg sugar, and the risen starter from 1½ dkg yeast
2. Sprinkle this mixture into 30 dkg warmed flour in a mixing bowl
3. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon and let it rise near the stove
4. After approximately 1 hour, roll it out to finger thickness on a floured board
5. Cut out with a large doughnut cutter and let rise covered for another 30 minutes
6. Make a small indentation in the center of each pastry
7. Place them indentation-side down into hot fat and fry until light brown on both sides
8. Drain, sprinkle with powdered sugar, fill the indentation with jam, and serve warm

title: Beaver (Biber)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One beaver tail
- Bacon
- Truffles
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Roots
- Dried herbs
- Bay leaves
- Spices
- Lemon slices
- Salt
- Oyster or truffle sauce (optional)

## Directions

1. Take the tail from a beaver, lard it finely with bacon and truffles
2. Place in a casserole over heat
3. Add wine, vinegar, roots, dried herbs, bay leaves, spices, lemon slices and salt
4. Cook until tender
5. Serve with oyster or truffle sauce if desired
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bechamel-Nockerl (Béchamel Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Béchamel sauce
- butter
- sweet cream
- sugar
- ground vanilla

## Directions

1. Make Béchamel sauce and work it together
2. Set butter and sweet cream a quarter inch deep with sugar and ground vanilla on heat
3. When everything simmers, drop in the dumplings
4. Cook as for butter dumplings
5. Serve immediately
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Beef Loin English Style (Lungenbraten auf englische Art)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef loin
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Water
- Lemon peel
- Bay leaf
- Onions
- Cloves
- Whole pepper
- Capers
- Lemon
- Anchovies
- Bacon
- Green parsley
- Onion
- Butter
- Milk cream
- Pepper
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and membranes from the beef loin, wash it well, and salt it.
2. Prepare a pickling brine from a little wine, vinegar, water, lemon peel, a bay leaf, and a couple of onions studded with cloves.
3. Boil the brine and pour it hot over the beef loin, add some whole pepper, and let it stand overnight.
4. The next day, place it on the heat and let it stew until tender.
5. Take a handful of capers, place them on a board, and chop them together with half a lemon, a couple of washed anchovies with bones removed, a small piece of bacon, a little green parsley, and a small onion from the above brine.
6. Let this mixture cook in butter, place it with the meat, add half a seidel of milk cream, a little butter and pepper, and let it stew a little.
7. Skim off the fat, arrange the beef loin on the platter, and garnish with some lemon slices.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Beef Loin Studded (Gespickten Lungenbraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef loin
- Salt

## Directions

1. Take a fine beef loin, remove the skin cleanly, cut off the legs, and salt it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Beef Roulade, Braised (Roulade de boeuf braisée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 well-aged beef round steak
- Bacon strips (cut into finger-length pieces)
- Salt and pepper
- Onion
- Green parsley
- Capers
- Onion, root vegetables, cabbage, and whole spices (for the braising bed)
- Wine and broth (as needed)

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the beef round steak
2. Pound it with the flat side of the meat mallet so it becomes somewhat wider
3. Insert finger-length pieces of bacon that have been rolled in salt and pepper into the meat
4. Sprinkle the meat with pepper
5. Finely chop bacon, onion, green parsley, and capers; spread this mixture on the inner side of the meat
6. Roll the meat with the thicker side inward toward the thinner part, and wrap it tightly with kitchen twine
7. Line a casserole with onion, root vegetables, cabbage, and whole spices
8. Place the meat on top so that the open side faces upward
9. Brown the meat, then pour in enough wine and broth to cover the meat
10. Braise for 4-5 hours
11. When the roast is tender, remove it from the casserole

title: Befpieste Taubenbrüste (Filets de pigeons piqués)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeon breasts
- Root vegetables
- Speck (bacon)
- Whole spices
- Broth
- Red wine
- Butter
- Glace

## Directions

1. Remove the breasts as described for pheasant breasts, peel away the fine membranes, and lard the breasts.
2. Place the remaining meat of the pigeons with root vegetables, speck, and whole spices in a casserole, roast slightly, then add broth and red wine, and simmer well.
3. Pour half of this simmered and strained sauce into a shallow casserole.
4. Place the breasts with the larded side up on the sauce, cover with writing paper brushed with butter, and braise in the oven for approximately 1/2 hour, basting frequently.
5. After this time, remove the paper and roast the breasts until the speck becomes golden in color.
6. Brush the breasts with dissolved glace, arrange on a platter, and pour the remaining half of the strained sauce over them.

title: Beheizter Kalbsschlägel (Marinated Veal Leg)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- veal leg
- ground spices
- pencil-thick, half-finger-long bacon
- vinegar
- water
- wine
- thinly sliced vegetables (carrots, celery)
- 3 bay leaves
- cabbage
- lemon peel
- salt
- cream
- aspic

## Directions

1. After trimming the leg and removing the skin, pound it with a meat mallet, rub it with ground spices, lard it with pencil-thick, half-finger-long bacon and let it rest 1 hour.
2. Meanwhile make a brine of vinegar, water and some wine, add thinly sliced vegetables, 3 bay leaves, cabbage and lemon peel and let it cook well.
3. Pour this prepared brine over the unsalted leg and let it marinate 2–3 days, during which time drain the brine twice a day, bring it to a boil and pour it cold again over the leg, which should be turned daily.
4. Before pouring over it the last time, salt both the brine and the leg and let it stand another night.
5. After this time, place it on the spit and roast it, frequently basting with a brine mixed with cream.
6. To some brine which is standing in a casserole at the stove, add more ingredients and serve with the meat.

title: Behemotester (Behemoth Pastry)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams butter
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- some salt
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 3 decagrams pressed yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
- 54 decagrams flour
- melted butter for brushing
- finely ground sugar mixed with cinnamon
- finely cut almonds, raisins, and lingonberries

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy, gradually stir in the sugar, salt, egg yolks, and whole egg
2. Add the pressed yeast dissolved in lukewarm milk and the flour, stirring well together
3. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon, then cover and let it rise in a warm place for 1/2 hour
4. Place the dough on a flour-dusted board, roll it out to the thickness of a knife back, and cut into strips 3 fingers long and 1 finger wide
5. Sprinkle the strips with finely ground sugar mixed with cinnamon and finely cut almonds, raisins, and lingonberries, then roll up together
6. Lay the rolls side by side in a butter-greased casserole so that the end of the rolled dough faces upward, and brush with melted butter where rolls touch
7. Cover with a cloth and let rise in a warm place until the dough has risen again
8. Brush with melted butter and bake 3/4 to 1 hour
9. Remove the casserole, turn it out, separate the pastries from each other, arrange on a plate, sprinkle with sugar, and serve

title: Bekoichte Froschschenkel mit Wurzelwerk (Stewed Frog Legs with Root Vegetables)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lard
- Root vegetables, finely cut
- Onion
- 1 bay leaf
- Whole spices
- Water
- Vinegar
- Prepared frog legs
- Grated bread

## Directions

1. Place lard and finely cut root vegetables, onion, bay leaf, and whole spices in a casserole
2. Pour in equal amounts of water and vinegar
3. Cook covered
4. When the root vegetables are soft, add the prepared frog legs
5. Let them stew covered until tender
6. Remove the legs and place in the center of a serving bowl
7. Garnish with the cooked root vegetables and grated bread
8. Mix egg yolks with some cold soup, pour the hot broth over this, and serve the sauce in a sauce boat with the frog legs

title: Belgischer Salat (Salade à l'Italienne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beans, lentils, green peas (about 1/6 pound of each)
- 1 cooked red beet, diced
- 1 dark yellow beet, diced
- 4 pieces peeled asparagus tips, diced
- 4 pieces peeled pickled gherkins, diced
- 2 large cooked potatoes, diced
- White from 4 hard-boiled eggs, diced
- 4 pieces peeled capers
- 1 peeled herring, rinsed in milk
- Herring roe
- 2 tablespoons capers
- 1 eel and some truffles, optional
- 4 hard-boiled egg yolks, pressed
- 2 raw egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons French mustard
- About 8 tablespoons oil
- Juice of 1 lemon
- Several tablespoons strong vinegar
- Salt
- 1 heaping pinch of white pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the beans, lentils and peas
2. Dice all the vegetables and fish as described and place in a deep bowl
3. Press 4 hard-boiled egg yolks through a sieve
4. Add 2 raw egg yolks and 2 tablespoons French mustard
5. Gradually stir in about 8 tablespoons oil and the juice of 1 lemon
6. Add several tablespoons strong vinegar, some salt and 1 heaping pinch of white pepper
7. Mix everything well with the various vegetables
8. The salad should be thick and have a pleasant, sweet-sour taste
9. Arrange in a bowl and garnish carefully with various marinated fish varieties
10. This salad can also serve as a garnish for various cold dishes, namely fish, chicken with mayonnaise and aspic, and generally all meat dishes served with mayonnaise

title: Berberitzen (Barberries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful red, ripe barberries (Seinschafl, Sauerberen, Essigberen)
- 60 decilitres sugar
- 1 kilo sugar
- 1 litre water

## Directions

1. Pluck beautiful red, ripe barberries from their stems
2. Treat further as the cherries (Nr. 1845), only use 1 kilo sugar for 1 litre water instead of 60 decilitres

title: Bereitung gefüllter Kuttelfleische (Preparation of Stuffed Tripe)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- water
- onion
- green parsley
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook the well-cleaned and cut into larger pieces tripe in the necessary water completely tender.
2. Then remove it, place it in an earthenware casserole where onion, green parsley with some pepper and salt have previously been roasted in butter.
3. Here it is braised covered.

title: Berghoflor Souffle (Mountain Palace Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 hectograms flour
- 1.5 liters boiling milk
- 12 egg yolks
- 12 hectograms sugar
- 9 egg whites
- butter
- marmalade

## Directions

1. Place 9 hectograms flour in a casserole, stir well with 1.5 liters boiling milk, then continue stirring over the fire until the mixture becomes thick
2. Transfer to a bowl and continue stirring until it is cool
3. Gradually add 12 egg yolks and 12 hectograms sugar and stir well for half an hour
4. Fold in the snow from 9 egg whites lightly, put the whole into a casserole coated with butter and let bake for 3/4 hour
5. However, before it is done, coat it with dissolved marmalade
6. Mix the snow from 3 egg whites with 12 hectograms sugar, place on the baked mixture, dust with sugar, and dry in the open oven

title: Berliner Zuckerbögen (Berlin Sugar Bows)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound sugar
- One pound fine flour
- 6 whole eggs
- Aniseed
- Orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Work together the sugar, flour, and eggs into a mass like aniseed cake
2. Add aniseed and orange blossom water
3. Spread this mass on butter paper to the thickness of a good knife-back
4. Bake slowly for a quarter hour
5. Cut into strips three finger-widths wide
6. While still warm, bend over a round piece of wood the thickness of a wax candle
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bermsrudjen (Yeast Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 Deca butter
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 Deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/2 liter milk
- 42 Deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy, then stir in the sugar, salt, and egg yolks one after another
2. Mix in the yeast dissolved in milk and flour, combining well
3. Beat the dough until it pulls away from the spoon
4. Cover and let rest in a warm place for 1/2 hour
5. Divide the dough into 2 equal parts on a flour-dusted board and roll each out to fit the size of the baking pan
6. Line a buttered and floured baking pan with one sheet of dough
7. Spread with marmalade, leaving a finger-width border, and brush the border with beaten egg
8. Place the second dough sheet on top and press the edges together
9. Cover and let rest in a warm place for 1/4 hour
10. Brush with beaten egg, sprinkle with coarse sugar mixed with finely chopped almonds
11. Bake slowly for 3/4 to 1 hour

title: Bertram Vinegar (Bertram-flavored Vinegar)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Picked leaves from 5 bunches of Bertram
- 5 liters of good vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the picked leaves from 5 bunches of Bertram in a bottle
2. Pour 5 liters of good vinegar over them, but leave a space 3 fingers wide at the top of the bottle
3. Cork the bottle and bind it with linen cloth
4. Set it in the sun or a warm place for several weeks
5. Strain the vinegar through a linen cloth
6. Fill it into a bottle, cork it well and seal it

title: Bertram-Sauce (Tarragon Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Tarragon
- Butter
- Soup
- Salt

## Directions

1. Made the same way as the dill herb sauce, except one uses no sour cream

title: Beste Punschessenz (Best Punch Essence)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 loth fresh lemon juice
- 56 loth Jamaica rum
- 1 pound fine sugar
- 1 pound water
- egg white (optional)

## Directions

1. Shake 16 loth fresh lemon juice and 56 loth Jamaica rum together
2. Let stand quietly
3. After about 48 hours when the liquid has cleared, pour it off
4. Strain the sediment through blotting paper
5. Dissolve 1 pound fine sugar in 1 pound water (optionally clarify with egg white)
6. Mix both together to make an extract
7. Use the extract to make punch by combining with warm water or tea water
8. Sugar can also be added afterwards, which is even better
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Biberſchweif (Beaver Tail)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beaver tail with part of the backbone attached
- Bacon sheets
- Butter
- Onions
- Cloves
- Dried herbs
- Red wine
- Good bouillon
- Sauce Espagnole
- Capers
- Truffles
- Lemon juice
- Salt

## Directions

1. Leave part of the backbone attached to the beaver tail
2. Blanch the tail repeatedly with boiling water to remove scales
3. Line a long dish with bacon sheets, butter, onions, cloves and dried herbs
4. Place the beaver tail in the dish and sear it quickly over heat to color it
5. Add a glass of red wine and good bouillon
6. Let it cook slowly until done
7. Remove the tail and skim off fat
8. Mix the cooking liquid with Sauce Espagnole
9. Reduce briefly
10. Add capers, truffles and lemon juice
11. Serve with the sauce
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bier recht klar zu machen (To Make Beer Clear)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one Groſchen worth (or somewhat more) of grated stag's horn
- 2 Maß of the same beer

## Directions

1. For a properly fermented barrel of beer that is several days old, take grated stag's horn
2. Boil it several times with 2 Maß of the same beer
3. Fill it warm and carefully into the barrel
4. Let it rest undisturbed overnight
5. Fill the beer into bottles and it will be clear as ruby
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bier-Suppe (Beer Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- white beer, 3 seidels
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- sugar
- egg yolks, 8
- cream, half a seidel
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Simmer 3 seidels of white beer with some cinnamon, lemon peel and sugar until sweet enough
2. Beat 8 egg yolks in a pot
3. Gradually add half a seidel of hot cream
4. Pour in the hot beer while stirring vigorously
5. Serve over toasted bread slices
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bier-Suppe (Beer Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons cold egg
- 1 liter beer
- 12 deca sugar
- some cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stir 6 egg yolks and 6 tablespoons cold egg together well
2. Let 1 liter beer, 12 deca sugar and some cinnamon cook well and beat it into the egg yolks

title: Bier-Suppe (Beer Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons salted beef broth
- 1 liter beer
- 12 ounces sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stir together 6 egg yolks and 6 tablespoons salted beef broth
2. Bring 1 liter beer, 12 ounces sugar, and cinnamon to a good boil
3. Sprinkle the mixture into the boiling beer

title: Bier-Suppe à l'altemandaise (Kalt-Schale) (Beer Soup, Cold Bowl)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters beer
- 1/2 kilo sugar
- Lemon peels, finely cut
- Cinnamon, a small amount
- 1 pinch salt
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon cold milk
- 1/4 liter black bread rolls
- Small raisins and currants, together 12 deca

## Directions

1. Bring 2 liters beer to a boil with 1/2 kilo sugar, finely cut lemon peels, some cinnamon and 1 pinch salt.
2. Stir in 8 egg yolks which have been whisked with 1 tablespoon cold milk.
3. Strain through a sieve into a bowl and set cold.
4. Toast 1/4 liter black bread rolls in the oven.
5. Clean and search through small raisins and currants, together 12 deca, simmer in 1/2 liter water, drain and lay the raisins and currants on a cloth.
6. Add these and the dried bread rolls to the beer soup, mix well together and place the bowl on ice for several hours.

title: Bierhefen selbst zu machen (Making Beer Yeast)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Maß water
- 2 handfuls barley or wheat malt
- 1 coffeespoon cream of tartar salt
- 1 knife point cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Take 6 measures of water and 2 handfuls of barley or wheat malt
2. Cook slowly over gentle heat until reduced to 2 measures
3. Cool until lukewarm
4. Add 1 coffeespoon of cream of tartar salt and 1 knife point of cream of tartar
5. You will have usable yeast which need only be cleaned through a sieve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bierhefen selbst zu machen (To Make Beer Yeast Oneself)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Maß water
- 2 handfuls barley or wheat malt
- one spoonful wine stone salt
- one knife point of wine stone

## Directions

1. Take 6 Maß water and 2 handfuls of barley or wheat malt
2. Let it cook down over slow fire to 2 Maß
3. Cool it until it is lukewarm
4. Add one spoonful of wine stone salt and one knife point of wine stone
5. You have usable yeast, which you only need to clean by passing it through a sieve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bilberries (Whortleberries) - Method 1
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo fine large dried bilberries
- 1/2 kilo fine sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Place the bilberries in a casserole that has never contained fat
2. Mix thoroughly with the fine sifted sugar, but carefully so the bilberries do not get crushed
3. Place the casserole on the warm stove and let the bilberries warm while stirring constantly until they reach the proper degree of cooking, without however boiling
4. When they have reached the proper stage, set them aside and while still hot, place them in an earthen pot or glass
5. Let the bilberries sit for 3 days, after which time cover them with paper, pricking this paper several times with a needle
6. Each time bilberries are used, stir them well beforehand
7. Remove any excess liquid that forms over time - this is very good for use as a beverage mixed with soda water, ice water, etc., or for sauces

title: Bilberries (Whortleberries) - Method 2
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo selected dried bilberries
- 1 kilo fine sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the bilberries and sugar together lightly
2. Cover and place on the hot plate
3. Stir the bilberries frequently with a wooden spoon and bring to a boil
4. Once they have come to a boil, remove from the stove
5. Stir well and treat further as described above
6. Note that the bilberries must not boil longer than just once

title: Bilberries (Whortleberries) - Method 3
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo fine large bilberries
- 1 kilo fine sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Place the bilberries in a glass or earthen or porcelain container
2. Add the fine sifted sugar
3. Cover with paper, pricking several holes in it with a needle
4. Leave the bilberries to sit without cooking in a cool, airy place
5. From time to time stir the bilberries so the sugar mixes together

title: Birnen-Kompot (Pear Compote)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Steifen- or Meusel-Cateller pears
- Spice cloves
- Sugar syrup (made from 60 decigrams of sugar per liter of water, boiled 10-15 minutes and cooled)
- Lemon water (cold)

## Directions

1. Cut the stems of the pears slightly with scissors
2. Wash the pears and prick them five times near the stem with a larding needle, so the point comes out at the blossom end
3. Fill the preserving jars as full as possible with the unpicked pears, placing 1 clove in each jar
4. Pour the cooled cooked sugar syrup over the pears so it completely covers them, but leaving a small finger's breadth of space at the top of the jar
5. Bind the jars with pig's bladders that have been cut dry, washed three times in fresh cold water, and dried, laying the bladder smooth side up on the jar and tying very tightly with thin twine
6. Place all filled jars in a water bath, with each jar either packed in hay or wrapped in cloth so they do not break during cooking
7. Pour enough cold water so the jars stand halfway in it, cover and place on high heat
8. Remove the lid as soon as the water begins to boil so the bladders do not burst
9. Keep the jars in the boiling water for 3/4 hour
10. Remove the water bath from the heat, spread a cloth over it, place a lid on top, and let stand until the next day
11. Remove the jars, wash and dry them, and store in an airy place

title: Birnen-Strudel (Pear Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Strudel dough made from 1/2 liter flour
- peeled pears, cut into thin slices
- raisins
- currants
- finely ground sugar
- cinnamon, as desired

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough and let it rest
2. Stretch the dough until thin
3. Fill instead with pears as indicated for Apple Strudel (No. 1238), cut into thin slices
4. Treat further as for Apple Strudel (No. 1238)

title: Bischof (Bishop)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pieces beautiful bitter oranges
- 4 bottles good Pontac
- 2 pounds sugar
- Toasted dark bread crust, cinnamon and cloves (optional)

## Directions

1. With a knife, lightly score all sides of 8 beautiful bitter oranges with small cuts into the peel.
2. Roast them on a grate over very gentle charcoal fire.
3. Make deep cross-shaped cuts in them.
4. Place in an earthenware pot and pour 4 bottles of good Pontac over them.
5. Cover the pot well and set on hot ashes for 4 to 6 hours so the oranges infuse well.
6. You can press them several times with a spoon, or better yet, let them stand overnight.
7. Press through a napkin and sweeten with 2 pounds sugar.
8. The Bishop can be drunk either warm or cold.
9. You can add a roasted dark bread crust along with some cinnamon and cloves to infuse with it, but the pure orange aroma or taste is always preferable.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bischofbrot (Bishop Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 arg raw almonds, cut lengthwise
- 5 dkg chocolate
- 5 dkg citron, diced
- 15 dkg sugar
- 8 whole eggs
- 15 arg flour
- 15 dkg raisins

## Directions

1. Cut 15 arg raw almonds lengthwise and 5 dkg chocolate into pieces; dice 5 dkg citron
2. Beat 15 dkg sugar well with 8 whole eggs
3. Mix in 15 arg flour, almonds, chocolate, citron, and 15 dkg raisins
4. Fill into a buttered, floured biscuit mold
5. Bake slowly until light brown
6. Cool, turn out, and cut into thin slices only the next day

title: Bischofextract (Bishop's Extract)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- orange peel
- unripe small pomegranates (optional, one-third or one-fourth of the amount)
- wine spirit

## Directions

1. Cut the orange peel and pomegranates quite small, or crush the latter
2. Pour two to three times the weight of wine spirit into a bottle with a somewhat wide mouth
3. Do not fill the bottle completely
4. Seal with a bladder
5. Place in the sun or, if necessary, in gentle oven heat until everything has drawn out
6. Transfer the liquid to another bottle, including the expressed residue of the peel
7. Store in corked glass bottles, which preserves it for a long time
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bischoff (Bishop)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bitter orange
- 16 deciliters sugar
- 1/4 liter water

## Directions

1. Mark the peel of a bitter orange with a knife cut and roast the orange over coals
2. In the meantime, boil 16 deciliters sugar in 1/4 liter water somewhat thick
3. Add the orange and let it cool covered
4. Press it through a cloth to the prepared drink
5. This beverage can be served warm or cold

title: Bischoffs-Brot, anderer Art (Bishop's Bread, Another Way)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 drams butter
- 28 drams brown sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites
- 30 drams flour
- 2 to 3 tablets chocolate
- butter for greasing the baking form

## Directions

1. Beat 24 drams butter until light and fluffy
2. Gradually add 28 drams brown sugar and 12 egg yolks and beat well
3. Lightly fold in the stiffly beaten snow of 10 egg whites with 30 drams flour
4. Pour into a baking form greased with butter and dusted with flour
5. Soften 2 to 3 tablets of chocolate on a baking sheet so that a finger can be pressed in
6. Cut the chocolate into pieces the size of a small finger
7. When completely softened, insert these pieces into the raw batter in the form as desired
8. Bake slowly at moderate heat for approximately 1 hour

title: Biscoten
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 16 ounces vanilla sugar
- 8 egg whites
- 14 ounces flour
- finely ground sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. After stirring 8 egg yolks with 16 ounces vanilla sugar for 3/4 hour, gently fold in the stiffly beaten snow from 8 egg whites with 14 ounces flour
2. Using a pastry bag or syringe, pipe this mass onto a sheet covered with unglazed paper in biscuit form, sprinkle with finely ground sugar, and bake in low heat in the oven until golden
3. Remove with a thin knife from the sheet and place on a sieve
4. Instead of small biscotti, one can form arbitrarily large pieces from this mass

title: Biscotespudding (Biscuit Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces hard biscuits or biscuit rusks
- dissolved marmalade for spreading
- 3/5 liter cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 12 ounces powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Use 16 ounces hard biscuits, laying 2 and 2 together with the lower sides facing up
2. Spread dissolved marmalade between them
3. Beat 3/5 liter cream, 6 egg yolks and 12 ounces powdered sugar until frothy
4. Soak the biscuits in this mixture
5. Lay them side by side in a buttered mold lined with flour
6. Pour the remaining liquid over
7. Cover immediately and steam for 3/4 hour

title: Biscotten (Ladyfinger Biscuits)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 16 drams vanilla sugar
- 8 egg whites
- 14 drams fine wheat flour
- unblanched almonds for greasing the baking sheet
- fine powdered sugar for dusting
- 1 egg white
- 1 tablespoon fine sifted sugar
- 1 tablespoon fine powdered almonds
- candied peel for filling

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks with 16 drams vanilla sugar until pale
2. Fold in the stiffly beaten snow of 8 egg whites mixed with 14 drams fine wheat flour
3. Using a pastry bag or syringe, pipe the mixture onto a baking sheet greased with unblanched almonds in biscuit-like shapes
4. Dust finely with powdered sugar and bake at low heat in the oven until pale yellow
5. Remove from the baking sheet with a thin knife and place on a sieve
6. Alternatively, form heaps of any desired size from this mixture, dust with fine powdered sugar, and bake at low heat until pale yellow
7. When cooled completely, pair 2 and 2 together, brushing between them with warm candied peel
8. The stiffly beaten snow of 1 egg white mixed with 1 tablespoon fine sifted sugar and 1 tablespoon fine powdered almonds can be used to fill the biscuits instead of candied peel

title: Biscuit-Crème (Biscuit Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Baked biscuit, diced (24 drams)
- Cream (1/4 liter)
- Egg yolks (6)
- Vanilla sugar (8 drams)
- Marmalade
- Sugar, finely crushed (20 drams)
- Egg whites (6)
- Chocolate, grated (1 tablet)
- Spinach juice (1/2 teaspoon)
- Almond extract (several drops)
- Orange juice (1/4 teaspoon, mixed with 1 egg yolk)

## Directions

1. Cut baked biscuit into cubes and arrange in a butter-coated dish
2. Whip together cream, egg yolks and vanilla sugar and pour spoonwise over the biscuit
3. Cover with dissolved marmalade
4. Mix finely crushed sugar with the snow of egg whites
5. Divide into 5 parts
6. Color one part with grated chocolate, the second with spinach juice, the third with almond extract, the fourth with orange juice (mixed with egg yolk), leave the fifth white
7. Put each colored portion into a paper cornet and pipe decoratively onto the biscuit
8. Sprinkle with sugar and bake in an open oven for 3/4 hour

title: Biscuit-Torte (Biscuit Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deca caster sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 12 egg whites
- 24 Deca fine flour
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Stir the sugar with the egg yolks for 1/2 hour
2. Lightly fold in the snow from 12 egg whites simultaneously with 24 Deca fine flour
3. Fill a cake form brushed with butter and dusted with flour with this mixture
4. Bake at low heat for 3/4 to 1 hour
5. Turn the cake out onto a sieve and let it cool
6. Place it on a sheet of writing paper and cover with glaze of your choice
7. Optionally, after baking, cut the cake into layers, spread with jam, layer them together, and cover with glaze
8. Optionally, the stiffly whipped snow from 3 egg whites can be mixed with 12 Deca finely sifted sugar, 2 squares of grated chocolate, or 2 tablespoons of strained strawberries or raspberries, and this can be used instead of jam to spread between the layers

title: Biscuit-Torte mit Chocolate (Biscuit Cake with Chocolate)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deca caster sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 12 egg whites
- 24 Deca fine flour
- 16 Deca grated chocolate
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Stir the sugar with the egg yolks for 1/2 hour
2. When the sugar and egg have been stirred for 1/2 hour, mix in 16 Deca grated chocolate
3. Lightly fold in the snow from 12 egg whites simultaneously with 24 Deca fine flour
4. Fill a cake form brushed with butter and dusted with flour
5. Bake at low heat for 3/4 to 1 hour
6. Turn out and cool before glazing

title: Biscuitpudding (Biscuit Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 ounces sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 5 egg whites, beaten to snow
- 12 ounces fine flour
- Optional: 6 pieces blanched pistachios, 10 pieces pignoli, 5 shelled hazelnuts

## Directions

1. Beat 24 ounces sugar with 10 egg yolks for 3/4 hour
2. Gently fold in the snow from 5 egg whites simultaneously with 12 ounces fine flour
3. If desired, finely chop 6 blanched pistachios, 10 pignoli, and 5 hazelnuts together and fold in with the snow
4. Fill a buttered mold lined with flour
5. Cover and steam for 3/4 hour

title: Biskuit Soufflé mit Früchten (Biscuit Soufflé with Fruits)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka powdered sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- 14 Deka flour
- butter for coating the mold
- flour for dusting the mold
- various fruits or sponge fingers

## Directions

1. Put 16 Deka powdered sugar in a deep bowl, add 8 egg yolks one after another and beat for 1/2 hour.
2. Lightly fold in the snow of 8 egg whites together with 14 Deka flour.
3. Line a cake mold with sponge fingers or any desired fruits, as indicated for the 'Apfel Strudel Auflauf' (recipe 1372).
4. Pour the biskuit mass over and bake at low heat for 1 hour in the oven.

title: Biskuit, gerährt (Stirred Biscuit)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- juice and zest of ½ lemon
- 4 egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
- 11 dkg flour

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dkg sugar with 5 egg yolks for 30 minutes
2. Add juice and zest of ½ lemon
3. Lightly fold in 11 dkg flour together with 4 egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
4. Fill into a buttered, floured biscuit mold
5. Bake
6. The biscuit may be brushed with jam and iced, or baked further

title: Biskuitguglhupf (Biscuit Bundt Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 1/2 dkg butter
- 12 1/2 dkg flour
- 12 1/4 dkg sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 stiffly beaten egg whites
- 5 dkg raisins
- bread crumbs
- blanched almond halves

## Directions

1. Soften the butter without melting it and beat it
2. Stir in the flour spoonful by spoonful and beat the mass well and fluffy
3. If the mixture is too thick, place it on the stove and warm it with constant stirring until it becomes pliable
4. In another bowl, beat the sugar with 4 egg yolks until frothy and mix this mass well with the first
5. Finally, very lightly fold in 4 stiffly beaten egg whites and 5 dkg raisins
6. Fill the mass into a well-buttered or greased form sprinkled with bread crumbs and lined with blanched almond halves
7. Bake at a fairly sharp fire until light brown
8. Let the cake cool somewhat in the form after baking, turn it out and dust it with sugar immediately

title: Biskuitroulade (Biscuit Roulade)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7½ dkg sugar
- 4 whole eggs
- 7½ dkg flour
- 7½ dkg melted butter
- jam

## Directions

1. Beat 7½ dkg sugar with 4 whole eggs in a snow basin on the stovetop until thick
2. Mix in 7½ dkg flour
3. Quickly fold in 7½ dkg melted butter
4. Spread finger-thick onto a buttered, floured baking sheet
5. Bake in a moderately hot oven until light brown
6. Immediately brush with jam and roll up quickly, as the dough breaks when cooled
7. Return to the oven briefly so the roulade browns on top

title: Biskuittorte (Sponge Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 12½ dkg sugar
- 6 egg whites
- 12½ dkg flour

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg yolks with 12½ dkg sugar for 20 minutes
2. Fold in 6 stiff egg whites together with 12½ dkg flour
3. Fill the mixture into the form
4. Bake slowly
5. The cake can be cut through 1-2 times and filled in various ways
6. Fill with jam, ice white or sprinkle lightly with sugar

title: Bisquitschöberl ohne Butter (Biscuit Consommé without Butter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Stiff snow from 3 eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 6 Deca fine flour
- 1 pinch salt
- Nutmeg (if desired)

## Directions

1. Mix 3 egg yolks, 6 Deca fine flour with a pinch of salt and if desired a bit of nutmeg lightly into the stiff snow from 3 eggs, without stirring the snow
2. Pour into a shallow casserole dish greased and dusted with flour to a finger-height
3. Bake at moderate heat and use as with Kaiserschöberl

title: Bittere Maccaronen (Bitter Macaroons)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- About 1 pound bitter almonds
- 1 quarter pound sweet almonds
- 2.5 pounds sugar
- White of about 20 eggs

## Directions

1. Proceed as described for sweet macaroons
2. Shape the bitter macaroons into a round form
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bitterwein (Bitter Wine)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one handful Kardobenedikten (blessed thistle) herb
- one handful Tauſendguldenkraut (centaury) herb, both stripped from stems
- 1 loth mustard leaves without stems
- half loth Moskovitic rhubarb
- one handful young wormwood
- outer skin of 2 bitter oranges, cut very thinly
- 1 loth fennel seeds
- 9 Maß of the best old wine

## Directions

1. Cut all ingredients very finely together
2. Place them in a bottle with a large opening
3. Pour 9 Maß of the best old wine over it
4. Stopper the bottle and expose it to the sun for 1 or 2 days, or place it on a warm hearth
5. Shake it frequently during this time
6. Draw the wine off into small bottles and stopper them well
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Black Butter (Beurre Noir)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams chocolate
- 8 decagrams butter

## Directions

1. Roast the chocolate on a baking sheet in the oven
2. Place the roasted chocolate in a saucepan
3. Stir in 8 decagrams butter and when both are well mixed, place it on ice

title: Black Cake (Tourte au chocolat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca beaten butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 30 deca Perugia sugar
- 10 deca finely ground and sifted almonds with skin
- 26 deca finely grated chocolate
- 1 knife tip cinnamon
- equal amount finely chopped lemon peel
- 1 ground clove
- 5 egg whites
- 10 deca flour

## Directions

1. Gradually beat 5 egg yolks into 16 deca beaten butter
2. Add 30 deca Perugia sugar and beat for 1/2 hour
3. Mix in 10 deca finely ground and sifted almonds with skin, 26 deca finely grated chocolate, 1 knife tip cinnamon, equal amount finely chopped lemon peel, and 1 ground clove
4. Lightly mix in the snow of 5 egg whites with 10 deca flour
5. Fill into a buttered and floured form and bake approximately 1 hour in a moderately hot oven
6. Remove from oven and let stand in the form for 1/4 hour, then turn out and dust with finely ground sugar when completely cool
7. Alternatively, bake this mass in 4 layers. In this case, lightly mix the stiffly beaten snow of 3 egg whites with 10 deca finely ground sugar and 2 tablets of grated chocolate, spread this on 3 baked layers, stack them so that a spread side meets a dry side, place the fourth dry layer on top and cover the whole with chocolate or desired glaze. Baking in several layers is advisable as it easily becomes greasy in the middle otherwise

title: Black Radish Served as Sauce (Black Radish Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- about 3 pieces black radish
- salt
- 1 pinch pepper
- 2 tablespoons oil
- vinegar
- bread (optional)
- finely cut chives (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel about 3 pieces of black radish, wash them, dry them with a cloth, grate them quickly on a plate and stir together with some salt, 1 pinch of pepper, 2 tablespoons oil, and vinegar as needed
2. If desired, you can add some grated bread on top and finely cut chives when serving
3. Note: This sauce should be prepared only a few minutes before serving

title: Blanc-manger mit Gelatine (Blanc-manger with Gelatin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 deca gelatin
- 1 liter liquid
- 1/4 liter cold water
- 3/8 liter heavy cream
- 8 deca sugar
- 1/8 liter gelatin
- 3/8 liter black hot coffee
- 16 deca sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve 2 deca gelatin with 1/4 liter cold water on the hot plate with constant stirring
2. Mix 3/8 liter of the dissolved hot gelatin with 3/8 liter heavy cream mixed with 8 deca sugar and 1/8 liter gelatin to 3/8 liter black hot coffee with 16 deca sugar
3. Proceed further as described above

title: Blanc-manger Pudding (Blancmange Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca blanched, very finely ground almonds
- 1 liter cream
- 30 deca vanilla sugar
- 4 deca flour
- 24 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put blanched, finely ground almonds in a saucepan
2. Pour 1 liter cream over them
3. Stir over heat until it begins to boil
4. Strain through a sieve and let cool
5. Meanwhile, gradually stir 30 deca vanilla sugar and 4 deca flour with 24 egg yolks
6. Combine this mixture with the cooled cream
7. Fill into a mold brushed with butter and dusted with flour
8. Cook covered for 3/4 hour in a water bath

title: Blanc-manger von Hausenblasen mit Kaffee (Blanc-manger with Isinglass and Coffee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter cold, good cream with 8 Deka fine sifted sugar
- 1/2 liter clear, strained coffee with 20 Deka sugar
- 8 Deka dissolved, still warm isinglass (prepared with 1 liter cold water and reduced to 1/4 liter)

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 liter cold, good cream with 8 Deka fine sifted sugar into one saucepan
2. Put 1/2 liter clear, strained coffee with 20 Deka sugar into a second saucepan
3. Of 8 Deka dissolved, still warm isinglass, which has been prepared with 1 liter cold water and reduced to 1/4 liter, stir 2 parts to the warm coffee and 1 part into the warm cream
4. After setting a mold in ice 1 hour earlier, fill the prepared mass with alternating colors, 2 fingers high, starting with the black coffee mass
5. When filled, let the mass set well, which takes about 1/2 hour
6. Then turn it out, but without putting the mold in warm water beforehand; instead press somewhat on the mass with your fingers, which will loosen it more easily

title: Blanched Mushrooms (Champignons Blanchis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- butter
- lemon juice
- small firm mushrooms
- salt

## Directions

1. Place 1/2 liter of boiling water, 4 ounces of butter and the juice of 1 lemon in a casserole and bring to a boil
2. Cut away the earthy stem from 10 small firm mushrooms and carefully separate the stem from the cap
3. Peel the caps smooth and finely, peel the stems, place them in cold water and then with the stems and a pinch of salt in the broth described above
4. Bring to a boil once and set aside covered for use
5. Use the peels for soup or for a sauce

title: Blattes Eis (Blue Icing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared white icing
- indigo dissolved in warm water

## Directions

1. Prepare as white icing, stirring in some indigo (obtainable at the apothecary) dissolved in warm water

title: Blauen Kohl mit Kaſtanien (Red Cabbage with Chestnuts)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Yellowish-brown roux
- Red cabbage
- Water
- Good broth, 2 spoons
- Salt
- Sugar
- Roasted chestnuts
- Bratwurst

## Directions

1. Add the red cabbage (cleaned, blanched twice with boiling water, cooked soft, drained and finely cut) to a yellowish-brown roux
2. Stew for a while, add 2 spoons of good broth, salt and a piece of sugar
3. Stew a while longer, then add roasted chestnuts as desired
4. Arrange neatly on the dish when serving and garnish with bratwurst
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Blauer Kohl (Red Cabbage with Chestnuts)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 pieces of red cabbage
- salt water
- cold water
- 10 deciliters butter
- 10 deciliters flour
- 1/2 liter broth
- salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 12 deciliters sugar
- 40 pieces roasted and peeled chestnuts

## Directions

1. Strip the leaves from the cabbage stems, wash them, cook in salted water and then place in cold water
2. Make a roux from 10 deciliters butter and 10 deciliters flour
3. Add the expressed finely chopped cabbage, pour in 1/2 liter broth, add the necessary salt and 1 tablespoon sugar and let it cook well
4. If the cabbage is too thick, thin it with a little broth
5. For the chestnuts: let 12 deciliters sugar brown, add it with some water, add 40 roasted peeled chestnuts as desired, and let them braise covered
6. When serving, place the cabbage on a platter and arrange the braised chestnuts on top
7. Garnish as desired with calf's meat, finely minced meat, etc.

title: Blaugesottene Forelle (Trout Cooked au Bleu)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh trout
- vinegar
- salt
- water
- butter
- parsley
- Hollandaise sauce or egg yolk sauce (alternatively vinegar and oil, or mayonnaise)
- fish stock

## Directions

1. Have 1/4 litre of strong vinegar and some boiling water ready before beginning
2. Take one trout after another from its container, strike it on the head with the back of a knife and quickly place it back in the container
3. Prepare a board with cold water, take the trout one by one and cut the belly as quickly as possible from the snout toward the head, place immediately in cold water
4. With the right thumb, press hard on the gills from snout toward head, in this manner removing blood and entrails, and when you reach the head remove the entrails and heart with the thumb and finger
5. This removal of entrails must be done while holding the fish in water, where it remains until all trout are cleaned, which must be done as quickly as possible
6. Be careful not to disturb or wipe off the outer skin of the trout
7. Place the trout in a fish pan or shallow pan, laying them belly down so closely together that the head of one lies beside the tail of another
8. If they do not completely fill the space of the pan when lying so closely side by side, fill the remaining space with wrapping paper or a suitable piece of wood so they cannot move and let them stand 1 1/4 hours so the trout become completely stiff
9. Pour boiling vinegar over them so each one is thoroughly covered, then carefully pour hot salt water (for 1 kilo of trout use 2 strong tablespoons of salt) from the side along the edge so the liquid stands a strong finger's breadth above the fish, but the fish are not directly hit by the water as it is poured
10. Cover the trout with a sheet of blotting paper brushed with butter, place a small wooden spoon on this to hold the paper down slightly, and let them stand covered at that place on the stove where their broth can be kept near the boiling point but never comes to a boil (the broth should only pass through)
11. Let the trout stand for approximately 3/4 hours; as a sign of doneness the eyes will protrude like pearls
12. If serving them warm, remove them carefully from their broth just before serving, arrange them as they lay in the pan but not so crowded together, place some parsley between every 2 trout and garnish the platter with this as well
13. Serve with Hollandaise sauce, or blend 4 egg yolks with 4 tablespoons of cream, the juice of 1 lemon, 1/3 litre of fish broth and some salt and serve in a sauce boat, also serve vinegar and oil
14. If serving the trout cold, let them cool in their broth at a cool place, then carefully lift them onto the platter shortly before serving, place aspic or just green parsley between the trout and garnish the platter with this
15. Serve vinegar and oil or mayonnaise with them

title: Blaugesottener Hecht (Blue Pike)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter water
- 1/2 liter white wine
- 1 Spanish onion studded with 1 clove
- 1 bay leaf
- 5 sprigs thyme
- some rocambole
- 2 tablespoons salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare a broth from 1 liter water, 1/2 liter white wine, a Spanish onion studded with a clove, 1 bay leaf, 5 sprigs thyme, some rocambole, 2 tablespoons salt and pepper, simmering for 1/4 hour
2. Pour this broth around the edge of the pike as with blue-boiled carp and let stand approximately 1 hour
3. When sufficiently soaked through, carefully transfer the pike to the prepared serving dish
4. Strain 1 1/4 liters of the broth through cloth into a saucepan and pour the remainder over the pike
5. If the pike is not completely covered, dip a piece of linen or white writing paper in the broth and cover the pike with it, leaving a piece of wood in the pike's opening until serving
6. Add 2 ounces of isinglass or 1 1/2 dissolved gelatin to the 1 3/4 liters of broth and cook for 1 hour
7. If the liquid is not clear enough, clarify it with 2 egg whites in the same manner as with aspic
8. When set, pour the broth from the platter on which the pike rests and garnish it beautifully with the prepared, pearled, diced and boiled pike, a part of which can also be colored
9. Place a sprig of green parsley in the pike's opening after removing the wood
10. Any type of fish can be prepared in this manner

title: Blaugesottener Lachs (Salmon Cooked Blue)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Vinegar (1 liter, boiling)
- Salt water (boiling)
- Butter
- Lemon slices
- Parsley
- Butter for basting

## Directions

1. Cut the salmon under the head
2. Paint, remove, and wash it
3. Place the salmon on the insert of the fish kettle and bind lightly from head to tail with twine
4. Place the insert with the fish in the fish kettle, having put a piece of wood in the fish's belly
5. Pour 1 liter of boiling vinegar over the salmon
6. Pour boiling salt water from the side until the liquid stands finger-high above the fish
7. Cover the fish kettle with a sheet of buttered paper and a suitable lid
8. Let it stand on the stove for 1 to 1.5 hours, during which time the broth must always be simmering but not boiling
9. Remove the salmon with the insert, unbind it, and carefully place it on a platter garnished with lemon slices and parsley
10. Brush the fish with butter
11. For cold serving, let it cool in its broth, then remove and arrange with aspic and serve Mayonnaise
12. Place a lemon slice or sprig of parsley in the fish's belly in place of wood

title: Blitzkuchen (Lightning Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 14 dkg flour
- 14 dkg melted, almost boiling butter

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dkg sugar, 2 egg yolks, and 1 whole egg for 15 minutes.
2. Very quickly mix in 14 dkg flour and 14 dkg melted, almost boiling butter.
3. This must be done quickly as the butter will otherwise stiffen and the mass will be hard to spread.
4. Quickly spread fingerthick onto buttered, floured sheet and bake at higher heat until light brown.
5. Cut into pieces or strips while still warm, but remove from sheet only when cooled.
6. The slices can be brushed with marmalade, put together, and dusted with powdered sugar.

title: Blumenkohl mit Käse (Cauliflower with Cheese)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- water
- Parmesan cheese
- butter sauce
- grated cheese

## Directions
