1. After the cauliflower is cleaned and blanched in water, lay it on cloth to dry
2. Dress it on a dish and sprinkle Parmesan cheese over it
3. Make a butter sauce and also take grated cheese with it
4. Pour this sauce over the cauliflower
5. Sprinkle again with cheese
6. Then set it in an oven until it becomes beautifully golden and crispy
7. Remove the butter with blotting paper when serving and arrange the cauliflower hot
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Blutreinigender Hafertrank (Blood-Purifying Oat Drink)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds good oats
- 1 good handful chicory roots cut small
- 12 measures water
- 1 loth purified saltpeter
- 12 loth sugar or pure honey

## Directions

1. Take 1.5 pounds of good oats and clean them well
2. Wash several times with spring water in a sieve
3. Take 1 good handful of chicory roots cut small
4. Place the roots and oats in a clean pot
5. Pour 12 measures of water over
6. Cook until reduced to half
7. Strain the remaining 6 measures through a clean cloth
8. Add 1 loth purified saltpeter and 12 loth sugar
9. Allow to boil together over heat
10. Let cool in the same covered vessel for 24 hours in the cellar until completely cold
11. Clarify from the thick matter that has settled on the bottom into clean earthen vessels
12. Store for drinking
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Blutwurst (Blood Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- A couple dozen onions
- Butter
- Two pounds diced pork fat
- Salt, pepper, spices
- Finely chopped parsley
- Young onions
- Two measures blood
- One measure sweet cream
- Cleaned and washed pork intestines

## Directions

1. Peel the onions and cut into small cubes
2. Soften gently in butter
3. Add two pounds pork fat cut the same way to the onions
4. Add salt, pepper, spices, finely chopped parsley and young onion
5. Stir together one measure sweet cream with two measures blood in a pot
6. Pour this into the casserole with the other ingredients and stir thoroughly so the pork fat distributes well and does not become lumpy
7. The mass should be neither too thick nor too thin and must have a particularly good taste
8. Fill into cleaned and well-washed pork intestines using a sausage press
9. Tie both ends of each intestine securely
10. Throw into a kettle of very hot water that must not boil
11. Once the sausages start to become somewhat stiff, prick with a needle
12. As soon as no more blood runs out, remove from water, let cool, and grill afterwards
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Blutwürste von Karpfen (Blood Sausage with Carp)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- pork blood
- onions
- shallots
- butter
- basil
- thyme
- marjoram
- milk
- semmels (rolls)
- salt
- spices
- melted butter
- fatty pork intestines

## Directions

1. Prepare as with pike sausages, but use pork blood instead of eggs and milk
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Blätterteig Dorten (Puff Pastry Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 Deka flour for butter dough
- egg wash
- apricot jam
- gooseberry jam
- chopped almonds
- pearl sugar
- finely chopped candied citron
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Prepare butter dough from 56 Deka flour and roll out knife-back thickness
2. Cut 6 round discs using a cake pan or bowl
3. Lay them on a water-dampened baking sheet at 2-finger width apart
4. Make several cuts with a knife and brush with egg wash, keeping the edges knife-back width dry
5. Roll out the dough scraps thinly and cut out small shapes with forms
6. Brush with egg wash and sprinkle with pearl sugar mixed with chopped almonds, finely chopped citron and pistachios
7. Bake all 6 plates at fairly strong heat in the oven
8. When done, remove from oven and cool
9. Spread one plate with dissolved apricot jam, cover with a second disc and coat with gooseberry jam, and continue until all 5 plates are used
10. On top, place the plate decorated with dough, sprinkle the whole thing with finely ground sugar and serve in a bowl
11. Instead of jam, apple puree, egg cream, vanilla, chocolate, hazelnut, wine or any desired cream may be used

title: Boeuf à la Mode (Beef in the Fashion)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one fine Ortscherzel (beef cut)
- ham, cut lengthwise and in thickness of a small finger
- bacon, cut lengthwise and in thickness of a small finger
- some green parsley, finely chopped
- some shallots, finely chopped
- some mushrooms, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut some green parsley, shallots, and some mushrooms finely together, add some salt and pepper, and mix everything well together
2. Wrap the cut pieces of ham and bacon in this mixture
3. Lard the prepared meat with these pieces and place it in a casserole so that the piece of meat lies comfortably in it
4. Place leafwise into the casserole first
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Boeuf à la Mode (Beef à la Mode)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a beautiful beef brisket
- ham, cut into strips the length and thickness of a small finger
- bacon, cut into strips the length and thickness of a small finger
- fresh parsley, finely chopped
- shallots, finely chopped
- mushrooms, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper
- bacon, sliced in leaves
- onion
- yellow turnips
- white wine, one small drinking glass
- good broth, one small drinking glass

## Directions

1. Take a beautiful beef brisket and lard it thoroughly with strips of ham and bacon, both cut to the length and thickness of a small finger.
2. Finely chop some fresh parsley, shallots, and mushrooms together, add salt and pepper, and mix well.
3. Wrap the chopped ham and bacon strips in this mixture.
4. Lard the prepared meat with these wrapped pieces and place it in a casserole so the meat lies comfortably.
5. In the casserole, first lay sliced bacon, onion, and yellow turnips in leaves.
6. Place the meat on top, cover well, and let it stew slowly over gentle heat for four hours, turning it often so it does not burn.
7. Pour in one small drinking glass of white wine and an equal amount of good broth.
8. Continue stewing until the meat is tender enough, then remove the fat.
9. It is ready to serve. This Boeuf à la Mode can also be served cold.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Boeuf à la Mode (Beef à la Mode)
body:
## Ingredients

- 70-80 dkg black chuck or oxtail
- 5 dkg bacon
- 1 pickled cucumber
- 1 parsley root
- 1 yellow turnip
- 6 h root vegetables
- 6 dkg hot fat
- 1 onion
- bay leaf
- thyme
- 5 peppercorns and spice berries
- broth or water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, salt and pepper it
2. Cut yellow turnip, parsley root, cucumber, and bacon into finger-thick pieces
3. Make holes in the meat and insert the cut vegetables and bacon
4. In a casserole, roast the finely cut root vegetables, onion, bay leaf, thyme, peppercorns and spice berries in 6 dkg hot fat until dark
5. Add the meat, pour broth or water over it
6. Braise covered for 2 to 2½ hours, basting frequently and turning occasionally
7. Remove the meat and cut into thin pieces
8. Strain the sauce, season with vinegar
9. Bring the meat and sauce to a boil once more and serve on a long platter

title: Boeuf à la Mode (Braised Beef)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece beef chuck, approximately 4 kilos
- pepper
- bacon
- marrow
- cooked tongue
- sliced onion
- root vegetables
- 1/2 liter wine
- 3/4 liter vinegar
- water or broth
- black bread

## Directions

1. Pound and salt the beef chuck weighing about 4 kilos
2. Sprinkle with pepper
3. Using a small knife, make diagonal slits in the meat and insert finger-length pieces of bacon, marrow, and cooked tongue while drawing the knife out
4. Let it rest covered in a cool place until the next day
5. Add to the casserole: sliced onion, root vegetables, 1/2 liter wine, 3/4 liter vinegar, and enough water or broth so the liquid rises above the meat
6. Braise covered for 4-5 hours until tender
7. Add a piece of black bread and continue braising covered until done
8. Place the meat on a serving dish
9. Defatten the sauce and strain it over the roast
10. For a rosy appearance resembling corned beef, rub with saltpeter mixed with 1 teaspoon of sugar 48 hours in advance
11. Let rest covered and weighted
12. After this time, rinse it off, place in a casserole lined with bacon fat or marrow
13. Pour wine and rich broth over it so the liquid rises above the meat
14. Braise covered with bunches of savory but without root vegetables

title: Boeuf à la Mode anderer Art (Beef à la Mode Another Way)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brisket, approximately 4 kilos
- bacon
- tongue
- anchovy fillets
- bacon fat
- onion
- root vegetables
- spices
- 1 bay leaf
- broth

## Directions

1. Pound and salt a beef brisket weighing about 4 kilos
2. Using a small knife, make diagonal slits and insert half finger-length pieces of bacon, then tongue and anchovy fillets alternating with these
3. Line the bottom of a casserole with bacon fat, onion, root vegetables, spices, and 1 bay leaf
4. Place the meat on top
5. Braise covered for 1 hour, adding broth occasionally
6. Continue braising as the liquid reduces

title: Boeuf à la Mode of Another Kind
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- A beef brisket of about 4 pounds
- Lard cut into small pieces
- Tongue and anchovies
- Bacon strips
- Bacon sheets for lining
- Onion
- Root vegetables
- A few grains of mixed spices
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 liter white wine
- 1/8 liter soup
- Optional: black bread with 10 crushed beef marrow bones and 3 dried figs
- Optional: 4 shallots
- Optional: 1/4 liter vinegar mixed with 1/8 liter soup instead of wine

## Directions

1. Beat and fold a beef brisket of about 4 pounds
2. Make diagonal cuts with a small knife into which you insert half-finger-length pieces of lard while drawing the knife out of the meat, then alternately insert tongue and anchovies
3. Line the bottom of a casserole with bacon sheets, onion, root vegetables, a few grains of mixed spices and 1 bay leaf
4. Place the meat on top and let it steam covered for about 1 hour while occasionally adding some soup
5. After the liquid has evaporated, add 1 liter of white wine and 1/8 liter of soup and braise for 3 to 4 hours covered
6. Transfer the meat to another casserole, pour the strained sauce over it after removing the fat, and let it stand in a warm place
7. Serve with fine vegetables or macaroni, with the sauce in a sauceboat
8. Alternatively, place the meat whole in the casserole or add black bread with crushed beef marrow bones and dried figs to thicken the sauce, or add 4 shallots from the beginning
9. You may also substitute 1/4 liter vinegar mixed with 1/8 liter soup for the wine

title: Bofstock oder russischer Rostbraten (Beef Stock or Russian Roasted Beef)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Lung or loin roast
- Salt
- Melted butter
- Horseradish, grated

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from a lung or loin roast and cut schnitzel from it as thick as a finger crosswise
2. Salt them and dip them in melted butter
3. Roast them on both sides on the grill over strong heat until they are roasted through but still very juicy
4. Place on the serving dish and sprinkle grated horseradish on top
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Boiled Milk Soufflé (Soufflé de Bouille)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 tablespoons flour
- 0.5 liter cold milk
- 12 tablespoons butter
- 16 tablespoons vanilla sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- jam

## Directions

1. Slowly pour 0.5 liter of cold milk into 16 tablespoons of flour and stir over the fire until it forms a thick dough that separates from the spoon
2. Transfer to a deep bowl and stir in 12 tablespoons of butter and let cool
3. Beat 16 tablespoons of vanilla sugar with 8 egg yolks one after another
4. Mix this well with the cooled mixture
5. Lightly fold in the snow of 8 egg whites
6. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour with this mixture
7. Cover and cook 3/4 hours in a water bath or bake in the oven
8. Turn out onto a serving dish and serve with jam at table

title: Boiled Trout with Caraway (Gekochte Forellen mit Kümmel-Geschmack)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- small trout
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- caraway
- onion
- clove

## Directions

1. Kill and clean the trout as described for blue boiled trout
2. Wrap the cleaned trout in a clean cloth dipped in the water in which they lie, wringing it out so no air can enter
3. In a fish kettle, add 1½ liters water, ½ liter vinegar, 1 tablespoon salt, ¾ teaspoon caraway, and a small piece of onion spiked with 1 clove
4. Boil this mixture for approximately ¼ hour
5. Pour the broth through a sieve over the boiling hot trout, which should be in a fish kettle or flat pan
6. Proceed as with the blue boiled trout
7. The trout will have a very pleasant flavor, though not as beautiful a blue color as the blue boiled trout
8. The broth should cover the trout only just barely
9. Can be served with a white wine sauce or with champagne instead of vinegar, or simply in salt water and caraway or white wine

title: Boretsch mit Essig und Öl (Beet Greens with Vinegar and Oil)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Boretsch (cleaned, salted, and pressed)
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and finely chop boretsch well.
2. Salt it and press out the liquid thoroughly.
3. Mix with vinegar, oil, and pepper.
4. Fresh cucumbers, peeled and finely chopped, can be prepared the same way.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bottens Auflauf (Botten's Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- mass from baked mushrooms recipe
- 12 teaspoons sugar
- 1/4 liter milk
- egg yolks
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. From the same mass as given for 'Baked Mushrooms' (No. 1380), make croquettes and cut them fine
2. In a deep bowl, add 12 teaspoons of sugar, pour in 1/4 liter milk mixed with egg yolks with constant stirring and mix well with the cut croquettes
3. Finally, lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
4. Fill a butter-greased, flour-dusted form with the above mass and cook covered in a water bath for 3/4 to 1 hour
5. After this time, remove the form, wipe it, cover with a plate, turn it out and carefully remove the form
6. Serve the soufflé with desired chaudeau, fruit juice or cream

title: Brains with Kidneys
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brain
- Fine thinly sliced kidneys
- Fat
- White soup

## Directions

1. Prepare as for brain with truffles, but roast finely sliced thinly cut kidneys together with the brain at the same time
2. If desired, before serving add several spoonfuls of white soup and braise for a few minutes

title: Braised Beef (Boeuf à l'Etouffade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-aged beef loin of approximately 3 kilograms
- Bacon slices
- Onion
- Thyme
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 piece of garlic (if desired)
- Ginger, pepper, nutmeg, and cloves (as desired)
- 1.5 liters white wine
- 1.5 liters broth

## Directions

1. Line a casserole with bacon slices, some onion, and thyme; add 1 bay leaf and, if desired, 1 piece of garlic
2. Season the meat with salt, place it on the bed of aromatics
3. Season with ginger, pepper, nutmeg, and cloves as desired
4. Braise covered for approximately 1 hour
5. Pour 1.5 liters white wine and 1.5 liters broth over it
6. Braise for approximately 2 hours; if it reduces too much, add broth as needed
7. When serving, garnish it neatly and serve the skimmed sauce in a sauce boat

title: Braised Calf's Brain (Cervelle de veau en fricassée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brain
- Butter sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the brain of skin and blood and cook in salt water
2. Cut into pieces
3. Mix lightly with a well-made butter sauce

title: Braised Mutton Cutlets (Cotelettes de mouton braisees)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton cutlets
- Braising sauce (recipe no. 189)

## Directions

1. Make a braising sauce following recipe no. 189 and let it brown.
2. Strain the sauce.
3. Braise the cutlets in the strained sauce.
4. Serve with vegetables of choice or purée.

title: Braised Noodles (Ausgedünstete Nudeln)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 dkg flour
- 2 eggs
- salt
- water
- 5 dkg sugar
- 8 dkg butter
- 1 liter milk
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from the flour, eggs, salt and water
2. Cut into wide noodles
3. Add the noodles to 1 liter boiling milk in a pot together with 5 dkg sugar and 8 dkg butter
4. Cook until the milk has thickened
5. Beat ½ liter milk with 2 egg yolks and 5 dkg sugar
6. Pour this mixture over the noodles
7. Braise in the oven for 30-45 minutes
8. Transfer to a round serving dish, dust with sugar, and serve warm

title: Braised Partridges (Perdrix braises)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Old partridges
- Ham slices (12 deciliters)
- Thyme
- Yellow turnips
- Parsley
- Bay leaf
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lard, finely cut
- Soup
- White wine
- Black bread, cut into leaves
- Cream

## Directions

1. Pluck and flame old partridges that have been hung for several days in the game larder
2. Open, salt, dress, and lard them with finely cut lard
3. Line a casserole with ham slices, thyme, yellow turnips and parsley, all cut into leaves, and a bay leaf with some thyme, salt and pepper
4. Place the larded partridges in the casserole
5. Pour in soup and white wine
6. Let them braise slowly covered
7. When the partridges are half-cooked, add finely cut black bread and let it braise together
8. Remove the fat from the sauce and add cream if desired, which must be well incorporated
9. Remove the partridges, cut the binding string, draw it out carefully, divide the partridges lengthwise in the middle, arrange on a platter, cover with the lard slices and pour the defatted strained sauce over them
10. Serve with lentil puree, braised rice, macaroni, polenta or cabbage

title: Braised Pike with Butter in Flemish Style (Brochet à la flamande)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike (approximately 1 kilo)
- salt
- pepper
- 5 bunches parsley
- parsley
- chervil herb
- chervil
- 2 pieces mushroom
- 32 deca butter
- potatoes in salt water

## Directions

1. Scale the pike, open it, wash it, cut it into 2-finger-wide pieces, and place in a casserole
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. Cut very finely: 5 bunches parsley, equal amount chervil herb and chervil, and 2 pieces mushroom
4. Let roast in 32 deca butter and pour over the fish pieces
5. Let braise covered for approximately 1/4 hour, then arrange nicely on a platter and pour the juices over
6. Serve with potatoes cooked in salt water

title: Braised Pork Breast (Poitrine de porc braisée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pork breast
- caraway
- bay leaves
- Spanish onions
- cloves
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- broth or water
- herbs

## Directions

1. Cut the breast into strips two fingers wide and place in a casserole with ½ teaspoon of caraway, 2 bay leaves, 2 Spanish onions pierced with a few cloves
2. Season with necessary salt and pepper and let it braise uncovered for a short time
3. After about ¼ hour, pour in 1½ liters of white vinegar and enough broth or water so that it covers the meat, add some tied herbs and let it braise covered until the meat is tender
4. During this time the sauce should reduce to about a sixth of its volume
5. Place the meat on a platter and serve the defatted sauce separately
6. In the same manner one uses the neck and belly meat of pork

title: Braised Pork Leg with Root Vegetables (Cuissot de porc rôti aux racines)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pork leg
- root vegetables
- salt
- fat
- vinegar
- broth

## Directions

1. After a pork leg has been salted and left in root vegetables for 3 days as described in the spit-roasted version, place it with the root vegetables and some fat in a pan
2. Pour a little vinegar over it and roast for approximately 1½ to 2 hours
3. During this time, frequently baste the meat with the juices that form and some broth
4. When the meat has a beautiful golden-brown color, remove it, cut into pieces, arrange on a platter and serve with desired purée or salad

title: Braised Pork Loin (Lungenbraten)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pork loin
- salt
- pepper
- lemons
- pork rind
- fat
- sour cream
- capers
- mustard

## Directions

1. Season the pork loin with salt and pepper, lay peeled lemon slices cut the thickness of a knife-back on it, spread the thin rind of pork over it
2. Place in a pan containing fat and roast in the oven
3. Shortly before serving, pour some sour cream mixed with a tablespoon of capers over it and let it roast in well
4. Cut the meat lengthwise into desired pieces, arrange on a platter, pour the defatted juices over it and serve with braised rice, turnips, various vegetables or salad
5. Mustard is served with this meat

title: Braised Potatoes (Pommes de terre etuvees)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram raw peeled potatoes
- 10 ounces butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- 1/2 tablespoon finely cut parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the potatoes very finely into dice and put them in butter with some salt and pepper, cover this dish and braise slowly.
2. When the potatoes are soft, sprinkle them with finely cut parsley and serve them heaped up.

title: Braised Poularde (Poularde braisee au jus)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca of bacon or butter
- onion
- root vegetables
- whole spices
- white herbs
- poularde
- peeled lemon wedges
- bacon slices
- 1/2 liter of vinegar or 1/2 liter of wine
- 1 liter of broth

## Directions

1. Place bacon or butter, onion, root vegetables, whole spices, and white herbs in a casserole
2. Lay a flambeed, cleaned, trussed, and seasoned poularde on the root vegetables, covered with peeled lemon wedges and bound with bacon slices
3. Braise covered until the root vegetables are brown
4. Pour in 1/2 liter of vinegar or 1/2 liter of wine and 1 liter of broth and let the poularde braise covered until tender
5. Remove the poularde, remove the bacon and lemon wedges, cut the poularde into desired pieces, arrange on a platter and serve the defatted, strained juice in a sauce boat

title: Braised Poularde with Boiled Tongue and Bread Croutons (Poularde braisee aux croutons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde
- finely cut bacon
- cooked ox tongue
- bread croutons

## Directions

1. Clean and truss a plucked poularde, season it, and lard it with finely cut bacon, and treat it like the braised poularde
2. When done, remove the poularde, cut the twine, pull it out, and cut the poularde into desired pieces
3. Arrange pieces of beef tongue two fingers wide and one finger long alternately with the poularde pieces in a bowl
4. Make a jus sauce from the juice in which the poularde was braised and serve in a sauce boat
5. Border the bowl with imitated woodcock roe

title: Braised Sauerkraut (Choucroute à l'étuvée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deciliters fat
- 10 deciliters bacon
- 1 head onion finely chopped
- 1 heaped liter sauerkraut
- broth
- 1/8 liter butter sauce
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- salt
- vinegar or white wine

## Directions

1. Let 10 deciliters bacon brown in 12 deciliters fat and let 1 finely chopped head of onion brown with it
2. Add 1 heaped liter sauerkraut to the browned mixture and let it braise uncovered for 10 minutes
3. Pour broth over the sauerkraut so it just covers it
4. Let it braise gently covered, stirring occasionally
5. When done and braised, add 1/8 liter butter sauce, 1 tablespoon sugar, some salt and if necessary some broth and vinegar
6. Instead of vinegar, white wine can be used
7. Stir together and let it braise well
8. The sauerkraut should be pleasantly sweet and sour

title: Braised Veal Breast (Tendrons de veau braisés)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal breast
- salted water
- white butter sauce
- fresh parsley sprigs
- cleaned mushrooms

## Directions

1. Boil the veal breast in simmering salted water until it turns white.
2. Remove the veal breast and place it in cold water for a few minutes to maintain its color.
3. Cut the meat into pieces of your choice and place in a casserole.
4. Pour white butter sauce over it, enough so that the sauce reaches a finger's height above the meat.
5. Braise completely with parsley sprigs and cleaned mushrooms, removing the fat that rises during braising.
6. Pass the sauce into another casserole, thicken it with beaten egg yolks, and pour over the meat arranged on a platter.

title: Brandteig (Choux Pastry)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter milk
- 5 Deca butter
- 22 Deca flour
- 5 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Put 1/4 liter of milk and 5 Deca of butter in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
2. Stir in 22 Deca of flour and stir this dough over the fire until it is dry and separates from the spoon and saucepan.
3. Transfer the dough to a deep bowl and let it cool with occasional stirring.
4. Once cooled, stir in 5 whole eggs, which causes the dough to stretch and take on a yellow color.
5. Neither sugar nor salt should be added during the preparation of this dough.
6. Use for various purposes.

title: Brandteig zum Thee (Choux Pastry for Tea)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter cold cream
- 12 deca butter
- 15 deca flour
- 4 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 egg yolk (if needed to thin the dough)

## Directions

1. Put 1 liter cold cream and 12 deca butter into a saucepan
2. When this boils, add 15 deca flour while stirring constantly
3. Stir the dough quickly over the fire until it is completely dry and pulls away from the sides of the pan
4. Place the dough in a deep bowl and after it has completely cooled, gradually stir in 4 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
5. If the dough is too thick, thin it with 1 egg yolk
6. Fill the dough into a metal pastry bag with a small finger-thick opening
7. Hold the pastry bag over a buttered baking sheet, press continuously on the plunger so the dough comes out, and make 2 finger-long strips 2 fingers apart on the sheet
8. Brush them with beaten egg, sprinkle with pearl sugar mixed with finely chopped almonds, pistachios or pine nuts, and bake in a fairly hot oven until golden

title: Brandteig-Krapferl in die Suppe (Choux Pastry Balls in Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 liters milk
- 5 decigrams butter
- 22 decigrams flour
- 5 whole eggs
- 4 decigrams butter or lard for filling
- calf's brain
- parsley, finely chopped
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. In a casserole, bring 1.5 liters milk and 5 decigrams butter to a boil.
2. Stir in 22 decigrams flour and stir continuously over the fire until the dough is dry and pulls away from the spoon and casserole.
3. Transfer the dough to a deep bowl and let it cool with occasional stirring.
4. Beat in 5 whole eggs.
5. Form walnut-sized balls with a spoon, fry them brown in hot lard with continuous shaking, and place them on blotting paper.
6. For the filling: In a casserole, place 4 decigrams butter or lard with a teaspoon of finely chopped parsley, let it brown somewhat.
7. Add calf's brain (freed of membranes), some pepper, and the necessary salt, stir well together, and let it roast over the fire.
8. Cut the fried balls halfway through the middle and insert a teaspoon of the prepared filling, or fill them instead with any desired forcemeat, egg dish, or braised vegetables.
9. Arrange them elevated on a dish covered with a napkin and serve with soup.

title: Branntwein aus allerley Frucht (Brandy from Various Fruits)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Poor and unripe fruit from bad years
- Plums with crushed stones
- Small apples that seem worthless
- Few ripe grapes
- Slightly spoiled and cut fruit
- Hips, sloe berries, rowan berries and similar
- Various spices and culinary herbs (sage, savory, lavender flowers, anise and the like) in moderate measure
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Crush all the ingredients together
2. Bring it into fermentation through heat and yeast, moderating the fermentation
3. Distill it
4. If a second distillation seems necessary, add various roots, herbs and spices according to desired flavor
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Branntwein zu reinigen (Purified Brandy)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 Maß corn brandy with various spices
- 14 Maß milk
- 5 Pfund fresh beef cleaned of all fat

## Directions

1. Add the milk and beef to the brandy
2. Distill the mixture
3. At the beginning, check the receiving vessel and remove the forerun which has a fusel taste
4. All subsequently distilled brandy is pure, pleasant and spicy
5. Mix equal parts sugar water with it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bratwurst (Bratwürste)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-60 dkg bratwurst
- fat
- potato hash

## Directions

1. Place 50-60 dkg bratwurst in a saucepan with not too hot fat
2. Fry slowly brown on both sides for 10-15 minutes
3. Serve with potato hash

title: Bratwürste (Bratwurst Sausages)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork meat
- Pork fat, equal amount to meat
- Salt
- Crushed bay leaves
- Thyme
- Marjoram
- Pepper
- Garlic, optional
- Water
- Thin intestines, cleaned and soaked

## Directions

1. Finely chop the pork meat and pork fat together with a mincing knife.
2. Season the mixture with salt, crushed bay leaves, thyme, marjoram, pepper, and optionally garlic.
3. Thin the mixture with some water.
4. Fill the cleaned and soaked thin intestines with the mixture and twist into desired lengths.
5. Shortly before use, place lard in a shallow pan and lay the sausages in it.
6. Brush the sausages with fat, fry them, and turn them during cooking.
7. Serve immediately, as they lose flavor with longer standing.
8. Serve with mustard or desired vegetables.

title: Bratwürste von Hammelfleisch (Bratwurst of Mutton)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton leg with fine meat
- four pounds lean mutton
- one pound fresh bacon
- ground pepper
- half a Seidel of white wine
- lemon zest
- sausage herbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Scrape the fine meat without nerves and fat from a mutton leg
2. Chop it very finely with a knife
3. Take under four pounds lean mutton one pound fresh bacon and chop it into small cubes
4. Add some ground pepper, half a Seidel of white wine, lemon zest, and fine sausage herbs
5. Work the entire mass together
6. Stuff firmly into narrow pork intestines
7. If serving with vegetables, brush with butter and fry gently on a roasting rack
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bratwürste von Hirn (Bratwurst with Brains)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- brains from 2 veal heads and 2 pork heads
- onions, finely chopped (approximately one-half pound)
- scraped bacon
- 2 pounds pork belly meat
- 6 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- semmel (roll), soaked in milk and squeezed out
- sausage herbs
- milk
- fatty pork intestines
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Clean and soak the brains well, cook them, chop finely, and set aside
2. Chop about one-half pound of onions very finely and soften in scraped bacon
3. Cook 2 pounds of pork belly meat until soft, cut very finely, and place in a grindstone
4. Add the bacon and onions along with 6 eggs and stir together
5. Add some salt, nutmeg, and a milk-soaked and squeezed out semmel
6. Work everything well together
7. Finally stir in the brains
8. Cook the sausage herbs in milk and pour this into the mixture, which should not be too thin
9. When well stirred, fill into fatty pork intestines and tie securely
10. Cook the sausages in bouillon
11. Braise in brushed paper casings on a grill
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Bratwürste von Karpfen und geräuchertem Lachs (Bratwurst with Carp and Smoked Salmon)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 pound carp, cleaned
- smoked salmon, half the amount of carp
- semmel (roll), finely diced
- 1 sidel (about 0.5 liter) white wine
- onions, one handful
- rocambole (shallot-like plant)
- butter
- sausage herbs, finely pounded
- sardelles (anchovies), cleaned (one-half pound)
- capers (3 drams)
- lemon juice
- pork intestines
- paper casings
- butter

## Directions

1. Properly clean a 3 to 4 pound carp and cut out the fine good flesh
2. Cut half the amount of flesh from a smoked salmon
3. Finely dice one semmel and mix together with a sidel of white wine
4. Soften one handful of onions and some rocambole in butter and add to the above
5. Add finely pounded sausage herbs
6. Finely chop one-half pound of cleaned sardelles and 3 drams of capers together
7. Mix with the juice of one lemon under the rest
8. Fill the mixture into pork intestines, but not too firmly, otherwise they will burst
9. Divide into desired pieces and tie with twine
10. Place in paper casings brushed with butter
11. Roast slowly on a grill
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Braune Consomme (Brown Consomme)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fat (beef marrow, lard, beef fat or veal fat)
- 1 large Spanish onion cut into finger-thick slices
- 1 kilo lean beef
- 1/2 kilo veal meat
- 12 deca lean ham, leafy-cut
- 1 old, roasted hen and 1 old rooster or old dove
- light beef broth (if available) or 4 liters cold water (if not)
- 1 small piece yellow turnip
- 1 piece parsley
- 1 piece celery

## Directions

1. Line a casserolle with fat, which can be marrow, lard, beef fat or veal fat, and 1 large Spanish onion cut into finger-thick slices
2. Add 1 kilo lean beef, 1/2 kilo veal meat, and 12 deca lean ham, leafy-cut
3. Cover the casserolle with a lid, place on fire and let it brown very well (roast), but it must not burn
4. Pour 1 ladle full of cold water over it, let it evaporate well and add a second ladle full
5. Add 1 old, roasted hen and 1 old rooster or old dove
6. Pour light beef broth (if available) approximately 4 liters over it, or if not available, pour the same amount of cold water
7. When the rising foam has been removed, add 1 small piece yellow turnip, parsley and celery
8. Further treatment is as in recipe 3

title: Braune Coulis (Brown Coulis)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure of stock (Schü)
- 1/2 pound lean ham cut into cubes
- 2 to 3 spoonfuls of flour
- good stock

## Directions

1. Let 1 measure of stock reduce over moderately strong heat with 1/2 pound of lean ham cut into cubes until it becomes like a thick syrup or resembles dissolved glue
2. Stir in 2 to 3 spoonfuls of flour
3. Add again as much good stock so that a quarter of it must reduce to become properly thick or creamy
4. Stir this broth on the fire until it boils
5. Let it simmer on slow heat from the side
6. Remove all foam and fat coming to the surface with a spoon
7. Let it simmer until it becomes properly thick or creamy
8. Strain through cheesecloth but do not press through, because it would otherwise not remain clear, especially if vegetables were cooked in it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Braune Coulis auf eine andere Art (Brown Coulis, Another Way)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter (or proportionally more as needed)
- flour
- 1/2 pound lean ham cut into cubes
- onions sliced
- parsley roots
- good brown stock

## Directions

1. Melt 1/2 pound of butter (or proportionally more depending on desired quantity), adding enough flour so it becomes neither stiff nor too thick, but can be stirred comfortably
2. Stir this continuously on moderately strong heat until it takes on a light brown color
3. Add 1/2 pound of lean ham cut into cubes, some onion slices, and parsley roots
4. Braise or brown these in it for a few more minutes
5. Then pour in as much good brown stock as desired for the coulis
6. Let it cook very slowly from the side
7. During cooking, remove all rising fat and foam with a spoon
8. Once the coulis has cooked long enough to become properly thick, pass it through cheesecloth and use it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Braune Kirschen- oder Weichselkuchen (Brown Cherry Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter
- 2 tablets grated chocolate
- 12 deca honey
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan
- cherries or morello cherries

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deca butter until fluffy, gradually add honey
2. Gently mix in 2 tablets grated chocolate and 12 deca honey
3. Fill this mixture into a baking pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
4. Allow cherries or morello cherries to sink into the dough as desired
5. Bake for approximately 1 hour at low heat in the oven
6. Turn the cake out onto a sieve and serve the following day

title: Braune Kirschen- oder Weichselkuchen mit Butter und Mandeln (Brown Cherry or Morello Cherry Cake with Butter and Almonds)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca butter
- 30 deca vanilla sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- 32 deca almonds, finely ground and sifted
- 12 egg whites
- 2 tablets grated chocolate
- 12 deca black flour

## Directions

1. Beat 24 deca butter until fluffy in a deep bowl
2. Gradually add 30 deca vanilla sugar and 16 egg yolks, stirring for 2 1/4 hours
3. Add 32 deca almonds, finely ground with the shell and sifted
4. Gently mix in 12 egg whites simultaneously with 2 tablets grated chocolate and 12 deca black flour
5. Further treatment is as with the Brown Cherry or Morello Cherry Cake (No. 1460)

title: Brauner oder Kirsch-Kuchen (Brown or Cherry Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 deca finely pounded sugar
- 14 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 nutmeg
- 32 deca almonds with skin
- 14 egg whites
- 2 tablets grated chocolate
- 12 deca flour
- cherries or morello cherries

## Directions

1. Stir together 30 deca finely pounded sugar, 14 egg yolks, 1 teaspoon cinnamon, and 1 nutmeg for 3/4 hour
2. Add 32 deca almonds with skin, finely pounded and prepared
3. Lightly fold in the snow from 14 egg whites at the same time as 2 tablets grated chocolate and 12 deca flour
4. Fill this mixture into a form brushed with butter and dusted with flour
5. If desired, cherries or morello cherries are allowed to sink into the dough
6. Bake for approximately 1 hour at low heat in the oven
7. Turn the cake out onto a sieve and serve only the following day

title: Braunes Eis (Brown Icing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- white icing base
- 16 deka fine vanilla chocolate

## Directions

1. Mix 16 deka fine vanilla chocolate into the white icing base, but do not add lemon juice

title: Braunes Zwiebel-Puree (Purée d'oignons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- geröstete Zwiebel
- braune Suppe oder braune Sauce oder aufgelöste dunkle Glace

## Directions

1. um ein braunes Zwiebel-Puree zu bekommen, gießt man zur gerösteten Zwiebel braune Suppe, oder braune Sauce, oder aufgelöste dunkle Glace

title: Braungbünstete Rüben (Brown-braised Turnips)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Turnips
- Beef fat
- Flour
- Onion
- Soup
- Butter sauce or soup with sugar

## Directions

1. Once peeled, the preparation is exactly the same as for brown-braised kohlrabi

title: Braungebuttelte Kohlrüben (Brown-braised Kohlrabi)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 large kohlrabi
- Salt water
- 8 ounces beef fat
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Onion, finely cut
- Soup, enough to cover the kohlrabi
- Butter sauce or white soup with some sugar
- Kohlrabi leaves
- Cold water
- Meat for garnish

## Directions

1. Peel the kohlrabi, cut them into cubes, and boil them half-tender in salt water
2. In 8 ounces of beef fat, brown 1 tablespoon of flour, then brown some finely cut onion in it
3. Add the drained kohlrabi and pour enough soup over them so they are covered
4. When the kohlrabi are tender, add some butter sauce or white soup with sugar
5. Boil the kohlrabi leaves separately in salt water without covering them, then place in cold water and press well
6. Cut the leaves finely and mix them with the kohlrabi
7. Garnish with meat of your choice

title: Bread Pudding (Semmelpudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 8 dkg sugar
- 3 dkg citron (cut into thin strips)
- 5 dkg swelled almonds (cut into thin sticks)
- 3 dkg raisins
- 2 dkg currants
- 4 egg whites
- 3 bread rolls (rinded, cut into cubes, and moistened with milk)

## Directions

1. Beat 10 dkg butter until light, then gradually add 4 egg yolks mixed with 8 dkg sugar.
2. Mix in 3 dkg finely cut citron, 5 dkg swelled almonds cut into sticks, 3 dkg raisins, and 2 dkg currants.
3. Fold in 4 egg whites together with 3 bread rolls that have been rinded, cut into cubes, and moistened with milk.
4. Fill the mixture into a pudding mold and let it set in steam for ½ hours.
5. Loosen, turn out, and serve warm with chaudeau or vanilla cream.

title: Bread Pudding with Mulled Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 dkg butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 5 dkg sugar
- 1 dkg breadcrumbs
- 1 clove
- 1 nutmeg seed
- lemon zest, finely chopped
- 5 dkg grated chocolate
- 4 egg whites
- 8 dkg raw grated almonds
- red wine
- 3 cloves
- 1 piece cinnamon bark
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 5 dkg butter until creamy
2. Stir in 4 egg yolks, 5 dkg sugar, 1 dkg breadcrumbs, 1 crushed clove, 1 nutmeg seed, finely chopped lemon zest, and 5 dkg grated chocolate
3. Fold in 4 egg whites and 8 dkg raw grated almonds
4. Pour into a pudding mold and cook in a water bath for 1 hour
5. Turn out onto a platter, pour mulled wine over it, and serve warm
6. For the mulled wine: boil red wine with 3 cloves, 1 piece of cinnamon bark, and sugar to taste, then strain

title: Bread Roll Soufflé (Soufflé aux Pains Molles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 tablespoons peeled bread rolls, cut into flakes
- 0.25 liter milk
- 16 tablespoons softened butter
- 16 tablespoons vanilla sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- butter
- flour
- sugar or jam

## Directions

1. Cut 12 tablespoons of peeled bread rolls into flakes, place in a saucepan, pour 0.25 liter of milk over them and stir over the fire until it forms a thick mixture
2. Set aside and let cool
3. Meanwhile, mix 16 tablespoons of softened butter with 16 tablespoons of vanilla sugar
4. Add 10 egg yolks one after another, stirring
5. Mix this well with the cooled mixture
6. Lightly fold in the snow of 8 egg whites
7. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour with this mixture
8. Cover and cook 1/3 to 3/4 hours in a water bath or bake in the oven
9. Turn out onto a serving dish, dust with sugar or pour dissolved jam over it and serve at table

title: Bread Soup (Panade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter grated breadcrumbs
- 24 Deca butter
- 2.4 liters broth or water

## Directions

1. Slowly roast the grated breadcrumbs in 24 Deca butter until light brown
2. Add 2.4 liters broth or water

title: Bread Torte (Tourte au Pain-Bis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 deca powdered sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- 30 deca almonds, finely ground with skin and passed
- 3 tablespoons grated chocolate
- 12 deca grated house bread
- 6 tablespoons rum
- 1 knife point finely cut lemon zest
- 8 egg whites
- 1 tablespoon black flour
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan

## Directions

1. Stir 30 deca powdered sugar with 16 egg yolks for 1/4 hour
2. Add 30 deca finely ground and passed almonds, 3 tablespoons grated chocolate, 12 deca grated house bread moistened with 6 tablespoons rum, and 1 knife point finely cut lemon zest
3. Stir for 1/2 hour
4. Gently fold in the snow (stiffly beaten egg whites) from 8 egg whites with 1 tablespoon black flour
5. Fill a cake pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
6. Bake for approximately 1 hour in the oven at low heat

title: Breaded Salsify Another Way
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Softly cooked salsify roots
- Cloth
- Wine vinegar
- Hot lard
- Flour
- Bread crumbs
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Dry the softly cooked roots with a cloth, dip them in wine vinegar and bake them in hot lard, or alternatively, dry the softly cooked roots with a cloth, dip them in flour, then in beaten eggs, coat with bread crumbs, and bake them in hot lard shortly before serving.

title: Breaded Trout (Mehlierte Forellen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- small trout
- flour
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- lard or fat

## Directions

1. Use the smallest trout
2. Scale and clean the trout
3. Make small slits on both sides
4. Let them stand in a cool place for some time
5. Dry them thoroughly
6. Roll them on both sides in flour
7. Then roll them in beaten egg
8. Coat well with breadcrumbs
9. Fry them light brown in very hot lard for ¾ hour before serving
10. Arrange on a suitable platter
11. Garnish with fried green parsley and lemon slices
12. Larger trout should be cut into finger-length pieces and prepared the same way

title: Breaded Veal Cutlets (Côtelettes de veau à la viennoise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets (carbonades)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Fat

## Directions

1. The preparation is the same as for the natural cutlets, with the difference that the salted cutlets are lightly dipped in flour, then dipped in beaten eggs, sprinkled with bread crumbs and fried in a shallow casserole in very hot lard.
2. With a pastry server, they are turned over once the bottom side is brown and also fried brown on the second side.

title: Breton-style Purée (Purée à la bretonne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter dried, softly cooked peas
- butter sauce
- salt
- pepper
- garlic (optional)

## Directions

1. Mix 1 liter of softly cooked dried peas with some butter sauce
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. If desired, pass a small piece of crushed garlic through the sieve with the peas
4. Sour the peas to taste as desired

title: Bretonische Sauce (Breton Sauce: Sauce à la bretonne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons butter
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 1/4 liter white beans
- water
- some vinegar
- salt
- 1/2 liter broth
- optional: 1 knife point crushed garlic
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Make a light roux from 4 tablespoons butter and equal amount flour
2. Cook 1/4 liter white beans in water until tender, drain the water, and pass the beans through a sieve into the roux
3. Cook with some vinegar, salt, and 1/2 liter broth and serve
4. If desired, add 1 knife point of crushed garlic and white pepper

title: Bretzeln (Pretzels)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg flour
- 12 dkg butter
- 3½ dkg sugar
- lemon zest
- 2 hard passed egg yolks
- egg white
- coarse sugar or chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Make a dough of 14 dkg flour, 12 dkg butter, 3½ dkg sugar, lemon zest, and 2 hard passed egg yolks on the board
2. Form into a roll and cut into equal-sized pieces
3. Shape each into a long thin roll and form into a pretzel
4. Place on an ungreased baking sheet and bake in a sharp fire until light brown
5. Once cooled, brush with egg white and dip one side in coarse sugar or chopped almonds

title: Brezeln zum Tee (Tea Pretzels)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca beaten butter
- 7 deca vanilla sugar
- 1 pinch of salt
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 deca compressed yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter lukewarm cream
- 32 deca flour

## Directions

1. Mix the beaten butter, vanilla sugar, and salt well together
2. Add 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks one after another, stirring them in
3. Add the compressed yeast dissolved in lukewarm cream and the flour to the beaten butter, stir well and beat the dough until fine
4. Let the dough rise covered in a warm place until it has risen somewhat
5. Place the dough on a flour-dusted board and form pretzels, rings, twists, or other desired shapes
6. Place these on a baking sheet covered with buttered paper
7. Let them rise lightly covered for some time in a warm place
8. Brush with beaten egg and bake slowly in a fairly hot oven

title: Bries gebacken (Fried Sweetbread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rose (sweetbread)
- salt
- breadcrumbs for breading
- hot lard for frying
- spinach
- cooked lettuce

## Directions

1. Wash the sweetbread and blanch it (cook 10 minutes in salted water).
2. Cool and cut into thin slices.
3. Salt the slices, bread them, and fry them in hot lard until golden.
4. Drain well.
5. Serve with spinach and cooked lettuce.

title: Brioche Croquettes (Briochetten-Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs (for brioche dough)
- jam of choice
- flour for coating
- eggs for dipping
- breadcrumbs for coating
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Prepare a brioche dough from 3 eggs
2. Bake it in very hot lard as described in the brioche recipe
3. Cut each brioche into hand-width and finger-length pieces
4. Spread with jam of choice and roll together
5. Dip in flour, dip in beaten egg, roll in breadcrumbs, and fry in very hot lard
6. Continue treatment as for Chestnut-Croquettes (No. 1143)

title: Brioche Croquettes with Chocolate (Briochetten-Croquettes mit Chocolate)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs (for brioche dough)
- melted chocolate cream
- flour for coating
- eggs for dipping
- breadcrumbs for coating
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Prepare as Brioche-Croquettes, with the difference that they are spread with melted chocolate cream instead of jam

title: Brioche oder Anistelkuchen (Brioche or Anise Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deca flour
- 4 Deca fresh yeast
- Some dissolved fresh yeast
- Butter
- Eggs
- Salt
- Caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Place flour on a board and make a well in the center
2. Crumble the yeast into the well and add some dissolved yeast
3. Mix with a knife until a smooth, fairly firm dough forms
4. Cover and let rest for some time
5. Form the dough into 2-finger-long and thin rods or pretzels
6. Place on a buttered baking sheet, brush with beaten egg
7. Sprinkle with finely crushed salt mixed with caraway seeds
8. Bake until golden in the oven

title: Broccoli
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- broccoli
- salt water
- braising liquid
- coulis
- fine oil

## Directions

1. Clean the broccoli carefully to the tip
2. Cook it in salt water
3. Pour off the water
4. Cook it briefly with good braising liquid and some coulis
5. For variation you can serve it with fine oil to table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Brodierter Hecht (Broiled Pike)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- lard
- anchovies
- wood chips
- thin spit
- paper
- fat
- anchovy sauce
- potatoes
- lemon slice or parsley

## Directions

1. After scaling, opening and cleaning the pike, skin it by making a cut on the back between flesh and skin from tail to head, making another similar cut on the belly, then pulling the skin down toward the tail
2. Dry the fins and tail somewhat
3. Skewer the pike alternately with lard and anchovy pieces on both sides in two rows in a slanted direction toward the head
4. Sprinkle the pike with wood chips
5. Place it on a thin spit, bind it with triple paper that has been brushed with fat, and roast it at strong heat for approximately 1 hour
6. Remove the paper 1/2 hour before serving so the lard and fish take on a nice color
7. Remove from spit and arrange in a long dish
8. Serve with anchovy sauce and potatoes
9. Place a lemon slice or sprig of parsley in the fish's opening

title: Brodsuppe (Bread Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Strong meat broth
- Toasted bread rolls, sliced

## Directions

1. Pour simmering strong meat broth over toasted bread roll slices to make a good household soup suitable for breakfast and for refreshing sick persons.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Brodsuppe mit Bratwurst und Eiern (Rye Bread Soup with Bratwurst and Eggs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound bratwurst
- Whole eggs (one per person)
- Rye bread, very thinly sliced and crisped
- Fresh parsley, finely cut
- Chives, finely cut
- Fat, properly salted broth

## Directions

1. Slice rye bread very thin and dry it until crisp. Place in soup pot and sprinkle finely cut parsley and chives over it. Cut bratwurst into thumb-sized pieces and fry well. Add bratwurst pieces and whole eggs to the soup pot. Pour hot fat and properly salted broth over everything, cover and serve. When the soup is properly simmering, the eggs will reach proper firmness in a few moments.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Brodsuppe mit Roggen (Rye Bread Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Rye bread, very thinly sliced
- Fat, properly salted broth
- Fresh parsley, finely cut
- Chives, finely cut

## Directions

1. Slice rye bread very thin and dry it until crisp. Place in soup pot, sprinkle finely cut parsley and chives over it, pour hot fat and properly salted broth over it, cover and serve.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Brotgallerte (Bread Jelly)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 loth ordinary white bread
- half a measure of water

## Directions

1. Cook 16 loth of ordinary white bread in half a measure of water for one hour
2. Strain it through a sieve
3. Let the water evaporate over gentle heat until it becomes thick
4. Let it cool
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Broth for the Sick
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 clean plucked old hen
- 3 cleaned calf's feet
- 1 liter white wine
- 1½ liters water

## Directions

1. Cook the hen and calf's feet together with the white wine and water until reduced to 1½ liters
2. Strain it, remove the fat completely, lightly whip the meat, add the liquid back
3. When clear, season with salt
4. If not acidic enough, add lemon or vinegar
5. If brown broth is desired, add 1 spoonful of caramelized sugar and place in the coolness

title: Brotmaffer (Bread Water)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of bread or rolls
- 1 liter boiling water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Toast some slices of bread or rolls in the oven or before the fire until brown.
2. Put them in a pot, pour 1 liter of boiling water over them, and let it cool uncovered.
3. It is strained and drunk cold with or without sugar.

title: Brotsuppe (Bread Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Grated bread rind
- Water
- Wine
- Sugar
- Caraway
- Four loths rusks
- One and a half pounds water
- Two to three spoonfuls wine
- Half loth sugar
- One egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cook the grated bread rind in two parts water and one part wine
2. Add some sugar and caraway
3. Alternatively, cook four loths rusks in one and a half pounds water until sufficiently softened
4. Add two to three spoonfuls wine and half loth sugar beaten with an egg yolk
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Brottorte (Bread Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 14 dkg sugar
- 12 dkg raw grated almonds
- 8 egg whites
- 14 dkg grated chocolate
- 5 dkg rum-moistened bread or roll crumbs
- jam
- optional: mulled wine

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks with 14 dkg sugar for 20 minutes
2. Mix in 12 dkg raw grated almonds
3. Fold in 8 stiff egg whites together with 14 dkg grated chocolate and 5 dkg rum-moistened bread or roll crumbs
4. Fill the mixture into the form and bake
5. Brush inside and outside with jam and brown glaze, or fill only inside and pour warm mulled wine over it

title: Brown Linzer Torte (Tourte à la Linz)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 Deka butter
- 32 Deka dark flour
- 32 Deka blanched and passed almonds
- 24 Deka finely crushed sugar
- 1 knife tip lemon zest
- 1 knife tip spice
- 1 knife tip nutmeg
- 1 knife tip cinnamon
- 4 egg yolks
- jam for filling

## Directions

1. Rub butter and flour together finely on a board
2. Add blanched and passed almonds, sugar, lemon zest, spice, nutmeg, and cinnamon
3. Make a well and add egg yolks
4. Quickly work into a dough and cover
5. Let rest for 1/2 hour in a cool place
6. Roll out dough to finger thickness on a flour-dusted board
7. Press out with a torte form and place in a butter-greased and flour-dusted torte form
8. Brush with beaten egg
9. Make thin strips from remaining dough and arrange in a lattice pattern on top
10. Brush with beaten egg and edge with a finger-thick dough border
11. Brush the border with beaten egg
12. Bake for 1/2 to 3/4 hours
13. Remove and when completely cool, fill the lattice with jam and sprinkle sugar on the edges

title: Brown Ragout for Sweet Fillings
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Ingredients as in the preceding ragout
- Finely diced truffles
- Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as the preceding ragout
2. Add finely diced truffles and some Madeira wine
3. Use as before

title: Brustfern Ängere (Poitrine Pande)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Breast of beef
- Salt
- Pepper
- Marrow if desired
- Fat
- Bacon
- Root vegetables
- White wine
- Broth
- String

## Directions

1. Pound the breast of beef, wipe it with a cloth, remove the bones, and rub it thoroughly with salt, pepper, and marrow if desired.
2. Roll the thicker part inward and roll the meat against the thinner part, then bind with string.
3. Place a piece of fat, some bacon, and root vegetables in a casserole, lay the breast on top so that the end of the rolled meat comes to lie on top.
4. Cover the casserole and let the breast brown slightly, then baste it with as much white wine and broth as will cover the meat.
5. The meat must braise covered in the oven for approximately 2 hours.
6. Remove from the casserole and, after removing the string, serve. Pass and degrease the juices and serve them separately.
7. This roast can also be served cold and sliced.

title: Brustkernstück mit Kren überzogen (Breast Piece with Horseradish Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a beautiful piece of breast meat, six to seven pounds, boiled soft
- horseradish sauce, slightly thicker than usual for beef
- four egg yolks
- melted butter
- fine breadcrumbs
- fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Take a beautiful piece of breast meat of six to seven pounds, boiled soft in broth, remove it and strain it.
2. Make a horseradish sauce, only slightly thicker than customary for beef.
3. Stir four egg yolks into the horseradish sauce.
4. Coat the breast piece one finger thick with this mixture.
5. Pour melted butter over it and sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs.
6. Before serving, place it in the oven and let it become a beautiful breadcrumb color.
7. Garnish neatly with fresh parsley and serve.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Brösel-Pastetchehen (Breadcrumb Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka butter
- 12 Deka granulated sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- Snow from 4 egg whites
- 12 Deka breadcrumbs
- Butter for forms
- Flour for forms
- Finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 16 Deka butter until creamy and combine with 12 Deka granulated sugar
2. Add 8 egg yolks, one after another
3. Finally fold in the snow from 4 egg whites simultaneously with 12 Deka breadcrumbs
4. Fill butter-greased, flour-dusted almond molds to the halfway point
5. Bake 1/4 hour in the oven
6. Arrange elevated on a bowl and sprinkle with finely sifted sugar or serve with desired glaze

title: Brösel-Wurst (Consommé au pain blanc)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca butter
- 6 egg yolks
- salt
- 18 deca white breadcrumbs
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 12 deca butter until fluffy
2. Stir in 6 egg yolks and some salt
3. Lightly fold in 18 deca white breadcrumbs with the snow from 5 egg whites
4. Treatment same as for potato dumplings

title: Bröselteigbäckerei (Crumb Dough Baking) - Type 1
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg flour
- 10 dkg butter
- 7 dkg sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- pinch of cinnamon
- lemon zest
- egg white
- coarse sugar or chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Crumble 14 dkg flour with 10 dkg butter on the board
2. Mix with 7 dkg sugar, 2 egg yolks, a pinch of cinnamon, and lemon zest
3. Let the dough become stiff in a cool place
4. Roll out thin and cut into stars and rings
5. Place on an ungreased baking sheet and bake light brown
6. Treat like the pretzels: brush with egg white and dip in coarse sugar or chopped almonds

title: Bröſelknöderl (Breadcrumb Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 bread rolls
- water
- 5 dkg butter or fat
- 1 egg
- salt
- parsley
- 12-15 dkg breadcrumbs
- salt water

## Directions

1. Remove crusts from bread rolls and place in water
2. After about 5 minutes, remove and squeeze out very well
3. Cream 5 dkg butter or fat and stir in 1 egg, salt, finely chopped parsley, the bread rolls, and about 12-15 dkg breadcrumbs
4. Let stand for a while
5. Form small dumplings
6. Place in a casserole in boiling salted water
7. Cook slowly for 5 minutes

title: Bröſelteig⸗Apfelſtrudel (Crumb Dough Apple Strudel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 decagrams flour
- 12.5 decagrams butter
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 spoonful milk
- 1 spoonful sugar
- Lemon peel
- Salt
- Apples, peeled and cored
- 8-10 sugar cubes
- Lemon peel
- Water
- 3 decagrams raisins
- Egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Make a crumb dough from 14 decagrams flour, 12.5 decagrams butter, 1 egg yolk, 1 spoonful milk, 1 spoonful sugar, lemon peel, and salt (see doughs)
2. After resting, roll out thinly and cut into a rectangle
3. Place on an ungreased baking sheet
4. Put filling down the middle lengthwise
5. Fold the dough over from both sides
6. From the dough scraps, cut narrow strips, brush with egg white, and lay them crosswise on the strudel
7. Brush the strudel with beaten egg
8. Bake at high heat until light brown
9. Cool, cut into equal pieces
10. Sprinkle with sugar and cool before serving
11. For the filling: Peel, core, and quarter the apples; stew gently with 8-10 sugar cubes, some lemon peel, a little water, and 3 decagrams raisins; the filling must be dry and only used when cooled

title: Bubbert von Eyern (Egg Bubbert)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- butter
- eggs
- sugar
- grated lemon zest
- salt

## Directions

1. First stir a small spoonful of flour into a little melted butter
2. Beat as many eggs as needed
3. Add sugar, grated lemon zest, a little salt, and about one quarter-seidel of butter for every 8 eggs
4. Stir everything together well
5. Pour into a dish lined with cold butter
6. Place the dish in a baking pan on salt or sand, level it so it sits straight, and bake with heat from below and above
7. Alternatively, for no more than 8 eggs, place the dish over a pot of boiling water and cook it until set
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Buchteln (Filled Yeast Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25-30 dkg flour
- 8 dkg butter or beef lard
- salt
- 2 spoons sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 egg yolk
- yeast starter (Dampfl)
- lukewarm milk as needed
- jam, powidl (plum paste), quark filling, or poppy seed filling

## Directions

1. Warm the flour in a mixing bowl with salt and 2 spoons sugar.
2. Rub in 8 dkg butter or beef lard in pieces, add 1 egg, 1 egg yolk, the risen yeast starter, and lukewarm milk as needed.
3. Beat the dough until it detaches from the spoon, cover it, and let it rise near the stove.
4. When it has doubled in size, use a floured spoon to place small pieces of dough on a floured board, stretch them out with floured fingers, fill them with jam, powidl, quark filling, or poppy seed filling, close the dough over the filling, place them with the filled side down in a well-greased pan close together, brush each one on top and on the sides with melted fat, and fill the pan this way.
5. If the pan is too large, place a stick wrapped in paper against the last rolls.
6. Let them rise for another 15 minutes near the stove and bake them at moderate heat until light brown.
7. Turn them out, sprinkle with sugar while still hot, separate them, and serve cooled.

title: Burgunder Chaudeau (Burgundy Wine Chaudeau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter Burgundy wine
- 24 deca sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- Small piece of cinnamon
- 1 clove

## Directions

1. Bring 1/2 liter Burgundy wine to a boil with 24 deca sugar, 12 egg yolks, and if desired, a small piece of cinnamon and 1 clove
2. Treat further as described in the previous chaudeau recipe

title: Burgunders Soufflé (Burgundy Wine Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deka trimmed, thinly sliced rolls
- butter for coating the mold
- flour for dusting the mold
- dried currants
- raisins
- sliced almonds
- finely cut citron peel
- 1/2 liter red wine
- 24 Grams sugar
- cinnamon to taste
- nutmeg
- 1 piece lemon peel
- 7 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Bake the 28 Deka of trimmed, thinly sliced rolls in the oven.
2. Lay them two by two on top of each other, brushing between with any desired preserved filling.
3. Line the bottom of a mold brushed with butter and dusted with flour with these rolls.
4. Sprinkle these with dried currants, raisins, finely cut almonds, and finely cut citron peel.
5. Cover again with a layer of the rolls mentioned above and continue in this manner until all rolls are used.
6. Heat 1/2 liter red wine with 24 Grams sugar, cinnamon to taste, nutmeg, and 1 piece lemon peel, bringing it to a boil once, then set it aside.
7. After it has cooled completely, beat it with 7 egg yolks and pour it over the rolls in the mold.
8. Once it has absorbed somewhat, bake for 1 1/2 hours in the oven.

title: Burgundy Leaves
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Burgundy beet leaves
- Butter
- Flour
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the leaves and prepare the leaves as you would spinach.

title: Butter Cake (Tourte aa beurre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca butter
- 28 deca vanilla sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- 28 deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat 28 deca butter until fluffy, mix in 28 deca vanilla sugar
2. Gradually beat in 8 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
3. Lightly mix in the snow of 8 egg whites with 28 deca flour
4. Divide this mass into 2 parts, fill into buttered and floured cake forms and bake at low heat in the oven
5. Remove the hoops from the forms, place the cakes on a board or sieve, and after complete cooling, spread with dissolved jam
6. Stack them so that the sides that were on the baking sheet during baking have one side down and one up
7. Place this cake on a sheet of writing paper, pour hot jam over it, and after it is completely dry, cover with white glaze

title: Butter Dough Apple Strudel (Butterteig-Apfel-Strudel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg flour
- 21 dkg butter
- 1 egg
- salt
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- water
- egg white for brushing
- apples
- 8-10 cubes cube sugar
- water
- lemon peel
- 3 dkg raisins

## Directions

1. Prepare a butter dough from the flour, butter, egg, salt, vinegar, and water
2. Roll out into a rectangle and place on an ungreased baking sheet
3. Fill down the middle lengthwise
4. Fold the dough over from both sides to cover the filling
5. Brush with egg white
6. Cut narrow strips from the leftover dough and place them crosswise over the strudel
7. Brush with beaten egg and bake
8. Cut diagonally, dust with sugar, and serve cooled
9. For the filling: peel, core, and quarter the apples; braise with cube sugar, water, lemon peel, and raisins until soft; cool completely and use without the liquid

title: Butter Dough Currant Strudel (Butterteig-Ribibsel-Strudel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg flour
- 21 dkg butter
- 1 egg
- salt
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- water
- egg white for brushing
- preserved currants

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as the apple strudel, using preserved currants as filling instead of apples

title: Butter gefüllter Hecht (Pike Filled with Butter and Vinegar)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- vinegar (or tarragon vinegar)
- onion piece
- parsley root, cut in leaflets
- 5 sprigs thyme
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1/4 teaspoon caraway (optional if using tarragon vinegar)
- pike

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, put 1 part water and 1 part vinegar (tarragon vinegar if desired), 1 piece onion, some parsley root, all cut in leaflets, 5 sprigs thyme, 1 tablespoon salt, and optionally 1/4 teaspoon caraway (no caraway if using tarragon vinegar)
2. Let this simmer for 1/2 hour
3. Meanwhile, prepare the pike in the fish pan as for boiled pike (No. 894), which can also be cut into 2-finger-wide pieces
4. Pour the simmering broth over it, which should cover the pike and have an agreeably sour taste
5. The pike is otherwise treated as with boiled pike

title: Butter Halfen (Butter Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter flour
- 3 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
- 4 deca melted beef tallow
- 7 1/4 liter milk
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 6 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 8 deca lard
- clarified butter

## Directions

1. Put 1 liter flour into a deep bowl, make a well in the center, pour in the yeast mixture dissolved in lukewarm milk, stir a little together and let it stand covered in a warm place
2. Meanwhile, beat together 4 deca melted beef tallow, 7 1/4 liter milk, 1 tablespoon sugar, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 6 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs
3. Pour this mixture into the flour, stir well and beat the dough for a long time until it separates from the spoon, then let it stand covered in a warm place until it has risen to double height
4. When this has happened, put 8 deca lard in a casserole, let it become hot and put a few drops into each indentation of a pastry pan, then put 1 tablespoon of the dough into each
5. When the bottom side is brown, pour some hot lard or clarified butter from the side into each pastry, turn the halfen using a pastry needle or fork and bake the other side brown as well
6. Place the halfen on paper, lay 2 together and fill between them with whatever filling desired, arrange on a plate and serve sprinkled with sugar

title: Butter Mayonnaise (Mayonnaise à Beurre Frais)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 teaspoons butter
- 4 raw egg yolks
- 1/16 to 1 1/4 liters fine oil
- juice of 2 oranges or 2 tablespoons vinegar
- salt to taste
- 1 knife tip white pepper
- mustard or herbs optionally

## Directions

1. Beat 6 teaspoons of butter in a deep bowl until very fluffy
2. Gradually stir in 4 raw egg yolks, beat both together for 10 minutes and then place on ice
3. Stir in 1/16 to 1 1/4 liters of fine oil drop by drop along with the juice of 2 oranges or 2 tablespoons of vinegar
4. Stir in the necessary salt together with 1 knife tip white pepper
5. Keep uncovered on ice until use
6. Before use, mustard or herbs can be stirred in as mentioned for the previously described mayonnaise

title: Butter Nockerl (Noques au beurre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deka butter
- 6 tablespoons cold uncooked milk
- 1 tablespoon milk after each tablespoon
- 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
- approximately 1.5 liters flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until creamy
2. Stir in 6 tablespoons cold milk, then after each tablespoon add 1 tablespoon milk
3. Gradually add 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
4. Bind with approximately 1.5 liters flour
5. Using a spoon that is dipped in boiling water each time, drop pieces of dough into boiling soup or salted water
6. Alternatively, the egg whites can be beaten to stiff peaks and folded in

title: Butter Pastry (Butterbäckerei)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- 20 deca finely sifted sugar
- 6 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 20 deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks with sugar for half an hour
2. Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites together with flour
3. Spread finger-high on baking sheet lined with butter
4. Bake at moderate heat in oven
5. When done, cut into desired pieces or cut out shapes while still warm

title: Butter Pastry Dough (Pâte Feuilletée)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of the finest wheat flour
- Three quarters pound good unsalted butter, or butter freed of salt by multiple washings
- Two ripe eggs
- One spoon full of good French brandy
- One tablespoon full of sweet cream

## Directions

1. Lay half of the flour on a smooth, clean table, preferably on a marble plate, make a depression in it
2. Put the eggs, brandy, and cream into the depression
3. Work this all together into a firm dough so that it can be rolled out
4. Take half of the butter, lay it on the rolled out dough, fold the dough over the butter
5. Sprinkle with a portion of the remaining flour, choose a new spot on the table, then turn the dough mass so that the upper side lies on the flour-dusted spot
6. Roll the entire mass out and together several times, then take the other half of the butter and do the same as with the first, until all the flour is used
7. Then roll out the dough fairly thin and cut it according to the form you wish to give the pastry
8. Finally, lay the dough in layers one upon another to the height desired
9. Between each layer brush a little egg yolk, and brush it on the outside as well so that the pastry obtains a beautiful, golden-brown color
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Butter with Shallots (Beurre aux Echalotes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter (prepared as in the previous recipe)
- 3 crushed shallots

## Directions

1. Add the crushed shallots to the butter from the previous recipe
2. Mix well together
3. Serve especially with beef tenderloin

title: Butter with Shallots (Beurre aux Échalotes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- maitre d'hôtel butter
- 3 crushed shallots

## Directions

1. To the previously described maitre d'hôtel butter, add 3 crushed shallots and mix them well
2. Serve this butter especially with roasted beef

title: Butter's Torte with Almonds (Tourte aa beurre mit Mandeln)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deca sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon flour per egg
- 32 deca melted butter
- 32 deca finely ground and sifted almonds
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 32 deca sugar with 10 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
2. After each egg, mix in 1 tablespoon of flour
3. Add 32 deca melted butter and 32 deca finely ground and sifted almonds gradually, stirring well after each spoonful of almonds and adding 1 tablespoon butter
4. Lightly mix in the stiffly beaten snow of 10 egg whites
5. Divide this mass into three equal parts, fill into buttered and floured cake forms and bake at low heat for approximately 1 hour
6. Treat as the previously described cake

title: Butter-Sauce (Butter Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces butter
- 8 ounces flour
- 1/2 liter white beef broth

## Directions

1. Put 8 ounces butter in a saucepan and stir in 8 ounces flour
2. Gradually pour in 1/2 liter white beef broth and stir
3. Let it cook until boiling, then pass through a sieve
4. Use for various sauces and vegetables

title: Butter-Sauce with fine herbs (Sauce aux fines-herbes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter-Sauce
- green parsley
- savory
- tarragon
- sorrel
- salt water
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare like the previous recipe, but when serving, finely chop together a handful of green parsley, savory, tarragon, and sorrel (which have been previously cooked in salt water)
2. Stir into the sauce with a knife point of white pepper
3. This sauce is served with fish

title: Butter-Sauce with fresh parsley (Butter Sauce with Parsley)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter-Sauce flavored with lemon juice (from recipe 191)
- 1 tablespoon parsley leaves, plucked
- water

## Directions

1. Add 1 tablespoon plucked parsley leaves to the previous Butter-Sauce flavored with lemon juice, which have been briefly boiled in simmering water and then drained
2. This should only be done when serving, otherwise the beautiful green color of the boiled parsley will be lost

title: Butterbrot-Stangen mit Käse zum Thee (Butter Pastry Sticks with Cheese for Tea)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter pastry dough of finest kind, unsweetened
- Eggs, beaten
- Grated Parmesan or Emmental cheese

## Directions

1. Roll the butter pastry dough to the thickness of a knife back and cut into strips 2 fingers wide and 2 fingers long.
2. Brush lightly with beaten eggs, but not the edges.
3. Place grated Parmesan or Emmental cheese, one small-finger high, in the middle.
4. Fold the dough over on itself, press together, cut with a knife or needle, pierce the dough with a knife, brush with beaten eggs.
5. Bake in the oven and remove carefully with a knife.
6. Serve immediately. The sticks should be enjoyed fresh.

title: Butterbrote (Butter Breads)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg flour
- 21 dkg butter, diced
- 14 dkg sugar
- 14 dkg raw grated, sieved almonds
- egg white
- pistachio nuts

## Directions

1. Make a dough of 14 dkg flour, 21 dkg diced butter, 14 dkg sugar, and 14 dkg raw grated, sieved almonds on the board
2. Roll out thinly in portions as the dough breaks easily; if needed, knead in 1 small piece of butter
3. Cut into ellipse shapes and halve lengthwise to resemble bread slices
4. Place on an ungreased baking sheet and bake light brown
5. Cool and brush with vanilla butter, then place on baking sheet again and put in an open oven for a few minutes to dry
6. Brush the top with egg white and sprinkle with softened, chopped pistachios

title: Butterkoch (Butter Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 loth butter
- 16 eggs
- 12 loth sugar
- zest of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Beat 20 loth butter until smooth
2. Beat in 16 eggs, one after another
3. Add 12 loth sugar and zest of 1 lemon
4. Seal the dough well with a ring mold
5. Bake slowly until done
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Butternockerl (Butter Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- 2 egg whites
- 6 dkg flour

## Directions

1. To the mixture of 5 dkg butter, add 2 egg yolks and salt.
2. Fold in the stiffly beaten snow from 2 egg whites together with 6 dkg flour.
3. Let it stand for a while.
4. With a spoon dipped in hot broth, form small dumplings, place them in the strained boiling beef broth, and let them cook slowly for 5 minutes.
5. If the dough is too loose, add more flour.

title: Butterpaſtete mit Ragout (Butter Pastry with Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough, slightly thinner than a small finger
- egg white
- butter dough for border
- small crisps of butter dough
- butter sauce
- veal, diced
- veal sweetbreads
- mushrooms
- butter
- parsley
- asparagus or cauliflower
- crayfish
- broth
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Using a warm knife, cut a piece of butter dough from a dish following its shape
