2. Brush the edge with egg white and apply a two-finger-wide band of butter dough neatly around the edge
3. From the remaining dough, cut out small crispels the size of a twenty-kreuzer piece
4. Arrange them around the wreath leaving a two-finger-wide space between them
5. Bake nicely
6. Prepare the ragout by making a butter sauce
7. Add diced veal cooked in salted water and some veal sweetbreads
8. Braise 15 mushrooms with butter and parsley
9. Cut asparagus or cauliflower, cooked in salted water, into oblong pieces
10. Mix with 20 to 30 crayfish, bring to a boil
11. Stir in 2 spoonfuls of broth mixed with an egg yolk
12. Add a little more broth
13. Once the butter dough is nicely golden, pile the ragout high in the opening
14. For fast days, fish such as sturgeon, pike, or salmon can be used instead of veal
15. On meat days, calf's head can also be used, cut into large dice after boiling and removing the bones
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Butterrot (Butter Confection)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deka beaten butter
- 12 deka lemon sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 16 deka blanched, finely chopped almonds
- 1 egg white, very finely chopped
- 1 tablespoon grated chocolate
- 1 pinch cinnamon
- 8 deka flour

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deka beaten butter gradually with 12 deka lemon sugar and 2 egg yolks
2. Mix in 16 deka blanched finely chopped almonds, 1 tablespoon grated chocolate, 1 pinch cinnamon, and 8 deka flour
3. Roll out the dough on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a small finger
4. Cut into desired pieces and place on a butter-greased sheet
5. Brush with egg
6. Bake, then cover with white glaze and dry in a hot oven
7. Optionally, sprinkle the cut pieces with blanched finely cut pistachios after glazing, which gives the appearance of finely cut chives

title: Butterscheiben (Butter Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg flour
- 10 dkg butter
- 7 dkg sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- jam

## Directions

1. Make a dough of 14 dkg flour, 10 dkg butter, 7 dkg sugar, and 2 egg yolks on the board
2. If soft, let rest in a cool place until stiff
3. Roll out and cut into rounds
4. Place on an ungreased baking sheet
5. Bake in a sharp fire until light brown
6. Cool, brush with jam, and sandwich two together
7. Sprinkle with sugar or ice

title: Butterteig (Butter Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- butter (equal amount to flour)
- pinch of salt
- 1 egg
- 2 spoonfuls vinegar or wine or lemon juice
- 2 spoonfuls cold water

## Directions

1. Place the flour on a pastry board. Knead one third of it with the butter and form it into a brick shape, place on ice.
2. With the remaining flour, salt, 1 egg, 2 spoonfuls vinegar (or wine or lemon juice) and 2 spoonfuls cold water, make a strudel dough, work it very well until tender, and let it rest under a cold pot for 15 minutes.
3. Roll it out into a rectangle, place the butter brick in the middle, fold the dough from top to bottom and vice versa, also from right to left over it.
4. Carefully tap with the rolling pin first in the middle, then at top and bottom so the butter distributes.
5. Lightly roll it out so that a long but not wide strip (approximately 45 cm long, 15 cm wide) is formed, fold it into thirds, turn the dough so the open sides are at top and bottom and roll it out again to a strip.
6. Fold together once more and roll out. After folding and rolling out a third time, fold the dough a third time, wrap in paper, place on ice or in a cold place.
7. Let it rest at least 20 minutes. After that, roll out and fold together three more times, place on ice until this has been repeated a third time.
8. Before using, it must rest for a full hour to become stiff. Then it can be rolled out as thick or thin as desired.
9. It is best to make the dough in the evening and use it the next day. Leftover pieces are placed together, tapped with the rolling pin and rolled out again. They should not be kneaded together by hand as the dough would become too warm and not work.
10. The dough is always placed on an ungreased sheet and needs a hot oven. The butter pastries (vol au vent) can be made earlier and reheated, as they are better warm, or they are baked and filled in the last moment. The dough bakes for approximately 8-15 minutes.

title: Butterteig feinster Art (Puff Pastry of the Finest Kind)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold and firm butter
- Finest flour
- A little salt

## Directions

1. Lay butter on the flour and beat them together widely with the butter.
2. Turn the dough so that the two open sides lie right and left, tap lightly with the rolling pin on the middle of the dough and push the butter through rolling until toward the edge.
3. Once the dough is rolled out lengthwise into a strip, fold it from right to left again so that it lies in 3 equal parts over one another.
4. Place it on a tray dusted with flour and let it rest covered at a cold place.
5. After about 1/4 hour, place the dough again on the flour-dusted board so that the open sides lie right and left, roll it out and proceed as before.
6. Repeat this three times, after which the dough can be used in various ways, for strudel, pastries, etc.

title: Butterteig mit Germ (Butter Pastry with Yeast)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 deca flour
- 50 deca cold firm butter, cut into small pieces
- 8 deca sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 8 egg yolks
- 4 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter cold cream

## Directions

1. Place flour on a board
2. Cut cold firm butter into small pieces and rub finely with both hands with the flour
3. Make a well in the middle
4. Add 8 deca sugar, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 8 egg yolks, and 4 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter cold cream into the well
5. Mix with a knife and work with the greatest speed with both hands into a smooth dough
6. Place on a flour-dusted sheet, cover with a cloth, and let rest in a cold place
7. After 2 1/4 hours, roll out strongly knife-back-thick on a flour-dusted board and can now be used for various purposes

title: Butterteig mit Marmelade (Butter Pastry with Jam)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter pastry dough made from 7-8 parts flour
- Jam of choice
- Eggs, beaten

## Directions

1. Cut the knife-back-thick butter pastry dough into 2 parts
2. Roll one part onto the rolling pin and then unroll it immediately on a water-dampened baking sheet
3. Spread with jam of choice, but only so far that a 2-finger-wide border remains free on all four sides
4. Brush the border with beaten eggs
5. Roll the second part of the dough onto the rolling pin and roll it over the jam so that the edges lie exactly on top of each other and press them together lightly
6. Make several cuts in the top pastry sheet with a knife
7. Brush the dough with beaten eggs
8. Place in the oven and bake at strong heat for 1.5 to 3/4 hours
9. Once the butter pastry is completely baked, brush it very thinly with the mass it was filled with and dry it in the somewhat overfilled oven for a few minutes
10. Remove the sheet from the oven and cover the butter pastry after it has cooled completely with spun sugar or glaze of choice
11. Once this is dry, cut the butter pastry into pieces of choice

title: Butterteig und Mürbeter Teig zu süßen Mehlspeisen (Puff Pastry and Shortcrust Pastry for Sweet Flour Dishes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 Deca flour
- Butter
- 4 Deca finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as the puff pastries mentioned above, with the difference that to the specified measure (56 Deca) 4 Deca finely sifted sugar is added.

title: Butterteig unterer Art (Puff Pastry of a Lower Kind)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 Deca cold and firm butter
- One third of 56 Deca finest flour
- 3/4 of 56 Deca flour
- 1/8 teaspoon of salt
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 quart of cream or sour cream
- 3 tablespoons rum or wine

## Directions

1. Beat 56 Deca cold and firm butter and one third of 56 Deca finest flour together lightly as indicated for 'Butterteig feinster Art' No. 1045.
2. Place on a flour-dusted board, cover with cloth and set aside.
3. On a board place the remaining 3/4 of 56 Deca flour, make a depression in the middle, add 1/8 teaspoon salt, 4 egg yolks, 1/4 quart of cream or sour cream, and 3 tablespoons rum or wine, and work into a smooth dough as indicated for 'Butterteig feinster Art'.
4. Place on a flour-dusted tray, cover with cloth and let rest at a cold place.
5. After 1/4 hour, place the dough on the flour-dusted board and roll out to the size of the butter.
6. Place the prepared cold butter on the rolled-out dough and proceed as indicated for 'Butterteig feinster Art' (No. 1045).
7. Use for pastries, croustades, biscuits, strudels, etc.

title: Butterteig zu Stefanies Sufferln und kleinen Bäckereien (Puff Pastry for Stefanies Sufferln and Small Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 Deca beaten butter
- 8 Deca finely crushed sugar
- 1 knife-point finely cut lemon peel
- 3 egg yolks
- 32 Deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat 24 Deca beaten butter gradually with 8 Deca finely crushed sugar, 1 knife-point of finely cut lemon peel and 3 egg yolks until well mixed.
2. Mix thoroughly with 32 Deca flour.
3. Smooth the dough on a flour-dusted board and let it rest covered at a cold place.
4. Once the dough feels firm, roll it out on a flour-dusted board knife-back thick, divide it into 2 equal parts.
5. Roll one part onto a rolling pin and unroll it onto a buttered baking sheet.
6. Brush the dough with beaten egg, leaving a finger-width border free on all 4 sides, which brush with whipped egg white.
7. Roll the second part of the dough onto the rolling pin and place it over the brushed dough so that the edges lie evenly on one another, pressing them together.
8. Brush the dough with whipped egg and bake in the oven at medium heat until golden.
9. Remove the baking sheet and once the dough has completely cooled, glaze it with glaze.
10. Once the glaze is completely dry, cut the dough into finger-width strips.

title: Butterteig, feinster Art (Puff Pastry, Finest Type - Feuilletage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 drachm flour
- 56 drachm very cold and firm butter
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 4 egg yolks
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1/4 liter cold water

## Directions

1. Place flour on a pastry board and cut very cold and firm butter into small pieces into one-third of the flour using a knife
2. With a pastry roller, lightly strike until the flour has absorbed the butter and the butter appears flaky
3. Form the butter in an oblong shape, place on a board dusted with flour, cover with a cloth, and set in a cool place or on ice
4. Make a well in the remaining flour on the board, add 1/2 teaspoon salt, 4 egg yolks, and the juice of 1/2 lemon
5. Stir while gradually adding 1/4 liter cold water with the left hand, using the right hand to form a dough
6. Work the dough as you would strudel dough with both hands until blisters appear
7. If the dough contains too much moisture and sticks to the board, dust both hands and board with a little flour, but do not work in too much flour or the dough will become too stiff
8. Once blisters appear, place the dough on a flour-dusted board, dust with flour, and roll out with a pastry roller while the butter is still cold
9. Place the cold butter on top and fold the dough together with the butter on both sides
10. Turn the dough so the open sides are on the right and left, lightly tap the middle of the dough with the pastry roller, and push the butter through by rolling until it reaches the edges
11. Once the dough is rolled out lengthwise into a strip, fold again from right to left so it lies in 3 equal layers on top of each other
12. Place on a flour-dusted sheet pan and let rest covered in a cool place
13. After approximately 1/4 hour, place the dough again on a flour-dusted board

title: Butterteig-Torte (Butter Pastry Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter pastry of finest type, made from 56 deca flour
- Eggs, beaten
- Cooked poppy seeds
- Cooked apple filling
- Compote
- Jam of choice
- Cream of choice
- Chestnut purée
- Apple compote with wine and raisins
- Roasted chestnuts dusted with sugar

## Directions

1. After resting for 1 hour, roll out the dough knife-back-thick on a flour-dusted board
2. Cut out about 5 round plates using a cake form and place them 3 fingers apart on a water-dampened baking sheet
3. Make several cuts in each round plate with a knife
4. Brush with beaten eggs
5. On one of the plates, lay finger-wide strips of butter pastry cutout or scraped in a lattice pattern, 2 fingers from the edge, with a finger-wide border remaining free all around
6. Lay a border of the same width on this border, brush with beaten eggs, leaving knife-back-wide borders dry
7. Place all plates in the oven and bake at fairly strong heat
8. Remove the plate from the oven and detach it carefully from the sheet with a thin knife
9. Brush 4 of the baked plates with cooked poppy seeds, cooked apple filling, compote, jam, cream of choice, chestnut purée, or apple compote with wine and raisins
10. Layer them so that a brushed side is covered by a dry side and place last the plate that was covered with the lattice, whose small diamond shapes are filled with stewed cherries or currants, jam of choice, or with the mass used to brush the plates, and dusted with finely pounded sugar
11. If filled with chestnut purée, cover the diamond shapes with roasted chestnuts dusted with sugar and serve the cake warm or cold as desired

title: Butterteigspatzetti mit Brütel gefüllt (Butter Dough Pastry with Brütel Filling)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- marmalade or preserved fruit

## Directions

1. Prepare them like the small Vol au vent (Cachets) pastries (No. 1073), but fill them with desired marmalade or preserved fruit instead of forcemeat

title: Butterteigsterne (Butter Dough Stars)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deka flour
- eggs
- marmalade as desired
- sugar

## Directions

1. When you have prepared a butter dough from 28 Deka flour and rolled it out to knife-back thickness, cut finger-long and finger-wide strips with slanted ends
2. Take 6 such strips, of which the two lowest are the longest, the two middle ones somewhat shorter and the two uppermost the shortest, and layer them crosswise to form a star
3. Before placing one strip on another, brush the middle of each dough strip with beaten egg so that one holds the other
4. Press the top strip strongly in the middle with the finger, which creates a depression
5. Place these stars on a sheet dampened with water using a knife, make several incisions in each strip with a knife, brush the stars with beaten egg and bake them at fairly strong heat in the oven
6. When this is done, remove them from the sheet with a thin knife, fill the depression made with the finger in the middle with desired marmalade, sprinkle with finely ground sugar and serve
7. They can also be baked in hot lard

title: Buttetertig feinster Art zu Wildbrerett (Finest Puff Pastry for Game)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry (No. 1045) without sugar
- Beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Roll out the puff pastry (No. 1045) without sugar on a flour-dusted board 2.5 knife-back widths thick, and as desired cut out round, leaf-like, crescent-shaped, or square shapes.
2. Place them with a spoon in three-finger distance apart on a board moistened with water, brush with beaten eggs while keeping the edges knife-back-wide dry, and bake yellow at strong heat in the oven.
3. With a thin knife, loosen the puff pastry from the board and use it as garnish for game dishes.

title: Buttetertig-Straße (Puff Pastry Strip)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry (No. 1045)
- Beaten eggs

## Directions

1. After the puff pastry (No. 1045) has rested for 1/4 hour, roll it out on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a small finger and, as desired, cut it round, square, or oval, which should always match the shape of the designated dish.
2. Cut out the center of this pastry sheet so that only a 3-finger wide border remains.
3. Place the cut-out puff pastry on a board moistened with water and brush the pastry with beaten eggs in such a way that a knife-back-wide border remains.
4. The remaining scraps of puff pastry are also rolled out, cut or stamped as desired, and laid in any pattern on the puff pastry ring.
5. Brush these also with beaten eggs, but not the edges.
6. Place the puff pastry ring in the oven and bake it initially at strong heat, later at moderate heat.
7. So that the puff pastry does not become too dark, it can be covered with paper after it turns yellow.
8. The puff pastry is then removed from the oven, placed in the designated dish, and served with a ragout or served two rings stacked on top of each other, make a knife-back-thick cover from the same dough, bake it at the same time next to the 2 rings, arrange the ragout or fricassee in the center, place the baked cover on top, and serve a sauce in a sauce boat.

title: Béchamel
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound veal
- 1.5 pounds ham
- Yellow turnips
- Small onions
- 2 cloves
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- 0.5 pound butter
- 5 tablespoons flour
- 0.5 measure (Maß) cream
- Milk (if needed)
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut 1 pound veal and 1.5 pounds ham into small pieces.
2. Place in a casserole with some yellow turnips, small onions, 2 cloves, bay leaves, thyme, and 0.5 pound butter.
3. Let everything draw slowly on low coals, stirring often to prevent the meat from sticking to the bottom or darkening.
4. Stir 5 tablespoons flour into the casserole.
5. Once well mixed, pour in 0.5 measure of cream.
6. Stir with a cooking spoon until the sauce begins to boil.
7. Simmer gently for 1.5 hours until it reduces properly.
8. If it becomes too thick, add some milk.
9. Do not salt heavily as ham is already present.
10. The sauce should bind and look like porridge.
11. If still too thick when serving, add some cream or good milk, strain through cheesecloth, and use as needed.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Béchamel Soufflé (Soufflé à la Béchamel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 tablespoons butter
- 16 tablespoons sugar
- 16 tablespoons flour
- 1.5 liters hot milk
- 9 egg yolks
- 9 egg whites
- 8 tablespoons finely ground almonds or hazelnuts (optional)

## Directions

1. Place butter, sugar, and flour in a saucepan and finely mash with a spoon
2. Pour in 1.5 liters of hot milk and stir quickly over the fire until the dough separates from the pan
3. Transfer to a deep bowl and let cool completely
4. Stir in 9 egg yolks one after another
5. Lightly fold in the snow of 9 egg whites, and if desired, 8 tablespoons of finely ground almonds or hazelnuts
6. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour with this mixture
7. Cover and cook 3/4 to 1 hour in a water bath or bake slowly in the oven at low heat
8. If desired, add 5 tablespoons of strong black coffee with the milk

title: Böhm. Knödel (Bohemian Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-3 sliced rolls (Schneidssemmeln)
- 0.5 liter milk
- 25-30 decagrams flour
- 1 egg
- Salt
- Fat for roasting
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. In a basin, pour a small amount of milk (which has been whisked with 1 egg and salt) over the flour
2. Stir to a firm dough
3. Mix this dough with the rolls cut into cubes
4. Let it rest for a while
5. Using a slotted spoon, scoop out dumplings and place them in plenty of boiling salt water for 8 minutes
6. Lift them onto a round platter
7. Pour over them breadcrumbs roasted in fat

title: Böhmische Dalken (Bohemian Dalken)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 dkg melted butter
- 2 dl lukewarm milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1-2 spoons sugar
- salt
- 30 dkg warmed flour
- 1½ dkg yeast starter (Dampfl risen)

## Directions

1. Whip together 5 dkg melted butter with 2 dl lukewarm milk, 2 egg yolks, 1 whole egg, 1-2 spoons sugar and salt
2. Add to 30 dkg warmed flour in a tall pot
3. Mix in the risen yeast starter
4. Whip the dough well and set near the oven to rise
5. When doubled in height, stir it and proceed as with the cream dalken
6. If the dough is too loose, add more flour

title: Böhmische Erbsen (Pois secs à la bohémienne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter dried peas
- 2 liters cold water
- salt
- roasted onions
- bread crumbs
- boiled meat, bacon or similar

## Directions

1. Place 1 liter of dried peas in 2 liters of cold water and cook slowly for two hours so they become evenly tender
2. While cooking, carefully skim off any foam that rises to the surface
3. Drain the water and salt the peas
4. Place on a platter and pour roasted onions and equal amount of bread crumbs over them
5. Serve with boiled meat, bacon or similar
6. If desired, smoked pork (Kaiser-fleisch) can be cooked until tender with the peas

title: Böhmische Erbsen in neuer Art
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- dried peas
- salt water
- onions
- butter for roasting
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the peas as described in the Böhmische Erbsen recipe until tender and remove the skins
2. Pour into a shallow casserole dish so the peas are about 2 fingers deep
3. Spread 1 head of onion, cut lengthwise and roasted until golden, over the peas
4. Sprinkle heavily with bread crumbs
5. Bake uncovered in the oven for half an hour until a crust forms
6. Serve in the casserole or arrange on a platter so the crust is not destroyed
7. Can be served with meat, roasted poultry or pigeons as indicated above

title: Böhmische Erbsen mit Schweinfleiſch (Bohemian Peas with Pork)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- large dried peas
- a good piece of bacon
- salt
- fatty meat broth
- fresh bacon for serving
- breadcrumbs
- tender cooked pork from the breast

## Directions

1. Choose large dried peas and fill a pot only half full with them.
2. Add fresh water, a good piece of bacon, and salt.
3. Keep the peas well covered so the husks can be removed.
4. When they are boiled down, add fatty meat broth.
5. When they begin to soften, let them cook completely down.
6. Do not stir them with a spoon so they do not get crushed; instead, shake the pot frequently and finally make a strong fire around it so they dry well.
7. When serving, tip them onto a dish, take a piece of fresh bacon, cut it small, let it turn golden in a pan, sprinkle the peas with breadcrumbs, pour the hot bacon over them.
8. Make small thumb-sized pieces from tender cooked pork breast and arrange them on top of the peas after plating.
9. Butter may be used instead of bacon for rendering. Make holes in the peas here and there so the fat can penetrate better.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Böhmische Maultaschen (Bohemian Pasta Pockets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca butter
- 4 deca finely pounded sugar
- 1 pinch of salt
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
- 50 deca flour
- filling with stewed fruit and vanilla as desired

## Directions

1. Stir together 16 deca butter with 4 deca finely pounded sugar, 1 pinch of salt, and 5 egg yolks
2. Add 3 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk and 50 deca flour, mix well
3. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon
4. Follow the same treatment as for the previous Maultaschen, but fill the indentations generously with stewed fruit and vanilla

title: Bötzche Kücheln (Bötzche Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough from any yeast dough recipe
- Flour for dusting
- Lukewarm fat for coating fingers
- Hot fat for deep frying
- Sugar for garnish

## Directions

1. Take equal-sized pieces of dough with a spoon and place them on a flour-dusted board.
2. Flatten them lightly and evenly with floured hands and let them rise uncovered in a warm place until they increase in size.
3. Once risen, stretch the dough gently in the center with fingertips dipped in lukewarm fat, creating a hole about finger-thickness.
4. Hold each piece in the middle with three fingertips, allowing the edges of the dough to rest over your hand, and carefully place the dough in hot fat with fingertips directed downward.
5. Fry until golden on both sides.
6. Remove with a slotted spoon and place briefly on paper.
7. Arrange on a dish and dust with sugar before serving.

title: Büftek mit Erdäpfelpürce (Beef Steaks with Potato Purée)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- flour
- meat broth
- salt
- beef from lung roast
- 3 onions of medium size
- a strong handful of fresh parsley
- half a teaspoon of ground pepper
- fat

## Directions

1. Make a purée or pressed dish of potatoes as follows: cook the potatoes, peel them, and when they have cooled, grate them on a grater
2. In a casserole, warm a piece of butter, add one cooking spoon of flour, which should brown slightly but not become dark
3. Add the grated potatoes and pour good meat broth over it in proportion, stir it until it looks like a cook's work, salt it and let it cook well
4. From a lung roast, which must first be washed and beaten well, cut thin round slices, salt them, mix together 3 onions of medium size, a strong handful of finely chopped fresh parsley, and half a teaspoon of ground pepper
5. Put some spoonfuls of fat in a flat pan, place the meat slices with the cut ingredients in it, and let it stew until the slices are cooked through; the onion and parsley inside should only become light yellow
6. When serving, the potato purée, which is mixed with the strained fat from the slices, comes first on the dish, then the slices thickly coated with the stewed onions and parsley are laid in order on top, and brought to table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Büftek mit Erdäpfelpüree (Beef Steak with Potato Purée)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Butter
- Flour
- Good meat broth
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook and peel the potatoes.
2. Once they are cold, grate them on a grater.
3. In a casserole, warm a piece of butter.
4. Add one cooking spoon of flour, which should take on some color but not brown.
5. Add the grated potatoes and pour good meat broth over them in proportion.
6. Stir it well until it has the consistency of butter.
7. Salt it and let it cook well.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Cacao (Cocoa)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cacao beans and shells
- 1/2 liter water
- 3 spoonfuls cacao
- cold water
- milk and sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Roast cacao beans and shells, pound and mix them
2. Place 1/2 liter water on the fire, when it boils add 3 spoonfuls cacao and stir until it boils again
3. After 10 to 15 minutes pour in some cold water and let the cacao rest quietly to the side until it is clear
4. Serve with milk and sugar

title: Caeraaumett (Palate of Beef)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef palate
- salt
- water
- soup
- lemon sauce, béchamel sauce, butter sauce, or green parsley sauce
- Sauce Piquante with capers or anchovies
- boiled rice, Riz-à-Bisi, puff pastry cut out with cookie cutters, or similar garnish

## Directions

1. Cook the beef palate in strongly salted water until the fine skin can be loosened.
2. Cut it lengthwise in half, remove the spongy part filled with coagulated blood, and cut into 2 finger-widths wide oval pieces.
3. Cook the pieces completely tender in the soup.
4. Serve the hot palate pieces covered with any desired sauce: lemon sauce, béchamel sauce, butter sauce with finely cut green parsley, Sauce Piquante, or with piquant caper or anchovy sauce.
5. Garnish with boiled rice, Riz-à-Bisi, puff pastry cut out with cookie cutters, or similar.

title: Calf Brains in Shells (Coquilles de cervelle de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brain prepared as for brain with Périgueux sauce
- Shell dishes
- Breadcrumbs
- Parmesan cheese, grated, or fresh finely cut herbs

## Directions

1. Prepare the brain as described in recipe 546
2. Fill the shells heaping with the mixture
3. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs
4. Place shells on a baking sheet and bake for 3/4 hour
5. Remove and if desired, sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese or fresh finely cut herbs
6. Arrange on a platter lined with a napkin
7. Serve as a first course, intermediate course, or with tea

title: Calf Brains in Shells with Mayonnaise and Aspic (Coquilles de cervelles de veau à la mayonnaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brain, freed of skin, cooked in salted water and vinegar
- Shell dishes
- Mayonnaise
- Aspic, chopped

## Directions

1. After the brain is completely cooled, cut into pieces of choice
2. Fill the centers of the shells somewhat heaping with the pieces
3. Garnish with chopped aspic
4. Place shells on ice 1.5 hours before serving
5. Cover the brain with one teaspoon of mayonnaise

title: Calf Brains in Wafers (Cervelle de veau frite)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brain to be fried
- Wafers of double the size of the brain slices
- Water
- Beaten eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Very hot fat, about 2 fingers deep

## Directions

1. Dip wafers in water
2. Quickly fill the wafers with the brain
3. Dip in beaten eggs and coat with breadcrumbs (panate)
4. Fry in very hot fat

title: Calf Brains in Wine or Egg Batter (Beignets de cervelle de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brain cooked in salted water
- Wine or egg batter

## Directions

1. Once the cooked brain is completely cooled, cut into pieces of choice
2. Dip in wine or egg batter
3. Fry in very hot fat
4. Serve with fine vegetables or fresh parsley

title: Calf Brains with Mayonnaise (Mayonnaise de cervelle de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Root vegetables and onion, sliced thin
- Lemon zest
- Parsley sprigs
- Spices
- Water
- Vinegar
- Calf brain
- Mayonnaise
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Green salad

## Directions

1. Put thinly sliced root vegetables, onion, a little lemon zest, parsley sprigs and spices in a saucepan
2. Add water and vinegar and cook until soft
3. Strain through a sieve and cook the brain in the strained broth until completely shrunken
4. Set aside with the broth until ready to use
5. Using a serving spoon, remove the brain, place on cloth
6. Once the water has drained sufficiently, cut into pieces of choice
7. Arrange piled on a platter, cover with mayonnaise
8. Garnish with hard-boiled eggs and green salad

title: Calf Brains with Mayonnaise and Aspic (Cervelle de veau en mayonnaise et aspic)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brain, freed of skin, cooked in salted water and vinegar
- Mayonnaise
- Aspic, chopped

## Directions

1. After the brain is completely cooled, cut into pieces of choice
2. Pile on the edge of a platter, garnish with chopped aspic
3. Place on ice for a while
4. Before serving at table, cover the brain with several spoonfuls of desired mayonnaise
5. Serve mayonnaise additionally in a saucepan

title: Calf Kidneys (Rognons de coq)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf kidneys
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the calf kidneys in lukewarm water
2. Cook them once in a lemon juice, similar to the preparation of calf combs

title: Calf's Brain in Crayfish Butter (Kalbshirn in Krebsbutter)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brain
- Braise
- Bacon slices
- Good white sauce
- Crayfish butter
- Croutons
- Crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Wash the brains well and cook them in a good braise, covered with bacon slices.
2. Prepare a good white sauce and enrich it with crayfish butter, seasoning well.
3. Remove the brains from the braise and arrange them on a plate.
4. Garnish with croutons and crayfish tails.
5. Serve sauce over them.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Calf's Brain with Anchovy Butter and Caviar (Cervelle de veau au beurre d'anchois)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brain
- Anchovy butter
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Aspic
- Butter
- Caviar
- 8 olives
- 1 teaspoon capers
- Smoked salmon
- Mayonnaise or mustard (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the brain in salt water, then cut into pieces when completely cooled
2. Arrange the cut brain alternately around a dish with anchovy butter and hard-boiled eggs
3. Line the edge of the dish with chopped aspic
4. In the center place a basket formed from ordinary butter, which is filled with caviar
5. Cover the whole with 8 olives from which the pits have been removed
6. Sprinkle with about 1 teaspoon capers and add some pieces of smoked salmon cut into small pieces
7. Alternative arrangement: use brain, head lettuce, hard-boiled eggs and anchovy butter instead of the butter basket

title: Calf's Brain with Black Butter (Cervelle de veau au beurre noir)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brain
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare the brain as in the vinegar recipe
2. Arrange around a dish and pour black butter over it
3. To prepare the black butter: stir butter over low heat until the rising foam disappears and the butter is chocolate brown
4. Mix the browned butter with necessary salt, some pepper and a little vinegar
5. Bring to a boil once and strain through a fine sieve over the brain

title: Calf's Brain with Fricassee Sauce
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brain
- Fricassee sauce

## Directions

1. Let the brain soak in cold water for several minutes, then remove the skin
2. Place the brain in boiling salt water and cook until it completely contracts
3. Remove with a serving spoon and arrange on a dish with fricassee sauce

title: Calf's Brains with Eggs (Cervelle de veau aux oeufs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brain
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 2 whole eggs
- Salt
- Finely cut green parsley
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Place the peeled brain in 8 tablespoons of hot butter
2. When it has completely contracted, beat it lightly apart with a serving spoon
3. Mix in 2 whole eggs, some salt, finely cut green parsley and some pepper
4. Alternatively, instead of stirring the eggs with the brain, serve fried eggs on top of the roasted brain when plating

title: Calf's Foot Soup (Kuttelfleck Suppe)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cleaned calf's feet
- water
- black bread
- garlic
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place cleaned calf's feet in a casserole, cover with water about 3 fingers above the meat
2. Simmer until soft
3. Cut thinly sliced black bread and roast with some garlic in desired fat in a deep bowl
4. Place the soft-cooked calf's feet, which must be cut finely, with the bread and pour seasoned and peppered broth from the calf's feet over it
5. Instead of garlic, finely cut roasted onion can be used

title: Calf's Meat Prepared with the Appearance and Taste of Marinated Tuna (Art und Weise, das Kalbfleisch mit dem Aussehen und dem Geschmack eines marinierten Thunfisches zuzubereiten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- White fresh calf's leg
- One quarter pound very dry finely grated salt
- Onions
- Whole parsley
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Ginger
- Garlic
- Cranberries
- Pepper
- Three washed finely pounded anchovies
- Good white wine
- Fine oil

## Directions

1. Take a white fresh calf's leg and cut out the inner part (the fricandeaux) as large as possible.
2. Let it stand for a couple of days in a cool place to tenderize; in summer no longer than one day.
3. Remove the skin and sinews and divide the piece into four parts.
4. Have ready a quarter pound of very dry finely grated salt and rub the calf pieces thoroughly with it as in the preparation of salted beef.
5. Place it in a stone pot, add sliced onions, whole parsley, thyme, a bay leaf, ginger, garlic, cranberries, pepper, and three washed finely pounded anchovies.
6. Mix everything well together and bind with a cloth.
7. After four days, turn it over and let it stand bound for another four days.
8. Then pour off half the liquid and put the other half with the calf pieces and other ingredients into a casserole.
9. Add a bottle of good white wine and let it cook slowly over gentle heat.
10. When it is tender, set it aside, place it in the same terrine or pot it was in before, and put it in a cool place to marinate well.
11. The next day, place the meat on a hair sieve to drain, then into a clean terrine or glass, and pour in enough fine oil to cover it completely.
12. Finally bind it well with a bladder.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Calf's Mesentery in Italian Manner (Kalbsgekröse auf italienische Art)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's mesentery
- Butter
- Finely chopped mushrooms
- Flour
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Good bouillon
- Fine oil
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the mesentery well, wash it, and cook it in a good braise.
2. Remove it and cut it into filets.
3. Sauté onions in butter with finely chopped mushrooms over gentle heat for a while.
4. Dust with a little flour and add salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
5. Pour in good bouillon and a tablespoon of fine oil.
6. When it has cooked briefly, place the cut mesentery pieces in the sauce and let them simmer a little.
7. Press lemon juice into it and serve very hot.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Candied Melon Slices in Vinegar (Grüne Melonen-Spalten in Essig)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 melon
- 1/2 liter water
- 1/4 liter vinegar
- 60 Deca sugar
- 3 pieces cloves
- Cinnamon
- Nutmeg
- 1 small piece vanilla

## Directions

1. Remove skin and seeds from the melon, cut the remainder into arbitrary pieces
2. Cook the pieces in boiling lemon water and then dry on cloth
3. Meanwhile, prepare 1/2 liter water, 1/4 liter vinegar, 60 Deca sugar, 3 pieces cloves, some cinnamon, nutmeg, and 1 small piece of vanilla, and let it boil
4. Add the melon pieces and let them cook for 5-8 minutes
5. Let them cool covered in the liquid, then fill the melon pieces into jars
6. Pour the juice over them so they are covered, bind them and cook like the Muscatel pears (No. 1842) for 3/4 hours in steam

title: Candied Morello Cherries (Zuckergekochte Weichseln)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo Morello cherries
- 80 Deca sugar
- 1/2 liter water

## Directions

1. After removing the stems, cook 80 Deca sugar with 1/2 liter water until heavy-cooked
2. Pour lukewarm over the Morello cherries and let them stand in a warm place for several hours
3. Strain the juice and add 4 Deca sugar
4. Cook quickly so the beautiful color is not lost and add the Morello cherries
5. Let them cook a few minutes and then fill into jars as hot as possible
6. Place them unclosed in a breezy location
7. After several days bind them with writing paper, which you pierce with a needle
8. Treat excess juice as indicated for heavy-cooked quince slices

title: Candied Peaches, Melons, and Apricots (Eingekochte Pfirsiche, Melonen, Marinen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe peaches, melons, or apricots
- 80 Deca sugar
- 1/2 liter water

## Directions

1. Choose fairly ripe peaches, melons, or apricots and cut them, freed from pits and skin, into arbitrary pieces
2. Place them in a porcelain pot
3. Cook 80 Deca sugar with 1/2 liter water until heavy-cooked (No. 1859)
4. Pour lukewarm over the fruit and let it stand covered
5. On the second day strain and proceed further like the heavy-cooked quince slices (No. 1861)
6. The same applies for excess juice

title: Candied Quince Slices as Compote (Eingekochte Quitten-Spalten als Kompot)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Quince apples or pears
- Lemon juice
- Lemon water
- 80 Deca sugar
- Cold water
- Cochineal or elderberry juice (for red color, optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the quince apples or pears into arbitrary pieces and remove the seed cavities
2. Peel each piece and rub very strongly with lemon juice
3. Throw into cold, then boiling lemon water and cook until they feel soft
4. Quickly place into cold water and then onto a cloth to dry
5. Transfer to preservation jars and pour over enough cold, boiled sugar water (80 Deca sugar per liter of water) so that the cracks are covered
6. Bind the jars and cook them like the Muscatel pears (No. 1842) for 3/4 hours in steam
7. If you want the quinces to be red, add a few drops of cochineal or elderberry juice when filling the jars

title: Caramel Soufflé (Soufflé au caramel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 drams sugar
- cold water
- 12 drams flour
- 1/2 liter cold cream
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- 16 drams butter
- 16 drams sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Dip 12 drams sugar in water, place in a casserole, and heat without stirring until the sugar is brown.
2. Add 6 tablespoons cold water and simmer until the sugar is completely dissolved and liquid.
3. Set aside and let cool.
4. Pour 1/2 liter cold cream over 12 drams flour and stir.
5. Add 5 egg yolks, 3 whole eggs, 16 drams butter, and the cooled, dissolved caramel sugar.
6. Stir over heat until the mixture has thickened considerably.
7. Set aside and let cool.
8. Beat 16 drams sugar with 8 egg yolks for 1/4 hour.
9. Slowly mix this into the thickened, cold mixture.
10. Lightly fold in the snow from 8 egg whites.
11. Fill a buttered, flour-dusted melon mold.
12. Cover and steam or bake for 3/4 hour in a water bath.

title: Caramel-Creme (Caramel Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Loth sugar
- Peel of one lemon
- One Obertaſſe water
- 8 Obertaſſen water
- One Vierding sugar
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take 3 Loth sugar, the peel of one lemon, and one Obertaſſe water in a casserole
2. Let it reduce to caramel in the casserole until it becomes beautifully brown
3. Pour in 8 Obertaſſen of water and add one Vierding sugar
4. After bringing all to a good boil, let it cool
5. Stir 6 egg yolks well, pour in the cold mixture, and pass it a couple of times through a fine sieve
6. Fill into cups, place them in a casserole full of boiling water, and cover with a lid with coals on top
7. The cream will be ready in ten minutes and is served cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Cardinal (Cardinal)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- sparkling wine
- 1/4 liter pineapple juice

## Directions

1. Prepare sparkling wine, add 1/4 liter pineapple juice
2. Serve cold

title: Cardinal anderer Art (Cardinal Another Way)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram sugar in pieces
- thin peel of 1 orange (without white pith)
- juice of 3 oranges
- 1/4 liter Rhine wine
- 1/4 liter very cold champagne
- 1/8 liter juice of concentrated berries (optional)

## Directions

1. In a soup pot, add 1/4 kilogram sugar cut into pieces and the thin peel of 1 orange with no white pith
2. Add the juice of 3 oranges and 1/4 liter Rhine wine
3. Mix well, fill into bottles and set on ice
4. Shortly before serving, strain this liquid through a cloth and add 1/4 liter very cold champagne
5. The flavor can be enhanced with 1/8 liter juice of concentrated berries

title: Cardoons (Cardons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cardoon leaves
- water
- ham slices
- broth
- wine

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves from the stalk one by one, cut them to finger length
2. Cook with plenty of water and ham slices until the fine skin can be rubbed away
3. Drain and rub each piece lightly with the fingers so they appear fleshy
4. Cook them gently in some strained broth with wine, but only enough to cover the cardoons, covered
5. Remove from broth onto cloth, arrange nicely and cover with ham slices that were cooked in the broth

title: Cardoons with Butter Sauce (Cardons, sauce au beurre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cardoons
- water
- ham slices
- butter sauce

## Directions

1. Treat the cardoons exactly as the previous recipe, then pour butter sauce without ham over them

title: Carpe au bleu (Blue Boiled Carp in Its Scales)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- one carp of any desired size with flawless scales
- 2 liters water
- 1.5 liters vinegar
- 1 onion pierced with 1 clove
- 5 sprigs fresh parsley
- pepper
- 2 tablespoons salt
- 0.5 liters boiling vinegar
- green parsley for garnish
- Hollandaise sauce or beaten mustard sauce
- horseradish
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. To kill the fish, place it on a table, hold it firmly by the head fins with the left hand and strike a strong blow on the fish's head with the meat hammer in the right hand
2. Cut open the belly, make an incision lengthwise at the head and a deeper incision lengthwise at the tail
3. Clean and wash it internally and leave it unscaled
4. In a fish pan place 2 liters water, 1.5 liters vinegar, an onion pierced with 1 clove, 5 sprigs fresh parsley, some pepper and 2 tablespoons salt
5. Let this cook for approximately 1/4 hour
6. Place the carp on the insert of the fish pan such that the head and tail are straight and the belly is spread out, loosely bind the head and tail and place a suitable piece of wood or a soup spoon in the mouth so it stays open, with a piece protruding so it can be removed when serving
7. Set the carp in the fish pan on the stove and pour 0.5 liters boiling vinegar over it, then carefully add the strained broth along the edge so it does not flow directly onto the carp
8. Let it stand covered for approximately 1 hour, during which time the liquid must always be very hot but must not boil
9. Shortly before serving, remove the insert, untie the twine and carefully transfer the fish to a flat dish or a board lined with parchment paper
10. Garnish the carp with green parsley and parsley can also be placed in the fish's cavity instead of the wood which is removed
11. Serve with Hollandaise sauce or beaten mustard sauce, horseradish and boiled potatoes

title: Carpe à l'allemande (Carp in Sour Brown Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams butter or lard
- 1 piece finely cut onion
- very little finely sliced root vegetables
- 12 decagrams flour
- 0.25 liter vinegar
- 0.5 liter water
- 3 sprigs thyme
- 2 crushed juniper berries
- 1 bay leaf
- 5 grains each of spice
- 5 sprigs parsley tied together
- 1 small piece of lemon peel
- approximately 1 kilo fallen fish, cut into 2 finger wide pieces

## Directions

1. In 16 decagrams butter or lard place 1 piece finely cut onion and very little finely sliced root vegetables, let this braise covered somewhat, add 12 decagrams flour and let it brown
2. Then add 0.25 liter vinegar, 0.5 liter water (in total as much liquid as sauce is needed), 3 sprigs thyme, 2 crushed juniper berries, 1 bay leaf, 5 grains each of spice, 5 sprigs parsley tied together and 1 small piece of lemon peel
3. Let this cook for 0.5 hour
4. In a pot place approximately 1 kilo fish cut into 2 finger wide pieces

title: Carpe à la bohemienne (Bohemian Carp with Black Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 decagrams butter or lard
- 1 piece onion, finely sliced
- some root vegetables, finely sliced
- the chopped carp head
- 10 pieces blanched raisins
- 10 pieces raw dried prunes, pitted
- 16 decagrams finely sliced black bread
- 8 decagrams dark and 8 decagrams light raisins
- 5 sprigs thyme
- 5 sprigs fresh parsley
- 2 bay leaves
- 5 grains each of spice
- 1 spice nail
- 1 very small piece of cinnamon
- 4 decagrams sugar
- 1.5 liters beer
- 0.25 liter red wine
- 0.5 liter vinegar
- carp blood (if available)
- blanched almonds
- fish broth if needed
- one carp of approximately 2 kilos

## Directions

1. In a pot place 12 decagrams butter or lard, 1 piece onion, some root vegetables, all finely sliced, the chopped carp head, 10 pieces blanched raisins, 10 pieces raw dried pitted prunes, 16 decagrams finely sliced black bread, 8 decagrams dark and 8 decagrams light raisins, 5 sprigs thyme, 5 sprigs fresh parsley, 2 bay leaves, 5 grains each of spice, 1 spice nail, 1 very small piece of cinnamon and 4 decagrams sugar
2. Let this braise covered for 15 minutes, then pour it with 1.5 liters beer, 0.25 liter red wine and 0.5 liter vinegar and let it braise covered
3. If the carp blood is available, stir it with vinegar and add it to the sauce and stir
4. If the sauce is too thick, add some fish broth and pass it
5. As soon as this is done, add blanched raisins, currants, some raisins and peeled finely cut almonds and let it come to a boil once more
6. This sauce must be sweet-sour and bitter and have a pleasant spicy taste
7. If it is too thin, add some butter sauce; if it is not dark enough, stir in a few spoonfuls of caramel sugar
8. The fish of approximately 2 kilos is meanwhile cut and prepared in the same way as indicated for the 'Sour Boiled Carp with Root Vegetables'
9. Arrange it with some sauce on the dish and serve desired dumplings or semolina strudel with it
10. The remaining sauce is served in a sauce boat
11. Any kind of fish can be prepared in this way

title: Carpe à la marinière (Sour Boiled Carp with Root Vegetables and Dill)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter water
- 0.5 liter vinegar
- 1 onion, finely diced
- root vegetables, finely diced
- 1 bay leaf
- some thyme
- pepper
- salt as needed
- one carp
- dill, grated
- mustard or mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Cook a broth of 3/4 liter water and 0.5 liter vinegar with 1 finely diced onion and finely diced root vegetables, 1 bay leaf, some thyme, pepper and necessary salt
2. Let this cook for 1/4 hour
3. While this is cooking, scale the fish, open it, clean it, split it lengthwise and cut it into pieces 2 fingers wide, which are sprinkled with salt
4. Split the head lengthwise in 2 parts and add it to the softened root vegetables
5. After 1/4 hour add the fish pieces and let them come to a boil once, then set them aside covered in the pot
6. Shortly before serving, place the fish on the dish, which can be garnished with the cooked root vegetables and grated dill, and add some of the broth
7. Serve mustard or mustard sauce with it

title: Cauliflower Browned in Fat with Cheese (Abgeſchmälzener Carviol mit Käse)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Salt
- Water
- Butter
- Grated Parmesan or Swiss cheese

## Directions

1. Split the cauliflower into several pieces, peel off all the hard rind, cut the pieces lengthwise to be fairly equal, and throw them into cold water to remove the raw taste.
2. Simmer it in salted water until tender, arrange it in a dish brushed with butter, and press it down with a clean cloth so it becomes fairly level on top.
3. Sprinkle it with melted butter and spread it fairly thickly with grated Parmesan or Swiss cheese.
4. Bake it in a hot oven until the cheese turns a golden-yellow crust.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Celery Purée Soup (Purée de sellerie)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces butter
- 1 thinly sliced Spanish onion
- 3 thinly sliced mushrooms
- some finely chopped green parsley
- 2 cleaned celery heads, thinly sliced
- approximately 2 liters beef soup
- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- pepper
- salt
- roasted bread

## Directions

1. In 16 ounces butter, roast together 1 thinly sliced Spanish onion, 3 thinly sliced mushrooms and some finely chopped green parsley
2. Add 2 cleaned celery heads, thinly sliced to the roasted mixture
3. Pour in approximately 2 liters of beef soup and let it cook until soft
4. Pass through a fine sieve with 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
5. Add pepper and the necessary salt and bring to a boil
6. When serving, add roasted bread

title: Celery Salad (Zellersalat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 celery root
- cold water
- vinegar
- salt
- 2 cubes cube sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the celery
2. Place in a small pot with cold water, vinegar, salt, and 2 cubes of sugar
3. Cook for 30 minutes, drain
4. Dress with vinegar and oil
5. Use to garnish the potato salad

title: Cervelatwurst (Cervelat Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- lean meat from a pork shoulder
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- basil
- bacon, finely diced
- white beef intestines

## Directions

1. Cut the lean meat from a pork shoulder
2. Remove all sinews
3. Chop it first, then pound it finely
4. Add salt, pepper, spices, and basil
5. Finely dice the bacon and work it well into the meat
6. Stuff it firmly into white beef intestines
7. Cure it properly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chai (Tea)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- black tea
- sugar (to taste)
- rum (to taste)

## Directions

1. Make strong black tea and add sugar and rum to taste

title: Champagne Auflauf (Champagne Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 1/2 liter Champagne wine
- 8 teaspoons sugar
- 4 teaspoons beaten butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- butter for the pan
- finely crushed sugar for the pan
- baked biscuit

## Directions

1. In a glazed pot, add 5 egg yolks, which are whisked on the hot plate with 1/2 liter Champagne wine mixed with 8 teaspoons of sugar until it begins to thicken and rises in height, then set aside and whisk until completely cooled
2. To this, beat 4 teaspoons of beaten butter with 6 egg yolks and finally lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
3. A desired butter-greased, finely crushed sugar-dusted form is lined with baked biscuit, half of the chaudeau is poured in, which is again covered with biscuit and the second half of the chaudeau is poured over
4. Let it cook covered for 1/2 hour in a water bath, after which time the soufflé is turned out onto a plate and baked in the oven for a few minutes; dust it with finely crushed sugar and serve immediately
5. This soufflé must be made very carefully

title: Champagner Punch (Champagne Punch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 80 deciliters sugar in small pieces
- 1 liter coffee
- juice of 5 lemons
- 1/2 liter rum or arrack
- 1 1/2 liters champagne

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, add sugar and coffee and let it boil
2. Add the juice of 5 lemons, 1/2 liter rum or arrack, and 1 1/2 liters champagne
3. Let it heat, strain the punch through a sieve into a soup pot or bowl
4. Serve it hot

title: Champagner-Punsch (Champagne Punch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 80 drachms sugar in small pieces
- 1 liter water
- juice of 5 lemons
- 1/4 liter rum or brandy
- 1/2 liter Champagne

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, place 80 drachms sugar in small pieces and 1 liter water
2. Let this boil, then add the juice of 5 lemons, 1/4 liter rum or brandy and 1/2 liter Champagne
3. Let it become hot, strain the punch through a sieve into a soup pot or tureen and serve it hot

title: Champagner-Sulz (Champagne Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 decagrams sugar, clarified
- 7 decagrams isinglass, hot and dissolved
- 1/4 liter Champagne, instead of wine, orange and lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Orange Sulz (see recipe 1660)
2. Use Champagne instead of wine, orange and lemon juice
3. Do not add orange peel to the sugar

title: Champignon-Sauce mit Wein (Mushroom Sauce with Wine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 strong tablespoon green parsley
- 8 pieces washed mushrooms
- Butter
- Some white soup
- Light butter sauce
- Some white wine
- Juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Cut 1 strong tablespoon green parsley and 8 pieces washed mushrooms finely and braise in butter
2. Pour with some white soup, simmer and set aside
3. Add the braised mushrooms to a light butter sauce with some white wine and the juice of one lemon
4. Let it boil once more and pass through a sieve
5. This mushroom sauce must be of light color and pleasant acidity

title: Champignon-Suppe (Mushroom Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cleaned, washed and chopped mushrooms
- fresh water
- butter
- finely minced onions and parsley
- pepper and salt
- some mace
- strong stock or pea soup
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Place the fresh, cleaned, washed and chopped mushrooms temporarily in fresh water so they stay white
2. Let finely minced onions and parsley simmer somewhat with butter in a casserole over charcoal fire
3. Add pepper, salt and some mace, fill with strong stock or pea soup
4. Let the mushrooms cook well in it
5. Before serving, thicken the mushrooms and add toasted bread slices to the soup
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Champignon-Suppe (Mushroom Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms, cleaned, washed and cut into pieces
- butter
- finely chopped onions
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- strong meat broth or pea soup
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Lay the cleaned, washed, and cut mushrooms in fresh water so they remain white
2. In a casserole on a charcoal fire, briefly stew finely chopped onions and parsley in butter
3. Add pepper, salt, and some nutmeg flower
4. Fill with strong meat broth or pea soup and let the mushrooms cook thoroughly
5. Before serving, thicken the mushrooms and add toasted bread slices to the soup
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Champignonfülle (Mushroom Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms
- 4 dkg hot butter
- chopped parsley
- some soup or water
- salt
- lemon juice
- several spoonfuls cream

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the mushrooms, chop them finely.
2. In hot butter, let chopped parsley come to a boil, add the mushrooms.
3. Pour in some soup or water, add salt, lemon juice, and several spoonfuls of cream.
4. Let simmer for 8-10 minutes. There should not be much liquid.
5. Spread on the Fridatten, roll them, place in a buttered dish.
6. Pour milk mixed with 1 egg yolk over them.
7. Place in the oven for 15-20 minutes to bake.
8. Serve in the dish.

title: Champignons (Pickled Mushrooms)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms, both large and small
- Strongly salted water
- Sharp vinegar
- Mace
- Allspice
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Bay leaves
- Melted mutton fat

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms, both large and small, separate them from their stems, then split them
2. Place them in boiling, strongly salted water and let them come to a boil once or twice
3. Let them drain in a colander, and also press them out
4. Make sharp vinegar hot with mace, allspice, cloves, pepper and bay leaves
5. Throw the mushrooms in and let them come to a boil
6. After everything has cooled as quickly as possible, pour it into glass jars
7. After three days, pour the purest melted mutton fat over it so that it covers the whole about several lines thick
8. Bind the glass bottles tightly with strong paper and place them in cool locations
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Champignons einzumachen (Preserved Mushrooms)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- salt
- water
- wine vinegar
- mace
- cloves
- pepper
- bay leaves
- mutton fat

## Directions

1. When the mushrooms are cleaned and the stems cut off, cut the large ones into pieces; the smaller ones remain whole and are washed in cold water.
2. Let water with some salt come to a boil, place the mushrooms in it so they cook a little, then remove them again and drain the water in a colander.
3. While they cool, bring wine vinegar to a boil with some whole mace, cloves, pepper, and bay leaves; when the vinegar boils, add the mushrooms and let them cook only briefly.
4. Place them with the vinegar and spices in preserving jars.
5. On the third day, pour melted fresh mutton fat over them and place the jars in a cool location.
6. When you wish to use some, remove the fat from the top.
7. Alternatively, after the mushrooms are cooked and drained, you may mix them with the spices, placing them in a preserving jar in layers: first a layer of fresh green bay leaves, some crushed pepper, whole mace, then a layer of mushrooms, then again green bay leaves and so forth; let the wine vinegar come to a boil and cool again, then pour it over them and cover them with mutton fat.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Champignons gefüllt (Stuffed Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8–10 mushrooms
- 3 bread rolls
- 5 dkg hot butter
- parsley, finely chopped
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- ½ egg, beaten

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the mushrooms.
2. Cut out the stems and hollow out the heads.
3. Finely chop the stems and the scooped-out flesh.
4. Stew in hot butter with finely chopped parsley for a few minutes.
5. Mix with breadcrumbs, salt, and beaten egg.
6. Fill the mushroom heads with this mixture.
7. Place in a buttered omelet pan, add a little soup, and place in the oven for 10–12 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, cut thick bread slices and fry them brown in hot lard.
9. Place on a platter, set one mushroom on each crouton, and garnish with parsley.

title: Champignons mit Brot (Mushrooms with Bread)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- several pieces of mushrooms
- water
- fresh butter
- salt
- white pepper
- coulis
- bouillon
- small bread rolls
- thick sweet cream
- eggs
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Clean and wash several pieces of mushrooms
2. Cook them in water for a few minutes
3. Lay them in cloth to dry
4. Pass them in fresh butter with salt, white pepper
5. Add some coulis and bouillon
6. When they are cooked, rub the rind from small bread rolls
7. Hollow them out inside
8. Pour some thick sweet cream into a ragout
9. Fill the rolls with it
10. Serve very hot. If you want the ragout with mushrooms to be white, bind them with eggs, add some nutmeg, and stir in sweet cream
11. Then you can use them as entremet or ragouts as needed. If you have no small mushrooms, take large ones and cut them into fine pieces, which must be washed in water a couple of times
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Champignons-Sauce (Mushroom Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms, finely sliced
- Passed butter sauce

## Directions

1. Add cleaned, finely sliced mushrooms to the passed butter sauce
2. Braise and serve in a sauce boat or pass through a sieve beforehand if desired

title: Champignonsfilets (Mushroom Fillets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cutlets à 25 dkg
- 5-6 mushrooms
- 1 onion
- parsley
- broth or water
- salt
- pepper
- fat for searing

## Directions

1. Prepare the cutlets the same way as the Rhineland fillets and quickly sear on both sides
2. In the remaining fat, roast 1 finely chopped onion and parsley, add the meat, pour in broth or water and braise covered under frequent basting for 2 hours
3. 1 hour before serving, add the cleaned, washed mushrooms, finely chopped or cut into strips, and braise them together
4. Dust the sauce lightly and serve unpassed with the meat

title: Champignonsoße (Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5–6 mushrooms
- salt
- water or broth
- 4 dkg butter
- parsley roux
- lemon juice
- 4–5 spoonfuls cream

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the mushrooms, slice them into leaves, cook them 20–30 minutes in salted water or broth.
2. Make a parsley roux with 4 dkg butter and pour mushroom water over it.
3. Add the cooked mushrooms, season with lemon juice and salt.
4. Add 4–5 spoonfuls of cream and cook for 20 minutes.

title: Chapon Braisé à la Sauce de Gibier (Braised Capon with Game Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 braised capon with game marinade
- bacon slices
- finely sliced black bread

## Directions

1. The capon, prepared as in the previous recipe with game marinade, is placed in a casserole with the marinade, the capon is covered with bacon slices and braised with 9 decagrams of finely sliced black bread until tender

title: Chapon Farci (Stuffed Capon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 decagrams veal meat
- 14 decagrams bacon
- 3 whole eggs
- 6 decagrams crusts removed, softened in milk, well pressed rolls
- 4 tablespoons meat cream
- 4 cleaned, washed, finely diced anchovies
- 12 crayfish tails or 6 shelled oysters
- 8 decagrams finely sliced truffles
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Chop the veal meat and bacon very finely with a sharp knife
2. Place in a mortar and pound with 3 whole eggs, 6 decagrams of crusts removed, softened in milk and well pressed rolls, and 4 tablespoons of meat cream until fine
3. Pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
4. Add 4 cleaned, washed, finely diced anchovies, 12 crayfish tails or 6 shelled oysters and 8 decagrams of finely sliced truffles
5. Salt and season to taste and mix well together
6. Truss the capon as for chickens, salt it, fill it with the above forcemeat, truss it, bind it with bacon and roast it on a spit or in the oven
7. When finished roasting, cut it into pieces of choice, reassemble it on a platter and garnish it with watercress and with lemon and orange wedges
8. Serve the capon with any salad desired and the resulting juices

title: Chapon Rôti (Roasted Capon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 capon
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the roasted capon in the same manner as roasted pheasant
2. The difference is that it must be salted 1 hour before roasting

title: Chapon Rôti aux Anchois (Roasted Capon with Anchovies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned capon
- salt
- cleaned, finely cut anchovies
- butter
- watercress
- lemon wedges

## Directions

1. Salt the cleaned capon 1 hour before roasting
2. Rub the capon with cleaned, finely cut anchovies
3. Truss it and roast it on a spit for 1 and 1/2 hours, basting frequently with butter
4. After this time, remove the capon, cut it into pieces of choice, arrange these in a platter
5. Pour the capon's own, defatted juices over it and garnish the meat with watercress and lemon wedges
6. Alternatively, the trussed capon can be placed in a pan containing butter and roasted in the oven with frequent basting with the resulting juices
7. The capon may be larded before roasting with finely cut bacon and anchovies if desired

title: Chapon à la Sauce de Gibier (Capon with Game Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned, trimmed, beaten, trussed capon
- game marinade
- butter
- capers
- cleaned, finely diced anchovies
- cream
- meat glaze
- watercress
- lemon wedges
- simulated snipe liver

## Directions

1. A cleaned, trimmed, beaten, trussed capon is larded, placed in a suitable vessel, covered with game marinade and, if time permits, left to soak for 1 day in the marinade
2. 8 hours is also sufficient
3. After this time, remove the capon and roast it by frequent basting with butter for 1 to 1 and 1/2 hours on a spit
4. Or place the capon in a pan containing butter and roast it under frequent basting with the resulting juices in the oven
5. If desired, a butter sauce may be made with some marinade and simmered well
6. After this, add some cream, and once this has thinned, add 1 tablespoon of capers and cleaned, finely diced anchovies
7. Let the sauce simmer once and serve it in a sauceboat with the capon
8. The capon, which has been cut into pieces of choice and reassembled on a platter, is garnished with simulated snipe liver, lemon wedges and watercress

title: Chartreuse de Pommes (Apple Chartreuse)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- About 12 beautiful apples
- Three quarters pound sugar
- Lemon juice
- Butter
- Apple marmalade
- Apricot marmalade
- Various preserved fruits: apricots, currants, etc.

## Directions

1. Peel about 12 beautiful apples, give them form with a pastry cutter, and lay them immediately in water with lemon juice so they remain white.
2. Clarify three quarters pound sugar and mix with lemon juice.
3. Lay the cut-out apples, which must always be in an oblong round form, in the clarified sugar, and bring them to a boil a couple of times.
4. Remove them and lay them on a hair sieve.
5. Cook the apple scraps remaining from the cutting into a marmalade and let them cool.
6. Butter a casserole, layer the oblong-cut apples symmetrically in a circle around the bottom.
7. Spread apple marmalade thickly on top, fill the middle of the casserole with apricot marmalade, then continue with apple marmalade until the casserole is full.
8. Place it for a moment in boiling water and turn it out onto a platter.
9. Cook a good syrup from the remaining juice and pour it over.
10. Garnish these apples with various preserved fruits, apricots, currants, etc.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chateaubriands (Double Beefsteak)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 skinned tenderloin
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Hot fat
- 1 piece raw or cooked truffle, cut into leaves
- 4 spoonfuls Madeira wine
- Demi-glace or meat stock gravy

## Directions

1. From a skinned tenderloin, cut 4 finger-thick slices.
2. Flatten these with the flat of a knife, press lightly together so that they have the proper height and rounding.
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, lightly dip both sides in flour.
4. Place in a pan containing hot fat and fry 5-7 minutes on each side, about 1/4 hour in total.
5. For each Chateaubriand, place 1 piece of raw or cooked truffle cut into leaves in a casserole, pour 4 spoonfuls of Madeira wine into it and let it boil.
6. Add some demi-glace or gravy made from 2 tablespoons of meat stock and pour this with the truffles over the Chateaubriands arranged on a platter.

title: Chaudeau
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l white wine
- 16-20 pieces of cube sugar
- 4-6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. In a chaudeau pot, let the cube sugar dissolve in ½ l white wine.
2. Add 4-6 egg yolks and whip on a hot stove plate until it rises and becomes thick.
3. Alternatively, whip everything in a snow kettle over steam until thick and foamy.

title: Chaudeau (Wine Custard Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter white or red wine
- 12 egg yolks
- 24 deca sugar

## Directions

1. 1/4 hour before serving, pour 1/2 liter white or red wine into a snow kettle
2. Beat in 12 egg yolks
3. Add 24 deca sugar
4. Whip over heat with a whisk until the mass rises and becomes thick, but do not boil
5. Use immediately
6. For red chaudeau, stir in a few drops of almond juice shortly before serving
7. Alternatively, use a tall, narrow pot over heat where the mass bubbles

title: Chaudeau Frozen Dessert (Glace au chaudeau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter wine
- 12 egg yolks
- 24 deca grams granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare a chaudeau from 1/2 liter wine, 12 egg yolks, and 24 deca grams granulated sugar
2. Place it in cold water and whisk or beat it until it becomes thick
3. Fill the freezing container with it and proceed as with the Vanilla Cream Frozen Dessert (Recipe 1720)

title: Cherry Compote (Compot von Kirſchen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sour cherries
- 0.5 pounds sugar
- water
- cold water

## Directions

1. Cut the stems of the cherries halfway off.
2. Pour cold water over them.
3. Dissolve the sugar on charcoal fire with a little water, then cook it down almost to syrup.
4. The cherries must first be drained well of water, then throw them into the syrup.
5. Bring to a strong boil several times.
6. Skim off the foam with a spoon.
7. Allow to cool and serve in a compote dish.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Cherry Ice (Kirſchen-Gefrornes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds sour cherries
- 1 pound sugar
- 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Wash 6 pounds of sour cherries with stems removed
2. Pound the cherries with their pits together
3. Pass through a fine sieve
4. Clarify 1 pound of sugar
5. Mix the strained cherries and the juice of 4 lemons with the clarified sugar
6. Work it very carefully as it freezes with difficulty because the cherries have more watery parts than other fruit
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chestnut Croquettes (Kastanien-Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 roasted chestnuts
- 25 roasted chestnuts (to be processed)
- 12 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons vanilla sugar
- 1 pinch salt
- some cream
- 6 egg yolks
- flour for coating
- eggs for dipping
- breadcrumbs for coating
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Shell 50 roasted chestnuts, pound and pass 25 of them through a sieve
2. Add 12 tablespoons butter, 8 tablespoons vanilla sugar, 1 pinch salt, and some cream to the passed chestnuts
3. Stir over heat until it becomes a dough
4. Set aside and after it has cooled somewhat, gradually stir in 6 egg yolks
5. After completely cooling, place the mass on a flour-dusted board and divide into portions
6. Arrange them evenly separated, place a halved chestnut in the middle of each, and cover evenly with the dough
7. Dip in beaten egg, roll in breadcrumbs, and fry in very hot lard
8. Remove with a slotted spoon when light golden, place briefly on blotting paper, and arrange in a bowl covered with a napkin

title: Chestnut Pudding (Pouding aux marons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 decagrams chestnuts
- 6 decagrams butter
- 3 decagrams flour
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- 8 decagrams finely pounded sugar
- 6 decagrams blanched raisins
- 4 decagrams crushed almond paste
- 12 decagrams finely diced marrow
- 10 pieces lengthwise cut dates
- 5 dissolved nuts
- 4 tablespoons rum
- 4 tablespoons cream
- butter for the mold
- flour for the mold

## Directions

1. Roast chestnuts, peel, divide in half
2. Pound 1 half with 6 decagrams butter, pass through a sieve
3. Mix with 3 decagrams flour, 3 egg yolks, 3 whole eggs, 8 decagrams finely pounded sugar, 6 decagrams blanched raisins, 4 decagrams crushed almond paste, 12 decagrams finely diced marrow, 10 lengthwise cut dates (pits removed), 5 dissolved nuts, 4 tablespoons rum, and 4 tablespoons cream
4. Mix well
5. Cut the remaining chestnut half into pieces and fold in
6. Fill a buttered mold dusted with flour
7. Bake covered in steam for 1 hour

title: Chestnut Purée (Purée de marrons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked chestnuts
- melted butter
- cream
- sugar
- nutmeg (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel any desired quantity of cooked chestnuts
2. Pass through a sieve
3. Place in melted butter
4. Add cream
5. Stir in some sugar and, if desired, a little nutmeg
6. Simmer briefly
7. Keep the chestnut purée thick enough that it can be served heaped

title: Chicken in Pastry Batter Fried (Ponlets en pâte frite)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Wine or egg batter
- Fat for frying

## Directions

1. Prepare as indicated for 'Chickens braised in their own juices' (No. 822). After they are cooked tender, set them aside in their liquid and after they have cooled completely, remove them, dry them, cut them into pieces as desired, dip them in wine or egg batter and fry them in hot fat.
2. Remove them with a skimming ladle and treat them as the preceding ones. They can also be served after the soup.

title: Chicken Purée Soup (Purée de volaille)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- bouquet of green parsley
- 3 pieces of mushrooms, thinly sliced
- 1 whole chicken
- 1/2 liter white soup
- 2 liters soup
- 8 ounces soft peas
- 4 hard egg yolks
- salt

## Directions

1. Place butter, a small bouquet of green parsley and 3 pieces of thinly sliced mushrooms in a casserole
2. Add 1 whole chicken and roast it
3. After roasting, add 1/2 liter of white soup and let it braise gently
4. Remove the chicken, skin it, and remove the bones
5. Pound the bones into the casserole where the chicken was braised
6. Add 2 liters of soup and boil well
7. To the removed chicken meat, add 8 ounces of soft peas and pound this together very finely in a mortar
8. Place the pounded mixture in a casserole, pour the chicken soup over it, stir in the necessary salt and let it boil well
9. Pass it through with 4 hard egg yolks, place the pot in hot water so the soup does not boil, but stir it frequently with a serving spoon
10. The soup is usually served with braised rice or fried bread
11. You can also add chicken breast cut into pieces and peas boiled in salt water

title: Chicken Ragout Soup with Bread Slices (Hühnerragouttsuppe mit Semmelschnitten)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ head cauliflower
- 2 mushrooms
- 1 bread roll
- 2-3 chickens
- chicken liver
- salt
- soup greens (optional)
- 8 dkg butter
- parsley
- bread slices or bread dumplings

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the young chicken, and place it in a pot with cold salted water and optional soup greens, except for the liver.
2. Cook for 1-1½ hours depending on the age of the chicken.
3. Add the liver later in the cooking process.
4. Remove the chicken and cut it into pieces.
5. Make a light roux with 8 dkg butter and parsley, and pour it into the strained chicken broth.
6. Add the chicken pieces, cauliflower, and mushrooms (which have been cleaned, washed, and separately cooked in salted water for 20 minutes).
7. Cook everything together well.
8. Serve the soup with bread slices or bread dumplings.

title: Chicken with Cream, Crayfish, and Capers (Poulets Braises à la Crème)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cleaned chickens
- fat
- 1 head onion, thinly sliced
- 1 bay leaf
- 3 sprigs thyme
- ¼ liter dry or light wine
- ¾ liter cream
- potatoes or turnips
- 2 tablespoons capers or 1½ teaspoons chopped green parsley or chervil

## Directions

1. Cut each of 2 cleaned chickens into 4 pieces with the shoulder joint cut through the back
2. Place in a casserole containing fat, thinly sliced onion, bay leaf, and thyme
3. Cover and braise
4. When the liquid that forms has evaporated, pour in ¼ liter wine
5. Let some of this evaporate and pour ¾ liter cream over
6. Cover and braise briefly
7. When chickens are half-tender, add finely diced potatoes and braise together
8. Remove chickens, arrange in a serving dish, garnish with potatoes, and pour sauce over
9. Optionally stir in 2 tablespoons capers or 1½ teaspoons finely chopped green parsley or chervil and bring to a boil once
10. Instead of potatoes, diced turnips or macaroni cooked in salted water can be used to garnish

title: Chicken with Green Sauce (Poulets Braises Sauce Verte)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- sauce ingredients from chicken with lemon sauce recipe
- 1 tablespoon spinach puree
- juice of 1½ lemons

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Chicken with Lemon Sauce
2. In the strained butter sauce, stir in 1 tablespoon spinach puree and juice of 1½ lemons

title: Chicken with Lemon Sauce (Poulets Braises Sauce Piquante)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens
- 8 decagrams butter
- 8 decagrams flour
- ½ liter soup stock
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped green parsley
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare chickens as in the recipe for 'Chickens in Their Own Juice'
2. Roast equal amounts of butter and flour
3. Add ½ liter soup stock and cook through
4. Strain this sauce
5. Stir in 1 tablespoon finely chopped green parsley and juice of 1 lemon

title: Chicken with Rice (Poulets Braises au Riz)
