author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fat
- onion, thinly sliced
- root vegetables
- mushrooms
- whole spices
- 2 cleaned and dressed chickens
- butter
- paper
- 1.5 liters of soup stock

## Directions

1. Line a casserole with fat, thinly sliced onion, root vegetables, mushrooms, and whole spices
2. Place 2 cleaned and dressed chickens on top
3. Cover with buttered paper and braise
4. After about 15 minutes, remove the paper
5. Pour 1.5 liters of soup stock over
6. Cover and braise until chickens are tender
7. Remove chickens, cut into pieces, and arrange in the center of a serving dish
8. Mix some of the strained sauce with cooked rice and use to garnish the chickens
9. Serve remaining sauce in a gravy boat

title: Chicken with Tarragon and Wine (Poulets Braises à l'Estragon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fat
- onion
- root vegetables
- whole spices
- 2 cleaned, plucked, and dressed chickens
- wine
- soup stock
- bacon strips or buttered paper
- tarragon leaves
- 3 egg yolks
- cold wine

## Directions

1. Place fat, onion, root vegetables, and whole spices in a casserole and roast briefly
2. Add 2 cleaned, plucked, and dressed chickens
3. Pour wine and soup stock over
4. Cover with bacon strips or buttered paper
5. Braise slowly until tender
6. Remove chickens, cut each into 4 pieces, but reassemble them
7. Arrange in a serving dish, scatter finely chopped tarragon leaves over, and pour the strained sauce over
8. Optionally, whisk 3 egg yolks with a little cold wine and blend well with the strained sauce, pour some over the chickens and serve the rest in a gravy boat

title: Chocolade-Schnitte (Chocolate Slices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deca butter
- 16 Deca sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 16 Deca almonds, blanched, chopped and sieved
- 16 Deca grated chocolate
- 6 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 12 Deca flour
- transparent chocolate glaze

## Directions

1. Place 16 Deca butter in a deep bowl and stir very little
2. Mix in gradually 16 Deca sugar, 6 egg yolks, 16 Deca blanched chopped and sieved almonds, and 16 Deca grated chocolate
3. Lightly fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites from 6 eggs with 12 Deca flour
4. Spread on a butter-brushed baking sheet knife-back width thick and proceed as with the Genua-Schnitte
5. Cover heavily with transparent chocolate glaze
6. This dough must not be stirred long like the Genua-Schnitte dough, or it will collapse when baking

title: Chocolade-Suppe (Chocolate Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- chocolate, 4 small tablets
- milk, 3 seitels
- sugar
- butter
- egg yolks, 3
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Grate 4 small tablets of chocolate into 3 seitels of milk
2. Cook with a little sugar and a small piece of butter, stirring vigorously
3. Pour the soup over 3 egg yolks in a pot
4. Serve over toasted bread slices cut into small pieces
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chocolat (Hot Chocolate)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 tea cups (about 4 tablets) chocolate
- 1.5 liters milk

## Directions

1. Place 20 tea cups chocolate in one saucepan and 1.5 liters milk in a second saucepan, place both on the stove
2. When the chocolate has become soft, pour it with some boiling milk and stir until it is completely smooth and shiny
3. Gradually add the remaining boiling milk while stirring constantly, let the chocolate boil well, bubble up and boil once more, then serve
4. For water chocolate: prepare the same way, using water instead of milk
5. For egg butter chocolate: for 1/2 liter use about 3 egg butters, which you sprinkle with some cold milk and then strain into the chocolate
6. If desired, add 8 tea cups sugar at the beginning and serve with powdered sugar and whipped cream

title: Chocolat-Strudel (Chocolate Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- cold chocolate cream

## Directions

1. Prepare it like the Almond Strudel (No. 1090) but use cold chocolate cream instead of the almond filling

title: Chocolate Cake (Schokoladekuchen)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dkg butter
- 12 dkg sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 12 dkg softened or grated chocolate
- 4 egg whites
- 12 dkg flour

## Directions

1. Cream together the butter with sugar and egg yolks gradually
2. Mix in the softened or grated chocolate
3. Fold in the flour together with the beaten egg whites
4. Spread the mixture finger-thick on a buttered, floured baking sheet
5. Bake at a low heat
6. While still warm, cut into rectangles
7. Let cool, then sandwich pairs together with jam or sweetened whipped cream
8. Dust with sugar or decorate with whipped cream

title: Chocolate Drops (Trauben von Chocolate)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 22 deca sugar
- 1 table spoon grated chocolate
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, chocolate, and lemon juice well in a deep bowl
2. Lightly fold in the snow from 1 egg white
3. With a coffee spoon, take equally large pieces and loosen with the handle of a second spoon
4. Place in small heaps at finger-width distance on a baking sheet lined with wafers
5. Sprinkle with finely cut almonds and finely pounded sugar
6. Dry at low heat for approximately 2 hours in the oven
7. Remove from the oven once completely dry

title: Chocolate Pudding (Schokoladepudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dkg butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 7 dkg sugar
- 12 dkg grated chocolate
- 3 egg whites
- 7 dkg raw grated almonds
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Beat 7 dkg butter until light, then add 4 egg yolks and 7 dkg sugar gradually.
2. Mix in 12 dkg grated chocolate.
3. Fold in 3 egg whites together with 7 dkg raw grated almonds.
4. Fill the mixture into a pudding mold and cook in steam for 1 hour.
5. Loosen, cool, turn out onto a platter, and decorate with sweetened whipped cream before serving.
6. If serving warm without whipped cream, keep in steam until serving, then loosen and immediately turn out onto a porcelain platter.

title: Chocolate Torte (Tourte au Chocolat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca chocolate
- 14 deca butter, beaten
- 16 deca sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 8 deca almonds, finely ground with skin and passed
- 3 egg whites
- 8 deca black flour
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan
- chocolate or other glaze

## Directions

1. Place 14 deca chocolate on a baking sheet and soften in the oven
2. Mix smoothly with 14 deca beaten butter
3. Add 16 deca sugar and stir with 3 egg yolks for 1/4 hour
4. Add 8 deca finely ground and passed almonds and stir for another 1/4 hour
5. Fold in the snow (stiffly beaten egg whites) from 3 egg whites together with 8 deca black flour
6. Fill a cake pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour to a height of 2 fingers
7. Bake for 3/4 hour at low heat
8. Turn out onto a sieve after this time
9. Once completely cooled, cover with warm or cold chocolate or other glaze

title: Chocolate Torte with Almonds (Tourte au Chocolat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca powdered sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- 16 deca almonds, finely ground with skin and passed
- 28 deca grated chocolate
- 12 egg whites
- 12 deca black flour
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan
- glaze

## Directions

1. Stir 28 deca powdered sugar with 16 egg yolks for 1/4 hour
2. Add 16 deca finely ground and passed almonds and 28 deca grated chocolate
3. Gently fold in the snow (stiffly beaten egg whites) from 12 egg whites with 12 deca black flour
4. Fill a cake pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
5. Bake for 1 hour at low heat
6. Remove the ring and once completely cooled, cover with glaze of choice

title: Chocolate Torte with Bread (Tourte au Chocolat avec du Pain)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca vanilla sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- 22 deca grated chocolate
- 16 deca grated house bread
- 8 egg whites
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan
- glaze

## Directions

1. Stir 28 deca vanilla sugar with 16 egg yolks for 1/3 hour
2. Add 22 deca grated chocolate and 16 deca grated house bread, mix well
3. Gently fold in the snow (stiffly beaten egg whites) from 8 egg whites
4. Fill a cake pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
5. Bake for 1 hour at low heat
6. Once completely cooled, cover with glaze of choice

title: Chocolates Biscuit
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ounces butter
- 15 ounces sugar
- 4 tablets chocolate
- 8 ounces blanched and ground almonds
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- 6 ounces flour
- blanched finely sliced almonds for topping

## Directions

1. Place 12 ounces butter, 15 ounces sugar, and 4 tablets chocolate in a pan and stir over heat until soft and smooth
2. Add 8 ounces blanched and ground almonds and mix well
3. Empty this mass into a deep bowl and stir until cold
4. Gradually add 4 egg yolks and gently fold in the stiffly beaten snow from 4 egg whites with 6 ounces flour
5. Spread this mass finger-thick on a buttered sheet and brush with beaten eggs
6. Sprinkle with blanched, finely sliced almonds and bake in moderate heat for approximately 3/4 hour
7. Immediately afterwards, pipe decoratively into desired forms or cut into 2-finger wide and finger-long pieces and serve cold

title: Chocolates Biscuit
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 ounces whole sugar
- 1/2 liter water
- 6 egg whites
- 25 ounces chocolate
- 4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Pour 1/2 liter water onto 50 ounces whole sugar and cook until it reaches the thread stage
2. Beat stiff snow from 6 egg whites and pour the cooked sugar into it, stirring until cold, but the mass should not become too cold
3. Soften 25 ounces chocolate in the oven, place in a bowl, and beat fluffy with 4 egg yolks
4. Mix this with the prepared mass and pipe onto a buttered sheet as desired as biscotti, wreaths, etc.
5. Place the sheet on a board, put in a full oven, and leave in until a crust forms
6. Now bake the biscotti with the board in the hot oven until completely dry, then carefully remove with a thin knife while still warm
7. A board must be placed under the sheet or the biscotti will bake too dark and crack as a result

title: Chocolates-Bufferln (Chocolate Kisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 26 deca blanched almonds
- 1 egg white
- 20 deca vanilla sugar
- 2 tablets grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Pound 26 deca blanched almonds very finely with 1 egg white
2. Place in a deep bowl with 20 deca vanilla sugar and 2 tablets grated chocolate
3. Mix well together
4. Lightly fold in the whipped whites of 2 eggs
5. Prepare as directed for the Nuss-Böusserln (recipe 1627)

title: Chocolates-Konditorei (Stefanie silotlicct)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca chocolate
- 12 deca sugar
- 2 egg whites
- 3 deca sugar
- 1 teaspoon fine pounded and sifted almonds, pistachios or pignoli
- Optional: hot marmalade

## Directions

1. Prepare a dough from 24 deca chocolate and 12 deca sugar as directed for Chocolates-Kugeln
2. Spread the warm dough on a sugar-sprinkled paper and roll out knife-back thickness with a sugar-dusted rolling pin
3. Have previously prepared stiffly whipped whites of 2 eggs mixed with 3 deca sugar and 1 teaspoon fine pounded and sifted almonds, pistachios or pignoli
4. Spread this mixture knife-back thickness on the rolled chocolate dough
5. Fold the dough once so the edges lie evenly together on all sides
6. Spread the folded dough again knife-back thickness with the egg white mixture
7. Cover this by folding the dough once more as indicated
8. Cut the dough into thumb-width strips, then cut these into finger-width squares
9. Dry in air
10. Work as quickly as possible as the chocolate mass becomes stiff when cooled
11. If this happens, return the chocolate mass to the double boiler and warm through with a few drops of water
12. Alternatively, hot marmalade may be used instead of the egg white mixture
13. The chocolate dough may also be filled into paper cases half full, topped with a little marmalade, then filled to the top with warm chocolate mass and dried in air

title: Chocolates-Kugeln (Chocolate Balls)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Small pieces of pineapple, citron, nuts, cherries, pignoli, almonds or similar fruits
- 24 deca chocolate
- 24 deca fine sifted sugar
- 1 egg white
- Colored pearl sugar

## Directions

1. Have ready small pieces of pineapple, citron, nuts, pitted cherries, pignoli, almonds or similar fruits
2. In a double boiler, melt 24 deca chocolate
3. Quickly stir in 24 deca fine sifted sugar to form a dough
4. While the dough is still warm, form hazelnut-sized balls, placing a piece of the prepared fruit in the center of each
5. Place on a sugar-sprinkled paper
6. Dip the balls in stiffly beaten egg white
7. Roll in colored pearl sugar
8. Dry in air and store
9. If the chocolate mass becomes too stiff, add a few drops of warm water

title: Chocolates-Strudel (Chocolate Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Strudel dough
- 16 deca chocolate
- 12 deca beaten butter
- 16 deca finely ground vanilla sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 deca bread crumbs, softened in milk and well pressed out
- 12 deca finely ground almonds
- 6 egg whites
- canary milk or cream for serving

## Directions

1. Treat this strudel exactly as the Ham Strudel (No. 1229), with the difference that it is filled with the following chocolate mass instead of ham
2. Place 16 deca chocolate on a baking sheet in the oven and leave until the chocolate is soft enough to be pressed in with a finger
3. Beat 12 deca butter and mix smooth with the warmed chocolate
4. Mix with 16 deca finely ground vanilla sugar
5. Stir in 8 egg yolks gradually
6. Mix well with 8 deca bread crumbs softened in milk and well pressed out, and 12 deca finely ground almonds, one after another
7. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
8. Cut the strudel into desired pieces
9. Arrange in a bowl and serve with canary milk or cream

title: Chokolade (Hot Chocolate)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 loth chocolate per cup
- water or milk

## Directions

1. Allow 2 loth chocolate per chocolate cup, cut or grated on a grater
2. Place in a pot and let come to a boil
3. Remove from the fire and let stand a little
4. Place cups in hot water
5. Stir the chocolate with a stirrer or whisk into foam
6. Use a small foam spoon to fill the cups with foam until they are completely heaped full
7. Optionally, lightly bake the foam with a glowing shovel held half an inch above the cups to prevent it from settling too quickly
8. Serve mostly with rusks or finely toasted roll
9. Milk can be used instead of water with the same procedure
10. If making chocolate in Rhenish wine or Champagne, ensure the Champagne does not burn, do not spare the sugar, and allow for 1 egg yolk per cup to whip the chocolate with
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chokolade-Confect (Chocolate Confection)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound marzipan dough
- 4 loth chocolate
- hot water
- cinnamon or vanilla

## Directions

1. Take 4 loth chocolate for 1 pound marzipan dough.
2. Grind the chocolate with a little hot water over the fire into a paste.
3. Pour this paste over the marzipan dough.
4. Add cinnamon or vanilla.
5. Work the mass well together and spread it on cut wafer sheets.
6. This confection also requires only moderate oven heat.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chokolade-Creme (Chocolate Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound fine chocolate
- One Seidel boiling milk
- 5 chicken stomachs
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Stir half a pound fine chocolate in one Seidel boiling milk until it is only lukewarm
2. Finely pound 5 chicken stomachs and add them
3. Sweeten the mixture sufficiently
4. Strain it through a hair sieve, set on warm ash, cover with a lid, and place some coals on top
5. Place the cream in the cellar to set
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chokolade-Liqueur (Chocolate Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of cacao beans
- 1 loth of cinnamon
- 1 quenchen of vanilla (optional)
- 2 measures of strong brandy
- 1 half pound of roasted cacao beans
- 1 half loth of cinnamon
- 1 half quenchen of vanilla
- 1 half quenchen of cloves
- 2 pounds of sugar
- 2 pounds of water

## Directions

1. Pound 1 pound of cacao beans and 1 loth of cinnamon (and 1 quenchen of vanilla if desired)
2. Pour over with 2 measures of strong brandy and let stand for 4 days, stirring meanwhile
3. Distill 1 measure from it
4. Take 1 half pound of roasted cacao beans and the same amount of cinnamon and vanilla as before, plus 1 half quenchen of cloves
5. Bring it with 2 measures to the remaining liquid
6. Let stand for 8 to 10 days in gentle heat
7. Filter it and mix with the distilled spirits
8. Dissolve 2 pounds of sugar in 2 pounds of water and add to the mixture
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chokolade-Liqueur (Chocolate Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cacao beans
- 1 loth cinnamon
- 1 quentchen vanilla (optional)
- 2 measures strong brandy
- half pound roasted cacao beans
- cinnamon (same amount as before)
- vanilla (same amount as before)
- quarter quentchen cloves
- 2 measures brandy (for second infusion)
- 2 pounds sugar
- 2 measures water

## Directions

1. Crush 1 pound cacao beans and 1 loth cinnamon, and if desired, add 1 quentchen vanilla
2. Pour 2 measures strong brandy over the mixture
3. Let it stand for several days, shaking it meanwhile
4. Distill off 1 measure through distillation
5. Take half pound roasted cacao beans and the same amount of cinnamon and vanilla as before, plus quarter quentchen cloves
6. Combine with 2 measures brandy and add to the remaining distilled spirit
7. Let it stand for 8 to 10 days in gentle warmth
8. Filter it and mix with the distilled spirit
9. Dissolve 2 pounds sugar in 2 measures water and add to the mixture
10. After the mixture has stood for several weeks, filter again
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chokolade-Maccaronen (Chocolate Macaroons)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet macaroon dough
- Half a pound chocolate
- About 2 spoonfuls of almond dough
- A spoonful of finely pounded cinnamon or a teaspoon of vanilla, or both as desired

## Directions

1. Prepare the sweet macaroon dough according to instructions
2. Place half a pound chocolate on a baking sheet and soften it over charcoal fire
3. Pour it onto a plate and add about 2 spoonfuls of the almond dough
4. Stir the mixture well together
5. Bring it into the mortar with the remaining dough mass
6. Add a spoonful of very finely pounded cinnamon or a teaspoon of vanilla, or both as desired
7. Work everything thoroughly through
8. Take careful precautions when baking
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chokolade-Viscuit (Chocolate Biscuit)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve egg yolks
- Half a pound sugar
- 6 Loth finely sieved chocolate
- 12 Loth fine flour
- Whites of 12 eggs
- Sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Stir twelve egg yolks, half a pound sugar, and 6 Loth finely sieved chocolate for a quarter hour as usual
2. Then gradually add 12 Loth fine flour, beat the whites of 12 eggs to snow, and fold in slowly
3. Finally fill this mixture into capsules, sprinkle them with sugar, and bake slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chopped Sweet Cabbage with Mutton Cutlets (Gehacktes Süßkraut mit Schöpsen-Coteletten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage heads
- Salted water
- Onions
- Flour
- Butter
- Caraway
- Broth
- Wine vinegar
- Mutton cutlets

## Directions

1. Let the cabbage heads cut in halves cook tender in salted water, then chop them finely, let finely cut onions and some flour brown in butter, add the cabbage with a little caraway, let it steam so, pour some broth and wine vinegar to it, let it boil up in it, and garnish it when serving with the mentioned cutlets.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Chopped Veal Fillets (Filets de veau hachés)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Eggs
- Bread
- Cooked veal
- Parsley
- Lemon peel
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Stir into 6 decagrams of softened butter 2 whole eggs, 8 decagrams of bread softened in water and well pressed, 10 decagrams of finely cut, roasted or braised meat leftovers, some roasted green parsley and, if desired, a little finely cut lemon peel.
2. When this mixture is well stirred together with the necessary salt and seasoning, fillets of any desired size are formed.

title: Chou farci (Stuffed Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 small cabbages
- Veal forcemeat
- Twine
- 8 ounces butter or lard
- Finely cut green parsley
- 1/2 liter soup
- Salt and spices

## Directions

1. Hollow out 10 small cabbages like sauerkraut cabbages
2. Fill with veal forcemeat
3. Fold the leaves over the filling and wrap with twine
4. Roast finely cut green parsley in 8 ounces butter or lard
5. Add the cabbage
6. Pour 1/2 liter soup over it, salt and spice it, and braise covered for about 3/4 hour
7. Loosen the twine
8. Cut the cabbages into 4 pieces
9. Thin the cabbage broth with some cream and serve with the cabbage

title: Chou-braisé (Braised Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large cabbages cut into quarters, or 10 small whole cabbages
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 12 ounces hot fat
- 8 ounces sugar
- 8 ounces finely cut bacon
- 1 onion, finely cut
- 1/2 liter soup
- 1/4 liter butter sauce
- Salt and spices

## Directions

1. Boil the cabbage in salt water, remove with a skimming spoon, and place in cold water
2. Heat 12 ounces fat, add 8 ounces sugar and let it brown
3. Add 8 ounces finely cut bacon and 1 finely cut onion
4. When this has roasted somewhat, pour 1/2 liter soup over it
5. Add the well-pressed cabbage and braise covered
6. Pour 1/4 liter butter sauce over it, spice it, and let it cook a bit longer
7. Remove the fat carefully and serve the cabbage with its broth on a platter, garnished as desired

title: Choux (Garnished Sauerkraut)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauerkraut
- Cloth

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as green cabbage (No. 329), except omit the butter sauce
2. Place the cabbage 2 fingers high on a cloth
3. Fold the cloth over it and press it with the flat hand, moving up and down repeatedly, until it has the appearance of a thumb-thick slice of wax
4. Carefully unfold the cloth
5. Cut the cabbage into finger-length pieces and use to garnish the meat
6. Some soft-cooked yellow turnips can be cut the same way and placed between the cabbage

title: Choux d'automne (Autumn Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large or 2 small heads of cabbage
- approximately 1/4 liter vinegar
- 1 liter water
- 1 teaspoon caraway seeds
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 12 decagrams butter
- 1 finely cut onion
- 10 decagrams flour
- white beef broth
- cabbage broth
- 1/4 liter cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage finely into noodle-like strips.
2. Cook with approximately 1/4 liter vinegar, 1 liter water, 1 teaspoon caraway seeds and the same amount of salt until soft.
3. Meanwhile, make a light golden roux from 12 decagrams butter, 1 finely cut onion and 10 decagrams flour.
4. Pour the roux with a ladle of white beef broth and some of the cabbage broth.
5. Add the strained cabbage to the roux and stir well together.
6. If desired, add 1/4 liter cream.
7. If too thick, thin with the cabbage broth.

title: Choux Liés (Braised Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large or 8 small heads of cabbage
- salt
- water
- 16 deciliters hot fat
- finely chopped onion
- a small piece of crushed garlic
- 16 deciliters flour
- 1.25 liters broth
- pepper
- 1/3 teaspoon marjoram

## Directions

1. Cut the stems from the cabbage heads, remove the leaves, and split them along the core, then place in cold water
2. Wash the leaves well and place in boiling salted water, letting them cook until soft
3. Heat 16 deciliters of fat, add finely chopped onion and if desired a small piece of crushed garlic, letting this brown slightly
4. Add 16 deciliters of flour and let it brown again with frequent stirring
5. Pour in 1.25 liters of broth while stirring constantly and let it thicken
6. Add the drained cabbage, broken up with a wooden spoon, along with pepper and 1/3 teaspoon of marjoram
7. Stir everything together well and let it cook
8. If the cabbage is too thick, thin it with some broth

title: Choux liés (Stewed Cabbage Leaves)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large or 8 small cabbages
- Cold water
- Salt water
- 16 ounces hot fat
- Finely cut onion
- Garlic, if desired
- 16 ounces flour
- Salt and spices

## Directions

1. Cut off the stems of 4 large or 8 small cabbages
2. Remove the leaves and cut them apart along the main vein and place in cold water
3. After washing well, place the leaves in boiling salt water and cook them soft
4. Add some finely cut onion and, if desired, a small piece of crushed garlic to 16 ounces hot fat and let roast somewhat
5. Add 16 ounces flour and continue cooking under

title: Choux Pastry (Éclair-Bäckerei)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca butter
- 24 deca sugar
- 24 deca flour
- 1/4 liter boiling water
- 12 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Place butter, sugar, and flour in saucepan
2. Pour boiling water over and stir constantly
3. Boil until dough releases from spoon
4. Set aside and let cool completely
5. Gradually stir in egg yolks one by one
6. Place dough on flour-dusted board
7. Form into desired shapes such as breads, rods, rings
8. Place on butter-lined baking sheet
9. Brush with water
10. Sprinkle with hail sugar
11. Bake at low heat in oven until pale golden

title: Choux rouges (Red Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 red cabbage, cored and ribs removed
- vinegar
- salt
- 12 decagrams fat
- 1 head of onion, finely cut
- 6 decagrams sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon caraway seeds
- 1/2 liter red wine
- 1/2 liter broth
- approximately 1/2 liter butter sauce

## Directions

1. After coring and removing the ribs from the cabbage, roll it together and cut finely into noodle-like strips.
2. Pour some vinegar over it, mix well with salt and let stand 1/2 hour.
3. In 12 decagrams fat, roast 1 finely cut head of onion.
4. Add the cabbage to the onion with 6 decagrams sugar and 1/2 teaspoon caraway seeds.
5. Cover and braise 15 minutes, then stir well.
6. Add 1/2 liter red wine and 1/2 liter broth and continue braising covered until the cabbage is completely soft.
7. Add approximately 1/2 liter butter sauce, stir well and let boil thoroughly.
8. If the cabbage is not sour enough, add the necessary vinegar and salt and let boil again.
9. Serve with smoked meat and sausages.

title: Choux rouges braisés (Braised Red Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 decagrams hot fat
- 6 decagrams crushed sugar
- 1 head of cabbage, finely cut
- 1 onion
- broth
- vinegar
- salt
- 3 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. In 12 decagrams hot fat, let 6 decagrams crushed sugar brown.
2. Let 1 finely cut head of onion roast lightly, then add the cabbage as described above and let braise together somewhat.
3. Add enough broth and vinegar so that it covers the cabbage.
4. When the liquid has completely evaporated and the cabbage is rather soft, dust with 3 tablespoons flour, stir well and let evaporate somewhat more.
5. Pour with approximately 1/2 liter broth, stir well and let boil.
6. Add the necessary salt and, if not sour enough, some vinegar.
7. The cabbage should be pleasantly sweet-sour.
8. You can also add butter sauce instead of the flour dust. The cabbage must be agreeably sweet-sour.

title: Choux à la bourgeoise (Green Braised Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large green cabbages cut into quarters, or 10 small whole cabbages
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 8 ounces butter or lard
- Finely cut green parsley
- 1 liter soup
- Butter sauce
- Salt and spices

## Directions

1. Boil the cabbage in salt water without covering so it does not lose its color
2. Put in cold water
3. Roast finely cut green parsley in 8 ounces butter or lard
4. Pour 1 liter soup over it
5. Add the well-pressed cabbage
6. Pour butter sauce over it, salt and spice it, and let it cook a bit longer

title: Choux-fleurs au gratin (Cauliflower with Cheese Crust)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cauliflower
- Butter
- Roux
- Cream
- Egg yolks
- Cold milk
- Parmesan cheese
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the cauliflower by cooking it soft in salt water as in the previous recipe
2. Make a light roux, thin it with cream, let it reduce somewhat on the fire, and stir in egg yolks mixed with a little cold milk
3. Butter a porcelain dish and place it on a trivet
4. Arrange the cauliflower heaped on the dish, pour the prepared sauce over it, sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese, pour melted butter over it spoonful by spoonful, and bake in the oven until golden

title: Choux-fleurs farcis (Stuffed Cauliflower)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cauliflower
- Butter
- Veal forcemeat
- Breadcrumbs and butter, or butter sauce, or any desired piquant sauce

## Directions

1. After peeling and soft-cooking the cauliflower in salt water, divide it into small rosettes and place on a sieve
2. Butter a charlotte mold well and place the dried cauliflower rosettes so they lie on the bottom and side walls of the mold
3. Fill the empty space in the center of the mold with veal forcemeat
4. Cover the mold and cook in steam for 1 hour
5. Turn the mold out onto a platter, pour roasted breadcrumbs in butter, or butter sauce, or any desired piquant sauce over the cauliflower

title: Choux-fleurs à la sauce béchamel (Cauliflower with Béchamel Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cauliflower
- Butter
- Flour
- White soup
- Cauliflower water

## Directions

1. Cut off the stems of the cauliflower and peel the fine skin
2. Place the cauliflower in cold water for 1/4 hour, then place in boiling salt water and cook until soft
3. Make a roux with butter and flour, pour in some white soup and cauliflower water, and bring to a boil
4. Carefully remove the cauliflower from the salt water, place it heaped on a platter, pour the butter sauce over it, and serve without further additions
5. The sauce can also be made with milk instead of soup

title: Choux-fleurs à la Villeroy (Breaded Cauliflower)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower, soft-cooked in salt water
- Egg or bread batter
- Hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Divide the soft-cooked cauliflower into small rosettes
2. Dip them in egg or bread batter
3. Fry them in very hot lard until golden
4. Place on blotting paper and serve hot on a platter arranged in a heap

title: Cimmelhnus Auflauf (Cinnamon Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk
- 20 teaspoons ground cinnamon
- 12 teaspoons butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 12 teaspoons vanilla sugar
- 1/2 hour beating time
- 6 egg whites
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan

## Directions

1. In 1/2 liter of boiling milk, whisk in 20 teaspoons of ground cinnamon with constant stirring and let it cook briefly
2. Add 12 teaspoons of butter, stir well and set aside
3. Meanwhile, beat 6 egg yolks and 12 teaspoons of vanilla sugar for 1/2 hour, add the cooled mixture, stir well together
4. Finally, lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
5. Fill half of this mass into a butter-greased, flour-dusted pan, place in the oven, let it set slightly, then cover with desired cooked filling, add the second half of the mass and bake covered for 3/4 hour in a water bath or in the oven

title: Citron Pastry (Zitronenpastry)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough (prepared as for almond tarts, Recipe 1611)
- Eggs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Roll out dough to the thickness of a knife back
2. Cut out round discs using a cutter
3. Place half the discs on a baking sheet lined with butter at finger-width distance apart
4. Brush with beaten eggs
5. From remaining dough rounds, cut out the center with a small cutter to create a ring
6. Place rings on the egg-brushed discs so the edges meet evenly
7. Brush the rings with beaten eggs
8. Coat with hail sugar
9. Bake at low heat in the oven until golden

title: Citron Tisane (Lemon Tisane for Fever and Weakness)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- two whole juicy lemons
- one Seidel of barley water
- four Loth of mulberry juice prepared with sugar
- sixteen Loth of Rhine wine
- four Loth of toasted bread

## Directions

1. Take two whole juicy lemons and cut them into pieces
2. Separate and discard the white spongy pith that sits between the flesh and the outermost yellow peel
3. Discard the pith and seeds
4. Crush the flesh
5. Pour one Seidel of barley water over it
6. Add the cut yellow peels
7. Add four Loth of mulberry juice prepared with sugar
8. Add sixteen Loth of Rhine wine
9. Add four Loth of toasted bread
10. Store and keep for drinking
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Citron Veal (Mou de veau au citron)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 calf thymus glands or 1 half calf thymus gland
- Salt water
- 16 dessert spoons butter
- Equal amount of flour
- Finely chopped parsley
- Onion (optional)
- 1/2 liter soup stock
- 1/4 liter vinegar
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1/4 liter sour cream (optional)
- 1 pinch lemon peel
- Juice of 1/2 lemon
- The prepared veal thymus gland

## Directions

1. Boil the calf thymus glands in salt water until soft.
2. Remove from water and cut into almond-like pieces once cooled.
3. In a pot, toast the flour in butter, add finely chopped parsley and onion if desired, and brown.
4. Deglaze with 1/2 liter soup stock and 1/4 liter vinegar.
5. Stir well together, salt and pepper to taste.
6. Add 1/4 liter sour cream if desired and 1 pinch of lemon peel.
7. Add the juice of 1/2 lemon with the prepared veal thymus gland pieces.
8. Let cook together well.
9. The sauce should be pleasantly sour and not too thick.
10. Optionally, a small head of cauliflower can be braised with it, but removed before serving.
11. The veal thymus gland can also be served with fried eggs if desired.

title: Citronen-Confect (Lemon Confection)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- marzipan dough
- egg white
- grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. Prepare like the cinnamon confection, but note that when dissolving the marzipan dough, use the white of the egg, not the yolk.
2. Instead of cinnamon, season this confection with grated lemon peel.
3. Cut various shapes from wafer sheets, spread the mass on them, and bake in moderate heat.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Clarified Sugar (Zucker zu läutern)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1 seidel water

## Directions

1. Bring 1 pound sugar to boil with a seidel of water
2. Skim well
3. Continue cooking until it stretches like a thread when you insert a spoon or wood into it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Clear Fish Broth (Consommé maigre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- About 1.5 kilos well-browned fish
- Some pieces of dark bread
- Some marrow roasted in fat
- Parsley and celery
- 2 liters pea water or water

## Directions

1. Pound the browned fish, dark bread, roasted marrow, parsley, and celery finely together
2. Pour in 2 liters pea water or water
3. Simmer well and let brown
4. Pass through a sieve
5. Simmer a bit more
6. Garnish with boiled bread slices

title: Cock's Combs (Crêtes de Coq)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken combs
- water
- butter
- lemon juice
- salt

## Directions

1. Take chicken combs that have been trimmed slightly at the tips and place them in lukewarm water so they become white
2. Once this is achieved, place them again in lukewarm water, remove them and lay them on a cloth
3. Salt them well and rub with the cloth until the fine skin comes off
4. Place them in cold water and keep them at a warm place until the water becomes lukewarm
5. Repeat this until the combs are completely white
6. Place 1 3/4 liters of water, 4 ounces of butter and the juice of 1 lemon in a casserole, add the chicken combs and bring to a boil only once
7. Use them for various ragouts

title: Cock's Combs (Hahnenkämme)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cock's combs
- warm water
- bouillon
- bacon slice
- lemon slice
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- fricassee or Sauce Espagnole
- white bread croutons

## Directions

1. Clean the cock's combs in lukewarm water to make them white
2. Remove the skin
3. Lay them in warm water for a while
4. Cook them in bouillon with a bacon slice, a lemon slice, salt, pepper, and a piece of butter the size of a walnut that has been rolled in flour
5. When they are soft, serve these cock's combs in fricassee or Sauce Espagnole
6. Garnish with croutons of white bread fried in butter cut in the style of cock's combs
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Cock's Kidneys (Rognons de Coq)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken kidneys
- lukewarm water
- lemon broth

## Directions

1. After cleaning the chicken kidneys in lukewarm water, prepare them like chicken combs by bringing them to a boil once in a lemon broth

title: Cod (Cabliau)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- fresh water
- salt
- water
- milk
- lemon slices
- onions
- bay leaves
- potatoes
- clear butter or hollandaise sauce

## Directions

1. Scale the fish
2. Gut it
3. Soak it in fresh water for some time
4. Tie it with twine
5. Cook it slowly with the same ingredients as the turbot
6. Serve with boiled and peeled potatoes and melted clear butter or hollandaise sauce
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Coffee Cream Cake (Kaffee streute Torte)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 8 deca blanched almonds
- 3 egg yolks
- 14 deca sifted sugar

## Directions

1. To the stiffly beaten snow of 4 egg whites, add 8 deca blanched almonds finely ground with 3 egg yolks, along with 14 deca sifted sugar and mix everything well together
2. Cover a baking sheet with white paper and divide

title: Coffee Ice (Kaffeh-Gefrornes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cream
- egg yolks
- clarified sugar
- 6 loth roasted good coffee
- 3 cups water

## Directions

1. Take the same amount of cream, egg yolk and clarified sugar as for vanilla ice and proceed as above
2. Pour 6 loth roasted good coffee with only 3 cups of water and filter
3. Add the coffee to the above mixture
4. Stir until cooled
5. Pour into the freezing container to process
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Coffee Pudding (Pouding au cafe)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams trimmed, finely sliced bread rolls
- 0.25 liter strong black coffee
- 16 decagrams whipped butter
- 16 decagrams finely pounded sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 14 decagrams almonds with skins, finely pounded and passed
- 6 egg whites
- butter for the mold
- flour for the mold
- cream or whipped cream

## Directions

1. Soften 16 decagrams trimmed, finely sliced bread rolls in 0.25 liter strong black coffee
2. Pass through a sieve
3. Beat 16 decagrams whipped butter with 16 decagrams finely pounded sugar and 6 egg yolks gradually
4. Add 14 decagrams almonds with skins, finely pounded and passed, with the coffee bread
5. Mix well
6. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
7. Fill a buttered mold dusted with flour
8. Bake covered in steam for 0.75 hour
9. Turn into a dish and serve with whipped cream or cream

title: Coffee Soufflé (Soufflé au café)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 drams flour
- 1/2 liter cold coffee cream
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- 24 drams sugar
- 16 drams butter
- 10 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Place 16 drams flour in a casserole and pour with 1/2 liter cold coffee cream.
2. Add 3 egg yolks, 3 whole eggs, and 24 drams sugar.
3. Stir over heat until the mixture thickens considerably.
4. Add 16 drams butter and stir in.
5. Set the thickened mixture aside.
6. After cooling completely, mix in 10 egg yolks one after another.
7. Lightly fold in the snow from 8 egg whites.
8. Fill a buttered mold, dusted with flour.
9. Bake for 3/4 hour at low heat in the oven.

title: Cold Chestnut Purée (Purée de marrons froids)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked chestnuts
- sugar
- cold cream

## Directions

1. Pass any desired quantity of cooked chestnuts through a sieve
2. Stir with some sugar and cold cream so that it forms a thick mass
3. Serve heaped

title: Cold Fricasseed Pike (Ein fricassirter kalter Hecht)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- butter
- lemon juice
- salt
- egg yolks
- fresh water

## Directions

1. Scale, gut and clean the pike thoroughly
2. Cut pieces of desired size
3. Place these pieces in a reine or casserole
4. Add a piece of butter and the juice of a lemon
5. Salt it, cover, and let it cook until tender
6. When the fish is cooked, stir together six or eight egg yolks with a few drops of fresh water and the juice of a lemon
7. Add this to the pike and stir a couple of times until it becomes quite thick
8. Arrange the pike with the sauce on a dish and let it cool before serving at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Cold Tenderloin Roast with Aspic (Filet de boeuf à la gelée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- A beautiful tenderloin roast
- Glass of Bordeaux wine or red wine
- Aspic
- Herb mayonnaise
- Green parsley or watercress

## Directions

1. Prepare and lard a beautiful tenderloin roast the same way as for braising.
2. Braise it in the same manner, with the difference that you add a glass of Bordeaux wine or red wine when braising.
3. Once the meat is completely tender, let it rest in the braising liquid and set in a cool place.
4. Once the fat begins to solidify, remove the roast from the pan, place it on a flat dish, cover it with a strip of white paper dipped in the braising liquid, place a long board on top, and weight it down so the tenderloin remains in a beautiful flat form.
5. Trim it neatly and arrange it in desired pieces decoratively on a dish.
6. Garnish the dish with diced aspic, spread herb mayonnaise on top of the meat, and decorate the dish neatly with green parsley or watercress.
7. Serve the remaining mayonnaise in a separate sauce boat.

title: Colinen (Fassolen) Sauce (White Bean Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ liters beans (fassolen)
- cold water
- 2 tablespoons oil
- salt
- 1 pinch pepper
- vinegar
- 1 teaspoon sugar (if desired)

## Directions

1. Place the beans with cold water on the fire and let them cook until soft
2. Strain and pass them through a sieve
3. Stir in 2 tablespoons oil, some salt, 1 pinch of pepper, vinegar to taste and if desired 1 teaspoon sugar

title: Compot of Early Cherries (Frühkirschen Compote)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound early cherries
- 0.5 pound sugar
- egg white

## Directions

1. Remove the stems halfway from the cherries
2. Clarify and skim half a pound of sugar with egg white
3. Place the cherries in the clarified sugar
4. Boil the mixture a couple of times
5. Serve in a bowl with the syrup
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Compot of Mirabelles (Mirabellen Compote)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- mirabelles
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Throw the mirabelles into boiling water
2. Remove the skin
3. Cook in syrup
4. Use as desired
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Compot von Frühkirschen (Early Cherry Compote)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound early cherries
- One half pound sugar
- Egg white

## Directions

1. From one pound of such cherries, half cut off the stems, then clarify half a pound sugar with egg white and skim it, lay the cherries in it, and bring them to a boil a couple of times.
2. Arrange them together with the syrup on a plate.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Compot von Himbeeren (Raspberry Compote)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful raspberries
- Liquid red currant jelly

## Directions

1. Take beautiful raspberries, remove the stems from them, arrange them on a compote dish, and cover them completely with liquid red currant jelly, which you pour on carefully.
2. Then serve them.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Compot von kleinen Pfirſichen (Small Peach Compote)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Small peaches
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take these as desired, throw them in boiling water, remove from fire and leave them in until the skin can be pulled off.
2. Then cook them with sugar and some cinnamon, the sugar must always be clarified, and arrange them.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Compot von Pflaumen (Plum Compote)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums
- Crushed sugar

## Directions

1. First remove the pits from the plums, then place them with crushed sugar and let them cook slowly for half an hour.
2. Arrange them on fine rusk slices or as desired.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Compot von Pfundbirnen (Pound Pear Compote)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pound pears
- Water
- Lemon slices
- Sugar
- Wine or water

## Directions

1. Peel these pears, cut them in half in the middle, remove the cores and any stones.
2. Cook them in a casserole with a couple of lemon slices in water until half soft, then lay them for a quarter hour in cold water.
3. Afterwards cook them very soft with sugar and wine or water until the sauce shows itself nice and jellied.
4. Arrange them on a plate and use them.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Compound Vinegar (Mixed Herb Vinegar)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 bunches of Bertram
- 1 bunch of Pimpinella
- 1 bunch of Verbena
- 1 bunch of Lemon balm
- 1 small bunch of Chamomile
- 1 bunch of Parsley
- 1 small bunch of Elderflowers
- 5 shallots
- 5 Carambola
- 1 bay leaf
- 3 Nutmeg flowers
- 5 juniper berries, crushed
- 1 tablespoon capers
- 1 teaspoon sweet mustard seeds
- 1 teaspoon bitter mustard seeds
- 3 cloves
- 5 grains of Allspice
- 5 peppercorns
- 1 knife-point of Alum
- 5 liters of good wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Place all ingredients in a bottle
2. Pour 5 liters of good wine vinegar over everything
3. Cork and bind the bottle, which should not be completely full
4. Set it in the sun for several weeks until the herbs settle to the bottom
5. Strain the vinegar through 2 linen cloths so it becomes completely clear
6. Fill it into bottles, cork and seal them well, and store in a cool place

title: Concombres (Cucumbers)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaped bowl of peeled, diced cucumbers
- 8 decagrams fat
- 6 decagrams flour
- 1 1/2 liters broth
- vinegar
- cold cream
- dill

## Directions

1. A quarter hour before serving, salt the peeled, diced cucumbers and let them stand.
2. Meanwhile make a roux from 8 decagrams fat and 6 decagrams flour.
3. Add the cucumbers and let them roast somewhat.
4. Pour with 1 1/2 liters broth and a little vinegar, stir well and let boil.
5. When serving, arrange on a platter, pour 1 tablespoon cold cream over and dust with finely cut dill.

title: Concombres farcis (Stuffed Cucumbers)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large, beautiful, straight green cucumbers
- veal forcemeat or fish forcemeat
- bacon leaves
- boiling salt water

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, cut them in half lengthwise, hollow them out and fill firmly with veal forcemeat or fish forcemeat.
2. Wrap them in thin bacon leaves.
3. Place in a shallow casserole so that the filled end lies against the side of the casserole.
4. Add enough boiling salt water to cover the cucumbers.
5. Braise covered until soft.
6. Carefully remove them, cut into desired pieces and use for dressing beef or other meat dishes.

title: Concombres frits (Fried Cucumbers)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- flour
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. After peeling the cucumbers, cut them lengthwise into four equal pieces, then into finger-length and finger-thick pieces or any desired pieces.
2. Salt them and let stand 10 minutes.
3. Place on a cloth, dry them off, dust lightly with flour and fry light golden in hot butter or fat.
4. Lay on tissue paper to remove excess fat.
5. Can also be fried in oil.
6. Serve quite hot as garnish with pot roasts, beef or similar.
7. The cucumbers can also be dipped in beer or wine batter before frying and served with meat dishes or fried fish.
8. The same method can be used for small gourds, but these must be served immediately after frying.

title: Conserve Pastry (Confitures-Bäckerei)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 deca finely sifted powdered sugar
- 10 deca flour
- 3 egg whites, stiffly beaten

## Directions

1. Lightly mix powdered sugar and flour with stiffly beaten egg whites
2. Fill into syringe or paper cone
3. Pipe mixture in desired figures onto baking sheet lined with parchment
4. Sprinkle with finely sifted sugar
5. Dry at low heat in open oven
6. Remove and loosen from baking sheet with fine knife
7. Let cool

title: Consommé (Semolina Consommé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dl milk
- 4 dkg butter
- salt
- 6 dkg semolina
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Bring 2 dl milk to a boil with 4 dkg butter and salt.
2. Stir in 6 dkg semolina spoonful by spoonful to make a thick mixture and let cool.
3. Fold in 2 egg yolks and 2 beaten egg whites.
4. Fill the mixture into a consommé mold.
5. Let it set in a steamer for 30-45 minutes.
6. Loosen, turn out, cut into pieces, and serve warm.

title: Consommé mit Leber (Liver Consommé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg beef liver
- 2 bread rolls
- milk
- 4 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape the liver, pass through sieve
2. Cream together butter, egg yolks, salt, and pepper
3. Mix in the liver, bread rolls that have been soaked in milk and well squeezed out, and pass through sieve, then fold in 2 egg whites
4. Cook mixture in consommé form for 30 minutes
5. Loosen, turn out of form, slice, and serve warm

title: Cooked Lettuce (Kochsalat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-4 larger bunches cooked lettuce
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as spinach
2. After cooking, chop coarsely

title: Cooked Salad Green (Welche Grünteil)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Young salad greens
- Salt water
- Cold water
- Herb butter or toasted bread crumbs or Dutch sauce or butter sauce

## Directions

1. After these have been cleaned and washed, boil them gently in salt water; arrange them in a heap on a plate and pour over them either herb butter, toasted bread crumbs, a Dutch sauce, or an ordinary butter sauce.

title: Cotelettes (Lamb or Veal Chops)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb or veal ribs with backbone
- butter
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- pepper (for lamb)
- egg (optional)
- white paper (optional)

## Directions

1. Take the ribs where the backbone is still attached, carefully remove the backbone, and cut one rib after another through
2. The ribs should protrude about two inches, remove the skin well, and scrape the fine meat to the back meat, which must remain two thumbs wide at the rib; scrape the rest of the rib completely white and clean
3. Pound the back meat broad with the flat blade of a knife, and then hack it cleanly with the back of a thick knife, after which try to give the cotelettes a good form
4. Dip the cotelettes in melted butter, salt them, sprinkle with finely grated breadcrumbs, and fry quickly on a greased grill, turning them often
5. When both sides are golden-brown, they are done frying
6. Alternatively, instead of just sprinkling with breadcrumbs, dip them in egg and sprinkle with grated breadcrumbs, then brown butter in a flat pan and fry them in it very quickly
7. For lamb cotelettes, add some ground pepper as well
8. These cotelettes can also be wrapped in white round-cut paper brushed with butter and fried on a grill not brushed with butter, but they must be turned often; when the paper turns brown, they are done
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Cotelettes d'Agneau frites (Fried Lamb Cutlets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb cutlets, trimmed
- Salt
- Flour
- Eggs, beaten
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter or lard, very hot
- Piquant sauce or fine vegetables

## Directions

1. Tap the trimmed cutlets with the back of a knife and salt them
2. Dip them in flour, then in beaten eggs
3. Roll them in breadcrumbs
4. Fry them in very hot butter or lard
5. Serve with piquant sauce or fine vegetables

title: Cotelettes d'Agneau à la Villeroy (Lamb Cutlets Villeroy)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb cutlets
- Salt
- Butter, hot
- Fricassee sauce
- Eggs, beaten
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter or lard, very hot
- Papillotes for the bones
- Fried potato straws
- Fine vegetables

## Directions

1. Cut the lamb cutlets so that the bone is free from meat
2. Tap them with the back of a knife and salt them
3. Put hot butter into a flat casserole, place the cutlets on it, let them rest for one minute, turn them over, and let them rest again until they have a white color
4. Remove them and place on a cloth
5. When the cutlets are completely cool, dip them in fricassee sauce
6. Place them next to each other on a platter and put it on ice until the cutlets are stiff
7. Dip them in beaten eggs, roll them in breadcrumbs, and fry them in very hot fat
8. Arrange them in a circle on a platter, cover the bones with papillotes, arrange fried potato straws around the edge of the platter, and serve with fine vegetables

title: Cratiflens Gele (Orange Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 oranges
- 3 lemons
- 1/4 liter measure
- wine to fill the remaining measure
- 7 stiff isinglass dissolved in 1 liter water
- 40 decagrams refined sugar (approximately 1/2 liter)
- a finger-length piece of orange peel

## Directions

1. Press the juice from 4 oranges and 3 lemons through a cloth and collect the completely clear juice in a 1/4 liter measure, filling the rest with wine.
2. Prepare 7 stiff isinglass by cooking in 1 liter water, then strain and cook together with the juice until reduced to 1/4 liter of liquid.
3. Heat 40 decagrams of refined sugar to which a finger-length piece of orange peel has been added.
4. Cook the isinglass and sugar together for approximately 10 minutes and strain through fine, filtered linen cloth (straining cloth) into a deep bowl.
5. Add the prepared mixture of orange juice, lemon juice and wine (totaling 1/4 liter) to the hot liquid, stir together, add clear lemon juice if necessary.
6. Pour the jelly mass (not exceeding 1 liter) into a mold that has been buried in crushed ice, ensuring it stands straight and level.
7. Cover the filled mold with a lid, place ice over it, cover well with cloth and set in a cold place for 2 to 3 hours.
8. When serving, remove the mold from ice, dip briefly in hot water, dry quickly, place a platter over it, invert the mold and slowly lift it off.
9. The jelly should be clear, transparent and so delicate that the slightest movement causes it to tremble. It should have a pleasant sweet-sour taste and melt in the mouth. Serve immediately to preserve its beauty.

title: Crayfish Bisque (Bisque d'ecrevisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 drams butter
- sliced onion
- carrots
- celery
- parsley
- mushrooms
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- caraway
- 20 cleaned live crayfish
- 1/2 liter cream
- 40 drams rice
- good broth or water
- crayfish claws and tails
- crayfish shells
- 2 liters broth
- glass of Cognac or Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Toast finely sliced onion, carrots, celery, parsley, and mushrooms (2 pieces of each) in butter until light yellow
2. Add salt, a pinch of cayenne pepper, a pinch of caraway, 20 cleaned live crayfish, and 1/2 liter of cream
3. Close the casserole tightly and steam everything
4. Separately, steam 40 drams of rice in good broth or water until soft
5. Remove the steamed crayfish from the broth
6. Remove and set aside the claws and tails in the soup pot
7. Pound the shells with the root vegetables and steamed rice
8. Press through a fine sieve into a casserole
9. Dilute with 2 liters of broth
10. Bring to a boil again, strain once more
11. Add a glass of Cognac or Madeira wine
12. Serve in the soup pot with the crayfish tails inside

title: Crayfish Boiled (Écrevisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 live, washed crayfish
- water to cover
- salt
- 1 tablespoon caraway
- 10 sprigs parsley tied together

## Directions

1. Place 20 live, washed crayfish in a casserole dish
2. Pour water over them until they are covered
3. Salt them well
4. Add 1 tablespoon caraway and 10 sprigs of parsley tied together
5. Cook covered for approximately 15-20 minutes until they turn red
6. Arrange them on a platter piled neatly (pyramid-shaped) on a clean napkin while hot
7. Sprinkle lightly with fresh parsley on top
8. Ensure all claws are directed upward; arrange some crayfish at the bottom of the dish with claws facing the edge
9. Alternatively, one can place the live crayfish directly into boiling water and wrap the caraway in a cloth napkin instead

title: Crayfish Butter (Beurre d'Ecrevisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 crayfish cooked in salted water
- 20 teaspoons butter

## Directions

1. From 15 crayfish cooked in salted water, remove the meat from the shell and pound the red shell very finely with 20 teaspoons of butter
2. Place this in a casserole and let it roast somewhat
3. Pour the roasted mixture through a sieve laid over a bowl with cold water and push it through so the butter becomes cold
4. When it is stiff, remove it from the cold water, give it a desired form and use it as needed

title: Crayfish Coulis (Coulis d'ecrevisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 medium-sized crayfish
- salt water
- meat broth as needed

## Directions

1. Boil the crayfish in salt water
2. Remove and set aside the claws and tails in the soup pot
3. Crush the crayfish shells with meat broth
4. Then strain through a sieve
5. Continue as with the previous soup

title: Crayfish in Madeira Wine (Écrevisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish as needed
- truffle, thinly sliced
- salt
- Madeira wine to cover

## Directions

1. Place the needed quantity of crayfish in a casserole
2. Add thinly sliced truffle
3. Season with salt
4. Add enough Madeira wine to cover the crayfish
5. Cover with a fitting lid
6. Cook for 7.5 hours
7. Let stand covered until serving
8. Arrange the crayfish on a dish, cover with a lid or napkin
9. Serve the sauce in a sauce boat, seasoned with butter and lemon juice if desired

title: Crayfish in Sour Cream (Écrevisses à la creme aigre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 sprigs fresh parsley
- 5 sprigs chervil
- 3 mushrooms
- 3 shallots
- 3 rocamboles, all very finely chopped
- 1.5 deciliters butter
- 1/4 liter vinegar
- 1 teaspoon caraway
- salt
- 1 liter cream
- 24 live, washed crayfish

## Directions

1. Finely chop the parsley, chervil, mushrooms, shallots, and rocamboles
2. Roast these ingredients briefly in 1.5 deciliters of butter
3. Add 1/4 liter vinegar, 1 teaspoon caraway, salt, and 1 liter cream
4. Allow this to reduce well
5. Place 24 live, washed crayfish in a casserole
6. Pour the prepared sauce through a sieve over the crayfish
7. Cook covered well for 1.5 hours
8. Arrange them in a deep dish and cover with a napkin
9. Serve the sauce in a sauce boat

title: Crayfish in White Wine (Écrevisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 live, washed crayfish
- approximately 1 liter white wine
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place 30 live, washed crayfish in a casserole dish
2. Heat approximately 1 liter of white wine that has been well seasoned and somewhat peppered
3. Pour the boiling wine over the crayfish
4. Cook covered for approximately 10 minutes until they turn red
5. Serve as previously described

title: Crayfish Juice (Schulz) Soup
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 crayfish boiled in salt water
- 20 drams butter
- 4 drams toasted semolina slices
- 2 heads of onion
- 2 halves roasted or baked fish heads
- finely sliced white and yellow roots
- flour
- pea water
- salt
- pepper
- 12 drams rice cooked in milk

## Directions

1. Remove the gall from the boiled crayfish
2. Pound the crayfish and place in a casserole with butter
3. Add toasted semolina slices, onion heads, roasted or baked fish heads, finely sliced white and yellow roots, and flour
4. Steam everything until tender
5. Pour the steamed mixture with 2 liters of pea water
6. Bring to a boil, strain, and season with salt and pepper
7. Add 12 drams of rice cooked in milk to the soup
8. You can also add less rice and instead use toasted semolina slices

title: Crayfish Ragout Soup (Potage aux ecrevisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 crayfish
- 1 teaspoon caraway
- sprigs of green parsley
- salt water
- 16 drams butter
- flour
- 8 drams rice
- 2 liters pea water or water
- 3 Madeira apples or 3 spoonfuls of Madeira marmalade (optional)
- salt
- 12 drams rice (cooked)
- crayfish tails
- 1/4 liter cooked green peas

## Directions

1. Place crayfish with caraway and green parsley in boiling salt water and simmer until they turn red
2. Remove and set aside the claws and tails in the soup pot
3. Pound the shells and fat in a mortar and steam them in a casserole over water
4. Press them through a sieve into another casserole
5. Heat the crayfish butter, add flour, and toast it
6. Add 8 drams of rice and pour in with 2 liters of pea water or water
7. Bring to a boil with constant stirring
8. If desired, add 3 Madeira apples torn apart or 3 spoonfuls of Madeira marmalade
9. Simmer slowly for 1 hour
10. Strain through a sieve into a casserole and season with salt
11. Serve with 12 drams of cooked rice, crayfish tails, and 1/4 liter of cooked green peas, or only with toasted semolina bread and crayfish tails

title: Crayfish Sausages (Boudins d'écrevisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 smallest crayfish
- salt water
- braised or cooked trout
- 1 chicken breast
- 1 goose liver cooked in salt water
- crayfish shells
- 12 deciliters butter
- 24 deciliters trimmed, in milk-soaked, well-pressed panettone
- crayfish butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- pepper
- nutmeg
- salt
- sausage casings

## Directions

1. Boil 30 of the smallest crayfish in salt water
2. Shell them completely, reserve the cooked flesh
3. Finely pound the crayfish shells
4. Toast them in 12 deciliters butter for 10-15 minutes
5. Press through a cloth into cold water to extract the crayfish butter
6. Finely dice the cooked trout, chicken breast, and goose liver
7. Mix with the reserved crayfish flesh
8. Add the crayfish butter, 2 egg yolks, 1 whole egg, the panettone, pepper, nutmeg, and salt to taste
9. Mix everything well
10. Thoroughly clean the sausage casings in fresh water and soak for 7.5 hours
11. Fill the casings with the prepared mixture using a syringe
12. Tie into finger-length pieces with twine
13. Slowly fry in butter on both sides
14. Serve with fine vegetables or purées

title: Crayfish Soufflé (Soufflé d'écrevisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 crayfish, cooked in salted water
- 20 ounces butter
- 8 ounces blanched almonds
- 12 ounces crusts of bread, softened in milk
- 4 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 4 raw egg yolks
- 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- 6 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Cook crayfish in salted water, remove tails and claws, pound shells with butter to prepare crayfish butter
2. Blanch and finely pound almonds, then pound again with softened bread crusts and hard-boiled egg yolks
3. Beat the crayfish butter until fluffy, add raw egg yolks one by one
4. Mix in the pounded almond mixture, cayenne, nutmeg, and the crayfish tails and claws
5. Gently fold in the whipped egg whites
6. Fill a butter-greased, flour-lined mold
7. Cover and steam for 1/2 hour or bake in the oven
8. Turn out onto a serving dish and serve with sweet cream or béchamel sauce as desired

title: Crayfish à la Bordelaise (Écrevisses à la Bordelaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deciliters fresh butter
- 5 leaves each of onion, carrot, parsley, leek, celery
- 8 deciliters lean, raw meat
- 2 deciliters soup glace
- 4 spoonfuls Paradise apple purée
- 4 spoonfuls cognac
- 15 crayfish
- salt
- pepper
- 3/4 liter white Bordeaux wine

## Directions

1. Place the butter in a deep casserole
2. Arrange the onion, carrot, parsley, leek, and celery leaves on top
3. Add the lean raw meat
4. Braise everything covered together
5. Add the soup glace, Paradise apple purée, and cognac
6. Place 15 crayfish on top so they rest on their legs
7. Season with salt and pepper
8. Add 3/4 liter of white Bordeaux wine
9. Cook covered until the crayfish turn red
10. Transfer the crayfish to a dish and cover to keep them warm
11. Reduce the sauce, strain it, and serve in a sauce boat with the hot crayfish

title: Cream Soufflé (Soufflé à la crème)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter cream
- juice of 1 lemon
- 3 egg yolks
- 8 ounces bread crusts, softened in milk
- 16 ounces butter, beaten
- 7 egg yolks
- 8 ounces blanched almonds
- 2 egg whites
- 5 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Put cream and lemon juice in a casserole, beat in 3 egg yolks, cover and simmer gently until curdled
2. Strain and press lightly to remove all liquid
3. Pass the curdled mixture through a sieve with the softened bread crusts
4. Beat the butter and add 7 egg yolks one by one
5. Mix in the blanched pounded almonds with the 2 egg whites and the passed bread mixture
6. Gently fold in the 5 whipped egg whites
7. Fill a butter-greased, flour-lined mold
8. Cover and steam for 1/2 hour or bake in the oven
9. Turn out into a dish, pour hot marmalade over it, and serve immediately

title: Cream Soufflé (Soufflé à la crème)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces flour
- 16 ounces granulated sugar
- 1/3 liter cold milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Put flour and granulated sugar in a casserole, slowly add cold milk while stirring
2. Heat and stir until the mixture is well cooked
3. Pour into a deep dish and cool completely
4. Add egg yolks one by one
5. Gently fold in the whipped egg whites
6. Fill a butter-greased, flour-lined mold
7. Bake at low heat for 3/4 hour in the oven or bake in the casserole itself
8. Turn out onto a dish, sprinkle with sugar, and serve

title: Creme Gefrorenes mit Erdbeeren (Strawberry Cream Ice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 deca sugar
- 1 liter cold water
- 1/2 liter sieved strawberry pulp
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Dissolve 50 deca sugar in 1 liter cold water
2. Stir in 1/2 liter sieved strawberry pulp and freeze as described in Vanilla Ice (Recipe 1720)
3. Meanwhile beat 4 egg whites to stiff peaks
4. Add this to the freezing bowl with the frozen strawberries, mix well with the mixture
5. Tablet this so it does not become grainy and let it finish freezing covered

title: Creme im Dunst mit Chocolate (Steamed Cream Pudding with Chocolate)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca chocolate
- 1/2 liter boiling water

## Directions

1. It is prepared in the same way as the 'Creme im Dunst mit Vanille' (No. 1426). One softens 16 deca chocolate on a baking sheet in a warm oven, then puts it into a saucepan, stirs it thoroughly smooth with 1/2 liter boiling water over the fire and mixes it into the already prepared cream without vanilla.

title: Creme im Dunst mit Kaffee (Steamed Cream Pudding with Coffee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca coffee
- 1/2 liter hot cream
- 16 deca sugar
- 4 tablespoons cream
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg

## Directions

1. Roast 8 deca coffee and pour 1/2 liter of hot cream over it and cover it so that it absorbs the coffee aroma. After approximately 1/4 hour, strain it through a sieve, place it on the stove and let it boil with 16 deca sugar. Then stir together in 4 tablespoons cream 8 egg yolks and 1 whole egg, gradually pour in the prepared cream, place it on the stove for a few moments while constantly stirring, pass the mass through a sieve, fill it into the mold, and proceed further as indicated in the preceding number 1424 for cooking the cream.

title: Creme im Dunst mit Karamel (Steamed Cream Pudding with Caramel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 deca sugar for caramel
- 6 tablespoons water
- 1 liter cream
- 12 deca sugar
- salt
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg

## Directions

1. Brush a mold with butter and dust it with flour. After 20 deca sugar has already been cooked into caramel (see No. 1420), pour one third of it into the mold. To the remaining sugar add 6 tablespoons water and when it has dissolved, boil it with 1 liter cream, 12 deca sugar and, if desired, 1 pinch of salt. Meanwhile stir together in a saucepan 8 egg yolks and 1 whole egg, gradually pour in the prepared cream, place it on the fire for a few moments while constantly stirring and pass it through a sieve. Then the mass is filled into the mold, this is placed into the water bath and treated further as indicated under No. 1424.

title: Creme im Dunst mit Mandeln (Steamed Cream Pudding with Almonds)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca blanched almonds
- water
- 16 deca sugar
- 1 liter milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg

## Directions

1. After 24 deca blanched almonds have been pounded very finely with some water, boil them with 16 deca sugar in 1 liter milk for 1/2 hour. Then strain the mixture through a sieve, mix in 8 egg yolks and 1 whole egg and proceed further as indicated.

title: Creme im Dunst mit Vanille (Steamed Cream Pudding with Vanilla)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca vanilla sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1/2 liter very cold good cream

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan 16 deca vanilla sugar, 8 egg yolks and 1 whole egg and stir it together with 1/2 liter very cold, good cream, put it into a snow-beating bowl and beat it for some minutes over the fire with the whisk until it is lukewarm, it must not boil. Then pour it through a sieve into a deep dish and from there into any desired mold, buttered and dusted with flour, or into cups, and proceed further as indicated above (No. 1424).

title: Creme Schnitte (Cream Slices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter pastry dough prepared as for fruit slices
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 16 deca vanilla sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter cream
- 6 egg whites, stiffly beaten

## Directions

1. Prepare as fruit slices, but fill with the following cream mass instead of jam
2. Place 2 tablespoons flour in a mixing bowl with 16 deca vanilla sugar and 6 egg yolks
3. Whip this with the whisk over the fire until it becomes a thick mass
4. Remove from fire and after complete cooling, lightly fold in the snow from the 6 egg whites

title: Creme von bittern Maccaroni (Bitter Macaroni Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- one spoonful fine flour
- 2 seidel milk
- one quarter pound sugar
- one lemon
- half pound bitter macaroni
- half pound small raisins

## Directions

1. Stir 12 egg yolks with one spoonful fine flour and 2 seidel milk clear
2. Add one quarter pound sugar onto which a lemon has been grated, half pound bitter macaroni which is cut several times, and half pound small raisins
3. Boil once over constant stirring and stir cold again
4. Draw the egg white snow under the cream, pile it up on a plate, bake it in an oven that is no longer hot, and serve warm
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Creme von Champagner Wein (Champagne Wine Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 eggs
- 32 half eggshells wine
- quarter pound sugar
- lemon
- whole cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat 16 eggs into a new pot
2. Pour in 32 half eggshells of wine
3. Mix in quarter pound sugar rubbed with a lemon and some whole cinnamon
4. Let come to a gentle boil over low fire with vigorous beating
5. The cream must become quite thick and foamy
6. Arrange on a plate or in serving shells
7. Pass around with the shells
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Creme von Erdbeeren (Strawberry Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds beautiful strawberries
- 1 seidel cream
- one quarter pound sugar
- one piece cinnamon
- 6 ground chicken gizzard membranes

## Directions

1. Wash 2 pounds beautiful strawberries and press them through a hair sieve as finely as possible
2. Cook 1 seidel cream with one quarter pound sugar and one piece cinnamon
3. When the cream is lukewarm, add 6 ground chicken gizzard membranes and strain several times through a hair sieve
4. Place on some warm ash, cover with a lid, and lay only a few coals around it
5. Let the cream stand a quarter hour so it becomes like a jelly
6. Set the plate in the cellar, though it is always better if this is done in a cream cup
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Creme von Himbeeren (Raspberry Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds fresh raspberries
- 16 egg yolks
- 2 glasses champagne wine
- quarter pound sugar

## Directions

1. Select 2 pounds fresh raspberries well and pass through a hair sieve
2. Beat 16 egg yolks into a pot
3. Add 2 glasses champagne wine, quarter pound sugar, and the raspberry juice
4. Let sputter on charcoal fire until everything becomes a thick foam
5. Pile the cream on a plate like a mountain
6. Serve cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Creme von Kaffeh (Coffee Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One Seidel water
- 8 Loth coffee
- Half a Seidel good cream
- 16 whole eggs
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Cook 8 Loth coffee in one Seidel water as usual
2. Add half a Seidel good cream lukewarm to it
3. Beat 16 whole eggs in a basin, and with constant stirring add the sweetened coffee to the cream
4. Arrange the cream on a plate according to the method given above
5. Serve the cream cold at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Creme von Kirschen (Cherry Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds cherries
- 16 egg yolks
- strong glass Madeira wine
- whole cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pound 2 pounds cherries together with the stones quite small
2. Pass through a hair sieve
3. Beat 16 egg yolks in a pot
4. Add the cherry juice, a strong glass Madeira wine, a piece of whole cinnamon, and some sugar to taste
5. Let come to a boil once over charcoal fire with constant stirring, so it becomes a thick foam
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Creme von Weinbeeren (Grape Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 measures juice from ripe grapes
- quarter pound sugar
- lemon
- 12 egg yolks
- 2 strong glasses Malaga or Tokayer wine
- ripe grapes

## Directions

1. Press 2 measures juice from ripe grapes
2. Let this reduce to half a measure
3. Add quarter pound sugar and let cool
4. Put 12 egg yolks in a pot
5. Beat with 2 strong glasses Malaga or Tokayer wine
6. Pour in the reduced juice
7. Let come to a boil once over charcoal fire with constant stirring
8. Line the plate with ripe grapes
9. Pour the cream cold over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Creme von Zuckerbrot (Sugar Bread Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons fine flour
- 8 loth biscuit
- 1 seidel Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Stir 20 egg yolks, 3 tablespoons fine flour, 8 loth biscuit, and 1 seidel Rhine wine together thoroughly
2. Let it boil once over constant stirring
3. It must become quite thick and is served cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Creme à Pestosade von Rahm (Cream à Pestosade of Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon fine flour
- 1 seidel cream
- 16 egg yolks
- one and a half quarter pound sugar
- one lemon
- one piece whole cinnamon
- egg whites from the above eggs
- 4 loth finely chopped, sugared orange peels

## Directions

1. Stir one tablespoon fine flour with 1 seidel cream gradually until clear
2. Stir 16 egg yolks into a casserole, pour in the cream, grate one and a half quarter pound sugar on a lemon and mix it under, also add one piece whole cinnamon
3. Stir everything over not too strong a fire until it boils
4. Beat the whites from the above eggs into very stiff snow and stir it under the mass while it is still warm
5. With a tablespoon, spoon it onto a serving dish so it forms a mountain
6. Sprinkle 4 loth finely chopped, sugared orange peels over it and serve cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Cremes-Krapfen (Cream Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter cream
- 8 tablespoons vanilla sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 6 egg yolks
- Butter for the mold
- Flour for the mold
- Flour for dredging
- Eggs for coating
- Breadcrumbs for rolling
- Lard for frying
- Powdered sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Put 1/4 liter cream in a deep dish and stir it well with 8 tablespoons vanilla sugar, 1 tablespoon flour and 6 egg yolks.
2. Fill it into a mold brushed with butter and dusted with flour and let it steam covered for 1/2 hour.
3. After this time, remove the mold, cover it with a dish, turn it over and carefully remove it.
4. After completely cooling, cut out fritters of any desired size with a round cutter, or cut strips into square pieces.
5. Dip these in beaten eggs, roll in breadcrumbs and fry them on both sides until golden yellow in very hot lard, shaking the pan frequently.
6. Remove them with a skimming spoon, arrange them mixed with other similar dishes in a dish, sprinkle with finely powdered sugar and serve hot.

title: Cremes-Torteletten (Cream Tartlets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deka flour
- 12 deka butter
- 4 deka sugar
- 5 lightly cooked, sieved egg yolks
- 1 knife point finely cut lemon peel
- beaten egg white for brushing
- 16 deka vanilla sugar
- 8 deka egg butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
