- 0.25 liter cream
- citron glaze

## Directions

1. Put 12 deka flour, 12 deka butter, 4 deka sugar, 5 lightly cooked sieved egg yolks, and 1 knife point finely cut lemon peel on a board and work together into a dough
2. Roll out knife-back thick and cut out round shapes with a small cutter
3. Brush the edges with beaten egg white
4. Roll out the dough scraps into thin noodles and place them on the brushed edges
5. Place the tartlets on a butter-brushed baking sheet with a knife
6. In a mixing bowl, combine 16 deka vanilla sugar, 8 deka egg butter, 1 tablespoon flour, and 0.25 liter cream, stir together at moderate heat until thick, then set aside
7. Once completely cool, fill the tartlets using a piping bag, brush the dough edges with egg white, and bake in the oven
8. Once done, remove the tartlets from the sheet, glaze with citron glaze leaving the edges free, and place a spoon handle in the middle of each one

title: Croquettes für Gastmahl vom Fisch (Fish Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 tablespoons of any braised fish flesh
- 1/8 liter boiled peas
- 10 pieces softened crayfish tails
- 3 hard-boiled eggs, cut with a knife
- 5 pieces cleaned, finely diced pickled cucumbers
- 4 tablespoons soaked, squeezed out breadcrumbs without crust
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- Milk liver herbs if available
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Shred the fish flesh and place in a deep bowl
2. Add boiled peas, softened crayfish tails, hard-boiled eggs cut with a knife, finely diced pickled cucumbers, soaked and pressed breadcrumbs, egg yolks, whole egg, herbs if available, salt and pepper
3. Mix everything together well and proceed as with the Ragout Croquettes

title: Croquettes von Eiern (Egg Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 hard-boiled, shelled, thinly sliced eggs
- 3/4 liter thick béchamel sauce
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Flour for dusting
- Eggs for dipping
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard for frying

## Directions

1. Mix the hard-boiled eggs with the thick béchamel sauce
2. Season with salt, pepper and nutmeg
3. After completely cooling, treat as the Ragout Croquettes
4. Dust with flour, dip in beaten eggs, roll in breadcrumbs and fry in hot lard

title: Croutons
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ Schneidemmels (sliced bread rolls)
- 6 dkg butter
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Cut the bread rolls first in half, then into leaf-like slices.
2. Melt the butter in a small pot.
3. Dip the bread slices in the melted butter, then turn them on both sides in grated Parmesan.
4. Lay them in an omelet pan and bake them in the oven at moderate heat until light brown.
5. Serve them separately.

title: Crème au Café (Coffee Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter cold cream coffee
- 12 deca sugar
- 4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put 3/4 liter cold cream coffee, 12 deca sugar, and 4 egg yolks in a snow kettle
2. Beat over heat with a whisk until the mass begins to thicken

title: Crème au Caramel (Caramel Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca crushed sugar
- 1/4 liter cold water
- 1/2 liter cream
- 4 egg yolks
- 6 deca sugar

## Directions

1. Put 12 deca crushed sugar in a saucepan and let it brown
2. Gradually pour 1/4 liter cold water onto it
3. Let it boil well and then cool
4. Meanwhile, beat 1/2 liter cream with 4 egg yolks and 6 deca sugar
5. Put this in a snow kettle
6. Add the cooled caramel sugar
7. Beat over heat until the mass begins to thicken
8. Use immediately

title: Crème au Chocolat (Chocolate Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter water
- 12 deca sugar
- 12 deca (3 tablets) chocolate
- 1/2 liter cold cream
- 4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil 1/2 liter water with 12 deca sugar
2. Meanwhile, melt 12 deca (3 tablets) chocolate in the oven on a baking sheet
3. Put the chocolate in a saucepan
4. Gradually pour the boiling sugared water onto it
5. Stir until completely smooth and fully cooked
6. Put 1/2 liter cold cream and 4 egg yolks in a snow kettle and mix
7. While on the heat, slowly pour in the chocolate and beat with a whisk until the mass begins to thicken, but do not boil
8. Use immediately
9. If desired, strain the mass through a sieve

title: Crème au Gratin (Cream au Gratin)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- half a measure sweet cream
- sugar
- orange blossom
- a couple tablespoons fine flour

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks into a casserole
2. Stir in gradually half a measure sweet cream
3. Add sugar and orange blossom
4. Carefully stir in a couple tablespoons fine flour and beat well so it does not become lumpy
5. Place a dish with some cream on hot ashes
6. When it begins to cook, pour the entire mass in
7. Stir for some time
8. Let set over gentle charcoal fire so the cream gets a nice crust on the bottom of the plate
9. Finally burn it with a shovel and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Crème aux Amandes (Almond Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 pieces sweet almonds
- 5 pieces bitter almonds
- Cream

## Directions

1. Blanch 20 sweet almonds and 5 bitter almonds
2. Grind with some cream
3. Treat further as in the Hazelnut Cream recipe

title: Crème aux Fruits (Fruit Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Desired marmalade
- Warm water

## Directions

1. Dissolve desired marmalade with some warm water
2. Use as desired

title: Crème aux Fruits Gratinée (Cream with Fruits, Gratinéed)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour (4 drams)
- Sugar (14 drams)
- Milk (1/4 liter cold)
- Egg yolks (8)
- Egg whites (8)
- Peaches, apricots or similar fruits (halved, pitted)

## Directions

1. Put flour and sugar in a casserole, pour in cold milk slowly and stir over heat until thick
2. Empty into a deep dish and stir until cold
3. Add egg yolks one after another
4. Finally fold in the snow of egg whites
5. Line the bottom of a butter-coated dish so that the fruit halves lie with their skin side down
6. Pour the cream mixture over, sprinkle heavily with finely crushed sugar
7. Bake for 1/4 hour in an open oven

title: Crème aux Oranges (Orange Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca sugar
- 1 orange
- Cream
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 liter cream

## Directions

1. Rub 12 deca sugar on an orange and let it dissolve in a few spoonfuls of cream
2. Meanwhile, beat 4 egg yolks with 1/2 liter cream
3. Put this in a snow kettle
4. Add the dissolved sugar
5. Beat with a whisk over heat until the mass begins to thicken
6. Use immediately

title: Crème Brûlée (Burnt Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- 1 measure cream
- quarter pound sugar
- lemon
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat 12 egg yolks into a casserole
2. Stir well with 1 measure cream
3. Add quarter pound sugar rubbed with a lemon and a piece of cinnamon
4. Stir on not too fast fire until it comes to a boil once
5. Slowly draw in the snow from the egg whites
6. Arrange the cream on a plate
7. Pour over a cup of currant jelly and marble with a spoon
8. Place the plate in a warm oven; the cream should become only gold yellow, not brown
9. Serve warm
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Crème d'Avelines (Hazelnut Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca shelled hazelnut kernels
- 6 tablespoons cream
- 1/2 liter cream
- 10 deca sugar
- 4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Grind 14 deca shelled hazelnut kernels with 6 tablespoons cream
2. Cook in 1/2 liter cream with frequent stirring
3. Press through a cloth
4. Beat with 10 deca sugar and 4 egg yolks over heat with a whisk
5. Continue beating until the mass begins to bind and thicken
6. Use immediately

title: Crème à l'Espagnole (Cream Spanish Style)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef marrow (6 drams)
- Cold cream (1/2 liter)
- Egg yolks (14)
- Vanilla sugar (16 drams)
- Marmalade
- Sugar (16 drams finely crushed)
- Egg whites (4)

## Directions

1. Stir beef marrow slowly with cold cream, then add egg yolks and vanilla sugar
2. Fill into a butter-coated, flour-lined mold and cover
3. Poach for 3/4 hour
4. When done, turn out onto a dish and cover with dissolved marmalade
5. Mix finely crushed sugar with the snow of egg whites
6. Fill into paper cornettos and pipe decoratively onto the marmalade
7. Sprinkle with sugar and dry in an open oven for 1/2 hour

title: Crème à la Reine (Queen's Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 egg yolks
- tablespoon flour
- 1 measure Malaga wine
- sugar
- 2 lemons
- fine chopped pistachios

## Directions

1. Stir in a pot with 30 egg yolks a tablespoon flour clearly
2. Pour in 1 measure Malaga wine
3. Beat well with a whisk for half a quarter hour
4. Add only as much sugar as needed for rubbing 2 lemons, so the cream does not become too sweet
5. Beat all ingredients over gentle fire with the whisk until it becomes a thick cream and comes to a gentle boil once
6. Arrange on a dish
7. Sprinkle with quarter pound fine chopped pistachios
8. Serve cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Crème à la Vanille (Vanilla Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 12 deca vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 liter cream, 6 egg yolks, and 12 deca vanilla sugar in a snow kettle
2. Beat over heat with a whisk until the mass begins to thicken

title: Crème-Rissoles (Cream-Filled Pastry Crescents)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rissole dough
- any cream filling
- eggs for coating
- pearl sugar
- finely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Prepare rissole dough as in the recipe for Rissoles mit Farce
2. Fill with any desired cream
3. Cut out into semicircles
4. Place on a board moistened with water
5. Coat with beaten eggs, sprinkle with pearl sugar and finely chopped almonds
6. Bake in the oven at medium heat until golden

title: Crèpes in Französischer Art (Crepes, French Style)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 liter flour
- 3 whole eggs
- 0.5 liter cold milk
- 1 pinch salt
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Place 0.5 liter flour in a deep bowl and gradually stir in 3 whole eggs, 0.5 liter cold milk and 1 pinch salt so that no lumps form but the batter is smooth and fluid
2. Pour 2 tablespoons of this batter on a hot, fat-coated frying pan so that the pan is evenly coated with it
3. When the lower side has a yellow color, carefully turn the crepe over with a spatula or a large fine knife so that it is not damaged, pour a little hot fat from the side if necessary and bake the second side also yellow
4. After placing the baked crepe in a bowl kept warm and continuing the baking in this manner until the batter is finished, coat with apple sauce or any marmalade or almond filling

title: Crômekrapferl (Cream Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- choux pastry made from: 2 dl water, 7 dkg butter, salt, 14 dkg flour, 3-4 small eggs
- filling cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare choux pastry from water, butter, salt, flour and eggs (see Teige section).
2. With a small spoon, pipe small puffs onto a buttered sheet.
3. Bake at higher heat until light brown.
4. They should rise high and be hollow inside.
5. Make a test puff first to check if the dough is the right consistency.
6. If the dough is too thin or too stiff, add flour or an egg accordingly.
7. Do not open the oven door too often during baking or they will collapse.
8. Cut the puffs on the side, fill with cream, sprinkle with sugar and serve.

title: Crômeſchnitten (Cream Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagrams flour
- 18 dekagrams butter
- 1 egg
- salt
- 2 spoons vinegar or wine
- some water
- vanilla cream

## Directions

1. The butter dough must be started the evening before.
2. Divide the finished butter dough into 2 parts.
3. Roll each part thinly to the size of a baking sheet.
4. Bake on the sheet and cool completely.
5. Spread vanilla cream on one part.
6. Divide the second part into equal-sized pieces and lay them close together on the cream.
7. Divide the lower part according to the cuts, place on a platter and sprinkle with sugar.

title: Cröme (Cream Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- 2-3 tablespoons flour
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 piece vanilla
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. In a snow basin, mix some milk with flour until smooth.
2. Add the remaining milk, egg yolks, vanilla and sugar to taste.
3. Beat thick over steam and cool before using.
4. If the cream is not thick enough, mix some milk and flour in a small pot and pour into the cream.
5. You can also fold in 1-2 egg whites or 1/2 l whipped cream into the cold cream.

title: Cucumbers à la Saint-Hubert (Gurken à Saint Hubert aufbewahren)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers with small seeds
- salt
- vinegar (see No. 1901)
- oil

## Directions

1. Choose a good variety of cucumbers with small seeds.
2. Cut them into leaves, lengthwise strips, or as desired after splitting.
3. Sprinkle with salt, lay on cloth, and dry them with the cloth.
4. Place the cucumbers in jars and pour cooled salted vinegar (see No. 1901) over them.
5. Pour a finger's-breadth of oil on top of the vinegar.
6. Seal the jars, which must not be completely full, with beeswax and corks, and store in a cool, airy place.

title: Cuissot de Porc rôti naturel (Naturally Roasted Pork Leg)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork leg, skinned
- Salt
- Caraway
- Garlic, crushed (optional)
- Fat
- Fat, melted

## Directions

1. Rub the leg of a skinned pig with salt, caraway and if desired, crushed garlic
2. Put some fat into a roasting pan, place the leg on it, pour melted fat over the meat
3. Roast at moderate heat, basting frequently with the pan juices, for 1 1/2 to 2 hours in the oven
4. After this time, remove the meat, cut it into pieces, place these on a platter and serve the sauce in a sauceboat
5. The sauce can be soured and the fat removed as desired

title: Cèpes (Cèpes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cèpes
- butter
- green parsley

## Directions

1. From the peeled cèpes, remove both the lower part of the stem and any worm-damaged portions
2. Cut the cèpes into thin slices
3. Place them in butter with roasted green parsley and braise them until tender
4. Use them as a vegetable or as a garnish for various meat dishes

title: Côte de boeuf in der Braise (Beef Rib in Braise)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece from the side rib
- coarse bacon
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- ground cloves
- bacon leaves
- whole onions
- carrots
- bay leaf
- thyme
- parsley
- bouillon or water

## Directions

1. Flatten the rib piece and trim it
2. Lard it with coarse bacon
3. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, nutmeg, and finely ground cloves
4. Lay bacon leaves in an oblong casserole, then the rib, which you cover again with bacon
5. Add many whole onions, carrots, a bay leaf, thyme and parsley
6. Let everything brown quickly over charcoal fire
7. Finally fill it with bouillon or water
8. Let it cook through; it must be very tender, but the sauce should be reduced quickly so it glazes itself
9. First skim off the fat
10. Place the rib on a platter, pour some bouillon into the sauce, and serve it underneath
11. Glaze it with evenly cut carrots and parsnips, glazed onions in butter, roasted potatoes, minced cabbage or white cabbage, which must all be cooked until tender, nicely arranged, and glazed
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Dames Pudding
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 16 deca rind-removed, finely cut rolls
- milk
- parsley
- 1 champignon
- butter for roasting
- 16 deca roasted, finely cut meat
- salt
- nutmeg
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 12 deca butter until fluffy
2. Gradually stir in 6 egg yolks
3. Mix in 16 deca rind-removed finely cut rolls soaked in milk
4. Add some finely cut parsley and 1 champignon roasted in butter
5. Add 16 deca roasted finely cut meat, some salt, and nutmeg
6. Mix everything well together
7. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
8. Cook in steam or bake

title: Dampfnudeln (Steamed Noodles)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 2 pounds fine flour
- 2 tablespoons good thick beer yeast
- some lukewarm milk
- 1 piece butter the size of an egg
- 1 egg
- salt
- 1 piece butter for the pan
- 1 inch milk
- a couple pieces of sugar
- cream for the sauce
- egg yolks
- sugar
- ground vanilla

## Directions

1. Take approximately 2 pounds fine flour in a bowl, make a hole in the middle of the flour, and work in 2 tablespoons good thick beer yeast and some lukewarm milk to make a dough, let it stand for a while until it has risen well
2. Take a piece of butter the size of an egg, let it melt in the warm milk, beat in 1 egg, salt it a little, and make the dough neither too soft nor too stiff, but it must be beaten well
3. Cover it with a cloth and let it rise
4. Then make noodles from it in desired size and let them rise again on the board
5. In a large pan melt a piece of butter, pour in 1 inch of milk, and add a couple pieces of sugar
6. Lay the steamed noodles in the pan, but not too crowded together, cover with a well-fitting lid, and heat from below until they begin to cook down
7. Then pull the heat out a little, lift the finished noodles out with a noodle scraper somewhat later, and serve them either plain or with a sauce prepared from cream, egg yolks, sugar, and ground vanilla
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Dampfnudeln (Steamed Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 dkg flour
- 6 dkg butter
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- 3 spoonfuls sugar
- yeast
- warm milk
- 1 liter milk
- 5 dkg almonds, softened and chopped
- Canary milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Warm 25 dkg flour in a mixing bowl, rub in 6 dkg butter
2. Add 3 egg yolks, salt, 3 spoonfuls sugar, the risen yeast starter, and lukewarm milk as needed
3. Beat the dough until it pulls away from the spoon
4. Let it rise near the stove
5. Place it on a floured board, form round noodles, dip them in melted butter
6. Lay them in a baking pan in which 1 liter milk has been warmed
7. Sprinkle with 5 dkg softened, chopped almonds, possibly add another layer of noodles, sprinkle with almonds again
8. Let them rise again, bake at moderate heat
9. Arrange on a round platter, pour Canary milk over them and serve sprinkled with sugar

title: Das Tranchieren des Spanferkels (Carving Suckling Pig)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig head
- Suckling pig body

## Directions

1. Cut off the head and divide it lengthwise down the middle
2. Cut each half apart again so that 4 pieces result from the head
3. Arrange these on a platter so that the division of the head is not noticeable
4. Without damaging the skin, separate the shoulders from the back, likewise the legs
5. Cut off the belly pieces, divide them into 4 pieces and place them against the head lengthwise
6. Cut the back into strips three fingers wide and lay these on the belly pieces
7. On both sides of the back, place the shoulders cut into desired pieces toward the head
8. Finally, place the two legs cut into pieces together so that they appear as one whole

title: Date Pudding (Pouding aux dattes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 decagrams trimmed, finely sliced bread rolls
- 0.25 liter boiling milk
- 12 decagrams butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 14 decagrams granulated sugar
- 8 egg whites
- 8 decagrams finely pounded almonds
- 18 decagrams finely minced dates
- some dissolved almond kernels
- butter for the mold
- flour for the mold

## Directions

1. Soften 12 decagrams trimmed, finely sliced bread rolls in 0.25 liter boiling milk
2. Stir over heat until well softened
3. Mix in 12 decagrams butter and set aside
4. Beat 8 egg yolks with 14 decagrams granulated sugar for 1/2 hour
5. Add the bread roll mixture and beat well
6. Lightly fold in the snow from 8 egg whites simultaneously with 8 decagrams finely pounded almonds, 18 decagrams finely minced dates (pits removed), and some dissolved almond kernels
7. Fill a buttered mold dusted with flour
8. Bake covered in steam for 0.75 to 1 hour

title: Dattelbaisers (Date Meringues)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 1 egg white
- 14 dkg chopped nuts
- 14 dkg dates, cut into strips

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dkg sugar with 1 egg white until stiff
2. Mix in chopped nuts and dates
3. Using two spoons, form small mounds on buttered sheet
4. Dry rather than bake in the oven

title: Dattelbaiſers (Date Meringues)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 1 egg white
- 14 dkg chopped nuts
- 14 dkg dates, cut into noodle-like strips

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dkg sugar with 1 egg white in a wide mixing basin
2. Mix in 14 dkg chopped nuts and 14 dkg dates cut into noodle-like strips

title: Datteln gefüllt (Filled Dates)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful dates
- 10 dkg sugar
- 1 egg white
- A few drops of rum or lemon juice
- Softened chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Cut dates open on one side
2. Remove the pits
3. Mix sugar with egg white, a few drops of rum or lemon juice, and softened chopped almonds
4. Form the mixture into shapes matching the pit shape
5. Fill the dates with this mixture

title: Datteltorte (Date Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg whites
- 21 dkg sugar
- 21 dkg grated nuts or raw grated almonds
- 21 dkg finely cut dates
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Beat sugar into stiff egg whites
2. Fold in grated nuts or raw grated almonds, finely cut dates, and lemon juice
3. Fill mixture into form
4. Bake at lower heat
5. When cooled, turn out of form
6. Cover with chocolate glaze

title: Den Spargel (Asparagus Preserved)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- flour or bran
- salt
- fat
- pitch

## Directions

1. During the best asparagus season, up to the end of it, select the finest asparagus spears, cut them, wash them clean, and dry them with a cloth.
2. Mix flour or bran, which must be very well dried, with some roasted salt, place a layer of this mixture in a well-bound barrel, treated on the outside with melted pitch to make it airtight (probably much higher than wide).
3. Layer asparagus over it, then spread half an inch of the mixture again, continuing in this manner by laying asparagus and spreading the mixture over it until the barrel has only 2 inches of space remaining.
4. The topmost layer must consist of the mixture; after it is firmly pressed down, pour melted fat over it and set the barrel in a dry place.
5. In large households, many small barrels of this kind are made, so that eight dishes for twelve persons can be prepared from one of them.
6. The asparagus keeps throughout the winter, and one can see for oneself that the bran or flour and fat are not lost.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Der ausgelegte Küberl- oder Bübertsalat (Arranged Chicory Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- salad, washed
- chicory
- soft boiled salsify root, peeled
- aspic jelly

## Directions

1. Wash the salad and place it on a plate
2. Take a chicory and make a neat bouquet from it, placing it in the center of the salad
3. Take a soft boiled peeled salsify root, cut it into leaf-like pieces
4. Arrange these leaves neatly around the bouquet
5. Take aspic jelly, cut it into small pieces, and arrange them around the edge of the plate, then it is finished
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Der ausgelegte Rapunzelsalat (Arranged Corn Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- corn salad, washed
- white boiled cauliflower rose
- hard boiled eggs
- soft boiled beets
- egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned corn salad on a plate
2. Set a white boiled cauliflower rose in the center
3. Take hard boiled eggs and soft boiled beets
4. Remove the yolks from the eggs and finely cut the whites and yolks of the eggs as well as the beets separately
5. Use these three components to neatly arrange the salad as desired, then it is finished
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Der brennende Pudding (The Flaming Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One seidel of good butter
- Crumbs of one and a half semmel (rolls)
- 4 loths of swollen, finely pounded almonds
- 4 loths of butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 loths of citronat, cut into large cubes
- Preserved green walnuts
- Green prunes
- Pitted morello cherries
- Almonds (of all these equal parts)
- Egg whites
- Good rum

## Directions

1. Pour one seidel of good butter over the crumbs of one and a half semmel
2. Heat on low coals with 4 loths of swollen, finely pounded almonds until it boils, then let it cool
3. Beat 4 loths of butter, 2 whole eggs, and 3 egg yolks gradually until fluffy and stir in
4. Add cut into large cubes: 3 loths of citronat, preserved green walnuts, green prunes, pitted morello cherries, and almonds in equal parts and in such quantity that little of the semmel is visible
5. Beat the whites of the 3 eggs to stiff peaks and fold in so nothing is visible
6. Take a mold with a thick post in its center, brush with butter, fill with pudding, and place in steam for one hour
7. When serving, turn it out; in the moment it is brought to the table, fill the opening with good rum, place some pieces of sugar in it, light it, and bring it flaming to the table
8. The bottom of the mold should be artfully lined with citronat cut into half moons and swollen, split almonds
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Der Essig der vier Räuber (Vinegar of the Four Robbers)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 measures of the best and purest vinegar
- rue, 1 handful
- sage, 1 handful
- mint, 1 handful
- wormwood, 1 handful
- lavender, 1 handful
- 1.5 loth of camphor

## Directions

1. Set rue, sage, mint, wormwood, and lavender (1 handful each) into 2 measures of the best and purest vinegar
2. Let the mixture stand for digestion at a warm place for 4 days, preferably in the sun
3. Let it steep and strain it, then press it out
4. Pour it into bottles
5. Add 1.5 loth of camphor to it
6. Use by rinsing the mouth with it and drinking a small sip, particularly when visiting the sick
7. Wash face and hands with it for additional protection
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Destillirtes Brotwaſſer (Distilled Bread Water)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds homemade bread
- 6 to 7 measures good spring water
- 2 pieces lemon peel about two fingers long

## Directions

1. Take 2 pounds of homemade bread and wrap it in cloth
2. Place it in a distilling flask
3. Pour 6 to 7 measures of good spring water over it
4. Add about 2 pieces of lemon peel about two fingers long
5. Distill it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Die Bereitung der Muscheln (Preparation of Mussels)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- white wine
- lemon juice
- mussels, well cleaned
- butter sauce
- 3 egg yolks
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Put some butter, white wine, and lemon juice in a casserole
2. Add well-cleaned mussels and let them steam covered until they open
3. Remove the empty half-shell
4. Pass the liquid through a sieve into a casserole
5. Make a butter sauce with this liquid and cook it well
6. Beat 3 egg yolks with some sour cream and stir into the sauce
7. Arrange the mussels on a serving dish and serve the sauce in a sauce boat
8. Must be served very hot

title: Dillenkrauts Sauce (Dill Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter or lard
- Equal amount of flour
- 4 tablespoons finely chopped dill herb
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley
- Necessary soup
- 1/4 liter sour cream

## Directions

1. In 8 tablespoons butter or lard add equal amount of flour
2. Let it roast with 4 tablespoons finely chopped dill herb and 1 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley
3. Pour with the necessary soup and 1/4 liter sour cream, simmer and let cook well

title: Dillsauce (Dill Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 bunches fresh dill
- 4 dkg fat
- 5-6 tablespoons cream
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare like sorrel sauce but add lemon juice along with the cream

title: Dobostorte (Dobos Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 12½ dkg sugar
- 4 stiff egg whites
- 12½ dkg flour
- butter and flour for pan
- 10 dkg finely crushed cube sugar for caramel

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks with sugar for 20 minutes
2. Fold in stiff egg whites and flour
3. Butter and flour a baking sheet, draw a circle from cake form
4. Spread one quarter of batter to form thin layer, bake light brown
5. Place cake form on baking sheet, cut dough layer round and loosen from sheet
6. Continue baking batter in layers this way
7. Spread filling on each layer, leaving one layer unfilled
8. Stack all but one filled layer, surround with filling, top with caramel-coated layer
9. For filling: Beat 8 dkg butter with 1 egg, mix with 8 dkg sugar and 1 dkg cocoa
10. For caramel: Melt and brown 10 dkg finely crushed cube sugar in small pot, stirring until no lumps remain, pour quickly on smooth side of prepared dough layer, smooth with buttered knife and let cool momentarily, mark individual cake portions with buttered knife and cut through when glaze is completely stiff

title: Domestic Pork Prepared in Wild Manner (Zahmes Schweinfleisch auf wilde Art zu bereiten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork from the leg, filets, and cutlets
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Cloves
- Juniper
- Bay leaves
- Basil
- Thyme
- Good wine vinegar
- Sprigs of spearmint
- Green walnut shells

## Directions

1. Cut the pork into proper pieces and place it in a marinade consisting of salt, coarse pepper, cloves, juniper, bay leaves, basil, thyme, and good wine vinegar.
2. Add several sprigs of spearmint and green walnut shells.
3. Leave it to marinate for ten or twelve days in a cold place.
4. The pork will then take on the taste and color of wild boar meat.
5. For best results, choose a not too large and not too fatty young pig. To verify its age, make a small cut in the rind; if it tears easily, it is correct.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Dorsch kalt (Cold Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Fisch (fish)
- 10 l Vögerlsalat (corn salad)
- 1 rote Rübe (beet)
- 1 Gurke (cucumber)
- 1 Zitrone (lemon)
- 2 l Kapern (capers)
- 15 dkg Butter (butter)
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Oil

## Directions

1. Scale the fish, clean it, gut it, wash it and place in milk water.
2. Bind it at the head and tail tightly and loosely in the middle, otherwise it will remain raw in the middle.
3. Bring much water with 10 spoons of salt and ¼ l vinegar to a boil in a fish pan and poach the fish for 10-15 minutes depending on size.
4. Remove from heat and let it cool first in the broth, then remove and let it cool completely.
5. Unbind the fish, place it on the platter and spray with 15 dkg well-whipped butter.
6. Arrange the corn salad seasoned with salt, vinegar and oil around it.
7. Rim the platter alternately with cooked beets sliced into leaves, cucumber and halved lemon slices and decorate the whole with capers or caviar.

title: Dreiercke (Polterstypfeln) (Fried Triangles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- same dough as for Schneebällen
- marmalade
- egg wash

## Directions

1. Prepare the same dough as for Schneebällen, roll it out knife-back thick, cut or stamp out square pieces
2. Put some marmalade on the middle of each piece, coat the edges with beaten eggs, and fold the pieces over so that they form a triangle
3. Press the edges together lightly, but in the middle the triangles must be very thin
4. Bake them under frequent shaking of the saucepan in very hot fat and sprinkle with sugar immediately

title: Dressirte Pastete (Decorated Pastry)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cold pastry dough
- pastry filling of choice
- poultry or game
- bacon slices
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Take as much cold pastry dough as needed for the lid, make it a finger's thickness
2. Work the remaining dough together like a round loaf
3. Press a hole into it with your fist, shaping a pot the size of a medium flowerpot, a finger's thickness or thinner as you prefer
4. First place the dough on a sheet of paper
5. Press the remaining dough inward with your fist on the bottom
6. Using a crimping iron, crimp a border on the outside of the bottom
7. Line the inside of the pastry with filling of choice
8. Arrange poultry or game inside, filling the spaces with farce
9. Cover everything with bacon slices
10. Brush the outer edge 2 finger-widths with egg yolk
11. Lay the lid over the pastry and press it firmly onto the brushed side
12. Cut off the excess
13. Crimp the pastry all around with the named iron
14. Make a hole in the lid the size of a seidel bottle
15. Set a small chimney on top
16. Brush the pastry with beaten eggs and bake in a not too hot oven for 3 to 4 hours slowly, depending on whether the pastry is larger or smaller
17. The pastry can also be decorated with flowers cut from the same dough, but these must first be brushed with egg
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Dreyfuß (Spiced Wine Drink)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure Pontac or Ofner wine
- 0.5 loth cinnamon
- 0.5 quintal vanilla
- 12 loth sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- cold Pontac

## Directions

1. Heat 1 measure Pontac or Ofner wine, 0.5 loth cinnamon, 0.5 quintal vanilla and 12 loth sugar on the fire until boiling
2. Beat 8 egg yolks with some cold Pontac
3. As soon as the wine boils, pour the egg mixture into it
4. Hold over the fire for a few minutes while beating constantly until it rises
5. Serve in cups
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Dried Plums in Sauce
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- dried plums
- water
- butter sauce
- red wine
- sugar
- spices
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Place dried, washed plums in a casserole
2. Pour enough water over them so it covers them and let them cook soft
3. Make a butter sauce
4. Add the broth in which the plums were cooked and some red wine
5. Add the softened plums with as much sugar as desired, spices, and finely cut lemon peel
6. Let simmer

title: Duck, Roasted (Duckente, gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- brine/marinade
- lemon peel
- butter
- flour
- duck liver
- cream
- capers

## Directions

1. Pluck the duck, singe it slightly, and remove the entrails
2. Salt it well and let it lie in brine for one day
3. Lard it with lemon peel, roast it on a spit to a golden-brown color, and baste it several times with brine and cream
4. Let some butter become hot, add some flour and let it brown, then add the finely cut liver of the duck and let it stew
5. Pour in some of the brine and the drippings from the duck during roasting, along with a couple spoonfuls of cream and some capers
6. Place the duck on a dish, pour the sauce over it, and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Dunkler eingebrauntc Erdäpfel (Darker Browned Potatoes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- mushrooms
- watercress
- cooked potatoes

## Directions

1. These are prepared like French potatoes, except the roux is allowed to brown somewhat darker and mushrooms and watercress are added

title: Durchgestrichene Frosch-Suppe (Frog Leg Coulis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 frog legs
- 8 decagrams butter
- 1 piece onion
- 4 pieces cleaned, thinly sliced champignons
- lemon juice
- 1 1/4 liters wine
- 8 hard-boiled eggs
- 14 decagrams butter
- flour
- 3 liters broth

## Directions

1. Wash the frog legs, dry them with a cloth, and remove the toes
2. Roast them in 8 decagrams of butter with onion and champignons
3. Add lemon juice and 1 1/4 liters of wine and braise gently until tender
4. Pound this mixture with 8 hard-boiled eggs very finely
5. Heat 14 decagrams of butter and add flour to make a roux
6. Roast the roux and pour on 3 liters of broth
7. Cook slowly for 1 hour, removing the fat as it rises
8. Add the braised frog legs and pass through a fine sieve into a casserole
9. Bring the soup to a boil before serving, season it, and serve with fried bread slices, rice, noodles, or other soup garnishes

title: Durchgetriebene Gurkensauce (Strained Cucumber Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Onions
- Cucumbers
- Green parsley
- Beef broth
- Vinegar
- Sour cream
- Seasonings

## Directions

1. Make a light roux from butter, flour, and onions.
2. Cut cucumbers into noodle-like pieces and add to the roux.
3. Add green parsley.
4. After this has roasted for a while, pour in beef broth, a little vinegar, and sour cream.
5. Season to taste.
6. Let cook well.
7. Pass through a sieve into the sauce boat.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Durchmarsch (Potato Noodles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 medium potatoes
- 12 hectograms hot fat
- salt
- 1/4 liter flour
- salted water

## Directions

1. After potatoes are boiled soft and peeled, crush them into smaller pieces with a wooden spoon
2. Add to 12 hectograms hot fat
3. Season to taste and stir well
4. Add noodles made from 1/4 liter flour, cut and boiled in salted water
5. Stir well together
6. When hot, place on a plate and serve immediately

title: Dutch Torte (Tourte à la Hollandaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 Deka butter
- juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Beat 30 Deka butter until fluffy
2. Add the juice of 1/2 lemon

title: Dünn geschnittenes Wildschweinfleiſch (Thinly Cut Wild Boar Meat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked or braised venison
- onions
- butter
- vinegar
- Espagnole sauce
- salt
- pepper
- bread croutons or poached eggs

## Directions

1. Cut away the black skin from the cooked or braised venison
2. Divide the meat into fillets
3. Cut a dozen onions in the middle and finely break them into layers
4. Sauté in butter until nicely browned
5. Pour vinegar and a couple of serving spoonfuls of Espagnole sauce into it
6. Add salt and pepper and let it cook briefly
7. Remove the fat from the top
8. Lay the fillets in the sauce and keep very hot until serving (do not boil)
9. When serving, garnish with croutons or poached eggs
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eau Divine (Divine Water)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 measures wine spirit
- essential lemon oil (1 quentchen)
- essential bergamot oil (1 quentchen)
- 4 ounces double orange blossom water
- sugar water (made from 8 measures water and 4 pounds sugar)

## Directions

1. Pour 4 measures wine spirit into a pitcher
2. Add 1 quentchen essential lemon oil and 1 quentchen essential bergamot oil
3. Add 4 ounces double orange blossom water
4. Prepare sugar water by dissolving 4 pounds sugar in 8 measures water
5. Add the sugar water to the mixture
6. Shake everything together well
7. Let it stand for 6 to 8 days with frequent stirring
8. Filter the liquid
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Echte Dampfnudeln (True Steamed Dumplings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka butter
- 1 tablespoon finely ground sugar
- 1 knife point salt
- 9 egg yolks, one after another
- 2 Deka yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter lukewarm milk
- 24 Deka flour
- 1/4 liter cream
- 4 Deka finely ground sugar
- 4 Deka melted butter
- Crayfish butter or ordinary butter for brushing

## Directions

1. Beat 16 Deka butter until fluffy, gradually add 1 tablespoon finely ground sugar, 1 knife point salt and 9 egg yolks one after another
2. Mix this well with 2 Deka yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter lukewarm milk and 24 Deka flour and beat the dough until it detaches from the spoon
3. Let it rise covered in a warm place until it has doubled in height
4. Place the dough on a flour-dusted cloth, roll it out strong finger-thickness and cut out round plates with a crumpet cutter
5. In a casserole brushed with crayfish butter or ordinary butter, place 1/4 liter cream, 4 Deka finely ground sugar and 4 Deka melted butter and, when this has become lukewarm, lay the round cut-out dough plates next to each other in it
6. Place the casserole in a warm place
7. When the dough has risen once more to double height, brush it with melted crayfish butter and bake it for 1/2 to 3/4 hour in the oven
8. When the dumplings are finished baking, turn out the casserole, separate the dumplings from one another, arrange them in a bowl and serve at table with custard or desired cream

title: Echte Schildkröten-Suppe (Vrai Tortue - True Turtle Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 medium-sized tortoises
- 1 hour in lukewarm water
- 20 decagrams butter
- 1 piece finely sliced Spanish onion
- sliced champignons
- 1 yellow turnip
- 1 celery root
- 1 parsley root
- 2 1/2 liters water or pea water
- 8 decagrams rice

## Directions

1. Place the tortoises on a table and hold a small, glowing coal to the back of each to make them extend their heads and feet
2. For large animals, quickly cut off the feet with a hatchet; for small ones, use a saber or tongs
3. Place the tortoises in lukewarm water for 1 hour to release the blood and impurities
4. Place them in a casserole with cold water and cook until the green color loosens from the shoulders (approximately 3/4 hour)
5. Meanwhile, in another casserole with 20 decagrams of melted butter, arrange the finely sliced Spanish onion, champignons, yellow turnip, celery, and parsley root
6. Add the tortoises with their shells and braise covered and slowly for approximately 1/3 hour until brown
7. Pour on 2 1/2 liters of water or pea water and add 8 decagrams of rice
8. Cook slowly for approximately 1 hour
9. Place the tortoises on a board, remove their shells, and cut them into desired sized pieces
10. Pass the soup through a sieve and remove the fat
11. Season with salt and spices if desired
12. Bring to a boil with the tortoise meat for approximately 10 minutes
13. Serve with fried noodles, crayfish quenelles, or fried bread slices
14. Optionally enhance with wine

title: Echten Yoghurt (True Yogurt)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter fresh milk
- Contents of one bottle of 'Vindobona' yogurt pure cultures

## Directions

1. Boil the fresh milk well and cool to 50°C
2. Pour the contents of the 'Vindobona' yogurt pure culture bottle into the milk, stir well, and fill into bottles or pots
3. Prepare a larger vessel with 55-degree water
4. Place the filled bottles into this vessel with water and cover the vessel
5. Position this vessel at a location on the stove that maintains an average temperature of approximately 40°C, best achieved by placing the larger vessel on a second smaller vessel filled with water on the warm stove
6. Let the bottles rest quietly in the warm water for approximately 3 to 3¼ hours
7. Carefully remove the bottles from the water and place them on a table without shaking them during transport, and let them cool slowly
8. Once the milk begins to thicken, bring it to a cool place to become completely thick
9. For further preparation of true yogurt, use one tablespoon of finished yogurt per liter of milk, treated as previously described

title: Edelmännischer Lungenbraten (Noble-Style Braised Beef Tenderloin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tenderloin
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- shallots
- green parsley
- beef lung pieces
- bacon strips or fat
- ham slices
- 2 small heads of cabbage
- onions
- yellow turnip
- parsley root
- celery
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 clove
- 1 liter white wine
- 1/2 liter broth

## Directions

1. Free the beef tenderloin of veins and skin as with a naturally roasted tenderloin
2. Thread it alternately with bacon strips and beef lung pieces that have been rolled with salt, pepper, crushed shallots, and finely cut green parsley
3. In a casserole, lay bacon strips or fat, optionally some ham slices, 2 small finely cut onions, some yellow turnip, parsley root and celery (one small piece of each), 1 bay leaf, 1 clove and some pepper
4. Place the meat on top and braise covered for approximately 1/2 hour
5. Once it has reduced, add 1 liter white wine and 1/2 liter broth and braise the tenderloin until completely tender
6. Remove to a board, cut into desired pieces, lay on the platter and can be garnished with prepared vegetables
7. Strain the defatted sauce, add broth or wine if necessary according to taste, let it boil somewhat and serve in a sauce boat

title: Egg Dish in Croustades (Oeufs brouilles en croustades)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Scrambled egg ragout (from recipe 1015)
- Croustades (from recipe 1122)

## Directions

1. Prepare scrambled eggs with ragout as in recipe 1015.
2. Fill croustades with the egg mixture and serve.

title: Egg Dish in Shells (Coquilles aux oeufs brouilles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Scrambled egg ragout (from recipe 1015)
- Shells
- Bread rolls, baked in butter

## Directions

1. Prepare scrambled eggs with ragout as in recipe 1015.
2. Fill warmed shells with the egg mixture.
3. Top with baked bread rolls or milk bread pieces and serve.

title: Egg Noodles in Milk (Nockerl in der Milch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 whole eggs
- flour as needed
- 1 liter milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 0.5 liter sour milk

## Directions

1. Mix 3 whole eggs with enough flour to make a thin, fluid dough
2. Slowly pour this dough into 1 liter of boiling milk under constant stirring and cook thick
3. Meanwhile, beat 2 egg yolks with 4 tablespoons sugar and 0.5 liter of sour milk
4. Pour this into the cooked dough, stir well
5. Bring once to a boil
6. Arrange in a serving dish and dust with desired preserves

title: Egg Omelette (Omelette)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- Pepper
- 10 ounces butter or lard
- Optional fillings: fried calf's brain, crawfish tails, mixed herbs, asparagus tips, truffles, cooked ham, or braised mushrooms

## Directions

1. Beat 12 whole eggs with 1/2 teaspoon salt and pepper to taste until well combined.
2. Heat 10 ounces butter or lard in a sauté pan until hot.
3. Pour the beaten eggs into the pan and bake quickly while gently moving and pushing the set portions.
4. When no liquid remains on the surface, lift the omelette from the pan into a bowl using two serving spoons.
5. If desired, sprinkle with fried, diced calf's brain, or extracted crawfish tails, or finely chopped mixed herbs, or asparagus tips cooked in salted water, or finely diced truffles, or cooked, diced ham.
6. Fold the omelette once over, arrange in a bowl, and sprinkle with the same ingredient used for filling.

title: Egg Pancake (Eyerpfanzel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 loth butter
- ten hard-boiled eggs
- a bread roll soaked in milk
- fresh parsley
- two whole eggs
- two egg yolks
- half a seidel of cream
- salt
- pounded pepper

## Directions

1. Beat 6 loth butter until fluffy, chop ten hard-boiled eggs finely together, take a bread roll soaked in milk and fresh parsley, chop everything finely and add it to the butter.
2. Stir in two whole eggs and two yolks, and add half a seidel of cream along with a little salt and pounded pepper.
3. Butter a casserole dish, add the mixture, and bake until golden.
4. When baked, turn it out onto a dish and serve.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Egg Rolls (Rolladen von Eyern)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- mushrooms, coarsely chopped
- salt
- chopped fresh parsley
- pounded pepper
- a handful of fine bread crumbs
- several spoonfuls of cream
- ten to twelve eggs
- butter for the omelet pan

## Directions

1. Let a piece of butter become warm, add coarsely chopped mushrooms, salt them, and add some chopped fresh parsley and pounded pepper.
2. Let everything stew until the sauce has reduced.
3. Add a handful of fine bread crumbs and several spoonfuls of cream, and let it stew again.
4. Beat ten to twelve eggs in a pot, salt and stir them.
5. In an omelet pan, make a little butter hot, add a small portion of the eggs, and let them spread out in the pan to form the shape of an omelet.
6. Turn it to the other side, turn it out quickly, and continue until all are cooked.
7. Fill them with the mushroom mixture, roll them together, butter a dish, place several spoonfuls of cream on it, lay the rolls in, and they are ready after a brief boil.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Egg Soufflé (Ponding d'oeufs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces flour
- 1/3 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon ground white pepper
- 1/2 liter milk
- 12 egg yolks
- 12 egg whites, beaten
- 8 ounces hot butter
- Clarified butter
- Parmesan cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Place 8 ounces flour in a deep bowl with 1/3 teaspoon salt and 1 teaspoon white pepper.
2. Gradually pour in 1/2 liter milk while stirring constantly.
3. Add 12 egg yolks one after another while stirring.
4. Gently fold in the beaten snow from 12 egg whites.
5. Pour the mixture into an egg pan containing 8 ounces hot butter.
6. Bake in the oven at moderate heat for approximately 1/8 hour until the tops begin to dry.
7. When they begin to dry, brush with clarified butter. When they turn golden, remove from the oven and serve in the egg pan at table, or divide into pieces with a serving spoon, arrange in a bowl, and serve with grated Parmesan cheese.
8. This dish must be prepared in time before serving, as it must be brought to table immediately after baking and cannot wait.

title: Eggs Colored Red (Eier roth zu färben)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 decagrams logwood shavings
- 1 piece alum
- 3 liters water
- eggs

## Directions

1. One day before, soak 24 decagrams of logwood shavings with 1 piece of alum in 3 liters of water in an earthen dish
2. Boil this for 3-4 hours, then set it aside
3. Boil it again the next day for the same length of time and repeat this for 3-4 days or even longer
4. If the liquid has reduced too much, add water as needed
5. Once the decoction has achieved a beautiful red color, place the eggs to be dyed into it (they must not have been washed beforehand) and boil them for 20 minutes
6. Then place them in cold water, remove them, lay them on a cloth or board, or on 2 wooden spoons so the liquid drains off
7. Once they are completely dry, coat them with dissolved gum or with a greased fine linen cloth
8. If one wishes to dye the eggs red with chemical dye, they are hard-boiled without being washed beforehand and then placed in fresh water
9. Pour cold water into a deep bowl, add 1 tablespoon of chemical dye, place the eggs in it and let them lie there until they have achieved a beautiful red color, light or dark as desired
10. Then let them dry as before and may coat them with gum if desired, though not with fat
11. The red dye used for this purpose can be obtained from any drugstore under the name 'Red dye for coloring eggs'
12. By this method, eggs can be dyed not only red, but any color desired

title: Eggs in Brown Butter (Oeufs au beurre noir)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces butter
- 12 eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- 8 ounces butter
- 3 tablespoons vinegar

## Directions

1. Shake 16 ounces butter in a pan over high heat until it turns dark brown.
2. Break in 12 eggs one after another, season with salt and pepper to taste.
3. Heat over the fire until the eggs are almost completely set.
4. Arrange the eggs in a bowl using two serving spoons.
5. In a separate pan, brown 8 ounces butter and add 3 tablespoons vinegar, let it come to a boil once, and pour over the arranged eggs.

title: Eggs in Shrimp Butter with Ragout
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 hard-boiled eggs
- 6 ounces crayfish butter
- 8 ounces breadcrumbs, deveined and softened in milk
- egg yolks from the hard-boiled eggs
- 2 raw yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1 spoon of cream
- salt
- pepper
- crayfish ragout
- lemon sauce or other sauce

## Directions

1. Cut off the white of 12 hard-boiled eggs at the top down to the yolk and carefully remove them.
2. In a mortar, finely pound 6 ounces crayfish butter, 8 ounces deveined breadcrumbs softened in milk, the removed egg yolks, 2 raw yolks, 1 whole egg, 1 spoon of cream, salt, and pepper; pass through a sieve.
3. Fill a desired form brushed with crayfish butter and dusted with flour with half the prepared mass, insert the eggs with their opened side facing up, fill them with crayfish ragout, and cover everything with the second half of the mass.
4. Cover this filled form, place in a hot water bath for 3/4 hour, then turn the eggs into a dish and serve with lemon sauce or other desired sauce.

title: Eggs with Ragout (Eyer mit Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of pike
- salted water
- crayfish tails
- mushrooms
- asparagus, boiled in salted water
- butter
- flour
- cream
- salted simmering water

## Directions

1. Boil a piece of pike in salted water, remove the bones, and shred it into pieces.
2. Cut crayfish tails, some mushrooms, and a little asparagus that has been boiled in salted water into small pieces.
3. Stew these together in butter and dust a little flour on top.
4. Place several spoonfuls of cream and the ragout on a dish.
5. Poach the eggs in salted simmering water only until they set together, but not until they are hard.
6. Remove the eggs with a slotted spoon and lay them on the ragout.
7. Let the ragout with the cream come to a simmer and serve on this dish.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eiche-Konsomme (Egg Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca cooked meat (leftovers)
- some roasted parsley
- 1 mushroom
- 1 bread roll softened in milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- some salt
- some nutmeg
- 1 knife point white pepper
- 1/2 liter cold soup

## Directions

1. Pound the cooked meat very finely together with roasted parsley, mushroom, and the bread roll softened in milk, and pass through a sieve
2. Add 3 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, some salt, some nutmeg, 1 knife point white pepper, and 1/2 liter cold soup
3. Mix this mixture together well
4. Fill into a form greased with butter and dusted with flour
5. Cook in a water bath for 1 hour

title: Eier a la minute (Eggs à la minute)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water
- Salt
- Pache or puree
- Goose liver
- Frog cutlet
- Cock's comb
- Bread slices in butter

## Directions

1. Put eggs with a spoon as needed into a casserole with boiling water and cook covered for 7 minutes.
2. After this time, remove them quickly, place in cold water and shell them.
3. Once done, place them again in salted, lukewarm water where they are then arranged slightly off-center in a bowl and garnished with pache or puree.
4. Or place the pache in the center of the bowl, garnish it with the eggs and place between each egg a piece of goose liver, or a frog cutlet, or a cock's comb, or bread slices roasted in butter.

title: Eier gebacken (Baked Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-6 fresh eggs
- 2 rolls
- 2 tablespoons cream
- 4 dkg butter
- chopped parsley
- 2 hard-boiled egg yolks
- salt
- 4-6 dkg breadcrumbs
- hot lard for frying
- spinach or cooked lettuce for serving

## Directions

1. Heat 4 dkg butter in a saucepan
2. Fry chopped parsley in it
3. Add milk-soaked, well-pressed rolls
4. Add 2 hard-boiled egg yolks and salt
5. Let cool
6. Fold in 2 stiff egg whites and 4-6 dkg breadcrumbs
7. Form into egg or croquette shapes on a floured board
8. Bread and fry in hot lard
9. Serve with spinach or cooked lettuce

title: Eier gefüllt (Stuffed Eggs, Cold)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-6 fresh eggs
- 3 anchovies
- 1½ rolls
- 2 tablespoons cream
- 4 dkg capers
- 6 dkg butter
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Hard boil eggs for 12 minutes, cool completely, and peel
2. Cut in half lengthwise or widthwise and remove yolks
3. Pass the yolks through a sieve with the cleaned anchovies
4. Press through the deringed milk-soaked, well-pressed rolls
5. Stir into 6 dkg butter creamed
6. Add salt, pepper, cream, and mustard
7. Fill the halved eggs with this mixture
8. Garnish with parsley, capers, or caviar
9. Alternatively, fill using a pastry bag, piping the remainder into the center of a glass dish and arrange the eggs around it

title: Eier mit Mayonnaise (Eggs with Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 fresh eggs
- 8 dkg bird lettuce or 2 heads lettuce
- 1 pickled cucumber
- 2 anchovies
- 4 dkg capers
- 2 dkg chives
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Hard boil eggs for 12 minutes, halve them, and remove yolks
2. Fill with warm or cold mayonnaise
3. Garnish with anchovy strips
4. Sprinkle with chives
5. Arrange eggs in the center of a glass dish
6. Surround with prepared green salad
7. Place chopped yolks and lemon and cucumber slices between
8. Decorate to taste with capers or caviar

title: Eier mit Schinken (Eggs with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 fresh eggs
- 10 dkg rolled ham
- salt
- hot butter or fat
- parsley for garnish
- spinach or cooked lettuce for serving

## Directions

1. Scramble the eggs with salt and mix with chopped ham
2. In a loaf mold, add some hot butter or fat
3. Fill with some of the egg mixture
4. Let cook until set, then turn out onto a plate
5. Garnish with parsley
6. Serve with spinach or cooked lettuce

title: Eier weich zu kochen (Soft-Boiled Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Take some softened salt, rub the eggs with it and wash them in cold water.
2. Place them with a spoon into boiling water, cover and cook for approximately 5 minutes.
3. Remove them and place in a napkin laid on a plate to serve.
4. Note that freshly laid eggs or eggs with thin shells require less time for soft-boiling than older eggs or those with thick shells.
5. If you use an egg cooker at table, place the eggs directly in cold water and remove after cooking 2-3 minutes.

title: Eier-Croquettes (Egg Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 teaspoon finely cut parsley
- 1 piece finely cut mushroom
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 8 hard-boiled, finely cut eggs
- 3 tablespoons cream
- Breadcrumbs in equal amount to cream
- Flour for dusting
- Eggs for dipping
- Breadcrumbs for rolling
- Hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Roast the parsley and mushroom lightly in butter
2. When cooked, mix in the 2 whole eggs, add the hard-boiled eggs, cream and breadcrumbs
3. Mix well, place on a flour-dusted board and form eggs, dumplings or small sausages from this mixture
4. Dust with flour, dip in beaten eggs, roll in breadcrumbs and fry in very hot lard
5. Serve as garnish with vegetables or sauces, or as a dish by itself

title: Eier-s Hohllitten (Egg Cream Rolls)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 tablespoons flour
- 1/4 liter cream
- 2 whole eggs
- 8 tablespoons sugar
- white wax
- muslin paper

## Directions

1. Gradually stir together 12 tablespoons flour, 1/4 liter cream, 2 whole eggs, and 8 tablespoons sugar.
2. Once well beaten, brush a metal iron with white wax, wipe it with muslin paper, and lightly brush with wax again.
3. Spread the mixture onto the iron to the thickness of a knife-back.
4. Once baked golden, while still on the stove, cut the mixture into rectangular pieces and roll them around a rounded stick or wooden spoon handle.

title: Eierglühwein (Egg Mulled Wine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 28 deciliters vanilla sugar
- juice of 1 orange
- 1 liter white or red wine

## Directions

1. In a whisk bowl, add 8 egg yolks, 28 deciliters vanilla sugar, the juice of 1 orange and 1 liter white or red wine
2. Mix well, place the whisk bowl on the fire where the mixture is beaten with the whisk until it is foamy
3. Serve warm in glasses

title: Eierpunsch (Egg Punch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter cold water
- 40 drachms sugar
- peel of 1/4 orange
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 1.5 liters Rhine wine
- juice of 3 oranges
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1/4 liter rum or brandy

## Directions

1. In a pot, dissolve 40 drachms sugar in 1/2 liter cold water
2. Add the peel of 1/4 orange, 2 whole eggs and 4 egg yolks, whipping them in
3. In a snow bowl, place 1.5 liters Rhine wine, pour in the whisked mixture, place it on the fire and beat with a whisk until the mixture is hot, but it must not boil
4. After adding the juice of 3 oranges, 2 lemons and 1/4 liter rum or brandy, strain the punch through a fine sieve, beat it again hot and serve it either immediately or cold

title: Eierteig (Egg Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 liters flour
- 1/2 liter milk
- 8 Deca melted butter
- Necessary spice
- Egg whites from 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Put 1 1/2 liters flour into a deep bowl.
2. Pour 1/2 liter of milk gradually while stirring constantly.
3. Mix with 8 Deca melted butter and necessary spice.
4. Lightly fold in the whipped egg white from 4 eggs.

title: Ein Anderer Baumkuchen (Another Tree Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds sugar
- 1.5 pounds butter
- 3 pounds fine flour
- 1.5 seidelan sweet cream
- 20 eggs
- half a lot cinnamon and cloves

## Directions

1. Stir the sugar and butter together as in the previous mixture
2. Add the flour gradually
3. Add the cream
4. Add the eggs and spices
5. Wrap a conical piece of beech wood (called the tree) 1.5 feet long in two sheets of white paper, wrapping it tightly with twine
6. Attach the wrapped wood to a spit and wedge it firmly with wood at the fire
7. Make the fire from small wood so it can be controlled
8. Have someone turn the spit
9. When the paper around the tree is golden yellow, pour some of the batter onto a lid or platter onto the tree, keeping it turning
10. Place a roasting pan underneath to catch dripping batter and return it to the mixture
11. Apply the first layer the thickness of a small finger; if it sets well, it will form small points that grow larger
12. When golden yellow over charcoal fire with constant turning, apply the second layer the same way as the first
13. Continue until all the batter is used, always turning not too quickly
14. Finally let the cake dry for a while over a dying fire
15. When the fire has completely died, let it stand on the wood for another half hour to dry completely
16. Trim it even with the tree at both ends and remove it
17. Tap the spit against the wall and the cake will release from the tree with twine and paper together
18. Note: the tree must be firmly wedged to the spit so it does not slip; hold a napkin gently underneath with both hands when tapping to catch the cake, but do not be fearful
19. If it sticks anywhere, reach in from the top, loosen the twine and pull it out completely; the paper will come off with it
20. Such a cake keeps well and does not become stale quickly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ein gezierter Salat mit gefärbten Eyern (An Decorated Salad with Colored Eggs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Salad (fresh or head lettuce)
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Red beet vinegar
- Blue cabbage
- Water
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Choose salad as desired, fresh or head lettuce, and clean it finely
2. Hard-boil as many eggs as needed
3. Chop the egg whites separately into fine pieces
4. Make three kinds of colors: red from red beet vinegar, blue by cooking a head of blue cabbage in vinegar then straining and cooling it, and green by cooking the same blue cabbage in water instead of vinegar, then straining and cooling
5. Divide the egg white into four parts
6. Place one quarter in each of the three colored liquids and let them steep for a couple of hours until colored
7. Leave the remaining quarter part white
8. Chop the egg yolks separately
9. Spread the chopped yolks over the salad and then sprinkle the four colored egg white pieces in a pleasing and neat arrangement
10. Dress the salad only when it is to be used
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ein grünes Ragout mit einem rothen Reif auf der Schüſſel (A Green Ragout with a Red Ring on the Platter)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound fresh butter
- crayfish butter
- four small rolls
- milk
- four egg yolks
- one whole egg
- salt
- nutmeg
- crayfish tails, finely cut
- stewed veal or lamb meat, very finely cut
- parsley sauce

## Directions

1. Make a beautiful crayfish butter from a quarter pound fresh butter
2. Peel the rind from four small rolls and soak them in milk
3. When soft, squeeze them out and blend with the crayfish butter
4. Stir in four egg yolks and one whole egg
5. Add proper salt and nutmeg
6. Mix in finely cut crayfish tails
7. Butter a platter and form a ring from this mixture as in the previous recipe
8. In the middle of the ring place stewed veal or lamb meat in very small pieces in parsley sauce
9. When well cooked, place it in the middle of the ring
10. Brush the ring again with crayfish butter
11. Place it for half an hour in a moderately hot oven
12. Before serving, remove the skin from the top of the stew and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ein Hospod von gelben Rüben und Pasternack (A Hotpot of Yellow Carrots and Parsnips)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- large yellow carrots
- parsnips
- meat broth
- butter or fat
- flour
- French forcemeat made from pork and bacon, or bratwurst
- smoked tongue or ham

## Directions

1. Take large, fine yellow carrots and an equal amount of parsnips, scrape them clean, and cut each kind lengthwise into thin slices.
2. Put them in good meat broth and cook until soft.
3. Strain off the broth and cut more pieces of yellow carrot into small, long pieces like trimmed noodles.
4. Braise these in butter or fat until very soft, dust with a little flour, and add good meat broth or some of the broth in which these roots were cooked.
5. Make a French forcemeat, preferably from pork and bacon. If you don't have this, take good bratwurst, squeeze it out, make it softer with good broth.
6. Take a casserole dish as large as needed, grease it well with fat, line the inside and bottom with the cooked roots, arranging them so that a yellow carrot alternates with a parsnip root, so that a narrow and a wide part meet each other each time.
7. Then it is coated around with this forcemeat finger-thick in layers: each time a layer of the small braised yellow carrots, and again a layer of finely cut smoked tongue or ham, then again a forcemeat, and continue this way until the casserole is full.
8. Cover it again with these roots, and let it bake in a heated oven for one hour.
9. When serving, turn it out onto a platter so it comes out nicely. Strain off the fat and serve it to the table.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ein kaltes Boeuf à la Mode (Cold Beef à la Mode)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- A large piece of lean beef
- Wine vinegar
- Water
- Onions
- Aromatic herbs
- 4 veal or beef feet, finely chopped (if the meat weighs 18 to 20 pounds)

## Directions

1. Lard and season a large piece of lean beef
2. Place it in a deep pot or braising vessel
3. Cover the meat halfway with wine vinegar and water
4. Add onions and aromatic herbs
5. If the meat weighs 18 to 20 pounds, add 4 veal or beef feet finely chopped
6. Cover well and braise slowly in an oven, or place in a baking oven overnight until tender
7. Remove the meat and strain the sauce
8. Clarify the sauce and let it aspic
9. Slice the meat and chop the aspic
10. Pour the aspic over the meat and serve cold for supper
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ein mageres Stück Rindfleisch mit Weichsel- oder Himbeer-Glace (Lean Beef with Sour Cherry or Raspberry Glace)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- A lean piece of beef from the top round
- Sugar
- Fat
- Onion, cut
- Glace or beef broth
- Raspberry or sour cherry juice

## Directions

1. Take a lean piece of beef from the top round, cook it properly soft
2. In a casserole, brown a piece of sugar with fat, add a handful of cut onion which must become quite soft in the sugar
3. Place the beef in it, which uncovered must become beautifully brown on all sides
4. Then add a little glace or beef broth and let it reduce properly
5. Before serving, add a couple of spoonfuls of raspberry or sour cherry juice, let this reduce very quickly while it must be turned often, and ensure that it gets a beautiful glace on both sides
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ein Ragout oder Eingemachtes mit einem weißen Reif auf der Schüſſel (A Ragout or Stew with a White Rice Ring on the Platter)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound rice
- good meat broth
- three egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg
- butter
- stewed veal or chicken
- crayfish sauce
- crayfish butter
- flour
- crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Cook rice in good meat broth until very thick
2. Stir in egg yolks while the rice is still warm
3. Add salt and nutmeg
4. Butter a flat platter and form a round rice ring on it
5. In the middle of the ring place stewed veal or chicken in crayfish sauce
6. Make a butter sauce from crayfish butter and flour
7. Add the meat or chicken with crayfish tails to the sauce
8. Put the stew with sauce in the ring
9. Place it in a hot oven or tube for a quarter hour and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ein Rindfleisc in der Braise (Beef in the Braise)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a beautiful piece of beef from the rib or shell, cooked soft
- good beef fat
- three to four large split onions
- yellow turnips
- parsnip
- celery, cut
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Take a beautiful piece of beef from the rib or shell and cook it soft.
2. In a casserole, take good beef fat.
3. Add three to four large split onions, some yellow turnips, parsnip, and celery, cut, to the fat and let them braise.
4. Before serving, add the meat and a little beef broth to the braised vegetables.
5. When plating, place the meat on the plate and arrange the vegetables with the little sauce that surrounds them around it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ein rothes Eingemachtes oder Ragout, mit einem grünen Reif auf der Schüſſel (A Red Stew or Ragout with a Green Ring on the Platter)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- plenty of spinach juice, clarified
- two small rolls, peeled
- milk
- about one pound fine veal forcemeat
- a couple of eggs
- a handful of finely cut green parsley
- butter
- stewed chicken
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Take plenty of clarified spinach juice and soak two small peeled rolls in milk
2. When soft, take about one pound fine veal forcemeat and stir in the rolls with a couple of eggs
3. Add a handful of finely cut green parsley
4. Stir in the made spinach juice until the forcemeat is beautifully green
5. Form a ring from this mixture on a buttered platter, as before
6. In the middle of the ring place a stewed or braised chicken in tomato sauce
7. Leave this for half an hour in a warm oven
8. You can place a buttered sheet of paper on the ring and stew so the color does not fade too much from the heat
9. Remove the paper and serve the ragout
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ein weißes Ragout oder Eingemachtes, mit einem gelben Reif auf der Schüſſel (A White Ragout or Stew with a Yellow Ring on the Platter)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- six to eight pieces of beautiful dark yellow turnips
- good meat broth
- butter
- bread crumbs
- four egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg
- stewed veal or lamb meat
- white butter sauce
- carrots or cauliflower

## Directions

1. Clean and wash six to eight pieces of beautiful dark yellow turnips
2. Cook them in good meat broth until completely soft
3. Strain the broth and press the yellow turnips through a sieve
4. Take the pressed turnips and blend them with butter and a handful of bread crumbs
5. Mix in four egg yolks, a little salt and nutmeg
6. Form a ring from this mixture on a buttered platter
7. In the middle place a stewed veal or lamb meat ragout with white butter sauce
8. You can also add some carrots or cauliflower
9. The stew can also be made with chicken
10. Leave it for half an hour in a warm oven or tube and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ein ſehr erfriſchender ſäuerlicher Trank (A Very Refreshing Acidic Drink)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 loth peeled oats
- half a measure water
- 2 loth citric acid and mulberry juice prepared with sugar
- 2 quentchen cinnamon water

## Directions

1. Take 1 loth of peeled oats
2. Cook in half a measure of water for half an hour
3. Strain through a sieve
4. Add 2 loth each of citric acid and mulberry juice prepared with sugar, and 2 quentchen cinnamon water
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Einbrennsuppe (Roux Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- finely cut onions
- several cloves
- a little caraway
- a piece of lard
- 3 spoonfuls of flour
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a very dark brown roux from a piece of lard and 3 spoonfuls of flour
2. Add finely cut onions, several cloves and a little caraway
3. When everything is very brown, pour in as much boiling water as needed
4. Salt it and let it cook well until quite thick
5. Before serving, strain it through a sieve and serve over small diced bread or house bread in a bowl
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eine andere Kraftsuppe (Another Strength Broth)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two measures fresh water
- Two pounds lean beef
- One pound mutton or mutton feet
- One calf bone
- One old pigeon
- Two yellow turnips
- Two parsnip roots
- Two parsley roots
- Two celery stalks
- Leek
- One beautiful whole onion
- Several whole cloves
- A couple cloves garlic
- One bay leaf
- Salt

## Directions

1. Set a pot with two measures fresh water, two pounds lean beef, one pound mutton or mutton feet, a calf bone, and an old pigeon over the fire
2. When it has formed scum, add two yellow turnips, two parsnip roots, two parsley roots, two celery stalks, leek, and one beautiful whole onion
3. Let it simmer over low heat
4. Insert several whole cloves
5. Add a couple cloves garlic and one bay leaf
6. Cover the pot with a lid that fits well and seal it with plain dough
7. Let the soup simmer for three hours
8. Strain off the broth
9. Give it twice a day to drink for moistening
10. Salt as desired
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eine Erdäpfelsuppe (A Potato Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 or 12 medium potatoes, peeled
- much fat
- handful of onions
- parsley
- 2 spoons of fine flour
- beef broth or strong broth
- baked bread rolls cut into strips

## Directions

1. Slice 10 or 12 medium peeled potatoes into fine slices.
2. Put a handful of onions, parsley, and the potatoes into a casserole with much fat.
3. Let them braise together well, dust with 2 spoons of fine flour, and after it has stood for a while, fill it with good beef broth or better strong broth.
4. Add some baked bread roll strips and let them cook well together.
5. Before serving, pass it through a sieve and serve over baked diced rolls.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eine falsche Maurachensuppe (A False Morel Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- raw chicken livers
- chicken gizzards
- parsley
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- salt
- nutmeg blossom
- lard for frying

## Directions

1. Chop some raw chicken livers and gizzards together finely with parsley.
2. Mix them with a couple of eggs, add bread crumbs, some salt, and nutmeg blossom.
3. Form small morals from this mixture.
4. Dip each one in beaten eggs and sprinkle with bread crumbs.
5. Fry them in not too hot lard until the end.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eine falsche Maurachensuppe (False Morel Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- raw chicken livers
- chicken stomachs
- parsley
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- nutmeg flower

## Directions

1. Chop the raw chicken livers and stomachs finely together with parsley
2. Mix with a couple of eggs, add breadcrumbs, some salt and nutmeg flower
3. Form small morels from this mixture
4. Dip each in beaten eggs and coat with breadcrumbs
5. Fry in not too sweet lard until golden
6. Place the fried morels in soup made from ordinary broth or consommé
7. Let it simmer once more and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eine grillirte Rindsbrust mit Erdäpfeln (Grilled Beef Brisket with Potatoes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- A fatty piece of beef (brisket) of desired size
- Butter
- Salt
- Bread crumbs
- Potatoes, peeled and round
- Fat from the braising liquid

## Directions

1. Cook a fatty piece of beef (brisket) of desired size soft in a good braise
2. Coat it well with butter, salt it, and sprinkle with bread crumbs
3. Roast it on both sides on the grill
4. In the braise of this brisket cook uniformly round, peeled potatoes until soft
5. Add the fat found in the braise, arrange on the serving dish with the brisket on top, and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eine kalte Paſtete mit Wild- oder Kalbsfarce (A Cold Pastry with Game or Veal Forcemeat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- water
- flour
- eggs
- salt
- venison or hare
- bacon
- anchovies
- pepper
- shallots
- onions
- lemon peel
- red wine
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil half a pound of butter in half a measure of water
2. Make a dough from 4 pounds of flour with this mixture and 2 to 3 eggs on a board and salt it
3. The dough must be as firm as possible, so all the water may not be necessary depending on flour quality
4. When the dough is like bread dough, i.e., very fine, wrap in cloth and let it rest
5. Meanwhile make a fine forcemeat from the flesh of a venison loin or freshly shot hare, a quarter pound of bacon, the same amount of cleaned anchovies, pepper, shallots, onions, and lemon peel
6. The forcemeat must be well strained, add a glass of red wine and 4 to 5 egg yolks
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eine russische Suppe (A Russian Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- white cabbage
- cabbage, 6 to 7 pieces each cleaned
- 4 pounds beef from the breast
- 1 old hen
- 5 measures water
- cabbage heads, finely chopped
- butter
- flour
- 2 pounds raw lean ham, diced
- salt
- pepper
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Split white cabbage and cabbage, 6 to 7 clean pieces of each
