2. Set 4 pounds beef from the breast and an old hen in 5 measures of water over the fire
3. Let it simmer with cabbage for a couple of hours
4. Braise some finely chopped cabbage heads in a piece of butter with a couple of spoonfuls of fine flour
5. Fill the soup with broth from the meat
6. Add the meat, finely chopped, along with the disjointed hen and the cooked cabbage
7. Cut 2 pounds of raw lean ham into small pieces and add to it
8. Let everything cook briefly together
9. Salt and pepper it
10. Before serving, a few spoonfuls of sour cream can be added
11. Serve with meat and ham at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eine sehr schmackhafte, hart geräucherte Leberwurst (A Very Tasty, Heavily Smoked Liver Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh calf liver, one third
- Pork liver, one third
- Grated white bread soaked in meat broth, one third
- Salt and spices to taste
- Fine herbs
- Fresh bacon cut into fine dice, about half the amount of the mass

## Directions

1. Clean and finely chop the livers
2. Mix all ingredients well together
3. Season and spice well with spices and fine herbs
4. Add about half the amount of fresh bacon cut into fine dice
5. Mix everything well together
6. Fill this mass into well-cleaned intestines
7. Bind them well
8. Lay them in hot water to set
9. Remove them and let them drain
10. Hang them in smoke until they become quite hard
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eine trockene Essigmutter, zu mancherley Gebrauch (A Dry Vinegar Mother for Various Uses)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- wine stone (tartar)
- good wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the wine stone until the water runs clear
2. Dry and powder it
3. Evaporate good wine vinegar over the powder by pouring it on and evaporating it 7 to 8 times repeatedly
4. Store the resulting highly sour powder in glass containers for travel
5. To use: dissolve a few ounces of the powder in a beer glass with wine or water, and it will become sour like vinegar
6. To restore vinegar that is spoiling: add the wine stone powder 4 to 5 times to it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eine verdünnende, erweichende und kühlende Tifane (A Thinning, Softening and Cooling Tisane)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 loth crushed white poppy seed
- 4 quentchen oats
- 12 quentchen crushed raw kitchen peas
- 7 quentchen each borage and marshmallow leaves
- 4 loth scorzonera root
- 1.5 measures water
- 2 loth each currants and elderberry pulp

## Directions

1. Take 2 loth crushed white poppy seed, 4 quentchen oats, 12 quentchen crushed raw kitchen peas, 7 quentchen each of borage and marshmallow leaves, and 4 loth scorzonera root
2. Cook together for one hour in 1.5 measures of water
3. Strain through a sieve
4. Add 2 loth each of currants and elderberry pulp
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Einen Gemſenſchlaͤgel gut zu bereiten (Preparing Chamois Leg Well)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- chamois leg
- salt
- bacon for larding
- half a seidel of Tokayer wine
- half a seidel of red Ofner wine
- vinegar
- cloves
- various herbs
- Spanish onion
- cream
- flour
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Wash the leg well, skin it, salt it, and let it lie in salt for an hour
2. Lard it
3. Prepare the marinade by simmering together the Tokayer wine, red Ofner wine, vinegar, cloves, and various herbs
4. Pour the hot marinade over the leg and let it lie well covered for several days, adding a Spanish onion to the marinade
5. To use, roast the leg
6. Add a little cream and flour to the marinade, let it simmer a little, and strain it over the leg
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Einen Karpfen gebacken (Fried Carp)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- salt
- flour
- water
- breadcrumbs
- fat for frying
- parsley

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the carp and cut it into pieces
2. Salt the pieces well and let them sit in salt for a while
3. Turn each piece in flour, dip it quickly in fresh water, and coat it again well with fine breadcrumbs
4. When all pieces are prepared, fry them golden brown in hot fat (which must always be very hot; if not hot enough they will be greasy and not crisp)
5. Serve the fish warm, sprinkled with green parsley
6. Fish of all kinds are prepared for frying in this manner
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Einfache Brot-Tifane (Simple Bread Tisane)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 loth ordinary white bread
- half a measure water

## Directions

1. Take 16 loth of ordinary white bread
2. Cook it with half a measure of water in a well-closed earthen pot for one hour
3. Add back as much water as was lost through cooking
4. Strain through a sieve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Einfache Limonade (Simple Lemonade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter fresh water
- juice of 3 lemons
- 20 deciliters sugar

## Directions

1. In 1/2 liter fresh water, add the juice of 3 lemons and 20 deciliters sugar
2. When the sugar is dissolved, add 1/4 liter fresh water
3. Mix and serve the lemonade

title: Eingebrannte getrocknete Fißolen (Haricots secs liés)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- garlic (optional)
- 8 ounces fat

## Directions

1. Finely cut onion and if desired some garlic and roast in a deep roux made from 8 ounces of fat

title: Eingebrannte grüne Fißolen (Haricots verts liés)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- green kidney beans
- salt water
- 12 ounces butter
- 12 ounces flour
- green parsley
- onion
- garlic (optional)
- soup stock
- vinegar
- sorrel (optional)
- 1/2 liter cream

## Directions

1. Remove the strings from the green kidney beans, break them in two and cook in salt water
2. Make a roux from 12 ounces of butter and in it roast some finely chopped parsley, onion, and if desired, a little garlic
3. Add 12 ounces of flour and roast
4. Stir in soup stock and some vinegar and let the roux cook well
5. Add the cooked kidney beans with some finely chopped sorrel
6. Pour in 1/2 liter of cream, stir well together and let it cook
7. Garlic and onion can be omitted

title: Eingebrannte Kartoffeln (Browned Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- 4 dkg fat
- 1 onion
- broth or water
- 1 spoonful vinegar
- salt
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Wash, cook, and peel the potatoes, quarter them
2. Make an onion roux with 4 dkg fat and pour it up with broth or water
3. Add the potatoes, 1 spoonful vinegar, salt and some marjoram
4. Cook well for 15 minutes

title: Eingelegenenes Polonaise (Quenelles à la polonaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka butter, beaten
- 8 whole eggs
- 20 Deka peeled, diced, milk-soaked breadcrumbs, well pressed
- 8 Deka diced marrow
- 1/2 liter cooked green peas
- 1 cooked, small, diced yellow turnip
- 1 cooked, diced red turnip
- 1 hard-boiled egg, diced
- 16 Deka finely chopped, roasted meat
- 8 pieces cooked and halved chicken combs
- 1 1/8 liter broth
- some salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Add the eggs gradually to the beaten butter
2. Add the breadcrumbs, marrow, peas, yellow and red turnips, hard-boiled egg, and meat
3. Add chicken combs if available
4. Add broth, salt and pepper
5. Mix everything well together
6. Cook in a napkin in salted water like the preceding Grünbund
7. On fast days, use naturally roasted fish meat from pike, shad, turbot, carp or coalfish instead of meat; cooked stockfish flesh can also be used
8. On fast days, sprinkle with Parmesan cheese
9. On meat days, garnish with small cut vegetables, roasted goose liver, and small bread pieces roasted in butter
10. Place a cooked chicken comb, roasted goose liver, or sausage piece between two toasted bread crusts on top in the middle
11. Serve quite hot with the soup

title: Eingelegte saueres Sauertraut (Viennese-Style Sauerkraut)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large pot of sauerkraut
- about 2 liters cold water
- 10 tablespoons fat
- 1 head of onion, finely cut
- about 10 tablespoons flour
- 1 ladle full of white beef broth
- vinegar or white wine and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cover 1 large pot of sauerkraut with approximately 2 liters of cold water, place on heat and cook very gently
2. In 10 tablespoons of hot fat, roast 1 finely cut head of onion slightly, but it must not turn brown
3. Add approximately 10 tablespoons flour to it, roast under frequent stirring until it has a yellow color
4. Pour the roux onto the broth with 1 ladle full of white beef broth under constant stirring
5. Strain the sauerkraut and add it to the roux, adding as much of the sauerkraut water as needed, stir together and cook well
6. If it should be too little sour, add some vinegar or white wine and salt
7. The sauerkraut must have a pleasantly mild flavor

title: Eingemachter Kalbskopf (Braised Veal Head)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole veal head with skin
- Veal brains
- Farce (fine ground raw meat)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Sauce

## Directions

1. Blanch the whole veal head with its skin
2. Cook it in a braised preparation
3. Remove and lay out; cut the skin lengthwise and remove the brains
4. Prepare a well-seasoned farce and mix in the brains
5. Stuff the veal head and ears with this farce
6. Season with salt and pepper
7. Coat in melted butter, beaten eggs, and fine bread crumbs
8. Bake in a hot oven until golden yellow
9. Serve with a piquant sauce
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eingemachtes Kalbfleiſch mit Bröſelknöderln (Stewed Veal with Breadcrumb Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-70 dkg boned veal shoulder or front rib
- salt
- water
- 6 dkg butter
- veal broth
- lemon juice
- 1 small head of cauliflower
- 2 mushrooms

## Directions

1. Wash the veal and place in cold salted water
2. Cook for 1 hour and remove
3. Slice the meat
4. Make a parsley roux with 6 dkg butter and pour veal broth over it
5. Add the meat and cook well
6. Sour with lemon juice
7. Add the cauliflower and mushrooms cut into leaves, which were cooked separately in salted water
8. Do not stir much
9. Serve on a round platter with the dumplings

title: Eingerührte Eiersoetfe in Schmalt (Scrambled Eggs in Lard)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 whole eggs
- 4 tablespoons milk or cream
- Salt
- Pepper
- 12 deca grams very hot lard or butter

## Directions

1. Whisk 12 whole eggs with 4 tablespoons milk or cream, the necessary salt and some pepper.
2. Pour into a pan or egg dish casserole in which 12 deca grams very hot lard or butter is contained and treat further as for Scrambled Eggs with Bacon.

title: Eingerührte Eiersoetfe mit Speck (Scrambled Eggs with Bacon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca grams bacon, finely diced
- 12 whole eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chives, finely cut

## Directions

1. Dice 12 deca grams bacon finely, put it in a skillet or egg dish pan and roast until it looks glassy.
2. Meanwhile, whisk 12 whole eggs well, season to taste with pepper and add to the roasted bacon and cook with constant stirring and lifting of the eggs as they begin to set.
3. They are then arranged as desired firm or more liquid with a spoon in a warm bowl and sprinkled with finely cut chives.
4. It can also be served in the egg dish pan, which is placed on a bowl.

title: Eingespritzte Suppe (Piped Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca flour
- 3 whole eggs
- 2 liters beef broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Put 8 deca flour in a pot, add 3 whole eggs and stir into a smooth thick dough
2. Let it run into 2 liters of boiling beef broth through a small funnel
3. If the dough is too thick, add 1 or more eggs; if too thin, add more flour
4. When the surface of the soup is covered with the piped dough, remove it with a wooden spoon and add it to the soup pot containing some soup
5. Continue this process until all the dough is used
6. Pour any remaining soup needed into the soup pot

title: Einlaufsuppe (Drop-In Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-70 dkg black scherzl (beef)
- 1 egg
- 1 spoon flour
- 1 pinch salt

## Directions

1. In a small pot, stir together 1 egg, 1 spoon flour, and 1 pinch salt very smoothly for 15 minutes.
2. Let this mixture drip from high above into the strained boiling beef broth and cook for 5 minutes.

title: Eischokolaum in Eis-Kaffee-Form (Chocolate Pudding in Ice Mold)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca vanilla sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 12 deca flour
- 6 egg whites
- grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Make a biscuit mass from 16 deca vanilla sugar, 6 egg yolks, 12 deca flour and the snow from 6 egg whites
2. Spread this on two baking sheets knife-edge thick
3. Dust one of them heavily with grated chocolate
4. When both are baked, remove from the sheet and let stand in a cool place for several hours to soften and be cut better
5. Cut a star shape from the brown biscuit mass and use it to cover the bottom of the corresponding smooth mold with the brown side
6. Cut pieces from the other biscuit to fill the empty spaces in the star, ensuring the yellow side faces the mold so it contrasts with the black star
7. Cut strips of desired width and cut equal-sized triangles from these, the smaller the more elegant
8. Line the walls of the mold with these so that the edges of a yellow triangle always connect to those of a black one
9. Place the lined casserole in ice and fill with whipped bitter almond cream or any cream with isinglass or gelatin
10. Let stand 1 hour covered in ice
11. Press slightly on the walls with fingers and turn out
12. If necessary, carefully loosen the biscuit mass from the walls with a thin knife and tap the mold lightly when turning out
13. Instead of baked biscuit mass, biscotti can also be used

title: Eiskipferl (Iced Crescents)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 egg whites
- 25 dkg sugar
- 25 dkg softened, finely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Beat 7 egg whites with 25 dkg sugar over steam until thick
2. Spread softened, finely chopped almonds on a pastry board
3. Take small pieces of the beaten mixture, roll them in almonds
4. Form into small crescents
5. Place on buttered sheet
6. Bake at low heat until light brown

title: Eisschaum-Kuchen mit Marillen oder Pfirsichen (Ice Foam Cake with Apricots or Peaches)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 deca caster sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites
- 24 deca flour
- halved and stoned apricots or peaches

## Directions

1. Beat 30 deca caster sugar with 10 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
2. Lightly mix in the stiff peaks of 10 egg whites with 24 deca flour
3. Fill into a baking pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
4. Add halved and stoned apricots or peaches, arranged so that 3 and 3 pieces come one upon the other
5. Bake at low heat for 1 1/2 to 3/4 hours
6. Treat as with the Brown Cherry or Morello Cherry Cake (No. 1460)

title: Eisscheiben (Iced Wafers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 19 dkg flour
- 17 dkg butter
- 7 dkg sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 egg whites
- 18-20 dkg sugar for icing
- Jam for filling

## Directions

1. On the board, make a crumbly dough from flour, butter, sugar, and egg yolks
2. Let it rest
3. Roll out and cut rounds or rings
4. Place on unbuttered sheet
5. Bake at medium-high heat until light brown
6. Brush with icing made from 2 egg whites beaten with 18-20 dkg sugar
7. Place back on sheet and let icing dry in open oven
8. Brush one side with jam and sandwich together

title: Elderberry Juice (Altermoodsaft)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe elderberries
- Powdered sugar (1/2 kilo sugar per 1/4 liter juice)
- Juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Remove the elderberries from their stems, crush them, and strain the juice through a felt sack
2. Add powdered sugar, namely 1/2 kilo sugar per 1/4 liter of juice
3. Dissolve the sugar and boil it with the juice of 2 lemons
4. When the liquid begins to become thick and has cooled, fill it into glasses or small jars, cork them, bind with bladders, and store in a cool place

title: Eleganter Indianer (Turkey à la Danbe)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned and trussed turkey
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lard (pencil-thick and half-finger-long)
- Lard
- Root vegetables
- Onion
- 1 bay leaf
- 3 sprigs thyme
- 1/4 liter white wine
- Broth

## Directions

1. Rub the inside and outside of the turkey with salt and pepper.
2. Truss it like pheasant (recipe 735) and lard the breast with pencil-thick, half-finger-long strips of lard.
3. Line a saucepan with lard, root vegetables, onion, 1 bay leaf, and 3 sprigs thyme.
4. Place the turkey with the larded side up on top and braise it covered.
5. After approximately 1/4 hour, pour 1/4 liter white wine and some broth over it.
6. Cover the turkey with double-layered, buttered paper and braise it slowly for approximately 2 hours.
7. After this time, set the turkey aside, let it rest in the braising liquid for a while, then remove it, remove the lard and twine, cut it into desired pieces, place them in a bowl, and serve with the strained, defatted, and strained braising liquid.
8. The turkey can be served with any salted starches.

title: Endive as Vegetable (Chicorée à l'étuvée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Endive (Chicorée)
- Salt water
- Cold water
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth or cream
- Salt

## Directions

1. Finely clean the endive, wash it, place it in boiling salt water, and cook it uncovered until tender; place in cold water, press it, and chop it finely, then place in a casserole.
2. Blend a piece of butter with some flour, add this to the endive, season with salt, and pour broth or cream over it, or pour a butter sauce over it and let it boil.
3. Serve this with Calf Cutlets, fricandeau, etc.

title: Endivienſalat in einer Butterſauce (Endive Salad in Butter Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful yellow endive
- Salt water
- Butter
- Milk cream, 4 to 5 spoons
- Fine bread crumbs
- Veal carbonades

## Directions

1. Remove the green leaves from beautiful yellow endive, wash it (divided into 4 parts), rinsing several times
2. Cook it in salt water so it does not become too soft, then drain
3. Coat a dish with butter, spread 4 to 5 spoons of cream over it
4. Arrange a layer of salad neatly over it, then more cream and butter, then salad again, etc., until full
5. Top with cream and several butter pieces, sprinkle with fine bread crumbs
6. Apply heat above and below and let it cook well
7. Garnish with veal carbonades
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Englische Bratwürste (English Bratwurst)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound young pork meat, fat or lean
- one pound veal meat
- one pound kidney fat
- half pound bread rolls
- milk
- six fresh sage leaves
- lemon zest
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- sausage herbs
- Madeira or Rhine wine (about one cup)

## Directions

1. Scrape skin and nerves from all the meat
2. Chop finely together with the milk-soaked bread rolls, sage leaves, lemon zest, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and sausage herbs
3. If desired, work in about one cup Madeira or Rhine wine for fine flavor
4. Wet the bread board with fresh water, roll out the mass, and form bratwursts of equal length
5. Grill them, lay on paper brushed with butter on a roasting rack, and let them take color, or fry them in a pan with some butter
6. Alternatively, fill the sausage forcemeat into pork caul, mix in diced truffles that have been passed through minced bacon and finely chopped onions, form equal crepinettes, and grill on both sides on a roasting rack
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Englische Creme (English Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter white wine
- 12 egg yolks
- 32 deca sugar
- 4 deca dissolved warm isinglass
- 1/2 liter water
- 1/8 liter reduction
- 2 deca gelatin
- 1/4 liter water
- 1/2 liter whipping cream
- drops of alkermes juice

## Directions

1. Place 1/4 liter white wine, 12 egg yolks and 32 deca sugar in a snow bucket
2. Put on the fire and beat until the mass begins to rise
3. Remove from fire and continue beating until cold
4. Stir in 4 deca dissolved warm isinglass (prepared with 1/2 liter water and reduced to 1/8 liter) or 2 deca gelatin dissolved in 1/4 liter water
5. When this is full but not cold, fold in 1/2 liter cream whipped to foam on the ice
6. Fill into a desired mold and proceed as with Whipped Bitter Almond Cream (No. 1713)
7. If desired, color the cream pink with a few drops of alkermes juice

title: Englische Torte (English Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1 pound ground sugar
- 15 eggs
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Discard half of the egg white
2. Whip the eggs in a clean pot for half an hour until foaming
3. Add the sugar
4. Add the flour and finely sifted vanilla
5. Beat the dough until it begins to thicken
6. Brush some forms with melted butter
7. Fill them only half full
8. Bake
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Englischen Pudding mit einer Weinsoße (English Pudding with Wine Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 rolls (Mundsemel)
- cream or milk for soaking
- 1/2 pound butter
- 12 egg yolks
- 2 pounds bone marrow, diced
- 1/2 pound crushed sugar
- 2 pounds finely cut almonds
- 2 pounds beautiful raisins
- zest of 1 lemon
- egg whites (from the eggs used)

## Directions

1. Remove the crusts from 2 rolls, cut them into pieces, and soak them in cream or milk.
2. Beat 1/2 pound butter with 12 egg yolks into a foam, then add 2 pounds bone marrow diced into cubes, 1/2 pound crushed sugar, 2 pounds finely cut almonds, the well-squeezed bread, and 2 pounds beautiful raisins gradually.
3. Add the rubbed zest sugar from a couple of lemons, then slowly fold in the egg white beaten to snow.
4. Proceed with this mass as described above, but it must cook for two hours.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Englischer Plums-Pudding (English Plum Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca butter, beaten
- 20 deca vanilla sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 28 deca finely diced rolls soaked slightly in milk
- 14 deca raisins
- 3 tablespoons fine rum
- 14 deca finely diced suet
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 16 deca butter with 20 deca vanilla sugar until mixed
2. Beat in 12 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
3. Add 28 deca finely diced rolls soaked in milk, 14 deca raisins, 3 tablespoons fine rum, and 14 deca finely diced suet
4. Mix well
5. Lightly fold in the snow from 10 egg whites
6. Fill a buttered and floured mold with a cylinder in the center with this mixture
7. Steam covered for 3/4 to 1 hour
8. Turn out onto a platter
9. Fill the center cavity with 10 to 12 deca finely pounded sugar and pour 1/16 liter rum over it
10. Light on the table
11. Serve immediately with desired fruit juice or Chaudeau

title: Englischer Punch (English Punch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1 liter coffee
- thin peel of 2 sweet oranges and 2 lemons (without white pith)
- 1/4 liter rum
- 1/4 liter Rhine wine
- 1/4 liter Burgundy wine
- juice of 4 oranges and 4 lemons

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, add sugar, coffee, and the thin peels of 2 sweet oranges and 2 lemons, without any white pith remaining
2. Let it boil for approximately 2 minutes
3. Add 1/4 liter rum, 1/4 liter Rhine wine, and 1/4 liter Burgundy wine
4. Stir in the juice of 4 oranges and 4 lemons
5. Let it stand covered in a warm place
6. Serve hot in a soup pot or bowl

title: Englischer Punsch (English Punch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo sugar
- 1 liter water
- very thinly cut peel of 2 sweet oranges
- very thinly cut peel of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, place 1 kilo sugar, 1 liter water and the very thinly cut peel of 2 sweet oranges and 2 lemons

title: Englischer Rum-Pudding (English Rum Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilo suet, skinned
- 25 deca flour
- 1/4 kilo large raisins
- 1/4 kilo small raisins
- 1/4 kilo currants
- 26 deca finely chopped citron
- 15 deca sugar
- 6 whole eggs
- 6 tablespoons rum or cognac or desired liqueur

## Directions

1. Finely chop 1/2 kilo skinned suet and 25 deca flour together
2. Beat this mixture for 1/3 hour until light
3. Add 1/4 kilo large raisins, 1/4 kilo small raisins, 1/4 kilo currants, 26 deca very finely chopped citron, 15 deca sugar, 6 whole eggs, and 6 tablespoons rum or cognac or desired liqueur
4. Mix everything well together
5. Set the dough in a cool place to become slightly firmer
6. Bind into a buttered napkin
7. Cook for 1 hour
8. Serve with Chaudeau
9. Can also be cut cold and keeps for several weeks

title: Englisches Roastbeef (English Roast Beef)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- A good piece of beef from the rib (rib piece), aged 6 to 8 days and at least 3 kilos
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bacon slices
- Potatoes for garnish

## Directions

1. Remove the sinews from the beef, pound it, and wipe it dry with a cloth without washing it. Do not damage the fat on top; remove the tendons from the thick upper side.
2. Rub the meat thoroughly with pepper and salt and let it rest for 1 hour.
3. Line a flat pan with bacon slices, place the meat on it, and roast it at the highest possible heat for 1 hour.
4. After this time, remove it from the oven where it should have formed a crust. Cut it into fairly thin slices and serve garnished with potatoes.
5. The roast should show a beautiful rose color inside when cut. Note that the meat must be basted very frequently with its own juices during roasting.
6. For a very thick piece or meat from a strong animal, roast for about 1.5 hours; for a thinner piece or meat from a young animal, roast for only 0.75 to 1 hour.
7. Serve cold and it is excellent, particularly suitable for tea.

title: Englisches Roastbeef mit den Seiten gebraten (English Roast Beef with the Sides Roasted)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- A well-aged piece of beef from the rib, 3 kilos, with the backbone removed but ribs left on
- Salt
- Pepper
- Tendons and top fat (3 finger widths from the thicker side) removed

## Directions

1. Prepare the meat as described in the previous recipe, removing the backbone but keeping the ribs, and removing the tendons and top fat (3 finger widths from the thicker side only, not from the thinner side).
2. Pound the meat, salt it, sprinkle with some pepper, and proceed as described in the previous recipe.

title: English Beefsteaks (Beefsteaks à l'anglaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-aged, skinned tenderloin roast
- Salt and pepper
- Oil or butter
- Flour
- Fat for frying
- Herb butter, maitre d'hotel butter, or mustard butter
- Lemon slices
- Fried eggs (optional)
- Onions
- Salt and vinegar cucumbers
- Braised cabbage

## Directions

1. Cut two-finger-thick slices from a well-aged, skinned tenderloin roast.
2. Beat these only lightly, press them back together so they have the proper thickness and rounding.
3. Sprinkle them with salt and pepper, drizzle some drops of oil or butter on them, and let them rest in this state for about an hour.
4. Heat fat in a pan until very hot.
5. Dip the beefsteaks very lightly in flour on both sides and fry them on each side 3 minutes, thus 6 minutes total at high heat.
6. They must still be red and bloody when cut.
7. Arrange on a dish and place some herb butter, anchovy butter, or maitre d'hotel butter in the middle of each piece, and add lemon slices.
8. Beefsteaks can be covered with fried eggs when serving; furthermore garnish them with onions, from which you strip the skin with a knife and scrape the onion with it, also with salt and vinegar cucumbers, braised cabbage and the like.
9. Note particularly that the fat for the beefsteaks must be very clean and as hot as possible.
10. You can also fry the beefsteaks without dipping them in flour, but by doing so they will be less juicy, since the flour forms a thick crust which prevents the juice from escaping quickly from the meat.
11. Once the beefsteaks are arranged on the dish, skim the fat from the sauce, add some soup or dissolved aspic to it, and pour this boiled into a sauce boat.

title: Engliſches Filet (English Fillet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg tenderloin
- 10 dkg bacon
- salt
- pepper
- very hot fat
- 3 dkg butter
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Remove all bones, fat, and skin from the meat.
2. Salt and pepper it and lard it.
3. Place it in a pan with very hot fat, skin side down.
4. Sear on the stovetop, then place in a hot oven.
5. Roast for 30-45 minutes at high heat, basting frequently.
6. When the top is brown, turn it over.
7. Transfer to a plate and place in an open oven.
8. Make a sauce by pouring off the fat, browning 3 dkg butter in the pan, and adding a little broth or water. Bring to a boil and serve separately.
9. Slice the meat very thin (it should be pink inside) and arrange on a long platter with garnishes.

title: Ente mit Teig, am Spieße gebraten (Duck with Dough, Roasted on the Spit)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One young fat duck
- Salt
- Pepper
- Dry fine herbs
- Good poultry forcemeat
- Butter
- Bacon slices
- Thin pâte brisée
- Buttered paper
- Sauce pikante

## Directions

1. Clean and gut a young fat duck, remove the breastbone and thigh bones, rub with salt, pepper, and dry fine herbs
2. Fill it with good poultry forcemeat, sew it up again, and dress as for roasting
3. Let butter melt in a casserole and pass the duck in it, also bind bacon slices around it, and wrap it entirely in a thinly rolled out pâte brisée
4. Around this wrap buttered paper and tie it with string
5. Roast the duck at moderate fire on the spit, and when roasted, remove the paper, dress it on the plate, and serve sauce pikante underneath
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Epaule d'Agneau Farcie (Stuffed Lamb's Shoulder)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- veal forcemeat
- twine
- salt water
- bacon lardons, pencil-thick and half-finger long

## Directions

1. When the shoulder bone has been removed and the resulting cavity filled with veal forcemeat, tie the shoulder with twine (i.e., sew it together), place in cold water, set to the fire and let it come to a boil.
2. Remove the meat, place it again in cold water, dry with a cloth, salt it, lard it with pencil-thick, half-finger long pieces of bacon and further treat as the glazed lamb leg.

title: Erbsen-Reis Pilau (Pea and Rice Pilau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter steamed or stewed peas
- 16 deka steamed rice (Pilau)

## Directions

1. Mix 1/4 liter steamed or stewed peas well with 16 deka steamed rice
2. Arrange in a bowl and serve

title: Erbsenbruhe zu Fastenspeisen und Fastensuppen zu verwenden (Pea Broth for Fasting Dishes and Fasting Soups)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one seidel of dried well-sorted peas
- 3 halves of pure river water
- celery
- parsley
- yellow turnip roots
- some baked bread slices
- some whole pepper and mace

## Directions

1. Fill a pot with the peas and water and let it cook
2. Add celery, parsley, yellow turnip roots, some baked bread slices, some whole pepper and mace
3. Let it simmer slowly, then strain the broth and use it as needed
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erbsensuppe (Pea Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Seidel large, clean peas
- 3 Seidel soft water
- Butter
- Flour
- Green parsley
- Salt
- Flour dumplings

## Directions

1. Put cleaned peas with soft water on the fire and cook until very soft
2. Let a piece of butter become hot, add a good spoon of flour so it turns golden yellow
3. Add green parsley and let it simmer briefly
4. Pour the broth with the peas over it
5. Let it cook down a little, then push through a sieve
6. Return to the pot, salt, and serve over carved flour dumplings
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erbsensuppe (Pea Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter peas
- water
- 10 deca butter
- flour
- diced onions
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook 1/2 liter peas in water until soft and pass through a sieve
2. Make a light roux with 10 deca butter and flour
3. Roast finely chopped onions in the roux
4. Pour in 2 liters water and add the passed peas with salt
5. Stir well, let simmer, and pass through a sieve

title: Erdaepfel-Croquettes (Potato Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 tablespoons butter
- 1.5 liters riced potatoes
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy and mix well with the riced potatoes
2. Gradually add 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
3. Season with salt, nutmeg and finely cut lemon zest
4. Place this dough on a flour-dusted board and form small sausages 1 finger long and 2 fingers thick
5. Fry these in very hot lard
6. These croquettes are served as garnish for roasted beef or boiled beef
7. Dumplings can also be formed from this mixture

title: Erdbeer-, Himbeere-, Ribisels, Ananaseis (Strawberry, Raspberry, Currant, Pineapple Glaze)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- finely sifted, unscented sugar
- about 6 tablespoons of strained juice from fresh strawberries, raspberries, currants or pineapples

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same way as the Orange Glaze (No. 1560), only that finely sifted, unscented sugar is used instead of sugar and about 6 tablespoons of strained juice of fresh strawberries, raspberries, currants or pineapples is used instead of orange juice.

title: Erdbeer-Gefrornees mit Kern und Obersgefrornees (Strawberry Frozen Dessert with Core and Cream Frozen Dessert)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 deca sugar
- 8 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Stir 20 deca sugar with 8 egg yolks well together in a snow-beater

title: Erdbeer-Krapfen (Strawberry Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram fresh strawberries
- 24 tablespoons finely powdered sugar
- Wafers moistened with water
- Wine batter
- Lard for frying
- Powdered sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Mix 1/2 kilogram fresh strawberries well with 24 tablespoons finely powdered sugar.
2. Fill 1 teaspoon full of this mixture into wafers moistened with water, dip in wine batter and fry quickly until golden yellow in very hot lard.
3. Remove with a skimming spoon, place on paper towels for a few moments, arrange heaped in a dish, sprinkle with finely powdered sugar and serve immediately.

title: Erdbeer-Sulz (Strawberry Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 decagrams sugar
- 1 liter water
- 1/4 kilo fresh strawberries
- cochineal coloring or alkermes juice
- 1 lemon peel
- 7 decagrams isinglass dissolved and heated
- 2 lemons
- white wine
- optional: 1/8 kilo white and red currants instead of strawberries

## Directions

1. Clarify 40 decagrams sugar in 1 liter water (No. 1542).
2. Crush 1/4 kilo fresh strawberries and pour one-third of the hot, clarified sugar over them, cover well and let stand overnight.
3. The next day, dissolve cochineal coloring or a few drops of alkermes juice in the remaining clarified sugar while hot, add lemon peel, cover and pour hot over the strawberries.
4. Stir and strain through cloth, then add hot to 7 decagrams of dissolved, boiling isinglass (Orange Jelly, No. 1660).
5. Strain the jelly mass through fine linen, add juice from 2 lemons and enough white wine to make the total mass 1 liter.
6. Proceed as with Orange Jelly.
7. Alternatively, use 1/8 kilo strawberries mixed with 1/8 kilo white and red currants instead of 1/4 kilo strawberries, omitting cochineal coloring and lemon juice.
8. Note: juice and sugar together should only total 1/2 liter and isinglass should be 1/2 liter, totaling 1 liter together.

title: Erdbeerbombe (Strawberry Bombe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 35 dkg strawberries
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Himbeerbombe.

title: Erdbeereis (Strawberry Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dl unsweetened strawberry pulp
- 2 lemons
- 2 dl spun sugar (approximately 18 dkg cube sugar cooked with 1 dl water for 15 minutes)
- juice of 1½ lemons
- 2 dl cold water

## Directions

1. Mix the strawberry pulp with 2 dl spun sugar, the juice of 1½ lemons and 2 dl cold water, and fill into a tall, narrow container.
2. Insert the sugar scale and it should read 18 degrees.
3. If it sinks too deep, add more sugar; if it doesn't sink deep enough, add cold water until the scale shows the correct degree.
4. The solution must not have fewer than 18 degrees, but also not more, as the mass will otherwise not freeze.
5. Strain through a hair sieve and freeze (see Vanilla Ice Cream).
6. Alternatively, 25 dkg wild strawberries can be sieved, measured and prepared in the same way.

title: Erdbeeren-Gefrorenes mit Eiern und Obere (Strawberry Ice Cream with Eggs and Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 deca sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 liter good, unboiled cream

## Directions

1. Stir 20 deca sugar with 8 egg yolks in a saucepan well
2. Gradually add 1 liter good, unboiled cream and heat over the fire, beating until it begins to thicken, without boiling
3. After the mixture has been passed through a fine hair sieve, let it cool while continuously beating with the snow whisk and treat it further as described in Vanilla Ice (Recipe 1720)
4. When it is frozen solid and worked well, mix 1/2 liter sieved strawberries with it

title: Erdbeeren-Gelee (Strawberry Jelly)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound deer antler gelatin
- 1 Maß water
- 3 half-measures ripe wild strawberries
- a little water
- 8 egg whites
- 1/2 pound sugar
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1/2 bottle sweet wine

## Directions

1. Cook 3/4 pound deer antler gelatin with 1 Maß water in the known manner, so that it becomes even thicker than for rose jelly
2. Cook 3 half-measures of ripe wild strawberries with a little water over gentle heat, then pass them through a hair sieve without pressing them through
3. Pour this juice to the strained deer antler gelatin
4. Beat 8 egg whites to stiff peaks and fold them in
5. Bring the mixture to a boil over heat and set it aside
6. Add 1/2 pound sugar, the juice of 2 lemons, and 1/2 bottle sweet wine
7. Pass through a cloth as usual
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdbeeren-Liqueur (Strawberry Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure strawberries
- half measure good cherry spirit or plum brandy
- 4 loth linden blossom water
- 4 loth rose water
- 4 loth sloe blossom water
- 4 loth hawthorn water
- 4 loth black cherry water
- 8 loth cinnamon water
- 1 loth cinnamon
- 2 quentchen cloves
- 14 pounds Canary sugar

## Directions

1. Take 1 measure strawberries and half measure cherry spirit or plum brandy
2. Pour both into a glass flask
3. Place in a cool location for 8 days
4. Shake well once daily
5. Press through a cloth
6. To 1 measure of pressed juice, add 4 loth each of linden blossom water, rose water, sloe blossom water, and hawthorn water
7. Add 4 loth black cherry water and 8 loth cinnamon water
8. Add 1 loth cinnamon and 2 quentchen cloves
9. Add 14 pounds Canary sugar
10. Place the glass in the sun for 8 days
11. Shake well daily
12. Filter the prepared liqueur and fill into bottles
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdbeeren-Suppe (Strawberry Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping plate of strawberries
- 1 1/2 liters wine
- 1/2 liter wine
- 1 liter water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- baked bread slices

## Directions

1. Let 1 heaping plate of strawberries braise with 1 1/2 liters wine until the strawberries fall apart, then pass through a sieve
2. Pour 1/2 liter wine and 1 liter water onto it, add sugar and cinnamon, stir together well
3. Serve with baked bread slices in the soup pot

title: Erdbeerensaft zum Gefrorenen (Strawberry Syrup for Frozen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Freshly picked wild strawberries
- 1 kilo strained strawberries
- 1 kilo finely pounded sugar
- Almond oil (optional)

## Directions

1. Freshly picked wild strawberries are passed through a fine sieve, then weighed
2. Then in an earthen or porcelain bowl 1 kilo strained strawberries are stirred with 1 kilo finely pounded sugar for 3 hours
3. Then filled into bottles, topped according to desire with some almond oil
4. Fitted with a suitable stopper and sealed
5. The bottles are then kept at a cool, breezy place, where however they must not lie, but always stand
6. This juice is used not only for frozen desserts, but also for creams, whipped cream and sauces

title: Erdbeerenwaßer (Strawberry Water)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 2 measures or 4 pounds ripe wild strawberries
- 1/2 measure or 1 pound raspberries
- juice of 1 lemon
- 2 measures water
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Take approximately 2 measures or 4 pounds ripe wild strawberries and 1/2 measure or 1 pound raspberries
2. Place them in an earthenware vessel
3. Crush them
4. Add the juice of 1 lemon
5. Pour 2 measures water over it
6. Let it stand for half an hour
7. Press it through a napkin
8. Sweeten to taste
9. Pass it through a hair sieve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdbeerenwaſſer (Strawberry Water)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 2 measures or 4 pounds of ripe wild strawberries
- 1 half measure or 1 pound of raspberries
- juice of one lemon
- 2 measures of water

## Directions

1. Place the strawberries and raspberries in an earthenware vessel
2. Crush them
3. Add the juice of one lemon
4. Pour 2 measures of water over them
5. Let it stand for half an hour
6. Press it through a napkin
7. Sweeten to taste
8. Pass it through a hair sieve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdmuire (Grieß) Knödel (Earthed Semolina Quenelles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter semolina
- 1/8 liter roll crumbs roasted in 1 1/2 Loffel lard or finely diced bacon
- 1/2 teaspoon finely cut parsley roasted in 10 Loffel lard
- salt
- 3/4 liter boiling water
- parsley bunch for cooking

## Directions

1. Mix under 1 liter semolina 1/8 liter roll crumbs that have been roasted in 1 1/2 Loffel lard or finely diced bacon
2. Pour heated 1/2 teaspoon finely cut parsley roasted in 10 Loffel lard over the semolina
3. Add salt and stir well
4. Pour 3/4 liter boiling water over it and stir until it forms a firm dough
5. Form knödel of desired size and cook in salted water with a bunch of parsley, but not too many in one pot

title: Erdäpfel anderer Art zum Garnieren (Potatoes Another Way for Garnish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon
- potatoes
- salt
- paprika

## Directions

1. Brown bacon slightly, use it to sauté the softly cooked, sliced potatoes
2. Salt them and sprinkle with paprika

title: Erdäpfel auf eine andere Art (Potatoes Prepared Another Way)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Boiling water
- Salt
- Butter or beef fat

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes raw and cut them into pieces the size of a thumb.
2. Wash them thoroughly clean.
3. Pour boiling water over them in a suitable pot; salt them properly and add a piece of butter or beef fat, then let them cook slowly until soft.
4. Before they reach the proper point of doneness, melt a piece of butter or fat until the foam begins to fall away.
5. Quickly strain the potatoes, pour them into a bowl or casserole, and finish them with this butter.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdäpfel mit Dillenkraut (Potatoes with Dill)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- cream
- cooked potatoes
- 1 strong tablespoon finely chopped dill

## Directions

1. The preparation is like potatoes with ham, except instead of ham add 1 strong tablespoon finely chopped dill

title: Erdäpfel mit Kapern (Potatoes with Capers)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- cream
- cooked potatoes
- 1 strong tablespoon finely chopped capers
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare them like potatoes with ham, except instead of ham add 1 strong tablespoon finely chopped capers and parsley

title: Erdäpfel mit Majoransauce (Potatoes with Marjoram Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- green parsley
- onion
- marjoram
- soup
- cooked potatoes

## Directions

1. These are prepared like French potatoes, except the flour browned in butter receives fresh parsley, onion and marjoram and soup instead of cream

title: Erdäpfel mit Petersilie (Potatoes with Parsley)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- cream
- cooked potatoes
- 1 strong tablespoon finely chopped fresh parsley

## Directions

1. The preparation is the same as for potatoes with ham, except instead of ham add 1 strong tablespoon finely chopped fresh parsley

title: Erdäpfel mit Roastbraten (Potatoes with Roast Beef)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds roast beef, bones removed, beaten and salted
- Fat
- 4 handfuls onion, finely cut
- Milk cream
- Butter or beef lard
- Boiled potatoes, cut lengthwise
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Let the 2 pounds of roast beef stand for 1/4 hour
2. Braise in a pan with a little fat and 4 handfuls of finely cut onion until soft, turning the meat frequently
3. Cut the meat into equal oblong pieces
4. Return to the pan with 1/4 measures of good milk cream and braise a little longer
5. Coat a platter with butter or beef lard and spread milk cream on it
6. Layer boiled, lengthwise-cut potatoes, salt them
7. Add a layer of braised roast beef with braised onions, and continue layering until all is layered
8. Top with milk cream and a few pieces of butter
9. Sprinkle with bread crumbs, apply heat from above and below, and let it bake a nice golden color
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdäpfel mit Sardellen (Pommes de terre aux anchois)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- cream
- cooked potatoes
- 2 tablespoons finely chopped anchovies
- 1/2 tablespoon finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as potatoes with ham, but instead of ham use 2 tablespoons finely chopped anchovies and 1/2 tablespoon finely chopped parsley

title: Erdäpfel mit Schinken (Pommes de terre au jambon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams butter
- 4 decagrams flour
- 1/2 liter cream
- cooked potatoes
- 6 tablespoons finely chopped ham
- 5 hard-boiled eggs, diced (optional)

## Directions

1. Brown the butter and flour together
2. Pour on cream with constant stirring
3. Add one ladle of cooked, diced potatoes and 6 tablespoons finely chopped ham
4. Stir well together, bring to a boil and serve
5. If desired, also serve with 5 hard-boiled, diced eggs

title: Erdäpfel Pudding (Potato Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 teaspoons beaten butter
- 12 teaspoons lemon sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 24 teaspoons cooked, passed, but cold potatoes
- 12 teaspoons peeled almonds, finely ground with 2 egg whites
- 6 egg whites
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan

## Directions

1. When 14 teaspoons of beaten butter are mixed little by little with 12 teaspoons of lemon sugar and 8 egg yolks, add 24 teaspoons of cooked, passed, but cold potatoes with 12 teaspoons of peeled almonds, finely ground with 2 egg whites, mix well together and finally lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
2. Fill this mass into a butter-greased, flour-dusted form and cook covered in a water bath for 1/2 hour, then turn into a bowl and serve with cream

title: Erdäpfel zu kochen (Boiled Potatoes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-washed potatoes of equal size
- Cold water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place well-washed potatoes of equal size with their skins in a pot with cold water, which should not completely cover the potatoes.
2. If they are somewhat large, pour in cold water as soon as they begin to cook, and continue to do so repeatedly until they are cooked through after about half an hour or three-quarters of an hour.
3. Drain the water and set the pot back over the fire until all moisture has evaporated.
4. Add some salt during cooking.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdäpfel-Knödel (Potato Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca beaten butter
- 50 deca cooked potatoes
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 1/16 liter milk
- equal amount of flour
- some salt
- 1 teaspoon finely cut green parsley

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until creamy.
2. Stir in the cooked potatoes that have been hot-sieved, egg yolks, whole eggs, milk, flour, salt, and green parsley.
3. Let stand covered for 1/2 hour.
4. Form knödel from the mixture.
5. Cook them in boiling salted water for 1/4 hour.

title: Erdäpfel-Knödel, gebraten (Fried Potato Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca beaten butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- some salt
- a knife point of finely cut green parsley
- medium-sized fried potatoes

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until creamy.
2. Stir in whole eggs, egg yolks, salt, and green parsley.
3. Remove the marrow from medium-sized fried potatoes and push it through a sieve.
4. Add to the beaten butter and mix well together.
5. About 1 hour before mealtime, shape small knödel from the potato mixture and fry them in lard.
6. Place them on blotting paper and add them to the soup.

title: Erdäpfel-Pastete mit Karotten und Unverfälschten (Potato Pastry with Carrots and Crayfish Tails)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 24 decagrams boiled, strained potatoes
- 8 decagrams debarked rolls, softened in milk
- Salt to taste
- Egg white, beaten stiff
- Puff pastry, finger-thick
- Hard-boiled eggs, sliced leaflike
- Truffles, cleaned and cut into fine dice
- Crayfish tails, dissolved
- Crayfish shells
- 10 decagrams crayfish butter
- 10 decagrams flour
- 1/2 pint milk cream
- Salt and lemon juice to taste
- Beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Cream 16 decagrams of butter until fluffy, add 4 egg yolks one after another and mix well with 24 decagrams of boiled, strained potatoes, 8 decagrams of debarked rolls softened in milk and squeezed out well, and the necessary salt; lastly fold in the beaten egg white lightly.
2. Line a buttered form with flour, dusted with puff pastry finger-thick on the bottom.
3. Spread the bottom with half of the forcemeat, then layer with hard-boiled eggs cut into leaflike slices, cleaned truffles cut into fine dice, and dissolved crayfish tails, which are covered with the second half of the forcemeat and this covered with a finger-thick puff pastry plate fitted to the size of the form.
4. Make several cuts with a knife in this pastry cover, brush with beaten eggs, and bake the pastry at fairly strong heat in the oven.
5. During this time prepare 10 decagrams of crayfish butter from crayfish shells, add it to a saucepan, let it melt, roast 10 decagrams of flour in it, which you pour 1/2 pint of milk cream over, season with necessary salt and some lemon juice, and let cook thoroughly while stirring constantly.
6. When the pastry is baked, remove it from the oven, turn it out onto a dish, carefully cut the cover all around and remove it, pour the sauce described above into the pastry, cover again with the lid, and serve.
7. The sauce can also be served separately in a sauce boat if desired.

title: Erdäpfel-Polenta mit Käse (Potato Polenta with Cheese)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deka butter, beaten
- 4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deka butter gradually with 4 egg yolks
2. Continue as described in the recipe

title: Erdäpfel-Puree von fleurierten Erdäpfeln
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Erdäpfel
- 8 Deca Butter
- 1/4 Liter Ober

## Directions

1. die Erdäpfel werden in der Röhre gebraten
2. die Häulen werden weggeschnitten
3. die heißen Erdäpfel werden passiert
4. weitere Zubereitung wie oben angegeben

title: Erdäpfel-Salat mit Zwiebel (Potato Salad with Onion)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Erdäpfel (potatoes)
- Salz
- Öl
- Essig
- rohe Zwiebel

## Directions

1. Nachdem die Erdäpfel wie beim vorhergehenden Salat gekocht, geschält, fein blätterig geschnitten und gesalzen sind, werden sie mit dem nötigen Öl, Essig und fein nudetig geschnittener roher Zwiebel gut untereinandergemischt.

title: Erdäpfel-Souffle (Potato Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 tablespoons butter
- 24 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 50 tablespoons cooked, pressed, cooled potatoes
- 8 egg whites
- finely sifted sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Beat 16 tablespoons butter until fluffy
2. Gradually beat in 24 tablespoons powdered sugar and 8 egg yolks one after another
3. Mix in 50 tablespoons cooked, pressed, cooled potatoes
4. Lightly fold in the beaten egg whites from 8 eggs
5. Fill the mixture into a form brushed with butter and dusted with flour
6. Cover and steam for 3/4 hours or bake in the oven
7. Turn the soufflé out onto a platter, sprinkle with finely sifted sugar, and serve
8. Optionally, 12 tablespoons finely sifted and pressed hazelnuts or 12 tablespoons blanched almonds finely sifted with 1 egg white can be mixed in with the potatoes

title: Erdäpfel-Strudeln (Potato Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deka butter
- 2 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs
- 32 Deka hot mashed potatoes, preferably fried
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped parsley
- salt
- 1/8 liter milk cream
- 2 eggs for snow
- 1/8 liter flour for dough
- 2 tablespoons semolina

## Directions

1. Whip 12 Deka butter until fluffy
2. Stir in 2 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs
3. Add 32 Deka hot mashed potatoes, parsley, salt, and 1/8 liter milk cream
4. Fold in snow from 2 eggs
5. Spread on stretched strudel dough made from 1/8 liter flour
6. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons semolina over it
7. Treat as previous strudels

title: Erdäpfelbrot (Potato Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 dkg flour
- a pinch of salt
- 2 spoonfuls sugar
- ½ kg cooked, passed, still warm potato
- 1 egg
- yeast leaven from 2 dkg yeast
- some spoonfuls milk
- 10 dkg butter, cut into pieces
- 5 dkg currants
- egg yolk for brushing

## Directions

1. Warm the flour in a mixing bowl, add a pinch of salt, 2 spoonfuls sugar, ½ kg cooked, passed, still warm potato, 1 egg, the yeast leaven from 2 dkg yeast, and some spoonfuls milk
2. Beat the dough until it releases from the spoon
3. On the board, knead it with 10 dkg cut butter until the butter is completely mixed in
4. Knead in 5 dkg currants
5. Divide the dough into 2 parts
6. Form each part into loaves and place on a greased baking sheet near the oven to rise
7. Brush with beaten egg yolk
8. Bake in a moderately hot oven until brown
9. The dough must be firm

title: Erdäpfelbrot (Potato Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 dkg flour
- salt
- 2 spoonfuls sugar
- 1/2 kg cooked, passed, still warm potatoes
- 1 egg
- 2 dkg yeast
- milk
- 10 dkg butter, cut into pieces
- 5 dkg raisins

## Directions

1. Warm the flour in a bowl, add a pinch of salt, 2 spoonfuls sugar, 1/2 kg cooked, passed, still warm potatoes, 1 egg, the yeast and several spoonfuls of milk, and beat the dough until it releases from the spoon
2. On a board, knead it with 10 dkg cut-up butter until the butter is completely mixed
3. Knead in 5 dkg raisins and divide the dough into 2 parts
4. Shape each part into loaves, place them on a greased baking sheet near the stove to rise, brush with beaten egg yolk

title: Erdäpfelknödel (Potato Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- good portion of starchy potatoes
- half as much butter as potatoes
- 6 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- finely chopped parsley
- 3 egg whites
- bouillon
- sauce bechamel
- clarified butter
- toasted white bread
- good meat broth

## Directions

1. Wash a good portion of starchy potatoes, cook them, and pass them through a wire sieve while still warm after peeling
2. Put them in a grindstone and add half as much butter as potatoes, 6 egg yolks gradually worked in, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and finely chopped parsley
3. Work the whites of 3 eggs well underneath
4. Form dumplings from this, cook them in bouillon, and serve them with béchamel sauce, clarified butter, toasted white bread, and good meat broth
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdäpfelknödel auf andere Art (Potato Dumplings Another Way)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound grated cooked potatoes
- 1/2 pound bacon cut in cubes
- 1/2 pound white bread
- some finely chopped onions
- 6 eggs
- 3 good spoonfuls cream
- salt
- nutmeg
- some spoonfuls fine flour
- meat broth
- salt water
- grated bread rolls toasted golden in butter
- brown butter
- smoked pork meat
- dried fruit

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes, peel them, and grate on a grater
2. For 1 pound grated potatoes, roast 1/2 pound bacon cut into cubes, the same amount of white bread, along with some finely chopped onions
3. Stir the potatoes with 6 eggs and 3 good spoonfuls cream with salt and nutmeg until smooth
4. Add the roasted bread and bacon, and some spoonfuls of fine flour
5. Form round dumplings from this and cook them in meat broth and salt water
6. Do not arrange these until just before they are to be served, then serve with grated bread rolls toasted golden in butter and brown butter
7. Commonly smoked pork meat and dried fruit are also served with them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdäpfelknödel aus Schmalz gebacken (Fried Potato Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 3 whole eggs
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 spoonfuls orange blossom water
- 3 spoonfuls good cream
- 4 loth sugar
- some nutmeg blossom
- 3/4 pound grated potatoes
- lard for frying
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Stir 1/4 pound butter with 3 whole eggs and 5 yolks to a foam
2. Add 3 spoonfuls orange blossom water and the same amount of good cream, along with 4 loth sugar and some nutmeg blossom
3. Stir in 3/4 pound grated potatoes and add more so the mass becomes stiff enough to make small round dumplings
4. Fry them golden in lard and serve with a cream sauce
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdäpfelkoch (Potato Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 4 quarter-pounds sugar
- 5 loth potatoes
- zest of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Beat 1/4 pound butter in a bowl until fluffy
2. Beat in 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks, well beaten
3. Stir in 4 quarter-pounds sugar, 5 loth potatoes, and zest of 1/2 lemon
4. Pour into a buttered casserole
5. Bake slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdäpfelnudeln (Potato Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg potatoes
- 1 egg
- salt
- 3 dkg butter
- 2 spoonfuls cream
- flour

## Directions

1. Make a dough from potatoes, egg, salt, butter, cream, and flour (see potato dough)
2. Form into a roll
3. Cut into equal-sized pieces and shape into short thick noodles
4. Place in a casserole with boiling salted water
5. Cook slowly for 4-5 minutes
6. Carefully transfer to a platter
7. Pour over with breadcrumbs toasted in fat and serve

title: Erdäpfelpolenta mit Käse (Potato Polenta with Cheese)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 decagrams butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 pinch nutmeg
- 30 decagrams cooked or fried and mashed potatoes
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Gradually mix 14 decagrams butter with 4 egg yolks, 1 tablespoon salt, a pinch of nutmeg and 30 decagrams cooked or fried and mashed potatoes
2. Lightly fold in the snow from 4 egg whites
3. Fill a buttered form lined with breadcrumbs with the potato mixture
4. Cook covered in steam for 3/4 hour
5. Remove the form and turn out the pudding
6. Cut into desired pieces and arrange on a dish
7. Sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese, pour hot butter over it
8. Serve with beef, tenderloin, game or cold roast

title: Erdäpfels Salat mit Sardellen (Potato Salad with Anchovies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Erdäpfel (potatoes)
- Salz
- Essig
- Öl
- 2 hartgekochte Eier
- 4 geputzte Sardellen
- Rocamboie (optional)
- weißer Pfeffer (optional)
- Hartig und Heringe (optional)
- französischer Senf (optional)

## Directions

1. Die ausgewaschenen Erdäpfel werden mit etwa Salz gekocht, geschält, fein blätterig geschnitten, in eine tiefe Schüssel gegeben, und, so lange die Erdäpfel noch warm sind, gesalzen, mit etwa gutem Essig bespritzt und einige Zeit stehen gelassen. Unterdessen püriert man 2 hartgekochte Eierdotter in eine tiefe Schüssel und rührt dies mit 4 Esslöffel Öl und etwa Essig auf. Wenn die Erdäpfel vollkommen erkaltet sind, gibt man 4 Stück geputzte, fein würfelig geschnittene Sardellen mit den abgerührten Eierdottern über die Erdäpfel, wenn man will, auch ein kleines Stück zerbrückten Rocamboie, etwa weißen Pfeffer dazu, mischt dies alles gut untereinander und verwendet nun den Salat.

title: Erdäpfels-Strudel (Potato Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca butter
- 16 deca vanilla sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 32 deca mashed potatoes (cooked and passed through a sieve)
- ⅓ teaspoon salt
- ¼ liter milk cream
- 6 egg whites
- 16 deca raisins
- breadcrumbs
- flour for dough

## Directions

1. Mix 16 deca butter with 16 deca vanilla sugar
2. Gradually stir in 6 egg yolks
3. Blend in 32 deca mashed potatoes, ⅓ teaspoon salt, and ¼ liter milk cream, one after another, mixing well
4. Lightly fold in the snow (whipped egg whites) from 6 egg whites last
5. Spread this filling on the finely stretched strudel dough
6. Sprinkle with 16 deca raisins and some breadcrumbs
7. Roll the dough together lightly and treat as described in the Sweet Semolina Strudel recipe

title: Erdäpfelsauce (Potato Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- A couple of floury potatoes
- Wine
- Meat broth
- Sugar
- Lemon juice
- Fine spices
- Butter

## Directions

1. Peel potatoes raw and cook them in wine until soft enough to press through smoothly.
2. If the sauce is too thick, add some meat broth.
3. Season with sugar, lemon juice, and fine spices.
4. Add a piece of butter.
5. Let everything cook together.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erdäpfelschöberl (Potato Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deca butter
- A knife-point of green parsley
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 liter semolina
- 1 liter flour
- 16 Deca cooked, passed potato
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat 12 Deca butter until fluffy
2. Mix in a knife-point of green parsley, 6 egg yolks, 1 liter semolina and the same amount of flour
3. Add 16 Deca cooked, passed potato and some nutmeg
4. Stir everything well together and lightly mix in the snow from 6 eggs
5. Bake as with Griesschöberl

title: Erdäpfelstangerl (Potato Sticks)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg flour
- 15 dkg butter
- 21 dkg cooked, peeled, passed, cool potatoes
- egg yolk for brushing
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. On the board make a smooth dough from 15 dkg flour, 15 dkg butter, and 21 dkg cooked, peeled, passed, cool potatoes
2. Fold and knead the dough
3. Roll it out and cut into strips with a pastry wheel
4. Lay them on an unbuttered baking sheet
5. Brush with egg yolk
6. Sprinkle with caraway seeds
7. Bake until light brown
8. Serve immediately

title: Erdäpfeltorte (Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- juice and zest of ½ lemon
- 5 stiff egg whites
- 7 dkg potato flour

## Directions

1. Beat sugar and egg yolks fluffy for 15 minutes
2. Add lemon juice and zest
3. Fold in stiff egg whites together with potato flour
4. Fill into form
5. Bake slowly
6. Cut in half, fill with jam, coat with jam on top and glaze white, or fill only inside and sprinkle with sugar

title: Ergeance (Barley and Almond Drink)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Barley water
- 12 deka peeled almonds

## Directions

1. Prepare barley water, pound some spoonfuls of it with 12 deka peeled almonds.
2. Pour the remaining barley water over them and let them rest quietly.
3. Pour the almonds through a strong clean cloth, overflow them again with some barley water, squeeze them again, and add finely pounded sugar to taste.

title: Erquickender Brey in der äußersten Fiebermattigkeit und in Gallenkrankheiten (Refreshing Pap in Extreme Fever Weakness and Bilious Diseases)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- two drams of freshly pressed juice from mulberries, currants, or cherries, or in a season when these fruits are not fresh, two drams of pressed pap from these fruits
- egg yolk
- one scruple of lemon juice
- two drams of white wine
- finely rubbed bread and sugar

## Directions

1. Take two drams of freshly pressed juice from mulberries, currants, or cherries, or pressed pap if fresh fruit is unavailable
2. Add the egg yolk
3. Add one scruple of lemon juice
4. Add two drams of white wine
5. Add finely rubbed bread and sugar until it becomes a thin pap
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erweichende Fleischbruhe fur Schwindsuechtige (Softening Meat Broth for Consumptives)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds pounded veal meat
- Four loths pure barley
- One and a half measures water
- Eight loths salsify root
- Half pound common lettuce
- Eight loths lamb's lettuce

## Directions

1. Cook the pounded veal meat and barley together in a well-sealed vessel with one and a half measures water for three hours
2. At the end, add eight loths salsify root, half pound common lettuce, and eight loths lamb's lettuce
3. Let it simmer for another quarter hour
4. Strain through a sieve
5. Drink one teacup full every two hours
6. If the broth is too nourishing and too thick, dilute with warm water as desired
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erweichende Krebsbruhe mit Wurzeln und Krautern (Softening Crayfish Broth with Roots and Herbs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds live crayfish
- Two measures water
- Eight loths oat roots
- Four loths salsify roots
- Two loths ox tongue leaves
- Three loths borage

## Directions

1. Cook the live crayfish for one hour in two measures water
2. Remove the crayfish, pound them together with their shells
3. Cook them again in the previous water for four hours, adding water from time to time as it evaporates
4. Strain through a sieve
5. Add eight loths oat roots, four loths salsify roots, two loths ox tongue leaves, and three loths borage
6. Let everything simmer a couple of times and strain through a sieve again
7. Take one teacup full every two hours
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Erweichende und kuhlende Bruehe (Softening and Cooling Broth)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One well-washed calf lung
- One spoonful cleaned rice
- Red currants
- Black currants
- Raisins
- Dates
- Figs
- Two loths of each
- Two reinettes apples

## Directions

1. Cook all ingredients together in a sufficient quantity of water for several hours
2. Strain through a sieve
3. Drink one to two cups full every two hours
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eröffnende Tisane (Opening Tisane)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one Loth each of parsley root, burdock root, and asparagus root
- one Seidel of water
- one handful each of chicory leaves and chervil herb
- two Quentchen of polychrest salt
- two Loth of orange syrup

## Directions

1. Take the root of parsley, burdock, and asparagus, one Loth each
2. Scrape and cut them into pieces
3. Let them cook in one Seidel of water for a good hour
4. Finally add chicory leaves and chervil herb, one handful each
5. Let it boil several more times
6. Strain it through a sieve
7. Dissolve two Quentchen of polychrest salt in it
8. Add two Loth of orange syrup
9. Drink half a beer glass full every two hours, or one whole glass every two hours
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eröffnende und krampfstillende Tisane (Opening and Cramp-Stilling Tisane)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one handful of pure barley
- one half Loth each of Scorzonera and chicory root
- three Maß of water

## Directions

1. Take one handful of pure barley, Scorzonera, and chicory root, one half Loth each
2. Cook these together in three Maß of water until two Maß remain
3. Strain through a sieve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Escalopes de foie-gras aux truffes (Goose Liver Escalopes with Truffles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- butter
- truffles
- morel mushrooms
- blanched mushrooms
- asparagus tips
- Madeira sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare these escalopes as in the previous recipe.
2. Sprinkle the liver pieces in the casserole with 32 decagrams of peeled, thinly sliced truffles.
3. Let these roast and serve alongside.
4. Small morel mushrooms, blanched mushrooms, and asparagus tips can be arranged decoratively alongside and Madeira sauce added.
5. These escalopes can also be arranged in a baked vol-au-vent or richly garnished with puff pastry.

title: Escalopes de foie-gras à jus (Goose Liver Escalopes with Jus)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- jus sauce
- lemon juice
- small roasted onions
- roasted bread pieces

## Directions

1. Cut the goose liver into elongated strips, half a finger thick and 3 fingers wide.
2. Brush a shallow casserole well with butter and lay out the liver pieces separately.
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and cover with a sheet of paper brushed with butter.
4. 10 minutes before serving, place the casserole on the heat and move it gently for several minutes.
5. Turn the liver pieces one by one and continue moving them over the heat for the same length of time.
6. Arrange these pieces in a circle on a platter.
7. To the butter in the casserole, add some jus sauce with a little lemon juice and pour this over the goose liver escalopes.
8. In the center, small roasted onions can be added and the platter garnished with roasted bread pieces.

title: Escalopes de Lièvre (Hare Escalopes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- One large field hare
- Butter
- Onion
- Crushed juniper berries
- Spices, 5 grains of each type
- 1 bay leaf
- 5 sprigs thyme
- 1 piece black bread, finely cut
- 1.25 liters red wine
- 0.33 liter vinegar
- 0.33 liter water
- Salt and pepper
- Puff pastry for garnish
- Polenta, noodles, or similar optional

## Directions

1. Choose a large, beautiful field hare
2. After the hide is removed, carefully remove the thin skins
3. Carefully remove all the hare meat from the bones
4. Place the tenderest legs and hare trimmings in a casserole with some onion, 5 crushed juniper berries, 5 grains of each spice, 1 bay leaf, 5 sprigs thyme, 1 piece finely cut black bread, 1.25 liters red wine, 0.33 liter vinegar and 0.33 liter water
5. Let this braise covered for approximately 2 hours
6. Meanwhile, cut the meat from the hare back into pieces 2 finger-widths broad, 1 finger-width long, and knife-back thick
7. Cut similar pieces from the thigh meat
8. Gently flatten all pieces with a flat knife and sprinkle with salt and pepper
9. Lay these hare pieces in a shallow casserole buttered and lined with butter-brushed paper
10. Let this stand covered in a cool place until serving
11. Strain the contents of the first casserole through a sieve over a deep bowl
12. Pass the strained liquid into a casserole and let it dissolve; if too thick, add 2 tablespoons Madeira wine
13. About 8 minutes before serving, place the casserole over strong heat and brown the escalopes about 2 minutes on each side
14. Arrange the prettier pieces in a circle on a platter and place the less beautiful escalopes in the center
15. If the sauce is too thin, stir 1 tablespoon flour into the butter remaining in the casserole and pour this with the sauce over the escalopes
16. Garnish with puff pastry, or serve with polenta, sauce, noodles, or similar

title: Eschierschwein (Braised Beef Tongue)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tongue
- salt water
- bacon
- yellow turnips
- parsley root
- celery
- bay leaf
- mugwort
- 1 liter white wine
- 1/4 liter soup
- yellow turnips or other vegetables for garnish
- rice, lentil purée, or puff pastry cut out with cookie cutters

## Directions

1. Cut the beef tongue into half-finger-length pieces and cook in salt water for 1/2 hour.
2. Meanwhile, line a casserole with some bacon, 1 sliced yellow turnip, 1 parsley root, 1 piece of celery, all cut in leaf-like pieces, 1 bay leaf, and some bunches of mugwort.
3. Place the pre-boiled tongue pieces on top, cover, and let brown.
4. Add 1 liter of white wine and 1/4 liter of soup and let braise covered for 3 hours slowly.
5. Arrange the tongue pieces on a serving dish, garnish with the yellow turnips that braised with it or with other desired vegetables, or serve with rice, lentil purée, or puff pastry cut out with cookie cutters.
6. Skim fat from the braising liquid, pass through a sieve, and serve in a sauce boat.

title: Esterhazy-Rostbraten (Esterhazy Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 roasts à 25 dkg
- 6 tablespoons root vegetables
- 2 anchovies
- 1 pickled gherkin
- 4 tablespoons capers
- 1 ring onion
- 1 bay leaf
- thyme
- 5 peppercorns and spice berries
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- lemon zest
- fat for roasting

## Directions

1. Wipe the roasts, remove fat and bones, pound them, salt and pepper them, brush with the farce, roll and tie them
2. In a casserole, roast the root vegetables (cleaned, washed and cut into strips), 1 ring onion, 1 bay leaf, thyme, 5 peppercorns and spice berries in hot fat until dark
3. Add the meat, pour in some water and vinegar, cover and braise for 1-1½ hours, frequently adding hot water or broth
4. Remove the meat, untie it, dust the sauce, pass it through a sieve, return to the casserole, bring to a boil again and serve either separately or with the meat
5. For the farce: bone the anchovies and finely chop them with the gherkin, parsley, capers, ½ onion, 1 bay leaf and lemon zest

title: Estouffade (Estouffade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- A beef rump of 3 kilos
- Salt and spices
- 12 ounces ham
- 12 ounces tongue
- 12 ounces lard
- Bacon sheets for lining
- Finely cut onion pieces
- Root vegetables
- 1 bay leaf
- Mixed spices
- Beef marrow bones
- 5 crushed juniper berries
- 3 pieces dried figs
- 1 piece black bread
- 1 liter white or red wine
- Soup as needed

## Directions

1. Beat the beef well and rub with salt and spices
2. Cut 12 ounces each of ham, tongue and lard into equal pieces and insert alternately into the meat
3. Line the bottom of a casserole with bacon sheets, finely cut onion pieces, root vegetables, 1 bay leaf, some spices, beef marrow bones, 5 crushed juniper berries, 3 pieces dried figs and 1 piece black bread
4. Place the meat on top and let it braise covered for a short time, then add 1 liter white or red wine and braise covered for 3-4 hours
5. If the liquid evaporates too much, add some soup
6. Serve with macaroni, rice, potatoes or finely cut turnips

title: Estragon-Sauce (Tarragon Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- chives
- tarragon
- anchovies
- egg yolks
- oil
- French mustard

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as mustard sauce, with the difference that instead of chives, 1 tablespoon of tarragon is added

title: Estuffade (Braised Meat Stew)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons, capons, veal or lamb meat
- Butter or bacon
- Flour or semolina
- Spices
- Wine
- Lemon peel
- Herbs

## Directions

1. Beat the meat well, salt it, and dust it with flour or semolina
2. Roast it in browned butter or bacon until golden
3. Cook it slowly in meat broth
4. Add spices, wine, lemon peel and good herbs as desired during cooking
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eyer in Essig und saurer Schmette (Eggs in Vinegar and Sour Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, a small piece
- Flour
- Vinegar
- Eggs, as many as needed
- Egg yolks
- Sour cream (Schmette)
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Melt a small piece of butter in a saucepan and let a little flour turn yellowish in it.
2. Pour vinegar on it and let it cook in the roux.
3. Break eggs into it one beside the other without stirring them.
4. Beat a few egg yolks with sour cream, strain, add some of the sour sauce, pour it over the eggs, and let it simmer together.
5. Ensure the eggs do not become hard.
6. Before serving, dust with sugar and cinnamon.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eyer mit Pickelhäringen (Eggs with Pickled Herring)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pickled herrings, one pair
- Butter
- Eggs, as many as needed
- Water, one tablespoon
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the pickled herrings clean, remove the skin, bone them, and chop finely.
2. Melt butter in a pan, break in as many eggs as needed.
3. Add one tablespoon of water, salt, and pepper.
4. Stir in the chopped pickled herring and make a scrambled egg dish, which should be somewhat moist.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eyer-Ragout (Egg Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, as many as needed
- Sweet or sour cream (Schmette)
- Nutmeg, grated
- Pepper
- Salt
- Green parsley, chopped
- Butter

## Directions

1. Boil as many eggs as needed until completely hard, cut them in half lengthwise, and place them in a saucepan.
2. Pour sweet or sour cream over them, add grated nutmeg, pepper, salt, chopped green parsley, and a piece of butter.
3. Let this cook with the eggs until a thick broth forms.
4. Arrange neatly on a dish and garnish the edge with hard-boiled whole eggs.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eyer-Ragout - Alternative (Egg Ragout - Alternative Method)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, as many as needed
- Butter
- Nutmeg, grated
- Salt
- Pepper
- Shallots, chopped
- Thyme, finely grated
- Pea broth (Erbsenbrühe)
- Shallots
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Roast eggs in hot ashes until completely hard, shell and slice them.
2. Let them fry in butter and add grated nutmeg, salt, pepper, chopped shallots, and finely grated thyme.
3. When the eggs have taken on a brownish color, pour some pea broth over them and salt slightly.
4. Rub a plate with shallots, arrange the ragout on it, and squeeze some lemon juice over it.
