source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eyerkuchen mit Semmelbröseln (Egg Cake with Breadcrumbs) - Fridate
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- breadcrumbs
- milk
- salt
- sugar
- candied lemon zest
- egg yolks
- egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Soak breadcrumbs in milk for about 2 hours until they absorb the liquid well
2. Press through a cloth
3. Add salt, sugar, and finely chopped candied lemon zest
4. Add egg yolks and beaten egg whites and mix together
5. Pour into a casserole well-coated with butter
6. Bake in the oven or under a cake cover with heat from above and below
7. Finally turn the cake out onto a serving dish
8. Glaze with a hot shovel
9. Adjust the quantity of breadcrumbs and eggs according to the number of people to be served
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eyerpunsch (Egg Punch à l'Punch aux Oeufs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lemons
- Sugar
- 4 lemons
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 measure Rhine wine
- 1 seidel arrack
- 1 seidel water
- 12 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Rub the peel of 2 lemons onto sugar.
2. Put this sugar, along with the juice of 4 lemons, 1 pound sugar, 1 measure Rhine wine, 1 seidel arrack, 1 seidel water and 12 whole eggs in a kettle.
3. Set it on a charcoal fire and beat it with a white whisk until the foam rises high.
4. Serve this punch in drinking glasses.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eyerſchmalz (Scrambled Eggs with Nutmeg)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, any number
- Butter or cream (Schmette)
- Nutmeg flower (Muscatblüthe)
- Butter for cooking
- Chives (Schnittling), finely cut

## Directions

1. Break any number of eggs into a pot and stir them with sufficient butter and a little nutmeg flower.
2. Let the butter melt in a pan, pour the eggs into it, stir them frequently, and do not let them become hard or grainy.
3. When serving, sprinkle with finely cut chives.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eyerſchmalz (Scrambled Eggs with Smoked Fish)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked fish, already cooked, cut into small thin slices
- Butter
- Eggs, any number
- Salt
- Spices

## Directions

1. Cut cooked smoked fish of any kind into small thin slices and add adequate butter.
2. Let them heat in a pan.
3. Break any number of eggs into a pot and beat them well.
4. Salt them adequately and add some spices.
5. Pour them over the fish slices, stir everything together well, and do not let the eggs clump together.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Eyerſchnitte (Egg Slices)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, as many as needed
- Fine flour
- Green parsley
- Salt
- Ginger
- Nutmeg flower (Muscatblüthe)
- Butter

## Directions

1. Stir eggs, add fine flour, green parsley, salt, ginger, and nutmeg flower, and mix everything well together.
2. Tie it in a serviette and simmer it like a pudding in water.
3. When it has cooked long enough, slice it, bring the slices into a casserole with hot butter, bake them in it, and serve dry.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fabrik-Champagner aus Obst (Factory-Made Champagne from Fruit)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- selected pears that are very juice-rich and sweet for the finest sort
- good sweet apples for the second sort
- ordinary good pears for the third sort
- substantial addition of sugar for the first two sorts
- sugar added only to the bottles for the third sort
- sugar dissolved in water
- cream of tartar dissolved in warm must
- bottles with carbonated gas

## Directions

1. Let the fruit ripen very much and use it as soon as possible
2. For the finest sort, rub the fruit on machines without question on account of the larger quantity of wine being made; the rubbed fruit falls into a tub
3. For the other two sorts, press the fruit in the ordinary way
4. Clean the fruit carefully beforehand; peel only the finest sort
5. Put the crushed fruit into linen sacks under the press and press only carefully and gradually
6. After pressing for some time, turn the sacks and stir the mass and press again moderately; for each new pressing take new sacks and everything newly used must be cleaned first
7. For the better sorts, let the juice settle first in tubs before filtering the clear juice through linen cloth into the barrel
8. For the third sort, the juice goes directly from the press into the barrel
9. The barrels must have had white wine in them previously
10. Dissolve sugar in water and skim it over fire
11. Roll the barrels into the cellar and add some cream of tartar dissolved in warm must and several bottles with carbonated gas
12. After several days fermentation begins, and then drill the tap holes in the bottom and drive the pipes
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fach-Knödel (Chopped Meat Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 10 deca bread crumbs
- 1/2 deca cooked, finely cut meat
- some green parsley
- 1 mushroom
- 1 piece of rhubarb if desired
- some salt
- 5 deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy.
2. Stir in egg yolks and whole eggs.
3. Add bread crumbs that have been peeled, cut into dice, and soaked in milk.
4. Add finely cut cooked meat, green parsley, finely cut and roasted mushroom and rhubarb if desired.
5. Add the roasted ingredients with some salt and flour to the mixture.
6. Mix well together.
7. If the dough is too soft, add 1 tablespoon of white bread crumbs.
8. Form knödel of any desired size.
9. Cook them in beef broth and serve in soup.

title: Faisan Rôti Dressé (Roasted Pheasant, Dressed)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pheasant
- White paper
- Salt
- Twine (spagat)

## Directions

1. Using the eye of a trussing needle, draw out the entrails of the pheasant and replace them with a piece of white paper
2. Hang the pheasant by its feet in an airy place for 8-10 days
3. Pluck the pheasant, singe it over a flame or hot charcoal, remove any remaining pinfeathers, and wipe with a cloth
4. Lay the pheasant head toward you, pull the neck skin tight with the left hand, cut the skin from the back to the head with a knife, and separate it completely from the head
5. Carefully loosen and draw up the gullet and crop from this skin
6. Reach into the pheasant's interior and loosen the mucous membranes all around with your index finger
7. Turn the pheasant head away from you, pinch the skin under the belly with thumb and index finger, and cut it fingernail-width downward
8. Make an equally long cut upward toward the belly and carefully remove the heart, liver, and stomach
9. Wipe the pheasant inside and outside with a cloth without putting it in water
10. Cut off the head at the neck, close to the back, but leave the neck skin attached to the pheasant
11. If desired, scrape off the claws from the feet
12. Salt the pheasant inside and outside, then truss it as follows: Lay the pheasant on its back, place your left hand on the pheasant's breast, press the thighs toward the back with thumb and middle finger to make the breast protrude, and insert a trussing needle threaded with twine just under the wing joint, pulling it out on the other side at the same spot, pulling the twine through so that 3 fingers' width protrudes
13. Turn the pheasant over (lay it on its breast), at the same side where you last came out at the thigh, pull out the wing's inside joint, insert the needle a thumb's width away, pull the twine through the neck skin and back, come out at the same spot on the other side's wing, insert the needle a thumb's width below, pull the twine out, tie it to the twine protruding from the thigh, and cut it half a finger's length long
14. Twist the fingers back behind the neck
15. After this is done, insert the needle under the leg's wing joint, pull the twine out on the opposite side at the same spot, holding the twine over the foot, pull the needle through just under the breastbone, and tie the twine with the protruding end together over the opposite foot

title: Falsche Chocolade-Suppe (False Chocolate Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of beef lard
- a piece of sugar
- a pair of spoonfuls of flour
- crushed cinnamon
- cloves
- 2 measures of boiling milk

## Directions

1. Make a brown roux from a piece of beef lard and a piece of sugar, which must become chestnut brown in the lard
2. Add a pair of spoonfuls of flour, some crushed cinnamon and cloves
3. Add 2 measures of boiling milk and let it cook together for a while
4. The sweetness of the sugar should be slightly noticeable
5. If desired, mix in 3 egg yolks and pour over toasted bread slices
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Falsche Rindsuppe (False Beef Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one gutted but unscaled tench
- a handful of peas
- whole spices
- onion
- parsley
- a piece of celery
- some lard

## Directions

1. Put the tench with a handful of peas, whole spices, onion, parsley, a piece of celery and some lard in a pot
2. Pour water over it and let it cook for two hours
3. Strain it through a sieve and obtain a broth into which you can cook a flour dish before serving
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Falscher Thunfisch (Mock Tuna)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- salt
- root vegetables
- 1 onion ring
- 5 peppercorns and spice seeds
- 1 bay leaf
- approximately 1 dl vinegar
- 3 egg yolks
- butter
- 1 anchovy, boned and passed through a sieve

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, lightly salt it, roll it, and bind it.
2. Cut the root vegetables into leaf-like slices.
3. Put them in a pot with the onion ring, peppercorns, spice seeds, bay leaf, vinegar, and salt in plenty of cold water.
4. Add the meat and cook for 1 to 1½ hours.
5. Strain 1 liter of the broth into a snow pot and let it cool.
6. Beat it with 3 egg yolks over steam (over a pot with hot water) until thick.
7. Stir in butter melted in thin threads, but not hot.
8. At the end, mix in the boned and passed anchovy.
9. Remove the meat, unbind it, slice it, arrange it on a long platter, pour the sauce over it, and serve immediately.

title: Falten ohne Germyteig (Pastries without Yeast Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter
- 8 deca finely pounded sugar
- 2 teaspoons salt
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/2 liter flour
- 1/2 liter milk
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deca butter until fluffy
2. Gradually add 8 deca finely pounded sugar, 2 teaspoons salt, and 6 egg yolks
3. Slowly add 1/2 liter flour and 1/2 liter milk, always adding a little milk first
4. When the dough is smooth and free of lumps, lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
5. Further treatment, namely baking, is specified in the 'Germ-Pastries' (recipe 1273)
6. These pastries can be served with grated Parmesan cheese if desired, in which case use 8 deca grated Parmesan instead of sugar in the dough
7. Before serving, sprinkle the pastries with grated Parmesan and serve with cream sauce as specified in 'Pastries in Cream Sauce' (recipe 1276)
8. These pastries can be prepared with stewed and sugar or also with Spanish wine as in 'Strawberries with Spanish Wine' (recipe 1453)

title: Farce-Knödel in verschiedenen Farben nach Harlequin (Harlequin-Colored Farce Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilo calf forcemeat
- spinach juice to color green
- cochineal to color red
- white part left uncolored
- salted water
- lard

## Directions

1. Divide the forcemeat into 4 equal parts.
2. Color one part green with spinach juice, another part red with cochineal, and leave the third part white.
3. Form walnut-sized knödel from each of these parts.
4. Before serving, cook the green, red, and white knödel in salted water for 15 minutes.
5. Fry the knödel made from the fourth part of the forcemeat in lard until they have a beautiful golden color.
6. Add all the knödel to a soup pot and serve with white, hearty soup.
7. Note: Cochineal can be replaced with 1 teaspoon grated carrot.

title: Farced Chicken Drumsticks (Cuisses de poulets farcis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken drumsticks with skin
- Herb forcemeat
- Butter
- White wine
- Broth
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Remove the drumsticks carefully with the skin, lay them on a moistened board and cut them apart, then remove the thighbone while keeping the knucklebone attached.
2. Flatten the drumsticks slightly with the flat of a knife, salt and pepper them, fill each with a teaspoon of forcemeat of choice, then sew them closed so that the bones protrude slightly and each drumstick forms a cutlet shape.
3. In a casserole add some fat, lay in the prepared drumsticks, and after they have braised somewhat, pour white wine and broth over them and let them braise covered until tender.
4. Remove the drumsticks, arrange them neatly formed side by side in a shallow casserole, pour the defatted cooking liquid over them, cover with paper, place a somewhat smaller lid on the paper so the drumsticks are pressed, and let them stand in a warm place.
5. The roasted chicken breasts are arranged with the drumsticks, arranged in a circle in a serving dish, the defatted liquid is poured over and served with fine vegetables.

title: Farcierte Kalbschnitzel (Stuffed Veal Cutlets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal, about 10 cutlets of knife-back thickness
- Bread
- Capers
- Butter
- Parsley
- Onion
- Mushrooms
- Egg
- Onion
- Carrot
- Mushrooms
- White wine or vinegar
- Soup

## Directions

1. Cut about 10 knife-back thick cutlets from a veal leg
2. Take 2 cutlets and pound them in a mortar or pass through a meat grinder with 8 ounces of bread softened in water and well pressed out, and 4 capers
3. Meanwhile, roast in 4 ounces of butter 1 tablespoon of finely cut green parsley, some onion, and finely cut mushrooms
4. Add the roasted mixture to the pounded veal in the mortar
5. Pound again with one whole egg, strain
6. Spread this paste knife-back thick on the pounded and salted cutlets
7. Roll up these cutlets and tie them with string
8. Place 1 finely cut head of onion, 1 small piece of carrot, and 2 mushrooms, thinly sliced, in a casserole with fat
9. Lay the cutlets side by side in the casserole on the vegetables
10. Cover with a paper bag brushed with butter
11. Let stew covered for 15 minutes
12. After this time, pour in 1/2 liter of white wine or 1/2 liter of vinegar and some soup
13. Stew until completely tender
14. Cut the cutlets into desired pieces, arrange heaped on a serving dish
15. Pour the defatted, strained sauce over them
16. Note: the cutlets must remain well covered during stewing, and there must always be 2 fingers depth of sauce in the casserole

title: Farsell-Pudding (Forcemeat Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 whole eggs
- flour as needed
- 1/3 liter boiling milk
- 12 deca butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 deca vanilla sugar
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Prepare a firm dough from 2 whole eggs and the needed flour
2. Grate the dough on a grater
3. Stir into 1/3 liter boiling milk and cook thoroughly
4. Add 12 deca butter and mix well, then set aside
5. Beat 8 egg yolks with 16 deca vanilla sugar for 1/3 hour
6. Slowly add the cooled dough mixture and mix well
7. Lightly fold in the snow from 8 egg whites
8. Fill a buttered and floured mold with this mixture
9. Steam covered for 3/4 hour

title: Fasan, gebraten (Roasted Pheasant)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pheasant
- salt
- lard for larding, finely chopped
- butter for basting
- lemon peel for garnish
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cleanly pluck the pheasant, remove the entrails, wash it thoroughly, salt it well
2. Lace it elegantly with finely chopped lard and baste it frequently with butter
3. When it is roasted through, garnish it with lemon peel, baste it with lemon juice and serve it to the table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Faschierte Hühner (Stuffed Chicken)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful meaty chickens
- piece of veal
- lard
- bread roll soaked in milk
- two eggs
- nutmeg
- salt
- parsley roots
- yellow turnips
- beef broth
- mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the chickens well and soak them briefly in warm water so they become white
2. Cut them open lengthwise along the back and remove the legs, keeping the thighs and wings attached
3. Take a piece of veal, remove the skin and all fibrous parts
4. Chop the veal together with some lard into small pieces
5. Soak a bread roll in milk, squeeze it well, beat two eggs into it, and stir over heat until very thick
6. Pound the meat and bread together in a mortar until very fine
7. Add nutmeg and salt
8. Dry the chickens with a cloth, fill with the stuffing
9. Sew the skin back together on the back and shape properly
10. Cover with thin slices of lard
11. Place in a casserole with parsley roots and yellow turnips
12. Pour one or two ladles of beef broth over
13. Braise slowly for half an hour and prepare a mushroom sauce
14. When serving, remove the lard from the chickens, place on a dish, and pour the sauce over
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Faschierte Kohlrüben (Stuffed Kohlrabi)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 small kohlrabi
- Salt water
- Cold water
- Farce (forcemeat), approximately 1/2 pound for this quantity
- Sugar
- Soup
- Butter
- Butter sauce

## Directions

1. Remove and discard the green part, peel the kohlrabi carefully so no unevenness results
2. From the root end, cut a lid the thickness of a knife back, and hollow out the kohlrabi
3. Cook the kohlrabi and their lids in salt water until tender
4. Remove carefully and place in cold water, then on a sieve
5. When dry, fill the kohlrabi with farce
6. Place the lids on top and arrange them in a casserole smeared with butter so the lids rest on the bottom of the casserole
7. Add some sugar and pour soup over them
8. Cover the kohlrabi with a sheet of paper smeared with butter so the edges rest on the paper inside the casserole
9. Cook for 3/4 hour
10. Arrange them cone-shaped on a serving dish with the green side up and pour the sauce, which has been mixed with butter sauce, over them

title: Faschierte Maurachen (Stuffed Maurachen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- half pound veal
- several handfuls of maurachen
- parsley
- salt
- ground pepper
- nutmeg
- semmel (bread roll) soaked in milk
- cream
- 2 egg yolks
- large maurachen
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Let a piece of butter warm in a casserole; add half a pound of veal and several handfuls of maurachen, which have been washed well beforehand, and let it stew down
2. When stewed, chop it fine with some parsley, add salt, ground pepper and a little nutmeg, along with half a semmel soaked in milk and well pressed out, and pound the whole thing finely in a mortar, to which add a few spoonfuls of cream and 2 egg yolks
3. Take large, well-washed maurachen, salt them, and let them sit in salt for a while; then dry them well, remove the stem, and clean them well from inside
4. Fill them with the minced meat, cut off the stem, and press it back in; then put small pieces of butter and some fresh parsley in a pan, place the maurachen in it, pour several spoonfuls of fresh cream over them, add a good handful of breadcrumbs, and a few small pieces of butter, apply heat from below and above, and let them bake slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Faschiertes Sauerkraut (Stuffed Sauerkraut)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound young pork
- small piece of bacon
- handful of washed rice
- salt
- ground pepper
- 2 whole eggs
- sauerkraut leaves
- thin light yellow roux (made from fat and flour)
- onion, finely cut
- half a measure of sour cream
- ham, sliced

## Directions

1. Chop together 1 pound young pork and a small piece of bacon
2. Place in a mixing bowl and add a handful of washed rice, some salt, ground pepper, and 2 whole eggs
3. Mix everything well together
4. Remove the ribs from the sauerkraut leaves
5. Fill the leaves with the meat mixture
6. Roll up tightly together
7. Place in a pot with finely cut sauerkraut and set to cook for a couple of hours
8. Make a thin light yellow roux, add finely cut onion and let it brown slightly
9. Add the sauerkraut to the roux and let it cook for at least another hour
10. Before serving, add half a measure of sour cream
11. Serve garnished with the stuffed rolls and sliced ham on the side
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Faschiertsuppe von Rohrhühnern (Clear Soup with Guinea Fowl)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca butter
- finely cut onions
- root vegetables
- 2 plucked guinea fowls
- water as needed for soup
- 12 deca butter
- eggs

## Directions

1. Put 12 deca butter, finely cut onions and root vegetables into a casserole, lay 2 plucked guinea fowls on top and braise covered
2. When the root vegetables are brown, pour in as much water as needed for soup and let the fowls cook covered until tender
3. Meanwhile make a brown roux from 12 deca butter, pour this in with the strained broth in which the fowls were cooked, stir together and let cook well
4. Cut the fowls into neat pieces and serve in the soup with Faschiertl, roasted croutons or similar

title: Faschingskrapfen (Carnival Fritters)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound sifted flour
- salt
- one tablespoon ground sugar
- half a seidel lukewarm cream
- five egg yolks
- three loth melted good beef lard
- two tablespoons yeast

## Directions

1. Place the flour near a heated oven one day before use to make it dry and lukewarm
2. Put three-quarters pound sifted flour in a bowl, salt it, and add one tablespoon ground sugar
3. Take half a seidel lukewarm cream, five egg yolks, three loth melted beef lard, and two tablespoons yeast, and mix everything well together
4. Take as much of the flour as needed to make the fritters, taking care not to make the dough too stiff - it is better to take a bit more flour out than to have it too firm; you can always add more flour later if needed
5. Sieve the mixture through a sieve into the bowl, stir it with the flour until the dough releases from the spoon, and then beat it well for a few moments
6. Flour the board well, place the dough on it, and roll it out two knife-backs thick
7. Dip the fritter cutter well in flour and cut out the fritters with it
8. Lay them on a cloth dusted with flour, place them by the warm oven, and let them rise well - they should become twice as high as they were when cut out
9. Do not make the dough firmer than prescribed, otherwise they will not be so light, will have too many bubbles, and will be neither as good nor as beautiful as these fritters should be
10. When frying, the lard must not be too hot, and the pan in which you fry them must be covered immediately after the fritters are laid in and must remain covered until they are golden brown on one side, but then no longer
11. Once they are equally baked on both sides, remove them, lay them on blotting paper in a sieve so the fat drains completely, keep them warm, and serve them dusted with fine sugar
12. These fritters should be beautifully light and have a white coating
13. If desired, you can also serve stewed fruit warm in a bowl alongside them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Faschinskrapfen (Carnival Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- 38 dkg flour
- salt
- 3 dkg sugar
- 7 dkg butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 liter lukewarm milk
- 1 1/2 dkg yeast
- jam
- lard

## Directions

1. Warm the flour in a bowl, add a pinch of salt and 3 dkg sugar
2. In a small pot, melt the butter, beat it well with 4 egg yolks and 1/2 liter lukewarm milk (everything should be only lukewarm), add to the flour, stir in the yeast
3. Beat the dough very smooth for about 15 minutes, cover and place near the stove to rise
4. After it has risen (about 1 1/2-2 hours), roll it thin on a well-floured board and cut out with a fritter cutter
5. Place hazelnut-sized jam in the middle, lay a second sheet of dough on top with the upper side down, press the edges with floured fingers, cut out the fritters again and place them on a floured board without touching the edges
6. Cover with a napkin and let them rise near the stove
7. When they have risen on one side, turn them over and let them rise again
8. Place them with the upper side down in a baking pan with plenty of hot lard, being careful not to touch the edges when placing them in
9. Cover the pan and shake it gently
10. When the fritters are golden brown, turn them and finish baking uncovered
11. Let them drain on paper and serve dusted with sugar, completely cooled

title: Faschirter Hase (Hashed Hare)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cold hare
- venison
- onion
- roasted goose liver
- bread roll
- beef broth
- egg yolks
- cream
- pepper
- goose fat
- breadcrumbs
- hare or venison shank
- brown roux
- broth
- vinegar
- lemon peel
- capers
- cream

## Directions

1. Take a piece of cold hare and a piece of venison, some onion, half of a roasted goose liver, a bread roll soaked in beef broth.
2. Chop this all finely together, add two egg yolks, two spoonfuls of cream, and ground pepper, and stir all well.
3. Brush a mold with goose fat, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, add part of the forcemeat, then thin slices of hare or venison shank, layer them in the middle of the mold, add the remaining forcemeat.
4. Apply heat above and below and bake nicely.
5. Make a somewhat brown roux, fill with broth, add some vinegar, lemon peel, capers, and finally a couple spoonfuls of cream, let come to a boil.
6. Turn the hare onto a dish and pour the sauce over it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Faschirter Kohl (Minced Cabbage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- young cabbage
- salt water
- veal cutlet
- bacon
- bread roll
- milk
- cooked cabbage
- butter
- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- ham slices

## Directions

1. Take a beautiful cabbage that is still young and not large
2. Clean and wash it thoroughly
3. Cook the head whole in salt water
4. Strain off the water and lay it on a sieve so the water drains completely
5. Make the filling as follows: cut a veal cutlet, some bacon, and a bread roll soaked in milk
6. Press it out well
7. Take some cooked cabbage and cut everything very finely together
8. Beat a piece of butter, add the cut filling into it
9. Beat in two whole eggs and two yolks
10. Add a little salt and ground pepper
11. Take the cabbage on a board
12. Carefully loosen the leaves and place the filling on them
13. Cover it again with leaves
14. Form a cabbage head from it and wrap it in flour
15. Put some bacon leaves in a cooking pot, some ham slices, lay the cabbage on top so it does not stick
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Faschirtes in Kälbernetz (Veal Forcemeat in Caul)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- bacon
- onion
- roasted Indian fowl or capon
- bread roll
- milk
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- caul
- brown broth
- lemon peel
- cream
- capers
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Let half a pound of veal braise with some bacon and onion.
2. Chop together the braised veal with bacon, onion, roasted fowl or capon, a bread roll soaked in milk, some parsley, all finely.
3. Add salt and ground pepper, stir in one whole egg and two yolks.
4. Brush a casserole with butter, sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs, lay in the caul and forcemeat.
5. Cover again with caul, put the lid on, apply heat above and below, and bake it nicely.
6. Make a small roux, fill with brown broth, add some lemon peel, and let cook well.
7. Finally add two spoonfuls of cream, small capers, and lemon juice, and let come to a boil.
8. Turn the forcemeat onto a dish, pour sauce over it, and garnish with small cut-out lemon peel. When the forcemeat is baked, it goes into the sauce and is simmered a quarter hour.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fastenkräutersuppe (Lenten Herb Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deca butter
- finely chopped sorrel
- finely chopped chervil
- green parsley
- celery
- lamb's lettuce
- salt
- nutmeg
- 2 liters water
- toasted bread rolls
- cream and egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. In 32 deca butter, slowly braise the finely chopped sorrel, chervil, green parsley, celery, and lamb's lettuce
2. Add salt and nutmeg
3. Pour in 2 liters water and let simmer for 3/4 hour
4. Pass the soup through a sieve and serve with toasted bread rolls
5. Some cream whisked with egg yolks can be added if desired

title: Fastensuppa (Lenten Root Vegetable Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pieces cooked turnips
- 3 pieces onion
- 2 carrots
- 1 head of celery
- 32 deca butter
- 1/2 liter dried peas
- some sorrel
- chervil
- parsley
- 3 liters cold water
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop the turnips, onions, carrots, and celery very finely and place in a casserole with 32 deca butter
2. Roast until golden yellow in color
3. Add 1/2 liter dried peas along with some sorrel, chervil, and parsley
4. Pour in 3 liters cold water and let simmer slowly for approximately 2 hours
5. Let it rest covered for about 1/2 hour but do not cook further
6. Strain through a sieve into an earthenware pot and set aside until needed

title: Fastensuppa vom Aal (Lenten Eel Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 eel
- 2 liters court bouillon
- 2 liters seasoned lenten root vegetable soup

## Directions

1. Lay the eel on a board and make a strong cut two fingers above the tail on the broad side with a sharp knife so the blood can run out
2. To skin the eel, hang it by the head on a hook, cut the skin around, loosen it carefully with a cloth, and pull it off quickly
3. Alternatively, pierce the eel on a spit and singe on all sides over fire or spirit flame until the skin forms a crust, then quickly wipe the skin and fat off with a rough cloth
4. Roast and bone an eel weighing 1 kilo, cut the flesh carefully from the bones into thin slender pieces
5. Cook gently in 2 liters court bouillon
6. Put the eel pieces in the soup pot and pour over them 2 liters of seasoned lenten root vegetable soup in which the eel was cooked

title: Faſchirter und gebratener wälſcher Hahn (Stuffed and Roasted Guinea Fowl)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- guinea fowl
- chicken livers, whole or leafed
- truffles, boiled
- small whole onions, several dozen
- butter or chopped bacon
- salt
- pepper
- basil
- fond (schü)

## Directions

1. Prepare the guinea fowl in the known manner for roasting
2. Blanch the onions in broth until half cooked
3. Hack together the chicken livers, truffles, onions, salt, pepper, and basil and pass through butter or chopped bacon
4. Stir everything well together with a little fond
5. Fill the guinea fowl with this mixture so nothing falls out
6. Roast it slowly and nicely on the spit
7. Serve with clear fond underneath
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Feine Germstrudel (Fine Yeast Strudel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound butter
- 6 egg yolks
- half seidel sweet milk cream
- 2 spoonfuls good yeast (Germ)
- 2 spoonfuls pounded sugar
- salt
- flour
- cut almonds
- citron (Citronat)
- currants (Zibeben)
- wine berries (Weinberl)

## Directions

1. Beat quarter pound butter quite fluffy
2. Stir in 6 egg yolks
3. Add half seidel sweet milk cream, 2 spoonfuls good yeast, 2 spoonfuls pounded sugar, and some salt
4. Add enough flour so the dough would be good for making a pastry, roll it out finger-thick
5. Fill it with cut almonds, citron, currants (with the kernels removed), and wine berries
6. Roll it together, place it on a butter-brushed baking sheet, let it rise in a warm place, and bake it beautifully
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Feine Liqueurs, Ratafia von Birnen (Fine Liqueurs, Pear Ratafia)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 pounds or 12 measures of very good must
- 6 measures of must
- 6 measures of good, rectified spirits
- 2 quenchen of anise
- 2 quenchen of cloves
- 1 quenchen of cinnamon powder
- 13 pieces of crushed apricot and peach kernels, or in their absence, plum kernels

## Directions

1. Simmer 25 pounds or 12 measures of very good must in the kettle until reduced to half, keeping it at low heat and skimming it
2. Pour the remaining 6 measures of must into a bottle and add the same amount of good, rectified spirits
3. Add 2 quenchen of anise and 2 quenchen of cloves, 1 quenchen of cinnamon powder, and 13 pieces of crushed apricot and peach kernels, or plum kernels in their absence
4. Leave the well-stopped vessel at a warm stove for two days
5. Strain the liquid, return it to the bottle, and after some months, filter it and fill into bottles
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Feine Macaroni (Fine Macaroni)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca macaroni
- salt water
- 2 liters beef broth
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. First, cook 16 deca macaroni for a few minutes in salt water
2. Drain them, then add them to 2 liters of boiling beef broth
3. Let them simmer for approximately 15 minutes so they swell
4. When serving the soup, sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese

title: Feiner Kaiserschmarn (Fine Kaiser Scramble)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deka butter
- 12 deka vanilla sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 6 egg yolks
- 16 deka flour
- 1/4 liter milk
- 6 egg whites (snow)
- 6 deka raisins
- 6 deka lingonberries
- 6 deka hot butter

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deka butter until fluffy
2. Mix in 12 deka vanilla sugar, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 6 egg yolks one after another
3. Add 16 deka flour and 1/4 liter milk, mixing slowly but simultaneously, always some flour and some milk
4. Gently fold in the snow from 6 egg whites with 6 deka raisins and 6 deka lingonberries
5. Pour this dough into a casserole containing 6 deka hot butter
6. Place in the oven and bake until golden
7. When finished, break up the dough with a small pastry knife
8. Arrange in a bowl, sprinkle with finely ground sugar, and serve

title: Feiner mürber Teig zu flauem Kuchen (Fine Short Dough for Flat Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca flour
- 28 deca butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- 8 deca sugar
- 1/16 liter white wine

## Directions

1. Put 28 deca flour and 28 deca butter on a board, work both together finely with the hands
2. Make a depression in the middle and add 2 egg yolks, a little salt, 8 deca sugar, and 1/16 liter white wine
3. Mix everything well with a knife and work into a smooth dough
4. Place on a flour-dusted board and let rest covered in a cold place
5. Then roll out the dough on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a small finger, cut the edges evenly
6. Roll the dough onto a roller, unroll onto a buttered baking sheet and prick several times with a pastry fork
7. Cover the dough thickly with cherries or any other desired fruit, which must always be placed skin-side down, but only so that on all 4 sides a 2-finger-wide empty space remains, which is folded over the cherries
8. Brush with beaten eggs and bake at fairly strong heat in the oven
9. When done, dust the cake with finely pounded sugar, cut into desired pieces, arrange on a platter and serve warm or cold

title: Feines Weihnachtsbrot (Fine Christmas Bread)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca ground, ground almonds
- 12 deca hazelnut kernels
- 12 deca currants
- 12 deca dates
- 12 deca figs
- 6 deca citron
- 1 kilo dried pears (prunes)
- 1 kilo dried plums
- or if no pears available, twice as much plums (both soft-cooked and cut with a knife)
- 12 deca juniper berries
- 12 deca raisins
- 24 deca sugar
- 4 tablespoons rum
- 2 deca melted pistachios
- 4 deca pine nuts (finely cut in leaves)
- 1/2 kilo fresh grapes or Malaga grapes
- finely cut peel of 1/2 orange and 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 kilo black bread dough
- 12 deca black flour
- yeast dough as for milk bread

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients well and moisten with 4 tablespoons rum, let it stand overnight.
2. The next day add melted pistachios, pine nuts, fresh grapes, citrus peels, and black bread dough.
3. Add 12 deca black flour and work everything together well.
4. Form into small loaves or hand-wide, 3-inch high and any length strips.
5. Wrap the formed loaves or strips in dough similar to the mixture or yeast dough as for milk bread.
6. Place them on a sheet lined with buttered paper with the overlapped dough seam-side down, brushed with egg.
7. Let them rise at a warm place for approximately 1.5 hours lightly covered.
8. Brush with egg and bake approximately 1.5 hours slowly in the oven.
9. When removed, immediately wipe them with a cloth moistened in water.

title: Fenchel-Wasser and Kümmel-Wasser (Fennel Water and Caraway Water)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon fennel or caraway seeds
- 0.5 liter boiling water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Put 1 teaspoon of fennel or caraway seeds in a coffee pot.
2. Pour 0.5 liter of boiling water over it and let it boil for a quarter hour.
3. Strain it and serve cold or warm. In the latter case, sugar can be added.

title: Festlicher Kaiserschmarn (Festive Kaiser Scramble)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deka flour
- 1/4 liter milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 5 egg whites (snow)
- 8 deka hot butter

## Directions

1. Place 14 deka flour in a deep bowl
2. While stirring constantly, pour in 1/4 liter milk
3. When smooth, add 5 egg yolks, 1 tablespoon sugar, and 1/2 teaspoon salt, one after another
4. Stir well together
5. Gently fold in the snow from 5 egg whites
6. Pour the dough into a casserole containing 8 deka hot butter
7. Proceed further as described for the kleine Kaiserschmarn
8. The sugar may also be omitted

title: Feuchte Waffeln (Moist Waffles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 16 deca flour
- 4 deca finely pounded sugar
- 1 knife-tip salt
- 1/4 liter whipped cream
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 16 deca butter until fluffy, add 6 egg yolks one after another and then mix this with 16 deca flour, 4 deca finely pounded sugar and 1 knife-tip salt, stirring for another 1/4 hour. Meanwhile, pour 1/4 liter whipped cream into a mixer and beat it with the snow whisk to a thick foam, give this with the snow from 6 egg whites to the batter mentioned above and fold together lightly.
2. When you have brushed both parts of the hot waffle iron with melted butter or unsalted bacon, add 1 tablespoon of the above batter, close the iron firmly and bake as described for 'Waffles from Batter' (No. 1648).

title: Filet am Spieße gebraten (Fillet Roasted on a Spit)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a beautiful fillet well covered with fat
- pepper
- thyme
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take a beautiful fillet well covered with fat
2. Trim the fat immediately
3. Rub the fillet well with pepper and thyme
4. Insert a long skewer through it
5. Bend the fillet at both ends and tie the whole thing to a larger spit
6. Roast it slowly
7. Baste it with butter and lemon juice
8. When it is nicely in its juices, pour off the fat
9. Place the fillet on a platter
10. Serve a piquant sauce or tomato sauce alongside in a sauce boat
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Filet de boeuf à l'Esterhazy (Esterhazy Beef Filet)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef filet with bones and sinews removed
- 16 Deca bacon
- 6 anchovies
- 1 strong tablespoon capers
- 1 small onion
- 4 sprigs parsley
- Fat
- 1 onion
- Some root vegetables
- 12 Deca black bread
- 5 grains spice
- 8 sprigs marjoram
- Bones and scraps from the filet

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from a small beef filet, trim it, and carefully cut it from the thinner side so deep that you can unfold it without dividing it into two pieces
2. Finely chop 16 Deca bacon, 6 anchovies, 1 strong tablespoon capers, 1 small onion, and 4 sprigs parsley on a cutting board with the back of a knife
3. Spread this mixture on the inner, already salted side of the filet
4. Roll the filet together and wrap it with twine
5. In a casserole, add fat, 1 onion, some root vegetables, 12 Deca black bread (all cut into thin slices), 5 grains spice, 8 sprigs marjoram, and the bones and scraps removed from the filet

title: Filet de boeuf à l'indienne (Indian-Style Beef Fillet)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice fillet
- fine bacon
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- finely ground cloves
- thyme
- basil
- fine oil
- butter
- lemon juice
- breadcrumbs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and fat from a nice fillet and trim it well
2. Lard one side symmetrically with fine bacon
3. Cut the other side lightly across the grain and fill it with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, finely ground cloves, thyme and basil, moderately
4. Pour fine oil over it and let it marinate for a while
5. Roast it on a spit
6. Baste it with butter and lemon juice
7. Before it comes off the spit, sprinkle the side of the fillet that is not larded with breadcrumbs and finely chopped parsley
8. It must be in its juices when served
9. Serve an Italian sauce, piquant sauce, or pepper sauce with it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Filet de boeuf à la mariée (Bride's Beef Fillet)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole fillet
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- coarse bacon
- fine breadcrumbs
- mushrooms
- parsley
- onions
- veal sweetbread
- basil
- three egg yolks
- eggs
- strong braise

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and fat from a whole fillet
2. Split it lengthwise and flatten it well
3. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and spices
4. Lard it with coarse bacon
5. Make a fine farce from finely grated bread, crushed mushrooms, parsley, onions, bacon or veal sweetbread, salt, pepper, basil and three egg yolks, well combined
6. Spread this farce lengthwise on both pieces of the fillet
7. Cover them with beaten eggs
8. Roll them up
9. Bind them in a clean cloth
10. Cook them in a strong braise until done
11. When serving, remove the cloth, untie it
12. Glaze the fillet nicely
13. Serve a truffle sauce underneath
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Filet Sauté (Sautéed Tenderloin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Skinned tenderloin
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Hot fat
- Meat gravy

## Directions

1. Cut finger-thick slices from a skinned tenderloin.
2. Lightly flatten these with the flat of a knife, salt and pepper them.
3. Dip both sides in flour and quickly fry in hot fat in a pan.
4. Arrange on a platter and serve with meat gravy.

title: Filet à la Colbert (Fillet Wrapped in Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 to 1.20 kg tenderloin
- 10 dkg bacon
- 1 large or 2 small heads of cabbage
- 3 mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- fines herbes
- breadcrumbs
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves from the cabbage.
2. Wash the good leaves and place in plenty of boiling salt water.
3. Cook for 5-8 minutes, then drain and lay flat on a board.
4. Prepare and roast the English Fillet as directed.
5. Brush the cooked fillet with fines herbes.
6. Place it skin side down on the cabbage leaves.
7. Fold the leaves over the meat on both sides until completely wrapped.
8. Place in a roasting pan, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, add a little broth or water nearby, and braise at high heat for 20-25 minutes more.
9. Slice thinly and serve on a long platter with the pan sauce.

title: Filets = Schnitzel (Filet Sauté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Finger-thick slices from a well-aged beef roast or tenderloin, 3 fingers wide and 1 finger long
- Salt and pepper
- Hot lard for frying
- Parsley or tarragon butter
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Cut finger-thick slices from a well-aged beef roast or tenderloin that are 3 fingers wide and 1 finger long
2. Cut them neatly, beat, salt and pepper them
3. Fry quickly in hot lard
4. When serving, place a piece of parsley or tarragon butter and a lemon slice on each slice

title: Filets de Volaille aux Légumes (Chicken Fillets with Vegetables)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken breasts
- fine skin removed
- meat beaten
- very fine bacon
- butter
- finely sliced root vegetables
- mushrooms
- peas
- spices
- white stock
- brown sauce
- dissolved meat glaze
- fine vegetables or puree or ragout or pasta

## Directions

1. Remove chicken breasts as indicated for braised chicken breasts, remove the fine skin, beat the meat and lard it with very fine bacon (spitchcock)
2. In a casserole lined with butter, finely sliced root vegetables, mushrooms, peas and spices, place the larded breasts
3. Let them braise covered briefly
4. Pour white stock over them and braise covered until tender
5. Remove them and place in a shallow casserole containing some brown sauce
6. Braise them in the oven until the bacon has a golden color
7. Brush the breasts with dissolved meat glaze and arrange them in a circle on a platter
8. Pour the remaining juices over the breasts and place fine vegetables, puree, ragout or pasta in the center of the platter
9. Serve sauce separately

title: Filets à la Colbert
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Tenderloin slices
- Vegetables
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as Filets Sautés.
2. When arranged on the platter, garnish them with various vegetables and breaded fried eggs or fried eggs.

title: Filsoleteig (Fillo Pastry Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 Deca fine flour
- 28 Deca cold, firm butter
- 1/4 teaspoon of salt
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 1/8 liter milk cream
- 3 tablespoons white wine or juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Spread 56 Deca fine flour on a board.
2. Cut 28 Deca cold, firm butter into small pieces with a knife and rub them finely with the flour.
3. Make a depression in the middle into which add 1/4 teaspoon of salt, 3 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, 1/8 liter of milk cream and 3 tablespoons of white wine or the juice of 1 lemon.
4. Mix together with a knife, work the mass into a smooth dough, place on a flour-dusted tray, cover with cloth and let rest at a cold place for 1 hour.
5. After this time, roll out the dough on a flour-dusted board, fold together and repeat as indicated for 'Butterteig feinster Art' (No. 1045).

title: Fine Pickled Cucumbers (Finely Pickled Gherkins)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Small cucumbers, picked in dry weather
- Salt
- Cucumber herb
- Birch leaves
- Linden leaves
- 5 sweet mustard seeds
- 5 bitter mustard seeds
- Vinegar (prepared 2 days earlier from 2 parts vinegar with 1 part water, plus 5 shallots, 5 Carambola, and 5 grains each of mixed spices)

## Directions

1. Prepare the vinegar 2 days in advance by boiling 2 parts vinegar with 1 part water
2. In 5 liters of prepared vinegar, add 5 shallots, 5 Carambola, and 5 grains each of various spices, boil for half an hour removing foam, and let cool in an earthenware dish
3. Pick small cucumbers in dry weather, lay them on cloth, sprinkle with salt, and rub with the cloth until they shine, taking no more than 1 hour
4. Place them in an earthenware dish, mix them together, let them sit for quarter hour, and drain on a sieve
5. Line the bottom of prepared jars with some cucumber herb, birch leaves, and linden leaves
6. Add a layer of cucumbers, then herbs and leaves again, continuing until the jar is full
7. On top place 5 sweet and 5 bitter mustard seeds
8. Pour the prepared cold vinegar over them, leaving a space 3 fingers wide at the top
9. Bind with covers and store in a cool, airy place
10. After 3 to 4 weeks the cucumbers can be enjoyed
11. If the vinegar becomes cloudy, strain it, mix fresh vinegar with water, boil and skim it, let it cool completely, and pour over the cucumbers, binding the jars as before
12. Always use a wooden spoon to remove cucumbers and ensure remaining cucumbers stay covered with vinegar and well bound
13. If vinegar is absorbed, replenish with fresh

title: Fisch-Farce (Fish Forcemeat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pike or perch flesh, about 1 kilo, cleaned and deboned
- 12 Deca of peeled, leafy-cut rolls, grated in milk
- 14 Deca butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- Salt
- A knife-point of white pepper
- A knife-point of nutmeg
- Flour, 1 teaspoon
- Potato flour (if available)

## Directions

1. Take pike or perch flesh, skin it, remove the meat from the bones and cut it finely
2. Cut the peeled, leafy-cut rolls into thin pieces and grate them in milk, then stir them on the fire into a paste
3. Allow this paste to cool, then pound it together with the fish flesh in a mortar
4. Add the butter, egg yolks, and one whole egg, along with salt, then beat it well together
5. Pass it through a hair sieve into a deep bowl
6. Add flour (or potato flour if available), then if desired, a knife-point of white pepper and the same amount of nutmeg
7. Stir it well together and use as needed
8. Can be used on fasting days instead of chicken forcemeat, or on meat days instead of veal forcemeat

title: Fischbäuschel-Suppe (Fish Milt Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- clean fish milt, cut into pieces
- a good portion of roe
- water, 1 measure
- vinegar, 2 seitels
- bay leaves, 2
- lemon peel
- tripe greens (Kuttelkraut)
- onion, 1 head cut into 4 pieces
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- pepper
- saffron
- butter or good beef drippings
- yellow roux
- cream

## Directions

1. Put cleaned and cut milt and a good portion of roe into a pot with 1 measure of water and 2 seitels of vinegar
2. Add 2 bay leaves, lemon peel, tripe greens, onion cut into 4 pieces, salt, nutmeg flower, a little pepper and saffron
3. Cook all together for 2 hours
4. Make a yellow thin roux from butter or good beef drippings
5. Thicken the soup with the roux and simmer for another quarter hour
6. At the end, add a couple of spoonfuls of good cream
7. Carefully remove the onion, tripe greens and bay leaves before serving over toasted bread
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fischknödel mit Erbsensuppe (Fish Dumplings with Pea Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pieces of carp or pike
- 4 lots of butter
- 2 small bread rolls soaked in milk
- 2 tablespoons of cream
- 1 whole egg and 5 egg yolks
- mace and salt
- beef lard
- pea broth
- bread slices

## Directions

1. From 2 pieces of carp or pike remove the bones, peel off the skin and chop it finely
2. Stir 4 lots of butter fluffy with 2 small bread rolls soaked in milk, 2 tablespoons of cream, 1 whole egg and 5 egg yolks, mace and salt
3. Give the finely chopped fish into this batter and mix well, then make small dumplings from it
4. Cook the dumplings in beef lard or cook them in pea broth
5. When serving, add a previously made strained pea soup on top, in which one can also serve baked or water-cooked bread slices
6. If the farce is pounded and driven through a hair sieve, it turns out much better
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fischleber (Fish Liver)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Onion
- Vinegar
- Cream
- Broth

## Directions

1. Roast in hot butter with onion.
2. Then cook with vinegar, cream and broth.
3. Season to taste and arrange with the fish.

title: Fischmilchsuppa (Carp Roe Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece onion
- 1 piece from each root vegetable
- some pieces of dill (dill herb)
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 finger-width piece of lemon peel
- 1 chopped carp head (if available)
- approximately 2 liters cold water
- vinegar
- 10 deca fat
- flour
- 1/2 liter cream (if desired)
- salt
- cleaned carp milt and roe

## Directions

1. Cut into a casserole 1 piece onion, 1 piece from each root vegetable, some dill, 1 bay leaf, 1 finger-width piece of lemon peel, and if available, 1 chopped carp head
2. Pour in approximately 2 liters cold water, add as much vinegar as needed, and let cook 1 hour
3. Make a dark roux with 10 deca fat and flour and pour into the boiling liquid, stirring frequently so no lumps form
4. Add 1/2 liter cream and necessary salt if desired, let simmer well
5. Strain through a sieve into another casserole and cook again
6. Add the cleaned and well-washed carp milt and roe to the boiling soup and let simmer approximately 1/2 hour
7. Remove the roe pieces, cut into pieces, and put in the soup pot, then pour the soup over them; it should have a pleasant sour taste and brown color
8. Serve with toasted rolls

title: Fischpudding (Fish Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg Angler or Schellfish (monkfish or haddock)
- ½ l Weißwein (white wine)
- 2 Schneidsemmeln (bread rolls)
- 2 dkg Parmesan (parmesan)
- 2 Sardellen (anchovies)
- 1 Zitrone (lemon)
- 2 ganze Eier (whole eggs)
- 5 dkg zerlassene Butter (melted butter)
- Mehl (flour)
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Remove bones and chop 30 dkg of the prepared fish, pass it with the bread rolls (soaked in water and well pressed out) along with 2 whole eggs, grated Parmesan, 5 dkg melted butter, salt, pepper and some flour, mixing well.
2. Remove bones from the remaining fish, cut into cubes, salt and pepper.
3. Place a layer of the filling in a well-buttered, breadcrumbed pudding mold, then a layer of fish on top, alternating filling and fish, ending with filling.
4. Cook the pudding for 1 hour in a water bath, remove, loosen, unmold, pour the following sauce over it and serve.
5. For the sauce: In 4 dkg butter, let finely chopped onion turn light yellow, add flour to make a light, light roux, pour in with water or broth, add 3 peppercorns and spice berries and the deboned, finely chopped anchovies and let the sauce cook well. Strain it, pour in the wine, bring to a boil again and finally mix in 2 egg yolks (which must first be stirred with some cold water so they do not curdle). After the binding, the sauce must not boil anymore.

title: Fischsuppe (Fish Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of carp or pike
- bread slices baked in two eggs
- bouillon or pea broth
- salt
- the usual root vegetables
- croutons (baked bread)
- some nutmeg

## Directions

1. Fill a casserole with pieces of carp or pike with bread slices baked in two eggs, fill with bouillon or pea broth
2. Salt and cook it with the addition of the usual root vegetables until tender
3. After straining through cheesecloth, add croutons and some nutmeg before serving
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fischsuppe (Fish Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deca butter
- 2 pieces thinly chopped onions
- 2 cooked turnips
- 1 small piece celery
- 1 parsley root
- 1/3 kilo carp
- 1/2 kilo pike
- 1/2 kilo tench or 1/2 kilo bream
- some frogs and 1 crayfish (if available)
- 1 liter cold water
- spices (optional)

## Directions

1. Put 32 deca butter, 2 pieces of thinly chopped onions, 2 cooked turnips, 1 small piece of celery, and 1 parsley root in a casserole and heat
2. Clean and cut into pieces the fish (carp, pike, tench or bream, frogs and crayfish) and add to the vegetables
3. Let roast covered, then add 1 liter of cold water and braise covered
4. Spices may be added if desired
5. Pour in 2 liters cold water and let simmer slowly for 2 hours
6. Set the soup aside, remove the fat carefully, strain through a sieve, and use

title: Fischsuppe (Fish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Root vegetables, leafed-sliced
- 1 onion
- 5 peppercorns and spice berries
- 1 bay leaf
- Pinch of thyme
- 5 decagrams hot fat
- Fish head, halved
- Salt water
- Vinegar
- Dark roux of 5 decagrams butter or fat
- Beuschel (fish offal), cooked separately 15-20 minutes
- Bread slices for serving

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and slice the root vegetables into leaves
2. In a pot, roast the root vegetables with 1 onion, 5 peppercorns and spice berries, 1 bay leaf, and a pinch of thyme in 5 decagrams hot fat until dark
3. Wash the halved fish head and cook it in salt water
4. Pour this water over the roasted root vegetables
5. Season with vinegar and stew for 1-1¼ hours, stirring frequently
6. Pass the mixture through a sieve
7. Add a dark roux made from 5 decagrams butter or fat
8. Season with salt and vinegar
9. Add the beuschel which has been cooked separately in a pot for 15-20 minutes
10. Cook well together
11. Serve with bread slices

title: Fisolensuppe (Bean Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned beans
- Green parsley, finely cut
- Salt
- Wine vinegar
- Butter
- Flour
- Onion, finely cut
- Lemon peel
- Roasted bread rolls

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned beans very soft in a pot
2. Skim off the broth frequently
3. Add finely cut green parsley, salt, and a little wine vinegar
4. Give the soup the proper thickness with a roux made of butter, flour, and finely cut onion
5. Add a little lemon peel
6. Serve over roasted bread rolls
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fiſchbeuſchelſuppe (Fish Offal Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 roe fish offal
- 16 dkg vegetables
- 1 bread roll
- fish head
- salt water
- vinegar
- 5 dkg butter or fat
- flour

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and slice the vegetables into leaves.
2. Roast them with 1 onion, 5 peppercorns and spice kernels, 1 bay leaf, a trace of thyme in 5 dkg hot fat until dark.
3. Wash the halved fish head and cook it in salted water.
4. Pour this water over the vegetables, season with vinegar and braise for 1-1.25 hours, stirring frequently.
5. Strain the soup and add a dark roux made from 5 dkg butter or fat.
6. Season with salt and vinegar.
7. Add the offal, which has been cooked separately in a small pot for 15-20 minutes.
8. Cook together well and serve with bread slices.

title: Fiſolen anglais (Green Beans, English Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- salted water
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Trim the ends of the green beans and remove the strings.
2. Cut the beans diagonally into thin pieces.
3. Place in boiling salted water and cook uncovered for 25-30 minutes.
4. Drain and arrange.
5. Top with small pieces of fresh butter.

title: Fiſolen francais (Green Beans, French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 dkg butter
- a little flour
- cooked green beans
- vegetable water or broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Heat 5 dkg butter in a saucepan, add a little flour, add the cooked and drained green beans, pour in some vegetable water or broth, season with salt, and let cook down for 10 minutes.

title: Flammgebranntes (Flaming Drink)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter wine
- 28 deciliters rum or arrack
- 20 deciliters sugar

## Directions

1. In an earthenware bowl, add 1 liter wine and 28 deciliters rum or arrack in which to dissolve it
2. Break 20 deciliters sugar into pieces, dip one after another in rum or arrack, place each on a perforated spoon, light the sugar and let it melt into the wine
3. After all 20 deciliters are burned, mix the liquid and serve it

title: Fleckerl-Suppe (Soup de pâte allemande)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- flour to accommodate 2 eggs
- 2 eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Using same dough as for noodles, cut dried, finely rolled dough into 2-finger-wide strips
2. Stack strips and cut small squares (Fleckerl)
3. See noodle soup for further preparation
4. Alternatively, rolled dough can be cut like ribbons with a crinkle wheel and called Lasagne Soup, or cut into Schifferln with a crinkle wheel or knife

title: Fleisch (Braised Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat
- Salt
- Caraway
- 5 dkg fat
- Water

## Directions

1. Wash the meat
2. Rub with salt and caraway
3. Bind if desired
4. Heat 5 dkg fat in a casserole until hot
5. Place meat in and brown on all sides
6. Add a small amount of water
7. Cover and braise for 2 hours, turning often and adding water as needed
8. Slice and serve with the brown sauce on a long platter

title: Fleisch (Meat Stew with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-70 dkg beef (Rindszapfen)
- 6 dkg hot fat
- 1 finely chopped onion
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- broth or water
- 2 tablespoons capers
- 2 bunches carrots
- 2 kohlrabi
- ½ kg round potatoes
- 3 mushrooms
- finely chopped lemon zest
- marjoram
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the meat and cut into large cubes
2. In 6 dkg hot fat, sauté 1 finely chopped onion until lightly browned
3. Add the meat and 1 tablespoon vinegar
4. Pour in broth or water and braise covered for 2-2½ hours
5. Add finely chopped lemon zest, capers, marjoram, carrots cooked in salted water and cut into noodle shapes, kohlrabi cut into cubes, and potatoes, plus raw chopped mushrooms
6. Let everything braise together a while longer
7. Serve the meat on a round dish

title: Fleischknödel (Meat Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25-30 dkg cooked beef
- 6 dkg smoked ham
- 8 dkg ham bacon
- 4 dkg bacon
- some bread crumbs
- 30 dkg salted flour
- 4-5 ladles boiling water
- 1 chopped onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- fat for roasting
- bread crumbs for serving
- sauerkraut or lettuce salad

## Directions

1. In a bowl, pour 4-5 ladles of boiling water over 30 dkg salted flour, stir smooth, and cover.
2. When cooled, form equal-sized dumplings from the dough, flatten on a floured board.
3. Place a meat ball inside each dumpling and seal the dough over it.
4. Cook the dumplings in a pot of boiling salted water for 8-10 minutes.
5. Lift onto a dish and pour over bread crumbs roasted in fat.
6. Serve with sauerkraut or lettuce salad.
7. For the filling: Heat 4 dkg fat, sauté 1 finely chopped onion and parsley, add finely chopped beef, ham, bacon, and bread crumbs with salt, pepper, and broth, simmer a few minutes, and form into equal-sized balls.

title: Fleischlaberln (Beef Patties)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40-50 dkg beef or veal roast remnants
- 1 bread roll soaked in water
- a piece of onion
- parsley
- 1 egg
- salt
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- hot lard for frying
- cooked lettuce, spinach, or sauerkraut

## Directions

1. Soak a bread roll in water, squeeze out well.
2. Mince the beef or veal with the squeezed bread roll, onion, and parsley twice.
3. Mix with 1 egg, salt, and pepper.
4. Form into patties, roll in bread crumbs.
5. Fry in hot lard.
6. Serve with cooked lettuce, spinach, or sauerkraut.

title: Fleischpastete (Meat Pastry)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted veal or other meat scraps
- 4 softened rolls without crusts, wrung out well
- 60 decagrams meat
- 16 decagrams butter
- 6 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- 8 decagrams breadcrumbs
- green peas
- 1/4 liter cream
- sugar sauce (sauce tartare)

## Directions

1. Cut leftover roasted veal or other meat scraps very fine
2. Add 4 softened, wrung-out rolls without crusts and chop everything very fine with a chef's knife
3. With 60 decagrams of meat, beat 16 decagrams of butter until fluffy, add 6 eggs one by one, stir in the meat, salt, pepper, 8 decagrams breadcrumbs and mix very well
4. Brush a mold with butter and dust with flour
5. Place a layer of the beaten mixture in the mold, then a layer of braised green peas, then meat mixture again and so forth
6. Once everything is used up, cook the cake in steam, turn it out of the mold, pour 1/4 liter of cream over it and place it in the oven so the cream thickens, or serve with sugar sauce (sauce tartare) instead
7. One may also use thinly sliced truffles instead of green peas, or thinly sliced eggs with finely cut mushrooms

title: Fleischpuffer (Meat Patties)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 80 drachm raw, boned meat of any kind
- 60 drachm fat (beef fat or bacon), finely chopped
- 2 whole eggs
- 8 drachm crusts of bread, soaked in water and squeezed dry
- 1 bay leaf
- sprigs of thyme
- salt and pepper as needed

## Directions

1. Finely chop together the meat and fat
2. Pound in a mortar with 2 whole eggs, squeezed bread crusts, 1 bay leaf, and sprigs of thyme
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. With a spoon, take portions of this forcemeat and place on a water-moistened board
5. Smooth with a knife and shape as desired, like fish cakes or sausages
6. Dust with flour
7. 10 minutes before serving, place in a shallow casserole containing hot butter
8. Brown on each side for approximately 4 minutes with gentle movement
9. Pour off the fat, cover the patties with brown soup and an equal amount of cream
10. Add a little lemon juice if desired and bring to a boil once
11. Serve with rice, macaroni, or puree

title: Fleischschtrudel (Meat Strudel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg flour
- 1 egg
- salt
- ½ dl lukewarm water
- 35 dkg cooked beef or roast leftovers or ½ cooked pork spleen
- 3 dkg hot fat
- minced onion
- parsley
- beef broth or water
- pepper

## Directions

1. Make a strudel dough from 15 dkg flour, 1 egg, salt, and ½ dl lukewarm water
2. Let rest under a warm casserole for 15 minutes
3. Stretch the dough not too thin and brush with egg white
4. Prepare the filling: finely chop 35 dkg cooked beef or roast leftovers or ½ cooked pork spleen
5. Heat 3 dkg fat in a casserole, sauté minced onion and parsley until golden
6. Add the meat and moisten with a little broth or water
7. Season with salt and pepper and keep warm
8. Distribute the filling on the stretched dough, trim the edges, and roll up the strudel
9. Using a floured spoon handle, measure off equal-sized pieces, cut them through, place on a floured board
10. Lay them in a large casserole in boiling beef broth for 8 minutes

title: Fleiſch (Braised Meat with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece of meat
- 6 dkg hot fat
- 1 large finely chopped onion
- broth or water
- potatoes, diced
- carrots, cut into noodle-like strips
- celery
- green peas
- mushrooms
- salt water for cooking vegetables
- cream
- red wine

## Directions

1. Pound the meat, wash it, salt and pepper it, and lard it.
2. In 6 dkg hot fat, brown 1 large finely chopped onion until dark brown.
3. Add the meat and pour in broth or water.
4. Let it braise covered for 2 to 2½ hours, turning often and adding liquid as needed.
5. 45 minutes before serving, add the diced potatoes, noodle-cut carrots, celery, green peas, and mushrooms that have been pre-cooked in salt water.
6. Let them braise until tender.
7. Dust the sauce lightly with flour, add cream and red wine, and bring to a boil.
8. Serve the meat and vegetables on a long platter with the sauce.

title: Foamed Quince Pudding (Gefäumtes Quittenkoch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- quince pieces
- one-half pound quince pulp
- same amount sugar
- Seville orange peel, finely cut
- lemon peel, finely cut
- ten fresh eggs

## Directions

1. Cook some pieces of quince, scrape them finely, and beat them through a hair sieve
2. From the quince use one-half pound, put the same amount of sugar and finely cut Seville orange and lemon peel in it
3. Beat foam from ten fresh eggs onto a sieve and add it piece by piece
4. It must be stirred together for a while
5. Then put it on a sheet lined with wafers and arrange it beautifully high and round
6. Cut a cross in the top and bake it in the same way in a cool oven
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Forellen blau geſotten (Trout Boiled Blue)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- broth for cooking
- hot wine vinegar
- hot butter for serving
- parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Do not scale the trout; cut open toward the head, gut, and wash well
2. In the meantime, simmer the broth in a pan for 30 minutes
3. Place trout on a dish and pour hot wine vinegar over to make them blue
4. Carefully slide them into the broth so they are completely covered
5. Pull the pan back and let the fish poach for 10-12 minutes
6. Lift with a slotted spoon onto a long serving dish, back side up
7. For warm serving: drizzle with hot butter and serve boiled potatoes and hot butter separately
8. For cold serving: let cool in the broth, lift onto the dish, garnish with parsley, and serve vinegar and oil separately

title: Forellen, blau abzusieden (Trout Boiled Blue)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Trout
- wine vinegar
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Trout must not be removed from water until needed for cooking
2. Strike the trout on the head, quickly slit the belly, remove the insides, wash it clean so the blood comes out
3. Immediately spray good wine vinegar on it
4. Set water with salt on the fire, and when it boils, add the fish with the vinegar and let it cook
5. When serving, add fresh wine vinegar again over it, cover the trout, and serve it cold or warm as desired
6. If served cold, it must remain in the broth
7. Prepared this way it will remain beautifully blue
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Frankfurter Leberknödel (Frankfurt Liver Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's liver
- onions
- parsley
- marjoram
- 1 pound bread in 1 measure milk
- nutmeg
- salt
- 0.5 pounds bacon, diced and roasted
- 5 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- fine flour

## Directions

1. Clean the calf's liver and scrape out all the veins
2. Chop finely with some onions, parsley, and marjoram
3. Soak 1 pound bread in 1 measure milk
4. Combine the chopped liver with the soaked bread, nutmeg, salt, and roasted diced bacon
5. Stir in 5 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks with enough fine flour to make the mixture stiff enough to form dumplings
6. Cook the dumplings in salted water until cooked through
7. Better if cooked in bouillon instead
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Franz. Omelette (French Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 dkg sugar
- 2 dkg flour
- Butter
- Jam

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites until stiff peaks form
2. Stir in 4 egg yolks, 2 dkg sugar, and 2 dkg flour
3. Pour into a large, well-buttered omelet pan
4. Bake at moderate heat for 8-10 minutes until light brown
5. Loosen and quickly tip onto a plate
6. Spread with jam
7. Fold together
8. Sprinkle with sugar
9. Serve quickly

title: Französische Andouilles (French Andouilles / Black Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful, fatty, cleaned pork intestines
- wine
- pork fat
- pork meat
- sausage herbs
- thyme
- basil
- marjoram
- pepper
- nutmeg
- bouillon or braise

## Directions

1. Cut clean pork intestines into desired pieces
2. Season properly and marinate with some wine
3. Cut equal amounts of pork fat and pork meat the same size as the intestines
4. Mix everything together with sausage herbs, thyme, basil, marjoram, pepper, and nutmeg
5. Add the marinated intestines and work everything together well
6. Fill into pork intestines, not too firmly but rather loosely so they do not burst
7. Bind both ends well with twine
8. Cook in bouillon or braise
9. When cold, use in various ways: grill them as they are, or bread them with grated bread and grill, or keep them for a long time in winter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Französische Erdäpfel (Pommes de terre à la française)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams butter
- 8 decagrams flour
- finely chopped onion
- bay leaves
- lemon peel
- soup
- 1/2 liter cream
- salt
- spices
- cooked potatoes, leaf-like sliced
- fresh parsley

## Directions

1. In 8 decagrams butter roast an equal amount of flour
2. Add finely chopped onion, bay leaves and lemon peel
3. Stir well together
4. Pour soup over it
5. Once this has cooked with 1/2 liter cream, salt and spices, add approximately one ladle of cooked, leaf-like sliced potatoes with finely chopped fresh parsley
6. Stir well together and let simmer once more
7. If too thick, thin with soup

title: Französische grüne Erbsen (French Green Peas)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter fine green peas
- salt water
- 12 ounces butter
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped green parsley
- 1 tablespoon cream
- 3 tablespoons white soup

## Directions

1. Cook 1 liter of fine green peas in salt water until tender
2. Drain the liquid and place the peas in a casserole dish
3. Mix 12 ounces of butter with 1 tablespoon of finely chopped green parsley and 1 tablespoon of cream, beating well together
4. Add this mixture to the cooked peas
5. Stir in 3 tablespoons of white soup
6. Cover and let stand on the hot plate until ready to serve

title: Französische Wurzelsuppe (French Root Vegetable Soup / Julienne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece white turnip
- 1 dark and 1 light yellow turnip
- 1 piece parsnip
- 1 piece celery root
- 2 carrots
- 1 small head celery
- 1 piece Spanish onion
- 2 mushrooms
- 2.5 liters beef broth
- salt and spices as needed

## Directions

1. Peel and wash all vegetables, then cut them finely into small pieces.
2. Let them cook gently in 2.5 liters of beef broth.
3. Once salt and spices have been added, serve the soup with roasted bread roll or braised rice, or Pasch dumplings.
4. If desired, 4 to 6 tablespoons of cooked butter sauce can be stirred into this soup.

title: Französische Wurzelsuppe (French Root Vegetable Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow turnips
- celery
- parsnip
- leeks
- onion
- beef lard
- cabbage
- head lettuce
- chervil
- sorrel
- good meat broth
- consommé
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Clean yellow turnips, celery, parsnip, leeks, and onion and cut lengthwise
2. Let them simmer soft in beef lard
3. Add cabbage, head lettuce, chervil, and sorrel cut lengthwise
4. When everything is soft together, fill with good meat broth (or better, half consommé if available)
5. Let it come to a boil with it
6. Before serving, add nicely browned bread slices
7. Let them boil with these for a couple more minutes
8. Serve moderately salted at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Französischer Salat (French Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg kipfel potatoes
- prepared root vegetables (yellow turnip, parsley root, celery)
- 1 apple
- 1 cucumber
- 1 red beet
- 2 fresh hard-boiled eggs
- ½ l cooked peas
- mustard
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- mayonnaise
- salad greens (optional)
- 10 h bird's lettuce

## Directions

1. Wash, cook, drain, and peel ½ kg potatoes, cut into small dice
2. Cut prepared root vegetables into small dice, cook in salted water for 20 minutes, and drain
3. Peel and dice the apple, cut the cucumber
4. Mix potatoes, root vegetables, apple, cucumber, mayonnaise, peas, mustard, salt, oil, and vinegar together
5. Pile in a cone shape in the middle of a glass bowl
6. Slice or cut 2 hard-boiled eggs into 8 pieces
7. Wash and cook the red beet for 2 hours, peel, cut into thin slices or julienne strips
8. Wash salad greens and dress with salt, vinegar, and oil
9. Decorate the cone with hard eggs, red beet, and salad greens

title: Französischer Senf (French Mustard)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- good yellow mustard
- very strong vinegar, strengthened by frost
- marjoram, 1 loth per measure of vinegar
- tarragon (Drachenkraut), 2 loth per measure of vinegar
- thyme, 1 loth per measure of vinegar
- sage, 1 loth per measure of vinegar
- rocambole onions (Roccombolenzwiebeln), 2 loth per measure of vinegar
- mustard powder, 2 pounds
- pounded sugar, 1 pound or more
- cinnamon, 12 loth
- ginger, 12 loth
- allspice (Neugewürz), 12 loth
- fennel seeds (Fenchelſamen), 1 quenchen
- vinegar, as needed

## Directions

1. Pound good yellow mustard in a mortar and warm it in a pan until the oil can be pressed out with the fingers
2. Press it strongly and pound it into fine powder
3. To one measure of very strong vinegar that has been strengthened by frost, add 1 loth marjoram, 2 loth tarragon, and 1 loth each of thyme and sage, all dried and finely chopped, then add 2 loth rocambole onions
4. Let this steep in the sun for 5 to 6 days, then strain it through
5. Take 2 pounds of the mustard powder, 1 pound of pounded sugar or more, 12 loth each of cinnamon, ginger, and allspice (all pounded), and 1 quenchen of fennel seeds
6. Mix everything well and make it into a paste with as much vinegar as necessary
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: French Omelette (Omelette à la française)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites, beaten stiff
- 1 tablespoon fine flour
- 1/2 tablespoon sugar
- a little salt
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon hot butter
- 1 tablespoon marmalade
- sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Mix the stiff egg whites with the flour, sugar, salt, and egg yolks lightly.
2. Pour 1 teaspoon hot butter into a hot frying pan.
3. Spread the prepared mixture somewhat and bake in the oven for approximately 8 minutes.
4. Spread 1 tablespoon marmalade on top.
5. Fold the omelette together using a wooden spoon, slide onto a dish, dust with sugar, and serve.

title: French Pastry Dough (Pâte Brisée)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Six pounds flour
- 3 pounds butter
- 2 ounces salt
- 10 egg yolks
- 1 glass water

## Directions

1. Place the flour on a table and make a depression in it
2. Into this depression put the butter, salt, egg yolks from the eggs, and one glass of water, more or less according to the proportion of other ingredients
3. Work the butter with the water, eggs, and salt
4. Ensure the butter is very soft, gradually mix in the flour, and gather everything together well
5. Once this dough is well compressed, knead it with your fingers until it has thoroughly combined; if it is too dry, moisten it somewhat
6. Do not knead this dough more than twice, as it could become grainy, especially in summer, and therefore not hold together properly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: French Semolina Soup (Soupe au pain blanc à la francaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 22 drams peeled semolina rolls, cut into flakes
- 2 liters cold water
- 16 drams butter
- 5 strands saffron, grated
- salt
- spices (optional)
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter cream

## Directions

1. Place the sliced semolina rolls in a casserole with cold water, butter, and grated saffron
2. Bring to a boil while constantly stirring
3. Season with salt and spices if desired
4. Allow to simmer
5. Mix egg yolks with 1/4 liter of cream, strain well
6. Add the egg yolk and cream mixture to the soup while constantly stirring
7. This produces a somewhat thick but flavorful soup

title: French Style Green Peas (Pois verts à la française)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter fine green peas
- Salt water
- 12 drams butter
- 1 tablespoon finely cut green parsley
- 1 spoonful cream
- 3 spoonfuls white broth
- Salt

## Directions

1.
