After 1 liter of fine green peas have been boiled tender in salt water, drain the liquid and place the peas in a casserole; add 12 drams butter worked well with 1 tablespoon finely cut green parsley and 1 spoonful cream; stir in 3 spoonfuls white broth and mix well; let stand covered on the warm stove until needed.

title: Fribattcn (Omelettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- dough made from 1.5 liters flour, 1.5 liters milk, and 3 whole eggs
- hot lard

## Directions

1. Heat some lard in a casserole until very hot.
2. Add a small amount of lard to a flat pan with a handle and let the pan become very hot.
3. Pour the fat back into the casserole and add approximately 2 tablespoons of dough to the pan, spread about paper-thin.
4. Place the pan back on the fire and add some fat from the side.
5. Flip the omelette with a pastry spoon and add a little more fat.
6. Place the browned omelettes on blotting paper and continue until the dough is finished.
7. The omelettes can be cut into strips or decorative shapes as desired.
8. Note: It is especially important that the lard for frying is very hot, otherwise the mushrooms and omelettes will fall apart.

title: Fribatten mit Creme im Dunst Gedünstet (Crepes with Cream, Steamed in Broth)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- crepes
- cream filling
- 0.5 liter milk
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 6 hectograms sugar
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Prepare the crepes as indicated in 'Baked Crepes' (No. 1451), coat with any thick but smooth cream, roll together and cut into half-finger-long pieces
2. Place these in a short casserole lined thickly with butter and dusted with flour
3. Beat 0.5 liter milk with 3 whole eggs, 3 egg yolks and 6 hectograms sugar together, pour over the crepes
4. Let them cook covered for 3/4 to 1 hour in the broth or bake in the oven

title: Fribatten mit Spanischem Aufguß (Crepes with Spanish Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- baked crepes
- marmalade
- Spanish almond paste
- 4 egg whites
- 6 hectograms fine powdered sugar
- butter
- marmalade

## Directions

1. Fill the baked crepes with any marmalade, roll together and divide each one into two parts
2. Lightly mix the firm snow from 4 egg whites with 6 hectograms finely powdered sugar
3. Spread some Spanish almond paste on a round bowl coated with butter, place the filled crepes cut into pieces on top, cover these again with the paste and continue alternately until everything is used and the bowl is arranged heaped
4. The last layer is paste, dust with finely powdered sugar, then let dry in the open oven for 0.5 hour
5. While baking, turn the bowl in the oven several times so that the almond paste gets an even color
6. When a yellow crust has formed, take the bowl from the oven and sprinkle the crust with warm marmalade

title: Fribattenschöberl (Consommé with Pancakes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- Flour
- 1/2 liter simmering milk
- 10 Deca butter
- 5 egg yolks
- Salt
- Nutmeg (optional)

## Directions

1. Make baked fribatts from 4 eggs, cut them into small pieces and cook them in 1/2 liter simmering milk and let them cook through
2. Beat 10 Deca butter until fluffy and beat in 5 egg yolks
3. Add some salt, the prepared fribatts, and if desired some nutmeg and stir everything well together

title: Fricandeau of Salmon (Fricandeaux vom Salm)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- bacon
- ham
- braise
- glace
- sauce royale
- thick cream

## Directions

1. Clean and dry the salmon thoroughly
2. Cut it into wide pieces
3. Lard with bacon and ham
4. Cook slowly in a braise
5. Brush each piece with glace
6. Cook a sauce royale very briefly
7. Pull some thick cream into the sauce
8. Arrange the sauce on a plate
9. Place the fricandeau in it and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fricassee d'Agneau aux Champignons (Lamb Fricassee with Mushrooms)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb meat, cut into pieces
- Lukewarm water
- Butter
- Mushrooms, cleaned and finely sliced
- Green parsley, finely chopped
- Flour
- Broth as needed

## Directions

1. Cut the lamb meat into pieces and place in lukewarm water
2. Let it rest for 1/2 hour so it becomes white in color
3. Put butter into a casserole and braise some cleaned, finely sliced mushrooms in it
4. Add finely chopped green parsley
5. Dust with flour and roast it slightly
6. Pour the necessary broth over it
7. Add the dried meat from the water, stir well together, cover and braise gently
8. During braising, stir the meat carefully and frequently
9. When braised, place the meat on a platter, remove fat from the sauce and pour it over the meat

title: Fricassee d'Agneau aux Petits Pois (Lamb Fricassee with Peas)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces butter
- 1/2 liter fine peas
- Lamb meat, cut into pieces
- Flour with finely chopped green parsley
- Broth as needed

## Directions

1. Put butter into a casserole with 1/2 liter fine peas and the lamb meat cut into pieces
2. Let it braise covered
3. When the peas are tender, add some flour mixed with finely chopped green parsley
4. Pour the necessary broth over it
5. Let it braise until the meat is tender
6. Arrange the meat and sauce on a platter and serve

title: Fricassee of Lamb (Fricaſſee von Lammfleiſch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb meat
- butter
- beef broth
- wine
- ginger
- pepper
- cloves
- flour
- salt
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Chop the meat into small pieces, wash it clean, let it sweat in a casserole with butter, and cover it so it becomes nicely yellow.
2. Meanwhile, make a sauce from beef broth, wine, ginger, pepper and cloves, let it cook, add the meat to it, pour some of the butter over it, and bind it with flour.
3. Then let it cook sufficiently, season it with salt, and garnish when serving with finely cut lemon peel.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fricassee of Larded Veal (Fricaſſee von geſpicktem Kalbfleiſch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- veal cutlet from the leg
- small bacon
- beef broth
- pepper
- mace
- whole onions
- garlic cloves

## Directions

1. Cut thick slices from a veal cutlet, about three fingers wide, tap them slightly with a hammer, and lard them as one would a partridge with small bacon.
2. Place them in a pan, let them brown, pour beef broth over them, and add pepper, mace and whole onions.
3. When it has reduced somewhat, add a couple of cloves of minced garlic and let it simmer in the sauce.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fricassee of Leftover Roasted Veal (Fricaſſee von uͤbrig gebliebenem Kalbsbraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted veal
- butter
- beef broth
- salt
- pepper
- mace
- half a small onion
- egg yolks
- vinegar
- parsley

## Directions

1. Remove the brown surface from the roast, cut it into pieces, sweat it in a casserole with butter, add beef broth, salt, pepper, mace, and a half small diced onion, let everything cook together for a short time.
2. Then stir a couple of egg yolks with a little vinegar, add some finely chopped parsley to it, and pour it into the veal, after which let it become thick over the fire.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fricassee of Pigeons (Fricaſſee von Tauben)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- butter
- mushrooms
- green parsley
- beef broth
- wine
- mace

## Directions

1. Cut the pigeons into four pieces, let them roast in hot butter, add some mushrooms and green parsley, pour beef broth and wine over them, and let everything come to a boil together.
2. Finally, add some mace and a small piece of butter.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fricassée of Chicken with Mushrooms (Fricassée de Poulets aux Champignons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- finely picked and dressed chickens
- fat
- onion, thinly sliced
- root vegetables, thinly sliced
- mushrooms, thinly sliced
- whole spices
- soup stock

## Directions

1. Place finely picked and dressed chickens in a casserole containing fat, thinly sliced onion, root vegetables, mushrooms, and whole spices
2. When the liquid that forms has evaporated, pour in enough soup stock to cover the chickens
3. Braise uncovered until tender
4. Remove chickens and strain the sauce through a sieve
5. Prepare a butter sauce with this sauce
6. Cut chickens into attractive pieces
7. Arrange on a serving dish and pour the sauce over

title: Fricaſſirte Fröſche (Fricasseed Frogs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Onion
- Pea broth
- Lemon peel
- Frogs
- Milk cream
- Egg yolks
- Cold water
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Warm a piece of butter in a pan
2. Add a spoonful of flour and half an onion
3. Let it cook only a moment until it does not brown
4. Fill with pea broth
5. Add some lemon peel and let it cook for a while
6. Place the frogs in the sauce
7. Add a spoonful of milk cream and let simmer somewhat more
8. In a small pot, take two or three egg yolks, half a spoonful of cold water and the juice of a lemon
9. Strain the sauce into this mixture
10. Heat constantly at a coal fire while stirring
11. Pour over the frogs on a platter
12. Sprinkle with lemon peel
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fridatten mit Champignonfülle (Pancakes with Mushroom Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- pancakes
- 3 large mushrooms
- 4 dkg hot butter
- chopped parsley
- some broth or water
- salt
- lemon juice
- some cream
- ½ l milk
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and finely chop the mushrooms
2. In 4 dkg hot butter, lightly fry chopped parsley
3. Add the mushrooms and pour in some broth or water
4. Add salt, lemon juice, and a few tablespoons cream
5. Let stew for 8-10 minutes (should not be very wet)
6. Spread the mushroom mixture on the pancakes and roll
7. Place in a buttered dish
8. Pour milk beaten with 1 egg yolk over
9. Bake in the oven for 15-20 minutes

title: Fridatten mit Nierenfülle (Pancakes with Kidney Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- pancakes
- pork kidneys
- 5 dkg hot fat
- ½ scallion
- vinegar
- water
- salt
- parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Skin, wash, and slice the kidneys thinly
2. 15 minutes before serving, lightly brown ½ scallion in 5 dkg hot fat
3. Add the kidneys and roast them quickly over high heat
4. Add a few drops of vinegar and water
5. Let roast a few more minutes and salt
6. Spread on the pancakes
7. Roll the pancakes with the closed side down on a flat dish
8. Make cuts on top so the filling is visible

title: Fridatten à la Carline (Pancakes à la Carline)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l milk
- 1 egg
- salt
- flour as needed
- 20-25 dkg cooked beef hash, or 2 small pork kidneys, or 3 large mushrooms
- parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Make pancakes from the milk, 1 egg, salt, and flour (see Menu 16)
2. Distribute the hot prepared hash on the pancakes, roll them, place on a dish, garnish with parsley, and serve
3. Alternatively, fill them with kidneys or mushrooms

title: Fridattennudeln (Fried Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l milk
- 1 egg
- salt
- 12-15 dkg flour
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Whisk the milk with 1 egg, salt and flour until smooth.
2. Pour some melted fat into a Palatschinken pan, let it get hot, pour it back, pour in a small ladle of dough, let it spread thin and round, and fry on both sides until light brown.
3. Add fat to the pan again, let it get hot and repeat.
4. Roll the finished Fridatten, cut into noodle-like strips and serve warm.

title: Fried Artichokes (Artichauts frits)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt water
- lemon juice
- beer or wine batter
- hot lard

## Directions

1. Peel and remove the leaves from the artichokes, cook them soft in salted water with lemon juice
2. Dip in beer or wine batter, fry in hot lard and serve heaped on a platter

title: Fried Calf's Brain (Friture de cervelle de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brain
- Flour
- Beaten eggs
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Clean and peel the brain and cook in salt water
2. When completely cooled, cut in half and cut each half lengthwise
3. Dip in flour, then in beaten eggs, and coat in breadcrumbs (bread)
4. Shortly before serving, fry in very hot fat

title: Fried Chicken (Backhühner)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned and washed chicken
- salt
- breadcrumbs for breading
- hot lard
- lemon slices
- parsley

## Directions

1. Quarter the cleaned and washed chicken and salt it.
2. Let it sit for one hour.
3. Dry it between cloth, bread it, and fry it very slowly in hot lard.
4. Drain and arrange on a platter with lemon slices and parsley decoration.
5. The neck with head can remain on one quarter and be fried together.
6. Stomach and liver can also be breaded and fried last.

title: Fried Chicken Breast Fillets (Filets de poulardes frites)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken breast fillets with wing bone attached
- Flour
- Eggs, beaten
- Breadcrumbs
- Fat for frying

## Directions

1. Separate the two breast parts lengthwise from the carcass so that the wing bone remains attached.
2. Flatten it with a flat knife.
3. Handle them as the fried chickens.

title: Fried Chicken Drumsticks (Cuisses de poulets frits)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken drumsticks
- Flour
- Eggs, beaten
- Breadcrumbs
- Fat for frying

## Directions

1. Prepare the drumsticks as for the stuffed chicken drumsticks and braise them until tender.
2. Set them aside with their liquid and after they have cooled completely, remove them, lay them on cloth and let them dry.
3. Dip them in flour, dunk them in beaten eggs, roll them in breadcrumbs and fry them on both sides in a casserole with finger-high very hot fat until golden.
4. Arrange them neatly in a serving dish, sprinkle them with fried green parsley and line the edge of the dish with lemon wedges.
5. They can also be served with fine green peas, puree, vegetables etc.

title: Fried Chicken Viennese Style (Ponlets frits à la Viennoise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Young, tender chicken
- Flour
- Eggs, beaten
- Breadcrumbs
- Fat for frying (pork fat and beef marrow)
- Salt
- Green parsley, fried
- Salad or compote

## Directions

1. Choose a young, tender bird. Dip the chickens, which have been freshly killed, into boiling water, lay them on a board, flatten them with the flat of the hand and quickly remove the feathers.
2. When this is done, remove the entrails and clean the chickens in cold water. Poke out the eyes, break off the beak but leave the head attached.
3. After cutting off the legs to the knee, split the chickens lengthwise and divide each half sharply at the thighbone.
4. Twist the wings backward toward the neck, cut into the joints of the thighbones and tuck the thighbones inward.
5. Salt the pieces, dip them in flour, dunk them in beaten eggs, roll them in breadcrumbs and just before serving fry them in a casserole in finger-high hot fat (one part pork fat and one part beef marrow) until golden on both sides.
6. Remove the chickens with a skimming ladle, place them on blotting paper in a serving dish and put them in the open oven.
7. Meanwhile, dip the chicken wings, necks and feet in flour, eggs and breadcrumbs, fry them in the hot fat, then remove them with the skimming ladle, place them in a serving dish and add the fried chicken.
8. Sprinkle them with fried green parsley and serve with salad or compote of choice.

title: Fried Eggs (Oeufs frites)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Lard or clarified butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Heat lard or clarified butter to a finger's height in a casserole until very hot.
2. Break eggs into the hot fat and fry for a few minutes.
3. When serving, sprinkle with a little salt and pepper and serve with sauces, cabbage salad, or similar dishes.

title: Fried Pike Fillets (Filets von Hechten gebacken)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and washed pike
- salt
- vinegar
- eggs
- flour
- white bread, grated
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from a cleaned and washed pike as if making pike forcemeat
2. Cut into pieces approximately two fingers thick
3. Lay in salt and vinegar for half an hour
4. Dry well
5. Dip several times in beaten eggs
6. Coat with a mixture of flour and grated white bread
7. Fry quickly in butter or lard until golden
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Fried Potatoes (Pommes de terre frites)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Raw potatoes
- Round cutter
- Salt
- Hot lard

## Directions

1. Peel raw potatoes, cut them out with a round cutter so they all have the same form, cut them into slices, salt them slightly, bake them in hot lard, remove them with a wooden spoon as soon as they have a beautiful golden color, and serve them immediately with various meat dishes. They can also be served on a napkin at tea.

title: Fried Potatoes Second Method (Pommes de terre frites)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Raw potatoes
- Cold water
- Fat
- Salt

## Directions

1. Peel raw potatoes, wash them in cold water, and cut them into slices 2 knife-back-widths thick.
2. Fill deep pans (as shown in Plate II with wire sieves inside) three-quarters full with fat and place on the fire. Put the cut potatoes into one pan in hot fat, stirring constantly until they have a fine crust but still feel soft in the middle.
3. Lift them out with the sieve, salt them, and shake them into the fat in another pan which has meanwhile become boiling hot, let the potatoes bake golden yellow with constant stirring, then transfer them to blotting paper or clean cloth to remove excess fat, and arrange them immediately on a platter.
4. They must be served immediately.

title: Fried Salsify (Salsifis frits)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Stewed salsify roots from recipe 366
- Egg yolks
- Hot lard

## Directions

1. To the stewed salsify roots, stir in a few egg yolks and then bake them piece by piece together with the sauce in very hot lard.

title: Fried Stockfish (Morue Rötie)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked stockfish
- Flour
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Hot fat

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked stockfish into desired pieces.
2. Dip these pieces in flour, dunk in whisked, beaten eggs, roll in breadcrumbs.
3. Fry on both sides until golden in very hot fat.
4. Remove with a slotted spoon, place on blotting paper and arrange in a bowl, sprinkled with blanched, chopped parsley and garnished with lemon wedges.
5. The fried stockfish can also be served with vegetables or mustard sauce.

title: Fried Trout in Oil (Forellen in Öl gebrätelt)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- small trout
- salt
- fine table oil or olive oil

## Directions

1. Scale the small trout, wash inside and outside thoroughly
2. Make three diagonal slits on each side
3. Rub with salt
4. Place in a bowl
5. Pour fine table oil (or olive oil if available) over them
6. Let stand for 1/3 hour
7. Pour the oil from the bowl onto a wide pan
8. Add more oil if needed
9. Fry the trout on both sides in the hot oil
10. Arrange on a platter
11. Garnish with green parsley and lemon slices
12. Serve with mustard, beaten mustard sauce, and cooked potatoes

title: Fried Young Chickens (Ausgebackene junge Hühner)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- water
- parsley
- shallots
- onions
- basil
- cloves
- lemon peel
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- bouillon
- eggs
- white bread, grated
- flour
- lard (schmalz)

## Directions

1. Clean and singe the chickens
2. Cut them properly and lay in lukewarm water to become white
3. After an hour, spread them on a clean cloth and let them dry
4. Make a marinade of fine parsley, shallots, onions, basil, cloves, lemon peel, some vinegar, salt, pepper and bouillon
5. Set the cut chickens in the marinade on hot ashes so they can take on a good flavor
6. Finally remove them from the marinade to cool
7. Dip in beaten eggs
8. Coat with grated bread and flour
9. Fry somewhat quickly in lard until golden
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Friederiken-Torte (Friederike Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg butter
- 12 dkg sugar
- 12 dkg swollen grated almonds
- grated nuts
- 3 dkg raw grated almonds
- 7 dkg sugar
- lemon zest
- jam

## Directions

1. Make crumb dough on board from butter, sugar, and swollen grated almonds
2. Let rest 15 minutes
3. Divide into 4 parts and roll each round like cake form base
4. Place each dough layer on unbuttered baking sheet
5. Bake at higher heat
6. Coat two layers with nut filling, third layer with jam
7. Assemble together and place fourth layer on top
8. Press down
9. Let cake stand 1-2 days
10. Sprinkle with sugar or glaze white
11. For nut filling: Cook grated nuts, raw grated almonds, sugar, and lemon zest with 1 dl water for several minutes, then cool

title: Frisch Posteten (Fish Pâté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Fried or braised fish, cut into small pieces
- Piquant sauce with wine, especially Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Prepare these like the 'Poultry-Pasteten' (No. 1075), with the difference that they are filled, instead of with hache, with hard-boiled eggs or with any fried or braised fish cut into small pieces, to which one adds several spoonfuls of piquant sauce with wine, especially Madeira wine.

title: Frivatetten Brüssel (Consommé aux Paquerets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1/2 liter milk
- 10 deca butter
- 5 egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg (optional)
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Make beaten fritters from 4 eggs
2. Cut these into noodle-like pieces
3. Cook in 1/2 liter boiling milk until done
4. Meanwhile, beat 10 deca butter until fluffy
5. Beat in 5 egg yolks
6. Add some salt, the fritters, nutmeg if desired, and mix everything well
7. Lightly fold in the snow from 5 egg whites
8. Cook in steam or bake and treat like Gries-Schwerl

title: Frosch-Suppe (Frog Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- frogs, cleaned
- butter
- finely chopped green parsley
- flour
- pea water
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- cream
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Braise the cleaned frogs in a piece of butter with finely chopped green parsley
2. Dust with a couple spoonfuls of flour
3. Braise again
4. Fill with pea water
5. Add a little salt and nutmeg flower
6. When the soup has reduced and the frogs are soft, add 2 spoonfuls of cream
7. Serve over toasted bread
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Frosch-Suppe (Frog Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 peeled and finely cut turnips
- 1 liter pea water
- 4 cleaned, thinly sliced champignons
- 5 decagrams butter
- 10 whole frogs or 20 frog legs
- 2 liters pea water or broth
- frog bones
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter cream
- Madeira wine, white table wine, or champagne (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the turnips in 1 liter of pea water
2. Roast the champignons in 5 decagrams of butter
3. Add the whole frogs or frog legs to the champignons and cook until tender in 2 liters of pea water or broth
4. Remove the meat from the frog bones and set aside
5. Pound the frog bones with the cooked turnips and champignons
6. Stir this mixture together well with the soup
7. Season and bind with 4 egg yolks and 1/4 liter of cream mixed together
8. Pass through a sieve
9. Add the frog meat back to the soup
10. Serve with fried turnips
11. Optionally add Madeira wine, white table wine, or champagne

title: Frosche Cotelettes (Frog Cutlets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Frog legs
- Salt
- Pepper (optional)
- Flour
- Eggs, beaten and salted
- Breadcrumbs
- Hot lard or butter
- Puree or vegetables

## Directions

1. Skin the frog legs and remove the lower legs and cross bone
2. Since one frog leg has little meat, remove the meat from one leg and place it on another leg, ensuring the bone protrudes
3. Tap the meat slightly and form it to resemble a cutlet
4. Salt the cutlets and optionally sprinkle with pepper
5. Dredge in flour
6. Dip in beaten, salted eggs
7. Roll in breadcrumbs
8. Fry in hot lard until golden
9. Remove with a slotted spoon
10. Arrange round-shaped on a serving plate
11. Place any desired puree or vegetables in the center

title: Frucht-Rissoles (Fruit-Filled Pastry Crescents)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rissole dough
- any stewed fruit
- sugar for dusting
- optional: beaten egg whites, egg, and breadcrumbs for alternate filling

## Directions

1. Prepare rissole dough as in the recipe for Rissoles mit Farce
2. Fill with any desired stewed fruit
3. Cut out and fry as described in the basic rissole recipe
4. Dust with sugar and serve as a dessert
5. Alternative filling: use beaten egg whites, egg, and breadcrumbs, using 6 ounces of each

title: Fruchtenimmer (Fruit Juice Beverage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice from stewed fruit
- Water

## Directions

1. Mix the juice from stewed fruit with some water.

title: Fruechtchen-Rissoles (Fruit Rissoles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Rissole dough with farce (see Dir. 1123)
- Desired cooked fruit

## Directions

1. Prepare them like rissoles with farce (see Dir. 1123), with the difference that instead of farce, use any desired cooked fruit
2. After the rissoles have been baked, dust them with sugar and arrange them in a bowl, serve as a flour dish
3. The fruit rissoles can also be filled with whipped egg whites, 3 tablespoons, honey and breadcrumbs, 6 tablespoons of each

title: Fruit Pastry for Marmalade (Früchtchen-Bäckerei zum Marmeladebelag)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Finely sifted sugar
- Cold firm marmalade
- Water

## Directions

1. Spread finely sifted sugar on board
2. Make depression in center
3. Add enough cold firm marmalade to make stiff dough
4. Moisten hands with water and form into desired figures such as apples and pears
5. Place on white writing paper
6. Dry in well-heated oven

title: Frösche in Fricassee-Sauce (Frog Fricassee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Approximately 20 frogs
- Salt water
- Butter
- Flour
- Water from cooking the frogs
- Egg yolks
- Soup

## Directions

1. Cook the frogs in salted water until tender
2. Meanwhile, roast flour in hot butter
3. Pour the water in which the frogs were cooked onto this, stirring and cooking well
4. Mix egg yolks with a ladle of cold soup and stir into the sauce
5. Arrange the cooked frog legs in a serving bowl
6. Pour the sauce over them and serve

title: Fröſche, gebackene (Fried Frogs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful, large frog legs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Parsley
- Onions
- Good wine vinegar
- Beer batter or eggs and breadcrumbs
- Lard for frying
- Parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Wash the frog legs
2. Marinate them for some time in salt, pepper, fine herbs, parsley, onions and good wine vinegar
3. Dry them thoroughly
4. Coat in beer batter and fry in lard, or alternatively coat in beaten eggs and grated breadcrumbs
5. Serve with fried parsley as garnish
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Früchte-Brot (Fruit Bread)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 24 drams brown sugar
- 15 egg whites
- 24 drams flour
- 4 drams powdered almonds
- 4 drams finely cut citron
- 4 drams currants
- 4 drams raisins
- 4 drams candied peel
- 4 drams candied lemon peel
- butter for greasing the baking form

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg yolks with 24 drams brown sugar for 1/2 hour
2. At the same time, fold in the stiffly beaten snow of 15 egg whites with 24 drams flour, 4 drams powdered almonds, 4 drams finely cut citron, 4 drams currants, 4 drams raisins, 4 drams candied peel, and 4 drams candied lemon peel
3. Pour the mixture into a baking form greased with butter and dusted with flour
4. Bake at moderate heat for approximately 1 hour (baking time depends on how full the form is)
5. Turn out onto a sieve or board and slice the bread into thick pieces the following day

title: Früchten-Eis (Fruit Icing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- white icing base
- fruit juice or marmalade

## Directions

1. Prepare as white icing, only using any desired fruit juice instead of lemon juice, namely the sieved pulp of fresh fruit or marmalade dissolved with some lukewarm water
2. If the color of the icing is not beautiful enough, one can add alkermes or cochineal color for red, or egg yolks for yellow if needed

title: Früchten-Kuchen (Fruit Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 egg yolks
- 16 deca sugar
- 6 egg whites
- 12 deca semmel crumbs
- small fruits such as gooseberries

## Directions

1. Beat 7 egg yolks with 16 deca sugar for 1/4 hour
2. Lightly mix in the stiff peaks of 6 egg whites with 12 deca semmel crumbs
3. Fill into a baking pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
4. Allow desired small fruits, such as gooseberries and the like, to sink into the dough
5. Treat as with the Brown Cherry or Morello Cherry Cake (No. 1460)

title: Früchten-Strudel (Fruit Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca beaten butter
- 20 deca vanilla sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter milk cream
- 6 egg whites
- Strudel dough made from 1/4 liter flour
- blanched almonds, cut into thin sticks
- peeled oranges, cut into cubes
- fine citron peel, cut into thin strips
- figs, cut into fine pieces
- dates

## Directions

1. Beat 12 deca butter with 20 deca vanilla sugar
2. Stir in 6 egg yolks one after another
3. Mix with 1/4 liter milk cream
4. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
5. Prepare strudel dough from 1/4 liter flour, stretched thin
6. Spread with this mixture
7. Sprinkle with blanched almonds cut into thin sticks, peeled oranges cut into cubes, fine citron peel, figs and dates cut into fine pieces
8. Roll lightly together
9. Treat as for Ham Strudel (No. 1229)

title: Frühjahrs-Sauce (Spring Sauce: Sauce à la printanière)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 1 handful each of spinach and sorrel leaves
- 1 bunch green parsley and chervil
- some chives and tarragon
- salt water
- butter sauce
- some lemon juice
- 1 1/2 liters broth
- several spoonfuls wine

## Directions

1. Put spinach, sorrel leaves, green parsley, chervil, chives, and tarragon in boiling salted water without covering it so it retains its color
2. Put it in cold water and let this drain
3. Press the cooked greens strongly and cut finely
4. Put it in a butter sauce with some lemon juice
5. Pour in 1 1/2 liters broth and several spoonfuls wine
6. Let it boil thoroughly and pass it

title: Frühlings- oder Sommer-Suppe (Spring or Summer Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- young yellow turnips and young white turnips in equal quantity
- 3 liters broth
- 1 handful green beans, cut into pieces
- equal amount of green peas
- thin asparagus
- 2 pieces beautiful cauliflower

## Directions

1. Cut young turnips into desired pieces or cut out with a cutter, wash thoroughly.
2. Pour 3 liters of broth over them and cook until fairly tender.
3. Add 1 handful of green beans cut into pieces, an equal amount of green peas and thin asparagus.
4. Half an hour later, cut 2 pieces of beautiful cauliflower into small florets and add.
5. Let this soup cook until everything is tender and, after it has been properly seasoned, serve it over roasted bread rolls.

title: Frühlingskräutersuppe (Spring Herb Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 h spring herbs (chervil, blessed thistle, ground ivy, sorrel, spinach, yarrow)
- 6 dkg butter
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the herbs
2. Place in a pot with cold salted water and cook for 20 minutes
3. Strain and chop finely
4. Make a roux with 6 dkg butter, pour on beef broth and add the herbs
5. If desired, thicken lightly and serve with bread slices

title: Frühlingskräutersuppe (Spring Herb Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chervil
- Bertram herb
- Ground ivy
- Sorrel
- Spinach
- Yarrow
- Cold salt water
- 6 dkg butter
- Beef broth
- Bread slices

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the herbs
2. Place in a pot with cold salt water
3. Cook for 20 minutes
4. Strain and chop finely
5. Make a roux with 6 dkg butter
6. Pour beef broth over the roux
7. Add the herbs
8. Bind if desired
9. Serve with bread slices

title: Fürstenbrot (Prince's Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar equal in weight to 2 eggs
- 2 whole eggs
- Flour equal in weight to 2 eggs
- Raisins equal in weight to 2 eggs
- Softened almonds, cut into sticks

## Directions

1. Beat sugar equal to 2 eggs with 2 whole eggs for 15 minutes
2. Mix in flour, raisins, and softened, stick-cut almonds
3. Spread in a long, not-too-wide form on buttered sheet
4. Bake at moderate heat until light brown
5. Slice thin only the next day

title: Galantine Indianer (Turkey Galantine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey
- Stuffing (triple the amount needed for a normal stuffed turkey)
- Cold pâté stuffing (optional)

## Directions

1. The treatment is exactly the same as described in recipe 854 'Galantine von Poularbe', with the difference that for a turkey you need triple the amount of stuffing.
2. Alternatively, the turkey can also be filled with cold pâté stuffing and served as a galantine.

title: Galantine of Another Kind (Galantine de poulet et foie-gras)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whole duck liver
- 16 deca of bacon
- 16 deca of pork or veal meat
- 6 deca of bread rolls softened in milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 8 deca of diced second half of duck liver
- 8 deca of truffles
- 8 deca of bacon

## Directions

1. Cut the duck liver, bacon, and pork or veal meat very finely
2. Place in a mortar and pound very finely with the bread rolls softened in milk and well pressed out, egg yolks, and whole egg
3. Pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
4. Cut the remaining duck liver, truffles, and bacon into fine dice
5. Add this to the passed meat, season, add spices, and mix well together
6. Fill the loosened skin of the poularde with this mixture and treat further as indicated in the preceding recipes

title: Gallerte von Krebsen und Schnecken (Crayfish and Snail Jelly)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 crayfish
- 30 snails
- One loth of fresh butter
- Two cleaned and finely chopped veal feet
- 8 pounds (two and a half measures) of spring water
- Half a quench of ground nutmeg
- A handful of chervil herb

## Directions

1. Clean the crayfish and snails as if they were to be eaten
2. Pound them small
3. Set them with the fresh butter on a gentle fire
4. Add the chopped veal feet and spring water
5. Let them cook together until only one pound of broth remains
6. Throw in the ground nutmeg and handful of chervil herb
7. Let it boil once more and press it through linen cloth
8. Set it in the cellar so it congeals like a jelly
9. This amount provides food for four to five days
10. A wasted patient who digests well takes two spoonfuls each morning, midday and evening, dissolved in about six spoonfuls of unsalted chicken broth
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gallerte von Schildkröten (Turtle Jelly)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of lean veal
- Liver, heart, blood and meat from one turtle of ordinary size
- Half a measure of water

## Directions

1. Cook the veal, turtle liver, heart, blood and meat with the water for several hours until it becomes thick
2. Strain it through and let it cool
3. The patient takes two to three loth of this in four to six cups of thin broth
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Game Butter (Beurre de Gibier)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from a roasted woodcock
- 10 decagrams fresh butter
- 5 juniper berries

## Directions

1. Pound the meat from the roasted woodcock with 10 decagrams fresh butter and 5 juniper berries very finely in a mortar
2. Strain through a sieve
3. Let it cool
4. In the same way, butter can be prepared from all kinds of game, not only wild game but also domestic poultry
5. With wild game meat, a piece of truffle braised in Madeira wine can be added to the butter

title: Gans, im Ofen gebraten (Goose, Roasted in the Oven)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One goose
- Bacon
- Small whole onions
- Salt
- Pepper
- Seasonings
- Bouillon
- White wine
- Small English potatoes (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the goose as is proper, dry it well, and place it in an oblong roasting pan with bacon, whole small onions, salt, pepper, and seasonings
2. Pour in some bouillon and white wine, and roast it in the oven at moderate heat
3. Baste it often from time to time and turn it
4. When it is roasted through, skim off the fat, dress the goose on the plate, garnish it all around with the onions, and give the fond, reduced briefly and strained, over it
5. Alternatively, small English potatoes can be roasted with the goose, glazed, and used to garnish it
6. The geese may also, for variety, be filled with cooked quince or apple marmalade, with raisins, chestnuts, etc., when they are roasted
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gansbraten (Roasted Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5-3 kg goose
- apples or rolls for stuffing
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the goose, season well inside and out with salt
2. Optionally fill with apples or rolls, place in a roasting pan, add some water beside it, and roast in the oven for 2-3 hours depending on size and age
3. Baste frequently with its own juices and turn once
4. If too much fat accumulates, skim it off before it browns
5. Carve the goose, arrange on a round platter in its original shape, and serve with the sauce which should be a nice brown color
6. Sauerkraut salad or compote can be served alongside

title: Gansleber (Braised Goose Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-2 goose livers
- hot fat
- swollen almonds, cut into sticks
- cloves
- optionally: onion rings
- rice as side dish

## Directions

1. The liver should be nice and rosy. If not completely fresh, wash it and place in cold milk for 2 hours, then wipe dry, otherwise just wipe with melted fat.
2. You can lard it with swollen almonds cut into sticks and cloves.
3. Place it with the rounded side down in a casserole with some hot fat.
4. Let it braise covered for 20-30 minutes, turn it and let it brown on this side as well.
5. Transfer to a dish and salt it.
6. Pour the remaining fat over it.
7. Optionally, let an onion ring turn golden in the fat beforehand.
8. Serve with rice as a side dish. You can also garnish it beautifully: fill a rice mold rinsed with hot water with 30 dkg braised rice, turn it out onto a dish, place the liver in the middle on top, pour the sauce over it, and arrange cooked cauliflower or cabbage sprouts around it.

title: Ganslsuppe (Young Goose Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 young goose
- 1 roll
- 8 dkg butter
- parsley
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the young goose, and cook in salted water for 1.5-2 hours, except for the liver
2. Prepare a parsley sauce with 8 dkg butter and pour it over the strained goose broth, season with salt
3. Cut the young goose into small pieces (except head and feet), add to the soup, simmer for 25 minutes, and serve with bread slices
4. If the liver is not large enough to be roasted separately, add it after 1 hour and let it cook with the rest

title: Geb. Knöderl (Baked Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dkg butter or fat
- 1 egg
- salt
- 2 bread rolls (Semmeln)
- finely chopped parsley
- 8 dkg breadcrumbs
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Cream together 4 dkg butter or fat
2. Mix in 1 egg, salt, 2 bread rolls (trimmed, soaked in water, and well drained), finely chopped parsley, and 8 dkg breadcrumbs
3. Form into dumplings
4. Fry in hot lard, drain, and serve warm

title: Gebacked Apple Slices (Fried Apple Slices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Flour
- Eggs, beaten
- Breadcrumbs
- Fat, very hot
- Sugar, finely ground
- Optional: Rum

## Directions

1. Using a fine round corer, remove the seed cavity and peel the apples
2. Cut them into small finger-thick slices
3. Sprinkle with sugar, moistened with a little rum if desired, and let rest for some time
4. Take the apple slices with a fork, dip in egg batter, and fry on both sides in very hot fat until golden brown, shaking the pan frequently
5. Remove with a slotted spoon, place briefly on blotting paper, arrange on a platter, and serve hot with finely ground sugar sprinkled on top

title: Gebacked Apricots (Fried Apricots)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots
- Sugar, finely ground
- Egg batter
- Fat, very hot

## Directions

1. Cut apricots in half and remove the pits
2. Cut the pits finely
3. Place apricots in a deep bowl, sprinkle with finely ground sugar, and let rest for about 1 hour
4. Take apricots with a fork, dip in egg batter, and fry in very hot fat until light brown
5. Remove with a slotted spoon, place briefly on blotting paper, arrange on a platter, and serve hot with sugar sprinkled on top

title: Gebacked Elderflower (Fried Elderflower)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Fully bloomed elderflower
- Cold water
- Egg batter, egg batter, or thin pancake batter
- Fat, very hot
- Sugar

## Directions

1. To clean, dip the fully bloomed elderflower several times in cold water and place on a cloth to drain
2. Dip the flowers up to the stem in egg batter, egg batter, or thin pancake batter
3. Fry in very hot fat until light brown
4. Remove with a slotted spoon, place briefly on blotting paper, arrange on a platter, and sprinkle with sugar as desired

title: Gebacked Peaches (Fried Peaches)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches
- Egg batter
- Fat, very hot
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Gebacked Apricots (see recipe 1170)

title: Gebacked Plums (Fried Plums)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Softly cooked dried plums
- Blanched almond kernels
- Egg or egg batter
- Fat
- Sugar
- Optional: grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Place softly cooked dried plums on a cloth and dry well
2. Remove the pits and insert blanched almond kernels in their place
3. Dip in egg batter or egg and flour batter
4. Prepare as for Gebacked Apple Slices (recipe 1169)
5. When fried, they may be rolled in grated chocolate mixed with finely ground sugar, after which they are called Schlosserbubchen

title: Gebacked Puffs (Fried Puffs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough as for Gebacked Pies with Plums (recipe 1178)
- 16 Deca rinsed currants (or raisins)
- Fat, very hot
- Sugar, finely ground

## Directions

1. When the dough has risen about one-third higher, mix 16 Deca washed currants into it
2. Using a teaspoon, form pieces of desired size that are blunt at the tip of the spoon and taper into a string at the opposite end
3. Place in very hot fat and let fry evenly, shaking the pan frequently
4. Remove with a slotted spoon, place briefly on blotting paper, arrange in a bowl, sprinkle with finely ground sugar, and serve with a sauce

title: Gebacked Roses and Acacia Blossoms (Fried Roses and Acacia Blossoms)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- White fully bloomed roses or white acacia blossoms
- Egg batter
- Fat, very hot
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Gebacked Elderflower (recipe 1173)

title: Gebackene Creme (Baked Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- about 1/2 Mass (about 1 liter) good milk
- 2 spoonfuls fine flour
- 4 eggs
- 1/4 pound finely sifted sugar
- a nutmeg-sized piece of butter
- orange blossom water or lemon peel or some orange water
- bread crumbs
- lard for frying
- sugar for coating

## Directions

1. Cook about 1/2 Mass of good milk down and set it aside
2. In a bowl, beat 2 spoonfuls of fine flour with 4 eggs, then mix this with the milk very well so it does not become grainy
3. Let it come together on gentle charcoal heat, but do not boil
4. While constantly stirring, add 1/4 pound of finely sifted sugar, a nutmeg-sized piece of butter, and a few drops of orange blossom water
5. Place it back on the charcoal and stir constantly until it becomes thick
6. Coat a cake sheet with butter and pipe the cream a thumb-thick onto it
7. When it has cooled, cut it into oblong or square pieces, coat them with bread crumbs, and fry them nicely in lard
8. After sprinkling with sugar, glaze them in the oven
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebackene Crème à Pangloise (Baked Cream à Pangloise)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 mass and 3 seidel milk
- one piece cinnamon
- 15 whole eggs
- one quarter pound bitter almonds
- three eighth pound sugar
- one lemon

## Directions

1. Let 1 mass and 3 seidel milk with one piece cinnamon boil several times
2. Beat 15 whole eggs in a pot with one quarter pound bitter almonds finely grated on a grater, three eighth pound sugar equally finely grated, and the zest of one lemon also grated, and whisk for a quarter hour
3. Pour the hot milk to it while stirring very strongly
4. Pour the cream onto a plate and let it become firm in a lukewarm oven
5. It can be served cold or warm, or the plate can be set on a casserole with boiling water, covered with a fitting lid, and the cream allowed to become firm in this way
6. All creams must not stand too hot, otherwise they become blistery, which is not good
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebackene Erdäpfelnudeln (Fried Potato Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dkg butter
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- 30 dkg flour
- 1/4 kg cooked, passed, warm potatoes
- yeast dough
- milk
- hot lard
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cream 6 dkg butter and stir in 3 egg yolks and salt
2. Add 30 dkg flour, 1/4 kg cooked and passed warm potatoes, risen yeast dough, and a few spoonfuls of lukewarm milk
3. Mix and knead the dough smooth on a floured board
4. Form thick noodles and place to rise near the stove
5. Fry in hot lard
6. Drain and serve with sugar

title: Gebackene Frösche (Fried Frogs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Frogs
- Salt water
- Flour
- Eggs, beaten and salted
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard or butter, very hot
- Vegetables (optional)

## Directions

1. Use only the thighs of the frogs
2. Peel off the green skin
3. Remove the lower legs
4. To separate the two legs, remove the hip bone
5. Salt the frog legs
6. Dredge in flour
7. Dip in beaten, salted eggs
8. Roll in breadcrumbs
9. Fry on both sides in very hot lard until golden
10. Remove with a slotted spoon
11. Arrange in a serving bowl and serve with vegetables

title: Gebackene Kürbisse
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- gefüllter Kürbis
- Salz
- Mehl
- Butter oder Öl
- Wein- oder Bierteig

## Directions

1. gefüllten Kürbis schneitet man der Länge nach in starke messerrückendick Spalten
2. diese werden gesalzen und 10 Minuten zugedeckt stehen gelassen
3. sie werden mit Mehl bestäubt und in heißer Butter oder Öl lichtgelb gebacken
4. sie können auch in Wein- oder Bierteig getaucht und dann gebacken werden
5. Kürbisse müssen sofort, wenn sie gebacken sind, serviert werden, da sie durch das längere Stehen zu weich werden

title: Gebackene Lerchen (Baked Larks)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- larks
- fresh bacon
- breadcrumbs
- flour
- ginger
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. After the larks have been properly cleaned and gutted, skewer them on a small iron spit
2. Between every two larks, place a small piece of fresh bacon
3. Sprinkle them with breadcrumbs, flour, ginger, pepper and salt, which must all be well mixed together
4. Drizzle them with butter so they remain juicy
5. Before serving, toast breadcrumbs in butter until golden
6. Sprinkle the arranged larks with these crumbs
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebackene Mäuse (Fried Raised Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 dkg flour
- salt
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 5 dkg butter
- 1 egg
- 1½ dkg yeast
- a few tablespoons lukewarm milk
- hot lard for frying
- sugar for sprinkling or grated chocolate for coating

## Directions

1. Warm 25 dkg flour with salt and 2 tablespoons sugar
2. Rub in 5 dkg butter
3. Add 1 egg, the activated yeast, and a few tablespoons of lukewarm milk
4. Beat the dough until it leaves the spoon
5. Cover and place near the stove to rise
6. When nearly doubled in volume, use a spoon dipped in hot lard to drop dough into plenty of hot lard
7. Fry until light brown on both sides
8. Drain and serve either sprinkled with sugar or rolled in grated chocolate

title: Gebackene Nockerl (Fried Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk
- 6 dkg butter or fat
- 1 egg
- Pinch of salt
- 30-40 dkg flour
- Lard for frying

## Directions

1. Cream together 6 dkg butter or fat with 1 egg, a pinch of salt, and the milk
2. Stir in 30-40 dkg flour
3. Beat the batter well
4. Using a spoon dipped in hot lard, drop small dumplings into plenty of hot lard
5. Fry until light brown
6. Let drain
7. Keep warm in the open oven until serving

title: Gebackene Orangen-Creme (Baked Orange Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a measure good cream
- 12 egg yolks
- sugar
- a couple of oranges
- lemons
- flour
- breadcrumbs
- lard

## Directions

1. Make a cream from half a measure good cream with 12 egg yolks, sufficiently sweetened, rubbing with a couple of oranges and lemons
2. Prepare it in the previous manner, place in simmering water, and cook until done
3. When the cream is quite cold, cut into pieces
4. Turn in flour and breadcrumbs
5. Fry quickly in lard
6. Better taste if turned in wine batter and fried in lard
7. For the batter: take 2 handfuls flour, a couple tablespoons fine oil, sugar rubbed with a lemon, mix with white wine to a fluid batter
8. Beat the whites of 6 eggs to snow, stir in, and fry slowly in lard
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebackene Seezungen auf Wiener Art (Fried Sea Tongues, Viennese Style)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo sea tongues
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fresh green parsley, finely cut
- Onion, finely minced
- Flour
- Paradise sauce

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the sea tongues according to the method for gudgeons
2. Cut into equal-sized pieces and brush with oil
3. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, finely cut green parsley and finely minced onion
4. Let rest for 1 hour
5. Dry with a cloth, dip in flour and fry golden on both sides
6. Arrange in a bowl and serve with Paradise sauce

title: Gebackene Topfennudeln (Baked Quark Noodles)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 3 handfuls quark (curd), well pressed
- 4 Loth (about 2 oz) lard
- 1 Seidel (about 1/2 liter) milk
- 2 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 3 spoonfuls yeast
- salt

## Directions

1. Put 1 pound flour in a bowl with 3 handfuls of well-pressed quark, 4 Loth lard, and 1 Seidel milk
2. Let everything warm up and bubble well in a pot
3. Add 2 eggs, 2 egg yolks, and 3 spoonfuls of yeast
4. Pour this in gradually, beat the dough well, and salt it lightly
5. Form oblong noodles from the dough
6. Make a cut in the middle of each noodle and let them rise well
7. Bake them very carefully in lard
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebackene Äpfel (Fried Apple Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Maschen-Äpfel apples
- Flour for dredging
- Eggs for coating
- Breadcrumbs for rolling
- Lard for frying
- Powdered sugar for serving
- Preserves or jam for filling

## Directions

1. Cut a round disk from the side of the apple stem, leaving the stem on the disk.
2. Press a small cutter into the center of each apple, turn it slightly and pull it out with the core house - the apple should not be pierced completely but a knife-back-thick base must remain.
3. After the apples and round disks are peeled, dip them in flour, dip in beaten eggs, roll in breadcrumbs and fry them on both sides until golden yellow in very hot lard.
4. Remove the apples with a skimming spoon, fill the empty space with any desired preserve, cover with the round disks on which the stems are located, sprinkle with finely powdered sugar and serve hot.
5. Usually the apples are served mixed with cream fritters.

title: Gebackener Aal (Breaded Eel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- Flour
- Beaten eggs
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the eel and clean it thoroughly
2. Cut lengthwise in 3 places and salt it
3. Dip in flour, dunk in beaten eggs, and sprinkle with breadcrumbs
4. Fry golden on both sides
5. Arrange in a bowl and serve at table

title: Gebackener Hering (Fried Herring)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 herring
- Milk
- Flour
- Eggs, beaten
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter or lard, very hot
- Green parsley
- Lemon wedges
- Vegetables (optional)

## Directions

1. Skin the herring, remove head and tail, and dissolve the bones
2. Soak the herring in milk for some time
3. Dredge in flour
4. Dip in beaten eggs
5. Roll in breadcrumbs
6. Fry on both sides in very hot lard until golden
7. Arrange in a serving bowl, sprinkle with green parsley
8. Garnish with lemon wedges or serve with vegetables
9. Alternatively, the herring can be fried whole after being skinned, but must be scored on both sides

title: Gebackener Kalbskopf (Fried Calf's Head)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's head
- Salt water
- Salt
- Flour
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Fat for frying

## Directions

1. After preparing and plucking the calf's head as indicated, cook it very tender in salt water
2. Place in cold water, then remove and clean with a cloth and dry
3. Cut into pieces three fingers wide and one finger long
4. Sprinkle with some salt
5. Dredge in flour, dip in beaten eggs, then coat in breadcrumbs
6. Fry in very hot fat two fingers deep
7. Fry the pieces on both sides

title: Gebackener Leberpudding (Baked Liver Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one fresh liver
- 1/2 Pfund smoked bacon
- lemon peel
- shallots
- salt
- nutmeg
- 1 Pfund grated bread crumbs soaked in milk
- 10 whole eggs
- thyme or basil
- fresh butter
- caul

## Directions

1. Scrape the raw liver so that all veins and membranes separate
2. Put it in a dish
3. Stir in 1/2 pound smoked bacon, finely cut, some finely cut lemon peel and shallots, salt and nutmeg
4. Stir in 1 Pfund grated bread crumbs soaked in milk
5. Gradually add 10 whole eggs, and if desired, some thyme or basil
6. Fill the mass into a casserole thickly coated with fresh butter
7. Cover it with caul soaked an hour beforehand
8. Bake it slowly in the oven
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebackener Pudding mit Mandeln (Baked Almond Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pfund almonds
- finely soft-boiled lemon peel
- 4 Pfund sugar
- several spoonfuls rose water
- 4 Pfund butter
- 8 eggs
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Pound 1/2 pound almonds with finely soft-boiled lemon peel, 4 pounds sugar, and several spoonfuls of rose water in a mortar
2. Melt 4 pounds butter
3. Beat 8 eggs and 4 egg whites very thickly
4. Mix the remaining ingredients together
5. Grease a rimmed baking dish with butter
6. Add the mixture and bake slowly for one hour
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebackener Reiß (Baked Rice)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound rice
- milk
- sugar
- finely cut orange peel
- salt
- fresh butter
- a couple of sweet macaroons
- 4 egg yolks
- eggs for coating
- fine bread crumbs
- lard for frying
- sugar for finishing
- ground cinnamon for finishing

## Directions

1. Wash 1/4 pound rice a couple of times in warm water, then in good milk and sugar with finely cut orange peel, some salt and fresh butter, not too soft
2. Pound a couple of sweet macaroons and stir them into the cooked rice
3. Remove from heat and slowly stir in 4 egg yolks
4. Using a tablespoon, form small balls, shape them nicely round, and coat them first in beaten eggs, then in fine bread crumbs
5. A few minutes before serving, fry them until golden, sprinkle with sugar and finely ground cinnamon while still hot, and serve hot
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebackenes Kindskoch (Fried Milk Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cream
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- lard
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a beautiful thick milk pudding from cream
2. Let it boil well
3. Spread on a sheet and let cool
4. Cut into strips
5. Turn in eggs and bread crumbs
6. Fry in lard
7. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebackne Hachs-Krapfen in die Suppe (Fried Meat Fritter in Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- scraps of butter or short crust dough
- flour
- leftover meat
- 1 egg
- lard
- soup

## Directions

1. Mix flour with scraps of butter or short crust dough
2. Knead it out to the thickness of a knife back
3. Make a good hash from leftover meat
4. Beat an egg into it
5. Spread it one knife-back thick on the dough
6. Fold the dough double
7. Cut small fritters with a spoon
8. Fry them in lard
9. Serve with soup
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebetzter Rehschlägel (Marinated Roe Deer Leg)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Roe deer leg, skinned and spitted
- Marinade (as described in No. 703)
- Butter or fat
- Paper for wrapping

## Directions

1. After skinning and spitting the leg, but not salting it, baste it with the marinade specified in No. 703
2. Cover it with a sheet of paper brushed with butter or fat and let it stand covered in a cool place
3. Every day the meat must be turned in the marinade, but on the third or fourth day the marinade is reheated and poured back over the leg strained
4. Do not let it stand longer than eight days
5. Then treat it further as you would the marinated loin
6. Note that if the leg or game in general is very large, naturally cook more liquid with the marinade

title: Gebraten Spanferkel (Roasted Suckling Pig)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig
- Root vegetables
- Vinegar
- Red wine
- Salt
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Cut open the suckling pig and remove the entrails
2. Wash the suckling pig thoroughly, let it soak in water for some time, then remove it and dry it with a cloth
3. Cut it into desired pieces
4. Cook it like boiled meat with root vegetables in vinegar-flavored liquid and red wine
5. When the meat is sufficiently tender, remove it with a slotted spoon onto a platter
6. Pour some broth over it and garnish with horseradish or butter sauce or vinegar and oil
7. Be very careful so that the meat does not boil too much

title: Gebratene Champignons (Grilled Mushrooms)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 liters flour
- 1.5 liters milk
- 3 whole eggs
- beef and pork lard, approximately 1.5 kilos combined

## Directions

1. In a deep bowl, place 1.5 liters flour and gradually pour in 1.5 liters milk while stirring constantly until the dough is completely smooth.
2. Beat in 3 whole eggs and let it rest somewhat.
3. Heat beef and pork lard in a small baking pan until very hot.
4. Dip the mushroom mold into the hot fat, let it become very hot, remove it, turn it over so all fat drips off, quickly dip it halfway into the dough, then quickly place the mold back in the hot fat and hold it there until the mushroom has a beautiful brown color.
5. Carefully remove the baked mushroom with a spoon (as it is very fragile) and place it on a sieve lined with blotting paper, which will absorb any excess fat.
6. Fill these mushrooms either with soup or with ragout.
7. Work quickly to finish the mushrooms.

title: Gebratene Enten mit Orangensaft (Roasted Ducks with Orange Juice)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two ducks
- Butter
- Parsley
- Onions
- Shallots
- Basil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Grated nutmeg
- Orange slices
- Orange juice
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Clean and dress the two ducks properly for roasting
2. Take a piece of butter, roll it repeatedly in finely chopped parsley, onions, shallots, basil, salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
3. Stuff each duck with such a piece of butter with herbs in the belly and put them on the spit
4. When they are nearly done, dress them on a plate, make several cuts lengthwise, sprinkle salt and pepper on them, cover them with orange slices
5. Skim off the fat dripped from the ducks and give it under the ducks
6. Serve them quite hot, squeeze orange juice on them, and garnish the plate with croutons
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebratene Gans mit Kastanien (Roasted Goose with Chestnuts)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One fat goose
- One pound chestnuts
- Goose liver
- Some veal
- Parsley
- Onions
- Butter
- Clear sauce or sauce pikante

## Directions

1. Clean and wash a fat goose well, dry it thoroughly inside and outside, and salt it
2. Roast a pound of chestnuts and cut them into pieces
3. Prepare a well-seasoned forcemeat with the finely chopped goose liver, some veal, parsley, onions, and the chestnuts
4. Let this forcemeat half cook in butter over gentle charcoal fire, stirring frequently, and set aside to cool
5. Fill the goose with it, dress it well so nothing falls out, roast it nicely on the spit or in the oven
6. When the fat is separated, serve it with clear sauce or sauce pikante
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebratene Ganz (Braised Goose)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- salt and pepper
- bacon slices
- raw ham (12 ounces)
- onion and root vegetables
- bay leaf
- spices
- broth (1 liter)
- white wine (1 liter)

## Directions

1. Clean the goose very thoroughly, open it, remove the insides and wash it.
2. Cut off the neck at the breast bone and the wings at the second joint, push the meat from the thighs of the third joint to the body, and remove these thighs as well as the feet below the knee.
3. Tuck the wings under the breast and truss the goose.
4. Sprinkle it with salt and pepper.
5. Put bacon slices into a casserole along with 12 ounces of raw ham, then onion and root vegetables, all finely sliced, 1 bay leaf and whole spices.
6. Place the goose on top and braise covered.
7. After 1/2 hour, pour 1 liter of broth and 1 liter of white wine over it and let the goose braise covered until completely tender - about 1 hour for a young goose, 2 hours for an old one.
8. Remove it then, degrease and pass the broth.

title: Gebratene Indianerflügel (Fried Turkey Wings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey wings
- Fat

## Directions

1. Place the plucked and cleaned wings in a saucepan with some fat and braise them covered for approximately 1/2 hour.

title: Gebratene Indianpflügel (Fried Turkey Wings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey wings, plucked and cleaned
- Fat
- Soup
- Salt
- Spices
- Sauce
- Breadcrumbs
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Place the plucked and cleaned wings in a casserole containing some fat and braise covered for approximately 1/2 hour.
2. Pour some soup over them, salt and season them, and braise covered until they are soft.
3. Set them aside and let them cool in the broth.
4. When cooled, remove the wings and place on a cloth.
5. After completely cooling, cut the wings into desired pieces, brush with bound sauce, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, dip in beaten eggs, coat again in breadcrumbs, and bake in a casserole in finger-high, very hot fat.

title: Gebratene Kalbsleber (Fried Veal Liver)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- salt
- flour
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- fat
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into finger-long and knife-back-thick slices, as with roasted calf liver, lightly salt them, dredge on both sides in flour, dip in beaten salted eggs, coat in breadcrumbs and fry in finger-deep, very hot fat.
2. Arranged on a platter, serve it with fine vegetables at table.

title: Gebratene Lerchen (Roasted Larks)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Larks
- Lard
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Roast the larks like the Krammetsvögel, with the difference that they are not bound with lard strips, but during roasting are dusted with fine breadcrumbs.

title: Gebratene Lerchen (Roasted Larks)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Larks
- Fine semolina crumbs

## Directions

1. Roast larks the same way as thrushes, with the difference that they are not bound with bacon strips, but during roasting are sprinkled with fine bread crumbs

title: Gebratene Striechente (Roasted Teal)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- teal duck
- salt and spices
- fat or butter

## Directions

1. After the ducks are plucked, hold them over a flame or spirits lamp to singe them, then wash them, but do not scrub - only wipe inside and out clean with a cloth.
2. Season and truss them.
3. Either spit-roast them for 15-20 minutes over strong heat, or place them in the oven where they are basted with melted fat for 3/4 to 1 hour until beautifully golden brown with crispy skin, basting frequently.
4. Remove the trussing strings, carve the ducks and arrange them garnished with orange and lemon slices.
5. Orange sauce or any desired compote may also be served with them.

title: Gebratene Zwetschkenknödel von Germteig (Fried Plum Dumplings with Yeast Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- dough and plums as for Zwetschkenknödel von Germteig
- fat for frying
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as in Zwetschkenknödel von Germteig (Recipe 1225), with the difference that instead of cooking them in water, fry them in a saucepan in very hot fat
2. Remove with a slotted spoon, arrange on a plate and sprinkle with desired spice sugar

title: Gebratenen Indian à l'Italienne (Roasted Turkey Italian Style)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey (Indian)
- poultry livers
- truffles or mushrooms
- mushrooms or truffles, chopped
- young onions, chopped
- parsley, chopped
- oil or butter
- butter and flour
- good fond
- white wine
- lemon juice
- garlic (optional)
- bacon sheets and paper

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the turkey for roasting
2. Make a forcemeat from some poultry livers, truffles or mushrooms, well seasoned
3. Fill the turkey with this and prepare it, threading it on the spit
4. When it takes color, wrap it with bacon sheets and paper, roast it slowly, and frequently baste with hot butter
5. Meanwhile finely hack some cleaned mushrooms or truffles, young onions, and parsley, pass through oil or butter, then add a piece of butter mixed with flour
6. Add some good fond and a small glass of good white wine and let the sauce cook well when seasoned
7. Garlic can sometimes be added, but it can easily be omitted
8. Finally squeeze lemon juice into it, remove the bacon sheets, arrange the turkey on the platter, and serve this sauce hot underneath
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebratener Aal (Eel Roasted on a Spit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- salt
- pepper
- sage or bay leaves
- lemon
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. After preparing the eel as already described, cut it into 3-finger wide pieces; these are skewered crosswise on a small spit; after each piece put either 2 sage or 2 bay leaves. Dust them with salt and pepper and tie them to a large spit, roast the eel pieces quickly (approximately 20 minutes) and serve them immediately with lemon pieces. You can also, when the fish is half roasted, sprinkle it with breadcrumbs and finish roasting it this way. Instead of on the spit, you can roast the eel in the same way in a flat pan in the oven.

title: Gebratener Hase (Roasted Hare)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare
- Butter
- Marinade and cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the head and front legs from the hare, or leave them and position them together, either one over the other or crossed
2. After the hare is skinned and prepared, spit it and roast it like venison, either plain or with marinade and cream

title: Gebratener Kalbsschlägel mit Mark (Roasted Veal Leg with Marrow)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- veal leg
- marrow, cut
- truffles, cut (optional)
- bacon
- butter
- thinly sliced onion
- mushrooms
- approximately 1/2 liter white wine
- white soup
- salt

## Directions

1. After the leg has been trimmed, skinned and salted, lard it with cut marrow and, if desired, cut truffles and bacon.
2. Put butter, thinly sliced onion and mushrooms into a casserole, place the leg on top and pour approximately 1/2 liter white wine and white soup over it, but altogether only enough so that the liquid does not cover the meat, and braise until the meat is soft.
3. Then remove it, place it on a platter, skim the fat from the sauce and pour the strained sauce over the meat.

title: Gebratener Schöpfenschlägel (Roasted Mutton Leg)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton leg
- Bacon
- Mutton meat
- Green parsley
- Basil
- Thyme
- Marjoram
- Aromatic herbs
- Ginger
- Nutmeg
- Butter
- Gooseberry juice
- Later: grape juice
- Capers
- Wine
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Remove the skin as thinly as possible
2. Lard the leg with bacon and roast as usual
3. Chop another piece of mutton meat finely
4. Mix it with green parsley, basil, thyme, marjoram, and other fragrant herbs as desired
5. Add ginger, a little nutmeg, butter, a couple spoonfuls of expressed gooseberry juice (or later expressed grape juice), capers, and wine
6. Let it cook together
7. Before serving, chop a couple of eggs finely, mix breadcrumbs into them, add to the broth, and let it boil once more
8. Baste the leg on the spit several times with this broth
9. Remove from spit, place on a plate, and pour the broth over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebratenes Lammfleisch (Fried Lamb)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb pieces, 2 fingers wide and 1 finger long
- Salt
- Flour
- Eggs, beaten
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter or lard, very hot
- Green parsley, finely chopped
- Salad or fine vegetables

## Directions

1. Cut the front part of the lamb, namely the breast and shoulder, into pieces 2 fingers wide and 1 finger long
2. Salt the pieces and let them rest for 1/4 hour
3. Dip them in flour, then in beaten eggs
4. Roll them in breadcrumbs
5. Fry them on both sides in very hot butter or lard
6. Remove them with a wooden spoon and let them rest briefly on blotting paper
7. Arrange them neatly on a platter, cover with fried green parsley, and serve with salad or fine vegetables

title: Gebratenes Schöpsenstück von der Rippe (Roasted Mutton Rib Piece)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton from the lower part of the back
- thin slices of ham
- bacon strips seasoned with pepper and shallots
- thin twine
- paper
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Carefully loosen the skin from the lower part of the back with a knife so it remains whole and attached at the bottom
2. Lay some thin slices of ham and bacon strips seasoned with pepper and shallots on it so the entire back is covered
3. Draw the skin back over it
4. Wrap it with thin twine
5. Wrap the back with paper
6. Roast it on a spit
7. When it is almost done roasting, remove the paper
8. Sprinkle it with breadcrumbs
9. Let it roast completely until tender
10. You can make a brown sauce with capers and olives, or a ragout to serve with it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebratenes wälsches Huhn (Roasted Guinea Fowl)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- young and fat guinea fowl, slaughtered some days before
- salt
- bacon sheets for binding
- Sauce Robert

## Directions

1. Clean it and eviscerate it properly
2. Dry it off, flame it, cut off the wings, and prepare it for roasting
3. Season it with salt
4. Bind it with bacon sheets and place it on the spit
5. Roast it slowly so it gradually takes on color
6. When done and arranged on the platter, serve with Sauce Robert
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gebrätene, junge Rebhühner (Roasted Young Partridges)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- young partridges
- fine lard
- lard slices
- twine
- fat
- salt

## Directions

1. After the partridges have been plucked, stuffed, opened and cleaned, dress them as indicated for Capon No. 735.
2. Thread the breasts with fine lard or wrap them with lard slices, bind these with fine twine and roast the birds over strong fire for approximately 1/4 hour on the spit.
3. After this time, remove the partridges, cut the twine, carefully pull it out, divide the birds lengthwise down the middle, arrange them on a platter, cover with the lard slices and pour the defatted juice over them.
4. Alternatively, the partridges can be roasted in the oven by placing them in a pan in which there is some fat, and basting them frequently during roasting with the resulting juice.

title: Gebäckene Creme in Chocolate (Baked Cream in Chocolate)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cream mass from baked cream in crusts recipe
- wafer cookies
- water
- beaten eggs
- bread crumbs
- fat for frying
- grated chocolate mixed with sugar

## Directions

1. Cut pieces from the steamed cream mass using a round cutter
2. Wrap in moistened wafer cookies
3. Dip in beaten eggs
4. Sprinkle with bread crumbs
5. Fry in hot fat
6. Roll in grated chocolate mixed with sugar

title: Gebäckene Creme in Krusten (Baked Cream in Crusts)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams flour
- 12 decagrams spice sugar (vanilla, orange, or lemon)
- 1 liter milk
- 6 egg yolks
- butter for greasing mold and dusting

## Directions

1. Mix flour and spice sugar in a deep bowl
2. Gradually stir in 1 liter milk and 6 egg yolks
3. Pour into a buttered mold dusted with flour
4. Cook in steam until the mass is completely firm, which will take approximately 1/2 to 3/4 hour
5. Turn out onto a flour-dusted board and let the mold rest briefly over it
6. When the cream is completely cooled, cut into desired pieces (finger-long and finger-wide strips, square pieces, or use a round cutter)
7. Wrap each piece in very thin, finest puff pastry
8. Brush with beaten eggs
9. Sprinkle with bread crumbs as desired
10. Fry in hot fat
11. Sprinkle with desired spice sugar when serving

title: Gebäckene Creme in Oblaten (Baked Cream in Wafers)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cream mass from previous recipe
- wafer cookies
- water
- beaten eggs
- bread crumbs
- fat for frying
- spice sugar

## Directions

1. Cut pieces from the steamed cream mass
2. Wrap in moistened wafer cookies
3. Dip in beaten eggs
4. Sprinkle generously with bread crumbs
5. Fry immediately in very hot fat
6. Serve sprinkled with spice sugar

title: Gebäckene Schäumchen von Mandelteig (Baked Almond Foam Puffs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams blanched almonds mixed with 1 egg white, finely ground
- 1/4 liter butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 6 decagrams spice sugar
- 8 decagrams flour
- 4 egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Combine ground almonds and egg white mixture in a deep bowl
2. Stir well with 1/4 liter butter, 5 egg yolks, spice sugar, and flour
3. Lightly fold in the beaten egg whites
4. Bake puffs as indicated in the recipe for 'Baked Puffs'
5. Fill with chantilly cream or any desired cream
6. If serving cold, fill with whipped cream, strawberries, or other desired filling

title: Gebünstete Erbsen (Pois verts à l'Etuveé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter fine green peas
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 8 ounces butter
- water
- butter sauce
- salt
- green parsley (optional)

## Directions

1. Place 1 liter of rinsed fine green peas in a casserole
2. Add 1 tablespoon of sugar and 8 ounces of butter
3. Add enough water to just cover the peas
4. Cover and braise until the peas are tender
5. Add some butter sauce, salt, and if desired, a pinch of finely chopped green parsley
6. Mix well and serve

title: Gedämpfte Gänsleber in Aspik (Braised Goose Liver in Aspic)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- aspic
- truffles
- tongue
- hard-boiled eggs
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. When the goose liver is braised, let it stand covered in the casserole until the liver is completely cold.
2. Remove and trim away the fat, wipe clean with a cloth.
3. Cut into strips the thickness of a knife blade and of any desired width.
4. Place a form in ice and pour a finger-high layer of cooled aspic into it.
5. When this has set, decorate with truffles, nicely cut-out tongue, hard-boiled egg white, and lettuce leaves.
6. Add some more aspic and when set, add the goose liver pieces.
7. Continue this way with aspic and liver pieces until the form is full, with aspic on top.
8. After this has stood 1 hour in ice, treat it as the "goose liver cake" with aspic.

title: Gedämpfter Aalfisch (Braised Eel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- butter
- onion
- root vegetables
- thyme
- mixed vegetables
- bay leaf
- red wine
- fish broth
- flour

## Directions

1. After skinning and cleaning the eel, roll it up snail-fashion so that the tail is twisted inward first and the head is directed outward. To keep the eel in this position, pierce it with small skewers, tie it to the bottom of a fish pan and salt it. Into the fish pan put meanwhile 16 pieces butter, 1 small onion, some root vegetables, all cut into leaves, 5 bunches of thyme, from each kind of vegetable 5 grains and 1 bay leaf; let this simmer somewhat, then lay the eel on it, pour over it with 1/2 liter red wine and as much broth that the liquid covers the fish. Let it braise covered for approximately 3/4 to 1 hour. Then lift it out with the bottom, place it on a cloth, cover it with a second cloth so that the fish fat soaks into it, remove the twine and place the eel on the platter. From the broth make a sauce with some butter and flour, strain this and degrease it, if you have some add Madeira wine, bring to a boil and serve in a sauce boat.

title: Gedämpfter Hase (Braised Hare)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare
- Bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Ground bay leaves
- Thyme
- 1 onion
- Vegetables, finely cut
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 sprigs thyme
- 2 pieces clove spice
- Spices, 5 grains of each type
- 6 juniper berries
- Garlic (as desired)
- 0.375 liter white wine
- 0.375 liter soup

## Directions

1. Skin and prepare the hare
2. Thread it with bacon seasoned in salt, pepper, ground bay leaves and thyme
3. Wrap the hare in bacon slices and tie it with twine
4. Put 1 onion, some finely cut vegetables, 1 bay leaf, 2 sprigs thyme, 2 clove spices, 5 grains of each spice, 6 juniper berries, and garlic if desired in a long casserole
5. Lay the hare on top
