6. Pour approximately 0.375 liter white wine and the same amount of soup over it
7. Let this braise covered slowly for approximately 1.5 hours
8. Remove the hare and serve in pieces on a platter
9. Remove fat from the braise, reduce it slightly and serve as sauce
10. Alternatively, this dish can be served cold; let the hare cool in the braise and stand until it sets to jelly
11. When serving, remove twine and bacon, proceed as with venison back, and garnish neatly with aspic

title: Gedämpfter Rostbraten (Braised Pot Roast)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Rostbraten (pot roasts), somewhat thicker slices
- Speckschnitte (bacon strips)
- Salz, Gewürz, fein gefrorener Petersilie und Schalotten (salt, spices, finely frozen parsley and shallots)
- Schinken und Speckplatten (ham and bacon sheets)
- Zwiebel (onion)
- Gelbe Rübe (yellow turnip)
- 1 Lorbeerblatt (bay leaf)
- Wein (wine)
- Optional: Glace (glaze)
- Kartoffelpuree oder gefülltes Kraut oder Linsen Puree oder Knobeln (potato puree or stuffed cabbage or lentil puree or garlic)

## Directions

1. Cut the pot roasts somewhat thicker and lard them with bacon strips that have been rolled in salt, spices, finely frozen parsley and shallots.
2. Line a casserole with ham and bacon sheets.
3. Place the pot roasts on top and add some onion, yellow turnip and one bay leaf.
4. Braise covered for a short time.
5. Then add some wine and let the meat braise gently until tender.
6. Before serving, if you have it, glaze the pot roasts.
7. Strain the broth into a casserole, reduce it somewhat, degrease it and serve in a sauce boat or pour over the pot roasts.
8. Serve with pressed horseradish or stuffed cabbage, or lentil puree, or garlic and the like.

title: Gedämpftes Rindfleisc (Braised Beef)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a beautiful piece of beef (hip-shank)
- salted and elongated cut tongues
- bacon
- salt
- fresh bacon
- onion
- yellow turnips
- parsley root
- celery, sliced in leaves
- lemon peel
- whole pepper
- belly herb (Kuttelkraut)
- half wine
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Lard a beautiful piece of beef (hip-shank) with salted and elongated cut tongues and bacon, and salt it.
2. In a braising pan, put fresh bacon, onion, yellow turnips, parsley root, and celery, sliced in leaves.
3. Add lemon peel and whole pepper, and belly herb.
4. Lay the beef on top, add half wine, some vinegar and water, and let it stew well covered.
5. When soft enough, remove the meat and skim off the fat.
6. Let the root vegetables cook down well.
7. Before plating, put the meat back in the pan and let it braise a little more.
8. Place it on a plate, pass one portion of the sauce through a sieve over it, and serve the other portion of sauce in a bowl.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gedämpftes Rindfleiſch (Steamed Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef for soup
- 8 h vegetables
- 1 onion
- 5 peppercorns and spice kernels
- 1 bay leaf
- some thyme
- 5 dkg hot fat
- water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare beef as usual for soup.
2. One hour before serving, clean, wash and slice the vegetables into leaves.
3. Roast the vegetables, onion, peppercorns, spice kernels, bay leaf and thyme in hot fat until dark.
4. Remove the beef from the soup and place it in the vegetables.
5. Pour on a little water, cover and braise, pouring in more liquid occasionally.
6. The sauce should be nicely brown.
7. Remove the beef, dust the sauce with flour, strain it, season with vinegar and cook together with the beef again.
8. Slice the beef and serve with the sauce on a long platter.

title: Gedünstete Kalbsleber (Braised Veal Liver)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- bacon pieces
- sliced bacon
- whole yellow turnips
- salt
- pepper
- white wine
- broth
- vegetables
- rice

## Directions

1. Skin a calf liver and lard it with pencil-thick, finger-long pieces of bacon.
2. In a casserole put sliced bacon, lay the liver with the larded side up, add whole, cleaned yellow turnips, the necessary salt and, if desired, pepper, pour some white wine and broth over it and let it braise covered for about 1 hour.
3. The liver is then removed, cut into desired pieces and the strained juices served with vegetables or rice alongside the liver.

title: Gedünstete Rettiche (Radishes Braised)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- small monthly radishes
- butter
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Peel small radishes and place in a baking dish with butter and sugar
2. Pour water over them until it reaches them
3. Braise until tender
4. Pour in several tablespoons of butter sauce, stir well together and serve as garnish

title: Gedünstete Rettiche (Stewed Radishes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- kleine Monatsrettiche
- Butter
- Zucker
- Wasser
- Butter-Sauce

## Directions

1. kleine Monatsrettiche werden geschält und mit Butter und Zucker in eine Casserole gegeben
2. soviel Wasser darüber gegossen, daß es über dieselben zusammengeht
3. Rettiche werden weich gedünstet
4. einige Löffel Butter-Sauce werden hinzugegossen
5. alles wird gut untereinander gerührt und als Garnierung zu Tische gegeben

title: Gedünstete Rolladen (Braised Beef Rolls)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- beef suitable for stewing, cut into hand-wide, lengthwise pieces, no thicker than a knife back
- salt and pepper
- one brain, cooked in salt water
- green parsley
- small diced onion
- handful of breadcrumbs
- one spoonful of broth
- small piece of bacon, diced
- bacon
- onion
- yellow turnips
- a few spoonfuls of flour
- broth
- vinegar
- lemon peel, cut
- two spoonfuls of cream milk
- lemon peel, cut lengthwise

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef suitable for stewing and cut it into hand-wide, lengthwise pieces, but no thicker than a knife back
2. Beat and salt them
3. Cook a brain in salt water, cleanly remove the skin, chop it fine, and braise it in butter with some green parsley
4. Add some finely chopped onion, salt, pepper, and a handful of breadcrumbs, also one spoonful of broth, let it come to a boil and cool
5. Chop a small piece of bacon finely, mix it with the brain, coat the meat with it, and tie it together with twine
6. Put bacon, onion, and yellow turnips in a casserole, lay the rolls in it and braise them until they have braised their sauce into themselves and become brownish
7. Take them out, put a couple spoonfuls of flour in the casserole, let it brown, then fill it with broth
8. Add some vinegar and lemon peel, let the sauce cook well, strain it, lay the rolls in it again, add two spoonfuls of cream milk and let it braise a bit more
9. Arrange the rolls in a row on the dish, strain sauce over them and sprinkle with lengthwise-cut lemon peel
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gedünstete Zwiebelsauce (Braised Onion Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoonful fat
- one piece butter
- 6 to 7 onions
- one spoonful flour
- broth to achieve proper consistency
- one tablespoon vinegar
- 3 cloves
- lemon peel, finely cut

## Directions

1. Heat one spoonful fat in a small pan with a piece of butter until it becomes a nice golden-brown color
2. Cut 6 to 7 onions into fine strips and add to the pan
3. Add one spoonful of flour
4. Pour in enough broth to achieve the proper consistency
5. Finally add one tablespoon vinegar, 3 cloves, and finely cut lemon peel
6. Let everything cook thoroughly together
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gedünsteten Hecht mit Sardellen (Stewed Pike with Anchovies)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pike
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onions
- shallots
- lemon peel
- anchovies
- parsley
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the pike as with the previous recipe, then place it whole or cut into pieces in a pot with salt and pepper
2. Add a piece of butter, finely cut onions, shallots, and lemon peel, and braise it well
3. Before it is completely cooked through, add some finely cut anchovies with parsley and a little lemon juice
4. Arrange the pike with this sauce on a serving dish
5. Alternatively, it can be cut into pieces, fried with butter, anchovies, and lemon peel
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gedünsteter Reis Pilau (Steamed Rice Pilau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deka rice, washed and blanched
- cold water
- 8 deka butter
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 small Spanish onion
- 5 bundles fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Place 16 deka rice in a casserole
2. Pour in enough cold water to cover it by a finger's height
3. Add 8 deka butter, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1 small Spanish onion, and 5 bundles of fresh parsley
4. Stir well together and let the rice steam covered for 10-15 minutes
5. The liquid will evaporate and each grain of rice will remain separate
6. Remove the onion and parsley and use the rice as a garnish for various meat dishes
7. If a crust is desired on the rice, add 2 deka butter after the rice has steamed, and let it roast uncovered for a few minutes

title: Gedünstetes Rindfleisc mit einer Kruste von Erdäpfeln (Braised Beef with a Potato Crust)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- beef, cooked soft
- bacon slices
- onion
- yellow turnips
- flour
- good broth
- red wine
- kohlrabi
- yellow turnips
- three large potatoes
- butter, about the size of half an egg
- fresh green parsley, finely chopped
- salt
- two whole eggs
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook the beef soft, then place it in a casserole with bacon slices, onion, and yellow turnips, turning it often so it browns on all sides.
2. Remove it, dust a spoonful of flour on it, and when the flour has browned, fill halfway with good broth and halfway with red wine.
3. Once it has cooked through, strain it.
4. Using a hollow iron, form small balls from kohlrabi and yellow turnips, place them in the sauce, and cook them soft.
5. While this happens, cook three large potatoes and peel them; once cold, grate them on a grater.
6. Beat a piece of butter the size of half an egg, and mix well with the grated potatoes, some finely chopped fresh parsley, salt, two whole eggs, and two egg yolks.
7. Coat the upper part of the meat evenly one finger thick with this mixture and bake it in a hot oven until the crust is beautifully brown.
8. When plating, place the meat in the middle of the plate, the small brown sauce on the bottom of the plate, and form a wreath around it with the braised kohlrabi and yellow turnips.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefickter Mittelrücken (Larded Veal Cutlet)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast and rib bones
- Lard pieces
- Salt
- Spices
- Soup aspic glaze

## Directions

1. After the breast is removed from the piece and the rib bones are hacked to 3 finger lengths, the veal back remains from the second to seventh rib
2. Hack away the backbone bones with the ribs, remove all skin from the thick meat, rub with lard and spices
3. Lard it with pencil-thick pieces of lard, half-finger length long
4. Quickly roast it on a spit
5. Coat the meat with dissolved aspic glaze, remove from the spit, cut into pieces, arrange on a platter and serve with any desired brown sauce, fine vegetables, or salad

title: Geflügel mit Oliven (Poultry with Olives)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Bacon or other fat (finely sliced)
- Onion (1 piece)
- Parsley
- Thyme (5 bunches)
- Juniper berries (5 pieces)
- Bacon slices
- Wine
- Soup broth
- Black bread (8 ounces, thinly sliced)
- Olives (12 pieces, peeled and pitted)
- Cream

## Directions

1. Put finely sliced bacon or other fat into a casserole, also 1 piece of onion, some parsley, thyme thinly sliced, 5 bunches of thyme, 5 pieces of juniper berries, then lay the dressed and salted poultry on it, cover it with bacon slices, and let it fry a little covered.
2. Pour over with as much wine and broth that the liquid covers it, add 8 ounces of thinly sliced black bread, and let the poultry braise until tender; then remove it, defat and strain the liquid, and add 12 pieces of peeled and pitted olives and some cream, adjust the taste if necessary with lemon juice, and serve this sauce with the poultry.
3. All kinds of poultry, especially older poultry, can be prepared in this manner.

title: Gefrorener Hasenkuchen (Jellied Hare Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram boned hare or venison meat
- 66 decagrams cut bacon
- 5 juniper berries
- 2½ decagrams bacon
- 25 decagrams smoked, formed tongue
- equal amount of truffles
- salt and spices to taste
- butter
- flour
- bacon slices

## Directions

1. Pound hare or venison meat finely in mortar with 66 decagrams cut bacon and 5 juniper berries
2. Pass through a sieve
3. Cut the 2½ decagrams bacon, tongue, and truffles into small cubes
4. Mix cubed ingredients with the pâté, salt, and spices
5. Brush a mold with butter, line with flour, and cover with thin bacon slices
6. Press the pâté in and cover with bacon slices that have been cut several times
7. Place the mold in boiling water and cook covered for 2 hours, with ¾ of the mold in water
8. Refill evaporated water as needed
9. After cooking, turn out and let cool covered with the bacon slice
10. Prepare a cleaned mold in ice and pour finger-high aspic into it
11. When set, garnish with cooled tongue, side of a hard-cooked egg, and cut truffles
12. Pour finger-high aspic over these additions
13. When fully cooled, trim the cake in the mold and carefully place in the mold
14. Fill the space between mold and cake with melted aspic
15. After standing in ice for 1 hour, remove the mold, dip in hot water for a moment, dry, turn out onto a dish, carefully remove the mold, and border the dish edge with neatly cut aspic

title: Gefrorener Punsch (Frozen Punch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Orange-Frozen (No. 1753)
- 1/2 liter Champagne wine
- 1/8 liter Rum or Arrack

## Directions

1. Prepare an Orange-Frozen (No. 1753) and then fold it well with 1/2 liter Champagne wine and 1/8 liter Rum or Arrack.
2. Serve it in glasses.

title: Gefrornes vom Champagner-Wein (Champagne Wine Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle Champagne
- 6 lemons
- Three quarter pound clarified sugar
- 1 measuring cup water
- Egg whites beaten to snow

## Directions

1. Take 1 bottle Champagne, 6 lemons, three quarter pound clarified sugar, 1 measuring cup water, and egg whites beaten to snow
2. Prepare as for Maraschino ice cream
3. Can also be made in this way from Muscat, Rivalta, Marsala, Madeira and generally from all white liqueur wines
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefrornes von Birnen (Pear Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- About 30 pieces ripe Bergamotte or Butter pears
- Water
- Juice of 3 lemons
- 1 pound sugar, clarified

## Directions

1. Peel about 30 pieces of ripe Bergamotte or Butter pears
2. Cut into 4 pieces and remove the cores
3. Cook with a little water into a marmalade
4. Press through a hair sieve into a terrine
5. Add the juice of 3 lemons
6. Clarify 1 pound sugar and when cooled mix with the pear mixture
7. Place in the freezing pan
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefrornes von Erdbeeren (Strawberry Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 and a half pounds strawberries
- 5 loth red currants (Ribisel)
- 1 pound sugar, clarified

## Directions

1. Press 2 and a half pounds strawberries and 5 loth red currants through a hair sieve
2. Mix in 1 pound clarified sugar
3. After cooling, stir until it is time to pour everything into the freezing pan
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefrornes von Himbeeren (Raspberry Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries
- Sugar (somewhat less in proportion than for strawberry ice cream)

## Directions

1. Make as for the preceding strawberry ice cream, only taking somewhat less sugar in proportion
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefällter Karpfen (Pickled Carp)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp, filleted
- Vinegar
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Once the carp has been cooked soft in its broth, it is left to cool in the broth.
2. Remove it from the broth, pull off the skin, arrange the pieces in a bowl and serve with vinegar and egg yolk.

title: Gefällter Rheinischen (Pickled Rhine Fish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned Rhine fish
- Salt water
- Gravy or sauce
- Egg yolks
- Lemon slices
- Potatoes
- Vinegar
- Oil

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned Rhine fish into pieces as desired and place in a casserole.
2. Pour boiling salted water over it and let it come to a boil once.
3. Set aside and let it stand covered in the broth.
4. When cold, remove the fish, pull off the skin, place in a casserole and pour over gravy which has been thickened with egg yolks.
5. Bring to a boil several times.
6. Arrange the Rhine fish with the sauce in a bowl.
7. Alternatively, after cooking in salted water, cut into pieces as desired, arrange in a bowl and serve with lemon slices and potatoes.
8. Or, once the fish has cooled in its own broth, serve with vinegar and oil.

title: Gefällter, angesötterter Karpfen mit Wurzelwerk (Pickled Carp with Root Vegetables)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Fat
- Root vegetables, finely cut
- Onion
- 1 bay leaf
- Whole spices
- Vinegar
- Water
- Carp, filleted and cut into pieces
- Butter
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put fat, finely cut root vegetables, onion, 1 bay leaf and whole spices into a casserole.
2. Pour vinegar and water over and cook well.
3. When the root vegetables are soft, add the filleted carp cut into pieces and let it cook for a few minutes.
4. Remove the carp, pull off the skin, arrange in a bowl and garnish with the finely cut root vegetables.
5. Make a butter sauce from the broth and thicken it with a few egg yolks.
6. Serve the sauce in a sauceboat with the carp.

title: Gefärkielter (Gemürkelter) Rostbraten (Stuffed Pot Roast)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Rostbraten (pot roasts)
- 16 Deca Speck, fein geschnitten (16 deca bacon, finely cut)
- Zwiebel und Petersilie, fein geschnitten (onion and parsley, finely cut)
- 6 Deca rohes Reichfleisch (6 deca raw meat)
- Salz (salt)
- Optional: Pfeffer (pepper)
- Fett (fat)
- Fein geschnittenes Wurzelwerk (finely cut root vegetables)
- Zwiebel (onion)
- Mehl (flour)
- 3/4 Liter Suppe (3/4 liter broth)
- Optional: braune Suppe (brown broth)
- Optional: Rahm (cream) und Kapern (capers)
- Optional: Erdäpfel (potatoes), Sardellen (anchovies)
- Optional: sauren Rahm (sour cream)

## Directions

1. Take 5 pot roasts and pound out 4 of them flat.
2. Finely mince the 5th piece with 16 deca bacon, some onion and parsley using a chef's knife.
3. Mix this filling with 6 deca raw meat, some salt, and pepper if desired.
4. Divide the filling into 4 portions and spread knife-back thickness on each pounded pot roast.
5. Roll each piece up and tie lightly with twine.
6. In a casserole add some fat, finely cut root vegetables and some onion.
7. Place the breaded, rolled pot roasts on top, let them braise briefly, then pour 3/4 liter broth over them.
8. Cover with a lid and braise gently, which takes 1 to 1.5 hours.
9. Place on a platter, remove the twine and slice neatly.
10. Add some brown broth to the sauce, let it come to a boil briefly and pour over the pot roasts.
11. Alternatively: degrease the sauce, add some cream and capers, let it boil and pour over.
12. Or: add finely diced raw potatoes and 6 pieces of cleaned and cut anchovies to the filling instead of meat. Before the rolled pot roasts are fully braised, degrease the sauce, add sour cream and finish braising.
13. Or: finely chop a small head of cabbage, braise it in butter, and mix with finely cut pot roast, some finely chopped ham, some caraway, and pepper if desired; proceed as above.
14. Or: pound together 8 deca bacon, 1 softened and well-pressed roll, some onion, 9 raw parsley sprigs, anchovies, capers, some lemon peel, the 5th piece of pot roast and 2 whole eggs; season with salt and spices, sieve and spread on the pot roasts, then proceed as above.
15. For any of these preparations you can substitute 4 tablespoons of finely cut cooked ham for the anchovies.
16. Or: mix the 5th piece of finely cut pot roast with the filling of 16 deca bratwurst, which is also highly recommended for cabbage-filled pot roasts.

title: Gefäumtes Koch (Foamed Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 loth sifted sugar
- 4 loth fruit sauce
- 4 eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. In a bowl, combine 3 loth sifted sugar and 4 loth fruit sauce
2. Beat the egg whites from 4 eggs to stiff peaks
3. Stir for a quarter hour
4. Butter a cake sheet
5. Spread the mixture on it and bake slowly in a cake pan
6. Sprinkle with sugar when done
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefüllte Enten mit Sauce-Haschee (Stuffed Ducks with Sauce Haschee)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ducks
- Veal forcemeat
- Duck livers
- Mushrooms
- Anchovies
- Fine dry herbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Bacon slices
- Sauce Haschee

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the ducks properly and loosen the skin from the breasts
2. Fill the breasts with a well-prepared veal forcemeat to which you must add the duck livers, mushrooms, anchovies, fine dry herbs, salt, pepper, and nutmeg
3. Pound all this together in a mortar and use it as mentioned above
4. Bind the ducks carefully so the forcemeat does not fall out
5. Also bind the ducks with bacon slices and roast them over not too strong fire on the spit
6. When serving, give a sauce haschee underneath and glaze the ducks nicely with it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefüllte Erdäpfel (Filled Potato Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- mass from plum croquettes recipe
- beaten eggs
- bread crumbs
- grated chocolate mixed with sugar
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Form small dumplings from the plum croquette mass
2. Dip in beaten eggs
3. Roll in bread crumbs
4. Fry in very hot fat until golden brown
5. Remove with a baking spoon
6. Roll in grated chocolate mixed with sugar
7. Arrange on dish and serve

title: Gefüllte Erdäpfel (Stuffed Potatoes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- large potatoes, not mealy
- butter
- eggs
- lemon peel
- nutmeg flower
- salt
- cream
- breadcrumbs
- optional: meat forcemeat on meat days

## Directions

1. Take large but not mealy potatoes, scrape them clean, rinse them, cut off a piece from the top, and hollow them out.
2. Beat together a piece of butter with a couple of eggs, lemon peel, nutmeg flower, salt, a little cream, and the scooped-out potato flesh.
3. Add as many breadcrumbs as needed to make the forcemeat somewhat thick.
4. Fill the hollowed potatoes with this mixture, place the cut-off pieces on top, put them with butter in a baking dish, cover the top with butter slices, braise them over heat, pour cream over them, and let them finish cooking.
5. On meat days, fill them with a forcemeat of choice.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefüllte Eyer (Filled Eggs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- bread roll
- milk
- butter
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- sour cream
- breadcrumbs
- chives

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard and shell them
2. Cut them in half lengthwise
3. Remove yolks and chop small with a piece of bread roll soaked in milk
4. Drive off butter, stir in the bread
5. Add nutmeg, pepper, salt, and green parsley
6. Fill the egg halves so a mound forms on top
7. Arrange in a row in a dish rubbed with lard and sour cream
8. Pour butter over them
9. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs
10. Pour sour cream over so it does not cover the eggs
11. Add green parsley and chives
12. Bake with heat from below and above
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefüllte Eyer (Stuffed Eggs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, as many as needed
- Butter
- Bread (Semmel), soaked in milk
- Sour cream (Schmette)
- Pepper
- Salt
- Green parsley
- Bread crumbs (Semmelbrösel)
- Chives (Schnittling)

## Directions

1. Boil eggs until hard, shell them, and cut them in half lengthwise.
2. Remove the yolks, chop them with a piece of bread soaked in milk, then mix with butter.
3. Add sour cream, pepper, salt, and green parsley to the yolk mixture.
4. Fill the egg white halves with this mixture, mounding it on top.
5. Arrange the filled egg halves in a row in a dish brushed with lard and sour cream.
6. Pour melted butter over them, sprinkle with bread crumbs, and add sour cream without covering the eggs.
7. Garnish with green parsley and chives.
8. Bake with heat from above and below in the oven.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefüllte Fischsuppa (Filled Fish Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- head of carp or other fish
- flour
- bread crumbs
- 8 deca bread sliced thin
- 1 piece onion
- whole eggs
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut the head of carp or other fish into pieces, bread with flour and bread crumbs, fry with 8 deca thinly sliced bread, 1 piece onion and whole eggs in lard

title: Gefüllte Hopfen-Strudeln (Filled Hop Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deka butter
- 5 whole eggs
- 48 Deka mashed hops
- 1 1/8 liter milk cream
- salt
- finely chopped green parsley
- 1/2 liter flour for dough
- 4 Deka white bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Whip 12 Deka butter until fluffy
2. Beat in 5 whole eggs one by one
3. Add 48 Deka mashed hops, milk cream, salt, and parsley
4. Mix well
5. Spread on strudel dough made from 1/2 liter flour
6. Sprinkle 4 Deka bread crumbs over it
7. Roll lightly and treat as lung strudel

title: Gefüllte Hühner (Poulets farcis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 young cleaned chickens
- veal forcemeat, chicken forcemeat or liver forcemeat
- green peas
- asparagus peas
- finely cut crayfish tails (if available)
- 8 deciliter butter
- onions
- root vegetables
- mushrooms
- whole spices
- soup

## Directions

1. Flame 2 young chickens, singe them, wash and prepare them
2. Make a forcemeat by mixing veal forcemeat, chicken forcemeat or liver forcemeat with green peas, asparagus peas and finely cut crayfish tails if available
3. Stuff the chickens with this forcemeat and dress them
4. In a casserole, place 8 deciliter butter, onions, root vegetables, mushrooms and whole spices
5. Lay the chickens inside and braise covered
6. When the liquid that forms has evaporated, pour the necessary soup over them
7. Braise covered until tender
8. Remove the chickens, make a butter sauce with the liquid, let it reduce well and remove the lard completely
9. Halve the chickens, place in a platter and serve the sauce alongside

title: Gefüllte Kalbsmilch (Stuffed Calf Sweetbread)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- calf sweetbread
- breadcrumbs soaked in milk
- green parsley
- marjoram herb
- basil
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- egg
- egg yolk
- salt water

## Directions

1. The larger part of a calf sweetbread is sliced open and the interior is scraped onto a clean cutting board with a knife back.
2. To this add breadcrumbs softened in milk, some green parsley, a small sprig of stripped marjoram herb, and if desired basil and bacon.
3. Cut all of this finely together, place in a deep bowl, salt it, season with some pepper and if desired nutmeg, stir in 1 whole egg and 1 egg yolk.
4. With this mixture, fill the sweetbread, sew it shut and cook gently in salt water.
5. Then remove it and either roast it at the fire or fry it with butter in a pan, then cut the sweetbread into desired slices and serve it with fine vegetables, or serve it in the soup pot.

title: Gefüllte Kohlrabi (Stuffed Kohlrabi)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Young kohlrabi
- Salt water
- Cooked or stewed veal
- Half a roll soaked in milk
- Green parsley
- Onion, size of a hazelnut
- Scooped out kohlrabi
- Crawfish tails or green peas, as desired
- 2 spoons of cream
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg yolks
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- A little nutmeg
- 2 spoons of flour
- Warm butter
- Good broth
- 2 spoons of sweet cream

## Directions

1. Cook young kohlrabi in salt water until half soft
2. Cut off the top and hollow them out well
3. For the filling: finely chop a piece of cooked or stewed veal, half a roll soaked in milk, some green parsley, an onion the size of a hazelnut, and some of the scooped out kohlrabi
4. Add crawfish tails or green peas if desired
5. Mix all in a bowl with 2 spoons of cream, 1 whole egg, 2 egg yolks, salt, ground pepper and a little nutmeg
6. Fill the kohlrabi and replace the caps
7. For the butter sauce: let 2 spoons of flour turn light brown in a piece of warm butter, fill with good broth, add ground nutmeg and 2 spoons of sweet cream
8. Place the filled kohlrabi in this sauce, cover with white paper and then an iron lid
9. Cook slowly so they remain white with heat applied above and below
10. Or for brown stewed kohlrabi: heat the butter, add roughly crushed sugar, and when brown add the kohlrabi (previously tied together with string), browning them on all sides
11. Remove them, add yellow turnips and onion pieces to the pan, dust with flour, and let cook well with some broth
12. Place kohlrabi in another pan, strain the sauce over it, place on heat, and when cooked through, arrange on the dish and serve with fried chicken, etc.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefüllte Nürteln (Filled Pastry Rolls)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams butter
- 4 decagrams finely ground sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 3 decagrams pressed yeast dissolved in 1/2 liter lukewarm milk
- 56 decagrams flour
- melted butter for brushing
- 1 teaspoon of filling such as poppy seed, nut filling, compote or marmalade

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy, gradually stir in the sugar, salt, egg yolks, and whole egg
2. Mix in the pressed yeast dissolved in lukewarm milk and the flour, stirring well
3. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon
4. Cover with a cloth and let it rise in a warm place
5. When risen, place the dough on a flour-dusted board, roll it out to the thickness of a small finger, and cut into strips 3 fingers wide and 1 finger long
6. Fill each strip with 1 teaspoon of desired filling such as poppy seed, nut filling, compote or marmalade
7. Roll up the filling in the dough and lay the rolls in a butter-greased casserole so that the ends of the rolled dough face upward
8. Brush with melted butter at the points where the rolls touch each other
9. Cover with a cloth and let rise in a warm place until the dough has risen again
10. Brush with melted butter and bake slowly for 3/4 to 1 hour
11. Remove the casserole, turn out the rolls, separate them from each other, arrange on a plate, sprinkle with sugar, and serve

title: Gefüllte Orangen (Stuffed Orange Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared orange jelly mass
- 10 to 12 oranges
- optional: alkermes juice or cochineal for coloring

## Directions

1. Cut a lid at the stem end of the oranges and hollow them out with a spoon, leaving only a thin upper shell that must not tear.
2. Place all hollowed oranges immediately in cold water.
3. Once the necessary number of oranges (10 to 12) are hollowed, remove them from water and let them drain on cloth.
4. Place them on ice and fill with the prepared jelly mass, put the lid on top and let them set.
5. Arrange on a platter, alternating whole oranges with orange halves and placing green leaves between them.
6. Alternatively, color portions of the finished jelly mass with alkermes juice or cochineal, then fill the oranges alternately with white and red jelly spoonwise, allowing each application to set before adding another color.
7. Once filled, cut the oranges into halves.
8. Keep the jelly masses in deep bowls at a lukewarm location so they do not set, warming gently if needed to dissolve if they do set.

title: Gefüllte Paradieäpfel (Tomates farcies au gratin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10-12 medium tomatoes
- 4 tablespoons finely chopped veal or chicken meat
- 4 decagrams bread softened in milk, well wrung out
- finely chopped fresh parsley
- finely chopped onion
- pepper
- 4 decagrams cooked rice
- 1 egg
- salt
- 8 decagrams butter, whipped until fluffy
- cream
- breadcrumbs
- lard
- grated Parmesan cheese
- butter sauce

## Directions

1. From 10-12 medium tomatoes carefully pull out the stems and empty the juice and seeds through the opening
2. Pass some of the juice through a sieve
3. Mix 4 tablespoons finely chopped veal or chicken meat, 4 decagrams bread softened in milk and well wrung out, finely chopped fresh parsley and onion, a knife point of pepper, 4 decagrams cooked rice, 1 whole egg, salt and the juice from the tomatoes with 8 decagrams butter whipped until fluffy
4. Fill the hollowed out tomatoes with this mixture
5. Place them close together in a baking dish lined with lard and sprinkled with breadcrumbs
6. Pour cream over them
7. Cover and braise in the oven for approximately 1 hour
8. A few minutes before removing from the oven, sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese
9. Arrange piled on a platter and serve with well-cooked butter sauce
10. The Parmesan cheese can also be omitted

title: Gefüllte Schnecken (Stuffed Snails)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 cleaned snails
- Salt water

## Directions

1. Place cleaned snails in boiling salted water
2. Cook for approximately 20 minutes
3. Remove the snails from their shells using a pastry needle
4. Remove the head

title: Gefüllte Schnecken (Stuffed Snails)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 pieces cleaned snails
- salted water
- 2 pieces mushrooms
- 5 sprigs fresh parsley
- 1 half-finger-length piece lemon peel
- 4 pieces peeled capers
- 8 drams butter
- 3 tablespoons breadcrumbs
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/2 liter cream
- milk cream
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Place 20 cleaned snails in boiling salted water and cook for approximately 20 minutes
2. Remove snails and extract them from their shells using a dressing needle
3. Remove the head, intestines, and small stone from inside the shell
4. Chop the remaining snail meat into pieces
5. Finely chop 2 pieces mushrooms, 5 sprigs parsley, 1 half-finger-length piece lemon peel, and 4 pieces peeled capers together
6. Roast this mixture in 8 drams butter
7. Add 3 tablespoons breadcrumbs and the chopped snails to the roasted mixture
8. Once completely cooled, mix with 3 egg yolks and 1/2 liter cream and set in a cool place
9. Clean the removed shells with warm water and salt, place on a sieve, and once completely dry, fill with the prepared cold mixture
10. Place the filled shells in a casserole, cover with milk cream, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, drizzle with melted butter
11. Bake for approximately 1/2 hour
12. Remove from oven, arrange on a napkin-covered dish, and serve with vinegar and oil

title: Gefüllte Schnitzel mit Kapern und Rahm (Veal Cutlets with Capers and Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin, finger-thick slices
- Filling as for Stuffed Cutlets
- Onion
- Carrot
- Mushrooms
- White wine or vinegar
- Soup
- Cream
- Capers

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Stuffed Cutlets
2. After the defatted sauce is strained, add 1/2 liter of cream and 2 tablespoons of capers
3. Bring to a boil and pour over the arranged cutlets

title: Gefüllte und gebackene Eyer (Stuffed and Baked Eggs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- breadcrumbs
- almonds
- raisins
- sugar
- cinnamon
- fresh eggs
- lard
- wine
- seasoning

## Directions

1. Cut hard-boiled eggs in half lengthwise
2. Mash the yolks in a bowl
3. Mix in finely grated breadcrumbs, thinly sliced almonds, small raisins, sugar, and cinnamon
4. Bind with a couple of fresh eggs
5. Fill the egg white halves with the mixture so it is heaped up
6. Fry them in lard
7. Heat wine in a small pan with sugar and a little fine seasoning
8. Arrange the eggs on a serving dish
9. Pour the wine sauce over them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefüllte Zitronen (Stuffed Lemon Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared lemon jelly mass
- lemons

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as Gefüllte Orangen (No. 1661), cut into desired pieces and use as decoration.

title: Gefüllte, Gebratene Tauben (Stuffed, Roasted Pigeons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons
- Veal breast forcemeat

## Directions

1. The pigeons are feathered (plucked), flamed, gutted, washed, and the neck, head and feet are cut off. The leg bone is pushed back, the thighbone is tucked inward and with the index finger from behind toward front the skin is separated from the meat. The pigeons are salted inside and outside and filled with the forcemeat of veal breast, namely the mass is spread between the skin and breast down to the thighs and the wings are turned inward against the back over the skin of the neck so that the skin is held secure by this. The pigeons are then placed in a pan, covered with hot fat and roasted in the oven for about 1/2 hour with frequent basting. After this time they are removed, the string is cut, pulled out, each pigeon is cut lengthwise in the middle and arranged on a dish with any salad served alongside. The pigeons can instead of breadcrumb forcemeat be filled with liver forcemeat.

title: Gefüllte, gebäckene Schäumchen (Filled, Baked Puffs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ingredients same as baked almond foam puffs
- cream or chantilly filling

## Directions

1. Prepare puffs as in the baked almond foam puffs recipe
2. Fill with desired cream or chantilly cream

title: Gefüllte, geibrattene Tauben (Stuffed, Roasted Pigeons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons
- Speck (bacon)
- Semmel (bread roll) or liver forcemeat
- Hot fat
- Salad

## Directions

1. Make an incision in the skin between the breast and thighs down to the thigh bones and turn the wings inward against the back so that the skin holds them in place.
2. Place the pigeons in a pan, pour hot fat over them, and roast in the oven for approximately 1/4 hour, basting frequently.
3. Remove the pigeons from the oven, cut the string, remove the stuffing, and cut each pigeon lengthwise down the middle.
4. Arrange on a platter and serve with salad of your choice.
5. The pigeons can be filled with liver forcemeat instead of bread forcemeat.

title: Gefüllter gebratener Milchtau (Stuffed Roasted Turkey)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned and plucked turkey
- Same stuffing as used for 'gefüllte Halbsbrust' (recipe 611)
- 1 piece of bread roll or 1 roasted chestnut
- Butter
- Paper

## Directions

1. Let the plucked and cleaned turkey rest for 1 hour after salting.
2. Prepare the same stuffing as specified for 'gefüllte Halbsbrust' (recipe 611).
3. Fill the crop skin with this stuffing up to the thighs and close the crop opening with a piece of bread roll or a roasted chestnut.
4. Truss the stuffed turkey, place it on a spit, wrap in buttered paper, and roast slowly.
5. After approximately 1 3/4 hours, remove the paper and roast the turkey at bright fire until brown and crispy.
6. Alternatively, place the stuffed turkey in a pan with butter and roast it in the oven, basting frequently with the pan drippings.
7. Cut the roasted turkey into desired pieces, reassemble them, place in a bowl, and serve with salad or compote of choice.

title: Gefüllter geibrattener Indiant (Dindon farci)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Plucked, cleaned turkey
- Salt

## Directions

1. After salting the turkey, let it rest for 1 hour.

title: Gefüllter grüner Paprika (Stuffed Green Paprika)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-6 paprika peppers
- 25 dkg pork shoulder
- 1 tomato
- 1/2 cup cream
- 12 dkg cooked rice
- 4 dkg fat
- 1 onion
- 1 egg
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut off the tops of the peppers at the stem and hollow them out.
2. For the filling: finely chop the meat. In a pan, heat 4 dkg fat and sauté finely chopped onion and parsley, add the meat and brown for 15 minutes.
3. Add 12 dkg cooked rice, the insides of the tomato, salt and pepper, and braise for 30 minutes.
4. Mix with 1 egg and a few spoonfuls of cream.
5. Fill the peppers with this mixture, place them in a pan with a little fat, pour cream over them, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, and braise for 30 minutes.
6. Place on a serving dish, skim the fat from the cooking liquid, bring the remainder to a boil with broth, and pour over the peppers.

title: Gefüllter Hecht (Stuffed Pike)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike
- salt
- water
- 200 grams pike flesh
- 10 decagrams breadcrumbs soaked in milk and squeezed dry
- 6 cleaned and washed crayfish tails
- 1 mushroom
- 5 bunches finely chopped parsley
- 200 grams butter
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 140 grams butter
- pepper
- cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Lay the pike on a board. Cut the skin on the back from tail toward head and separate it 2 finger-widths from the head on each side.
2. Carefully pull the skin from the flesh and spread it completely on the board.
3. Remove all flesh from the skeleton using a small knife, taking care not to damage the head and skin.
4. Leave the skeleton covered with spread skin, sprinkle lightly with salt and cover with a cloth dampened in cold water.
5. Make the forcemeat: Finely chop 200 grams pike flesh with 10 decagrams breadcrumbs that have been soaked in milk and pressed dry, 6 cleaned and washed crayfish tails, 1 mushroom that has been roasted with 5 bunches finely chopped parsley in 200 grams butter.
6. Pound this finely in a mortar, then add 3 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks and mix well.
7. Add 140 grams butter and pound together.
8. Press through a sieve, season with salt and pepper, and stir well.
9. Spread this forcemeat on the skeleton, coat well, and draw the skin together on the back.
10. Insert a piece of wood from the mouth to the tail, which must protrude during roasting.
11. Hold the fish by the head and tail and place it in a pan containing 200 grams butter.
12. Brush with melted butter, cover with buttered paper, and roast in a moderate oven, frequently basting with its own juices for approximately 45 minutes to 1 hour.
13. If roasting with cream, after 15 minutes pour 500 milliliters cream over it and continue basting frequently.
14. Garnish with fresh parsley, lemon slices, and serve with potatoes, or arrange on a platter and garnish with forcemeat quenelles, crayfish, crayfish tails, truffles if desired, and fried smelt.
15. Serve with Hollandaise or mustard sauce or butter sauce with lemon juice.

title: Gefüllter Kalbsmagen (Stuffed Veal Stomach)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- small cabbage head
- salt water
- green parsley
- onion
- cabbage, finely cut
- beef or veal fat
- breadcrumbs soaked in milk
- savory
- caraway
- eggs
- egg yolk
- veal stomach
- flour
- cream
- capers

## Directions

1. The leaves from a small cabbage head are separated, cooked in salt water, removed, dried with a cloth and finely cut with a sharp knife.
2. In fat, roast finely cut green parsley and some onion, add the cut cabbage with rendered, milk-softened, well-squeezed breadcrumbs, salt it, season with savory and some caraway and let it braise covered.
3. Then set it aside and when it is completely cooled, stir in 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks.
4. The stomach from a calf, after being carefully cleaned, is filled with this mixture, then sewn shut and cooked gently in salt water for about 2 hours.
5. Lay the stomach in a casserole containing hot butter, roast it on both sides until golden and then cut it into desired pieces.
6. In a casserole put 1 tablespoon of flour, gradually pour in, while constantly stirring, 1/3 liter of cream and stir this until it begins to boil, then add 1 tablespoon of capers, let it boil once and serve with the prepared veal stomach.

title: Gefüllter Kalbsschnitz mit Mittraham (Larded Veal Cutlet with Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlet of any size
- Bacon
- Fat
- Cream

## Directions

1. Salt a veal cutlet on both sides
2. Lard it with pencil-thick, half-finger-long bacon
3. Place in a casserole with fat
4. Brown on both sides
5. Pour off the fat
6. Baste the cutlet with cream
7. Let it braise in the oven until it has a light yellow color on both sides
8. Capers may be added if desired

title: Gefüllter Kohl (Stuffed Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head cabbage
- 20 dag smoked meat
- 8 dag bacon
- 5 dag fat
- 1 onion

## Directions

1. Clean the cabbage but do not separate, wash it, place in boiling salt water and cook uncovered 4-6 minutes depending on size, remove and drain, cool it.
2. Fold back the leaves, brush with filling, press together so the head regains its former shape and wrap lightly with twine.
3. In a casserole, heat 5 dag fat, let a finely chopped onion lightly brown, lay some bacon slices, then the cabbage on top, let it braise in the oven under frequent basting with broth or water for 1 hour.
4. Remove the twine, serve the cabbage on a round dish.
5. For the filling: finely chop the smoked meat and mix with a water-soaked, well-squeezed roll, salt, pepper and chopped parsley.

title: Gefüllter Lungenbraten (Stuffed Beef Tenderloin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef tenderloin
- 16 deca of bacon
- 6 shallots
- 1 strong tablespoon capers
- 1 anchovy
- 4 sprigs parsley
- fat
- 1 onion
- some root vegetables
- 12 deca black bread
- 5 grains spice
- 8 sprigs parsley
- bones from the tenderloin
- salt
- beef broth
- 1/4 cream
- 1/4 liter cream

## Directions

1. Carefully cut a small beef tenderloin lengthwise from the thinner side so that it can be opened but not divided into two pieces
2. Finely chop 16 deca of bacon, 6 shallots, 1 strong tablespoon capers, 1 anchovy, and 4 sprigs parsley on a cutting board with a knife
3. Spread this mixture on the inner, cut side of the tenderloin
4. Roll the tenderloin together and wrap it with twine
5. In a casserole, place fat, 1 onion, some root vegetables, 12 deca black bread, all cut into thin slices, 5 grains spice, 8 sprigs parsley, and the bones removed and broken from the tenderloin
6. Place the meat on top so that the open side faces upward
7. Braise covered for 1/2 hour
8. Add enough broth so the meat is almost covered and braise covered for about 1.5 hours in the oven until tender
9. Remove the meat and skim the fat from the sauce
10. Add 1/4 cream to the sauce and let it boil
11. In a second casserole, place the skimmed fat, then the meat freed from the twine, cover with 1/4 liter cream and roast in the oven until it gets a brown crust
12. Strain the sauce, let it boil somewhat and pour it over the meat cut into desired pieces
13. The sauce must be thick and piquant and can also be served in a sauce boat
14. Garnish the tenderloin with potatoes, macaroni, small cucumbers and the like

title: Gefüllter Nierenbraten (Stuffed Veal Loin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca lard
- 4 deca rolls softened in cold water and well wrung out
- 3 pieces cleaned and washed mushrooms
- 1 small piece onion
- 6 bunches fresh parsley
- 5 pieces cleaned crayfish
- 1 small veal loin with the sinews removed
- Twine
- Wood shavings
- Hot fat
- Broth
- Aspic glaze

## Directions

1. Cut the lard, rolls, mushrooms, onion, parsley, and crayfish very finely with a knife
2. After the veal loin is sufficiently trimmed, brush the inside with the finely chopped filling
3. Roll it together, turning the thick part inward and rolling the meat toward the thin part
4. Bind it with twine and place it on wood shavings in a roasting pan, positioning it so that the end of the backbone faces upward
5. Cover the meat with some warm fat and after 1/2 hour pour some broth alongside the roast
6. After the meat has roasted for 1.5 hours with frequent turning and basting, remove it
7. Loosen the twine, cut the meat into pieces, coat them with dissolved aspic glaze, and arrange on a platter

title: Gefülltes Kraut (Stuffed Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cabbage head
- 0 tablespoons fat
- 10 tablespoons hash
- 8 tablespoons very finely cut smoked ham
- 4 tablespoons soaked rolls without crust, well pressed
- 2 small pieces potatoes, cut into cubes
- 1/4 liter milk cream
- 2 whole eggs
- salt and spices
- 1 knife-point paprika, if available
- 3 small pieces finely cut cooked mushrooms
- salt water or fatty broth
- 3/4 hour
- twine
- 1 1/2 liters cream

## Directions

1. Dip 1 large cabbage head in boiling water
2. Remove the 3 outer coarse leaves by letting the stem remain at the bottom
3. Take out the inner leaves (petals), cut finely, roast slightly in approximately 0 tablespoons fat with a little broth and stew gently
4. Mix together 10 tablespoons hash, 8 tablespoons very finely cut smoked ham, 4 tablespoons soaked rolls without crust, well pressed, 2 small pieces potatoes cut into cubes, 1/4 liter milk cream, 2 whole eggs, some salt and spices, and if available 1 knife-point paprika, 3 small pieces finely cut cooked mushrooms
5. Place this mixture in the middle of the cabbage, overlap the leaves, bind with twine
6. Place in a casserole, pour salt water or fatty broth over it and cook covered for 3/4 hour
7. Then strain the liquid and pour 1 1/2 liters cream over it, then let it stew for about 1/2 hour
8. Before serving, loosen the twine from the head, place it on a platter, salt and season according to taste the juice that has formed with paprika, and pour the juice over the cabbage head

title: Gefülltes Spanferkel (Stuffed Suckling Pig)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig liver
- Bacon, soaked
- Mushrooms
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Sage
- Veal meat from the loin
- Veal kidney and its fat
- Nutmeg
- Cloves
- Onions
- Thyme
- Parsley
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Make a forcemeat from the pig's liver, soaked bacon, mushrooms, salt, pepper, fine herbs, and sage
2. Let it sweat in a casserole
3. Fill the suckling pig with this forcemeat from the inside
4. Sew it closed so the filling does not escape
5. Roast as usual
6. Alternatively: remove the skin from the raw suckling pig as one would strip a hare skin
7. Cut out the lean meat and chop it together with veal from the loin, veal kidney and its fat, salt, pepper, nutmeg, cloves, onions, thyme, and parsley
8. Fill this mixture into the skin, keeping the feet up to the joints
9. Sew it closed and bake it in a pie pan or oven
10. Remove the twine when serving
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gefülltes Spanferkel (Stuffed Suckling Pig)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and prepared suckling pig
- forcemeat

## Directions

1. From a cleaned and prepared suckling pig as described in the previous recipes, loosen the spine, joints, shoulders, and the legs from the feet to the claws
2. Fill the empty space created with forcemeat and sew the opening closed
3. Line a roasting pan with thinly sliced speck, place the suckling pig with the belly side down on it
4. Roast in the oven under frequent basting with speck for 1-1 1/2 hours
5. After this time, remove it, carve it, and serve with red cabbage, green salad, or the like

title: Gefülltes, in Wein getauchtes Brot (Filled Bread Soaked in Wine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- small rolls
- stewed apples
- sweetened red wine
- flour
- beaten eggs
- bread crumbs
- fat for frying
- any desired spice sugar or cream filling options: chocolate, vanilla, hazelnut cream

## Directions

1. Trim the rolls
2. Cut off a lid at the side
3. Remove the crumb from inside
4. Fill with stewed apples
5. Close with the cut-off lid
6. Dip in sweetened red wine
7. Dust with flour
8. Dip in beaten eggs
9. Sprinkle generously with bread crumbs
10. Fry in very hot fat
11. Arrange heaped in a dish and sprinkle with spice sugar
12. Alternatively fill with chocolate, vanilla, or hazelnut cream, close with lids, dip in sweetened milk, and proceed as above
13. If filled with chocolate cream, sprinkle with grated chocolate mixed with sugar when serving

title: Gekochene Grieß-Knödel (Cooked Semolina Quenelles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 Loffel butter
- 2 tablespoons water
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- salt
- 18 Loffel semolina
- 9 Loffel semolina

## Directions

1. Beat 14 Loffel butter until fluffy
2. Stir in 2 tablespoons water, 2 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, salt and 18 Loffel semolina
3. Let stand covered for 1/2 hour
4. Stir in 9 Loffel semolina and let stand covered for 2 hours
5. Form small knödel and cook 1/4 hour in salted water
6. Cool with cold water, let stand covered for 5 minutes, then remove with a skimming spoon and put in the soup

title: Gekochte Gerste (Cooked Barley)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 spoonfuls pearled barley
- 1 liter boiling broth
- salt
- 2 egg yolks (optional)
- lemon juice or sour cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash 16 spoonfuls pearled barley in cold water
2. Stir into 1 liter boiling broth and cook slowly until soft
3. If the broth becomes too thick, thin with hot clear broth as needed
4. If desired, beat 2 egg yolks with lemon juice or sour cream and stir into the cooked barley soup
5. Serve at table

title: Gekochter Hecht (Boiled Pike)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike
- salt water
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil a pike in salt water.
2. Remove all bones and mix the shredded pike in butter sauce to which lemon juice has been added.
3. Arrange heaped on a platter and garnish with decorative butter paste cut out as desired, or place in a butter paste mold or pastry form.

title: Gekochter Schinken (Boiled Ham)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ham
- 5 juniper berries, crushed
- 1 bay leaf
- sprigs of thyme
- water

## Directions

1. Scrape the skin off the ham, leaving the ham bone slightly protruding
2. Place the ham in a large casserole with the skin side down
3. Add 5 crushed juniper berries, 1 bay leaf, and some sprigs of thyme
4. Fill the casserole with boiling water and cook the ham slowly and covered
5. If the broth tastes strongly of salt, drain it and replace with boiling water during cooking
6. Once the ham is tender, which can be tested by inserting a fork, remove it and place on a board with the skin side up
7. Cover with another board, weight it down with a heavy weight, and let it cool
8. The next day, remove the weight and board, loosen the skin slightly, replace it, and cut thin slices as needed
9. Arrange on a plate and garnish with chopped aspic, and serve with mustard and horseradish

title: Gelatiniertes Spanferkel (Jellied Suckling Pig)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 calf feet
- 2 pig feet

## Directions

1. Chop 4 calf feet and 2 pig feet and place in a casserole

title: Gelbe Rüben (Carrots)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots, same quantity as for kohlrabi
- 8 ounces butter
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- Water
- Butter sauce
- Parsley, finely cut

## Directions

1. Peel the carrots, cut them into pieces of your choice or scoop them out
2. Place them in a casserole with 8 ounces of butter and 1 tablespoon of sugar
3. Pour water over them and cook the carrots tender
4. Instead of stirring, shake the casserole from time to time to avoid breaking the carrots
5. Before serving, add some butter sauce, sprinkle a teaspoon of finely cut parsley over them, and let them cook a little longer

title: Gelbe Rüben (Yellow Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-6 yellow carrots
- salt
- 5 dkg fat
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the carrots and cut them into noodle-like strips.
2. Place them in cold salted water and cook for 30 minutes.
3. Make a light roux from 5 dkg fat, pour it up with the carrot water or broth, add the carrots, salt to taste, and cook for 20 minutes.

title: Gelbliches Eis (Yellowish Icing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared white icing
- 1 to 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Prepare as white icing, only stirring in 1 to 2 egg yolks thoroughly

title: Gelee von Orangen (Orange Jelly)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's feet stock
- half a bottle of Rhine or French wine
- 12 oranges
- 3 lemons
- half a pound of sugar
- whole cinnamon

## Directions

1. Prepare the calf's feet stock with 12 pieces as in Rhine wine jelly
2. Rub the zest of the lemons into sugar, without adding other sugar
3. Rub the zest of the oranges into sugar along with half a pound of sugar
4. Add the sugar with lemon zest, 3 parts Rhine wine, and the cinnamon to the stock
5. Place the yellow zest of oranges and their juice on a plate with the remaining wine
6. Pass this through a jelly bag onto the dish on which the jelly is to be arranged
7. Clarify the calf's stock with egg white as in Rhine wine jelly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gelee von Rheinwein (Rhine Wine Jelly)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve scalded calf's feet
- 2 measures of water
- one bottle of Rhine wine
- half a pound of sugar
- zest of one lemon
- juice of one lemon
- egg white from 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Slit the calf's feet at the hoof and divide them apart, remove the long middle bone and discard it
2. Cook the feet with 2 measures of water for 4 hours, skimming often but without salting
3. Pass them through a hair cloth
4. Set them again on charcoal fire to reduce to half and become a thick stock
5. Add one bottle of Rhine wine, half a pound of sugar with the zest of one lemon rubbed into it, and the juice, without letting it boil so the fine flavor does not escape
6. Clarify the jelly with the foam from 6 eggs
7. Filter it
8. Fill into the mold
9. Set in a cool place
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gelee von verſchiedenen Farben (Jelly of Various Colors)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared orange or Rhine wine jelly
- sugar-dried fruits: mirabelles, reineclodes, apricots, grapes, and orange slices

## Directions

1. Pour approximately 2 fingers' height of finished orange or Rhine wine jelly into a mold
2. If possible, let it set on ice for a while
3. Arrange sugar-dried fruits symmetrically in it: mirabelles, reineclodes, apricots, grapes, and orange slices
4. Pour cooled jelly over these fruits
5. When sufficiently set and the fruits are well covered with jelly, arrange more fruits on top
6. Fill the mold completely with jelly and let it set well
7. When using, place the mold in hot water and immediately turn it out onto the plate
8. Do not leave the mold in hot water too long so that too much of the jelly does not melt
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gemeine Wilde Ente (Common Wild Duck)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon or marrow
- onion
- root vegetables
- juniper berries, crushed
- spices (5 grains of each type)
- thyme sprigs
- bay leaf
- sliced black bread (8 ounces)
- red wine (7/8 liter)
- vinegar (7/8 liter)
- broth
- cream (1/4 liter)
- butter (8 ounces)
- flour (8 ounces)
- sugar (2 ounces)
- salt and spices to taste
- puff pastry
- parsley

## Directions

1. Put some bacon or marrow, onion and root vegetables, all finely sliced, into a casserole dish along with 5 crushed juniper berries, 5 grains each of various spices, sprigs of thyme, and 1 bay leaf.
2. Place the prepared duck on top, cover it with bacon slices and roast uncovered.
3. After this, add 8 ounces of finely sliced black bread along with 7/8 liter red wine, 7/8 liter vinegar and broth, so that the liquid barely covers the duck.
4. Let it braise covered until the duck is tender - a young duck will need about 1 hour, an old duck about 2 hours.
5. Remove the meat and place it with the duck into a shallow casserole dish and set it in the oven.
6. After a few minutes, pour 1/4 liter of cream over the duck and let it reduce well.
7. In the meantime, make a fairly dark butter sauce from 8 ounces butter and 8 ounces flour, pour this over the reduced broth, add 2 ounces sugar and necessary seasoning, stir well, bring to a boil again, pass through a sieve and remove the fat.
8. Arrange the carved duck on a platter, pour some sauce over it and serve the rest in a sauce boat.
9. Garnish the duck with puff pastry cut into desired shapes and fresh parsley.

title: Gemischte Suppe von Rogen, Krebsen und Schnecken (Mixed Soup of Roe, Crayfish, and Snails)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked carp roe
- Cooked snails, finely cut
- Crayfish tails, removed from shells
- Crayfish claws
- Good pea broth
- Butter
- Spices
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place cooked carp roe in a bowl
2. Add cooked, finely cut snails, removed crayfish tails and claws
3. Pour good pea broth over everything
4. Add butter and spices
5. Salt as needed and let it come to a boil once
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gemischte Torte (Mixed Cake; Tourte à l'Alliance)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- various cake batters
- lingonberry jam or cream
- glaze
- fruits

## Directions

1. Bake finger-height plates from various cake batters and let them cool completely.
2. Spread each cooled plate with desired lingonberry jam or cream, then stack them together.
3. Cut the edges evenly all around with a fine, sharp knife.
4. Cover the entire cake with a glaze of choice and, once dry, decorate with fruits as desired.

title: Gemischte Torte Nr. 1 (Mixed Cake No. 1)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 decagrams butter, beaten
- 10 decagrams granulated sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 8 decagrams almonds with skin, finely ground and sifted
- 6 egg whites
- 1 bar grated chocolate
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Gradually mix 6 decagrams beaten butter with 10 decagrams granulated sugar and 6 egg yolks, stirring for 1/2 hour.
2. Add 8 decagrams finely ground and sifted almonds with skin, mix well, and lightly fold in the beaten snow of 6 egg whites mixed simultaneously with 1 bar of grated chocolate.
3. Fill a buttered cake form dusted with flour with this mixture and bake at moderate heat for 1/2 hour in the oven, then place on a sieve to cool.

title: Gemischte Torte Nr. 2 (Mixed Cake No. 2)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams finely ground sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 8 decagrams melted butter
- 8 decagrams flour
- 4 egg whites
- butter
- flour
- cream or marmalade

## Directions

1. Stir 8 decagrams finely ground sugar with 4 egg yolks for 1 1/2 hours.
2. Mix in 8 decagrams melted butter with 8 decagrams flour gradually, adding 1 tablespoon flour and 1 tablespoon butter alternately, then lightly fold in the stiffly beaten snow of 4 egg whites.
3. Fill a buttered cake form dusted with flour with this mixture and bake at moderate heat in the oven for 1 1/2 hours, then place on a sieve.
4. Once completely cooled, cut away the crust on the side that was on top during baking, spread with desired cream or marmalade, and place the mixed cake No. 1 (cut as described) on top.
5. If the edges do not fit exactly, trim them evenly with a sharp, thin knife and cover the entire cake with glaze.

title: Gemischter Salat (Mixed Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg fingerling potatoes
- 1 small celery root
- white beans
- ¼ l lentils
- 1 head lettuce or 10 dkg lamb's lettuce
- 1 red beet
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- salt
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Prepare potatoes as potato salad
2. Sort and wash beans and lentils, cook separately in cold water with some baking soda for 1½ hours more steaming than boiling
3. Drain beans and lentils, dress while still warm with salt, vinegar, and oil
4. Prepare celery root as salad
5. Wash red beet, cook in plenty of water for 1½-2 hours, cool, and slice thinly
6. Clean and wash lettuce or lamb's lettuce, cut head lettuce into noodle-like pieces, dress with salt, vinegar, and oil
7. Heap potato salad in center of glass bowl, arrange beans and lentils alternately around it, place lettuce between
8. Garnish with hard-boiled eggs cut into 8 pieces, celery, and red beet

title: Gemiſchtes Zugemüſe (Mixed Vegetable Side Dish)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- large yellow turnips
- kohlrabi
- salsify (Eckäpfel)
- butter
- finely cut parsley
- flour
- broth
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare large yellow turnips, kohlrabi, and salsify
2. Using a hollow iron, cut small balls from each vegetable
3. Place butter in a casserole and let it warm
4. Add the finely cut green parsley and the vegetables
5. Let them steam a little, but do not let them brown
6. Dust a little flour over them
7. Let them steam a while longer
8. Pour in some broth, but not much, so no sauce is visible
9. Add some sugar
10. Let it steam until the vegetables are tender enough
11. Serve with veal carbonades
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gemüse in Essig (Vegetables in Vinegar)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 parts white vinegar
- 1 part saffron
- spices
- some mixed spices
- green beans
- young small yellow turnips
- small onions
- small cauliflower pieces
- shallots
- rocambole
- artichoke hearts
- small cucumber pieces
- mushrooms
- asparagus
- salt
- salt water
- fresh picked cucumbers
- dill leaves
- chervil leaves
- green pepperoni (paprika)
- 5 cloves
- 5 bitter peppercorns

## Directions

1. Mix 2 parts white vinegar with 1 part saffron, spices, and some mixed spices and simmer for approximately 1/2 hour (skim off the foam that forms)
2. Let it cool in an earthen, glass, or porcelain vessel
3. Cook each type of vegetable - green beans, young small yellow turnips, small onions, small cauliflower pieces, shallots, rocambole, artichoke hearts, small cucumber pieces, mushrooms, and asparagus - separately in salt water until half-cooked
4. Place each in cold water and lay on a sieve or cloth to drain
5. For the cucumbers: place the smallest variety that have been picked in dry weather on a cloth, sprinkle with salt, and rub with the cloth until they become moist, then place on another cloth or sieve to drain slightly
6. Complete this process for the cucumbers within 1 hour
7. Line the bottom of prepared glasses with some dill leaves, chervil leaves, and green pepperoni (paprika)
8. Arrange the cucumbers and then the mixed vegetables in attractive layers, alternating with cucumbers, filling the glasses almost full
9. For each 1/2 liter glass, add 5 cloves and 5 bitter peppercorns
10. Pour the prepared cold vinegar over, ensuring the glass is not completely full
11. For ordinary household use, tie with twine; for storage of glasses, cork and seal them
12. For storage, keep at a cool, airy location
13. If the vinegar decreases over time, add water-diluted cold vinegar
14. If the vinegar becomes cloudy or creamy, strain it off, boil it, let it cool, and pour it back over the vegetables, keeping them covered and weighted at a cool, airy place

title: Gemüse in Kunst gekocht (Vegetables Preserved in Brine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh asparagus
- lemon water
- salt
- salt water
- tin foil
- bladders

## Directions

1. Lightly scrape fresh asparagus, break off the woody parts, and rinse briefly in cold water
2. Place in boiling lemon water with a little salt and cook for 3 minutes
3. Transfer to cold water and then place in glasses so that the asparagus heads point upward
4. Pour over with light, cooked, and cooled salt water so that the asparagus is covered 2 fingers high
5. Tie the glasses, which must remain 1 finger width empty at the top, with tin foil and bladders
6. Process in brine for 1/2 hour
7. All types of freshly picked vegetables can be preserved this way
8. When using, strain off the water, place in hot water, and prepare like fresh vegetables with butter or in sauces

title: Gemüseauflauf (Vegetable Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ liter green peas
- ½ kg cabbage sprouts
- 1 small head cauliflower
- 1 bunch carrots
- some green beans (optional)
- 3 mushrooms
- ¾ liter milk
- 5 dkg butter
- 4 dkg flour
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook the peas, cleaned and washed cabbage sprouts, cauliflower florets, and diced carrots and beans in salted water for 25-30 minutes.
2. Drain and let drip dry.
3. In a pot, make a roux with 5 dkg hot butter and 4 dkg flour until light golden, pour in the milk, stir continuously until thick, and let cool.
4. Mix the cooled sauce with 3 egg yolks, salt, the vegetables, thinly sliced mushrooms sautéed in butter, and 3 beaten egg whites.
5. Fill into a buttered soufflé dish, bake at moderately high heat for 30-45 minutes.
6. Serve immediately in the baking dish.

title: Gemüsepureesuppe (Vegetable Puree Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 h root vegetables
- 1 sliced bread roll (Schneidſemmel)
- 1 onion
- 5 peppercorns and spice berries
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 piece black bread crust
- vinegar
- hot water
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and cut the root vegetables into thin slices
2. Roast 1 onion, the root vegetables, 5 peppercorns and spice berries, 1 bay leaf and a piece of black bread crust in a little fat until dark
3. Splash with vinegar, pour on hot water and stew gently for 1½ hours until soft
4. Pass through a sieve, place in a casserole, pour on beef broth
5. Season if needed with more vinegar and cook through
6. Serve with bread slices
7. If too thin, add a light roux and let cook well

title: Gemüsesalat Saint Scott (Macedoine Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large cooked potatoes, thinly sliced
- 4 pieces pickled gherkins, thinly sliced
- 1 red beet, roasted in the oven, peeled and thinly sliced
- 1 artichoke heart, soaked in salt water, cut into small pieces
- 20 cooked green beans, broken in the middle
- 10 asparagus spears, soaked in salt water, cut into finger-length pieces
- Some cooked celery leaves, thinly sliced
- Some thinly sliced yellow beets
- Vinegar
- 4 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon French mustard
- Salt and pepper
- 1 raw egg yolk
- 8 tablespoons oil
- 4 tablespoons tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut all vegetables as described and place in a deep bowl
2. Pour some vinegar over and let stand covered for 10 minutes
3. In the meantime, press 4 hard-boiled egg yolks through a sieve into a deep bowl
4. Add 1 tablespoon French mustard, some salt and pepper
5. Stir with a raw egg yolk, then gradually add 8 tablespoons oil and 4 tablespoons tarragon vinegar
6. Mix everything well with the vegetables
7. The salad should be thick and well bound
8. Serve with various roasted meats or roasted fish

title: Genevan Torte (Tourte à la Genève)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deka beaten butter
- 28 Deka orange sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 whole eggs
- 8 Deka blanched almonds
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1.5 knife tips cinnamon
- 6 hard-boiled and passed egg yolks
- 30 Deka flour

## Directions

1. Mix beaten butter with orange sugar
2. Add egg yolks and whole eggs one by one
3. Add ground almonds, cinnamon, and hard-boiled egg yolks
4. Stir for 1/2 hour
5. Lightly mix in flour
6. Brush round or square paper sheets finger-high with the mixture
7. Proceed as in the 'Viennese Torte' (no. 1486)

title: Genfer Torte (Cake from Geneva)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deca butter
- 28 Deca vanilla sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 28 Deca fine flour
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy
2. Mix in the vanilla sugar
3. Stir for 1/4 hour
4. Gradually add the 6 egg yolks and stir for another 1/2 hour
5. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites simultaneously with 28 Deca fine flour
6. Proceed as with the Biscuit-Torte (No. 1498)

title: Genießer-Zimtsbrötchen (Gourmet Cinnamon Rolls)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams butter
- 4 decagrams finely ground sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 5 egg yolks
- 4 decagrams pressed yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
- 30 decagrams flour
- melted butter for brushing
- cinnamon mixed with finely ground sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy, mix in the finely ground sugar and salt, then gradually stir in the egg yolks
2. Mix in the pressed yeast dissolved in lukewarm milk and the flour, stirring well together
3. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon
4. Cover the dough and let it rise in a warm place until doubled in height
5. Lay the dough on a flour-dusted board, roll it out to the thickness of a knife back, and cut it into strips 3 fingers long and 2 fingers wide
6. Sprinkle the strips with finely ground sugar mixed with cinnamon, roll them together, and lay them side by side in a butter-greased casserole with the ends of the rolled dough facing upward
7. Brush the areas where the rolls touch each other with melted butter
8. Cover with a cloth and let rise in a warm place until slightly higher
9. Brush with melted butter, pour 1/2 liter of boiling milk mixed with 2 tablespoons sugar over them, and bake slowly for 1 hour
10. Turn out the casserole, separate the rolls, arrange on a plate, sprinkle with sugar, and serve

title: Genua-Schnitte (Genoa Slices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 Deca butter
- 18 Deca sugar
- 4 whole eggs
- 18 Deca flour
- 1 tablespoon rum
- marmalade as needed
- transparent glaze

## Directions

1. Place the butter in a deep bowl and stir only slightly so it does not become fluffy
2. Add 1 whole egg, 1 tablespoon flour and 1 tablespoon sugar, mix together
3. Repeat this process until all 4 eggs are used
4. Add the remaining flour and sugar simultaneously, mix well and fold in 1 tablespoon rum
5. Spread this mixture knife-back width thick on a baking sheet brushed with butter and bake light yellow in the oven for a short time
6. Remove the sheet from the oven and cut the dough with a long thin knife into two layers
7. Spread one layer with dissolved marmalade and cover with the second layer so the edges lie evenly
8. Spread the upper dough also with marmalade, and after it has cooled completely, cover with a transparent glaze
9. Cut the whole into finger-long and 2 finger-width wide pieces
10. Can also be served warm with Chaudeau

title: Genuaschnitte (Genoa Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dkg butter
- 4 whole eggs
- 18 dkg sugar
- 1 tablespoon rum
- 18 dkg flour
- Jam for filling

## Directions

1. Beat butter until soft
2. Gradually add 4 whole eggs, sugar, and rum, stirring well
3. Gradually mix in flour by spoonfuls
4. Spread on buttered, floured sheet (not to the edges)
5. Bake at medium-high heat until light brown
6. While still hot, cut into rounds or squares
7. Cool, remove from sheet
8. Spread with jam, sandwich together
9. Ice or sprinkle with sugar as desired

title: Genüdslicher Französcher Wind (Delicate French Meringue)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 16 Deca finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites until stiff.
2. Lightly fold in 16 Deca finely sifted sugar.
3. Fill this mixture into a pastry bag and pipe equal-sized mounds onto a baking sheet coated with unsalted bacon fat.
4. Sprinkle with finely sifted sugar and dry in a cool, open oven.
5. Alternatively, the mixture can be dropped with a spoon to create rose-shaped mounds.

title: Geriebenes (Grated Noodles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter flour
- salt
- 2 whole eggs
- approximately 2 liters beef broth

## Directions

1. Place 1/2 liter flour on a board, make a well in the middle
2. Add salt and 2 whole eggs
3. Knead into a very firm dough
4. Rub through a grater, spreading grated pieces on board with flat hand to dry before cooking
5. In approximately 2 liters boiling beef broth, cook grated dough with constant stirring
6. Cook 8-10 minutes

title: Germ-Waffeln (Yeast Waffles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 Deca butter, beaten
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 Deca orange or lemon sugar
- 4 Deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter milk
- 30 Deca flour
- 5 egg whites, beaten
- butter or unsalted bacon fat for oiling the waffle iron
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Gradually beat into 30 Deca beaten butter: 6 egg yolks, 4 Deca orange or lemon sugar, and 4 Deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter milk, mixing them one after another.
2. Lightly fold in the beaten egg whites from 5 eggs together with 30 Deca flour.
3. Let the dough rise covered in a warm place until it has increased by a third in volume.
4. Oil both sides of the waffle iron with melted butter or preferably unsalted bacon fat.
5. Place 2 spoonfuls of the dough in the iron, close it well, place in coals, and after several minutes turn it to the other side and place in the coals for an equal time.
6. Remove the waffle with two forks, place on a plate, and keep in a warm place.
7. Continue baking waffles until the dough is used up.
8. Shortly before serving, sprinkle with sugar and serve with fruit sauce or desired warm sauce.
9. Note: The first baked waffles usually do not turn out well. Grease the iron moderately but repeatedly. When finished baking, do not wash the iron but only clean it with paper.

title: Germguglhupf (Yeast Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg butter
- 7 dkg sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- a pinch of salt
- 35 dkg warmed flour
- yeast leaven from 1½ dkg yeast
- some spoonfuls lukewarm milk
- 5 dkg currants

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until very fluffy
2. Mix in 7 dkg sugar, 2 egg yolks and 1 whole egg thoroughly
3. Add a pinch of salt, 35 dkg warmed flour, yeast leaven from 1½ dkg yeast, and some spoonfuls lukewarm milk
4. Mix the whole, beat the dough well and fold in 5 dkg currants
5. Fill the prepared mold
6. Let it rise uncovered near the oven until the mold is full
7. Bake the cake in moderate heat until light brown

title: Germguglhupf sehr fein (Fine Yeast Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg butter
- 8 dkg sugar
- 3 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- lemon zest
- 20 dkg warmed flour
- a pinch of salt
- 3 spoonfuls lukewarm milk or cream
- yeast leaven from 1½ dkg yeast
- 4 dkg currants

## Directions

1. Beat the butter for 15 minutes
2. Gradually mix in 8 dkg sugar, 3 whole eggs, 1 egg yolk and lemon zest
3. Add 20 dkg warmed flour and a pinch of salt, 3 spoonfuls lukewarm milk or cream, and yeast leaven from 1½ dkg yeast
4. Mix the dough well
5. Beat it for several more minutes
6. Fold in 4 dkg currants
7. Fill the dough into the mold
8. Let it rise covered
9. Bake it in weaker heat until light brown

title: Germknödel oder bayerische Kügel (Yeast Dumplings or Bavarian Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼-½ dkg yeast
- ½ liter milk
- ½ kg plum butter (Powidl)
- 30 dkg flour
- 5 dkg butter
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 whole egg
- salt
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- lukewarm milk
- flour for dusting
- breadcrumbs roasted in fat

## Directions
