1. Warm 30 dkg flour in a mixing bowl and crumble in 5 dkg butter
2. Add 1 egg yolk, 1 whole egg, salt, 2 tablespoons sugar, the risen starter (Dampfl), and a few tablespoons lukewarm milk
3. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon and let it rise near the stove
4. When it has nearly doubled in size, use a floured spoon to place small pieces of dough on a floured board
5. Pull them apart with floured fingers, fill with plum butter, press the edges together, and shape into dumplings
6. Cook in a saucepan with plenty of boiling salted water for 8-10 minutes
7. Transfer to a round dish, pour breadcrumbs roasted in fat over them, and serve
8. Alternative toppings: grated poppy seeds or gingerbread with hot fat poured over

title: Germstreuchteh (Yeast Crumb Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 Decaliter butter
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 Decaliter fresh yeast dissolved in 1/2 liter milk
- 42 Decaliter flour

## Directions

1. Beat 14 Decaliter butter until fluffy.
2. Add 1 tablespoon sugar, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 5 egg yolks one after another, stirring them in.
3. Mix this with 3 Decaliter fresh yeast dissolved in 1/2 liter milk and 42 Decaliter flour until well combined.

title: Germstrigel (Yeast Braid)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg flour
- 8 dkg butter, cut into pieces
- a pinch of salt
- 2-3 spoonfuls sugar
- 1 egg
- yeast leaven from 2 dkg yeast
- some spoonfuls lukewarm milk
- 5 dkg currants
- beaten egg for brushing
- swollen, chopped almonds or pearl sugar

## Directions

1. Warm the flour in a mixing bowl, rub in 8 dkg cut butter
2. Add a pinch of salt, 2-3 spoonfuls sugar, 1 egg, yeast leaven from 2 dkg yeast, and some spoonfuls lukewarm milk
3. Mix the dough and beat it well
4. On the board, knead it for 20-30 minutes until quite fine
5. Gradually knead in 5 dkg currants
6. Divide the dough into 9 parts: 4 large, 3 medium, and 2 small
7. Form the 4 large parts into fairly long rolls and braid them together
8. Place on greased baking sheet
9. Form the remaining parts correspondingly smaller, roll and braid them
10. Press the four-strand braid in the middle, place the three-strand braid on top, press again, and place the two-strand braid over it
11. Let the braid rise near the oven
12. Brush with beaten egg
13. Sprinkle with swollen, chopped almonds or pearl sugar
14. Bake in weaker heat until light brown

title: Germteig Finest Style (Finest Yeast Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 deca beaten butter
- 6 deca vanilla sugar
- 1 knife point salt
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- 2 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter lukewarm milk
- 48 deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter gradually with vanilla sugar, salt, egg yolks, and whole eggs
2. Add the yeast dissolved in milk and flour, mix well
3. Beat the dough until it lifts from the spoon
4. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled
5. Place dough on a flour-dusted board, roll out to the thickness of a small finger
6. Cut out with an oblong pastry cutter
7. Place half of the obtained pieces on a butter-greased baking sheet at a distance of 2 finger widths apart
8. Brush with melted butter, sprinkle with vanilla or almond sugar, cover each piece with a lid
9. Cover with cloth and place in a warm spot
10. Once the dough has risen somewhat, brush with beaten egg, sprinkle with white pearl sugar, and bake slowly in the oven
11. When the dough is pale yellow, loosen from the baking sheet with a very thin knife and arrange on a platter

title: Germteig for Parisian and Vienna Strudels (Yeast Dough for Strudels)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca beaten butter
- 1 tablespoon finely pounded sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
- 60 deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter gradually with sugar, salt, egg yolks, and whole eggs
2. Add the yeast dissolved in milk and flour, mix well
3. Beat the dough until it lifts from the spoon
4. Cover and let rise in a warm place until risen and doubled in volume

title: Germudeln anderer Art (Yeast Dumplings, Another Way)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough as prepared in Nr. 1258
- Lard for brushing
- 1/2 liter boiling milk
- 2 tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as described in Nr. 1258
2. After the dough has risen covered in a warm place, use a spoon to place pieces of desired size in a casserole brushed with lard
3. Lay them closely next to each other, brush them meanwhile with melted lard
4. Cover them with a cloth and let them stand in a warm place until they have risen
5. Brush them with melted lard, pour over them 1/2 liter boiling milk mixed with 2 tablespoons sugar and bake for 3/4 to 1 hour in a fairly hot oven
6. After this time, remove the casserole from the oven, turn it out, dust the dumplings with sugar and serve at table

title: Geroestete Wurfel (Roasted Noodle Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/2 liter flour
- 10 deca butter
- 2 liters beef broth

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough from the eggs and flour
2. Rub this dough on a grater
3. Roast the noodles in 10 deca butter until they take on a yellowish color
4. Otherwise proceed as indicated in the previous soup recipe
5. If it is to be used for seasoning, pour the roasted noodles with 1/4 liter boiling soup and let it evaporate

title: Gerollte Kalbsschulter mit Reis (Rolled Veal Shoulder with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-80 dkg boned veal shoulder
- salt
- butter or fat pieces
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the meat and salt it
2. Tie it up
3. Place skin-side down in a pan
4. Top with butter or fat pieces
5. Add some water beside it
6. Roast in the oven for 1½ hours, basting and replenishing hot water frequently
7. Remove, untie, slice, and serve with the pan juices

title: Gerste-Soufflé (Barley Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 egg yolk
- necessary flour
- 1/2 liter boiling milk
- 10 Deca butter
- 1 pinch of salt
- 8 Deca vanilla sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 11 egg whites (snow)

## Directions

1. When a grated barley has been prepared from 1 egg, 1 egg yolk and the necessary flour, stir this into 1/2 liter boiling milk, cook until thick, stir with 10 Deca butter and 1 pinch of salt and let cool
2. Meanwhile stir 8 Deca vanilla sugar with 6 egg yolks for 1/2 hour, add the cooled barley, mix well and finally fold in lightly the snow from 11 egg whites
3. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour and cook covered for 3/4 hours in steam or bake in the oven
4. When serving, sprinkle the soufflé with finely pounded sugar and serve with dissolved jam or cream

title: Gerstelschöberl (Barley Bread Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- Corresponding amount of flour
- 1-2 liters boiling milk
- 8 Deca butter
- 8 egg yolks
- Salt
- Snow from 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Make a grated barley bread from 2 eggs and a corresponding amount of flour, add it to 1-2 liters boiling milk and let it cook through
2. Beat 8 Deca butter until fluffy, add 8 egg yolks and some salt and stir in the prepared barley
3. Lightly mix in the snow from 8 eggs
4. Further handling is as with Griesschöberl, except the barley schöberl comes to the table together with the soup

title: Gerstenbrey oder Orgeat (Barley Pap or Orgeat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- hulled and soaked barley
- sweet almonds with skin removed
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take hulled barley that has been soaked
2. Rub it with the hands
3. Cook it for seven or eight hours
4. Place it in a stone mortar
5. Rub it with blanched sweet almonds and press it through
6. Add sugar
7. Place it in a silver or earthen bowl on glowing coals
8. Let it cook very gently, then it is ready
9. Those who prefer it thicker may cook the pressed mixture again; those who prefer it thinner may leave it uncooked
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gerstenbrèy oder Orgeat (Barley Cream or Orgeat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled barley, soaked and rubbed by hand, cooked for seven or eight hours
- sweet almonds, blanched
- sugar
- silver or earthen dish
- glowing coals
- milk (optional)

## Directions

1. Take the prepared barley and place it in a stone mortar
2. Rub it together with blanched sweet almonds and press it through
3. Add sugar according to taste
4. Place the mixture in a silver or earthen dish on glowing coals and let it cook very gently until finished
5. Some prefer it thinner, others thicker; if you cook the pressed mixture again it becomes thicker and more nourishing; if you do not cook it, it remains thinner and is preferred by many, though it nourishes less
6. It may be taken 2 to 3 or 4 times daily depending on whether it is thicker or thinner
7. Sometimes milk is added toward the end of cooking, which makes it more pleasant, but is not suitable for those who have a fever
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gerstenbrühe (Barley Broth)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 deka barley grains
- 1 liter cold water
- Sugar
- Wine
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash the barley grains somewhat, then put them in 1 liter of cold water.
2. Let them boil until they burst.
3. Let them stand for some time until the thick part has settled to the bottom.
4. Strain it through a fine sieve or a cloth and add sugar, wine, and lemon juice as desired.

title: Gerstenscbleim-Suppe (Barley Cream Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- barley
- broth
- roasted bread pieces
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare barley the same way as rice, but barley must cook 1 hour longer than rice
2. When serving, add roasted bread pieces to the barley cream soup
3. Serve with 1 lemon cut into 8 pieces
4. This soup is recommended for sick people

title: Geräucherte Leberwurst (Smoked Liver Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fatty raw or uncooked liver, finely chopped
- One quarter of the broth from the previous recipe
- Cleaned intestines

## Directions

1. Use the ingredients from the previous recipe, but make it somewhat fattier
2. Add raw or uncooked liver, finely chopped
3. Add only one quarter of the aforementioned broth
4. Fill into cleaned intestines
5. Hang for two to three days in smoke
6. Can be eaten raw and uncooked; best served cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Geräucherter (gefeilter) Hausen (Smoked Hausen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked hausen
- water
- salt
- caraway seed
- vegetables or purée or boiled potatoes with butter

## Directions

1. Cook the smoked hausen in water with some salt and caraway seed, covered, until soft (approximately 1/2 to 1/3 hour)
2. Remove from water and cut into pieces
3. Arrange in a serving dish and serve with vegetables or purée or boiled potatoes with butter

title: Geröstete Grießsuppe (Roasted Semolina Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 dkg fat
- 8 dkg semolina
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. In a casserole, let 8 dkg semolina turn light brown in 8 dkg fat
2. Pour over with cold water
3. Salt to taste and let the soup cook well

title: Geröstete Kalbsleber (Sautéed Veal Liver)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- salt
- flour
- fat
- onion
- vinegar

## Directions

1. After freeing the calf liver from its skin and cutting it into strips the thickness of a knife back and finger length, it is lightly salted, dredged on both sides in flour and fried in very hot fat.
2. If one desires onion with the liver, finely cut onion is added alongside the liver and allowed to brown with it.
3. The roasted liver is then neatly arranged on a platter, the remaining juices are soured with a little vinegar, somewhat reduced, poured over the liver and garnished with the fried onion.
4. However, the frying of the liver should only occur shortly before serving, as the liver becomes tough through longer standing.
5. This liver can be fried in its natural state, without vinegar and onion.

title: Geröstete Nieren (Sautéed Veal Kidneys)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fat
- veal kidneys
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- broth or white wine
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Put some fat in a casserole and roast finely cut onions in it.
2. Meanwhile, skin the kidneys, cut them into thin slices, and add them to the roasted onions.
3. Dust with some flour, salt and pepper them, stir well together, roast for 5 to 10 minutes.
4. Pour broth or white wine over them, add a pinch of marjoram, stir everything well together and serve in a bowl.

title: Gerührte Eyer mit Semmel (Scrambled Eggs with Bread)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread crumbs (Semmelbrösel)
- Milk
- Salt
- Sugar
- Lemon peel, chopped
- Fresh egg yolks
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Soak bread crumbs in milk for about 2 hours until very soft, then press through a fine sieve.
2. Add a little salt and sugar and chopped lemon peel.
3. Beat in as many fresh egg yolks as needed and mix with the bread.
4. Beat the egg whites to stiff snow and fold in.
5. Coat a casserole with butter, put the mixture in it, and bake in the oven or under a cake cover with heat from above and below.
6. Turn it out onto a dish, dust with sugar, and glaze it with a hot shovel.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gerührte Rahmnudeln (Stirred Cream Noodles)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 4 eggs
- salt
- 1/2 Seidel (about 1/4 liter) cream
- flour
- 1 Mass (about 2 liters) boiling milk
- 1/2 pound butter for finishing
- crushed sugar
- 4 or 5 egg yolks for the sauce
- sugar for the sauce

## Directions

1. Beat 1/4 pound butter with 4 eggs until clear, add a little salt and 1/2 Seidel cream
2. Stir in as much flour as needed so the dough can be worked
3. Using a knife on a floured sheet, form oblong small sausages
4. Boil them in 1 Mass of boiling milk
5. Meanwhile, let 1/2 pound butter in a cassole become very hot and layer the small sausages from the milk into it in layers
6. Sprinkle crushed sugar over them and set a lid with coals on top
7. When the noodles are golden baked, stir the remaining milk with 4 or 5 egg yolks, add some sugar, and serve separately with the noodles
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gerüsteter Indiant (Dindon rôti)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Young turkey, plucked, scalded, and cleaned
- Speck (bacon) slices
- Butter
- Watercress
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the wings, neck, and feet from the young turkey.
2. Season the turkey inside and out, let it rest for 1 hour, then dress it as described for pheasant (No. 735).
3. Cover it with sliced speck, place on a spit, wrap it in several sheets of paper brushed with butter, and roast over moderate heat for approximately 1 1/2 hours.
4. After this time, remove the paper and roast the turkey over bright heat until it becomes a nice brown color.
5. Alternatively, place the dressed turkey covered with speck in a pan containing fat and roast in the oven.
6. Remove the turkey, cut into pieces, reassemble them, arrange on a platter, and garnish with watercress which is sprinkled with salt and sprinkled with vinegar.
7. A turkey of medium age should, if it is to be roasted, lie in the feathers for several days after the entrails have been removed so that the meat becomes tender.
8. An older turkey can no longer be roasted, but should be braised instead.

title: Geschlagener Rahm (Whipped Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- egg whites from 8 eggs
- 1 seidel good cream
- 1 seidel Spanish wine
- sugar
- lemon peels

## Directions

1. Carefully beat off the whites from 8 eggs and pour in 1 seidel good cream and 1 seidel Spanish wine
2. Sweeten as desired, beat it with a clean small whisk, and add some lemon peels
3. When the snow is strong enough, take some from the top with a spoon, pile it on a plate, and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Geschnittenes Wurm (Cut Noodle Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/3 liter flour
- 2 liters beef broth

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough from the eggs and flour
2. Work this dough and then cut it very finely with a sharp knife
3. Rub it with both flat hands several times
4. Pass it through a sieve so that it looks like fine barley
5. Continue cutting and sieving the remaining dough until it is all processed
6. In 2 liters of boiling beef broth, add this fine noodle with constant stirring and let it simmer for 8-10 minutes
7. Remove any foam that forms, strain the soup, and serve at table

title: Geschnittenes Wurm (Cut Worm Noodles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/3 liter flour

## Directions

1. Make firm dough from 2 whole eggs, 1 egg yolk, and 1/3 liter flour
2. Work and knead dough
3. Cut very finely with a sharp knife
4. Rub several times with flat hands
5. Push through a sieve

title: Geschäumte Bittere Mandel's Creme (Whipped Bitter Almond Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 bitter almonds
- 20 sweet almonds, blanched
- 1/2 liter cream
- 5 egg yolks
- 10 deca sugar
- 8 deca dissolved warm isinglass
- 1/3 liter cold water
- 1/8 liter reduction
- 2 deca dissolved gelatin
- Coffee Cream with Gelatin No. 1090
- 1/2 liter whipping cream

## Directions

1. Pound 5 bitter and 20 sweet blanched almonds very finely
2. Cook with 1/2 liter cream and press through a cloth
3. Stir with 5 egg yolks and 10 deca sugar over the fire until thick
4. Pass the mass through a hair sieve into a deep bowl
5. Add 8 deca dissolved warm isinglass (prepared with 1/3 liter cold water and reduced to 1/8 liter) or 2 deca dissolved gelatin (Coffee Cream with Gelatin No. 1090) to the cream
6. Stir until it begins to thicken and is completely cold
7. 1 hour earlier, place 1/2 liter whipping cream in a snow bucket on ice
8. Beat the cream to stiff foam and fold in the cream
9. Coat a desired mold with fine oil
10. Bury in ice and fill with the prepared cream
11. Let stand several hours
12. Shortly before serving, dip the mold briefly in hot water, wipe dry quickly and turn out onto a platter
13. Garnish the cream with meringues or Spanish wind

title: Geschäumte Mandel-Suppe (Foamed Almond Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca blanched almonds
- some milk
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 2 liters lukewarm milk
- 4 egg yolks
- 8 deca sugar
- some cinnamon or vanilla
- some cold milk

## Directions

1. Blanched almonds are, so they do not become oily, finely ground with some milk
2. Stir them with 1 tablespoon flour into 2 liters lukewarm milk and let cook under constant stirring until well cooked
3. In a pot which is twice as large as the mass, beat 4 egg yolks, stir in 8 deca sugar, some cinnamon or vanilla and a few spoonfuls cold milk
4. Pour the boiling milk through a sieve into it, beat on the fire until it becomes foamy and serve the soup

title: Gesetzte Eyer (Poached Eggs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour cream (Schmette)
- Butter, in small pieces
- Eggs, as many as needed
- Salt

## Directions

1. Line a tin dish with sour cream, crumble butter on it, break in as many eggs as needed so they remain whole.
2. Set over heat, let them simmer, and salt them.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gesetzte Eyer mit Sardellen (Soft Eggs with Anchovies)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour cream (Schmette)
- Butter, in small pieces
- Anchovies, finely chopped and boned
- Eggs, as many as needed
- Salt

## Directions

1. Coat a dish with sour cream.
2. Add small pieces of butter and finely chopped, boned anchovies, and let the butter melt.
3. Break eggs into the dish so they remain whole, as many as needed.
4. Salt them and let them come to a simmer.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gesottene Brotschnitte für Sechswöchnerinnen, Verwundete und hitzige Fieberkranke (Boiled Bread Slices for Women in Childbed, the Wounded, and Those with Acute Fevers)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of rolls or white bread
- melted butter
- warm water

## Directions

1. Cover the bottom of a bowl with thin slices of rolls or white bread
2. Pour a little melted butter over it
3. Lay another layer of thin bread on top
4. Pour more butter over it
5. Continue in this manner until the bowl is full
6. Pour a little warm water over it, but only so much that no water is visible
7. Cover it well
8. Let it boil gently over a mild fire
9. The dish is then ready
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gesottene Semmel für Kranke (Boiled Roll for the Sick)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- round pieces of a roll
- milk
- egg yolk
- butter
- plum or damson broth

## Directions

1. Take round pieces of a roll and cut away the rind
2. Soak the roll pieces in milk for several hours
3. Gently press them through a cloth
4. Roll the roll pieces in egg yolk several times
5. Simmer them in butter
6. Pour plum or damson broth over them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gesottener Hecht (Boiled Pike)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- water
- salt
- 1 bay leaf

## Directions

1. Scale, open and clean the pike
2. In a fish pan, add necessary water and salt with 1 bay leaf
3. When the water boils, place the pike in (the water must cover the pike)
4. As soon as the water returns to boiling, remove from heat
5. Let the pike stand approximately 3/4 hour while the water maintains simmering temperature but must not boil
6. When serving, place the pike on a suitable platter or on thin board covered with a napkin that must be sewn together on the underside of the board
7. Arrange plucked green parsley around it and garnish the fish with diced potatoes boiled in salt water
8. Serve with melted butter, Hollandaise sauce, thin anchovy sauce, or strained mustard sauce
9. The cuts in the fish need not necessarily be made
10. The pike can also be divided into 2-finger-wide pieces, each cut multiple times (scored)

title: Gespickte kälberne Schnitzel (Larded Veal Cutlets)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shank
- bacon
- salt
- fat
- onion
- cloves
- flour
- breadcrumbs
- broth
- lemon juice
- lemon peel
- sardelles
- nutmeg
- mussels
- wine

## Directions

1. Cut two fingers wide and four fingers long cutlets from a veal shank, not too thick.
2. Lard them with finely cut bacon, salt them slightly, and place in hot fat.
3. Stick an onion with cloves and place it with them, let turn golden brown.
4. Take some flour and breadcrumbs, sprinkle on the cutlets, and braise until it turns brownish.
5. Pour a ladle full of broth over, add juice of half a lemon, some cut lemon peel, two finely chopped sardelles, and some nutmeg.
6. Wash fresh mussels with wine, add them, and let come to a boil. The mussels can also be shelled.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gespickter Lungenbraten (Larded Beef Tenderloin)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef tenderloin
- salt
- one part vinegar
- two parts water
- onion pieces
- yellow turnips
- lemon peel, cut
- two bay leaves
- whole pepper
- lard for larding
- one measure of cream milk

## Directions

1. Take a beautiful beef tenderloin, cleanly remove the skin, cut off the legs and salt it
2. Make a marinade from one part vinegar and two parts water, add onion pieces, yellow turnips, cut lemon peel, two bay leaves, and some whole pepper
3. Let it boil well and pour it hot over the beef tenderloin, cover it with a lid and let it stand for twelve to twenty-four hours in a cool place
4. Take the beef tenderloin and lard it in three or four rows
5. Remove the onions and yellow turnips from the marinade and lay them in a pan, place the beef tenderloin on top
6. Add heat above and below and let it braise slowly for two hours until it takes on a yellowish-brown color
7. Pour one measure of cream milk over the beef tenderloin, let it braise for another quarter hour until the sauce has a beautiful yellow color
8. Place the beef tenderloin on the dish and serve the sauce underneath
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gespickter und gebeitzter Kalbsschlägel (Larded and Marinated Veal Leg)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Small veal leg
- Bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Brined bread
- Wine vinegar
- Cloves
- Nutmeg
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Skin the leg and lard it with bacon
2. Place it in a marinade for 24 hours
3. Remove and rub with salt
4. Roast it beautifully and juicily on a spit
5. Soak a slice of house bread in a small pot
6. Pour good wine vinegar over it
7. Season with pepper, cloves, and nutmeg
8. Let it simmer
9. When serving, garnish with thin and neatly cut lemon slices
10. Strain the broth into the dish
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gestoßene Lebersuppe (Pressed Liver Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound calf's liver
- 3 parts of a bread roll
- 2 eggs
- lard
- good broth
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Bake 1/4 pound calf's liver, 3 parts of a bread roll, and 2 eggs in lard
2. Pound everything finely together in a mortar
3. Put it in a pot
4. Fill with good broth to which nutmeg has been added
5. Let it cook for half an hour
6. Press through a sieve
7. Return to the pot
8. Let it cook a bit longer
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gestreifte Sulz (Striped Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Orange jelly mass
- Spinach juice or other coloring agents

## Directions

1. Prepare an orange jelly mass
2. Divide it into several equal parts and color them as desired with spinach juice to make green, with alkermes juice to make red, with violet flowers to make gray, etc.
3. These differently colored jelly masses are poured into the mold alternately, 2 fingers high, and each time the poured jelly is left to freeze before more jelly mass is poured and so continued until the mold is full
4. One must be careful to pour the same amount each time so the mold will be properly filled

title: Gesundheits-Punch mit Kikuruj (Health Punch with Kikuruj)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sugar in small pieces
- juice of 1 lemon
- juice of 4 oranges
- 1/4 liter rum
- 1/8 kilogram rinsed kikuruj
- 1 liter water

## Directions

1. Prepare like the Gesundheits-Punch mit Reis
2. Instead of rice, boil 1/8 kilogram rinsed kikuruj in 1 liter water for 1/4 hour
3. Strain and add to the punch components

title: Gesundheits-Punch mit Reis (Health Punch with Rice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sugar in small pieces
- juice of 1 lemon
- juice of 4 oranges
- 1/4 liter rum
- 12 deciliters rice
- 1 liter water
- 4 egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. In a porcelain bowl, add sugar broken into small pieces with the juice of 1 lemon and the juice of 4 oranges along with 1/4 liter rum
2. Set this covered in a warm place
3. In the meantime, boil 12 deciliters rice in 1 liter water for approximately 3/4 hour
4. Add the boiling rice to the dissolved sugar instead of tea
5. If desired, whip 4 egg yolks and add them

title: Gesundheits-Punch mit Weizen (Health Punch with Wheat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sugar in small pieces
- juice of 1 lemon
- juice of 4 oranges
- 1/4 liter rum
- 1/8 liter wheat grains
- 1 liter cold water

## Directions

1. Like Gesundheits-Punch mit Reis, but instead of rice, stir 1/8 liter wheat grains in 1 liter cold water
2. Boil slowly for 1/2 hour
3. Shock with some cold water
4. Allow the grains to settle
5. When the grain water becomes clear, strain it into the punch components

title: Gesürztes Compot in Lemon Jelly (Candied Compote in Lemon Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lemon jelly
- Various fruits such as redcurrants, pitted cherries, Angelica pieces, pineapple pieces, nuts, and other desired compote

## Directions

1. Prepare a lemon jelly and pour it into a mold set in ice to a depth of 2 fingers
2. Once this is set, decoratively arrange with a pastry needle redcurrants, hollowed-out pieces of pineapple, nuts, Angelica or similar, but so that the walls of the mold remain untouched
3. Carefully pour 2 fingers high of jelly mixture along the wall
4. Let it freeze again and then in the same way arrange various desired compote
5. Pour jelly over it and continue this way until the mold is full
6. Of course, jelly mixture must be used at the end
7. Cover and handle further as with orange jelly (No. 1060)
8. Small fruits such as currants, pitted cherries and similar can be used raw or preserved
9. Other fruit must however be steamed or cooked in steam

title: Gesüßte Apfel (Candied Apples)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- About 10 beautiful Manzanita apples
- Lemon juice
- Cold water
- 32 deka sugar
- 1/4 liter water
- Juice of 1/2 lemon
- 2 deka isinglass
- 7/8 liter cold water
- 1/4 liter water for reduction
- Marmalade
- Angelica cut into neat pieces
- Green almonds cut into neat pieces

## Directions

1. Peel about 10 beautiful apples, hollow out 5 pieces and halve the others
2. Rub all with lemon and place in cold water with lemon juice to keep them white
3. Boil 32 deka sugar, 1/4 liter water, and the juice of 1/2 lemon, remove the rising foam
4. Cook the apples in this clarified sugar until somewhat tender
5. Place them in cold water with some lemon juice
6. Prepare 2 deka isinglass by stirring it with 7/8 liter cold water and reducing to 1/4 liter
7. Pour this while still warm into the sugar, mix both together and let it cool
8. Pour into a mold that has been previously placed in ice, filling finger-high with the sugar mixture
9. Let this freeze, then arrange the apples very decoratively, finger-width apart, filling the hollowed ones with marmalade
10. Also use whole redcurrants and neatly cut pieces of Angelica or green almonds
11. Pour the remaining sugar mixture over, let it set solid, dip the mold briefly in hot water, turn the apples out onto a dish and serve

title: Getrocknete Bohnen oder Fisolen zu kochen (Dried Beans or Haricots)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- dried beans
- water
- fat or butter
- onion
- parsley
- meat broth
- egg yolks
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil the beans in fresh water until very soft.
2. Strain off the water.
3. Let fat or butter run in a pan with finely cut onion and parsley.
4. Add the beans with a little good meat broth and let them boil up a little before serving.
5. Take several egg yolks mixed with a little wine vinegar, fricassee the beans with this mixture, and serve.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Getrocknete Fißolen mit Butter (Haricots verts au beurre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney beans
- 8 ounces butter
- 1 teaspoon green parsley
- 1 lemon
- salt
- ground pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the kidney beans as for salad
2. Place the cooked kidney beans in a casserole dish
3. Mash 8 ounces of butter with 1 teaspoon of green parsley and the juice of 1 lemon
4. Mix with the kidney beans, some salt, and a pinch of ground pepper
5. Let stand covered on the hot plate for a few minutes
6. Stir well together and when the beans are hot throughout, serve

title: Gewöhnliche braune Suppe (Ordinary Brown Broth)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca leafy-cut fat (beef marrow, lard, veal kidney fat or marrow)
- 12 deca beef liver
- 1 piece veal bone
- 1/3 kilo beef broth meat
- 1/2 kilo chopped beef bones
- 1 piece yellow turnip
- 1 piece parsley
- 1 piece onion
- 1 table piece (approximately 2 kilo) beef
- approximately 4 liters cold water
- 1/4 liter cold water
- poultry scraps (if available)
- spice clove, 3 grains pepper, 3 grains allspice (optional)

## Directions

1. Place leafy-cut fat in a casserolle, either beef marrow, lard, veal kidney fat or marrow
2. Add 12 deca beef liver, 1 piece veal bone, 1/3 kilo beef broth meat, 1/2 kilo chopped beef bones, 1 piece yellow turnip, 1 piece parsley, and 1 piece onion, all leafy-cut
3. Cover with a lid, place on fire and let it brown well (brown roast)
4. Meanwhile, take a table piece weighing approximately 2 kilo, pound it, wash it out, bind it with twine, place it on fire with approximately 4 liters cold water and bring to a boil
5. Remove the foam that forms cleanly
6. When the roasted mixture with meat and liver has evaporated well and has taken on a brown color, pour 1/4 liter cold water over it and let it evaporate
7. After 10 minutes, add a ladle full from the boiling broth and let it cook 10 to 15 minutes
8. Then add the roasted mixture from the casserolle into the meat pot
9. If you have poultry scraps, add them to the pot as well, and then let it cook covered for 3 hours slowly
10. A quarter hour before using the broth, move the pot aside so it no longer boils
11. Then remove the fat cleanly, strain the broth through fine, clean cloth and use as desired
12. The meat remains covered in a warm place, after which the twine is removed and the meat is served at table as needed
13. If you wish to add spices, add a spice clove, 3 grains pepper and 3 grains allspice with the root vegetables

title: Gewöhnliche Buchteln (Ordinary Buchteln)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 Deka flour
- 3 Deka yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter lukewarm milk
- 8 Deka melted fat
- 1/8 liter milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 heaping tablespoon sugar
- Filling of choice
- Fat for brushing and cooking

## Directions

1. Place 60 Deka flour in a deep bowl, make a well in the middle, pour in 3 Deka yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter lukewarm milk into this well
2. Stir it together somewhat, dust it a little with flour and let it stand covered in a warm place
3. Beat together 8 Deka melted fat, 1/8 liter milk, 2 egg yolks, 1 whole egg, 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1 heaping tablespoon sugar and pour this into the so-called sponge after it has risen
4. Stir well together and beat the dough until it detaches from the spoon
5. Let it stand covered in a warm place
6. When the dough has risen somewhat, use a slotted spoon dipped in hot fat to scoop out pieces of desired size, place them in a fat-brushed casserole and brush them on the sides with melted fat
7. Cover them with a cloth and let them rise somewhat in a warm place
8. Brush them once more with melted fat and bake them for 3/4 to 1 hour in the oven

title: Gewöhnliche Grieß-Suppe (Common Semolina Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 spoonfuls fine semolina
- approximately 2 liters boiling broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Add 16 spoonfuls fine semolina under constant stirring to approximately 2 liters boiling broth
2. Let cook for 1/2 hour, salt, and serve

title: Gewöhnliche Waffeln (Plain Waffles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca finely pounded sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- 16 deca flour
- unsalted bacon

## Directions

1. Put 16 deca finely pounded sugar into a deep bowl, stir 8 egg yolks one after another into it and, after it has been stirred for 1 1/2 hours, fold in the snow from 8 egg whites together with 16 deca flour lightly.
2. Coat a fork with unsalted bacon and brush both parts of the hot waffle iron with it, fill it with 1 spoon of the above batter, close it well and bake the waffle as described for 'Waffles from Batter' (No. 1648).

title: Gewöhnlicher Roastbraten mit Zwiebel (Côte de Boeuf aux Oignons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Roastbraten
- Salt
- Hot fat
- Onions, finely cut
- Broth

## Directions

1. Pound the roastbraten, salt them, and fry them quickly in hot fat.
2. Sprinkle finely cut onions over them and turn the meat as soon as it begins to turn yellow.
3. Once the roastbraten are fried on both sides, remove them and pour some broth over the fat in the pan, let it simmer a little, and pour this sauce over the roastbraten.

title: Gewöhnlicher Rostbraten mit Zwiebel (Ordinary Pot Roast with Onions)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Rostbraten (pot roasts)
- Zwiebel (onions), finely cut
- Heißes Fett (hot fat)
- Suppe (broth)
- Optional: Mehl (flour)

## Directions

1. Stuff the pot roasts and sear them in hot fat.
2. Sprinkle finely cut onions over them.
3. Turn the meat as soon as it begins to turn yellow.
4. Once the pot roasts are browned on both sides, remove them from the pan.
5. Add some broth to the fat in the pan, let it come to a boil briefly, and pour this sauce over the pot roasts.
6. For juicier results, dust the pot roasts with a little flour before adding them to the hot fat.

title: Gewöhnliches Erdbeeren-Gefrorenes (Strawberry Ice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 liter fresh strawberries
- 28 deca fine sugar
- 1/4 liter cold water
- juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Pass 1 1/4 liter fresh strawberries by means of a wooden spoon through a hair sieve into a porcelain bowl
2. Stir 28 deca fine sugar with 1/4 liter cold water, mix this with the juice of 2 lemons into the strawberries
3. Pass the whole again through a fine sieve and stir well together
4. To determine if this mixture contains the necessary sugar content for freezing, use the so-called sugar gauge, by which one can determine exactly how many degrees of sugar the mixture contains
5. Weigh the prepared strawberry juice with this sugar gauge by dipping it like a thermometer into the liquid where the gauge should show 22 degrees
6. If the juice does not reach this degree, add the necessary sugar stirred in some water, while in the opposite case

title: Gewürz-Maccaronen (Spice Macaroons)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet macaroon dough
- A spoonful of finely pounded cinnamon
- 6 to 8 finely pounded cloves
- A spoonful of finely chopped candied orange peel
- A spoonful of finely chopped citron
- Zest of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. To the sweet macaroon dough add a spoonful of finely pounded cinnamon, 6 to 8 finely pounded cloves, a spoonful of finely chopped candied orange peel, a spoonful of finely chopped citron, and the zest of 2 lemons
2. Stir all these ingredients thoroughly with the almond dough
3. Form into oval or round shapes
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gewürz-Maccaronen-Torte (Spice Macaroon Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Wafer sheets
- Spice macaroon dough
- Sugar (about 8 to 12 loths)
- Orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Glue together as many wafer sheets as required for the size of the cake
2. Place the dish intended for serving on the wafer sheet and cut it to size
3. Cover it to a finger's thickness with the aforementioned spice macaroon dough
4. Push it into the oven
5. While the cake bakes, put about 8 to 12 loths of sugar into a pan and cook it with orange blossom water until it shows high bubbles when stirred with a skimming spoon
6. When the cake is baked light brown, pour the sugar mass over it
7. Return it to the oven for a couple of minutes so the glaze dries
8. These cakes are delicious in aroma and taste
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Geſchlagener Rahm (Whipped Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cream as needed
- one spoonful of double-distilled orange blossom water
- finely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Place fresh cream in an earthenware pot
2. Add one spoonful of double-distilled orange blossom water and finely ground sugar
3. Whip it to foam
4. Using a skimming spoon, place the foam on a hair sieve set over a soup pot to catch dripping cream
5. Pour the dripped cream back into the pot
6. Repeat until it becomes entirely foam, stirring together each time fresh cream is added
7. Arrange pyramidally on the dish
8. Set it in a cool place to keep it fresh until serving
9. Garnish the dish with meringues
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Geſchlagener ſaurer Rahm (Whipped Sour Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 sidels of thick sour cream, not bitter-tasting
- 8 loths of finely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Take 3 sidels of thick sour cream that does not taste bitter
2. Add 8 loths of finely ground sugar
3. Whip it into thick foam, taking about a quarter hour
4. Do not whip the thick cream too long as it easily becomes buttery
5. Serve it like the preceding whipped cream
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gigot d'Agneau au Jus (Lamb Leg with Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- anchovy fillets
- parsley, green
- butter
- lamb leg, trimmed and boned
- fat
- mushrooms
- root vegetables
- onion
- whole spices
- red wine
- broth
- glace

## Directions

1. Cut mushrooms, anchovies and green parsley very finely and brown in butter.
2. Place the trimmed and boned lamb leg with the roasted mixture in a casserole containing fat, mushrooms, root vegetables, onion and whole spices, and braise covered until tender.
3. After about 2 hours, add red wine and broth and let the meat braise covered again.
4. When this is done, remove the meat, cut it into desired pieces, arrange on a platter, reduce the strained, defatted juice with dissolved glace and pour over the arranged meat.

title: Gigot d'Agneau Glace (Glazed Lamb Leg)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fat
- onion
- root vegetables
- whole spices
- lamb leg, trimmed and larded
- wine
- broth
- soup sauce

## Directions

1. In a casserole, add fat, onion, root vegetables, whole spices and the trimmings from the lamb leg, place the trimmed and larded leg with the larded side up and let it braise covered.
2. When the forming juice is almost evaporated, add some wine and broth and let it braise covered again.
3. Remove the meat, place it in a pan, pour half of the strained broth over it and roast it in the oven with frequent basting until the fat has taken on a golden color.
4. Glaze the meat with dissolved soup sauce, cut it into desired pieces, reassemble them on a platter and pour jus-sauce at the side.

title: Gingerbread Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dkg grated hazelnuts
- lemon zest
- 3 dkg finely chopped citron
- 2 dkg chopped Araneini
- 1 egg
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Mix the grated hazelnuts, lemon zest, finely chopped citron and chopped Araneini together
2. Stir in 1 egg and sugar to taste

title: Glace or Aspic Extract (Extrait)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 kg beef and the same amount of veal meat
- 2 old hens
- 2 old partridges or 2 old pigeons
- 5 liters boiling water

## Directions

1. Place beef, veal, hens, and partridges or pigeons in a pot with 5 liters boiling water
2. Cover well and simmer slowly for 6 hours like a stockpot
3. Strain through hair sieve into a casserole, remove fat completely
4. Reduce on strong fire to 2 liters, place in earthenware vessel and let stand overnight
5. Remove the fat that has formed
6. Place the liquid in a casserole on open fire, stirring constantly until it runs thick from the spoon
7. Pour into oiled forms, a plate, or casserole and place in an airy cool location
8. In several days, remove the set glace, cut into desired pieces, and place on a wooden platter in an airy location to dry completely
9. Wipe the glace from time to time with a clean cloth; if mold appears, wash with cold water and dry with clean cloth
10. Note: no salt, root vegetables, or spices may be used in this broth, as it would not keep
11. To use: dissolve 2 decagrams glace in ½ liter cold water with salt to taste, bring slowly to boil for good meat soup
12. Can also be used for sauces

title: Glace à l'Ananas en Verres (Pineapple Frozen in Glasses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter Pineapple-Frozen, prepared, firmly frozen and finely worked
- Stiffly beaten snow from three egg whites

## Directions

1. Once you have 1 liter of Pineapple-Frozen prepared, firmly frozen and finely worked, fold the stiffly beaten snow from three egg whites well into it.
2. Fill the mass into glasses and serve it.

title: Glace-finierter Kapaun auf Wildbretsart (Capon Glazed with Game Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 capon, prepared as described
- bacon slices
- finely cut black bread
- 1/4 litre cream
- capers (optional)
- finely cut gherkins (optional)
- pitted olives (optional)

## Directions

1. Place the prepared capon with the sauce in a casserole, lay bacon slices on the capon and braise it with finely cut black bread until tender
2. Remove the capon and place it in a shallow pan, pour 1/4 litre of cream over it and let it brown for approximately 10 minutes
3. Meanwhile, pass the sauce through a sieve, remove the fat, pour the cream from the capon into the sauce, stir it well together and bring it once to a boil
4. If desired, some capers, finely cut gherkins and pitted olives can be stirred into the sauce
5. Cut the capon into pieces, reassemble it and arrange it on a platter, garnish with puff pastry and serve with the sauce

title: Glacierte Erdbeeren (Glazed Strawberries)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- garden strawberries, fresh
- thinly refined sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the strawberries in fresh water and dry them well on a sieve
2. Roll them in very thinly refined sugar
3. Use the sugar instead of egg white because of their great moisture content
4. Proceed as described above
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Glacierte Maronen (Glazed Chestnuts)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1 pound fine refined sugar
- 1/2 measure fresh water
- a little egg white
- a few drops lemon juice
- chestnuts

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound sugar to the crack stage: take 1 pound fine refined sugar and 1/2 measure fresh water, clarify it with a little egg white, and cook it over strong heat in a copper unlined casserole
2. Test the sugar by dipping a smooth stick first in cold water, then in the sugar, and quickly back into the water; bite it between your teeth—if it cracks and does not stick to your teeth, it is ready
3. Add a few drops of lemon juice to the sugar
4. Lightly roast and peel the chestnuts beforehand and stick them on wooden skewers
5. Dip them in the sugar, let them drip off, and turn them slowly until they have cooled somewhat
6. When the sugar begins to cool, warm it again by shaking it frequently
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Glacierter Schinken (Glazed Ham)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large ham
- 5 juniper berries, crushed
- 1 bay leaf
- sprigs of thyme
- water
- fingerthick black bread dough

## Directions

1. Place the ham in a casserole with 5 crushed juniper berries, 1 bay leaf, and some sprigs of thyme
2. Fill with cold water and cook the ham slowly for about 1 hour
3. Remove the ham, dry it with a cloth, and wrap it the next day in fingerthick black bread dough
4. Make several cuts so the dough does not burst during baking
5. Bake for 3 hours in the oven or baking chamber

title: Glacirter Salm (Glazed Salmon)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Bacon
- Butter
- Fifteen small onions
- Truffles
- Mushrooms
- Coulis
- Bouillon
- A glass of white wine
- Juice of a lemon
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the salmon, skin it, and lard it beautifully with bacon, and season it well.
2. In a cassole, sweat in butter fifteen small onions, truffles, and mushrooms over charcoal until golden, let the salmon brown in it, then briefly bring to a boil with some coulis and bouillon.
3. Add a glass of white wine and the juice of a lemon.
4. Do not salt it strongly; when it is nicely dressed and glazed on the plate, pour the sauce over it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Glasiertes Apfelkompott (Glazed Apple Compote)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 Maschanzger apples
- One quarter pound sugar
- Water
- Lemon peel

## Directions

1. Hollow out the cores from 20 Maschanzger apples, then peel them and throw them in fresh water.
2. Make a caramel from one quarter pound sugar, and as soon as it takes on a yellowish color, remove it from the fire.
3. Lay the apples next to one another in the casserole, pour a small glass of water to it, add a lemon peel, and let it cook slowly and well covered until the apples are soft and glazed.
4. Let them cool and arrange them on the compote dish.
5. From what remains in the casserole, cook a syrup with one quarter pound sugar and some water, pass it through a hair sieve, and when it is cold, pour it over the apples.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Glaze for Pariser Stangerl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg whites
- 12-15 dkg sugar

## Directions

1. Stir 2 egg whites with 12-15 dkg sugar until thick and smooth

title: Glaze for Punch Pastries
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 dkg fine sugar
- 4 spoons rum
- 1/2 egg white
- 1-2 spoons hot water
- a few drops Breton

## Directions

1. Stir 20 dkg fine sugar, 4 spoons rum, 1/2 egg white, 1-2 spoons hot water and a few drops of Breton until well combined

title: Glazed Mutton Shoulder (Hammelſchulter, glacirt)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton shoulder
- Bacon
- Melted butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Mushrooms
- Truffles
- Poultry forcemeat
- String
- Sauce-Hash or truffle sauce

## Directions

1. Separate all bones from the shoulder
2. Lard it with fine bacon
3. Turn it repeatedly in melted butter, salt, pepper, finely chopped herbs, mushrooms, and truffles
4. Cover it with fine poultry forcemeat
5. Roll it up and bind it with string, giving it a nice shape
6. Cook it like a fricandeau
7. Serve with a sauce-hash or truffle sauce
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Glazed Mutton Tongues (Glacirte Hammelszungen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton tongues
- Bacon, finely cut
- Ham
- Veal meat
- Bunch of herbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Meat glaze
- Sauce of choice

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the tongues
2. Lard them with finely cut bacon
3. Cook them tender in a braise with ham, veal meat cuts, and a bunch of herbs
4. Season well
5. Arrange the cooked tongues
6. Pass some of the braise into another casserole
7. Add a piece of glaze to it
8. Place the tongues in the casserole
9. Leave the casserole on hot ashes until they glaze themselves beautifully golden
10. When serving, give any desired sauce with them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Glazed Veal Fillets (Filets de veau tracés)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal forcemeat
- Flour
- Fat
- Bouillon glace

## Directions

1. When fillets have been cut out from the forcemeat prepared as described above, they are dipped in flour on both sides and fried in a casserole in finger-thick, very hot fat.
2. They are then arranged on a serving dish and the fillets are covered with dissolved bouillon glace.

title: Glühwein (Hot Spiced Wine)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 whole eggs
- yolks from 4 eggs
- 1 measure Rhenish wine
- 12 loth sugar
- pieces of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Place 4 whole eggs and yolks from 4 eggs in a casserole
2. Gradually stir in 1 measure of Rhenish wine
3. Strain through a hair sieve so that the egg membranes remain behind
4. Add 12 loth sugar and some pieces of cinnamon
5. Place on the fire and beat continuously with a whisk until it rises in a thick foam
6. Serve in cups
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Glühwein (Mulled Wine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter red wine
- 1/4 kilogram sugar
- orange and lemon peel
- cinnamon pieces
- spices

## Directions

1. In 1 liter red wine add 1/4 kilogram sugar, some orange and lemon peel, pieces of cinnamon and some spices
2. Let it become very hot but not boil
3. Strain through a fine sieve
4. This mulled wine is always served warm in small glasses

title: Glühwein (Mulled Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l red wine
- 6 cloves
- 1 piece cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil cloves, cinnamon, and sugar in red wine for 15 minutes
2. Strain through sieve

title: Goldwüchsige Marillen-Marmelade (Golden Apricot Preserves)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe soft apricots
- sugar equal in weight to the apricots without kernels

## Directions

1. Break the ripe soft apricots in half and remove the kernels.
2. Place the apricots with sugar equal to their weight in an earthenware dish, stir well together, and let stand covered overnight.
3. The next day place them in a saucepan over heat and cook while stirring constantly until the preserves reach the correct consistency, being very careful that it does not lose its yellow color and become dark through overcooking.
4. Fill hot into jars as indicated in the Finest Apricot Preserves recipe.

title: Goose Liver Cutlets with Meat Gravy (Escalopes de foie-gras au jus)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- less beautiful goose livers
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parchment paper

## Directions

1. Use less beautiful goose livers for this dish
2. Cut them into oblong strips, half finger thick, 3 fingers wide
3. Butter a flat glazed casserole well, arrange the liver pieces in it at intervals, dust with some salt and pepper, and cover with buttered parchment paper
4. 10 minutes before serving, place the casserole on the fire and move it lightly for several minutes, then turn each liver slice piece by piece, move them over the heat again for the same length of time

title: Goulash
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef from the front quarters of the ox, or tenderloin
- 16 decagrams diced bacon
- 1 large head onion, finely chopped
- 1 teaspoon caraway seeds
- Meat stock
- Salt
- Pepper
- Paprika

## Directions

1. For goulash, choose a well-aged piece of beef from the front quarters of the ox. Tenderloin can also be used if it is not too expensive.
2. After cleaning the meat thoroughly, cut it into pieces of desired size, cube-shaped.
3. Place 16 decagrams of cube-cut bacon in a casserole and let it roast until it looks glassy.
4. Add 1 large head onion, finely chopped, and let it roast a little.
5. Add the meat and 1 teaspoon of caraway seeds, let it cook covered until the resulting liquid has evaporated.
6. Add enough stock so that it covers the meat.
7. Only when the meat is tender, season it with salt and the point of a knife full of paprika (or as desired with paprika alone) and let it boil a little.
8. The sauce should not be too thin when running from the spoon.
9. Before serving, the fat should be removed carefully.

title: Goulash of Another Kind
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Kernel fat or bacon
- Onion
- Root vegetables
- Caraway seeds
- Salt
- Spices
- Paprika
- Vinegar
- Water
- Flour
- Sour cream

## Directions

1. Skin a piece of beef and cut it into pieces of desired size, cube-shaped.
2. Kernel fat or bacon and onion, both finely cut, are roasted somewhat with cube-cut root vegetables.
3. Add the meat, also a little caraway, salt, spices, paprika, then add enough vinegar and water so that the liquid comes over it.
4. Let it braise until the meat is half tender, then stir 4 decagrams of flour with 1/2 liter of sour cream and pour it to the goulash, letting it braise covered under frequent stirring until tender.
5. If 4 decagrams of gingerbread is braised with it, the flour can be omitted.

title: Goulash of Another Kind (Caraway Meat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Cube-cut beef
- Vinegar
- Water
- 1 whole onion
- Caraway seeds
- Paprika
- Salt and spices
- Sour cream
- Flour

## Directions

1. Place the cube-cut meat in a casserole with enough vinegar and water to cover it.
2. Season with 1 whole onion studded with a spice clove, 1/2 teaspoon caraway and paprika.
3. Cook gently until tender, then salt.
4. If desired, mix 8 tablespoons of sour cream with 2 tablespoons of flour, add it and let it boil.
5. Remove the onion before serving.

title: Grafels Sazmarrit
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon or lard
- potatoes
- salt
- pepper (optional)

## Directions

1. Finely cut bacon is roasted golden in butter or lard
2. Softly cooked, leaf-like sliced potatoes seasoned with salt and, if desired, pepper are roasted in it until they have a brown crust
3. Pile them up on a serving dish and serve immediately

title: Graham Bread Torte (Tourte aux Biscuits de Graham)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca powdered sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- 25 deca baked, grated graham bread
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan
- jam of choice
- glaze

## Directions

1. Stir 16 deca powdered sugar and 8 egg yolks together for 1/2 hour
2. Gently fold in the stiffly beaten snow (egg whites) from 8 egg whites together with 25 deca baked, grated graham bread
3. Fill a cake pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
4. Bake for 1 hour in a not too hot oven
5. Divide the baked and completely cooled cake into two layers
6. Spread one layer with jam of choice
7. Top with the second layer
8. Cover the assembled cake with glaze of choice, which is necessary as the cake would otherwise be too dry

title: Grammelspgatscherfln (Cracklings Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- arbitrary quantity of cold cracklings (rendered lard)
- 1 pinch of salt
- paprika (optional)
- 1 whole egg or 1 egg yolk
- wine, rum or schnapps
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Place an arbitrary quantity of cold cracklings on a board and cut them very finely with a knife
2. Add 1 pinch of salt and if desired 1 pinch of paprika
3. Beat in 1 whole egg or 1 egg yolk, wine, rum or schnapps
4. Work it with the necessary flour into a fairly stiff dough and let it rest for 1/2 hour
5. Roll out the dough 1 or 2 fingers thick and cut out with a round cutter of arbitrary size
6. Place these gatscherfln on a baking sheet covered with buttered paper or brushed with butter
7. Cut in several times on top with a knife, brush with beaten egg, sprinkle with coarse salt, and bake at stone-hot temperature

title: Grated Barley with Candied Peel (Geriebene Gerste mit Zierrattenem Sinn)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- flour as needed
- 0.25 liter milk
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons lemon or orange sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- 3 egg whites
- 12 tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Make a firm dough from 1 egg and enough flour as it will hold
2. Grate this dough on a grater
3. Cook the grated barley in 0.25 liter of boiling milk under constant stirring until well cooked
4. Add 8 tablespoons butter and 8 tablespoons lemon or orange sugar
5. Stir well together and let it cook covered until thickly done
6. Set aside and when it has cooled slightly, stir in 4 egg yolks
7. Lightly fold in the snow from 4 egg whites
8. Pour this mixture into a buttered dish and bake for 0.5 hour in the oven
9. After this time, cover the top crust with egg white snow mixed with 12 tablespoons sugar
10. Dust this with finely ground sugar and bake in an open oven for about 10 minutes
11. Remove the dish and serve on a second serving dish

title: Grazer Zwieback (Graz Rusks)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 stiff egg whites
- 13 dkg vanilla sugar
- 11 dkg flour
- 5 dkg melted, hot butter

## Directions

1. Stir 13 dkg vanilla sugar and 11 dkg flour with a wooden spoon into 5 stiff egg whites.
2. Mix in 5 dkg melted, hot butter.
3. Fill the mass into the buttered, floured biscuit form and bake slowly.
4. The next day, cut the rusks into thin slices and place on a baking rack or sheet in the open oven to dry.
5. The slices must be dried slowly and become beautifully white and fine, feeling hard on both sides.
6. Immediately roll them on a flat plate in very finely sieved vanilla sugar.
7. If well dried, they keep for a very long time.
8. The mass in the form should only be two fingers high, otherwise it will not bake through and is difficult to dry properly.

title: Green Butter (Beurre Vert)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams butter
- 1 tablespoon spinach thick juice
- 1 knife point white pepper
- some salt

## Directions

1. Place the butter in a mortar with the spinach thick juice, white pepper, and salt
2. Pound all together and pass through a fine sieve
3. With a wooden spoon, take the strained mixture from the sieve in long strips, place it on a plate, and let it cool

title: Green Mayonnaise with Biscuit (Sauce Verte au Biscuit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 handful spinach
- 10 sprigs green parsley and chervil
- 1 teaspoon finely cut tarragon
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 12 teaspoons biscuit soaked in water and well squeezed
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste
- vinegar to taste
- 4 tablespoons oil

## Directions

1. Cook spinach with 10 sprigs of green parsley and chervil in salted water, place in cold water, drain the water, press the cooked mixture strongly and finely pound it together with 1 teaspoon finely cut tarragon, 6 hard-boiled egg yolks, and 12 teaspoons of biscuit soaked in water and well squeezed in a mortar
2. Pass through a sieve and only before serving mix with a little salt, the necessary pepper, some vinegar, and 4 tablespoons of oil to make a sauce
3. The mixing of vinegar and salt happens only before serving, as otherwise the green color would be lost

title: Green Peas (Grüne Erbsen)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- cold salt water
- 5 dkg butter
- parsley
- pea water
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Shell the peas and place them in a pot with cold salted water.
2. Cook for 20-30 minutes.
3. Make a parsley roux with 5 dkg butter and pour in pea water.
4. Add the peas, season with salt and sugar.
5. Cook together for 20 minutes.
6. For very young peas: place them in a casserole with 5 dkg butter, pour in a little water, season with salt and sugar, and braise for 20 minutes, dust slightly, and cook a few more minutes.

title: Green Peas Steamed in Bottle (Grüne Erbsen in Dunst gekocht)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh green peas
- salted water
- cold water

## Directions

1. Place fresh green peas in boiling salted water, cook until half-tender, and place in cold water.
2. Fill Champagne bottles or other suitable steam jars halfway with the peas.
3. Pour very lightly salted, steamed, and cooled water over the peas, leaving three fingers' breadth of space at the top.
4. Stopper well and seal with tinfoil and corks.
5. Steam for 1 hour, then store in a cool, airy place (see Preserves Recipe No. 1842).
6. Note: the water poured over the peas in the bottle should be only lightly salted.
7. When using the peas, drain the water, warm them in salted water, and prepare as desired.
8. Small green string beans and asparagus can be preserved the same way, but not in Champagne bottles.

title: Green String Beans in Salted Water (Grüne Pifolen in Salzwasser eingelegt)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Green string beans
- grape leaves
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the strings from both sides of each bean.
2. Cover the bottom of a small barrel or earthenware dish with grape leaves.
3. Add a two-finger-high layer of beans, sprinkle with salt, and repeat until the vessel is full.
4. Cover with a lid and weight it down with a stone or similar.
5. When the beans stored in a cool place are to be used, first wash them, soak until tender, and use for salad or vegetable dishes.

title: Green String Beans in Vinegar (Pifolen in Essig - Grüne Schoten)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Green string beans
- salted water
- cold water
- vinegar (prepared as cucumber vinegar)
- oil

## Directions

1. String the green beans and either cut into noodle-like strips, break into pieces, or leave whole if small.
2. Cook them in boiling salted water until half-tender, then place in cold water and drain.
3. Fill jars with the beans and pour over them cooled, boiled vinegar prepared as for pickled cucumbers.
4. Pour a finger's-breadth of fine oil over the vinegar.
5. Seal the jars, which must not be completely full, with glass covers and corks.
6. Store in a cool, airy place.

title: Gries Schmarn mit Eier (Semolina Scramble with Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter Gries (semolina)
- 1/2 liter Milch (milk)
- 4 Eidotter (egg yolks)
- 1/2 teaspoon salt (finely ground)
- 1/4 liter cream
- 24 deka butter or lard
- 1/8 liter boiling milk (for finishing)
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pour 1/2 liter Gries into a deep bowl
2. While stirring constantly, add 1/2 liter milk
3. Mix in egg yolks gradually, combined with finely ground salt
4. Mix in 1/4 liter cream thoroughly
5. Cover and let stand for 2 hours until the semolina has swollen well
6. Heat 24 deka of butter or lard in a pan until hot
7. Add the Gries mixture and let it steam in the oven for 1 hour
8. A few minutes before serving, pour 1/8 liter of boiling milk over it
9. Stir well and let it steam a little more
10. Arrange the Schmarn in a bowl and serve at table sprinkled with sugar

title: Gries Schmarn ohne Eier (Semolina Scramble without Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 1 liter Gries (semolina)
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 24 deka lard

## Directions

1. Stir 1 liter milk slowly into 1 liter Gries
2. Add 1/2 teaspoon salt
3. Cover and let stand for 2 hours
4. Heat 24 deka lard in a casserole until hot
5. Pour the Gries mixture into it
6. Treat further as described for the Gries Schmarn mit Eier

title: Gries-Pudding (Consommé au pain de semoule)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 20 deca semolina
- salt
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 16 deca butter until fluffy
2. Stir in 5 egg yolks, 20 deca semolina, and some salt
3. Lightly fold in the snow from 5 egg whites
4. Fill into a mold greased with butter and lined with flour
5. Cook in steam for 3/4 hour
6. Turn the pudding out onto a platter
7. Let cool, cut into desired pieces
8. Pour boiling soup over and let stand covered for 10 minutes

title: Griesknoedel (Semolina Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure cream
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/2 pound semolina
- 12 egg yolks
- salt
- 4 loth sugar
- grated lemon zest
- milk for cooking
- brown butter for serving
- prune compote for serving

## Directions

1. Bring the cream and butter to a boil
2. Add the semolina and stir over the fire until it pulls away from the pan dryly
3. Let cool sufficiently
4. Gradually stir in 12 egg yolks with a little salt, 4 loth sugar, and grated lemon zest
5. Using a spoon, form dumplings on a flour-dusted board
6. Cook the dumplings in simmering milk
7. Serve with brown butter and prune compote
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Griesknödel (Semolina Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure cream
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/2 pound semolina
- 12 egg yolks
- salt

## Directions

1. Make 1 measure of cream with 1/4 pound butter boiling
2. Then add 1/2 pound semolina and stir on the fire until it detaches from the pan
3. When it has cooled sufficiently, gradually work in 12 egg yolks with a little salt
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Griess-Nockerl in der Schüssel (Semolina Dumplings in the Bowl)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 tablespoons butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- 1/2 liter semolina
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1.5 liters boiling milk
- sugar for sprinkling
- cinnamon for sprinkling (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat 12 tablespoons butter until fluffy
2. Gradually beat in 3 egg yolks and 3 whole eggs one after another
3. Mix in 1/2 liter semolina and 1/2 teaspoon salt thoroughly
4. Let this mixture rest for 1 hour so the semolina can swell
5. Using a spoon, place dumplings of desired size into 1.5 liters of boiling milk
6. Let them come to a boil covered
7. Place in the oven to give them a golden color
8. Arrange on a platter, sprinkle with sugar and optionally with cinnamon, and serve

title: Griesschöberl (Semolina Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter boiling milk
- 25 Deca semolina
- Salt
- 12 Deca butter
- 8 egg yolks
- Snow from 8 eggs
- Flour

## Directions

1. Stir 25 Deca semolina into 1 liter boiling milk, let the semolina cook completely, add some salt and pour into a deep bowl
2. Beat 12 Deca butter until fluffy, beat in 8 egg yolks
3. Add the prepared semolina, stir everything well together
4. Lightly mix in the snow from 8 eggs
5. Pour into a shallow casserole dish greased with butter and dusted with flour and let it bake for 3-4 hours
6. Cut the schöberl into pieces of any size or cut out with a cutter
7. A few minutes before serving, add the cut pieces with some soup to the soup pot so the schöberl can warm up, then add the necessary soup and serve

title: Grieß-Garkeln (Semolina Dumplings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 spoonfuls semolina
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Combine 15 spoonfuls semolina and 2 eggs together with hands until crumbly
2. Spread the semolina on a board and let dry completely
3. Stir into 2 liters boiling broth

title: Grieß-Nockerl (Noques à la semoule)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deka butter
- 4 eggs
- 25 Deka semolina total, including 4 tablespoons already stirred in
- 2 tablespoons milk

## Directions

1. Beat 12 Deka butter until creamy
2. Gradually add 4 eggs, adding 1 tablespoon semolina after each egg
3. Mix in 2 tablespoons milk
4. Let the dough rest covered for 2 hours
5. Proceed as with the flour Nockerl

title: Grieß-Soufflé (Semolina Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter semolina
- 1 liter boiling milk
- 14 Deca butter
- 1 pinch of salt
- 14 Deca lemon sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites (snow)
- chocolate (optional)

## Directions

1. Slowly dissolve 1/4 liter semolina in 1 liter boiling milk and cook until thick, then add 14 Deca butter and 1 pinch of salt, stir well together and let cool
2. Meanwhile stir 14 Deca lemon sugar with 10 egg yolks for 1/2 hour, add the cooled semolina mass, stir together and finally fold in lightly the snow from 10 egg whites
3. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour with this mass and cook covered for 3/4 hours in steam or bake in the oven
4. Sprinkle the soufflé with sugar and serve with dissolved jam or any cream
5. If desired, 2 tablets of grated chocolate can be stirred into one half of the semolina mass before filling the form, then pour it into the other half in the form, but wafer sheets must be laid between

title: Grießauflauf (Semolina Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- 20 dkg semolina
- 10 dkg butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 spoonfuls sugar
- finely chopped lemon zest
- 4 egg whites
- butter and sugar for the mold

## Directions

1. Bring milk to a boil in a pot
2. Slowly stir in 20 dkg semolina, stirring constantly until thick, and cool
3. Mix with creamed butter, egg yolks, sugar, and lemon zest
4. Lightly fold in stiffly beaten egg whites
5. Fill into a well-buttered and sugared soufflé dish
6. Bake light brown over moderate heat
7. Sprinkle with sugar and serve in the dish

title: Grießknödel (Semolina Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 dkg semolina
- salt
- 5 dkg hot fat
- 1/2 l boiling water
- fat for roasting breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Salt the semolina and pour hot fat over it in a bowl.
2. Stir it, then pour boiling water over it.
3. Let it stand covered for 1 hour.
4. Form dumplings and place them in a pot of boiling salted water.
5. Let them cook slowly for 5-8 minutes.
6. Lift onto a round platter, pour over with breadcrumbs roasted in fat and serve as the Grießſtrudel.

title: Grießkroquettes (Semolina Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 litre milk
- 16 dkg coarse semolina
- hot lard
- grated chocolate or sugar

## Directions

1. Cook 16 dkg coarse semolina spoonwise into 1/4 litre boiling milk until thick
2. Let cool
3. Form equal-sized pieces into croquettes
4. Bread them
5. Fry in hot lard
6. Drain
7. Roll in grated chocolate or sugar and serve warm

title: Grießschmarren (Semolina Schmarren)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ l Milk
- 35 dkg Semolina
- 1 Egg
- 5 dkg Raisins
- Salt
- 6 dkg Butter or fat
- Sugar
- Compote or plum roast

## Directions

1. Let semolina soak in milk for 1 hour in a pot
2. Mix in egg, raisins, and salt
3. Heat butter or fat in a flat pan
4. Pour in mixture and place in oven for 45 minutes
5. Pierce with a wooden spoon and let cook, stirring frequently
6. Should be grainy yet soft
7. Arrange on a round platter, sprinkle with sugar, and serve with compote or plum roast
8. Alternative method: bring milk to boil, add semolina by the spoonful, add salt, sugar, and butter or fat, pour into flat pan and proceed as above

title: Grießtorte (Semolina Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 20 dkg sugar
- lemon zest
- 15 dkg raw grated almonds
- 6 stiff egg whites
- 10 dkg finest semolina

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks and sugar
2. Add lemon zest and raw grated almonds
3. Fold in stiff egg whites together with finest semolina
4. Fill into cake form
5. Bake slowly
6. Cut cake in half, coat with jam, reassemble
7. Cover with rum or lemon ice or serve with warm wine

title: Grießzweckerl (Semolina Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dkg flour
- 3 eggs
- salt
- water
- 10 dkg fat
- 18 dkg semolina
- ½ 1 milk

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough and cut into larger fleckerln
2. Cook in boiling salted water for 5 minutes, drain and shock
3. In a casserole, let the semolina turn light brown in hot fat, pour in with the milk
4. Add the fleckerln, salt them and warm in the oven while stirring frequently
5. Serve as a sweet course or as a side dish

title: Grießſtrudel (Semolina Strudel)
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough made from: 20 dkg flour, 1 egg, salt, 1 dl lukewarm water
- filling made from: 9 dkg fat, 3 egg yolks, salt, more than 1/2 l cream, 11 dkg semolina, 3 egg whites
- flour
- fat for roasting
- Zwetſchkenröſter (plum sauce)

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough from flour, egg, salt and water (see Teige section).
2. Let it rest, then stretch thin.
3. Cut off the edges and brush the dough with egg white.
4. Prepare the filling: beat egg whites, then beat in fat, egg yolks, salt, cream and semolina.
5. Distribute the filling on the dough and roll into a strudel on the table.
6. Using a floured wooden spoon handle, press through to mark equal-sized pieces and cut with a knife.
7. Place on a floured board.
8. Cook the pieces in boiling salted water for 5-8 minutes in a large pot.
9. Lift onto a round platter, pour over with breadcrumbs roasted in fat and serve with Zwetſchkenröſter as a sweet dish or without it as a side to Beuſchel.

title: Griftel zum Garnieren (Potato Croquettes for Garnish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- butter
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Peel raw potatoes and cut them egg-shaped or into strips
2. Cook them covered in salted water
3. Drain them, place a piece of butter on top and let it melt
4. Sprinkle with parsley and mix with the potatoes
5. Serve as garnish for beef, venison or fish

title: Grilled Lamb Shanks (Grillirte Schenkeln)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb shanks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sauce Robert

## Directions

1. Score the shanks
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. Grill them on the grate with considerable heat
4. Serve with Sauce Robert
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Grilled Ox Palate with Skewers (Grillirte Ochſengaumen mit Spießchen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- ox palate
- calf's udder
- braising liquid
- beef lard
- truffles
- mushrooms
- parsley
- onions
- flour
- egg yolks
- lemon juice
- white bread, grated
- butter
- clear broth or Sauce Espagnole or clear piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the ox palate and remove the skin
2. Cook it soft with calf's udder in a braise
3. Cool until it can be removed and cut into large, even cubes
4. Put a quarter pound beef lard in a casserole
5. Sauté thinly sliced truffles and mushrooms with finely chopped parsley and onions
6. Dust with proportional flour
7. Fill with the braising liquid
8. Cook briefly and bind with 6 or 8 egg yolks so the sauce becomes quite thick
9. Set the diced palate pieces into it
10. Draw off with lemon juice and set it cold again
11. On a silver skewer thread the palate pieces, alternating with truffle and udder and mushrooms
12. Coat evenly with sauce
13. Roll in fine, white grated bread and continue until everything is well covered with bread
14. Melt a good piece of butter and stir it with some egg yolk
15. Dip each skewer in it and coat thickly with grated bread
16. Fry on three sides on a grate, and give the fourth side color with a glowing shovel
17. Serve with clear, strong broth or Sauce Espagnole or clear piquant sauce
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Grillierte Tauben (Broiled Pigeons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons, well cleaned
- Butter or oil
- Egg
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the pigeons lengthwise down the middle.
2. Loosen the bones and beat the meat with the flat side of a meat cleaver.
3. Salt and pepper the meat, dip in melted butter, and coat with bread crumbs.
4. Place buttered or oiled writing paper on a grill, put the pigeons on it, and broil 15-20 minutes over moderate heat on both sides until golden.
5. Alternatively, place them in a shallow pan with some fat and cook quickly over heat.

title: Grive en Salmis (Thrush in Salmis Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Thrush
- Shallots

## Directions

1. Prepare like the Salmis dish described at recipe 749.

title: Grog (Patriotic Grog)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter boiling water
- 1/4 liter rum
- 28 deciliters sugar

## Directions

1. In 1 liter boiling water, add 1/4 liter rum and 28 deciliters sugar
2. Can be served warm or cold

title: Gros-Zittchen (Large Zittchen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deka rum sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 32 deka almonds with skin, finely chopped and sifted
- 8 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 8 deka bread crumbs
- 6 deka flour
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put 32 deka rum sugar and 8 egg yolks in a deep bowl
2. Beat together for 1/2 hour
3. Mix in 32 deka almonds with skin, finely chopped and sifted
4. Lightly fold in stiffly beaten egg whites from 8 eggs simultaneously with 8 deka bread crumbs, 6 deka flour, 1 tablespoon cinnamon, and nutmeg
5. Fill a butter-greased and flour-dusted aniseed bread mold with this mass
6. Bake slowly in the oven at low heat
7. When done, remove and let cool completely
8. Cut into thick knife-blade-wide slices
9. Cover with desired glaze and let dry

title: Grunnkresse's Salat (Watercress Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Grunnkresse (watercress)
- Salz
- Essig
