## Directions

1. Die Grunnkresse wird abgerupft, gewaschen, in ein Tuch gegeben und das Wasser abgeschüttelt; weil diese Pflanze sehr zart ist, wird erst kurz vor dem Servieren das nötige Salz und Essig dazu gegeben. Öl darf zu diesem Salat nicht genommen werden.

title: Grösel Torte ohne Eier (Brioche Cake without Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 deca beaten butter
- 16 deca cinnamon sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 40 deca almonds, peeled and finely pounded
- juice of a second lemon
- finely cut lemon peel
- 30 deca flour
- egg white, beaten
- preserved fruit filling of choice
- vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 30 deca beaten butter with 16 deca cinnamon sugar and the juice of 1 lemon.
2. Gradually mix in 40 deca peeled and finely pounded almonds (mixed with the juice of a second lemon), some finely cut lemon peel, and 30 deca flour.
3. Roll out the dough finger-thick on a flour-dusted board.
4. Cut two round discs and place them in a buttered cake form dusted with flour.
5. Fill between the two discs with preserved fruit filling.
6. Brush the upper disc with beaten egg white and lay strips of dough (rolled into little fingers' thickness) across it in a lattice pattern.
7. Brush the lattice also with beaten egg white and bake at low heat for approximately 1 hour.
8. After this time, place the cake on a sieve and after it has cooled completely, fill the four corners of the lattice with preserved filling of choice.
9. Sprinkle with vanilla sugar and serve.
10. This cake is hard and can easily crack when cutting.

title: Grünbund für Suppen (Vegetable Dumplings for Soups)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deka butter, beaten
- 6 whole eggs, added one by one
- 26 Deka finely chopped, roasted or boiled meat leftovers
- some lard
- 1 knife point of white pepper
- 1 teaspoon of fresh parsley
- 14 Deka breadcrumbs
- 1/2 liter milk
- butter for greasing the cloth

## Directions

1. Beat 12 Deka butter until creamy
2. Add 6 eggs one by one
3. Add the finely chopped meat leftovers with some lard, white pepper, and parsley
4. Moisten 14 Deka breadcrumbs with 1/2 liter milk and mix well into the preceding mixture
5. Butter a cloth in the middle, place the mixture on it, bind tightly with twine
6. Place in boiling salted water and cook covered for 3/4 to 1 hour
7. Remove the cloth bundle, place on a platter and serve with the soup, or cut into pieces and add to the soup
8. Garnish with small baked potato dumplings and roasted small bread pieces if serving whole, or sprinkle with finely chopped chervil if desired

title: Grünbund-Nockerl (Queulies au pain blanc)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Deka butter, beaten
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- some salt
- 20 Deka white breadcrumbs moistened with 4 tablespoons milk

## Directions

1. Beat 10 Deka butter until creamy
2. Stir in 2 egg yolks, 1 whole egg and some salt
3. Mix in the white breadcrumbs moistened with milk
4. Form and cook as with the other Nockerl recipes

title: Grüne Bohnen mit Schöpsenfleisch (Green Beans with Mutton)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Young beans
- Butter
- Cream
- Egg yolks
- Cooked mutton meat
- Fine-cut shallots
- Pepper
- Salt
- Meat broth
- Nutmeg
- Ginger
- Bread crumbs
- Vinegar
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut young beans after removing stems and any fibers into small diagonal pieces and wash
2. Place butter in a casserole, let the beans sweat for a while, then pour cream over them and let them cook soft
3. Combine the cream with a pair of egg yolks from the beans and pour back over them, but do not boil further
4. At serving, sprinkle a little butter over them and top with cooked mutton meat
5. Alternatively: boil the cut beans in salt water, braise them in butter when drained, add finely cut shallots, pepper, salt, and pile in the center of the platter, then garnish with mutton cutlets, feet, sausages, or only with bread fried in butter
6. Or: cook the cut and rinsed beans in sufficiently salted meat broth, place in a casserole, pour some of the broth over them, add a little butter, nutmeg and ginger, and let cook; then add roux or bread crumbs and let the broth become thick, and garnish as desired
7. Or: without thickening, beat three egg yolks in a pot, add some vinegar, whisk thoroughly, add a little butter, pour the bean broth underneath, and whisk constantly so it does not curdle; finally pour the broth over the beans and let it soak through without boiling further
8. When serving, sprinkle with melted butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Grüne Erbsen (Pois verts)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter fine green peas
- salt water
- 12 ounces butter
- flour as needed
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook 1 liter of fine green peas in boiling salt water until tender
2. Meanwhile, prepare a butter sauce with 12 ounces of butter, flour as needed, 1 teaspoon of sugar, and salt, cooking well
3. Add the drained peas to the butter sauce
4. Cover and keep warm until ready to serve

title: Grüne Erbsen auf französische Art (Petits Pois à la Française)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter fine green peas
- salt water
- 5 ounces butter
- 2 ounces sugar
- salt
- toasted bread cubes (Croutons)

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/2 liter of fine green peas in salt water for 5 minutes uncovered
2. Drain the water thoroughly
3. Place the peas with 5 ounces of butter and 2 ounces of sugar in a casserole dish
4. Cover and braise until the butter has dissolved
5. Stir and adjust seasoning with sugar and salt as needed
6. Heap on a platter and garnish with toasted bread cubes

title: Grüne Erbsensuppe (Green Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter green or yellow peas
- 1 pair sausages
- salt
- baking soda
- 5 dkg fat
- onion

## Directions

1. Sort and wash the peas
2. Place to cook with twice the height in cold water and a little baking soda for 1¼ hours, stirring occasionally
3. Drain the peas
4. Add to a light onion roux made from 5 dkg fat
5. Pour the pea water over it
6. Cook well together and press through a sieve
7. Return to the casserole and season with salt
8. A few minutes before serving, add the washed sausages, cut into slices, and let them cook together
9. Serve with bread slices

title: Grüne Farce (Green Forcemeat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Several handfuls of young spinach, washed
- Water

## Directions

1. Wash the spinach and pound finely in a mortar
2. Press out the juice through a cloth into a saucepan
3. Place the expressed matter in boiling water until it coagulates like curds
4. Press through a hair sieve and let the water drain off completely
5. Mix this forcemeat into poultry forcemeats
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Grüne Fisolen (Green Beans)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Young green beans, trimmed and cut lengthwise
- Simmering water
- Fat
- Green parsley, finely cut
- Garlic, finely cut
- Good broth
- Milk cream
- Fried veal cutlets

## Directions

1. Blanch young green beans, trimmed and cut lengthwise, in simmering water and let stand for a while
2. Heat 2 spoonfuls of fat
3. Add a good amount of green parsley and a little finely cut garlic
4. Add the beans after they have been well drained
5. Braise them with frequent stirring
6. Pour good broth with 5 to 6 spoonfuls of milk cream over them
7. Garnish finally with fried veal cutlets
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Grüne Fisolen mit Einbrenn (Green Beans with Roux)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg green beans
- salt
- 5 dkg fat
- bean water or broth
- pepper
- vinegar
- sugar (optional)
- cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the beans by cutting off the ends and removing the strings
2. Wash the beans and cut into noodle-like pieces or into 3-4 pieces
3. Place in boiling salted water and cook uncovered for 45 minutes
4. Make a roux from 5 dkg fat and mix with bean water or broth
5. Add the cooked beans to the roux
6. Season with salt, pepper, and vinegar; sweeten if desired
7. Simmer for 20 minutes
8. Cream can be added if desired

title: Grüne Fißolen (Haricots verts)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- green kidney beans
- salt water
- green parsley
- butter sauce
- salt
- white pepper
- lemon juice (optional)
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Break off both ends of the green kidney beans and remove the strings from the sides
2. Cut lengthwise or diagonally as desired, or break into pieces
3. Cook in salt water and drain well
4. Place in cold water and let stand
5. In a butter sauce, roast some finely chopped green parsley
6. Add the cooked green kidney beans
7. Season with salt and white pepper and bring to a boil
8. Can also be soured with lemon juice and sweetened with 1 spoonful of sugar if desired

title: Grüne Fißolen auf englische Art (Haricots verts à l'anglaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh, fine green kidney beans
- salt water
- fresh butter
- green parsley
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the fresh, fine green kidney beans as described, cook tender in salt water
2. Drain well and arrange raised on a platter
3. A piece of fresh butter mixed with finely chopped green parsley and some salt is placed on the hot kidney beans in pieces and served

title: Grüne oder Graseyer (Green Eggs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- parsley
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- peas broth
- seasoning

## Directions

1. Hard boil as many eggs as needed
2. Cut them in half
3. Remove the yolks to a bowl and mash well with a spoon
4. Chop parsley very fine
5. Toast it with a little breadcrumbs in butter
6. Mix into the egg yolks
7. Beat in a few fresh eggs as needed to make a thick filling
8. Stir everything together well
9. Fill the egg white halves with the mixture, heaping it up like a small hill
10. Bake in a dish brushed with butter and dotted with small pieces of butter
11. Prepare a green sauce by pounding parsley in a mortar
12. Mix with some peas broth
13. Add butter and seasoning
14. Let the sauce simmer only briefly so it does not lose its color
15. Pour the green sauce over the baked eggs
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Grüne Sauce (Green Sauce: Sauce ravigote verte)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons finely cut sorrel
- equal amount green parsley
- tarragon
- sour sorrel
- 6 tablespoons butter
- 6 tablespoons flour
- 1/2 liter broth
- some cream

## Directions

1. Pound finely the sorrel, green parsley, tarragon, and sour sorrel with 6 tablespoons butter in a mortar
2. Add 6 tablespoons flour and mix well
3. Put this in a saucepan and let it boil somewhat
4. Pour in 1/2 liter broth and some cream
5. Let it boil again

title: Grüne Suppe von Erbsen (Green Pea Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh green peas that are beginning to thicken
- water
- 2 stalks of leek
- onions
- 1/2 pound butter or beef fat
- flour
- 1 pound lean ham cut into cubes
- beef broth
- handful of fresh spinach
- white bread cut into small cubes
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Put an appropriate amount of fresh green peas into a casserole, cover with water, add 2 stalks of leek and some onions, and cook until done.
2. When all the water has cooked down, pound the peas in a mortar.
3. Melt 1/2 pound butter or beef fat, stir in one spoon of flour until it foams.
4. Add 1 pound of lean ham cut into cubes, stir the pounded peas through it, and add enough broth so the peas become properly thick.
5. Let this cook together for half an hour.
6. Pound a handful of fresh washed spinach finely in a mortar.
7. When the soup is properly thick and cooked down, strain it through a hair cloth along with the pounded spinach, then pass it through a sieve into a suitable pot.
8. Add necessary salt and keep it hot until serving.
9. Fry white bread cut into small cubes in butter until brown and hard, remove with a skimming spoon, and lay on paper to drain the fat.
10. Serve the soup over the fried bread cubes.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Grünes Eis (Green Icing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared white icing
- spinach leaves
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Mix a little spinach leaves into the prepared white icing, having first finely rubbed them with powdered sugar
2. This gives the icing a beautiful color, though the taste is not particularly pleasant, so it is used only for decoration

title: Grünkresse-Sauce (Watercress Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter
- approximately 3 tablespoons breadcrumbs
- 1 teaspoon flour
- 1/4 liter broth
- some vinegar
- finely cut watercress
- 1/4 liter wine
- salt

## Directions

1. Put butter in a saucepan with approximately 3 tablespoons breadcrumbs
2. When these are brown, add 1 teaspoon flour and let this also become brown
3. Pour in with 1/4 liter broth and some vinegar
4. Add finely cut watercress, 1/4 liter wine, and salt
5. Let it boil thoroughly

title: Gugelhupf (Babka-style Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 deca flour
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 2 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/16 liter warm cream
- 1 egg and 3 egg yolks beaten in 1/16 liter cream
- 4 deca melted butter
- flour for dusting
- butter for greasing the pan
- butter in small pea-sized pieces
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Place 40 deca flour, 1 teaspoon sugar and 1 tablespoon salt in a deep bowl
2. Dissolve 2 deca fresh yeast in 1/16 liter warm cream
3. Beat 1 egg and 3 egg yolks in 1/16 liter cream and add to the yeast mixture
4. Pour this into the flour mixture and stir together into a dough
5. Add 4 deca melted butter, stir well and beat the dough until it separates from the spoon
6. Cover with a cloth and let it rest in a warm place
7. When the dough has risen, place it on a flour-dusted board and roll out to the thickness of a small finger
8. Cut out round flat pieces using a doughnut cutter and place on a buttered baking sheet
9. Make 3 indentations in each piece with a spoon handle
10. Place pea-sized pieces of butter in each indentation and sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese
11. Bake in a fairly hot oven until golden and serve warm

title: Gugelhupf II (Gugelhupf with Almonds)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca butter
- 3 tablespoons finely ground sugar
- 1 pinch of salt
- 12 egg yolks
- 2 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 3 tablespoons milk
- egg white from 6 eggs
- 28 deca flour
- slivered almonds
- butter for greasing
- flour for lining the mold
- powdered sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Beat 24 deca butter until fluffy
2. Add 3 tablespoons finely ground sugar, 1 pinch of salt and 12 egg yolks one after another
3. Mix in 2 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 3 tablespoons milk
4. Lightly fold in egg white from 6 eggs simultaneously with 28 deca flour
5. Warm a gugelhupf mold, coat with butter, line with flour and arrange sliced almonds around the sides
6. Fill with dough, cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in height
7. Place in a fairly hot oven and bake slowly for 3/4 to 1 hour
8. Turn out onto a sieve and sprinkle with powdered sugar after completely cooling

title: Gugelhupf im Mittelaltertter (Medieval-style Gugelhupf)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 deca flour
- 28 deca butter
- 1 tablespoon vanilla sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 6 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
- finely cut lemon peel as desired
- raisins
- currants
- butter for greasing
- flour for lining the mold

## Directions

1. Place 50 deca flour and 28 deca butter cut into small pieces on a board and rub together finely
2. Place this in a deep bowl with 1 tablespoon vanilla sugar, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 6 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, 3 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk and finely cut lemon peel as desired
3. Mix well together and beat the dough until it separates from the spoon
4. Mix in raisins and currants
5. Proceed as described for the Ordinary Gugelhupf

title: Gugelhupf im Ordinären (Ordinary Gugelhupf)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca lard or drippings or 10 deca butter
- 1/8 liter milk
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 1/2 teaspoons salt
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 3 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 3/4 liter lukewarm milk
- 1 liter flour
- 8 deca raisins
- 8 deca currants
- butter for greasing
- flour or breadcrumbs for lining the mold

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deca lard or drippings or 10 deca butter with 1/8 liter milk until fluffy
2. Add 1 tablespoon sugar, 1 1/2 teaspoons salt, 3 egg yolks and 1 whole egg one after another, stirring
3. Mix in 3 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 3/4 liter lukewarm milk and 1 liter flour
4. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon
5. Mix in 8 deca raisins and 8 deca currants
6. Warm a gugelhupf mold with a hole, coat heavily with butter and line with flour or breadcrumbs
7. Fill the mold with dough, distribute evenly, cover and place in a warm location
8. When the dough has risen to double its height, place the mold in the oven and bake initially at strong heat, later at moderate heat for 1 hour
9. Remove the mold from the oven, turn out onto a sieve and sprinkle with powdered sugar after cooling completely
10. To check if it is baked through, insert a pastry needle; if it comes out dry without dough, it is done

title: Gugelhupf im Ölbittertetter (Gugelhupf with Oil and Bitter Flavor)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca butter
- 10 egg yolks
- 4 deca finely ground sugar
- 5 grams salt
- 2 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter cold milk
- 56 deca flour
- egg white from 2 eggs
- 14 deca raisins
- butter for greasing the mold
- flour for lining the mold

## Directions

1. Beat 28 deca butter until fluffy
2. Add 10 egg yolks one after another
3. Add 4 deca finely ground sugar and 5 grams salt
4. 10 minutes before use, dissolve 2 deca fresh yeast in 1/4 liter cold milk and stir at a warm location until the yeast is completely dissolved and lukewarm
5. Add this yeast mixture simultaneously with 56 deca flour to the butter mixture and beat the dough until it separates from the spoon
6. If the dough is too stiff, add 3 tablespoons milk
7. Lightly fold in egg white from 2 eggs and 14 deca raisins
8. Coat a gugelhupf mold with butter, line with flour and fill with dough
9. Place 2 tablespoons on the mold and cover with a cloth to prevent air from entering
10. Let the dough rise near the hearth until it has doubled in height
11. Place in a moderately hot oven and bake for 1 hour
12. Turn out onto a sieve and sprinkle with sugar when cold

title: Gugelhupf mit Weinstein und Natron (Gugelhupf with Cream of Tartar and Baking Soda)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter
- 10 deca sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 1/2 liters cold milk
- 28 deca flour
- 13 grams cream of tartar
- 5 grams baking soda
- egg white from 4 eggs
- butter for greasing
- flour for lining the mold

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deca butter until fluffy
2. Add 10 deca sugar and 4 egg yolks one after another
3. Mix with 1 1/2 liters cold milk and 28 deca flour
4. Mix in 13 grams cream of tartar and 5 grams baking soda
5. Lightly fold in stiffly beaten egg white from 4 eggs
6. Fill a butter-coated, flour-lined mold and bake at moderate heat in the oven for 1 hour

title: Gulyás de Mouton aux Pommes de Terre (Hungarian Mutton Goulash with Potatoes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton shoulder
- fat or bacon
- onions, finely cut
- rocambole
- cumin
- salt
- seasonings
- paprika
- broth
- potatoes, raw and diced
- flour
- sour cream
- parsley sauce (optional)
- rice or noodles (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton shoulder into fairly large, cube-like pieces.
2. In a casserole, add some fat or bacon, finely cut onions and rocambole, and let these brown.
3. Add the meat with some cumin, salt, seasonings if desired, paprika and some broth, and braise gently until somewhat tender.
4. Before the goulash is completely tender, add diced raw potatoes at the side of the casserole and braise them together with the meat.
5. Stir some flour into sour cream, bring it to a boil with the sauce, remove from heat and combine everything well.
6. Braised rice or noodles can also be served with it, or parsley sauce can be stirred into the broth instead of sour cream.

title: Gummer Torteletten (Summer Tartlets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deka butter
- 16 deka rye flour
- 14 deka almonds with shell, finely pounded and passed through a sieve
- 14 deka flavored sugar
- 1 knife point nutmeg
- 1 whole egg
- sugar syrup
- 8 deka blanched, cut into sticks almonds
- equal amount pistachios
- finely cut citron
- 1 drop almond extract

## Directions

1. Rub 14 deka butter finely with 16 deka rye flour, add 14 deka finely pounded and sieved almonds with shell, 14 deka flavored sugar, 1 knife point nutmeg, and 1 whole egg
2. Work together into a dough and let it rest covered in a very cold place for about 1.5 hours
3. Roll out the dough knife-back thick on a floured board and cut out round shapes with a tartlet cutter
4. Place on a butter-brushed baking sheet, brush with beaten egg white, and bake quickly in the oven
5. Remove from the sheet with a knife and cool on a board or sieve
6. Meanwhile, cook 14 deka sugar with the necessary water until it spins threads
7. Mix this syrup with 8 deka blanched almonds cut into sticks, equal pistachios, finely cut citron, and 1 drop almond extract
8. Top each tartlet with 1 teaspoon of this mixture and let dry

title: Gurke's Salat (Cucumber Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Gurken (cucumbers)
- Salz
- Essig
- Öl
- Pfeffer (optional)
- Knoblauch (optional)
- Rocamboie (optional)
- Schnittlauch (optional)
- saure Rahm (optional)
- geriebener Käse (optional)

## Directions

1. Man schält die Gurken, gibt sie in kaltes Wasser, schneidet sie fein blätterig, gibt Salz, Essig und Öl dazu, vermischt alles gut und serviert sie. Man kann auch die Gurken, nachdem sie blätterig geschnitten und gesalzen sind, 1/2 Stunde stehen lassen, ausdrücken und dann erst Essig und Öl dazu geben. Es ist aber vorzuziehen, die Gurken nicht mit dem Salze stehen zu lassen, da sie an Geschmack verlieren und auch schwerer zu verdauen sind. Man kann auch Pfeffer, zerbrückten Knoblauch oder Rocamboie dazu geben und die Gurken mit fein geschnittenem Schnittlauch bestreuem. Statt Öl können auch einige Esslöffel voll saurer Rahm genommen werden. Auch kann man geriebenen Käse dazugeben.

title: Gurken (türkischer Setzert)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- junge, zarte, fingerlange Gurken
- Salzwasser
- Butter
- Semmelbrößel
- anderes Gemüse

## Directions

1. die junge, zarte, fingerlange Gurken werden in Salzwasser weich gekocht
2. mit Butter und Semmelbrößel abgeschwelt
3. mit anderem Gemüse serviert

title: Gurken-Sauce (Gherkin Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Some onion
- Green parsley
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 large peeled carrot, cut into cubes
- Some soup
- 6 tablespoons butter
- Equal amount of flour
- 1-2 teaspoons finely chopped dill herb
- 1 1/4 liters cream
- Some salt

## Directions

1. Let some onion and green parsley roast in 2 tablespoons butter
2. Braise 1 large peeled carrot, cut into cubes, with some soup until the gherkin pieces are soft
3. Make a roux from 6 tablespoons butter and equal amount of flour
4. Braise the gherkins with 1-2 teaspoons finely chopped dill herb, 1 1/4 liters cream and some salt
5. Stir together well and, if desired, pass through a sieve

title: Gurken-Sauce anderer Art (Gherkin Sauce, Another Way)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Some onion
- Green parsley
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons flour
- Some soup
- Some vinegar
- 1/4 liter sour cream
- 1 large peeled gherkin
- Salt water
- 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped dill herb

## Directions

1. Let some onion and green parsley roast in 8 tablespoons butter
2. Add 8 tablespoons flour, pour it with the necessary soup, some vinegar and 1/4 liter sour cream
3. Let it cook, allow it to simmer, stir well together and pass through a sieve
4. Cut 1 large peeled gherkin into cubes, blanch in salt water, strain and dry well
5. Add to the passed sauce and boil briefly
6. Before serving, sprinkle the sauce with 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped dill herb
7. If using pickled sour gherkins instead of fresh ones, cut them finely into thin slices and add to the passed sauce without letting them simmer in it beforehand

title: Gurken-Sauce mit Senf und Sardellen (Cucumber Sauce with Mustard and Anchovies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- sorrel
- borage, 10 sprigs each
- 4 pieces cleaned anchovies
- 1 tablespoon capers
- 5 pieces small pickled cucumbers
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 4 deca bread soaked in water and pressed out
- 1 spoon French mustard
- 4 tablespoons oil
- pinch of pepper
- salt
- vinegar or lemon juice as needed

## Directions

1. Boil parsley, sorrel, and borage in salted water
2. Put in cold water, drain, and press out well
3. Cut finely with 4 pieces cleaned anchovies, 1 tablespoon capers, and 5 pieces small pickled cucumbers
4. Pound together in a mortar with 6 hard-boiled egg yolks, 4 deca bread soaked in water and pressed out, and 1 spoon French mustard
5. Pass through a sieve into a bowl
6. Place on ice
7. Stir in approximately 4 tablespoons oil, 1 pinch pepper, and some salt
8. If it is not sour enough, adjust with vinegar or lemon juice

title: Gurkensauce (Cucumber Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cucumbers, peeled and cut to thickness of knife back
- butter
- flour
- broth
- lemon peel, cut in strips
- pepper
- juice from half a lemon
- 2 to 3 spoonfuls milk cream
- beef soup (alternate method)
- onion, finely cut
- clarified butter
- lemon peel
- lemon juice or white vinegar

## Directions

1. Braise in a casserole the peeled and knife-thick sliced fresh cucumbers with butter and a little flour
2. After braising, add as much broth as required, strips of lemon peel, pepper, and the juice from half a lemon
3. Finally add 2 to 3 spoonfuls of milk cream
4. Alternatively: simmer the cucumbers in beef soup, let finely cut onion run golden in clarified butter, add the cucumbers with the soup, some lemon peel, lemon juice or white vinegar, and 2 to 3 spoonfuls of milk cream at the end
5. Take care that the cucumbers cook as little as possible
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gute Bratwurst (Good Bratwurst)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat from the leg of a young well-fattened pig, cleaned of skin and all gristle
- Two pounds lard for every four to five pound leg
- Zest of one lemon
- Salt and pepper
- Half a measure of water
- Saitlinge (striped sheep intestines)

## Directions

1. Finely chop the cleaned leg meat
2. Finely chop the lard and lemon zest
3. Salt and pepper well
4. While constantly mixing, pour half a measure of water into the mixture
5. Fill one finger thick into Saitlinge intestines
6. Use as desired
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gute bürtiger Roastbraten (Côte de Boeuf à la Bonne Femme)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Roastbraten, cut finger-thick
- Salt
- Flour
- Fat
- Vinegar
- Caraway
- Broth
- Parsley butter with lemon juice
- Bacon pieces (6-7 per piece, finger-long, seasoned with pepper and salt)

## Directions

1. Cut the roastbraten finger-thick, pound them but not too thin, dust with salt, dip them in flour, and fry them in a pan in some fat until they take on a golden-brown color.
2. Pour the juice in which the roastbraten were previously roasted into a casserole and pour some vinegar, some caraway, and enough broth over it to cover the roastbraten.
3. Cover and let them braise until tender, then lay them on a plate and place in the center of each piece some parsley butter with lemon juice.
4. Serve the degreased juice of the roastbraten in a sauce boat.
5. Alternatively, each piece of roastbraten can be threaded through with approximately 6-7 pieces of bacon, finger-long and thick, seasoned with pepper and salt, which gives the juice a good flavor.

title: Gute Leberwurst (Good Liver Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pork lung or pluck
- calf pluck and mesentery (for several sausages)
- fatty pork or fat
- water
- four rolls
- three to four onions
- a piece of garlic

## Directions

1. Take a pork lung or pluck (as much as needed; for several sausages one can also add calf pluck and mesentery), some fatty pork or fat
2. Over-cook this in water beforehand, but not until soft, and then carefully remove all glands and cartilage
3. Cut everything into small pieces and chop it together more finely or more coarsely as desired
4. For two plucks and a couple pounds of meat, cut four rolls into pieces and add them to the broth in which the meat for it was boiled
5. After they become soft, cut them into it, add three to four finely cut onions along with a piece of finely cut garlic
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Guter Weinesssig (Good Wine Vinegar)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One measure of very sour wine vinegar
- Two measures of country wine

## Directions

1. Let one measure of very sour wine vinegar and two measures of country wine stand in a container made of two well-fired clay vessels for several days in sun or oven warmth
2. Fill it up again with two measures, and continue thus until the container is full
3. Let it sour for at least eight days
4. Filter two measures from it and pour into the other container
5. Fill the first one again, and gradually fill up the second as well
6. When you need to use from the first, refill it from the second, and fill the second again with wine
7. In this way you always have good wine vinegar
8. If you throw in some pieces of sugar from time to time, the vinegar becomes much more spirited
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gâteau Fourré oder Erdbeerschnitte (Filled Cake or Strawberry Slices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Knife-back-thick butter pastry dough
- 4 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 12 deca fine sifted sugar
- Fine sugar for dusting
- Strawberries or currants or raspberries (optional)
- Whipped cream, sweetened and well-drained (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut 3-finger-wide strips of butter pastry dough from the knife-back-thick pastry, of desired length
2. Place on a baking sheet
3. Make zigzag marks with a knife
4. Brush with egg and bake
5. Lightly fold 12 deca finely sifted sugar into the stiff snow of 4 egg whites
6. Pipe 2 fingers high onto one part of the slices
7. Dust with fine sugar and dry in the oven for 5 minutes
8. Place another baked sheet on the dried snow and dust with sugar
9. Cut into pieces of choice and serve
10. Strawberries, currants, or raspberries can be folded under the snow
11. Alternatively, fill the slices instead of with snow with sweetened, whipped, well-drained cream, but these must not be dried in the oven, but rather served immediately

title: Gänseabatis-Suppe (Goose Giblets Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- one cleaned goose head with eyes removed and throat removed
- cleaned goose stomach
- skinned goose feet
- goose wings with quills removed

## Directions

1. Clean a goose head with eyes and throat removed
2. Clean the goose stomach, skinned feet, and wings with quills removed
3. Wash thoroughly and cook tender in meat broth
4. Prepare an Estragon soup from this
5. Lay up the goose head, cut into neat pieces
6. Continue as with the Ragout Soup

title: Gänseleberfarce (Goose Liver Forcemeat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- several small fattened goose livers
- half a pound of truffles, cut into slices
- onions, parsley and shallots, sautéed in butter and then finely cut
- bacon, scraped with a knife, half as much as goose liver
- soaked and squeezed out bread roll
- 5 or 6 whole eggs
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut the goose livers finely with a chopping knife
2. Sauté the truffles in butter
3. Finely cut the sautéed onions, parsley and shallots
4. Prepare bacon scraped with a knife, half the amount of goose liver
5. Soak and squeeze out bread roll
6. Pound all ingredients finely in a mortar with pepper, salt and nutmeg
7. Use this forcemeat for both cold and warm pastries, as well as for stuffing roasted poultry on the spit and goose livers studded with truffles
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Gänsleber-Kuchen mit Aspik (Goose Liver Cake in Aspic)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- bacon
- bread crumbs
- juniper berries
- mushrooms
- egg yolks
- whole egg
- salt
- pepper
- truffles
- smoked tongue
- calf's sweetbread
- aspic
- parsley

## Directions

1. Finely chop 32 decagrams of goose liver and 16 decagrams of bacon.
2. In a mortar, combine with 12 decagrams of bread crumbs softened in milk and well pressed out, 5 juniper berries, 3 pieces of thinly sliced mushrooms roasted in 4 decagrams of butter, 4 egg yolks, and 1 whole egg, some salt and pepper.
3. Pound together and pass through a sieve into a deep bowl.
4. Add 12 decagrams of ground truffles, 12 decagrams of smoked, softly cooked tongue, and if available, 12 decagrams of softly cooked calf's sweetbread.
5. All must be finely diced and well mixed.
6. Fill into a form brushed with butter and dusted with flour.
7. Place a thin layer of bacon on top with a cut made in the middle and cook 2 hours covered in a water bath.
8. Turn out onto a funnel plate and let the form rest over it for another 1.5 hours.
9. Remove the form, clean it, and place in ice.
10. Pour a finger-high layer of cooled aspic into it and let it set.
11. Decorate with finely cut-out truffles, nicely arranged tongue or hard-boiled eggs and some lettuce leaves or parsley sprigs.
12. Pour another finger-high layer of aspic and let cool well.
13. Turn out the cake (which has been trimmed in the meantime) onto it in the same position as it was in the form.
14. Fill the empty space in the middle with aspic, which should also completely cover the cake above.
15. After this has stood 1 hour in ice, hold briefly in warm water, wipe the form, and quickly turn the cake onto a flat platter.
16. Garnish with neatly cut aspic or decorate with colored butter.
17. The cake can also be cut into pieces and these laid in aspic.

title: Gänsleber-Ragout (Goose Liver Ragout)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon or lard
- goose liver
- salt
- Madeira or white wine
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. In a casserole, add some bacon or lard, lay a cleaned goose liver sprinkled with salt and basted with some lard on top.
2. Let this braise covered for 8 minutes.
3. Add approximately 1.5 liters of Madeira or white wine and let the liver braise for another 1.5 hours covered.
4. Place the liver on a board and cut finely with a carving knife.
5. Return to the ragout in the casserole and add salt, pepper, and wine as needed.
6. Braise once more and use this ragout, which can be made from goose liver leftovers, for small pies or puff pastry in pastry form and for various dishes.
7. A few spoonfuls of butter sauce can also be added.

title: Hachierte Beefsteaks (Ground Beefsteaks)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kilos deboned tenderloin
- 12 decagrams bacon
- 1 mushroom
- Some parsley
- 1 small head onion
- 12 decagrams diced, leafy-cut bread crusts, soaked in water and well pressed out
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- Salt
- Spices
- Flour
- Butter or fat

## Directions

1. Finely chop 2 kilos of deboned tenderloin with 12 decagrams of bacon.
2. With a chopping knife, mince 1 mushroom, some parsley, and if desired a small head onion.
3. Mix all this well together.
4. Add 12 decagrams of diced, leafy-cut bread crusts that have been soaked in water and well pressed out, then 2 whole eggs and 1 egg yolk, salt and spices.
5. Mix well and chop once more with the chopping knife.
6. Spread the mass on a flour-dusted board, form into thick pieces or 4 beefsteaks, making them 1-2 fingers high and of desired size.
7. Dust them on both sides with flour and fry them in very hot butter or fat for 12-15 minutes.
8. To make all beefsteaks uniform in size, you can use a cookie cutter to mark out the shape beforehand, or first form equal-sized knots from the mass from which you then shape the beefsteaks.
9. Serve with potato purée, potato hash, vegetables, rice, vegetables, or a Suprême or any piquant sauce.

title: Hacké de Veau (Chopped Veal Filets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Deca butter, beaten
- 2 whole eggs
- 8 Deca bread softened in water, well pressed out
- 10 Deca finely cut cooked or braised meat leftovers
- some roasted, green parsley
- a little finely cut lemon zest, if desired
- salt
- pepper
- flour for dusting
- beaten, stale eggs for coating
- breadcrumbs for rolling
- butter for frying, very hot

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and stir in 2 whole eggs, the softened and pressed bread, the finely cut meat leftovers, roasted green parsley, and lemon zest if desired
2. Season well with salt and pepper and mix everything thoroughly
3. Form filets of any desired size, or patties thick as a knife back
4. Dust these on both sides with flour
5. Brush them with beaten, stale eggs
6. Roll them in breadcrumbs
7. Fry them in a flat baking pan in very hot butter on both sides until golden yellow
8. Place them on a platter and arrange them in a serving dish

title: Hacké de Veau aux Oeufs (Chopped Veal with Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Deca butter
- finely cut mushrooms
- parsley, finely cut
- 8 Deca flour
- soup stock as needed
- juice of one lemon
- white wine, if desired
- salt
- pepper
- finely cut, cooked or braised veal meat, approximately one plate full
- puff pastry, as desired
- eggs à la Minute or fried eggs
- bread slices, fried in butter

## Directions

1. In butter, roast some finely cut mushrooms and parsley equally
2. Dust this with 8 Deca flour and let it roast
3. Pour the necessary soup stock on it
4. Add the juice of one lemon and some white wine if desired
5. Salt and season it
6. Stir it well together and let it cook together with the finely cut, cooked or braised veal meat, approximately one plate full
7. Arrange it on a serving dish and garnish it with puff pastry prepared as desired and eggs à la Minute or fried eggs and bread slices fried in butter
8. Pour the defatted juice over it

title: Hafermehlforte (Oat Flour Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 17 dkg butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 17 dkg sugar
- 17 dkg raw grated almonds
- 5 stiff egg whites
- 17 dkg buckwheat flour

## Directions

1. Beat butter alone, then with egg yolks and sugar well
2. Mix in raw grated almonds
3. Fold in stiff egg whites together with buckwheat flour
4. Fill into form
5. Bake slowly
6. When cooled, cut in half, fill with jam
7. Glaze brown or sprinkle with sugar

title: Hagebutten-Getschnetich-Soße (Rose Hip Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo ripe, soft rose hips
- 80 grams sugar
- 3/4 liter water

## Directions

1. Take approximately 1 kilo quite ripe, soft rose hips, slit them lengthwise, remove the stars and the hairy material and put them in a warm place to become softer
2. After some time put them in a saucepan with 80 grams sugar and 3/4 liter water, place on the stove and let cook under frequent stirring
3. When the rose hips have been passed through a sieve, let them come to boil again and fill them, still hot, into jars
4. Then put them in a breezy place and after 5 days bind with writing paper, which is perforated with a needle

title: Hagebutten-Sauce (Rose Hip Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- hot butter
- flour
- rose hip sauce or whole rose hips
- broth
- red wine
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. In hot butter add flour
2. If you have rose hip sauce, add 1/4 liter to the roux and pour it with some broth and red wine
3. If using whole rose hips, crush approximately 1.5 liters and add to the sauce
4. Pour with the necessary broth and some wine
5. Season with sugar and a little salt
6. Let it cook well and strain it
7. Instead of broth, you can use the stock from roasted game when pouring the roux

title: Hagebutten-Sauce mit Senf (Rose Hip Sauce with Mustard)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons French mustard
- 1 tablespoon rose hip marmalade
- sugar
- pepper as desired
- salt

## Directions

1. Put 3 tablespoons French mustard in a sauce boat
2. Add 1 tablespoon rose hip marmalade
3. Mix well together
4. Add necessary sugar
5. Add pepper and salt as desired

title: Hagebutten-Suppe mit Wein (Rosehip Soup with Wine)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- rosehips, about 1 and a half pounds for 12 persons
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- sugar
- wine, 1 measure
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Put the rosehips in a 3-seidel pot, add a piece of cinnamon and the peel of a lemon
2. Cook until soft
3. Strain through a fine hair sieve
4. Add sugar as desired
5. Bring to a boil again with 1 measure of wine
6. Serve with toasted bread slices
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Halb-Gefroese Groesses (Half-Frozen Largest)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Half-frozen mixture
- Butter sauce
- 4 egg yolks
- Flour for dusting

## Directions

1. Poach the half-frozen mixture until tender and cut into small cubes
2. Make a butter sauce and beat 4 egg yolks into it
3. Add the frozen mixture, let it cool, place it on a flour-dusted board
4. Form small sausages or dumplings from it and proceed as with the Ragout Croquettes

title: Halfen in Rahm-Sauce mit Parmesankäse (Pastries in Cream Sauce with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- pressed yeast
- milk
- melted butter or lard
- egg yolks
- whole eggs
- salt
- 4 deca butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 liter cream
- 1/3 teaspoon grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Ordinäre Halfen (Nr. 1275), only without sugar
2. When the halfen are finished baking, lay 2 together with 1/3 teaspoon grated Parmesan cheese between them and arrange on a plate
3. Put 4 deca butter and 2 tablespoons flour in a saucepan and pour in 1 liter cream while stirring constantly, stir on the heat until it boils
4. Pour the boiling cream sauce over the arranged hot halfen, which must be completely covered with sauce, and put them in the hot oven
5. After 10 minutes remove the dish, place on another plate and serve as an entree after the soup, or in the evening for supper or tea

title: Halte Casserolle Pastete (Cold Cassole Pâté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kilo butter dough
- Butter
- Spices
- Thin bacon slices
- Arbitrary forcemeat
- Raw meat (pork, veal, game or poultry)
- Beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare ½ kilo butter dough and let it rest.
2. Butter a small cassole and line it with spices, then line it with thinly rolled and neatly cut-out butter dough in any desired pattern.
3. Cut a 2 knife-back-thick dough plate to fit the size of the cassole bottom, place it on the decoration brushed with egg, and press lightly.
4. Cut a strip of butter dough rolled out 2 knife-back-thick, as wide and long as the height and circumference of the cassole require.
5. Brush the diagonally cut ends of the strip with beaten egg and position the strip at the side wall of the cassole so that the edge of the strip and the bottom plate edge touch, and the both egg-brushed ends of the strip overlap well.
6. To give the connection of the side wall with the bottom plate more security, brush a strongly knife-back-thick and 2 finger-wide butter dough strip with beaten eggs and lay this brushed side at the joint of the dough so that one half of the strip's width adheres firmly to the bottom and the other half to the side wall.
7. Line the bottom and side walls with thin bacon slices, brush 2 fingers high with arbitrary forcemeat, and place arbitrary raw meat (pork, veal, game, or poultry) on top.
8. Brush the remaining empty space and the forcemeat with forcemeat, place 1 bacon slice over it, and cover this with a dough plate fitted to the size of the cassole, whose edge must be pressed together with that of the side wall.
9. Score the lid several times with a knife, brush with beaten eggs, and bake the pâté for 2 hours in the oven.

title: Halte Grüne Sauce (Mayonnaise with Anchovies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled, strained egg yolks
- 3 raw egg yolks
- 1/2 to 1 1/4 liters oil
- juice of 2 lemons or 1 tablespoon vinegar, or some drops of dissolved citric acid or 4 tablespoons very strong tarragon vinegar
- salt to taste
- 2 knife tips pepper

## Directions

1. Put 6 hard-boiled, strained egg yolks and 3 raw egg yolks in a deep bowl and stir them for 15 minutes on ice
2. Into this, stir in 1/2 to 1 1/4 liters of oil gradually, drop by drop
3. Add the lemon juice or vinegar drop by drop
4. Finally stir in some salt with 2 knife tips of pepper
5. Serve this sauce with stiffened, cold meat or cold vegetables

title: Ham Crescents
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 dkg flour
- 16 dkg butter, cut into pieces
- a pinch of salt
- 1 egg yolk
- yeast leaven from 1½ dkg yeast
- 10 dkg cooked ham
- 1½ dkg grated Parmesan
- 1½ spoonfuls cream
- egg white for brushing

## Directions

1. On the board make a yeast crumb dough from 16 dkg flour, 16 dkg cut butter, a pinch of salt, 1 egg yolk, and yeast leaven from 1½ dkg yeast
2. After kneading, let it rise under a warm pot for 1 hour
3. Roll out thin and cut into squares
4. Place filling in the center of each square
5. Brush the edges with egg white
6. Press together over the filling and form into crescents
7. Place on greased baking sheet near the oven to rise
8. Before baking, brush with beaten egg or egg yolk
9. Bake in sharper heat
10. Serve cold with tea or warm with spinach or mushroom sauce
11. For filling: mix 10 dkg cooked ham with 1½ dkg grated Parmesan and 1½ spoonfuls cream

title: Ham Fritter Filling (Schinkerkrapferl Fülle)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- anchovies
- cream

## Directions

1. Chop the ham finely
2. Mix with the passed anchovies and some cream

title: Ham Fritters (Schinkenkrapferl)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg ham
- ⅛ liter cream
- 40 dkg raw peeled potatoes
- 8 dkg butter
- 1 egg yolk
- salt
- 25-30 dkg flour
- flour for dusting
- egg white for brushing
- hot lard for frying
- mushroom sauce, spinach or lettuce salad

## Directions

1. Cook the 40 dkg raw peeled potatoes, drain and pass through a sieve, let cool
2. Beat 8 dkg butter, mix with 1 egg yolk, salt, the potatoes and 25-30 dkg flour
3. Work on a floured board, roll out thinly
4. Cut out with a cutter, put filling in the middle
5. Brush the edges with egg white, lay a second sheet of dough on top, press the edges together
6. Cut out again with a smaller cutter
7. Fry in hot lard
8. Serve warm with mushroom sauce, spinach or lettuce salad

title: Ham Fritters with Yeast (Schinkenkrapferl mit Germ)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg cooked, passed, still warm potatoes
- 1 dkg yeast
- several tablespoons milk
- 10 dkg ham
- 1-2 anchovies
- 2 dkg Parmesan
- 14 dkg flour
- 1½ dkg melted butter
- 1 egg yolk
- salt
- lukewarm milk
- egg white for brushing
- hot lard for frying
- grated cheese
- mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Mix 14 dkg flour on the board with the potatoes, yeast, 1½ dkg melted butter, 1 egg yolk, salt and some lukewarm milk
2. Knead smooth
3. Let the dough rise under a warm saucepan for 30 minutes
4. Roll out thinly and cut with a fritter cutter
5. Put filling in the middle, brush the edges with egg white, lay a second sheet of dough on top
6. Press the dough together
7. Place the fritters near the oven to rise
8. Fry in hot lard
9. Drain and sprinkle with grated cheese, serve with mushroom sauce

title: Ham Soufflé (Schinkenauflauf)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 dkg ham
- ⅛ liter cream
- 6 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- pinch of salt
- pepper
- chopped ham
- cream
- 40 dkg cooked, peeled potatoes, sliced thinly
- breadcrumbs
- 1 hard-boiled egg, sliced
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix the 6 dkg butter rub with 2 egg yolks, pinch of salt, pepper, chopped ham, cream and 40 dkg cooked, peeled, thinly sliced potatoes
2. Fill half of the mixture into a buttered soufflé dish lined with breadcrumbs
3. Drip melted butter on top, add breadcrumbs and 1 hard-boiled egg sliced
4. Add the other half of the mixture on top, sprinkle with breadcrumbs again, drip with butter
5. Bake at moderate heat for 20-30 minutes
6. Can also be served as an evening dish

title: Ham with Baked Bread Slices (Schinken mit gebackenen Semmelſpalten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- small cut and well-soaked pieces of ham
- one seidel of broth
- one glass of good white wine
- small cut green parsley
- chives
- bread slices (semmeln)
- lard (schmalz)
- stewed potatoes or mushrooms for garnish

## Directions

1. Lay small cut and well-soaked pieces of ham in a brine or casserole
2. Pour one seidel of broth and one glass of good white wine over it
3. Add a little small cut green parsley and chives
4. Simmer everything until soft and only very little sauce remains
5. Note that the ham must be well soaked of salt and the broth only lightly salted
6. Cut bread slices (semmeln) of the same size as the ham pieces
7. Fry them in lard
8. Lay the ham on top and pour the small sauce over it
9. Around the rim of the dish, form a border of stewed potatoes or mushrooms
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ham-and-eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 dkg ham
- 6 fresh eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. In an omelet pan, let a piece of butter become hot and fry the ham on both sides.
2. Break the eggs individually into a small bowl, then let them slide onto the ham.
3. Salt and pepper them.
4. Place in the oven for approximately 8 minutes until the eggs are set.
5. Sprinkle them with chopped chives.
6. Serve in the pan or on a plate.

title: Hammelthau-Schöberl (Mutton Fat Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter simmering milk
- 25 Deca mutton fat
- 10 Deca butter
- 8 egg yolks
- Salt
- Snow from 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Cook 25 Deca mutton fat in 1/2 liter simmering milk and let it cook through with occasional stirring
2. Beat 10 Deca butter until fluffy, beat in 8 egg yolks
3. Add the prepared mutton fat and some salt, stir everything well together and lightly mix in the snow from 8 eggs
4. Handle further as with Griesschöberl

title: Haricots d'asperges (White Asparagus Beans)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- White asparagus beans
- Butter and breadcrumbs, or vinegar and oil

## Directions

1. Prepare them as the green beans are prepared
2. Serve them either with butter and breadcrumbs or, if they are to be served cold, with vinegar and oil

title: Haricots flageolets (Butter Beans with Butter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter beans, soft-cooked in salt water and drained
- 16 ounces butter
- 1 teaspoon finely cut green parsley
- Salt
- White pepper
- Nutmeg
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Shake the drained beans with 16 ounces butter, 1 teaspoon finely cut green parsley, salt, white pepper, nutmeg, and the juice of 1 lemon until the butter is incorporated
2. Arrange them heaped on a platter and garnish with roasted breadcrumbs or with any desired meat

title: Haricots secs liés (Stewed Dried Beans)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Finely cut bacon
- Garlic, if desired
- 8 ounces fat
- Required soup
- Vinegar
- Herbs and spices as needed
- Salt
- 1/2 liter soft-cooked beans

## Directions

1. Let finely cut bacon and, if desired, some garlic roast in a deep saucepan with 8 ounces fat
2. Pour soup and some vinegar over it
3. Add herbs, salt, and necessary spices and bring to a boil
4. Remove the herbs
5. Add 1/2 liter soft-cooked beans, stir well together, and let them cook

title: Harlekin Soufflé (Soufflé à l'Arlequin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Béchamel soufflé mixture
- 2 tablets grated chocolate
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Prepare a Béchamel soufflé mixture
2. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour with half of the mixture
3. Mix the second half of the soufflé with 2 tablets of grated chocolate and add on top
4. Cook 3/4 to 1 hour covered in a water bath or bake in the oven
5. Turn out onto a serving dish and serve with chocolate sauce, cream, or whipped cream

title: Harte Mandel-Märcherei (Hard Almond Confection)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams peeled almonds
- 1 egg white
- 16 decagrams fine sifted sugar
- flour
- marmalade
- glaze

## Directions

1. Pound peeled almonds very finely with 1 egg white and 16 decagrams fine sifted sugar into a dough
2. Place on a flour-dusted board and spread knife-back-thick
3. Cut into two parts
4. Spread marmalade on one part and cover with the second part
5. Press together and cut into desired pieces
6. Place on a baking sheet and bake in the oven until maize-yellow
7. Remove from oven and after completely cooling, spread with dissolved marmalade
8. Once dry, cover with desired glaze

title: Harte Pastete mit Makkaroni und Béchamel (Hard Pastry with Macaroni and Béchamel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bowl-shaped pastry case of hard dough
- Béchamel sauce made with butter
- 2 or more eggs
- Large macaroni, thoroughly cooked and strained
- 2 spoonfuls of good butter
- A knife-tip of cinnamon
- Salt

## Directions

1. Make a bowl-shaped pastry case from hard dough as described previously
2. Prepare a Béchamel sauce with butter and add a couple more eggs than usual
3. Cook the large macaroni thoroughly, strain it well
4. Stir the macaroni into the Béchamel sauce along with a couple spoonfuls of good butter, a knife-tip of cinnamon, and a little salt
5. Fill the open pastry case with this mixture
6. Bake uncovered in a not-too-hot oven for one hour
7. Serve immediately to the table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hartgekochte Eier (Hard-Boiled Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. Treat the same as soft-boiled eggs, but cook for 1/4 hour and then immediately place in cold water.

title: Hartgekochte Eier mit Kräutern und Sardellen (Hard-Boiled Eggs with Herbs and Anchovies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Hard-boiled shelled eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Grated herbs
- Sardelles

## Directions

1. Cut hard-boiled shelled eggs into 4 parts, place in a bowl, dust with salt and pepper, pour vinegar and oil over and sprinkle with grated herbs.
2. Then clean sardelles, separate them, remove the bones, wash them and roll them snail-shaped.
3. Arrange them on the bowl between the eggs.

title: Hase auf Matrosenart (Hare Sailor's Style)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one hare, almost completely roasted
- half a measure of small onions
- half a pound of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- one slice of lean ham
- one measure of white wine
- one measure of broth
- bay leaves
- one lemon, sliced
- several crushed cloves
- blood from poultry
- small chopped anchovies (optional)

## Directions

1. When the hare is almost completely roasted, let it cool and cut it into pieces approximately two fingers wide
2. Cut half a measure of small onions into four parts and sweat them soft in half a pound of butter
3. Add to this one tablespoon of flour, one slice of lean ham, one measure of white wine, and as much broth, along with several bay leaves
4. Let this sauce cook through several times, then add one lemon cut into slices along with several crushed cloves
5. Lay the hare in it and let it cook very slowly for half an hour
6. When serving, this sauce can still be finished with one teacup full of blood from poultry; one can also add some finely chopped anchovies if desired
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hase in der schwarzen Sauce (Hare in Black Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Skinned hare
- 32 deca butter
- 3 bay leaves
- Salt and pepper
- 0.5 liter red wine
- 0.75 liter soup
- 0.25 liter cream
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 4 deca sugar
- Hare blood (if available)

## Directions

1. Cut the skinned hare into 2 finger-width pieces
2. Put these in a casserole with 32 deca butter, 3 bay leaves, some salt and pepper, 0.5 liter red wine and 0.75 liter soup
3. Let this braise covered for approximately 1 hour
4. Remove the hare pieces
5. Stir 0.25 liter cream with 2 tablespoons flour, pour this into the sauce and season with 4 deca sugar, and if available, with hare blood
6. If the sauce is too thick, pour in some spoonfuls of red wine, let it come to a boil, then strain and bring to a boil again
7. Skim off the fat and pour the sauce over the hare pieces arranged neatly on a platter
8. Serve with polenta, noodles of choice, small fried onions or similar

title: Hase Pâteten (Hare Pâté Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- 1 onion
- Root vegetables
- 1 kilo chopped pork meat
- Young hare liver
- ¼ liter red wine
- Spices
- Thyme
- 5 crushed juniper berries
- 1 bay leaf
- 32 deca flour
- Butter dough
- Egg

## Directions

1. Put butter, 1 onion, and root vegetables, all thinly sliced, into a casserole.
2. Add 1 kilo chopped pork meat and braise covered.
3. Add the young hare liver, ¼ liter red wine, spices, thyme, 5 crushed juniper berries, and 1 bay leaf, and braise covered until soft.
4. When done, remove all meat from the bones, strain the liquid, and finely pound all remaining ingredients in a mortar, then pass through a sieve. It should be as thick as a purée.
5. Make butter dough from 32 deca flour of the finest kind and roll it out strong, knife-back thickness, in strips 2 hand-widths wide and ½ meter long.
6. Spread the meat mixture about 1 finger high and 3 fingers wide in the middle of the strips.
7. Fold the dough from one side over the meat, brush the top with egg, then fold from the second side over it, so that it overlaps only a finger width, and brush with egg as well, but leave the edge of this dough side 1 finger wide unbrashed (like 'Butter Dough Strudel').
8. Score the dough several times with a knife and place the filled butter dough on a baking sheet wetted with cold water.
9. Bake at fairly strong heat.
10. Cut into desired pieces and serve cold or warm as an appetizer for supper or tea.

title: Haselhühner, gebraten (Roasted Hazel Grouse)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- hazel grouse
- salt
- lard for larding and for inserting in breast

## Directions

1. Pluck the hazel grouse, open it up, wash it, salt it, reach under the breast and insert a small piece of lard in it, then lard it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Haselnuss-Kochli (Hazelnut Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka butter
- 16 Deka sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 30 Deka fine ground, sieved hazelnut kernels
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat butter until fluffy
2. Stir in 16 Deka sugar and 8 egg yolks one after another
3. Mix this with 30 Deka fine ground, sieved hazelnut kernels
4. Lightly mix in the snow from 8 egg whites
5. Put this mixture into a butter-brushed, flour-dusted mold
6. Cook ½ to ¾ hour either in steam or bake in the oven
7. Sprinkle with fine ground sugar immediately and serve

title: Haselnussbaisers (Hazelnut Meringues)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 15 dkg sugar
- 12 dkg coarsely chopped hazelnuts or nuts
- 5 dkg finely diced citron

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites with 15 dkg sugar over steam until thick
2. Add coarsely chopped hazelnuts and finely diced citron
3. Form small mounds using 2 small spoons on buttered sheet
4. Bake at moderate heat

title: Haselnusskrapferl (Hazelnut Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg grated hazelnuts, nuts, or raw grated almonds
- 15 dkg sugar
- 1-2 egg whites
- Jam for topping

## Directions

1. Mix grated nuts, sugar, and egg white(s) in a bowl
2. Form small balls
3. Place on buttered sheet
4. Make an indent on top
5. Bake in oven at low heat
6. Top with jam and sprinkle with sugar

title: Haselnusskuchen (Hazelnut Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10½ dkg butter
- 10¼ dkg sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 14 dkg roasted hazelnut kernels, grated
- 3 egg whites
- 1 spoon breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Beat 10½ dkg butter first alone, then with 10¼ dkg sugar and 3 egg yolks until well beaten.
2. Stir in 14 dkg roasted, shelled, grated hazelnuts, 3 egg whites, and 1 spoon breadcrumbs.
3. Spread the mass onto buttered, floured sheet and bake at medium heat.
4. Cut the cake into pieces while still warm, or cut out with a round cutter.
5. Dust with powdered sugar or brush with marmalade, stack together, white ice, and decorate the top with a hazelnut or candied cherry.

title: Haselnusszwieback (Hazelnut Rusks)
body:
## Ingredients

- 17 dkg sugar
- 3 whole eggs
- 17 dkg flour
- 14 dkg grated hazelnuts

## Directions

1. Beat 17 dkg sugar with 3 whole eggs with the whisk in the mixing bowl until thick.
2. With a wooden spoon, well mix in 17 dkg flour and 14 dkg grated hazelnuts.
3. The mass is thick; fill it into the buttered, floured biscuit form and bake slowly.
4. The next day, cut into thin slices.
5. Serve unbaked or baked, rolled in vanilla sugar, and served with tea.

title: Haselußeis (Hazelnut Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l milk
- 6 dkg hazelnuts
- 5 dkg sugar
- butter for coating the sheet
- 3 dl milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1 piece vanilla
- 9 dkg sugar

## Directions

1. Roast 6 dkg hazelnuts in a pan with 5 dkg sugar until dark, place on a buttered sheet and cool.
2. Roughly crush them in a mortar and then grind them finely in the mortar with some milk.
3. Mix them into the whipped vanilla cream (3 dl milk, 3 egg yolks, 1 whole egg, 1 piece vanilla, 9 dkg sugar).
4. The sugar used for roasting the hazelnuts must be deducted from the vanilla ice sugar because the mass will otherwise not freeze.
5. Pass the whole thing through a hair sieve, cool it and freeze like Vanilla Ice Cream.

title: Hasenbraten (Roasted Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small hare back and leg
- 12 dkg bacon
- 1 lemon
- 4 tablespoons capers
- 1 dkg cream
- salt
- pepper
- fat or butter
- vinegar
- water
- lingonberries

## Directions

1. Skin and wipe the hare
2. Salt and pepper it
3. Lard it and place in a pan
4. Sprinkle with onion rings and lemon peel
5. Baste with hot fat or butter
6. Add a little vinegar
7. Roast for about 1 to 1½ hours, basting frequently with its own juices and adding hot water
8. Before it is done, gradually baste with the cream so the sauce has a nice brown color
9. Remove the hare and press the juices, strain them, and let boil once more
10. Cut the back into pieces, arrange on a warm platter, and place the leg in its former shape at the bottom
11. Top the hare with thin lemon slices, alternating with capers and lingonberries
12. Garnish one side with vol au vent and the other with rice
13. Decorate with curled parsley
14. Serve the sauce separately

title: Hasenpastete (Hare Pâté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 decagrams finely cut hare meat
- 32 decagrams pork meat
- 16 decagrams finely cut bacon
- 1 piece scraped marrow
- 3 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 8 decagrams trimmed, water-softened, well-pressed rolls
- salt to taste
- 1 knife tip of pepper
- 5 juniper berries
- 1 spice clove
- 1 sprig thyme
- 4 decagrams bacon cut into small cubes
- 4 decagrams cooked, smoked tongue, cut the same way
- 1 small raw goose liver, cut into small cubes
- 5 pieces peeled gherkins, cut into small cubes as desired
- diced truffles if available
- butter

## Directions

1. Pound all ingredients (first batch) very finely in a mortar
2. Pass through a sieve into a deep bowl and stir well together
3. Mix in the cubed bacon, tongue, goose liver, gherkins, and truffles with the pâté
4. Fill a long mold brushed with butter and lined with flour
5. Strike the mold several times on a flat hand so the pâté compacts firmly
6. Cut parchment paper to fit the mold size and make three cuts with a knife
7. Brush with butter, place on the pâté, and cook covered in steam for 1½ hours
8. After this time, turn out onto a board and let stand covered with the mold for ½ hour
9. Serve warm with a mushroom sauce, or on the next day brush with dissolved aspic, slice into knife-back thickness, arrange on a dish, and garnish with chopped and cut-out aspic

title: Hasenpasteté (Hare Pastry)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine puff pastry
- Egg yolk
- Hollowed semolina bread
- Paper
- Good ragout of hare

## Directions

1. Roll fine puff pastry to the thickness of a small finger for the bottom base of the pastry
2. Place it on a baking sheet and on top place a round hollowed-out semolina bread wrapped in paper in the shape of a mountain
3. Brush the pastry all around with egg yolk
4. Lay a lid rolled from the same pastry of the same size over it
5. Cut it away all around the pastry so that you can press circles with the back of a knife in a row around it to give it a more handsome form
6. Brush with egg yolk and bake for half an hour
7. Once it is beautifully yellow, cut out a lid to remove the paper together with the semolina bread
8. Fill with a good ragout of hare
9. Serve hot
10. Various poultry can also be used for this pastry
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hazel Hens, Roasted (Haselhühner, gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- hazel hens
- bacon
- butter
- oysters
- potatoes
- lemon peel
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pluck the hazel hens, open them, wash them, and salt them
2. Lift the breast and insert a small piece of bacon into it
3. Lard them and roast them to a golden-brown color
4. Make a short sauce from butter, some oysters, potatoes, lemon peel, and lemon juice
5. Arrange the roasted hazel hens on a dish and pour the sauce over them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hazelnut Cookies (Haselnussplatzchen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 deca beaten butter
- 8 deca sugar
- a pinch of finely cut lemon zest
- 6 hard-boiled, passed egg yolks
- 24 deca flour

## Directions

1. Mix the beaten butter with sugar, lemon zest, and gradually add the hard-boiled, passed egg yolks
2. Add flour and mix well
3. Work the dough on a flour-dusted board and let it rest covered in a cool place
4. After approximately half an hour, roll out the dough on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a knife back
5. Cut out rounds with a cookie cutter, and make a small hole in the center with a small cutter to create thumb-width rings
6. Work the resulting scraps back into a dough, let rest covered in a cool place for half an hour, roll out to the thickness of a knife back, and treat as described above
7. Place the cut rings on a butter-greased baking sheet using a knife, brush with beaten eggs, sprinkle with hail sugar mixed with coarsely cut almonds, and bake pale yellow at low heat

title: Hazelnut Macaroon (Haselnuß-Macaroni)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 deca sugar
- 2 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 16 deca very finely ground, sifted hazelnut cream

## Directions

1. Beat sugar and stiffly beaten egg whites for 1.5 hours
2. Add finely ground hazelnut cream and mix well
3. On baking sheet lined with wafers, place walnut-sized pieces at 3 finger-widths apart
4. Press indentation in center with spoon handle and spread mixture around edge to form small wreaths
5. Sprinkle with finely ground sugar
6. Dry in well-heated oven
7. Remove small wreaths from wafers
8. Before serving, fill center indentation as desired with jam or small pieces of pineapple, citron, and the like

title: Heavily Candied Quince Slices (Schwergekochte Quitten-Spalten)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Quince apples or pears
- Lemon juice
- Lemon water
- 80 Deca sugar from heavy syrup
- 4 Deca sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the quince apples or pears into arbitrary pieces and remove the seed cavities
2. Peel the quinces and rub with lemon juice
3. Place in cold, then boiling lemon water and cook until moderately soft
4. Strain and place in an earthen vessel
5. Pour 80 Deca of heavy-cooked sugar (No. 1859) over the quinces and let them stand overnight in a warm place
6. Strain the juice and add 4 Deca sugar, let it boil again while removing the foam, then pour over the fruit
7. Repeat this process for 6 days
8. On the last day, cook the quinces with the juice together, place some of the juice in the jars, then quickly add the quinces and the juice which must cover them two fingers high
9. Place the filled jars unclosed in a breezy location and after several days bind them with writing paper, which you pierce with a needle to let air in
10. If there is too much juice on the second day, remove the excess clear juice, strain it through cloth, fill into jars, bind them and cook like Muscatel pears (No. 1842) for 3/4 hours in steam
11. Leave enough juice for the quinces to be completely covered
12. If you wish the quince slices to be red, add dissolved cochineal color when cooking for the last time

title: Heavily Cooked Cherries in Steam (Schwergekochte Kirschen im Dunst)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Morello cherries
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Treat them like the heavy-cooked Morello cherries
2. Treat excess juice like the quince slices cooked in steam

title: Hecht gebraten (Fried Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small pike
- 3 anchovies
- ½ l cream
- butter
- onion rings
- soup
- crescent potatoes
- parsley
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Scale the pike, cut it open, clean it, and wash it well. Salt it.
2. Let it sit for 1 hour, then dry it with a cloth.
3. Make incisions on top or on the side, insert pieces of anchovy or finely cut mushrooms.
4. Spread the bottom of a roasting pan with butter, lay onion rings on it, and place the pike with the back up into it.
5. Baste it frequently with hot butter, soup, and later with cream for 25-30 minutes without too high heat.
6. Lift it with a fish spatula onto a dish.
7. Arrange crescent potatoes around it that have been tossed in butter and parsley.
8. Decorate it with parsley and lemon slices.
9. Strain the sauce and serve separately. Pike is prepared the same way, requiring slightly longer roasting time.

title: Hecht kalt (Cold Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike of 1-1¼ kg
- much water
- 10 spoonfuls salt
- 1/4 l vinegar
- 2 lemons for garnish
- 4 h capers
- 1 red beet
- butter
- lemon juice
- caviar
- sauce tartare
- parsley
- hard eggs

## Directions

1. In a fish pan, bring much water with 10 spoonfuls of salt and 1/4 l vinegar to a boil.
2. Clean and wash the pike, tie it to the insert, lower it into the fish pan, then immediately pull it back out.
3. Let it simmer for 10-12 minutes depending on the size of the fish.
4. Let it cool first in the broth, then remove it.
5. Once completely cooled and untied from the insert, place it on the fish dish.
6. Spray it on the sides with butter that has been beaten beforehand, drizzle it with lemon juice.
7. Decorate it with capers, top with lemon slices that have been topped with caviar.
8. Serve it with tartare sauce.
9. Garnish it with parsley, hard eggs, and red beet slices.

title: Hecht mit Mayonnaise und Aspik (Pike with Mayonnaise and Aspic)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike
- salt water
- mayonnaise
- aspic
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare a pike as you would boiled pike and let it cool in the stock.
2. Remove it, pull off the skin, remove the bones, break the pike into pieces as desired.
3. Arrange the pieces on a platter, pour mayonnaise over them, and garnish the platter beautifully with aspic and hard-boiled eggs.

title: Hecht, gebraten (Fried Pike)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pike
- salt
- butter
- lemon juice
- Pike liver

## Directions

1. After the pike is cleaned and well salted, thread it lengthwise onto a spit
2. Roast it very slowly over fire, brushing it frequently with butter, and continually baste it with lemon juice
3. Meanwhile, take out the liver, brush it with butter, and fry it on both sides on the grate in its juices
4. When the pike is roasted, place it on a serving dish, insert the liver into its mouth, pour melted butter over it once more, and serve it at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hechtfarce (Pike Forcemeat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat from a 3-pound pike
- Small onion, chopped and sweated in butter
- 3 bread rolls (with crusts removed, rest cut, soaked in milk and squeezed out, or cooked into a paste in milk)
- 1/4 pound marrow or butter
- 3 whole eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Carefully cut the pike meat and remove all bones
2. Chop very finely and rub tender in a mortar
3. Rub the meat with the onion, bread, marrow or butter, eggs, salt, pepper and nutmeg for a long time until very fine
4. Press the forcemeat through a brass sieve
5. Form small dumplings and use as garnishes and in stuffed dishes
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Heißabgesottene Hühner (Hot Boiled Chicken)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- small meaty chickens
- salt
- onion
- yellow turnips
- green parsley
- pepper
- allspice
- three or four cloves
- water
- vinegar
- butter
- flour
- cream

## Directions

1. Clean the chickens well, salt them slightly, and dress them nicely
2. Place in a casserole with onion, yellow turnips, green parsley, pepper, allspice, and three or four cloves
3. Fill with three parts water and one part vinegar
4. Let simmer until the chickens are half cooked
5. Warm a piece of butter, add two spoonfuls of flour, let it brown slightly
6. Strain the sauce onto it
7. Add two or three spoonfuls of cream
8. Return the chickens to the sauce
9. Let them cook completely through
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Helle Sulz mit Creme (Clear Jelly with Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Clear orange or lemon jelly mass
- Rice with cream, or vanilla, or any cream, or striped blanc-manger

## Directions

1. Make a clear orange or lemon jelly
2. Pour into a high, flat form set in ice, enough jelly mass so the bottom is covered 2 fingers thick and let it freeze
3. Beforehand, freeze rice with cream, or vanilla, or any cream, or striped blanc-manger in a round form, which is approximately 3 fingers smaller in diameter than the high form chosen for this jelly
4. Place this cream carefully on the 2 fingers thick clear jelly poured in, but so that the upper side lies face down on the jelly, with a 2 finger-wide empty space remaining at the walls
5. Fill the middle empty space with jelly and then fill the form with the remaining clear jelly mass
6. Let freeze and proceed further as for Orange Sulz (see recipe 1660)

title: Helmstedter Hirsebrei (Helmstedt Millet Porridge)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- millet porridge cooked in milk
- 4 tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Place the millet porridge, which has been cooked well in milk, into a casserole dish brushed with butter
2. Top with 4 tablespoons butter and bake for 1/2 hour in the oven
3. Serve the dish in the casserole or turned out onto a platter

title: Herb Butter (Beurre de Montpellier)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- tarragon leaves
- chives
- green parsley
- chervil
- sorrel
- burnet
- watercress
- salad burnet
- 8 sprigs each
- 8 small onions
- 1 piece rocambole
- 24 teaspoons butter
- lemon juice
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Finely cut tarragon leaves, chives, green parsley, chervil, sorrel, burnet, watercress, and salad burnet (8 sprigs each), 8 small onions, and 1 piece of rocambole
2. Pound very finely in a mortar with 24 teaspoons of butter, some lemon juice, a little salt, and the same amount of pepper
3. Pass through a sieve and arrange very neatly on a plate

title: Herb Sauce (Fine Herb Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- about 1 handful spinach
- about the same amount of common soup herbs
- 4 pieces capers
- some tarragon
- 1 tablespoon capers
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons oil
- vinegar
- water
- sugar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from about 1 handful of spinach and about the same amount of common soup herbs, wash the former, boil it in salt water until soft, strain the water and place in cold water and press out well
2. Chop it very finely with 4 pieces of capers, some tarragon and 1 tablespoon capers, pound the chopped mixture together with 6 hard-boiled egg yolks and pass through a sieve
3. Mix with 4 tablespoons oil, some vinegar and water and stir sugar and pepper to taste underneath

title: Herbst-Kraut (Autumn Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large or 2 small heads of cabbage
- approximately 1 1/4 liters vinegar
- 1 liter water
- 1 teaspoon caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Cut 1 large or 2 small heads of cabbage finely into noodles
2. Pour approximately 1 1/4 liters vinegar and 1 liter water over it, add 1 teaspoon caraway seeds

title: Hering am Rost gebraten (Herring Grilled on the Grill)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 herring
- Butter
- Oil
- Paper

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the herring as described in previous instructions
2. Wrap it in paper brushed with butter and oil
3. Grill on each side for approximately 4-5 minutes
4. When cooked through, unwrap from the paper and arrange in a serving bowl
5. Serve with warm mustard sauce, crayfish sauce, or sauerkraut

title: Hering mit Mafchauer Äpfeln (Herring with Mashhausen Apples)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 completely prepared herring
- Mashhausen apples, peeled
- Lemon juice or vinegar
- Oil

## Directions

1. Prepare the herring according to previous instructions and peel the Mashhausen apples
2. Cut both the herring and apples into cubes
3. Mix well together with lemon juice or vinegar and oil
4. Arrange in a serving bowl

title: Hering mit Äpfeln (Herring with Apples)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 completely prepared herring
- Mashhausen apples
- A small apple corer
- Herring milk
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the herring according to recipe 974 and cut into desired pieces
2. Arrange the pieces round-shaped in a serving bowl
3. To remove the core housing of a Mashhausen apple, press a small corer into the side of the stem, turn it slightly and pull it out with the core housing
4. Do not pierce through the apple completely so that a bottom remains
5. Hollow out the apple further until it is only half a finger thick in the opening, then peel it
6. Mix the strained herring milk with a few drops of oil, vinegar and some pepper
7. Fill the hollowed-out apple top with the herring milk mixture
