8. Place it in the center of the arranged herring

title: Herring Prepared (Daring zu bereiten)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 herring
- Water
- Milk
- Herring roe
- Pickled apples
- Capers
- Vinegar
- Oil

## Directions

1. Place 1 herring on a board moistened with water and lightly tap with the handle of a knife.
2. Using a knife, lightly cut the skin from tail to head, continuing this at the side of the head and pulling the skin down from the head to the tail.
3. When this is done on both sides, remove the head and tail, loosen the spine, remove the bones and soak the herring 1 hour in soaked milk, then dry on a cloth.
4. Cut both halves of the herring into three pieces diagonally and arrange them in a bowl so that the two pieces lie beside each other.
5. Similarly, cut the herring roe and pickled apples into neat pieces and garnish the herring with these.
6. Place the head and tail in their proper positions and sprinkle the whole thing with capers, serve with vinegar and oil.
7. A little green parsley can be placed in the herring's mouth.

title: Herring Sauce (Herring Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rinsed herring (Pomeranian)
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 2 peeled Manzanilla apples with seeds removed
- 4 ounces grated rolls soaked in water and well pressed out
- some shallots
- 5 juniper berries
- 1 pinch pepper
- 2 tablespoons oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Skin one rinsed herring and remove the bones
2. Place 6 hard-boiled egg yolks, 2 peeled Manzanilla apples with seeds removed, 4 ounces of grated rolls soaked in water and well pressed out, some shallots, and 5 juniper berries in a mortar
3. Pound everything together with the herring and the milt finely and pass through a sieve
4. Stir the sieved mixture with 1 pinch of pepper, 2 tablespoons oil, and the necessary vinegar and serve this sauce with cold roasted meat or any roasts

title: Herring with Apples (Harens aux pommes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 completely prepared herring
- Muscovy apples

## Directions

1. Cut the completely prepared herring into desired pieces and arrange these in a circle in a bowl.
2. To remove the core cavity of a Muscovy apple, press on the side of the stem.

title: Herring with Muscovy Apples (Hering mit Mutschaudsfer Äpfeln)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 prepared herring
- Peeled Muscovy apples
- Lemon juice or vinegar
- Oil

## Directions

1. Prepare the herring as above and cut peeled Muscovy apples into cubes.
2. Mix well together with lemon juice or vinegar and oil and arrange in a bowl.

title: Heurige Kartoffeln (New Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 kg potatoes
- 5 dkg butter
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Scrape the potatoes
2. Place in cold salt water
3. Cook for 15-20 minutes
4. Strain
5. Place in a casserole with 5 dkg hot butter and chopped parsley
6. Toss together

title: Himbeer's Limonade (Raspberry Lemonade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter fresh water
- raspberry juice (to taste)
- juice of 3 lemons
- sugar (to taste)

## Directions

1. In 1 liter fresh water, add raspberry juice to taste, the juice of 3 lemons and sugar to taste

title: Himbeer-Krapfen (Raspberry Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries
- Powdered sugar
- Wafers moistened with water
- Wine batter
- Lard for frying

## Directions

1. Preparation is the same as for Strawberry Fritters, using raspberries instead of strawberries.
2. Fresh cherries and other berries can be used the same way as strawberries and raspberries, only the stems must be removed.

title: Himbeerbombe (Raspberry Bombe)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh raspberries
- whipped cream
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Pass the raspberries through a sieve.
2. Mix with firmly whipped cream and sugar to taste.
3. Follow the further procedure as in Feiertagsmenu 1: Kaffeebombe.

title: Himbeerenwaſſer (Raspberry Water)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- juice of one lemon
- 2 measures of water per 1 measure of raspberry juice
- finely ground sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Crush the raspberries in an earthenware vessel
2. Press them through a napkin
3. To 1 measure of juice, add 2 measures of water and the juice of one lemon
4. Add finely ground sugar to taste
5. Pass it through a hair sieve or napkin
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Himbeerenſaft (Raspberry Juice)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare like cherry juice, with the note that if after some time the juice becomes thin, place it in a casserole and cook again until it becomes thick
2. Cut round white papers and dip the inner side in brandy and finely pounded sugar
3. When the juice is filled into glasses, place such a paper on the juice and tie the glass with a bladder
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Himbeererôme (Raspberry Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberry pulp
- 12 dkg vanilla sugar
- whipped cream
- 1 dkg dissolved gelatin

## Directions

1. Mix the raspberry pulp with vanilla sugar, whipped cream, and dissolved gelatin.
2. Fill a pudding mold rinsed with water.
3. Bury in ice for 2–3 hours.
4. Loosen, hold in hot water for a moment, unmold.
5. Garnish if desired with whipped cream and serve.

title: Himbeers- und Johannisbeer-Sulz (Raspberry and Currant Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- currants

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as Strawberry Jelly (No. 1664).

title: Himbeers-Marmelade (Raspberry Preserves)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries (quantity not specified)
- powdered sugar equal in weight to the raspberry juice

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same way as the Currant Preserves (recipe 1873).
2. If possible, use wild raspberries as they are darker and more robust.

title: Himbeersaft (Raspberry Syrup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries
- 1/2 kilo sugar per liter of clear juice

## Directions

1. Put an arbitrary quantity of raspberries into a deep bowl
2. Crush them completely and let them stand for 24 hours, until the juice separates from the flesh and fermentation has begun
3. Press the crushed raspberries in small portions through a strong cloth, fill the juice into a bottle, bind this with a muslin cloth and let it stand for 8-10 days until a rose-colored sediment has formed and the juice is fairly clear
4. Pour off the clear juice and filter the rest through filter paper
5. To every liter of clear juice add 1/2 kilo sugar, namely put the sugar and the respective quantity of raspberry juice in a saucepan, boil it for 1/4 hour under constant rapid stirring, strain it through flannel and fill it into bottles only when it has cooled
6. In the preparation of raspberry syrup neither iron nor enameled dishes may be used, as the juice would become blue or black from it
7. Instead of flannel one can also use a finer linen cloth

title: Himmel mit Butter (Batter Puffs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 tablespoons fine flour
- 14 tablespoons vanilla sugar
- 1 pinch salt
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons rum
- 1/2 liter cream
- 3 tablespoons melted, clarified butter

## Directions

1. Place 14 tablespoons fine flour, 14 tablespoons vanilla sugar, and 1 pinch salt into a deep bowl.
2. Gradually add 2 whole eggs, 2 egg yolks, 2 tablespoons rum, and 1/2 liter cream, stirring very well.
3. Mix in 3 tablespoons melted, clarified butter until well combined.
4. The mixture should flow in a thin stream from a spoon.
5. Prepare Hoblthuppen (cream puffs) using this batter as described in No. 1655.

title: Himmelbrandt-Thee (Himmelbrandt Tea)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Himmelbrandt tea
- Boiling water
- Sugar
- Milk

## Directions

1. Pour boiling hot water into a pot to warm it, pour this out after a few minutes, and add 1 tablespoon full of Himmelbrandt tea (for 1 cup).
2. Pour it over with a few spoonfuls of boiling hot water and after a few minutes add the remaining necessary water.
3. Strain this tea through a very fine sieve and drink it like coffee with sugar and milk.

title: Hippocras (Spiced Wine)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 bottles good Rhenish or white wine
- 1 pound sugar
- 2 loth cinnamon
- some white whole peppercorns
- peel from 1 lemon
- 1 grain ambergris
- sugar

## Directions

1. Combine 4 bottles good Rhenish or white wine with 1 pound sugar, 2 loth cinnamon, some white whole peppercorns and peel from 1 lemon
2. Cover well and let stand for 24 hours
3. Place 1 grain ambergris (pounded fine with sugar and wrapped in linen) in a filter bag
4. Strain the wine through several times
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hirn in Muscheln (Calf's Brain in Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's brain
- 5 mushrooms
- 3 dkg Parmesan
- 2 anchovies
- several spoonfuls white wine
- 6 dkg hot butter
- 1 finely chopped onion or shallots
- parsley
- 1½ trimmed, water-soaked, squeezed rolls
- egg
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Wash the brain and remove the membrane.
2. In hot butter, let finely chopped onion or shallots, parsley, and cleaned mushrooms come to a boil.
3. Add the trimmed, water-soaked, squeezed rolls and the brain (blanched or unblanched).
4. Braise for a few minutes with the addition of wine and salt to a thick paste.
5. Fill this into buttered shells.
6. Pour a little beaten egg over it.
7. Sprinkle grated cheese on top.
8. Top with anchovy strips (these can also be omitted).
9. Place the shells on a sheet and let them gratinate in the oven for 10-15 minutes.
10. Serve the shells on a dish or cup with a folded napkin.

title: Hirn mit Ei (Brains with Egg)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's or pork brain
- 5 dkg hot butter
- finely chopped parsley
- salt
- white pepper
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Remove the membrane from the brain and wash well.
2. In a small pot, heat 5 dkg butter and sauté finely chopped parsley.
3. Add the brain, salt, and white pepper, roast for a few minutes.
4. Break an egg into the pot and roast for a few more minutes.

title: Hirn-Knödel (Calf Brains Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- same base as white bread knödel
- 1 pound cooked and sieved or diced calf brains
- some white pepper
- 3 deca white bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare the base mixture as for white bread knödel.
2. Boil the calf brains in salted water, sieve or cut into pieces, and stir into the mixture.
3. Add white pepper and white bread crumbs.
4. Form and cook knödel as directed in the preceding recipe.

title: Hirn-Konsommé (Calf Brain Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf brain
- cold water
- salt water
- a second calf brain
- approximately 8 deca butter
- parsley
- 3 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- some salt
- 1 knife point white pepper
- 1/2 liter white beef soup

## Directions

1. Place 1 calf brain in cold water and remove the skin carefully
2. Place it again in cold water, then in boiling salted water
3. Cook until the brain is properly contracted
4. Place on a sieve and cover with cloth
5. Take a second skinned brain and roast parsley in approximately 8 deca butter and braise both slightly
6. Mix this with 3 whole eggs, 4 egg yolks, some salt, 1 knife point white pepper, and 1/2 liter white beef soup, stirring well together
7. Pour this mixture into a form greased with butter and dusted with flour
8. Cut the brain cooked in salt water into cubes
9. Distribute the cubes in the form under the liquid mixture
10. Cook the whole thing in a water bath for 1 hour

title: Hirn-Nockerl (Noques à la cervelle de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deka butter
- 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
- 20 Deka peeled, diced, milk-soaked breadcrumbs
- 1/2 pound veal brains, cooked in salted water and passed through a sieve
- some salt
- 1 knife point pepper
- 6 Deka flour

## Directions

1. Beat 12 Deka butter until creamy
2. Stir in 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
3. Mix in the breadcrumbs and veal brains
4. Add salt, pepper and flour
5. Form into Nockerl as with the preceding recipe

title: Hirn-Pudding (Consommé à la cervelle de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf brain
- cold water
- salt water
- 12 deca butter
- 4 egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg
- 16 deca breadcrumbs
- milk
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Put 1 calf brain in cold water
2. Clean and remove skin
3. Cook in salt water and pass through a sieve
4. Beat 12 deca butter until fluffy
5. Stir in the brain, 4 egg yolks, some salt, nutmeg, and 16 deca breadcrumbs soaked in milk
6. Lightly fold in the snow from 4 egg whites
7. Fill into mold and bake or cook in steam

title: Hirn-Rissoles (Brain-Filled Pastry Crescents)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rissole dough
- brain
- green parsley
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- milk

## Directions

1. Prepare rissole dough as in the recipe for Rissoles mit Farce
2. Clean and skin the brain, then braise it in butter with finely chopped green parsley
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Bind with egg yolks mixed with a little milk
5. Use this brain mixture as filling following the method for Rissoles mit Farce

title: Hirnsuppe (Brain Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's brain
- beef broth
- 15 to 20 mushrooms of medium size
- parsley, finely chopped
- butter
- 1/2 cooking spoon of flour
- pepper, ground
- nutmeg
- bread rolls, baked

## Directions

1. Cook a calf's brain in beef broth
2. Press the cooked brain through a sieve
3. Coarsely chop 15 to 20 mushrooms of medium size
4. Put finely chopped parsley in a casserole with a piece of warmed butter
5. Let the mushrooms simmer in the butter for a while
6. Dust with half a cooking spoon of flour
7. Let it simmer a bit longer
8. Add the brain
9. Fill with good beef broth
10. Season the whole with ground pepper and nutmeg
11. Let it simmer well together
12. Serve over baked bread rolls
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hirnsuppe (Brain Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brain
- Beef broth
- 15 to 20 mushrooms of medium size
- Parsley, finely cut
- Butter
- Half a cooking spoon flour
- Good beef broth

## Directions

1. Cook calf brain in beef broth. Push through a sieve. Coarsely chop 15 to 20 medium-sized mushrooms and place with finely cut parsley in a casserole with a piece of warm butter. Let the mushrooms simmer a while, dust with half a cooking spoon of flour, let simmer a bit more, add the brain and fill with good beef broth.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hirnsuppe (Brain Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dkg beef marrow bones
- 1 calf brain
- 3-4 mushrooms
- 1 roll
- 5 dkg butter
- parsley
- 2 tablespoons flour
- beef broth
- salt
- pepper
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Wash the brain, remove the skin, blanch it, and pass through a sieve
2. Clean, wash, and finely chop the mushrooms
3. In a saucepan, roast chopped parsley in 5 dkg hot butter, add the mushrooms and stew for 15 minutes
4. Add 2 tablespoons flour and the brain, season with salt and pepper
5. Pour in strained beef broth, simmer for 20 minutes, and serve with bread slices

title: Hirnwurst (Brain Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple beef brains
- a couple calf brains
- a quarter Pfund marrow
- two small trimmed rolls
- good milk
- salt
- pepper
- finely cut lemon peel
- two whole eggs
- two egg yolks
- casings

## Directions

1. Wash and strip the membrane from a couple beef and a couple calf brains, and chop them along with a quarter pound of marrow very finely
2. Dip two small trimmed rolls in good milk, squeeze them out, and mix them in
3. Season with salt and pepper as needed and add finely cut lemon peel
4. Beat two whole eggs along with two egg yolks and stir them in well
5. Fill the mixture into casings and tie well
6. Blanch and over-cook them, then roast them on the grate
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hirschbeefsteak (Venison Beefsteak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram venison filet
- 5 dekagrams bacon
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- hot fat
- butter
- parsley
- Maitre d'hôtel butter

## Directions

1. Remove skin, bones and fat from the filet and cut into two-finger-wide pieces.
2. Lightly pound with the back of a knife.
3. Lard the pieces.
4. Place on a plate, drizzle with melted butter, cover with paper dipped in butter and let stand until ready to cook.
5. Just before cooking, salt and pepper them.
6. Coat one side with flour.
7. Place flour-side down in a flat pan with very hot fat.
8. Quickly brown on both sides for 4-5 minutes.
9. Arrange on a long platter.
10. Place a piece of Maitre d'hôtel butter on each beefsteak.
11. Garnish with curly parsley and serve the drippings alongside.

title: Hirschziemer, Glaciert (Glazed Venison Loin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison loin (upper part of the haunch)
- Salt
- Spices
- 16 spoonfuls bacon or other fat
- Root vegetables
- 1 onion, cut into thin leaves
- Thyme
- 5 grains each type of spice
- 5 crushed juniper berries
- 1 piece black bread, cut into thin slices
- 1 liter red wine
- 1/4 liter vinegar
- Water
- Butter
- Bread crumbs roasted in butter
- Sugar
- 1 spice clove
- Cinnamon
- Orange and lemon slices
- Parsley
- Lingonberry or rosehip sauce

## Directions

1. Rub the venison loin with salt and spices and let it rest for 2 hours.
2. In a casserole, place bacon or other fat, root vegetables, onion cut into thin leaves, thyme, 5 grains each type of spice, 5 crushed juniper berries, and a piece of black bread cut into thin slices.
3. Lay the loin on top.
4. Pour 1 liter red wine, 1/4 liter vinegar, and some water over it, using enough liquid so that it covers the loin.
5. Cover with a sheet of parchment paper brushed with butter and braise gently for approximately 2 to 3 hours until tender.
6. Once tender, remove to a board and cut into desired pieces.
7. Arrange the pieces on a platter closely together so that everything appears as a whole.
8. If desired, sprinkle roasted bread crumbs on top.
9. Sprinkle sugar mixed with 1 clove and some cinnamon that has been ground together, then sprinkle plain sugar on top, which is then glazed (caramelized) with a hot shovel.
10. Garnish the platter with orange and lemon slices and parsley, and serve with lingonberry or rosehip sauce.
11. If desired, skim the fat from the sauce and serve it with the meat.
12. If there are leftovers, preserve them in the sauce.

title: Hirseauflauf (Millet Porridge)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 1/3 liter hulled millet
- 12 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons sugar
- a pinch of salt
- finely sifted sugar
- grated pepper cake (Lebkuchen)

## Directions

1. In 1 liter of boiling milk, stir in 1/3 liter of hulled millet while stirring constantly and let it cook well
2. When the mixture begins to thicken, add 12 tablespoons butter, 8 tablespoons sugar, and a pinch of salt and stir well
3. When the millet porridge is cooked soft and thick, arrange it in a bowl and sprinkle with finely sifted sugar and grated pepper cake, then serve

title: Hirsen-Decoct (Millet Decoction)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one Loth each of millet, currants, and figs
- one Seidel of water

## Directions

1. Take one Loth each of millet, currants (small raisins), and figs
2. Cook them for one hour in one Seidel of water
3. Strain through a sieve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hobelspäne (Planed Shavings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 4 egg yolks
- 12 deca sugar
- 8 deca flour
- wax

## Directions

1. Mix the well beaten snow from 4 egg whites well with 4 egg yolks, 12 deca sugar and 8 deca flour. A baking sheet coated with wax and hollowed out is thinly coated with this mass and baked in a fairly hot oven until golden yellow, then you immediately cut it with a long fine knife into finger-wide strips, but twist these over wooden spoon handles and only remove them after a few moments, which gives them the form and appearance of wood shavings.
2. The rolling up of the strips over the wooden spoon handle must happen as quickly as possible, as the dough will otherwise become too stiff and not roll up. If desired, you can place 1/2 spoon full of the above batter at 2 hand-widths distance on a baking sheet coated with wax, which you stretch thin lengthwise with a knife or spoon and bake in the oven until the dough releases from the sheet, whereupon you quickly roll one strip after another over a wooden spoon handle and remove after a few moments.

title: Hohihuppen (Hohihuppen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 22 deca flour
- 12 deca vanilla sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1/8 liter cream
- wax
- blotting paper

## Directions

1. Put 22 deca flour and 12 deca vanilla sugar into a deep bowl, gradually add 4 egg yolks, 1 whole egg and 1/8 liter cream and stir this very well until it flows smoothly from the spoon.
2. A flat wooden Hohihuppen form is heated over charcoal fire; when it is very hot, coat it with white wax, wipe it with blotting paper, lightly coat it again with wax and pour 1 spoon full of the prepared mass onto the hot iron, close it tightly and place it over the fire; only gradually is it then pressed together so that the dough does not fall apart through too rapid heating of the iron.
3. After about 1 minute, turn the iron over the coals and after an equally long time remove it, open it, whereupon the Hohihuppe should have a golden brown color. Then quickly remove it from the iron and roll it still warm over the Hohihuppen wood.

title: Hollunder (Black Elder Berries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- very ripe elderberries picked in dry weather
- finely ground sugar
- 2 tablespoons sugar per jar

## Directions

1. Pick very ripe elderberries in dry weather from their stems
2. Fill into preserving jars
3. Add 2 tablespoons finely ground sugar to each jar
4. Bind and cook as the Muscatel pears (Nr. 1842) in steam

title: Holländische Sauce (Dutch Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Onions
- Butter
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Glace
- Egg yolk
- Good vinegar or lemon juice
- Spinach juice (a few drops)

## Directions

1. Sweat onion slices cut into pieces with a piece of butter and a couple spoonfuls of flour on low coals.
2. Fill the casserole with good bouillon and add a piece of glace.
3. Cook the whole thing briefly and skim well.
4. Skim off the fat.
5. Finish the sauce with egg yolk, some good vinegar or lemon juice, and a few drops of spinach juice.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Homard à l'Américaine (Lobster American Style)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 deca fine oil or 6 deca butter
- 1 onion head
- 3 small carrots
- 1 liter white wine
- 1/2 liter bouillon
- Cayenne pepper
- Salt and pepper
- 1 living, cleaned lobster or langoustine
- 3 spoonfuls tomato purée
- 2 spoonfuls cognac
- 3 spoonfuls Madeira wine
- 1 spoonful (optional) meat glaze

## Directions

1. Put oil or butter in a shallow pot with finely cut onion head and carrots, let roast pale yellow
2. Pour with 1 liter white wine and 1/2 liter bouillon
3. Add a knife point of cayenne pepper and let reduce by half
4. Meanwhile, cut a living, cleaned lobster or langoustine into finger-wide pieces
5. Sprinkle with necessary salt and pepper
6. Half an hour before serving, add the cut lobster to the pot with the braised ingredients
7. Let it braise covered until the shell turns red
8. Add 3 spoonfuls tomato purée, 2 spoonfuls cognac, 3 spoonfuls Madeira wine and if desired 1 spoonful meat glaze
9. Let everything come to a boil once more covered
10. Arrange the lobster pieces on a deep dish and pour the sauce over them
11. Alternatively, remove the shells and pass the sauce through a sieve

title: Homemade French Wine from Raisins (Franzwein aus Rosinen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Raisins of the largest and best sort
- One pound of raisins per pitcher of soft water
- A small addition of sugar
- Tartar (Weinstein)

## Directions

1. Take raisins of the largest and best sort and tear them into small pieces, which is better than cutting them
2. For every pitcher of soft water, use one pound of such raisins
3. Place the raisins in a wine barrel and allow them to ferment on the ground
4. A small addition of sugar and tartar does no harm
5. Before fermentation is fully underway and even at its beginning, roll the barrel several times throughout the day or stir the mass
6. Once fermentation is finished, bring the barrel to the cellar
7. After filling it up, let it rest quietly until the wine is clear and bright
8. Then draw the wine into bottles and it can be drunk
9. This yields a spirited and lovely wine that is healthy and delightful, and cheaper, and indeed usually also better, than purchased French wine
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Homöopathischer Kaffee (Homeopathic Coffee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted ground coffee
- roasted pounded acorns
- cold water
- cream and sugar for serving

## Directions

1. For homeopathic coffee, take a mixture of roasted ground coffee and roasted pounded acorns
2. Steep 1 spoonful of this in 1/2 liter water covered, but only for a few minutes
3. Add some cold water and let the coffee stand at the side until the coffee has settled to the bottom
4. Pour the clear coffee into the pot, place this in hot water and serve the coffee with cream and sugar

title: Homöopathischer Kaffee (Homeopathic Coffee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted, ground coffee
- Roasted, crushed hazelnuts

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of roasted, ground coffee and roasted, crushed hazelnuts.
2. For 1 liter of water, use 1 tablespoon of this mixture, covered in boiling water for a few minutes only.
3. Add several spoonfuls of cold water and let the coffee settle on the side until the grounds have settled to the bottom.
4. Pour the clear coffee into the pot, place this in hot water, and serve the coffee with cream and sugar.
5. Figs, almonds, and chicory coffee are additions that can be added to ordinary coffee.

title: Honig oder Meth (Honey Mead)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Maß honey
- 8 Maß spring water
- Hops (0.5 pound per 100 pounds honey)
- optional: Mace, Cardamom
- optional: egg white
- pure yeast

## Directions

1. Best made on warm summer days.
2. Bring 8 Maß of spring water per 1 Maß of honey into a kettle and hang a sachet with hops (0.5 pound per 100 pounds honey) and, if desired, also with mace, cardamom and the like inside.
3. Simmer slowly over charcoal fire and skim diligently. If you add egg white before cooking, the impurities separate more easily.
4. Once all is skimmed, remove the fire from under the kettle and allow the mass to cool to the point that no egg can any longer be hard-boiled in it. The cooking should not last too long overall.
5. Test with a freshly laid egg: if it sinks so deep that only the tip is visible, the mead is correct; if it sinks completely under, there is still too much water, which must be evaporated; if it swims on top, there is too much honey and more water must be added.
6. When cooled, fill into a barrel with an addition of purest yeast, though some space must remain on top.
7. Once fermented, it remains for 3 months and is then drawn off and treated like wine.
8. Note: it is preferable to use the water left over from wax pressing instead of common water; one can also bring the honey cooked and skimmed with 4 to 8 parts water over gentle fire directly to barrels and let it ferment without addition, but add yeasts or some other fermentation agent to weak mead; and a well-prepared mead is said to equal the best and most delightful Spanish wines.
9. If it is sent far from its homeland and has not sufficiently fermented, it will enter into new fermentation in warmth and not rarely bursts the bottles as violently as Champagne wine.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hopfen Tascherlein (Hop Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- noodle dough
- flour
- 16 hectograms hop filling
- 1 egg
- salt
- eggs for brushing
- salted water
- butter-roasted bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare noodle dough and roll out very thin on a flour-dusted board
2. Mix 16 hectograms prepared hops with 1 egg and necessary salt
3. Distribute the hop mixture along the dough at 3-finger intervals in rows, leaving 3-finger-wide empty dough borders between rows
4. Brush the dough around the hop mounds with beaten eggs
5. Fold the empty dough over the filling, press gently, and cut half-moons between each pair of mounds using a pastry cutter
6. Place in boiling water and cook covered
7. After 8-10 minutes, remove with a slotted spoon, place in a pot with butter-roasted bread crumbs, stir well, and arrange on a plate

title: Hopfen-Sauce (Hop Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- hop stems
- salt water
- 6 tablespoons hot butter
- 6 tablespoons flour
- some finely cut green parsley
- 1 1/2 liters broth
- several spoonfuls cream

## Directions

1. In early spring when hops are not yet leafed, take the stems, cook them in salt water, drain them, press them out, and cut them finely
2. Put 6 tablespoons flour in 6 tablespoons hot butter, add some finely cut green parsley, let it roast somewhat
3. Pour it with 1 1/2 liters broth and several spoonfuls cream
4. Add the cooked and finely cut hops and let this boil thoroughly

title: Hopfensprossen (Hop Sprouts)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- hop sprouts
- water
- butter
- flour
- lean ham
- onions
- bouillon
- sugar
- egg yolk
- lemon juice
- cotelettes

## Directions

1. Clean the hop sprouts and cook them almost tender in water
2. Sweat a piece of butter and flour
3. Add a slice of lean ham and a couple of onions
4. Salt and fill with bouillon
5. When the hop sprouts that are already somewhat cooked have been washed properly white, put them in the sauce
6. Also add a small piece of sugar
7. Let them cook gently
8. At serving time, bind the sauce with egg yolk and some lemon juice
9. Remove the onions and the ham
10. Garnish them with cotelettes
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hopfentriebe Gemüse (Houblons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- erste Hopfentriebe
- Salzwasser
- Butter
- grüne Petersilie
- Zwiebel
- Zucker
- Salz
- Muskatnuss
- weiße Suppe

## Directions

1. die ersten Triebe werden sorgfältig gereinigt
2. sie werden in kochendem Salzwasser weich gekocht
3. sie werden abgeseiht und auf ein Sieb oder Tuch gelegt
4. wenn sie trocken sind, läßt man sie in Butter mit grüner Petersilie, wenn man will Zwiebel, etwas Zucker, Salz und Muskatnuss dünsten
5. dies wird mit einigen Löffeln weißer Suppe aufgegossen
6. alles wird gut untereinander gerührt und gehäuft auf einer Schüssel angerichtet

title: Horseradish in French Manner (French-Style Horseradish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and peeled horseradish
- 3 tablespoons hot broth
- 2 tablespoons oil
- salt
- vinegar
- pepper (if desired)
- finely cut chives (if desired)
- crushed paprika (if desired)

## Directions

1. Grate one spoonful of cleaned and peeled horseradish, place it in a bowl and pour 3 tablespoons of hot broth over it, set it aside and let it cool
2. Then stir the horseradish with 2 tablespoons oil, some salt and the necessary vinegar together well
3. When serving, add some pepper on top, if desired finely cut chives and some crushed paprika
4. This horseradish is also used for turnips, strudel and all fish dishes

title: Horseradish Sauce (Horseradish Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons flour
- 1-2 liters stock
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- 1/4 liter milk cream
- Several tablespoons grated horseradish
- 1 egg yolk
- 2 tablespoons cream

## Directions

1. To 8 tablespoons butter, let 8 tablespoons flour roast equally, pour it with about 1-2 liters stock
2. Add some salt, nutmeg, about 1/4 liter milk cream, and several tablespoons grated horseradish and let boil
3. Before serving, whisk 1 egg yolk with 2 tablespoons cream and stir it into the boiled milk-horseradish sauce

title: Hosel or Salt Cucumbers (Höfels oder Salz-Sühr­ten)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized green cucumbers
- horseradish leaves
- bay leaves
- cucumber herb
- salt
- cloves
- allspice
- bay leaves
- mustard seeds
- vinegar
- sweet and bitter mustard seeds
- cold salted water

## Directions

1. Take medium-sized green cucumbers, cut off both ends (capers), and make a small cut in the middle of each with a knife, then place in cold water.
2. Cover the bottom of a barrel, crock, or glass with horseradish leaves, grape leaves, and cucumber herb.
3. Sprinkle with salt, cloves, allspice berries, bay leaves, and some mustard seeds.
4. Add one or two layers of cucumbers and cover with leaves.
5. Continue this process until the vessel is nearly full.
6. Mix sweet and bitter mustard seeds and scatter over the cucumbers.
7. Fill the spaces with cold, salted water that should not be too sour.
8. Cover the cucumbers and weight down with a wooden plate so water covers them.
9. Store initially at a moderately warm place until fermentation begins.
10. To eat the cucumbers sooner, add some sourdough or a piece of black bread tied in a cloth.
11. When they smell sour, move them to a cool place and shake them occasionally.
12. If liquid evaporates, refill with water.
13. If cucumbers are not sour enough, add vinegar as desired.
14. Always ensure remaining cucumbers are covered with liquid when removing some.
15. Mustard seeds are added because they aid digestibility of the cucumbers.

title: Hospod (Layered Vegetable and Tongue Mold)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Kohlrabi, neatly cut
- Yellow turnips
- Cabbage
- Artichokes
- Skirret
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth
- Goose fat
- Thin bacon slices
- Boiled pickled tongues
- Ham, nicely cut

## Directions

1. Braise neatly cut kohlrabi, yellow turnips, cabbage, artichokes, and skirret each separately in a little butter until soft
2. Dust each with a little flour, add a small amount of broth, and shake well so they do not fall apart
3. Line the bottom of a mold brushed with goose fat with thin bacon slices
4. Layer the cabbage, then a layer of boiled pickled tongues cut into thin slices
5. Add another layer of braised kohlrabi, then another layer of tongue, then layers of yellow turnips, tongue, artichokes, again tongue, and finally skirret
6. Place the mold in a steam bath for a quarter hour
7. Turn it out onto a platter, line the edge with nicely cut ham
8. Remove the bacon from the top and serve the dish quickly to the table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hoſpot zu machen (To Make Hotpot)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Kohlrabi, neatly cut
- Yellow turnips
- Cabbage
- Artichokes
- Cherries
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth
- Goose fat
- Thin bacon slices
- Cooked pickled tongues, thinly cut

## Directions

1. Stew the neatly cut kohlrabi, yellow turnips, cabbage, artichokes and cherries separately in a little butter until soft
2. Dust each with a little flour, add a small amount of broth and shake well so they do not fall apart
3. Coat the bottom of a mold with goose fat and line with thin bacon slices
4. Add a layer of cabbage, then a layer of cooked pickled tongues cut into thin pieces
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Huhn oder Poulet als Galantine (Galantine of Chicken or Poulard)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken or pork forcemeat
- Veal
- Lard
- Bread soaked in milk
- Butter
- Eggs
- Egg yolk
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Pepper
- Flammeed poulard or old hen
- Cooked tongue
- Lard
- Small pickled gherkins
- Goose liver
- Breast meat

## Directions

1. Finely mince chicken or pork forcemeat, veal, and lard with a chef's knife
2. Place in mortar and finely pound together with bread soaked in milk and pressed dry, butter, whole eggs, and egg yolk
3. Pass through sieve and mix thoroughly with salt, nutmeg, and pepper
4. From one flammeed poulard or old hen of large size, cut off wings at the uppermost joint and remove legs
5. Cut skin from back to head, loosen the neck and gullet from the neck skin and remove both
6. Remove and discard the head, but keep the neck skin attached to the bird
7. Dampen thumb and forefinger with cold water and gently slide skin from the bones, using a sharp knife where necessary
8. Spread the unbroken skin on a dampened board
9. Cut cooked tongue into 6 strips thumb-thick and 2 fingers long, cut lard into similar strips, cut 6 small pickled gherkins, 1 small goose liver, and the removed breast meats into strips finger-wide and 2 fingers long
10. Spread the skin 2 fingers thick with the forcemeat mixture and lay the tongue, lard and other strips lengthwise with colors alternating and thumb-width of forcemeat between each strip
11. When first layer is filled, spread again 2 fingers thick with forcemeat and repeat the layer as before
12. Cover again with forcemeat, then draw up the skin on both sides, lifting slightly so filling distributes well and close the opening
13. Tie closed with strong thread the openings left from removing legs and wings so forcemeat does not escape, and sew shut the neck hole
14. Butter half a cloth, place the filled chicken on it and roll up like a sausage, very carefully so filling does not shift
15. Bind the cloth with twine at both ends of the chicken and in the middle over the chicken
16. In a large casserole, arrange vegetables and herbs cut into strips with about 5 grains of various spices and add about 4 liters of water and bring to a good boil
17. Place the bound chicken in this boiling water and cook covered for one hour
18. After this remove carefully with two slotted spoons, place on a board with cloth, cover with a second board and weight lightly
19. The next day remove weight and board, cut the twine, unroll the cloth, wipe off the galantine and brush with dissolved aspic glaze as desired
20. Once completely dry, cut the galantine into thick knife-back slices, arrange in a platter and serve

title: Husarenkrapferl (Hussar Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dkg flour
- 14 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 7 dkg sugar
- Jam for topping

## Directions

1. Make a crumbly dough from flour, butter, egg yolks, and sugar without letting it rest
2. Shape into a roll
3. Cut equal pieces
4. Form balls
5. Place on unbuttered sheet and indent the top
6. Bake at medium-high heat until light brown
7. Top with jam
8. Work quickly as the dough becomes stiff

title: Hygienisch Einwandfreien Sauermilch (Hygienically Sound Sour Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter good fresh milk
- Contents of one bottle of 'Vindobona' pure cultures for 'Sauermilch'

## Directions

1. Boil the fresh milk as usual and allow to cool to 30°C
2. Pour the contents of the 'Vindobona' pure cultures bottle for 'Sauermilch' into the milk at this temperature, stir well, and fill into bottles or pots
3. Let the vessels stand quietly near the stove (on the plate warmer) for 8 to 10 hours
4. After this time, the milk should already be thick and should then immediately be placed in cold water or an icebox
5. For further preparation, take one tablespoon of the finished sour milk per liter of fresh milk and proceed as initially described

title: Häringe en Roquelaure (Herrings en Roquelaure)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful herrings
- Herring kidney fat
- Bacon
- Shallots
- Parsley
- Lemon peel
- Egg yolks
- Grated bread
- Dried herbs
- Butter
- White paper

## Directions

1. Soak the herrings and separate them.
2. Remove all bones.
3. Chop the meat of some herrings very finely with some kidney fat.
4. Scrape bacon into the mixture.
5. Chop shallots, parsley, and lemon peel very finely.
6. Mix everything together with a couple of egg yolks, grated bread, and dried herbs.
7. Pound together well in a mortar.
8. Spread the farce onto the side of each herring where the bones were removed, so it looks like a whole herring again (the head must be cut off).
9. Cut white strong paper into approximately the shape of the herring, only slightly longer and wider.
10. Spread butter on the paper, place the herring on it, and wrap the edges firmly around it.
11. Fry on a grill for about a quarter hour over brisk heat.
12. Turn very frequently.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Häringsalat mit Erdäpfeln (Herring Salad with Potatoes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Herring
- Potatoes
- Onions
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Clean a herring, remove the bones and cut the remaining part into small dice
2. Mix it with cleaned and cut potatoes and onions
3. Place on a serving dish
4. Dress with vinegar, oil and sugar
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Häschee-Wandeln (Minced Meat Pastries)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted, finely chopped veal
- butter
- pepper
- lemon peel
- lemon juice
- 2 egg yolks
- butter pastry dough
- egg white

## Directions

1. In a pan or casserole, put roasted, finely chopped veal with a piece of butter, some pepper and lemon peel, let it stew down
2. Add lemon juice and 2 egg yolks, stir well together, and let it cool on a plate
3. Make a butter pastry dough, roll it out, cut out small pieces with a small pastry cutter
4. Put one piece on a board, fill a teaspoon of minced meat into it, place another piece on top, press them together slightly, and continue until all are done
5. Brush with egg white, place in a pie pan, and bake until cool
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Häuptel's Salat (Lettuce Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Häuptel (lettuce)
- Essig
- Öl
- Schnittlauch
- hartgekochte Eier

## Directions

1. Man entfernt die äußeren Blätter, schneidet die gelben Blätter bei den Rippen voneinander, schneidet die Häuptel in vier Teile, wäscht den Salat gut aus, gibt ihn in ein Tuch, und, indem man die vier Enden desselben zusammennimmt, wird der Salat ausgeschleudert. Wenn das Tuch das Wasser gut eingezogen hat, wird der Salat kurz vor dem Anrichten nach Bedarf gesalzen, mit Essig und Öl gut untereinandergemischt und nach Belieben mit fein geschnittenem Schnittlauch bestreut. Hartgekochte Eier, in vier bis sechs Teile geteilt, werden auf den angerichteten Salat gelegt.

title: Hühner am Rost fleüraten (Chickens Roasted on a Spit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Plucked, cleaned chickens
- Butter
- Bound sauce
- Breadcrumbs
- Eggs
- Lemon slices
- Ragout or other garnish (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the chickens lengthwise down the middle, remove the breast and back meat, cut the legs or thighs below the knee, push the meat of the thighs back slightly, and tuck the wings under the thighs.
2. Flatten the chickens with a meat pounder, salt them, place in a casserole brushed with butter, cover with butter and roast for 15-20 minutes over medium heat.
3. Remove from heat, let them cool in the butter, then place in a bowl.
4. Brush the now completely cooled chicken pieces on both sides with bound sauce, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, dip in the butter in which the chickens were roasted, and coat again with breadcrumbs.
5. Place a paper brushed with butter on a roasting rack, arrange the chicken pieces side by side on it, brown on both sides, then cut each piece in half, arrange these pieces in a circular pattern in a bowl, place desired ragout or similar garnish in the center, and garnish the rim of the bowl with lemon slices.
6. The chickens can alternatively be roasted in a shallow casserole with some fat instead of on a spit.

title: Hühner Fasch (Chicken Farce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- raw breast of a large hen or poularde
- 8 deca bread rolls, softened in milk
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg yolks
- some salt
- 8 deca butter

## Directions

1. Remove the raw breast from a large hen or poularde and remove the skin
2. Stir 8 deca bread rolls softened in milk over the fire until they resemble a dough and let cool
3. Pound this mass together with the chicken breast, 1 whole egg, 2 egg yolks, some salt, and 8 deca butter and pass through a sieve
4. Form into quenelles, dumplings, or snail-shaped mounds
5. Using two coffee spoons dipped in hot water, scoop the quenelles into a buttered shallow casserole, not too close together
6. Pour boiling water over them so it covers them
7. Cover and let simmer, then remove with a slotted spoon and serve with brown soup

title: Hühner im eigenen Saft gedämpft (Chickens Braised in Their Own Juice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 plucked, salted chickens
- 4 tablespoons butter wrapped with 5 sprigs of parsley
- Bacon slices
- Soup
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. After inserting 4 tablespoons of butter wrapped with parsley sprigs into each chicken, dress them and sprinkle with lemon juice.
2. Line a casserole with bacon slices, place the chickens in it, cover with bacon slices and let them braise covered slowly.
3. Once the chickens' own juice has evaporated, pour some soup over them and braise until they are soft.
4. Remove the chickens, cut the twine, pull it out, cut the chickens into desired pieces, arrange them in a circular pattern in a bowl, pour the defatted cooking juice over them, and place ragout, braised mushrooms, truffles, or braised rice in the center of the bowl.

title: Hühner in einer Blutſauce (Chicken in Blood Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Vinegar
- Chicken blood
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bacon
- Onion
- Cloves
- Whole pepper
- Allspice
- Flour
- Broth
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. When the chickens are slaughtered, catch the blood in a pot with some vinegar, stir it with the vinegar so it does not coagulate.
2. When the chickens are cleaned, salt them lightly and dress them nicely, arrange some bacon leaves in a casserole, cut an onion in half in the middle, lard each part with three or four cloves, add some whole pepper and a few grains of allspice, add the chickens, and let them stew without browning.
3. Remove them, dust two spoonfuls of flour on them, let it become brownish, fill with broth, add some lemon juice, and let it cook well together.
4. Strain it through a sieve, add the chickens, and let them cook until tender.
5. Then mix the blood well with the sauce and let it cook together for only a few minutes.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hühner in einer Kapernsoße (Chicken in Caper Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- butter
- flour
- onion
- broth
- white vinegar
- cream
- capers
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Dress the chickens nicely and blanch them
2. Warm a piece of butter, add two spoonfuls of flour, let it brown slightly
3. Add a whole onion and fill with broth
4. Let the sauce reduce and strain it through a sieve
5. Place the chickens in the sauce
6. Add one spoonful of good white vinegar, three spoonfuls of cream, a handful of capers, and finely cut lemon peel
7. Let it simmer until the chickens are completely cooked
8. Arrange the chickens on a dish and pour the sauce over
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hühner in einer Paradeisäpfelsoße (Chicken in Tomato Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- butter
- flour
- tomatoes
- broth
- onion
- cream

## Directions

1. Clean the chickens and cut into usual pieces
2. Braise with butter
3. Dust with a little flour
4. Add strained tomatoes that have been previously well cooked with broth and a whole onion
5. Let the chickens cook until soft enough
6. At the end add one spoonful of good cream
7. Arrange nicely on a dish and pour the sauce over
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hühner in einer Pomeranzensoße (Chicken in Orange Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three small meaty chickens
- lard
- butter
- flour
- brown broth
- half pomerance
- half lemon
- sugar
- three or four cloves
- whole sweet pomerance

## Directions

1. Clean, dress, and lard the chickens with finely cut lard
2. Roast them, but not too much, just until they get a nice color
3. In a casserole or pan, make a nice golden-brown roux with butter and flour
4. Fill with brown broth
5. Add the peel and juice from half a pomerance and half a lemon
6. Add a piece of sugar and three or four cloves
7. After the sauce has simmered a while, strain it through a sieve
8. Place the chickens in it and let them boil a bit longer
9. Before serving, take a whole sweet pomerance, remove the peel, cut into twelve pieces
10. Garnish the chickens with the peel pieces and pour the sauce (after carefully removing the fat) over
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hühner mit grünen Erbsen und Krebsschwanzstücken (Chicken with Green Peas and Crayfish Tails)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- small chickens
- butter
- green peas, one Seidel
- green parsley
- crayfish tails, shelled

## Directions

1. Blanch small chickens and cut them into four pieces
2. Make a butter sauce and lay the chickens in it
3. Braise one Seidel of green peas with butter and green parsley until soft
4. Add the peas to the chickens along with the shelled crayfish tails
5. Let it cook for another quarter hour
6. To serve, place the chickens in the center of the dish, arrange the peas like a ring around the edge, and garnish with crayfish tails such that the peas remain two fingers' width visible
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hühner mit grünen Erbſen und Krebsſchweifchen (Chicken with Green Peas and Crayfish Tails)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Small chickens
- Butter
- Butter sauce
- Green peas
- Green parsley
- Crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Blanch small chickens and cut them into four parts.
2. Make a butter sauce, add the chickens to it.
3. Sweat a pint of green peas with butter and green parsley until soft.
4. Add them to the chickens, also add shelled crayfish tails, and let it cook for another quarter hour.
5. When serving, place the chickens in the middle of the dish, arrange the peas like a ring around the edge, garnish with crayfish tails so that the peas always remain visible two fingers wide.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hühner mit Hirsebrei gefüllt (Chicken Stuffed with Millet Porridge)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/16 liter millet
- 1/4 liter soup
- Salt
- Pepper
- 8 tablespoons finely diced meat
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Prepare the millet porridge as follows: for 2 chickens, measure 1/16 liter of millet into a casserole, add 1/4 liter of soup, season with salt and pepper to taste, and cook slowly until the millet forms a thick porridge.
2. Add 8 tablespoons of finely diced meat, mix well and set aside.
3. When the mixture has cooled, beat in 2 egg yolks, mix well and fill the chickens with this mixture.
4. Prepare the chickens as described for the recipe reference given.

title: Hühner mit Krebsbutter-Farcierung (Chicken with Crayfish Butter Stuffing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chicken livers, roasted beforehand in butter
- 10 pieces shelled crayfish tails and claws, finely cut
- 12 tablespoons crayfish butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 2 pieces mushrooms or morels, braised in butter
- 8 tablespoons dried figs, soaked in water, well pressed out
- Salt
- Spices

## Directions

1. Finely cut the roasted chicken livers with the shelled crayfish tails and claws.
2. Beat the crayfish butter until fluffy and gradually add the whole eggs and egg yolk.
3. Mix in the cut livers and crayfish, the braised mushrooms or morels, and the soaked figs, season to taste with salt and spices.
4. Mix everything well together, fill the chickens with this mixture, and prepare further as described for filled roasted chickens.

title: Hühner mit Mandelstüge (Chicken with Almond Stuffing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 yolks of butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 pinch of nutmeg
- 10 dried figs, soaked in water, well pressed out, minced
- 8 tablespoons shelled almonds, finely blanched with some milk
- 8 tablespoons shelled almonds, finely cut into sticks
- 4 tablespoons finely flaked or oblong cut citron
- 4 tablespoons small raisins (currants)
- 4 tablespoons raisins
- 1 strong pinch of fresh parsley
- 4 tablespoons sugar (to taste)

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy, gradually add the whole eggs and egg yolk.
2. Mix in the finely minced figs, the blanched almonds, the almond sticks, citron, raisins, currants, parsley and sugar to taste.
3. Mix everything well together and fill the chickens with this mixture.
4. Prepare further as described for roasted chickens (No. 818).

title: Hühner mit Mayonnaise, Salat und hartgekochten Eiern (Chicken with Mayonnaise, Salad and Hard-Boiled Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Chervil leaves
- Lettuce heads
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Mayonnaise
- Aspic
- Salt
- Spices

## Directions

1. Prepare chicken as for 'Im eigenem Safte gedämpfte' (reference 822)
2. After steaming and cooling in its broth, remove and portion: cut both legs from the backbone, remove breasts from both sides without breaking the ribs, separate the spine from the thigh piece, cut remaining meat into pieces
3. Place meat in a deep bowl, dress with vinegar and one spoon of oil, sprinkle with finely cut chervil leaves, cover and let sit for several hours
4. Clean lettuce heads by removing large green outer leaves, cutting larger yellow leaves leaving only the heart, cutting hearts into sections and washing in spoonfuls with water
5. Remove lettuce, press out water well, place in a bowl, dress with vinegar and oil to taste, salt and season, mix well
6. Line the bottom of a deep platter with cut lettuce
7. Arrange meat pieces on top in layers, brushing each layer with mayonnaise, arranging breasts on top
8. Cover entire dish with mayonnaise
9. Cut hard-boiled eggs each into 4 pieces, trim one side and arrange alternately with lettuce hearts around the edge
10. Garnish the whole dish with neatly cut and chopped aspic and serve mayonnaise in a sauce boat

title: Hühner mit Milchrahm und Lemonienpalten (Chicken with Cream and Lemon Slices)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- two small chickens
- good cream
- lemon
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the chickens well, wash several times with warm water, salt them
2. Cut into usual pieces and let sit in salt for a quarter hour
3. Put good cream in a casserole
4. Cut a lemon into thin slices and carefully remove the pits
5. Put half the lemon slices in the cream, then half of the chicken pieces
6. Continue layering: cream, remaining lemon slices, remaining chicken
7. Top with cream and three to four Loth of butter in small pieces
8. Note: shake the dish rather than stir it, and do not cook longer than until done, or the chickens will lose their appearance and good taste
9. When serving, arrange the chickens on a dish, place the lemon slices on top, and pour the sauce over
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Hühner mit Nudeln (Chicken with Vermicelli)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickens
- Vermicelli
- Salt water

## Directions

1. Prepare the chickens as described for 'Chickens Braised in Their Own Juice' (No. 822).
2. Cook the vermicelli in salt water, then drain.
3. Mix the cooked vermicelli well with the juice from the chickens and serve with the chickens arranged in a circular pattern on a platter.

title: Hühner mit Sardellen (Poulets braises aux anchois)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and prepared chickens
- finely cut sardelles (anchovies)
- lard
- onions
- root vegetables
- whole spices
- soup

## Directions

1. After cleaning and preparing chickens, spit them with finely cut sardelles instead of bacon
2. In a casserole, place lard, onions, root vegetables and whole spices
3. Lay the chickens on top and braise covered
4. When the liquid that forms has evaporated, pour some soup over them
5. Braise the chickens until tender
6. Remove them, cut into desired pieces, arrange on a platter
7. Serve with sardelle sauce

title: Hühnerbrust mit Ragout (Chicken Breast with Ragout)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Butter
- Truffles
- Prepared tongue
- Mushrooms (Champignons)
- Cock's combs
- Cock's kidneys
- Marrow knuckles
- Calf's sweetbread (larded and cut into neat pieces)
- Calf's brain
- Calf's head (white and soft-boiled, pieces cut out decoratively)
- Calf or veal bones
- Olives (dissolved from jars)

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken up to the breast, separate the throat and crop with a cloth and pull it out.
2. Cut the skin over the breast, carefully remove it, bend the thighs apart, and loosen the meat of the breast from the breastbone and skeleton with a sharp small knife.
3. From each chicken, two breasts are obtained, each of which has an underbreast attached by a strong tendon.
4. Carefully pull out this tendon, smooth the small breasts with a knife, and place them on a plate.
5. Pound the large breasts with a flat knife and scrape off the fine skin with a sharp knife.
6. Butter a casserole heavily and lay the large and underbreasts next to one another.
7. Cut prepared truffles and cooked tongue into small pieces, cut them out round, fix these with some glaze on the wider side of the breast, and cover with a sheet of paper brushed with butter and fry briefly on one side only over medium heat for a few minutes.
8. When the breasts have a white color, remove them and arrange them in a bowl, the large ones alternately with the small ones, running round.
9. In the middle of the bowl place the following ragout: prepared mushrooms, cock's combs, cock's kidneys, marrow knuckles, calf's sweetbread (larded and cut into neat pieces), truffles, crayfish tails, calf's brain, and decoratively cut-out pieces from a white and soft-boiled calf's head.
10. Mix all this well, place it arranged beautifully in the middle of the bowl, and push the better-looking pieces to the outside.
11. If desired, sprinkle the sauce with pitted olives dissolved from jars.
12. The remaining chicken meat is finely pounded together with the bones and a gravy sauce is prepared from it, some of which is poured over the arranged bowl and the rest is served in a sauceboat.

title: Hühnerbrust mit Sauce (Chicken Breast with Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Poultry
- Juniper berries
- Lard
- Wine
- Broth
- Black bread
- Olives
- Cream
- Lemon juice
- Salt

## Directions

1. Lay the dressed and browned poultry on juniper berries
2. Cover with lard and braise covered briefly
3. Pour wine and broth over so the liquid covers the poultry
4. Add black bread cut into strips and braise until tender
5. Remove the poultry and degrease
6. Pass the sauce through a sieve
7. Add peeled olives and cream to the sauce
8. Adjust seasoning with lemon juice if needed
9. Serve the sauce with the poultry

title: Hühnerconsommé (Chicken Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Das Fleisch eines gebratenen Huhns
- 0,5 Liter Rindfleischsuppe
- 3 Eigelb
- 4 ganze Eier
- Salz
- fein geschnittene Petersilie
- Butter
- Mehl

## Directions

1. Das Fleisch eines gebratenen Huhns wird abgelöst und sammt den Beinen im Mörser gestoßen.
2. Das Gestoßene wird in eine Casserolle gegeben und mit 0,5 Liter Rindfleischsuppe aufgegossen.
3. 1,5 Stunden kochen lassen und dann durch ein Sieb gießen.
4. Inzwischen werden in einen Topf 3 Eigelb, 4 ganze Eier, etwas Salz und fein geschnittene Petersilie gegeben und gut untereinander gerührt.
5. Die abgeseihtе Suppe wird dazugegossen und sprudelt sie ab.
6. Es wird in eine mit Butter ausgeschmierte, mit Mehl gestaübte Form gegeben und 1 Stunde im Punkt kochen lassen.
7. Hierauf wird es herausgestürzt, in beliebige Stücke geschnitten und diese in die in dem Suppentopf befindliche heiße Suppe gegeben.

title: Hühnerragout (Chicken Ragout)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Calf's sweetbread
- Prepared mushrooms
- Cock's combs
- Cock's kidneys
- Truffles
- Calf's head (white and soft-boiled, cut into neat pieces)
- Sauce (bound)

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken the same way as for chicken with butter sauce or stewed chicken.
2. When it is tender-braised, cut it into any desired pieces and arrange these running round in a bowl or pastry nest.
3. Meanwhile, braise a calf's sweetbread, cut it with the meat into any desired pieces; prepare 10 pieces of prepared mushrooms, the same number of cock's combs, cock's kidneys, truffles, and a calf's head white and soft-boiled in salt water, cut into neat pieces.
4. Arrange all this beautifully in the middle of the chicken arranged running round in the bowl and pour over with some bound sauce; serve the remaining sauce in a sauceboat.
5. It must be served very hot.
6. The calf's sweetbread, which has been rinsed several times in lukewarm water, can if desired be braised at the same time as the chicken.

title: Hühnerragout (Chicken Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 roasting chicken
- 2 large mushrooms
- 1 small head cauliflower
- fat
- 5 dkg butter
- parsley
- flour
- soup
- salt
- optionally: peas, asparagus heads, sweetbreads, cock combs, goose liver, tongue

## Directions

1. Place the chicken prepared for roasting in a pan with some fat and roast it in the oven on both sides for 30 minutes.
2. Divide it into 8 pieces, optionally remove the skin.
3. Cook the stomach and liver separately for 1 hour.
4. Cook mushrooms and cauliflower beforehand for 20 minutes.
5. Make a butter mixture of 5 dkg butter, parsley, and flour.
6. Dilute with the soup in which the stomach was cooked or with the cauliflower soup.
7. Add the chicken and salt.
8. Cook well for 20 minutes.
9. Cut the stomach and liver into cubes, add cut mushrooms and cauliflower, but do not stir too much anymore.
10. Serve on a round dish. Optional garnishes: cooked young green peas, asparagus heads cooked separately, blanched cut sweetbreads, cooked cock combs, fried goose liver, and finely cut tongue can be mixed in.

title: Hühnersextrakt für Rekonvalescentеn (Chicken Extract for Convalescents)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 alte Hühner
- 1 Zwiebel

## Directions

1. 4 alte Hühner werden in Stücke zerschnitten, mit einer Zwiebel in einen irdenen Topf getan, welcher luftdicht verschlossen wird, um jede Absonderung des Dampfes zu verhindern.
2. Hierauf stellt man den Topf 3/4 Drittel in einen Kessel mit kochendem Wasser und läßt dies 5 Stunden lang kochen.
3. Das verdampfende Wasser wird durch siedend heißes ersetzt.
4. Nach der angegebenen Zeit wird der Topf herausgehoben, sorgfältig aufgemacht und der dorin befindliche Extrakt durch ein reines Tuch gеseiht.
5. 4 Hühner können ungefähr 0,5 Liter Extrakt geben.
6. Das Fett wird rein abgenommen und der Geschmack des Extraktes mit etwas wenig Salz gehoben.

title: Im Tücherte Polenta (Polenta Cooked in Cloth)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams flour
- 1/2 liter cream
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- salt
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Put 16 decagrams flour in a deep bowl
2. Gradually add 1/2 liter cream and stir well
3. Add 4 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs and salt, and stir
4. Butter a white, dense cloth (serviette) and sprinkle it with flour, dust the cloth somewhat
5. Hang it in a deep bowl and put the dough mixture on it
6. Tie the cloth tightly above the dough
7. Put it in boiling water and cook for 3/4 hour
8. Remove the polenta from the cloth and arrange on a dish
9. Pour hot butter over it
10. Serve with beef, roast or game
11. Can be served whole or in pieces

title: Imitierte Kastanien (Imitation Chestnuts)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca blanched, finely pounded almonds
- 16 deca fine sifted sugar
- Small pieces of pineapple, citron, nuts, chocolate, or pitted cherries
- Hot chocolate glaze
- Egg white
- Fine pounded sugar
- Spun sugar
- Small paper cases

## Directions

1. Pound 16 deca blanched, finely pounded almonds with 16 deca fine sifted sugar until a dough forms
2. Form into nut-sized knobs, placing a small piece of pineapple, citron, nut, chocolate, or pitted cherry in the center of each
3. Place on paper sprinkled with fine pounded sugar
4. Hold each knob with a decoration needle and dip into hot chocolate glaze only as far as a small spot the size of half a cross remains dry
5. Place on a sugar-sprinkled paper and dry
6. Alternative preparation: Dip the knob in egg white, roll in fine pounded sugar, and hold over a brightly burning spirit flame for several minutes to achieve the appearance of a roasted chestnut
7. Third method: Dip in spun sugar and place in small paper cases

title: Imstetten's Torte in the English Style (Tourte de pâte brisée à l'anglaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 Deka beaten butter
- 10 Deka caster sugar
- 4 hard-boiled and passed egg yolks
- 48 Deka flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter with caster sugar and hard-boiled and passed egg yolks for 1/4 hour
2. Mix this well with flour
3. Shape the dough into a loaf on a flour-dusted board and cover it
4. Let it rest for 1/2 hour in a cold place
5. Roll out the dough on a flour-dusted board and proceed further as indicated in the recipe for 'brown pâte brisée torte' (no. 1482)

title: Imstetten's Torte in the French Style (Tourte de pâte brisée à la française)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 48 Deka beaten butter
- 16 Deka caster sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 60 Deka flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter with caster sugar and egg yolks for 1/4 hour
2. Add flour and mix
3. Shape the dough into a loaf on a flour-dusted board and cover it
4. Let it rest in a cool place for 1/2 hour
5. Roll out the dough to finger thickness
6. Cut out 3 round discs and place them in torte forms brushed with butter and lined with flour
7. Bake at low heat for approximately 1 hour
8. Once removed and completely cooled, stack them
9. Brush between layers with jam and cover the whole with a glaze

title: In Salzwasser gekochter Barsch (Perch Cooked in Salt Water)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Perch
- Salt water (boiling)
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as the blue-cooked salmon
2. Instead of vinegar, pour only boiling salt water over it
3. Serve with Hollandaise, Mustard, or Butter sauce with lemon juice

title: Indian als einen Igel (Guinea Fowl as a Hedgehog)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- guinea fowl
- ragout made from various preferred ingredients
- small spits of boxwood or other wood
- fat livers
- veal sweetbread
- ham
- egg white
- breadcrumbs
- lard

## Directions

1. After the guinea fowl is gutted, split the breast from one end to the other
2. Remove the breastbone
3. Fill it with a good ragout made from various preferred ingredients
4. Sew it closed again
5. Cook it in a common braise until done
6. Once this is done, cut small spits from boxwood or other wood
7. Stud them with pieces of fat livers, veal sweetbread, ham and similar items
8. Dip these in egg white
9. Sprinkle them with breadcrumbs
10. Fry them in lard
11. Place the guinea fowl with the breast facing down in the serving dish
12. Leave the neck attached, which serves as a tail
13. Cover the back with the spits, of which you must have enough so the guinea fowl is sufficiently covered
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Indian, gebraten (Roasted Turkey)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey (Indian)
- salt
- bacon for larding
- semmel (bread roll) soaked in milk
- butter for basting

## Directions

1. Clean the turkey well and cut it open
2. Remove the breastbone, wash it thoroughly several times, and salt it well
3. Lard the breast neatly with bacon
4. Place a milk-soaked semmel in the head
5. Roast it over steady heat
6. If the turkey is large, it will take nearly two hours to roast; during roasting baste it with butter
7. Serve with the drippings from roasting
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Indianer Krapfen (Indian Crullers)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg whites
- 32 deka fine powdered sugar
- unsalted lard for the baking sheet
- fine powdered sugar for sprinkling
- 18 deka flour
- whipped cream
- vanilla sugar
- chocolate or preserve glaze

## Directions

1. Mix 8 egg whites lightly with 32 deka fine powdered sugar
2. Using a piping bag, pipe the mixture onto a baking sheet lined with unsalted lard in elongated heaps of desired size
3. Sprinkle with fine powdered sugar and dry in a moderately heated oven
4. When a biscuit made from 18 deka flour and baked knife-back thick has cooled completely, cut it into round finger-thick slices using a grapefruit corer
5. From the completely dry and cold Spanish biscuit, cut out the center of the base and hollow it out nearly to the tip with a small knife, being careful as it breaks easily
6. Once all are hollowed out, fill them with whipped cream mixed with vanilla sugar, place them on the aforementioned biscuit slices
7. Glaze with chocolate or preserve glaze by placing a spoonful on a wooden spoon and drizzling it spoonwise with hot chocolate glaze over a saucepan so the drippings can be caught and reused
8. Serve the crullers as soon as they are dry

title: Indianer mit Brei gefüllt (Turkey Filled with Grain)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey
- Grain stuffing (double the amount used for chicken)

## Directions

1. The preparation is the same as in recipe 823 ('Hühner mit Brei gefüllt'), with the difference that for a turkey you must make double the amount of stuffing used for chicken.

title: Indianer mit Krebsbutter Farce (Turkey with Crayfish Butter Stuffing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey
- Crayfish butter stuffing (double the amount used for chicken)

## Directions

1. The treatment is the same as specified under recipe 821 ('Hühner mit Krebsbutter Farce'), with the difference that for a turkey you must make double the amount of stuffing.

title: Indianer mit Mandelfülle (Turkey with Almond Filling)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey
- Almond filling (double the amount used for chicken)

## Directions

1. The preparation is the same as in recipe 820 ('Hühner mit Mandelfülle'), with the difference that for a turkey you must make double the amount as specified for chicken.

title: Indianer mit Schinken Farce (Turkey with Ham Stuffing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca butter
- 8 deca not completely white braised ham
- 16 deca finely cut and salted ham
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/8 liter cream
- Salt to taste
- 1 knife point of pepper

## Directions

1. Beat 8 deca butter until fluffy.
2. Add 8 deca not completely white braised ham, 16 deca finely cut and salted ham, 2 whole eggs, 1 egg yolk, 1/8 liter cream, necessary salt, and 1 knife point of pepper.
3. Stir everything well together.
4. Fill the cleaned, prepared, and salted turkey with this mixture.
5. Follow the same further preparation as described for 'gefüllter Indianer' (stuffed turkey).

title: Indianer mit Truthahnleber Farce (Turkey with Foie Gras Stuffing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned turkey
- Liver stuffing
- Truffles, sliced into leaves

## Directions

1. Except that the turkey is filled with liver stuffing instead of bread stuffing, the treatment is otherwise exactly the same as described for 'gefüllter Indianer' (stuffed turkey).
2. Thinly sliced truffles are added.

title: Indianer mit Trüffeln gefüllt (Truffle-Stuffed Turkey)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small turkey
- 1 1/2 kilo truffles
- 1 kilo truffles (cut into strips)
- 1/4 kilo truffles
- 1/4 kilo lard
- Fine herbs
- Shallots
- Thyme
- Salt
- Paper
- Lard plates

## Directions

1. For 1 small turkey, take 1 1/2 kilo truffles.
2. Clean them and cut them finely; cut 1 kilo of the truffles into strips.
3. Finely chop the remaining 1/4 kilo truffles and 1/4 kilo lard, place in a saucepan, add finely cut herbs, shallots, some thyme, and necessary salt.
4. Pluck a young, fat turkey immediately after bleeding, carefully remove the entrails, and fold the breast bone on both sides.
5. Truss the turkey and clean it inside and outside with a cloth.
6. Cut the turkey liver finely, add it to the truffles in the saucepan, and roast for 10-15 minutes.
7. Place the roasted mixture in a deep bowl, mix in the truffle strips cut into pieces, and let cool.
8. When cooled, fill the still warm turkey with this mixture, especially the crop, to give it a more beautiful appearance.
9. Sew the opening shut and wrap the turkey in lard plates and paper.
10. Let it rest for 5-6, or at most 8 days in a cool place, or it can be roasted the same day with the truffles, but the turkey must be at a cooler temperature.
11. After this time, remove it, take off paper and lard, salt the turkey, truss it, bind it with inserted lard, and roast on the spit or in the oven for approximately 2 hours.
12. If you want the turkey roasted white, wrap it in paper during roasting; if you want it golden, remove the paper 1/3 of an hour before finishing and roast it at a brighter fire.
13. Turkey with truffles is usually prepared in winter.

title: Indianerbäckerei (Indian Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dkg flour
- 7 dkg butter
- 7 dkg raw grated almonds
- 5 dkg grated chocolate
- 2 egg yolks
- Jam for filling
- 1 egg white
- Pearl sugar

## Directions

1. On the board, make a dough from flour, butter, raw grated almonds, grated chocolate, and egg yolks
2. After resting, roll out and cut shapes as desired
3. Place on unbuttered sheet and bake
4. Alternative: cut rings, brush with jam, sandwich together, brush with egg white, and roll in pearl sugar
5. Can be used as Christmas pastries

title: Indianerflügel (Turkey Wings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey wings
- Ground turkey meat
- Broth
- Household broth or preserves

## Directions

1. After plucking and cleaning the wings, they can be cooked with the ground turkey meat in the broth and then, cut into neat pieces, served in the broth.
2. Alternatively, the softly cooked, neatly cut wings can be served in household broth or preserves.

title: Indianerkrapfen (Indian Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 8 dkg sugar
- 4 egg whites
- 8 dkg flour
- 10 dkg chocolate
- 20 dkg cube sugar
- Water as needed
- Sweetened whipped cream

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg yolks with 8 dkg sugar for 15 minutes
2. Gently fold in 4 beaten egg whites with 8 dkg flour
3. Fill buttered, floured Indian puff molds
4. Bake first a few minutes in open oven, then in closed oven until light brown
5. Turn out and cool
6. Hollow out from the flat side
7. Place with rounded side up for glazing
8. Coat with chocolate icing made from 10 dkg chocolate and 20 dkg cube sugar with water
9. Place sweetened whipped cream between 2 puffs

title: Indianertorte (Indian Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 10 dkg butter
- 1 whole egg
- 7 dkg sugar
- 5 dkg grated chocolate
- 2 egg yolks
- 7 dkg sugar
- 1 spoon water or milk
- 5 dkg grated chocolate
- 8 dkg chopped butter
- pralines

## Directions

1. Make sponge cake from egg yolks
2. Cut through twice
3. Fill with either cold or warm whipped cream
4. Glaze brown on top and decorate with pralines
5. For cold filling: Beat 10 dkg butter, stir in 1 whole egg, 7 dkg sugar, 5 dkg grated chocolate
6. For warm filling: Beat 2 egg yolks, 7 dkg sugar, 1 spoon water or milk over steam until thick, cool, stir in 5 dkg grated chocolate and 8 dkg chopped butter, can make more cream to coat outside and sprinkle with swollen chopped almonds

title: Indiauer Krapfen (Indian Crullers - Alternative Method)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg whites
- 16 deka sugar
- 10 deka egg butter
- 24 deka flour
- thin warm vanilla marmalade
- chocolate glaze
- whipped cream mixed with sugar or cream

## Directions

1. Add 16 deka sugar, 10 deka egg butter, and 24 deka flour to stiff snow from 10 egg whites, stir lightly and quickly
2. Using a piping bag, pipe uniform large heaps onto a baking sheet lined with white paper
3. Bake in the oven at low heat slowly for 1 hour until golden
4. Remove from the sheet and hollow them out with a fine knife on the underside
5. Join every two together, brush them with a brush using thin warm vanilla marmalade, and cover with chocolate glaze
6. Only when you use them are they taken apart on the side and filled with whipped cream mixed with sugar or cream and served

title: Italianisches Rindflleisch (Braised Beef à l'Italienne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- A beef rump of 3 kilos
- Pencil-thick, finger-long pieces of lard dipped in spices
- Lard
- Onion
- Root vegetables
- Spices
- Soup
- Cream
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Beat and fold the beef and insert pencil-thick, finger-long pieces of lard that have been dipped in spices
2. In a casserole, place lard, onion, root vegetables and spices, lay the meat on top, pour soup over it and braise for about 3 hours
3. Transfer the meat to another casserole, strain the defatted sauce over it, pour cream over it, sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and braise in the oven until it develops a brown crust
4. Serve with macaroni or other thinned flour preparations or pickled cucumbers (salt or mustard cucumbers) or olive sauce

title: Italienische Maccaroni (Italian Macaroni)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound macaroni
- 7 pounds butter
- 7 pounds Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Cook macaroni in salted water until soft
2. Cool in cold water and drain well
3. Place in a pan and add 7 pounds butter and 7 pounds Parmesan cheese per pound of macaroni
4. Proceed as with rice
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Italienische Polenta (Italian Polenta)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 halves salted water
- polenta or corn meal (similar to semolina but yellowish)
- butter
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Heat 3 halves salted water in a pan until it boils
2. Stir in polenta or corn meal until a very thick dough forms
3. Work the dough with a spoon as finely as possible
4. Shape into a round ball on a board, wrap in a napkin, and let sit for one hour
5. Cut into slices with a thick string or knife back
6. Thickly butter a cassole and sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese
7. Layer slices of polenta alternately with grated Parmesan cheese and butter pieces until all is used, with the last layer covered with cheese
8. Bake in strong heat for one hour
