9. When serving, drain off the fat, turn out after loosening the edges with a knife, and serve
10. The polenta should have a crisp crust and light brown appearance
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Italienische Reisknödel (Italian Rice Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds rice
- good bouillon
- 0.5 pounds beef marrow
- 0.5 pounds breadcrumbs
- butter for roasting
- 8 whole eggs
- 12 loth grated Parmesan cheese
- nutmeg
- salt
- butter for assembling
- celery and parsley roots (optional)

## Directions

1. Blanch the rice in water
2. Cook the rice until soft and brief in good bouillon with the beef marrow
3. Toast the breadcrumbs from grated rolls in butter
4. Stir the toasted crumbs, 8 whole eggs, 12 loth grated Parmesan cheese, and nutmeg into the rice
5. Test the mixture for sufficient salt
6. Butter a napkin and divide the mixture into three parts
7. Bind each dumpling separately with string
8. Cook for one hour in good bouillon to which celery, parsley roots, and similar may be added as desired
9. Serve with grated Parmesan cheese and brown butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Italienische Sauce (Italian Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- A handful of blanched almonds
- Sugar
- Provençal oil
- Good wine vinegar
- Finely chopped chives
- Parsley
- Anchovies or boned herrings (optional)

## Directions

1. Pound the egg yolks from 6 hard-boiled eggs together with a handful of blanched almonds and some sugar in a mortar until very fine.
2. Mix on a sauce plate with a couple spoonfuls of Provençal oil and good wine vinegar.
3. Add finely chopped chives and parsley, and if desired, some finely chopped anchovies or boned herrings.
4. Serve with beef or cold roasts.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Italienische Suppe (Italian Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 yellow turnip
- 1 parsley root
- 1 piece of celery
- 1 small cabbage
- 1 kohlrabi
- 6 dekagrams Parmesan
- 1/2 kilogram macaroni
- 8 dekagrams butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and cut the root vegetables, kohlrabi and cabbage into fine noodle-like pieces
2. Put them in cold salted water, except the cabbage, and cook for 1¼ hours
3. After about 20 minutes, add the cabbage and cook together
4. Make a roux with 8 dekagrams butter, pour the vegetable water on it, cook well, salt
5. Add the vegetables and cook together
6. Add 4 dekagrams macaroni cooked in salted water, cut small, and serve the soup with grated Parmesan cheese

title: Italienischer Reis (Risotto) zur Suppe (Rice à l'Italienne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca butter
- 1 piece finely chopped onion
- 3 pieces cleaned, washed, dried mushrooms, finely chopped
- 60 deca rice
- 1/8 liter white beef broth
- approximately 1.5 liters beef broth
- salt
- 8 deca grated Parmesan cheese
- fried sausages or other garnish

## Directions

1. Heat 24 deca butter until very hot in a casserole
2. Lightly roast 1 piece of finely chopped onion
3. Add 3 pieces of cleaned, washed, dried, finely chopped mushrooms to the onion and let them roast lightly without browning
4. Add 60 deca rinsed rice to the roasted ingredients and let it roast for approximately 4 minutes with frequent stirring
5. Add 1/8 liter white beef broth and let it simmer covered for 15 minutes
6. If the broth evaporates before the rice is done, add approximately 1.5 liters more broth and some salt and let it simmer for another 10 minutes
7. Add 8 deca grated Parmesan cheese to the rice and stir well
8. On a platter, place a portion of the risotto using a wooden spoon and arrange pieces of fried sausages or other garnish around it

title: Italienischer Salat in Muscheln (Italian Salad in Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg Kipfler potatoes
- 1 apple
- 10 dag root vegetables
- 1 milk herring
- 2 anchovies
- 10 dag lamb's lettuce
- 1 pickled gherkin
- 1 red beet
- 2 dag capers
- 3 egg yolks for mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the root vegetables, cut into small dice, place in cold salt water and cook 30-45 minutes, then drain.
2. Cut the cooked cold potatoes into dice, as well as the apple and gherkin.
3. Clean the herring, remove the bones and innards, wash it. Cut one half into larger pieces and the other half and the cleaned anchovy into small dice.
4. Mix everything with a cold mayonnaise made from 3 egg yolks (see Mayonnaise Salad: Menu 14), fill into shells, decorate each one with prepared lamb's lettuce or head lettuce, red beets, hard-boiled egg slices, capers, and anchovy strips.
5. Serve the shells on a cup or glass plate with a folded napkin.
6. Alternatively, season the diced mixture without mayonnaise with vinegar, oil, and mustard, fill into shells, spread warm or cold stirred mayonnaise over it and decorate as above.
7. The herring roe can be mixed in or used as decoration.

title: Italienischer Salat in Muscheln (Italian Salad in Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg Kipfler potatoes
- 1 apple
- 10 h root vegetables
- 1 mild herring
- 2 anchovies
- 10 h corn salad
- 1 pickled cucumber
- 1 red beet
- 2 h capers

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the root vegetables, cut them into small cubes, put in cold salt water, cook for 30-45 minutes, strain.
2. Cut the cooked cold potatoes into cubes, as well as the apple and cucumber.
3. Debone and clean the herring, wash it.
4. Cut one half into larger pieces, the other half and the deboned anchovy into small pieces.

title: Jam Soufflé (Schaumsoufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces sugar
- 16 ounces marmalade of choice
- 2 whole eggs
- 5 egg whites, whipped
- blanched almonds
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Put sugar and marmalade in a deep dish
2. Beat with 2 eggs for 1/2 hour
3. Gently fold in the stiffly whipped egg whites
4. Mound on a butter-greased dish
5. Arrange with sliced blanched almonds as desired
6. Sprinkle with finely powdered sugar
7. Bake in the open oven for 1 1/2 hours

title: Japanischer Schweinsbraten (Japanese Pork Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-80 dekagrams pork shoulder
- 1 celery root
- 10 liters Kremser mustard
- 6 liters cream
- 5 dekagrams fat
- 1 onion
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, salt it, optionally bind it
2. In a roasting pan, brown a finely chopped onion in 5 dekagrams fat until light golden
3. Add the finely sliced celery, the mustard and the meat
4. Roast for 1-2 hours under frequent basting with its own juice and additional liquid
5. Defat the juice, strain it, pour it onto 1/2 liter thick cream, cook well and serve with the meat

title: Jellied Calf Brains (Cervelle de veau à la gelée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- One cleaned calf brain, freed of skin, cooked in salted water
- Aspic
- Fine green parsley
- Chervil
- Sauce thickened with aspic
- Truffles

## Directions

1. After the brain is cooked, placed on cloth and dried, put it in a saucepan
2. Cover with sauce bound with aspic, mixed with finely cut green parsley and chervil
3. Let it come to a boil once and set aside to cool
4. Pour a finger's depth of melted aspic into a mold set in ice
5. Once set, arrange the brain neatly on it
6. Cover again with melted aspic, let it set, add another layer of brain with truffles
7. Continue until the mold is full
8. After standing covered in ice for 1 hour, remove, rinse, dip briefly in boiling water
9. Dry with a cloth, turn out onto a platter, remove the mold
10. Garnish the turned-out dish with chopped aspic

title: Johannisbeeren-Gefrornes (Currant Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 and a half pounds red currants (Ribisel)
- 1 pound ripe cherries
- 1 small glass wine
- Three quarter pound clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Select 2 and a half pounds red currants and strip them from the stems
2. Take 1 pound ripe cherries, remove the stems and pits
3. Press these fruits through a hair sieve into a bowl
4. Press through with a small glass of wine to extract the strength of the fruits
5. Stir in three quarter pound clarified sugar, cool everything well, and then process as usual
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Johannisbeeren-Kuchen (Currant Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter
- 20 deca finely pounded sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 16 deca bread crumbs
- 25 deca currants

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deca butter until fluffy
2. Gradually add 20 deca finely pounded sugar and 6 egg yolks, stir for 1/2 hour
3. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites at the same time as 16 deca bread crumbs
4. Fill into a form brushed with butter and dusted with flour
5. 25 deca picked currants are placed in the dough 4 berries at a time, sinking them into the dough
6. Bake at low heat for approximately 1 hour in the oven
7. Turn the cake out onto a sieve and serve only the following day

title: Johannisbeerenſaft (Currant Juice)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound pressed juice
- 1 pound sugar

## Directions

1. For 1 pound of pressed juice take 1 pound sugar, let them cook together and skim off the foam well
2. Treat it otherwise like cherry juice
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Jourwecken (Stuffed Bread Loaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bread roll made from bread dough
- 8 dkg ham, not too finely chopped
- 4 dkg tongue, not too finely chopped
- 3 anchovies, not too finely chopped
- 1 pickled cucumber, not too finely chopped
- 2 hard eggs, not too finely chopped
- 1 hard egg yolk, not too finely chopped
- 2 spoonfuls capers, not too finely chopped
- 10 dkg butter
- salt
- mustard

## Directions

1. Cut off one end of the bread roll
2. Completely hollow out the roll with a long knife
3. Chop ham, tongue, anchovies, pickled cucumber, hard eggs, hard egg yolk, and capers not too finely
4. Mix with 10 dkg butter
5. Add salt and mustard
6. Fill the bread roll with this mixture
7. Press together
8. Wrap in parchment paper and a cloth
9. Let it lie in a cool place for 1-2 days
10. Cut into thin slices
11. Arrange slices in a wreath shape on a glass platter

title: Juliennesuppe (Julienne Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dekagrams neck pot roast
- 16 liters root vegetables
- 1 small head of cabbage
- 1/4 kilogram fresh green peas
- 1/4 kilogram green beans
- 1/4 kohlrabi
- 1/2 head cauliflower
- 2 mushrooms
- 6 dekagrams butter
- 1 onion
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and cut the root vegetables, cabbage, kohlrabi and beans into fine noodle-like pieces
2. 1½ hours before serving, brown a finely chopped onion in 6 dekagrams hot butter until light golden
3. Add the cut vegetables except the cabbage, sauté for a while
4. Pour in hot water and braise until soft
5. After 1/2 hour, gradually pour the strained beef broth on top, salt
6. 20 minutes before serving, add the cabbage, separately boiled mushrooms, cauliflower florets and peas

title: Junge Huͤhner, gebraten (Roasted Young Chickens)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- salt
- hot butter for basting
- breadcrumbs (optional)
- bacon for larding (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean them well and press in the breastbone, then remove it
2. Salt them and bend the feet in
3. Roast them over a fresh fire on the spit
4. Frequently baste them with hot butter
5. If desired, dust them with breadcrumbs and let them roast a little more
6. Serve quickly
7. They can also be larded with finely cut bacon
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Junge Kohlrüben mit Geschommel (Young Kohlrabi with Béchamel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peeled kohlrabi
- Butter
- Pepper
- Sugar
- Oil
- Milk
- Salt

## Directions

1. Using a thin corer, scoop small kohlrabi from a peeled kohlrabi
2. Braise them uncovered in butter slowly and season with pepper and sugar during cooking
3. Drain the butter and replace it with béchamel sauce by mixing oil into the drained butter, letting it brown slightly, adding milk, and seasoning with a little salt
4. Pour this sauce over the kohlrabi

title: Junge Monathrettige als Zugemüse zu kochen (Young Monthly Radishes as a Side Dish)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- small, equal, young monthly radishes
- sugar
- fat or lard
- radishes (blanched beforehand)
- flour
- meat or brown soup

## Directions

1. Take small, equal, young monthly radishes
2. Scrape and wash them clean
3. Set a small piece of sugar with fat or lard on heat and let it become chestnut brown
4. Add the radishes, which must have been blanched beforehand
5. Let them braise gently until tender
6. Sprinkle a little flour over them
7. Add some meat or brown soup
8. Let it reduce briefly
9. Serve to table. You can also lay bratwurst or fried foods on top as garnish
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Junge Monathrettige als Zugemüse zu kochen (Young Monthly Radishes as a Side Dish)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- small, young monthly radishes
- sugar
- fat or lard
- meat or brown soup broth
- flour

## Directions

1. Take small, uniform young radishes, scrape and wash them clean.
2. Put a piece of sugar with fat or lard on heat and let it become chestnut brown.
3. Add the radishes, which must have been blanched beforehand, let them braise in it until soft, dust with a little flour, add some meat or brown soup broth, let it cook down completely, and serve to the table.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Junger Hase (Young Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- the young of a hare
- some blood
- 6 dkg root vegetables
- cold water
- salt
- vinegar
- 5 peppercorns and spice berries
- 1 onion
- 2 bay leaves
- thyme
- 6 dkg fat
- 2 pieces sugar
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. Wash the hare or, if freshly killed, place in cold water with salt, vinegar, cleaned root vegetables cut into thin slices, peppercorns, spice berries, onion, bay leaves, and thyme.
2. Cook for 1½ hours.
3. In hot fat, brown 2 pieces of sugar, make a dark roux with flour, and pour the strained broth over it.
4. Piece the hare, add with the blood to the sauce, and simmer until cooked.
5. Serve with bread, semolina, or noodle dumplings.

title: Jungschweinbraten (Roast Young Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork with skin
- salt
- caraway seeds
- hot water

## Directions

1. Wash the meat
2. Rub with salt and caraway seeds
3. Place skin-side down in a roasting pan with hot water
4. Roast in the oven for 30 minutes
5. Remove and score the skin by cutting small squares into it with a sharp knife
6. Place back in the pan skin-side up
7. Roast for another 1-1½ hours at higher heat, basting frequently with its own juices
8. Serve as above

title: Jus (Schü) or Brown Strong Broth
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- good bacon, not too heavily smoked, two finger-widths and one knife-back thickness in slices
- onions, sliced similarly
- lean beef, cut into 2-inch thick slices
- veal shanks (Jarrets)
- scraps of carbonade or similar
- water or bouillon, as much as needed
- celery heads, cleaned
- parsley
- yellow root vegetables
- savoy cabbage heads
- mace leaves

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into slices two finger-widths and one knife-back thickness, and lay them side by side on the bottom of a flat copper casserole in which you wish to cook the schü
2. Cut onions into similar slices and cover the bacon with them
3. Cut lean beef into 2-inch thick slices and lay these over the onions, along with some veal shanks and any scraps of carbonade you may have
4. Set the casserole covered over a gentle fire and let the contents braise slowly until the meat juices settle on the bottom in a light brown color
5. Add as much water or better yet bouillon as you wish to have schü, or as the meat requires to be strong and good
6. Remove the foam that rises to the surface and let the schü draw slowly from the side rather than boil, so that it becomes clear
7. Finally add some cleaned celery heads, parsley, yellow root vegetables, some savoy cabbage heads, and a few mace leaves, and let everything cook together until the meat is quite tender
8. Pour the schü through a napkin or fine hair sieve into an earthen vessel and preserve it for use
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Jus-Sauce (Jus Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces marrow
- 1 onion studded with spices
- 1/2 bay leaf
- savory and saffron
- some veal meat
- a little ham
- roe and liver of a hen if available
- 1/2 liter broth
- good wine
- beaten egg white

## Directions

1. Put 8 ounces marrow, a spice-studded onion, 1/2 bay leaf, savory, saffron, some veal meat, and a little ham into a saucepan, along with roe and liver of a hen if available
2. Let it roast; when light brown, pour in 1/2 liter broth and some good wine
3. Let it simmer slowly for 1 hour
4. When the sauce is very clear, add some beaten egg white
5. Let it simmer slowly for another 1/2 hour
6. Pass the whole thing through a sieve
7. Serve with various dishes

title: Jus-Sauce, another kind (Jus Sauce, Tartar)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces fat
- some beef and marrow, cut into leaves
- 5 carrots
- 1 small chopped veal bone
- 1/2 liter broth
- 1/3 liter broth
- 1/4 liter wine
- 8 ounces black bread
- 8 ounces butter
- 8 ounces flour

## Directions

1. Put 8 ounces fat, beef and marrow (cut into leaves), and 5 carrots into a saucepan
2. Add a small chopped veal bone and cook it with 1/2 liter broth
3. When the root vegetables are well cooked, add another 1/3 liter broth, 1/4 liter wine, and 8 ounces black bread
4. Let it cook for 1 hour and pass through a sieve
5. In 8 ounces butter, toast the same amount of flour, pour in the strained mixture, and stir until it boils
6. If bread is added, omit the butter and flour

title: Jägerpudding (Hunter's Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 1/2 measure cream
- 6 tablespoons fine flour
- 1 pound raw kidney fat
- 1 pound large raisins
- 1 pound small raisins
- 2 loths citron
- 1 pound grated sugar
- 1 glass brandy

## Directions

1. Beat 10 egg yolks and 6 egg whites together in half a measure of cream.
2. Take 6 tablespoons fine flour, 1 pound raw kidney fat, 1 pound large raisins (freed from pits), and 1 pound small raisins (carefully washed).
3. Add 2 loths citron, 1 pound grated sugar, and 1 glass brandy to the mass.
4. When everything is well stirred together, cook it as already described.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Jägertorte (Hunter's Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 10 dkg butter
- 5 dkg sugar
- 3 dkg grated hazelnuts
- jam
- schlagobers (whipped cream)

## Directions

1. Make sponge cake from egg yolks
2. Cut in half
3. Fill with jam or following filling
4. Coat top and sides with ½ schlagobers and sprinkle with chopped pistachios
5. For filling: Beat 10 dkg butter, mix with 5 dkg sugar and 3 dkg grated hazelnuts

title: Kaffecrèmetorte (Coffee Cream Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg butter
- 15 dkg sugar
- 5 whole eggs
- 5 spoons very strong black coffee
- 25-30 small biscuits
- 1 dl schlagobers (whipped cream)
- sugar
- swollen chopped pistachios

## Directions

1. Beat butter, gradually add sugar, whole eggs, and very strong black coffee
2. Coat bottom of cake form with this cream
3. Lay a layer of small biscuits closely together with no gaps
4. Cover with cream, then biscuits again, continuing until all used
5. Coat with cream at the end, place paper on top
6. Set pot or pan with cold water on top to weigh down mass
7. Let stand one day
8. Remove paper, loosen edges, turn out of form
9. Cover with sweetened schlagobers
10. Sprinkle with swollen chopped pistachios, or make rosettes or rings with star piping tube if more schlagobers available

title: Kaffee Eintopf, alterer Art (Old-Fashioned Coffee Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Hektogram coffee
- 1.2 liters heavy cream, boiling
- 16 Hektogram flour
- 16 Hektogram milk
- Spanish almond paste
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Immediately after roasting, pour boiling heavy cream over 16 hectograms of coffee and let stand covered
2. After completely cooling, strain through a sieve
3. Slowly pour the mixture into 16 hectograms of flour while constantly stirring
4. Treat further as indicated in the previous 'Coffee Souffle' recipe
5. After baking and turning out into a bowl, immediately coat with Spanish almond paste as desired, dust with fine sugar
6. Dry in the open oven for 10-15 minutes and serve immediately to table

title: Kaffee Streute Torte (Coffee Layered Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 8 decagrams peeled almonds
- 3 egg yolks, finely ground
- 14 decagrams sifted sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 10 decagrams butter
- 3 tablespoons strong black coffee
- 16 decagrams butter
- coarse sugar
- finely sliced almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form, then fold in 8 decagrams of peeled almonds ground finely with 3 egg yolks, along with 14 decagrams of sifted sugar, mixing well.
2. Line a baking sheet with paper and divide the mixture into three equal parts, spreading each finger-height thick with a knife on the paper, forming three round plates of equal size.
3. Sprinkle with finely sifted sugar and bake in the oven at moderate heat for 1 1/4 hours.
4. Meanwhile, stir together 3 egg yolks, 10 decagrams butter, and 3 tablespoons strong black coffee in a small pot over low heat until the mixture becomes thick, but do not allow it to boil.
5. Beat 16 decagrams butter in a deep bowl until fluffy, then stir in the cooled coffee mixture and continue stirring until completely combined.
6. Once the baked plates have cooled completely and are removed from the paper, spread the filling between the layers, stack them together, and spread the remaining mixture on the top plate.
7. Sprinkle with coarse sugar and finely sliced almonds, or decorate as desired with a paper piping bag filled with the cream.

title: Kaffee-Creme mit Gelatine (Coffee Cream with Gelatin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 liters strong black coffee essence
- 16 Deca sugar
- 0.5 liters water
- 2 Deca gelatin
- 0.5 liters heavy cream

## Directions

1. Add 16 Deca sugar to 1.25 liters strong black coffee essence and heat until very hot, but do not boil.
2. Dissolve 2 Deca gelatin in 0.5 liters water, then pour the dissolved and steaming hot gelatin (but not boiling) into the sweetened hot coffee and stir in a deep bowl until lukewarm.
3. Fold in 0.5 liters heavy cream to this mixture.
4. Immediately transfer into a mold brushed with fine oil, which has been buried in ice 1 hour earlier.
5. Proceed as directed for Rice with Vanilla Cream (No. 1689).
6. Note: Work very quickly when preparing this cream. If ice is not available, very cold water may be used. For 2 Deca gelatin, the total liquid should be 1 liter.

title: Kaffee-Eis (Coffee Glaze)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 drams of finely sifted sugar
- 1 1/6 liter of very strong hot coffee without sugar

## Directions

1. Stir 32 drams of finely sifted sugar and 1 1/6 liter of very strong hot coffee without sugar together for 1 hour.
2. It must be so thick that it drops heavily from the spoon.
3. Pour the glaze onto the cake, bread, Gugelhupf or the object in question and spread it with a knife dipped in very hot water; also stroke the running glaze back up with the knife as long as it holds.
4. If the glaze is too thick, add coffee; if it is too thin, add sugar.
5. This cannot be determined because the sugar is not uniform.

title: Kaffee-Gefrornees ohne Eier (Coffee Frozen Dessert without Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter strong coffee
- 1 liter double-strength unsweetened cream
- 20 deca sugar

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients cold and stir together
2. Place in the freezing box and treat further as indicated for Gefrorne mit Vanille (No. 1720)

title: Kaffeebombe (Coffee Bomb)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dl very strong black coffee
- 10 dkg powdered sugar
- 1/2 liter firmly whipped cream
- salt
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Mix the coffee very well with 10 dkg powdered sugar and fold in the firmly whipped cream
2. Fill the mixture into a bomb mold rinsed with water, cover with a white sheet of paper, put on the lid, and seal the edge well with butter or fat so that no salt water can penetrate
3. Completely bury the mold in finely chopped ice mixed with 1/4 kg rock salt and let it stand for at least 4-5 hours
4. Remove the fat, hold the mold in hot water, and turn out like a cream

title: Kaffeecréme (Coffee Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter very strong black coffee
- 16 dkg cube sugar
- 1 dkg gelatin
- 1/2 liter whipped cream

## Directions

1. In a small pot, dissolve 16 dkg cube sugar in the coffee, bring to a boil, immediately remove from heat and simmer slowly for several minutes
2. Allow to cool, dissolve 1 dkg gelatin, and whip the cream
3. Mix the cooled coffee mixture with the whipped cream and slowly pour the melted gelatin through a tea sieve while stirring continuously
4. Fill the mixture into a pudding mold rinsed with water
5. Bury in ice for 2-3 hours, then loosen, hold briefly in hot water, turn out, and optionally decorate with whipped cream

title: Kaffeeeis (Coffee Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l milk or cream
- 1 dl strong black coffee
- 5 egg yolks
- 12 dkg sugar

## Directions

1. Whisk the milk with 5 egg yolks, 12 dkg sugar and the black coffee over gentle heat until slightly thickened.
2. Strain it and freeze like Vanilla Ice Cream.

title: Kaffeepudding (Coffee Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ dl boiling milk
- 6 dag butter
- 6 dag sugar
- 1 dl strong black coffee
- 12 dag flour
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 dag sugar
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Rapidly stir the butter, sugar, coffee, and flour into the boiling milk
2. Remove from heat and stir until the dough is cold
3. Fold in the egg yolks, sugar, and beaten egg whites
4. Fill the mixture into a pudding mold and let it set in a water bath for 1 hour
5. Turn out, cool, coat with the following crème, and serve

title: Kaffeepudding Crème (Coffee Pudding Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ liter milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 8 dag sugar
- 4 spoons strong black coffee
- ¼ liter whipped cream (Schlagobers)

## Directions

1. Beat the milk, egg yolks, sugar, and coffee over a water bath until thick
2. Cool and fold in the whipped cream

title: Kaffees-Creme mit Hausenblasen (Coffee Cream with Isinglass)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka roasted Mocha coffee
- 1/4 liter boiling cream
- 24 Deka fine sugar
- 4 Deka isinglass with 1/2 liter water, reduced to 1/2 liter
- 1/2 liter whipped cream

## Directions

1. Roast 16 Deka Mocha coffee until golden brown, add it to 1/4 liter boiling cream, cover and let it cool, then strain it through cloth and add 24 Deka fine sugar
2. If you have previously prepared 4 Deka isinglass with 1/2 liter water and reduced it to 1/2 liter, mix this with the strained mixture, set it on ice and stir this mass until it begins to thicken, then mix in 1/2 liter whipped cream, fill a mold and freeze it in ice

title: Kaffeh-Branntwein (Coffee Brandy)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds sugar
- 1 pound roasted and ground coffee beans

## Directions

1. Take 4 pounds sugar and stir in 1 pound roasted and ground coffee beans
2. Let them ferment together
3. Distill the mixture
4. Pour the spirit over another pound of coffee beans
5. Let it macerate with the beans for several days in a well-sealed bottle
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kaffeh-Creme (Coffee Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 measures sweet cream
- strong black coffee
- sugar
- 9 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook 3 measures sweet cream well
2. Pour in strong black coffee, colored as if one wanted to drink it
3. Sweeten sufficiently with sugar
4. Beat 9 egg yolks in a clean pot, beat well
5. Pour in the mixed coffee gradually and beat vigorously
6. Pass through a sieve
7. Treat as the pistachio cream above
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kaffer's Chocolate Beverage (Kaffer's Chocolate Drink)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter water
- 12 deca sugar
- 12 deca (3 tablets) chocolate

## Directions

1. Boil 1/2 liter water with 12 deca sugar
2. Meanwhile, melt 12 deca (3 tablets) chocolate in the oven on a baking sheet
3. Put the chocolate in a saucepan
4. Gradually pour the boiling sugared water onto it
5. Stir over heat until the chocolate is completely smooth and fully cooked
6. Use as desired

title: Kaiser Wilhelm Torte (Kaiser Wilhelm Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 11 dkg butter
- 15 dkg sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 11 dkg softened chocolate
- 6 stiff egg whites
- 9 dkg flour
- 2 egg yolks
- 7 dkg sugar
- 1 tablespoon water
- 4 dkg grated chocolate
- 6 dkg butter
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Cream together 11 dkg butter with 15 dkg sugar gradually
2. Add 6 egg yolks one by one
3. Add 11 dkg softened chocolate
4. Fold in 6 stiff egg whites together with 9 dkg flour
5. Fill the batter into the form and bake slowly
6. For the filling: beat 2 egg yolks with 7 dkg sugar and 1 tablespoon water over steam until thick
7. Cool the filling and gradually stir in 4 dkg grated chocolate and 6 dkg butter until smooth
8. Fill and frost the torte with brown glaze
9. Sprinkle with pistachios

title: Kaiser Wilhelm-Torte (Kaiser Wilhelm Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 11 dkg butter
- 15 dkg sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 11 dkg softened chocolate
- 6 stiff egg whites
- 9 dkg flour

## Directions

1. Beat butter and gradually add sugar, egg yolks, and softened chocolate
2. Fold in stiff egg whites together with flour
3. Fill mixture into form

title: Kaiser-Birnen (Kaiser Pears)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Kaiser pears (not too soft)
- Lemon juice
- Lemon water (cold and hot)
- Sugar water (made from sugar syrup as described in recipe 1841)
- Cinnamon stick (optional, to replace missing stems)

## Directions

1. Cut the pears in half lengthwise through the stem
2. Remove the core with an apple corer or knife
3. Peel the pears and rub with lemon juice
4. First place in cold lemon water, then in boiling lemon water
5. When they begin to soften, quickly immerse them three times in fresh cold water
6. Place in the already prepared preserving jars containing some sugar water
7. Fill with the remaining sugar water and treat as the Meusel-Cateller or Kelsen pears (recipe 1842)
8. If any pears are missing stems when finishing as compote, they may be replaced with a piece of cinnamon stick as desired

title: Kaiserschmarren (Emperor's Schmarren)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ l Milk
- 18 dkg Flour
- 3 Egg yolks
- 6 dkg Sugar
- Salt
- 6 dkg melted butter
- 3 Egg whites
- 8 dkg Butter
- Sugar
- Compote

## Directions

1. Mix milk with flour, egg yolks, sugar, salt, and melted butter until smooth
2. Fold in beaten egg whites
3. Heat butter in a large flat pan
4. Pour in mixture not too thick and bake on the stove without stirring until light brown
5. Turn over and bake evenly light brown on the other side
6. Divide into small pieces with a fork and roast a little longer
7. Arrange on a round platter, sprinkle with sugar, and serve warm with compote
8. Note: if the pan is too small and the mixture is poured too thick, it will not bake through

title: Kaiserschnitzel (Emperor Veal Cutlet)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin, finger-thick slices
- Bacon
- Fat
- Sour cream
- Soup
- Lemon juice
- Lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cut finger-thick slices from a veal loin
2. Pound them lightly
3. Salt them
4. Lard one side with fine bacon
5. Dip in hot fat and place larded side up in a casserole
6. Roast in the oven for several minutes until bacon is slightly golden
7. Pour off the fat and baste the meat with sour cream
8. Continue roasting with frequent basting
9. Remove meat to a serving dish
10. Add some soup to the defatted, strained sauce and add lemon juice
11. Cut the lemon peel into fine strips and add to the sauce
12. Stir well, strain, and serve with the cutlets

title: Kaiserschöberl (Imperial Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deca butter
- 3 tablespoons milk
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 pinch salt
- Snow (egg white) from 6 eggs
- 16 Deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat 12 Deca butter until fluffy
2. Gradually stir in 3 tablespoons milk
3. Add 6 egg yolks and 1 pinch of salt
4. Mix in the snow from 6 eggs along with 16 Deca flour at the same time into the mixture
5. This must be done carefully so the snow does not get beaten down too much
6. Pour into a shallow casserole dish greased with butter and dusted with flour to a finger-depth
7. Bake at moderate heat, turn out onto a board and let cool
8. Can be cut into pieces of any size or cut out with a cutter before adding to soup
9. Add to the soup only a few minutes before serving, as it otherwise absorbs too much soup and becomes too soft

title: Kakes (Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dkg butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 12½ dkg sugar
- 37 dkg flour
- Lemon zest
- ½ packet Oetker baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat butter in a bowl for a few minutes
2. Mix in 2 whole eggs, sugar
3. Later add flour, lemon zest, and baking powder
4. Place dough on floured board and knead
5. Roll out in portions
6. Cut with cake cutter
7. Place on lightly buttered sheet
8. Bake at moderate heat until pale yellow
9. If no cake cutter available, use other cutters and prick with a needle so they rise better

title: Kalbfleiisch-Farce (Veal Forcemeat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 80 drachm well-skinned veal fat
- 40 drachm skinned calf meat, finely chopped (usually from the round)
- 6 whole eggs
- brandied dough made from 1/2 milk
- 1 bay leaf
- salt as needed
- spices

## Directions

1. Combine the veal fat with the finely chopped calf meat
2. Add 6 whole eggs, brandied dough made from 1/2 milk, 1 bay leaf, salt as needed, and spices
3. Pound everything together finely in a mortar
4. Use in the same manner as capon forcemeat or add to soup as consommé

title: Kalbfleisch mit unreifen Stachelbeeren (Veal with Unripe Gooseberries)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal
- Warm water
- Salt
- Unripe gooseberries
- Bread slices, lightly toasted
- Meat broth
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Set the meat with warm water on the fire, salt it, skim it clean, and let it simmer until soft
2. Meanwhile, place unripe gooseberries with a pair of lightly toasted bread slices in a pot
3. Pour meat broth and a little vinegar over them and let them simmer until very soft
4. Press them through a sieve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kalbs Fasch (Veal Farce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo veal from the knuckle
- 16 deca bread rolls softened in milk
- 12 deca butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- some salt

## Directions

1. Pound 1 kilo veal from the knuckle finely in a mortar
2. Add 16 deca bread rolls softened in milk and well pressed out, 12 deca butter, 4 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, and some salt
3. Pound everything together well, pass through a sieve, stir well together, and use as needed
4. Can be used to form roulades, quenelles, dumplings, and cutlets for ragout

title: Kalbsageröse (Calf Mesentery)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- calf mesentery
- vinegar
- salt water
- salt
- nutmeg
- pepper
- butter sauce
- vinegar or wine or lemon juice

## Directions

1. The calf mesentery is cleaned in sour water, then cooked in salt water, removed, cut into desired pieces, combined with finely cut green parsley, some salt, nutmeg and a little pepper in a well-made butter sauce and brought to a boil.
2. The sauce can be soured to taste with some vinegar, wine or lemon juice.

title: Kalbsbruſt gefüllt (Stuffed Veal Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg veal breast
- 4-5 bread rolls
- 6 dkg melted butter
- salt
- 1-2 eggs
- finely chopped parsley
- breadcrumbs as needed

## Directions

1. Have butcher undercut the veal breast
2. Wash and salt inside and outside
3. Fill with prepared filling, spread filling slightly apart
4. Fasten opening with skewer or sew closed
5. Place in roasting pan with skin side down
6. Place piece of butter or fat on top, add some water
7. Braise at first moderate, then higher heat for 2-2½ hours, basting frequently with own juices and adding more liquid
8. Turn when brown on top
9. Remove from pan, take out skewer, cut out ribs, divide meat into pieces
10. Serve with pan juices that have been brought to boil with a piece of butter
11. For filling: soak bread rolls in water, squeeze out well, mix with melted butter, salt, eggs, and finely chopped parsley, thicken with breadcrumbs if needed

title: Kalbsfleisch in Butters Sauce (Veal in Butter Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Leaf-cut neck meat
- Mushrooms
- Spices
- Veal meat from the shoulder
- Broth
- Nutmeg
- Lemon peel

## Directions

1. Put some butter in a casserole, add leaf-cut neck meat, mushrooms and spices if desired
2. When sufficiently roasted, add the veal meat from the shoulder and braise covered for approximately 1/4 hour
3. After this time, pour in the necessary broth and continue braising until the meat is tender
4. Remove the meat, strain the broth through a sieve, make a butter sauce with nutmeg and lemon peel
5. Remove the bones and cut the meat into pieces, arrange with the sauce

title: Kalbsfricandeau mit Risipisi (Braised Veal with Risipisi)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-80 dkg veal fricandeau or boned veal shoulder
- 5 dkg bacon
- 6 dkg root vegetables
- 1/2 liter cream
- 1/4 liter wine
- 6 dkg hot fat
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, salt it, lard it, and optionally roll and tie it
2. In a roasting pan, lightly brown the cleaned, washed, and thinly sliced root vegetables and one onion ring in 6 dkg hot fat
3. Add the meat and braise it in the oven for 1.5-2 hours, basting frequently with its own juices and adding more liquid as needed
4. Remove the meat, dust the pan juices, lightly strain them, add cream and wine, return to the pan and simmer with the meat for another 10 minutes
5. Slice the meat thinly, arrange on an oblong platter in its original shape, and serve with the sauce

title: Kalbsgulyas (Veal Goulash)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-70 dkg veal loin, rib, or shoulder
- 6 dkg hot fat or 6 dkg diced bacon
- 1 finely chopped onion
- 3 pinches paprika
- water
- ½ liter cream

## Directions

1. Wash the meat and cut into cubes, salt lightly.
2. In a pot, heat 6 dkg fat or fry diced bacon, sauté finely chopped onion.
3. Add 3 pinches of paprika, then the meat.
4. Pour in a little water, cover, and simmer for 1 hour with occasional stirring and adding water as needed.
5. Strain the sauce, pour cream over it, and simmer for 10 more minutes.
6. Serve with noodles, dumplings, or rice.

title: Kalbskopf in Wein- oder Seinteig gebacken (Calf's Head Fried in Wine or Batter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's head
- Salt water
- Wine batter or egg batter
- Fat for frying

## Directions

1. Preparation is the same as for fried calf's head, with the difference that instead of flour, eggs, and breadcrumbs, the tender-cooked pieces are dipped in wine batter or egg batter
2. Fry in hot fat

title: Kalbskopf mit Fricassee-Sauce (Calf's Head in Fricassee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a calf's head with the ear
- Salt water
- Fricassee sauce

## Directions

1. Cook half a calf's head with the ear completely tender in salt water
2. Strain it, cut off the ear, divide the meat into desired pieces
3. Arrange the pieces on a platter
4. Place the ear in the middle, cutting it several times at the upper edge to fan it out
5. Serve with fricassee sauce

title: Kalbskopf mit Kren (Calf's Head with Horseradish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's head
- Vinegar
- Water
- Salt
- Whole peppercorns
- Sprigs of thyme
- Root vegetables, finely cut

## Directions

1. In equal parts vinegar and water, add some salt, whole peppercorns, some sprigs of thyme, and finely cut root vegetables
2. Cook a prepared calf's head in this liquid
3. When the head is completely tender, remove it and cut into desired pieces
4. Arrange the pieces on a platter, garnish with the root vegetables and grated horseradish
5. Serve with vinegar and oil

title: Kalbskoteletts (Veal Chops)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal chops (cutlets)
- Flour
- Fat

## Directions

1. After the chops have been cut, the bone is held for each individual one, the rib bone is scraped, and the meat is pushed back
2. The chops are pounded flat with a meat pounder or back of a knife without damaging the meat
3. Salt them, dust with pepper if desired
4. Lightly dip both sides in flour
5. Brown both sides in very hot fat until golden
6. Cover each rib bone with a papillote

title: Kalbsmilj Einlagen für die Suppe (Calf's Sweetbread Garnish for Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's sweetbread
- 12 decigrams butter
- 2 whole eggs and 1 egg yolk
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped parsley
- 1 knife point of nutmeg
- 1 knife point of white pepper
- 16 decigrams white breadcrumbs, softened in milk

## Directions

1. Blanch the calf's sweetbread.
2. Beat 12 decigrams butter until fluffy.
3. Beat in 2 whole eggs and 1 egg yolk and stir this into the prepared sweetbread.
4. Stir in a teaspoon of finely chopped green parsley, a knife point of nutmeg, and the same amount of white pepper.
5. Mix it with 16 decigrams white breadcrumbs, softened somewhat in milk, into a dough.
6. In a greased, floured mold, cook it in a steam bath.

title: Kalbsragout in Muscheln (Veal Ragout in Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 dag veal shoulder or rack
- 1/2 head cauliflower
- 2 mushrooms
- Parmesan cheese
- 5 dag butter
- 5 dag butter, parsley and flour for roux
- lemon juice
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, cut into small squares, salt it, place in a casserole with 5 dag hot butter, pour in a little water, cover and braise 45 minutes.
2. Remove the meat to a plate and pour the juice over it.
3. In the casserole, make a roux from 5 dag butter, parsley and flour, add the meat with its juice, let it reduce, add lemon juice, separately cooked cauliflower separated into florets, and sliced mushrooms.
4. Stir minimally.
5. Fill the ragout into buttered shells, place on a baking sheet, sprinkle with grated cheese and breadcrumbs, drizzle melted butter over it, gratinée in the oven 15-20 minutes.
6. Serve the shells on a cup with a folded napkin.

title: Kalbsragoutsuppe mit Semmelschnitten (Veal Ragout Soup with Bread Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 h veal bones or ½ veal head
- 1 small head cauliflower
- 2 mushrooms
- 1 bread roll
- 8 dkg butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the veal head or bones, place in cold salted water and cook for 1 hour.
2. Remove the meat from the bones and cut into small pieces.
3. In a saucepan, make a parsley roux with 8 dkg butter, add the veal bone broth, add the meat, season with salt, and cook well for 15 minutes.
4. Before serving, add the divided cauliflower and the thinly sliced mushrooms, which have been cooked separately in salted water for 20 minutes.
5. Serve the soup with bread slices or bread dumplings.

title: Kalbsrücken in der Röhre gebraten (Veal Loin Roasted in the Oven)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin
- Salt
- Fat

## Directions

1. Prepare a veal loin according to the instructions above
2. Salt it without removing the skin, place it in a pan containing fat, cover with warm fat and roast fairly quickly in the oven

title: Kalbsrückenmark mit weißem Ragout (Veal Marrow with White Ragout)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal marrow
- Salt water
- Hen marrow
- Hen combs
- Mushrooms

## Directions

1. Poach the removed loin marrow in boiling salt water for approximately 2 minutes
2. Remove and add to a white ragout of hen marrow, hen combs and prepared mushrooms
3. Stir carefully together so it does not break and serve hot on a platter
4. This ragout can also be used mixed with other ragouts

title: Kalbsschlägel (Roasted Veal Leg on the Spit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- small veal leg
- silver handle or paper band
- butter
- salt
- white soup
- 3 tablespoons dissolved soup aspic

## Directions

1. For a small veal leg, remove the outer skin, hack off the knee bone at the so-called joint above the knee disc, separate the meat from the bone and push it back.
2. The bone must be trimmed such that when serving a silver handle or paper band can be attached.
3. After the leg has been completely cleaned and skinned, salt the meat well on all sides and let it rest for a while.
4. Then wrap the meat with several sheets of paper well coated with butter, bind these with twine and roast the meat on a spit at moderate heat for approximately 2 hours.
5. Then remove the paper, dust the meat again with salt, baste it with melted butter and white soup and let it roast another approximately 15 minutes until the meat has a light brown color.
6. Then remove it from the spit, cut it into pieces as desired and arrange these on a platter so that the meat retains its former shape.
7. The bone is provided with a paper band or silver handle.
8. To the sauce remaining in the pan add 3 tablespoons of dissolved soup aspic, let it come to a boil together and serve it separately with the meat.

title: Kalbsschlägel im eigenen Saft gedünstet (Veal Leg Braised in Its Own Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- veal leg
- slices of ham and bacon
- thinly sliced onion
- 1 bay leaf
- white pepper (optional)

## Directions

1. After the veal leg has been trimmed as previously indicated, wiped with a cloth and blanched, pound it, salt it and, if desired, season it with white pepper.
2. Place several slices of ham and bacon and thinly sliced onion along with 1 bay leaf in a casserole, place the leg on top and braise it well covered in the oven at moderate heat, turning it frequently, until soft.
3. This must, however, be done at moderate heat so that the liquid released does not burn.
4. When the leg is soft enough, arrange it on a platter and serve the defatted sauce with it; if too little sauce is present, it is increased with some soup.

title: Kalbsschlägel in der Röhre gebraten (Veal Leg Roasted in the Oven)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- veal leg
- sweet, rinsed milk (optional)
- cold water
- salt
- truffles (optional)
- bacon
- small sausage, smoked and cooked
- fat

## Directions

1. After the veal leg has been trimmed as indicated for the spit-roasted version, if it is not white enough, soak it 1 hour in sweet, rinsed milk.
2. Remove it from there, wash it in cold water, salt it well and lard the leg, if desired, with truffles, bacon, a small sausage (smoked, cooked)—namely: prick the meat with a small knife and as you withdraw the knife, insert pencil-thick, half-finger-long, salted bacon inside.
3. In this way the leg can be larded more or less heavily.
4. Place the leg in a pan to which fat has been added, pour some hot fat over it and roast it in the oven at moderate heat for 2–3 hours depending on the size of the leg, frequently basting with the sauce that forms.

title: Kalbsschlägel, besonders gut zugerichtet (Veal Leg, Specially Prepared)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh veal leg
- Bacon, thumb-thick pieces
- Rosemary
- Bay leaves
- Wine
- Water
- Pepper
- Ginger
- Nutmeg
- Cloves
- Basil
- Clay

## Directions

1. Skin a beautiful fresh veal leg
2. Lard it with bacon pieces the thickness of a small finger, using a particularly long larding needle to ensure the bacon is threaded through completely
3. Hack off the bone completely
4. Place it in a vessel lined with rosemary and bay leaves
5. Pour two-thirds wine and one-third water over it
6. Season with pepper, ginger, nutmeg, and cloves
7. Add more rosemary, basil, and bay leaves
8. Cover the vessel tightly and seal it with clay
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kalbsschläget, besonders gut zugerichtet (Veal Knuckle, Particularly Well Prepared)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one fresh veal knuckle
- lard the thickness of a small finger
- rosemary
- bay leaves
- two parts wine
- one part water
- pepper
- ginger
- mace
- cloves
- basil

## Directions

1. Skin a beautiful fresh veal knuckle
2. Lard it with lard the thickness of a small finger, using a particularly long larding needle so the lard can be drawn through completely
3. Remove the bone from the knuckle entirely
4. Place it in a pot lined with rosemary and bay leaves
5. Pour two parts wine and one part water over it
6. Season with pepper, ginger, mace, and cloves
7. Add some more rosemary, basil, and bay leaves
8. Cover the pot tightly and seal it with clay so the spirit cannot escape
9. Set it on coals and let it simmer slowly
10. When the knuckle is sufficiently braised or stewed, carefully remove the clay and lid from the pot
11. Lift out the knuckle, place it in a bowl, and strain the broth over it through a sieve
12. During stewing, move the pot back and forth occasionally so the knuckle does not stick to the bottom
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kalbsschwanz mit Reis (Veal Tails with Rice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds butter
- 3 pounds veal tails, cut into finger-length pieces
- 1/2 liter water
- 1/4 liter rice
- parsley, tied in sprigs
- 1 piece cleaned mushroom
- 1 onion spiked with a clove

## Directions

1. Put 2 pounds butter with 3 pounds veal tails, which have been cut into finger-length pieces, into 1/2 liter water and let them cook.
2. When they begin to become soft, add 1/4 liter rice along with some sprigs of bound parsley, 1 piece of cleaned mushroom, and a small onion spiked with a clove.
3. Braise this until the rice is soft.
4. Before serving, remove the onion and parsley, and place the rice with the pieces on a platter to serve.

title: Kalbsschwanz, glasiert (Glazed Veal Tails)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- veal tails
- salt water
- cold water
- thinly sliced bacon
- white soup
- aspic glaze
- rice, garnished

## Directions

1. Cut the veal tails into finger-length pieces and after some rinsing, cook them 15 minutes in salted water.
2. Transfer them from there to cold water.
3. After they have completely cooled, remove them and place them in a casserole lined with thinly sliced bacon and cover the meat with equally sliced bacon.
4. Pour white soup over them and let them cook until soft.
5. Then set it aside, arrange the bacon slices after some cooling in a shallow casserole, place the tail pieces one after another on top, coat them with dissolved aspic glaze, place the casserole briefly in the oven, arrange the pieces on a platter and serve with garnished rice.

title: Kalbsteak (Veal Steak)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin, 2 finger-thick slices
- Flour
- Fat

## Directions

1. Cut 2 finger-thick slices from a veal loin
2. Pound the slices
3. Salt them
4. Shape them round
5. Dip one side in flour
6. Place floured side down in a pan with hot fat
7. Brown on both sides

title: Kalbsvögerln (Veal Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-70 dkg veal loin
- 7 dkg bacon
- ½ liter cream
- 5 dkg hot fat
- 1 finely chopped onion
- broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, salt it, and lard with bacon strips.
2. In a pot, heat 5 dkg fat and sauté finely chopped onion.
3. Add the meat, pour in a little broth, and simmer for 1¼ hours, adding broth as needed.
4. Strain the sauce, possibly pass through a sieve, pour cream over it, simmer for 10 minutes.
5. Serve on a round dish with dumplings or noodles.

title: Kalbszunge (Calf's Tongue)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's tongue
- Lukewarm water
- Salt water
- Piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Soak the calf's tongue in lukewarm water to rinse it
2. Cook it in salt water until the outer skin can be removed
3. When this has happened, divide it lengthwise, cut into desired pieces
4. Serve with a piquant sauce, or fry it like calf's head

title: Kalbszunge gebacken (Fried Veal Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal tongue
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- lard for frying
- spinach, cooked lettuce, or head lettuce for serving

## Directions

1. Wash the tongue, place in cold salt water and cook 1-2 hours.
2. While still warm, peel off the skin.
3. Slice the tongue, salt it, bread it, fry in hot lard.
4. Serve with spinach, cooked lettuce, or head lettuce.
5. Alternatively, cook and slice the tongue and place in finished Polish sauce (see Menu 40: Karpfen schwarz) and serve as an evening dish.

title: Kalte Erdbeerensupptie (Cold Strawberry Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters ripe strawberries
- 1 kilo crushed sugar
- 2 liters white wine (preferably Rhine wine)
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Lay 2 liters ripe strawberries on a cloth and select with a needle approximately one-third of the most beautiful berries, which are then placed in a vessel and set on ice.
2. Press the remaining strawberries through a sieve into an earthenware bowl.
3. Stir the sieved strawberries with approximately 1 kilo crushed sugar until well combined.
4. Pour 2 liters white wine (preferably Rhine wine) with the juice of 1 lemon into the mixture.
5. Strain the whole once more through a sieve into a soup pot already buried in ice.
6. Cover the pot and let it sit cold for approximately 3 to 4 hours.
7. Add the whole strawberries, stir everything together lightly, and serve in a deep glass or porcelain bowl at table in soup bowls or in glasses suitable for this purpose.

title: Kalte Himbeeren-Suppe (Cold Raspberry Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters raspberries
- 1 kilo sugar
- 1/2 liter water
- 2 liters white wine
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Place 2 liters raspberries in very fresh water, then immediately lay them on a cloth and select with a needle approximately one-third of the most beautiful berries, which are placed in a large bowl, scattered with sugar, and set on ice.
2. Let 1 kilo sugar boil well in 1/2 liter water, pour this hot over the remaining raspberries and let it sit covered for approximately 3 hours.
3. Strain this through a sieve, pour 2 liters white wine and the juice of 1 lemon into it, strain it once more through a sieve into a porcelain bowl.
4. Mix in the whole raspberries and serve the soup at table.

title: Kalte Hühner mit Mayonnaise und Aspic-Sauce (Cold Chicken with Mayonnaise and Aspic Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Herbs or mayonnaise of choice
- Cold aspic sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken as for 'In eigenem Safte gedämpfte' (reference 822)
2. Once the chicken is tender, set aside in its broth
3. After completely cooling, remove and cut into desired pieces
4. Coat each piece with herb mayonnaise or cold aspic sauce
5. Arrange raised in the center of a platter
6. Cover with mayonnaise
7. Border the platter with neatly cut or shaped aspic

title: Kalte Konfektürglasur (Cold Confectionery Icing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deka finely sifted sugar
- 1/10 liter clarified cold sugar
- 1 teaspoon Maraschino

## Directions

1. Put 32 deka finely sifted sugar in a deep bowl
2. Gradually stir in 1/10 liter of clarified cold sugar and then 1 teaspoon Maraschino
3. After beating this finely for approximately 1 hour, a thick flowing mass should form (the icing should not be so thick that it drips heavily from the spoon)
4. Coat a cake knife-back thick with this icing, if necessary dry it for a few minutes in a lukewarm oven, and then set it cold
5. Use a knife for coating the cake

title: Kalte Lachs-Pâtete (Cold Salmon Pâté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Several finger-thick slices of salmon fillet
- Truffle pieces, pencil-thick and half-finger long
- Flounder, cut similarly
- 1 bay leaf
- Onion, thinly sliced
- Fresh parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon juice
- Butter dough
- Fish farce
- Bacon slices

## Directions

1. Cut several finger-thick slices from the salmon fillet.
2. Lard these with pencil-thick and half-finger-long pieces of truffle and similarly cut flounder.
3. Place them in a deep bowl, cover with 1 bay leaf, thinly sliced onion, some fresh parsley.
4. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, spray with lemon juice, and let stand covered for several hours, turning occasionally.
5. Prepare the butter dough and form the pâté as described in the 'Goose Liver Pâté' recipe (No. 1057).
6. Once the dough of the prepared pâté is lined inside with thin slices of bacon, spread the bottom 2 fingers high with fish farce, lay the salmon slices upon it, spread again 2 fingers high with farce, and continue until the pâté is completely filled.
7. Proceed as described in the 'Goose Liver Pâté' recipe.
8. This pâté, which is served on fast days, can be enjoyed both warm and cold; in the latter case, however, only after 2 days.

title: Kalte Pastete mit Auerhahn (Cold Pastry with Capercaillie)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 capercaillie, cleaned and gutted
- Pepper
- Salt
- Crushed cloves
- Fragrant herbs
- Hard dough pastry case
- Pastry forcemeat
- Good raw ham

## Directions

1. Cut the capercaillie open along the back and remove the bones
2. Rub it overnight with pepper, salt, crushed cloves, and fragrant herbs
3. Fill a hard dough pastry case with forcemeat
4. Place the marinated capercaillie in the middle
5. Cover it completely with the forcemeat and top with thin slices of good raw ham
6. Bake with a properly made lid
7. The remaining bones and carcass can be used to make a jelly or aspic to serve with the pastry
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kalte Pasteteufarce (Cold Pastry Filling)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds veal from the leg, skinned and chopped
- 2 pounds bacon, chopped
- bread soaked in milk, rubbed and squeezed out
- finely chopped onions
- shallots
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs
- 6 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Chop 4 pounds veal from the leg and 2 pounds bacon very finely
2. Add bread that has been soaked in milk, rubbed, and squeezed out
3. Sweat finely chopped onions and shallots in butter
4. Add salt, pepper, spices, and fine herbs
5. Pound everything together with 6 whole eggs in a mortar
6. Ensure the filling is well seasoned, as cold foods require more seasoning
7. This filling can be used for all kinds of cold pastries
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kalte Sardellensauce (Cold Anchovy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-3 anchovies
- 3 hard egg yolks
- water
- vinegar
- oil
- mustard
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the anchovies
2. Press the anchovies with 3 hard egg yolks through a sieve
3. Mix with some water, vinegar, oil, mustard, and salt to taste
4. The sauce can also be served with cold meat

title: Kalte Schnittlauchſauce (Cold Chives Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 hard egg yolks
- 2 rolls, rind removed and soaked in water
- cold water
- vinegar
- oil
- mustard
- salt
- 4 h chives, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Pass the hard egg yolks, then the squeezed-out rolls.
2. Mix with some cold water, vinegar, oil, mustard and salt.
3. Finally stir in the finely chopped chives.
4. The sauce can also be served with cold meat.

title: Kalte Topfentorte (Cold Curd Cheese Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg flour
- 15 dkg butter
- 6 dkg softened grated almonds
- 4 dkg sugar
- 1 egg
- 3 egg yolks
- 5 dkg sugar
- curd cheese, passed through a sieve
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Prepare crumbly dough from flour, butter, almonds, sugar, and egg according to recipe for Teige (doughs)
2. After resting, roll out dough on floured board not too thin, cut to size of cake base (medium form)
3. Place dough sheet in ungreased cake form
4. Form half of remaining dough into a finger-thick roll, press it flat somewhat at the edge in the form
5. Bake at high heat until light brown, let cool completely
6. Spread filling on top
7. Form thin rolls from remaining dough, lay them in a lattice pattern over the filling
8. Brush with egg, bake at higher heat until light brown
9. Remove from form, cool completely, dust with sugar, serve
10. Prepare filling by stirring together 3 egg yolks with 5 dkg sugar until foamy
11. Mix in passed curd cheese
12. Gently fold in 3 egg whites at the end
13. Bake cake the day before serving

title: Kalte Topfentorte (Cold Quark Torte)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dekagrams flour
- 15 dekagrams butter
- 6 dekagrams swollen grated almonds
- 4 dekagrams sugar
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Make a crumb dough from 15 dekagrams flour, 15 dekagrams butter, 6 dekagrams swollen grated almonds, 4 dekagrams sugar and 1 egg
2. After resting, roll out the dough on a floured board not too thin, cut to the size of the torte base (medium form)
3. Place the sheet in the unbuttressed torte form
4. Form a finger-thick roll from half of the remaining dough, press it flat slightly in the form at the edge
5. Bake at high heat until light brown, let cool and then spread the filling on it
6. Form thin rolls from the remaining dough, place them in a grid pattern over the filling
7. Brush with egg, bake at higher heat until light brown
8. Remove, cool, sprinkle with sugar and serve

title: Kalter Krautsalat (Cold Cabbage Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of cabbage
- salt
- caraway seeds
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Remove the cabbage from bad leaves and stems, wash it, cut it into fine noodle-like pieces
2. Put it into boiling salted water and cook for 10-15 minutes
3. Drain it and place in a bowl
4. While still warm, mix with some salt, caraway seeds, vinegar and oil to taste
5. Cool completely and serve on a round dish
6. The salad must stand for at least 1 hour, as otherwise the cabbage will be tough

title: Kalter Pasteteiteig (Cold Pastry Dough)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds fine flour
- 6 loth butter
- 3 eggs
- 1 or 2 seidels boiling water

## Directions

1. Under 4 pounds of fine flour, work in 6 loth butter
2. Make a deep hole in the middle
3. Sift the flour
4. Beat 3 eggs into the hole
5. Mix these with the remaining ingredients
6. Pour in 1 or 2 seidels of boiling water as needed
7. Work everything as quickly as possible into a firm dough
8. Form the dough into a round ball
9. Wrap it in a napkin, as it is too hot to work with at this stage
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kanarienmilch (Canary Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ liter milk
- 4 egg yolks
- sugar

## Directions

1. Thicken ½ liter milk with 4 egg yolks and sugar to taste on the stove

title: Kandierter Zucker (Candied Sugar Threads)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar, cooked to the sixth stage
- 1/2 teaspoon finely ground platinum or cream of tartar
- almond oil
- alum or cream of tartar for cooking

## Directions

1. Cook sugar until it reaches the sixth stage (hard crack stage), at which point add 1/2 teaspoon finely ground platinum or cream of tartar
2. Place a few drops of this sugar onto a form brushed with almond oil
3. Draw a thread from this sugar, winding it around the form like a glaze
4. When the sugar begins to set on a spoon, warm it again
5. Alum or cream of tartar should be cooked with the sugar from the beginning so that the thread for spinning lasts longer

title: Kapaun mit Auſtern (Capon with Oysters)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- calf sweetbread, blanched
- fresh oysters
- mushrooms
- anchovies
- pepper
- nutmeg
- lemon peel
- thyme
- egg yolk
- bacon sheets
- clear fond or pepper sauce

## Directions

1. Flame, eviscerate, and wash the capon, dry it, and carefully slip your hand between the skin and breast so the latter does not tear
2. Finely hack together a blanched calf sweetbread, several fresh oysters, mushrooms, anchovies, pepper, nutmeg, lemon peel, and thyme, mixed with an egg yolk
3. Stuff the capon between the loosened skin with this mixture
4. Fill the body with oysters and prepare it for roasting so the stuffing and oysters do not fall out
5. Cover it with bacon sheets and roast it slowly on the spit
6. When done, remove it, lay lemon on it, and arrange with clear fond or pepper sauce
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kapaun mit Auſtern gefuͤllt und gebraten (Roasted Capon Filled with Oysters)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- butter, two lots
- breadcrumbs, one handful
- oysters, twenty
- salt
- nutmeg
- more butter and flour for the sauce
- good broth
- lemon juice
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Warm a piece of butter and add breadcrumbs and twenty shelled oysters, a little salt and nutmeg, stirring well together
2. Fill the capon with this mixture and roast it nicely in its juices
3. Make a golden roux from butter and flour
4. Add a spoonful of good broth, the roasting sauce from the capon, twenty oysters, and lemon juice, and let all cook together
5. When serving, mix the sauce with egg yolks
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kapauner-Farce (Capon Forcemeat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 drachm raw, boned meat from capon breasts
- 32 drachm bacon
- 32 drachm bread crusts, soaked in wine and squeezed dry
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- brandied dough made from 1/2 liter milk
- salt and spices as needed

## Directions

1. Finely chop together the capon meat and bacon with a kitchen knife
2. Place in a mortar and pound together with bread crusts soaked in wine, 3 egg yolks, 1 whole egg, and brandied dough
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Season to taste with salt and spices
5. Use for stuffing or fill a loaf mold and cook in steam

title: Kapauner-Farce anderer Art (Capon Forcemeat, Alternative Method)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 drachm roasted meat from boned capon breasts
- 40 drachm cooked calf's udder, finely chopped
- 32 drachm bread crusts, soaked in water and squeezed dry
- 1/2 tablespoon finely chopped fresh parsley
- equal amount of finely chopped mushrooms
- 3 egg yolks
- 10 drachm butter

## Directions

1. Place bread crusts in a casserole with 1/2 tablespoon finely chopped parsley, equal amount of finely chopped mushrooms, 3 egg yolks, and 10 drachm butter
2. Cook over heat, stirring constantly, until a thick mass is formed
3. Set aside and let cool completely
4. Beat in 2 more egg yolks and stir over heat until the dough pulls away from the spoon
5. Transfer the dough to a deep bowl and set aside to cool
6. Pound this cold bread dough and the chopped meat together finely in a mortar
7. Pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
8. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
9. Use as forcemeat filling

title: Kapauner-Wurst (Capon Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Breast and all good meat from a capon braised in its own juices
- Two bread rolls (Semmeln), trimmed and soaked in milk
- Finely cut lemon zest
- Three to four egg yolks
- Salt
- Nutmeg flower

## Directions

1. Take the breast and all good meat from a capon that has been braised in its own juices and cut very fine with a knife
2. Cook two trimmed bread rolls soaked in milk on the fire while constantly stirring until soft
3. After fine cooking, add the finely cut capon meat, finely cut lemon zest, three to four egg yolks, necessary salt, and nutmeg flower
4. Stir everything well together
5. Fill into intestines of desired size
6. Blanch them in water
7. Then roast them on the grill
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kapern-Sauce (Caper Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a little onion, finely diced
- a few spoonfuls flour browned in butter or other fat
- one quarter-litre milk
- 4 loth capers
- broth if needed
- lemon juice
- pepper
- lemon peel (optional)

## Directions

1. Let finely diced onion run golden in butter or other fat with a few spoonfuls of browned flour
2. Add one quarter-litre milk and 4 loth capers
3. If too thick, pour in more broth
4. If the sauce is to be served over a roasted hare or calf's leg, add the pan drippings from the roast and let it boil together thoroughly
5. Season with lemon juice, pepper, and lemon peel as desired
6. Pour the sauce over the roast when serving
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Karameldis, helles (Light Caramel Glaze)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- a proportionate quantity of butter
- sugar and water (as for 'Clear Sugar' No. 1542)

## Directions

1. Cook a proportionate quantity of butter until it reaches the thread stage ('clear sugar' No. 1542).
2. When a cake has been covered with any hot caramel and somewhat over-dried in the oven, pour the sugar over it and set the cake cold.
3. For a cake or a cake of about 30 drams, 24 drams of sugar are cooked with 1/4 liter of water to the thread stage.

title: Karamelpudding (Caramel Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ liter milk
- ½ liter whipped cream (Schlagobers)
- 5 dag sugar
- 7 dag butter
- 7 dag flour
- 2 dl boiling milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. In a pot, brown the sugar, then quickly mix with the butter and flour
2. Pour in the boiling milk, bring to a boil, remove from heat, and stir until the mixture is cold
3. Fold in the egg yolks and beaten egg whites
4. Fill into a pudding mold and let set in a water bath for 1 hour
5. Turn out, cool, pour the following crème over it, and garnish with sweetened whipped cream

title: Karamelpudding Crème (Caramel Pudding Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dag sugar
- 1 dl water
- ½ liter milk
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 dag sugar

## Directions

1. In a pot, toast the sugar until dark, then deglaze with water and bring to a boil
2. Pour into a mixing bowl, mix with the milk, egg yolks, and sugar, and beat over a water bath until thick

title: Karfiol (Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head cauliflower
- salted water
- butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and divide the cauliflower into florets.
2. Place in cold salted water and cook for 20-30 minutes.
3. Drain and arrange.
4. Pour over with bread crumbs roasted in butter.

title: Karfiol mit Butter (Cauliflower with Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head cauliflower
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves and lower stalk from the cauliflower, wash it, place in cold salt water, cook covered 20-30 minutes, drain it.
2. Carefully place on a dish with the beautiful side facing up, pour over with breadcrumbs toasted in hot butter until still liquid.

title: Karfiol mit Béchamel (Cauliflower with Béchamel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head cauliflower
- 2 dag Parmesan cheese
- 1/2 liter milk
- 4 dag butter
- flour
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook the cauliflower head until soft, drain it, place in a buttered dish and brush with cooled béchamel, sprinkle with grated cheese or breadcrumbs, gratinée in the oven 15-20 minutes.
2. Serve in the dish.
3. For the béchamel: heat 4 dag butter in a smaller casserole, add flour to make a light roux, pour in the milk, salt it, let it cook down to a thick consistency for a few minutes and cool completely.
4. Stir in 3 egg yolks, then fold in 3 stiffly beaten egg whites, spread this mixture over the cauliflower.

title: Karfiol mit Hollandaise (Cauliflower with Hollandaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head cauliflower
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 spoon tarragon vinegar
- 1 spoon lemon juice
- 1 spoon water
- 10 dag clarified butter
- salt
- mustard
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Make a warm mayonnaise (see Appetizers: Eggs with Mayonnaise) from 4 egg yolks, 1 spoon tarragon vinegar, 1 spoon lemon juice, and 1 spoon water.
2. Pull it from the heat slightly and stir in 10 dag clarified but only lukewarm butter thread by thread.
3. Season with salt, mustard, and vinegar and serve separately to the cooked cauliflower.
4. The sauce must be thick-liquid.
5. It can also be served with asparagus or cooked warm fish.
6. If the hollandaise cannot be served immediately, place in a water bath so it does not break.
7. If it breaks, add a little cold water and stir until smooth.

title: Karfiol mit Schinken oder Käse (Cauliflower with Ham or Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 small heads cauliflower
- 5 dag ham
- 2 dag Parmesan cheese
- butter

## Directions

1. Place the cooked cauliflower on a plate with parchment paper, add salt if needed, sprinkle one head with chopped ham and the other with grated cheese, serve hot butter separately.
2. You can also sprinkle the cauliflower with chopped parsley.

title: Karfiolsuppe (Cauliflower Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large head of cauliflower
- salt
- water
- 6 dkg butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Remove leaves from cauliflower, divide, and wash
2. Place in cold salted water
3. Cook for 20 minutes and pass through a sieve
4. Make a parsley roux with 6 dkg butter
5. Pour the cauliflower water over it
6. Add the strained cauliflower mixture
7. Salt and cook for 15 minutes
8. Serve with bread slices

title: Karotten glasiert (Glazed Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- 5 dkg hot butter
- 5-6 pieces of sugar cubes
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the carrots into thin pieces, or if small, leave whole and prepare the same way as the glazed turnips.

title: Karpfen gebacken (Fried Carp)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carp, scaled and gutted
- Salt
- Breadcrumbs for breading
- Lard or fat for frying
- Lemon slices
- Parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Clean, gut, and wash the carp
2. Cut the fish in half and cut three-finger-width pieces through it
3. Hack the pieces
4. Salt each piece well and let rest for 1 hour
5. Wipe off with a cloth
6. Bread the pieces thoroughly
7. Fry very slowly in plenty of hot lard or fat
8. Drain on blotting paper
9. Arrange on a long platter
10. Top with lemon slices and parsley garnish
11. Serve
12. If cooking the heads as well, fry them whole at the very end very slowly

title: Karpfen in einer böhmischen Sauce (Carp in Bohemian Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- wine vinegar
- beer
- onions
- lemon peel
- parsley roots
- celery
- butter
- bread crust
- sugar

## Directions

1. Open the carp as large as needed, remove the insides, carefully remove the gallbladder, take out the blood and add a couple drops of wine vinegar to it
2. Cut the carp into pieces as desired
3. Place half vinegar and half beer on the fire in a pan or casserole, add some cut onions, lemon peel, some pieces of parsley roots, and finely cut celery
4. Boil this together well, then add the carp pieces with the bundle and a piece of butter, and let everything cook well and slowly
5. You can also let some bread crust cook with it if desired
6. Before serving, add the blood and a piece of sugar, let it boil a little more with these additions, and arrange the carp with the vegetables on the table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Karpfen in saurer brauner Sauce (Carp in Sour Brown Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decilitres butter or fat
- 1 piece finely chopped onion
- very little finely chopped root vegetables
- 12 decilitres flour
- 1/4 litre vinegar
- 1/2 litre water (or as much liquid as needed for sauce)
- 3 sprigs thyme
- 2 crushed juniper berries
- 1 bay leaf
- 5 grains of each type of spice
- 5 sprigs parsley tied together
- 1 small piece of lemon peel
- approximately 1 kilo carp, cut into 2-finger wide pieces
- carp blood
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 1 1/4 litres sour cream
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. In 16 decilitres of butter or fat, add 1 piece of finely chopped onion and very little finely chopped root vegetables
2. Allow this to roast covered for a short time
3. Add 12 decilitres flour and let it brown
4. Add 1/4 litre vinegar, 1/2 litre water (or as much liquid as needed for sauce), 3 sprigs thyme, 2 crushed juniper berries, 1 bay leaf, 5 grains of each type of spice, 5 sprigs parsley tied together, and 1 small piece of lemon peel
5. Let this simmer for 1/2 hour
6. Place approximately 1 kilo of carp, cut into 2-finger wide pieces, in a casserole
7. Pour the sauce over and let it simmer covered for another 1/2 hour
8. Arrange the fish elevated on a platter
9. Mix the carp blood with 1 tablespoon vinegar, then with 1 1/4 litres sour cream and 3 egg yolks, stirring well
10. Stir this mixture into the sauce
11. Pour part of this sauce over the fish and serve the rest in a sauceboat
12. Serve with dumplings, noodles, or potato dumplings

title: Karpfen mit Paprika-Sauce (Carp with Paprika Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 decilitres butter
- 1 piece finely chopped onion
- approximately 1 kilo cleaned carp, cut into 2-finger wide and 1-finger long pieces
- 5 sprigs parsley tied together
- approximately 1/2 litre sour cream
- paprika to taste
- capers or lemon juice to taste

## Directions

1. Place 10 decilitres butter in a casserole, add a piece of finely chopped onion
2. Add approximately 1 kilo of cleaned carp, cut into 2-finger wide and 1-finger long pieces, and 5 sprigs parsley tied together
3. Let this roast covered for approximately 15 minutes
4. Pour approximately 1/2 litre sour cream over it
5. Let this simmer covered for 1/4 hour
6. Add paprika to taste, or a tablespoon of capers or lemon juice as desired
7. Let it come to a boil
8. Carefully remove the fish so it does not fall apart
9. Serve with potatoes, noodles, or similar

title: Karpfen schwarz oder böhm. Fisch (Carp Black or Bohemian Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5-1 kilogram carp (male)
- 6 decagrams root vegetables
- Some fish blood
- 8-10 dried prunes
- 0.5 liter beer
- 10 decagrams elderberry sauce
- 2 decagrams gingerbread
- 1 onion
- 6 peppercorns and spice berries
- 4 cloves
- A piece of ginger
- Lemon peel
- 1 piece of garlic
- Grated gingerbread
- Salt
- Vinegar
- 2 bay leaves
- Pinch of thyme
- Elderberry sauce
- Water
- Sugar for browning
- 2 decagrams fat or butter
- Flour
- Raisins
- 6 swelled, sliced almonds
- Several half nuts

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and slice the root vegetables into leaves
2. Cook the vegetables with 1 onion, fish blood, 6 peppercorns and spice berries, 4 cloves, a piece of ginger, lemon peel, 1 piece of garlic, grated gingerbread, salt, vinegar, beer, 2 bay leaves, a pinch of thyme, and elderberry sauce with enough water to cover for about 1 hour
3. Cut the fish into two-finger-width pieces
4. Place the fish pieces in the broth and let them simmer gently for 9-10 minutes
5. Remove the fish and keep it warm
6. Strain the broth through a sieve
7. In a casserole, brown 1 spoonful of sugar in 2 decagrams hot fat or butter
8. Add a very small amount of flour, stir it, and let it cool
9. Pour the broth over it
10. Add the prunes, some raisins, 6 swelled sliced almonds, and several half nuts
11. Season with salt, acid, and sugar to taste
12. Let it cook for 15-20 minutes
