13. Pour the sauce over the fish pieces and serve with Bohemian dumplings

title: Karpfen, blau geſotten (Blue Carp)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.2 kilograms carp
- 6 decagrams root vegetables
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Water for court-bouillon

## Directions

1. In a large casserole, cook a court-bouillon (see explanations) with plenty of salt and vinegar for 30 minutes
2. Prepare the fish and halve it
3. Cut into three-finger-width pieces
4. Place these pieces in the boiling court-bouillon
5. Withdraw from heat immediately and let rest for 8-10 minutes
6. Lift onto a long platter
7. Serve warm with boiled potatoes, or cold with vinegar and oil, or with sauce tartare

title: Karpfen, mit Blut gedünstет (Carp Braised with Blood)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- salt
- butter
- onion
- wine vinegar
- pepper
- cloves
- lemon peel
- bay leaves
- pea broth
- fat
- sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Open the carp so you get the blood cleanly, scale it, and cut it into pieces like the previous recipe
2. You can also keep the bundle if the gallbladder has been removed from it
3. Sprinkle the carp with a little salt
4. Put a piece of butter and a little cut onion on the bottom of a pot, place the carp on top
5. Add a little wine vinegar, pepper, several cloves, lemon peel, and bay leaves
6. Braise the carp with these, turn the pieces once, and when it is almost braised through, add a little pea broth
7. Make a brown roux with a piece of fat, a piece of sugar, and a spoonful of flour, add it to the pot
8. Let it boil a little more with these additions
9. Before serving, add the carp blood, let it come to a boil once more, arrange the carp on a dish, and pour the sauce over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Karpfen-Pâtete (Carp Pâté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 scaled carp
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Butter dough
- Cold fish farce

## Directions

1. Open, clean, and wash a scaled carp.
2. Sprinkle with salt and braise in the oven with occasional basting.
3. Meanwhile, line a fish mold brushed with butter and lined with flour with finger-thick rolled-out butter dough.
4. Spread 1 finger high with cold fish farce.
5. Place the roasted but cold carp upon it, spread with some farce again, and fill up.
6. Cover with a finger-thick butter dough plate fitted to the size of the fish mold, whose edge is pressed together with that of the side wall.
7. Score the dough plate several times with a knife and brush with beaten eggs.

title: Karpfen-Ragout (Carp Ragout)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decilitres butter or fat
- 1 piece onion
- some root vegetables, finely chopped
- 1 bay leaf
- 5 sprigs thyme
- 5 grains of each type of spice
- 1 fish head, chopped
- 1 kilo carp, cut into 2-finger wide and 1-finger long pieces
- 1/3 litre white or red wine
- some meat broth or water
- 8 decilitres black bread

## Directions

1. In a casserole, place 16 decilitres butter or fat, then a piece of onion and some root vegetables, finely chopped, 1 bay leaf, 5 sprigs thyme, and 5 grains of each type of spice
2. Lay the chopped fish head on top and let it simmer covered for approximately 1/4 hour
3. Add 1 kilo carp cut into 2-finger wide and 1-finger long pieces and 1/3 litre white or red wine, some meat broth or water, and 8 decilitres black bread
4. Let this simmer covered and gently for 1/2 hour
5. Arrange the fish pieces going around on a platter, strain and degrease the sauce
6. Adjust the seasoning with vinegar or lemon and salt, and let the sauce come to a boil
7. Pour over the fish
8. In the centre, serve a ragout of crayfish, mushrooms, artichoke hearts, and similar items

title: Kartoffel-Puree (Purée de pommes de terre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- weich gekochte Erdäpfel, ungefähr 1/2 Kilo
- 8 Deca Butter
- 1/4 Liter reifer Ober- oder Milch
- Salz
- braun geröstete Zwiebel oder Semmelbrößel

## Directions

1. weich gekochte Erdäpfel werden geschält und in eine Casserole passiert
2. 8 Deca Butter wird beigegeben
3. mit 1/4 Liter reifer Ober- oder Milch übergossen
4. gesalzen und gut verrührt und gekocht
5. braun geröstete Zwiebel oder in Butter geröstete Semmelbrößel wird darüber gegeben

title: Kartoffel-Rostbraten à l'Esterhazy (Beef Roast Esterhazy)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fat or marrow from beef roast
- 1 large onion
- 1 small yellow turnip
- salt
- seasoning
- flattened beef roast pieces
- black bread
- vinegar or wine and broth or water
- 1/2 liter cream
- 1 tablespoon capers
- anchovy fillets

## Directions

1. Place fat and minced beef bones in a casserole
2. Add 1 large onion and 1 small yellow turnip, both sliced leafy
3. Add salt and seasoning to taste
4. Place the flattened beef roast pieces on top
5. Braise covered until they have reduced
6. Add a piece of black bread and enough vinegar or wine and broth or water so the liquid rises just above the meat
7. Braise covered for 1 hour
8. Transfer the roast to another casserole
9. Remove fat from the sauce and strain it over the roast
10. Add 1/2 liter cream
11. Add 1 tablespoon chopped capers and anchovy fillets, as many pieces as there are roast pieces
12. Braise these gently until completely tender
13. 1/2 liter cream is sufficient for 6 roast pieces

title: Kartoffel-Sauce (Potato Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- About 8 tablespoons fat
- Some finely sliced onion
- Equal amount of root vegetables finely sliced
- Some herbs and spices
- 8 tablespoons flour
- 6 tablespoons vinegar
- About 1/2 liter soup
- Some tablespoons sour cream
- Some salt
- Some lemon zest

## Directions

1. In a saucepan add about 8 tablespoons fat, some finely sliced onion, equal amount of finely sliced root vegetables, some herbs and spices
2. Let it roast and dust with 8 tablespoons flour
3. Add 6 tablespoons vinegar, about 1/2 liter soup, some tablespoons sour cream, some salt and some lemon zest
4. Let it cook very well

title: Kartoffel-Sauce (Potato Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter
- thinly sliced onions
- diced root vegetables
- some parsley and spices
- 8 tablespoons flour
- 6 tablespoons vinegar
- approximately 1/2 liter broth
- several spoonfuls sour cream
- salt
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Put butter in a saucepan with thinly sliced onions and diced root vegetables, some parsley and spices, and let it roast
2. Dust with 8 tablespoons flour
3. Add 6 tablespoons vinegar, approximately 1/2 liter broth, several spoonfuls sour cream, some salt, and lemon peel
4. Let it boil very thoroughly
5. Pass the sauce through a sieve over raw, peeled, diced potatoes
6. Let it continue cooking covered until the potatoes are completely tender

title: Kartoffel-Sauce mit Sardellen (Potato Sauce with Anchovies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- some onion
- 8 tablespoons butter or lard
- 2 large boiled, passed potatoes
- 6 pieces finely cut anchovies
- 1/2 liter broth
- several spoonfuls sour cream

## Directions

1. Let some onion roast in 8 tablespoons butter or lard
2. Add 2 large boiled, passed potatoes and 6 pieces finely cut anchovies
3. Pour in with 1/2 liter broth and several spoonfuls sour cream
4. Let it boil thoroughly
5. Pass it through a sieve or serve unpasseed as desired

title: Kartoffel-Wurst (Consommé garni au pain de pommes de terre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 deca butter
- 18 deca cooked, pressed potatoes
- 1 teaspoon fine Gries
- salt
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg whites
- butter for greasing
- flour for lining

## Directions

1. Beat 6 deca butter until fluffy
2. Mix in 18 deca cooked, pressed potatoes
3. Add 1 teaspoon fine Gries, some salt, 2 egg yolks and 1 whole egg, mixing well together
4. Lightly fold in the snow from 2 egg whites
5. Grease small molds with butter and line with flour
6. Fill halfway
7. Cook in steam and serve with brown sauce

title: Kartoffelkloße (Potato Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Vierding (about 4 oz) butter
- 3 whole eggs
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 spoonfuls sweet cream
- 4 Loth (about 2 oz) sugar
- orange blossom water
- 3/4 pound grated potatoes
- lard for frying
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Beat butter with 3 whole eggs and 5 egg yolks into a foam
2. Add 3 spoonfuls of sweet cream and 4 Loth sugar, and some orange blossom water
3. Slowly stir in 3/4 pound of grated potatoes until the mass is stiff enough to form dumplings
4. Fry them until golden in lard
5. Serve with a cream sauce
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kartoffelknödel (Potato Dumpling)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg potatoes
- 2 sliced bread rolls
- Salt
- 1 tablespoon semolina
- Flour
- Bread cubes roasted in fat

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes in their skins the day before
2. Strain and refrigerate
3. Before use, peel and pass through a ricer
4. Mix with bread cubes that have been roasted in hot fat and cut into cubes
5. Add salt, 1 tablespoon semolina, and flour stirred in by the spoonful
6. Add flour until dumplings show no cracks
7. Form dumplings
8. Place in a casserole in much boiling salt water
9. Cook slowly for 5-8 minutes
10. Serve like Semmelknödel

title: Kartoffelpolenta (Potato Polenta)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram round potatoes
- 7 dekagrams butter
- 3 eggs
- salt
- 3 dekagrams Parmesan
- 15 dekagrams flour

## Directions

1. To the creamed 7 dekagrams butter, stir in 3 eggs, salt, 1/2 kilogram cooked, passed, cooled potatoes, 3 dekagrams Parmesan and 15 dekagrams flour
2. Place the mixture in a well-buttered napkin and proceed as with the Serviettenknödel
3. Pour melted butter over the arranged slices

title: Kartoffelpudding (Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dkg butter
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- 6 bread rolls (Semmeln)
- ½ liter milk
- 18 dkg cooked and passed potatoes
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cream together 7 dkg butter
2. Mix in 3 egg yolks and salt
3. Add 6 bread rolls (trimmed, soaked in milk, and well drained)
4. Mix in 18 dkg cooked, passed, and cooled potatoes
5. Fold in 3 stiffly beaten egg whites
6. Fill into a pudding mold and steam for ¾ hour
7. Loosen, turn out, pour melted butter over, and serve warm

title: Kartoffels Torte (Potato Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka finely ground sugar
- 9 egg yolks
- 8 Deka mashed potatoes with skin, strained
- 4 Deka finely chopped citron
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped orange zest
- cinnamon
- 1 crushed spice clove
- 48 Deka roasted, strained potatoes
- 9 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir together 16 Deka finely ground sugar with 9 egg yolks
2. Add 8 Deka mashed potatoes with skin that have been strained, 4 Deka finely chopped citron, 1 teaspoon finely chopped orange zest, cinnamon, 1 crushed spice clove and 48 Deka roasted, strained potatoes
3. Stir for 1.5 minutes
4. Lightly fold in the whites of 9 eggs
5. Pour this mixture into a cake pan coated with butter and lined with white breadcrumbs
6. Bake at low heat for approximately 1 hour in the oven

title: Kartoffelschmarren (Potato Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- 8 dkg fat
- 1 chopped onion

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes, place them in cold salted water, cook for 20-30 minutes, drain them, peel them, and cut them into leaf-like slices.
2. In a casserole, roast 1 chopped onion in 8 dkg hot fat (the onion can also be omitted).
3. Add the potatoes, salt them, and roast them with frequent stirring until they have a brown crust.

title: Kartoffelstrudel (Potato Strudel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 dkg Flour
- 1 Egg
- Salt
- Butter (walnut-sized)
- Almost 1 dl lukewarm water
- 5 dkg Raisins
- 10 dkg Butter (for filling)
- 8 dkg Sugar
- 3 Egg yolks
- ½ Lemon (juice and zest)
- 6 dkg softened grated almonds
- 26 dkg cooked mashed cooled potatoes
- 2 dl Cream
- 3 Egg whites

## Directions

1. Make strudel dough from flour, egg, salt, butter, and lukewarm water
2. After resting, stretch thinly on floured cloth
3. Dot with butter and trim edges
4. Spread filling on dough and sprinkle raisins on top
5. Roll up strudel and place in buttered baking pan
6. Bake on higher heat until light brown
7. Baste frequently with hot milk during baking
8. Cut into pieces, sprinkle with sugar, and serve warm
9. For the filling: cream together butter, sugar, and egg yolks, add lemon juice and zest, grated almonds, mashed potatoes, and cream, fold in beaten egg whites (whites should be beaten while dough is resting)

title: Kartoffelsuppe (Potato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg round potatoes
- salt
- caraway
- marjoram
- water
- 5 dkg fat
- 6 tablespoons root vegetables (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel and wash the potatoes
2. Cut them into cubes
3. Set them to cook with salt, caraway, marjoram, and water for 30 minutes
4. Make a light onion roux from 5 dkg fat
5. Pour the potato water over it
6. Add the potatoes and cook well together
7. Optionally, add separately cooked root vegetables cut into cubes

title: Kastanien kochen (Boiled Chestnuts)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Kastanien (chestnuts)
- kaltes Wasser

## Directions

1. Die Kastanien werden am Rücken eingeschnitten und in eine Casserolle gegeben, mit so viel kaltem Wasser übergossen, daß sie davon bedeckt sind, und zugedeckt gekocht, bis sie weich sind.

title: Kastanien-Kochli (Chestnut Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka butter, beaten
- 16 Deka vanilla sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 20 Deka roasted or cooked and sieved chestnuts
- 8 egg whites
- Optional: 1 knife-tip grated nutmeg and 6 pieces blanched, finely ground almonds

## Directions

1. Beat butter gradually with vanilla sugar, egg yolks, and roasted or cooked and sieved chestnuts
2. After ½ hour, lightly mix in the snow from 8 egg whites
3. Fill a butter-brushed, flour-dusted mold with this mixture
4. Bake for ¾ hour in the oven
5. If desired, nutmeg and blanched, finely ground almonds can be stirred in with the chestnuts
6. Serve the chestnut souffle with water-chocolate sauce and whipped cream

title: Kastanien-Radetzky-Torte (Chestnut Radetzky Cake; Tourte à la Radetzky)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 decagrams butter
- 32 decagrams sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- 32 decagrams roasted or cooked and sifted chestnuts
- 1 knife-tip of ground coffee
- 12 egg whites
- 4 tablespoons sifted chestnuts
- 8 decagrams sugar
- 2 egg whites
- 2 tablespoons rum
- 4 egg whites
- 16 decagrams finely sifted sugar
- 2 decagrams peeled pistachios
- equal amount of pignoli nuts
- finely sifted sugar
- 2 decagrams chopped pistachios and pignoli nuts

## Directions

1. Beat 24 decagrams butter until fluffy, then stir in 32 decagrams sugar and 16 egg yolks one after another.
2. Mix well with 32 decagrams roasted or cooked and sifted chestnuts and 1 knife-tip of grated coffee, then lightly fold in the beaten snow of 12 egg yolks.
3. Divide this mixture in two parts and fill into two buttered cake forms dusted with flour, baking at low heat for 1 1/2 to 3/4 hours.
4. Remove from the forms and place the baked plates on a sieve to cool completely.
5. Stir together 4 tablespoons sifted chestnuts, 8 decagrams sugar, 2 egg whites, and 2 tablespoons rum until well combined.
6. Spread this filling on one baked plate, cover with the second plate, and place on a baking sheet.
7. Beat 4 egg whites until stiff and fold in 16 decagrams finely sifted sugar, 2 decagrams peeled pistachios, and an equal amount of pignoli nuts.
8. Distribute this mixture evenly over the top of the cake, sprinkle with finely sifted sugar, and decorate with 2 decagrams chopped pistachios and pignoli nuts.
9. Dry in the open oven for 10 to 15 minutes, then remove and decorate the sides with meringue piping using a pastry bag, forming hollow ridges that should resemble cannon barrels.

title: Kastanien-Suppe (Chestnut Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo chestnuts
- boiling water
- cold water
- 1 small piece raw ham
- 1 small onion with 1 clove
- 1 small piece bacon
- 8 deca butter
- 1 liter beef broth
- 1 liter good beef broth
- salt
- roasted bread or bread slices

## Directions

1. Shell the chestnuts, put them in boiling water and let lie until the thin shell can easily be removed with a cloth
2. Put them in cold water and remove all impurities
3. Put the chestnuts in a casserole with 1 small piece raw ham, 1 small onion spiked with 1 clove, 1 small piece bacon, 8 deca butter and 1 liter beef broth and let braise covered until tender
4. Remove onion and ham, stir the chestnuts well, pour on 1 liter good beef broth and pass through a sieve
5. Before serving the soup must be stirred boiling hot, salt it and pour over roasted bread or bread slices

title: Kastanienkugeln (Chestnut Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg cube sugar
- Water
- ½ kg chestnuts
- Grated chocolate

## Directions

1. In a small pot, cook 10 dkg cube sugar with a few spoonfuls of water until thick
2. Wash chestnuts, make a cut on the flat side
3. Cook in a pot for 1 hour
4. Strain, peel, and press through a sieve
5. Mix with the sugar to make a paste
6. Form into small balls
7. Roll in grated chocolate
8. Let dry

title: Kastanienreis (Chestnut Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg chestnuts
- sugar to taste
- milk
- ½ l whipped cream

## Directions

1. Wash chestnuts, make slit on round side, cook in plenty of water for 1½ hours
2. Drain and cool slightly
3. Shell and pass through sieve
4. Stir with sugar to taste and some milk to make thick paste
5. Press mixture through inverted grater into center of glass bowl
6. Arrange sweetened whipped cream around the edges, or arrange whipped cream in center and chestnut rice around edges

title: Kastanientorte (Chestnut Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 14 dkg raw grated almonds
- 6 stiff egg whites
- 25 dkg cooked, passed chestnuts (40 dkg raw)
- 6 dkg vanilla sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 spoons water
- 8 dkg grated chocolate
- 8 dkg chopped butter
- ¼ l schlagobers (whipped cream)
- angelica or candied fruits

## Directions

1. Beat sugar and egg yolks for 30 minutes
2. Mix in raw grated almonds
3. Fold in stiff egg whites together with cooked, passed chestnuts
4. Fill into form
5. Bake at moderate heat
6. Cut in half, coat with filling, reassemble
7. Glaze brown or coat with sweetened schlagobers, decorate with angelica or candied fruits
8. For filling: Beat 6 dkg vanilla sugar with 2 egg yolks and 2 spoons water over steam until thick, cool, fold in 8 dkg grated chocolate and 8 dkg chopped butter, and lightly mix in ¼ l schlagobers

title: Kauli in Buttersauce (Cauliflower in Butter Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower, cleaned and cut into quarters if roses are large
- Salt water
- Flour
- Butter
- Good broth
- Nutmeg, finely ground
- Milk cream
- Fried chicken or frog cutlets

## Directions

1. Cook cleaned cauliflower in salt water
2. Make a roux with 2 to 3 spoonfuls of flour in warmed butter
3. Fill with good broth and add a little finely ground nutmeg and 2 to 3 spoonfuls of milk cream
4. Arrange the cauliflower with shortened stems in beautiful order in the shape of a rose on the platter, raised high
5. Pour the well-cooked sauce over it and let it come to a boil
6. Ensure the roses show beautifully when serving
7. Garnish with fried chicken or frog cutlets
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kauli- oder Blumenkohlsalat (Cauliflower Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful white cauliflower
- Salt water
- Fresh water
- Vinegar
- Oil

## Directions

1. Cook beautiful white cauliflower in whole florets in salted water
2. When the florets are soft enough, drain off the water
3. Rinse them with fresh water so they remain beautifully white
4. Salt them slightly
5. Arrange them neatly on a serving dish
6. Serve with vinegar and oil
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kefir (Kefir)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh milk
- Contents of one bottle of 'Vindobona' kefir pure cultures

## Directions

1. Proceed the same way as in the preparation of sour milk described above, using 'Vindobona' kefir pure cultures as the ferment instead

title: Kernfett-Knödel ohne Semmeln (Bone Marrow Quenelles without Rolls)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter flour
- 1 1/4 liter sour cream
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- salt
- 22 Loffel bone marrow, freed from all skin, finely diced, soaked and squeezed out in cold water

## Directions

1. Mix into 3/4 liter flour 1 1/4 liter sour cream, 2 whole eggs, 1 egg yolk and salt into a dough
2. Add 22 Loffel bone marrow that has been freed from skin, finely diced, soaked and squeezed out in cold water
3. Stir well together and let stand covered for 1/2 hour
4. Put knödel of desired size into boiling beef broth and cook covered for 20 minutes
5. Serve either with soup or drizzled with butter and roll crumbs and serve with roasts, lung roasts, roast beef, etc.

title: Kernwasser (Kernel Water)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds of sugar
- 9 measures of water
- 12 measures of brandy
- 1 pound of crushed bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Dissolve 4 pounds of sugar in 9 measures of water
2. Add 12 measures of brandy and 1 pound of crushed bitter almonds
3. Let the mixture stand for some time in the sun
4. Filter it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kidneys with Piquant Sauce (Rognons de veau sauce piquante)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Root vegetables, finely sliced
- Vinegar
- Water

## Directions

1. Cook finely sliced root vegetables in vinegar and water

title: Kipfelkoch (Kipferl and Apple Casserole)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 old kipferl (croissant-like pastries)
- ½ liter milk
- 2 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- ½ kg apples
- raisins
- 5 dkg sugar
- 5 dkg melted butter

## Directions

1. Slice the kipferl into fine leaves, pour over with ½ liter milk beaten with 2 eggs and let stand for 30 minutes.
2. In a well-buttered casserole form, place first a layer of kipferl, then a layer of peeled apples sliced into leaves, raisins and sugar.
3. Fill the form this way, pour over with ¼ liter milk beaten with 2 egg yolks and 5 dkg sugar.
4. Drizzle with 5 dkg melted butter, bake in the oven for 30–45 minutes and serve in the form.
5. Instead of apples, pitted cherries can be used, in which case omit the raisins.

title: Kirchschetslitz Potato Puree Soup (Puree aux pommes de terre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 drams onion
- 1 piece celery
- 1 carrot
- 1 parsley leaf
- 16 drams butter
- approximately 2 liters cold water
- 1 heaping liter peeled potatoes, sliced
- 1/4 liter cream
- nutmeg
- salt

## Directions

1. Finely slice the onion, celery, carrot, and parsley
2. Toast these vegetables in butter
3. Add approximately 2 liters of cold water
4. Add sliced peeled potatoes and simmer until completely soft
5. Pass the entire mixture through a sieve
6. Add 1/4 liter of cream, nutmeg, and salt
7. Bring to a boil once more
8. If the potato soup is too thick, it can be thinned with broth, milk, or water

title: Kirsch-Limonade (Cherry Lemonade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter fresh water
- juice of 3 lemons
- 1/4 liter cherry juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. In 1 liter fresh water, add the strained juice of 3 lemons, 1/4 liter cherry juice and sugar

title: Kirsch-Strudel (Cherry Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Strudel dough made from 1/2 liter flour
- fresh cherries, pitted
- stems removed

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Apple Strudel (No. 1238)
2. Fill instead with apples with raw cherries, freed from stems
3. Treat further as indicated

title: Kirsch-Äpfel (Cherry Apples)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherry apples
- Apple corer
- Lemon water (cold and hot)
- Sugar water (made from sugar syrup as described in recipe 1841)

## Directions

1. Core the apples with an apple corer
2. Peel them neatly
3. Place each apple immediately in cold lemon water
4. Then place all apples together in hot lemon water where they should cook for 5 minutes
5. Remove the apples and quickly immerse them three times in fresh cold water
6. Place them in the preserving jars which should already contain some sugar water
7. Pour the remaining sugar water over them and proceed as already explained

title: Kirschbranntwein (Cherry Brandy)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 21-27 pieces of cherries
- 1 piece clove
- 1 small piece cinnamon
- 1 liter good brandy (corn, wheat or fruit brandy)

## Directions

1. Place cherries, 1 piece clove and 1 small piece cinnamon in a bottle
2. Pour 1 liter good brandy over them
3. Seal the bottle well and proceed as with the Herbal Brandy

title: Kirschel-Sulz (Cherry Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cherry juice
- refined sugar
- isinglass

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as Orange Jelly (No. 1660), using cherry juice.

title: Kirschen and Wetzeln (Cherries and Medlars)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- large, dry, black cherries
- 2-3 fingers high of sugar water
- sugar for lightly inserted preserved fruit (as in Nr. 1841)
- 60 decilitres sugar
- medlars
- stems

## Directions

1. Remove stems from large, dry, black cherries and place them in preserving jars
2. Pour in 2-3 fingers high of sugar water (sugar for lightly inserted preserved fruit, Nr. 1841) with 60 decilitres sugar
3. Proceed further as with the Muscatel pears (Nr. 1842)
4. For medlars, treat them similarly, only cut the stems somewhat shorter and leave them on, and boil 80 decilitres sugar in 1 litre water

title: Kirschen aufzubewahren (Preserving Cherries)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- black, sour cherries
- bottles with large openings
- cork stoppers
- resin

## Directions

1. Pick black, sour cherries when fully ripe on clear sunny days, not shortly after rain
2. Pick with gloved hands
3. Cut the stems very close to the cherries with scissors
4. Place them carefully into very well-dried bottles with large openings
5. Cork them well and seal with resin so no air can enter
6. Set in a cellar; they can be kept fresh until after Christmas, especially if put up late
7. Alternatively, after placing cherries in bottles and stopping them with a well-fitting lid bound tightly with a bladder, hang them in a well, where they will also keep very well
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kirschen und Weichseln (Cherries and Morello Cherries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Large, dry, black cherries
- Sugar water (made from sugar syrup with 60 decigrams sugar per liter, as described in recipe 1841)
- Morello cherries
- Sugar water (made with 80 decigrams sugar per liter of water)

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the cherries
2. Place them in preserving jars
3. Pour 2 to 3 fingers' height of sugar water into the jars
4. Proceed as with the Meusel-Cateller pears (recipe 1842)
5. For morello cherries, cut the stems slightly, leave them attached, and use sugar water made with 80 decigrams sugar per liter of water, treating them the same way

title: Kirschen- oder Weichsel Gnaden mit Mandeln (Cherry or Morello Cherry Blessings with Almonds)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 deca butter
- 30 deca peeled almonds
- 2 egg whites
- 30 deca lemon sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites
- 30 deca flour
- cherries or morello cherries

## Directions

1. Beat 30 deca butter until fluffy
2. Mix with 30 deca peeled almonds, finely ground with 2 egg whites
3. Stir in 30 deca lemon sugar and 10 egg yolks one after another
4. Finally lightly mix in the stiff peaks of 10 egg whites simultaneously with 30 deca flour
5. Fill 2 fingers high into a baking pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
6. Allow cherries or morello cherries, 2 and 2 one upon the other, to sink into the dough
7. Bake for 3/4 hours in an adequately hot oven
8. Further treatment is as with the Brown Cherry or Morello Cherry Cake (No. 1460)

title: Kirschen- oder Weichsel Stufen mit Butter und Mehl (Cherry or Morello Cherry Layers with Butter and Flour)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 deca butter
- 30 deca orange sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- 30 deca flour
- cherries or morello cherries

## Directions

1. Stir 30 deca butter with 30 deca orange sugar and 10 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
2. Lightly mix in the stiff peaks of 8 egg whites with 30 deca flour
3. Fill into a baking pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
4. Allow cherries or morello cherries to sink into the dough as desired
5. Bake for approximately 1 hour at low heat in the oven
6. Treat as with the Brown Cherry or Morello Cherry Cake (No. 1460)

title: Kirschen- und Zwetschkenkuchen (Cherry and Plum Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 dkg butter
- 3 whole eggs
- 16 dkg sugar
- 16 dkg flour
- 1 kg cherries or plums
- Sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Cream together 16 dkg butter
2. Gradually stir in 3 whole eggs and 16 dkg sugar
3. Stir in 16 dkg flour by the spoonful
4. Spread finger-thick on a buttered, floured baking sheet
5. Cover with cherries or pitted, halved plums with round side down
6. Bake at higher heat until light brown
7. Cut, sprinkle with sugar
8. Serve cooled

title: Kirschenkuchen mit Mandeln und Mehl (Cherry Cake with Almonds and Flour)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca butter
- 24 deca caster sugar
- 8 deca peeled almonds
- juice of one lemon
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- 28 deca flour

## Directions

1. Mix 28 deca butter with 24 deca caster sugar until well combined
2. Add 8 deca peeled almonds, finely ground with lemon juice, and 8 egg yolks
3. Stir for 1/2 hour
4. Lightly mix in the stiff peaks of 8 egg whites with 28 deca flour
5. Fill into a baking pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
6. Further treatment is as with the Brown Cherry or Morello Cherry Cake (No. 1460)

title: Kirschenstudien (Cherry Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough as prepared for flat cakes
- Fresh cherries or morello cherries
- Butter for the baking sheet
- Egg for brushing
- Finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough for flat cakes
2. Roll out strongly knife-back thick on a flour-dusted board
3. Cut so long and wide that it fits the size of the baking sheet on which the cake will be baked
4. Roll up loosely on the rolling pin (strudel roller) and unroll directly onto a butter-greased baking sheet
5. Cover densely with fresh cherries or morello cherries, leaving a 2-finger-wide empty border on all 4 sides
6. Fold up the border and brush with beaten egg
7. Bake in the oven for 3/4 to 1 hour at fairly strong heat
8. Remove from oven, sprinkle with finely sifted sugar, cut into desired pieces, arrange in a bowl and serve warm or cold as desired

title: Kirschentorte (Cherry Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs worth of butter
- 3 eggs worth of sugar
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 eggs worth of flour
- ½ kg cherries

## Directions

1. Cream together butter weighing the same as 3 eggs with sugar weighing the same as 3 eggs
2. Gradually add 3 whole eggs
3. Mix in flour weighing the same as 3 eggs
4. Pour the mixture into a cake form
5. Top with pitted and wiped cherries
6. Bake at moderate heat
7. Turn out and serve cooled without powdered sugar

title: Kirschknödel (Cherry Dumplings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough, potato dough or yeast dough
- fresh cherries, 4 to 5 per dumpling

## Directions

1. Prepare as plum dumplings, according to choice of strudel dough, potato dough, or yeast dough
2. Instead of one plum, put 4 to 5 fresh cherries in each

title: Kirſchenwaſſer (Cherry Water)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe cherries
- a few cloves
- cinnamon
- 2 measures of water per 1 measure of cherry juice

## Directions

1. Remove the cherries from their stems
2. Pound them with the pits, a few cloves, and cinnamon in a mortar
3. Let them stand for several hours in an earthenware vessel
4. Press them through linen cloth
5. To 1 measure of juice, add 2 measures of water
6. Sweeten to taste
7. Pass it through a napkin
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kirſchenſaft (Cherry Juice)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour cherries
- Three quarter pound sugar per pound of reduced juice

## Directions

1. Pound a quantity of sour cherries and press the juice through a clean napkin
2. Cook it for one hour
3. For every pound of reduced juice take three quarter pound sugar and let both cook until it becomes thick
4. To ensure the juice is reduced enough, pour a few drops on a plate; if it jells immediately, it is done
5. When the juice is cooled, fill it into preserve jars, tie with pig bladders, and keep in a dry place
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kleine Blutwürste (Small Blood Sausages)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half Pfund marrow
- finely cut onions
- shallots
- half Maß good pork or calf blood
- half Maß sweet cream
- salt
- pepper
- marjoram
- narrow casings
- warm water
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut half a pound of marrow into small dice and let it brown in a pot with finely cut onions and shallots
2. Add to half a Maß of good pork or calf blood half a Maß of sweet cream
3. Mix together with salt, pepper, and marjoram
4. Fill into narrow casings and let it simmer in warm water for a while until the blood coagulates
5. Before use, turn them in butter and roast them on the grate
6. They serve as well for side dish as for vegetables
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kleine Erbsen auf englische Art (Petits Pois à l'Anglaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fine green peas
- salt water
- butter
- green parsley
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook fine green peas uncovered in salt water until tender
2. Drain the water thoroughly
3. Arrange the peas on a platter
4. Place a piece of butter mixed with finely chopped green parsley and some salt on top of the peas in small pieces
5. Serve

title: Kleine Käses Kuchen (Small Cheese Cakes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter milk
- 8 deka butter
- 16 deka flour
- 3 whole eggs
- 12 deka grated Parmesan cheese
- salt

## Directions

1. Put 1/4 liter milk and 8 deka butter in a saucepan and bring to a boil together
2. Mix with 16 deka flour
3. Stir over heat until the dough releases from the spoon
4. Transfer to a deep bowl and stir until cold
5. Gradually mix in 3 whole eggs, 12 deka grated Parmesan cheese, and some salt
6. Place walnut-sized pieces on a butter-greased sheet at finger-width distance
7. Brush with beaten eggs
8. Bake at moderate heat in the oven
9. Serve sprinkled with grated Parmesan cheese with tea or as an appetizer

title: Kleine Pästechen mit Béchamel (Small Pastries with Béchamel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry, half finger-thick
- egg yolk
- chicken breasts, diced
- béchamel sauce

## Directions

1. Roll out puff pastry half a finger thick and cut pastries round
2. Place on a baking sheet not too close together
3. Brush with egg yolk and press a circle in the middle with a cutter
4. Bake nicely
5. Once baked, remove the soft dough from the middle
6. Fill with diced chicken breasts in well-prepared béchamel
7. Serve hot
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kleine Speck-Knödel (Small Bacon Quenelles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 Loffel fresh, finely diced bacon
- 24 Loffel grated, finely diced rolls mixed with milk
- 6 Loffel butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- salt
- 6 Loffel flour

## Directions

1. Put the fresh, finely diced bacon in a saucepan and heat it until the bacon is glossy
2. Mix the grated, finely diced rolls with milk, then add the bacon over it and mix well
3. Beat 6 Loffel butter until fluffy, stir in 3 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs
4. Mix in salt, 6 Loffel flour with the bacon and rolls
5. Let stand covered for 1/2 hour
6. Form knödel of desired size and cook as with the previous knödel

title: Kleine Specksknödel (Small Bacon Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca fresh bacon
- 24 deca bread crumbs
- milk
- 6 deca butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- some salt
- 6 deca flour

## Directions

1. Cut the fresh bacon into fine dice and place in a saucepan.
2. Heat it on the fire until the bacon is glossy.
3. Add the bread crumbs that have been cut into dice and soaked in milk.
4. Mix well together.
5. Beat 6 deca butter until creamy.
6. Stir in 3 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, and salt.
7. Add 6 deca flour and the bacon-bread mixture.

title: Kleine Vögel (Small Birds)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Small birds
- Salt
- Lard or butter
- Onion
- Butter
- Paper
- Breadcrumbs
- Onion, roasted
- Purée

## Directions

1. After they are plucked, flamed and cleaned, they are dried with a cloth and salted. Put finely sliced lard or butter in a shallow casserole, cut a piece of onion very finely and add it, then place the birds breast-side down, cover them with buttered paper and stew for 1/2 hour. 20 minutes before serving, place them on strong heat and let them brown quickly, turn the birds around, dust the breasts with breadcrumbs, baste them with their own juices and arrange them raised on a dish with golden roasted onion. Serve with any purée, especially potato purée or also sauerkraut and similar.

title: Kleiner Spanischer Wind (Little Spanish Meringue)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 14 Deca vanilla sugar
- signoli or blanched, finely cut almonds, or similarly prepared pistachios
- finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites until stiff.
2. Lightly mix in 14 Deca vanilla sugar.
3. Using a pastry bag, syringe, or coffee spoon, pipe sharp-pointed mounds onto a baking sheet coated with unsalted bacon fat.
4. Top the points with signoli or blanched, finely cut almonds, or similar prepared pistachios.
5. Sprinkle the whole with finely sifted sugar and let dry slowly in a moderately warm oven.

title: Kleines Flazout in einer Kruste (Pastete) (Small Ragoût in a Pastry Crust)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough, strong knife-back thickness
- Butter
- Flour
- Dried raw peas
- Ragoût of any kind
- Butter dough

## Directions

1. Cut strong knife-back thick butter dough into squares matching the size of the sandal molds.
2. Line sandal molds brushed with butter and dusted with flour with the squares, then fill with dried raw peas, cut away the dough hanging over the edge of the mold all around, place the sandal molds on a baking sheet and bake the pastries at quite strong heat in the oven.
3. When they have a golden color, remove the molds from the oven, cover with a plate, and holding both together firmly, quickly turn the mold and carefully remove it after letting it sit for a few moments.
4. Carefully place the baked shells on a platter, remove the remaining peas inside with a fork, fill the shells with any ragoût, cover with a lid formed from the same dough as the shells and baked to match, place in a platter covered with a napkin and serve hot.

title: Klumscel-Nudeln (Klumscel Noodles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 deka butter
- 5 deka flour
- 14 deka flour
- 4 deka fresh yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
- 7 deka cooled but not cold butter
- 5 deka sifted sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 pinch finely cut lemon peel
- 35 deka flour
- 1/4 liter lukewarm milk

## Directions

1. Work 21 deka butter with 5 deka flour by hand into a dough on a board
2. Roll flat with a rolling pin and let rest in a cool place
3. In a deep bowl, combine 14 deka flour and in its center pour 4 deka fresh yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
4. Lightly stir and cover, let stand in a warm place
5. When this yeast has risen sufficiently, add to it 7 deka cooled but not cold butter, 5 deka sifted sugar, 3 egg yolks, 1 pinch finely cut lemon peel, and 35 deka flour
6. Mix well with 1/4 liter lukewarm milk, and beat the dough until it releases from the spoon
7. Roll out on a flour-dusted board to finger thickness
8. Lay the above butter on it and fold together with the dough as described for butter dough of finest type (No. 1045)
9. After folding 3 times and rolling out to finger thickness, cut into 3 finger-wide and equally long pieces
10. Place on a butter-greased and flour-dusted sheet, making a small indentation with the finger in the center of each piece
11. Cover the sheet with a cloth and place in a warm spot
12. When the pieces have risen about halfway higher, brush with beaten eggs
13. Fill the indentation made with the finger with desired filling and bake at fairly strong heat
14. These cakes require very careful attention

title: Knoblauch's Sauce (Garlic Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece finely crushed garlic
- Some butter
- Butter sauce

## Directions

1. Mix 1 piece finely crushed garlic with some butter
2. Stir into the butter sauce and pass through a sieve

title: Knoblauchsauce (Garlic Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- garlic
- vinegar
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- beef soup
- vinegar
- spices

## Directions

1. To remove the unpleasant taste from the garlic, simmer it first in vinegar
2. Roast the garlic, finely chopped, until golden in butter
3. Mix in breadcrumbs roasted in butter
4. Pour in as much beef soup and vinegar as needed
5. Season as desired and let simmer slowly
6. Before serving, add one piece of butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Knoblauchsauce (Garlic Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Garlic
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs
- Beef broth
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. To remove garlic's unpleasant taste, first simmer it in vinegar.
2. Roast the finely chopped garlic in butter until golden yellow.
3. Mix in breadcrumbs that have been roasted in butter.
4. Add as much beef broth and some vinegar as needed.
5. Let it simmer slowly, seasoned to taste.
6. Before serving, add a small piece of butter.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Knurrhahn in Ol gebraten (Gurnard Fried in Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- 70 dkg Knurrhahn (gurnard)
- Oil
- Petersilie (parsley)
- Sardelle (anchovy)
- Salt
- Milchwaſſer (milk water)

## Directions

1. Scale the fish, clean it, wash it and place in milk water for 30 minutes.
2. Make an incision on the side, salt it and let it rest for 30 minutes, then wipe it off.
3. Heat some spoons of oil in a pan, lay the fish in it, fry on both sides for 10-15 minutes and lift onto a platter.
4. Let finely chopped parsley heat in the oil, add possibly 1 strained anchovy and pour it over the fish.

title: Knurrhahn in Öl gebraten (Gurnard Fried in Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- gurnard fish
- milk water
- salt
- oil
- parsley, finely chopped
- anchovies, passed (optional)

## Directions

1. Scale the fish, remove the guts, wash them, place in milk water for 30 minutes
2. Make an incision on the side, salt them, let them rest for 30 minutes and wipe them off
3. Heat several spoonfuls of oil in a pan, place the fish in it, fry them on both sides for 10-15 minutes and transfer them to a platter
4. In the oil, let finely chopped parsley run through, add optionally 1 passed anchovy and pour it over the fish

title: Knuschtuli's Biscotti
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 14 ounces finely sifted sugar
- 14 ounces flour

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites until stiff
2. Lightly mix in 14 ounces finely sifted sugar and 14 ounces flour
3. Fill this mass into a pastry bag and pipe finger-long biscotti or desired figures onto a baking sheet covered with writing paper
4. Sprinkle with finely ground sugar and bake in low heat in the oven

title: Knödel von Lapin (Rabbit Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit fillets
- rabbit meat from the shanks
- white bread
- milk
- good butter
- salt
- nutmeg
- pepper
- fine dried herbs
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cut some nice fillets from the rabbit, scrape the meat from the shanks, separate skin and sinews
2. Pound finely on a grindstone and then pass through a wire sieve
3. Soak white bread in milk, press it out very strongly, put it with good butter and the fillets in the grindstone
4. Mix in salt, nutmeg, pepper, and fine dried herbs
5. Beat in eggs one at a time, until 5 are added, and pound together a long time until everything is well mixed
6. Form the dumplings on paper brushed with butter and cook them in bouillon or salted water
7. If the mixture is too stiff, beat in another egg, work it well through, and use as desired
8. Make a piquant sauce from the bones
9. Note that when making these dumplings, equal parts butter, rabbit meat, and pressed white bread should be taken
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Knöderl (Dumplings with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- finely chopped ham from 7 dkg roll ham
- 2 stiffly beaten egg whites
- 5 dkg fine breadcrumbs
- 2 dkg flour

## Directions

1. Cream the butter, add egg yolks, salt, chopped parsley, pepper and finely chopped ham
2. Fold in egg whites, breadcrumbs and flour
3. Let stand for a while, form small dumplings and place in boiling beef broth for 5 minutes

title: Kohl eingebrannt (Braised Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-3 heads cabbage
- 4 dkg fat
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean, separate, and wash the cabbage.
2. Place in boiling salted water and cook uncovered for 25-30 minutes.
3. Drain and chop coarsely.
4. Make a roux with 4 dkg fat, add the cabbage water or broth, add the cabbage, season with salt and pepper, and cook down for 20 minutes.

title: Kohl gerollt (Rolled Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large heads cabbage
- 8 dag bacon
- hash
- rice (optional)
- breadcrumbs
- soup broth

## Directions

1. Clean the cabbage, separate leaf by leaf, wash it, place the beautiful leaves in boiling salt water for 10 minutes, then drain and dry well.
2. Lay the leaves flat on a board, brush with hash, roll them, place in an omelet pan, pour in a little broth.
3. Cut the bacon into slices, make some slits in them, cover the cabbage with it, sprinkle breadcrumbs on top, place in the oven to gratinée for 10 minutes.
4. Serve as a side dish to roast beef or beefsteak or as an evening dish.
5. Fresh steamed rice or leftover rice can be mixed into the hash.
6. Leftover cabbage can be used for soup or bound.

title: Kohl mit Reiß und Schinken (Cabbage with Rice and Ham)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful firm cabbage
- Salt water
- Yellow roux
- Finely cut onion
- Pepper
- Broth
- Rice
- Goose fat
- White paper
- Ham, finely cut into triangular pieces
- 5 spoons of cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/2 Seidel of good broth
- Smoked beef

## Directions

1. Wash the cabbage well and cook in salt water
2. Make a yellow roux, let some finely cut onion brown slightly in the roux, add the cabbage and some pepper, pour some broth over it
3. Cook until the sauce is completely reduced
4. Stew the rice separately
5. Coat a pan with goose fat, cut white paper to fit the bottom and coat with the same fat
6. Line the bottom with triangular thin-cut ham pieces arranged so nothing of the bottom shows
7. Add a layer of rice, then a layer of cabbage (stems must be removed), alternating until the pan is full
8. Pour 5 spoons of cream, 6 egg yolks and 1/2 Seidel of good broth (all well mixed) over
9. Place in steam and cook for a good hour
10. Carefully turn onto a serving dish
11. Remove the paper and garnish with beautifully cut smoked beef
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kohl-Strudeln (Cabbage Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cleaned, washed cabbage heads
- salt
- 16 Deka butter
- pepper
- marjoram
- 6 whole eggs
- 1/8 liter milk cream
- 1/2 liter flour for dough
- 6 Deka bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil cabbage heads in salt water, drain and chop very finely
2. Heat 16 Deka butter and braise cabbage with salt, pepper, and marjoram
3. Put into a sieve to drain
4. Beat in 6 whole eggs and add 1/8 liter milk cream
5. Spread on strudel dough made from 1/2 liter flour
6. Sprinkle 6 Deka bread crumbs over it
7. Roll together and treat as previous strudels

title: Kohlen-Wasser (Charcoal Water)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pieces of glowing wood charcoal
- 0.5 liter cold water

## Directions

1. Instead of toasted bread, throw some pieces of glowing wood charcoal into 0.5 liter of cold water.
2. Let it stand uncovered and strain it after some time.

title: Kohlminestrasuppe (Cabbage Minestrone Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large head of cabbage
- ½ kg rice
- 3 dkg Parmesan
- 3 dkg butter
- 2 dkg butter or fat
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Remove bad leaves and stalks from the cabbage, wash and cut into noodle-like pieces
2. Heat 3 dkg butter in a casserole, add the cabbage, salt it and roast under frequent stirring and adding very little broth or water for 30 minutes
3. Braise 8 dkg rice with a scoop of water, salt and 2 dkg butter or fat covered, without stirring much, until soft for 30 minutes
4. Mix the grated Parmesan with the cabbage and rice, put into the soup pot and pour the strained beef broth over it

title: Kohlpflanzen (Cabbage Plants)
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 h cabbage plants
- 5 dkg butter
- parsley
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the plants.
2. Place in boiling salted water and cook uncovered for 25-30 minutes.
3. Drain and chop finely.
4. Make a parsley butter sauce with 5 dkg butter, add the vegetable water or broth, add the cabbage plants, season to taste with salt and pepper, and cook down well.

title: Kohlrüben (Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-6 turnips
- salt
- 5 dkg fat
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the turnips and cut them into leaf-like or dice-like pieces.
2. Place them in cold salted water and cook for 30 minutes.
3. Make a light roux from 5 dkg fat, pour it up with the turnip water or broth, add the turnips, salt to taste, and cook for 20 minutes.

title: Kohlrüben gefüllt (Stuffed Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-6 young kohlrabi
- hashé of 25 dkg roasted veal
- 1/2 cup cream
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the top off each kohlrabi, peel, and hollow out.
2. Cook the kohlrabi with their tops in salted water for 20-30 minutes, drain, and cool.
3. Fill with hashé and replace the tops.
4. Place them in a buttered omelet pan, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, dot with butter.
5. Bake for 10-15 minutes in the oven, basting with cream during baking.
6. Serve on a round plate as a first course or with fine garnish.

title: Kohlrüben glafiert (Glazed Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- 5 dkg hot butter
- 5-6 pieces of sugar cubes
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Peel, wash, and slice the turnips thinly or into cubes.
2. In a saucepan, brown 5-6 sugar cubes in 5 dkg hot butter, add the turnips, season with salt, add a very small amount of water, cover, and stew for 1 hour under occasional shaking of the pan, lightly dust with flour at the end if desired.

title: Kohlrüben mit Butter und Brösel (Kohlrabi with Butter and Breadcrumbs)
body:
## Ingredients

- young or old kohlrabi
- salt
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Peel the kohlrabi, cut them first into fine leaf-like slices, then into noodle-like strips.
2. Place in cold salted water and cook covered for 1 hour (young ones need only 20 minutes), then drain.
3. Place on a serving dish and pour over breadcrumbs toasted in butter.
4. They require a lot of butter.
5. They can also be served as a salad with vinegar and oil.

title: Kohlsuppe (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 smaller heads cabbage
- 1 bread roll
- 6 dkg butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove bad leaves and stalks from the cabbage and wash well.
2. Place in a pot with boiling salt water, cook uncovered for 20 minutes.
3. Strain and pass through a sieve.
4. Make a roux with 6 dkg butter, pour the cabbage water over it, add the passed cabbage, cook for 15 minutes, optionally thicken, and serve with bread rolls.

title: Kohlwürstelpudding (Cabbage Sausage Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 smaller cabbage heads
- 25 dkg salted pork
- 1/2 cup cream
- 10 dkg Parmesan
- 2 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Remove the cabbage of cores and bad leaves, wash it, place in boiling salted water for 5 minutes, drain it, and lay the leaves one by one on a board.
2. For the filling: Mix 2 eggs with half the cream, salt, pepper, and the chopped ham.
3. Fill the cabbage leaves and roll them into sausages.
4. Butter and flour a pudding mold. Mix 2 eggs with the grated cheese.
5. Fill the mold with a portion of eggs and cheese, place cabbage sausages on top, then alternate layers of eggs with cheese and sausages until all is used.
6. Cook the pudding 1 hour in steam, loosen it, turn it out, and serve with hot butter and grated cheese.

title: Kohlwürstl Pudding (Cabbage Sausage Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 smaller cabbage heads
- 25 dag smoked meat
- 1/2 liter cream
- 10 dag Parmesan cheese
- 2 eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Remove the stalks and bad leaves from the cabbage, wash it, place in boiling salt water for 5 minutes, drain, lay the leaves one by one on a board, fill and roll into small sausage shapes.
2. For the filling: mix 2 eggs with half the cream, salt, pepper, and the chopped ham.
3. Butter and flour the pudding mold, add 2 eggs...

title: Kohlſproſſen (Brussels Sprouts)
body:
## Ingredients

- brussels sprouts
- salted water
- 3 dkg hot butter

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the sprouts.
2. Place in a pot with cold salted water and cook uncovered for 15-20 minutes.
3. Drain and place in a saucepan with 3 dkg hot butter, toss in the butter.

title: Kraftbrey für Genesende (Strength Pap for the Recovering)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolks
- sugar
- cinnamon
- almond milk

## Directions

1. Take the yolks from eggs, being careful that no white remains
2. Stir the yolks often and strongly
3. Add sugar and cinnamon
4. Pour in almond milk
5. Cook it into a thin pap
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kraftbrühe mit Wein und Gewürzen (Strength Broth with Wine and Spices)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one well-crushed capon
- twelve live crayfish
- half a pound of white bread, soaked in milk overnight
- two drams of small raisins (currants)
- twelve pieces each of bugloss berries and sebesten
- half a dram each of cardamom, cubeb, nutmeg flower, and cinnamon
- one handful each of endive and lettuce
- one measure of water
- one and a half seidel of malmsey wine

## Directions

1. Crush the capon well and prepare the crayfish
2. Soak the white bread in milk overnight
3. Crush all ingredients small
4. Place everything in a large tin bottle
5. Pour one measure of water and one and a half seidel of malmsey wine over it
6. Seal the bottle tightly
7. Set it in a large kettle with water
8. Let it cook for six hours
9. Press it through a cloth
10. Skim off the fat
11. Allow the remainder to become thick at a cool place
12. Each morning and evening, give several spoonfuls in thin meat broth or warm water
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krafts Suppe für Kranke (Strong Beef Soup for the Sick)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Kilo ausgebeinutes, mageres Ochsenfleisch
- 1 Liter kaltes Wasser
- 1 Prise Salz
- 1 Kaffeelöffel gebrannter Zucker (Karamell)

## Directions

1. 1 Kilo ausgebeinutes, mageres Ochsenfleisch wird in Stücke geschnitten und in einem Mörser fein gestoßen.
2. Das fein gestoßene Fleisch wird in eine Casserolle gegeben und 1 Liter kaltes Wasser darauf gegossen.
3. Zum Kochen stellen und das ganze 15 bis 20 Minuten kochen lassen.
4. Ein Tuch über einen irdenen Topf legen, den Inhalt der Casserolle darauf gießen und die Flüssigkeit durchpressen.
5. Diese Suppe in eine reine Casserolle oder einen Porzellan-Topf übergießen und 1 Prise Salz und 1 Kaffeelöffel gebrannten Zucker (Karamell) dazugeben, damit die Suppe ein besseres Aussehen erhält.

title: Krammetsvögel (Thrushes with Bread Croutons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Thrushes
- Binding thread
- Bacon strips
- Thin spit
- Large spit
- Bacon
- Bread slices
- Semolina crumbs
- Mushroom or onion puree
- Bread slices for garnish

## Directions

1. Pluck, singe, clean and draw the thrushes
2. Dress with binding thread and layer with inserted bacon strips, bind and thread on thin spit, then bind to large spit
3. Roast quickly over strong fire for 6-8 minutes
4. Alternatively, line a casserole with bacon and bread slices, lay the drawn thrushes on top and roast at strong heat for 15 minutes
5. Garnish with bread slices and bacon
6. Serve with puree, especially mushroom or onion puree, with bread slices interspersed
7. Prepare the sauce of thrushes as partridge sauce, Nr. 753

title: Krammetsvögel-Salmis (Thrushes in Salmis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Thrushes
- Ingredients as for partridge salmis

## Directions

1. Prepare as partridge salmis, Nr. 749

title: Krapfeln (Fritters) Von Pistazien-Marmelade (Pistachio Marmalade Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deca finely ground sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 2 egg whites, stiffly beaten

## Directions

1. Stir together 16 Deca finely ground sugar, juice of 1 lemon, and 2 stiffly beaten egg whites for 3-4 hours.
2. Take 1 coffee spoon of this mixture and pipe raised mounds onto a baking sheet coated with gauze using the handle of a second coffee spoon.
3. Sprinkle with finely powdered sugar and dry in a moderately warm oven, but they should only dry on the outside while remaining soft inside.
4. When properly dried, remove from the baking sheet, pair them up two by two, and join them with marmalade.

title: Krapfen feinfter Gattung (Fine Pastry Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 Decaliter dried, warm, sifted, fine flour
- 4 Decaliter sugar
- 5 grams salt
- 1 liter milk, divided into 3 equal parts
- 3 Decaliter fresh yeast
- 14 Decaliter melted butter
- 10 egg yolks
- Marmalade or jam for filling
- 6-8 Decaliter beef lard
- 7/8 Decaliter pork lard of better quality
- Vanilla sugar for garnish

## Directions

1. Heat a deep bowl and add 60 Decaliter dried, warm, sifted, fine flour and 4 Decaliter sugar with 5 grams salt.
2. Divide 1 liter of milk into 3 equal parts.
3. In one saucepan, add 1/8 liter lukewarm milk and dissolve 3 Decaliter fresh yeast in it.
4. In a second saucepan, add 1/8 liter lukewarm milk and stir in 14 Decaliter melted butter.
5. In a third saucepan, add 1/8 liter milk and sprinkle 10 egg yolks into it.
6. Make a well in the flour and gradually stir in the yeast dissolved in milk, then sift the milk with eggs through a sieve and add it, finally pour in the milk with butter.
7. Stir constantly while adding the liquids.
8. Beat the mixture into a light, fine dough by stirring everything well together, using a wooden spoon to work the dough mass from the sides, holding the spoon vertically against the bottom and pressing some dough with the back of the spoon against the hands, moving from right to left and from left to right.
9. Continue beating quickly and without interruption until the dough is fine, smooth, and shiny and releases from the spoon.
10. Place half the dough on a flour-dusted board, dust lightly with flour on top, and roll out very lightly with a rolling pin until the dough is about finger-thickness.
11. Mark the Krapfen with a cutter by pressing lightly without cutting through.
12. Place half a teaspoon of marmalade in the center of each marked circle.
13. Cut out round discs with a smaller cutter, place them marked-side up over the marmalade, and press down lightly with fingertips around the filling.
14. Using a slightly smaller cutter, cut out both parts together to form the fritter.
15. Place on a warm, flour-dusted board with the side that was previously on the board now facing up.
16. Cover finished fritters with a warm cloth.
17. Add remaining dough pieces back to the bowl and work in approximately 3 tablespoons of lukewarm milk, then proceed as before.
18. Place fritters three fingers apart on a board, cover with a warm cloth, and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size.
19. Heat 1 kilo beef lard and 7/8 kilo pork lard in a saucepan until very hot.
20. Place the first risen fritters in the hot lard with the lower side facing up, cover the saucepan, and after 2-4 minutes lift the lid.
21. When the lower side turns brown, turn the fritters with a wooden stick, leave the saucepan open, shake it slightly, and blow away the foam that forms so it does not stick to the fritters.
22. The fritters should be light enough to sink only halfway into the lard, which must be maintained at an even frying temperature.
23. Once both sides are baked, which takes 6-8 minutes total, remove with a slotted spoon and place on paper with the last-fried side down.
24. After a short time, transfer to another piece of paper.
25. Just before serving, dust lightly with vanilla sugar and serve warm and heaped on a napkin-lined dish.
26. Dissolved marmalade may be served separately in a sauceboat if desired.
27. This recipe yields approximately 40-50 fritters.

title: Kraut Strudel (Sauerkraut Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 heads sauerkraut
- 12 ounces hot fat
- 1 teaspoon caraway
- pepper
- paprika
- 1/2 liter flour
- melted butter
- breadcrumbs
- optionally: 1/4 kilo braised or stewed pork, or the same amount of pickled ham, or 1/4 kilo braised sausage

## Directions

1. Remove the ribs from 2 heads of sauerkraut, cut them roughly, then chop very fine with a knife
2. In a saucepan with 12 ounces hot fat, place the pressed-out sauerkraut so all liquid runs out
3. Add 1 teaspoon caraway and let cook covered, stirring often, until soft
4. Season with pepper and paprika to taste, set aside and cool
5. Prepare finely pulled strudel dough from 1/2 liter flour, brush with melted butter
6. Top with the cooled braised sauerkraut, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, then treat as in Ham Strudel
7. The sauerkraut filling distributed on the dough can optionally be sprinkled with 1/4 kilo braised or stewed pork, or the same amount of pickled ham, each very finely chopped, or 1/4 kilo braised sausage cut into small pieces

title: Kraut-Halfen (Cabbage Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- pressed yeast
- milk
- melted beef tallow
- salt
- egg yolks
- whole eggs
- fresh finely cut and salted cabbage
- pepper
- cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough in the same way as for Böhmische Halfen (Nr. 1273), only without sugar
2. When finished baking, lay 2 together and fill instead of with powdered sugar with the following cabbage filling
3. Braise the fresh finely cut and salted cabbage in fat, season to taste with sugar and pepper and baste frequently with cream while braising
4. When the cabbage is completely tender, use it warm for filling

title: Krautess Suppe (Herb Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 portion common soup herbs
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 3 tablespoons flour
- 2 liters meat broth
- salt as needed
- 8 tablespoons heavy cream or sour cream
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. After the herbs are well washed and finely cut, braise them in 8 tablespoons of butter.
2. Add 3 tablespoons flour and let braise a bit longer.
3. Pour in 2 liters of meat broth, cook through and strain.
4. Add necessary salt.
5. Before serving, bind with 8 tablespoons of heavy cream or sour cream and 3 egg yolks.

title: Krautkäserlein (Cabbage Noodles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- 1/4 hectogram fat
- salt
- flour
- salted water

## Directions

1. Cut, salt, and squeeze out the cabbage as indicated in the Kraut-Strudel recipe
2. Place it in a pot containing 1/4 hectogram fat
3. Braise covered with frequent stirring until soft
4. Season to taste
5. Add noodles made from flour, cut and boiled in salted water
6. Let them become hot with frequent stirring
7. Heap onto a plate and serve

title: Krauts Salat (White Cabbage Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Liter Wasser
- 1/2 Liter Essig
- Salz
- 1 Teelöffel Kümmel
- Kraut (white cabbage)
- 1 Pfund Speck

## Directions

1. 2 Liter Wasser, 1/2 Liter Essig, etwa Salz und 1 Teelöffel voll Kümmel wird in eine Casserolle gegeben und zum Kochen gebracht, dann gibt man das fein länglich, nudetig geschnittene Kraut in den Sud, läßt es fünf Minuten kochen, legt es mittels eines Packlöffels auf eine Schüssel, und zwar richtet man es gehäuft an. Unterdessen werden 1 Pfund Speck würfelig geschnitten gelb geröstet und mit dem Fett über das Kraut gegossen. Stehen darf es mit dem Speck nicht, weshalb man ihn erst vor dem Einrichten darüber geben darf.

title: Krauts Suppe (Sauerkraut Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauerkraut
- 10 tablespoons lard
- 1/8 liter of beef broth
- onion, finely cut
- flour
- 1 liter good beef broth
- approximately 1 liter good broth
- salt and spices
- wine or vinegar as needed
- fried bratwurst
- smoked sausages
- fried goose liver
- eggs (one per person)
- optionally smoked, cooked tongue

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of sauerkraut in water until soft.
2. In a casserole, heat 10 tablespoons of lard; add 1/8 liter of beef broth and finely cut onion, and toast the flour with the onion.
3. Pour 1 liter of good beef broth over this, stirring constantly to prevent lumps from forming.
4. Strain the cooked sauerkraut and pass the broth through a sieve.
5. Pour in approximately 1 liter of good broth, stir very well together and let simmer.
6. Add necessary salt and spices.
7. If the sauerkraut is not sufficiently sour, add wine or vinegar as needed so the soup obtains the correct flavor.
8. Once everything is well cooked, add some pieces of fried bratwurst, cut into pieces, some smoked sausages and one small cut, fried goose liver to the soup pot.
9. For as many persons as there are, beat that many eggs: hold a ladle with some broth in it over the simmering soup, beat the eggs into it and let them stay in until they set.
10. Add these to the soup pot containing the sausages and sauerkraut soup.
11. Alternatively, smoked, cooked tongue cut into small pieces can be added to the soup.
12. The sauerkraut soup must be hearty, spicy and flavorful.
13. Serve the groats on a separate dish without garnish with the sauerkraut soup.

title: Krebs-Farce (Crayfish Forcemeat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ingredients for Anchovy Forcemeat
- crayfish tails and claws instead of anchovies
- crayfish butter instead of regular butter

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as Anchovy Forcemeat, substituting crayfish tails and claws for anchovies and using crayfish butter instead of regular butter

title: Krebs-Meridon (Crayfish Meridon)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 mug cream
- crumbs from 2 rolls
- 5 loths crayfish butter
- tails and claws from 40 to 50 crayfish, cut only down the middle
- 1 mug very young green peas, previously braised in butter
- salt
- 2 whole eggs
- 5 egg yolks
- 5 egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Pour 1 mug cream over crumbs from 2 rolls
2. Beat 5 loths crayfish butter until fluffy
3. Add the squeezed-out roll crumbs and the removed tails and claws from 40 to 50 crayfish, which are cut only down the middle
4. Add 1 mug very young green peas that were previously braised in butter but retained their color
5. Add some salt, then gradually stir in 2 whole eggs and 5 egg yolks, beating until fluffy
6. Finally fold in the stiffly beaten snow from 5 eggs
7. Brush a mold with crayfish butter and line it decoratively with whole crayfish tails, green peas, or asparagus heads
8. Place in steam for one hour
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebs-Strudel (Crayfish Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca crayfish butter
- 16 deca powdered sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 12 deca blanched almonds, finely ground with 2 egg whites
- ½ liter rum
- 4 egg whites
- ½ liter flour
- cooked crayfish claws and tails
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Mix 12 deca crayfish butter and 16 deca powdered sugar well together
2. Gradually stir in 6 egg yolks and blend this mixture well
3. Add 12 deca blanched almonds (finely ground with 2 egg whites), ½ liter rum, one after another, mixing well
4. Lightly fold in the snow (whipped egg whites) from 4 egg whites last
5. Prepare strudel dough from ½ liter flour and stretch it finely as described in the Young Strudel recipe
6. Spread with the prepared filling above
7. Sprinkle with crayfish claws and tails (cut finely) and crayfish shells, then with breadcrumbs
8. Treat further as described in the Sweet Semolina Strudel recipe

title: Krebsbutter (Crayfish Butter)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- tails from 24 cooked crayfish
- shells and claws from the crayfish
- 1/4 pound butter
- cold water

## Directions

1. Remove the tails from 24 cooked crayfish
2. Remove the gall and belly contents
3. Pound together only the shells and claws with 1/4 pound butter until small
4. Fry this mixture in a pan on the coals, sprinkling with a little cold water, until well cooked out
5. Strain the butter through a cloth dipped in cold water and press it well
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebse, faschierte (Stuffed Crayfish)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Salt
- Green parsley
- Caraway
- Rice
- Butter
- Thirty crayfish tails
- Pepper
- One egg yolk
- Three spoonfuls of cream
- White wine
- Cut green parsley

## Directions

1. Boil the crayfish with salt, green parsley, and some caraway, clean them, and take them apart.
2. Braise some rice with butter and finely chopped green parsley, then coarsely chop thirty crayfish tails together, add some salt and pepper, and let it cool.
3. Mix it well with one egg yolk and three spoonfuls of cream, fill the crayfish shells with it, lay the lower part back on top so it looks as if the crayfish were whole, press the shell firmly together.
4. Lay them on a platter, add some butter on top, also some chopped green parsley, and six spoonfuls of cream, some spoonfuls of white wine, caraway, and the crayfish.
5. Apply heat from below and above, and let them boil well, then they are done.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebse-Strudeln (Crayfish Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deka shrimp butter
- 3 eggs and 3 egg yolks
- 1 Deka trimmed, finely diced rolls soaked in milk
- 1 1/8 liter milk cream
- salt
- white pepper
- 1 knife point of parsley
- 3 egg whites for snow
- 1/2 liter flour for dough
- finely chopped shells and meat of 15 crayfish
- 8 Deka bread crumbs
- 1/4 liter cooked green peas

## Directions

1. Whip 12 Deka shrimp butter until fluffy
2. Stir in eggs and yolks one by one
3. Add diced rolls in milk, milk cream, salt, pepper, and parsley
4. Fold in snow from 3 egg whites
5. Spread on finely stretched strudel dough made from 1/2 liter flour
6. Scatter crayfish shells and meat, bread crumbs, and green peas over it
7. Roll lightly and treat as lung strudel

title: Krebskoch (Crayfish Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- the crust from a small roll
- one measure cream or milk
- half a pound crayfish butter
- seven pounds finely ground almonds
- six egg yolks
- twenty-five crayfish tails, finely chopped then ground
- one lemon, zest grated on sugar
- sugar to taste
- dough for the rim

## Directions

1. Cut the crust from a small roll and simmer it in one measure cream or milk like a child's pudding
2. Allow it to cool
3. Prepare half a pound crayfish butter and allow it to cool
4. Take seven pounds finely ground almonds, six egg yolks, and twenty-five crayfish tails that have been finely chopped and then ground
5. Put all of this into the pudding
6. Stir it well for one and a half hours
7. Grate one lemon on sugar and add the grated zest
8. Sweeten it to taste with sugar
9. Make a rim of dough on a sheet pan and bake it
10. Spread the sheet and rim with crayfish butter
11. Pour the stirred mixture into it
12. Bake it in a heated oven for three-quarters of an hour and serve it sweetened
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebsnudeln (Crayfish Noodles)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Loth (about 6 oz) fine flour
- egg yolks
- lukewarm milk
- 2 spoonfuls beer yeast (Germ)
- 4 spoonfuls crayfish butter
- salt
- crayfish butter for spreading
- cut crayfish tails
- crayfish butter for the pan
- boiling milk with sugar

## Directions

1. Put 12 Loth fine flour in a bowl, beat in egg yolks, and pour in some lukewarm milk
2. Add 2 spoonfuls of beer yeast and 4 spoonfuls of crayfish butter, salt the mass slightly
3. Beat the dough well until it releases from the spoon
4. Dust a noodle board with flour and pour the dough onto it
5. Roll it out to the thickness of a knife back, spread crayfish butter on it, and sprinkle cut crayfish tails on top
6. Cut wide strips and roll them together
7. Coat a cooking pan with crayfish butter, lay the noodles in it
8. After they have risen for a quarter hour, pour boiling milk with some sugar into the noodles
9. Provide heat from below and above, and they are finished in a quarter hour
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebspudding (Crayfish Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 unsalted boiled and prepared crayfish
- 2 pounds crayfish butter
- 10 egg yolks
- crayfish meat
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg flowers
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Finely chop the meat from 10 unsalted boiled crayfish that have been prepared.
2. Beat 2 pounds crayfish butter into a foam, beat in 10 egg yolks, and mix well with the chopped crayfish meat, salt, pepper, nutmeg flowers, and enough breadcrumbs so that it becomes a ball.
3. Proceed with this mass as already described.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebspästechen (Crayfish Pastries)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- crayfish forcemeat
- crayfish tails, cut
- egg yolk
- crayfish butter
- crayfish ragout

## Directions

1. Place small pastry cases made from puff pastry in small molds, about the size of a walnut
2. Fill each with crayfish forcemeat and some cut crayfish tails
3. Brush with egg yolk and cover with a lid
4. Bake brushed with crayfish butter until nicely golden in the oven
5. Remove the lid and score the forcemeat
6. Pour good crayfish ragout into each pastry
7. Replace the lids and serve warm
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebsschöberl (Crayfish Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 Deca crayfish butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 20 Deca diced bread rolls soaked in milk
- Claws and tails from 10 crayfish
- Snow from 6 eggs
- Green peas soaked in crayfish water (optional, 1/8 liter)

## Directions

1. Beat 14 Deca crayfish butter until fluffy, gradually stir in 6 egg yolks
2. Add 20 Deca diced bread rolls soaked in milk and the extracted claws and tails from 10 crayfish
3. Stir everything well together and lightly mix in the snow from 6 eggs
4. Can optionally mix in 1/8 liter green peas soaked in crayfish water
5. This schöberl can be steamed or baked and then added to the soup

title: Krebsstrudel (Crayfish Strudel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- dough as in previous recipe
- 0.5 pound crayfish butter
- crayfish mousseline made from 6 eggs
- 20 small crayfish tails
- 0.25 pound butter
- 0.25 pound crayfish butter
- 1 bread roll soaked in milk
- 2 eggs and 2 yolks
- sugar
- sweet simmering cream and sugar for cooking

## Directions

1. Make dough as in previous recipe and stretch it out similarly
2. Make crayfish butter and a crayfish mousseline from 6 eggs
3. Chop 20 small crayfish tails very fine
4. Work 0.25 pound butter with the mousseline and chopped tails, adding sugar
5. Add to the crayfish butter a bread roll soaked in milk, 2 eggs and 2 yolks, and work together
6. Spread this filling thinly on the strudel
7. Roll together as before and place in a pan brushed with crayfish butter
8. Pour sweet simmering cream and sugar around it
9. Apply heat above and below
10. Serve when beautifully browned
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebssuppe mit Ragout (Crayfish Soup with Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 crayfish
- pike heads, 2 baked
- bread rolls roasted in drippings
- pea broth
- nutmeg flower
- ground pepper
- asparagus heads, parboiled
- roach (Maurachen), braised
- pike liver
- pike, shredded
- crayfish tails
- crayfish butter
- flour
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Parboil 30 crayfish
2. Free 15 of them from the bile, which is then pounded
3. From the remaining crayfish, remove the tails and claws and set them aside
4. Pound 2 baked pike heads with some bread rolls roasted in drippings, to which you add good pea broth and let simmer
5. Strain through a sieve
6. Add some nutmeg flower and ground pepper
7. Prepare parboiled asparagus heads, braised roach, pike liver, shredded pike, and crayfish tails
8. Put a little crayfish butter in a casserole, dust with a little flour and stir in the above ingredients
9. Pour the strained crayfish soup over it and bring to a boil again
10. Serve over toasted bread slices
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebswürste à la Duchesse (Crayfish Sausage à la Duchesse)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish, approximately 25 pieces
- milk
- semmels (rolls)
- 6 eggs
- butter
- salt
- spices

## Directions

1. Break open approximately 25 live crayfish
2. Chop the tails very finely
3. Soak semmels in milk
4. Mix the chopped tails with the milk-soaked semmels and 6 eggs
5. Cut off the heads from the shells
6. Remove the bitter parts
7. Pound the shells with a good piece of butter in a mortar until fine
8. Soften them covered for a while
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebswürste à la Duchesse (Crayfish Sausages à la Duchesse)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- about twenty-five crayfish
- milk-soaked bread rolls
- egg yolks
- butter
- cream
- two to three hard-boiled egg yolks
- salt
- mace
- fresh marjoram
- cleaned pork intestines

## Directions

1. Break the live crayfish and chop the tails very finely
2. Soak bread rolls in milk and add to the chopped crayfish tails along with egg yolks
3. From the shells, cut off the heads and remove the bitter parts
4. Pound the shells with a good piece of butter in a mortar until fine
5. Sweat the pounded shells covered over coals for a while
6. Add cream proportionally and let it come to a boil several times
7. Strain through a fine cloth into the crayfish mixture
