8. Add two to three hard-boiled egg yolks, salt, mace, and a little fresh marjoram
9. Work the entire mass well together until it is neither too thin nor too thick
10. Fill into cleaned pork intestines and cook in milk
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krebä Pudding (Crayfish Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 teaspoons hot lard butter
- 8 teaspoons flour
- 1/4 liter boiling cream or milk
- 9 egg yolks
- 16 teaspoons sugar
- crayfish claws and tails, finely cut
- 4 egg whites
- 8 teaspoons white breadcrumbs
- crayfish butter

## Directions

1. In 8 teaspoons of hot lard butter, add 8 teaspoons of flour, stir both well together, once it begins to foam, add 1/4 liter of boiling cream or milk, stir it at the stove until the dough leaves the spoon and set it aside
2. Meanwhile, beat 9 egg yolks with 16 teaspoons of sugar for 1/2 hour, slowly pour this to the cooked but cooled mass, mix well with finely cut crayfish claws and tails and finally lightly fold in the snow from 4 egg whites with 8 teaspoons of white breadcrumbs
3. Fill a mold lined with crayfish butter and greased with this mass, which is then cooked covered in a water bath for 3/4 hours

title: Krebä Pudding mit Ragout (Crayfish Pudding with Ragout)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish pudding mass
- green peas, cooked
- calf's liver, cooked and cut into pieces
- salt

## Directions

1. The preparation is the same as in the description of the 'Crayfish Pudding' (No. 1383), only here the sugar is replaced with cooked green peas and cooked calf's liver cut into desired pieces and the necessary salt
2. This pudding is garnished with small ragout and served immediately

title: Krebſe, faſchirte (Stuffed Crayfish)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Salt
- Green parsley
- Caraway

## Directions

1. Boil the crayfish with salt, green parsley and some caraway
2. Clean and separate them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kren mit Essig und Öl (Horseradish with Vinegar and Oil)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Horseradish
- Breadcrumbs
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Sugar or salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the horseradish.
2. Grate it with a little breadcrumbs.
3. Mix with vinegar, oil, and sugar, or alternatively with salt and pepper if desired.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krennfleisch (Pork with Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 dkg lean and fatty pork
- 1 kg sauerkraut
- salt
- caraway
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the sauerkraut, wash lightly, place in a pot with salt, caraway, vinegar, and water.
2. Simmer for 45 minutes.
3. Add the lightly washed, diced pork and simmer for another 45 minutes.

title: Kreus Sauce (Horseradish Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons flour
- 3/4 liter white beef stock
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- 1 cup grated horseradish

## Directions

1. A butter sauce of 8 tablespoons butter and the same amount of flour is poured with 3/4 liter white beef stock
2. Add some nutmeg and salt and let boil
3. Add 1 cup grated horseradish
4. The horseradish must not boil

title: Kreusfleisch (Pork Head or Belly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pork head or ½ kg pork belly from a young pig
- 8 dkg root vegetables
- 10 dkg horseradish
- 1 onion
- 8 peppercorns and spice berries
- 2 bay leaves
- thyme
- salt
- vinegar
- cold water

## Directions

1. Wash the pork or head 2-3 times very well.
2. Place in cold salted water with vinegar, cleaned root vegetables cut into thin slices, onion, peppercorns, spice berries, bay leaves, and thyme.
3. Cook for 1-1½ hours.
4. Place the head on a dish, stuff the snout and ear with curled parsley, garnish the head with vegetables and horseradish.
5. Slice the meat, arrange on a long dish, garnish similarly, and serve with boiled potatoes.

title: Kronawetter, gebraten (Roasted Game Birds)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Kronawetter birds
- Bacon
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Bind these birds, like larks, with bacon, stick them on small spits, and roast them
2. Alternatively, roast them without bacon and sprinkle them with breadcrumbs
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Krone von verschiedenen Zeigen (Croque en Bouche)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram sweet chestnuts
- 10 to 12 decagrams fine sifted sugar
- hot praline sugar
- candied chestnuts
- melted chocolate
- whipped cream mixed with sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the sweet chestnuts crosswise and place them in a casserole with enough cold water to cover them
2. Cook for approximately half an hour until the interior of the chestnuts is soft and mealy
3. Remove the chestnuts with a spoon and pass them through a sieve into a deep bowl
4. Mix the sifted sugar with the passed chestnuts thoroughly
5. Form walnut-sized balls from this mixture
6. Using a piping needle, pick up each ball individually, dip it in hot praline sugar, and lay two rows of balls side by side in a predetermined bowl
7. Cover this with a second double row also dipped in hot praline sugar
8. Form somewhat smaller balls, dip them individually in hot praline sugar, and place them on the center of the two double rows
9. Build the crown by placing proportionally large crown points made of smaller chestnut balls dipped in sugar
10. When the crown is complete, dust it with melted chocolate
11. Fill the empty space created in the middle of the crown with whipped cream mixed with sugar, to which you may add any desired flavor

title: Krone von verschiedenen Zeugen (Croque en bouche - Crown of Various Confections)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram sweet chestnuts
- cold water

## Directions

1. Make slits (chamfer) in 1 kilogram sweet chestnuts
2. Place in a casserole in enough cold water to cover them
3. Cook about 1/2 hour until the interior of the chestnuts is soft and mealy
4. After the water is drained, remove with a spoon

title: Kroquettes von Fisch (Fish Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 dkg leftover cooked pike or pike-perch
- 6 dkg hot fat
- onion, finely chopped
- parsley, finely chopped
- flour
- soup
- 1 egg yolk
- breadcrumbs for breading
- hot lard for frying
- lettuce

## Directions

1. Bone the leftover fish and press it well.
2. In hot fat, let finely chopped onion and parsley start to color; add a little flour, pour in a small amount of soup, let it reduce, add the fish, and mix with an egg yolk if desired.
3. The mixture must be firm.
4. Divide into small pieces and form croquettes.
5. Bread them, fry in hot lard until golden, and drain.
6. Serve with or without salad.
7. Arrange the croquettes on a flat round platter with a cake paper and place curled parsley in the center.

title: Kroquettes von Kohl (Cabbage Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 small firm heads of cabbage
- 2 water-soaked rolls, well pressed
- some fat
- ½ finely chopped onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- 2-3 tablespoons flour
- breadcrumbs for breading
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Clean, divide, and wash the cabbage
2. Place in boiling salted water and cook for 10 minutes
3. Drain very well and chop
4. Mix with 2 water-soaked, well-pressed rolls
5. In a saucepan, heat some fat and briefly fry the finely chopped onion and parsley
6. Add the cabbage, salt, pepper, and 2-3 tablespoons flour
7. Stir and let cool
8. If the mixture is too soft, add more flour or breadcrumbs
9. Form into croquettes, bread them, and fry in hot lard
10. Drain and serve with head lettuce salad

title: Kroquettes von Schinken (Ham Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg lean rolled ham
- ½ l milk
- 4 dkg hot butter
- some flour
- salt
- 2 hard-boiled eggs, chopped
- breadcrumbs for breading
- hot lard for frying
- spinach or cooked lettuce for serving

## Directions

1. In hot butter, lightly brown some flour
2. Pour in the milk and stir
3. Add salt, 2 hard-boiled chopped eggs, and the chopped ham
4. Form croquettes on a floured board
5. Bread them and fry in hot lard
6. Drain and serve with spinach or cooked lettuce

title: Kräftige Suppe mit Reis (Hearty Rice Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Kilo überbratwnes Kalbfleisch
- 1 Kilo gebratenes Schöpfenfleisch
- 1 gebratenes Huhn
- 1 gebratenes Rebhuhn
- 1 Stück gebratener Käse
- 4,5 Liter kaltes Wasser
- 16 Decagramm Reis
- Salz nach Geschmack

## Directions

1. 1 Kilo überbratwnes Kalbfleisch, 1 Kilo gebratenes Schöpfenfleisch, 1 gebratenes Huhn, 1 gebratenes Rebhuhn, 1 Stück gebratener Käse werden in 4,5 Liter kaltes Wasser gegeben und zum Kochen gebracht.
2. Um die Suppe sämig zu bekommen, gibt man 16 Decagramm Reis dazu und läßt dies gut ungedeckt 3 Stunden langsam kochen.
3. Während des Kochens nimmt man das Fett, das sich oben bildet, rein ab.
4. Nachdem diese Suppe die angegebene Zeit gekocht hat, seiht man sie zuerst durch ein gröberes und dann durch ein feineres Sieb.
5. Salzt sie nach Bedarf und serviert sie in kleinen Bechern.
6. Das verkochtе Fleisch kann sauer eingemacht, gefärbt oder zu Suppenspeisen verwendet werden.

title: Kräftiges Mus für Schwache (Strengthening Pap for the Weak)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- thin white bread slices
- rose water or cinnamon water
- sugar
- almond milk

## Directions

1. Cut thin white bread slices
2. Toast them until yellow
3. Pour rose water or cinnamon water over them so they become soft
4. Sprinkle sugar on top
5. Rub everything together with almond milk to make a pap
6. It will dissolve on the tongue
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kräpfchen aus Schmalz (Fried Pastry Puffs with Jam)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds dry fine flour
- half pound butter
- 5 whole eggs
- egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons good beer yeast
- 4 loth sugar
- half Mass good milk
- nutmeg
- preserved raspberries, currants, or apricots
- jam with almonds, sugar, and cinnamon
- lard

## Directions

1. Warm the milk so butter melts in it
2. Beat the eggs very smooth
3. Add milk with butter and remaining ingredients plus a little nutmeg
4. Strike the flour into this
5. Beat the dough with a cooking spoon until it detaches
6. Transfer to table and work in a little more flour so it rolls out easily
7. Roll out straw-stalk thickness
8. Lay a row of small heaps of jam filling mixed with almonds, sugar, and cinnamon across the width, not too close together
9. Fold the outer side over
10. Cut puffs with a pastry wheel
11. Let rise in warmth
12. Fry golden yellow in lard
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kräuter-Branntwein (Herbal Brandy)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- several bunches of finely cut milfoil
- 3 bunches wormwood
- 1 bunch mugwort
- 1 bunch melissa
- some pieces of orange blossoms and orange peel
- 1 clove
- 7 tea cups grape berries
- 2 tea cups finely cut licorice root
- 5 pieces crushed juniper berries
- 5 grains white pepper
- 1 teaspoon mace and equally much nutmeg
- 1 liter corn brandy
- 16 tea cups sugar
- 1/2 liter water
- optional: 1 teaspoon anise, some orange blossoms or 1 small piece vanilla

## Directions

1. Place all ingredients listed in a bottle
2. After sealing the bottle, let it stand 3 to 4 weeks in the sun and shake it frequently
3. After this time, strain the liquid into a second bottle and dilute with sugar water if desired
4. To make the sugar water: boil 16 tea cups sugar in 1/2 liter water for 1/4 hour, to which you can add 1 teaspoon anise, some orange blossoms or 1 small piece vanilla if desired
5. Cool and strain, then mix with the strained brandy
6. Pour good brandy again over the ingredients remaining in the bottle

title: Kräuterbutter für Schwindsüchtige (Herb Butter for Consumptives)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three good handfuls each of rue and sage
- half a seidel of sweet cream

## Directions

1. Chop the rue and sage small
2. Place them with the sweet cream in a stone pot
3. Let it stand for twenty-four hours
4. Churn the butter
5. Strain the butter through a loose linen cloth, leaving the herbs behind
6. Have the patient eat herb bread with this butter as often as hunger strikes
7. Do not allow the patient to eat other foods until the butter is consumed
8. The patient may drink usual beverages as normal
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kräuteressig (Herb Vinegar)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- good vinegar
- basil
- fennel
- tarragon
- melissa
- thyme
- chervil and dill
- celery
- parsley root
- pimpinella
- lavender blossoms
- rosemary blossoms
- orange and lemon peels
- foreign spices as desired

## Directions

1. Take basil, fennel, tarragon, melissa, thyme, chervil and dill, celery, parsley root, pimpinella and other desired herbs
2. Add some lavender blossoms, rosemary blossoms, some orange and lemon peels, and foreign spices as desired
3. Proceed as with French vinegar
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kräutersalat (Herb Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cress
- sorrel
- watercress
- tarragon
- borage
- pimpernel
- fennel herb
- lemon balm
- sugar beets
- cowslips
- yellow violets
- oil
- rose vinegar

## Directions

1. Select herbs which one likes most, such as cress, sorrel, watercress, tarragon, borage, pimpernel, fennel herb, lemon balm, sugar beets, cowslips, yellow violets, etc., using some or all of them
2. Wash them thoroughly
3. Prepare with oil and vinegar like other salad
4. Use rose vinegar instead of ordinary wine vinegar for better flavor
5. One can also sweeten the salad a little, and if desired, surround it with another salad
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kräutersalat mit Blumen (Herb Salad with Flowers)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Young fresh salad
- Garden cress
- Young chervil
- Fine onion tubes or small young onions
- Blue borage flowers
- Violets (March violets, cowslips, yellow and blue)
- Monthly flowers
- Bertram leaves
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Good Provençal oil
- Vinegar
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the salad, cress, chervil, and onions together thoroughly
2. Pick the flowers and add to the salad: blue borage, March violets, cowslips, yellow and blue violets, monthly flowers, and Bertram leaves
3. Mix all together
4. Hard-boil some egg yolks, crush them very fine
5. Pour good Provençal oil, vinegar and sugar over them
6. Dress the salad with this mixture and serve at table
7. Herb vinegar can also be used, and those who dislike sugar can use salt instead
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kräutersauce (Herb Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Chives
- Savory
- Parsley
- Basil
- Onion stems
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Sugar (to taste, optional)

## Directions

1. Finely chop with a chopping knife: chives, savory, parsley, basil, and onion stems.
2. Mix well with vinegar and oil.
3. Add sugar to taste if desired.
4. Serve with cold fish or roasts.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kräutersauce auf spanische Art (Herb Sauce Spanish Style)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one glass Provençal oil
- 3 to 4 slices ham, diced
- several onions, diced
- mushrooms, diced
- garlic cloves, diced
- shallots, diced
- bay leaves
- basil
- thyme
- fine spices
- beef broth
- several spoonfuls capers
- lemon peel
- anchovies
- one glass wine

## Directions

1. On coal fire, braise in one glass of Provençal oil the diced ham, onions, mushrooms, garlic cloves, and shallots with bay leaves, basil, thyme, and fine spices until golden-yellow
2. Pour some beef broth over it
3. Cook it through with several spoonfuls of capers, lemon peel, and anchovies
4. Skim off the foam
5. Add one glass of wine
6. Strain through a hair sieve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kräutersuppe (Herb Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- leek
- chervil
- parsley
- spinach
- sorrel
- young onions
- garden cress
- flour
- butter or good beef fat
- good beef broth
- egg yolks
- cold water
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Wash leek, chervil, parsley, spinach, sorrel, young onions, and garden cress clean and chop everything finely together with a chopping knife.
2. Let some flour cook with butter or good beef fat in a pot until lightly browned, then braise the chopped herbs in it for a while.
3. Fill with good beef broth and let it come to a boil, but not for too long, otherwise the herbs will lose their color.
4. Before serving, for about 3 measures of soup, beat 6 egg yolks in a pot with some spoons of cold water.
5. Pour this under constant stirring into the soup, stirring until the soup begins to thicken.
6. Serve over toasted bread.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kuehlende und blutreingende Kraeutersuppe (Cooling and Blood-Purifying Herb Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Young leaves and stems of lettuce
- Young leaves and stems of endive
- Young leaves and stems of purslane
- Young leaves and stems of pig's ear
- Twelve loths of each
- Six loths sorrel
- Butter
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Thin meat broth

## Directions

1. Wash the young leaves and stems of lettuce, endive, purslane, and pig's ear in warm water
2. Gently press out the water
3. Cook gently with thin meat broth
4. Add butter, salt, and some nutmeg
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kukumern- oder Gerkensalat (Cucumber Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- Young radishes (optional)
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Peel cucumbers if they are not yet too large and cut them into fine thin slices
2. If desired, some young radishes can be cut into thin slices and mixed in
3. Salt them and dress immediately with vinegar, oil and pepper
4. Alternatively, some let them sit in salt for some time and then press the water out thoroughly, though this method is said to be unhealthy because they become tough when pressed and are much harder to digest
5. Serve as a salad
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kusikus-Wasser (Couscous Water)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deka washed couscous
- 1 liter water
- Finely pounded sugar
- Several egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil 16 deka washed couscous in 1 liter of water for about half an hour.
2. Strain it through a fine sieve, add finely pounded sugar as desired, and sprinkle in several egg yolks.

title: Kuttelfleisch mit Eisbein (Tripe with Knuckle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kilos tripe
- butter
- green parsley
- onion
- salt
- breadcrumbs soaked in broth
- pepper
- nutmeg
- cooked green peas
- puff pastry
- butter
- flour
- grated Parmesan cheese
- rice

## Directions

1. After cleaning, 2 kilos of tripe are slowly cooked until tender, then cooled and cut into noodle-like strips.
2. In a casserole containing hot butter, add finely cut green parsley and finely cut onion, let these roast somewhat, then add the cut tripe, salt to taste and let braise covered for a while.
3. Then add breadcrumbs softened in broth, well-pressed and passed through a sieve, with a pinch of pepper, some nutmeg and 1/4 liter of cooked green peas, stir well and set aside.
4. After completely cooling, a buttered and floured mold is lined with knife-back-thick rolled-out puff pastry, a layer of the above mixture is placed on it, this is sprinkled with grated Parmesan cheese, this is covered with several spoonfuls of piled rice, another layer of the above mixture is placed on top and this is continued until everything is used up, then it is baked.

title: Kälberlungen-Muß (Veal Lung Puree)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One veal lung
- Sugar candy equal in weight to the lung

## Directions

1. Place the veal lung and sugar candy together in a clean, well-sealed pot
2. Set this pot in a kettle full of water
3. Let it cook for three to four hours until it becomes like a juice or puree
4. Take three spoonfuls each morning and evening
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kälberne Bratwürste mit Mark (Veal Bratwurst with Marrow)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- veal leg
- bone marrow
- white wine
- spices
- cleaned mutton intestines

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from the leg and scrape from the nerves
2. Make it fine first with a knife, then grate on a grater
3. Mix the finely diced marrow underneath
4. Season well and moisten with some white wine
5. Fill this mass into cleaned mutton intestines
6. Divide into lengths as desired
7. Fry slowly on a roasting rack
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kälberner Schlägel mit geselchten Zungen gespickt (Veal Shank Larded with Smoked Tongues)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shank
- cooked smoked tongues
- sardelles
- bone marrow
- butter
- onions
- cloves
- lemon peel
- lemon juice
- wine
- broth
- capers
- cream

## Directions

1. Flay a medium shank, wash and salt it.
2. Prepare cooked smoked tongues, sardelles, and bone marrow; cut all into lengthwise pieces.
3. Make holes in the shank with a narrow knife and lard it alternately with tongues, sardelles, and marrow.
4. Line a casserole with butter, three onions stuck with cloves, lemon peel and juice.
5. Place the shank on top, pour a seidel of wine and half as much broth.
6. Cover well, apply heat above and below, and let braise slowly for two hours, turning several times.
7. When completely braised and tender, chop a handful of capers.
8. Place the shank in another casserole, strain the sauce over it, add the capers and some spoonfuls of cream.
9. Let it braise a little longer, arrange the shank on a dish, pour sauce over it, and serve.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kälbernes Brüstel in der Glace (Glazed Veal Breast)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful veal breast
- Bacon, finely sliced
- Ham pieces
- Shallots or chives
- Fresh parsley
- Brown broth
- Ground veal meat
- Bacon
- Bread crumbs
- Milk
- Egg yolks
- Beef broth

## Directions

1. Remove the ribs from the veal breast and trim the shield
2. Blanch it by placing in boiling water for a few moments, then cool in cold water
3. Lard it in five or six rows with finely sliced bacon, trimming the bacon points so it resembles a hedgehog
4. Place in a casserole with ham pieces, shallots or chives, and a small bunch of fresh parsley
5. Fill the casserole with brown broth, cover, and braise until tender
6. Remove the breast and strain the sauce through a sieve
7. Return the sauce to the casserole and simmer until it becomes a glaze with little sauce remaining
8. Return the breast to the glaze and coat it thoroughly on all sides
9. Place in the center of the serving dish and pour the sauce over it
10. For the garnish: make a farce from half a pound of veal, eight loth of bacon, a bread crumb soaked in milk, all finely chopped and pounded in a mortar
11. Add two or three egg yolks to form a dough, then small-cut shallots and finely chopped parsley
12. Form into a thick sausage on a floured board, cook in beef broth
13. When cooked, slice knife-thick pieces and arrange around the edge of the dish to form a crown
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kälbernes Brüstel mit Spargel, Krebsen und grünen Erbsen gefüllt (Veal Breast Filled with Asparagus, Crayfish and Green Peas)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast
- Bread crumb
- Milk
- Asparagus
- Crayfish
- Green peas
- Crayfish butter
- Fresh parsley
- Egg
- Egg yolk
- Beef broth
- Mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Wash the veal breast clean and prepare it for filling
2. Soak a bread crumb in milk
3. Boil the asparagus and crayfish, remove them, and dice them finely along with the asparagus
4. Make a crayfish butter from the shells
5. Press out the bread crumb, dice it finely, and braise it slightly with finely chopped fresh parsley
6. Add the asparagus with the crayfish tails and some green peas, braise again briefly
7. Remove from heat, season and salt it, and let it cool slightly
8. Beat in one whole egg and one egg yolk, mix well together
9. Fill the breast with this mixture
10. Place in a cooking vessel on skewers, pour beef broth over it, and braise until tender
11. Before serving, prepare a mushroom sauce and pour it over the breast
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Kälbersaft-Pudding (Consommé de farce de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca rind-removed rolls
- milk
- 34 deca raw veal meat
- 6 deca butter
- 2 whole eggs
- white pepper
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/4 liter milk

## Directions

1. Cut 8 deca rolls finely into leaflets, soak with milk and stir over heat until it looks like dough
2. Place on a plate and let cool
3. Meanwhile cut 34 deca raw veal meat very finely
4. Put the meat with the prepared rolls in a mortar and pound very finely with 6 deca butter and 2 whole eggs
5. Pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
6. Add a pinch of white pepper, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 2 whole eggs, 1 egg yolk, and 1/4 liter milk
7. Mix well together
8. Fill into a mold greased with butter and lined with flour
9. Cook in steam, turn out, cut into pieces, and place in the soup
10. For ragout, place in a round rice mold

title: Käse-Soufflé (Cheese Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- 16 Deca flour
- 10 Deca butter
- 1/2 liter cream
- some salt
- 6 egg yolks
- 10 Deca grated Parmesan cheese
- 9 egg whites (snow)

## Directions

1. Put 3 egg yolks, 3 whole eggs, 16 Deca flour, 10 Deca butter, 1/2 liter cream and some salt into a saucepan and stir over the fire until it has turned into a thick mass, then empty it into a deep bowl
2. After it has cooled, stir in gradually 6 egg yolks and 10 Deca grated Parmesan cheese and finally fold in lightly the snow from 9 egg whites
3. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour with this mass, which is then baked for 1/4 hours in the oven
4. This soufflé is served at table immediately, sprinkled with grated Parmesan cheese
5. This mass can also be baked in paper cups and served in these

title: Käsecroutons (Cheese Croutons)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ bread rolls
- 6 dkg butter
- 5 dkg Parmesan cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Slice the bread rolls first in half, then into leaves.
2. Melt 6 dkg butter in a small bowl, dip the bread slices in it, turn them on both sides in grated Parmesan.
3. Place them in an omelet pan, bake them at moderate heat in the oven until light brown and serve separately.

title: Käsekuchen (Cheese Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 deca flour
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 knife point of salt
- 2 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/16 liter cold cream
- 1 egg and 3 egg yolks in 1/16 liter cream
- 4 deca melted butter

## Directions

1. Put 40 deca flour, 1 teaspoon sugar and 1 knife point of salt in a deep bowl
2. Dissolve 2 deca pressed yeast in 1/16 liter cold cream and beat 1 egg and 3 egg yolks in 1/16 liter cream, pour into the flour, stir together into a dough, pour in 4 deca melted butter, stir well and beat until the dough separates from the spoon
3. Cover with a cloth and let rest covered in a warm place
4. When the dough has risen, put it on a floured board, roll out to the thickness of a little finger and cut out round cakes using a pastry cutter

title: Königliche Sauce (Royal Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsley
- Shallots
- Mushrooms
- Truffles
- A few cloves of garlic
- Some finely chopped ham or lean bacon
- Bay leaves
- Onion slices
- Butter
- Beef broth
- Egg yolks
- Blanched pistachios

## Directions

1. Braise parsley, shallots, mushrooms, truffles, garlic, ham or bacon, bay leaves, and onion slices in butter.
2. Pour in beef broth in proportion.
3. Let cook well.
4. Pass through a fine sieve.
5. Thicken the sauce with egg yolks.
6. Add blanched pistachios.
7. Serve over meat.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Königlicher Punsch (Royal Punch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- peel of 1/2 pineapple
- 80 drachms sugar
- juice of 2 oranges
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1/8 liter water
- 1/2 liter good red wine
- 1/2 liter white wine
- Champagne wine
- 1/8 liter brandy or rum
- 1/4 liter maraschino

## Directions

1. After rubbing the peel of 1/2 pineapple on 80 drachms sugar, press its juice together with the juice of 2 oranges and 2 lemons
2. In a soup pot, place the sugar broken into pieces, pour 1/8 liter water to it and let the sugar dissolve
3. To this, pour 1/2 liter good red wine, the same amount of white wine, Champagne wine, 1/8 liter brandy or rum, and 1/4 liter maraschino and mix everything well
4. Fill this punch into bottles, place on ice and then serve cold

title: Kümmel-Stanzel mit Butter (Caraway Sticks with Butter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Deca flour
- 8 Deca butter
- 1 pinch of salt
- 1 Deca fresh yeast dissolved in 6 tablespoons cold milk

## Directions

1. Place flour on a board and rub in the butter finely
2. Make a well in the center, add salt and dissolved yeast
3. Mix and work into a smooth, firm dough
4. Cover and let rest for some time
5. Form into sticks or pretzels and proceed as for the Salt-Sticks of Yeast Dough (recipe 1297)

title: Kümmelkotelettes (Caraway Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4–6 pork cutlets, about 20 dkg each
- caraway seeds, crushed
- salt
- 4 dkg fat or butter
- water

## Directions

1. Wipe the cutlets clean, tap them lightly.
2. Rub both sides with crushed caraway seeds and salt.
3. Heat 4 dkg fat in a large casserole, add the cutlets and brown on both sides.
4. Pour in a little water and braise them covered for 30–45 minutes.
5. Turn them frequently and pour in a little hot water if needed.
6. The sauce should be a nice brown color.

title: Kürbisse anderer Art (Giraumont à la crème)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Deca Fett
- 6 Deca Mehl
- 1/2 Liter Rahm
- gefallene Kürbisse

## Directions

1. von 8 Deca Fett und 6 Deca Mehl wird eine Einbrenn bereitet
2. diese wird mit 1/2 Liter Rahm aufgegossen
3. alles wird gut untereinander gerührt
4. wenn es gut gekocht hat, gibt man die gefallenen, gut ausgedrückten Kürbisse hinein
5. sie lässt sich aufkochen

title: Kürbisse ungarisch (Giraumont à la hongroise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ein gefeilter Kürbis
- Salz
- 8 Deca Fett
- 6 Deca Mehl
- 1/2 Liter Wasser
- Essig
- Dillenfraut
- kalter Rahm

## Directions

1. gefeilter Kürbis wird fein würfelig geschnitten und mit Salz bestreut
2. bleibt 1/4 Stunde stehen
3. eine Einbrenn von 8 Deca Fett und 6 Deca Mehl wird gemacht
4. Kürbisse werden, nachdem man sie vorher gut ausgedrückt hat, in die Einbrenn gegeben
5. sie werden etwas geröstet
6. sie werden mit 1/2 Liter Wasser und ein wenig Essig übergossen
7. alles wird gut untereinander gerührt und läßt sich verkochen
8. fein geschnittenes Dillenfraut kann mitgekocht werden
9. wenn die Kürbisse auf der Schüssel angerichtet sind, gibt man einige Löffel kalten Rahm darüber

title: Lachs auf dem Roste (Salmon on the Grill)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of salmon of three or four pounds
- Vinegar
- Finely chopped herbs
- Fine oil

## Directions

1. Scale the salmon, remove the central bone, marinate it in vinegar with plenty of finely chopped herbs for about an hour, then dry it off.
2. Prick it with a knife, brush it with fine oil, and slowly roast it on the grill.
3. You must turn it and brush it while roasting.
4. After the salmon has roasted for half an hour this way, arrange it and serve with a hollandaise sauce poured over it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Lachs mit feinen Kräutern (Salmon with Fine Herbs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- About three pounds of fatty smoked salmon
- Green farce
- Chopped anchovies
- Small cucumbers
- Mushrooms
- Truffles
- Half a bottle of white wine
- Bay leaf
- Fine herbs
- Half a pound of butter
- One spoonful of flour
- Peel and juice of a lemon or orange

## Directions

1. Cut the salmon lengthwise into pieces about three fingers wide and one finger long.
2. Make some incisions in each piece and spread green farce well into them.
3. For the farce, mix chopped anchovies, small cucumbers and mushrooms, and always add some truffles.
4. Layer the salmon tightly in a cassole, pour half a bottle of white wine over it, add a bay leaf and fine herbs.
5. Cook the salmon a quarter hour this way, knead one spoonful of flour into a quarter pound of butter, and add it to the sauce in small pieces.
6. Take the peel of a lemon or orange and its juice as well, let everything cook together, and serve.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Lachs mit Mayonnaise (Salmon with Mayonnaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Mayonnaise
- Aspic (various colors)
- Lemon slices
- Butter
- Crayfish
- Truffles
- Calf's combs

## Directions

1. Place the completely rested, blue-cooked salmon carefully with the insert on a platter
2. Decorate on the back with silver skewers on which one puts crayfish, truffles, and calf's combs
3. Arrange around the fish on the platter: crayfish, variously colored, chopped or neatly cut-out aspic, lemon slices, colored butter and similar items
4. Place a lemon slice in the fish's belly instead of wood
5. Ensure that everything looks tastefully and neatly arranged
6. Serve Mayonnaise separately in a sauce boat

title: Lachs Schnitteln (Salmon Fillets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Scale and skin the salmon
2. Cut 2 knife-back thick round slices from the flesh
3. Lay these slices next to each other in a casserole brushed with butter
4. Dust with salt and pepper
5. Pour melted butter over them and cover with a round sheet of buttered paper
6. Shortly before serving, place on medium heat and fry 2 to 3 minutes
7. Turn each piece and fry the other side equally long
8. Arrange the slices in a circle on a platter
9. Put Hollandaise, Mustard, or Butter sauce with lemon juice in the center

title: Lamb Chops (Lamms-Cotelettes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb chops
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Meat glaze
- Espagnole

## Directions

1. Beat and trim the chops
2. Turn them in butter, salt, pepper, and fine herbs
3. A quarter hour before serving, place them on strong charcoal fire
4. Turn them quickly on both sides
5. Pour off the butter
6. Add a piece of glaze and let it dissolve while shaking
7. Dress the chops neatly
8. Add a little worked espagnole in the roasting pan
9. Pour it in the middle of the platter and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Lammernes in Bertramsauce (Lamb Breast in Tarragon Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of lamb
- butter
- flour
- onion
- tarragon (Bertram)
- broth
- vinegar
- cream
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Blanch the lamb breast and cut into pieces.
2. Let a piece of butter become warm, add a spoonful of flour and let it brown slightly.
3. Add an onion cut into four parts and a bundle of tarragon.
4. Fill with broth, add some vinegar and two spoonfuls of cream.
5. Add the lamb pieces and let simmer until tender.
6. Before serving, arrange the lamb on a dish and strain the sauce.
7. Let the sauce come to a boil again, whisk two egg yolks in a pot, add the sauce to them, and pour over the meat.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Lammschlägel anderer Art (Lamb Leg Another Way)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- anchovies
- green parsley
- butter
- lamb leg, boned and trimmed
- fat
- mushrooms
- root vegetables
- onion
- whole spices
- red wine
- broth
- glace

## Directions

1. Cut mushrooms, anchovies and green parsley very finely and brown in butter.
2. A boned and trimmed lamb leg is stuffed with the roasted mixture, placed in a casserole in which there are fat, mushrooms, root vegetables, onion and whole spices, and braised covered until tender.
3. After about 2 hours, add red wine and broth and let the meat braise covered again.
4. When this is done, remove the meat, cut it into desired pieces, arrange on a platter, cook the strained, defatted juice with dissolved glace and pour it over the arranged meat.

title: Lapin à la Poulette (Rabbit with Poulette Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces butter
- 2 pieces mushrooms, thinly sliced
- 1 piece onion
- Several sprigs parsley
- 1 rabbit, skinned and cleaned
- Soup stock
- 8 ounces flour
- Egg yolks (optional)
- Cold milk (optional)
- Cream (optional)
- Vinegar, lemon juice, or white wine (optional)
- Madeira wine and truffles (optional, for brown preparation)

## Directions

1. Put 16 ounces butter in a casserole with mushrooms, onion, and parsley sprigs
2. Cut the cleaned rabbit into 2-finger-wide pieces and add to the casserole
3. Cover and brown the rabbit well
4. Pour some soup stock over it and simmer until tender
5. Remove the cooked meat and place in a casserole, pouring some of the cooking liquid over it and keeping it warm in a warm place
6. Meanwhile, brown 8 ounces flour in the same amount of butter, pour the cooking liquid over it, stir well, and let it simmer
7. Pour this brown butter sauce over the arranged meat on a platter
8. Optionally, whisk 3 egg yolks with a few spoonfuls of cold milk and mix well with the butter sauce
9. Or add some cream and let it simmer well
10. The butter sauce can also be seasoned with some vinegar, lemon juice, or white wine
11. For brown preparation, keep the butter sauce darker and enhance the flavor with Madeira wine and truffles
12. All game given with piquant sauce can also be garnished with small roasted onions and butter paste

title: Larded Goose Liver (Foie-gras braise à la crème)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 beautiful, large goose liver
- fine bacon for larding
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- 2 pieces finely sliced mushrooms
- 5 bunches of parsley tied together
- 1/2 liter sour cream
- approximately 4 tablespoons soup
- 1 tablespoon capers

## Directions

1. Clean the goose liver as described above, lard it lengthwise with fine bacon, and dust with salt and pepper
2. Put some butter in a casserole, place the larded liver on top, cover it with a thin, sliced bacon slice, and let it braise covered
3. After 10 minutes, baste the goose liver with 1/2 liter sour cream and approximately 4 tablespoons of soup and let it braise covered for 1.5 hours
4. Remove it to a serving dish and reduce the sauce slightly, which can also have 1 tablespoon of capers added to it
5. Serve the sauce with the goose liver, garnished with steamed rice or macaroni

title: Larded Veal Fillets (Filets de veau piqués)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal round
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bacon
- Meat mallet
- Bouillon
- Bouillon glace

## Directions

1. Cut strips from a veal round that are finger-long and two fingers wide.
2. After beating these with a meat mallet, salt them and lard with bacon, place them larded side up in a shallow braising casserole in very hot fat and let them braise in the oven with frequent basting with a little bouillon and their own juices.
3. When the bacon has a light yellow color, place the pan on the stove, spread the strips with dissolved bouillon glace, arrange them on a serving dish and serve with the de-fatted juices.

title: Larks, Roasted on the Grate (Lerchen, auf dem Roste gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- larks
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Pluck the larks and remove the heads
2. Carefully remove the small entrails so that the fat is not pulled out with them
3. Place them in water for a quarter hour
4. Salt them, roast them on a lark spit over the grate, basting frequently with butter
5. Sprinkle them with breadcrumbs
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Leber auf Tirolerart (Liver Tyrolean-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 dkg calf's liver
- ½ liter cream
- 6 dkg bacon
- 5 dkg hot fat
- salt
- pepper
- water
- fried potato pancakes or dumplings

## Directions

1. Prepare the liver, cut into slices, salt, pepper, and lard with bacon strips.
2. 30 minutes before serving, place in a pot with 5 dkg hot fat, brown lightly.
3. Pour in a little water and simmer for 15 minutes.
4. Strain the sauce and pour cream over it, finish simmering.
5. Serve with fried potato pancakes or dumplings.

title: Leber auf Wildbretart (Calf's Liver in Game Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 dkg calf's liver
- 4 h root vegetables
- 4 dkg bacon
- 1/2 l cream
- 5 dkg hot fat
- 1/2 onion
- 3 peppercorns and spice seeds
- water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. In 5 dkg hot fat, brown the cleaned, washed, thinly sliced root vegetables with 1/2 onion, 3 peppercorns and spice seeds, and the diced bacon until dark golden.
2. Add the skinned, washed, salted, and peppered liver, pour in some water and vinegar, cover and braise for 1/2 to 1 hour.
3. Remove the liver and cut into slices. Strain the sauce, add cream, let it reduce, put the liver back in and simmer a few more minutes.
4. Serve with rice, noodles or potato pancakes.

title: Leber auf Wildbretart (Liver Venison-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 dkg calf's liver
- 4 dkg root vegetables
- 4 dkg bacon
- ½ liter cream
- 5 dkg hot fat
- ½ onion
- 3 peppercorns and spice berries
- salt
- pepper
- water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. In hot fat, roast the cleaned, washed root vegetables cut into thin slices with ½ onion, peppercorns, and spice berries until dark golden.
2. Add diced bacon.
3. Add the cleaned, washed, salted, and peppered liver.
4. Pour in a little water and vinegar, cover, and simmer for ½-1 hour.

title: Leber gebacken (Fried Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-60 dkg calf's liver
- salt
- bread crumbs
- hot lard
- cooked lettuce, spinach, or potato salad

## Directions

1. Remove the membrane from the liver, wash, and cut into slices.
2. Salt just before frying.
3. Coat in bread crumbs.
4. Fry in hot lard.
5. Serve with cooked lettuce, spinach, or potato salad.

title: Leber geröstert (Roasted Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 dkg calf's liver
- 6 dkg hot fat
- 1 onion
- salt
- pepper
- water or broth
- vinegar
- fried potato pancakes or rice

## Directions

1. In hot fat, lightly sauté a whole onion and push aside.
2. Add the cleaned, washed liver and roast on both sides over high heat for 10-15 minutes.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Pour in a little water or broth with a few drops of vinegar.
5. Serve immediately with fried potato pancakes or rice.

title: Leber-Nockerl (Noques au foie)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 Deka scraped liver
- 1/4 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley roasted in butter
- 1 knife point marjoram
- 30 Deka finely chopped marrow
- 20 Deka peeled, diced, milk-soaked breadcrumbs
- 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
- some salt, pepper and spices as desired
- 6 Deka breadcrumbs
- boiling salted water

## Directions

1. Combine the liver with the parsley, marjoram, marrow, breadcrumbs, eggs, salt, pepper and spices
2. Pound and press through a sieve into a deep bowl
3. Stir in 6 Deka breadcrumbs
4. Let rest covered for 3/4 hour
5. Place Nockerl into boiling salted water and cook covered for 8-10 minutes
6. Remove with a skimming spoon
7. Note: 2 tablespoons finely chopped onions can also be roasted with the parsley if desired

title: Leber-Pudding (Poudding de foie de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 deca calf's liver
- 1 teaspoon finely cut green parsley
- 1 small piece onion
- pinch of marjoram
- butter
- 16 deca rind-removed, finely cut rolls
- milk
- 10 deca butter
- 4 egg yolks
- salt
- spices (optional)
- 1 1/4 liter white breadcrumbs
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Scrape and finely cut 60 deca calf's liver
2. Meanwhile roast 1 teaspoon finely cut green parsley, 1 small piece onion, and a pinch of marjoram in some butter
3. Pound 16 deca rind-removed finely cut rolls soaked with milk with the liver and the roasted mixture and pass through a sieve
4. Beat 10 deca butter and stir in the passed liver, 4 egg yolks, some salt, and spices if desired
5. Lightly fold in 1 1/4 liter white breadcrumbs mixed with the snow from 4 egg whites
6. Fill into a mold greased with butter and lined with flour
7. Cook in steam

title: Leber-Reis (Riz de foie)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- liver mixture as in the preceding recipe
- boiling salted water
- hot broth

## Directions

1. Place a colander over a saucepan containing boiling salted water
2. Press the liver mixture through with a cooking spoon into the boiling soup or salted water
3. Remove the liver rice that forms with a skimming spoon and place in a soup pot containing 4 fingers-width of hot soup
4. Continue until all the dough is used
5. A colander can be used instead of the sieve to press through the liver dough
6. Pour the required hot soup over the liver rice and serve
7. Add 2 tablespoons flour at the same time as the Nockerl

title: Leberknödel (Liver Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg liver and spleen together
- 2 bread rolls (Schneidemmels)
- a small piece of finely chopped onion
- 1½ bread rolls soaked in water
- 4 dkg butter or fat
- 1 egg
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- a little marjoram
- 6 dkg fine breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Wash the liver and spleen, scrape them, and pass them through a sieve with a small piece of finely chopped onion and 1½ bread rolls soaked in water and well pressed out.
2. Mix the whole thing with a mixture of 4 dkg butter or fat, 1 egg, salt, pepper, chopped parsley, and a little marjoram.
3. Mix in 6 dkg fine breadcrumbs.
4. Form small dumplings and put them into the strained boiling beef broth for 5 minutes.

title: Leberknödel mit Grieß (Liver Dumplings with Semolina)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- 64 deca strained calf's liver
- 24 deca rolls, rind removed and diced, soaked in 1/8 liter milk
- 26 deca semolina

## Directions

1. Beat 12 deca of butter until fluffy.
2. Gradually stir in 5 egg yolks and 3 whole eggs.
3. Add 64 deca of strained calf's liver, 24 deca of finely diced rolls (rind removed and softened in 1/8 liter milk), and 26 deca of semolina.
4. Let this rest for 1 hour.
5. Form dumplings from the dough and place them in simmering salted water.
6. Cook covered for approximately 15-20 minutes, depending on the size of the dumplings.
7. They will become considerably larger during cooking.
8. Remove them with a skimmer, place on a plate, sprinkle with breadcrumbs roasted in butter or, if desired, with grated horseradish, and drizzle with melted butter.
9. If they are for soup, add nothing to them.

title: Leberkoch (Liver Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound butter
- one-quarter pound ground almonds
- the soft part of a small roll soaked in milk and squeezed out
- one-half pound sliced veal liver
- two loth citron
- the zest of half a lemon, finely chopped
- sugar to taste
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat one-quarter pound butter until very fluffy
2. Stir in one-quarter pound ground almonds, the soft part of a small roll that has been soaked in milk and squeezed out, one-half pound sliced veal liver, two loth citron, the finely chopped zest of half a lemon, and enough sugar to make it sweet
3. Add a bit of nutmeg
4. Stir everything together for half an hour
5. Make a rim of short pastry on a sheet pan and bake it
6. Spread the sheet and rim with butter
7. Pour the liver mixture into it
8. Bake it slowly for three-quarters of an hour and serve it sweetened
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Leberpuresuppe mit gebackenen Knöderln (Liver Puree Soup with Baked Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter root vegetables
- 10 dkg liver and spleen
- 5 dkg fat
- 1 onion
- 5 peppercorns and spice berries
- 1 bay leaf
- thyme
- a piece of black bread crust
- vinegar
- water or beef broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean and slice the root vegetables
2. In a pot, roast the vegetables with 5 dkg fat, 1 onion, scraped liver and spleen, 5 peppercorns and spice berries, 1 bay leaf, thyme, and a piece of black bread crust until dark brown
3. Sprinkle with vinegar and pour in water
4. Braise for 1¼ hours, stirring frequently and adding more liquid as needed
5. Press everything through a sieve
6. Add water or leftover beef broth, season to taste with vinegar and salt, and cook well
7. Alternative: instead of liver, use leftover game meat and bone remnants, then it is called wild puree soup

title: Leberreis (Liver Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg liver and spleen together
- 2 bread rolls (Schneidemmels)
- a small piece of finely chopped onion
- 1½ bread rolls soaked in water
- 4 dkg butter or fat
- 1 egg
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- a little marjoram
- 6 dkg flour

## Directions

1. Wash the liver and spleen, scrape them, and pass them through a sieve with a small piece of finely chopped onion and 1½ bread rolls soaked in water and well pressed out.
2. Mix the whole thing with a mixture of 4 dkg butter or fat, 1 egg, salt, pepper, chopped parsley, and a little marjoram.
3. Instead of breadcrumbs, use flour and press the mass through the reverse side of a grater into the broth.

title: Lebersknödel (Calf Liver Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deca calf liver
- a small piece of bacon, optional
- 1/4 teaspoon finely cut onion
- some green parsley
- a knife point of marjoram
- 16 deca bread crumbs
- 16 deca butter or bacon fat
- 1 whole egg
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 1/4 teaspoon salt
- a knife point of white pepper
- 16 deca bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Finely scrape the calf liver and optional bacon with a knife.
2. Roast the finely cut onion with some green parsley in a small amount of fat, pressing the fat through a sieve so that the onion remains behind.
3. Add this fat to the liver.
4. Add the bread crumbs that have been soaked in water and well pressed out.
5. Add marjoram, whole egg, egg yolk, salt, white pepper, and bread crumbs.
6. Mix everything well together, cover, and let stand for 1 hour.
7. Form knödel of any desired size and treat as the preceding knödel.

title: Lebersknödel ohne Eier (Beef Liver Knödel without Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deca beef liver
- 16 deca bread crumbs
- some finely cut green parsley
- 1/2 teaspoon onion
- 8 deca bacon fat or butter
- a knife point of ground pepper
- salt
- 18 deca broth
- 2 tablespoons flour
- a pinch of marjoram

## Directions

1. Finely chop the beef liver.
2. Place the bread crumbs that have been soaked in water and well pressed out in a deep bowl with some finely cut green parsley.
3. Roast the onion in 8 deca bacon fat or butter, then press this fat through a sieve so the onion remains behind and add to the liver.
4. Add ground pepper, salt, broth, flour, and marjoram.
5. Mix everything well together, cover, and let stand for 1/4 hour.
6. Form knödel of any desired size.
7. Treat as with the preceding knödel.

title: Leberwurst (Liver Sausage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilo pork meat from head, shoulder, or neck
- 1/2 kilo pork liver
- 1/4 kilo bacon
- Meat seasoning
- Pepper
- Salt
- 1 onion, finely minced and roasted
- 6 tablespoons raw, well-rinsed rice, or 3/4 liter barley cooked in water, well drained and cooled
- Cleaned and washed intestines
- Twine

## Directions

1. Boil the meat, liver, and bacon together, then finely chop on a board.
2. Add meat seasoning, pepper, salt, the roasted minced onion, and either the raw rice or the cooked barley.
3. Mix everything together thoroughly.
4. Fill the cleaned and washed intestines with the mixture, tie with twine, and bind at intervals.
5. Cook in boiling salted water for 1/2 hour.
6. If the sausages are to be stored, lay them separately in a cool place.
7. If they are to be fried, place them in hot fat and fry brown on both sides.

title: Lebkuchen (Gingerbread)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg honey
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 1 ground clove
- 6 dkg sugar
- ½ kg flour
- 1 whole egg
- 4 dkg butter
- Not quite 1 teaspoon baking soda
- 6 dkg grated hazelnuts for filling
- Lemon zest
- 3 dkg finely chopped citron
- 2 dkg chopped orange peel
- 1 egg for brushing
- Almonds for topping

## Directions

1. Heat honey with spices until boiling
2. Mix on the board with flour and remaining ingredients
3. Work the dough well
4. Cover and let rest one day
5. Roll out and cut various shapes
6. Add filling to the center
7. Cover with dough sheet
8. Press edges firmly
9. Brush with egg
10. Top with half an almond
11. Place on unbuttered sheet
12. Bake at moderate heat until light brown
13. Let rest 4-5 days before serving
14. For filling: mix grated hazelnuts, lemon zest, chopped citron, and chopped orange peel with 1 egg and sugar to taste

title: Lebkuchen (Gingerbread)
body:
## Ingredients

- honey
- spices
- 1/2 kg flour
- 1 whole egg
- 4 dkg butter
- not quite 1 teaspoon baking soda

## Directions

1. Let the honey cook with the spices
2. Mix it on the board with the flour and remaining ingredients
3. Work the dough well
4. Let it rest covered for one day
5. Roll it out and cut various shapes
6. Place filling in the middle
7. Lay a sheet of dough on top
8. Press the edges firmly together
9. Brush with egg
10. Top with half an almond
11. Place the gingerbread on an unbuttered baking sheet
12. Bake at moderate heat until light brown
13. Let them rest for 4-5 days before serving

title: Leichte Kraftssuppe (Light Broth)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo boned, lean beef
- 1 kilo veal meat
- 1 old, cleaned chicken cut into 4 pieces
- 1 old, roasted hen or old rooster
- 4 liters cold water
- 1 piece parsley
- 1 piece yellow turnip
- 1 piece celery
- 1 small piece onion with 1 spice clove inserted (optional)
- salt (to taste)

## Directions

1. Chop 1 kilo boned, lean beef and 1 kilo veal meat
2. Add 1 old, cleaned chicken cut into 4 pieces and 1 old, roasted hen or old rooster
3. Place in a casserolle and fill with 4 liters cold water
4. Cover and place on fire, letting it cook slowly for 1 hour
5. Then add 1 piece parsley, yellow turnip, celery and if desired, 1 small piece onion with a spice clove inserted
6. Let it cook slowly for 2-3 more hours, so that the total cooking time is 3-4 hours
7. Set it aside, remove the fat that has formed cleanly, and strain it through a fine sieve into an earthen vessel
8. Add salt when the broth is being used
9. If necessary, strain the broth through fine cloth

title: Leichte Nüsse in Dunst verkocht (Light Walnuts Cooked in Steam)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- green walnuts
- lemon juice
- cold lemon water
- boiling lemon water
- 80 decilitres sugar
- cold sugar water
- vanilla (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove green walnuts at the beginning of June
2. Let them lie for several days
3. Remove the green skin down to the white, cut the walnut neatly
4. Rub with lemon juice
5. Throw into cold, then into boiling lemon water and cook fairly soft
6. Pass three times into cold water and fill the jars with them
7. Pour so much cold sugar (of 80 decilitres) into these that the walnuts are completely covered
8. Bind with papers and cook further as the Muscatel pears (Nr. 1842) for 3-4 hours in steam
9. One can cook a small piece of vanilla in the sugar

title: Lemon Ice (Citronen-Gefrornes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 lemons
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 seidel water

## Directions

1. Rub half of 18 lemons on a pound of sugar
2. Scrape the yellow from the sugar into a terrine
3. Press the juice from the 18 lemons into it
4. Clarify the remaining sugar with a seidel of water
5. Add the cooled sugar to the mass
6. Pass everything through a fine sieve
7. Pour into the freezing container and prepare
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Lemon Soufflé (Soufflé aux Citrons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 tablespoons softened butter
- 16 tablespoons vanilla sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 tablespoons blanched almonds, finely ground, with 1 egg white
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks, passed through a sieve
- 10 tablespoons finely diced citron
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix the softened butter with vanilla sugar until well combined
2. Add 8 egg yolks one after another, stirring
3. Gradually mix in the blanched almonds (ground with 1 egg white), hard-boiled egg yolks, and finely diced citron
4. Lightly fold in the snow of 6 egg whites
5. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour with this mixture
6. Cover and cook 3/4 to 1 hour in a water bath or bake in the oven
7. Turn out onto a serving dish and dust with finely ground sugar and serve at table

title: Lemon Soufflé, Another Kind
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 tablespoons flour
- 0.5 liter cold water
- 14 tablespoons lemon juice
- 1 lemon
- 7 egg yolks
- 7 egg whites
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Put flour in a saucepan and gradually pour in 0.5 liter of cold water, stirring over the fire until it forms a thick dough that separates from the spoon
2. Transfer to a deep bowl and let cool
3. Meanwhile, beat 14 tablespoons lemon juice (from 1 lemon) with 7 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
4. Gradually mix this into the cooled mixture
5. Lightly fold in the snow of 7 egg whites
6. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour with this mixture
7. Cover and cook in a water bath or bake slowly in the oven at low heat for 1.5 hours, during which time it should rise to twice its height
8. Turn out onto a serving dish, dust with finely ground sugar, and serve immediately
9. If desired, before baking or cooking, insert baked pralines into the mixture

title: Lendenbraten auf ungarische Art (Loin Roast Hungarian Style)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One whole loin roast, skinned
- Several small rolls
- Fresh bacon
- Salt
- Butter
- Vinegar
- Sour cream
- Grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Skin a whole loin roast well and cut it crosswise into small schnitzel about as thick as a knife-back
2. Peel the crust off several small rolls and cut them lengthwise into very thin slices
3. Take fresh bacon and cut it into fine schnitzel of the same thickness as the roll and meat schnitzel
4. Sprinkle the meat schnitzel with a little salt and arrange them properly on top of each other in this order: one schnitzel of meat, one schnitzel of bacon, and one schnitzel of roll, continuing until everything is well fitted together
5. Insert two small skewers through them and press firmly together so that it returns to the shape of a loin roast
6. Bind it with a roasting spit above and below and roast for 2 hours very slowly
7. Before roasting, brush with butter and vinegar, and lastly with sour cream
8. Before serving, sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese over it and seek through a couple of minutes with strong fire to give it a beautiful crust
9. When serving, place the drained sauce on the dish and lay the roast on it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Lentil Purée Soup (Purée de lentilles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter lentils, cleaned and washed
- 1 parsley root
- 1 yellow turnip
- 1 onion
- some parsley and leek roots
- 10 ounces butter
- 2.5 liters beef soup
- white pepper
- salt
- bread croutons
- vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook 1/2 liter of cleaned, washed lentils until soft, strain and pass through a sieve
2. Clean and peel 1 parsley root, 1 yellow turnip, 1 onion and some parsley and leek roots, slice finely
3. Roast this in 10 ounces butter, pour in 2.5 liters of beef soup and let simmer 1/2 hour slowly
4. Pass the vegetable soup through a sieve, remove the fat cleanly and thin the lentil purée with it
5. Bring to a boil again and remove scum and fat once more
6. Season with salt, white pepper and serve with bread croutons
7. If desired, this soup can be soured with vinegar or lemon juice

title: Lentilles liées (Stewed Lentils)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lentils
- Roux (dark)

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as the beans, except that the roux must be dark brown instead of light

title: Lerchen in Aspik (Larks in Aspic)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Larks
- Aspic jelly
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Goose liver or truffles

## Directions

1. These are prepared like the larks in croustades. When the larks in the casserole in which they were stewed have cooled completely, they are carefully removed, laid on a cloth and each lark is well dried. Pour into a deep mold that was put on ice 1/2 hour earlier, strong finger-high layer of melted aspic. While this sets, cut from 1 hard-boiled egg strips 2 fingers wide, which you cut out with a round cutter. This is then cut out again with a three numbers smaller cutter, creating a ring. Similar rings are cut out from knife-back thick pieces of tongue. Lay these pieces alternating in color on the now set aspic and place the cut-out tongue pieces in the white rings, and in the red ones the egg pieces. You can use some pieces of goose liver or truffles for this decoration. Pour finger-high aspic again on top and when completely set, lay the stewed larks on top so that the heads rest downward on the aspic and the breasts are turned toward the outer wall. So that the birds remain securely in place, support them with some chopped aspic and you can also place a piece of stewed goose liver next to each lark. When all larks are placed in the mold, carefully pour melted aspic around the edge so that the birds are completely covered. After this has set firmly, remove the mold from the ice and proceed further as with the goose liver cake in aspic. Shortly before serving, place herb mayonnaise in the center of the dish, neatly arranged. All lark dishes are recommended as side dishes, main courses, for supper or tea, or also for breakfast.

title: Lerchen in Croustades (Larks in Croustades)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Larks
- According to number of persons

## Directions

1. Take larks according to number of persons, usually taking several pieces more
2. After plucking and singeing the larks, cut them lengthwise along the entire back and carefully loosen the bones

title: Lerchen in Krustaden (Larks in Croustades)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Larks
- Cold water
- Salt
- Veal or calves' liver pâté
- Lard
- 1/4 litre broth
- Madeira or white wine
- Glaze
- Demi-glace sauce
- Lemon juice
- Truffles
- Butter pastry or risotto rice

## Directions

1. Take the number of larks according to the number of persons to be served, usually taking some extra pieces. After the larks are plucked and flamed, they are cut lengthwise along the entire back and the bones are carefully removed, though head and neck are left attached. Lay them all on a board that has been previously moistened with cold water and dust them with a little salt. Then place in each bird 1 teaspoon of veal or calves' liver pâté, sew the skin of the bird back together, filling the eye sockets with pâté and placing the beak in the breast. Put some lard in a casserole, place the birds with heads upward, closely packed together, and if necessary, fill any excess space in the casserole with bread pieces. Cover the larks again with lard strips, add 1/4 litre broth and some Madeira or white wine, and let them stew covered for 1/3 hour. Serve them in small croustades, placing one lark with head upward in each croustade instead of pâté, and lightly coat the larks with dissolved glaze. Strain the cooking liquid, skim off fat, mix with some demi-glace sauce, lemon juice and thinly sliced truffles, and serve alongside. Alternatively, the larks can be arranged upright in a butter pastry shell with pâté in the middle so that the heads point upward; serve with the mentioned sauce with some demi-glace. The larks can also be served with steamed rice, namely place the rice raised on a round, deep bowl, press with a spoon that has been dipped in hot broth in the middle of the rice, while pushing rice from the edge of the bowl toward the middle with a tablespoon so that the rice forms a nest shape, upon which place the larks in the middle. With each of these serving styles the larks are lightly coated with glaze and the mentioned sauce is served. They can also be served in a polenta nest.

title: Light, Inexpensive Broth
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pork bones
- 6 pig's feet
- 1 ox foot
- 1⅔ kg ham bone
- 4 whole onions
- ½ piece celery
- yellow turnips
- parsley
- 1 bay leaf
- ammonia
- butter herbs
- 10 grains each of spice varieties
- 1 liter white wine
- 2½ liters vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Hack the bones and feet into small pieces, rinse them, place in a pot with 6 liters cold water
2. Bring to boil, carefully remove foam and fat
3. Add the whole onions, celery, yellow turnips, parsley, bay leaf, ammonia, butter herbs, and spices
4. Simmer slowly for 5 hours
5. Remove fat completely, add 1 liter white wine, 2½ liters vinegar, and salt
6. Simmer another hour
7. Strain through fine sieve into earthenware vessel, let stand overnight
8. Treat as with the previous broth
9. Yields 3 to 4 liters

title: Likör-Sulz (Liqueur Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 decagrams sugar, clarified
- 7 decagrams isinglass, hot and dissolved
- 1/2 liter liqueur of choice, instead of wine, lemon and orange juice

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Orange Sulz (see recipe 1660)
2. Instead of wine, lemon and orange juice, use 1/2 liter of liqueur of choice

title: Limburger-Sauce (Limburger Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces butter
- 8 ounces flour
- 1/4 liter white wine
- equal amount of white broth

## Directions

1. Toast equal amounts of flour in 8 ounces butter
2. Pour this with 1/4 liter white wine and equal amount of white broth
3. Stir well together and let it cook well

title: Limonade (Lemon Beverage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure water
- 3 beautiful juicy lemons
- sugar rubbed with the zest of one lemon
- 1/4 pound sugar
- optional: 1 seidel wine

## Directions

1. Take 1 measure water
2. Use 3 beautiful juicy lemons, rub the yellow zest from one of them onto sugar
3. Press the juice from all 3 lemons onto it
4. Stir well together
5. Sweeten with 1/4 pound sugar
6. Pass through a napkin
7. If desired, add 1 seidel wine, which greatly enhances the taste of the lemonade
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Limonien-Liqueur (Lemon Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow peel of 6 lemons
- 1.5 pounds sugar
- 1 glass water
- 5 Seidel brandy

## Directions

1. Cut the yellow peel from the lemons, remove the white part cleanly, then cut the yellow peel finely and place in a bottle or jug
2. Add 5 Seidel brandy to the bottle
3. Boil 1.5 pounds sugar in 1 glass water until well skimmed
4. Pour the sugar mixture into the brandy
5. Let stand for three weeks
6. Shake well and let stand for another twelve days
7. It becomes better the older it gets
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Limonien-Liqueur (Lemon Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow peel from 6 lemons
- 5 seidels brandy
- 1 and one-half pounds sugar
- 1 glass water

## Directions

1. Cut off the yellow peel from 6 lemons
2. Clean away the white part completely
3. Cut the yellow peel finely
4. Put it into a bottle or jug containing 5 seidels brandy
5. Simmer 1 and one-half pounds sugar in 1 glass water until well skimmed
6. Pour the sugar syrup under the brandy
7. Let stand for three weeks
8. Shake well and let stand for another twelve days
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Limonien-Syrup (Lemon Syrup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 beautiful juicy lemons
- 2 pounds sugar
- 1/2 measure water

## Directions

1. Take 18 beautiful juicy lemons
2. Rub the zest from 6 of them onto sugar
3. Press the juice over the rubbed zest
4. Cook 2 pounds sugar with 1/2 measure water to make a syrup
5. Stir the rubbed zest and lemon juice into the syrup while still warm
6. Pass both through a fine sieve
7. When cooled, fill into bottles
8. To use, pour a small portion into sufficient water and stir
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Lined Walloon Lamb Shanks (Ausgefüterte wälsche Haßgenſchen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb shanks
- Truffles
- Poultry livers
- Calf sweetbreads
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Mushrooms, finely chopped
- Shallots
- Onions
- Basil
- Scraped bacon
- Egg yolks
- Bacon strips
- Sauce italienne

## Directions

1. From several lamb shanks, separate the bones leaving a short piece attached
2. Prepare a good salpicon from truffles, poultry livers, and calf sweetbreads
3. Add salt, pepper, spices, finely chopped mushrooms, shallots, onions, basil, and scraped bacon to the salpicon
4. Stir in a couple of egg yolks
5. Fill the shanks with this mixture and sew them closed, maintaining their shape
6. Cover them with bacon strips
7. Cook them in a strong but short braise until tender, and they should have a beautiful white appearance
8. When serving, place sauce italienne underneath
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Linsen mit Einbrenn (Lentils with Roux)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg lentils
- salt
- 5 dkg fat
- water or broth
- vinegar
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Sort and wash the lentils
2. Soak overnight
3. In the morning, drain the soaking water and add fresh cold water to cover
4. After bringing to a boil, reduce heat and simmer for 1½ hours or more
5. Prepare a dark yellow roux from 5 dkg fat
6. Add the cooked lentils to the roux
7. Season with vinegar, salt, and sugar if desired
8. Add water or broth and simmer for 20 minutes

title: Linsensuppe (Lentil Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- brown broth from meat or fish
- a baked pike, tench or carp head
- baked bread slices
- crushed pepper
- butter
- flour
- lentils
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a brown broth from meat or fish and a baked pike, tench or carp head
2. Pound it very finely with baked bread slices
3. Add some crushed pepper
4. Make a brown roux with butter and flour
5. Add the cleaned, picked, cooked and salted lentils and the pounded ingredients with the broth
6. Let it cook well, sour it a bit, force it through a fine sieve
7. Salt and season it, then serve over baked pike liver and toasted bread slices
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Linzer Torte with Hard-Boiled Egg Yolks (Tourte à la Linz)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka blanched almonds
- 2 egg whites for grinding almonds
- 10 hard-boiled and passed egg yolks
- 32 Deka butter
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 32 Deka flour

## Directions

1. Grind blanched almonds very finely with egg whites
2. Finely grind with hard-boiled and passed egg yolks
3. Place on a board and work with lemon juice and flour into a dough
4. Cover and let rest for 1/2 hour in a cold place
5. Roll out dough to finger thickness on a flour-dusted board
6. Proceed as in the 'sugar dough torte' (no. 1479)

title: Linzerteigbäckerei (Linzer Pastry Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dkg flour
- 14 dkg butter
- 7 dkg raw grated almonds
- 10½ dkg sugar

## Directions

1. Make a crumbly dough from the flour, butter, raw grated almonds and sugar
2. Let it rest
3. Roll it out
4. Cut it into desired shapes
5. Place the pastry on an unbuttered baking sheet
6. Bake until light brown

title: Linzertorte (Linz Torte)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Three-quarters pound butter
- Three-quarters pound sugar
- Three-quarters pound flour
- Three-quarters pound almonds
- Zest of one lemon
- Rheinwein
- Preserved fruit, such as cherries, apricots, etc.
- Egg white foam

## Directions

1. Take butter, sugar, flour, and almonds, three-quarters pound each
2. Also take the zest of one lemon, pound the almonds with a little Rheinwein, scatter the flour on the pastry board, and make a dough
3. Brush paper with butter, roll out a piece of the dough finger-thick on it, and cut out a base
4. Spread preserved fruit on it as desired, such as cherries, apricots, etc.
5. From the remaining dough that is rolled out, make a lattice over the torte, brush this with egg white foam, and bake for a good hour
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Linzertorte (Linzer Torte)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg flour
- 10 dkg butter
- 10 dkg sugar
- 10 dkg raw grated almonds
- 3 hard-boiled sifted egg yolks
- 1 ground clove
- 1 pinch cinnamon
- finely chopped lemon zest
- apple jam

## Directions

1. Rub together 10 dkg flour and 10 dkg butter on a board
2. Knead with 10 dkg sugar, 10 dkg raw grated almonds, 3 hard-boiled sifted egg yolks, 1 ground clove, 1 pinch cinnamon, and finely chopped lemon zest into a dough
3. Let the dough rest for 30 minutes and divide into three parts
4. Roll out one part to the size of the torte form bottom and place in the form
5. Form a roll from the second part, place in the form, and press the dough against the wall
6. Spread the bottom with apple jam
7. Form several thin rolls from the third part, arrange them in a lattice pattern on top, and press at the edges
8. Bake at higher heat
9. You may fill additional jam into the gaps and cut the torte evenly

title: Linzertortelettes (Linzer Tartlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dkg flour
- 7 dkg sugar
- 7 dkg grated hazelnuts
- 7 dkg fine breadcrumbs
- 14 dkg diced butter

## Directions

1. Quickly make a dough from the flour, sugar, grated hazelnuts, fine breadcrumbs and diced butter
2. Let it rest
3. Roll it out in portions
4. Cut with a round cutter
5. Place the tartlets on an unbuttered baking sheet
6. Bake at higher heat until light brown
7. If the dough has not rested well, the tartlets will usually come out of shape; then cut them again with a smaller cutter while still hot
8. Brush them on the flat side with jam
9. Press them together
10. Glaze them white, red or brown
11. Decorate them with candied fruit

title: Linſen mit Repphühnern (Lentils with Partridges)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned partridges
- Large lentils
- Capon fat or fat from another roast
- Onions
- Bacon
- Lemon peel
- Lemon juice
- Wine

## Directions

1. Cut the heads off the cleaned partridges, bend the wings around, salt and truss them with thin skewers
2. Cook beautiful large lentils until soft, keeping them whole and removing the husks completely
3. After draining the water, stew the lentils in a pan with some capon fat or fat from another roast and finely cut onions
4. The partridges can be roasted or stewed with bacon, lemon peel, a little lemon juice, onions and some wine
5. Pour the small sauce from the partridges onto the lentils and let them stew together for a short while
6. When serving, place the partridges in the middle of the dish and arrange the lentils over them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Liqueur mit Anis (Anise Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 and one-quarter pounds sugar
- 1 glass water
- 3 ounces anise
- half seidel water
- 3 seidels brandy

## Directions

1. Let 1 and one-quarter pounds sugar cook with 1 glass water until the sugar is completely and well dissolved
2. Scald 3 ounces anise with half seidel of boiling hot water
3. Cover and let stand for half an hour
4. Pour under the boiling sugar
5. Remove from fire and pour in 3 seidels brandy
6. Mix well together and pour into a stone jug
7. Set in the sun, or in winter on the stove, and let stand tightly stoppered for three weeks
8. After three weeks, filter and fill into bottles and cork well
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Liqueur von Marillen (Apricot Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 good, ripe, juicy apricots
- 1 Maß brandy
- 1.5 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Cut 25 apricots into small pieces
2. Crack the pits, peel them, and crush them roughly
3. Place everything in a large glass
4. Add 1 Maß brandy and 1.5 pounds sugar
5. Bind the glass well
6. Let stand for three weeks, stirring twice daily
7. Filter, fill into bottles, cork well, and store in a cool place
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Liqueur von Marillenkernen (Apricot Kernel Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound healthy apricot kernels, some of which must be bitter
- 1 Maß brandy
- half Maß water
- some cinnamon
- 1.5 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Crush apricot kernels slightly in a mortar, but do not pound them so they do not become oily
2. Place in a large preserving glass
3. Add 1 Maß brandy, half Maß water, some cinnamon, and 1.5 pounds sugar
4. Let stand for 9 days, stirring twice daily
5. On the tenth day, filter through filter paper
6. Fill into bottles, cork well, and let stand for some months
7. The later it is used, the better it becomes
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Liqueur von rothen Früchten (Red Fruit Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds black sweet cherries
- 3 pounds raspberries
- 3 pounds strawberries
- brandy (half measure per measure of juice)
- sugar (half pound per measure of juice)

## Directions

1. Crush the cherries and crush the raspberries and strawberries
2. Put all together in a clean pot
3. Cover and let stand for 5 to 6 hours in a cool place
4. Press through thin cloth
5. For each measure of juice, add half measure brandy and half pound roughly crushed sugar
6. Put together in a large glass
7. Seal well with a bladder
8. Shake vigorously 2 to 3 times daily
9. After standing for four weeks, filter the liqueur
10. Fill into bottles, cork well, and let stand in a cool place for at least eight months
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Liqueurseis (Liqueur Glaze)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- finely sifted sugar
- any liqueur

## Directions

1. The same procedure as with Orange Glaze (No. 1560), except that ordinary powdered sugar is used instead of sugar, and any liqueur is used instead of orange juice.

title: Liqueurwasser von Erdbeeren (Strawberry Liqueur Water)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe strawberries picked before sunrise
- 1 large coffee spoon of lemon juice
- 8 ounces of fine sugar
- 6 to 8 ounces of spirits

## Directions

1. Pick ripe strawberries before sunrise or before the sun shines on them
2. Clean them of stems and leaves
3. Crush them with a wooden club in a stone mortar
4. Add 1 large coffee spoon of lemon juice
