5. Stir gently with a wooden stick and bring to a glass vessel, where the mixture stands covered for some hours
6. Weigh 8 ounces of fine sugar and bring it to a stone pot
7. Pour the mixture over the sugar
8. When the sugar has dissolved, add 6 to 8 ounces of spirits
9. Stir everything together, strain through a filter bag, and keep in a cool place
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Liver Purée Soup (Purée de foie)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilo calf's liver
- flour
- fat
- 1 piece thinly sliced onion
- 1 piece mushroom
- 3 pieces finely sliced bread
- 2 whole eggs
- approximately 3 liters beef soup
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Slice 1/2 kilo of calf's liver thinly, coat in flour and fry in hot fat until brown
2. Remove with a basting spoon to a dish covered with blotting paper
3. In the hot fat, fry 1 piece thinly sliced onion, 1 piece mushroom, 3 pieces finely sliced bread and 2 whole eggs until brown
4. Add the fried mixture to the liver in a mortar and pound it fine
5. Place this pounded mixture in a casserole and pour approximately 3 liters of beef soup over it
6. Cook slowly for 1 hour, stirring frequently
7. Pass through a fine sieve, return to the casserole and bring to a boil again
8. Remove the fat that forms
9. In a pot, whisk 3 egg yolks with 1/4 liter cream, then slowly add the boiling liver soup, season with salt and pour into a soup pot over bread cubes cut into pieces and fried in butter

title: Lièvre en Aspic (Hare in Aspic)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmis (hare ragout)
- Aspic jelly
- Truffles
- Hare back and legs

## Directions

1. Pour a finger-high layer of cooled aspic into a mold set in ice
2. Once set, cover with some truffles and pieces of hare and steamed goose liver
3. Add a finger-high layer of salmis on top
4. Layer with hare back pieces
5. Pour another finger-high layer of cooled aspic on top
6. Continue alternating layers until the mold is full
7. Pour aspic on top as the final layer
8. Cover and set in ice for 1 hour
9. Remove the mold, dip briefly in hot water, dry with a cloth, and turn out onto a platter
10. Garnish the edge of the platter with neatly cut aspic
11. Serve as an entrée or side dish

title: Lobster Salad (Salade de homard)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster
- Red roe of the lobster
- Oil
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Mayonnaise
- Diced aspic
- Olives
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Head lettuce

## Directions

1. Cook the lobster
2. Remove and finely pound the red roe in a mortar
3. Pour oil over it, stir, pass through a sieve and set aside in a cool place
4. Cut the large meat pieces neatly, sprinkle with salt and pour vinegar and oil over them
5. Mix the prepared lobster roe with mayonnaise, which becomes pale red
6. Place diced aspic in a dish, arrange the lobster meat around it in a circle
7. Cover with mayonnaise, then add more lobster pieces covered with the rest of the mayonnaise
8. Garnish this salad very neatly with variously colored aspic, olives, hard-boiled eggs, head lettuce and similar items

title: Lobster Tail (Homard queue)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster tail
- Aspic or mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Cut the tail open on the side
2. Remove the upper cover
3. Remove the meat carefully
4. Cut into neat pieces
5. Arrange neatly in the shell
6. Decorate with aspic or mayonnaise

title: Lobster Warm with Sauce (Homard chaud, sauce à la hollandaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster
- Salt water
- Butter or oil
- Fine vegetables or purées

## Directions

1. Clean the lobster carefully in cold water with a knife
2. Place it in a suitable pot and add enough boiling salt water so that it comes 3 finger-widths above the lobster
3. Cover it. Cooking time depends on size: smaller ones 3/4 hour, larger ones 1 hour
4. When serving warm, remove the claws and tail, and place the front part on a dish covered with a napkin
5. Cut the tail lengthwise through, cut the shell through from the claws and remove the meat carefully, cut it neatly and place it back on the shell
6. Do the same with the small claws
7. Reassemble the lobster on the dish and lightly pour the cooking liquid over it
8. Serve with a warm sauce: sauce tartare, sauce à la hollandaise, or another fine wine sauce
9. Everything must be done very quickly and carefully so it arrives at the table warm

title: Lobster with Cold Sauce (Homard à sauce froide)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster
- Eggs of the lobster (if available)
- Small meat pieces
- 4 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 2 raw egg yolks
- Shallots (as desired)
- Rocambole (as desired)
- 4 tablespoons French mustard
- 6 tablespoons fine oil
- Tarragon vinegar or lemon juice
- Finely cut parsley
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook the lobster as described above
2. After complete cooling, remove from the cooking water
3. Cut lengthwise through the lobster
4. From the claws, cut away the shell finger-wide and carefully remove all meat
5. If lobster eggs are present, combine them with the small meat pieces, 4 hard-boiled and 2 raw egg yolks, shallots, rocambole and 4 tablespoons French mustard as desired
6. Pass through a sieve and mix with 6 tablespoons fine oil, some tarragon vinegar or lemon juice, finely cut parsley, pepper and necessary salt into a thick sauce
7. Arrange the lobster on a dish or in the lobster shell and small claws
8. Pour some sauce over it and serve the remainder in a sauce boat

title: Lobster with Mayonnaise (Homard à la Provence)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster
- Head lettuce
- Lobster meat scraps
- Mayonnaise
- Whipped mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Treat the lobster as described in recipe 959
2. Cut head lettuce into oblong pieces
3. Mix the lobster meat scraps and some spoonfuls of mayonnaise together
4. Stir well and fill the shells and claws with this mixture
5. Arrange the cut lobster meat neatly on top
6. Decorate with whipped mayonnaise using a pastry bag with optional decorative designs
7. Serve additional mayonnaise in a sauce boat

title: Lungen-Strudel (Lungs Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter flour
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 4 ounces butter
- 1 whole egg
- lukewarm water

## Directions

1. Place 1/2 liter flour on a board, add 1/4 teaspoon salt and 4 ounces butter, rub fine with both hands
2. Make a hollow in the center, add 1 whole egg and mix by adding necessary lukewarm water with a knife, mixing well together
3. Work into a smooth dough with both hands as specified in Lungen-Strudel (Recipe 75)

title: Lungenbraten am Spieß (Beef Tenderloin on the Spit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- well-aged beef tenderloin
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- paper
- glace
- small roasted potatoes
- potato purée
- macaroni
- brown piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Take a well-aged beef tenderloin, skin it as with a naturally roasted tenderloin, spit it and sprinkle with salt and pepper
2. Insert a small spit lengthwise through the middle and bind it to a large spit with this
3. Place 4 sheets of paper brushed with butter on the tenderloin so that the buttered side lies against the meat, then bind with string
4. Roast slowly for 1 hour
5. 1/2 hour before the meat is completely tender, remove the paper and make a stronger flame so the tenderloin gets a nice color
6. Before removing from the spit, dust again with salt
7. Arrange on a platter, brush with dissolved glace and garnish with small roasted potatoes, potato purée, macaroni and various other items
8. Serve a brown piquant sauce in a sauce boat

title: Lungenbraten auf Wildbretart (Beef Tenderloin with Game Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- large beef tenderloin
- 3 tablespoons table oil
- fat and skin
- 2 small onions
- 1 small piece yellow turnip and celery
- 15 dried plums
- 16 deca black bread
- 10 juniper berries
- spice grains
- 1 clove
- 5 sprigs thyme
- 2 pieces crushed rocamboles
- 20 fresh or dried rosehips or 2 tablespoons rosehip sauce
- 1 liter red wine
- 1/2 liter broth
- cream
- 1/4 liter cream
- 4 deca sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- capers
- puff pastry
- purée
- macaroni
- beef marrow dumplings
- parsley
- watercress

## Directions

1. Skin a removed, cut, somewhat peppered and spitted large beef tenderloin
2. Place on a platter and cover with 3 tablespoons table oil
3. Meanwhile, line a casserole with the removed fat and skin, 2 small finely cut onions, 1 small piece yellow turnip and celery, 15 dried plums without pits, 16 deca finely cut black bread, 10 crushed juniper berries, 5 grains of each type of spice, 1 clove, 5 sprigs thyme, 2 pieces crushed rocamboles and 20 pieces fresh or dried rosehips or 2 tablespoons rosehip sauce
4. Place the spitted and oiled tenderloin on top, braise covered for 1/2 hour, then add 1 liter red wine and 1/2 liter broth and braise covered slowly for 1.5 hours
5. Remove the fat, place it in another casserole and lay the tenderloin covered with cream on top to roast uncovered in the oven for 15-30 minutes so it gets a nice color
6. To the sauce with the root vegetables add 1/4 liter cream and 4 deca sugar, let it boil, strain and let it boil again
7. If the sauce is too thin, mix the cream with 1 tablespoon of flour and add it, then strain and boil
8. Garnish the tenderloin on the platter with game pâté or variously cut puff pastry, fine purée, macaroni, beef marrow dumplings, or give a mixed garnish
9. Decorate the ends of the platter with sprigs of green parsley or watercress
10. Mix the sauce, which must be piquant and thick, with 1 tablespoon capers and serve in a sauce boat after removing the fat
11. This tenderloin serves 10 to 12 persons

title: Lungenstrubel oder Fleisch-Strubel (Lung Strudel or Meat Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca lard, butter, or pork fat
- 1/2 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 1 pinch marjoram
- Optional: 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped onion
- 8 deca breadcrumbs
- 62 deca cooked, finely chopped calf or lamb lung
- Salt and spices to taste
- Optional: pepper
- 2 whole eggs
- 1/2 liter flour for the dough
- 1 whole egg for the dough
- Lukewarm water as needed

## Directions

1. Place 8 deca of lard, butter, or pork fat in a casserole, add 1/2 teaspoon of chopped parsley, 1 pinch of marjoram (and if desired, 1/2 teaspoon of finely chopped onion), and roast.
2. Add 8 deca of breadcrumbs.
3. Mix this well together until hot, then place it in a bowl.
4. Add the necessary salt, spices, and if desired, a pinch of pepper.
5. Beat in 2 whole eggs and stir everything together well.
6. For the strudel dough, place 1/2 liter of flour on a board, make a depression in the middle, add 1 pinch of salt, 1 whole egg, and add lukewarm water gradually, working it with the right hand or a broad knife into the flour while supporting with the back of a knife held in the left hand.
7. The dough should stretch easily.
8. Knead it on the board until it separates from your hand.
9. Dust the board with flour, place the dough on it, work it with the palm and fingers until blisters appear on the dough.
10. Place the dough on a flour-dusted board so that the worked side is on the bottom and the smooth, pointed side is on top.
11. Cover it with a deep, warm bowl and let it rest for 1/3 hour.
12. Cover a table with a cloth, dust it well with flour, place the dough in the middle, dust it with flour, and stretch it somewhat with a rolling pin (Strudel roller).
13. Then stretch it out fully by holding both hands so that the thumbs point inward and the dough rests on both index fingers, stretching the dough over the cloth to a certain length.
14. Then lay it on the cloth and stretch it completely thin with your fingertips.
15. Cut away the edges, which never become completely thin, then brush the dough with beaten eggs using a brush and distribute the cold filling evenly along the edge of the dough.
16. Fold the dough over on one side 3 fingers wide, sprinkle this fold with the remaining filling, and roll up the dough together by holding the cloth lightly raised.
17. Dust the strudel lengthwise with flour, divide it with a wooden spoon dipped in water into pieces of desired size (approximately 2-3 fingers wide) so that when pressed up, the filling is pushed back on both sides of the piece and the dough closes.
18. Press it together on both sides with two fingers and the thumb and place it on a flour-dusted board.
19. Place the strudel pieces in simmering salted water and cook covered for 10-12 minutes.
20. Then remove them with a skimmer and place them in the hot soup, which should come to the table immediately covered.
21. Note that the lung strudel, like all cooked strudels that follow, must be served immediately after being removed from the simmering water, as they become firm with longer standing.

title: Lustiger Dennis Strudel (Cheerful Dennis Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 deca flour
- 4 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter milk
- 4 deca melted butter
- 6 tablespoons milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1.5 teaspoons salt
- 26 deca beaten butter
- 16 deca Demerara sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 5 egg whites beaten to peaks
- raisins
- currants
- finely chopped citron
- elongated cut dates

## Directions

1. Place flour in a deep bowl, make a well in the middle, pour in yeast dissolved in milk, stir briefly and cover to rest
2. Meanwhile, whisk together melted butter, milk, egg yolks, whole egg, sugar, and salt
3. Add this mixture to the flour, mix well and beat the dough until it lifts from the spoon
4. Cover and let rise in a warm place
5. Prepare the filling: beat butter with Demerara sugar, egg yolks, and whole egg, then fold in beaten egg whites
6. Once the dough has doubled in height, roll out on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a small finger
7. Cut into 2 pieces and spread each with a third of the filling
8. Fold dough over filling, brush the fold with filling, sprinkle with raisins, currants, citron, and dates
9. Roll up the dough, place on a butter-greased baking sheet, press the ends together and fold them slightly upward
10. Cover and let rise in a warm place
11. Brush with melted butter and bake at moderate heat for 1/2 hour
12. Cut into pieces as desired, arrange on a platter and serve with custard milk or cream, or serve cold with tea or coffee

title: Löffelspritzkrapferl (Spoon Choux Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter milk
- Lard for frying
- Powdered sugar for serving
- Warm marmalade for serving

## Directions

1. Prepare a choux pastry from 1/4 liter milk, place it on a board moistened with water and using an inverted spoon handle dipped in hot lard, drop pieces of any desired size into the hot lard, which must be stirred.
2. The pieces fall to the bottom at first but rise again after a few moments.
3. When the surface of the lard is fairly covered, stop adding the dough pieces as they will puff up twice their size during frying.
4. Move the pan gently during frying and turn the fritters with a skimming spoon so that they get an even color.
5. When they have a brown, crispy crust, remove them with a skimming spoon, place on paper towels, arrange in a dish, sprinkle with finely powdered sugar and serve with warm marmalade.

title: Lübeck Cookies (Lübecker Täfelchen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca beaten butter
- 8 deca muscovado sugar
- 3 egg yolks and 1 whole egg
- 12 deca flour
- 2 pinches cinnamon
- some nutmeg
- 1 pounded clove

## Directions

1. Mix beaten butter with muscovado sugar, egg yolks, and whole egg gradually
2. Add flour mixed with cinnamon, nutmeg, and clove, mixing well
3. Place covered at a very cool location until dough becomes stiff
4. Roll out dough on flour-dusted board to the thickness of a knife back
5. Cut out in hard or any desired forms and treat as described for the Almond Cakes (No. 1611)

title: Macaroni with Béchamel and Parmesan (Macaroni mit Béchamel und Parmesankäse)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni prepared as 'Faded Macaroni' (No. 1206)
- 8 tablespoons of butter
- 8 tablespoons of flour
- 1 liter of milk
- 4 egg yolks
- salt
- cold milk
- grated Parmesan cheese
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Prepare and cool the macaroni as described in 'Faded Macaroni' (No. 1206)
2. Make a roux from 8 tablespoons of butter and 8 tablespoons of flour
3. Whisk in 1 liter of milk and cook well
4. Add 4 egg yolks mixed with some cold milk and mix with grated Parmesan cheese
5. Spread some sauce on the bottom of a buttered platter or casserole
6. Layer with a portion of the cooked but cold macaroni and continue alternating
7. Top with remaining sauce, sprinkle strongly with grated Parmesan cheese and drizzle with melted butter
8. Place the casserole in the oven for 1/4 hour
9. Remove from oven, transfer to a serving platter and serve hot
10. If using a casserole, turn out onto a platter before serving

title: Macaroni with Ham (Macaroni mit Schinken)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 liter of sour cream
- 40 tablespoons of cooked, lean, finely sliced ham
- 24 tablespoons of cooked, drained macaroni
- salt as needed
- 4 egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Place 4 egg yolks in a deep bowl
2. Add 1/2 liter of sour cream gradually
3. Add 40 tablespoons of cooked, lean, finely sliced ham
4. Add 24 tablespoons of cooked, drained macaroni
5. Add salt as needed
6. Gently fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 4 eggs
7. Fill a buttered and floured mold with this mixture
8. Bake in the oven for 3/4 hour
9. Remove from oven, turn out onto a platter and serve the macaroni hot

title: Macaroni with Starch (Macaroni mit Stärke)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- salt
- hot butter
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Break macaroni into smaller pieces
2. Add to boiling salted water and cook covered until soft
3. Drain in a colander and rinse with cold water
4. In a casserole with hot butter, add the drained macaroni
5. Stir well together and arrange hot on a platter
6. Serve with grated Parmesan cheese

title: Macaronisuppe mit Ravioles (Macaroni Soup with Ravioli)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- salt water
- soup
- grated Parmesan cheese
- brown or any preferred soup

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into half-finger-length pieces
2. Add them to boiling salt water and cook covered until soft
3. Remove them with a wooden spoon, add them to brown or preferred soup in the soup pot
4. Serve with grated Parmesan cheese

title: Maccaronen-Creme (Macaroon Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a measure cream
- quarter pound sugar
- lemon
- 4 bitter macaroons
- 4 biscuits
- orange blossom
- 8 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Place half a measure cream with quarter pound sugar rubbed with a lemon on charcoal fire and cook
2. Pound 4 bitter macaroons and 4 biscuits very fine
3. Add some orange blossom
4. Beat 8 egg yolks well
5. Stir in the rest
6. Pass the cream through a hair sieve into bowls
7. Cook in a double boiler or water bath
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Maccaronen-Liqueur (Almond Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 half pound of selected good bitter almonds
- 9 measures of brandy
- 5 pounds of sugar
- 1 measure of water
- 2 measures of orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Pound 1 half pound of selected good bitter almonds in a stone mortar
2. Pour 9 measures of brandy into a jug over the almonds and stir diligently
3. After 14 days, draw the mixture through a water bath over gentle and even fire
4. When 5 measures have been obtained, mix in the sugar water
5. Prepare sugar water by dissolving 5 pounds of sugar in 1 measure of water and 2 measures of orange blossom water
6. If the liqueur is too spirituous, add more sugar water
7. Filter the whole
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Maccaroni (Pasta)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg maccaroni
- 5 dkg butter or fat
- salt

## Directions

1. Wipe the maccaroni, break in 3-4 pieces
2. Place in much boiling salted water and cook ½-¾ hour while stirring frequently
3. Drain and shock in cold water
4. Place in a casserole with butter or fat, salt it and warm in the oven while stirring frequently

title: Maispudding (Corn Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pfund cornmeal
- 10 Pfund boiling water
- 4 Pfund syrup or sugar
- linen bag

## Directions

1. In a large bowl, stir 3 pounds cornmeal with 10 pounds boiling water
2. Mix in 4 pounds syrup or sugar at the same time
3. Pour the entire mixture into a linen bag, tie it so there is enough room for it to swell
4. Cook it in a pot with boiling water for 6 hours
5. Replace the evaporated water constantly with boiling water
6. When removed, the pudding weighs 10 pounds
7. It must not fall apart
8. The linen bag or napkin should be dipped in hot water beforehand
9. The cooking must never stop
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Maispudding (Corn Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds cornmeal
- 10 pounds boiling water
- 4 pounds syrup or sugar

## Directions

1. In a large bowl, stir 3 pounds cornmeal with 10 pounds boiling water, mix in 4 pounds syrup or sugar at the same time, then pour the whole thing into...
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Maitre d'hôtel Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- finely chopped parsley
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Knead butter with finely chopped parsley and some lemon juice on a board with a knife.
2. Form into a small roll and cut into small pieces to place on the beefsteaks.

title: Maitre d'Hôtel Butter (Beurre à la Maitre d'Hôtel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 teaspoons butter
- 1 tablespoon finely cut green parsley
- some chervil optionally
- salt to taste
- white pepper to taste
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Finely pound 15 teaspoons of butter with 1 tablespoon of finely cut green parsley, some chervil (if available), a little salt, white pepper, and the juice of 1 lemon in a mortar and crush it
2. Add to steaks or cutlets

title: Maitre d'hôtel Potatoes (Maitre d'hotel Kartoffeln)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- 6 dkg hot butter
- finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes raw and cut them nicely
2. Place them in cold salted water
3. Cook for 20 minutes and drain
4. In a saucepan, let finely chopped parsley run in 6 dkg hot butter
5. Pour in the potatoes and toss them in the butter

title: Maitre-Butter (Maitre d'Hotel Butter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 decagrams butter
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh parsley (if available)
- some chervil
- a little salt
- white pepper
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Pound the butter finely in a mortar with the chopped parsley, chervil, salt, white pepper, and lemon juice until well mixed and crushed
2. Serve with beefsteaks or cutlets

title: Makkaroni in Muscheln (Macaroni in Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 dkg macaroni
- 4 dkg tongue or rolled ham
- 3 dkg Parmesan
- 5 dkg butter
- beef broth or milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Wipe the noodles, cut them into small pieces, cook in boiling salted water for 30 minutes, stirring often, drain and rinse with cold water.
2. Make a butter roux with 5 dkg butter and thin it with cold beef broth or milk.
3. Add the noodles, chopped tongue, part of the grated cheese, and salt to the sauce and braise for a while.
4. The mixture must be thick.
5. Fill into buttered shells, sprinkle cheese on top and drizzle hot butter over it.
6. Place the shells on a baking sheet, bake in the oven for 10-15 minutes until golden, and serve on a plate with a folded napkin.

title: Malte (Confectioner's Glaze)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 drams of finely sifted sugar
- 1/4 liter of clarified, cold sugar
- 1 tablespoon of Maraschino

## Directions

1. Put 32 drams of finely sifted sugar into a deep bowl.
2. Stir in gradually 1/4 liter of clarified, cold sugar, then 1 tablespoon of Maraschino.
3. Stir this finely for about 1 hour, by which a thickly flowing mass should form (the glaze must not be so thick that it drops heavily from the spoon).
4. Spread the glaze on a cake knife-back thick; if necessary, dry it a few minutes in the lukewarm oven and then set it cold.
5. When coating the cake, use a knife that is always dipped in very hot water, but allow it to drip off.
6. This glaze must be of shining, matte-white color and fine taste.
7. One can prepare various glazes in this way by adding any liqueur or flavored sugar.

title: Malte Pastete vom Ochsenfleisch (Cold Beef Pâté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kilos beef (preferably the round)
- Salt
- Bacon pieces, half a finger long and pencil thick
- Salted pork rinds or salted tongue
- 1 cassole
- Onion
- Root vegetables
- 1 bay leaf
- Thyme
- Several sprigs of fresh parsley
- Bacon
- Ham
- Some broth
- ½ liter wine
- Butter
- Paper
- ½ kilo calf forcemeat (cold pâté forcemeat)
- Butter dough
- Thin bacon slices
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the 2 kilos of beef, salt it, and lard it alternately with half-finger-long and pencil-thick pieces of bacon and salted pork rinds or salted tongue, then shape it nicely round.
2. Line a cassole with onion, root vegetables, 1 bay leaf, thyme, several sprigs of fresh parsley, bacon and ham, place the meat on top, pour some broth and ½ liter wine over it, cover with paper brushed with butter, seal with a fitting lid, and braise the meat slowly.
3. When the meat is completely tender, set the cassole aside and let the meat cool in its juices.
4. Remove the meat, strain the sauce and set it aside.
5. Finely chop the cooked ham or bacon that was in the pot and mix it with ½ kilo calf forcemeat (cold pâté forcemeat).
6. Prepare a pâté from butter dough as indicated for the Goose Liver Pâté, line it with thin bacon slices, and brush the bottom with 2 fingers high of the forcemeat prepared above.
7. Place the meat on top, brush it with the remaining forcemeat, cover this with thin bacon slices and cover them with a small-finger-thick dough plate, which is scored several times with a knife and then brushed with beaten eggs.
8. Bake the pâté for 1½ to 2 hours at moderate heat in the oven.
9. After this time, loosen and remove the lid, pour the strained sauce inside, put the lid back on, and set the pâté to cool.

title: Maltheserkuchen (Maltese Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound fine sweet almonds
- egg white
- half pound ground sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- half hour of stirring
- zest of one lemon
- 4 loth melted butter
- half pound fine flour
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Pound half pound fine sweet almonds with some egg white until tender
2. Stir for half an hour with half pound ground sugar and 16 egg yolks
3. Add lemon zest and 4 loth melted butter
4. Finally stir in half pound fine flour and the egg white of 8 eggs whipped to peaks slowly
5. Bake in ordinary small forms for half an hour slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Mandel mit Schokolade (Almond Cake with Chocolate)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces butter
- 12 ounces lemon sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons cold butter or custard
- snow from 3 egg whites
- 16 ounces almonds
- butter for greasing the molds
- flour for dusting the molds

## Directions

1. Beat 4 ounces butter until fluffy
2. Mix in 12 ounces lemon sugar
3. Stir in 6 egg yolks gradually
4. Add 3 tablespoons cold butter or custard
5. Gently fold in the snow from 3 egg whites together with 16 ounces almonds
6. Butter the molds, dust with flour, and fill half-full
7. Cook covered in steam for 15-20 minutes, or bake in the oven
8. Turn the cakes out and serve elevated on a platter with chocolate sauce of choice

title: Mandel Soufflé (Almond Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka beaten butter
- 16 Deka powdered sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 16 Deka blanched, very finely ground almonds with egg white
- 4 Deka trimmed rolls soaked in milk
- 10 egg whites
- butter for coating the mold
- flour for dusting the mold

## Directions

1. Beat 16 Deka butter with 16 Deka powdered sugar.
2. Add 10 egg yolks one after another and stir.
3. Mix well with 16 Deka blanched, finely ground almonds and 4 Deka trimmed rolls soaked in milk and well drained.
4. Lightly fold in the snow of 10 egg whites.
5. Fill a mold brushed with butter and dusted with flour with this mass, cover and bake for 1/2 hour in steam or in the oven.
6. If it is baked in steam, any desired fruit juice is added.

title: Mandel Soufflé mit Grieß (Almond Soufflé with Semolina)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter semolina
- 1/2 liter boiling milk
- 8 Deka butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 10 Deka powdered sugar
- 10 Deka blanched, finely ground almonds
- 5 egg whites
- butter for coating the mold
- flour for dusting the mold

## Directions

1. Stir 1/4 liter semolina into 1/2 liter boiling milk and let it thicken.
2. Add 8 Deka butter, mix well and set aside to cool.
3. Beat 5 egg yolks with 10 Deka powdered sugar for 1/2 hour.
4. Mix well with 10 Deka blanched, finely ground almonds and the cooled semolina mass.
5. Lightly fold in the snow of 5 egg whites.
6. Fill a mold brushed with butter and dusted with flour and cover and bake for 3/4 hour in steam or in the oven.

title: Mandel Soufflé mit Äpfeln (Almond Soufflé with Apples)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deka beaten butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 Deka powdered sugar
- 12 Deka finely ground and passed almonds
- 1 1/2 liter cream
- 8 egg whites
- butter for coating the mold
- flour for dusting the mold
- pippin apples
- marmalade

## Directions

1. Beat 12 Deka butter with 8 egg yolks, 16 Deka powdered sugar, 12 Deka finely ground almonds, and 1 1/2 liter cream gradually and stir.
2. Lightly fold in the snow of 8 egg whites.
3. Core pippin apples, peel them and fill the resulting hollow with marmalade.
4. Place them in a mold brushed with butter and dusted with flour at finger-width distance and pour the mass over.
5. Bake in the oven at low heat for 1 hour.
6. When this is done, remove the cake mold, discard the rim and arrange the baked cake on the cake plate in a bowl and serve.

title: Mandel- oder Weißelkuchen mit Mandeln und Bröseln (Almond or White Cake with Almonds and Crumbs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca vanilla sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 16 deca blanched almonds
- 2 egg whites
- 8 egg whites
- 8 deca bread crumbs
- white wafer
- black cherries or morello cherries

## Directions

1. Beat 16 deca vanilla sugar with 10 egg yolks for 1/4 hour
2. Add 16 deca blanched, very finely pounded almonds with 2 egg whites
3. Stir for 1.5 hours and lightly fold in the snow from 8 egg whites at the same time as 8 deca bread crumbs
4. Fill this mixture into a form brushed with butter and dusted with flour, 2 fingers high
5. Cover the mixture with a white wafer and place black cherries or morello cherries on top, which are covered with the remaining mixture
6. Bake for approximately 1 hour in the heated oven
7. Turn the cake out onto a sieve and serve only the following day

title: Mandel-Kipferl (Almond Kipfels)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites, firmly beaten
- 16 decagrams sugar
- 12 decagrams peeled, finely cut almonds or pignoli

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites firmly
2. Mix very well with 16 decagrams sugar and 12 decagrams peeled, finely cut almonds or pignoli
3. Form into peaks on a wax-coated baking sheet using a paper cone or piping bag
4. Dry in an oven at full heat

title: Mandel-Milch's Blanc-manger (Almond Milk Blanc-manger)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram almonds, of which 20 pieces are bitter
- cold water
- 1 casserole
- 1 liter cold water
- 40 deca finely sifted caster sugar
- 8 deca dissolved warm isinglass
- 2 deca gelatin
- 1/2 liter water

## Directions

1. Soak 1 kilogram almonds (including 20 bitter pieces) in cold water for 24 hours
2. Remove the shells
3. Pound the almonds very finely with some water
4. Place in 1 casserole with 1 liter cold water on the stove
5. Stir until it comes to a boil, but do not let it boil
6. Press through a cloth into a casserole
7. Add 40 deca finely sifted caster sugar
8. Let warm and stir in 8 deca dissolved warm isinglass well
9. Fill this mass into a desired mold that was buried in ice 1 hour earlier
10. Proceed as with Rice with Vanilla Cream (No. 1689)
11. Alternatively use 2 deca gelatin dissolved in 1/2 liter water and mix hot with 3/4 liter strained almond milk

title: Mandel-Pastetchehen (Almond Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deka flour
- Butter for dough
- Almond filling as specified for almond strudel
- Butter for forms
- Flour for forms
- Egg for brushing
- Finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare a fine puff dough from 28 Deka flour (No. 1045) and let rest 3/4 to 1 hour
2. Roll out knife-back thick on a flour-dusted board and cut into strips approximately four fingers wide
3. Line each butter-greased and flour-dusted almond mold with a piece of dough
4. Fill with 1 tablespoon almond filling as specified for almond strudel
5. Fold the 4 corners lightly over the filling in the middle
6. Fasten one corner to another with beaten egg and brush the remaining dough with it, but leave a knife-back-width border dry along the edges
7. Place the almond molds on a baking sheet in the hot oven for 1/2 to 3/4 hours
8. Remove the sheet from the oven, lift the almonds from the forms with a fork and arrange elevated in a bowl
9. Sprinkle with finely sifted sugar and serve

title: Mandel-Purtner mit spanischer Süßmasse (Almond Purtner with Spanish Sweetmass)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- mass as prepared for Mandelbäckerei (recipe 1617)
- wafer strips

## Directions

1. Prepare the same mass as in Mandelbäckerei (recipe 1617)
2. Spread knife-back-thick on wafer strips that are 2 fingers wide and finger-long
3. Bake on a baking sheet at low heat in the oven until pale yellow
4. Remove and after completely cooling, decorate with Spanish sweetmass as desired
5. Sprinkle with sugar and dry in the oven

title: Mandel-Schnitte (Almond Slices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Deca powdered sugar
- 1 egg white
- 16 Deca blanched almonds, very finely chopped with 1 egg white
- 8 Deca finely chopped citron
- wafer strips
- glaze of any desired kind

## Directions

1. Stir 10 Deca powdered sugar with 1 egg white and 16 Deca blanched almonds, very finely chopped with 1 egg white for 1 hour
2. Mix well with 8 Deca finely chopped citron
3. Place this mixture finger-long on wafer strips and dry on a baking sheet in the very hot oven
4. When done, remove the slices and after cooling completely, cover with glaze of any desired kind

title: Mandel-Schokolade (Almond Chocolate)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 drams brown sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 drams blanched almonds (finely ground with 1 egg white)
- 4 egg whites
- 6 drams flour
- butter for greasing the baking sheet
- warm candied peel for filling
- powdered sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Beat 16 drams brown sugar and 8 egg yolks together for 1/8 hour
2. Add 16 drams blanched almonds (finely ground with 1 egg white) and beat well
3. Lightly fold in the stiffly beaten snow of 4 egg whites with 6 drams flour
4. Spread the mixture thickly on a baking sheet greased with butter
5. Bake at moderate heat in the oven until pale yellow
6. Remove the dough from the sheet with a very thin long knife
7. Brush with warm candied peel, fold the dough 2 fingers wide on the longer side, brush again with candied peel, and roll up completely
8. Dry for a few more minutes in the oven
9. Remove and dust with sugar after cooling completely, then cut into pieces of desired size
10. Alternatively, tortes can be made: spread the dough the thickness of a knife back on the torte sheet, bake until light yellow, and continue as desired

title: Mandel-Strudel (Almond Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 deca sugar
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 16 deca blanched, finely ground almonds
- 2 egg whites
- Strudel dough
- melted butter
- selected raisins

## Directions

1. Place 10 deca sugar in a deep bowl
2. Stir in 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks gradually
3. Mix with 16 deca blanched, finely ground almonds
4. Lightly fold in the snow from 2 egg whites
5. Brush stretched strudel dough with melted butter
6. Distribute the mixture on top
7. Sprinkle with 16 deca selected raisins
8. Roll dough together
9. Treat as for Rice Gries Strudel (No. 1236)

title: Mandel-Strudel anderer Art (Almond Strudel, Another Way)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca beaten butter
- 12 deca finely ground vanilla sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 16 deca blanched almonds, finely ground with 2 egg whites
- 1/4 liter milk cream
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 12 deca butter with 12 deca finely ground vanilla sugar
2. Stir in 6 egg yolks one after another
3. Mix gradually with 16 deca blanched almonds finely ground with 2 egg whites and 1/4 liter milk cream
4. Lightly fold in the snow from the remaining 4 egg whites
5. Stretch strudel dough made from 1/2 liter flour thin, as indicated for Lung Strudel (No. 75)
6. Brush with the almond filling
7. Treat further as for Rice Gries Strudel (No. 1236)

title: Mandel-Torte (Almond Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 Deca orange or vanilla sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- 30 Deca peeled almonds
- 4 egg whites
- 8 egg whites
- 8 Deca fine flour
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Stir the sugar with the egg yolks for 1/3 hour
2. Add the almonds (which have been peeled and finely ground with 4 egg whites) and mix well
3. After stirring for another 1/4 hour, lightly fold in the snow from 8 egg whites simultaneously with 8 Deca fine flour
4. Fill a cake form brushed with butter and dusted with flour with this mixture
5. Bake at low heat for 3/4 to 1 hour in the oven
6. After this time, turn the cake out onto a sieve and let it cool
7. Once cooled, place the cake on a sheet of writing paper so that the former bottom side faces up
8. Cover the cake with glaze of your choice and only serve to table once completely dry
9. For the glaze: beat the stiffly whipped snow from 2 egg whites with 3 tablespoons of strained strawberries or raspberries and 10 Deca sugar for 1/2 hour
10. Apply to the cake, distribute evenly with a knife (dipped several times in hot water), dust with finely ground sugar, and dry in the overfilled open oven for approximately 10 to 15 minutes

title: Mandel-Torte mit Zimmt (Almond Cake with Cinnamon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deka fine ground and sieved almonds
- 1½ liter cream
- 8 egg whites
- Marzipan apples with kernels removed and filled with marmalade of choice
- Butter for the pan
- Flour for dusting
- Powdered sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Beat butter until fluffy, gradually stir in fine ground and sieved almonds and 1½ liter cream
2. Lightly mix in the snow from 8 egg whites
3. Remove the kernels from marzipan apples and fill the resulting empty space with marmalade of choice
4. Lay them in a butter-brushed, flour-dusted mold at finger-width distance
5. Pour the above-mentioned mixture over them and bake in the oven at low heat for 1 hour
6. Remove the cake mold, remove the rim, and arrange the baked item on a cake plate in a bowl, serving it sprinkled with fine powdered sugar
7. Can also be baked in a glass roll, then pushed out with a pastry spoon and served that way

title: Mandel-Torte mit Zimmt (Almond Souffle with Cinnamon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka butter, beaten
- 16 Deka fine ground powdered sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 Deka almonds, finely ground with the skin
- ½ teaspoon cinnamon
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat butter with fine ground powdered sugar
2. Add egg yolks one after another
3. Mix fine ground almonds with cinnamon and add to the mixture
4. Lightly mix in the snow from 8 egg whites
5. Fill this mixture into a butter-brushed, flour-dusted mold
6. Let it cook covered for ½ hour in steam or bake in the oven

title: Mandel-Waffeln (Almond Waffles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca peeled almonds
- 2 egg whites
- 16 deca powdered sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 6 deca flour

## Directions

1. Put 24 deca peeled almonds into a mortar and pound them together with 2 egg whites into a dough. Meanwhile, stir 16 deca powdered sugar together with 8 egg yolks. Mix this well with the very finely pounded almonds and lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites together with 6 deca flour. Bake as described for 'Waffles from Batter' (No. 1648).
2. When the waffle is golden baked, remove it with 2 forks, place it on a plate and set this in a warm place. Continue baking waffles until the batter mixture is used up, then shortly before serving sprinkle them with sugar and serve with fruit sauce or any warm glaze.
3. The heating of the iron must not happen too strongly, but must be repeated frequently. When you have finished baking the waffles, the iron must not be washed but only cleaned with blotting paper.

title: Mandelbaïsers (Almond Meringues)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 3 stiff egg whites
- 17 dkg soaked grated almonds

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar with the stiff egg whites and soaked grated almonds
2. Make small mounds with 2 spoons onto a buttered baking sheet
3. Dry them in the oven

title: Mandelbilten anderer Art (Almond Confections of Another Kind)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites, firmly beaten
- 16 decagrams sugar
- 16 decagrams peeled, finely cut almonds
- wax
- fine pounded sugar
- ice

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites firmly and stir with 16 decagrams sugar for 1/2 hour
2. Add 16 decagrams peeled, very finely cut almonds and stir again for 1 1/2 hours
3. Spread knife-back-thick with almond mass on a wax-coated baking sheet
4. Sprinkle with fine pounded sugar and dry in the oven
5. Once done, immediately cut into pieces that are 2 fingers wide and finger-long
6. Place on a warmed, bent baking sheet and after complete cooling, cover with desired ice

title: Mandelbogen (Almond Bows)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 20 decagrams fine sifted sugar
- 8 decagrams finely cut almonds
- 8 decagrams almonds cut with a sabre knife
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites in a bowl to stiff snow
2. Add 20 decagrams fine sifted sugar and stir together over the fire until hot, being very careful that the sugar does not burn
3. Add finely cut and sabre-knife-cut almonds and lemon juice
4. Stir with a pastry spoon over the fire until hot and thick throughout, being careful the mass does not burn
5. Spread this thick almond mass knife-back-thick on wafer strips that are 2 fingers wide and finger-long
6. Place on a baking sheet that has been warmed in the oven
7. Sprinkle with fine pounded sugar and dry in the oven

title: Mandelbögen (Almond Arches)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12½ dkg raw grated almonds
- 17 dkg sugar
- 3-4 egg whites, added gradually

## Directions

1. Stir the raw grated almonds and sugar with 3-4 egg whites, added gradually, to a stiff mass
2. Spread it thinly on wafers
3. Cut strips from it
4. Sprinkle with soaked chopped almonds
5. Place on an unbuttered baking sheet
6. Bake at low heat
7. Immediately place over a pastry roller while hot

title: Mandelformen (Almond Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter
- 3 tablespoons fine powdered sugar
- 1 knife point of salt
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 3 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter milk
- 28 deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deca butter until fluffy, add 3 tablespoons fine powdered sugar, 1 knife point of salt, 8 egg yolks and 1 whole egg one after the other, mix well with the yeast mixture and 28 deca flour, beat the dough until it separates from the spoon
2. Brush almond molds with butter, dust with flour and fill halfway with the dough, let stand covered in a warm place
3. When the dough has risen to double height, put the almond molds in the oven where the dough bakes slowly
4. When finished, turn out the almond cakes, arrange on a plate and serve sprinkled with sugar

title: Mandelkarten (Almond Cards)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg flour
- 7 dkg butter
- 7 dkg raw grated almonds
- 7 dkg sugar
- lemon zest
- lemon juice
- 1 pinch cinnamon
- 1 crushed clove
- 1 spoon milk

## Directions

1. Knead the flour, butter, raw grated almonds, sugar, lemon zest, lemon juice, cinnamon, crushed clove and milk on the board to a dough
2. After resting, roll it out thin
3. Cut it into rectangles (like cards)
4. Place the cards on an unbuttered baking sheet
5. Brush them with beaten egg
6. Top them with halved soaked almonds
7. Bake until light brown

title: Mandelkoch von Limonien (Almond Cake with Lemon)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound ground almonds
- 3/4 pound sugar
- 1 large lemon

## Directions

1. In a bowl, combine 3/4 pound ground almonds and 3/4 pound sugar
2. Grate 1 large lemon on a grater and mix in well
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Mandelkren (Almond Horseradish Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quarter-litre good milk cream
- one small spoonful flour
- one ladle broth
- one handful pounded and swollen almonds
- one piece sugar
- grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 quarter-litre of good milk cream with one small spoonful of flour
2. Add one ladle broth, one handful of pounded and swollen almonds with one piece of sugar
3. Finally add some grated horseradish
4. Stir well again and bring to the fire a couple of times until it comes to a boil, then the sauce is ready
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Mandelkren (Almond Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dkg Butter
- 1 dkg Flour
- Milk
- Salt
- Sugar
- 3 dkg softened chopped almonds
- Horseradish

## Directions

1. Heat butter in a casserole
2. Stir in flour
3. Pour in milk and bring to a boil
4. Season with salt and sugar
5. Add softened chopped almonds and cook well
6. Just before serving, add cleaned grated horseradish

title: Mandelmilch (Almond Milk)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilo sweet almonds
- 1 tea cup bitter almonds
- boiling water
- cold water
- sugar, as desired
- 2 spoonfuls orange flower water

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over 1/2 kilo sweet almonds and 1 tea cup bitter almonds, shell them, wash several times and pound finely with some cold water
2. Place them in a porcelain bowl, pour 1 liter cold water over them and let this stand for about 1/2 hour
3. Press the almonds through a strong clean cloth, rinse them again with some water and press through once more
4. To this almond milk add sugar as desired and 2 spoonfuls of orange flower water
5. Preserve in bottles
6. When serving the almond milk warm, hold the bottle or glass in warm water

title: Mandelmoisch (Almond Drink)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram sweet almonds
- 1 deciliter bitter almonds
- boiling water
- cold water

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/2 kilogram sweet and 1 deciliter bitter almonds with boiling water, skin, wash several times and pound finely with some cold water

title: Mandels-Kässerln (Matronen) (Almond Macarons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 decagrams powdered sugar
- 16 decagrams peeled almonds
- 1 egg white
- fine sifted sugar
- wafers

## Directions

1. Stir together for 1 hour powdered sugar, peeled almonds pounded very finely with 1 egg white, and 1 egg white
2. Using a coffee spoon or piping bag, place nutmeg-sized heaps on a wafer-covered baking sheet
3. Sprinkle with fine sifted sugar and dry in a full oven
4. Cut down from the wafer

title: Mandels-Pudding (Almond Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca butter, beaten
- 16 deca sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 18 deca blanched almonds, finely pounded with 2 egg whites
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 12 deca butter with 16 deca sugar and 8 egg yolks for 1 hour
2. Add 18 deca blanched almonds finely pounded with 2 egg whites
3. Mix well
4. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
5. Fill a buttered and floured mold with this mixture
6. Steam covered for 1 hour
7. Serve with desired fruit juice

title: Mandelschmarn (Almond Scramble)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deka butter
- 12 deka finely ground sugar
- 6 deka peeled almonds with 1 egg yolk, finely ground
- 7 egg yolks
- 1/2 liter milk
- 14 deka flour
- 6 egg whites (snow)
- 10 deka very hot butter

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deka butter until fluffy
2. Mix in 12 deka finely ground sugar, 6 deka ground almonds, and 7 egg yolks, one after another
3. Slowly mix in 1/2 liter milk and 14 deka flour alternately, always some milk and some flour
4. Gently fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
5. Pour this dough into a casserole containing 10 deka very hot butter
6. Treat further as described for the kleine Kaiserschmarn

title: Mandelstrudel a la Kellerstorfer (Almond Strudel a la Kellerstorfer)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deka flour
- 16 Deka cinnamon or orange sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 Deka peeled almonds
- 3 egg whites
- 5 egg whites for snow

## Directions

1. Make a butter dough from 28 Deka flour as described in the butter dough recipe (No. 1045) and let it rest in a cool place
2. Meanwhile, put 16 Deka cinnamon or orange sugar in a deep bowl, stir in 8 egg yolks one after another
3. Stir this mixture for half an hour with 16 Deka peeled almonds finely pounded with 3 egg whites
4. Lightly fold in the snow (beaten egg whites) from 5 egg whites
5. Roll out the rested butter dough on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a knife back, cut into two equal parts
6. Distribute the almond filling evenly along the middle of both pieces lengthwise
7. Brush the edges of both narrow sides with beaten egg and treat further as described for the Apple Strudel made with butter dough
8. Place a dampened wooden spoon on both sides of the strudel so that the filling cannot push the dough apart during baking

title: Mandelstrudel à la Bellerotorfer (Almond Strudel à la Bellerotorfer)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 decagrams flour
- 16 decagrams vanilla or orange sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 decagrams peeled, finely ground almonds with 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Make a puff pastry from 28 decagrams flour as described in the puff pastry instructions (No. 1045) and let it rest in a cool place.
2. Meanwhile, place 16 decagrams of vanilla or orange sugar in a deep bowl, add 8 egg yolks one after another, stirring well, then mix with 16 decagrams of peeled almonds very finely ground with 3 egg whites.

title: Mandelsöubding mit Gewürz (Almond Pudding with Spices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 ounces butter, beaten
- 24 ounces powdered sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 28 ounces almonds, finely ground with shell and then passed through sieve
- cinnamon, nutmeg, fine-cut lemon peel, spice mixture to taste
- 6 egg whites, beaten to snow
- 4 tablets grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat 14 ounces butter with 24 ounces powdered sugar and 10 egg yolks, adding them one by one
2. Add 28 ounces finely ground almonds, then add cinnamon, nutmeg, lemon peel, and spices
3. Mix well and gently fold in the snow from 6 egg whites simultaneously with 4 tablets grated chocolate
4. Fill a buttered mold lined with flour
5. Cover and steam for 1 hour
6. Serve with chocolate or cream of choice

title: Mandelsöubding mit Maraschino (Almond Pudding with Maraschino)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 ounces butter, beaten
- 16 ounces sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons genuine maraschino
- 16 ounces almonds, finely ground with shell and then passed through sieve
- 6 egg whites, beaten to snow
- 1/2 liter milk, prepared bruised chestnuts

## Directions

1. Beat 14 ounces butter with 16 ounces sugar, 10 egg yolks and 3 tablespoons genuine maraschino for 1 hour and 15 minutes
2. Add 16 ounces finely ground almonds, mix well
3. Stir again for 1 hour and 15 minutes
4. Gently fold in the snow from 6 egg whites simultaneously with crushed chestnuts prepared from 1/2 liter milk
5. Fill a buttered mold lined with flour
6. Cover and steam for 3/4 hour

title: Mannheimer Wafer (Mannheim Wafer)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces anise
- 1 ounce fennel
- 2 measures purified brandy
- 1 measure water
- 4 pounds sugar
- 3 measures water
- 1 seidel brandy

## Directions

1. Crush 2 ounces anise and 1 ounce fennel
2. Pour 2 measures purified brandy and 1 measure water over the mixture
3. Let it stand for 8 days
4. Distill the mixture
5. Dissolve 4 pounds sugar in 3 measures water to make sugar water
6. Add the sugar water to the distillate
7. Mix in 1 seidel brandy
8. Filter the liquid
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Maraschino Ice (Marasquino-Gefrornes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cream
- egg yolk
- clarified sugar
- maraschino

## Directions

1. Take cream, egg yolk and clarified sugar in the same measure and treat as the previous recipes
2. Pour the maraschino in last, as much as you believe will make it strong enough
3. Mix well with the stirring spatula
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Maraschino-Gelee (Maraschino Jelly)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Rhine wine jelly base
- a couple of good cups of genuine Maraschino

## Directions

1. Make it exactly like Rhine wine jelly
2. When it is ready enough to run through the jelly bag, add a couple of good cups of genuine Maraschino
3. Proceed in the manner described above
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marasquino (Maraschino)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 pounds of ripe cherries
- 5 pounds of cherry leaves
- 1 pound of sugar per measure of spirits
- 1 pound of water

## Directions

1. Crush 100 pounds of ripe cherries together with 5 pounds of cherry leaves
2. Bring to wine fermentation with yeast and then distill
3. Draw the obtained liquid again through a water bath
4. Add 1 pound of sugar dissolved in 1 pound of water per measure of spirits
5. Alternatively, first draw off the spirits, then draw over the fragrant water to prepare the sugar solution, and add 8 ounces of this solution per measure of liqueur
6. Age to improve quality
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marillen- oder Zwetschkenknödel (Apricot or Plum Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- 6 dkg butter
- salt
- 1 egg
- 2 spoonfuls cream
- flour
- fresh apricots or plums
- sugar
- salt water

## Directions

1. Make a potato dough from the potatoes, 6 dkg butter, salt, 1 egg, 2 spoonfuls cream and flour (see doughs section).
2. Form into a roll and cut into equal-sized pieces.
3. Stretch these pieces apart, wrap a wiped apricot or plum in each and form into dumplings.
4. Instead of the stone, place a small piece of sugar in the fruit.
5. Place the dumplings in a casserole in boiling salt water and let them cook slowly for 8 minutes.
6. Carefully remove them with a slotted spoon, let them drain.
7. Toss them in a casserole where 10 dkg hot butter or fat has been used to toast breadcrumbs until dark.
8. Place on a round dish and serve sprinkled with sugar.

title: Marillen-(Himbeeren-)Suppe (Apricot Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 apricots
- 1 kilo sugar
- 1/2 liter water
- 2 liters white wine or Rhine wine
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Peel 15 apricots, divide them in half, cut one-third into slices, scatter these with sugar and set on ice.
2. Remove the kernels from the apricot pits, crush them, and place them with the remaining apricots in a bowl and pour over with 1 kilo sugar which has been well boiled in 1/2 liter water, let sit covered for 1/2 hour.
3. After this time, strain it, pour it with 2 liters white wine or better Rhine wine and the juice of 1 lemon, mix in the cut apricots and let sit on ice in a deep porcelain or glass bowl for approximately 4 hours.

title: Marillen-Kochli (Apricot Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Deka butter
- 8 Deka apricot marmalade
- 8 Deka fine ground powdered sugar
- 7 egg yolks
- 7 egg whites
- Butter for the mold
- Powdered sugar for dusting
- Biscuit

## Directions

1. Beat butter with apricot marmalade until fluffy
2. Add fine ground sugar and 7 egg yolks one after another and stir for ¾ hour
3. Lightly mix in the stiff snow from 7 egg whites
4. Line a butter-brushed, powdered sugar-dusted mold or bowl densely with biscuit
5. Fill with the above-mentioned mixture
6. Bake for 1 hour in the oven, whereby the souffle rises to twice its height

title: Marillen-Kuchen mit Mandeln (Apricot Cake with Almonds)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter
- 14 deca caster sugar
- 16 deca peeled almonds
- 2 egg whites
- 8 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 12 deca semmel crumbs
- halved apricots

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deca butter with 14 deca caster sugar, 16 deca peeled almonds finely ground with 2 egg whites, and 8 egg yolks for 1/4 hour
2. Lightly mix in the stiff peaks of 6 egg whites with 12 deca semmel crumbs
3. Fill into a baking pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
4. Arrange halved apricots on top
5. Bake for approximately 1 hour in an adequately hot oven
6. Turn the cake out onto a sieve and serve the following day sprinkled with sugar

title: Marillen-Marmelade feinfter Art (Finest Apricot Preserves)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kilograms fresh ripe apricots
- 1 kilogram powdered sugar
- half of the apricot kernels, blanched and dissolved

## Directions

1. Break the ripe soft apricots in half and remove the kernels.
2. Place 1 kilogram of apricots with 1.25 kilograms of powdered sugar in a saucepan over heat and cook rapidly while stirring constantly for 1/4 to 1/2 hour.
3. When the apricots are completely cooked and liquid, pass them quickly through a very clean sieve into an earthenware or porcelain bowl.
4. Return the passed mixture immediately to the saucepan on heat and cook for approximately 1/4 hour while stirring.
5. A few minutes before filling the preserves into jars, fold in the blanched almonds made from half of the available apricot kernels.
6. Put only 1 tablespoon of hot preserves into each prepared jar first to warm them, then fill gradually.
7. Place the jars in an airy location and after 8 days cover with perforated parchment paper.
8. Do not skim the preserves while cooking. When cooking the strained mixture, be very careful that it does not become too dark but maintains a beautiful yellow color.
9. The preserves should drip from a spoon.

title: Marillen-Marmelade mittlezeit (Medium Apricot Preserves)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots (quantity not specified)
- sugar equal in weight to the apricots without kernels

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as the Finest Apricot Preserves (recipe 1870), with the difference that only as much sugar is used as the apricots without kernels weigh.
2. Omit the dissolved apricot kernels.

title: Marillenkoch (Apricot Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maß apricot sauce
- 3/4 pound almonds, not too finely ground
- 6 whole eggs
- 14 egg yolks
- sugar to taste
- zest of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. In a bowl, combine 1/2 maß apricot sauce and 3/4 pound not too finely ground almonds
2. Stir for a quarter hour
3. Beat 6 whole eggs and 14 egg yolks gradually into the mixture
4. Stir well
5. Add sugar to taste and the zest of 2 lemons
6. Stir it on one side slowly and beautifully
7. Fill into a well-sealed ring mold, as it tends to flow out
8. Bake slowly and cool for one hour
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marinated Chicken with Horseradish (Poulets Marines au Raifort)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- blonde and light yellow hens
- 1 head onion
- 3 slices root vegetables
- 1 piece parsley root
- 1 piece leek
- 3 sprigs thyme
- 5 grains each of mixed spices
- 1 clove
- 2 cleaned and dressed chickens
- ¼ liter wine
- ¼ liter water
- ⅛ liter vinegar
- 2 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons butter
- sour cream
- grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Very finely dice onion, root vegetables, parsley root, and leek and place in a casserole
2. Bind together thyme, mixed spices, and clove in a small bag and add to the vegetables
3. Place 2 cleaned and dressed chickens on top
4. Pour ¼ liter wine, ¼ liter water, and ⅛ liter vinegar over
5. Cover and poach chickens until tender
6. Remove chickens and cut each into 4 pieces
7. Arrange in a serving dish, pour some sauce over, and garnish with strained root vegetables and grated horseradish
8. Whisk 2 egg yolks with 3 tablespoons butter and sour cream
9. Gradually stir in the cooking liquid as needed
10. Serve this sauce with the chickens in a gravy boat

title: Marinierte Fische (Fish Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel, tuna, Rhine salmon, sea pike, gudgeon, herring, smoked salmon, sardines, Russian marinated sardines
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Shallots
- Star anise
- Caviar
- Large piece of butter
- Cleaned, split sardines
- Smoked fish or anchovies
- Capers
- Olives
- Lemon slices
- Orange pieces
- Sherry wine

## Directions

1. Cut the skinned, marinated or boiled fish into neat pieces and pour vinegar and oil over them
2. If desired, crush shallots and star anise finely, pour vinegar over them, let stand for 1/4 hour and then strain over the fish
3. Serve the salad in a round bowl with the fish arranged elevated if possible
4. From a large piece of butter, scrape a leaf using a tablespoon or blunt knife, holding the knife so the butter pushes against the thumb to form a ribbed leaf of any desired size
5. Arrange the butter leaves to form a dome or shell shape, place it elevated in the center of the fish and fill it shortly before serving with caviar
6. Garnish the bowl with cleaned, split, washed sardines with bones removed, arrange them in a spiral shape and place some smoked fish or capers in the center
7. Decorate the dish with olives that have had the pit carefully removed and replaced with a small piece of smoked salmon or anchovy
8. Also garnish with small butter flowers shaped and filled with caviar or olives
9. Add colored butter, capers, lemon slices, orange pieces and similar items
10. Serve vinegar and oil on the side
11. Serve as an appetizer with supper, tea or breakfast
12. Serve with Sherry wine

title: Marinierte Wilde Hühner mit falschem Schnepfenfotin (Marinated Wild Chickens with False Woodcock Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cleaned, flamed and prepared chickens
- 5 juniper berries
- stripped thyme
- salt
- whole spices
- 1/2 bay leaf
- lard
- onions
- root vegetables
- 1/4 liter raw wine
- 1/8 liter soup
- 4 deciliter black bread, cut into flakes
- 1/2 liter cream
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Crush 5 juniper berries, stripped thyme, salt, whole spices and 1/2 bay leaf in a mortar
2. Rub 2 cleaned chickens inside and out with this mixture, dress them, spit them and let rest 2-3 hours
3. Line a casserole with lard, onions, root vegetables, 1/2 bay leaf, the necks and giblets of the chickens
4. When lightly roasted, pour 1/4 liter raw wine and 1/8 liter soup over
5. Place 2 chickens with 4 deciliter black bread cut into flakes inside and braise covered slowly until tender
6. Remove the lard from the liquid, place it in a shallow casserole, lay the chickens on top
7. Pour 1/2 liter cream over them and braise in the oven approximately 10 minutes
8. Remove the chickens, cut into desired pieces and arrange on a platter
9. Defatten and strain the braising liquid, season to taste with salt, add lemon juice, stir well and serve in a sauce boat
10. Garnish the chickens with liver forcemeat (false woodcock forcemeat) made as follows: In 4 deciliter butter or lard, add a knife-tip of finely cut onions, 2 crushed juniper berries, 1 finely cut mushroom, 1 strong pinch of parsley
11. Let this roast lightly, then add the chicken livers cleaned from the gallbladder and a knife-tip of stripped thyme, stir well together and roast
12. Put the roasted mixture in a mortar and mix well with 2 deciliter trimmed bread rolls moistened in water and well pressed out, or black bread
13. Strain through a sieve into a bowl, stir with an egg yolk and season to taste with salt and spices
14. Spread on 2-finger wide and 1-finger long bread slices, place on a sheet for several minutes in the oven until the forcemeat dries slightly
15. If desired, the bread slices may be roasted in butter before spreading with forcemeat

title: Marinierter Kalbsschwanz (Marinated Fried Veal Tails)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 veal tails
- salt water
- lemon juice
- green parsley
- thinly sliced Spanish onion
- 1 bay leaf
- salt
- spices
- egg batter or wine batter
- very hot fat

## Directions

1. After cutting 3 veal tails into 2-finger-wide pieces and cooking them until soft in salt water, remove them once they are completely cooked.
2. Place them in a bowl with lemon juice, green parsley, thinly sliced Spanish onion, 1 bay leaf, the necessary salt and spices and let stand 1 hour.
3. After this time, dip the pieces one after another in egg batter or wine batter and fry them in fingerless, very hot fat.

title: Marinierter Karpfen (Marinated Carp)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece onion, very finely cut
- garlic, finely crushed (optional)
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- vinegar as needed
- oil

## Directions

1. Mix the finely cut onion with crushed garlic if desired
2. Stir this mixture with 1 tablespoon vinegar
3. Pour the vinegar over cold carp pieces
4. Let stand for 1 hour
5. Before serving, pour oil and more vinegar over the fish

title: Marinierter Karpfen (Marinated Carp)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece onion
- crushed garlic if desired
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- vinegar as needed
- fried cold carp pieces
- oil

## Directions

1. Cut 1 piece onion very finely and add some finely crushed garlic if desired
2. Mix this with 1 tablespoon vinegar and pour with the necessary vinegar over fried cold carp pieces
3. Let it stand for one hour
4. About 5 minutes before serving, pour oil and some more vinegar over it

title: Marksknödel (Beef Marrow Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca finely cut marrow
- 3 grated bread rolls
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- some salt
- 20 deca flour
- approximately 1/8 liter milk

## Directions

1. Place the finely cut marrow in a deep bowl and chill it.
2. After 10 minutes, beat it until fluffy.
3. Stir in 3 bread rolls that have been grated and cut into fine dice and soaked in 1/8 liter milk, 2 whole eggs, 2 egg yolks, and some salt.
4. Mix in approximately 20 deca flour.
5. Form knödel of any desired size and cook them in salted water.
6. If desired, a knife point of nutmeg can also be added.

title: Markstrudel (Bone Marrow Strudel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough
- butter
- 6 loth finely pounded sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- quarter pound bone marrow, finely diced (removing the small bones carefully)
- 4 loth citron (Citronat), finely cut
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Prepare and draw out the strudel dough finely, and when it has dried, brush it with butter
2. Beat 6 loth finely pounded sugar with 6 egg yolks well together
3. Stir in the finely diced bone marrow and 4 loth finely cut citron
4. Last, fold in the snow (stiff peaks) from 3 egg whites
5. Spread the filling on the strudel dough, roll it together, place it on a butter-brushed bowl, add a little cream on top
6. Bake the strudel beautifully in the oven
7. When baked, sprinkle it with sugar and serve at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Markt-Wurst (Consommé à la moelle de boeuf)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca marrow
- 2 deca butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 16 deca rind-removed, finely diced rolls
- milk
- salt
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Let 12 deca marrow become soft
2. Pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
3. Add 2 deca butter and beat until fluffy
4. Stir in 3 egg yolks, 1 whole egg
5. Add 16 deca rind-removed rolls finely diced and soaked with milk
6. Add some salt
7. Lightly fold in the snow from 3 egg whites
8. Treatment same as for potato dumplings

title: Marlebarder Holantwrint (Marlebard-style Pastry Rounds)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams butter
- 4 decagrams finely ground sugar
- 1 knife-point of salt
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 decagrams pressed yeast dissolved in 6 tablespoons lukewarm milk
- 54 decagrams flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy and mix well with the finely ground sugar and salt
2. Gradually stir in the egg yolks
3. Add the pressed yeast dissolved in lukewarm milk and the flour, stirring well together
4. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon, then cover and let it rise in a warm place
5. When the dough has risen about 3 fingers higher, place it on a flour-dusted board and roll out to the thickness of a knife back
6. Cut out round discs using a doughnut cutter, spacing them 2 fingers apart

title: Marmelade von Aprikosen (Apricot Marmalade)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds ripe apricots
- apricot kernels from the pits
- 5 pounds 8 loth sugar

## Directions

1. Take 8 pounds of very ripe apricots
2. Remove the pits
3. Press through a fine sieve until nothing remains but the skins, which are discarded
4. Place the pressed apricots over low charcoal heat
5. Allow the liquid to evaporate
6. Shell and cut the apricot kernels lengthwise
7. Add a handful of these kernels to the marmalade
8. Stir well and set aside
9. Clarify and cook 5 pounds 8 loth of sugar as described
10. Carefully stir in the marmalade
11. When it begins to thicken and drops cleanly from a spoon, set it to cool
12. Fill into jars or boxes
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marmelade von Birnen (Pear Marmalade)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds pears
- water

## Directions

1. Peel 6 pounds of pears, such as Isenbart or Bergamotte varieties
2. Cut into four pieces
3. Remove the core
4. Place in fresh water
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marmelade von gemischten Früchten (Mixed Fruit Jam)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries
- Raspberries
- Currants
- 1 kilo such juice with 1 1/4 kilo powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Strawberries, raspberries, currants are quickly passed through a sieve and to 1 kilo such juice 1 1/4 kilo powdered sugar is given
2. This is immediately placed on the fire and cooked very quickly under constant stirring for approximately 10-15 minutes
3. Then, still hot like the apricot jam, filled into jars

title: Marmelade von Himbeeren (Raspberry Marmalade)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds raspberries
- 4 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Pass 8 pounds of well-selected raspberries through a fine sieve
2. Place the sieved berries in a casserole over a charcoal fire
3. Stir constantly until reduced to half
4. Clarify and cook 4 pounds of sugar as described above
5. Add the berry mass to the sugar
6. Proceed as for cherry marmalade
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marmelade von Kirschen (Cherry Marmalade)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pounds ripe cherries
- 6 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the cherries and remove the stems
2. Pit them with a pointed stick
3. Place in a casserole over a charcoal fire
4. Let the liquid cook down somewhat, stirring frequently with a wooden spoon until reduced to half
5. Clarify 6 pounds of sugar and cook until it reaches the small crack stage (petit casse), which is when a drop in cold water cracks between the teeth
6. Add the cherries to the sugar
7. Stir well over the charcoal fire until it reaches the proper consistency, which is when you can see the bottom of the casserole when stirring
8. When half-cooled, pour into jars or boxes
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marmelade von Reineclauden (Greengage Marmalade)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds ripe greengages
- 4 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Select 6 pounds of beautiful ripe greengages
2. Cut open and remove the pits
3. Pass through a fine sieve
4. Place on a charcoal fire until thick and the watery liquid is absorbed
5. Clarify and cook 4 pounds of sugar as described
6. Slowly stir in the fruit mass
7. Continue cooking over the charcoal fire until it reaches the proper thickness
8. After cooling, fill into boxes or jars
9. Plum (Zwetschken) and mirabelle marmalade are prepared the same way
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marmoriertes Eis (Marbled Icing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- white icing without lemon juice
- grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Prepare white icing without lemon juice
2. Once it is sufficiently beaten, add 1/4 deka of grated chocolate in the last moment, which, however, should be mixed only very lightly with the icing

title: Marzipan Blood Sausage (Blutwurst von Marzipan)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound red marzipan
- half pound white marzipan dough
- clean, fat-free dried pork intestines
- almond oil

## Directions

1. To 1 pound red marzipan, take half pound white marzipan dough
2. Cut everything into small irregular pieces and mix together
3. Take clean, transparent, fat-free dried pork intestines
4. Stuff the mixed marzipan pieces tightly in and press well together
5. Moisten your fingers with almond oil during this work, which makes the sausage glossy
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marzipan Bratwursts (Bratwürstchen von Marzipan)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound chocolate
- 1 pound marzipan dough
- 1 loth finely ground cinnamon
- 6 to 8 powdered cloves or some vanilla
- coarsely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Set quarter pound chocolate on a baking sheet over charcoal fire to soften and melt
2. Throw it into an earthen vessel to dissolve it with a little water
3. Add 1 pound marzipan dough, 1 loth finely ground cinnamon, 6 to 8 powdered cloves or some vanilla
4. Color the mass red
5. Knead this mixture well through and form sausages from it
6. Scatter coarsely chopped almonds on the table where you roll them
7. Let the finished sausages lie on paper for several days to dry
8. Then bake at moderate heat
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marzipan Ham (Marzipan-Schinken)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds marzipan dough
- finely ground sugar
- 1 loth finely ground cinnamon
- 1 loth finely ground cloves
- quarter pound chocolate
- water
- optional: vanilla or chocolate
- optional: crumbled macaroons

## Directions

1. Take 8 pounds marzipan dough and work it with finely ground sugar
2. Divide the mass into two unequal parts of 5 and 3 pounds
3. Color the larger portion red and season it with 1 loth cinnamon and 1 loth cloves, both finely ground
4. Form the known ham shape
5. With a knife make cuts in the middle of the red dough, which represents the lean meat
6. Fill them with white dough, which represents the fat
7. Stretch the rest of the remaining white marzipan dough with a rolling pin and lay it as an upper fat layer, 2 to 3 fingers high, done skillfully and naturally
8. To represent the rind of the ham, cook quarter pound chocolate in water quite thick
9. Brush the surface of the ham several times with it
10. The chocolate must be applied warm
11. If you want to make this confection more delicious, take some vanilla or chocolate and sprinkle the rind with crumbled macaroons, as cooks cover cooked ham with a bread crust
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marzipan Nuts (Marzipan-Nüsse)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- marzipan dough
- finely ground cinnamon
- flour
- water and sugar glaze
- compote of choice
- arabic gum solution

## Directions

1. Take a piece of marzipan dough and knead it thoroughly with finely ground cinnamon
2. Stretch the mass with a rolling pin to the thickness of half a finger
3. Cut into small pieces and fill forms (of pear wood) in which two nutshell halves with their markings and ridges must be carved
4. Dust the forms beforehand with finest flour
5. Press the dough firmly into them and scrape off excess with a knife
6. Turn the dough out of the forms and continue with the remaining mass
7. Lay these marzipan nutshells hollow side down on paper and let dry for 4 days
8. Then place in a weakly heated oven for a quarter hour
9. Coat with a glaze of water and sugar and let dry for a couple minutes in the oven
10. Fill the interior of each nutshell with compote of choice
11. Brush the edges with a solution of arabic gum and join them two by two together
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marzipan Raspberries (Himbeeren von Marzipan)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound marzipan dough
- juice of 4 lemons
- quarter pound raspberry jelly
- cochineal
- ground sugar
- handful of extracted flour
- quarter seidel raspberry juice
- granulated sugar (a la gréle)

## Directions

1. Take 1 pound marzipan dough, juice of 4 lemons, quarter pound raspberry jelly, and some cochineal
2. Stir everything well together with a wooden spatula
3. Since the mass becomes somewhat thin from the addition of these liquids, thicken it through addition of ground sugar and a handful of extracted flour
4. Form raspberry-like balls from this dough
5. Let them dry well on paper for 8 to 10 days
6. When this is done, pour a quarter seidel raspberry juice into a deep earthen vessel
7. Add some powdered sugar and some cochineal
8. Throw a handful of marzipan raspberries in and color them in it
9. Have ready a vessel with granulated sugar (a la gréle) - this is made by separating the very fine sugar dust from ground sugar through a silk sieve, then sieving what remains through a hair sieve, what stays in this is called in technical language granulated sugar
10. Roll the marzipan fruits moistened with raspberry juice in this granulated sugar so the grains adhere firmly to them
11. Lay them carefully to dry
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Marzipankugeln (Marzipan Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg whites
- 21 dkg sugar
- 16 dkg soaked grated almonds
- currant jam

## Directions

1. Stir 2 egg whites with 21 dkg sugar
2. Mix in 16 dkg soaked grated almonds
3. It must be a stiff mass from which you form equal-sized balls
