4. Press in the top and fill with currant jam
5. Glaze them either white or red

title: Maschink- oder Reindlrostbraten oder -Beefsteak (Machine or Roasting Pan Roast or Beefsteak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 roast or 1 beefsteak of 25 dkg
- 2 anchovies or 4 mushrooms
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- kipfel potatoes, raw sliced
- butter

## Directions

1. Trim the meat of skin, fat and bone, wipe it off, tap lightly with the back of the knife, salt, pepper it.
2. In a pan on the stove, quickly sear both sides in some fat (5 minutes).
3. Put some fat in the roasting pan, lay in the roast or beefsteak, then layer raw sliced kipfel potatoes, salt, pieces of anchovy or sliced mushrooms, butter pieces, then more potatoes and ingredients, continuing until the pan is full.
4. Cover and place in the oven for 45 minutes.
5. Serve the meat in the roasting pan. Instead of anchovies, sliced kohlrabi or yellow turnips can be used.

title: Matelote (Sailor's Stew) from Carp (Carp à la matelote)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carp, scaled, gutted and cleaned
- 1 small fresh eel
- 8 decilitres butter
- 1 large Spanish onion
- 1 piece yellow turnip
- parsley and celery, all finely chopped
- 1 bay leaf
- 4 sprigs thyme
- 5 grains of each type of spice
- 1 spice clove
- some nutmeg
- 1 litre red wine
- 1/2 litre broth
- 16 decilitres butter
- 12 decilitres flour
- small roasted onions
- stewed mushrooms
- crayfish cooked in wine
- toasted bread pieces

## Directions

1. Scale, gut and clean 1 carp, then cut it and 1 small fresh eel into 2-finger wide pieces
2. In a casserole, place 8 decilitres butter with 1 large Spanish onion, 1 piece yellow turnip, parsley and celery, all finely chopped, 1 bay leaf, 4 sprigs thyme, 5 grains of each type of spice, 1 spice clove, and some nutmeg
3. Layer the carp and eel pieces and small pieces of calf's brain on top
4. Pour 1 litre red wine and 1/2 litre broth over
5. Mix 16 decilitres butter with 12 decilitres flour and place on top
6. Simmer covered for approximately 3/4 hour, then set aside for 1/4 hour
7. Arrange the fish pieces on a platter, garnish with small roasted onions, stewed mushrooms, crayfish cooked in wine, and toasted bread pieces
8. Pour the sauce, which must be thick and defatted, over the top

title: Matrosenfleisch (Sailor's Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-70 dkg loin chops, thickly sliced
- 2 anchovies
- Salt
- Pepper
- 6 dkg fat
- 1 large finely chopped onion
- 1 small teaspoon paprika
- Cold water
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove skin from meat
2. Cut first into strips, then into diagonal cubes
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Heat 6 dkg fat in a casserole
5. Toast 1 large finely chopped onion
6. Add 1 small teaspoon paprika
7. Deglaze with a splash of cold water
8. Add the meat
9. Pour in some vinegar and water
10. Cover and braise for 2 hours, adding water occasionally
11. After 30 minutes, add the boned and finely chopped anchovies
12. Let braise together
13. Serve on a round or rectangular platter

title: Maultaschen (Württemberg Pasta Pockets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca butter
- 4 deca finely pounded sugar
- 1 pinch of salt
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 6 tablespoons lukewarm milk
- 54 deca flour
- filling as desired

## Directions

1. Cream together 16 deca butter with 4 deca finely pounded sugar and 1 pinch of salt
2. Stir in 4 egg yolks one after another
3. Add 2 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 6 tablespoons lukewarm milk and 54 deca flour, mix well
4. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon
5. Cover and let rise in a warm place until it has risen three finger-widths higher
6. Dust a board with flour and roll out the dough 2 knife-back thicknesses thick
7. Cut out round discs with a dough cutter and place 2 finger-widths apart on a butter-brushed baking sheet
8. Push the center of each round disc apart with fingers so a small-finger-width rim forms around the edge
9. Fill the depression with desired filling
10. Cover and let rise in a warm place until somewhat higher
11. Brush the rims with beaten egg
12. Cover the filling with Spanish windforce mixture knife-back thickness thick
13. Sprinkle with finely cut almonds mixed with white hail sugar
14. Bake slowly in the oven at moderate heat

title: Maurachen-Suppe (Roach Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- roach (Maurachen)
- butter
- finely chopped green parsley
- flour
- pea broth
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg flower
- cream

## Directions

1. Wash the roach until completely clean
2. Braise finely cut roach with butter and finely chopped green parsley
3. Dust with one spoonful of flour and braise again
4. Add as much pea broth as needed
5. Salt and pepper it
6. Let it simmer well with a little nutmeg flower
7. Before serving, you can add one spoonful of cream and serve over toasted bread slices
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Mayonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon tarragon vinegar
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- 1 tablespoon water
- oil
- salt
- mustard

## Directions

1. In a mixing bowl over a hot stovetop, beat the 4 egg yolks with 1 tablespoon tarragon vinegar, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, and 1 tablespoon water with a whisk until thick
2. Cool
3. Mix in oil, salt, and mustard

title: Mayonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 1½ dl oil
- ½ lemon
- mustard
- salt
- white pepper (optional)

## Directions

1. In a porcelain bowl, beat 3 egg yolks well with a whisk.
2. Stir in the oil thread by thread until the mayonnaise is thick.
3. Mix in the lemon juice thread by thread.
4. Add mustard, salt, and optional white pepper.
5. Keep the mayonnaise on ice until use.
6. To fix curdled mayonnaise: beat 1 egg yolk until foamy in another bowl, then whisk in the curdled mayonnaise thread by thread with constant stirring.

title: Mayonnaise from Hard-Boiled Egg Yolks (Mayonnaise von hartgekochtem Eigelb)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 hard-boiled, strained egg yolks
- 4 raw egg yolks
- 1 3/4 liters oil in ice
- juice of 2 lemons or 2 tablespoons vinegar
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Stir 4 hard-boiled, strained egg yolks with 4 raw egg yolks in a deep bowl for 15 minutes on ice
2. After this time, stir in 1 3/4 liters of oil in ice, the juice of 2 lemons or 2 tablespoons of vinegar drop by drop gradually
3. However, one can begin adding the vinegar or other acid only after half of the oil is mixed with the egg yolks
4. One must be very careful and precise with this treatment so the mayonnaise does not separate (become curdled)
5. Stir in pepper and salt at the end
6. Keep the mayonnaise covered on ice
7. If it has curdled, put 2 raw egg yolks in a very cold bowl set in ice and slowly beat the mayonnaise back together with it

title: Mayonnaise from Raw Egg Yolk (Raw Egg Yolk Mayonnaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 raw egg yolks
- ¼ liter oil in ice
- juice of 1 lemon or 2 tablespoons vinegar
- salt
- 2 pinches pepper

## Directions

1. Place 6 raw egg yolks in a deep bowl and stir them over ice
2. Then stir in dropwise and gradually ¼ liter of ice-cold oil with the juice of 1 lemon or 2 tablespoons of vinegar
3. When vinegar and oil are well mixed, add the necessary salt and 2 pinches of pepper and let this mayonnaise stand covered on ice until use
4. This mayonnaise is served with boiled cold chicken, likewise with roasted brain and with delicate fish dishes

title: Mayonnaise from Raw Egg Yolks (Mayonnaise Don rohem Eigelb)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 raw egg yolks
- 1/4 liter oil in ice
- juice of 1 lemon or 2 tablespoons vinegar
- salt to taste
- 2 knife tips of pepper

## Directions

1. Put 6 raw egg yolks into a deep bowl and stir them on ice
2. Stir in the oil drop by drop gradually along with the lemon juice or vinegar
3. When vinegar and oil are well mixed, add the necessary salt and 2 knife tips of pepper
4. Keep this mayonnaise covered on ice until use
5. Serve with boiled cold chicken, similarly boiled ham, and with fatty fish dishes

title: Mayonnaise Salad (Mayonnaisesalat)
body:
## Ingredients

- kipfel potatoes
- mayonnaise
- vinegar
- salt
- bird's lettuce or head lettuce

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes and place them in cold water.
2. Cook for 25 minutes, drain, cover with cloth and let sit for ½ hour.
3. Peel and cut into thin slices.
4. Mix with mayonnaise, some vinegar, and salt.
5. Serve cooled.
6. Garnish with bird's lettuce or finely cut head lettuce.

title: Mayonnaise von Fisch (Fish Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked fish scraps of pike or shad, or a small such fish about 1/2 kg, best is the tail piece of a larger shad
- 20 dkg lettuce salad
- 2 anchovies
- some capers
- aspic if desired
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the fish, wash it, and place in boiling water in a pan with some salt and 1/2 liter vinegar.
2. Remove the pan from heat and let the fish poach for 8-10 minutes.
3. Remove the fish, cool it, remove the bones, and press the fish between your hands (treat fish scraps the same way).
4. Form into a cone, place in the center of a glass bowl, coat with thick mayonnaise.
5. Decorate the cone with capers and rolled anchovies.
6. Surround with prepared lettuce salad, hard egg slices, and optionally red or white aspic.
7. Alternatively, the fish can be mixed with mayonnaise or mixed with tartar sauce, filled into shells, coated with mayonnaise, and decorated with capers.

title: Mayonnaise with Herbs (Sauce Ravigote)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 sprigs tarragon leaves
- 5 sprigs green parsley
- some sorrel
- burnet
- 5 sprigs chervil
- 5 juniper berries
- 4 teaspoons butter
- 1 tablespoon oil
- mayonnaise made from raw egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon French mustard optionally

## Directions

1. Finely cut 5 sprigs of tarragon leaves, 5 sprigs of green parsley, some sorrel, burnet, and 5 sprigs of chervil
2. Pound them finely in a mortar with 5 juniper berries, 4 teaspoons of butter, and 1 tablespoon of oil and pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
3. Mix in the mayonnaise made from raw egg yolks (No. 209) and then serve
4. If desired, 1 tablespoon of French mustard can also be stirred in

title: Meat Dumplings (Fleiſchknödel)
body:
## Ingredients

- hash of 25-30 dkg cooked beef
- 6 dkg roll ham
- 8 dkg ham fat
- 4 dkg bacon
- some bread crumbs
- 30 dkg salted flour
- 4-5 scoop-fuls boiling water

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over 30 dkg salted flour in a pot, stir smooth and cover
2. When cooled, form equal-sized dumplings from the dough on a floured board, flatten slightly
3. Place a meatball inside, close the dough over it

title: Medlar Juice (Mispelbeersaft)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Half-medlars
- 1/2 liter medlar juice
- 42 grams sugar
- Spices (cloves and cinnamon bound in a cloth)

## Directions

1. Remove the medlars from their stems, crush them with the cores, and let them stand in an earthenware or porcelain bowl in a cool place for 2 days
2. Strain the juice through a felt sack and pour what has run out into bottles, but do not fill these completely
3. Add the crushed sugar, namely 42 grams sugar per 1/2 liter
4. Add spices (cloves and cinnamon) bound in a cloth
5. When this medlar juice has stood in the sun for 2-3 weeks, seal the bottle and store it in a cool place
6. This juice is used for glazes, creams, as an addition to sauces and various dishes
7. One can also prepare a very good liqueur from this juice by adding a mixture of brandy or fruit brandy after 5-10 weeks

title: Mehl Soufflé (Flour Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Deka flour
- 6 Deka hot butter
- 1/4 liter milk
- 6 Deka cold butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 12 Deka sugar
- 8 egg whites
- butter for coating the mold
- flour for dusting the mold
- baked biscuit
- liqueur or dissolved marmalade

## Directions

1. Roast 8 Deka flour in 6 Deka hot butter for a short time.
2. Pour in 1/4 liter milk and stir over heat until the dough releases from the pan.
3. Pour it into a deep bowl and stir cold with 6 Deka cold butter.
4. Beat 8 egg yolks and 12 Deka sugar for 1/2 hour, then gradually add to the cooled dough mass.
5. Lightly fold in the snow of 8 egg whites.
6. Fill half of this mass into a mold brushed with butter and dusted with flour.
7. Arrange baked biscuits that have been soaked in liqueur or dissolved marmalade on top.
8. Distribute the second half of the mass evenly over it and bake covered for 3/4 hour in steam or in the oven at medium heat.
9. Turn the cake out into a bowl, pour dissolved marmalade over if desired, and serve immediately.

title: Mehl- oder schmölz-haltige Semmel-Knödel (Flour or Lard-containing Roll Quenelles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 Loffel butter, or 12 Loffel lard or skimmed fat
- 24 Loffel finely diced rolls, mixed with 1/8 liter milk
- 1/8 liter milk
- 1 egg yolk
- 2 whole eggs
- salt
- 20 Loffel flour

## Directions

1. Heat 14 Loffel butter, or 12 Loffel lard or skimmed fat and pour over 24 Loffel finely diced rolls mixed with 1/8 liter milk
2. Beat 1/8 liter milk with 1 egg yolk and 2 whole eggs and pour over it as well
3. Add salt and bind with 20 Loffel flour
4. Form knödel of desired size

title: Mehl-Soufflé mit Äpfeln (Flour Soufflé with Apples)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deca flour
- 1/4 liter cold milk
- 16 Deca butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 Deca powdered sugar
- 8 egg whites (snow)
- Muscateller apples
- jam for filling

## Directions

1. Put 16 Deca flour into a saucepan, pour 1/4 liter cold milk to it while stirring constantly and stir it over the fire until the dough loosens from the spoon, then add 16 Deca butter, stir well and set aside
2. Meanwhile stir 8 egg yolks with 16 Deca powdered sugar for 1/4 hour, add the cooled flour mass, mix well and finally fold in lightly the snow from 8 egg whites
3. Peel Muscateller apples after the core has been removed, fill the resulting empty space with any jam, place them in a buttered form dusted with flour at finger-breadth distance, cover with the flour mass described above and bake for 1/4 hours in the oven
4. Remove the form, take away the rim and arrange the soufflé on the cake plate in a bowl and sprinkle with finely pounded sugar
5. Instead of apples any fruits can be inserted

title: Mehlschmarren (Flour Schmarren)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l Milk
- 2 Eggs
- Salt
- 20 dkg Flour
- 8 dkg Butter or fat

## Directions

1. Mix milk with eggs, salt, and flour until smooth
2. Heat butter or fat in a flat pan until hot
3. Pour in flour mixture and let cook on the stove for 30 minutes, stirring frequently
4. The schmarren should be soft yet grainy
5. Sprinkle with sugar and serve on a round platter with compote

title: Meister Wirnberger Lebkuchen à la Waldshut (Master Wirnberger Gingerbread à la Waldshut)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 56 deka finely pounded sugar
- 9 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 8 deka finely cut citron
- 8 deka finely cut orange peel
- 8 deka finely cut lemon peel
- 2 deka cinnamon
- 3 pounded spice cloves
- 1 pinch nutmeg
- 48 deka fine dry flour
- 48 deka blanched almonds, finely cut with a razor and roasted golden in the oven

## Directions

1. Beat 3 egg yolks for 1/4 hour
2. Add 56 deka finely pounded sugar and mix very well together
3. Stir in stiffly beaten egg white from 9 eggs for 3/4 hour
4. Add 8 deka finely cut citron, 8 deka finely cut orange peel, 8 deka finely cut lemon peel, 2 deka cinnamon, 3 pounded spice cloves, 1 pinch nutmeg, and 48 deka fine dry flour, stir well together
5. Finally mix in 48 deka blanched almonds finely cut with a razor and roasted golden in the oven
6. Cut round slices from wafers of desired size
7. Spread 1 to 2 fingers high with the above mass, but not to the very edge as it easily adheres to the sheet during baking
8. Place a blanched almond or a piece of finely cut citron in the center of each slice
9. Put on a sheet and bake at moderate heat in the oven until completely dry

title: Melonen-Marmelade (Melon Jam)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe melons
- Powdered sugar (1/2 kilo sugar per 1 kilo melon)

## Directions

1. From ripe melons remove the watery parts and the skin, cut the rest into pieces
2. Put these with the powdered sugar in a cassole, where they are allowed to stew gently covered
3. Then quickly passed through a sieve and filled into preserve jars
4. These are bound when the jam has set and cooked in steam for 3/4 hours like the nutmeg pears

title: Melonen-Sauce (Melon Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablespoons butter
- 6 tablespoons flour
- 1 piece finely cut melon (preserved or fresh)
- 1/4 liter broth
- some wine or the juice in which the melon was preserved
- some vinegar
- some sugar

## Directions

1. Make 6 tablespoons butter hot, stir in equal amount flour, and add 1 piece finely cut melon and roast it somewhat
2. Gradually pour in 1/4 liter broth while stirring constantly, letting it boil with some wine or the juice in which the melon was preserved, or also a little vinegar, then with some sugar
3. Pass it and use it as a sauce for roasted meat
4. Small pieces of cut melon can be added to it

title: Mieringschmutter Salat (Mild Green Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Mieringschmutter (greens)
- Essig
- Öl
- Zucker
- Salz

## Directions

1. Nachdem dieser gepult und gewaschen ist, wird das Wasser abgegossen, der Salat gesalzen, und mit Essig, Öl und etwa Zucker gut untereinandergemischt.

title: Milanese Torte (Tourte à la Milanaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Deka orange blossoms
- 6 tablespoons cold water
- 30 Deka blanched almonds
- 28 Deka beaten butter
- 16 Deka lemon sugar
- 1 knife tip cinnamon
- 8 egg yolks
- 30 Deka flour

## Directions

1. Soak orange blossoms in cold water for approximately 2 hours
2. Strain through a sieve and grind very finely with blanched almonds
3. Mix beaten butter with lemon sugar
4. Add the ground almonds, cinnamon, and egg yolks one by one
5. Stir for 1/2 hour
6. Mix in flour
7. Proceed as in the 'Viennese Torte' (no. 1486)

title: Milchrahm-Nockerl (Sour Cream Noodles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter sour cream
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 liter flour
- salted water
- 1/8 liter toasted bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Beat 1/4 liter sour cream, 4 egg yolks, and 1/2 teaspoon salt well together
2. Gradually pour into 1/2 liter flour in a deep bowl while stirring constantly
3. Beat the dough until it falls from the spoon
4. Using a spoon kept in hot water, place noodles of desired size into boiling salted water
5. Cover and cook for approximately 10 minutes
6. Pour into a colander and rinse with cold water
7. Place in a pot containing 1/8 liter toasted bread crumbs
8. Stir well together and serve hot as a garnish for roasted lungs, paprika chicken, etc.

title: Milchrahm-Suppe (Cream Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter milk puree
- 3 tablespoons flour
- salt
- caraway
- 1 1/4 liters boiling water

## Directions

1. Stir together milk puree with 3 tablespoons flour, some salt and caraway
2. Stir into 1 1/4 liters boiling water, cook and pass through a sieve
3. The vessel in which this is cooked must however be twice as large as the mass occupies, so that it does not boil over while cooking

title: Milchrahmnockerl (Milk Cream Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Seidel cream
- 3 Loth melted butter

## Directions

1. Take 1 Seidel cream with 3 Loth melted butter.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Milchrahmnockerl (Milk Cream Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Seidel (about 1/2 liter) cream
- 3 Loth (about 1.5 oz) melted butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 4 spoonfuls flour
- 1 inch high cream for cooking
- sugar (optional)
- vanilla (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat the cream, melted butter, and egg yolks well together
2. Stir 4 spoonfuls of flour into the mixture
3. When the mixture is light and fluffy, take 1 inch high cream in a pan or cooking vessel
4. Once the cream simmers, place the dumplings into it and provide heat from below and above
5. If desired for better flavor, add sugar and vanilla
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Milchsuppe mit einem Rande von Eyerschnee (Milk Soup with a Border of Egg Snow)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- egg whites
- egg yolks
- a pair of spoonfuls of fine flour
- crushed sugar
- cinnamon water
- hot sweet milk
- thin small sugar rolls

## Directions

1. Beat egg whites on a bowl with a wide rim to snow
2. Place this on the rim and let the foam dry facing the heat
3. Rub sugar on the outer side and hold a glowing shovel over it so the whole rim becomes brown
4. Beat several egg yolks with a pair of spoonfuls of fine flour, some crushed sugar and cinnamon water
5. Gradually pour in hot sweet milk while beating it to foam
6. Finally add very thin and small sugar rolls to the plated soup
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Milchsuppe mit Orangenblüten (Milk Soup with Orange Blossoms)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter boiling milk
- 2 deca orange blossoms
- 1 liter boiling milk
- finely cut bread slices
- 1/4 liter cold milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 1/3 liter boiling milk
- 8 deca sugar
- 1 1/2 teaspoons salt
- 1/4 liter with orange blossoms

## Directions

1. Into 1/4 boiling milk put 2 deca orange blossoms and let stand covered and warm for about 1 hour
2. Pour 1 liter boiling milk over finely cut bread slices in the soup pot and cover
3. Beat 8 egg yolks in 1/4 liter cold milk; in 1/3 liter boiling milk put 8 deca sugar and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt, then mix in the 1 liter milk with the 8 egg yolks
4. Add the 1/4 liter with orange blossoms, stir everything together well and pour into the milk with bread slices. This yields 2 liters of soup.

title: Milchsuppe mit Reisknödelchen (Milk Soup with Rice Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 loth rice
- water
- 5 eggs
- sugar
- a teacup of melted butter
- crushed cinnamon
- grated lemon peel
- butter
- milk
- crushed almonds
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Soak 8 loth rice in pure water overnight, drain it
2. Cook the rice very soft and stiff, then let it cool
3. Stir in 5 eggs, sugar according to taste, a teacup of melted butter, crushed cinnamon and grated lemon peel
4. Form small smooth dumplings and bake them in a butter-greased cake pan until brown
5. Prepare milk cooked with crushed almonds, egg yolks, sugar and cinnamon
6. Serve the milk soup over the dumplings after the soup has formed a skin
7. Dust with cinnamon
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Milk Cream Pudding (Milchrahmkoch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound fresh good butter
- five whole eggs
- five egg yolks
- one roll soaked in milk
- sugar
- one seidel sweet milk cream
- lemon peel, finely cut

## Directions

1. In a mixing bowl, beat one-quarter pound fresh good butter until fluffy
2. Beat in five whole eggs and five egg yolks
3. Crumble a roll that has been soaked in and pressed out of milk under it
4. Sweeten it
5. Coat a dish with butter
6. Pour in one seidel sweet milk cream and finely cut lemon peel
7. Arrange everything in, apply heat top and bottom, and bake it beautifully in a cake pan
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Millefeuille (Mille-Feuille)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff dough
- Butter
- Flour
- Eggs
- Filling (beliebigem Fasch or Pâté)
- Pastry decorations from puff dough

## Directions

1. Roll out chilled puff dough knife-back-thick and cut approximately 10 discs using a cake pan
2. Cut a large square from the rolled puff dough and lightly fold the corners together in the middle
3. Place in a buttered and flour-dusted cake pan with the four corners spread apart and draped over the sides
4. Brush the bottom finger-high with filling or pâté and cover with a puff dough disc
5. Continue alternating layers until the form is full
6. When the final dough disc is added, make several cuts with a knife and fold the four corners over each other
7. Brush with beaten eggs and add puff pastry decorations brushed with beaten eggs
8. Place the cake pan on a sand-strewn baking sheet and bake at moderate heat for 1 to 1.5 hours
9. Turn the pastry into a bowl and serve at the table
10. This dish can also be made with strudel dough instead of puff dough

title: Millet in Milk (Hirfebrei in der Milch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 0.33 liter millet
- 12 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons sugar
- 1 pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Bring 1 liter of milk to a boil
2. Stir in 0.33 liter of washed millet under constant stirring and cook well
3. When the mixture begins to become thick, add 12 tablespoons butter, 8 tablespoons sugar, and 1 pinch of salt
4. Stir well together
5. When the millet is soft and cooked thick, arrange in a serving dish dusted with finely ground sugar and grated gingerbread (Lebkuchen) and serve

title: Milzschnitte (Spleen Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dag butter
- ¾ egg
- salt
- pepper
- finely chopped parsley
- 5 dag beef spleen, washed and scraped
- 2 slices of bread (Schneidssemmeln)

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and beat in the egg, salt, pepper, parsley, and the washed, scraped, and passed spleen, and a few breadcrumbs if needed
2. Cut 2 slices of bread first in half, then into leaf-like strips
3. Spread the spleen mixture on one side, then fry in plenty of hot lard on both sides
4. Drain and serve warm

title: Minced Meat Roast with Pea Puree
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dkg beef sirloin
- 30 dkg pork shoulder, boned
- 2 rolls
- 4 dkg bacon
- 1/2 cream
- 1 egg
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Remove bones, skin, and fat from the meat, wipe dry, and cut into cubes
2. Pass through a meat grinder twice with 1½ rolls (soaked in water and squeezed), 1/4 onion, and parsley
3. Place in a bowl and mix with 1 egg, salt, pepper, and bacon cut into small cubes
4. Place on a board sprinkled with breadcrumbs and shape into 2 rolls
5. Place in a pan, top with fat pieces, add a little water
6. Roast in the oven for 1 hour, basting frequently with its own juices and hot water
7. After 30 minutes, gradually baste with cream
8. Transfer to a platter, slice, and serve with the pan drippings

title: Mit Trüffeln gefüllter Kapaun (Capon Stuffed with Truffles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilo bacon, cut into strips
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1/2 kilo cleaned truffles, cooked in Madeira wine, finely sliced

## Directions

1. Treat the capon as described above
2. In 1/4 kilo of bacon cut into strips, add the necessary salt and, if desired, some pepper, 1/2 kilo of cleaned truffles cooked in Madeira wine and finely sliced, and stuff the capon with this mixture as described above
3. Prepare the poultry in the same manner

title: Mitgoult-Croquettes (Meat and Liver Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 tablespoons of any braised, finely cut meat
- 16 tablespoons finely diced goose liver
- 1 teaspoon of green parsley
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- Salt
- Pepper to taste
- Butter sauce (if needed)
- Sour cream (if needed)
- Flour for dusting
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard for frying

## Directions

1. Mix the meat, goose liver, parsley, egg yolks, whole egg, salt and pepper well together
2. If the mixture is too dry, mix it well with some butter sauce or sour cream
3. Place the mixture on a flour-dusted board, form small sausages 1 finger long and 2 fingers thick
4. Dip them in beaten, whipped eggs, roll them in breadcrumbs and fry them quickly until golden in very hot lard
5. Remove with a slotted spoon, arrange them nicely in a bowl and serve as garnish with vegetables and purees or with any desired hot dish
6. They can also be served as a dish by themselves

title: Mittag ach's Suppe (German-style Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 Deciliter hot butter
- approximately 14 Deciliter flour
- 2 liters cold beef broth
- salt
- parsley, mushrooms, or morels for flavoring
- optional: calf's head, small pieces, egg noodles, green peas, toasted rolls, or a mixture

## Directions

1. Add approximately 14 Deciliter flour to 14 Deciliter hot butter
2. Let it roast
3. Pour it over with 2 liters cold beef broth and stir well
4. Add salt and let it come to a boil
5. Flavor with parsley, mushrooms, or morels as desired
6. Add optional ingredients such as calf's head, small pieces, egg noodles, green peas, toasted rolls, or a mixture

title: Mittelfeine Krapfen (Medium-Fine Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 Decaliter dried, warm, sifted, fine flour
- 4 Decaliter sugar
- 5 grams salt
- 1 liter milk, divided into 3 equal parts
- 3 Decaliter fresh yeast
- 12 Decaliter butter or 10 Decaliter beef lard
- 6 egg yolks
- Marmalade or jam for filling
- 6-8 Decaliter beef lard
- 7/8 Decaliter pork lard of better quality

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as Fine Pastry Fritters, using only 6 egg yolks instead of 10, and using 12 Decaliter butter or 10 Decaliter beef lard instead of 14 Decaliter butter in the dough.

title: Mittlere Speck-Knödel (Medium Bacon Quenelles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 Loffel bacon, finely diced
- 32 Loffel rolls, finely diced, mixed with 1/8 liter milk
- 1/8 liter milk
- 3 whole eggs
- salt
- knife point of finely cut parsley
- 1 liter flour

## Directions

1. Dice bacon finely and roast in a saucepan over heat until the bacon appears glassy
2. Pour over 32 Loffel finely diced rolls that have been mixed with 1/8 liter milk and stir well
3. Beat 1/8 liter milk with 3 whole eggs, pour over the rolls, add salt, a knife point of finely cut parsley and 1 liter flour, stir well
4. Let stand covered for 1/2 hour
5. Dip a spoon in hot water and fill with as much dough as you want to make one knödel and round it with a cooking spoon, or dip hands in water and form the knödel this way
6. Put the knödel into boiling salted water

title: Mixed Vegetables Pickled (im Spinat eingelegt)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Green beans
- small young yellow carrots
- small onions
- cauliflower florets
- shallots
- rocamboles
- artichoke bottoms
- small cucumber slices
- mushrooms
- asparagus
- vinegar
- water
- clove
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare the vinegar one or two days ahead: boil 2 parts wine vinegar with 1 part water, 1 clove, and some nutmeg for about half an hour, removing foam that forms, then cool in an earthenware, glass, or porcelain vessel.
2. Vegetables to be pickled are: green beans, young small yellow carrots, small onions, cauliflower florets, shallots, rocamboles, artichoke bottoms, small cucumber slices, mushrooms, and asparagus.
3. All vegetables must be very young.
4. Cook each type separately in salted water until half-tender, then place in cold water and drain on a sieve or cloth.

title: Mock-Tortue-Suppe (Mock Turtle Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams raw beef fat
- 8 decagrams marrow fat
- 1 head of onion cut in slices
- 2 pieces each of carrots, parsley, and celery
- 1 kilo cooked veal bones
- 1 kilo beef bones
- 1/2 kilo shin bones
- 4 liters broth or water
- 5 peppercorns
- 1 spice clove
- 1 small piece nutmeg
- 10 decagrams potato starch
- 1 pinch white pepper
- 1/8 liter Madeira or white wine
- paradise apple puree or whole paradise apples
- 3 truffles braised in wine
- 3 braised champignons
- 1 piece soft-cooked calf's head
- calf's brain, diced

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a casserole with 8 decagrams raw beef fat and 8 decagrams marrow fat
2. Add sliced onion head, carrot, parsley, and celery pieces
3. Place on these 1 kilo cooked veal bones, 1 kilo beef bones, and 1/2 kilo shin bones
4. Seal the casserole well and braise the contents until the fat becomes clear
5. Pour off the fat and add 4 liters of broth or water
6. Add 5 peppercorns, 1 spice clove, and 1 small piece of nutmeg
7. Cook slowly covered for 3 hours
8. Remove all fat, strain through cloth, and bring to a boil again
9. Mix 10 decagrams of potato starch with 1 pinch of white pepper and thin with 1/8 liter of Madeira or white wine until flowing
10. Stir this into the boiling soup using a whisk, stirring constantly
11. Add paradise apple puree or whole paradise apples
12. Cook slowly for 1 hour with frequent skimming
13. Before serving, add 3 truffles braised in wine, 3 braised champignons, 1 piece of soft-cooked calf's head, and diced calf's brain

title: Mohn Torte (Poppy Seed Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dkg sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 stiff egg whites
- 16 dkg grated poppy seeds

## Directions

1. Beat 18 dkg sugar with 6 egg yolks well
2. Fold in 6 stiff egg whites together with 16 dkg grated poppy seeds
3. Fill into the form and bake slowly
4. Cool, dust with sugar or frost with white glaze

title: Mohn- und Nußkipfel (Poppy Seed and Nut Crescent Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg flour
- 11 dkg butter
- risen yeast starter (Dampfl)
- 1 pinch salt
- 2 spoons sugar
- 1 egg
- finely chopped lemon zest
- 1 spoon lukewarm milk
- poppy seed or nut filling
- egg white for brushing
- beaten egg for brushing before baking

## Directions

1. On a board, rub together 21 dkg flour with 11 dkg butter
2. Add the risen yeast starter, 1 pinch salt, 2 spoons sugar, 1 egg, finely chopped lemon zest and 1 spoon lukewarm milk
3. Knead into dough and let rise under a warm casserole for 1 hour
4. Roll out thinly, cut into rectangles
5. Brush the edges with egg white, place filling in the middle and press edges together
6. Form into crescent shapes, place on greased baking sheet
7. Set again near the oven to rise
8. Before baking, brush with beaten egg and bake at moderate heat until light brown
9. Sprinkle with sugar and serve cooled

title: Mohn- und Nußstrudel (Poppy Seed and Nut Strudel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dkg flour
- 10 dkg butter
- salt
- 2 spoons sugar
- 1 whole egg
- warm milk as needed

## Directions

1. Warm the flour in a mixing bowl, rub in the 10 dkg butter, add salt, 2 spoons sugar, 1 whole egg, the risen starter, and warm milk as needed.
2. Beat the dough until it releases from the spoon. It should be firm; if needed, knead further on a board.
3. Roll out into a rectangle, brush with fat, brush edges with egg white.
4. Spread the cooled filling on top, roll up the strudel, let it rise on a greased sheet near the oven, brush with beaten egg, and bake at lower heat until light brown.
5. Cut, sprinkle with sugar, and serve cooled.

title: Mohnfülle (Poppy Seed Filling)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter finely ground poppy seeds
- 1-2 Deka vanilla sugar
- 1/8 liter boiling milk
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped lemon zest
- 1 blanched almond

## Directions

1. Place ground poppy seeds and vanilla sugar in a saucepan
2. Pour 1/8 liter boiling milk over them
3. Stir over heat until the mixture thickens
4. Mix in 1 teaspoon finely chopped lemon zest to taste and 1 blanched almond

title: Mohnfülle (Poppy Seed Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- grated poppy seeds
- milk
- 2 spoons sugar
- 4 dkg butter
- 3 dkg raisins
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cook the grated poppy seeds with the milk, 2 spoons sugar, 4 dkg butter, 3 dkg raisins, and lemon peel for 30 minutes with frequent stirring until thick.
2. Use the filling after it has cooled.

title: Mohnstipperl mit Eiertaig (Poppy Seed Twists with Egg Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 4 deca powdered sugar
- 1 pinch of salt
- 4 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/2 liter lukewarm milk
- 60 deca flour
- 1/4 liter poppy seeds ground very fine
- 1/8 liter boiling milk
- 1/2 to 9 or 12 deca sugar or syrup as needed
- 1/2 pinch finely cut lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cream butter with egg yolks, powdered sugar, and salt
2. Add fresh yeast dissolved in milk and flour, mix well
3. Beat until dough separates from spoon
4. Cover and let rise one-third higher in warm place
5. Meanwhile prepare poppy filling: pour 1/8 liter boiling milk over finely ground poppy seeds, stir in sugar or syrup and lemon peel, cook well and set aside to cool
6. Roll out dough to knife-back thickness on flour-dusted board
7. Cut into desired-size squares
8. Brush each corner with beaten egg and top with 1 teaspoon poppy filling
9. Roll up from the brushed corner to enclose filling, twisting into peaked shape
10. Place on butter-brushed baking sheet 3 fingers apart
11. Cover and let rise 1/4 to 1/2 hour in warm place
12. Brush with beaten egg
13. Bake slowly in oven
14. Arrange on serving dish and sprinkle with pounded sugar

title: Mohnstrudel (Poppy Seed Strudel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound butter
- 8 egg yolks
- spoonful pounded sugar
- just over half seidel cream
- 3 spoonfuls good yeast (Germ)
- salt
- half mass flour
- half seidel clarified honey
- half mass finely pounded poppy seeds
- milk
- small piece butter
- finely pounded vanilla
- finely cut lemon zest

## Directions

1. Beat half pound butter fluffy
2. Beat in 8 egg yolks one by one, well stirred
3. Add a spoonful of pounded sugar, just over half seidel cream, 3 spoonfuls good yeast, some salt, and half mass flour
4. Mix everything well, beat the dough finely, and let it rise a little
5. For the filling, take a strong half seidel of clarified honey and half mass finely pounded poppy seeds
6. Place it on a gentle coal fire and add enough milk so it can be spread, also a small piece of butter, finely pounded vanilla, and finely cut lemon zest
7. When soft enough, set it aside and let it cool
8. Take the dough on a flour-dusted board, roll it two knife-backs thick
9. Spread the filling equally thick on it, roll the strudel together, place it on a butter-brushed baking sheet, let it rise well again, and bake it with sustained heat
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Mohrnutte (Poppy Seed Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 ounces butter
- 18 ounces sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 18 ounces finely ground poppy seeds
- 10 ounces finely chopped citron
- 6 egg whites
- 18 ounces blanched and ground almonds

## Directions

1. Beat 18 ounces butter until fluffy
2. Stir in 18 ounces sugar for 1 hour
3. Gradually add 6 egg yolks together with 18 ounces finely ground and ground poppy seeds
4. Add 10 ounces finely chopped citron, then gently fold in the stiffly beaten snow from 6 egg whites together with 18 ounces blanched and ground almonds
5. Pour the mass into a desired lined mold and bake in a not very hot oven for 1 hour
6. Sprinkle with sugar or cover with chocolate glaze and treat further as the Nebrücken

title: Mokka Torte (Mocha Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- flour
- sugar
- eggs
- 12 dkg tea butter
- 8 dkg sugar
- strong black coffee
- 7 dkg sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons black coffee
- 8 dkg butter
- swelled chopped almonds
- sugar for dusting
- for glaze: sugar
- black coffee

## Directions

1. Make a biscuit torte from 6 egg yolks
2. For cold stirred filling: stir together 12 dkg tea butter with 8 dkg sugar and mix in several tablespoons of strong black coffee
3. For warm beaten filling: beat 7 dkg sugar with 2 egg yolks and 4 tablespoons black coffee over steam until thick, cool, and stir in 8 dkg butter
4. Fill the torte with either filling
5. The torte can be spread with cream inside and outside and piped with rosettes using a pastry bag
6. For this you need about twice as much cream as the basic amount
7. If the torte is only spread with cream on the outside, sprinkle swelled chopped almonds on top; if filled only inside with cream, cover with coffee glaze
8. For the glaze: add several tablespoons of hot black coffee to the sugar instead of rum and water, and stir well
9. The sugar must be very finely sifted if piping the torte

title: Molke zu bereiten (Preparing Whey)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter old milk
- 2 grams powdered rennet

## Directions

1. To obtain sweet whey, put 1 liter of old milk in a coffee pot and add 2 grams of powdered rennet (obtainable at the apothecary) to it.
2. Stir it well together and let it become hot to the boiling point, whereby the rennet curdles.
3. Strain this through a cloth and obtain the pure sweet whey.

title: Mollerweiche Eier (Medium-Boiled Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. Treat the same as soft-boiled eggs, but cook for 8 minutes instead of 5.

title: Moorhuhn (Hazel Grouse)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Young or old hazel grouse
- Preparations as for wood grouse

## Directions

1. Preparation is the same as for hazel hens

title: Morels with Cream (Morilles à la Crème)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- morels
- butter
- parsley
- béchamel sauce
- cream
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned morels into rings
2. Braise them in butter and parsley
3. Add some béchamel sauce with cream, salt and nutmeg
4. Mix everything well together, season and serve

title: Moulins Pâtés (Bratwurst in Butter Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- bratwurst, roasted or cold
- eggs

## Directions

1. Strongly knife-back-thick rolled-out butter dough is cut into strips 2 fingers wide and once again as long, and these are then brushed with beaten eggs so far that at the edge where it was cut, a knife-back-width margin remains dry.
2. From roasted or cold bratwurst, the skin is removed, the filling is laid lengthwise in the middle of the egg-brushed strips at equal distance from the edges, the dough is now folded over a small-finger's breadth, and a cut edge must come to lie on top.
3. The roulades are then brushed with beaten eggs, at the edge however where the dough overlaps, a knife-back-width margin is left dry, and the roulades are placed on a water-moistened baking sheet across its width so that the upper-lying margin with the cut side is turned toward the back wall of the oven.
4. The roulade pâtés are then placed in the oven and baked yellow at fairly strong heat.

title: Mupp Eiförmig Saftete (Egg-Shaped Pastry Case)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Paper
- Writing paper
- Water
- Butter dough, 2 knife-back thickness
- Eggs
- Strong butter dough, knife-back thickness

## Directions

1. Form a ball from paper the size of a small head and cover it with writing paper, then place it on a water-wetted baking sheet.
2. Cut a square of butter dough 2 knife-back thickness in size to match the ball, fold the 4 corners slightly over each other in the center, lay the dough on the paper ball, spread the 4 corners apart at the top and fold them downward onto the baking sheet, then push them together into folds to create a dome-like form which is brushed with beaten eggs.
3. Cut 2 finger-wide and 7.5 cm long ribbon-like strips from strong butter dough, roll one strip together so that one diagonally cut end of the strip protrudes somewhat and the second end is fastened with egg white.
4. Carefully pull up the protruding end slightly and place the rolled band on the midpoint of the dome so that the bands lie on the dome and the protruding end stands upright like a peak.
5. Lay the remaining dough bands on the dome after cutting one end diagonally so that the diagonally cut points always stand free, and lay the bands in small folds down to the edge of the dome, directing them upward and fastening each fold with beaten eggs.
6. Decorate the entire dome in this way with the bands closing at the top of the dome but running apart at the bottom edge at 3 finger-widths distance.
7. Cut the pastry evenly around the edge, brush with beaten eggs, and bake at quite strong heat for 1/2 to 3/4 hours.
8. After half the time, the baking sheet on which the pastry sits must be turned so it gets an even color.
9. When this is done, remove the pastry from the oven, hold it up with the help of two knives while carefully pulling out the paper inside.
10. Place this pastry on a platter on which any meat, poultry, or salmi has been prepared beforehand and serve immediately.
11. Note: before beginning preparation of the pastry, always first check the height of the oven and form the pastry accordingly.
12. If the pastry is prepared for game, serve the sauce in a sauce boat.

title: Muscazon Torte (Tourte à la Muscazon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deca finely ground blanched almonds with skin
- 32 deca finely ground sugar
- 8 deca black molasses
- some vinegar
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- juice of one lemon
- some finely chopped lemon peel
- 1 wafer sheet

## Directions

1. On a kneading board mix 32 deca finely ground blanched almonds with skin, 32 deca finely ground sugar, 8 deca black molasses, and some vinegar together well
2. Make a depression in the middle, add 3 egg yolks, 1 whole egg, lemon juice with some finely chopped lemon peel
3. Work everything into a dough and let rest in a cool place for 1/2 hour
4. Roll out the dough finger-thick on a floured board and cut out a round disc, which fits into the prepared buttered and floured cake form with a wafer sheet
5. Spread the dough in the form with egg wash
6. Cut the remaining dough into small, neat shapes, place them on the egg wash in any arrangement, and brush with beaten egg
7. Bake in the oven at low heat for 1/2 to 3/4 hours
8. Place on a sieve and let cool. Use this cake after 1 to 2 days as it becomes firmer with time

title: Mushroom Purée (Purée de Champignons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 1 liter of mushrooms
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons rolls softened in cold water
- butter sauce
- 1/4 liter boiling cream
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from approximately 1 liter of mushrooms
2. Wash the mushroom caps
3. Cut the mushroom caps and stems very finely along with 8 tablespoons of rolls that have been softened in cold water and well pressed out
4. Braise this in 4 tablespoons butter
5. Gradually add butter sauce with 1/4 liter of boiling cream, the necessary salt, and some nutmeg
6. Stir smooth and cook covered
7. When well cooked, pass through a fine strainer
8. Stir in some butter
9. Thin with cream if necessary
10. Stir together well and serve

title: Mushroom Soup (Schwammerlsuppe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6-8 mushrooms or champignons
- 4 dkg butter
- salt
- parsley
- 8 dkg butter
- water or broth
- ½ liter cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the mushrooms, cut into thin slices
2. Place in a pot with 4 dkg hot butter and braise for ½ hour, stirring occasionally
3. Make a parsley roux with 8 dkg butter and moisten with water or day-old broth
4. Add the mushrooms, season with salt, cook well
5. Optional: add a few tablespoons of cream to the soup

title: Mushroom Soup (Soupe aux Champignons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deca butter or fat
- Flour as needed
- Finely chopped onion
- 2 liters water
- Salt and spices
- 1 plate (about 0.5 liters) finely sliced mushrooms
- 6 Deca butter
- Green parsley
- 0.5 liters milk
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Make a light roux from 16 Deca butter or fat and flour, with finely chopped onion
2. Pour in 2 liters water and cook well
3. Add salt and spices as desired
4. Wash the mushrooms well
5. Sauté them in 6 Deca butter with some green parsley and stew with 0.5 liters milk and vinegar as needed
6. Pour this into the cooked and strained soup
7. Simmer for about 1.5 hours
8. Remove the fat carefully and serve with boiled bread and the mushrooms
9. If mushrooms are not desired in the soup, strain before serving
10. Best mushrooms for soup are champignons, morels, or cultivated varieties

title: Mushrooms with Egg (Schwämme mit Ei)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6-8 medium porcini mushrooms or champignons
- ⅛ liter cream
- 6-8 dkg hot butter
- finely chopped onion
- finely chopped parsley
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the mushrooms, slice thinly or chop
2. In a saucepan, let finely chopped onion and parsley run in 6-8 dkg hot butter
3. Add the mushrooms, let them stew covered for 30-40 minutes, adding cream as needed
4. Beat 1 egg and add it, let it steam through
5. Mushrooms can also be served with fried eggs
6. Mushrooms should not be reheated

title: Muskazient Torte (Nutmeg Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 egg yolks
- 14 dkg sugar
- 5 dkg breadcrumbs
- 4 dkg citron
- 8 dkg orange peel
- 2 ground cloves
- 2 allspice berries
- 1 pinch cinnamon
- lemon zest
- 7 stiff egg whites
- 14 dkg grated almonds

## Directions

1. Beat 7 egg yolks with 14 dkg sugar for 20 minutes
2. Mix in 5 dkg breadcrumbs, 4 dkg citron, 8 dkg orange peel (finely cut), 2 ground cloves, 2 allspice berries, 1 pinch cinnamon, and lemon zest
3. Fold in 7 stiff egg whites together with 14 dkg grated almonds
4. Fill the batter into the torte form and bake
5. Turn out and cut through
6. Spread with jam, dust with sugar or frost white, and decorate with half swelled almonds and angelica

title: Mustard (German-style Mustard)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters of clear wine must
- 8 Deca of sugar
- 16 Deca sweet mustard flour
- 16 Deca bitter mustard flour
- 7 juniper berries
- 5 peppercorns
- 5 grains of Allspice
- 1 clove
- 1 small piece of nutmeg

## Directions

1. In a very clean dish where no iron has ever been, reduce 2 liters of clear wine must to about half its volume, removing the foam that forms
2. Before the must is sufficiently reduced, add 8 Deca of sugar
3. Sift 16 Deca sweet mustard flour and 16 Deca bitter mustard flour through a sieve into a bowl
4. Crush together in a mortar 7 juniper berries, 5 peppercorns, 5 grains of Allspice, 1 clove, and 1 small piece of nutmeg
5. Sift this through the sieve as well and mix well with the mustard flour
6. Make a well in the mixture and slowly pour the hot must while stirring constantly for about half an hour until the mustard becomes thick
7. The mustard should be thin enough to flow easily from a spoon; if not, add more hot must
8. Allow it to cool slightly covered with a cloth in the bowl at a cool, dust-free location
9. On the second day, fill the mustard into jars
10. On the third day, cover with dampened, well-wrung paper covers, bind them, and store in a cool place

title: Mustard Butter (Beurre à la Moutarde)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 10 decagrams white butter
- white pepper
- 1 strong tablespoon French mustard
- spinach thick juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Press the hard-boiled egg yolks into a deep bowl
2. Beat them fluffy with 10 decagrams white butter
3. Season with salt and some white pepper
4. Gradually stir in 1 strong tablespoon of French mustard, adding it bit by bit
5. Some spinach thick juice may also be mixed in

title: Mustard Cucumbers (Mustard-flavored Pickled Cucumbers)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Small green salted cucumbers
- Salt
- 1 teaspoon bitter mustard seeds per jar
- 1 teaspoon sweet mustard seeds per jar
- Vinegar (recipe 1901)

## Directions

1. Peel small green salted cucumbers
2. Cut them in half lengthwise and carefully remove the seeds
3. Salt them well and let them stand covered in an earthenware vessel overnight
4. The next day, spread them on a hair sieve or cloth so they lie on the cut side to drain the water well
5. Pack them tightly in half-liter jars or similar sized containers standing upright lengthwise
6. In each jar add 1 teaspoon bitter mustard seeds and 1 teaspoon sweet mustard seeds
7. Fill with vinegar (recipe 1901)
8. Bind with covers and let them boil for half an hour in a water bath

title: Mustard Powder (Senf Pulver)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds mustard powder
- 0.5 pound ground sugar, or more
- 12 loths cinnamon, ground
- 12 loths ginger, ground
- 12 loths allspice, ground
- 1 quenchen fennel seeds
- Very strong vinegar, fortified by frost
- 1 loth marjoram per measure of vinegar
- 2 loths tarragon herb, dried and chopped
- 1 loth thyme, dried and chopped
- 1 loth sage, dried and chopped
- 2 loths rocambole onions

## Directions

1. To the very strong vinegar fortified by frost, add 1 loth marjoram, 2 loths tarragon herb, thyme and sage (1 loth each), all dried and well chopped, then add 2 loths rocambole onions
2. Let this infuse in the sun for 5 to 6 days, then strain through
3. Take 2 pounds of the mustard powder, 0.5 pound ground sugar or more, cinnamon, ginger, and allspice ground (12 loths of each), and 1 quenchen fennel seeds
4. When well mixed, combine with as much vinegar as necessary to form a paste
5. Store in a suitable place, well sealed
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Muster (Model)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams fine sifted sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 8 decagrams peeled, finely cut almonds
- 8 decagrams almonds cut very finely with a sabre knife
- oil

## Directions

1. Put 16 decagrams fine sifted sugar and juice of 1 lemon in a bowl
2. As soon as the sugar foams yellow, mix with finely cut and sabre-knife-cut almonds
3. Fill oil-coated molds or other forms with this warm mass
4. Press firmly with a whole lemon into the form so that the decoration on the form is firmly impressed into the mass
5. Turn the impressed mass out (knock out) into a bowl and continue in this manner until the mass is used up, keeping it warm throughout

title: Mutton Cutlets à la Minute (Cotelettes de mouton à la minute)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton cutlets
- Butter, fat, or oil
- Salt and pepper
- Paper for papillotes
- Braising sauce or mustard sauce or vegetables of choice

## Directions

1. Cut the cutlets neatly and beat them slightly.
2. Dip in butter, fat, or oil, salt briefly before use.
3. Grill on both sides on a grill grate over strong heat.
4. Cover the bones with papillotes (paper cuffs).
5. Arrange the cutlets neatly on a platter.
6. Place a thick braising or mustard sauce or vegetables of choice in the center.
7. These cutlets can also be given as a garnish with mushroom, truffle, asparagus, or calf's liver preparations.
8. Alternatively, they can be braised in a light (à la minute) pan with little fat.

title: Mutton Hash with Fine Herbs (Hammelshaſchee mit feinen Kraͤutern)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted mutton leg
- Butter
- Shallots, finely chopped
- Mushrooms, finely chopped
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Croutons
- Poached or fried eggs

## Directions

1. Cut out the meat from a roasted mutton leg
2. Remove the skin and any sinews
3. Mince the meat finely with a chopping knife
4. Place a piece of butter in a casserole
5. Lightly pass a portion of finely chopped shallots in it
6. Add finely chopped mushrooms and parsley
7. Season them properly
8. After they have sweated for several minutes, add some flour and bouillon
9. Let it cook completely
10. Finally stir in the chopped meat and mix it well while warm, but it must not boil
11. When dressed, garnish it with croutons and place poached or fried eggs on top
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Mutton Leg à la Seven Hours (Gigot de mouton à la sept heures)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 leg of mutton
- Salt and pepper
- Lard
- Fine table oil
- Soup stock
- Parades sauce
- Raw potatoes, diced

## Directions

1. Prepare as the previous roasted leg, but use soup stock instead of wine and vinegar for deglazing.
2. Make a standard Parades sauce and stir it with the leg's drippings.
3. When the leg is almost completely tender, add diced raw potatoes next to the leg in the pan.
4. Let them braise until soft.
5. Arrange the meat with the potatoes on a platter.
6. The sauce can also be served in a sauceboat.

title: Mutton Leg, Venison-Style (Hammelſchlägel auf wilde Art)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fat and tender mutton leg
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Wine
- Onions
- Coriander
- Lemon
- Butter
- Pepper sauce

## Directions

1. Take a fat and tender mutton leg and beat it well
2. Skin it like venison
3. Lard it
4. Marinate it for several days in vinegar, salt, pepper, nutmeg, wine, onions, coriander, and a lemon with the peel cut off
5. If you need it immediately, heat the marinade and pour it hot over the meat
6. Skewer the mutton leg
7. Baste it with the same marinade and butter which you place in the roasting pan
8. Roast it on the platter
9. Serve it with a pepper sauce or with the drippings from the roasting pan, from which the fat must be skimmed
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Mutton Meat with Turnips (Ragout de mouton aux navets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Neck, breast, and shoulder of mutton
- Bacon
- Onion, cut into leaves
- Flour
- Soup stock
- Bay leaf
- Salt
- White pepper
- Thyme
- White turnips, neatly cut

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces 2 fingers wide.
2. Let these brown with bacon and thinly sliced onion over fairly strong heat.
3. Remove the meat pieces and place in another pan.
4. In the remaining fat, stir in a few spoonfuls of flour and toast.
5. Deglaze with some soup stock.
6. Season with 1 bay leaf, salt, white pepper, and some thyme.
7. Pass through a sieve after boiling.
8. Pour over the meat pieces.
9. Add neatly cut white turnips.

title: Muß von Eyerweiß (Egg White Puree)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- White of five eggs
- Sugar
- One seidel of milk
- Orange water
- Grated lemon
- Sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Take the white of five eggs and whip it with sugar
2. Add one seidel of milk
3. Let it warm together with constant whisking
4. Place it in a bowl
5. Pour some orange water into it
6. Sprinkle grated lemon and sugar over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Muß von Zuckerwurzeln (Salsify Puree)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Salsify roots
- Milk or strong chicken or capon broth
- Egg yolks
- Cinnamon
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Slice the salsify roots in the manner of radishes into slices
2. Simmer them in milk, or preferably in a strong chicken or capon broth until very soft
3. Press it through a linen cloth
4. Add to the pressed mixture a little milk or chicken broth, several egg yolks, cinnamon and sugar to make a thin puree
5. Alternatively, if more broth is used, a soup can be made from it
6. According to circumstances, the spices may also be omitted
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Muß von Zuckerwurzeln (Salsify Purée)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify roots
- milk
- chicken or capon broth
- egg yolks
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut salsify roots into slices like radishes
2. Simmer them in milk or, better yet, in strong chicken or capon broth until very soft
3. Press through a fine cloth
4. Add a little milk or chicken broth to the pressed mixture along with some egg yolks, cinnamon, and sugar
5. The result should be a thin purée
6. More broth can be added to make a soup instead
7. The spices may be omitted depending on circumstances
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Möhrenkoch (Carrot Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow carrots, clean washed and scraped
- 5 eggs
- butter
- 11 eggs
- 10 egg yolks
- softened bread roll (Semmel), soaked and squeezed out
- three-quarters pound grated sugar

## Directions

1. Grate the clean washed and scraped yellow carrots finely on a grater without first soaking them in water
2. In a casserole, stir them completely dry, then beat in 5 eggs and continue stirring until completely dry throughout
3. Stir them smoothly in a mixing bowl (Weidling), add some butter that has been melted over the fire, as well as 11 eggs, and stir well together
4. Beat in 10 egg yolks, the soaked and squeezed out bread roll, and three-quarters pound grated sugar
5. Stir everything together diligently for a quarter hour
6. Pour the batter into the prepared bowl and ring mold, place it in the oven or torte pan, and let it bake slowly
7. Serve it warm or cold at table, although it is better eaten warm
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Mürbeter Teig (Shortcrust Pastry)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 Deca fine flour
- 42 Deca cold, firm butter
- 3/4 teaspoon of salt
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter of cream or milk cream
- 1/4 liter of white wine

## Directions

1. Spread 56 Deca fine flour on a board.
2. Cut 42 Deca cold, firm butter into small pieces with a knife, add 3/4 teaspoon of salt and rub the butter together with the flour using both hands.
3. Make a depression in the middle, add 4 egg yolks, 1/4 liter of cream or milk cream and 1/4 liter of white wine.
4. Mix with a knife and work with both hands as quickly as possible into a smooth dough until bubbles are visible.
5. Place on a flour-dusted tray, cover with cloth and let rest at a cold place for about 1 hour.
6. Further treatment of rolling out and folding is evident from the description of 'Butterteig feinster Art' (No. 1045).
7. Use for pastries, croustades and the like.

title: Nabel's Rice (Nabels Reis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 tablespoons rice
- 1 liter milk
- 16 tablespoons sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 3 tablespoons hot water
- juice of 1 orange
- juice of 1 lemon
- 3 egg whites
- 8 tablespoons vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Stir 24 tablespoons rice into 1 liter of boiling milk and let it slowly steam until soft and the milk is absorbed
2. Meanwhile, brown 16 tablespoons sugar in 2 tablespoons butter until golden
3. Add 3 tablespoons hot water, the juice of 1 orange and 1 lemon
4. Bring once to a boil
5. Pour this into the cooked rice and mix well
6. Let it steam for a few minutes
7. Set the rice aside and stir in 3 tablespoons rum after it has cooled completely
8. Butter a serving dish
9. Layer the rice with hot marmalade, applesauce or compote
10. Repeat until the dish is full
11. Cover with stiff egg white snow mixed with 8 tablespoons vanilla sugar
12. Dust with sugar and bake in an open oven for about 10 to 15 minutes
13. Remove the dish and serve immediately

title: Napoleon's Torte (Tourte à la Napoléon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca beaten butter
- 16 deca finely ground sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 16 deca almonds, finely ground with skin and passed
- 16 deca grated

## Directions

1. Beat 16 deca butter gradually with 16 deca finely ground sugar and 4 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
2. Add 16 deca finely ground and passed almonds and 16 deca grated

title: Napoleons Torte (Napoleon's Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca beaten butter
- 16 deca finely ground sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 16 deca finely ground blanched almonds with skin
- 16 deca grated chocolate
- 1 knife tip finely chopped lemon and orange peel
- equal amount cinnamon
- 1 ground clove
- 4 egg whites
- 8 deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat 16 deca of beaten butter gradually with 16 deca of finely ground sugar and 4 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
2. Add 16 deca of finely ground blanched almonds with skin, 16 deca of grated chocolate, 1 knife tip of finely chopped lemon and orange peel, equal amount of cinnamon, and 1 ground clove, stir well
3. Lightly mix in the snow of 4 egg whites with 8 deca of flour
4. Divide this mass into 3 parts, fill each into buttered and floured cake forms and bake 1/2 to 3/4 hours in a moderately hot oven
5. Place the layers on a sieve and after complete cooling, stack them together with jam or cream spread between them and cover with white glaze

title: Natronguglhupf (Soda Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg butter
- 14 dkg sugar
- 4 whole eggs
- 28 dkg flour
- lemon zest
- ½ aborted, lukewarm milk
- 1 spoonful rum
- 5 grams baking soda
- 10 grams cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Beat the butter for 15 minutes
2. Gradually mix in 14 dkg sugar and 4 whole eggs
3. Mix in spoonfuls of 28 dkg flour, lemon zest, ½ boiled, lukewarm milk and 1 spoonful rum
4. Mix 5 grams baking soda and 10 grams cream of tartar together
5. Lightly fold this mixture into the batter
6. Immediately fill into the mold and bake at sharper heat

title: Naturally Braised Tenderloin (Filet de Boeuf Naturel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Tenderloin, the piece between the spine and the flank
- Salt and pepper
- Lard from the tenderloin or bacon sheets
- Lard or bacon strips for larding

## Directions

1. Remove the bone and thick skin from the tenderloin first, then cut off all fat
2. With a small sharp knife, cut between the second very thin skin and the meat and, under gentle movement while holding the blade against the meat, separate the skin paper-thin from the meat from one end to the other
3. After salting and peppering, lard the smoothly skinned side, more or less heavily according to taste, with equally long, needle-like cut, salted lard in regular rows
4. Line a casserole or pan with lard from the tenderloin or bacon sheets, place the tenderloin and the separated skin on it and place lard or bacon on top
5. Braise in its own juice with frequent basting

title: Naturgebratener Rehschlägel (Roasted Young Roe Deer Leg)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Young roe deer leg
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Paper for wrapping
- Glace with piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Skin the leg and rub it with salt and pepper, then spit it
2. Wrap it with 2 sheets of paper brushed with butter and tie it
3. Place on the spit and roast for 1 hour at medium-strong heat
4. Half an hour before serving, remove the paper, increase the flame so the meat takes on a beautiful brown color
5. When serving, cover the bone with a paper frill and serve with some glace with piquant sauce
6. Alternatively, if roasting in the oven, put some fat in the casserole, lay the meat on 2 tablespoons, cover the top with bacon slices, roast for approximately 1⅓ hours and baste it with its own juices
7. Cut as desired and add the strained juice
8. Garnish with orange or lemon slices and serve with compote or sweet salad

title: Naturschnitzel (Natural Veal Cutlet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-80 dekagrams veal leg
- flour
- salt
- fat

## Directions

1. Remove skin and bones from the meat and cut into not-too-thin cutlets.
2. Lightly pound the cutlets.
3. 15 minutes before serving, salt them.
4. Coat one side with flour.
5. Place flour-side down into a flat pan with hot fat.
6. Brown quickly on both sides and remove to a plate to keep warm.
7. Once all cutlets are done, drain the fat.
8. Add a piece of butter to the pan and brown it.
9. Deglaze with a little broth or water and bring to a boil.
10. Arrange the cutlets on a long platter and pour the strained sauce over them.

title: Naturschnitzels (Natural Veal Cutlets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin, finger-thick pieces
- Flour
- Fat
- Brown soup
- Butter
- Soup glace (optional)
- Lemon
- Salad or potato pancake

## Directions

1. Cut finger-thick pieces from veal loin in a thin, layered manner
2. Pound with a moistened wooden mallet
3. Salt them, and pepper if desired
4. Dip both sides in flour
5. Place in a shallow pan with finger-high, very hot fat
6. When the bottom is light brown, turn the cutlets with a fork and brown the second side
7. Place finished cutlets in a casserole containing brown soup and a small piece of butter
8. Keep covered until all cutlets are browned
9. Arrange neatly on a serving dish
10. Remove fat from the pan, bring the remaining sauce to a boil with some brown soup
11. Pour this sauce over the cutlets
12. If dissolved soup glace is available, it may be spread over the cutlets
13. Serve with thinly sliced lemon pieces and salad or potato pancake

title: Neapolitan Tenderloin Roast (Filet de boeuf à la napolitaine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- A tenderloin roast, freed of bones and sinews, salted and peppered
- Fat for coating
- Large raw potatoes
- Butter or fat for roasting
- Brown sauce
- Capers or olives
- Truffles in Madeira wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Coat a tenderloin roast freed of bones and sinews with fat and braise it quickly in a very hot oven for 15 minutes.
2. Cut it into slices as thick as a finger, and cut equally sized pieces from very large raw peeled potatoes.
3. Roast the potato pieces in butter or fat.
4. Coat a shallow casserole with butter, lay a piece of tenderloin on it, then a piece of potato, then another piece of tenderloin, arranging them so that half of each piece covers half of the piece beneath it, continuing until all is arranged.
5. Pour brown sauce over it so it covers everything, and place it in the oven for a quarter hour before serving.
6. Braise it and arrange it on a round dish in a crown shape, and pour the sauce over it.
7. If desired, sprinkle with whole capers or olives cut into pieces and freed from pits.
8. The whole dish can also be poured over with truffles cut into leaves and cooked in Madeira wine at the time of serving.

title: Nebrückheit (Almond Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces butter
- 24 ounces finely ground sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 12 ounces blanched and ground almonds
- 12 ounces grated chocolate
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped lemon peel
- 6 egg whites
- 8 ounces breadcrumbs or dark breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Beat 16 ounces butter until fluffy
2. Gradually stir in 24 ounces finely ground sugar and 8 egg yolks for 1 hour
3. Mix in 12 ounces blanched and ground almonds, 12 ounces grated chocolate, and 1 teaspoon finely chopped lemon peel
4. Gently fold in the stiffly beaten snow from 6 egg whites with 8 ounces breadcrumbs or dark breadcrumbs
5. Fill into a buttered and floured Nebrücken mold and bake in a moderate oven for 3/4 hour
6. Turn out the baked cake onto a sieve and after complete cooling, cover with chocolate glaze
7. When the glaze is completely dry, pierce the cake lengthwise into 3 strips with neatly cut pistachios or blanched almonds cut the same way, and blanched, peeled and similarly cut pistachios, using a pastry needle to insert them so that two-thirds protrude

title: Neu et Pice (marinierter) Lungenbraten (Beef Tenderloin à la Marinière)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tenderloin
- 1/2 liter water
- 1/2 liter vinegar
- 1 piece onion
- 1 finger-length piece yellow turnip and parsley
- 1 small piece celery
- spice grains
- 10 crushed juniper berries
- 1 bay leaf
- 5 sprigs thyme
- fat
- white paper

## Directions

1. After removing the veins, fat and skin from a beef tenderloin, spit it unsalted
2. Prepare a marinade: 1/2 liter water, 1/2 liter vinegar, 1 piece onion, 1 finger-length piece yellow turnip and parsley, 1 small piece celery, all cut into thin slices, 5 grains of each type of spice, 10 crushed juniper berries, 1 bay leaf, 5 sprigs thyme
3. Boil this for approximately 1/2 hour
4. Place the tenderloin with the removed fat and detached skin in an earthen or wooden dish and pour the marinade, which must be completely cooled, over it so the liquid stands above the meat
5. Brush a sheet of white paper well with fat and cover the tenderloin with it, then also place a lid on top
6. Set the meat in a cool place and turn it the next day, but on the third day remove it from the marinade, boil the marinade and pour it cold over the tenderloin again

title: Nieren mit saurer Sauce (Veal Kidneys with Piquant Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- horseradish, finely sliced
- vinegar
- water
- butter
- flour
- sour cream
- roasted kidneys

## Directions

1. Finely sliced horseradish is well cooked in vinegar and water.
2. Then pass it through a sieve, pour this over a roux made from butter and flour, and let it simmer with sour cream.
3. Shortly before serving, add roasted kidneys, stir everything well together and serve on a platter garnished with fried eggs or poached eggs.

title: Nierenbraten (Kidney Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 120-140 dkg kidney roast
- 5 dkg hot fat
- water
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash the meat and salt it
2. Place skin-side down in 5 dkg hot fat and brown
3. Add a little water and roast in the oven at moderate heat for 1½-2 hours, basting frequently with its own juices and adding water as needed
4. Turn when the top is brown
5. Bring the sauce to a boil with a piece of butter
6. Slice the meat, chop through, and place on a long platter
7. Top with kidney sliced separately into slices and serve with the sauce

title: Nierenkoch (Kidney Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole kidney
- the soft part of a small roll soaked in milk
- one-half quarter pound butter
- one whole egg and two yolks
- finely chopped lemon zest
- fresh parsley
- butter for the sheet
- sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Chop one whole kidney and the soft part of a small roll that has been soaked in milk and squeezed out
2. Beat one-half quarter pound butter until very fluffy
3. Beat in one whole egg and two yolks
4. Add finely chopped lemon zest and fresh parsley
5. Stir everything together well
6. Make a rim on the sheet and spread both with butter
7. Put the kidney mixture into it
8. Apply heat both above and below
9. Bake it until golden brown and dust with sugar
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Nikus
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 bottles Pontac
- 1 seidel water
- 2 lemons
- 1 pound sugar
- Grated nutmeg
- 3 loths Bishop essence

## Directions

1. Put 2 bottles Pontac, 1 seidel water, the rubbed-off zest and juice of 2 lemons, 1 pound sugar and a little grated nutmeg in a tureen.
2. Set on charcoal fire until a white foam appears.
3. Add 3 loths of Bishop essence.
4. Let it steep a while longer.
5. Serve in customary punch glasses.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Nikus (Citrus Punch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 bottles Pontac
- 1 seidel water
- distilled substance and juice from 2 lemons
- 1 pound sugar
- a little grated nutmeg
- 3 loth bishop's essence

## Directions

1. Place 2 bottles Pontac, 1 seidel water, the distilled substance and juice from 2 lemons, 1 pound sugar and a little grated nutmeg in a terrine on coals
2. Heat until a white foam appears
3. Add 3 loth bishop's essence
4. Let it simmer a little longer
5. Serve in ordinary punch glasses
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Nockerl (Potato Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dkg butter
- 1 egg
- salt
- 15 dkg cooked, passed, cooled potatoes
- 4-6 dkg flour
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. To the beaten butter, stir in 1 egg, salt, the cooked and cooled potatoes, and flour.
2. Form small dumplings with a spoon.
3. Place them in hot lard and fry until light brown.
4. Drain and serve warm separately.

title: Noix de Veau au Risi-Bisi (Braised Veal Knuckle with Rice and Peas)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 braised veal knuckle
- risi-bisi (peas with rice)

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal knuckle as previously described, using risi-bisi instead of macaroni

title: Noix de Veau aux Macaronis (Braised Veal Knuckle with Macaroni)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 braised veal knuckle
- macaroni with Parmesan sauce or browned noodles

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal knuckle as in recipe 585, but place macaroni with Parmesan sauce or browned noodles in the center of the platter instead of onion purée

title: Noix de Veau aux Truffes (Braised Veal Knuckle with Truffles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilo truffles
- butter
- brown gravy
- juice of 1 lemon
- several spoonfuls Madeira wine
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Peel the truffles and cut them into knife-blade-thick slices
2. Braise these slices in butter for a few minutes
3. Add brown gravy, juice of 1 lemon, and several spoonfuls of Madeira wine, season to taste, stir well
4. Add this mixture to the veal knuckle as described in recipe 585

title: Noix de Veau à l'Étuvée (Braised Veal Knuckle with Lard)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal knuckle
- salt
- lard in pencil-thick, half-finger-length pieces
- lard, thinly sliced
- 12 deca of raw ham, as desired
- soup stock
- butter or Béarnaise sauce

## Directions

1. After removing the skin from the veal knuckle, salt it and thread it with pieces of lard
2. Place thinly sliced lard and as much raw ham as desired in a casserole, lay the knuckle on top, pour some soup stock over it, and let it braise gently covered
3. Remove the lard from the cooking liquid, then bind a lighter butter or Béarnaise sauce with the cooking liquid

title: Noix de Veau à l'Étuvée (Braised Veal Knuckle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal knuckle
- salt
- lard in thin strips
- shallots, sliced thin
- 4 bay leaves
- pepper
- butter mixed with cream for basting
- flour
- cream
- butter
- lemon peel, finely cut

## Directions

1. Salt the unsalted veal knuckle and let it marinate for 2-3 days, pouring off and reboiling the brine twice daily, always pouring it back cold over the knuckle, which should be turned daily
2. After this time, place the knuckle on a spit and roast it frequently, basting with a sauce mixed with cream
3. In a saucepan on the stove, place some of the brine and add the drippings from the roasting knuckle, letting it come to a boil
4. Gradually whisk flour with a few spoonfuls of cream into the sauce until it is smooth
5. Add butter and finely cut lemon peel to the boiling sauce and let it come to a boil
6. Cut the knuckle into desired pieces, arrange them on a platter so that the knuckle retains its original form, and serve with the sauce
7. Alternatively, the knuckle can be roasted in the oven, which is preferable

title: Noix de Veau à la Purée (Braised Veal Knuckle with Purée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 braised veal knuckle
- onion purée or other purée as desired

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal knuckle as in the previous recipe, then cut it into desired pieces
2. Arrange these pieces in a circle on a platter and place the chosen purée in the center

title: Noodle Casserole (Nudelauflauf)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 dkg flour
- 2 eggs
- salt
- ¼ liter milk
- 5 dkg butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 5 dkg sugar
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from flour, eggs, and salt
2. Cut into soup noodles
3. Bring ¼ liter milk to a boil in a pot and cook the noodles until thick, then cool
4. Cream 5 dkg butter together with 3 egg yolks and 5 dkg sugar
5. Fold in the cooled noodles and the beaten egg whites
6. Pour into a well-buttered casserole form
7. Bake at moderate heat for 30-45 minutes
8. Serve dusted with sugar in the casserole

title: Noodles with Loin and Fat (Nudeln mit Lenden und Spett)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound of loin, prepared as faded noodles (No. 1201)
- 32 tablespoons of strained mushrooms
- 8 tablespoons of finely diced, golden-roasted bacon

## Directions

1. Prepare faded noodles from 1/2 pound of loin
