2. Add 32 tablespoons of strained mushrooms and mix well
3. Arrange heaped on a platter
4. Pour 8 tablespoons of finely diced, golden-roasted bacon over the noodles
5. Serve hot

title: Noodles with Nuts (Nudeln mit Nüssen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles prepared as in 'Noodles with Poppy' (No. 1204)
- finely ground nuts
- sugar or breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare noodles as described in 'Noodles with Poppy' (No. 1204)
2. Instead of honey and poppy, add finely ground nuts mixed with sugar or breadcrumbs

title: Noodles with Poppy (Nudeln mit Mohn)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles prepared as faded noodles (No. 1201)
- 1/8 liter of finely ground poppy seed
- sugar as needed
- honey as desired

## Directions

1. Prepare noodles as described in faded noodles (No. 1201)
2. Mix 1/8 liter of finely ground poppy seed with the necessary sugar
3. Stir the noodles with honey as desired
4. Arrange heaped on a platter and serve hot

title: Noodles with Semolina (Nudeln mit Grieß)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg spoonfuls of semolina
- 12 tablespoons of hot butter
- 1 egg spoonful of cold water or milk
- salt to taste
- noodles prepared as described in 'Faded Noodles' (No. 1201)

## Directions

1. Toast 2 egg spoonfuls of semolina in 12 tablespoons of hot butter for a short time
2. Mix in 1 egg spoonful of cold water or milk thoroughly
3. Add the drained noodles that have been rinsed with cold water to the semolina
4. Season to taste and toast with frequent stirring
5. Arrange on a platter and serve hot
6. Alternatively, the noodles can be browned with breadcrumbs: toast 8 tablespoons of breadcrumbs in 16 tablespoons of hot butter, add the noodles, and stir frequently while heating

title: Nougat-Strudel (Nougat Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Strudel dough made from 1/2 liter flour
- nougat

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough from 1/2 liter flour and let it rest for 3-4 hours
2. Stretch it thin as indicated for Lung Strudel (No. 75)
3. Brush with nougat
4. Sprinkle with bread crumbs
5. Roll dough lightly together
6. Treat further as for Ham Strudel (No. 1229)

title: Nubel-Croquettes (Vermicelli Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Noodle dough made from 1 egg
- 0.5 liter boiling milk
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 3 egg yolks
- Sour milk
- Salt
- Flour for dusting
- Eggs for dipping
- Breadcrumbs
- Hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. If noodle dough has been made from 1 egg, roll it out finely and cut into fine noodles
2. Place these in 0.5 liter boiling milk with 2 tablespoons butter and let them cook thick covered, then set aside
3. Beat 3 egg yolks with a few spoonfuls of sour milk and pour into the cooked, cooled noodles
4. Season with salt and mix well
5. Place this mixture on a flour-dusted board and after completely cooling, form small sausages 2 fingers thick and 1 finger long from it
6. Dip these in beaten eggs, roll in breadcrumbs and fry in hot lard
7. When they have a nice golden color, remove with a slotted spoon and place on blotting paper
8. Serve these croquettes as garnish for boiled beef or roasted lungs
9. They can also be served as a flour dish

title: Nudel-Suppe (Noodle Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- flour to accommodate 2 eggs
- 2 eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a well in flour that holds 2 eggs
2. Break eggs into well, add salt
3. Work dough with both hands or a wide knife until smooth
4. Cut into pieces and form into small strips
5. Let rest and dry covered
6. Roll very finely like paper
7. When dried, cut into 2-finger-wide strips, stack, and cut into fine noodles
8. Drop into boiling soup and cook 8-10 minutes
9. Serve soup
10. For noodles used as garnish rather than in soup, roll dough only knife-back thick and cut so noodles are round

title: Nudeln (Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dkg flour
- 3 eggs
- salt
- 5 dkg hot butter or fat

## Directions

1. Make a dough from flour, eggs, and salt (see noodle dough)
2. Cut into wide noodles
3. Cook in plenty of boiling salted water for 5 minutes
4. Drain and cool with cold water
5. Place in a casserole with 5 dkg hot butter or fat
6. Season with salt and warm in the oven

title: Nudeln mit Griess (Noodles with Semolina)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles
- 2 tablespoons semolina
- 12 decagrams hot butter
- 1 tablespoon cold water or milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the noodles as described in 'Abgescbmalgene Nudeln'
2. Lightly roast 2 tablespoons semolina in 12 decagrams hot butter
3. Mix well with 1 tablespoon cold water or milk
4. Add the drained noodles that have been rinsed with cold water to the semolina
5. Salt to taste and roast while stirring often
6. Arrange on a dish and serve

title: Nuss-Böusserln (Nut Kisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca ordinary pounded nuts
- 10 deca fine pounded, blanched hazelnuts
- 1 pinch grated coffee
- 16 deca fine pounded sugar
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix 16 deca ordinary pounded nuts, 10 deca fine pounded blanched hazelnuts, 1 pinch grated coffee, and 16 deca fine pounded sugar together well
2. Lightly fold in the whipped whites of 2 eggs
3. Using a teaspoon, place nut-sized pieces on a baking sheet covered with wafers at finger-width intervals, marked with the handle of a second teaspoon
4. Sprinkle with fine pounded sugar
5. Dry slowly in a cool oven
6. When the kisses are dry outside and soft inside, cut them from the wafers

title: Nuss-Kochli (Walnut Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka butter, beaten
- 16 Deka sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 15 Deka fine ground common walnuts
- Equal amount of fine ground and sieved hazelnuts
- 1 knife-tip grated nutmeg
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat butter gradually with sugar and egg yolks
2. Mix in fine ground common walnuts and fine ground and sieved hazelnuts with grated nutmeg
3. Lightly mix in the snow from 8 egg whites
4. Fill this mixture into a butter-brushed, flour-dusted mold
5. Let it cook covered for ¾ to 1 hour in steam or bake in the oven
6. Turn out onto a plate and serve with sauce of choice
7. Can serve, for example, water-chocolate sauce in one saucière and whipped cream in another, serving the souffle with both

title: Nuss-Roulade (Nut Roll)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 16 drams sugar
- 6 egg whites
- 14 drams flour
- white wax for greasing
- tissue paper
- hazelnut filling or candied peel filling
- breadcrumbs
- 3 eggs for brushing
- pearl sugar for sprinkling
- butter for the hot plate

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg yolks with 16 drams sugar for 1/2 hour
2. Lightly fold in the stiffly beaten snow of 6 egg whites with 14 drams flour
3. Grease a warm baking sheet with white wax, wipe lightly with tissue paper
4. Spread the batter the thickness of a knife back on the sheet
5. Bake at low heat until pale yellow
6. Remove from the baking sheet with a thin long knife
7. Brush with hazelnut filling or dissolved candied peel, and if using the latter, sprinkle with breadcrumbs
8. Fold the dough 2 fingers wide on the longer side, brush with the same filling, then roll up completely
9. Brush with beaten eggs, sprinkle with pearl sugar, and dry for a few more minutes in the oven on the hot plate
10. Cool completely and cut into pieces of desired size

title: Nuss-Strudel (Nut Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Strudel dough
- melted butter
- finely ground nuts
- bread crumbs soaked in milk
- finely ground sugar
- 2 egg yolks, stirred with a little cold cream
- honey

## Directions

1. Brush stretched strudel dough with melted butter
2. Sprinkle with finely ground nuts
3. Sprinkle with bread crumbs soaked in milk and finely ground sugar
4. Sprinkle spoonwise with 2 egg yolks stirred with a little cold cream and a little honey
5. Roll dough together
6. Treat further as for Ham Strudel (No. 1229)

title: Nusskuchen (Nut Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 14 dkg sugar
- 14 dkg flour
- 10 dkg grated nuts
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 2 whole eggs, 2 egg yolks, 14 dkg sugar, and 14 dkg flour into the beaten 10 dkg butter.
2. Fill the mass into the buttered, floured small cake form.
3. Stir 10 dkg sugar, 10 dkg grated nuts, and 2 egg whites into a paste.
4. Spread this on the dough and bake slowly.
5. Serve with coffee or tea.

title: Nussroulade (Nut Roll)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 4 egg yolks
- 11 dekagrams sugar
- 5 dekagrams grated nuts, hazelnuts or almonds
- 5 dekagrams flour
- buttered paper

## Directions

1. Stir the 4 egg yolks, 11 dekagrams sugar, 5 dekagrams grated nuts into the stiff snow of 4 egg whites and lightly fold in 5 dekagrams flour
2. Line a baking sheet with buttered paper
3. Spread the mixture finger-high on it
4. Bake at moderate heat until light brown
5. Turn out onto a board, quickly remove the paper
6. Spread the filling on it, quickly roll up the roulade, sprinkle with sugar, chill immediately and later cut on the diagonal

title: Nussroulade Filling (Nut Roll Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- whipped cream
- 8-12 dekagrams grated nuts, hazelnuts or almonds
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Mix the stiffly whipped cream with 8-12 dekagrams grated nuts, hazelnuts or almonds and sugar to taste

title: Nussschnitte (Nut Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram nuts
- 1/2 liter whipped cream
- 7 dekagrams sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 7 dekagrams grated nuts
- 2 egg whites
- 4 dekagrams breadcrumbs
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Cream together 7 dekagrams sugar with 2 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs until foamy
2. Stir in 7 dekagrams grated nuts and with 2 egg whites at the same time 4 dekagrams breadcrumbs
3. Spread the mixture finger-thick on a buttered, floured baking sheet
4. Bake at moderate heat until light brown
5. Cut into larger rectangles while still warm and cool
6. Join every 2 together with sugared whipped cream, either sprinkle with sugar or coat with white glaze and decorate with half nuts

title: Nussstipperl (Nut Twists)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 pieces shelled nuts
- sugar
- 20 pieces pounded hazelnuts or almonds
- 1 tablespoon breadcrumbs
- 1/8 liter boiling milk or cream
- grated coffee or finely cut lemon peel as desired

## Directions

1. Prepare as Mohnstipperl but use nut filling instead of poppy seed
2. Pound shelled nuts with sugar
3. Mix with pounded hazelnuts or almonds and breadcrumbs
4. Pour 1/8 liter boiling milk or cream over mixture
5. Sweeten to taste, mix well and set aside cold
6. Optionally add grated coffee or finely cut lemon peel

title: Nut Boats Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg grated nuts
- 5 dkg sugar
- 2 dkg fine breadcrumbs
- 2 spoons honey
- pinch cinnamon
- 3 dkg grated chocolate
- 2-3 spoons hot milk

## Directions

1. Mix 10 dkg grated nuts, 5 dkg sugar, 2 dkg fine breadcrumbs, 2 spoons honey, a pinch of cinnamon, 3 dkg grated chocolate and 2-3 spoons hot milk to a thick porridge

title: Nut Pastry Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 spoons milk
- 7 dkg vanilla sugar
- 4 dkg grated chocolate
- 14 dkg grated nuts
- pinch cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook 4 spoons milk with 7 dkg vanilla sugar, 4 dkg grated chocolate, 14 dkg grated nuts and a pinch of cinnamon in a small pot for a few minutes
2. Let it cool

title: Nuts Strudel von Germteig (Nut Yeast Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- yeast dough as per recipe for Mocha Strudel 1256
- 8 deca nuts
- 8 deca roasted and ground hazelnuts
- 16 deca finely sifted sugar
- 1 pinch grated coffee

## Directions

1. Prepare yeast dough as specified for Mocha Strudel (No. 1256)
2. Prepare the nut filling: pound nuts very finely and mix with roasted ground hazelnuts, finely sifted sugar, and a pinch of grated coffee
3. Apply the nut filling to the strudel dough as specified for Poppy Seed Strudel

title: Nuß-Branntwein (Walnut Brandy)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter good brandy
- 9-13 halved green walnuts
- 1 piece clove
- 1 very small piece cinnamon
- 5 pieces juniper berries
- 1/2 liter water
- 16 tea cups sugar
- optional: some vanilla

## Directions

1. Place 1 liter good brandy in a bottle
2. Add 9-13 halved green walnuts (this must always be done before the end of June)
3. Add 1 piece clove, 1 very small piece cinnamon and 5 pieces juniper berries to the bottle
4. Seal well and let stand approximately 3 weeks, shaking the bottle frequently during this time (it can also stand in the sun)
5. After this time, strain the brandy into another bottle and dilute the drained liquid with sugar water if desired
6. To make the sugar water: boil 1/2 liter water with 16 tea cups sugar and optional vanilla for about 1/4 hour, cool and mix with the drained brandy
7. Pour brandy again over the walnuts remaining in the bottle

title: Nußbäckerei (Nut Pastry Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 17 dkg flour
- 14 dkg butter
- 7 dkg sugar
- 7 dkg grated nuts

## Directions

1. On the board, make a dough from the flour, butter, sugar and grated nuts
2. Roll out and cut into desired shapes
3. Place on an unbuttered baking sheet
4. Bake at moderate oven heat
5. Sprinkle the pastry with sugar

title: Nußfülle (Nut Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- grated nuts
- milk
- 3 dkg sugar
- lemon zest
- grated chocolate
- 3 spoons honey

## Directions

1. Cook the grated nuts with milk, 3 dkg sugar, lemon zest, grated chocolate and 3 spoons honey for 15-20 minutes, stirring frequently
2. Cool and serve

title: Nußkrapferl (Nut Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dkg flour
- 3 dkg sugar
- 7 dkg butter
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Knead the flour, sugar, butter and egg yolk on the board to a dough
2. After resting, roll out
3. Cut into rounds
4. Place on an unbuttered baking sheet
5. Bake at higher heat until light brown
6. Join 2 pieces together with filling
7. Top with ½ nut or sprinkle with sugar unglazed

title: Nußkugeln (Nut Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg cube sugar
- water
- 1/2 kg grated nuts
- 1/2 kg raw grated almonds

## Directions

1. Cook 15 dkg cube sugar with a small scoop of water to a thick consistency
2. Pour over the 1/2 kg grated nuts and 1/2 kg raw grated almonds mixed with part of the sugar
3. Stir together
4. Form balls with wet hands
5. Roll in grated chocolate
6. Dry them
7. Serve in paper cups

title: Nußnudeln (Nut Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg nuts
- 30 dkg flour
- 3 eggs
- salt
- 8 dkg hot butter
- 5 dkg sugar

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from 30 dkg flour, 3 eggs, and salt
2. Cut into wide noodles
3. Place in boiling salted water and cook for 5 minutes
4. Drain and pour cold water over them
5. Place in a casserole with 8 dkg hot butter
6. Heat in the oven and arrange on a round platter
7. Mix the grated nuts with 5 dkg sugar and sprinkle over the noodles

title: Nußschifferl (Nut Boats)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg flour
- 14 dkg butter
- 5 dkg sugar
- 14 dkg raw grated almonds
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Make a dough on the board from the flour, butter, sugar, raw grated almonds and 1 egg
2. After resting, form a roll
3. Cut equal-sized pieces from it
4. Press a piece of dough into a mold to form it
5. Fill the mold
6. Cover it with a sheet of dough cut to the shape
7. Place on a baking sheet
8. Bake the mass until light brown
9. After cooling, turn out the mold
10. Decorate the boat with ½ nut
11. Sprinkle with sugar

title: Nußtorte (Nut Torte)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 10 dkg grated nuts
- 6 egg whites
- 6 dkg flour
- 6 dkg melted butter
- filling made from 15 dkg grated nuts, 2 spoons sugar, some spoons milk, some rum
- white glaze
- candied angelica stems, candied cherries, halved nuts for decoration

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar with egg yolks for 30 minutes
2. Mix in the grated nuts
3. Gently fold in the egg whites together with the flour
4. Stir in the melted but not hot butter
5. Fill the batter into a buttered and floured cake form and bake slowly over moderate heat
6. Bake the day before serving
7. The next day, turn it over and cut in half
8. Spread the filling on one layer, place the smooth layer on top, spread filling on the edge and coat the torte with white glaze
9. When dry, decorate with angelica stems, candied cherries and halved nuts
10. For the filling: mix grated nuts with sugar, some spoons of milk and some rum into a thick paste (whipped cream can be used instead of milk)

title: Nüdel oder Eferseln (Noodle Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 liter boiling milk
- 12 teaspoons butter
- 12 teaspoons vanilla sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Prepare a noodle dough from 2 eggs and roll it thin, cut into noodles or eferl
2. Cook in 1/2 liter of boiling milk and let it cook briefly
3. Add 12 teaspoons of butter, stir well and set aside
4. Meanwhile, beat 12 teaspoons of vanilla sugar and 4 egg yolks for 1/4 hour, add the cooled mass, stir well together and finally lightly fold in the snow from 4 egg whites
5. Proceed as with the 'Brick Soufflé' (No. 1375)

title: Nürnberger Schnaps von Kräutern (Nuremberg Herb Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Quentchen each of flowers of Brandoſt (Origanum vulgare), wormwood buds, rosemary leaves, sage leaves, strawberry leaves and roots, betony leaves (Betonica officinalis), leaves and flowers of milk thistle (Carduus Mariam), red rose petals, lichen saxatilis
- 1 Quentchen each of the root of iris (Iris german.), calamus root, white Diptuna, and red gentian

## Directions

1. Gather these plants around St. John's Day
2. Clean and wash them
3. Allow the brandy to infuse with them for 14 days in moderate sun or oven heat
4. A second infusion can be made
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Nüsse (Walnuts)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- walnuts picked before St. John's (end of June)
- fresh water
- 1 litre water
- 60 decilitres sugar
- nutmeg
- vanilla
- cloves
- mace

## Directions

1. Pick walnuts before St. John's (end of June) from the tree
2. Pierce each piece five times with a skewer at the stem
3. Place in fresh water and soak for 12 days, changing the water daily, until they become completely black
4. Place in a casserole with hot water and scald for ½ hour
5. Drain and place in cold water and then in an earthen pot
6. Boil 1 litre water, 60 decilitres sugar and, wrapped in a cloth bundle, some nutmeg, vanilla, cloves and mace for ½ hour
7. Pour this liquid hot over the walnuts and let them stand overnight
8. The next day pour off the liquid, add 12 decilitres sugar, let it boil, pour it cold over the walnuts and repeat this for 3 days
9. On the 5th day cook the walnuts with the sugar once, thoroughly finished, fill in preserving jars where the liquid must completely cover the walnuts, bind and cook as the Muscatel pears (Nr. 1842) for 3-4 hours in steam

title: Obere-Gefrorenes mit Früchten (Cream Ice with Fruits)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared as Erdbeeren-Gefrorenes mit Eiern und Obere
- fresh raspberries
- currants
- pitted and sieved cherries
- maraschino cherries
- peaches
- other fruits
- juice of 2 lemons
- 2 deca more sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same way as Cream Ice with Strawberries (Recipe 1732) using fresh raspberries, currants, pitted and sieved cherries, maraschino cherries, peaches and all other fruits along with the juice of 2 lemons, whereby one can add 2 deca more sugar

title: Obere-Gefrorenes mit gerösteten Mandeln (Cream Ice with Roasted Almonds)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca peeled almonds
- 8 deca sugar
- 1/8 liter cream
- 1 liter good cream
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 deca sugar

## Directions

1. Toast 16 deca peeled almonds with 8 deca sugar until yellow
2. Pound them very finely with 1/8 liter cream and heat this with 1 liter good cream until boiling
3. Let cool covered and press through cloth
4. Add this liquid to 8 egg yolks and 16 deca sugar in a saucepan and proceed further as with Vanilla Ice (Recipe 1720)

title: Obere-Gefrorenes mit Haselnüssen (Hazelnut Cream Ice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca hazelnuts
- 12 deca hazelnut kernels
- 3/4 liter cream
- 1 1/4 liter cream
- 1 roasted coffee bean
- 8 egg yolks
- 20 deca fine sugar

## Directions

1. Pound 12 deca hazelnuts and 12 deca hazelnut kernels very finely with 3/4 liter cream
2. Add to 1 1/4 liter cream in a saucepan and stir over heat until the mixture is very hot, then add 1 roasted coffee bean and let cool covered
3. In a saucepan beat 8 egg yolks with 20 deca fine sugar
4. Press the cream through cloth and gradually add to the eggs, beating over heat until it begins to thicken, but it must not boil
5. Treat further as Vanilla Ice (Recipe 1720)

title: Obere-Gefrorenes mit Kastanien (Chestnut Cream Ice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca roasted or boiled peeled chestnuts
- 5 pieces peeled, crushed bitter almonds
- 1 liter boiling cream
- 40 deca fine sieved sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 3/4 liter beaten cream

## Directions

1. Crush 16 deca roasted or boiled peeled chestnuts with a wooden spatula on a board
2. Add with 5 pieces peeled, crushed bitter almonds to 1 liter boiling cream, simmer for a few minutes and cool
3. In a saucepan beat 40 deca fine sieved sugar with 8 egg yolks
4. Gradually add the cream with the chestnuts, place the saucepan over moderate heat and beat the mixture with the snow whisk until it begins to thicken, but it must not boil
5. Pass through a sieve and beat until stiff
6. Fill the freezing can and proceed as with Vanilla Ice (Recipe 1720), finally work 3/4 liter beaten cream into it

title: Obere-Gefrorenes mit Liqueur (Liqueur Cream Ice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared as Maraschino Ice
- any desired liqueur
- cochineal coloring
- 1/2 liter beaten cream

## Directions

1. Prepare as Ice with Maraschino (Recipe 1743)
2. Use any desired liqueur instead and after the ice is stirred, it can be colored pink with some cochineal coloring
3. When this liqueur ice is frozen solid and worked well, it can also be mixed with 1/2 liter beaten cream and served in glasses

title: Obere-Gefrorenes mit Maraschino (Maraschino Cream Ice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 deca fine sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 liter good, unboiled cream
- several spoonfuls Maraschino

## Directions

1. Add to a saucepan 20 deca fine sugar, 8 egg yolks and 1 liter good, unboiled cream
2. Beat over heat until it begins to thicken (without boiling), remove from heat and beat until cool
3. Pass this mixture through a hair sieve into a bowl, stir in several spoonfuls Maraschino and proceed further as with Vanilla Ice (Recipe 1720)

title: Obers mit Erbschaum (Cream Blanc-manger with Whipped Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 Deka almonds, blanched
- 1 liter milk
- 10 Deka fine sifted vanilla sugar
- 4 Deka dissolved, still warm isinglass (prepared with 1/2 liter cold water and reduced to 1/8 liter)
- 1/2 liter whipped cream

## Directions

1. After blanching 30 Deka almonds, pound them very fine, wetting them with some milk, and cook them with constant stirring in 1 liter milk, let this cool and press the liquid through cloth into a saucepan, to which add 10 Deka fine sifted vanilla sugar, heat this warm and add 4 Deka dissolved, still warm isinglass (which has been prepared with 1/2 liter cold water and reduced to 1/8 liter), then put it in an earthenware bowl and stir until it begins to thicken, after which mix in 1/2 liter whipped cream, place this mass in a mold set in ice, cool and unmold

title: Obers-Waffeln (Cream Waffles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter whipped cream
- 5 egg yolks
- 6 deca flour
- 4 deca orange sugar
- 1 knife-tip salt
- 4 egg whites
- 10 deca melted but cooled butter

## Directions

1. Pour 1/2 liter whipped cream into a snow beaker which is set in crushed ice and whip it with the snow whisk into a thick snow, let it stand covered for a while and pour off the buttermilk that will have settled in this time. Meanwhile stir 5 egg yolks and 6 deca flour with some whipped cream smooth and give this with 4 deca orange sugar and 1 knife-tip salt to the whipped snow, fold lightly and fold in the snow from 4 egg whites lightly. Pour 10 deca melted but cooled butter on top and fold in lightly.
2. Baking and further treatment is as described for 'Waffles from Batter' (No. 1648).

title: Obersgefrorne mit Kaffee (Cream Frozen Dessert with Coffee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter very strong black coffee without addition
- 3/4 liter unsweetened cream
- 20 deca sugar
- 8 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Combine very strong black coffee, unsweetened cream, sugar, and egg yolks in a snow-beater
2. Treat further as indicated for Gefrorne mit Vanille (No. 1720)

title: Obersgefrorne mit Vanille (Cream Frozen Dessert with Vanilla)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 20 deca of finely sifted sugar
- 1 half-finger-long piece vanilla
- 1 liter unsweetened, good cream

## Directions

1. Place egg yolks, sugar, and vanilla in a snow-beater (whisk bowl)
2. Gradually add 1 liter unsweetened good cream
3. Beat with the whisk over weak fire until the mixture begins to thicken (becomes thick), but it must not boil
4. Remove from fire and continue beating until lukewarm
5. Pass through a fine hair sieve into an earthenware or porcelain bowl
6. Fill the freezing box with this mixture and proceed as explained above

title: Obersgefrornees mit Caramel (Cream Frozen Dessert with Caramel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter liquefied caramel sugar
- cream
- egg yolks
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as Vanille-Gefrornees (No. 1720), with the difference that instead of vanilla, add 1/4 liter liquefied caramel sugar to the snow-beater with the cream, egg yolks, and sugar

title: Obersgefrornees mit Erdbeeren (Cream Frozen Dessert with Strawberries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 deca sugar
- 1/2 liter strained strawberry puree
- 1 liter cream

## Directions

1. Dissolve 50 deca sugar in 1 liter cold water
2. Stir 1/2 liter strained strawberry puree well into it
3. Freeze as Vanille-Gefrornees (No. 1720)
4. Meanwhile beat 1 liter cream to foam
5. Add this foam to the frozen strawberries in the box
6. Mix well with the mixture and distribute it, so it does not become grainy
7. Let freeze completely covered

title: Obersgefrornees mit Kaffee (Cream Frozen Dessert with Coffee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca fine roasted coffee
- 1 liter boiling cream
- 8 egg yolks
- 24 deca sugar

## Directions

1. Roast coffee until light brown
2. Pour hot into 1 liter boiling cream and cover
3. Let stand 1/4 hour
4. Place egg yolks and sugar in a snow-beater
5. Beat with whisk, gradually adding the strained cream
6. Beat over fire until it begins to thicken, but do not boil
7. Remove from fire and beat until lukewarm
8. Pass through a hair sieve
9. When completely cooled, fill into the freezing box

title: Obersgefrornees mit Orangenblüten (Cream Frozen Dessert with Orange Blossoms)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 deca roasted orange blossoms
- cream
- egg yolks
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as Orange Gefrornees (No. 1728), with the difference that instead of orange peel, throw 6 deca roasted orange blossoms into the hot cream

title: Obersgefrornees mit Orangenduft (Cream Frozen Dessert with Orange Scent)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow peel of 1 orange
- 1 liter boiling hot cream
- 24 deca sugar
- 8 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Carefully scrape off the yellow of an orange peel, ensuring no white peel remains
2. Place it in 1 liter boiling hot cream and let cool uncovered
3. Stir 24 deca sugar with 8 egg yolks well together
4. Gradually add the cooled cream
5. Place on fire and treat further as Vanille-Gefrornees (No. 1720)

title: Obersgefrornees mit Schokolade (Cream Frozen Dessert with Chocolate)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca vanilla chocolate
- 1 liter boiling cream
- 8 egg yolks
- 20 deca finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Soften chocolate on a board in the oven
2. Place in a saucepan and pour 1 liter boiling cream over it, stirring constantly
3. Stir smooth and let cool
4. Place egg yolks, sugar, and completely cooled chocolate in a snow-beater
5. Proceed further as indicated for Gefrorne mit Vanille (No. 1720)

title: Obersgefrornees mit Zitronensduft (Cream Frozen Dessert with Lemon Scent)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon peel
- cream
- egg yolks
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly as Cream Gefrornees with Orange Scent (No. 1728), with the difference that lemon peel is used instead of orange peel

title: Obsteßig (Fruit Vinegar)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit scraps of any kind
- wild apples and pears

## Directions

1. Collect all fruit scraps of whatever kind
2. Throw them into a clean barrel that must stand in a frost-free place, such as a cellar
3. Continue adding fruit scraps unworriedly until the barrel is full, regardless of how long it takes
4. After the end of winter, when there is no more fruit to add to the barrel, press out the fruit
5. Let the obtained juice stand in a clean container and skim off what settles as impure matter on top until everything becomes completely clean
6. Place the mass in the necessary heat
7. In a few weeks you will have a vinegar that is completely equal to ordinary wine vinegar and will have retained nothing of the rotten taste
8. For wild apples and pears: let them lie in the open in heaps, or on the floor if that is not possible, until they become soft, then treat them the same way as described for cultivated fruit
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Obſttortelettes (Fruit Tartlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg flour
- 14 dkg butter
- 7 dkg sugar
- egg white for brushing
- beaten egg
- cooked fruit such as apples, pears, peaches, plums, or cherries

## Directions

1. Make a crumbly dough from 21 dkg flour, 14 dkg butter, and 7 dkg sugar.
2. After resting, roll it out thinly.
3. Cut out rounds with a large cutter and brush the edges with egg white.
4. Shape thin rolls from the dough scraps and place them on the edges of the rounds.
5. Brush with beaten egg and place on an unbuttoned baking sheet.
6. Bake at high heat until light brown.
7. Once cool, top with various stewed or freshly cooked fruits.
8. These tartlets are better made a day ahead.

title: Oder-Konsommé (Udder Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 deca udder
- 3 egg yolks
- 4 whole eggs
- 1/3 liter oysters
- some salt
- some nutmeg

## Directions

1. Stir 6 deca udder, 3 egg yolks, and 4 whole eggs very finely
2. Mix in 1/3 liter oysters, some salt, and some nutmeg
3. Fill a form greased with butter and dusted with flour with this mixture
4. Cook in a water bath for 3/4 hour

title: Oelluber, gerősteter Sterz (Roasted Millet Polenta)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2/3 liter black millet
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 liter boiling water
- 12 decagrams hot lard

## Directions

1. Put 2/3 liter black millet and 3/4 teaspoon salt in a casserole
2. While stirring, heat it
3. Make a hollow in the middle and gradually pour about 1/2 liter boiling water into it
4. Stir well and let it boil for a few minutes
5. If the mass is too thick, add the necessary hot water
6. Cook until it becomes a fairly dry flour dough
7. Divide the dough into smaller pieces with a wooden spoon
8. Pour 12 decagrams hot lard over it and roast covered for a few minutes

title: Oesterreichische Fasten-Bouillon (Austrian Fasting Bouillon)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- whole onions
- celery
- green parsley
- leek
- parsnip
- butter
- tench or carp
- clear pea soup
- Parmesan cheese
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Cook peas in water very slowly so the bouillon remains clear
2. Cook with whole onions, celery, green parsley, leek and parsnip
3. Let the thick parts settle and strain through a fine sieve
4. In a casserole, line the bottom with thinly sliced onions
5. Add a piece of butter, finely cut parsnip, parsley and other root vegetables, celery and gundel herb
6. Let everything simmer gently, then place tench or carp in it and braise together slowly
7. Finally cook it with clear pea soup and a piece of Parmesan cheese, let it rest briefly and strain through a fine sieve
8. Serve over toasted bread slices
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Olio-Suppe (Olio Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- good consommé
- 3 pounds veal from the neck or bones
- 3 pounds lean beef
- beef foot
- couple calf feet
- old hen
- parsley
- parsnip
- yellow turnips
- 1 pound ham

## Directions

1. Put good consommé in a casserole, add 3 pounds veal from the neck or bones, 3 pounds lean beef, a beef foot, a couple calf feet, and an old hen
2. Add parsley, parsnip, and yellow turnips, a couple pieces of each, along with 1 pound ham
3. Simmer for 4 hours
4. Strain it clean and serve in bowls to drink
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Oliven-Sauce (Olive Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 anchovies
- 6 olives
- 1 tablespoon capers
- Butter
- Soup

## Directions

1. The preparation is the same as the anchovy sauce, except only 4 anchovies, 6 olives and 1 tablespoon capers are used
2. If desired, before serving two anchovies cut into cubes, 1 tablespoon whole capers and some tablespoons of cream can be added to the finished passed sauce
3. This sauce is served with roast meat but can also be added to veal schnitzel or in general to meat or game

title: Olla potrida für Fasttage (Olla Podrida for Fast Days)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned snails
- pea broth
- ginger
- nutmeg flower
- pepper
- maurach fish
- crayfish tails and claws
- artichoke bottoms
- fish pieces
- fish livers
- carp roe
- small bread dumplings
- asparagus
- young lettuce
- cauliflower
- crayfish broth
- butter

## Directions

1. Simmer cleaned snails with pea broth, ginger, nutmeg flower and pepper in a small pot
2. Simultaneously simmer maurach fish and strain the broth from both
3. Mix the maurach with the snails
4. Shell the tails and claws from cooked crayfish and add them
5. Add some beautiful sliced artichoke bottoms
6. Can also add half-overcooked filleted fish pieces, sliced overbrowned fish livers, carp roe, small bread dumplings, asparagus, young lettuce and cauliflower as available in season, all half-overcooked
7. Pour crayfish broth over everything in a wide pan
8. Add a good piece of butter
9. Season with nutmeg flower, pepper and ginger
10. Let it simmer until it becomes thick, for which purpose the broth is poured off several times and added back
11. When serving, sprinkle with nutmeg flower
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Olla potrida für Personen von schwachen Verdauungswerkzeugen (Olla Podrida for People with Weak Digestion)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Loth ham
- 2 pounds beef
- 1 pound veal
- 1 young chicken
- 1 young pigeon
- Roots, herbs, and vegetables

## Directions

1. Put the ham, beef, veal, young chicken, and young pigeon into a pot over the fire
2. Let the meat sufficiently foam and cook
3. Add as many roots, herbs, and vegetables as the market offers
4. Let it cook thoroughly
5. Arrange the vegetable soup in the middle of the table and the four types of meat with their accompaniments of anchovies, fresh butter, radish, cucumber salad, etc. around it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Omelette mit Käse (Cheese Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-4 whole eggs
- 2 dkg grated Parmesan cheese
- salt
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Prepare the omelet the same way as the ham omelet, substituting the cheese for the ham.

title: Omelette mit Schinken (Ham Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-4 whole eggs
- 4 dkg ham
- salt
- butter or fat
- parsley

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with a pinch of salt and chopped ham in a small bowl.
2. Heat a nutmeg-sized amount of butter or fat in an omelet pan until hot, pour in the mixture.
3. Break it up slightly with a fork so it can brown without burning.
4. When it is set, loosen it with a spatula, fold it together, lift it onto a plate, and garnish with parsley.

title: Omelette von Kräutern (Herb Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-4 whole eggs
- chopped herbs such as parsley, chives, chervil
- salt
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Use the same mixture as for the ham omelet, substituting chopped herbs for the ham.

title: Omelette à la Française (French Omelette)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg whites (4, stiffly beaten)
- Flour, fine (1 tablespoon)
- Sugar (1/2 tablespoon)
- Salt (a pinch)
- Egg yolks (4)

## Directions

1. Add flour, sugar, salt and egg yolks to the stiffly beaten egg white and stir lightly

title: Onion Soup (Soupe à l'oignon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deca butter
- 4 heads of onion
- 2 liters water
- Flour
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Parsley

## Directions

1. In 16 Deca butter, add 4 onion heads sliced into half rings about two fingers wide
2. Let them roast somewhat
3. Dust with the necessary flour and let them roast until golden
4. Pour in 2 liters water and simmer slowly for 1 hour
5. Season with salt, a little nutmeg, and spices if desired
6. Sprinkle a spoonful of finely chopped parsley over and serve with boiled bread slices in the soup pot
7. The soup can also be passed through a sieve before serving

title: Orange Blossom Soufflé (Soufflé à la fleur d'oranges)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 drams flour
- 1/2 liter cold cream or milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- 16 drams butter
- 16 drams sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 drams finely chopped and candied orange blossoms
- 6 egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Place flour in a casserole, stir with 1/2 liter cold cream until smooth.
2. Add 3 egg yolks, 3 whole eggs, 16 drams butter, and 16 drams sugar.
3. Stir over heat until the mixture has thickened considerably.
4. Set aside and let cool completely.
5. Gradually mix in 6 egg yolks and 6 drams finely chopped and candied orange blossoms.
6. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites.
7. Fill a buttered mold, dusted with flour, or pour into a buttered dish.
8. Bake at low heat for 3/4 hour in the oven.

title: Orange Frozen Dessert (Glace aux oranges)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/6 liter cold water
- 36 deca grams sugar
- Juice of 4 oranges
- Juice of 1 lemon
- Yellow zest of 2 oranges, finely cut

## Directions

1. Dissolve 36 deca grams sugar in 1/6 liter cold water
2. Add the juice of 4 oranges and 1 lemon to this
3. Add the yellow zest of 2 oranges, finely cut with a sharp knife
4. Let this liquid sit covered for about 1 hour
5. Weigh the mass to 22 degrees, pass it through a sieve, and proceed further as with the 'Ordinary Strawberry Frozen Dessert' (Recipe 1746)

title: Orange Horseradish (Orange Horseradish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 orange
- ½ ounces sugar
- 1 lemon
- some cold water
- 12 ounces grated rolls soaked in water and well pressed out
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- some vinegar
- 4 tablespoons oil
- 2 tablespoons grated horseradish
- salt

## Directions

1. Grate ½ ounce sugar from an orange and place this sugar together with the expressed juice of one orange, 1 lemon, and some cold water in a bowl and let it stand covered
2. Meanwhile, pass 12 ounces of grated rolls soaked in water and well pressed out together with 6 hard-boiled egg yolks through a sieve
3. Mix the dissolved orange horseradish with the passed mixture and some vinegar, 4 tablespoons oil, 2 tablespoons grated horseradish and some salt, stirring well
4. If it should be too thick, it can be thinned with some water
5. The taste should be pleasantly sweet and yet somewhat sour

title: Orange Ice (Orangen-Gefrornes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 oranges
- 4 lemons
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 seidel Madeira wine, Tokay, or sweet sparkling wine
- 1 small glass water

## Directions

1. Rub 8 oranges and 4 lemons on a pound of sugar
2. Put the rubbed portion into a soup pot along with the juice from the oranges and lemons
3. Add a seidel of Madeira wine, Tokay or sweet sparkling wine, and a small glass of water
4. Clarify the remaining sugar as usual
5. Add to the mass
6. Pass through a fine sieve into the freezing container and process
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Orange Pudding (Pomeranzenkoch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter, about the weight of two eggs
- three whole eggs
- one small roll soaked in milk
- one-half Seville orange peel
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat butter, about the weight of two eggs, and three whole eggs well together
2. Add one small roll, finely cut and soaked in milk
3. Rub the peel of one-half Seville orange with sugar
4. Press out the juice and add it
5. Sweeten everything to taste
6. Coat a casserole with butter and sprinkle it with breadcrumbs
7. Pour the pudding in, sprinkle the top again with breadcrumbs, and let it bake
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Orange Pudding (Pouding aux oranges)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams trimmed, finely sliced bread rolls
- 1 liter boiling milk
- juice of 1 orange
- 10 decagrams whipped butter
- 16 decagrams orange sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 14 decagrams peeled, finely pounded almonds
- 6 egg whites
- butter for the mold
- flour for the mold

## Directions

1. Cook 8 decagrams trimmed, finely sliced bread rolls in 1 liter boiling milk until well softened
2. Mix with the juice of 1 orange
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Beat 10 decagrams whipped butter with 16 decagrams orange sugar and 6 egg yolks gradually
5. Add 14 decagrams peeled, finely pounded almonds with the passed bread rolls
6. Mix well
7. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
8. Fill a buttered mold dusted with flour
9. Bake covered in steam for 1 to 1.5 hours
10. Serve with any desired sauce

title: Orangebitter Schnaps (Orange Bitters Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Maß strong and pure corn brandy
- 4 Loth green bitter oranges
- 4 Pfund white candy sugar
- 2 spoonfuls Cremor Tartari

## Directions

1. Pour the corn brandy over the green bitter oranges, candy sugar, and Cremor Tartari
2. Seal the vessel with a bladder through which a needle is inserted
3. Place it in the sun or at a moderately warm location
4. Shake the mixture several times daily
5. Pour it off when the brandy has completely clarified
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Orangen- oder Zitronen-Eis (Orange or Lemon Glaze)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 drams of finely sifted orange sugar
- juice of one whole orange

## Directions

1. Put 28 drams of finely sifted orange sugar and the juice of a whole orange into a deep bowl.
2. Stir this for 1/2 to 1 hour into a thick mass, to which, if it is too thick, 1 tablespoon of hot sugar can be added.
3. Pour it into a sugar pan or, in the absence of one, into a snowflake or saucepan, put it on the fire and let it stand there with constant stirring until the mass becomes liquid again.
4. Now coat the cake immediately with it; if necessary, dry it a few minutes in the lukewarm oven and set it cold.
5. Strawberry glaze is prepared the same way.

title: Orangen-Compot (Orange Compote)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges
- Clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the skin as carefully as possible from the oranges until the finest part, then cut them into round slices or divide them into sections and lay them in a casserole with clarified sugar for a quarter hour.
2. You must first have rubbed this sugar on the oranges.
3. When this has boiled a couple of times and the syrup is somewhat thick, arrange them for the table.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Orangen-Creme (Orange Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Peel of one orange
- Juice of 4 oranges
- 8 Loth sugar
- Half a Seidel water
- 5 eggs (whites separated)
- 5 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Remove the peel from one orange very thinly and place it in a casserole
2. Press the juice of 4 oranges into the casserole
3. Mix in 8 Loth sugar and half a Seidel water
4. Beat the whites of 5 eggs into stiff snow and stir slowly into the casserole
5. Set on a moderate coal fire and stir the mixture until it thickens
6. Pass everything through a hair sieve and let it cool
7. Beat 5 egg yolks and pour them with the above cream into a casserole
8. Set on a small coal fire and stir until it almost begins to boil
9. Let it cool, fill cream cups with it, and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Orangen-Creme (Orange Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one orange
- 4 oranges
- 8 loth sugar
- half seidel water
- 5 eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the zest from one orange very thinly and place it in a casserole
2. Squeeze the juice from 4 oranges into the casserole, mix in 8 loth sugar and half seidel water
3. Beat the whites of 5 eggs into snow and stir slowly into the casserole
4. Set it over not too strong a charcoal fire and stir the mass in it until it becomes thick
5. Pass everything through a hair sieve and let it become cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Orangen-Ratafia (Orange Ratafia)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 loth of dried orange peel
- 2 measures of once-rectified spirits
- 14 pounds of sugar
- 2 pounds of boiling water

## Directions

1. Soak orange peel in water and remove the inner spongy or marrow-like skin, leaving only the upper yellow part
2. Cut the yellow peel into fine pieces and dry it
3. Dissolve 14 pounds of sugar in 2 pounds of boiling water
4. Take 4 loth of the dried peel, 2 measures of once-rectified spirits, and the dissolved sugar
5. Let everything stand for some days in gentle heat
6. Filter into bottles
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Orangen-Salat (Orange Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Any desired number of oranges
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges and cut them into slices the thickness of a knife back, then cut these slices into two parts
2. Sprinkle them very generously with sugar and let them stand covered for 10 minutes so the sugar can be absorbed
3. Arrange them very decoratively on a compote dish, laying the larger and smaller pieces in a crown shape
4. A few stewed sour cherries can be placed in the center
5. Pour the orange juice over it by the spoonful
6. The preparation of this dish should be done shortly before serving, as the oranges lose their good flavor and become bitter with longer standing

title: Orangen-Sauce (Orange Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter sauce
- approximately 6 tablespoons sugar
- some finely cut orange zest
- juice of 1 lemon
- flesh of 1 orange
- peeled diced orange

## Directions

1. To a cooked, strained butter sauce add approximately 6 tablespoons sugar and some finely cut orange zest with the juice of 1 lemon
2. Remove the flesh of 1 orange, cut the peeled orange into dice, add it to the above
3. Let everything boil well

title: Orangeneis (Orange Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 oranges
- 3 lemons
- 30 dkg cube sugar
- 1 dl water
- 2½ lemons

## Directions

1. Rub 30 dkg cube sugar on the wiped oranges, add 1 dl water and cook-spin for 15 minutes.
2. Squeeze the oranges and 2½ lemons, mix with the spun sugar and 2 dl cold water, weigh with the sugar scale (19°), strain through a hair sieve and freeze like Vanilla Ice Cream.

title: Orangengelee (Orange Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 oranges
- 3 lemons
- 7 tablespoons isinglass in 1 liter water
- 40 tablespoons clarified sugar
- 1/2 liter water
- white wine
- 1 small piece orange peel

## Directions

1. Press the juice from 4 oranges and 3 lemons through cloth, ensuring it is completely clear.
2. Pour this juice into 1/4 liter measure, filling the remaining space with white wine.
3. Cook 7 tablespoons isinglass in 1 liter water and reduce to 1/4 liter liquid as previously described.
4. Pour 40 tablespoons clarified sugar (prepared according to No. 1542 in 1/2 liter water) to which a finger-length piece of orange peel has been added during clarification, to the isinglass liquid.
5. Let the sugar and isinglass together simmer approximately 10 minutes and strain through fine, tightly woven linen cloth.

title: Orangenlemonade (Orange Lemonade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- orange juice
- orange sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare like simple lemonade, but use orange juice instead of lemon juice and orange sugar instead

title: Oranges Eis (Orange Icing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- white icing
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Prepare white icing and add some orange juice (obtainable at the apothecary) to it

title: Oranges-Torte mit Butter (Orange Cake with Butter; Tourte aux Oranges)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 decagrams butter, beaten
- 24 decagrams orange sugar
- juice of one orange
- 8 raw egg yolks
- 8 hard-cooked egg yolks, sifted
- 4 egg whites
- 8 decagrams flour
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat 14 decagrams butter with 24 decagrams orange sugar and the juice of one orange until well combined, then stir for 1/2 hour.
2. Add 8 raw and 8 hard-cooked, sifted egg yolks, stir for another 1/2 hour, then lightly fold in the beaten snow of 4 egg whites mixed with 8 decagrams flour.
3. Fill a buttered cake form dusted with flour with this mixture and bake for 1 hour in a well-heated oven.
4. Once baked, place the cake on a sieve to cool completely, then cover with a glaze of choice.

title: Oranges-Torte ohne Butter (Orange Cake without Butter; Tourte aux Oranges)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 decagrams orange sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 30 decagrams peeled almonds
- juice of one lemon
- 6 egg whites
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Stir 24 decagrams orange sugar with 12 egg yolks for 1 1/4 hours, then add 30 decagrams peeled almonds, finely ground with lemon juice.
2. Stir for another 3/4 hour, then lightly fold in the beaten snow of 6 egg whites.
3. Fill a buttered cake form dusted with flour with this mixture and bake at low heat in the oven for 1 hour, then handle as described for the previous orange cake.

title: Ordinary Almond Mountains (Ordinäre Mandelberge)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound marzipan dough
- 4 to 5 egg whites
- quarter pound farina sugar
- half pound sweet almonds, cut into fine long pieces
- crushed cinnamon

## Directions

1. Dissolve half pound marzipan dough in 4 to 5 egg whites
2. Add quarter pound farina sugar, half pound sweet almonds which have been cut into fine long pieces, and some crushed cinnamon
3. Stir everything well together
4. Arrange the mass in small heaps on paper
5. Bake in moderate oven heat
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ordinary Sour Cream Soufflé (Soufflé à la crème aigre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 ounces flour
- 1.5 liters milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 12 ounces granulated sugar
- 8 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Gradually whisk flour into cold milk
2. Add egg yolks and granulated sugar one by one
3. Gently fold in the whipped egg whites
4. Fill a butter-greased, flour-lined mold
5. Cover and steam for 3/4 hour or bake in the oven

title: Ordinary Wild Duck (Gewöhnliche Wildente)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild duck
- Bacon or marrow
- Onion
- Root vegetables
- 5 crushed juniper berries
- 5 grains each type of spice
- 5 sprigs of thyme
- 1 bay leaf
- Bacon slices
- Black bread (8 teaspoons, thinly cut)

## Directions

1. Let the duck hang in its feathers for 2-3 days until tender, but not longer to avoid bad odor
2. Place some bacon or marrow, onion, and root vegetables (all thinly cut) in a casserole
3. Add 5 crushed juniper berries, 5 grains each of spices, 5 sprigs of thyme, and 1 bay leaf
4. Place the dressed duck on top
5. Cover with bacon slices
6. Let roast covered
7. Then add thinly cut black bread

title: Ordinäre Germudeln (Ordinary Yeast Dumplings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 Deka flour
- 4 Deka yeast dissolved in 4/8 liter lukewarm milk
- 1/2 liter milk
- 3 Stercks butter
- 1 whole egg
- 1 heaping tablespoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- Filling of choice

## Directions

1. Place 50 Deka of flour in a deep bowl, make a well in the middle, pour in 4 Deka yeast dissolved in 4/8 liter lukewarm milk into this well
2. Stir it together somewhat and let it rest covered for a short time
3. Beat together 1/2 liter milk, 3 Stercks butter, 1 whole egg, 1 heaping tablespoon sugar and 1/2 teaspoon salt until frothy and pour it into the yeast mixture
4. Stir well and beat the dough until it detaches from the spoon
5. Let it rise covered in a warm place until doubled
6. Using a tablespoon, form pieces of dough of desired size, place them on a flour-dusted cloth and spread them apart with floured fingers
7. Place 1 teaspoon filling of choice in the middle of each dumpling, fold the dough over the filling and press together with 4 fingers
8. Place these dumplings in a butter-brushed casserole, brush them on the sides where they lie next to each other with melted butter and treat them further as described for 'Gewöhnliche Buchteln' (Nr. 1262)

title: Ordinäre Halfen (Ordinary Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter flour
- 3 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter milk
- 4 deca melted butter or lard
- 1/4 liter milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Put 1 liter flour into a deep bowl, make a well in the center and pour in the yeast dissolved in milk, stir a little together and let stand covered in a warm place
2. Beat together 4 deca melted butter or lard, 1/4 liter milk, 2 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, 1 tablespoon sugar and 1/2 teaspoon salt, pour into the flour, stir well and beat the dough until it separates from the spoon, then let rise in a warm place
3. Further treatment is as described in the preceding numbers

title: Ordinäre Krapfen (Ordinary Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 Decaliter dried, warm, sifted, fine flour
- 4 Decaliter sugar
- 5 grams salt
- 1 liter milk, divided into 3 equal parts
- 3 Decaliter fresh yeast
- 10 Decaliter butter or 8 Decaliter beef lard
- 4 egg yolks
- Marmalade or jam for filling
- 6-8 Decaliter beef lard
- 7/8 Decaliter pork lard of better quality

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as Fine Pastry Fritters, using only 4 egg yolks instead of 10, and using 10 Decaliter butter or 8 Decaliter beef lard instead of 14 Decaliter butter in the dough.

title: Ordinäre Krensauce (Ordinary Horseradish Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- horseradish, cleaned and scraped
- breadcrumbs, grated on the same grater as the horseradish
- meat broth as needed
- one piece butter
- nutmeg, grated

## Directions

1. In a small pot, simmer the carefully scraped and grated horseradish with meat broth as needed
2. Add the necessary breadcrumbs grated on the same grater as the horseradish, to remove any remaining horseradish pieces
3. Simmer for about a quarter hour
4. Finally add one piece of butter and a strong knife-tip of grated nutmeg
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ordinäre Mandelberge (Ordinary Almond Mountains)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound marzipan dough
- 4 to 5 egg whites
- one quarter pound farina sugar
- half pound sweet almonds, cut into fine elongated pieces
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Dissolve half a pound marzipan dough in 4 to 5 egg whites.
2. Add one quarter pound farina sugar, half a pound sweet almonds which must be cut into fine elongated pieces beforehand, and some ground cinnamon.
3. Stir everything well together.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ordinäre Rostbraten mit Sardellenscauce (Ordinary Roasted Beef with Anchovy Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef from the rib
- Salt
- Butter
- Bread crumbs
- Beef broth
- Anchovies
- Juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Take the beef designated for this from the rib, cut as much as needed from it, salt it, knock it, and turn it in butter and bread crumbs
2. Roast it sufficiently on the grill
3. Then place it in a casserole, add some beef broth, anchovies, and the juice of a lemon
4. Let it reduce briefly and then serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ordinären grünen Salat (Ordinary Green Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- head or Bolognese lettuce
- oil
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. If it is head or Bolognese lettuce, cut out the finest and cleanest yellow parts from each
2. If they are heads or hearts, make only four parts from each
3. Wash them clean and press the water out thoroughly
4. Prepare the salad with oil, vinegar, and salt
5. Serve it to the table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ordinärer grüner Salat (Ordinary Green Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Head lettuce or Bologna lettuce
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Salt

## Directions

1. If using head or Bologna lettuce, cut out the finest and cleanest yellow part from each head
2. If using heads or hearts, make four parts from each one
3. Wash them clean and press the water out thoroughly
4. Dress the salad with oil, vinegar, and salt
5. Serve at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ordinäres Kletzenbrot (Ordinary Fruit Bread)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo dried plums
- 1/2 kilo dried pears (prunes) or twice as much plums
- 1/2 kilo grapes
- 20 pieces hazelnut kernels if available
- 1/4 kilo dried apple pieces
- some wine or liqueur of choice
- 1 tablespoon schnapps
- finely cut peel of 1 lemon
- 2 kilos black bread dough
- black flour to achieve proper consistency

## Directions

1. Prepare the mixture as for the 'Fine Christmas Bread' (recipe 1310), but do not wrap the formed loaf or strip in dough.
2. Instead dust with flour and place in a baking vessel or form also well dusted with flour.
3. Let it rise for 1.5 hours at a warm place lightly covered.
4. Lay buttered paper on top and after transferring to a sheet with this paper, bake slowly.

title: Orgeade or Almond Milk (Almond Beverage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet, select almonds
- 1 small glass of water
- 4 measures fresh water
- fine pounded sugar to taste
- a little orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Wash 1 pound of sweet, selected almonds three times in fresh water
2. Rub them with your hands
3. Pound them in a mortar or grinder, adding a small glass of water, very finely
4. Transfer to another vessel
5. Gradually stir in 4 measures of fresh water
6. Press well through a napkin
7. Sweeten with finely pounded sugar as desired
8. Add a little orange blossom water at the end, which gives this beverage an extremely pleasant taste
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ostoot (Mixed Vegetables)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Various types of turnips
- Salt water
- Soup
- Butter
- Green peas
- Sprouts
- Green beans, sliced diagonally
- Asparagus, cut into pieces
- Cold water
- Cauliflower
- Red beets, cut into pieces or scooped out
- Sugar
- Meat for garnish, or fried or baked fish

## Directions

1. Cook all varieties of turnips, each according to its type, in salt water
2. Place them in a casserole and braise them with some soup and butter
3. Cook green peas, sprouts, sliced green beans, and asparagus pieces separately in salt water until tender
4. Place the cooked vegetables in cold water and drain
5. Add the drained vegetables to the braised turnips
6. Pour some butter sauce over them, mix well together, and arrange heaped on a serving dish
7. Top with a cauliflower cooked in salt water
8. Pour more butter sauce over the arranged dish
9. For enhanced appearance, add red beets that have been braised separately in butter, sugar, and water
10. Garnish the dish with meat, or fried or baked fish

title: Palatschinken (Stuffed Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter milk
- 14-25 dkg flour
- 2-3 eggs
- salt
- fat for frying
- marmalade or topfen filling

## Directions

1. In a pot, beat together 1/4 liter milk with 14-25 dkg flour, 2-3 eggs, and salt until smooth
2. Heat some melted fat in the palatschinken pan and pour it back out
3. Pour in a small ladle of batter and let it run thin and round
4. Fry lightly on both sides and place on a flat plate
5. Repeat with the remaining batter
6. When all palatschinken are done, spread with marmalade, roll, sprinkle with sugar, and serve warm
7. Or spread with topfen filling, sprinkle raisins on top, roll, place in a well-buttered baking pan, and bake in the oven for 15-20 minutes under frequent basting with hot milk

title: Panade (Panade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 crusts removed, softened in cold water bread rolls
- 16 spoonfuls melted butter
- 2 liters boiling beef broth
- 0.25 liter cream
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Squeeze 6 bread rolls (crusts removed, softened in cold water) well
2. Add to 16 spoonfuls melted butter and stir over heat with a cooking spoon until it becomes a smooth dough that shines with fat
3. Gradually pour 2 liters boiling beef broth over this dough, stirring constantly
4. When the soup has cooked for 1/2 hour over moderate heat, mix with 0.25 liter cream in which 3 egg yolks have been beaten
5. Serve after that

title: Panadera Soup (Panadelsuppe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dkg diaphragm meat or beef broth
- 3 bread rolls

## Directions

1. Rinse the bread rolls, soak them in broth, beat them well, pour in the remaining strained broth.
2. Cook for a few minutes and optionally thicken with liaison.

title: Panamatorte (Panama Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 7 egg yolks
- 8 dkg grated chocolate
- 7 egg whites, beaten stiff
- 14 dkg grated raw almonds
- 15 dkg butter
- 15 dkg vanilla sugar
- 1 whole egg
- 8-10 dkg grated chocolate for the cream
- 5 dkg dark roasted, coarsely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dkg sugar with 7 egg yolks for 30 minutes, stir in 8 dkg grated chocolate and fold in 7 beaten egg whites and 14 dkg grated raw almonds.
2. Fill the mixture into the form and bake slowly.
3. The next day, cut through the cake, fill with cream, put together, brush inside and around with cream, and sprinkle with 5 dkg dark roasted, coarsely chopped almonds.
4. For the cream: beat 15 dkg butter, stir in 15 dkg vanilla sugar, 1 whole egg, and 8-10 dkg grated chocolate.

title: Panamatorte (Panama Torte)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 7 egg yolks
- 8 dkg grated chocolate
- 7 egg whites
- 14 dkg raw grated almonds
- 15 dkg butter
- 15 dkg vanilla sugar
- 1 whole egg
- 8–10 dkg grated chocolate
- 5 dkg dark roasted, coarsely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dkg sugar with 7 egg yolks for 30 minutes
2. Stir in 8 dkg grated chocolate
3. Fold in 7 egg whites and 14 dkg raw grated almonds
4. Pour the mixture into the mold and bake slowly
5. The next day, cut the torte and fill with crème
6. Assemble the pieces and spread crème on the outside and sides
7. Sprinkle with 5 dkg dark roasted, coarsely chopped almonds
8. For the crème: beat 15 dkg butter until creamy, stir in 15 dkg vanilla sugar, 1 whole egg, and 8–10 dkg grated chocolate

title: Panierte Schnitzel (Breaded Veal Cutlets)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin, finger-thick slices
- Flour
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Lard

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Natural Cutlets, but dip the flour-coated cutlets into beaten eggs
2. Coat with bread crumbs (paneer)
3. Fry in hot lard

title: Panierter Hirschziemer (Breaded Venison Loin)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- twine
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- whole pepper
- fine herbs
- bay leaves
- rosemary
- cloves
- onions, sliced
- grated rye bread crumbs
- sugar
- ground cinnamon
- cloves
- chopped almonds
- grated lemon peel
- egg, beaten
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Wash the loin clean and tie it with twine so it does not fall apart
2. Place it in a large pot with water and vinegar over heat
3. Season with salt, whole pepper, fine herbs, bay leaves, rosemary, cloves, and large sliced onions
4. Let it cook through completely
5. Mix grated rye bread crumbs with sugar, ground cinnamon, cloves, chopped almonds, and grated lemon peel
6. Remove the loin from the pot onto a baking pan and loosen the twine
7. Brush the meat with beaten egg and spread the grated and seasoned bread mixture over it
8. Pour some butter or fat over it, place it in the oven, and let it become golden
9. Once browned, serve it warm. As a variation, one can also brush the loin on the surface with a sugar glaze and let it dry in the oven
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Panierter Karpfen (Breaded Carp)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carp
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- flour
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Scale the carp well (see recipe 870)
2. Gut it, clean it well, split it, then cut it into 3-finger wide pieces
3. Make an incision on the skin side of each piece
4. Sprinkle with salt and let it stand covered in a bowl for 1/2 hour before serving
5. 1/2 hour before serving, coat the carp pieces well with breadcrumbs mixed with flour, or coat them until they are dry and fry from hot fat until light brown
6. Lay them on blotting paper so the excess fat is absorbed
7. Instead of mixing flour with breadcrumbs, you can add 1 tablespoon semolina to the flour, which makes the fish pieces crisp when frying
8. Or coat them only with flour or treat them like fried chicken
9. Serve any salad with it

title: Paprika Chicken (Poulets au Paprica)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams hot fat or lard
- 2 fallen and dressed chickens
- onion, finely diced
- flour

## Directions

1. Place 8 decagrams hot fat or lard in a casserole
2. Add finely diced onion as desired and place 2 fallen and dressed chickens in it
3. Cover and braise
4. When the liquid that forms has evaporated, dust the chickens with some flour
5. Pour over ⅛ liter of the cooking liquid

title: Paprika-Hühner (Poulets au paprica)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Decilit hot lard or bacon
- finely diced onions (to taste)
- 2 cleaned and dressed chickens
- flour
- 1/8 liter soup
- 1/2 liter sour cream
- salt
- paprika (to taste)

## Directions

1. In a casserole containing 8 deciliter hot lard or bacon, add finely diced onions to taste
2. Place 2 cleaned and dressed chickens inside and braise covered
3. When the liquid that forms has evaporated, dust the chickens with flour
4. Pour 1/8 liter soup over them and braise covered until tender
5. Pour 1/2 liter sour cream over the chickens, salt and season to taste with paprika
6. Let come to a good boil
7. Remove the chickens, cut them into desired pieces, arrange on a platter
8. Strain the sauce and pour over the chickens
9. Serve with noodles, turnips, or stuffed onions

title: Paprika-Hühner anderer Art (Poulets au paprica et lard)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- diced bacon
- finely cut onions
- 2 cleaned and prepared chickens
- 1/2 liter soup
- 1/2 liter sour cream
- salt
- 1 strong tablespoon paprika

## Directions

1. Put diced bacon and finely cut onions in a casserole
2. Cut 2 cleaned chickens each into 4 pieces, hack at the joints, twist wings back toward the neck, slightly cut the leg bone, and tuck the legs inward
3. Place the dressed chickens in the casserole with the root vegetables and braise covered
4. After approximately 1/2 hour, pour 1/2 liter soup and 1/2 liter sour cream over the chickens
5. Season with salt and 1 strong tablespoon paprika and braise covered until tender
6. Remove the chickens, arrange on a platter and pour the defatted sauce over them
7. The root vegetables may be omitted if desired

title: Paprikahühner (Paprika Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens
- 8 dekagrams butter or fat
- 1 large onion
- 2 knife tips paprika
- salt
- hot water
- 1 deciliter sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the chickens, quarter them and salt them.
2. In a casserole, heat 8 dekagrams of butter or fat and lightly brown 1 large onion until light yellow.
3. Add 2 knife tips of paprika to the onion.
4. Place the chicken pieces skin-side down into the casserole.
5. Pour some hot water beside them and braise them covered, turning occasionally and adding water as needed, for 1-1.5 hours depending on the size of the chickens.
6. Place the cooked chicken on a plate and strain the cooking liquid.
7. Return the liquid with the meat to the casserole.
8. Stir in the sour cream spoonful by spoonful and braise for another 10 minutes.
9. Serve the chicken on a round dish with the sauce.

title: Paprikakalbsbraten (Paprika Veal Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-80 dkg boned veal shoulder
- 1 liter cream
- 4 dkg fat
- water
- 5 dkg fat
- 1 finely chopped onion
- 2-3 knife tips paprika
- broth or water
- veal roasting juices

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, salt it, and bind it if necessary.
2. Heat 4 dkg fat in a roasting pan, add the meat, brown it on all sides, add very little water, and let it roast in the oven for 1 to 1½ hours, basting frequently and adding more liquid.
3. Meanwhile, in 5 dkg fat, roast 1 finely chopped onion until light yellow.
4. Add 2-3 knife tips of paprika, pour in broth or water, and let it cook well.
5. Dust it with flour, strain it through a sieve, pour it up with the cream, bring it to a boil again, mix it with the roasting juices, and pour it over the sliced meat.

title: Paprikakartoffeln (Paprika Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kg potatoes
- 5 dkg fat
- 1 finely minced onion
- 3 knife points paprika
- cold water
- salt
- ¼ l cream

## Directions

1. Heat 5 dkg fat in a casserole and sauté 1 finely minced onion until golden
2. Add 3 knife points of paprika
3. Quench with a ladleful of cold water
4. Add raw peeled and quartered potatoes and salt
5. Cover and cook for 30-35 minutes, adding water as needed

title: Paprikakartoffeln (Paprika Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 dkg fat
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 3 knife points paprika
- cold water
- raw potatoes, peeled and quartered
- salt
- cream
- flour

## Directions

1. In a casserole, let 1 finely chopped onion turn golden in 5 dkg fat
2. Add 3 knife points paprika
3. Quench with a ladle of cold water
4. Add raw peeled, quartered potatoes, salt and let them stew covered for 30-35 minutes, adding water occasionally
5. At the end stir in a few spoonfuls of cream and cook for another 5 minutes
6. If the cream is too thin, it must be thickened with 1 spoonful of flour

title: Paprikaschnitzel (Paprika Veal Cutlet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-80 dekagrams veal leg
- 1 deciliter sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove skin and bones from the meat and cut into cutlets.
2. Prepare exactly as the Paprikahühner.

title: Paradeis gefüllt (Stuffed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-2 per person: beautiful, firm, equally large, round tomatoes
- hashé from 20-25 dkg beef scraps
- grated Parmesan
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash the tomatoes, remove the stems, cut off the tops, and carefully hollow them out.
2. Fill with hashé, place in an omelet pan, sprinkle with grated Parmesan and breadcrumbs, dot with butter.
3. Bake for 10-15 minutes in the oven.
4. Alternatively, fill with braised rice instead of hashé.
5. The hollowed-out portions and tops can be added to soup to clarify it, or made into a sauce.

title: Paradeissuppe (Tomato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg tomatoes
- 1 bread roll
- water
- vinegar
- ½ onion
- 6 dkg fat
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and break the tomatoes
2. Stew with water, some vinegar and ½ onion for 30 minutes and pass
3. Make a roux from the fat, add water or broth
4. Add the strained tomatoes, season with salt, sugar and vinegar to taste
5. Boil well and serve with bread slices or with small cut macaroni cooked in salted water

title: Paradeissuppe (Tomato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- water
- vinegar
- 1/2 onion
- 6 dkg fat
- salt
- sugar
- bread slices or macaroni

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and break the tomatoes
2. Stew with water, some vinegar, and 1/2 onion for 30 minutes
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Make a roux with 6 dkg fat and pour water or soup over it
5. Add the strained tomato mixture
6. Season to taste with salt, sugar, and vinegar
7. Boil well and serve with bread slices or macaroni cooked in salted water, cut small

title: Paradies-Apfelsauce (Paradise Apple Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 to 7 paradise apples, cleaned and washed
- one spoonful flour
- butter, warmed
- broth if needed
- one piece sugar

## Directions

1. Braise 6 to 7 cleaned and washed paradise apples, cut into several pieces, in a casserole until very soft
2. Let one spoonful of flour in warmed butter run slightly brown
3. Dissolve the browned flour with the braised apples that have been pressed through a sieve
4. If too thick, add broth
5. Add one piece of sugar
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Paradise Apple Jam with Sugar (Paradies-Äpfel-Marmelade mit Zucker)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Paradise apples (1 kilo)
- Sugar (1/2 kilo sugar per 1 kilo of paradise apples)

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as Paradise Apple Jam (No. 1891) cooked in a steam bath
2. After pressing the apples through a sieve, cook them with sugar (1/2 kilo sugar per 1 kilo of paradise apples) with continuous, vigorous stirring for 1/2 hour
3. Fill this marmalade hot into glasses and store it like Apricot Marmalade (No. 1870)
4. When cooking, be careful so that the beautiful red color is not lost

title: Paradise Apples Preserved in Vinegar (Paradies-Äpfel in Essig eingelegt)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh, ripe paradise apples
- Wine vinegar (cooled)
- Rendered tallow (finger-thick layer)

## Directions

1. Place fresh, ripe paradise apples in a storage glass
2. Pour cooled wine vinegar over them and over this a finger-thick layer of rendered tallow
3. Bind the glass with a bladder
4. Alternatively, one can also mix vinegar and water in equal parts, boil them and pour this cooled mixture over the paradise apples
5. These can also be preserved instead of in glasses in earthenware or wooden vessels, which should however be narrower at the top so that less tallow is needed for pouring over, which must be melted again and poured over each time paradise apples are taken from the vessel
6. The vessels must moreover either be covered with a fitting lid or sealed airtight with paper or bladders

title: Paradise Apples Preserved in Water (Paradies-Äpfel in Wasser eingelegt)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh paradise apples
- Fresh water

## Directions

1. Wrap fresh paradise apples in vine leaves
2. Place them in a small jar and fill it completely with fresh water, which should be replaced once or twice daily to keep them fresh
3. It is best to use a jar with a tap so that the water can be drained easily
4. The jar must be completely filled with paradise apples

title: Pariser Schüsserl (Parisian Sausage Cups)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25-35 dkg Parisian sausage
- 30 h spinach
- hot fat
- 1 fried egg (optional)

## Directions

1. Carefully slice the sausage so the skin is not torn, otherwise no cup will form.
2. Quickly sear the slices on both sides in hot fat, remove them.
3. Fill with prepared spinach, optionally top with 1 fried egg.

title: Pariser Stangerl (Parisian Rods)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 dkg grated hazelnuts
- 20 dkg sugar
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix 20 dkg grated hazelnuts, 20 dkg sugar and 2 egg whites in a bowl
2. Place the mass on a board sprinkled with sugar
3. Knead it to a dough
4. Roll it out square
5. Brush with thick glaze
6. Cut into oblong pieces
7. Place on a buttered baking sheet
8. Bake at low heat

title: Parisian Schnitzel (Pariiser-Schnitzel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-80 dkg veal leg
- salt
- flour
- egg
- lard for frying
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Pound the veal slices and season with salt
2. Dredge first in flour, then in beaten egg
3. Fry in hot lard
4. Serve the same way as pork schnitzel, garnished with lemon slices

title: Parmesan Sticks
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dkg flour
- 4 dkg butter
- 4 dkg grated Parmesan cheese
- a knife tip of paprika
- a pinch of salt
- 1 spoonful cold water
- beaten egg for brushing
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. On the board rub 6 dkg flour with 4 dkg butter
2. Knead with 4 dkg grated Parmesan cheese, a knife tip of paprika, a pinch of salt and 1 spoonful cold water into dough
3. Roll out thin
4. Cut into very narrow, finger-long strips with a pastry wheel
5. Brush with beaten egg
6. Sprinkle with caraway seeds
7. Bake on lightly buttered sheet in moderate heat until very light brown
8. The thinner and narrower, the better they are
9. They must only be light brown, otherwise they taste bitter

title: Parmesan-Kalbsbraten mit Kartoffelpudding (Parmesan Veal Roast with Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 80-100 dkg veal shoulder or leg
- 6 dkg bacon
- 4 dkg Parmesan
- ½ liter cream
- 5 dkg hot fat
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, season with salt, lard it, place in a roasting pan with 5 dkg hot fat
2. Pour in water and roast in the oven for 1½ hours, basting and adding liquid frequently
3. Mix the meat juices with grated Parmesan and cream
4. Pour over the meat and serve on a long platter

title: Parmesanreis (Parmesan Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dkg butter
- 1 egg
- salt
- grated Parmesan
- 3 dkg breadcrumbs
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Cream together 4 dkg butter, 1 egg, salt, grated Parmesan, and 3 dkg breadcrumbs
2. Press the mixture through a fine grater into boiling beef broth
3. Let cook for 5 minutes

title: Partridges, Roasted (Repphühner, gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- bacon

## Directions

1. Clean the partridges well, wash them, and salt them
2. Cut very thin slices of bacon about the size of a hand
3. Bend the wings and feet of the partridges, dress them well, wrap them in these bacon slices, and roast them on a spit
4. During roasting, baste them frequently with butter
5. Alternatively, instead of wrapping them in bacon slices, one can lard them with finely cut bacon and then roast them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Paschenwürste (Pork and Grain Sausages)
