author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca black fat, diced
- 2 pieces onion, very finely minced
- 32 deca bacon, boiled in salted water for 15 minutes, drained, dried, and finely diced
- 1/2 liter groats (or barley or millet cooked in water or broth)
- Salt
- 1 knife-tip of pepper
- 1 crushed bay leaf
- Garlic, minced, optional
- 1/2 liter pork blood
- Cleaned intestines, well soaked

## Directions

1. Roast the diced black fat and add the finely minced onion.
2. Add the prepared bacon pieces.
3. Mix in the cooled groats (or barley or millet), salt, pepper, crushed bay leaf, and optional garlic.
4. Place the mixture in a deep bowl and add the pork blood when it is slightly overflowing.
5. Fill the cleaned, well-soaked intestines with this mixture, but not too tightly as they may burst during cooking.
6. Tie the intestines with twine every four fingers wide.
7. Boil them at gentle motion in salted water for approximately 10 minutes.
8. After cooling slightly, remove from the broth and store in a cool place.
9. Shortly before use, fry them brown in fat.

title: Pasta Torte (Pasta Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5½ dkg flour
- 2½ dkg butter
- 2 dkg sugar
- ½ egg yolk
- jam
- 5 egg whites
- 21 dkg vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Make a crumb dough from 5½ dkg flour, 2½ dkg butter, 2 dkg sugar, and ½ egg yolk
2. After resting, roll out round (to the size of the torte form bottom) and bake on an ungreased sheet at higher heat
3. Spread with jam
4. Mix stiff egg whites from 5 eggs with 21 dkg vanilla sugar
5. Pile the meringue smoothly and high onto the torte, sprinkle with sugar, optionally pipe rosettes on top, and place in the open oven for a few minutes until the meringue is baked golden

title: Pastete von Filets (Filet Pastry)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half finger-thick dough
- good veal forcemeat
- larded and marinated filets
- braising liquid
- bacon slices
- egg yolk
- sauce haché

## Directions

1. Cover a baking dish with dough rolled half a finger thick and cut round
2. Pipe a thick layer of good veal forcemeat onto the dough
3. Lay the filets, which have been larded, well marinated, cut into pieces, and braised until tender, on top
4. Place bacon slices on the filets
5. Brush the inner edge of the dough with egg yolk
6. Cover with a dough lid, brush the pastry, place on a baking sheet, and bake quickly
7. Cut out the lid, remove the bacon leaves, and pour sauce haché inside
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pasteté mit Aal (Pastry with Eel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One large eel
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Dry herbs
- Egg yolk
- Pike farce
- Coal fire
- Puff pastry
- Anchovy sauce or sauce made from the fond
- Spices

## Directions

1. Cut the head off a large eel
2. Skin it and remove the entrails
3. Remove the bones from it
4. Cut it into pieces two fingers wide
5. Score them once
6. Marinate them in vinegar, salt, and dry herbs
7. Remove them after one hour and dry them off
8. Brush the cuts in the eel with egg yolk
9. Fill them with pike farce
10. Braise it in dry herbs over charcoal fire for a quarter hour
11. Make a base of puff pastry in the previously mentioned manner
12. Lay the eel with balls formed from pike farce on top
13. Brush the pastry base
14. Lay a lid over it and form it nicely round
15. Brush again
16. Bake for three quarters of an hour
17. Pour an anchovy sauce or sauce made from the fond into it, which must be piquant
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pasteté mit Stockfisch (Pastry with Stockfish)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- About 5 pounds well-soaked stockfish
- Cold water
- Fresh water
- Puff pastry
- Egg yolk
- Pike farce balls
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Butter
- Cream sauce
- Good cream

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the soaked stockfish
2. Cut away the skin and divide it into pieces two fingers wide
3. Place it with cold water in a casserole over the fire until after half an hour it begins to draw
4. Pour off the water and pour fresh water over it
5. Repeat this once more until it finally comes to a gentle boil
6. Lay it out and set aside to cool
7. On a prepared tin plate lay puff pastry rolled to a small finger's thickness and cut to size
8. Arrange the stockfish and balls made from pike farce in layers on the same pastry
9. Sprinkle salt, pepper and nutmeg on the stockfish, and between it some small pieces of butter
10. Brush the edge with egg yolk and make a lid that is not too thick over it
11. Pour a cream sauce into it beforehand, for which good cream must be used
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pasteté von Filets (Pastry of Filets)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry
- Good veal farce
- Beautiful larded filets, well marinated
- Good braising liquid
- Bacon slices
- Egg yolk
- Sauce haché

## Directions

1. Cover a tin dish with pastry rolled to half a finger's thickness and cut round all around
2. Pipe a good veal farce somewhat thick
3. Then place the filets, which have been cut into pieces and braised tender in a good braising liquid, on top of it
4. Lay bacon slices on top
5. Brush the inner edge with egg yolk
6. Make a lid from pastry over it
7. Brush the pastry
8. Place on a sheet and bake quickly
9. Cut out the lid
10. Remove the bacon slices
11. Pour a sauce haché into it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pasteté von jungen Hühnern (Pastry of Young Chickens)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Young chickens cut into pieces like for a fricassee
- Melted butter
- Onions
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bunch of fine herbs
- Nutmeg
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Puff pastry
- Veal farce
- Wine
- Bacon slices
- Egg yolk
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place young chickens cut into pieces like for a fricassee on a clean cloth and well-washed
2. Braise them with a large piece of melted butter, onions, salt, pepper, a bunch of fine herbs, and nutmeg in a casserole
3. Dust 2 spoonfuls of flour into it
4. Shake it well together
5. Pour in about as much bouillon as needed
6. Let this sauce cook down until it becomes thick
7. Set it aside with the chickens to cool
8. Arrange the chickens with balls formed from veal farce on the puff pastry base laid on a dish, quite neatly
9. Sprinkle the chickens with some wine
10. Lastly lay bacon slices on top
11. Make a lid from pastry over it
12. Brush the pastry before baking
13. Once it is already yellowish, cut out the lid
14. Bind the remaining sauce with egg yolk
15. Add lemon juice
16. Finally pour it hot over the middle and pass it through a sieve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pea Pudding (Erbſenkoch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh, clean peas
- water
- butter
- roll, roasted in butter
- ten eggs
- eight egg yolks
- one pound sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Cook fresh, clean peas in water until half done, so that the skins come off by themselves
2. Remove the skins and let the peas dry
3. Pound them finely and smooth in a mortar
4. Rub in a mixing bowl a piece of butter under them, as well as roll roasted in butter
5. Beat ten eggs in it, always stirring very tender and smooth
6. Add eight egg yolks and stir everything well together again
7. Finally pour in one pound sifted sugar and stop stirring until it becomes completely smooth and tender
8. Put this batter in the dish and mold coated with butter, let it bake, and serve it well garnished and warm, for all fat-made puddings must be eaten warm
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pea Puree
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg dried peas
- cold water
- baking soda
- 6 dkg fat
- onion
- salt

## Directions

1. Sort and wash 1/2 kg yellow or green peas
2. Place in a pot with twice as much cold water and a little baking soda
3. Cover and cook for 1½ hours, stirring occasionally
4. Add a light onion roux made with 6 dkg fat
5. Pass through a sieve, dilute with water or broth if necessary, season with salt
6. Cook thoroughly
7. Arrange on a round platter, make steps with a round knife, and pour a browned onion rings in fat into the center so it runs down the steps

title: Pea Purée Soup (Purée de pois)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces butter
- 1/2 liter fresh peas, thoroughly cleaned and washed
- some parsley
- 1 piece butter
- 1 whole onion
- a small piece of raw, lean ham
- 1/2 liter beef soup
- salt
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Braise 1/2 liter of fresh peas in 16 ounces butter with some parsley, 1 piece butter, 1 whole onion, a small piece of raw lean ham and 1/2 liter beef soup
2. When the peas are soft, remove the onion and ham
3. Stir the peas well and pass through a sieve
4. Thin this purée with the necessary beef soup and let it come to a boil before serving
5. Season with salt and if desired add a knife-tip of white pepper
6. Alternatively, instead of fresh peas, 1/4 liter of dried peas can be used, in which case use only 2 ounces of sugar instead of 4

title: Pea Soup with Snails (Puree de pois aux limaces)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 cooked snails
- finely cut green parsley
- onion
- lemon peel
- 4 drams semolina breadcrumbs
- butter
- cream
- 2 whole eggs
- toasted semolina slices

## Directions

1. Clean the cooked snails and cut them finely
2. Mix with finely cut green parsley, onion, and lemon peel
3. Toast semolina breadcrumbs in butter and add along with some cream
4. Simmer well
5. Add 2 whole eggs, stir well together
6. Immediately spread on toasted semolina slices
7. Place the slices in a casserole whose bottom is covered with butter and keep warm covered
8. Before serving, pour good pea soup over them

title: Peach Frozen Dessert (Glace aux pêches)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 to 15 pieces beautiful peaches
- 28 deca grams sugar
- 1/8 liter cold water
- Juice of 2 lemons
- Almond essence or Maraschino liqueur

## Directions

1. Remove the stones from the peaches and pass them through a fine sieve into a deep bowl
2. Add 28 deca grams sugar dissolved in 1/8 liter cold water and the juice of 2 lemons to this mass
3. Stir in some almond essence or Maraschino liqueur so the liquid obtains a pale pink color
4. Pass the mass again through a sieve, weigh it to 22 degrees, and proceed further as with the 'Ordinary Strawberry Frozen Dessert' (Recipe 1746)

title: Peach Marmalade (Marmelade von Pfirſichen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pounds peaches of the best variety and sufficient ripeness
- 4 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the kernels from the peaches.
2. Press the peaches through a sieve so that only the skins remain.
3. Clarify 4 pounds of sugar according to the method for marmalade.
4. Stir the peach pulp into the clarified sugar.
5. Fill into jars or containers.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pear Compote (Birnen-Compot)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- cold water
- lemon juice
- sugar
- sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Peel the pears, cut them in half, and remove the kernels.
2. Place immediately in cold water with lemon juice to keep them white.
3. Cook them in syrup until very soft, also adding some lemon juice.
4. Once the syrup is cooked down to short, arrange the pears on the compote dish and pour the syrup over them.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pear Ice (Gefrornes von Birnen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 ripe bergamot or butter pears
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel about 30 ripe bergamot or butter pears
2. Cut into 4 pieces
3. Remove the cores
4. Cook with some water to a marmalade
5. Pass through a fine sieve into a terrine
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pear Marmalade (Marmelade von Birnen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds pears (such as Isenbart or Bergamotten variety)
- 4 pounds sugar
- fresh water

## Directions

1. Peel the pears, cut them into four pieces, remove the cores, and place in fresh water.
2. Cook the pears in the water for some time until they become soft.
3. Pour off the water and press the pear pulp through a sieve into a soup pot.
4. Clarify the 4 pounds of sugar, cook it, and skim off the foam.
5. Stir the pear pulp into the sugar.
6. Place on charcoal fire and stir constantly until it reaches the proper thickness.
7. Once cooled, fill into appropriate containers or jars.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Perl Graupen-Suppe (Pearl Barley Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 spoonfuls rolled barley (graupen)
- 0.75 liter white broth
- clear white or brown broth

## Directions

1. Place 16 spoonfuls rolled barley in a casserole
2. Pour 0.75 liter white broth over it and let it cook slowly until soft, covered
3. Remove the barley with a sachet spoon or press through a sieve
4. Add to clear white or brown broth in the soup pot

title: Petersilie, grün aufzubewahren (Parsley Preserved Green)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- butter

## Directions

1. In autumn when parsley is still good and vigorous, pick as much as you will need throughout the winter, clean it well, wash it thoroughly, and chop it very finely.
2. Take a good portion of butter and let it become hot, add the chopped parsley, let it fry a little, and then let it stand for a few minutes, whereupon remove it with a spoon.
3. Be sure to drain off the juice that has settled at the bottom, otherwise it will spoil; then place it in another vessel, let it melt again, and let it cool, and preserve it in this manner.
4. Prepared in this way it will keep for a long time and can be used very well.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Petersilien-Sauce (Parsley Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- Butter-Sauce (from recipe 186)

## Directions

1. Shortly before serving, add 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley to the strained Butter-Sauce
2. Stir well and bring to a boil once

title: Petersilienbutter (Parsley Butter)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one good handful finely chopped fresh parsley
- 1/4 pound butter

## Directions

1. Pound together the finely chopped parsley with 1/4 pound butter
2. Press through a hair sieve
3. Store for later use
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pfannen-Kuchen (Pancakes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 whole eggs
- 0.25 liter milk
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 coffee spoon salt
- 0.5 liter flour
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Beat 5 whole eggs with 0.25 liter milk, 1 tablespoon sugar and 1 coffee spoon salt together and gradually pour onto 0.5 liter flour while constantly stirring
2. When the batter is smooth, pour 1 tablespoon hot fat on a hot frying pan and evenly pour over it 3 tablespoons of the above-mentioned batter
3. When the lower side is baked brown, turn the batter over with a spatula and bake the second side brown as well
4. Lift the pancake with a spatula into a bowl, keep it warm and continue baking in this manner until the batter is finished
5. Coat the baked pancakes with preserved fruit as desired, roll together, cut into pieces as desired and arrange on a bowl
6. The above-mentioned batter can also be prepared as desired without the addition of sugar, and in this case coat the baked pieces with grated Parmesan cheese, egg dishes and the like instead of preserved fruit

title: Pfannenkuchen (Pancakes with Cherry Filling)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds fine flour
- 12 eggs
- 3/4 pound butter
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 3/8 pound ground sugar
- 3 spoonfuls good beer yeast
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- salt
- good cherry preserves
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- wine
- egg yolk
- lard for frying

## Directions

1. Keep 2 pounds fine flour warm in a bowl and 12 eggs equally warm in lukewarm water
2. Melt 3/4 pound butter in 1 cup sweet cream over a fire, but the milk must not boil
3. Beat all the eggs well and froth them, then stir in 3/8 pound ground sugar, 3 spoonfuls good beer yeast along with the eggs into the flour
4. Beat this until it becomes a dough that loosens from the pan, add cinnamon and lemon zest as desired, and also a bit of salt
5. Lay the dough on a floured baking table, work it together a bit, and then roll it out the thickness of a knife back
6. Prepare good cherry preserves, which must be cooked not too thick with sugar, cinnamon, lemon zest, and wine
7. Lay 2 spoonfuls of preserves in heaps on the dough, brush the dough around each heap of preserves with egg yolk
8. Beat a piece of dough the same size as the bottom over it, press down firmly around the preserves, and cut out these pancakes with a pastry wheel
9. Place them in a moderately warm location so they begin to rise
10. Then fry them slowly in lard, shaking the pan frequently during baking
11. When they are nice and golden, lay them on blotting paper and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pfeffers-Karpfen (Carp with Pepper Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decilitres goose fat or butter
- 1 head of finely chopped onion
- crushed garlic if desired
- the cleaned soft roe from the fish
- 2-finger wide and 1-finger long carp pieces
- the fish head, cut into small pieces
- some caraway
- 1 small piece onion
- root vegetables and thyme
- approximately 1 1/2 litres water
- 1/4 litre wine or 3/4 litre water and 1/8 litre vinegar if desired
- 8 decilitres blanched and finely pounded almonds
- white pepper
- salt to taste
- a small piece of sugar if desired

## Directions

1. In a casserole, place 8 decilitres goose fat or butter and a head of finely chopped onion, with crushed garlic and cleaned soft roe from the fish if desired
2. Let this roast a bit, then add the carp pieces cut into 2-finger wide and 1-finger long pieces
3. Let this simmer covered
4. Meanwhile, break the fish head into small pieces and let it cook with some caraway, 1 small piece onion, root vegetables and thyme, and approximately 1 1/2 litres water, and 1/4 litre wine or 3/4 litre water and 1/8 litre vinegar if desired
5. When this is well cooked, pour the broth over the steamed fish and let it come to a boil with 8 decilitres blanched and finely pounded almonds
6. Carefully place the fish pieces on a platter and keep them in a warm place until serving
7. Meanwhile, stir and cook the sauce well, strain it through a sieve into a platter, place it in a casserole and add white pepper, the necessary salt, and a small piece of sugar if desired
8. The sauce must be thick-liquid and piquant

title: Pfirsich-Marmelade (Peach Preserves)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe peaches
- powdered sugar equal in weight to the peaches without kernels
- currant preserves

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as the Plum Preserves (recipe 1875).
2. Choose beautiful ripe garden peaches and must work very quickly, always immediately and well coating the peaches in sugar after peeling.
3. A few minutes before removing this preserves from the heat, stir in some spoonfuls of currant preserves, which gives the peaches a hint of red color.

title: Pfirsiche (Preserved Peaches)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 pieces of the finest, ripe but not overripe, fairly hard peaches
- 5 pounds white sugar
- half a pound of spring water
- 5 to 6 minutes later
- 3 pounds strong spirits of wine
- 1 pound spirits of wine (added after 2 months)

## Directions

1. Wipe the peaches clean and pierce them with a strong needle
2. Dissolve 5 pounds white sugar with half a pound of spring water, skim in a pan, and cook gently until the sugar reaches the feather stage (when a small sample thrown into the air with a spatula forms feather-like flakes)
3. Remove the pan from the fire, wait 5 to 6 minutes, then bring the fruits in with a wooden spoon and stir them, simmer over gentle fire, stirring the peaches until they are uniformly warmed, then remove them individually and place on a sieve to be returned to the syrup from the pan removed from fire, adding what dripped from the sieve
4. Let everything stand cold for 24 hours, then repeat the process; the pan stands cold for 48 hours, and after it is boiled again, let the mixture cool
5. Place the peaches on the sieve, pour 3 pounds of strong spirits of wine onto the syrup, stir it well together, strain it through, and bring the strained liquid into a suitable container; let one fruit after another fall in, close the container tightly, place it in a cool location, shake it every 3 days until all fruits have sunk to the bottom
6. After 2 months add another pound of spirits of wine
7. Fruits prepared this way should retain color, smell, and taste for over a year
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pfirsiche, Marillen (Peaches and Apricots)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches of good quality
- apricots of good quality
- lemon juice
- cold lemon water
- boiling lemon water
- 80 decilitres sugar
- 1 litre water

## Directions

1. Choose good quality fruits that are somewhat hard and picked in dry weather
2. Cut peaches and apricots in half, remove the stones, carefully peel the fruits
3. Rub with lemon juice
4. Place first in cold, then in boiling lemon water
5. Cook peaches until fairly soft, leave apricots in only a moment
6. Pass three times through fresh cold water
7. Treat further as the Muscatel pears (Nr. 1842), using 80 decilitres sugar in 1 litre water instead of 60

title: Pfirſich-Gefrornes (Peach Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 peaches
- 1 pound peach puree
- Three quarter pound clarified sugar
- 2 small glasses Muscat wine
- Juice of 3 lemons
- Zest of 1 lemon rubbed on sugar
- Warm wine

## Directions

1. Peel 24 peaches and very carefully remove the flesh from the pits and press through a hair sieve
2. For 1 pound peach puree take three quarter pound clarified sugar, 2 small glasses Muscat wine, and the juice of 3 lemons; rub the zest of 1 lemon on sugar
3. Stir everything well together with a spatula
4. Pour a glass of warm wine into the hair sieve over the remainder of the peaches and press through
5. Then place everything in the freezing pan, pouring the wine in very slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pflaumen (Zwetſchken) lange aufzubewahren, auf andere Art (Long-Preserved Plums, Another Method)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums (Zwetschken)
- Wheat bran

## Directions

1. Pick the plums before they detach from the stem, thus with the stem still attached
2. Lay them in a dry chamber for 2-3 days
3. Then layer them in wheat bran, but without them touching one another
4. They will keep themselves for several months
5. When they are to come to the table, hold them for some time over water vapor so they swell up and look fresh
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pheasant with Lentil Puree (Faisan à la purée de lentille)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant, dressed and trussed
- Bacon slices
- 1.5 liters cooked and passed lentils
- Stock and white wine as for steamed pheasant

## Directions

1. Prepare as for steamed pheasant
2. Deglaze and defat the pheasant's juices
3. Mix the defatted juices with 1.5 liters of cooked and passed lentils thoroughly
4. Carve the pheasant, arrange on a platter, and garnish with the lentil puree

title: Pheasant, English Style (Fasan auf englische Art)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one beautiful pheasant
- forcemeat/farce
- bacon
- ham
- lemon peel
- butter
- ham
- cloves
- bay leaves
- mushrooms
- flour
- brown broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from a beautiful pheasant
2. Fill it with forcemeat and reshape it
3. Bind it with bacon, ham, and lemon peel in paper
4. Place it on a casserole lid brushed with butter and put it in the oven
5. For the sauce: let some butter become warm in a pan, add ham, cloves, bay leaves, and finely cut mushrooms
6. Dust some flour over it, pour some brown broth on top, and let it simmer
7. Finally add some lemon juice, let it boil, skim off the fat, and pour this short sauce over the pheasant
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pheasant, Roasted (Fasan, gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pheasant
- butter
- lard/bacon
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Clean the pheasant thoroughly by plucking it
2. Remove the entrails and wash it well
3. Salt it well
4. Lard it neatly with finely cut bacon
5. Roast it frequently basting with butter
6. When roasted, garnish it with lemon peel and serve it basted with the juices on the table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pickled Calf's Brain (Cervelle de veau en vinaigrette)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brain
- Water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bay leaf
- 1/2 liter tarragon vinegar
- Soup stock
- Tarragon sprigs
- Chervil sprigs
- Green parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned, skinned brain in equal parts water and vinegar
2. When the brain has completely cooled in this liquid, remove it and place on a cloth
3. Mix 1/2 liter of tarragon vinegar with good soup stock, season with salt, pepper and a bay leaf, and cook well
4. Cook tarragon sprigs, chervil sprigs and green parsley in salt water briefly, then drain well and chop very finely with a knife
5. Place this chopped mixture with the brain in a casserole, pour the prepared vinegar over it, let it come to a boil once and serve in a dish

title: Pickled Cucumbers with Mustard Seeds (Senfgurken)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- bitter mustard seeds
- sweet mustard seeds
- vinegar (see No. 1901)

## Directions

1. Remove the cores carefully and cover the cucumbers in an earthenware vessel overnight.
2. The next day, lay them on a hair sieve or cloth so they rest evenly and water can drain well.
3. Pack them very tightly in half-liter jars or other suitable containers, placing the cucumbers upright lengthwise.
4. Add 1 teaspoon of bitter mustard seeds and 1 teaspoon of sweet mustard seeds to each jar.
5. Fill with vinegar (see No. 1901), seal with beeswax, and let them steam for half an hour.
6. These cucumbers are superior to so-called salt cucumbers both in flavor and digestibility.
7. Peeled and pierced cucumbers can be treated the same way, but larger jars must be used accordingly and they must steam a quarter hour longer.

title: Pig's Ears with Parmesan Cheese (Schweinsohren mit Parmesankäse)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's ears
- Braise
- Butter
- Bread crumbs
- Grated Parmesan cheese
- Lard
- Piquant sauce
- Sauce Robert

## Directions

1. Clean the ears well and cook them soft in a braise.
2. When cold, turn them in butter.
3. Coat with bread crumbs and sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese on top.
4. Fry quickly in lard until they have a yellowish color.
5. Grill them as you would ox palates.
6. Serve with piquant sauce and Sauce Robert underneath.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pignolibuſſerln (Pine Nut Meringues)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg vanilla sugar
- 1-2 egg whites
- 14 dkg pine nuts

## Directions

1. Mix 14 dkg vanilla sugar with 1-2 egg whites
2. Fold in 14 dkg pine nuts
3. Make small mounds with 2 spoons on a buttered baking sheet
4. Bake at low heat

title: Pignolikipferl (Pine Nut Crescents)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg raw grated almonds or hazelnuts
- 7 dkg sugar
- 1-2 egg whites
- pine nuts

## Directions

1. Mix 14 dkg raw grated almonds or hazelnuts with 7 dkg sugar and 1-2 egg whites on a board sprinkled with sugar
2. Knead to a dough
3. Form into a roll
4. Cut pieces from it
5. Form each piece into a crescent
6. Roll the crescents in pine nuts, pressing them in slightly so they stick
7. Place the crescents on a buttered baking sheet
8. Bake at low heat until light brown

title: Pike Fillets (Filets de brochet)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pike
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- paper
- onion
- parsley root
- pike head and bones
- flour
- oil or fat
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons cream
- juice of 1 lemon
- fine dumplings

## Directions

1. Skin the pike, remove the rib bones and other remaining bones, and cut the flesh into pieces 2-3 fingers wide, 1 finger long, and half-finger thick
2. Butter a casserole with knife-back thickness of butter
3. Arrange the cut pieces next to each other, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and cover with a round-cut piece of paper brushed with butter
4. 1/4 hour before serving, place the casserole on fire, remove the paper, and fry the pieces 2-3 minutes on each side turning them over
5. While the pieces sit covered, make the following sauce: put 16 deca butter, some finely sliced onion and parsley root, the broken pike head and bones into a casserole
6. Let roast for a short time, dust with flour, add 1/4 liter wine and equal amount stock and some salt and let come to a boil
7. Strain everything and may be finished as desired with 3 egg yolks, 3 tablespoons cream and juice of 1 lemon, and pour over the pieces
8. Serve with any fine dumplings
9. Alternative preparations: pieces may be prepared like half-schnitzels and either dipped in flour and fried in finger-high fat, or treated like breaded schnitzels
10. Garnish with lemon slices and serve salad with it

title: Pike Fillets with Fine Herbs (Filet de brochet aux fines-herbes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 bunches chervil
- 5 bunches parsley
- chervil herb
- thyme
- 1 piece mushroom
- pike fillets
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop very finely with a knife: chervil, parsley, chervil herb, thyme (5 bunches each) and 1 piece mushroom
2. Place the pike pieces described in the previous recipe in the butter-brushed casserole, sprinkle with the chopped herbs and fry as indicated before
3. Prepare sauce as indicated in the pike fillets recipe and either pour over the arranged pieces or serve in a sauceboat
4. Serve with any fine dumplings or potatoes

title: Pike Grilled (Brochet grillé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- breadcrumbs
- clarified butter
- green mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Clean, open, and wash a pike, cut it lengthwise along the back, and remove the back and rib bones
2. Cut into 2-finger-wide and 2-finger-long pieces, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and let sit for some time
3. Dip in flour and breadcrumbs, dry quickly, dip in clarified butter, and sprinkle well with breadcrumbs
4. Grill on both sides over medium-strong fire
5. Serve green mustard sauce with it
6. Alternative: instead of on the grill, fish pieces may also be quickly fried in finger-high, very hot fat in a flat casserole

title: Pike in Fricassee (Hecht in einer Fricassee)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- gallbladder from liver
- salt
- wine vinegar
- water
- onions
- bay leaves
- fricassee broth
- clear pea broth or white fish consommé

## Directions

1. Scale and clean a pike thoroughly
2. Carefully remove the gallbladder from the liver (only the liver and gallbladder from pike are usable, not the rest of the entrails)
3. Salt it and let it rest in salt for a while
4. Cook it in half vinegar and half water with some onions and bay leaves
5. Meanwhile prepare a fricassee broth using clear pea broth or, better if available, a white fish consommé instead of meat broth
6. Place the fish on the dish and the fricassee over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pike Pudding (Hechtenkoch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one good piece baked pike
- one roll
- milk
- butter
- three whole eggs
- four egg yolks
- lemon peel
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pick the flesh from a good piece of baked pike away from the bones, but first remove the brown skin
2. Cut the flesh finely with a knife
3. Cook the crumb of one roll in milk like a panada and let it cool
4. Meanwhile beat a piece of butter fluffy
5. Beat in three whole eggs and four egg yolks
6. Add the panada, the cut pike, some lemon peel and sugar to taste
7. Coat a dish and mold with butter, pour the pudding in
8. Coat it with foamed egg white, sprinkle it with sugar, and bake it beautifully in a cake pan
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pike with Paprika Sauce (Brochet au paprika)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- paprika sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare as carp with paprika sauce (Recipe 884)

title: Pineapple Frozen Dessert (Glace à l'ananas)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small pineapple
- 12 deca grams sugar
- 12 deca grams sugar
- 1/4 liter cold water
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Peel 1 small pineapple and cut it into slices
2. Pound it with 12 deca grams sugar in a stone mortar
3. Dissolve 12 deca grams sugar in 1/4 liter cold water, mix this with the juice of 1 lemon into the pounded pineapple
4. Weigh the mass to 22 degrees, pass it through a fine sieve, and proceed further as with the 'Ordinary Strawberry Frozen Dessert' (Recipe 1746)

title: Pischinger Torte (Pischinger Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 whole eggs
- 12½ dkg sugar
- 5 dkg grated chocolate
- 15 dkg good butter
- 4-5 Karlsbader wafers
- 8-10 dkg butter
- 10 dkg vanilla sugar
- 4 dkg grated chocolate
- 2 egg yolks
- 5 dkg grated hazelnuts
- swelled chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Beat 3 whole eggs with 12½ dkg sugar and 5 dkg grated chocolate over steam with the whisk until thick
2. Cool and stir in 15 dkg good butter until the mass is smooth
3. Spread this cream on 4-5 Karlsbader wafers
4. The torte can be spread with cream on top and around and sprinkled with swelled chopped almonds; or make less cream and frost it brown
5. Alternatively, use a cold stirred cream: cream together 8-10 dkg butter with 10 dkg vanilla sugar, 4 dkg grated chocolate, 2 egg yolks, and 5 dkg grated hazelnuts

title: Pistazie-Creme (Pistachio Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a measure cream
- good piece of sugar
- quarter pound pistachios
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take half a measure cream, add good piece of sugar, and cook on fire
2. Shell a quarter pound pistachios and pound them fine
3. Beat 6 egg yolks in a pot
4. Slowly stir in pistachios and cream so everything combines well
5. Pass the cream through a hair sieve
6. Fill into bowls, not too full
7. Place bowls in a casserole with simmering water, watching carefully so water does not enter the bowls
8. Place some charcoal fire on the lid
9. When done, serve cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pistazien-Creme (Pistachio Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound pistachios
- one good spoonful brandy
- 1 seidel cream
- the yolk of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Grind one pound pistachios with one good spoonful brandy finely in a mortar
2. Put them in a casserole, pour in 1 seidel cream, beat in the yolk of 2 eggs, and whisk vigorously
3. Stir it over charcoal fire until it has an oblong thickness, only it must not boil
4. When cold, it can be served as cream
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pistazien-Gefrorenes (Pistachio Ice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Vanilla Ice prepared
- 10 pieces pistachios

## Directions

1. Prepare Vanilla Ice (Recipe 1720) and when it is nearly frozen, mix in 10 pieces pistachios that have been blanched, peeled and cut very finely

title: Piſtazien-Creme (Pistachio Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a measure cream
- A good piece of sugar
- A quarter pound pistachios
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take half a measure cream, throw a good piece of sugar in it and cook it down on the fire
2. Then peel a quarter pound pistachios and pound them clean
3. Beat 6 egg yolks in a pot and stir long
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Piſtazien-Gefrornes (Pistachio Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half measures sweet cream
- 1 pound pistachios
- Fresh water
- Lemon zest rubbed on sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- Three quarter pound clarified, cooled sugar
- Spinach drops

## Directions

1. To one and a half measures sweet cream add 1 pound pistachios, whose skins must be cleaned, and whose kernels are soaked in fresh water for a while
2. Drain and dry them thoroughly
3. Pound them fine in a mortar with some cream and lemon zest rubbed on sugar
4. Beat 10 egg yolks well in a pot and stir in three quarter pound clarified, cooled sugar
5. Stir the remaining cream with the whole mixture over gentle charcoal fire, stirring constantly until it begins to thicken slightly
6. Then work in a little spinach drops, remove from fire, press everything through a fine hair sieve
7. Stir until cooled and finally pour into the freezing pan
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Plant Leaves (Pflanzen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Young cabbage or turnip plant leaves
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 18 drams butter
- 18 drams flour
- Garlic (if desired)
- Onion (if desired)
- ½ liter broth
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves from the stems, wash them, and cook them uncovered in salt water.
2. Just before serving, make a roux from 18 drams butter and 18 drams flour; if desired, add some garlic and onion; pour the roasted flour with ½ liter broth over it, season with salt, and stir for 5 minutes.
3. Once this has boiled well, add the cooked, chopped plant leaves, let them boil again, and serve.
4. May also be prepared like Spinach No. 296.

title: Plum Croquettes (Zwetschgen-Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 kilo dried plums, half-cooked and finely cut
- 16 tablespoons blanched, finely pounded almonds
- 8 tablespoons sugar
- 8 tablespoons breadcrumbs, decrumbed, soaked in water, and well squeezed out
- some finely cut lemon zest

## Directions

1. Beat 8 tablespoons butter until fluffy
2. Gradually stir in 4 egg yolks
3. Mix in 1/4 kilo of dried, half-cooked, finely cut plums, 16 tablespoons of blanched, finely pounded almonds, 8 tablespoons sugar, 8 tablespoons of decrumbed breadcrumbs soaked in water and well squeezed out, and some finely cut lemon zest
4. Mix all ingredients well together

title: Plum Marmalade (Marmelade von Pflaumen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- sugar (equal weight to the plum juice)
- water

## Directions

1. Remove the kernels from the plums.
2. Place them in a newly glazed pot with a lid tied securely on top.
3. Place the pot in a vessel of boiling water and keep it there for a good hour, ensuring no water enters the pot.
4. Remove the pot from the water.
5. Pass the plums through a fine sieve.
6. Measure the juice and add an equal weight of clarified sugar.
7. Treat as with the preceding marmalades.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Plum Pepper
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- dried plums
- clove spice
- lemon peel
- sugar
- red wine
- butter sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare this dish like dried plums in sauce
2. The only difference is that the softly cooked plums are finely cut with a sharp knife
3. These are then added to the butter sauce with crushed clove spice, some finely cut lemon peel, and as much sugar and red wine as desired
4. Let simmer

title: Plum-Pudding
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pfund butter
- 1 Pfund fine dry flour
- 16 egg yolks
- 1/2 Pfund sugar
- zest of two lemons
- 1 Seidel sweet cream
- 1/2 Pfund small pitted raisins
- 3 Pfund large pitted raisins
- 10 egg whites
- fine-cut candied citron
- currants
- linen napkin

## Directions

1. Beat 2 pounds butter to foam
2. Stir in 1 pound fine dry flour and 16 egg yolks, adding several spoonfuls of flour after each egg is added, finishing with both together
3. Dissolve 1/2 pound sugar, over which the zest of two lemons has been grated, in 1 Seidel sweet cream
4. Stir this under the mass, along with 1/2 pound small and 3 pounds large pitted raisins
5. Fold in the snow of 10 eggs
6. Spread the butter-greased napkin over a suitable casserole or other dish, strewn with fine-cut candied citron and currants
7. Bind the mass in loosely so it can swell
8. Let it cook for 2 hours without interruption
9. When serving, pour a thick wine sauce over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Plums in Vinegar (Zwetschgen in Essig)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kilos beautiful fresh plums with bloom still on them
- 3/4 liter vinegar
- 1 kilo sugar
- Cinnamon
- Nutmeg
- Cloves
- Allspice

## Directions

1. Trim the stems slightly on each plum and pierce each plum five times near the pit
2. Place in an earthen vessel
3. Meanwhile, cook 3/4 liter vinegar with 1 kilo sugar, add cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, and allspice tied in a cloth, and let it boil for 1.5 hours
4. Pour over the plums and let them stand overnight
5. On the next day, place them with their juice on the stove, let them become hot and then cool again
6. On the third day, place the plums in preservation jars and pour the juice over them

title: Plums-Pudding von Biscuit (Plum Pudding with Biscuit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca flour for biscuit
- various fruits, citron, almonds, pistachios, dates as desired
- 1/2 liter milk or cream
- 3 whole eggs
- 5 egg yolks
- 8 deca finely pounded fruit

## Directions

1. Prepare and bake a biscuit from 14 deca flour
2. After completely cooling, cut round or into cubes
3. Place in a buttered and floured mold whose bottom has been lined with various fruits, citron, almonds, pistachios, dates in desired pattern, or alternately light and dark biscuit pieces
4. Cover with remaining biscuit mixed with desired fruits
5. Beat together 1/2 liter milk or cream with 3 whole eggs, 5 egg yolks, and 8 deca finely pounded fruit
6. Pour evenly over the biscuit
7. Steam covered for 1 hour

title: Poached Fish with Butter (Gekochter Fisch mit Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ liter vinegar
- 8 tablespoons salt
- root vegetables
- 1 onion
- 5 peppercorns
- 2 bay leaves
- thyme
- fish
- hot butter
- chopped parsley
- potatoes
- Hollandaise sauce

## Directions

1. In the fish pan, cook ¼ liter vinegar, 8 tablespoons salt, root vegetables, 1 onion, 5 peppercorns, 2 bay leaves and thyme for 30 minutes
2. Lower the fish into the pan, pull the pan back (the fish must not boil as it will fall apart)
3. Let the fish steep without boiling for 8-10 minutes
4. Lift it out carefully, tie it up, place quickly on the fish platter
5. Drip with hot butter, sprinkle with chopped parsley, garnish with potatoes
6. Serve with hot butter or Hollandaise sauce separately
7. Slice the vegetables and lay them on the rim of the platter

title: Pofeſen mit Hirn (Bread Slices with Brain)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-5 bread rolls (Schneidſemmeln)
- 1 pork or calf brain (Schweins- oder Kalbshirn)
- 1/4 liter milk

## Directions

1. Cut the bread rolls into slices, keeping each pair connected together
2. Spread the inner side with roasted brain (see Evening Dishes: Brain with Egg)
3. Dip the slices in salted milk
4. Bread them (paniér)
5. Fry them in hot lard until golden

title: Pofeſen mit Spinat (Bread Slices with Spinach)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread roll slices
- milk
- spinach
- cooked lettuce
- lentils

## Directions

1. Dip individual bread slices in milk
2. Bread them and fry in hot lard
3. Serve as a fasting dish with spinach, cooked lettuce, and browned lentils

title: Pohlnischer Salat (Polish Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarters taffet or fine herrings apples
- one quarter winter radish
- onion
- pair of herring, deboned
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- oil
- herring milk (if using soft roe herring)

## Directions

1. Peel three quarters of the taffet or fine herrings apples and one quarter of the winter radish
2. Cut them, removing the seed casings and all pithy parts, into slices
3. Finely chop these slices together with an onion and a pair of deboned herrings
4. Season with salt, pepper, vinegar, and oil
5. If using soft roe herrings, the salad gains greatly in flavor if one first curddles the milk in vinegar and then pours it over
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Poitrine de Veau Rôtie (Roasted Veal Breast)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal breast
- salt
- pepper, as desired
- butter for roasting
- warm butter for basting
- meat drippings

## Directions

1. After quickly rinsing the veal breast and drying it with a cloth, rub it with salt and pepper as desired
2. Place it in a roasting pan in which some butter is present
3. Cover the meat with warm butter and roast it in the oven for approximately 4½ hours, basting frequently with the drippings that form
4. When the meat is half roasted, loosen the bones and continue roasting until it has a brown color
5. Remove the meat from the pan, cut it from the thick side lengthwise into pieces of desired size, usually as thick as a knife back
6. Arrange the pieces on a serving dish and pour the defatted drippings alongside

title: Polenta
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter fat broth
- 1 tablespoon polenta flour
- 1/2 kilo polenta groats or flour
- hot butter
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Put 1 liter of fat broth into a fairly large casserole
2. Add 1 tablespoon polenta flour
3. When the broth boils, add 1/2 kilo polenta groats or flour
4. Make a cross in the flour with a knife, cut through it
5. Cook uncovered for 1/4 hour
6. Remove the polenta with a spoon and put it into a dish
7. Pour hot butter over it and sprinkle Parmesan cheese on top, which is also served separately

title: Polenta (Polenta)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter salt water
- 1 tablespoon Polenta flour
- 1/2 kilo Polenta grain or flour
- hot butter
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Place 1 liter of salt water in a rather large casserole
2. Add 1 tablespoon Polenta flour
3. When the water boils, pour in 1/2 kilo Polenta grain or flour
4. Make a cross in the flour with a knife, cut through it, and boil uncovered for 1/4 hour
5. Remove the Polenta with a spoon, place it in a bowl
6. Pour hot butter over it and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, which is served separately

title: Polnische Sauce (Polish Sauce: Sauce à la polonaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons ground almonds
- 8 tablespoons raisins
- 1 clove spice
- 8 tablespoons currants
- 2 tablespoons cinnamon
- 1 1/2 liters wine
- 6 tablespoons butter
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 4 tablespoons flour mixed with 4 tablespoons breadcrumbs
- optional: 4 tablespoons black bread or equal amount rose hips
- optional: 2 tablespoons raisins, equal amount rose hips, and equal amount cooked young turnips diced finely

## Directions

1. Put ground almonds, raisins, 1 clove, currants, and cinnamon in a saucepan
2. Pour in 1 1/2 liters wine and let it boil thoroughly
3. Meanwhile put 6 tablespoons butter in a saucepan and let it become hot
4. Add 4 tablespoons sugar to this
5. When this becomes brown, let 4 tablespoons flour mixed with 4 tablespoons breadcrumbs roast in it
6. Add what was cooked with the wine, stir it well together and serve this sauce with freshly formed veal cutlets and also with sliced fish
7. You can also cook 4 tablespoons black bread or equal amount rose hips with it, and if desired, also 2 tablespoons raisins, equal amount rose hips, and equal amount cooked, finely diced young turnips and serve them with it

title: Polnische Suppe (Polish Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/8 liter peas
- 1/8 liter beans
- 1/8 liter lentils
- 3 tablespoons groats
- 2 tablespoons rice
- some diced root vegetables
- some diced potatoes
- some salt
- some spices
- 8 Deciliter diced bacon
- either 4 kilos smoked meat (so-called Kaiser-fleisch), or 2 doves, or 1/2 kilo calf meat, or 1 young chicken
- 3 liters water
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Put peas, beans, lentils, groats, rice, diced root vegetables, diced potatoes, salt, spices, and diced bacon in a pot
2. Cut meat into arbitrary pieces and add to the above
3. Cover with 3 liters water and cook slowly
4. When it is well cooked such that the meat is tender and the soup is thick, add toasted bread and serve

title: Polnische Zunge (Beef Tongue Polish Style)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- 3/8 liter stock
- 1/4 liter soup
- sour cream
- capers
- 2 tablespoons currants
- 2 tablespoons small raisins
- 2 tablespoons finely sliced almonds or 2 tablespoons pignoli nuts cut into 2 pieces
- lemon peel
- diced lemon
- 1 tablespoon smoked, cooked, diced tongue
- 1 knife-tip juniper berries if desired

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue in the usual way
2. Prepare the caper sauce as above, but pour 3/8 liter stock and 1/4 liter soup over the browned meat
3. After adding cream and capers, add 2 tablespoons currants, 2 tablespoons small raisins, 2 tablespoons finely sliced almonds or pignoli nuts, lemon peel, diced lemon, 1 tablespoon smoked cooked diced tongue, and if desired, a knife-tip of crushed juniper berries
4. Heat through and pour over the sliced tongue

title: Polsterzipf (Filled Pastry Cushions)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg flour
- 7 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- 1 spoon wine or rum
- 1 spoon cream
- hot lard
- sugar
- Chaudeau or raspberry juice

## Directions

1. Make a crumb dough from the flour, butter, egg yolks, salt, wine or rum, and cream.
2. Roll it out thinly and cut it into three- or four-sided pieces.
3. Let them rest covered for 30 minutes.
4. Bake them in hot lard, let them drain.
5. Sprinkle them with sugar and serve them still warm with Chaudeau or raspberry juice.

title: Pomerangen-Liqueur (Orange Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half Maß brandy
- 2 bitter oranges
- three-quarter pound sugar

## Directions

1. Rub the peel of one orange on sugar
2. Add the whole other orange to the brandy
3. Add three-quarter pound sugar to half Maß brandy
4. Bind the glass well and stir twice daily
5. On the twenty-first day, filter, fill into bottles, cork well, and let stand for 6 to 7 months
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pomeranzen Salat (Orange Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh oranges
- sugar
- preserved sour cherry flesh

## Directions

1. Slice fresh oranges thinly, leaf by leaf
2. Arrange them neatly on a plate
3. Sprinkle sugar over them so they are sweet enough
4. Place preserved sour cherry flesh on each leaf
5. Serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pomeranzen-Branntwein (Orange Brandy)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- orange peels
- strong brandy

## Directions

1. Cut the orange peels into long narrow pieces like sauerkraut, after cleaning away the inner white skin
2. Throw them into a glass bottle
3. Pour strong brandy over them
4. Set in the sun until ready
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pomersche geräucherte Gans (Pomeranian Smoked Goose)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One goose
- Salt
- Saltpeter
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Paper for wrapping

## Directions

1. Fatten the goose very well, especially from the time it begins to lay on its keel
2. Provide oats, grain, bran, carrots and parsnips; give it as much movement as possible
3. Feed it clean good feed at night and provide fresh clean water, sometimes also river water
4. Pluck the goose only gently, taking only as many feathers as naturally come away in the hand
5. By Martinmas you will see a very different goose than usual
6. After slaughter and removing the entrails and everything, dissolve the bones
7. Rub the goose inside with salt, saltpeter, cloves and pepper
8. Rub it outside with salt
9. Fold the two halves of the goose with the inside facing each other, tie with twine, and press slightly between two boards placed at an angle in a dish
10. Baste the goose alternately with drippings, today on one side, tomorrow on the other
11. After ten days in the drippings, once it has dried a little, wrap in paper and bring into weak smoke
12. Cut into slices like salmon
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pommes de terre sautées (Sautéed Potatoes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- butter
- salt
- parsley
- breadcrumbs (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel cooked potatoes and cut them into thin slices
2. Salt them and place on a hot flat pan in very hot butter
3. When one side is brown, sprinkle with finely chopped fresh parsley, turn them over and let the other side brown
4. Alternatively, before roasting, dust with breadcrumbs, roast with them, and call them 'turned potatoes'

title: Pommes Duchesses (Duchess Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- 3 dkg grated Parmesan
- ½ kg cooked, passed, cooled potatoes
- flour
- 1 egg, beaten
- butter for the baking sheet

## Directions

1. Cream together the butter with 2 egg yolks, salt, grated Parmesan, and the cooked, passed, cooled potatoes.
2. Roll out the dough on a floured board to the thickness of a small finger and cut into rectangles or squares.
3. Press a grid pattern into the top with a knife.
4. Brush with beaten egg.
5. Place on a buttered, floured baking sheet and bake at moderately high heat until light brown.
6. Keep warm until serving.

title: Pommes Duchesses (Duchess Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dag butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- 3 dag grated Parmesan
- ½ kg cooked, passed, and cooled potatoes

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and beat in the egg yolks, salt, and grated Parmesan
2. Mix in the cooled mashed potatoes
3. Roll out the dough to the thickness of a small finger on a floured board and cut into rectangles or squares

title: Pommes meringues (Apples with Meringue)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Maschanzger apples
- Egg white
- Fine sugar

## Directions

1. Make a marmalade from 10 Maschanzger apples, which must be thick, arrange it on a dish.
2. Beat egg white with fine sugar into a meringue and spread it over the apples.
3. Let them take on a yellowish color in the oven and serve them.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pommes Princesses (Potato Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg round potatoes
- 8 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- salt
- pepper
- 3 dkg grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Cream together 8 dkg butter with 2 egg yolks, 1 whole egg, salt, pepper, and 3 dkg grated Parmesan.
2. Fold in ½ kg cooked, mashed, and cooled potatoes until well combined.
3. Fill a pastry bag fitted with a star tip.
4. Pipe small croquettes onto a buttered and floured baking sheet.
5. Brush with beaten egg.
6. Bake at high heat until light brown.
7. Serve warm.

title: Pommes soufflées (Puffed Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 dkg potatoes
- 4 dkg butter
- salt
- 1 egg
- flour
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Make a potato dough from the potatoes, butter, salt, egg and flour (see doughs section)
2. Roll out thin
3. Cut out with a small round cutter
4. Bake in hot lard, drain
5. Do not fry many at once

title: Pommes à la Crème Gratinée (Apples in Cream, Gratinéed)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks (8)
- Vanilla sugar (14 drams)
- Flour (2 tablespoons)
- Cream (1/4 liter)
- Blanched almonds, finely crushed (16 drams)
- Almond flour
- Egg whites (4)
- Marmalade
- Pitted apples (8-10)
- Butter

## Directions

1. Put egg yolks, vanilla sugar, flour and cream in a casserole and stir over heat until thick
2. Set aside and let cool
3. When cooled, add blanched, finely crushed almonds and egg yolks one after another
4. Finally fold in the snow of egg whites
5. Hollow out the core of 8-10 apples, fill with marmalade
6. Place the apples at finger-width distance in a butter-coated dish
7. Pour the almond mixture over them
8. Bake at moderate heat for 3/4 to 1 hour in the oven

title: Pommes à la Polonaise (Apples Polish Style)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter (1/8 liter melted)
- Bread crumbs, black (1 heaped tablespoon, grated)
- Périgueux (16 drams)
- Cloves (1 crushed)
- Cinnamon (1/4 teaspoon)
- Lemon peel (1/4 teaspoon, finely grated)
- Roasted bread mixture
- Peeled roasted chestnuts
- Apple sauce
- Currants
- Raisins

## Directions

1. In a casserole containing melted butter, add a heaped tablespoon of grated black bread, 16 drams of Périgueux, 1 crushed clove, 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon and equally finely grated lemon peel
2. Mix well together and let it brown, then set aside to cool
3. When cooled, line a butter-coated casserole, lined with pastry, with a layer of the roasted bread mixture
4. On this place peeled roasted chestnuts, sprinkle again with bread and cover with apple sauce mixed with currants and raisins
5. Give another layer of roasted bread and continue until the casserole is filled
6. The last layer should be roasted bread, which is then pressed down firmly with a spoon
7. Bake for 1/2 hour in the oven

title: Poor Man's Soup (Soupe aux pauvres gens)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Deca butter or 12 Deca lard
- Flour as needed
- 0.33 teaspoon cinnamon
- 2 liters broth

## Directions

1. In 10 Deca butter or 12 Deca lard, add flour as needed and let brown considerably
2. Add 0.33 teaspoon cinnamon, roasting it somewhat
3. Pour in 2 liters broth and stir as for burnt flour soup
4. Add spices if desired
5. Let everything cook, pass through a sieve, and serve
6. The soup should have a nice brown appearance

title: Poppy Seed Strudel von Germteig (Poppy Seed Yeast Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- yeast dough as per recipe 1253
- 1/3 liter finely pounded poppy seed
- 1/4 liter boiling milk
- 12 deca sugar
- poppy seed filling as specified

## Directions

1. Prepare the yeast dough from recipe 1253 and let it rise
2. Roll out the risen dough on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a knife back
3. Prepare filling: stir finely pounded poppy seed into boiling milk and cook with sugar
4. Add raisins as desired to the filling
5. Spread the filling on the dough
6. Fold the longer side of the dough 3 finger widths over, brush this fold with poppy seed filling
7. Roll up the dough completely, place on a butter-greased baking sheet
8. Cover and let rise in a warm place
9. Brush the strudel strongly with beaten egg and bake in the oven

title: Pork Kidneys in Champagne Wine (Schweinsnieren in Champagner Wein)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork kidneys
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Butter
- Finely chopped shallots
- Parsley
- Flour
- One glass champagne wine
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Slice the kidneys thinly and sprinkle with salt, pepper, and a little nutmeg.
2. Cook them in butter with finely chopped shallots and parsley for a while over high heat until the kidneys begin to stiffen.
3. Dust them with a little flour and pour in a glass of champagne wine.
4. Turn them frequently so they do not stick, but they must not boil.
5. Arrange croutons on a plate and serve the kidneys hot.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pork Kidneys in Champagne Wine (Schweinsnieren in Champagner Wein)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork kidneys
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Butter
- Shallots, finely chopped
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Slice the kidneys thinly
2. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and a little nutmeg
3. Let them go in butter with finely chopped shallots and parsley over high heat until the kidneys begin to stiffen
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pork Leg à la Broche (Cuissot de porc à la broche)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pork leg from a young pig
- salt
- root vegetables, sliced thin
- onions
- shallots
- pepper
- butter
- paper

## Directions

1. Choose the pork leg from a young pig, remove the thick skin (rind) and salt it well
2. Place thin-sliced root vegetables, onions, shallots and some pepper in a casserole, lay the salted leg on top and let it marinate for about 3 days, turning it frequently
3. After this time, thread it on a spit, wrap it with double-layered paper well coated with butter, tie it with twine and roast slowly for 1½ to 2 hours
4. Half an hour before the end of roasting, remove the paper and roast the meat over high heat so that it takes on a golden-brown color
5. Remove from the spit, cut into desired pieces, arrange on a platter and serve with onion purée or desired vegetables or salad

title: Pork Loin Roast (Carré de porc)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pork loin (carré)
- salt
- caraway
- garlic
- vinegar
- fat

## Directions

1. Salt the carré well, salt it, rub with caraway and if desired with some crushed garlic, sprinkle with vinegar
2. Place in a pan containing fat and roast in the oven, basting frequently with the accumulated juices
3. When the rind has taken on a light-brown color, cut it in a grid pattern (score it) with thumb-width intervals and finish roasting
4. Cut off the ribs and arrange them in a platter, serving the defatted juices with them

title: Pork Schnitzel (Schweinsschnitzel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40-50 dkg pork leg
- salt
- flour
- egg
- lard for frying
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into thin slices and pound
2. Season with salt just before cooking
3. Coat with flour and beaten egg
4. Fry in hot lard until golden brown
5. Drain on paper
6. Arrange on a long platter, garnish with lemon slices, and serve

title: Portugiesische Eyer (Portuguese Eggs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Hard-boiled egg yolks, as many as needed
- Wine
- Sugar
- Preserved lemon peel
- Butter
- Nutmeg
- Pistachios

## Directions

1. Grind hard-boiled egg yolks, add wine, sugar, and preserved lemon peel.
2. Pound everything well together in a mortar.
3. Let this mixture simmer in a buttered dish over charcoal fire until it becomes a thick but not hard paste.
4. Sprinkle with nutmeg and garnish with preserved lemon peel and pistachios.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Potato and Egg Casserole (Appetizer Dish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 boiled potatoes of smaller variety
- 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley
- salt
- butter
- flour
- hard-boiled eggs, sliced
- capers, peeled and finely diced
- 1/2 liter milk cream
- 3 whole eggs
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Peel the boiled potatoes and cut them into thin slices, mixing them well with 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley and some salt.
2. Butter the bottom of a casserole dish (which can also be brought to table) and dust it with flour.
3. Layer the cut potatoes, then sprinkle with hard-boiled eggs cut into slices and peeled capers finely diced.
4. Beat 1/2 liter of milk cream with 3 whole eggs and pour some spoonfuls over the eggs and capers.
5. Add another layer of potatoes, sprinkle with eggs and capers, and continue until the casserole is full.
6. If potatoes are the last layer, pour the remaining cream over them, sprinkle with bread crumbs, and bake in the oven for 3/4 to 1 hour, covered at first so the crumbs do not burn.
7. When the egg mixture shows no more liquid, remove from oven and serve at table in the casserole, or turn out into a dish earlier.
8. Alternatively, the whole dish can be made in a buttered bowl and baked in the oven, or lined in a buttered torte form with knife-thick layer of butter dough, filled with potatoes as described above, and baked in the oven. Before serving, remove the rim so the egg dish comes to table in the crust.

title: Potato Casserole (Timbale de pommes de terre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Approximately 1 kilogram raw potatoes
- Boiling salt water
- 20 ounces butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Short pastry dough
- Ham
- Sausage or goose liver

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes, cut them into pieces, and pour boiling salt water over them (only enough so the water goes 1/2 finger width above the potatoes), add 10 ounces of butter and cook them soft until they can be stirred into a puree, then stir in another 10 ounces of butter, salt, and some pepper.
2. Lightly brush the bottom and sides of a casserole or desired form with butter and line with short pastry dough. This can be rolled the thickness of a cord and arranged in the form of a spiral.
3. Put the stirred potatoes 1/2 finger high on the bottom of the form, cover them with smoked ham that has been cooked soft and cut into small pieces, fried sausages, or fried goose liver, add again as much potato as before, and continue until the casserole is full.
4. Then cover the opening with dough and bake the cake for about 1 hour at moderate heat.

title: Potato Croquettes (Croquettes de pommes de terre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Potato dough
- Rod-shaped forms

## Directions

1. Make the above dough, form it into small rods, and proceed as instructed.

title: Potato Dish (Erdäpfelſpeiſe)
body:
## Ingredients

- ⅓ kg round potatoes
- 3 eggs
- 3 anchovies
- ⅛ liter cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook, peel and cool the potatoes, slice them
2. Cut the hard-boiled eggs and cleaned anchovies into pieces
3. Layer alternately potatoes, eggs and anchovies in a buttered porcelain saucepan until full
4. Drip hot butter on top
5. Beat the cream with 2 eggs and pour over
6. Bake for 20 minutes
7. Serve in the same saucepan
8. Can also be served as a fasting dish

title: Potato Dumplings (Noques de pommes de terre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Approximately 1 kilogram potatoes
- 8 ounces butter
- 3 whole eggs
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Finely cut parsley
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Hot lard

## Directions

1. Roast the potatoes in glowing ashes or in the oven, pass them through a sieve, and then stir in 8 ounces of butter and 3 whole eggs, to which add also salt, some nutmeg, and finely cut parsley.
2. Pass this mixture through and let it cool. When the whole thing is cold and firm, form quenelles with a spoon, roll them in eggs and bread crumbs, bake them in hot lard and serve immediately. This is used as a garnish for roast beef and for various meat dishes.

title: Potato Noodles (Erdäpfel-Nüdeln)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 medium potatoes
- 1 whole egg
- flour as needed
- 1 teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes until soft, peel them, and pass through a sieve
2. After cooling completely, place on a board
3. Work into a smooth but not too soft dough with 1 whole egg, necessary flour, and 1 teaspoon of salt
4. Roll the dough on a floured board into thumb-thick ropes
5. Cut into finger-width pieces and form each into small finger-thick noodles with pointed or blunt ends
6. Add to boiling salted water and cook covered for 10 to 15 minutes
7. Drain in a colander and rinse with cold water
8. Toast 8 tablespoons of breadcrumbs or semolina in 16 tablespoons of hot butter
9. Add the drained potato noodles, season to taste, stir well
10. When hot, arrange in a platter using a spoon and serve

title: Potato Quenelles (Queiielles de pommes de terre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Potato dough from previous recipe
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Hot lard

## Directions

1. These are formed from the above dough, also rolled in eggs and bread crumbs, baked in hot lard and served immediately.

title: Potato Salad (Kartoffelsalat)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg potatoes
- salt
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Wash potatoes and place in cold water
2. Cook for 25-30 minutes, drain, and cool completely
3. Peel and slice thinly
4. Mix with salt, vinegar, and oil

title: Potato Sauce (Potato Puree Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- about 4 large potatoes
- 2 tablespoons oil
- salt
- 1 pinch pepper
- vinegar
- 1 teaspoon sugar (if desired)
- 1 tablespoon grated horseradish or chives (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook about 4 large potatoes until soft and pass them through a sieve
2. Stir in 2 tablespoons oil, some salt, 1 pinch of pepper, vinegar to taste and if desired 1 teaspoon sugar
3. If desired, you can add 1 tablespoon grated horseradish or chives

title: Potato Soufflé (Kartoffelsoufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 to 12 ounces butter
- 24 ounces granulated sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 50 ounces cooked, passed, pitted potatoes
- 8 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy
2. Add the granulated sugar and egg yolks one after another
3. Mix in the cooked, passed, and pitted potatoes
4. Gently fold in the whipped egg white snow
5. Fill a butter-greased, flour-lined mold
6. Cover and steam for 3/4 hour or bake in the oven
7. Turn out onto a serving dish and sprinkle with finely powdered sugar

title: Potato Soufflé without Butter (Kartoffelsoufflé ohne Butter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ounces granulated sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 50 ounces cooked, passed, pitted potatoes
- 8 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Beat the granulated sugar with egg yolks for 1/2 hour
2. Add the cooked, passed, and pitted potatoes
3. Gently fold in the whipped egg white snow
4. Fill a butter-greased, flour-lined mold
5. Bake for 3/4 hour in the oven
6. Turn out onto a serving dish and sprinkle with sugar

title: Potato Soup (Consis de pommes de terre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 drams butter or fat
- 1 teaspoon diced onion
- green parsley
- flour as needed
- 2 liters cold water
- 1 liter diced, washed, raw potatoes
- 1 pinch grated marjoram
- salt
- toasted semolina bread

## Directions

1. Toast the onion and green parsley in butter
2. Add flour as needed and toast until slightly yellow
3. Gradually add 2 liters of cold water while stirring to prevent lumps
4. Bring to a boil
5. Add diced raw potatoes, marjoram, and salt
6. Simmer until potatoes are completely soft
7. Serve with toasted semolina bread

title: Potatoes in Their Jackets (Pommes de terre en robe de chambre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Cold water
- Caraway seeds
- Salt
- Fresh butter
- Anchovy butter or goose fat

## Directions

1. Put the thoroughly washed potatoes in a pot, pour on only enough cold water to cover them, add some caraway seeds and salt, and cook them covered gently, which takes about 3/4 hour from when they begin to boil.
2. Then place them on a neatly folded napkin and serve with fresh butter. The potatoes can also be roasted under glowing ashes in pans or in the oven. If putting them in the oven, wash them first and then rub them with salt and caraway seeds.
3. One must always observe the moment when they become soft, as they lose much in flavor through too long cooking. Serve fresh butter, anchovy butter, or goose fat with them.

title: Potezen mit Hirn (Bread with Brain)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-5 sliced rolls
- 1 pork brain or 1/2 calf brain
- 3/4 liter milk
- salt
- breading

## Directions

1. Cut the rolls into slices so that every 2 remain connected together.
2. Spread the inner side with roasted brain, dip in salted milk, bread them, and bake them.

title: Poularde aux Huîtres (Chicken with Oysters Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 poularde (prepared as in Poularde in White Mussel Sauce)
- 12 oysters, shelled
- 8 decagrams butter
- 4 tablespoons wine vinegar crumbs
- 1/2 liter milk cream
- juice of 1 lemon
- oyster liquor
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Place shelled oysters in a casserole containing 8 decagrams of butter
2. Let them braise covered briefly
3. Add 4 tablespoons of wine vinegar crumbs
4. Once these have begun to heat, add 1/2 liter of milk cream, the juice of one lemon, and the oyster liquor
5. Season to taste and let simmer well
6. If the sauce is too thick, thin it with the liquid in which the poularde was braised
7. Cut the gently braised poularde into pieces of choice
8. Pour the oysters with their sauce over the poularde
9. For a brown oyster sauce, add some dissolved meat glaze to the strained sauce and let it simmer well

title: Poularde Braised with Sour Cream (Poularde à la creme aigre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca of fat
- root vegetables
- onion
- whole spices
- 1 bay leaf
- white herbs
- poularde
- 1/4 liter of white wine
- 1/4 liter of broth
- 1/4 liter of cream
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 tablespoon of capers

## Directions

1. Place fat, root vegetables, onion, whole spices, bay leaf, and white herbs in a casserole
2. Lay a cleaned, trussed, and seasoned poularde on top and let it braise covered
3. When the resulting juice has evaporated, pour in 1/4 liter of white wine and 1/4 liter of broth and let the poularde braise covered until tender
4. Pour 1/4 liter of cream over it, add the juice of a lemon, and cook this together well
5. Remove the poularde, cut it into desired pieces, and arrange in a bowl
6. Pass the sauce through a sieve into a casserole, add 1 tablespoon of capers, bring to a boil once, and serve with the poularde
7. The poularde can be garnished with various shellfish dishes cooked in salt water

title: Poularde im Pâteten Siegel (Poularde in Terrine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 poularde
- Bacon slices
- Farce
- Butter dough

## Directions

1. The poularde can be filled into a pâté terrine instead of butter dough.
2. Such a terrine is lined with slices of bacon, spread 2 fingers high with farce, and otherwise treated as described in the 'Poularde Pâté' recipe (No. 1060).

title: Poularde mit Krebsſauce (Poulard with Crayfish Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- poulard
- crayfish coulis with shelled crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Clean the poulard well and prepare it for roasting
2. Once nicely roasted in its juices, arrange it on the platter and serve with a good crayfish coulis with shelled crayfish tails underneath
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Poularde mit Wildbretsauce (Capon with Game Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 capon, prepared as described previously
- 1 tablespoon capers, cleaned and finely diced
- finely diced gherkins
- imitated woodcock sauce (see no. 838)
- lemon slices
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. Add 1 tablespoon of cleaned and finely diced capers and finely diced gherkins to the sauce, bring it once to a boil and add it to the capon in a sauce boat
2. The capon, prepared as described in the previous number, is arranged on a platter cut into pieces and reassembled
3. Garnish with imitated woodcock sauce, lemon slices and brown sauce

title: Poularde Pâté (Chicken Pâté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned poularde (hen)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Small truffles
- Veal farce (cold pâté farce)
- Thin slices of bacon
- Butter dough

## Directions

1. Cut the poularde along the back and lay it open; carefully pull out the bones while leaving the flesh attached to the skin.
2. Sprinkle the poularde with salt and pepper, lay small truffles upon it, spread with veal farce, add another layer of truffles, spread again with farce, and continue until the poularde is completely filled and has regained its natural form.
3. Prepare the butter dough and form the pâté as described in the 'Goose Liver Pâté' recipe (No. 1057).
4. Line the inside of the pâté with thin slices of bacon.
5. Spread the bottom 2 fingers high with veal farce, sprinkle with finely diced truffles, and place the poularde with its back upon it.
6. Spread the poularde with veal farce, fill the pâté with cut truffles and chopped goose liver, spread again with veal farce, and cover with a slice of bacon that has been scored several times beforehand.
7. Proceed as described in the 'Goose Liver Pâté' recipe.
8. Bake for 1½ to 2 hours.
9. Use ⅕ kilo of veal farce for the poularde and for filling the pâté.

title: Poularde with White Mussel Sauce (Poularde aux meules)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca of bacon or butter
- onion
- root vegetables
- whole spices
- poularde
- broth
- butter
- flour
- 4 cleaned, finely sliced crayfish tails
- mussels
- white wine

## Directions

1. Line a casserole with bacon or butter, onion, root vegetables, and whole spices
2. Place a cleaned, trussed, seasoned, and larded poularde on top and braise covered
3. When the resulting juice has evaporated, pour in enough broth so the liquid covers the poularde and braise covered until tender
4. Remove the poularde, place it in a casserole, and cover well
5. Remove the fat from the braising liquid, place in a casserole and toast an equal amount of flour, pour the defatted broth over it, stir together, and cook well
6. Remove the fat cleanly, pass the sauce through a sieve, and add 4 cleaned, finely sliced crayfish tails
7. Wash the mussels in white wine, place them in 9 deca of melted butter, braise them briefly, then pour in enough white wine to cover them and cook covered until done

title: Poulet à la Hongroise (Hungarian-style Chicken)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deciliter lard
- finely cut onions (to taste)
- 2 cleaned and dressed chickens
- 1 liter soup
- raw potatoes, cut into desired pieces
- paprika (to taste)

## Directions

1. In a casserole with 8 deciliter lard, add finely cut onions to taste
2. Place 2 cleaned and dressed chickens inside and braise covered for 1-2 hours
3. Pour 1 liter soup over the chickens
4. Add raw potatoes cut into desired pieces
5. Braise everything together until tender, season with paprika
6. Serve as paprika chicken

title: Poultry Croquettes (Croquette von Geflügel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roasted poultry meat
- béchamel sauce
- white bread, grated
- eggs
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from cold roasted poultry and cut it finely
2. Make a béchamel and mix it with the cut meat
3. Spread it on a flat casserole lid the thickness of a thumb
4. When cold, cut it out with a small patty form
5. Roll in grated white bread
6. Pull through beaten eggs
7. A second time coat thickly with grated bread
8. Shortly before serving, fry quickly in butter or lard
9. Serve immediately
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Preiselbeeren-Sauce (Lingonberry Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 1/8 liter lingonberries
- red wine
- cinnamon as desired
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Use the same procedure as for the quince sauce, but the lingonberries must be crushed and red wine used
2. You can also add 1 pinch of cinnamon

title: Preparation of Stuffed Veal Sweetbreads (Bereitung gefüllter Kalbsbries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal sweetbreads, cleaned and cut into large pieces
- Water
- Onions
- Green parsley
- Pepper
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions
