1. Boil the cleaned and cut veal sweetbreads in the necessary water until completely tender.
2. Remove them and place them in an earthenware casserole in which onions and green parsley with some pepper and salt have been first roasted in butter.
3. Cover them and braise with the same water in which they were boiled, basting spoonfully until the pieces become somewhat brown and the sauce becomes somewhat thick.

title: Pressw urst (Pressed Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pfund good ham
- 1 Pfund bacon
- 2 Pfund good fatty pork
- pork ears
- several onions
- a couple pieces of garlic
- salt
- pepper
- basil
- thyme
- a pig's stomach
- half vinegar and half water
- bay leaves
- cloves

## Directions

1. Cut the 1 pound ham, 1 pound bacon, 2 pounds pork, and pork ears uncooked into small diced and oblong pieces
2. Add several finely cut onions and a couple pieces of finely cut garlic
3. Season well with salt and pepper, and mix well with basil and thyme
4. Fill this mixture into a pig's stomach and tie it well on both sides
5. Place it in a casserole or pot, add half vinegar and half water so that it completely covers the stomach
6. Add several bay leaves and cloves to the liquid
7. Let it cook for a full 2 hours in this broth
8. Remove the stomach and place it between two bowls so it is compressed somewhat
9. Let the broth congeal into jelly, and when the stomach has cooled well, slice it and pour the jelly over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Prest's Krebswurst (Consommé au pain d'écrevisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pieces crayfish
- salt water
- 14 deca butter
- crayfish shells
- pepper
- parsley
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- 6 deca breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook 12 pieces crayfish in salted water
2. Remove claws and tails and set aside
3. Pound the shells with 14 deca butter in a mortar
4. Put in a casserole and toast slightly
5. Pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
6. Add some salt, a pinch of parsley, then gradually 4 egg yolks
7. Mix in the removed, finely cut claws and tails with the snow from 4 egg whites and 6 deca breadcrumbs
8. Treatment same as for potato dumplings

title: Preußiſcher Apfelkuchen (Prussian Apple Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.25 kilogram Masanzker apples
- 1 lemon
- 14 decagrams flour
- 11 decagrams butter
- 4 decagrams swelled, grated almonds
- 5 decagrams sugar
- 0.5 raw egg yolk and 1 hard-boiled egg yolk
- Finely chopped lemon peel
- 1 spoonful lemon juice
- Apples for filling
- Sugar cubes
- Lemon peel
- Water
- Raisins
- Egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Make a crumb dough from 14 decagrams flour, 11 decagrams butter, 4 decagrams swelled grated almonds, 5 decagrams sugar, 0.5 raw and 1 hard-boiled egg yolk, finely chopped lemon peel, and 1 spoonful lemon juice (see doughs)
2. After resting, roll out thinly and cut into a rectangle
3. Place on an ungreased baking sheet and bake in the oven until light brown
4. Spread the filling on top
5. Form thin rolls from the dough scraps, lay them in a lattice pattern on the filling
6. Brush with beaten egg and bake the cake at higher heat until done
7. Cut, sprinkle with sugar, and cool before serving
8. Make the filling the same way as for the Crumb Dough Apple Strudel

title: Preßburg Rods (Preßburger Stangeln)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca butter
- 8 deca flour
- 8 deca almonds finely pounded with their skin and passed through a sieve
- 8 deca vanilla sugar
- 1 pinch nutmeg
- finely cut lemon zest to equal measure
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Place butter and flour on a board and rub finely together with hands
2. Add almonds, vanilla sugar, nutmeg, and lemon zest
3. Work quickly with 2 egg yolks into a smooth dough and let become stiff covered in a very cool place
4. Roll out on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a small finger
5. Cut into thumb-width and finger-length rods and place at finger-width distance on a butter-greased baking sheet
6. Brush with beaten eggs and bake pale yellow at low heat in the oven
7. After removing from baking sheet and cooling, brush with any desired dissolved marmalade, and when completely dry, cover with glaze or decorate with Spanish almond paste, sprinkle with finely pounded sugar and dry

title: Profiteroles
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.25 liters water
- 7 dekagrams butter
- salt
- 11 dekagrams flour
- 3-4 small eggs
- 1 spoon sugar
- whipped cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a choux dough from the water, butter, salt, flour, eggs and sugar.
2. Using 2 small spoons, form oblong pastries on a buttered sheet.
3. Brush with egg white or beaten egg.
4. Bake at high heat until light brown and cool.
5. Cut them open on the side.
6. Fill with sweetened whipped cream.
7. Place on a glass platter and cover with chocolate sauce.

title: Profiteroles (Cream Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ liter water
- 7 dag butter
- salt
- 11 dag flour
- 3-4 small eggs
- 1 spoon sugar
- whipped sweetened cream (Schlagobers)
- chocolate sauce

## Directions

1. Make a choux pastry (Brandteig) from the water, butter, salt, flour, eggs, and sugar
2. Using 2 small spoons, form oblong puffs on a buttered baking sheet
3. Brush with egg white or beaten egg
4. Bake until light brown over brisk heat and let cool
5. Slice them open on the side, fill with sweetened whipped cream
6. Place on a glass dish and pour chocolate sauce over them

title: Prässeworst (Press Sausage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilo cooked ham or tongue
- 1 kilo cooked pork
- pig's ears
- 1 teaspoon green parsley
- marjoram
- onion
- 8 decagrams bacon, diced
- 8 decagrams fresh bacon, diced
- salt
- pepper
- 1/2 liter pig's blood
- pig's stomach

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked ham or tongue, cooked pork, and pig's ears into small cubes and place in a deep bowl
2. Roast the green parsley, marjoram, and onion together in 8 decagrams of diced bacon
3. Add the rendered fat without the onion and parsley to the cut meat along with 8 decagrams of diced fresh bacon, the required salt and pepper, and 1/2 liter of pig's blood, and mix well
4. Fill cleaned pig's stomach with the mixture and tie off both ends
5. Cook covered in salted water for 2 full hours
6. Place on a board, cover with another board, and weight it down
7. Once completely cooled, slice thinly, arrange in a bowl, and serve with vinegar and oil

title: Pudding aus geriebenen Mandeln (Pudding with Roasted Almonds)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ounces finely ground sugar
- 16 ounces blanched almonds
- 16 ounces sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 clove
- 8 egg whites, beaten to snow

## Directions

1. In a dry casserole, put 12 ounces finely ground sugar and let it brown
2. Add 16 ounces blanched almonds, roast them in the oven until golden, let cool completely, and grind finely
3. Beat 16 ounces sugar with 8 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
4. Add the ground almonds and stir with lemon juice and 1 clove for 1/2 hour
5. Gently fold in the snow from 8 egg whites
6. Fill a buttered mold lined with flour
7. Cover and steam for 3/4 hour
8. Turn out onto a plate and serve with orange cream

title: Pudding aux Amandes (Almond Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ounces butter, beaten
- 16 ounces sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 18 ounces blanched almonds, finely ground with 2 egg whites
- 6 egg whites, beaten to snow

## Directions

1. Beat 12 ounces butter with 16 ounces sugar and 8 egg yolks for 1 hour
2. Add 18 ounces blanched almonds finely ground with 2 egg whites, mix well
3. Gently fold in the beaten snow from 6 egg whites
4. Fill a buttered mold lined with flour
5. Cover and steam for 1 hour
6. Serve with fruit juice of choice

title: Pudding von Brandteig (Choux Pastry Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk for Brandteig (recipe 1052)
- 14 ounces butter, beaten
- 24 ounces powdered sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites, beaten to snow

## Directions

1. Prepare Brandteig from 1/2 liter milk, fill into a pastry bag
2. Press the dough through continuous pressure, cut into desired pieces, and fry in hot fat until golden with frequent shaking of the pan
3. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on blotting paper
4. Beat 14 ounces butter with 24 ounces powdered sugar
5. Add 8 egg yolks one by one and stir
6. Gently fold in the snow from 6 egg whites simultaneously with the fried Brandteig
7. Fill a buttered mold lined with flour
8. Cover and steam for 3/4 hour

title: Pudding von Erdäpfel (Potato Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure boiled potatoes
- 1/2 pound melted butter, warmed
- 6 eggs
- 1 pound pitted raisins
- 1 glass brandy
- butter for the cloth

## Directions

1. Peel cooked potatoes, crush them, and press them warm through a fine sieve.
2. Stir them together with 1/2 pound melted and slightly warm butter until quite thick, fold in 6 eggs, and add 1 pound pitted raisins and 1 glass brandy.
3. Bind this mass loosely in a butter-coated cloth, cook it for 1.5 hours in a large pot of simmering water, and pour a wine foam sauce over it when serving.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Erdäpfeln (Potato Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 teaspoons beaten butter
- 46 teaspoons passed potatoes
- 1 teaspoon salt
- finely cut green parsley
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 8 teaspoons finely cut ham or other filling
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan

## Directions

1. 16 teaspoons of beaten butter are stirred little by little with 46 teaspoons of passed potatoes, 1 teaspoon of salt, some finely cut green parsley and 6 egg yolks and finally lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
2. Fill half of this mass into a butter-greased, flour-dusted cake form, coat with 8 teaspoons of finely cut ham or other filling, but only so much that a finger-width border remains all around and cover this evenly with the second half of the potato mass
3. Place the pudding in the oven and bake it at low heat for 3/4 hours

title: Pudding von Fadennudeln (Noodle Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pfund fine noodles
- 1 Seidel cream
- milk
- 1/2 Pfund butter

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound fine noodles into 1 Seidel boiled cream and an equal amount of milk
2. Let them swell and cool
3. Beat 1/2 pound butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Fadennudeln (Noodle Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of fine noodles
- One seidel of cream, boiled
- Equal amount of milk
- Half pound of butter
- 12 egg yolks
- 4 pounds of crushed sugar
- Zest of one lemon
- Noodles

## Directions

1. Stir the fine noodles into a seidel of boiled cream and equal amount of milk, let them swell and cool
2. Beat half pound of butter with 12 egg yolks until foamy
3. Stir in 4 pounds of crushed sugar, the zest of one lemon, and the noodles
4. Fill the mixture into a casserole brushed thickly with fresh butter
5. Cover with netting soaked an hour before
6. Let it bake slowly in the oven for 3 hours, pouring boiling water as needed so the water reaches the height of the mass
7. Serve with a cream sauce poured over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Fleiſch (Meat Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 decagrams cooked beef
- 2 bread rolls soaked in milk
- 7 decagrams butter
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Create a base by beating 7 decagrams butter until creamy
2. Stir in 3 egg yolks
3. Add the cooled minced meat (hash)
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Add chopped parsley and well-drained bread rolls
6. Fold in 3 stiffly beaten egg whites
7. Fill the mixture into a buttered, floured pudding mold
8. Steam for 45 minutes until set
9. Turn out the pudding and serve warm with Hollandaise as an appetizer, or without sauce as a soup accompaniment

title: Pudding von gebratenen Hühnerbrüsten (Pudding of Roasted Chicken Breasts)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pfund crawfish butter
- 8 whole eggs
- 8 egg yolks
- 2 Pfund grated bread rolls soaked in milk and squeezed out
- finely chopped breast meat from chickens or capons
- 2 small handfuls flour
- 4 Loth sugar
- zest of one orange
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat 1 pound crawfish butter to foam, or stir other butter to foam
2. Beat in 8 whole eggs and 8 egg yolks, one after the other, stirring until foamy
3. Add 2 pounds grated bread rolls that have been soaked in milk and then squeezed out
4. Add finely chopped breast meat from chickens or capons
5. Add 2 small handfuls flour
6. Add 4 Loth sugar over which the zest of one orange has been grated
7. Add salt
8. Handle the mass as usual
9. When serving, add a Dutch sauce with wine
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von gebratenen Kapaunen oder Hühnern (Pudding of Roasted Capons or Chickens)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pfund almonds
- several eggs
- 13 Vierding sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 whole eggs
- 1 Pfund melted butter
- capon meat
- 6 peeled Borstorfer apples
- 1/2 Pfund candied citron
- 1 Pfund bread crumbs
- 2 Esslöffel flour
- nutmeg flower
- 1 Pfund rice
- 2 Seidel milk

## Directions

1. Pound 4 pounds almonds, which have been crushed fine with several eggs, with 13 Vierding sugar, 6 egg yolks, and 6 whole eggs for a quarter hour
2. Gradually add 1 pound melted, then mostly cooled butter
3. Mix in the capon meat, 6 peeled Borstorfer apples, 1/2 pound candied citron, finely cut, 1 pound bread crumbs, 2 spoonfuls flour, some nutmeg flower
4. Finally add 1 pound rice that has been soaked in 2 Seidel milk, drained cleanly, and cooled
5. Bind and cook as with the others
6. Serve with wine foam
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Hecht, gebacken (Baked Pike Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pike of several pounds
- salt water
- 50 cooked crayfish
- crayfish butter
- 1 Pfund grated bread crumbs
- 1 Maß milk
- 1/2 Maß cream
- salt
- nutmeg
- finely chopped anchovies
- 12 egg yolks
- 12 egg whites
- good butter dough

## Directions

1. Cook a pike of several pounds in salted water
2. Remove the bones from the flesh and shred it finely
3. Add finely cut meat from 50 cooked and shelled crayfish
4. Make crayfish butter, reserving as much as needed for the sauce after it cools
5. Toast 1 pound grated bread crumbs in the remaining crayfish butter
6. Fill it with 1 Maß milk and 1/2 Maß cream
7. Let it cook to a moderately stiff consistency
8. Season this batter, once cooled in a bowl, with salt and nutmeg
9. After a quarter hour, stir in finely chopped anchovies, 12 egg yolks, finely chopped pike and crayfish flesh, and lastly the whites of 12 eggs beaten to snow
10. Line a mold or casserole with good butter dough, fill in the mass, and bake for 1.5 hours
11. Serve with crayfish sauce, using the remaining crayfish butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Kalbsfüßen (Calf's Feet Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Six to eight calf's feet
- Salt water
- Rendered beef kidney fat, finely cut
- Salt
- 8 eggs
- Ground mace
- A little pepper
- Butter
- Chopped parsley
- Finely cut onions
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Boil six to eight calf's feet until soft in salted water
2. Cut the meat from the bones
3. Mix it with rendered beef kidney fat cut into small pieces, salt, 8 eggs, ground mace, a little pepper, chopped parsley, finely cut onions, and breadcrumbs
4. Stir everything together well
5. Cook in a napkin brushed with butter for 3 hours
6. When serving, pour over a parsley sauce made from beef broth, butter, and breadcrumbs
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Käſe (Cheese Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter cream
- 7 decagrams Parmesan cheese
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- 9 decagrams flour
- 3 egg whites
- 4 decagrams grated cheese
- peas
- chopped ham
- butter

## Directions

1. In a small pot, beat the cream with 3 egg yolks, a pinch of salt, and 9 decagrams flour until smooth
2. Fold in 3 stiffly beaten egg whites together with 4 decagrams grated cheese
3. Fill the mixture into a buttered, floured pudding mold
4. Steam for 45 minutes until set
5. Turn out onto a flat plate
6. Garnish with fresh green peas cooked and lightly stewed in butter, and chopped ham
7. Serve with hot butter and grated cheese on the side

title: Pudding von Leber, mit Kapernſauce (Liver Pudding with Caper Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One boiled calf's liver, finely grated
- One fatty goose liver, cut into small cubes
- Half pound of marrow
- Some finely chopped onions
- Half pound of butter
- 8 eggs
- 8 egg yolks
- Half pound of small raisins
- Finely cut lemon zest
- One pound of grated breadcrumbs, soaked in cream
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Grate the boiled calf's liver finely on a grater
2. Sauté the fatty goose liver cut into cubes, the marrow, and some finely chopped onions in half pound of butter
3. Gradually stir in 8 eggs, 8 egg yolks, and half pound of small raisins
4. Add finely cut lemon zest
5. Gradually combine with one pound of grated breadcrumbs soaked in cream, along with the liver and marrow, to make a mass
6. Season with salt and nutmeg
7. Prepare as other puddings that are cooked
8. When serving, garnish with lemon slices and pour caper sauce over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Mark (Bone Marrow Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 measure warm cream
- 1 roll (Mundsemel)
- 1 pound finely cut pure bone marrow
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 4 eggs
- 2 tablespoons brandy
- butter for the cloth

## Directions

1. Soak a rubbed roll, cut into pieces, in half a measure of warm cream.
2. Stir it together with 1 pound finely cut pure bone marrow, 1/2 pound sugar, 4 eggs, and 2 tablespoons brandy until quite thick.
3. Butter a cloth and proceed as with the others.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Milchbrot (Milk Bread Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound milk bread
- 16 egg yolks
- 4 loths fresh butter
- 1/2 pound crushed sugar
- zest of 1 lemon
- egg whites (from the eggs used)

## Directions

1. Rub off the crust from half a pound of milk bread, soak it, and squeeze it out, place it in a bowl, and beat in 16 egg yolks one after another, stirring for a good hour.
2. Then stir it for a while longer with 4 loths fresh butter, 1/2 pound crushed sugar, and the rubbed zest of a lemon.
3. Mix in the snow beaten from the above egg whites, treat it like any pudding, except that it must cook for 1.5 hours in water, and pour a wine sauce over it when serving.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Nierenfett (Kidney Fat Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pfund rendered kidney fat
- 1 Pfund raisins
- 4 Pfund sugar
- 3 round bread rolls
- milk

## Directions

1. Beat 1/2 pound rendered kidney fat until foamy
2. Stir in 1 pound raisins, 4 pounds sugar, and 3 round bread rolls from which the crust has been removed, cut into pieces, soaked in milk, and squeezed out
3. This pudding can either be cooked tied in a napkin or baked slowly in a butter-greased rimmed baking dish
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Pomeranzen (Bitter Orange Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 soft-cooked fresh bitter oranges
- 2 pounds finely cut white bread, soaked in cream
- 1/2 pound sweet almonds
- 2 pounds finely crushed sugar
- 1/4 pound finely cut citron
- 1 pound butter
- 18 egg yolks
- 12 eggs (for snow)
- wine sauce with red wine and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Press 8 soft-cooked fresh bitter oranges through a hair sieve.
2. Stir in 2 pounds finely cut white bread that has been softened in cream, 1/2 pound sweet almonds, and 2 pounds finely crushed sugar, along with 1/4 pound finely cut citron.
3. Beat 1 pound butter with 18 egg yolks into a foam, then gradually fold in the mass, and finally the snow from 12 eggs.
4. Cook the pudding in the usual manner and serve with a wine sauce made from red wine and cinnamon.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Rahm (Cream Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter cream
- 4 decagrams flour
- salt
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. In a small pot, beat the cream with 4 decagrams flour, a pinch of salt, and 3 egg yolks until smooth
2. Fold in 3 stiffly beaten egg whites
3. Prepare and garnish the same way as the Cheese Pudding

title: Pudding von Reiß und Kirſchen (Pudding of Rice and Cherries)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pfund rice
- 2 Pfund sour cherries
- 1/2 Pfund butter
- 10 eggs
- 7 Pfund sugar
- 4 Pfund flour
- zest of one lemon
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Wash the rice several times and let it swell in milk, then put it in a colander to drain
2. Pit the cherries with a feather quill, briefly cook them with sugar, and when cooled, add them to the mass
3. Set aside the whites of 5 eggs; you can also add some bread crumbs if desired
4. Otherwise treat this mass as usual
5. Cook in water for 12 hours
6. When serving, pour wine foam sauce over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Rindfleiſch (Beef Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds of lean beef, cut into long narrow strips then fine cubes
- Half pound of smoked bacon, cut the same way
- 8 well-beaten eggs
- Finely chopped onions
- Finely cut lemon zest
- Salt
- Mace
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Dried and finely grated basil
- 3 or 4 spoonfuls of hot butter

## Directions

1. Cut 3 pounds of lean beef into long, narrow strips, then into fine cubes
2. Cut half pound of smoked bacon the same way
3. Put into a bowl with 8 well-beaten eggs, finely chopped onions, finely cut lemon zest, salt, mace, cloves, pepper, and dried and finely grated basil
4. Pour 3 or 4 spoonfuls of hot butter over
5. Stir everything together well
6. Prepare as all other puddings
7. Cook for 4 hours
8. For the sauce: brown flour in butter until golden, let cool
9. Pour meat broth onto it
10. Add mushrooms, lemon slices, capers, mace, and pepper
11. Let it cook and pour over the pudding
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Rindsfüßen (Beef Feet Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef feet, boiled until very soft
- Butter
- 6 eggs
- Breadcrumbs soaked in milk
- Chopped veal
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Chopped chervil
- Onions

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from the bones of the boiled beef feet and chop it very finely
2. Beat butter until fluffy
3. Add 6 eggs gradually
4. Add some breadcrumbs soaked in milk and chopped veal
5. Add the chopped beef feet
6. Season with salt and nutmeg
7. Stir everything together well and proceed as with other puddings
8. For the sauce: make it from chopped chervil and onions sweated in butter, cooking it with a little flour scattered over it in meat broth
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding von Spinat (Spinach Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram spinach
- 6 decagrams butter
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- 1 bread roll soaked in milk
- 12 decagrams cooked spinach
- 3 spoonfuls cream
- 4 egg whites
- 8 decagrams fine breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Create a base by beating 6 decagrams butter until creamy
2. Stir in 3 egg yolks, salt, pepper, and chopped parsley
3. Add 1 bread roll soaked in milk, squeezed dry and passed through a sieve
4. Add 12 decagrams cooked, passed and drained spinach and 3 spoonfuls cream
5. Fold in 4 stiffly beaten egg whites together with 8 decagrams fine breadcrumbs
6. Fill the mixture into a buttered mold scattered with breadcrumbs
7. Steam for 45 minutes until set
8. Turn out the pudding and pour browned breadcrumbs fried in butter over it
9. Serve with hot butter on the side

title: Pudding with Black Bread (Pouding au pain-bis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 16 decagrams lemon sugar
- 1 pinch cinnamon
- equal amount finely cut orange peel
- 1 crushed clove
- 8 egg whites
- 16 decagrams black bread crumbs
- butter for the mold
- flour for the mold
- 0.5 liter red wine
- 32 decagrams sugar
- 1 clove
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks with 16 decagrams lemon sugar for 3/4 hour
2. Add 1 pinch cinnamon, finely cut orange peel, and 1 crushed clove, mix well
3. Lightly fold in the snow from 8 egg whites simultaneously with 16 decagrams black bread crumbs
4. Butter a mold, dust with flour, fill with this mixture
5. Bake in the oven at moderate heat for 1/2 hour
6. Meanwhile, heat 0.5 liter red wine with 32 decagrams sugar, 1 clove, and cinnamon
7. Turn the pudding into a dish and pour the hot wine sauce over it spoonwise
8. Serve only after the liquid has been completely absorbed
9. Cored and peeled small Mcintosh apples can be filled and placed in the pudding mixture before baking if desired

title: Pudding with Caramel Butter (Pouding au caramel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams trimmed, finely sliced bread rolls
- 1.5 ounces milk
- 8 decagrams caramelized sugar
- 10 decagrams whipped butter
- 16 decagrams finely sifted sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- butter for the mold
- finely sifted sugar for the mold

## Directions

1. Soften 16 decagrams trimmed, finely sliced bread rolls in 1.5 ounces boiling milk
2. Transfer to a saucepan containing 8 decagrams caramelized sugar, mix well
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Beat 10 decagrams whipped butter gradually with 16 decagrams finely sifted sugar and 6 egg yolks
5. Add the passed bread rolls, mix well
6. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
7. Butter a mold, dust with finely sifted sugar, fill with this mixture
8. Bake covered in steam for 1.5 hours

title: Pudding with Jelly (Pouding à la gelee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 decagrams whipped butter
- 14 decagrams finely pounded sugar
- 6 hard-boiled, passed egg yolks
- 12 decagrams peeled, finely pounded almonds
- cinnamon
- finely cut lemon peel
- 6 egg whites
- butter for the mold
- flour for the mold
- bitter almond cream

## Directions

1. Mix 14 decagrams whipped butter with 14 decagrams finely pounded sugar well
2. Add 6 hard-boiled, passed egg yolks
3. Add 12 decagrams peeled, finely pounded almonds, cinnamon, and finely cut lemon peel
4. Mix well
5. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
6. Fill a mold buttered and dusted with flour
7. Cook covered in steam for 1/2 hour
8. Serve with bitter almond cream

title: Pudding with Spanish White Wine (Pudding von spanischer Weiswein)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 decagrams granulated sugar
- 8 egg whites
- butter for the mold
- flour for the mold

## Directions

1. Lightly mix 28 decagrams granulated sugar with the stiff snow from 8 egg whites
2. Fill a buttered mold dusted with flour shaped like a melon
3. Cover with parchment paper if desired
4. Bake in steam for 0.5 to 0.75 hour

title: Pudding à la chapelure (Breadcrumb Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds milk bread crusts or fine roll crusts
- 1 pound butter
- 8 eggs
- 1 measure cream
- 10 eggs
- 1/2 pound crushed sugar
- 15 loths sweet almonds
- 1 loth bitter almonds
- zest of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Dry 2 pounds of milk bread or fine roll crusts in an oven, crush them finely, and sieve them through a hair sieve.
2. Beat 1 pound butter and 8 eggs into a foam.
3. Add to this pudding: 1 measure cream and 10 eggs; 1/2 pound crushed sugar, 15 loths sweet almonds combined with 1 loth bitter almonds, and the zest of 1 lemon.
4. Crush the bitter almonds with a couple of eggs, alternately adding a handful of fine bread and 1 spoon cream to the butter beaten to a foam.
5. Stir the sugar together with the sweet almonds and gradually add a spoonful at a time, add the remaining ingredients similarly gradually, and let it cook for 2 hours.
6. Serve with a wine foam when serving.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding à la Normande (Normandy Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg whites
- Peeled and finely ground almonds
- Previously mentioned vegetables
- 8 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg whites and mentioned vegetables together
2. Add finely ground and strained peeled almonds with the previously mentioned vegetables
3. Mix well and finally lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
4. Fill into a mold brushed with butter and dusted with flour
5. Cook covered for 3/4 hour in a water bath
6. Turn out onto a platter and serve with whipped cream or crème

title: Pudding à la Rodney
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of butter
- Seven pounds of sugar (one pound added later)
- One seidel of milk
- Three pounds of flour
- 16 egg yolks
- Salt
- Egg whites

## Directions

1. Heat half a pound of butter, seven pounds of sugar, and one seidel of milk in a casserole
2. Stir in three pounds of flour until the dough is stiff
3. Let it cool
4. Stir in one pound more of sugar, 16 egg yolks, and some salt
5. Continue stirring for a while
6. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in so nothing is visible
7. Bind the pudding in a napkin that has been soaked in water for an hour
8. Cook in water salted with meat broth for the appropriate time
9. For the sauce: Set one pound of washed sago with finely cut lemon zest on the heat
10. When it begins to boil and becomes somewhat clear, pour in one seidel of red wine
11. Add as much sugar as needed, lemon slices, several spoonfuls of currant jelly, and some whole cinnamon
12. Let the broth cook until the sago is fully swollen
13. Pour this sauce over the pudding when serving
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pudding à la Vanille (Vanilla Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca vanilla sugar
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Lightly mix 28 deca vanilla sugar with the stiff snow of 8 egg whites
2. Fill into a mold brushed with butter and dusted with flour
3. Cover with parchment paper if desired
4. Cook covered for 1/2 to 3/4 hour in a water bath
5. Turn out onto a platter
6. Pour desired dissolved and cooled marmalade over it
7. Serve immediately

title: Puff Pastry Pastries (Kases Pasteten)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff dough
- Eggs
- Filling (beliebigen Fasch)

## Directions

1. Roll out puff dough knife-back-thick and cut regular squares of desired size with a knife
2. Place on a water-moistened baking sheet and brush with beaten eggs, leaving a knife-back-width border at the edge
3. Place desired filling in the middle of each square
4. Cover with equally large squares arranged in a diamond shape
5. Press the filling down slightly and brush again with beaten eggs, leaving a finger-width border
6. Bake in the oven at fairly strong heat until golden yellow

title: Puff Pastry Pastries Another Way (Kases Pasteten anderer Art)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff dough
- Eggs
- Cold pâté (walnut-sized pieces)
- Beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Make puff dough and let it rest sufficiently
2. Roll out strongly knife-back-thick on a flour-dusted board
3. Cut out round discs using a pastry cutter
4. Place half the discs at finger-width distance on a water-moistened baking sheet
5. Brush with beaten eggs, leaving a finger-width border all around
6. Place a walnut-sized piece of cold pâté in the center of each disc
7. Cover each with the remaining round discs from the board, with the sides that were on the board facing upward rather than on the pâté
8. Press down lightly and brush with beaten eggs, leaving a strong knife-back-width border all around
9. Bake in the oven at fairly strong heat until golden yellow
10. Remove with a knife from the sheet and arrange in a bowl lined with a napkin

title: Puffballs (Puffballs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- puffballs
- butter
- green parsley
- cream
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Break off the stems and clean the puffballs inside
2. Peel them and place briefly in cold water, then drain
3. Dip them in butter, sprinkle with breadcrumbs and place in a well-buttered casserole
4. Braise them covered
5. Meanwhile, cut the removed stems with green parsley very finely and braise them in butter
6. Add cream, some salt and pepper and let it boil
7. Arrange the puffballs on a plate and pour this sauce over them
8. Puffballs can also be prepared like cèpes

title: Punch Ice (Punſch-Gefrornes)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cream
- egg yolk
- clarified sugar
- 1 seidel good arrack or rum

## Directions

1. Take the same mass as for other ices, but clarify somewhat more sugar
2. When sufficiently frozen in the freezing container, work in a seidel of good arrack or rum gradually with the stirring spatula before serving
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Punch Pastries Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- scraps of cut-out dough
- one third of the pastries
- 8 dkg cube sugar
- 6-8 spoons rum
- 1/2 liter water
- 1 spoon lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop the scraps of cut-out dough and one third of the pastries in a bowl
2. Cook 8 dkg cube sugar with 6-8 spoons rum, 1/2 liter water and 1 spoon lemon juice for a few minutes
3. Mix with the scraps to a thick porridge
4. Alternatively, bakery, torte and biscuit scraps can be used for the filling

title: Punch à la glace (Frozen Punch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Orange-Gefrorenes (No. 1753)
- 1/2 liter Champagne wine
- 1 1/8 liters Rum or Arrack

## Directions

1. Prepare an Orange-Gefrorenes (No. 1753) and work it together with 1/2 liter Champagne wine and 1 1/8 liters Rum or Arrack.
2. Serve in glasses.

title: Punch à la romaine (Frozen Punch in the Roman Manner)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca sugar
- 5 tablespoons cold water
- 4 liters egg white, firmly whipped
- 1 liter firm, very finely worked Orange-Gefrorenes
- 1 3/4 liters Champagne wine
- 6 tablespoons Arrack or Rum
- Juice of preserved pineapple, equal amount
- 1 tablespoon Allspice juice or 1 teaspoon Cochineal coloring

## Directions

1. Dissolve 12 deca sugar in 5 tablespoons cold water.
2. Mix this with the firm whipped egg white from 4 liters and blend into 1 liter firm, very finely worked Orange-Gefrorenes.
3. Stir in 1 3/4 liters Champagne wine, 6 tablespoons Arrack or Rum, an equal amount of juice from preserved pineapple, and 1 tablespoon Allspice juice or 1 teaspoon Cochineal coloring.
4. Serve this punch in glasses. The color should not be held too dark, but only appear very pale rose.

title: Punch-Torte (Punch Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 Deca flour
- Butter
- Flour
- Jam
- Punch essence or liqueur

## Directions

1. Prepare a biscuit mixture from 32 Deca flour
2. Fill a cake form brushed with butter and dusted with flour with this mixture
3. Bake at moderate heat for approximately 1 hour in the oven
4. After this time, turn the mixture out and when it has cooled completely, cut it in the middle to obtain 2 round slices
5. Cut one slice into cubes of equal size
6. Cover the bottom of the same cake form (in which the biscuit was first baked) with a sheet of writing paper greased with unsalted bacon
7. Arrange the cubically cut biscuit, with the cubes alternately dipped in punch essence and various colored liqueurs, close together in checkerboard pattern into the cake form to cover the bottom exactly
8. When this is done, cover the biscuit with boiling hot jam

title: Punsch (Punch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilo sugar cut into small pieces
- juice of 1 lemon
- juice of 4 sweet oranges
- 1/4 liter rum
- 2 drachms Russian tea
- 1 liter boiling water

## Directions

1. Place the sugar cut into small pieces with the juice of 1 lemon and 4 sweet oranges and 1/4 liter rum in a porcelain bowl
2. Let it stand covered in a warm place
3. In a second porcelain bowl, place 2 drachms Russian tea and pour 1 liter of boiling water over it
4. After 15 minutes, pour this to the dissolved sugar, stir well and serve immediately
5. A bottle of rum is placed on the table
6. Alternatively: place the very thinly cut peel of 1 orange and 1/2 kilo sugar that has been boiled in 1 liter water in a porcelain bowl, pour the syrup over it and cover
7. Press the juice from 5 oranges and 2 lemons, remove the peel, add the juice, add 1/4 liter rum and the same amount of tea, then serve the punch hot

title: Punsch auf englische Art (Punch à l'anglaise - English Punch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a lemon
- Three-quarter pound sugar
- 5 lemons
- 3 seidels boiling water
- 1 seidel rum
- 1 seidel genuine fruit brandy

## Directions

1. Peel half a lemon as finely as possible.
2. Cut the peel into small pieces and put it in a soup pot with three-quarter pound sugar.
3. Press the juice of 5 lemons into the pot.
4. Pour in first 3 seidels of boiling water, then 1 seidel of rum and the same amount of genuine fruit brandy.
5. Set the pot for several minutes on hot ashes or over a boiling water kettle.
6. Serve as usual.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Punsch auf gewöhnliche Art (Punch à l'ordinaire - Ordinary Punch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds sugar
- 4 lemons
- 12 lemons
- 3 measures boiling water
- Half a loth tea
- 1 bottle good arrack

## Directions

1. Rub the yellow of 4 lemons onto the 2 pounds of sugar.
2. Put the sugar in a soup pot.
3. Place the rubbed-off zest in a fine hair sieve or a colander lined with a napkin.
4. Press the juice of 12 lemons through the sieve.
5. Pour 3 measures of boiling water in which half a loth of tea has been steeped, along with 1 bottle of good arrack, over the mixture.
6. Cover the soup pot and set it on gentle charcoal fire.
7. When a white foam appears at the edge, remove it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Punsch Torte (Punch Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- eggs
- sugar
- water
- rum or punch
- lemon juice
- 10 sugar cubes
- jam

## Directions

1. Prepare a biscuit torte
2. Cut it apart the next day and fill it
3. If you have bakery or torte scraps, use them for the filling; or make a torte from half of the chocolate torte batter
4. In a small pot, cook several tablespoons of water, the same amount of rum or punch, lemon juice, and 10 sugar cubes for a few minutes
5. Pour over the scraps or chocolate torte, stir into a paste, and use as filling
6. Frost the torte red

title: Punsch à la Romaine (Roman Punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 biscuits
- 4 egg yolks
- 16 dkg sugar
- rum or punch to taste
- 1/2 whipped cream
- marmalade
- whipped cream for garnish

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg yolks with 16 dkg sugar until very foamy
2. Mix in rum or punch to taste and whipped cream
3. In a glass bowl, layer biscuits that have been spread with marmalade on the bottom
4. Pour the prepared mixture on top
5. Place the bowl on ice and let stand for 2–3 hours
6. Optionally garnish the top with 1/2 whipped cream before serving
7. Alternatively, fill glasses without biscuits, bury in ice, and top with whipped cream before serving

title: Punsch-Krapfen (Punch Crullers)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Spanish biscuit base
- punch-dipped biscuit pieces
- punch preserve glaze

## Directions

1. Prepare as indicated for Indian Crullers (recipe 1632), only with Punch Crullers the hollowed out Spanish biscuit is filled with a smaller piece of biscuit dipped in punch
2. Glaze them with punch preserve glaze instead of chocolate glaze

title: Punsch-Royal (Punch Royal)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds fine sugar
- 12 lemons
- 1 seidel water
- 1 loth fine tea
- 1 measure old Rhine wine
- 1 measure Burgundy
- 1 measure arrack
- Half a bottle good Maraschino
- 1 bottle Champagne wine

## Directions

1. Break 2 pounds of fine sugar into small pieces and put in a soup pot.
2. Pour the juice of 12 lemons and 1 seidel of water, in which 1 loth of fine tea has been steeped, through a napkin over the sugar.
3. As soon as the sugar has melted, pour in 1 measure of old Rhine wine, the same amount of Burgundy and arrack, then half a bottle of good Maraschino and 1 bottle of Champagne wine.
4. Stir everything well together.
5. Serve this punch cold.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Punsch-Schnitte (Punch Slices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deca finely sifted sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 10 Deca flour
- parchment paper
- previously prepared baked biscuit
- fine citron, chopped
- raisins
- currants
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Stir 16 Deca finely sifted sugar with 6 egg yolks for 1 1/2 hours
2. Lightly fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites from 6 eggs with 10 Deca flour
3. Spread this mixture knife-back width thick on a parchment-lined, filled baking sheet and bake pale yellow in the oven
4. Cut a previously prepared, cooled biscuit into finger-thick cubes and place on the pale yellow baked biscuit
5. Sprinkle with finely chopped citron mixed with raisins and currants
6. Fold the longer side of the dough 2 fingers wide, also cover with biscuit
7. Roll the dough up completely, set aside, weigh down with a light board
8. After about 1 hour remove the board, sprinkle the dough with sugar and cut into pieces of any desired size

title: Punsch-Sulz (Punch Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 decagrams sugar, clarified (see recipe 1542)
- 7 decagrams isinglass, hot and dissolved
- Punch essence or tea with rum, instead of wine, lemon and orange juice

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Orange Sulz (see recipe 1660)
2. Instead of wine, use punch essence or tea with rum

title: Punsch-Syrup (Punch Syrup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine sugar
- 2 pounds lemon juice
- 1.5 bottles rum or arrack

## Directions

1. Clarify the sugar
2. Add lemon juice and rum or arrack
3. Place on gentle charcoal fire and cook while stirring constantly, not for too long
4. Let cool
5. Pour rum in slowly
6. Stir well
7. Fill into bottles
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Punſch à la romain (Roman Punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 16 dkg sugar
- rum or punch to taste
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg yolks with 16 dkg sugar until very foamy, mix in rum or punch to taste, and add whipped cream.

title: Punſch-Gelee (Punch Jelly)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound of hartshorn
- 2 measures of water
- half a bottle of arrack
- three-quarters pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare like lemon jelly, but set the three-quarters pound of hartshorn with 2 measures of water instead of 3 halves to obtain more liquid to replace the wine missing here
2. When the jelly comes from the fire, pour in half a bottle of arrack
3. Use three-quarters pound of sugar instead of half a pound
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Punſch-Syrup (Punch Syrup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine sugar
- 2 pounds lemon juice
- One and a half bottles rum or arrack

## Directions

1. Clarify 1 pound fine sugar and add 2 pounds lemon juice as well as one and a half bottles rum or arrack
2. Place juice and sugar on gentle charcoal fire and cook not too long with constant stirring
3. Cool it down, pour the rum in slowly, stir it well, and fill it into bottles
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Punſchkrapfen (Punch Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 spoon fine breadcrumbs
- 6 stiff egg whites
- 15 dkg soaked grated almonds
- 7 dkg grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat 15 dkg sugar with 6 egg yolks for 30 minutes
2. Stir in 1 spoon fine breadcrumbs
3. Fold in 6 stiff egg whites with 15 dkg soaked grated almonds
4. Spread half the mass onto a buttered, floured baking sheet, not too thin
5. Bake until light brown
6. Immediately cut with a small pastry cutter
7. Mix 7 dkg grated chocolate into the other half and proceed the same way
8. Spread a dark layer quite thickly with filling
9. Place a light layer on top
10. Put the pastry in the cutter
11. Press down evenly with a matching weight so the edge becomes nice and smooth
12. Ice them with red glaze

title: Puree von getrockneten Erbsen (Purée de pois secs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Liter getrocknete Erbsen
- Salzwasser
- Butter
- Zucker

## Directions

1. wird ebenso bereitet, nur mit dem Unterschiede, daß man die 1 Liter getrockneten Erbsen längere Zeit kocht
2. und keinen Zucker dazugibt

title: Puree von grünen Erbsen (Purée de pois verts)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Liter grüne Zucker-Erbsen
- Salzwasser
- 1 Teelöffel voll Zucker
- 2 Deca frische Butter
- Salz
- Pfeffer
- geröstete Semmelschnitte
- verschiedene Fleischgattungen

## Directions

1. 1 1/2 Liter grüne Zucker-Erbsen werden in Salzwasser gekocht
2. abgeseiht und durchpassiert
3. in eine Casserole mit 1 Teelöffel voll Zucker gegeben
4. 2 Deca frische Butter wird hinzu getan
5. alles wird gut untereinander gerührt
6. wenn nötig, Salz und Pfeffer nach Geschmack beigegeben
7. verkochen lässt
8. die Erbsen werden gehäuft auf einer Schüssel mit gerösteten Semmelschnitten und verschiedenen Fleischgattungen angerichtet

title: Purée of Dried Peas (Purée de pois secs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter dried peas
- salt water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook 1 liter of dried peas in salt water for a longer time than fresh peas
2. Press through a sieve
3. Add salt and pepper to taste

title: Purée of Green Peas (Purée de pois verts)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 liters green fresh peas
- salt water
- 1 tablespoon poulette sauce
- 2 ounces fresh butter
- salt
- pepper
- roasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Cook 1.5 liters of green fresh peas in salt water
2. Press through a sieve
3. Pass through a fine strainer
4. Place in a casserole with 1 tablespoon of poulette sauce
5. Add 2 ounces fresh butter
6. Stir together well
7. Add salt and pepper to taste as needed
8. Arrange the peas heaped on a platter with roasted bread slices and various meat preparations

title: Pâte d'Orgeade (Almond Paste)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 4 loths bitter almonds
- a little water
- 1 pound finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch 1 pound sweet and 4 loths bitter almonds in boiling water until the skin can be removed
2. Wash them
3. Pound them very finely with a little water
4. Add 1 pound finely sifted sugar
5. Toast together over the fire with constant stirring until when you strike it with the flat of your hand, nothing of the mass sticks to it anymore
6. Remove the mass and when it has cooled, store it either in earthenware containers or form it into round sticks, roll in paper, and store in a cool place, not a damp one, until use
7. To make Orgeade, dissolve 1/4 pound of the mass in 1 measure of pure spring water
8. Let it steep for about an hour, then press through a napkin with some orange blossom water
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Pâté de cailles (Quail Pâté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 quails
- 1/2 veal forcemeat
- hare forcemeat, equal amount (see Cold Pâté Forcemeat No. 1035)
- butter dough

## Directions

1. The preparation and execution of this pâté is the same as specified for the 'Pâté of Poulardy,' with the difference that instead of 1/2 veal forcemeat, equal amount of hare forcemeat is used, and instead of the poulardy, 12 quails are used.
2. The quails, after the back has been cut open and the bones carefully loosened, must be filled with a small, cleaned truffle and the required hare forcemeat.
3. If desired, instead of one large pâté, 12 small ones can be made from the butter dough, which must always be shaped to correspond to the size of each individual quail.

title: Pâté Froid de Faisan (Cold Pheasant Pâté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 aged pheasant
- Hare farce (cold pâté farce)
- Truffles
- Farce

## Directions

1. Prepare as described in the 'Poularde Pâté' recipe (No. 1060), with the difference that hare farce is used instead of veal farce, and 1 aged pheasant, filled with truffles and farce as the poularde, is used instead.

title: Pâté Froid de Perdreaux (Cold Partridge Pâté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges
- Partridge farce
- Butter dough
- Bacon slices
- Truffles

## Directions

1. Prepare and treat the pâté the same way as described in the 'Poularde Pâté' recipe (No. 1060), with the exception that partridge farce is used instead of veal farce, and partridges are used instead of the poularde.

title: Pällfy's-Knödel oder Fetten-Knödel ohne Mehl (Palffy's Quenelles or Fat Quenelles without Flour)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rolls according to number of persons (8 rolls or 32 Loffel), finely diced and mixed with 1/2 liter milk
- 16 Loffel butter
- 6 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 20 Loffel finely diced bacon
- salt
- cloth

## Directions

1. For 8 rolls (or 32 Loffel), finely diced and mixed with 1/2 liter milk, take 16 Loffel butter and beat it fluffy
2. Beat in 6 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
3. Mix well with the rolls and 20 Loffel finely diced bacon
4. Add salt and put it in a cloth dipped in cold water, well wrung out and spread over a colander
5. Tie the cloth tightly with twine or gather the dough together
6. Put it in boiling salted water and let it cook covered for 1 1/2 hours
7. When serving, remove the cloth from the knödel, place it on a dish and pour browned roll crumbs over it and serve immediately

title: Pöckelfleisch oder Zunge (Pickled Beef or Tongue)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- A lean piece of beef or a tongue
- Ordinary salt
- Salt petre
- Coriander
- Bay leaves
- Pepper
- Juniper berries
- Rosemary
- Grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Take half ordinary salt and half salt petre and rub the beef or tongue designated for this well with it and place it in a screw-top vessel
2. Several pieces can however be placed together better
3. On each piece sprinkle some ordinary and salt petre, coriander, bay leaves, pepper and juniper berries, as well as some rosemary and a little grated horseradish
4. Screw it down well or weigh it down so that the brine covers it
5. Large pieces must always lie for 14 days
6. The tongues are cooked soft for use, and before serving are wrapped in a roll or scattered with various green herbs, served cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Quails in Salmis (Wachteln mit Salmis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Gutted, cleaned quails
- Bacon slices
- String
- Bacon (for lining)
- Root vegetables
- Onion
- Whole spices
- Red wine
- Soup
- Flour (8 teaspoons)
- Butter (8 teaspoons)
- Madeira wine
- Truffles (finely cut, optional)
- Semolina slices for crust

## Directions

1. Wrap the cleaned, seasoned, and dressed quails with bacon and bind with fine string
2. Line a casserole with bacon slices, root vegetables, onion, and whole spices
3. Place the quails inside and let roast covered briefly
4. Pour red wine and soup over them
5. Let simmer gently covered until tender
6. Remove the quails and set aside
7. Roast flour in butter, pour the strained cooking liquid over it
8. Mix well, add a few spoons of Madeira wine and finely cut truffles if desired
9. Bring to boil again
10. Remove bacon from quails and arrange them on a plate
11. Pour the salmis sauce over them
12. Line the edge of the plate with toasted semolina slices (croutons)

title: Quails with Fines Herbes (Wachteln an feiner Art)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Quails
- Salt
- Pepper
- 2 cleaned, sliced mushrooms
- 2 pieces of shallots
- 5 sprigs of parsley
- Butter (12 teaspoons)
- Flour (2 teaspoons)
- White wine (approximately 1/2 liter)
- Macaroni or steamed rice for serving

## Directions

1. Clean the quails with a cloth and draw the legs inside
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Finely cut the mushrooms, shallots, and parsley
4. Place the quails with these ingredients in a casserole with butter
5. Cover and roast for 10 minutes
6. Dust with flour and roast briefly
7. Pour approximately 1/2 liter white wine over it
8. Stir constantly until a thick sauce forms
9. Arrange the quails with the sauce on a plate
10. Serve with macaroni or steamed rice

title: Quails with Rice (Wachteln mit Reiß)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned quails
- bacon slices
- veal meat
- green parsley
- chives
- salt
- half a glass white wine
- several spoonfuls of good broth
- rice for garnish

## Directions

1. Put cleaned quails, some bacon slices, a piece of veal, a little green parsley, chives, some salt, half a glass white wine, and several spoonfuls of good broth into a casserole
2. Cook everything together on a gentle coal fire
3. When cooked, remove the quails
4. Clean the sauce of fat and strain through a sieve
5. Lay the quails in the middle of the dish
6. Pour the small sauce over them
7. Surround them with a rice border
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Quails, Roasted (Wachteln, gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- quails
- vinegar
- wine
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Clean the quails by plucking, washing, and salting them
2. Let them sit in salt for a while
3. Add half vinegar and an equal amount of wine
4. Add cinnamon and some cloves
5. Let them lie in the brine overnight
6. Roast them well in the juices
7. A small sauce can also be made from the brine
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Quince Compote (Compot von Quitten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- water
- sugar syrup
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces, cut them in half, and blanch in water like pears.
2. Once completely soft, cook them again strongly in syrup and lemon juice.
3. Once cooked through, arrange on a compote dish and pour the syrup over them, which must be cooked down to thickness.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Quince Marmalade (Marmelade von Quitten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful yellow and ripe quinces
- sugar (1 pound sugar per pound of quince pulp)
- juice of 2 lemons
- water

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces, cut them into four parts, and remove the cores.
2. Place in a pan with plenty of water on charcoal fire.
3. Cook until completely soft.
4. Pour off and press the pulp through a sieve.
5. Weigh the sugar: one pound of sugar per pound of quince pulp.
6. Clarify and cook all the sugar.
7. Stir the quince pulp into the sugar.
8. Add the juice of 2 lemons while cooking.
9. Keep on charcoal fire until thick, stirring to prevent burning.
10. Allow to cool before storing in jars or containers.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Quitten-Gefrornes (Quince Ice Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 beautiful quince pieces
- Water
- 1 pound strained quince puree
- Three quarter pound clarified sugar
- 2 glasses Malaga wine or Tokayer
- Zest of one lemon and juice of two lemons

## Directions

1. Cook 12 beautiful quince pieces in water until soft
2. Let them drain and press through
3. For 1 pound strained quince, take three quarter pound clarified sugar, 2 glasses Malaga wine or Tokayer, the zest of one and the juice of two lemons
4. Press through once more and make the ice
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Quitten-Käse (Quince Paste)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Old quince apples
- Cold water mixed with lemon juice
- Sugar (same weight as the quince pulp)

## Directions

1. Aged quince apples are peeled, cut into 4 parts, freed of the core and immediately put into a cassole in which cold water mixed with lemon juice is found
2. When the apples have been cooked soft in this water, they are taken out with a slotted spoon and passed through a sieve
3. Now put as much finely pounded sugar as the quince pulp weighs into a saucepan and stir it with the passed apples with a wooden spoon on the fire until everything has cooked down into a very thick mass, which is then set aside
4. Arbitrary forms which are brushed with oil and dusted with finely sifted sugar are filled with the solid quince mass, which is pressed in firmly with a spoon dipped in warm water and dried in a cool, open oven for 4 hours

title: Quitten-Liqueur (Quince Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- the largest and finest quinces
- 1 Maß brandy
- 3 halves quince juice
- 1 pound sugar per half of juice

## Directions

1. Take the largest and finest quinces
2. Peel them and remove the core house
3. Crush them in a large mortar
4. Press through a strong cloth
5. For 1 Maß brandy take 3 halves quince juice, and for each half take 1 pound sugar
6. Place everything together in a large glass, cover well
7. Let stand for 12 days
8. On the thirteenth day, filter through filter paper
9. Fill into bottles, cork well, and let stand for 6 months
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Quitten-Marmelade (Quince Jam)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Quince apples or pears, split
- Quince, peeled and rubbed with lemon juice
- Cold water then boiling lemon water
- 1 kilo quince, 1/2 kilo sugar and 1/2 liter water

## Directions

1. Quince apples or pears are cut in splits, the core removed, quinces peeled, each piece rubbed with lemon juice
2. Then thrown into cold, then into boiling lemon water and therein stewed fairly soft
3. Then put the quinces in boiling quince water (1 kilo quinces, 1/2 kilo sugar and 1/2 liter water)
4. When they are then quite soft cooked, they are quickly passed through a sieve, allowed to come to boil again and filled, still hot, into jars
5. Should the quinces have lost their color, add a few drops of cochineal at the last cooking
6. One can also let some pieces of orange blossoms steep with it

title: Quittenkoch (Quince Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 1 small bread roll, sliced leaf-wise and cooked in milk
- 1.5 quarter-pounds almonds, softened and finely ground
- 6 whole eggs
- 6 egg yolks
- zest of 1/2 lemon, finely cut
- 1/4 pound quinces, softened, pitted, cooked, and pressed through a sieve
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat 1/4 pound butter until fluffy
2. Add the soft interior of 1 small bread roll sliced leaf-wise and cooked in milk
3. When cooled and well beaten, add 1.5 quarter-pounds softened and finely ground almonds
4. Add 6 whole eggs and 6 egg yolks, each well beaten
5. Add finely cut zest of 1/2 lemon, 1/4 pound quinces softened, pitted, cooked, and pressed through a sieve
6. Add sugar to taste and a little cinnamon
7. Stir everything for another quarter hour
8. Butter a metal dish and ring mold
9. Fill the mixture in and bake slowly in a cake pan
10. Sprinkle with sugar afterwards
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Quittensalat (Quince Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful large quinces
- Water
- Good wine
- Sugar
- Lemon peel
- Whole cinnamon
- Cloves
- Almonds
- Citron
- Spanish sour cherries

## Directions

1. Take beautiful large quinces, peel them and cut them in half
2. Remove the seeds
3. Cook them in water until they become soft to the touch
4. Pour off the water and add good wine and sugar
5. Let them cook together until soft
6. Arrange them on a serving dish so they remain beautifully white
7. Add to the sauce lemon peel, whole cinnamon and cloves
8. Let them reduce to a nice thick consistency, strain them and pour the sauce over the quinces
9. Cover them with oblong-cut almonds, citron and Spanish sour cherries
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout de Mouton aux Navets (Mutton Ragout with Turnips)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton neck, breast, and shoulders
- bacon
- onions, leafy-cut
- flour
- bay leaf
- salt
- pepper
- thyme
- turnips, white

## Directions

1. Cut the neck, breast, and shoulders into pieces two fingers wide and let them brown in a casserole with bacon and finely cut onions over fairly strong heat.
2. Remove the browned meat pieces and place them in another casserole.
3. In the fat remaining in the original casserole, stir in several spoonfuls of flour, let it brown, and then deglaze with some broth.
4. Season with 1 bay leaf, salt, white pepper and some thyme, strain this, and pour over the meat pieces.
5. Place neatly cut white turnips at the side of the casserole and let everything braise together covered until tender.
6. Alternatively, braise the meat alone with the strained sauce and garnish with white turnips braised in butter.

title: Ragout for Sweet Fillings
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf sweetbreads
- Fricasseed meat
- Crayfish tails
- Asparagus tips
- Truffles (finely diced or cut in thin slices, as desired)
- Cooked chicken meat (roasted or braised) and some mushrooms
- Butter sauce (well-cooked)
- Wine and lemon juice (as needed)
- Egg yolks
- Cold cream
- Breadcrumbs or Parmesan cheese (if desired)

## Directions

1. Cook each ingredient separately
2. Dice the meat, crayfish tails, mushrooms, and other ingredients finely
3. Add to well-cooked butter sauce
4. Add wine and lemon juice as needed
5. When well-cooked, mix in egg yolks beaten with cold cream
6. Use as filling for baked mushrooms, pastries, baked scallops or mussels
7. For mussels: fill the ragout, sprinkle with breadcrumbs or Parmesan cheese, place on a sheet pan, bake in the oven, and serve as an appetizer or side dish

title: Ragout in Form von Puderzucker (Ragout in the Form of Powdered Sugar)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Ragout mixture (from Ragout Croquettes recipe)
- Flour for dusting
- Water-softened wafers
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard for frying

## Directions

1. Take the mixture used for Ragout Croquettes and place it on a flour-dusted board
2. Cut it into pieces 1 finger long and 2 fingers thick
3. Give these pieces a sugar hat shape by rolling them
4. Wrap them in water-softened wafers, dip them in beaten, whipped eggs, roll them in breadcrumbs and fry them golden in very hot lard
5. Remove with a slotted spoon, place them on blotting paper, arrange them on a napkin in a bowl and serve as garnish at table

title: Ragout of Mutton Tails (Ragout von Hammelſchweifen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton tails
- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Good coulis or espagnole
- Bouillon or stock
- Blanched calf sweetbreads
- Fine liver dumplings
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices

## Directions

1. Cook several mutton tails in the braise only halfway so they do not fall apart in the ragout
2. Remove them and cut them in half
3. In a casserole, sweat mushrooms in butter
4. Add good coulis or espagnole
5. Pour in bouillon or stock as needed so it can cook completely
6. Place blanched calf sweetbreads, the mutton tails, and fine liver dumplings into the sauce
7. Season the ragout well
8. Finish on gentle charcoal fire
9. The ragout should be neither too thick nor too thin
10. It can be served in soup pots, in Bernese casseroles, or in a pastry
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout of Veal (Ragout de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's spinal cord
- 12 ounces butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley
- 1 knife-tip of nutmeg
- 1 knife-tip of white pepper
- 16 ounces white bread crumbs, moistened with milk
- flour for lining the mold

## Directions

1. Blanch the calf's spinal cord
2. Beat 12 ounces butter until fluffy
3. Beat in 2 whole eggs and 1 egg yolk
4. Stir this into the prepared spinal cord
5. Stir in 1 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley, 1 knife-tip of nutmeg and 1 knife-tip of white pepper
6. Mix in 16 ounces white bread crumbs, moistened with milk, to form a dough
7. Cook in a buttered mold lined with flour in a steamer, or bake in a shallow casserole
8. Cut into desired pieces and add to the soup

title: Ragout of Veal (Ragout von Kalbfleiſch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of cold roasted veal
- butter
- flour
- broth
- cream
- ground pepper
- 25 to 30 asparagus pieces
- mushrooms
- green parsley
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Take a piece of cold roasted veal, cut it into large cubes, and make a butter sauce: let a piece of butter the size of an egg become warm, add a spoonful of flour, let it brown slightly, fill it with broth, add a spoonful of cream and some ground pepper.
2. Once the sauce is ready, add the veal pieces, also 25 to 30 pieces of asparagus and mushrooms, which should first be braised in butter and green parsley; let everything braise together well until no sauce remains.
3. Arrange it in a dish in the shape of a mountain, and place a wreath of puff pastry around it. If asparagus is no longer available, cauliflower may be used instead, which should first be boiled in salt water and cut into pieces.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout von Allerhand, mit Braiſen und Champignons (Ragout of Various Meats with Braised Meats and Mushrooms)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Palate
- Cow udder
- Calf ears
- Pork ears
- Feet (of various meats)
- Calf braises
- Salt water
- Mushrooms
- Crayfish claws and tails
- Oysters
- Flour
- Beef broth
- Nutmeg
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take palate, cow udder, calf ears, pork ears, and feet of each as much as needed, and simmer them with a pair of calf braises in salt water until tender.
2. The braises are larded lightly, the other parts are cleaned well; then cut them lengthwise, but not too small.
3. Cut some mushrooms into leaves.
4. You may also add crayfish claws and tails, as well as oysters.
5. Make the flour brown in a casserole, let the broth run light brown into it, then pour in beef broth, salt it, and add the finely cut pieces, season with nutmeg and lemon juice, and let it come to a boil once.
6. When serving, place the braises on top.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout von Champignons (Mushroom Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- coulis
- bouillon
- salt
- pepper
- Madeira wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms and scrape them well
2. Grill them
3. When they become reddish, put them in a casserole with some coulis, a little bouillon, salt, pepper, and a small glass of Madeira wine
4. Press the juice from half a lemon into it
5. Let the ragout cook for half an hour
6. Arrange it then
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout von Kalbs-Braiſen (Ragout of Veal Brains)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- veal brains
- beef palate, boiled and cleaned
- morels
- mushrooms
- onions, finely chopped
- bay leaves
- salt
- roux

## Directions

1. Cut ham into thin slices as much as needed and cook them properly
2. Wash the veal brains well, let them become firm again in warm water, clean them
3. Place them with the ham slices in a casserole
4. Cut boiled, well-cleaned, and cooled beef palate into small pieces along with morels and mushrooms
5. Put this in the casserole with finely chopped onions, bay leaves and salt
6. Make a somewhat brown roux, pour a little meat broth to it
7. Pour this over the ragout and let it cook together
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout von Maurachen, mit gebackenem Endivienſalat (Ragout of Sea Bream with Fried Endive Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Sea bream
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fresh parsley, finely chopped
- Pea broth
- Egg yolks
- Endive salad
- Cloves
- Nutmeg
- Bay leaves
- Onions, finely cut
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard

## Directions

1. Cut the sea bream lengthwise in half, wash them well, then brown them in a casserole, add salt, pepper, and finely chopped fresh parsley, let them simmer in pea broth until they become thick, then stir in a couple of egg yolks.
2. Meanwhile cook well-cleaned endive salad in pea broth seasoned with cloves, nutmeg, a couple of bay leaves, green parsley, salt, and finely cut onions.
3. When it is tender enough, remove it and let it drain.
4. Beat five or six eggs, dip the salad well into them, sprinkle it with breadcrumbs, fry it in hot, only lightly salted lard, and garnish the ragout with it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout von Repphühnern, in Breslauer Paſtetchen (Partridge Ragout in Breslau Pastries)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs
- Bread crumb paste
- Egg
- Two partridges
- Ham, diced
- Shallots, finely chopped
- Coriander
- Cloves
- Anise
- Peppercorns
- Wine
- Meat broth
- Sugar

## Directions

1. For the pastries, make a good puff pastry and roll it out.
2. Grease small pastry forms two fingers high with butter and sprinkle them with breadcrumbs; line them with this dough and place a piece of bread crumb paste cut into squares in the middle.
3. The lid is rolled out somewhat thicker than the bottom, and after being brushed with egg, is placed over the pastries, the excess cleanly cut off so the dough can rise high, and bake the pastries quickly.
4. For the ragout, roast two partridges, remove the breast meat and cut into small pieces, but pound the rest.
5. Set this on with some butter, a piece of ham cut into cubes, finely chopped shallots, coriander, some whole cloves, anise and peppercorns.
6. Sweat for a quarter hour, pour in half a pint of good wine, and let everything cook through well.
7. Then add some meat broth and let it cook again.
8. Strain the sauce through a hair sieve, place the finely cut partridges in it, and fill the pastries piping hot: namely remove the lids, remove the bread crumb paste, fill with as much filling as needed, then cover them again and sprinkle with sugar.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout von Schildkröten (Turtle Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Turtle
- Salt water
- Butter
- Pea water
- Green parsley
- Half a Seidel of young green peas
- Twelve finely chopped mushrooms
- Fifteen to twenty crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Boil the turtles in salt water until soft, break the shells in two parts, remove the cooked turtles, peel off the black skin from the feet and other parts, and carefully remove the entrails and liver so the gall does not burst.
2. Cut the turtles into four parts, make a small butter sauce, and fill it with pea water.
3. Take a piece of butter, some green parsley, half a Seidel of young green peas, twelve finely chopped mushrooms, fifteen to twenty crayfish tails, and let them braise.
4. When everything is well braised, add it to the turtles and let everything cook well together.
5. It should have only a little sauce when it is served at table.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout von Schnepfen (Woodcock Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcocks
- Wine
- Meat broth
- Butter
- Flour
- Woodcock liver, chopped
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- Mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut the woodcocks, after they have been well cleaned and steamed in half wine and half meat broth, into two parts.
2. Make butter hot, toast flour in it until beautifully brown, place the cut woodcocks in it, stir the chopped liver and what else is reckoned to woodcock ragout into the roux, pour over the wine and meat broth in which the woodcocks were cooked, season with cloves, pepper, nutmeg and salt, add some mushrooms, and let it come to a boil.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout von Schweinsfüßen (Pork Feet Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork feet
- Water
- Butter
- Flour
- Onions, finely cut
- Meat broth
- Nutmeg
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Salt
- Vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook the feet in water, let them remain until cold, then cut them into pieces.
2. Place a piece of butter in a casserole, brown a little flour and finely cut onions in it, add the cut feet as well, pour good meat broth over them, season with nutmeg, pepper, whole cloves and salt, and let them cook.
3. Before serving, add two spoonfuls of vinegar or squeeze the juice of a lemon into it, and let it come to a boil once more with it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout von Tauben (Ragout de pigeons aux marrons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 plucked, scalded, washed, and salted pigeons
- 16 deca of liver forcemeat (No. 1032)
- 10 deca of butter
- Flour
- White broth
- 16 deca of smoked sausage
- 30 pieces of roasted, peeled chestnuts
- 4 deca of sugar
- 20 small onions
- 10 pieces of boiled, dressed mushrooms

## Directions

1. Fill the pigeons with liver forcemeat, dress them, and place in a casserole containing 10 deca of butter.
2. Let them braise covered slowly for approximately 1 1/2 hours.
3. Remove the pigeons and place in another casserole.
4. Dust the butter remaining in the first casserole with flour, roast it, add white broth, stir well, and simmer.
5. Pour the sauce through a sieve over the pigeons and braise covered until they are tender.
6. 1/4 hour before they are completely tender, add 16 deca of smoked sausage to the pigeons.
7. Meanwhile, keep 30 roasted, peeled chestnuts warm with 4 deca of sugar in the same amount of butter.
8. Cut the pigeons into 4 pieces, arrange on a platter, garnish with chestnuts, 20 small onions braised in butter until white and tender, and 10 boiled, dressed mushrooms, and serve with the sauce.

title: Ragout von Trüffeln (Truffle Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- white coulis
- lemon juice
- croutons (roasted bread slices)

## Directions

1. Wash the truffles, peel them, and cut them into thin slices
2. Set a large piece of butter on fire in a casserole
3. Pass the truffles for a while
4. Add salt and pepper
5. Stir in a white coulis
6. When serving, skim off the fat and press lemon juice into it
7. Garnish them with croutons (roasted bread slices)
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ragout-Aepfel und Croquettes (Ragout Apples and Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Ragout mixture (from Ragout Croquettes recipe)
- Flour for dusting
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard for frying

## Directions

1. Use the same preparation as for Ragout Croquettes, with the difference that instead of sugar hats, form apples, pears or croquettes from the mixture in any desired size
2. Continue as with the Ragout Croquettes

title: Ragouts Suppe (Ragout Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- one softly cooked calf's head or ear, or together with cooked calf's sweetbread
- 12 Deciliter butter
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 2 pieces of thinly sliced mushrooms
- 1.5 teaspoon chopped parsley
- approximately 2 liters soup
- toasted rolls, small diced calf's head, or bread crumbles

## Directions

1. Cook a softly cooked calf's head or ear, or together with cooked calf's sweetbread
2. Place in cold water and then cut into arbitrary small pieces
3. Heat 12 Deciliter butter in a saucepan and add 4 tablespoons flour
4. When it foams, add 2 pieces of thinly sliced mushrooms and 1.5 teaspoon chopped parsley
5. Let it roast
6. Pour over with approximately 2 liters soup in which the calf's head and sweetbread were cooked
7. Stir over heat until it comes to a boil and let it simmer slowly for approximately 1.5 hours
8. Remove from heat, strain through a sieve, return to the saucepan
9. Add the diced calf's head and sweetbread, bring to a boil again
10. Serve with toasted rolls, small diced calf's head, or bread crumbles

title: Ragoût-Wurst (Consommé garni au pain de ragoût)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 16 deca finely cut roasted meat
- parsley
- salt
- 6 deca breadcrumbs
- milk
- peas or asparagus peas formed in salted water
- crayfish tails
- 1/16 liter old fat
- 1/2 liter cream
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 12 deca butter until fluffy
2. Beat in 6 egg yolks
3. Add 16 deca finely cut roasted meat, some parsley, salt, and 6 deca breadcrumbs that were moistened with milk
4. Add peas or asparagus peas formed in salted water and crayfish tails
5. Add 1/16 liter old fat and 1/2 liter cream
6. Mix everything well together
7. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
8. Bake or cook in steam
9. Can also use roasted or cooked fish meat

title: Rahm Schmarn (Cream Scramble)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter flour
- 1/4 liter sour cream
- 2 tablespoons finely ground sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites (snow)
- 8 deka hot butter

## Directions

1. Place 1/4 liter flour in a deep bowl
2. Gradually stir in 1/4 liter sour cream until smooth
3. Mix in 2 tablespoons finely ground sugar and 1/2 teaspoon salt
4. Stir in 8 egg yolks one after another
5. Gently fold in the snow from 8 egg whites
6. Pour this dough into a casserole containing 8 deka hot butter
7. Treat further as described for the kleine Kaiserschmarn

title: Rahm Strudel (Sour Cream Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/2 liter flour
- butter
- egg yolks
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough from 3/2 liter flour, let rest 1/2 hour
2. In the meantime, beat 8 ounces of butter with egg yolks

title: Rahm-Strudel (Cream Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ liters flour
- ½ liter milk
- 12 deca butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 12 deca sugar (vanilla or regular)
- ½ teaspoon salt
- ½ liter sour cream
- 24 deca passed poppy seeds or 24 deca breadcrumbs soaked in milk and pressed out, or ½ liter soft breadcrumbs
- 8 egg whites
- white breadcrumbs
- ¼ liter hot milk
- 2 egg yolks
- cold milk
- ⅛ liter milk
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare a strudel dough from 1½ liters flour and let it rest for ½ hour
2. Beat together 12 deca butter and 8 egg yolks gradually
3. Mix in 12 deca sugar (vanilla or regular), ½ teaspoon salt, and ½ liter sour cream
4. Blend in 24 deca passed poppy seeds or breadcrumbs (either 24 deca soaked in milk and pressed out, or ½ liter soft breadcrumbs)
5. Lightly fold in the snow (whipped egg whites) from 8 egg whites
6. Stretch the strudel dough as described in the Young Strudel recipe, spread it evenly with the filling, and sprinkle with white breadcrumbs
