7. Roll up and treat as described in the Ham Strudel recipe
8. If desired to prevent dryness, pour ¼ liter hot milk over it halfway through baking
9. After baking, cut into desired pieces, place on a serving dish using a pastry server, and sprinkle with finely crushed sugar
10. Beat 2 egg yolks with a few spoonfuls of cold milk, pour into ⅛ liter of hot milk, sweeten to taste, and serve separately as a sauce in a sauceboat
11. This milk is called 'Canary Milk'

title: Rahmdalken (Cream Dalken)
body:
## Ingredients

- cream (Rahm)
- 10 dkg flour
- 5 dkg sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Whip the cream well with 10 dkg flour, 5 dkg sugar, 3 egg yolks and salt
2. Fold in 3 beaten egg whites
3. Heat fat in a dalken pan, pour in 1 tablespoon of dough into each indentation
4. Bake light brown on both sides
5. Remove dalken, add more fat and dough and continue until all mixture is used
6. Spread jam between two dalken, press together, sprinkle with sugar and serve warm

title: Rahmstrudel (Cream Strudel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 dkg Flour
- 1 Egg
- Salt
- Butter (walnut-sized)
- Almost 1 dl lukewarm water
- ½ l Cream (for filling)
- 6 dkg Butter (for filling)
- 3 Egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons Sugar
- 2 tablespoons Breadcrumbs
- 3 Egg whites
- 1 l Cream
- ¼ l Milk (for basting)

## Directions

1. Make strudel dough from flour, egg, salt, butter, and lukewarm water
2. After resting, stretch thinly on floured cloth
3. For filling: cream butter, add egg yolks and sugar, mix in cream and breadcrumbs, fold in beaten egg whites (whites should be beaten while dough is resting)
4. Spread filling on dough and roll up
5. Place in buttered baking pan and bake until light brown
6. Baste frequently with hot milk during baking
7. Preparation method is the same as for Potato Strudel

title: Rahmsuppe (Cream Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ l water
- salt
- caraway seeds
- ¼-½ l cream
- flour
- bread rolls for croutons

## Directions

1. Bring ¼ l water to a boil with salt and caraway seeds
2. Whisk cream with a few tablespoons of flour until smooth
3. Stir the cream mixture into the boiling water
4. Let the soup simmer for 20 minutes
5. Serve with bread croutons

title: Rail Hens, Roasted (Rohrhühner, gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- rail hens
- brine/marinade
- butter
- cream

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the rail hens
2. Let them soak in water for one hour, then place them in brine for one day
3. Before putting them on the spit, salt and dress them, then roast them well
4. During roasting, baste them several times with the brine, and finally with cream and butter
5. When roasted, serve them on the table with the sauce poured over them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Randkuchen (Ring Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound butter
- 12 egg yolks
- zest of one lemon
- 1 pound fine flour
- half pound sugar
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir three-quarters pound butter with 12 egg yolks and lemon zest to foam
2. Mix 1 pound fine flour with half pound sugar together
3. Fold in the peaks of 6 eggs
4. Draw everything into the mass
5. Bake slowly in a ring form
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Raspberry Ice (Gefrornes von Himbeeren)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make as for strawberry ice
2. In proportion, use somewhat less sugar
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Raspberry Vinegar (Himbeers-Essig)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Selected ripe raspberries
- Wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Fill a bottle one-third full with selected ripe raspberries
2. Pour genuine wine vinegar over them, but the bottle should not be completely full
3. Cork it well and proceed as with Sour Cherry Vinegar (No. 1896)

title: Red Butter (Beurre Rouge)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 decagrams ordinary crayfish butter
- 5 decagrams ordinary butter
- 5 decagrams anchovy butter
- 3 hard-boiled, strained egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons oil
- 1 tablespoon strong vinegar
- salt
- cochineal or currant juice for coloring

## Directions

1. Beat the crayfish butter and ordinary butter fluffy with the anchovy butter
2. Add the strained hard-boiled egg yolks and beat fluffy
3. Gradually stir in 2 tablespoons of oil
4. At the same time as the oil, stir in 1 tablespoon strong vinegar
5. Salt the butter and color it slightly red with cochineal or currant juice

title: Red Sugar
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- hail sugar
- alkermes juice or dissolved cochineal dye

## Directions

1. Place the necessary hail sugar on a plate, moisten it with a few drops of alkermes juice or dissolved cochineal dye (both are obtainable at the pharmacy), and mix this with a metal spoon back and forth until the sugar has taken on an evenly beautiful red color and is completely dry. If one wants it darker, one must color it again after drying. The moistening must, however, always be done only in small measure, because too much moisture could dissolve the sugar; if time is pressing, one can also let the colored sugar dry in the oven, though it is better to do this in the air. Not only hail sugar but also the very fine sugar is colored in this way, only one must, after it has dried, pound and sieve it again. When the sugar is completely dry, it is stored in a glass jar.

title: Redcurrant Juice (Johannisbeersaft)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Redcurrants
- Redcurrant juice of the specified quantity

## Directions

1. Boil it for 1/4 hour with continuous rapid stirring
2. Strain it through flannel and fill it into bottles only when it has cooled
3. Do not use iron or enameled vessels during preparation of the redcurrant juice, as the juice would become blue or black from it; instead of flannel, one can also use a finer linen cloth

title: Rehbrücken (Roe Deer Loin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Roe deer loin
- Butter
- Large flat knife
- Sauce (piquant or other)
- Lingonberries or rowan berries sauce (optional)
- Buttermilk pastry for garnish (optional)

## Directions

1. In the raw state, trim the loin neatly, and with a kitchen mallet lightly strike the bones on the sides, but they must not be broken through so that the loin lies evenly lengthwise on the platter
2. The skinning, spitting and further treatment is the same as for the deer loin and roe deer leg
3. When serving, place the roe deer loin on a board and remove the meat from the bone using a large flat knife
4. Place the backbone on a platter, cut the meat into slices two fingers wide, lay them close together on the bone so that the loin looks completely whole, pour juice over it and garnish with buttermilk pastry
5. You can also serve lingonberries or rowan berries sauce with it

title: Rehruͤcken, gebraten (Roasted Venison Loin)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- vinegar
- onion
- bacon for larding
- paper
- marinade (beitze) and cream for basting

## Directions

1. Prepare the loin like the leg, namely marinated in vinegar and onion, and let it lie in the marinade for one or two days
2. Strip the skin cleanly
3. Lard it with bacon
4. Bind it with paper
5. Roast it slowly on the spit
6. Baste it several times with marinade and cream
7. When well roasted, arrange it on a platter and garnish like venison leg
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rehrücken (Venison Loin Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dkg sugar
- 1 whole egg
- 5 egg yolks
- 10 dkg raw grated almonds
- 5 dkg breadcrumbs moistened with rum
- 3 stiff egg whites
- 7 dkg grated chocolate
- jam
- swelled almonds or pine nuts
- for glaze: 25 dkg cube sugar
- water
- 12 dkg chocolate
- butter the size of a hazelnut

## Directions

1. Beat 10 dkg sugar with 1 whole egg and 5 egg yolks for 20 minutes
2. Stir in 10 dkg raw grated almonds and 5 dkg breadcrumbs moistened with rum
3. Fold in 3 stiff egg whites together with 7 dkg grated chocolate
4. Fill the batter into a buttered, floured large Rehrücken form and bake slowly
5. Turn out and cut apart the next day
6. Spread jam on the inside and outside, reassemble, frost with brown glaze, and stud with swelled almonds cut into sticks or pine nuts
7. For the glaze: combine 25 dkg cube sugar with corresponding water, 12 dkg chocolate, and butter the size of a hazelnut

title: Rehrücken ohne Dotter (Venison Loin Cake without Egg Yolks)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg butter
- 12 dkg sugar
- 12 dkg softened chocolate
- 5 stiff egg whites
- 12 dkg flour

## Directions

1. Cream together 14 dkg butter
2. Gradually stir in 12 dkg sugar
3. Mix in 12 dkg softened chocolate and 5 stiff egg whites together with 12 dkg flour
4. Fill the batter into a buttered, floured form and bake and finish as the previous recipe

title: Rehschlägel mit Rahm (Roe Deer Leg with Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Roe deer leg, skinned and spitted
- Fat
- Marinade (as described in No. 703)
- Onion
- Root vegetables
- Spices
- Juniper berries
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Water
- Butter
- Paper for wrapping
- Cream

## Directions

1. Prepare a marinade as specified in No. 703
2. Put some fat in a casserole, place the skinned and spitted leg on it, put it in the oven and baste it frequently with several spoonfuls of marinade during roasting
3. After approximately 1½ hours it will be tender
4. 1 hour before serving, baste it with ½ liter of cream and let it finish roasting
5. Cut the meat into pieces as desired, strain and pass the sauce and pour it over or serve in a sauceboat
6. Alternatively, place the leg directly in the casserole prepared with fat, onion, root vegetables, spices and juniper berries, and after it has gone down, baste it with equal parts wine, vinegar and water
7. Let it braise covered with a sheet of paper brushed with butter for approximately 1⅛ hours in the oven
8. Baste it with cream ⅓ hour before it is done and let it finish roasting uncovered
9. Transfer it with the fat skimmed from the sauce to another casserole and set it in a warm place
10. Strain the sauce and pour it over the cut meat or serve in a sauceboat

title: Reines Obst Dez. (Reine Glaubes Plums)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful Reine-Claudes plums that are ripe but not soft
- stems
- 80 decilitres sugar
- 1 litre water

## Directions

1. After weighing beautiful Reine-Claudes that are ripe but not soft, cut the stems short
2. Pierce each piece five times with a skewer snappily at the stone, the needle must go through to the opposite side
3. Place immediately in jars
4. Treat further as the Muscatel pears (Nr. 1842), only using 80 decilitres sugar in 1 litre water instead of 60

title: Reinglotte-Marmelade (Greengage Preserves)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram greengages
- 0.5 kilogram powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the ripe greengages, remove the kernels, and simmer 1 kilogram of greengages with 0.5 kilogram of powdered sugar in a covered saucepan.
2. After approximately 10 minutes, pass quickly through a sieve, cook in the same way, and fill hot into jars like Apricot Preserves.
3. The greengages should not be cooked too long as they will otherwise lose their green color.

title: Reis (Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bowl rice
- 1½ bowls water
- 5 dkg butter or fat
- 1 onion, studded with cloves (optional)
- salt

## Directions

1. Place the selected rice in a casserole with cold water (1 bowl rice to 1½ bowls water), 5 dkg butter or fat, optionally an onion studded with cloves, and salt.
2. After it has boiled for a few minutes, pull it back from the heat, cover it and let it steam for 30–40 minutes.
3. Do not stir the rice before the water has evaporated, or it will become mushy.
4. If after this time it is not yet soft, pour in a little hot water and let it finish steaming.
5. If serving in a mold, rinse the mold first with hot water, press the rice in and turn it out.
6. Garnish the center with curled parsley.

title: Reis mit Kraut (Rice with Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small head of cabbage, finely diced
- 12 deka lard, diced
- 16 deka rice, steamed (Pilau)

## Directions

1. Dice 1 small head of cabbage finely
2. Brown it in 12 deka diced hot lard
3. Prepare 16 deka steamed rice (Pilau)
4. Mix well with the steamed cabbage
5. Arrange in a bowl and serve with bratwurst or roasted liver at table

title: Reis mit Paradies-Apfel (Rice with Paradise Apple)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large paradise apples
- thin slices of lemon
- 16 deka steamed rice (Pilau)

## Directions

1. Break apart 4 large paradise apples
2. Steam them with a few thin slices of lemon
3. Press them through a sieve into 16 deka steamed rice
4. Stir well together
5. Arrange in a bowl and serve at table

title: Reis mit Vanillecreme (Rice with Vanilla Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deca washed rice
- 7.5 liters milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 0.5 liters milk
- 24 Deca finely pounded vanilla sugar
- 2 Deca gelatin
- 0.25 liters cold water
- 1.5 liters heavy cream

## Directions

1. Cook 16 Deca washed rice in 7.5 liters milk until very thick and set aside covered.
2. Beat 8 egg yolks, 0.5 liters milk, and 24 Deca finely pounded vanilla sugar together in a saucepan over heat, stirring until it begins to thicken, but do not allow to boil.
3. Press this mixture through a sieve into the rice and stir well together so that each grain of rice is separate, then place the rice on the stove.
4. Dissolve 2 Deca gelatin with 0.25 liters cold water in a saucepan over heat, stirring until completely dissolved and steaming hot, but do not allow to boil.
5. Add the dissolved gelatin together with the rice in a saucepan and stir both over low heat until well mixed, but do not allow to boil.
6. Transfer this mass to a deep bowl and stir until it cools.
7. Once cooled, whip 1.5 liters heavy cream to stiff peaks.
8. When the prepared cream feels lukewarm, fold the whipped cream in as quickly as possible until completely mixed.
9. Immediately transfer into a mold brushed with fine oil, which has been buried in ice 1 hour earlier.
10. Cover the mold with a plate and place a cloth over it.
11. After approximately 2 hours the cream will be sufficiently set.
12. Remove the mold from the ice, wipe it, press the cream away from the sides of the mold with your fingers, and turn the cream out onto a flat plate.
13. If it does not turn out easily, dip the mold briefly in hot water, wipe it dry, and turn out.
14. If the cream does not look quite beautiful with visible oil, quickly wipe it with a clean cloth.
15. Garnish with meringues, Spanish canes, small pastries, or fruit cooked in sugar.

title: Reis-Croquettes (Rice Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 tablespoons washed rice
- 0.5 liter boiling milk
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 whole egg
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 knife point of salt
- Flour for rolling
- Hot lard for frying
- 4 tablespoons sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Place the washed rice in 0.5 liter boiling milk and let it cook soft and thick covered
2. Stir in 2 tablespoons butter and let the rice cool
3. Mix in 1 whole egg and 1 egg yolk with 1 knife point of salt
4. Place the dough on a flour-dusted board, form small sausages or dumplings from it
5. Roll these in flour and fry in very hot lard
6. Remove with a slotted spoon and use as garnish for boiled beef, roasted lungs or the like
7. They can also be served as a flour dish at table, but then the dough must be seasoned with 4 tablespoons sugar
8. When serving, add any desired cream

title: Reis-Knödel (Rice Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 deca rice
- 1/2 liter milk
- 12 deca butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 8 deca bread crumbs
- some salt
- 1/16 liter cooked green peas
- 2 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Wash the rice in warm water.
2. Boil it in 1/2 liter milk until swollen and let it cool.
3. Beat the butter until creamy.
4. Stir in 2 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks.
5. Add the cooled rice with 8 deca peeled bread crumbs cut into dice and soaked in milk, and salt.
6. When ready to cook, add 1/16 liter fine cooked green peas and 2 tablespoons flour.
7. Form knödel of any desired size.

title: Reis-Kouler (Rice Couleur)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rice (as per recipe 1373, but pounded)
- cooked rice

## Directions

1. The preparation is the same as described for the Rice Soufflé (No. 1373), only in this case the raw rice is pounded a little in the mortar beforehand

title: Reis-Pudding (Rice Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca rice
- 1/2 liter boiling milk
- 12 deca butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 12 deca vanilla sugar
- 6 egg whites
- raisins, currants, pistachios, almonds, pignoli, and dates as desired

## Directions

1. Stir 24 deca rice into 1/2 liter boiling milk and cook thoroughly
2. Add 12 deca butter, mix well and let cool
3. Beat 6 egg yolks with 12 deca vanilla sugar for 1/2 hour
4. Add the cooled rice, mix well
5. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites
6. If desired, add raisins, currants, pistachios, finely cut almonds, pignoli, and dates with kernels removed
7. Fill a buttered and floured mold with this mixture
8. Steam covered for 3/4 hour

title: Reis-Pudding für Die Suppe (Consommé au pain de riz)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 deca rice
- hot water
- 1/4 liter boiling milk
- 8 deca butter
- 4 egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Wash 20 deca rice in hot water
2. Place on sieve to drain well
3. Stir into 1/4 liter boiling milk and let cook
4. Stir in 8 deca butter and place rice in a deep bowl
5. When completely cooled, stir in 4 egg yolks, some salt, and nutmeg
6. Lightly fold in the snow from 4 egg whites

title: Reis-Soufflé (Rice Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 Deca rinsed and dried rice
- 1/2 liter boiling milk
- 12 Deca butter
- 1 pinch of salt
- 8 egg yolks
- 14 Deca vanilla sugar
- 8 egg whites (snow)

## Directions

1. Stir 24 Deca rinsed and dried rice into 1/2 liter boiling milk and cook until thick, then add 12 Deca butter and 1 pinch of salt, mix well and set aside
2. Meanwhile stir 8 egg yolks with 14 Deca vanilla sugar for 1/2 hour, mix this well with the cooled rice and finally fold in lightly the snow from 8 egg whites
3. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour and cook covered for 3/4 hours in steam or bake in the oven
4. In the first case the soufflé can be poured with hot jam shortly before serving, in the other case it can be sprinkled with finely pounded sugar

title: Reis-Soufflé mit Äpfeln (Rice Soufflé with Apples)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rice mass (as per recipe 1373)
- apples, thinly sliced
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. The preparation is the same as described for the ordinary Rice Soufflé (No. 1373)
2. Fill half of this mass into a buttered form dusted with flour, put it in the oven, and as soon as the mass has drawn together, cover it with finely and thinly sliced apples, sprinkle with finely pounded sugar and cover with the second half of the rice mass
3. Put it in the oven and bake for 3/4 hours
4. Instead of apples the mass can be covered with any compote or sprinkled with 2 tablets of grated chocolate

title: Reis-Strudel (Rice Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ liter flour
- 32 deca rice
- 1 liter milk
- 12 deca butter
- 16 deca vanilla sugar
- ½ teaspoon salt
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- 16 deca raisins

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough from ½ liter flour and let it rest
2. Slowly cook 32 deca selected rice in 1 liter boiling milk
3. Pour the cooked rice into a deep bowl and stir cold with 12 deca butter, 16 deca vanilla sugar, and ½ teaspoon salt
4. Gradually stir 8 egg yolks one after another into the rice
5. Lightly fold in the snow (whipped egg whites) from 8 egg whites last
6. Stretch the strudel dough finely, brush with melted butter, distribute the rice mixture on top
7. Sprinkle with 16 deca selected raisins and proceed as described in the Ham Strudel recipe

title: Reisauflauf (Rice Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- 17 dkg rice
- vanilla
- 8 dkg butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 3 spoonfuls sugar
- 4 egg whites
- apples or grated chocolate (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook milk with 17 dkg rice and a piece of vanilla in a pot for 30-45 minutes, stirring frequently, until thick, and cool
2. Stir in creamed butter, egg yolks, and sugar
3. Lightly fold in stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Fill half the mixture into a well-buttered baking dish
5. If desired, add a layer of stewed apples or grated chocolate, then add the remaining mixture
6. Bake light brown over moderate heat
7. Cut into portions, sprinkle with sugar, and serve warm

title: Reisbudding (Rice Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound rice
- water
- half a measure of milk
- 4 eggs
- butter
- salt
- hollandaise sauce or rosehip broth

## Directions

1. Blanch half a pound of rice several times in boiling water
2. Cook it a couple more times with water
3. Add half a measure of milk and let it cook until thick
4. Mix in 4 eggs, some butter, and salt
5. Place everything in a cloth, tie it tightly
6. Place in a pot of boiling water and cook for two hours
7. Make a hollandaise sauce or rosehip broth and pour over before serving
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reiscreme (Rice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- 12 dkg rice
- vanilla
- whipped cream
- sugar
- 1 dkg gelatin
- cold water
- raspberry juice or whipped cream and candied cherries for serving

## Directions

1. Bring milk to a boil with 12 dkg rice and a piece of vanilla
2. Cook while stirring for ½ hour and cool
3. Mix with whipped cream and add sugar to taste
4. Slowly pour in dissolved gelatin (see instructions) and stir well
5. Fill into a pudding mold rinsed with cold water
6. Place in ice or in a container of cold water for 2-3 hours
7. Carefully loosen, hold the mold briefly in hot water, turn out the cream
8. Serve with raspberry juice or decorated with whipped cream, candied cherries, and stewed fruit
9. Chopped candied fruit can also be mixed with the rice

title: Reisfleisch (Rice Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-60 dkg calf's shank or shoulder
- 6 dkg bacon
- 30 dkg rice
- 3 dkg Parmesan
- 6 dkg hot butter or fat
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 2 knife-tips paprika
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. In 6 dkg hot butter or fat, let the diced bacon, then 1 finely chopped onion turn light brown.
2. Add 2 knife-tips of paprika, the lightly washed, diced, salted meat, pour in a little water and braise 1/2 to 1 hour, adding water and stirring frequently.
3. Add the separately braised rice, pour in a little broth or water, braise another 15 minutes.
4. Mix in the grated cheese and serve on a round or square dish.

title: Reiskrustaden (Rice Crusts)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dkg rice
- 2 dkg Parmesan
- 4 dkg flour
- hot lard
- hash filling
- spinach or cooked salad

## Directions

1. Braise the rice until very soft, place on a floured board, mix with Parmesan and 4 dkg flour and roll out two fingers thick.
2. With a round cutter, cut out slices and bread them.
3. With a small cutter, mark a lid on top, bake the crusts in hot lard, lift out the lid, fill with hash and place the lid back on.
4. Serve with spinach or cooked salad.

title: Reispaſtete (Rice Pastry)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish butter
- egg yolks
- egg whites
- rice cooked in milk
- veal sweetbreads
- crayfish tails
- asparagus
- mushrooms
- crayfish butter
- beef broth
- egg yolk
- egg white

## Directions

1. Beat a piece of crayfish butter and mix in 4 egg yolks
2. Add the beaten snow (egg whites) from the 4 eggs and a quarter pound of rice cooked thick in milk and cooled
3. Stir everything together well
4. Make a ragout from veal sweetbreads, crayfish tails, asparagus, and mushrooms, cutting everything into dice
5. Let it cook well in warm crayfish butter
6. Pour in 2 egg yolks mixed with cold beef broth
7. Add milk cream and butter
8. Continue until the casserole is full
9. Make a lid and brush with egg white
10. Let it bake slowly in the oven
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reiss-Nocki (Imperial Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 Deka blanched, rinsed, and finely ground rice
- 1⅔ liter boiling milk
- 14 Deka butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 14 Deka fine ground powdered sugar
- 14 Deka blanched, finely ground almonds
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook blanched, rinsed, finely ground rice in 1⅔ liter boiling milk
2. Stir well with 14 Deka butter and set aside
3. Beat 8 egg yolks and 14 Deka fine ground sugar for ⅛ hour
4. Add blanched, finely ground almonds with the expressed almond juice and mix well
5. Lightly mix in the snow from 8 egg whites
6. Fill into a butter-brushed, flour-dusted mold
7. Bake for ¾ hour in the oven

title: Reisschleim (Rice Slime)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Four loths rice
- Two pounds spring water

## Directions

1. Grind four loths rice to powder in a marble mortar
2. Let it cook in two pounds spring water until it becomes a clear broth
3. Press through cloth while very hot and squeeze out strongly
4. The mixture has the consistency of jelly
5. Mix one or several spoonfuls with warm water or meat broth
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reisschleim-Suppe (Rice Cream Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 spoonfuls rice
- 1 liter broth
- approximately 1.5 liters white or chicken broth

## Directions

1. Cook 16 spoonfuls rice in 1 liter broth until soft
2. Pour approximately 1.5 liters white or chicken broth over it, stir well together
3. Press through a fine sieve

title: Reissuppe für die Suppe (Rice Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 Deka rice
- hot water
- 1/4 liter boiling milk
- 8 Deka butter
- 4 egg yolks
- some lard
- nutmeg
- 4 egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice in hot water and strain it well
2. Stir it into 1/4 liter of boiling milk and let it simmer
3. Stir in 8 Deka butter and transfer the rice to a deep bowl
4. Once completely cooled, stir in 4 egg yolks, some lard and nutmeg
5. Gently fold in the beaten egg whites
6. Fill into a mold and cook in a water bath or bake like Kaiserschmarren

title: Reiß mürbe zu kochen (Tender Rice for Cooking)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- two to four drams of rice
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Obtain a tin ball with an opening at the top that holds at most six to eight drams of rice
2. Clean the ball well and wash it inside and out before each use
3. Place two or four drams of rice inside, as it constantly boils up during cooking
4. Place this ball in the pot where meat broth is cooking
5. Let it cook for approximately two hours and skim it
6. When it has cooked enough and swelled properly, remove the ball
7. Alternative: if you do not have a tin ball, place the rice in a cloth or napkin, tie it so that two-thirds space remains, and it cooks just as well in the cloth as in the ball
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reiß-Meridon (Rice Meridon)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound rice
- milk or good broth
- a piece of butter the size of an egg
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- tails and claws from 30 crayfish
- 2 or 3 soft-boiled stomachs of guinea fowl or geese
- some cooked calf's head, cut lengthwise
- salt
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound rice in milk or good broth and let it cool
2. Beat a piece of butter the size of an egg until fluffy
3. Add the rice to the butter, then gradually stir in 2 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks
4. Add the removed tails and claws from 30 crayfish, 2 or 3 soft-boiled stomachs of guinea fowl or geese, and some cooked calf's head cut lengthwise
5. Add some salt and finally fold in the beaten snow of 3 egg whites
6. Line the bottom of a cassole brushed with butter entirely with white paper and also brush the paper with butter
7. Arrange crayfish tails and asparagus heads boiled in salted water decoratively
8. Pour in the rice mixture and let it cook in steam for a full hour
9. Carefully turn it out onto a platter, remove the paper, and serve immediately
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reiß-Meridon (Rice Meridon)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound of rice
- Milk or good broth
- Butter, size of one egg
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- Tails and claws from 30 crayfish
- 2 or 3 soft-cooked stomachs of chickens or geese
- Some cooked calf's head, cut lengthwise
- Salt
- Whites from 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Cook half pound of rice in milk or good broth
2. Let it cool
3. Beat a piece of butter the size of an egg until fluffy
4. Add the rice to it
5. Gradually stir in 2 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks
6. Add the extracted tails and claws from 30 crayfish
7. Add 2 or 3 soft-cooked stomachs of chickens or geese
8. Add some cooked calf's head cut lengthwise
9. Season with salt
10. Finally stir in the snow from the whites of 3 eggs
11. Line the bottom of a casserole brushed with butter entirely with white paper
12. Brush the paper with butter
13. Artfully arrange crayfish tails and asparagus boiled in salted water on it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reißauflauf (Rice Soufflé)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound rice
- Water
- One measure milk
- One Seidel cream
- One piece cinnamon
- Half pound butter
- One-quarter pound sugar
- Zest of one lemon
- One-quarter pound large raisins
- Equal amount small raisins
- 16 egg yolks
- Egg whites from 12 eggs
- Butter for greasing
- Finely grated roll crumbs

## Directions

1. Wash the rice well, boil it in water with some salt, then cool it in cold water.
2. Cook one measure milk and one Seidel cream with a piece of cinnamon, mix the rice into it, and let it swell on weak fire.
3. When the rice is soft, remove it, add half pound butter, and set it to cool.
4. When cooled, stir in one-quarter pound sugar with grated lemon zest, one-quarter pound large raisins, an equal amount small raisins, and 16 egg yolks.
5. Finally add the snow from 12 eggs.
6. Grease the mold or ring well with butter and sprinkle it with finely grated roll crumbs.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reißauflauf mit Burgunder (Rice Soufflé with Burgundy)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice prepared as for ordinary rice soufflé
- Fresh butter
- Thin roll slices
- One measure Burgundy wine
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Lemon peel
- Several onions

## Directions

1. Coat the form with fresh butter and line it neatly with thin roll slices which are pressed firmly.
2. Place the rice prepared as for ordinary rice soufflé into it, cover the top again with thin roll slices which are lightly brushed with butter, and bake for 2 hours.
3. Cook one measure Burgundy with sugar, cinnamon, lemon peel and several onions, well covered, until reduced to half.
4. Strain through a hair sieve and serve in a sauce boat with the soufflé.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reißkoch (Rice Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- not quite one-quarter pound rice
- cream
- one-quarter pound butter
- two whole eggs and four yolks
- a bit of egg white foam
- sugar and cinnamon to taste

## Directions

1. Take not quite one-quarter pound rice and cook it in cream until white and thick
2. Add one-quarter pound butter and stir it together
3. Allow it to cool
4. Beat it until fine
5. Beat in two whole eggs and four yolks, stirring each one well
6. Add a bit of egg white foam, and sugar and cinnamon to taste
7. Serve it on a sheet pan with a rim or bake it in a cake pan
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reißpanade (Rice Panade)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- four drams of cooked rice
- warm water or meat broth
- lemon zest
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Place four drams of previously cooked rice in a small bowl
2. Rub it with a spoon
3. Cook it in warm water or meat broth with some lemon zest and a little grated nutmeg
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reißpudding mit einer Kirschensoße (Rice Pudding with Cherry Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound rice
- 1/2 portion cream
- 1 measure milk
- whole cinnamon
- 1/2 pound butter
- 16 egg yolks
- 2 pounds sugar
- zest of 1 lemon
- 1/2 pound small raisins
- 8 eggs (for snow)
- butter for the cloth

## Directions

1. Select and rinse the rice, then scald it with boiling water.
2. Fill it with boiling milk and cook slowly until thickened, allowing it to swell well while remaining granular.
3. Cook the prepared rice with half a portion of cream, one measure of milk, and some whole cinnamon, then let it cool.
4. Beat 1/2 pound butter with 16 egg yolks into a foam, stir in 2 pounds sugar with the zest of 1 lemon rubbed on it, then 1/2 pound small raisins, and carefully fold in the rice.
5. Slowly draw in the snow from 8 eggs.
6. Butter a clean cloth the size of a plate, lay it in a bowl, pour the mixture into it, and bind loosely so the mass can swell.
7. Hang the pudding in a pot of simmering water and let it cook continuously for 1.5 hours.
8. Serve with cherry sauce poured over the pudding.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Reißpudding mit einer Kirſchenſauce (Rice Pudding with Cherry Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of rice
- Boiling water
- Boiling milk
- Half a measure of cream
- One measure of milk
- Whole cinnamon
- One pound of butter

## Directions

1. Select the rice, rinse it, and blanch with boiling water
2. Fill with boiling milk and cook over slow heat until thick, allowing the rice to swell well while remaining grainy
3. Cook the prepared rice with half a measure of cream, one measure of milk, and some whole cinnamon
4. Let it cool
5. Beat one pound of butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Repphühner im Traubensaft marinirt (Partridges Marinated in Grape Juice)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- juice of unripe grapes
- lemon juice
- salt
- cloves
- shallots
- bay leaves
- flour
- white wine
- egg yolks
- melted bacon fat
- parsley

## Directions

1. Marinate the partridges in the juice of unripe grapes and lemon juice with salt, cloves, shallots and bay leaves
2. Let them soak for approximately three hours
3. Make a thin batter from flour, white wine, and egg yolks
4. Dip the partridges into it
5. Bake them in melted bacon fat
6. Arrange them with fried parsley
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Resimen - Spalten in Dunst (Quince Pieces in Steam)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- half-ripe quince
- quince skin
- soft center pulp

## Directions

1. Take a half-ripe quince, remove the skin and the soft part from the middle
2. Cut the firm part into neat pieces
3. Treat further as the peaches (Nr. 1850)

title: Rheinische Filets (Rhineland Fillets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cutlets à 25 dkg
- 1 onion
- 2 knife points paprika
- broth or water
- salt
- pepper
- fat for searing

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the meat, wipe, pound, salt and pepper it, lard it, quickly sear on both sides in hot fat
2. In the remaining fat, roast 1 finely chopped onion and 2 knife points of paprika, add the meat, pour in broth or water and braise covered under frequent basting for 2 hours
3. Dust the sauce very lightly and pour unpassed over the meat

title: Rhum (Rhum Mousse)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca orange or 24 deca citron sugar
- juice of 4 oranges and 4 lemons
- 12 egg yolks
- 3/4 liter white wine
- 3/6 liter rum
- 4 eggs (for snow)
- 4 deca isinglass
- 1/2 liter cold water
- 1/8 liter reduction
- 1 1/2 deca gelatin
- 1 liter water

## Directions

1. Put 24 deca orange or citron sugar in a snow bucket with the juice of 4 oranges and 4 lemons, 12 egg yolks, 3/4 liter white wine and 3/6 liter rum
2. Beat over the fire to a thick foam
3. Remove from fire and fold in the very stiff snow from 4 eggs
4. Mix in 4 deca isinglass (prepared with 1/2 liter cold water and reduced to 1/8 liter) or add 1 1/2 deca gelatin dissolved in 1 liter water hot and whisk with the whisk until cold
5. Fill the mass into a desired mold and proceed as with Whipped Bitter Almond Cream (No. 1713)

title: Ribisel (Redcurrants)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful large redcurrants
- red and white redcurrants (optional)

## Directions

1. Pluck beautiful large redcurrants from their stems
2. Fill preserving jars with them
3. Treat further as the medlars (Nr. 1845)
4. One can mix red and white redcurrants

title: Ribisel-Marmelade (Currant Preserves)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- currants (quantity not specified)
- powdered sugar equal in weight to the currant juice

## Directions

1. Pass any quantity of currants through a very clean sieve without first cutting them, working quickly.
2. Place the empty sieve on the scale, weigh it, then fill with the juice from the passed currants and add powdered sugar by weight equal to the juice (for 1 kilogram juice, 1 kilogram powdered sugar).
3. Place this mixture immediately on heat and cook while stirring constantly for 15 minutes.
4. While still hot, fill into jars the same way as the Apricot Preserves.
5. Be very careful that the beautiful red color is not lost through overcooking.
6. If you want the preserves to be even more beautiful and fine, add 1.25 kilograms of sugar for 1 kilogram of juice.
7. Note that the preserves do not thicken while cooking but only gain consistency when they cool in the jars.
8. If white currant preserves have also been made, you can fill the jars with both types, red and white currants, in alternating layers, but you must always let one layer set before adding the next.

title: Ribiselkuchen (Currant Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs' weight butter
- 3 eggs' weight sugar
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 eggs' weight flour
- ½ kg currants, washed and drained
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Beat together 3 eggs' weight butter with 3 eggs' weight sugar and 3 whole eggs gradually
2. Mix in 3 eggs' weight flour
3. Spread the batter finger-thick on a buttered, floured baking sheet
4. Top with ½ kg currants that have been rubbed and washed and drained
5. Sprinkle generously with sugar
6. Bake at higher heat until light brown
7. Cut into pieces, sprinkle with sugar, and serve cooled

title: Rice Pears or Apples (Reis-Birnen oder Reis-Äpfel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rice mass from recipe 1136
- flour for coating
- eggs for dipping
- breadcrumbs for coating
- hot lard for frying
- 1 clove per piece
- cinnamon stick pieces

## Directions

1. Form the same mass as the Rice-Croquettes (No. 1136) into pear or apple shapes
2. Roll in flour, dip in beaten egg, and roll in breadcrumbs
3. Fry in very hot lard
4. Remove with a slotted spoon, insert a clove on one side and a piece of cinnamon stick at the other side where the stem would be
5. Arrange in a bowl

title: Rice Pudding (Milchreis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 16 tablespoons washed rice
- 8 tablespoons sugar
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 1 pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Bring 1 liter of milk to a boil
2. Stir in 16 tablespoons of washed rice under constant stirring
3. Allow it to cook well until the rice is soft
4. Add 8 tablespoons sugar, 8 tablespoons butter, and 1 pinch of salt
5. Stir well together and let the rice cook covered until done
6. Arrange the rice on a serving dish and serve dusted with sugar and optionally with cinnamon or grated chocolate
7. If the rice is too thick, thin it with boiling milk

title: Rice Pudding with Apple Compote (Reis mit Apfel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Boskop apples
- 16 tablespoons washed rice
- 0.5 liter milk
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 16 tablespoons vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Core the Boskop apples and cut them into slices
2. Fry the slices on both sides until golden in very hot butter
3. Place in a serving dish
4. Stir 16 tablespoons of washed rice into 0.5 liter of boiling milk under constant stirring and cook until thick
5. Mix with 8 tablespoons butter and 16 tablespoons vanilla sugar
6. Set aside
7. When the rice has cooled slightly, arrange it in a mound on a serving dish
8. Make a depression in the center, place the fried apples in it
9. Cover the whole with Spanish meringue, dust with finely ground sugar
10. Bake in an open oven for 10 to 15 minutes

title: Rice Pudding with Spanish Meringue (Reis mit Spanischer Blüte)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 tablespoons washed rice
- 1.5 liters cream or milk
- 16 tablespoons granulated sugar
- juice and zest of 1 orange
- 3 egg whites
- powdered sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Cook 16 tablespoons of washed rice in 1.5 liters of boiling cream under constant stirring until soft and thick
2. Add 16 tablespoons granulated sugar and the juice and zest of 1 orange
3. Stir well together
4. Arrange on a serving dish
5. Cover evenly with meringue made from 3 egg whites
6. Dust heavily with finely ground sugar
7. Bake in an open oven for 10 minutes on low heat
8. Remove the dish and garnish with marmalade, stewed fruits or apples, or compote of choice
9. Place on a second serving dish and bring to table

title: Rice Rings with Compote (Reisreifen mit Kompot)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 liter milk
- 16 tablespoons washed rice
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons orange sugar
- 1 pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Bring 0.5 liter of milk to a boil
2. Stir in 16 tablespoons of washed rice under constant stirring and cook covered until soft and thick
3. Add 4 tablespoons butter, 8 tablespoons orange sugar, and 1 pinch of salt
4. Stir well together
5. When the rice is fairly thick, mound it on a serving dish
6. Press a round spoon into the center and use a tablespoon to form an even ring
7. Fill the center with applesauce or compote as desired

title: Rice Soup with Tomatoes (Soupe de riz aux tomates)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 washed and separated tomatoes
- Some onion
- 16 Deca red rice
- 8 Deca hot butter
- 2 liters water
- Grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Stew the tomatoes with some onion
2. Add the red rice to 8 Deca hot butter
3. Pour in 2 liters water
4. Simmer uncovered for about 1.5 hours slowly
5. Serve with grated Parmesan cheese
6. The soup can be served passed or unpassed; if passed, add cut bread starch

title: Rindfleisc auf englische Art (Beef in the English Manner)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a beautiful, well-salted piece of meat larded through and through with bacon (hip-shank)
- belly herb (Kuttelkraut)
- marjoram
- lemon peel, finely cut
- juniper berries, finely ground
- white wine, one part
- vinegar, one part
- water, one part
- onion
- garlic
- celery
- yellow turnips
- ham meat
- flour, a couple spoonfuls
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Take a beautiful, well-salted piece of meat larded through and through with bacon (hip-shank).
2. Mix belly herb, marjoram, finely cut lemon peel, and finely ground juniper berries together.
3. Rub the meat well with this mixture, place it in an earthenware dish, cover well, and let it stand overnight.
4. The next day, put into a casserole one part white wine, one part vinegar, and one part water; also add onion, garlic, celery, and yellow turnips.
5. Put the meat in as well and let it stew covered with a piece of ham meat until beautifully tender.
6. Shake it several times so it does not burn.
7. When tender enough, remove the meat and let the sauce reduce a little.
8. Dust a couple spoonfuls of flour into it, and when this has reduced for a while, add some beef broth.
9. Let it come to a boil again, put the meat back in, and before serving, place it on a plate and strain the small sauce over it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rindfleisc mit Hotpot (Beef with Hotpot)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- beef, cooked as usual
- six to eight bacon slices
- a whole onion
- yellow turnips, sliced in leaves
- good broth
- young kohlrabi
- large yellow turnips
- butter
- sugar
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Once the beef, cooked as usual, begins to become tender, lay six to eight bacon slices, a whole onion, and yellow turnips sliced in leaves in a braising pan, and place the beef on top.
2. Cover and braise slowly; from time to time pour good broth over it so it does not burn, turning it on all sides so it browns beautifully.
3. Take some young kohlrabi and large yellow turnips, and using a hollow iron as customarily used for kohlrabi, form small balls.
4. In a casserole, put a piece of butter, let it become hot, add a piece of sugar, and once it has melted, add the turnips and kohlrabi and let them braise until soft.
5. Give broth to them often so they do not brown too much.
6. Before serving, remove the small sauce from the fat, strain it through a sieve, arrange the braised kohlrabi and turnips like a ring around the plate, place the meat in the middle, and pour the sauce over it.
7. If serving with potatoes, treat the meat the same way, but you can also add a few pieces of ham cut in leaves, cook small potatoes, peel and braise them, then use them to garnish.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rindfleisch (Beef)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef from the tail shank
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Butter
- Vinegar
- Water
- Onion
- Capers
- Lemon peels

## Directions

1. Take beef from the tail shank kept in a cool place (cold kitchen), lard it, salt it, season with pepper
2. Coat thickly with flour and then brown it with butter on both sides until the flour is brown
3. Place it in a casserole and add vinegar and water
4. Add onion, capers, and lemon peels
5. Let it cook until the meat is tender and the sauce is reduced and thickened, then serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rindfleisch in der Braise (Braised Beef)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- A beautiful piece of beef from the rib or shank
- Good beef fat
- 3 to 4 large split onions
- Several yellow turnips
- Parsnip
- Celery
- Beef broth

## Directions

1. Soften a beautiful piece of beef from the rib or shank by cooking it
2. Heat good beef fat in a casserole
3. Add 3 to 4 large split onions, several yellow turnips, parsnip, and celery cut into pieces to the fat
4. Let them braise
5. Before serving, add the meat and a little beef broth
6. Place the meat on the serving dish and pour the vegetables together with the small amount of sauce that is present over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rindfleisch mit Senfsoße und Reis (Beef with Mustard Sauce and Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-70 dkg beef (Rieddeckel)
- 1 pair sausages
- 6 dkg hot fat
- 1 finely chopped onion
- a few pieces of bread cut into noodle shapes
- strained beef broth
- 5 dkg fat
- 1¼ tablespoons French mustard
- a few tablespoons cream
- salt

## Directions

1. 20 minutes before serving, in a saucepan heat 6 dkg fat and roast 1 finely chopped onion and a few pieces of bread cut into noodle shapes
2. Pour the strained beef broth over this
3. Add the sausages which have been washed and cut into leaf-like pieces and cook together for a few minutes
4. Optionally whisk in 1 egg, but the soup must not boil after this
5. For the sauce: Make a roux from 5 dkg fat and mix with broth or water
6. Add 1¼ tablespoons French mustard and a few tablespoons cream
7. Season with salt and cook well

title: Rindfleiſch auf böhmiſche Art gedämpft (Bohemian-Style Braised Beef)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of thin breast
- salt
- white beer
- wine
- beef broth
- vinegar
- peeled and sliced almonds
- raisins
- cloves
- ginger
- pepper
- grated lebkuchen (spice cake)
- sugar
- a pair of bay leaves
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Take a piece of thin breast, chop it into pieces one and a half fingers thick and beat it well
2. Sprinkle it with salt and brown it lightly on a grill
3. Layer it in an earthen pot and pour over it white beer, wine, beef broth and a little vinegar
4. Place coals around the pot, cover it and let the meat braise for quite some time
5. Then add peeled and sliced almonds, raisins, cloves, ginger, pepper, grated lebkuchen, sugar and a pair of bay leaves
6. If the broth is insufficient, add more wine, beef broth and vinegar
7. Stir it often so it absorbs the flavors well and keep adding coals until it is properly tender
8. When serving, sprinkle it with lemon peel
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rindfleiſch auf westphälische Art (Westphalian-Style Beef)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- well-marbled beef from the ribs, about the size of a hand
- proper seasoning
- one whole green onion
- some marjoram
- bread crumbs, a handful
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Chop a well-marbled piece of beef from the ribs into pieces about the size of a hand
2. Put them in a spacious pot, skim them well, and cook them with proper seasoning, a whole green onion and some marjoram, reducing to about two fingers' width
3. Seal the pot with a lid and place it by the fire so the meat can cook gently
4. When you notice the meat is nearly cooked through, open it and skim the fat from the broth
5. Add a handful of bread crumbs and lemon peel, bring it to a boil once more, and serve it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rindfleiſch in Most (Beef in Must or Grape Juice)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- lung roast
- salt
- wine
- fine spice herbs
- whole pepper
- ginger
- must (or shriveled grapes)
- a piece of sugar

## Directions

1. Take a lung roast, wash it out and salt it
2. Let it braise covered in a casserole with a little wine, fine spice herbs, whole pepper and ginger until it becomes completely tender
3. Pour must over it, or in the absence of must, press shriveled grapes through a clean cloth
4. Add a piece of sugar and let the sauce simmer until it becomes thick
5. When the meat is plated, strain the sauce over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rindfleiſch mit Eſtouffade (Beef with Estouffade)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a good piece of beef from the tenderloin
- spice and fine spice herbs
- salt
- bacon, sliced
- a piece of ham
- four small onions
- yellow turnips
- parsley roots
- celery
- lemon, sliced
- seasonings as desired
- four slices of baked bread
- half a measure of red wine
- one measure of good vinegar
- black dough for sealing

## Directions

1. Beat a good piece of beef from the tenderloin thoroughly
2. Mince spices with fine spice herbs, cut holes in the meat and insert the mixture
3. Salt it
4. In a cooking vessel place sliced bacon, a piece of ham, four small onions, yellow turnips, parsley roots, celery, sliced lemon, seasonings, and about four slices of baked bread
5. Lay the meat in the vessel, pour over it half a measure of red wine and one measure of good vinegar
6. Cover and seal the vessel around the rim with black dough
7. Place it on coals and let it steam gently for about six hours
8. Plate the meat and strain the sauce through a sieve over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rindfleiſch mit Paradeisſauce und Reis (Beef with Tomato Sauce and Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-70 dkg beef (ox tail)
- 6 h bones
- 4 h liver
- 4 h soup greens (parsley root, yellow turnip, celery, leek)
- ½ kg tomatoes
- 25 dkg rice
- Salt
- Vinegar
- 1 bay leaf
- 5 pepper and spice grains
- 2 cloves
- ½ onion
- 4 dkg fat for tomato sauce
- Sugar
- Red wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare the broth: Beat the meat, rinse it thoroughly along with the bones and liver in cold water. Place all in cold water to start. If desiring good, juicy meat, place it in boiling water to quickly close the pores so the protein does not cook out. For 1 kg meat take 2½-3 liters water. If placing meat in cold water, the broth will be better. Do not skim off the foam that forms when boiling—this is the protein, the nutrient. After boiling, add 1-2 spoonfuls of salt, after a few minutes add the cleaned, washed and cut greens, a piece of cabbage, 1 onion ring roasted in a small pan in some fat until dark yellow, several mushrooms (fresh or dried), 5 spice grains, some ground pepper and caraway. Let the broth cook slowly for 2-2½ hours. It should only boil on one side and must not be stirred. To clarify it, add some clean eggshells during cooking and if it boils too vigorously, add a spoonful of cold water. Strain it through a fine sieve or napkin.
2. Prepare the tomato sauce: Wash the tomatoes, remove the stems, divide them, and stew for 30 minutes with very little water, salt, vinegar, 1 bay leaf, 5 pepper and spice grains, 2 cloves, and ½ onion in a pot. Pass them through a sieve, add to a light roux made from 4 dkg fat, pour on broth or water, cook for 15-20 minutes, sweeten to taste and optionally sour with red wine.
3. Prepare the rice: Select the rice carefully and place in a pot with cold water to cook.

title: Rindsbeefsteak (Beef Beefsteak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.30-1.40 kilograms beef tenderloin
- flour
- salt
- hot fat
- butter
- parsley
- Maitre d'hôtel butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the meat like the venison beefsteaks and cut into two-finger-wide pieces.
2. Lightly pound with the back of a knife and push back into round shape.
3. Just before cooking, salt on both sides.
4. Coat one side with flour.
5. Place flour-side down in a flat pan with hot fat.
6. Brown and serve as the venison beefsteaks.

title: Rindsbraten (Beef Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-70 dkg table piece or oxtail
- 6 h root vegetables
- 1 onion ring
- 1 bay leaf
- thyme
- 5 peppercorns and spice berries
- 5 dkg fat
- water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop the cleaned, washed root vegetables into thin slices
2. Roast them dark with the onion ring, bay leaf, thyme, peppercorns and spice berries in the fat
3. Beat and wash the meat, salt and pepper it, add to the vegetables
4. Pour in some water and vinegar, cover and braise for 2-2½ hours, turning and adding water frequently
5. Remove the meat, strain and pass the sauce, season with vinegar or wine to taste, bring to a boil again and serve separately or with the meat

title: Rindsgulyas (Beef Goulash)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-80 dkg shank or rear roasting meat
- 6 dkg hot fat
- 1 large onion
- 2 knife-tips paprika
- cold water
- 1 piece dark bread crust
- 1 parsnip (optional)
- caraway
- marjoram
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. In a roasting pan, in 6 dkg hot fat, let 1 large onion turn dark golden.
2. Add 2 knife-tips paprika, pour in a scoop of cold water, add the washed, cut into strips then diagonal cubes, salted and peppered meat, 1 piece of dark bread crust, optionally a parsnip, some caraway, marjoram and vinegar.
3. Braise covered for 2 hours, adding water and stirring frequently.
4. Serve on a round dish. Side dishes: rotkohl, noodles, rice.

title: Rindszunge am Rost (Grilled Beef Tongue)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef tongue
- salt
- spices
- melted butter
- breadcrumbs
- fried parsley
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked tongue into finger-thick slices that are 1 finger long and 2 fingers wide
2. Dust with salt and spices, dip in melted butter and breadcrumbs, and fry on a grill
3. If no grill is available, fry quickly in a shallow pan with very hot fat about a finger-deep
4. Arrange the tongue in a star shape on a plate; place fried green parsley in the center and border the plate with lemon slices

title: Rindszunge in Papier (Beef Tongue in Papillotes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue cooked in salt water
- Madeira sauce with lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut a freshly cooked beef tongue into finger-thick slices
2. Prepare as cutlets in paper (papillotes)
3. Serve with Madeira sauce with lemon juice in a sauce boat

title: Rindszunge mit Kren (Beef Tongue with Horseradish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue (steamed, cooked, smoked or poached)
- grated horseradish
- scraped horseradish
- beet juice
- parsley

## Directions

1. One can use a steamed, cooked, smoked or poached tongue
2. Cut the tongue weakly into finger-thick slices and arrange in a circle on a plate
3. Place some grated horseradish in the center
4. Place small piles of scraped horseradish around the edge
5. Color 1 teaspoon horseradish in beet juice and place a small amount of this red-colored horseradish on each pile of white horseradish around the plate
6. Garnish the plate with finely plucked green parsley
7. Ensure the tongue comes to table quite hot
8. Serve with vinegar or vinegar horseradish

title: Rindszunge mit Madeira Sauce (Beef Tongue with Madeira Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- 4 liters water
- 1 handful salt
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 1 small head parsley root
- 1 finger-length piece yellow turnip
- parsley
- celery
- whole spices
- 16 tablespoons veal meat from the knuckle (shoulder)
- 1/2 hour
- 1/3 liter stock
- 1/4 liter soup
- 8 tablespoons butter
- equal amount flour
- juice of 1 lemon
- salt
- 4 tablespoons Madeira wine
- approximately 2 tablespoons sugar
- truffles

## Directions

1. Wash the beef tongue thoroughly, then cook it covered in 4 liters of water with 1 handful of salt for approximately 2 hours
2. Remove the coarse skin, which will peel off easily
3. Return the tongue to the broth and cook until completely tender, approximately 4 hours total cooking time
4. Cut the tongue lengthwise down the middle and arrange the sliced pieces in approximately 1 finger tall and 2 fingers wide portions on a platter, arranged so they form a whole tongue again
5. For the Madeira sauce: in a saucepan, put 8 tablespoons butter, 1 small head parsley root, 1 finger-length piece yellow turnip, parsley and celery, all cut into thin slices, and some whole spices, then place 16 tablespoons of diced veal meat from the knuckle on top
6. Brown this covered for 1/2 hour, then pour in 1/3 liter stock and 1/4 liter soup
7. Let cook covered
8. Make a roux from 8 tablespoons butter and equal amount flour, pour in the prepared broth, let it thicken well, then add juice of 1 lemon, adjust seasoning with salt if needed
9. Add 4 tablespoons Madeira wine and approximately 2 tablespoons sugar; if the sauce is too thick, add some soup
10. After removing from heat, pour the sauce over the cut tongue
11. When serving, finely sliced truffels may be added if desired

title: Rindszunge mit Madeira-Sauce (Beef Tongue with Madeira Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tongue
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. After the beef tongue is first washed clean, it is placed in 4 liters of water with 1 handful of salt and cooked gently covered for approximately 2 hours.
2. The thick skin will then easily peel off and after this is done, the tongue is returned to the broth and cooked completely tender. (A beef tongue takes approximately 4 hours cooking time in total.)
3. Then cut it lengthwise in half and arrange the pieces, cut obliquely into leaves, in approximately 1 finger long and 2 fingers wide strips on a platter, so that they form again a whole tongue.

title: Rindviehschnitzer Schnitzel (Hollandaise-style Veal Cutlet)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin, finger-thick slices
- Bacon
- Fat
- Sour cream
- Soup
- Anchovies
- Mixed vegetables
- Soup or salt water

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Emperor Cutlets, but add finely cut anchovies instead of lemon juice
2. Garnish with dark and light yellow turnips and other mixed vegetables, which are cut as desired and cooked tender in soup or salt water

title: Rindvogel-Goulash (Beef Roll Goulash)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Onion
- Marjoram
- Caraway seeds
- Paprika
- Salt
- Stock

## Directions

1. The preparation is the same as for goulash, with the difference that the onion is finely diced.
2. When it is golden roasted, add the meat finely sliced leafy with marjoram and proceed as with goulash.

title: Ringlotten-Marmelade (Mirabelle Plum Jam)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Mirabelle plums
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Over-stewed in a cassole covered
2. After approximately 10 minutes quickly passed through a sieve
3. Formed again for the same length of time and filled hot into jars like apricot jam
4. The mirabelle plums must not be cooked too long, as they otherwise lose their green color

title: Risen Lung Pudding (Aufgelaufenes Lungenkoch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound butter
- crumb of one small roll
- milk
- lung
- ten whole eggs
- salt
- one half-seidel good cream
- lemon peel, finely cut
- fresh parsley, finely cut

## Directions

1. Beat one-quarter pound butter fluffy
2. Soak the crumb of a small roll in milk
3. Cook the lung very soft and cut it very finely
4. Put it with the soaked roll in the beaten butter
5. Stir well
6. Take ten whole eggs, each well beaten, and some salt
7. Stir in one-half seidel good cream, a little finely cut lemon peel, and finely cut fresh parsley
8. When everything is mixed, stir for a quarter hour
9. Coat a metal dish and mold with butter, put the pudding in, and bake it slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Risen Veal Roast Pudding (Aufgelaufenes Koch vom kälbernen Braten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound butter
- one-quarter pound soaked and finely ground almonds
- two whole eggs
- eight egg yolks
- veal roast, baked
- crumb of one small roll soaked in milk
- marrow
- one-quarter pound finely sifted sugar
- lemon peel, finely cut

## Directions

1. Beat one-quarter pound butter fluffy in a mixing bowl
2. Stir in one-quarter pound soaked and finely ground almonds
3. Add two whole eggs and eight egg yolks, each well beaten
4. Cut the veal roast finely with a knife and pound it well with the crumb of a small roll soaked in milk in a mortar
5. Mix it under the above
6. Take about two kreuzer's worth of clean prepared and diced marrow
7. Finally add one-quarter pound finely sifted sugar and finely cut lemon peel
8. When everything is mixed, stir for a quarter hour
9. Coat a dish and mold with butter, put the pudding in, bake it slowly in a cake pan, sprinkle with sugar, and serve it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Risotto (Italian Rice Dish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 spoonfuls of cleaned rice
- 1/8 liter white beef broth
- 8 spoonfuls grated Parmesan cheese
- fried marrow bones or beef liver, or other fried or steamed meat
- chicken combs or poultry scraps
- steamed truffles (optional)
- strong brown broth
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Roast the cleaned rice for about 4 minutes under frequent stirring without letting it brown
2. Add 1/8 liter white beef broth and let it steam covered for 15 minutes
3. If the broth evaporates, add about 1.5 liters of broth and a little butter, and let it steam for another 10 minutes
4. Add 8 spoonfuls of grated Parmesan cheese and stir well
5. Using a sachet spoon, place a portion of risotto on a serving dish and cover it with pieces of fried marrow bones, beef liver, or other fried or steamed meat, chicken combs, or poultry scraps, and steamed truffles if desired
6. Cover this with the remaining risotto, then another layer of meat, then risotto again
7. Press everything together with 2 sachet spoons so it forms a ball on the dish
8. Sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese if desired
9. Serve with strong brown broth and grated Parmesan cheese

title: Rissoles mit Farce (Meat-Filled Pastry Crescents)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rissole dough (No. 1049)
- 1 teaspoon any farce or forcemeat
- eggs for coating
- breadcrumbs
- very hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Prepare rissole dough and roll to the thickness of a knife-back
2. Place 1 teaspoon of farce in rows 3 fingers apart along the length
3. Make a 3-finger-wide strip of empty dough between each row of filling
4. Brush dough around the farce mounds with beaten eggs
5. Fold dough over the farce, press down lightly
6. Cut out semicircles between each farce mound with a crescent-shaped cutter
7. Dip all cut-out rissoles in beaten eggs, coat in breadcrumbs
8. Fry in very hot fat on both sides
9. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on blotting paper
10. Arrange on a platter lined with a serviette and serve as an appetizer

title: Riſolen mit Haſchee (Fried Pastry Pockets with Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 decagrams cooked beef
- leftover game or roasted meat

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as Riſolen mit Hirn (Fried Pastry Pockets with Brain), using hash made from 25 decagrams cooked beef, game, or leftover roasted meat as filling
2. Can be served as a side dish to meat with sauce or with spinach like the other Riſolen

title: Riſolen mit Hirn (Fried Pastry Pockets with Brain)
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams flour
- 4 decagrams butter
- 1 egg yolk
- salt
- 1 spoonful milk
- 1/2 calf brain

## Directions

1. Rub together 16 decagrams flour with 4 decagrams butter
2. Add 1 egg yolk, a pinch of salt, and 1 spoonful milk; knead into dough
3. Let rest, then roll out thinly
4. Cut out rounds
5. Place filling in the center of each round
6. Brush the edges with egg white
7. Press the dough together over the filling
8. Place on an unfloured baking sheet
9. Fry in hot lard until golden
10. Drain and serve warm with cooked lettuce or spinach
11. For the filling: Wash the brain, remove the membrane, blanch it. In 3 decagrams hot butter, let chopped parsley begin to brown, add the brain, season with salt and pepper, let it stew for a few minutes while stirring. When cooled, fill the pastries. If the brain is too thin, thicken it with flour.
12. Serve on a flat plate on parchment paper, garnished with parsley

title: Riſolen mit Schinken (Fried Pastry Pockets with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- butter
- egg yolk
- salt
- milk
- flour
- milk
- 10 decagrams chopped ham

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough the same way as Riſolen mit Hirn
2. For the filling: In 4 decagrams hot butter, let some flour begin to brown, pour in 1/4 liter milk, let the mixture cook down thick, season with salt, and add 10 decagrams chopped ham
3. Proceed as with the other Riſolen

title: Riſotto (Risotto)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 decagrams rice
- 1 piece beef marrow
- cooked meat from 1 chicken or 1 goose
- tomato paste
- 5 decagrams Parmesan cheese
- 1/2 kilogram green peas
- 10 decagrams lard
- 1 spring onion
- chicken broth
- salt

## Directions

1. In 10 decagrams hot lard in a casserole, let 1 spring onion and the beef marrow begin to brown
2. Strain through a sieve and return the fat without the onion to the casserole
3. Add the cleaned rice and let it hiss for 3-4 minutes, but no longer
4. Pour in strained chicken broth so that it covers the rice by a finger's width
5. Stir and let the broth evaporate
6. Add salt and tomato paste, pour in more broth, and let the rice stew for approximately 30-45 minutes
7. Just before serving, add the grated cheese, the separately cooked peas, and the diced chicken meat
8. Let it stand for 5 more minutes
9. Serve immediately, scattered with cheese, as an appetizer or evening dish on a round plate
10. Instead of chicken broth, the rice can be cooked with goose broth and diced goose meat added

title: Roastbeef am Spieß gebraten (Roast Beef Roasted on a Spit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef as described above
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Paper
- String
- Fat for basting

## Directions

1. After salting and peppering the meat, let it rest for 1 hour.
2. Thread it lengthwise through the center onto a spit so it maintains even rotation.
3. Brush two sheets of paper laid together heavily with butter, wrap the meat with this paper, bind it with string, and roast it at strong heat for 1.5 hours, basting frequently with fat.
4. The paper prevents it from forming a heavy crust immediately.
5. Remove the paper 0.5 hour before the roasting is complete, increase the heat to give the roast a beautiful color, and continue roasting for another 0.5 hour under frequent basting on the spit.
6. Serve in its full juices and well-roasted. Serve the juice in a separate sauce boat.

title: Roastbraten (Braised Beef)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- A well-aged beef roast of about 3 kilos
- Salt and pepper
- 4 tablespoons lard or skimmed fat
- 4 pieces finely cut onion
- Root vegetables
- 4 bunches marjoram
- 5 crushed juniper berries
- 5 grains of mixed spices of every kind
- 3 pieces dried figs
- 1 liter white wine
- 1/2 liter soup
- 16 pieces black bread cut into pieces
- Optional: vinegar and soup to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the beef with the flat of the meat cleaver, then wipe clean with a cloth and rub with salt and pepper
2. In a casserole or pan, place 4 tablespoons lard, 4 pieces finely cut onion, root vegetables, 4 bunches marjoram, 5 crushed juniper berries, 5 grains of mixed spices and 3 pieces dried figs
3. Place the meat on top, sprinkle finely cut onion over it, pour 4 tablespoons lard over it and cover with paper brushed with lard
4. Let the meat stand in a cool place for about 2 hours if time permits, then place in the oven and braise covered for 1 hour, occasionally basting
5. Remove the fat, add 1 liter white wine, 1/2 liter soup and 16 pieces of black bread cut into pieces, and let the meat braise for another about 2 hours
6. Remove from the pan, strain the well-defatted sauce into a casserole and add vinegar and soup if needed to adjust the taste, bring to a boil and let stand warm
7. The meat must roast uncovered in the oven for about 1/2 hour more to develop a crust
8. When serving, slice as desired and garnish with rice, macaroni or similar; serve the sauce in a sauceboat

title: Roastbraten auf dem Rost gebraten (Côte de Boeuf Grillée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Roastbraten pieces (from the loin to the shoulders, one piece per person)
- Butter
- Parsley butter with lemon juice, or tarragon butter, or shallot butter with vinegar
- Salt
- Spices

## Directions

1. Roastbraten is the part found from the loin to the shoulders. Take as many pieces as there are people at table, remove the ribs, pound and salt the roastbraten, dip them in melted butter, and broil them quickly over strong heat on a grill.
2. After 2-3 minutes, turn them and broil them equally on the other side.
3. Arrange them on a plate and place in the center of each piece some parsley butter with lemon juice, or tarragon butter, or finely cut shallots cooked in butter with several spoonfuls of vinegar, some salt and spices, and pour this on the side of the plate or serve the juice in a sauce boat.

title: Roasted Apples (Pommes röties)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Muskät apples
- fine powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Wipe the Muskät apples with a cloth
2. Insert a small corer at the side of the stem
3. Twist slightly and draw out the core housing without piercing the apples, so that about two knife-backs of the bottom remain
4. Fill the hollow space created with finely powdered sugar
5. Place the apples in a flat, dry pan
6. Roast in the oven without any additional liquid
7. When the apples feel soft, arrange them heaped on a platter
8. Pour the thick juice created by roasting over them
9. Serve with various dishes or also for tea
10. In this way not only Muskät apples, but any variety of apple can be roasted
11. Particularly recommended for invalids

title: Roasted Brain with Onions (Cervelle de veau à l'oignons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons hot butter
- 1/2 teaspoon finely cut onion
- Calf's brain

## Directions

1. Roast 1/2 teaspoon of finely cut onion in 8 tablespoons of hot butter
2. When the onion is brown, add the peeled brain
3. Let it completely contract and serve quickly at table

title: Roasted Calf's Brain, Grilled (Cervelle de veau grillée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brain
- 3 tablespoons butter
- Salt
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1/2 teaspoon finely cut green parsley
- Lemon juice (optional)
- 1 tablespoon white breadcrumbs
- 1 spoon soup stock

## Directions

1. Soak the calf's brain in cold water for several minutes, then peel it
2. Place 3 tablespoons butter in a casserole and heat it
3. Add the peeled brain and sprinkle with necessary salt on top
4. Knead 3 tablespoons butter with 1/2 teaspoon finely cut green parsley and, if desired, some lemon juice, and place on the brain
5. Sprinkle with 1 tablespoon white breadcrumbs
6. Place in the oven and cook 10-15 minutes at high heat; the brain should completely contract during this time
7. Using a serving spoon, place the brain on a dish and pour the sauce, which has been mixed with 1 spoon of soup stock, from the side
8. Garnish with lemon slices

title: Roasted Chestnut Variations
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Kastanien (chestnuts)
- ausgelöste frische Zwetschgen, Äpfel oder Birnenspalten
- geröstete Semmelbröseln oder Semmelstücke
- Butter
- gebäckte Schmackerlen (optional)
- Maraschino, Rum oder Liqueure (optional)

## Directions

1. Gut ocrührt, zu dem Roller gegossen, welchen man dann noch unter öfterem Rühren 1/2 Stunde kochen läßt. Gleich anfangs können einige ausgelöste, frische Zwetschgen, Äpfel- oder Birnenspalten mit dem Roller gekocht werden. Wenn dieser schon in einer tiefen Schüssel angerichtet ist, kann man geröstete Semmelbröseln darüberstreuen oder den Roller mit in Butter gebäckenen Semmelstücken bestecken. Derselbe kann auch mit gebäckenen Schmackerlen untermengt und serviert werden. Auch können mit dem Ober einige Löffel voll Maraschino oder Rum oder beliebige Liqueure mitgekocht werden.

title: Roasted Chicken (Brathühner)
body:
## Ingredients

- dressed chickens
- fat pieces
- cold water
- hot water or broth
- butter

## Directions

1. Place the dressed chickens breast-side down in a pan and cover the top with fat pieces.
2. Pour some cold water beside them.
3. Roast first slowly, then at higher heat for ¾-1 hour, basting frequently with hot water or broth.
4. After 30 minutes, turn the chickens.
5. At the end, let a piece of butter brown in the pan juices.
6. Carve the chickens by first cutting off the wings and legs, then cutting the breast and back into 2-3 pieces.
7. On a warmed platter, place the back pieces first, then the breast pieces on top, and arrange the wings and legs on both sides so the chicken returns to its original shape.
8. Attach paper ruffles to the legs and decorate the chicken with curled parsley.
9. Pour the pan juices alongside the chickens.

title: Roasted Fish Head Soup (Soupe maigre à la reine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Head of a carp or other fish
- 8 Deca finely sliced rolls
- One piece of marrow
- A whole egg
- 8 Deca fat
- 8 Deca flour
- 2.5 liters beef broth or water

## Directions

1. Cut the fish head into pieces
2. Coat in flour and breadcrumbs
3. Fry in fat with the sliced rolls, marrow, and egg
4. Pound the fried mixture finely
5. In a casserole, heat 8 Deca fat and 8 Deca flour, stirring to toast
6. Add the pounded fish mixture
7. Pour in 2.5 liters beef broth or water
8. Simmer for 1 hour, stirring frequently
9. Pass through a sieve into a casserole
10. Skim off the fat
11. Pour into soup pot and garnish with boiled rolls or fried bread cubes

title: Roasted Lettuce Salad (Laitues farcies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 whole young lettuce heads
- Salt water
- Cold water
- Forcemeat (farce)
- 8 drams hot butter
- Fresh parsley, finely cut
- ½ liter broth

## Directions

1. Wash eight whole lettuce heads, place them in boiling salt water for a few minutes, then in cold water, and lay them out on a cloth; once the water has drained sufficiently, separate the leaves and place a spoonful of forcemeat in the center of each head.
2. Close the leaves and restore the heads to their original form.
3. In 8 drams hot butter, toast some finely cut fresh parsley, place the lettuce heads in it, pour ½ liter broth over them, and cook uncovered for about ½ hour.
4. When serving, remove them with a slotted spoon and pour a little butter sauce over the salad.

title: Roasted Mutton Leg with Parsley (Gigot de mouton)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 leg of mutton
- Salt and pepper
- Lard
- Fine table oil

## Directions

1. Beat the leg of mutton with the flat side of a meat cleaver and trim.
2. Rub the meat with salt and pepper.
3. Brush with fine table oil.
4. Cover with paper and let rest for 1 hour.
5. Place fat in a pan and lay the leg on it.
6. Roast in a hot oven at high heat, basting frequently with the drippings.
7. Remove the leg, cut into pieces of any size, arrange on a platter.
8. Serve with various vegetables.

title: Roasted Mutton Saddle (Selle de mouton rôtie)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 mutton saddle
- Salt and pepper
- Bacon pieces, pencil-thick and finger-long

## Directions

1. Remove the fine membranes and hack the ribs lengthwise several times.
2. Rub with salt and pepper to taste.
3. Lard with pencil-thick and finger-long pieces of bacon.
4. Prepare further as the roasted mutton leg.

title: Roasted Pork Leg Cutlet (Coissot de porc rôti)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pork leg from a young pig
- salt
- caraway
- fat
- broth

## Directions

1. Salt the pork leg well with the skin (rind) attached and rub with caraway
2. Place in a pan containing fat, cover the meat with fat and roast in the oven with frequent basting using broth and the accumulated juices
3. When the rind develops a glassy appearance during roasting, cut it in a grid pattern with a sharp knife at thumb-width intervals and continue roasting it brown and crispy by frequently basting with the juices
4. Cut the meat into desired pieces, arrange them on a platter so that the leg resumes its original form, and serve it with braised salad, sauerkraut, pea purée or similar

title: Roasted Pork Leg with Caraway (Roasted Young Pork Leg)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pork leg from a young pig
- salt
- caraway
- fat
- broth

## Directions

1. Salt the pork leg well with the skin (rind) attached and rub it with caraway
2. Place it in a roasting pan containing fat
3. Cover the meat with melted fat and roast it in the oven at moderate heat for about 2 hours, basting frequently with the juices that accumulate
4. Remove the meat, cut it into desired pieces, arrange on a platter and serve the sauce separately in a sauceboat
