5. The sauce can be soured and the fat removed as desired

title: Roasted Poularde (Poularde rötie)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde
- bacon

## Directions

1. After plucking, flambe the poularde and hold the feet especially in the flame so the scaly skin can be removed
2. The wings can be cut off entirely or the feathers removed from them
3. Open the poularde, clean it in cold water, dry it inside and out, and truss it like a pheasant
4. Lard the breast with fine bacon or cover with bacon slices
5. Wrap the poularde with a sheet of paper brushed with butter and bind with twine
6. Put the poularde on a spit and roast it at moderate heat for approximately 3/4 to 1 hour
7. Remove the paper and roast the poularde over bright fire for another 1/4 hour so it becomes a beautiful brown color
8. Remove it, cut the twine, pull it out, cut the poularde into desired pieces, arrange them in a bowl, cover with bacon slices and pour the defatted juice over it
9. Alternatively, the poularde can be placed in a pan with fat and roasted in the oven, basting frequently with its own juice

title: Roasted Quails (Gebratene Wachteln)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Quails
- Salt
- Grape vine leaves, cut
- Thin bacon slices
- String
- Defatted cooking liquid

## Directions

1. After plucking, singeing, and cleaning the quails with a cloth
2. Sprinkle inside and outside with salt
3. Wrap with cut grape vine leaves and thin bacon slices
4. Bind with string
5. Shortly before serving, roast on a spit for approximately 10-15 minutes over strong bright fire
6. Remove from spit, cut the string and remove it
7. Arrange the quails on a plate with the grape vine leaves and bacon
8. Serve with defatted cooking liquid
9. Alternatively, the quails can be roasted in a pan over strong heat with frequent basting in the oven
10. Or the quails can be placed on a spit with 1 semolina slice and bacon between each quail

title: Roasted Wild Game Fillets (Spiked and Truffle-Stuffed Wild Boar Fillets)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Black game, cleaned
- Bacon
- Truffles
- Vinegar
- Wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onions
- Parsley
- Dried herbs
- Butter
- Paper for wrapping

## Directions

1. Cut beautiful fillets from cleaned black game as desired
2. Spike the fillets with bacon and truffles
3. Place them in a marinade made of vinegar, wine, salt, pepper, many onions, parsley, and dried herbs
4. Add a piece of butter and let it simmer gently in a casserole on low charcoal fire until the fillets have absorbed the flavor of the marinade
5. Thread the fillets onto a spit, wrap them in paper, and roast them slowly
6. Baste them with butter and marinade
7. Make a sauce piquante from the marinade
8. Arrange the fillets beautifully on a plate, glaze them, and pour the sauce over top
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Roasted Wood Snipe with Croûtons (Becasses aux crontons)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Plucked and flamed woodcocks, not opened
- Lard slices, finely cut
- Strong broth bread slices
- Lard

## Directions

1. Pluck and flame woodcocks without opening them
2. Remove the eyes
3. Dress them by turning the feet inward so the claws clasp the thighs and the knee lies under the breast
4. Bend the long beak either into the thigh at the side or into the breast forward, or turn the head so the back of the head sits firmly on the breast with the beak pointing forward
5. Salt the dressed woodcocks and wrap them with finely cut lard slices and bind them
6. Shortly before serving, place them on a spit and roast for 15-20 minutes
7. During roasting, place strong thick bread slices cut into the pan below which is lined with lard, so that the escaping juice drips onto them
8. After this time, remove the woodcocks from the spit, loosen the lard, divide the woodcocks into 4 parts but reassemble them, arrange on a platter with the lard and place the bread slices at the edge of the platter

title: Roe Deer Back, Roasted (Rehrücken, gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- venison back
- vinegar
- onion
- brine/marinade
- bacon
- paper
- cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the back like the haunch, namely marinated in vinegar and onion, and let it lie in brine for one or two days
2. Remove the skin cleanly, lard it with bacon, cover it with paper, roast it slowly on a spit, and baste it a few times with brine and cream mixed together
3. When well roasted, place it on a dish and garnish it like the venison haunch
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Roe Deer Haunch, Roasted (Rehschlagel, gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- venison haunch
- vinegar
- water
- onions
- cloves
- garlic
- bay leaves
- lemon peel
- juniper berries
- pepper
- bacon
- cream

## Directions

1. Wash the haunch well, salt it, and let it stand in salt for a while
2. In a small pot, combine half vinegar and half water, two onions studded with cloves, one clove of garlic, two bay leaves, lemon peel, some juniper berries, and some whole pepper
3. Let the brine simmer and scald the haunch with it
4. Let it lie in the brine for two or three days, scalding it with boiling brine twice daily
5. When ready to use, remove the skin, lard it well with bacon, and roast it slowly on a spit
6. During roasting, baste it frequently with brine and cream mixed together
7. When well roasted, place it on a dish, garnish it like a hare, and serve with an anchovy or caper sauce made from the brine
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rogen-Suppe (Roe Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked roe
- pea broth
- pepper
- ginger
- nutmeg
- butter
- bread, cubed and toasted in butter

## Directions

1. Stir together the cooked roe
2. Let it come to a boil in strained pea broth
3. Add a little pepper, ginger, nutmeg and a small piece of butter
4. Serve the soup over bread that has been cubed and toasted in butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Roggenbrot (Rye Bread)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kilo rye flour
- 1/2 liter sourdough starter
- 1/2 liter lukewarm water
- salt
- caraway, anise, fennel, or coriander seeds (whole or crushed)
- lukewarm water as needed

## Directions

1. First, the so-called sponge must be set: Put 3 kilo rye flour in a large suitable vessel (such as a large bowl, wooden trough or small bread trough) and place it in a warm location several hours earlier
2. Dilute 1/2 liter sourdough starter with 1/2 liter lukewarm water and pour this into the flour in the middle of which a well has been made
3. Mix in enough flour so that this sponge becomes thick, dust it with some flour, cover it and let it stand overnight after setting it up in the evening
4. The next day, mix the necessary salt with caraway or anise, fennel or also coriander, whole or crushed, into the sponge
5. Work this with the flour and the required lukewarm water into a stiff dough for as long as it is fine, that is, knead the dough with both hands until it pulls away from them
6. Once this is achieved, place it covered near the warm stove and let it ferment

title: Roggens-Wäffer (Rye Water)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons rye flour
- 1 liter cold water

## Directions

1. Stir 2 tablespoons rye flour with 1 liter of cold water, let it come to a boil, and strain it through.

title: Rohmehl Knödel von Semmeltein (Semolina Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hectograms butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 10 hectograms decrustred semolina bread soaked in milk
- 6 tablespoons bread crumbs
- 6 tablespoons flour
- plums

## Directions

1. Beat 6 hectograms butter until fluffy
2. Gradually add 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
3. Mix with 10 hectograms decrustred semolina bread soaked and squeezed dry, 6 tablespoons bread crumbs, and 6 tablespoons flour
4. Let rest for 1/4 hour
5. Place dough on a flour-dusted board, press out with a noodle roller to the thickness of a finger, and cut pieces the size of plums
6. Place 1 plum in the center of each piece
7. Distribute the dough evenly over it and place the noodles in boiling salted water
8. Cook covered for 15 minutes, stirring the bottom occasionally with a wooden spoon so the noodles do not stick
9. After 15 minutes, remove with a slotted spoon

title: Rohrhuhnersupe (Corncrake Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one corncrake
- water
- leeks
- yellow turnips
- cabbage
- celery
- parsley
- various seasonings
- butter
- baked potato dumplings

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the corncrake and put it on the fire with water, following the same proportions as for beef
2. Cook it with leeks, yellow turnips, cabbage, celery, parsley and various seasonings like a beef soup
3. Strain it off, make a thin roux from butter
4. Let it simmer well with this soup
5. Put baked potato dumplings or something similar in the soup pot and serve the soup over them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rohrhuhnrs Pâtete (Corncrake Pâté, for Fast Days)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned and chopped corncrakes
- Fish farce
- Butter dough

## Directions

1. The cleaned and chopped corncrakes are braised and, once completely cold, are filled into the pâté with fish farce as described in the 'Poularde Pâté' recipe (No. 1060).

title: Rohrhühner in einer braunen Sauce (Reed Hens in Brown Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Reed hen
- Vinegar
- Water
- Kuttelkraut
- Bay leaves
- Lemon peel
- Onion
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Pea broth
- Wine

## Directions

1. Skin the reed hens, cut them into several pieces, wash them well.
2. Make a marinade: take half vinegar and half water, Kuttelkraut, bay leaves, lemon peel, onion, and salt.
3. When this marinade simmers, lay the reed hens in it and let them braise gently over heat until soft.
4. Make a brown sauce: take butter in a pan, make a small roux, and fill it half with the marinade in which the reed hens were cooked, and half with pea broth.
5. Let it come to a boil, then thin it with wine, lay the reed hens in it, and let them braise a little more.
6. Arrange the reed hens on a platter, strain the sauce through a sieve, and pour the brown sauce over them.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rolladen mit Champignons (Veal Rolls with Mushrooms)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- veal schnitzel from the leg
- leftover roast
- piece of bacon
- marrow
- cooked ham
- green parsley
- chives
- truffles
- salt
- pepper
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls of cream
- bacon strips
- butter
- mushrooms
- flour
- half coffee cup of white wine
- broth

## Directions

1. Cut fine schnitzels from a veal leg, pound them well; then take some leftover roast, a small piece of bacon, some marrow, a piece of cooked ham, green parsley, chives, some truffles, chop all this finely together
2. Mix salt, pepper, 2 egg yolks, and 2 spoonfuls of cream well together, spread this on the mentioned schnitzels, and roll them together
3. In a casserole, lay some bacon strips and place the rolls on them, cover with bacon strips, put the lid on the casserole, apply heat from below and above, and let them stew on a gentle charcoal fire for two hours
4. Strain off the fat, cut the rolls lengthwise, lay them on the dish so that the stuffing is visible
5. Take small, clean-washed mushrooms, let a piece of butter become hot, add green parsley, chives and the mushrooms, and let them stew until the sauce is completely reduced
6. Dust some flour on it, and let it stew down a little more; finally add half a coffee cup of white wine, the same amount of broth and 2 spoonfuls of cream, and let it cook until only very little sauce remains
7. Strain the fat from the sauce well, and pour it over the rolls; with the mushrooms, form a ring around the edge of the dish, and arrange the rolls in the middle of the dish
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rolled Ducks (Aufgerollte Enten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ducks
- Poultry forcemeat
- Bacon strips
- Spices
- Fine herbs
- Glass of wine
- Anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and gut a pair of nice ducks
2. Break out the bones
3. Season well
4. Beat them quite flat
5. Turn them several times in well-prepared poultry forcemeat
6. Roll them up and carefully bind them with bacon strips
7. Cook them in a braise with some spices, fine herbs, and a glass of wine, short and tender
8. Drain the fat
9. When serving, give anchovy sauce underneath
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rolled Egg Dish (Oeufs à la Palestine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 4 ounces butter
- Preserved cherries
- Sugar
- Optional: vegetables, peas, ham, truffles, ragout, Parmesan cheese, other meats, snails, or mushrooms

## Directions

1. Beat 12 eggs with 1/2 teaspoon salt until well combined.
2. Heat 4 ounces butter in a sauté pan and pour in a serving spoon of the beaten eggs.
3. When the egg sheet becomes slightly firm, lift it from the pan with the aid of two serving spoons into a bowl.
4. Continue with remaining egg mixture, baking successive sheets and removing each to the bowl.
5. Immediately after baking, each sheet is topped with preserved cherries, the sheets are rolled lightly together, arranged in a bowl, sprinkled with sugar, and placed in the hot oven for a few minutes so the eggs warm through.
6. Alternatively, instead of cherries, the egg sheets can be filled with fine vegetables, green peas, finely chopped ham, truffles, fine ragout, or grated Parmesan cheese, finely chopped other meats, breaded and fried fish, roasted snails, or finely chopped mushrooms braised in butter.

title: Romaine Lettuce (Kopfsalat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 heads Romaine lettuce (Kopfsalat)
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 12 drams butter
- 12 drams flour
- Broth
- Salt
- Green peas (4 spoonfuls, if desired)

## Directions

1. Take about 8 heads of Romaine lettuce (Rundsalat), divide each into 2 parts, remove the coarse stems, wash the salad, boil it in salt water, and place in cold water.
2. In 12 drams butter, make an equal amount of flour into a roux; add the pressed and somewhat chopped salad, pour ½ liter broth over it, season with salt, stir it, and let it boil well while stirring frequently.
3. If desired, add 4 spoonfuls of green peas.

title: Romaine Lettuce Another Way (La Romaine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful, large Romaine lettuce
- Salt water
- Sour salad dressing
- Butter
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Divide beautiful, large Romaine lettuce into 4 parts, wash it, boil it in salt water, remove it, place on a sieve, and when the water has drained, place it on a plate and sprinkle with several spoonfuls of sour salad dressing mixed with its juice and toasted bread crumbs in butter.

title: Rond de Veau aux Anchois (Rolled Veal with Anchovies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 kilo veal from the knuckle
- cleaned anchovies, half-finger-length pieces
- twine
- yellow turnips, thinly sliced
- parsley
- leek
- lard, cut into cubes
- 1/2 liter wine
- 1/8 liter vinegar
- 10 deca cleaned anchovies
- 8 deca capers
- parsley
- 1 clove garlic, crushed, if desired
- vinegar
- juice of 2 lemons
- 10 deca hot oil

## Directions

1. Pound the veal thin as paper after removing all sinews and skin, thread it with cleaned, half-finger-length pieces of anchovies, roll it up, and bind it with twine
2. Line a casserole with thinly sliced turnips, parsley, leek, cubed lard, and the trimmings from the anchovies wrapped in cheesecloth
3. Lay the bound veal on top, add 1/2 liter wine and 1/8 liter vinegar, and braise gently covered until tender
4. Set aside and let cool in the cooking liquid
5. Cut into thin slices and arrange on a platter
6. For the sauce: finely cut 10 deca cleaned anchovies with 8 deca capers and parsley, add crushed garlic if desired, vinegar, and juice of 2 lemons to a casserole containing 10 deca hot oil, bring to a boil once, and pour over the meat
7. Serve warm or cold with macaroni, cold aspic, and lemon wedges

title: Root Vegetable Broth for Preparing Other Light Broths (Bouillon maigre aux racines)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pieces yellow turnips
- 3 pieces onion
- 2 leeks
- 1 head celery
- 32 decagrams butter
- ½ liter dried peas
- sorrel
- chervil
- parsley
- 3 liters cold water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the turnips, onion, leeks, and celery very well, cut all thinly and place in a casserole with 32 decagrams butter
2. Roast until it has a golden yellow color
3. Add ½ liter dried peas with some sorrel, chervil, and parsley, pour with 3 liters cold water
4. Simmer slowly about 2 hours
5. Let stand covered about ½ hour but do not boil further
6. The soup will clarify from the quiet standing
7. Strain through sieve into an earthenware pot and set aside for further use

title: Rosa-Marzipan (Pink Marzipan)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Marzipan dough
- Sugar
- Cochineal
- Rose essence
- Rose water
- Fine, narrow, thread-like cut candied orange peel
- Diamond-shaped citron leaves
- Colored, sugar-preserved fruits

## Directions

1. Proceed exactly as taught above for White Marzipan.
2. Add a little cochineal and a few drops of rose essence to the sugar glaze.
3. Use rose water instead of orange blossom water.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rose Hips in Steam (Hagebutten im Dunst)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Approximately 1 kilo fresh, still hard rose hips
- 1.5 liter water
- 80 Deca sugar

## Directions

1. Split the rose hips open and remove the down and seeds with a small spoon
2. Place the rose hips in a warm location for several days so they become soft
3. In a tin-lined pot, put 1.5 liters water and 80 Deca sugar, let it boil for 15 minutes
4. Add the rose hips and let them simmer together for several minutes, stirring frequently
5. Remove the rose hips from the liquid and cook the liquid until somewhat thicker
6. Fill the rose hips into jars and pour the sugar over them so that it completely covers them
7. After complete cooling, bind the jars with bladders and proceed further like the Muscatel pears (No. 1842)

title: Rosehip Marmalade (Marmelade von Hagebutten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe and cleaned rosehips (Heehepetſchen)
- sugar (half pound per 7 loth of pulp)

## Directions

1. Press the rosehips through a fine sieve.
2. Weigh the pulp and measure half a pound of sugar for every 7 loth of pulp.
3. Clarify and cook the sugar.
4. Stir the pulp well into the sugar.
5. Once the marmalade reaches proper thickness, place in a cool place.
6. Fill into containers or jars.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rosen-Gelee (Rose Jelly)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Hirschhorn (deer antler gelatin)
- 1 Maß water
- 1 Loth Tournesol (litmus)
- egg white, beaten
- sugar
- 1/2 bottle rose water
- juice of 6 lemons
- 1/2 bottle good Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Cook the deer antler gelatin with 1 Maß water as described for strawberry jelly
2. Add the Tournesol to the cooked gelatin
3. Allow it to come to a boil once with the beaten egg white and sugar, which will give it a beautiful red color
4. Remove from heat and add 1/2 bottle of the best rose water, along with the juice of 6 lemons and 1/2 bottle good Rhine wine
5. Proceed as described in the general method
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rosetten (Filled Bread Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rolls of any size
- roasted brain or marrow, or carp milt with herring milt prepared in butter and parsley, or any other desired filling
- cold milk
- beaten eggs
- bread crumbs
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Trim the rolls and halve them
2. Cut each half deeply enough to be able to insert desired filling
3. Dip filled rolls in cold milk, allowing them to absorb some
4. Dip in beaten eggs
5. Sprinkle generously with bread crumbs
6. Fry well in hot fat
7. Serve as a vegetable side dish

title: Rosetten-Sulz (Rose Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams rose petals, freed of stamens and green parts
- 40 decagrams boiling, clarified sugar
- 7 decagrams isinglass, hot and dissolved
- Clear juice of 2 lemons
- Cherry water or additional liquid as needed

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same way as Violet Sulz (see recipe 1677)
2. Use 16 decagrams rose petals instead of violet flowers, freed of their stamens and all green parts

title: Rostboeuf garniert (Roasted Beef, Garnished)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.20 kg deboned beef sirloin
- 15 dg fat
- 4 dkg butter
- salt
- pepper
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Remove bones and fat from the meat, season with salt and pepper, and lightly bind if desired.
2. Heat 15 dg fat in a low pan until very hot, add the meat, sear on all sides over strong heat, place in a hot oven, and continue roasting for 25-30 minutes under frequent basting with its own juices.
3. Place on a plate and keep warm in the oven.
4. Make the sauce by pouring off the fat, browning 4 dkg butter in the pan, adding broth or water, bring to a boil, and strain.
5. Slice the meat very thinly, arrange on a long platter, garnish alternating around with 1 spoon of vegetables and between them 1 small scoop of rice, and decorate with parsley.

title: Rostbraten (Braised Beef Roast)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pieces beef roast
- fat
- 1/2 liter sour cream
- 1 1/2 liter brown broth
- garlic
- 1 bay leaf

## Directions

1. Pound the beef roast pieces, season them, and quickly brown them in fat
2. Transfer them to another casserole without the cooking fat
3. Pour 1/2 liter sour cream and 1 1/2 liter brown broth over them
4. Season with garlic and 1 bay leaf
5. Braise them covered over strong heat until tender
6. The sauce should be thick and light brown in color
7. Pour the sauce over the roast when serving

title: Rostbraten Fleisch (Roasted Beef)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef designated for roasting, from the rib piece
- Garlic
- Minced bacon fat
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut as much beef designated for roasting from the rib piece as needed
2. Salt it, knock it, season with garlic and minced bacon fat
3. Place it on the grill and roast it over glowing coals
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rostbraten in natura (Plain Beef Roast)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pieces flattened beef roast, not too heavily salted, optionally with pepper
- 1 piece onion, leafy sliced
- bunches of savory herb
- 1 teaspoon caraway seeds
- vinegar and water or broth

## Directions

1. Place the flattened beef roast pieces in a casserole
2. Add 1 piece of leafy sliced onion
3. Add bunches of savory herb
4. Add 1 teaspoon caraway seeds
5. Add enough vinegar and water or broth so the liquid rises above the meat
6. Braise until tender
7. Serve with potatoes

title: Rostbraten mit Erdäpfel (Beef Roast with Potatoes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- flattened beef roast pieces
- raw potatoes, cut into large pieces

## Directions

1. Prepare as described above
2. When the roast is half tender, add raw, large-cut potatoes to the side of the casserole in the cooking liquid
3. Braise them together with the meat until tender
4. Arrange and serve together

title: Rostbraten mit Erdäpfel anderer Art à l'ail (Beef Roast with Potatoes and Garlic)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- flattened and salted beef roast pieces
- bacon
- onion
- garlic (optional)
- 1/4 liter crumbs of black bread
- meat broth
- wine
- seasoning
- raw, peeled potatoes

## Directions

1. Place the flattened and salted beef roast pieces with bacon, onion, and garlic if desired in a casserole
2. Braise until brown
3. Sprinkle 1/4 liter of black bread crumbs over them
4. Add meat broth and wine, in total enough so the liquid barely covers the meat
5. Add seasoning to taste and raw, peeled potatoes
6. Braise covered until tender
7. Arrange the roast with the potatoes on a serving dish
8. Pour the defatted sauce over them

title: Rostbraten mit Knoblauch (Pot Roast with Garlic)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Rostbraten (pot roasts)
- Zwiebel (onions)
- Fein geschnittener Knoblauch oder Rocambole (finely cut garlic or rocambole)
- Fett (fat)
- Suppe (broth)

## Directions

1. Prepare as you would ordinarily prepare pot roast with onions, but instead of onions use finely cut garlic or rocambole.
2. Add the garlic at the same time you pour the fat with broth over the meat.

title: Rostbraten mit Knoblauch anderer Art (Pot Roast with Garlic, Another Way)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Rostbraten, finger-thick slices (pot roasts)
- Salz und Pfeffer (salt and pepper)
- Zerlassene Butter oder Fett (melted butter or fat)
- Fein geschnittener Knoblauch (finely cut garlic)
- Butter oder Fett (butter or fat)
- Salz und Pfeffer (salt and pepper)
- Essig (vinegar)
- Suppe (broth)

## Directions

1. Pound the finger-thick sliced pot roasts well, dust with salt and pepper, and brown in melted butter or fat.
2. Brown them quickly, either on a roasting pan or in a very hot pan with fat.
3. Arrange on a platter and coat with sauce.
4. For the sauce: quickly roast finely cut garlic in butter or fat over high heat, add some salt and pepper, deglaze with a few spoonfuls of vinegar and some broth, and pour this sauce over the pot roasts.

title: Rostbraten mit Malaga-Wein (Beef Roast with Malaga Wine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pieces beef roast
- 1 small bottle Malaga wine or 1/2 liter white wine
- broth
- brown butter sauce

## Directions

1. Pour 1 small bottle of Malaga wine or 1/2 liter white wine and some broth over 6 roast pieces
2. Place a fitting lid on top so the alcohol does not escape
3. Braise until tender
4. Remove the roast, trim neatly, and glaze if desired
5. Reduce the defatted cooking liquid slightly
6. Mix with a few spoonfuls of brown butter sauce

title: Rostbraten mit Rahm (Roast Beef with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-3 pieces of 25-30 dkg
- 1/2 l cream
- 6 dkg hot fat
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- paprika
- water or broth
- salt
- pepper
- 2 chopped anchovies (optional)

## Directions

1. Trim the meat of bones, fat and skin (these can also remain), wipe off, salt, pepper.
2. In a roasting pan, in 6 dkg hot fat, brown 1 finely chopped onion, add some paprika, lay in the meat.
3. Pour in water or broth and braise 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
4. Strain the sauce, add the cream and simmer a few minutes. Optional: 2 chopped anchovies can be braised with it.

title: Rostbraten mit Rahm-Sauce (Pot Roast with Sour Cream Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Rostbraten (pot roasts)
- Fett (fat)
- 1/2 Liter sauren Rahm (1/2 liter sour cream)
- 1.5 Liter braune Suppe (1.5 liters brown broth)
- Knoblauch (garlic)
- 1 Lorbeerblatt (bay leaf)

## Directions

1. Pound the pot roasts, sear them and quickly brown in fat.
2. Transfer them without the fat to another casserole.
3. Pour 1/2 liter sour cream and 1.5 liters brown broth over them.
4. Season with some garlic and one bay leaf.
5. Braise covered over high heat until tender.
6. The sauce should be thick and light brown in color.
7. Pour the sauce over the pot roasts when serving.

title: Rostbraten mit Reis und Paradiesäpfel (Beef Roast with Rice and Tomato)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beef roast pieces
- 1/8 liter rice
- about 3 tablespoons tomato marmalade

## Directions

1. Prepare as described above
2. Instead of potatoes, add 1/8 liter rice and about 3 tablespoons of tomato marmalade to the liquid of the half-tender roast
3. Braise until tender
4. Alternatively, use 3 pieces of raw tomato, torn apart with seeds removed, and braise without straining when serving
5. Rice can also be braised without tomatoes
6. Serve salt gherkins or mustard gherkins with this roast

title: Rostbraten mit Sardellen (Beef Roast with Anchovies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beef roast pieces
- finely chopped anchovy fillets
- green parsley
- flour

## Directions

1. Pound the roast very thin
2. Spread finely chopped anchovy fillets with green parsley on each piece
3. Fold each piece in half
4. Pound lightly with the back of a knife
5. Dip in flour and quickly fry on both sides

title: Rostbraten mit Zwiebel anderer Art (Pot Roast with Onions, Another Way)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Rostbraten, finger-thick slices (pot roasts)
- Zwiebel, geschnitten (onions, cut)
- Grüne Petersilie (green parsley)
- 1 Lorbeerblatt (bay leaf)
- Öl, Butter oder Fett (oil, butter or fat)
- Salz und Gewürz (salt and spices)
- Wein oder Essig (wine or vinegar)
- Suppe (broth)
- Mehl (flour)

## Directions

1. Pound the finger-thick sliced pot roasts, trim them neatly, dust with salt and spices, and brown in oil, butter or fat.
2. On a platter, arrange cut onions, green parsley and one bay leaf.
3. Place the pot roasts on top and let them lie covered for several hours.
4. Then quickly brown them on a flat pan or roasting pan.
5. Take the vegetables that remained on the platter, dust with flour, and brown them in the pan with the remaining fat.
6. Let them cook for a few minutes with several spoonfuls of wine or vinegar and some broth.
7. Serve these pot roasts with their own sauce or with brown piquant sauce.

title: Rostbraten mit Zwiebeln (Roast Beef with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pieces of 25-30 dkg
- 1 large onion
- 6 dkg hot fat
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- potato pancakes

## Directions

1. Trim the rostbraten of bones, skin and fat (these can also remain), wipe off, tap lightly.
2. Just before roasting, salt and pepper, dip one side in flour, place this side in a shallow pan with about 6 dkg hot fat.
3. At the same time add 1 large onion to roast with it, turn the rostbraten after a few minutes and roast both sides over high heat on the stove for a total of 12 minutes.
4. Serve the rostbraten with the onion on a long platter. Side dish: potato pancakes.

title: Rostbraten-Goulash (Roast Beef Goulash)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 decagrams bacon
- 1 large head onion
- 40 decagrams roast beef
- Paprika
- Salt
- Stock

## Directions

1. 5 decagrams of bacon and 1 large head onion are chopped very finely together with the chopping knife, placed in a casserole and golden roasted.
2. Meanwhile, 40 decagrams of roast beef is beaten thin, cut into finger-long pieces of equal width.
3. These pieces are added at the same time with 2 decagrams of paprika and some salt to the golden roasted onion and braised gently for 1 hour covered.
4. Then remove the fat, pour 1/2 liter of stock to it and let it boil well.

title: Rotes Eis (Red Icing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared white icing
- alkermes juice

## Directions

1. Stir alkermes juice (obtainable at the apothecary) into the prepared white icing until the mass takes on a desired red color
2. Use water instead of lemon juice

title: Rotilie-Strudel
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- jam or marmalade cream

## Directions

1. The preparation is explained in the description of the Almond Strudel (No. 1090), but fill it instead with jam cream or any desired marmalade

title: Rotkraut (Red Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of red cabbage
- 8 dkg hot fat
- 1 onion
- 8-10 pieces of sugar
- salt
- caraway seeds
- vinegar
- water
- red wine
- 1 apple (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove bad leaves and stalks from cabbage, divide into quarters, wash, and cut into fine strips
2. In a casserole, roast 1 onion dark yellow in 8 dkg hot fat
3. Brown 8-10 sugar cubes in the fat
4. Add the cabbage, then salt, caraway seeds, vinegar, and enough water to cover
5. Stew covered for 2-2½ hours, stirring frequently
6. Season further if needed with salt and sugar
7. Sour with red wine
8. An apple cut into cubes may be stewed with it

title: Roulade Pastries (Roulade-Pasteten)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff dough
- Eggs
- Filling (not specified)

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and place the filling in the middle of strips brushed with beaten eggs
2. Fold the dough finger-width overlapping, with a cut side facing up
3. Brush the roulades with beaten eggs, leaving a knife-back-width dry border where the dough overlaps
4. Place on a water-moistened baking sheet with the top edge cut-side facing the back wall of the oven
5. Bake in the oven at fairly strong heat until golden yellow
6. Remove with a knife from the sheet, arrange in a bowl, and serve warm as an appetizer, side dish, or for supper or tea

title: Roſen-Gelee (Rose Jelly)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- hartshorn
- 1 measure of water
- 1 loth of tournesol
- finely ground egg white and sugar
- half a bottle of the best rose water
- juice of 6 lemons
- half a bottle of good Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Cook hartshorn with 1 measure of water as mentioned for strawberry jelly
2. Add 1 loth of tournesol
3. Let it come to a boil once with the beaten egg white and sugar, from which it obtains a beautiful red color
4. Remove it from the fire
5. Add half a bottle of the best rose water, the juice of 6 lemons, and half a bottle of good Rhine wine
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Rubel in der Milch (Noodles in Milk)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter flour
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 liter boiling milk
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons sugar
- sugar for sprinkling
- cinnamon for sprinkling (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare noodles from 1/2 liter flour and 2 whole eggs as described for Abgeschmalzene Rubel (No. 1201)
2. Add them to 1 liter boiling milk while stirring constantly
3. Cook them thickly with 8 tablespoons butter and the same amount of sugar
4. Arrange on a platter, sprinkle with finely sifted sugar and optionally with cinnamon, and serve
5. If the noodles have cooked down too much, thin with boiling milk as needed

title: Rubeln in Croustades mit Schmätzern (Noodle Dumplings in Croustades with Browned Butter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 1 liter boiling custard or milk
- 8 ounces butter
- 12 ounces brown sugar
- 1 knife point salt
- butter pastry baskets
- 3 egg whites
- 12 ounces finely sifted flour
- optional: 1 piece grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Make fine dumplings from 4 egg yolks
2. Cook them in 1 liter of boiling custard or milk with constant stirring
3. Cover and cook for about 5 minutes
4. Add 8 ounces butter, 12 ounces brown sugar, and 1 knife point of salt
5. Stir well
6. Fill 1 tablespoon of the mixture into baked butter pastry baskets and arrange in a bowl
7. Alternatively, if horns (pastries) have been made, fill them with some dumplings and place in the croustades
8. Beat the snow from 3 egg whites and mix with 12 ounces finely sifted flour and optionally 1 piece of grated chocolate
9. Cover the whole with this mixture, dust with fine flour, and bake in the open oven for a few minutes

title: Rumpsteak (Entrecôte)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Rump steak cut finger-thick
- Pepper and salt
- Hot lard for frying
- Cream and gravy or soup
- Maître d'hôtel butter or parsley or tarragon butter
- Optional: lemon slices
- Optional: fried potatoes

## Directions

1. Lightly beat finger-thick cut rump steaks so they do not become too wide but retain their height
2. Sprinkle with pepper and salt
3. Quickly fry on both sides in hot lard
4. Place on a serving spoon, remove fat from the drippings and add some cream and gravy or soup, bring to a boil and pour to the side of the rump steak
5. Place a piece of Maître d'hôtel butter or parsley or tarragon butter in the middle of each piece of meat
6. Alternatively, you can add sliced lemon
7. Rump steak can also be served with the defatted own sauce and with fried potatoes

title: Rumpsteak (Rump Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pieces sirloin of 30 dkg
- 6 dkg hot fat
- flour
- 1 piece butter
- broth
- whole potatoes or pancakes
- spinach
- cooked salad
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Trim the meat of fat, bones and skin (these can also remain), wipe off, tap lightly.
2. Just before roasting, salt and pepper, dip one side in flour, place this side in a shallow pan with 6 dkg hot fat, quickly sear both sides over high heat.
3. Pour off the fat, heat 1 piece of butter in the pan, stir up the brown bits from the bottom, add broth, bring to a boil.
4. Serve the rumpsteak on a long platter with whole potatoes or pancakes, spinach, cooked salad. The meat should be well-aged, otherwise it remains tough and stringy.

title: Russische Suppe (Russian Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large light turnip and 1 dark yellow turnip
- 1 red turnip
- 4 peeled potatoes
- 2 heads of cabbage
- 1 head of onion (optional)
- Leftover roasted meat such as goose, duck, chicken, pork or roasted goose liver
- Butter or goose fat
- Raw turnips
- Salt, cayenne pepper or paprika as needed
- 1 liter broth
- Rice
- Optionally strawberry jam or various roasted sausages

## Directions

1. Peel and cut turnips, potatoes and cabbage thinly; also add onion if desired.
2. Cut leftover roasted meat into desired pieces.
3. Line a casserole with butter or goose fat, cover the bottom with raw turnips, lay a portion of the above vegetable mixture on top.
4. Sprinkle with necessary salt, cayenne pepper or paprika, then add a layer of cut, roasted meat and continue in this manner until the casserole is filled.
5. Rice should be added last.
6. Pour 1 liter of broth over all, close the casserole well and braise the contents for 1 hour.
7. If the rice is not yet soft enough, pour in additional broth as needed.
8. Turn everything out onto a platter and serve with clear broth.
9. If desired, strawberry jam or various roasted sausages can be added to the casserole.

title: Russkaia Faschia (Polish-Style Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter groats
- 2 whole eggs
- 24 tablespoons butter
- 4.5 teaspoons salt
- 1/2 liter boiling water

## Directions

1. In a casserole, add 1/2 liter of groats and 2 whole eggs and stir on a hot plate with a spoon until it is dry.
2. Add 24 tablespoons butter and 4.5 teaspoons salt to 1/2 liter of boiling water.
3. Loosen the groats with this and stir constantly for approximately 3 minutes over strong heat.
4. Remove from heat and let stand covered for 5 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, coat a ring mold generously with butter, press the groats into it, place it uncovered in the oven where it must bake slowly for 1 hour.
6. Before serving, turn the groats out onto a platter, pour some hot butter over it and garnish it like the Kugelhupf with bratwurst and fried goose liver.
7. Serve very hearty brown broth or sauerkraut soup with it.

title: Ränkeli Von Halsbutter Windmasse (Decorative Spirals from Egg White Paste)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 20 Deca vanilla sugar
- finely ground sugar mixed with finely cut almonds

## Directions

1. Mix the stiffly beaten egg whites thoroughly with 20 Deca vanilla sugar.
2. Fill this mixture into a pastry bag and pipe wreaths, pretzels, puffed shapes, or desired figures onto a baking sheet coated with wax or unsalted bacon fat.
3. Sprinkle with finely ground sugar mixed with finely cut almonds and dry in a moderately warm oven.

title: Rübeli-Perteile-Schöberl (Noodle Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- Flour
- 1 liter simmering milk
- 12 Deca butter
- 8 egg yolks
- Snow from 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from 3 eggs and flour and cut noodles from it
2. Cook the noodles in 1 liter simmering milk and let them cook through
3. Beat 12 Deca butter until fluffy and beat in 8 egg yolks
4. Add the prepared noodles, stir well together and lightly mix in the snow from 8 eggs
5. Further handling is as with Griesschöberl
6. Instead of noodles, one can also cut leeks

title: Rübiskel (Currants)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Large beautiful currants
- Sugar water (made as for morello cherries, recipe 1845)

## Directions

1. Remove the currants from their stems by pulling
2. Fill the preserving jars with them
3. Treat as the morello cherries (recipe 1845)

title: Sacher Torte (Sacher Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca chocolate
- 16 deca butter
- 16 deca finely ground sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan
- apricot jam, boiling hot
- chocolate glaze, warm or cold

## Directions

1. Soften 16 deca chocolate on a baking sheet in the oven
2. Beat 16 deca butter well
3. Add the softened chocolate and stir until cold
4. Gradually add 16 deca finely ground sugar with 6 egg yolks and stir
5. Gently fold in the snow (stiffly beaten egg whites) from 6 egg whites with 16 deca flour
6. Fill a cake pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour with this mixture
7. Bake for approximately 1 hour in the oven
8. Remove the ring and let the cake cool on a sieve
9. If desired, brush the cooled cake with boiling hot apricot jam
10. Once dry, cover with warm or cold chocolate glaze

title: Sachertorte (Sachertorte)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- jam
- Spanish windmill mass
- sugar
- optional: coffee essence, strawberry essence, or other essence

## Directions

1. Prepare a butter or beaten egg cake dough, roll out finger-thick, cut out round, fill into a buttered and floured cake form, and bake slowly in the oven
2. Remove the hoop from the form. After the cake has cooled, spread with jam, cover 3 fingers high with Spanish windmill mass
3. Decorate as desired using a paper cone with Spanish windmill mass, sprinkle with sugar, and let dry in an open oven
4. Instead of a cake made from butter dough or sugar dough, one can also use a Sachertorte, which however should only be finger-high
5. Optionally, add 1 teaspoon coffee essence, 1 teaspoon strawberry essence, or other essence to the Spanish windmill mass

title: Sachertorte (Sachertorte)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg butter
- 15 dkg grated chocolate
- 6 egg yolks
- 15 dkg sugar
- 6 egg whites
- 13½ dkg potato flour
- marmalade
- chocolate glaze

## Directions

1. Beat the butter for 15 minutes
2. Stir in the grated chocolate
3. Gradually mix in the egg yolks with the sugar
4. Beat the entire mixture for 45 minutes total
5. Gently fold in the egg whites with the potato flour
6. Fill the batter into the cake form and bake very slowly over weak heat
7. Bake the day before serving
8. The next day, cut the top level, turn it over, brush with marmalade and coat with chocolate glaze

title: Sago Auflauf (Sago Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 teaspoons weighed sago
- 1/2 liter boiling milk
- 6 egg yolks
- 12 teaspoons sugar
- 2 tablespoons breadcrumbs
- 5 egg whites
- butter for the pan
- flour for the pan

## Directions

1. Place 28 teaspoons of sago on a sieve, and once the water has drained well, whisk it into 1/2 liter of boiling milk with constant stirring and let it cook briefly
2. Set aside and let cool
3. Meanwhile, beat 6 egg yolks and 12 teaspoons of sugar for 1/2 hour, add the cooled but cooled sago, mix well and lightly fold in the snow from 5 egg whites along with 2 tablespoons of breadcrumbs
4. Fill a butter-greased, flour-dusted form with this mass and bake in the oven for 1 hour at low heat

title: Sago mit Milch (Sago with Milk)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sago, rinsed several times
- three seidels of boiled milk
- an arbitrary amount of sugar
- some cinnamon
- eggs

## Directions

1. Add sago which has been rinsed several times to three seidels of boiled milk together with an arbitrary amount of sugar and some cinnamon
2. When the sago has swollen well in the milk and cooked thick, bind it with eggs
3. Serve it cold or warm
4. Be careful that the milk does not curdle, but with this attention you will get a very good soup
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sago Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 50–70 dkg beef broth
- 1/2 kg sago

## Directions

1. Place 6 dkg sago in a pot with hand-high broth
2. Pour the remaining strained beef broth on top
3. Cook for 40 minutes
4. Serve

title: Sago-Schöberl (Sago Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 liters boiling milk
- 4 Deca washed sago
- 8 egg yolks
- Salt
- 4 spoons white bread crumbs
- Snow from 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Cook 4 Deca washed sago in 1 1/2 liters boiling milk, then pour it into a deep bowl and let it cool
2. Beat in 8 egg yolks, add some salt and 4 spoons of white bread crumbs and lightly mix in the snow from 8 eggs
3. Further handling is as with Griesschöberl

title: Sagossuppe mit rotem Wein (Sago Soup with Red Wine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deca sago
- water
- 1 1/4 liters water
- approximately 1/2 liter red wine
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook 16 deca sago in water, but not too soft
2. Strain off the water, pour 1 1/4 liters water to the sago according to the strength of the wine, and approximately 1/2 liter red wine
3. Add sugar so it is sweet enough
4. Let cook until the sago is transparent
5. Before serving some cinnamon can be added and stirred in well

title: Sagou-Suppe (Sago Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 spoonfuls fine brown sago
- 2 liters boiling water
- approximately 2 liters white or brown beef broth
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls cream
- lemon wedges

## Directions

1. Cook 8 spoonfuls fine brown sago in 2 liters boiling water for 1/2 hour until the grains are transparent
2. Pour the cooked sago through a sieve and rinse with cold water
3. Once the water has drained, add the sago to approximately 2 liters white or brown beef broth and let it cook for 1 hour
4. Put it in the soup pot and serve by beating 2 egg yolks and 2 spoonfuls cream with the sago soup and pour it in
5. Serve with lemon wedges
6. Alternatively, 8 spoonfuls unwashed sago can be added directly to boiling soup, cooked for 1 hour and served this way
7. Note that the fat must be completely removed from all these soups

title: Salamiwurst (Salami Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Pfund lean beef
- 3 Pfund bacon
- 3 Pfund pork
- 10 to 11 Loth salt
- 3 Loth cardamom
- 4 Loth ground pepper
- wide casings

## Directions

1. Remove all skin and connective tissue from the 5 pounds lean beef, 3 pounds bacon, and 3 pounds pork
2. Chop the meat very finely, the bacon somewhat more coarsely
3. Mix well together and let stand unsalted for 24 hours
4. Mix 10 to 11 Loth salt, 3 Loth cardamom, and 4 Loth ground pepper well into the chopped meat
5. Fill the mixture firmly into wide casings and tie well
6. Pierce the casings all around with needles
7. Hang in an air chimney or on an air loft, which should usually be done in a winter month
8. After hanging for 14 to 15 days, they can be stored for further use in a cool place
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Salat in der Form (Molded Salad à l'Italienne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Aspic, melted
- Red beets
- Truffles
- White from hard-boiled eggs
- Cooked green peas
- Cooked green beans
- White salad
- Fish varieties for garnish
- Colored butters
- Caviar
- Olives
- Fresh parsley or watercress

## Directions

1. Pour about finger-thick melted aspic into a mold brushed with oil
2. Layer with red beets, truffles, whites of hard-boiled eggs, green peas and green beans, carefully arranging with a larding needle on the cooled aspic without damaging the pattern
3. Cover carefully with melted aspic without disturbing the design
4. Pour another finger-thick layer of melted aspic and when completely cooled, add a layer of white salad
5. Repeat this layering process until the mold is filled
6. The top layer must be melted aspic
7. Cover the mold with a plate and cloth and let stand in ice for 2 hours
8. Remove the mold, dip briefly in boiling hot water, dry with a cloth, cover with an appropriate plate and invert quickly
9. Carefully remove the mold, garnish the molded salad with various fish varieties, colored butters, caviar, olives and decorate with fresh parsley or watercress
10. If the mold develops a hollow in the middle, fill the empty space with remaining white salad after turning out
11. Small molds can also be used for this salad

title: Salat von gekochtem Gemüse (Vegetable Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Various cooked vegetables
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Pepper
- Fresh parsley, finely chopped
- 2 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook various types of vegetables in salt water until soft
2. Drain the vegetables
3. Add vinegar, oil, some pepper and finely chopped fresh parsley
4. Mix everything well just before serving
5. Optional: mix 2 hard-boiled egg yolks with 4 tablespoons oil and necessary vinegar for better flavor

title: Salat von grünen Fisolen (Green Bean Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Young green beans
- Salt water
- Cold water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fresh green parsley, finely chopped
- Oil

## Directions

1. Trim the ends of the green beans and cut them into thin sticks
2. Cook in salt water
3. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to cold water
4. Drain well on a sieve
5. Place in a salad bowl with vinegar, some salt, a pinch of pepper, finely chopped green parsley and oil
6. Mix everything well together
7. Serve with various meat dishes
8. The beans can be broken into pieces or left whole, and parsley can be added or omitted as desired

title: Salat von Linsen (Lentil Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lentils, cooked until soft
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the lentils until soft
2. Prepare further as with the bean salad, but without the addition of horseradish

title: Salat von weißen Bohnen (White Bean Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- White beans (haricots), cooked in salt water
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Pepper
- Onion or garlic, optional
- Horseradish, grated

## Directions

1. Cook white beans in salt water
2. Season with vinegar, oil and some pepper
3. If desired, add some onion or garlic
4. Before serving, sprinkle the bean salad with some grated fresh horseradish
5. For 3/4 pound of bean salad, use 4 tablespoons of fine oil

title: Salepp-Wasser (Salep Water)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon salep
- 0.5 liter boiling water

## Directions

1. Put 1 teaspoon of salep in 0.5 liter of boiling water, let it come to a boil, let it stand for some time, and then strain it.

title: Salmi of Woodcock (Salmy von Schnepfen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks
- bacon strips
- bread (semmeln)
- lard (schmalz)
- good broth
- red wine
- woodcock entrails
- green parsley
- onion
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the woodcocks and remove and reserve the entrails
2. Wrap the woodcocks in bacon strips and roast them
3. Cut out the finest pieces from the breast
4. Pound the rest of the woodcocks together with bread cut into pieces that have been fried in lard in a large mortar until it becomes like a thick sauce
5. For two woodcocks use somewhat less than one whole bread loaf
6. Pass it through a fine hair sieve into a casserole
7. Fill it half with good broth and the other half with red wine
8. Cook until it becomes like a thick sauce
9. Meanwhile finely chop the woodcock entrails and add a little green parsley and very little finely cut onion
10. Sauté with butter
11. Cut large triangles from a bread loaf, fry in lard, and coat thickly with the sautéed mixture
12. Lay the breast pieces of woodcock on the bottom of the dish
13. Pour the salmi over it
14. Arrange the coated bread triangles in neat order around the rim of the dish
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Salmis de lievre a la Gelee (Jellied Hare Salmis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 skinned hare back with both skinned haunches
- 16 decagrams finely cut bacon
- 1 small onion
- some red wine
- broth
- 5 juniper berries
- 5 pieces each of various spices
- 1 cleaned goose liver
- butter
- aspic

## Directions

1. Place the hare back and haunches in a casserole with finely cut bacon and onion
2. Roast, pour over with some red wine and broth
3. Cook covered until tender
4. Remove the back and haunches, place on a board, and carefully bone the meat
5. Place the meat on a dish and cover with buttered paper so it forms a crust
6. Put the hare forelegs with the bones from back and haunches into the broth
7. Add 5 juniper berries and 5 pieces each of various spices
8. Cook covered until meat is tender
9. Add 1 cleaned goose liver and cook together covered for ½ hour
10. Strain the remaining broth
11. Pound the remainder finely in mortar and pass through sieve
12. Strain and stir well together
13. If the purée is thinner than needed, dilute with some of the broth
14. Place this purée (called Salmis) in a dish and set on ice
15. When serving, place finger-high finely chopped aspic in a dish
16. Add a portion of Salmis on top
17. Arrange pieces of back and haunches on this
18. Continue layering Salmis and meat until all is used
19. When arranged in a raised dome with Salmis as the final layer, smooth with a knife dipped in hot water and cover the surface with a piece of aspic down to the dish edge

title: Salmon with Ragout (Salm mit Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- truffles
- goose liver
- rooster combs
- calf's sweetbreads
- mushrooms
- onions
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- grated bacon
- egg yolk
- bacon
- ham
- wine
- strong braise
- piquant sauce
- fried bread slices
- crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Clean and dry the salmon thoroughly in the same manner as above
2. Stuff the belly with a good salpicon or ragout made from truffles, goose liver, rooster combs, calf's sweetbreads, and mushrooms all cut into dice, along with onions, parsley, salt, pepper, fine herbs, grated bacon and some egg yolk - this mixture must be well prepared beforehand
3. Once the skin is removed, lard the salmon with truffles, bacon and ham
4. Cook it slowly in a strong braise with wine
5. Skim off the fat
6. Dress and glaze the salmon
7. Serve a piquant sauce underneath
8. Garnish with fried bread slices, rooster combs, crayfish tails, truffles and other garnishes
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Salpicon (Salpicon Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- ox palate
- rooster combs
- beef sweetbreads
- crayfish tails
- truffles
- mushrooms
- cucumbers or small capers
- butter
- one tablespoon flour
- coulis or white sauce
- smoked tongue
- egg yolk
- lemon juice
- fine brown breadcrumbs
- crayfish butter

## Directions

1. Cook the ox palate, rooster combs and beef sweetbreads until tender in a good braise
2. Let cool
3. Cut into small dice, as well as the crayfish tails, truffles, mushrooms, and cucumbers or small capers
4. Sauté in butter
5. Dust with one tablespoon of flour
6. Add coulis or white sauce and let simmer briefly
7. Add some cooked smoked tongue
8. Season well
9. Bind with egg yolk and some lemon juice
10. Can also use leftover poultry
11. Fill into cream cups
12. Sprinkle fine brown breadcrumbs on top
13. Place a little crayfish butter on top
14. Allow to brown in a lukewarm oven
15. This ragout can be used for various dishes
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Salsify (Oyster Plant)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salsify roots
- Flour
- Water
- Vinegar
- Warm water
- Butter
- Good broth
- Salt

## Directions

1. These roots must be scrubbed very quickly, as they lose their color and turn red through prolonged handling in the hand; to prevent this, put some flour with water in a bowl, stir it well, add some vinegar and warm water, and as soon as a root is scrubbed, place it immediately in this mixture.
2. In 8 ounces of hot butter, let an equal amount of flour roast, then pour it with good broth, add the roots cut into desired pieces (which have been removed from the scrubbing solution and rinsed with fresh water), and let them cook gently with the necessary salt.

title: Salsify Another Way
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salsify roots
- Vinegar solution
- Cold water
- Thread
- Salt water
- Juice of one lemon
- Butter sauce
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. When the roots have been scrubbed as described above and placed whole in the vinegar solution, remove them, rinse them quickly in cold water, tie them together on both sides with thread (but not too tightly), and let them cook in salt water with the juice of a lemon.
2. When they are soft, remove them with a wooden spoon, place them on a platter, untie the threads, pour over them a butter sauce seasoned with some lemon juice, and serve without further additions.

title: Salt Sticks from Shortcrust Dough
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg flour
- 10 dkg butter
- a pinch of salt
- 1-2 spoonfuls cream
- egg yolk for brushing
- coarse salt or caraway seeds

## Directions

1. On the board rub 15 dkg flour with 10 dkg butter
2. Knead with a pinch of salt and 1-2 spoonfuls cream into dough
3. Form into finger-thick long rolls
4. Place on buttered, floured baking sheet
5. Brush with egg yolk
6. Sprinkle with coarse salt or caraway seeds
7. Bake until light brown using sharper heat

title: Salzbackerln Nockerl (Salt-Baked Noodles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter flour
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 2 whole eggs
- milk or cream as needed
- salted water
- 16 hectograms hot butter or fat

## Directions

1. In a deep bowl, combine 1 liter flour, 1 tablespoon salt, and 2 whole eggs
2. Stir with as much milk or cream as needed to make a smooth, pourable dough
3. Beat the dough until it falls from the spoon
4. Place dough on a board and strip pieces of desired size into boiling salted water using a knife kept in hot water
5. Cover and poach for 10-15 minutes
6. Pour into a colander, rinse with cold water, place in a pot containing 16 hectograms hot butter or fat, stir well, and use as a garnish when hot
7. Alternatively, pour 4 whole eggs beaten with 1/8 liter sour cream over the noodles in butter and braise covered for 10-15 minutes with frequent stirring
8. Heap onto a plate and serve as a flour dish with salad if desired

title: Salzburger Nockerl (Salzburg Dumplings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter flour
- a standard butter dough (No. 1052)
- 1 liter milk or cream
- 4 egg yolks
- 3/4 liter cold milk or cream
- 12 tablespoons powdered sugar
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Prepare a standard butter dough from 1/4 liter flour
2. Fill this dough into a pastry bag with a tube opening (not star-shaped)
3. 3/4 hour before serving, bring 1 liter milk or cream to a boil in a casserole
4. Apply gentle pressure to the pastry bag so the dough comes out of the tube, cutting it into pieces of desired size with a knife into the boiling milk
5. Let the dumplings boil covered, shaking the casserole frequently
6. Meanwhile, beat 4 egg yolks with 3/4 liter cold milk or cream and 12 tablespoons powdered sugar
7. Pour this mixture into the casserole with the dumplings, stir together, and place in the oven for several minutes
8. When the milk has cooked down sufficiently, arrange the dumplings on a platter, sprinkle with sugar, and serve

title: Salzburger Nockerl (Salzburg Soufflé Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dkg butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 7 dkg sugar
- Pinch of salt
- 4 egg whites
- 3 dkg flour
- 4 dkg butter
- 3 tablespoons milk
- Sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Cream together 6 dkg butter with 4 egg yolks, 7 dkg sugar, and a pinch of salt
2. Fold in 4 beaten egg whites along with 3 dkg flour
3. Heat 4 dkg butter and 3 tablespoons milk in an omelet pan
4. Pour batter in and smooth
5. Bake in the oven for 10-15 minutes until light brown
6. Using a spoon, cut out dumplings and place on a platter
7. Sprinkle with sugar

title: Salzburger Nockerl mit französischem Rum (Salzburg Dumplings with French Rum)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salzburg dumplings (No. 1328) finished cooking in milk
- 4 egg whites
- 16 tablespoons finely sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Arrange the cooked Salzburg dumplings on a platter
2. Evenly distribute the beaten egg whites from 4 eggs mixed with 16 tablespoons finely sifted sugar over the top
3. Sprinkle once more with finely sifted sugar
4. Bake in the oven for approximately 10-15 minutes with the oven open and serve immediately

title: Salzburger Nockerl mit Schmitterlinge (Salzburg Dumplings with Schmitterlinge)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salzburg dumplings cooked in milk
- Schmitterlinge (No. 1345)

## Directions

1. When the Salzburg dumplings are finished cooking in milk, line the bottom of a platter with Schmitterlinge
2. Add a portion of the dumplings, layer with more Schmitterlinge, and continue alternating until all dumplings are used
3. Sprinkle with finely sifted sugar and serve

title: Salzburger Nockerl mit Schnee (Salzburg Dumplings with Meringue)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salzburg dumplings (No. 1328)
- 4 egg whites
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. After the Salzburg dumplings have been prepared in milk and placed in the oven, lightly fold in the beaten egg whites from 4 eggs
2. Arrange the dumplings on a platter and sprinkle with sugar
3. Bake for 10 minutes in the open oven

title: Salzkartoffeln (Boiled Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kg potatoes
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes, quarter them, place in cold salt water, cook for 20–30 minutes.
2. Drain and serve on a round dish.

title: Salzsticks von Wasserteig zum Tee (Salted Sticks of Water Dough for Tea)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/8 liter cold water
- 4 Deca butter
- 16 Deca flour

## Directions

1. Place water and butter in a saucepan and bring to a boil
2. While stirring constantly, add flour gradually and stir over the fire until the dough is dry and pulls away from the spoon and pan
3. Spread the dough on a flour-dusted board and let cool covered
4. Roll the dough knife-back-thick, cut out with desired cutters, and place on a buttered baking sheet
5. Make light cuts with a knife, brush with beaten egg, sprinkle with finely crushed salt
6. Bake until golden in the oven
7. Remove from sheet with a knife and serve with tea

title: Sand Torte (Sand Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg butter
- 21 dkg sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon rum
- 6 stiff egg whites
- 21 dkg potato flour

## Directions

1. Beat 21 dkg butter for 20 minutes
2. Gradually stir in 21 dkg sugar and 6 egg yolks and beat again for 20 minutes
3. Mix in 1 tablespoon rum and 6 stiff egg whites together with 21 dkg potato flour
4. Fill into the form and bake very slowly
5. Fill with jam, dust with sugar, or spread jam on top and frost white

title: Sandtorte (Sand Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound flour
- 1 pound sugar
- zest of one lemon
- 15 eggs

## Directions

1. First stir butter to foam
2. Add one egg yolk after another
3. Mix sugar and flour together
4. Finally fold in the peaks of 3 eggs
5. Bake the tart slowly in a form that has a hole in the middle
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sandtorte (Sand Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg butter
- 21 dkg sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 spoon rum
- 6 stiff egg whites
- 21 dkg potato flour
- marmalade
- powdered sugar
- white icing

## Directions

1. Beat 21 dkg butter for 20 minutes.
2. Gradually stir in 21 dkg sugar and 6 egg yolks and beat again for 20 minutes.
3. Mix in 1 spoon rum and 6 stiff egg whites together with 21 dkg potato flour.
4. Fill the mass into the form and bake very slowly.
5. Fill with marmalade, dust with powdered sugar or brush the top with marmalade and white ice.

title: Sandtörtchen (Sand Tarts)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fresh butter
- 12 egg yolks
- zest of one lemon
- 1 pound finely sifted sugar
- 12 egg whites
- 1 pound flour

## Directions

1. Melt 1 pound fresh butter
2. Gradually stir in 12 egg yolks, lemon zest, and 1 pound finely sifted sugar
3. Whip the whites of the 12 eggs in a shallow bowl to stiff peaks
4. Mix the egg white into the mass
5. Finally stir in 1 pound flour by the spoonful
6. Brush small tin forms with melted butter
7. Fill them half-full with the mass
8. Place on a baking sheet
9. Bake for half an hour
10. Each batch yields 60 to 70 tarts of ordinary size
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sapajeau (Citron Egg Punch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lemons
- sugar
- 6 whole eggs
- 0.5 pound pounded sugar
- 1 bottle Rhenish wine or good Austrian wine

## Directions

1. Rub the zest from 3 lemons on sugar
2. Place the zest, lemon juice, 6 whole eggs and 0.5 pound pounded sugar in a casserole
3. Gradually stir in 1 bottle of Rhenish wine or good Austrian wine
4. Beat with a whisk over the fire until boiling hot and risen
5. Fill into chocolate cups
6. Serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sardellen (Anchovies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salted anchovies
- Water
- Vinegar
- Oil

## Directions

1. Gently wash the salted anchovies in lukewarm water
2. Remove the substances found on the back
3. Separate the anchovies and remove the bones
4. Coil them around the finger
5. Place on a designated plate or serving bowl
6. Pour vinegar and oil over them
7. Serve to table

title: Sardellen in Muſcheln (Sardines in Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 sardines
- 5 decagrams Parmesan cheese
- 6 decagrams butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- 2 egg whites
- 3 decagrams grated cheese
- breadcrumbs
- hot butter

## Directions

1. Create a base by beating 6 decagrams butter until creamy
2. Stir in 2 egg yolks, the boned and passed sardines, a pinch of salt and pepper, and 3 decagrams grated cheese
3. Fold in 2 stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Fill the mixture into buttered shells (not too generously, as the mixture rises)
5. Scatter cheese and breadcrumbs on top
6. Drizzle with hot butter
7. Place the shells on a baking sheet and gratinée in the oven for 8-10 minutes
8. Serve on a plate with a folded napkin
9. Do not open the oven during baking, as the mixture will collapse

title: Sardellen-Farce (Anchovy Forcemeat)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 drachm salted anchovies, cleaned and boned
- 16 drachm butter
- bread dough as specified for Capon Forcemeat
- salt and pepper as needed
- nutmeg
- 1 bay leaf
- chervil
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned anchovies into small dice
2. Place in a casserole containing 16 drachm butter and stew for a short time
3. Set aside to cool
4. Meanwhile, prepare a bread dough of the same consistency as for Capon Forcemeat
5. Once completely cooled, place the dough with the anchovies and butter in a mortar
6. Pound finely together with salt, pepper, nutmeg, 1 bay leaf, chervil, 3 egg yolks, and 1 whole egg
7. Pass through a sieve
8. Use as forcemeat for patties or loaf molds

title: Sardellen-Sauce (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pieces cleaned anchovies
- Some onion
- Green parsley
- 8 tablespoons butter or lard
- Equal amount of flour
- 1/2 liter soup
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut 4 pieces cleaned anchovies, some onion and green parsley finely
2. Make a dark roux from 8 tablespoons butter or lard and equal amount of flour
3. Add the finely chopped herbs, pour 1/2 liter soup over
4. Salt, simmer well and pass through a sieve

title: Sardellen-Sauce (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pieces cleaned anchovies
- some parsley
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 8 deca bread crusts, soaked in water and pressed out
- vinegar
- water
- 2 tablespoons oil
- pinch of pepper
- salt
- 1/2 coffee spoon sugar as desired

## Directions

1. Pound together the cleaned anchovies, parsley, hard-boiled egg yolks, and bread soaked in water and pressed out
2. Pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
3. Stir with some vinegar and water to make a thick sauce so that an inverted spoon stays covered
4. Add 2 tablespoons oil, a pinch of pepper, and some salt
5. Mix well
6. If desired, add 1/2 coffee spoon sugar before serving
7. You can also add some onion to the anchovies

title: Sardellenbutter (Anchovy Butter)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1/4 pound good anchovies, cleaned and washed

## Directions

1. Pound together 1 pound butter with 1/4 pound cleaned and washed good anchovies
2. Press the pounded butter through a fine hair sieve
3. Store for later use in sauces and forcemeats
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sardellenbutter (Anchovy Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 anchovies
- 8-10 dkg butter

## Directions

1. Knead the anchovies with butter on a cutting board with a knife
2. Place in a small pot and bring to a boil

title: Sardellenſalat (Anchovy Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Anchovies
- Wine
- Lemon
- Flowers
- Citrus fruits (lemons and oranges)
- Orange blossoms
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Pepper
- Preserved orange blossoms

## Directions

1. First soak the anchovies, after removing the bones, in wine for one hour
2. Place a small celery in the middle of a serving dish with an artfully cut lemon on top
3. Decorate it with flowers, encircle the lemon with anchovies winding around it, and arrange the remaining anchovies in a circle around it, with the tails lying on the edge of the dish
4. Line the dish edge with lemons or orange blossoms
5. Mix together vinegar, oil and some pepper and pour over the anchovies
6. Squeeze lemon juice over them, arrange thinly sliced lemon peel wound around the dish
7. Mix them alternately with sliced oranges and lemons, arrange them at the edge of the dish
8. Sprinkle the anchovies with flowerwork and preserved orange blossoms
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sardinen (Sardines)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Canned sardines in tin
- Lemon wedges
- Green parsley
- Serviette (napkin)

## Directions

1. Open the tin box containing the sardines with a sharp knife (or sardine opener if available), placing the tip of the knife along the edge of the lid, striking with a hammer and cutting through both narrow and one broad side
2. If lacking the strength to cut through with the knife, place the knife blade at the edge of the lid and tap carefully with a hammer on the back of the knife
3. Bend the opened lid backwards
4. Place the tin in a bowl covered with a serviette and garnish with lemon wedges
5. Alternatively, place a bouquet of green parsley in the center of a serving bowl, remove the sardines from the tin with a fork, arrange them so they lie on their belly with the head side touching the green parsley, fill the empty space between them with halved lemon slices, and cover everything with green parsley

title: Sauce Allemande (German Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Mushrooms
- Shallots
- Finely chopped anchovies
- Salt
- Pepper
- Good bouillon
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Mix butter and flour together well.
2. Add mushrooms, shallots, finely chopped anchovies, salt, and pepper.
3. Pass it over heat.
4. Let it cook a little with good bouillon.
5. When serving, stir in some egg yolks.
6. Press in lemon juice.
7. Pass the sauce through a fine sieve.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauce au Patriarche (Patriarchal Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken, cut into small pieces
- A piece of lean ham
- Some slices of veal
- Finely chopped bone marrow
- One cup of bouillon
- One glass of strong white wine
- A little coulis
- Finely chopped shallots
- Mushrooms
- A bundle of dried herbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Place the chicken, ham, veal, bone marrow, and one cup of bouillon in a casserole over moderate heat.
2. Once it has begun to render, pour in a glass of strong white wine, a little coulis, and the remaining bouillon.
3. Add finely chopped shallots, mushrooms, and a bundle of dried herbs.
4. Let the sauce reduce to half its volume.
5. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
6. Remove the various solids that were used to make the sauce.
7. Pass the sauce through a fine sieve.
8. Skim off the fat and foam, and use as desired.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauce aux Betteraves (Beet Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece cooked or roasted red beet
- 8 tablespoons butter
- Stock
- Red wine
- Nutmeg
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel 1 piece of cooked or roasted red beet and cut it very finely with a vegetable knife
2. Make a butter sauce from 8 tablespoons butter as previously described
3. Stir in the finely cut beets, necessary stock, some red wine, and nutmeg
4. Let it cook well with a little sugar, strain it through a sieve, and bring to boil once more

title: Sauce aux Carottes (Carrot Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter or lard
- 1 tablespoon crushed sugar
- 2-3 scraped, finely grated yellow carrots
- Stock
- 1/2 teaspoon finely cut green parsley
- Crushed nutmeg

## Directions

1. To 8 tablespoons butter or lard, add 1 tablespoon crushed sugar
2. When this is brown, add about 2-3 scraped, finely grated yellow carrots and let them braise
3. Dust the braised carrots with the necessary flour, pour with 1/4 liter stock
4. Add 1/2 teaspoon finely cut green parsley and some crushed nutmeg and bring to boil

title: Sauce aux Cornichons (Gherkin Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Some onion
- Green parsley
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 large peeled, diced gherkin
- Stock
- 6 tablespoons butter
- 6 tablespoons flour
- 1/2 teaspoon finely cut dill herb
- 1/4 liter cream
- Salt

## Directions

1. Some onion and green parsley are roasted in 2 tablespoons butter and 1 large, peeled, diced gherkin is braised with some stock until the gherkin pieces are tender
2. A roux is made from 6 tablespoons butter and the same amount of flour, the braised gherkins are cooked well with 1/2 teaspoon finely cut dill herb, 1/4 liter cream, and some salt
3. Stir together and, if desired, strain

title: Sauce aux Crevettes (Crayfish Butter Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 crayfish
- Butter
- Crayfish meat and claws
- Butter sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare a crayfish butter from 8 crayfish
2. Shortly before serving, add the extracted meat and claws to the cooked, strained butter sauce
3. Stir together and bring to boil once

title: Sauce aux Câpres (Caper Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons flour
- 6 tablespoons finely cut capers
- 1 crushed shallot or garlic
- Stock
- Vinegar or wine
- Hay
- 1/2 bay leaf
- 1 bunch thyme

## Directions

1. Place 8 tablespoons butter in a cassette dish and let it become hot, add 8 tablespoons flour
2. Into this roux, add 6 tablespoons finely cut capers, 1 crushed shallot or garlic, and let these roast slightly
3. Pour in with a little stock and the necessary vinegar or wine, stirring constantly, and bring to boil
4. Skim off the fat cleanly
5. Let hay, 1/2 bay leaf, and 1 bunch of thyme cook slowly with occasional stirring for about 1/2 hour
6. Strain the sauce through a sieve, let it become hot once more, and serve

title: Sauce aux Câpres, Alternative Method (Caper Sauce, Alternative)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons flour
- Stock
- Wine vinegar
- 1/4 liter cream
- 6 tablespoons whole capers

## Directions

1. Make a brown butter sauce from 8 tablespoons butter
2. Pour it with some stock and wine vinegar and let cook
3. Add 1/4 liter cream and strain the sauce over 6 tablespoons whole capers
4. Let boil once more

title: Sauce aux Tomates (Tomato Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 beautiful tomatoes
- 3 sprigs parsley
- 1/2 liter cold water
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons flour
- 1/4 liter wine
- Stock
- Sugar
- Salt
- Or 3/4 liter preserves instead of fresh tomatoes
- Vinegar (alternative to wine)

## Directions

1. 4 beautiful tomatoes are washed, broken apart from each other, placed with 3 sprigs parsley in a cassette dish
2. Poured with 1/2 liter cold water and braised covered with 2 tablespoons butter
3. Meanwhile, make a butter sauce from 8 tablespoons butter and 8 tablespoons flour
4. Pour it with the braised tomatoes, 1/4 liter wine, and as much stock as needed
5. Add the necessary sugar and some salt, let boil, and strain
6. If using about 3/4 liter preserves instead of fresh tomatoes, place it in a butter sauce of 8 tablespoons butter and the same amount of flour
7. Add 1/4 liter white wine and the necessary stock, sugar, and some salt, stir, and boil well
8. Instead of wine, one can also give the necessary acidity with vinegar, or it can also remain natural

title: Sauce d'Amandes au Raifort (Almond or Milk-Almond Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
- About 3 tablespoons flour
- 3/4 liter boiling milk
- 8 tablespoons butter
- Sugar to taste
- 2 tablespoons grated horseradish
- 4 peeled almonds, moistened with milk, finely crushed

## Directions

1. 1/4 liter lukewarm milk and about 3 tablespoons flour are stirred smooth, then gradually added to 3/4 liter boiling milk with constant stirring
2. Add 8 tablespoons butter, sugar to taste, and 2 tablespoons grated horseradish and let everything boil well together
3. For the preparation of almond sauce, one must use 1 tablespoon less flour and replace it with 4 peeled almonds, moistened with milk and then finely crushed
4. Proceed further as indicated above
5. The horseradish goes in at the end and must not boil

title: Sauce Lyonaise (Lyonnaise Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fifteen onions, sliced
- Half a pound of butter
- Flour
- Broth (Schü)
- A little sugar
- Some wine
- Nutmeg
- Wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Sauté the sliced onions in butter until they are brown and completely soft.
2. Add flour.
3. Fill the casserole with broth.
4. Add a little sugar, some wine, nutmeg, and wine vinegar.
5. Stir everything together well.
6. Cook until thick.
7. Finally, strain the sauce through a fine cloth.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauce Lyonaise (Lyonnaise Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 onions
- 0.5 pound butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Slice 15 onions.
