2. Sweat in 0.5 pound butter until they are brown and completely soft.
3. Add flour on top.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauce mit Frischen Trüffeln (Sauce with Fresh Truffles)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh truffles
- Onions, cut into cubes
- Butter
- Flour
- Salt
- Good broth (Schü)
- Wine
- Poultry meat
- Finely chopped truffles

## Directions

1. Wash the truffles carefully.
2. Sauté the truffle skins with diced onions in butter.
3. Stir in a little flour and salt.
4. Fill with good broth and cook together.
5. Cut the truffles themselves into slices.
6. Fill with broth and a glass of wine.
7. Cook properly and add the first sauce.
8. Make a farce from poultry and finely chopped truffles.
9. Form small dumplings from the farce and place them in the sauce.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauce nonpareille
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 glass wine vinegar
- Fine shallots
- Pepper
- Butter
- Brown coulis
- Salt
- Small cucumbers
- Cooked egg whites
- Cooked yellow turnips
- Cleaned anchovies
- Capers

## Directions

1. In a casserole, briefly cook a glass of wine vinegar with fine shallots, pepper, and a small piece of butter.
2. Add several spoonfuls of brown, properly salted coulis.
3. Let it cook clear and skim well.
4. Prepare separately: small cucumbers, the whites of cooked eggs, cooked yellow turnips, and cleaned anchovies cut into small dice.
5. When serving, mix all these with a spoonful of fine capers into the hot sauce.
6. The sauce must not boil after this.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauce Poivrade (Pepper Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter sauce
- 1 pinch of pepper

## Directions

1. Add 1 pinch of pepper to the cooked butter sauce
2. Let it boil several times and strain it through a sieve

title: Sauce Rémoulade Chaude (Hot Mustard Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons French mustard
- 1/4 liter cold white beef stock
- 1 strong pinch of salt
- Juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1 tablespoon rosehip marmalade (optional)

## Directions

1. Into a deep cassette dish or snowball whisk, place 8 tablespoons butter, 6 egg yolks, 6 tablespoons French mustard, 1/4 liter cold white beef stock, and 1 strong pinch of salt
2. Whisk over the fire with the whisk and bring to the proper heat, taking special care that it does not boil
3. Slowly drizzle in the juice of 1/2 lemon
4. This sauce is served with all kinds of meat dishes, for roasting, game birds and the like
5. When desired, add 1 tablespoon rosehip marmalade

title: Sauce Tartare
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deca butter
- 5 sprigs parsley
- equal amount tarragon leaves
- some white pepper
- some cayenne pepper
- 3 pieces anchovies
- 5 juniper berries
- 1 tablespoon capers
- 1 handful shallot
- 1 piece starfruit
- 1 piece pickled cucumber
- 3 raw egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons French mustard
- 1 tablespoon rose hip marmalade as desired

## Directions

1. Pound finely in a mortar: 4 deca butter, 5 sprigs parsley, tarragon leaves, white pepper, cayenne pepper, 3 pieces anchovies, 5 juniper berries, 1 tablespoon capers, 1 handful shallot, 1 piece starfruit, and 1 piece pickled cucumber
2. Pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
3. Mix the strained mixture with mayonnaise made from 3 raw egg yolks, 2 tablespoons French mustard, and optionally 1 tablespoon rose hip marmalade
4. Mix well together

title: Sauce Tartare (Tartare Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces butter
- 5 sprigs parsley
- the same amount of tarragon leaves
- some white pepper and some cayenne pepper
- 3 pieces capers
- 5 juniper berries
- 1 tablespoon capers
- 1 shallot
- 1 piece macaroni and 1 piece pickled cucumber

## Directions

1. Pound the capers, juniper berries, macaroni, and pickled cucumber finely in a mortar and pass through a sieve into a deep bowl
2. Mix this passed mixture with a mayonnaise prepared from 3 raw egg yolks (see there) and with 2 tablespoons French mustard and if desired 1 tablespoon peppery marmalade, stirring well
3. Except for the juniper berries, the ingredients mentioned above can instead of being pounded be cut very finely, in which case no butter is used

title: Sauce tartare (Tartare Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pickled cucumber
- 2 anchovies
- 4 h capers
- 6 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls Betramesfig vinegar
- 1 spoonful lemon juice
- 1 spoonful water
- salt
- mustard
- several spoonfuls oil
- parsley
- onion
- shallots
- chervil herb

## Directions

1. In a snow kettle, beat 6 egg yolks with 2 spoonfuls Betramesfig vinegar, 1 spoonful lemon juice, and 1 spoonful water on the hot stove plate until thick.
2. Let them cool, then mix them with some salt, mustard, and several spoonfuls of oil.
3. Mix in parsley, onion, shallots, chervil herb, ½ pickled cucumber, the deboned anchovies, and the capers, all finely chopped.
4. The sauce can be served with all cold fish and cold meat.

title: Sauce à l'Anglaise (English Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound ham
- Onions
- Roots
- Herbs
- Several cloves
- Butter
- Flour
- 0.5 measure bouillon
- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- 8 chopped anchovies
- Estragon (tarragon)
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Sweat 0.5 pound ham, onions, roots, herbs, and several cloves in butter.
2. Stir in some flour.
3. Fill the casserole with 0.5 measure of bouillon.
4. Pound the yolks from 4 hard-boiled eggs and add them with 8 chopped anchovies to the sauce.
5. Cook together.
6. Strain through cheesecloth.
7. Stir in coarsely chopped boiled tarragon and lemon juice.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauce à l'aurore (Aurora Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- White coulis
- Lemon juice
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- 4 hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Place some white coulis in a casserole.
2. Add the juice of 1 lemon, salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
3. Let it cook and skim well.
4. Strain the yolks from 4 hard-boiled, peeled eggs through a wire sieve so they look like fine noodles.
5. Add to the sauce when serving.
6. These egg yolks must not be stirred further or allowed to cook again.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauce à la Française (French Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons fat
- 1/2 liter cream
- 3 egg yolks
- 8 tablespoons tarragon butter
- 1 piece crushed garlic
- Juice of 1 lemon
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. In a cassette dish, place 4 tablespoons fat and 1/2 liter cream and stir together into a smooth batter
2. Add 3 egg yolks, 8 tablespoons tarragon butter, 1 piece crushed garlic, and the juice of 1 lemon
3. Season and whisk over the fire with the whisk for as long as it takes to combine well (but it must not boil)
4. If the sauce is too thick, thin it with white stock and then serve with poultry, fish, or game

title: Sauce à la Moscou (Moscow Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Shallots
- Crayfish butter
- Coulis
- Thick sour cream
- Lemon juice
- Ordinary butter (optional)

## Directions

1. Sauté ham and shallots in crayfish butter.
2. Fill with coulis and stir in thick sour cream.
3. Once everything is cooked, strain through a fine cloth.
4. Press lemon juice into the sauce.
5. This sauce can also be made with ordinary butter.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauce à la Moscou (Moscow Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Shallots
- Crayfish butter
- Coulis
- Thick sour cream
- Lemon juice
- Ordinary butter (alternative)

## Directions

1. Sweat ham and shallots in crayfish butter.
2. Fill with coulis.
3. Stir in thick sour cream.
4. Once everything is cooked, strain through cheesecloth.
5. Add lemon juice.
6. Can also be made with ordinary butter instead of crayfish butter.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Saucen von getrocknetem Obst (Dried Fruit Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter dried fruit
- 6 deca finely cut black bread
- 1/2 liter broth
- 1/4 liter red or white wine
- nutmeg as desired

## Directions

1. Crush 1/4 liter dried fruit
2. Put the crushed fruit in a casserole
3. Add 6 deca finely cut black bread
4. Pour 1/2 liter broth and 1/4 liter red or white wine
5. Season to taste
6. If desired, add 1 pinch of nutmeg
7. Let it cook well, stir and strain it
8. Serve this sauce with roasted game or similar meat dishes

title: Sauer-Molke (Sour Whey)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter cold milk
- Juice from 0.5 lemon or 1 tablespoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Put the juice from 0.5 lemon or 1 tablespoon of vinegar into 1 liter of cold milk.
2. Let it become hot to the boiling point and proceed further as described above.

title: Sauerampfer mit Fricandeaux (Sorrel with Fricandeau)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- sorrel
- butter or fat
- good meat soup
- salt
- veal shank
- fine bacon
- glace

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the sorrel thoroughly
2. Cut it finely with a chopping knife
3. Set butter or fat on heat, braise it like spinach
4. Add good meat soup and salt
5. Cut the fricandeau from a veal shank into two or three pieces: one from the nut, one from the kaiserbein, and one from the top shell
6. The fricandeau should be about one hand's breadth wide and somewhat elongated
7. Remove the skin and all nerves properly
8. Blanch briefly beforehand so they are easier to lard
9. Lard them on both sides with fine bacon
10. Lay these fricandeau in a braise
11. Let them cook until tender and soft
12. Before serving, turn them in glace so they become brown and get a beautiful shine
13. Lay them properly on the arranged sorrel
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauerampfer-Puree (Purée à l'oseille)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ungefähr 1/4 gepuffter Sauerampfer
- Salzwasser
- Butter-Sauce
- Salz
- Muskatnuss
- 1/4 Liter Ober

## Directions

1. ungefähr 1/4 gepuffter Sauerampfer wird in Salzwasser weich gekocht
2. vom Wasser abgesetzt und gut ausgedrückt
3. fein geschnitten und geröstet
4. zu dem Gerösteten gibt man eine dicke Butter-Sauce, etwas Salz und Muskatnuss
5. es wird von Zeit zu Zeit mit 1/4 Liter Ober aufgegossen
6. alles wird gut untereinander gerührt, läßt es etwas kochen und richtet es an

title: Sauerampfers Sauce (Sorrel Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons hot butter
- 1 plate full of washed sorrel leaves
- 8 tablespoons flour
- 1/4 liter soup
- Equal amount of sour cream

## Directions

1. Add 1 plate full of washed sorrel leaves to 8 tablespoons hot butter
2. Cover and braise until soft
3. Dust with 8 tablespoons flour, pour with 1/4 liter soup and equal amount of sour cream
4. Let it simmer and pass through a sieve
5. One can also cut raw sorrel leaves finely, but then do not pass the sauce through a sieve
6. If necessary, some tablespoons of sour cream can be added

title: Sauerampfersauce (Sorrel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 h sorrel
- 4 dkg fat
- 5-6 tablespoons cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and finely chop the sorrel
2. Cook in salted water for 10 minutes
3. Make a light roux from the fat, add the sorrel
4. Add the cream, cook together for 20 minutes

title: Sauerbeers (Barberry) Sulz (Barberry Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 decagrams sugar
- 1/4 liter very ripe, picked, cleaned barberries
- 1 finger-length piece of lemon peel
- 7 decagrams isinglass, hot and dissolved
- 1/2 liter white wine

## Directions

1. Clarify 40 decagrams sugar and add to it the barberries and lemon peel
2. Bring to a boil and strain through cloth
3. Pour the strained liquid to 7 decagrams of hot dissolved isinglass
4. Boil together for 10 minutes
5. Strain immediately through fine, thin linen cloth into a deep bowl
6. Add 1/2 liter white wine to this liquid, so that 1 liter of liquid results in total
7. Stir and proceed further as for Orange Sulz (see recipe 1660)

title: Sauerfraut mit Ragout (Sauerkraut with Ragout)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping plate of sauerkraut
- approximately 1 liter fatty broth
- approximately 10 tablespoons fat
- 1 kilo prepared fish or pork
- 2 crushed shallots or 1 piece garlic
- 1 teaspoon finely cut green parsley
- 8 tablespoons fat
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1/4 liter white wine
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter cream
- salt
- a little lemon juice if necessary
- bread crumbs
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 1/6 liters sour cream

## Directions

1. Cook 1 heaping plate of sauerkraut with approximately 1 liter fatty broth and approximately 10 tablespoons fat gently in a covered pot
2. Remove the bones from 1 kilo prepared fish or pork, break into pieces
3. Let 2 crushed shallots or 1 piece garlic with 1 teaspoon finely cut green parsley roast in 8 tablespoons fat, add 2 tablespoons flour to it, pour it with 1/4 liter white wine and let it cook well
4. In a pot, put 4 egg yolks and 1/4 liter cream, bubble together and pour the cooked sauce to the cream
5. Add the fish or meat and the sauce together in a casserole with some salt and, if necessary, a little lemon juice, stir well together and let it come to a boil
6. If there is still liquid present, it must be strained from the sauerkraut
7. Place a portion of the sauerkraut in a deep bowl, make a depression and place the fish ragout in it, fold the sauerkraut lightly over it and sprinkle with bread crumbs, pour 2 egg yolks mixed with 1 1/6 liters sour cream over it and place in the open oven for 1/3 to 3/4 hours
8. Instead of fish, one can use roasted pork, cooked ham, or roasted meat
9. The procedure remains the same
10. If desired, sprinkle with finely cut chives when serving

title: Sauerkraut mit Fasan (Sauerkraut with Pheasant)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- good sauerkraut
- butter or roasting fat
- flour
- meat broth
- onion
- pheasant

## Directions

1. Take good sauerkraut, press out the water, and braise it well in butter or roasting fat.
2. Dust it lightly with flour and add meat broth and finely cut onion, and let it cook well together.
3. Roast one or more pheasants completely in their juices.
4. Add them to the sauerkraut with the roasting fat, let it come to a good boil, plate it, and serve together with the sauerkraut.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sauerkraut Soup (Soupe à la choucroute)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter sauerkraut
- 10 ounces fat
- 1/8 liter flour
- 1 finely sliced onion
- 1 liter good beef soup
- approximately 1 liter good soup

## Directions

1. Cook 1 liter sauerkraut in water until soft
2. Place 10 ounces fat in a casserole
3. Add 1/8 liter flour and 1 finely sliced onion and roast the flour with the onion
4. Pour in 1 liter of good beef soup, stirring constantly to prevent lumps from forming
5. Strain the cooked sauerkraut and pass the soup through a sieve
6. Add the sauerkraut to the soup and pour in approximately 1 liter of good soup, mix very well together

title: Sauerkraut Viennese Style (Choucroute à la viennoise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large liter of sauerkraut
- 2 liters cold water
- 10 deciliters hot fat
- 1 head onion finely chopped
- 10 deciliters flour
- 1 ladleful white beef broth

## Directions

1. Place 1 large liter of sauerkraut in cold water and cook very soft uncovered
2. In 10 deciliters hot fat, let 1 finely chopped head of onion brown slightly, but it must not become dark
3. Add approximately 10 deciliters flour and brown it with frequent stirring until it has a yellow color
4. Pour the roux with 1 ladleful of white beef broth over the sauerkraut with constant stirring
5. Pass the sauerkraut through a sieve and add to the roux as much as is necessary

title: Sauerkraut with Veal Kidneys Steamed (Sauerkraut mit Kalbsnieren gedämpft)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauerkraut
- Roasted veal kidneys
- Butter
- Sour cream (Schmette)

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauerkraut as you ordinarily serve it at table.
2. Meanwhile, chop the roasted veal kidneys finely, brush a dish with butter, line it thinly with the chopped kidneys, place a layer of sauerkraut over it, then kidneys again, and continue alternating; however, the last layer must be sauerkraut.
3. Pour sour cream over this, cover the dish, set it over a gentle charcoal fire, and let the sauerkraut warm for a few minutes, after which it can be served to table.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Saure Rahm-Sauce (Sour Cream Sauce: Sauce à la crème aigre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablespoons fine flour
- 1/2 liter cream
- 10 tablespoons butter
- 1 knife point salt
- some pepper
- nutmeg
- 8 tablespoons butter (additional)
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Put 6 tablespoons fine flour in a saucepan and gradually stir in 1/2 liter cream
2. Add 10 tablespoons butter, 1 knife point salt, some pepper, and nutmeg
3. Mix everything well together and stir it on the fire until the sauce forms
4. Remove it from the fire and add 8 tablespoons butter in small pieces and the juice of 1 lemon
5. Stir it on the hot plate until the sauce has a foamy appearance, being careful not to let it boil
6. Serve it hot with boiled chicken and root vegetables

title: Saure Wiitten (Pickled Turnips)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- white turnips
- salt
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Peel and wash the white turnips
2. Cut noodle-like, as fine as desired, on a turnip slicer or tooth scraper
3. Place in a tub the same way as cabbage
4. Sprinkle with some salt and caraway seeds
5. Stamp down, weight, and treat further like cabbage

title: Sautéed Calf Brains (Friture de cervelle de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- One cleaned calf brain, freed of skin, cooked in salted water
- Flour
- Beaten eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Very hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Once the calf brain is completely cooked, divide it in half and slice each half lengthwise
2. Dip the pieces in flour, then in beaten eggs, then coat with breadcrumbs (panate)
3. Shortly before serving, fry them in very hot fat

title: Sautéed Kidneys (Rognons de veau sautés)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Fat
- Onion, cut into small pieces
- Kidneys, skinned and finely sliced
- Flour
- Salt and pepper
- Soup or white wine
- Marjoram

## Directions

1. Put fat and finely cut onion in a saucepan and roast
2. Cut skinned kidneys into fine slices and add to the roasted onion
3. Dust with a little flour, salt and pepper
4. Stir well together, roast for 5 to 10 minutes
5. Pour in some soup or white wine
6. Add a pinch of marjoram, stir everything well together
7. Serve in a platter

title: Sautéed Pheasant Breast Fillets (Filets de faisan sauté)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant, plucked and singed
- Butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. After plucking and singeing the pheasant, cut the skin back at the neck, remove the crop and esophagus with a cloth, and lift away the skin over the breast
2. Make an incision at the top just below the wings
3. Carefully detach the breast on each side from the breastbone and skeleton
4. Carefully remove the strong sinew connecting the two parts without damaging the underbreasts
5. Smooth the underbreasts with a knife and place on a plate
6. From the large breasts, remove the fine skin attached with a knife and smooth these as well
7. Salt both large and underbreasts, place side by side in a casserole, cover with a paper sheet buttered and shaped to fit the casserole, and set aside
8. A few minutes before cooking, place the casserole over the fire and sauté the breasts on both sides over moderate heat for a few minutes

title: Schaiden mit Marzipan (Shad with Marzipan)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- shad, cut into pieces, salted and fried
- heavy cream
- capers
- potatoes

## Directions

1. The shad cut into pieces, salted and fried is poured over with heavy cream, browned, some capers are added and potato purée or the like is served with it.

title: Schaiden, gekocht (Poached Shad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- scheiden (shad)
- butter
- salt water

## Directions

1. If you have a small shad, treat it like pike or perch. To poach it, cut it into thumb-thick pieces of arbitrary size. Fold these pieces, lay them on the bottom of a fish pan and with this into the pot; or, in the absence of that, put the fish pieces in a pan and pour boiling salt water over them, place a buttered piece of paper and a lid on top and let them simmer on the stove for approximately 1 hour, during which time the broth must always remain simmering hot, but must not boil. Arrange the fish pieces on a platter and serve with any desired sauce, such as: Dutch-style whipped mustard sauce, sugar sauce with lemon juice.

title: Schaiden, in Mehl gebraten (Shad Fried in Flour)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- shad
- flour
- salt
- fat or butter

## Directions

1. Cut the shad into arbitrary pieces, salt them and fry them in fat or dip them first on both sides in flour.

title: Schaiden, paniert (Breaded Shad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- shad
- flour
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- salt

## Directions

1. Dip the fish pieces in flour, then in beaten and salted eggs, then in breadcrumbs and bake them like calf cutlets. Serve fried fish pieces with potatoes; with breaded pieces serve any desired salad; with both you can serve lemon slices.

title: Schalottens Sauce (Shallot Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 shallots cut into 4 pieces
- 1 bay leaf
- About 6 tablespoons vinegar
- 8 tablespoons butter or lard
- Equal amount of flour
- 1/3 liter soup
- Salt
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cook 3 shallots cut into 4 pieces with 1 bay leaf in about 6 tablespoons vinegar
2. Make a dark roux from 8 tablespoons butter or lard and equal amount of flour
3. Add the cooked shallots with the vinegar to the roux
4. Pour 1/3 liter soup over it
5. Salt and sugar it to taste, let it simmer well and pass through a sieve

title: Schaumcreme-Gefrorenes (Viennese Cream Ice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Vanilla Ice prepared
- 1/4 liter beaten cream
- small pieces of crushed Schaumertolk

## Directions

1. Make the mixture as Vanilla Ice
2. When frozen, mix 1/4 liter beaten cream with small pieces of crushed Schaumertolk that has been prepared in the previously described way from 1/4 liter cream
3. When serving, decorate the ice, which can also be served in glasses, with chopped pieces of Schaumertolk as desired

title: Schaumrollen (Foam Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagrams flour
- 18 dekagrams butter
- salt
- 2 spoons wine
- some cold water
- 1 deciliter whipped cream

## Directions

1. Make butter dough from the ingredients.
2. Roll the finished dough long and thin.
3. Cut long two-finger-wide strips from it.
4. Loosely wrap them around the foam roll cylinder so that the dough edges overlap approximately half a centimeter.
5. Compress the dough slightly at the bottom.
6. Place the cylinders seam-side down on a buttered sheet.
7. Bake at very high heat until light brown.
8. After cooling, fill them with whipped cream using a pastry bag.
9. Sprinkle with sugar and serve.
10. The cylinders must be completely cooled before new strips are wrapped.

title: Scheiterhaufen (Bread and Apple Casserole)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 bread rolls
- ½ liter milk
- 1–2 eggs
- ½ kg apples
- sugar
- raisins
- butter

## Directions

1. Slice the bread rolls into leaves, place in a bowl, pour over with milk beaten with 1–2 eggs and let stand for 15 minutes.
2. Butter a casserole dish well, add a layer of bread, then a layer of peeled apples sliced into leaves.
3. Sprinkle with sugar and raisins, drizzle melted butter over.
4. Fill the form this way in layers, close with bread rolls, drizzle with butter, bake in the oven for 30–45 minutes.
5. Serve in the form.

title: Scheiterhaufen (Layered Bread Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/3 liter flour
- salt
- boiling salted water
- 8 egg yolks
- 1/2 liter sour cream
- 30 decagrams cooked, lean, finely sliced ham
- grated cheese
- 7.5 teaspoons salt
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from 1/3 liter flour
2. Cut the finely rolled cheese into pieces of desired size
3. Put the pieces into boiling salted water
4. Cook covered until they float on the surface of the water
5. Pour into a colander, rinse with cold water, and let stand
6. In a deep bowl, beat 8 egg yolks with 1/2 liter sour cream, 30 decagrams cooked, lean, finely sliced ham, grated cheese, and 7.5 teaspoons salt one after another
7. Gently fold in the snow of 8 beaten egg whites
8. Fill a buttered, flour-dusted mold with this mixture
9. Bake in the oven according to the height of the mixture; for example, 1/2 hour if the mixture is half-high, 1 hour if it is higher

title: Schellfish (Haddock)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 dkg haddock
- 8 dkg root vegetables
- 1/2 kg small round potatoes
- 1 onion
- 8 peppercorns and spice berries
- much vinegar
- salt
- milk water
- brown roasted butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare a brine consisting of root vegetables, 1 onion, 8 peppercorns and spice berries, much vinegar and salt, and boil for 30 minutes
2. Remove the skin from the fish, clean it, wash it and place in milk water
3. Place it in the boiling brine, pull back, and let it poach for 10-15 minutes depending on size
4. Transfer it to a platter, pour brown roasted butter over it and serve warm with small potatoes which have been tossed in butter and parsley

title: Schiffchen-Tascherl von Eiercreme (Little Boats with Egg Cream Filling)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca butter
- 6 deca fine powdered sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 2 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter lukewarm milk or cream
- 32 deca flour
- beaten eggs for brushing
- 1 tablespoon cream, custard or jam filling

## Directions

1. Beat 8 deca butter with 6 deca fine powdered sugar and 1/2 teaspoon salt until fluffy, add 2 egg yolks and 1 whole egg one after the other, add the yeast mixture and 32 deca flour, stir well and beat the dough until it separates from the spoon, let stand covered in a warm place
2. When the dough has risen somewhat, put it on a floured board, roll out strongly to the thickness of a knife back, cut into squares of desired size, brush the corners with beaten eggs
3. Put 1 tablespoon of cream, custard or jam filling in the middle of the squares, fold up the 4 corners in the middle, place on a buttered sheet and let stand lightly covered in a warm place
4. When risen after some time, brush with beaten eggs, sprinkle with blanched, finely cut almonds and bake at fairly high heat in the oven, then remove with a very thin spatula, arrange on a plate and serve sprinkled with sugar

title: Schildkröten in einer Blutsauce (Turtle in Blood Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Turtle
- Salt water
- Butter
- Onion
- Cloves
- Meat broth or cooking water
- Lemon peel
- Whole pepper
- Lemon juice
- Turtle blood

## Directions

1. Having let the turtle bleed out and preserved the blood in a bowl, place it in salt water over heat and boil until the shell can be opened.
2. Remove the shells, take out the turtle, peel off its outer skin, carefully remove the interior, and divide it into pieces of your choice.
3. Make a small brown roux with butter, add half an onion studded with cloves, fill it with meat broth or the water in which the turtle was cooked, add some lemon peel, whole pepper, and the juice of half a lemon.
4. Lay the turtle pieces in it, bring to a boil, add the turtle blood last, and let them cook a while longer, then they are done.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schildkröten in ihrer eigenen Schale (Turtle in Its Own Shell)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Turtle
- Salt water
- Butter
- Bread crumbs
- Lemon peel
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil the turtles very soft in salt water, open the shells, remove the turtles, divide them into four parts, and carefully preserve the juice found in the shell.
2. Carefully separate the gall from the liver so it does not burst.
3. Lay some pieces of butter in the turtle shell, add the juice found in it, then lay the cleaned turtle pieces back in, add some bread crumbs, some pieces of butter, chopped lemon peel, and the juice of a lemon.
4. Place them in a pan, apply heat from below and above, let them boil, and they are done.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schildkröten-Ragout (Turtle Ragout)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle, prepared as previously described, cut into small pieces
- crayfish claws
- 1 boiled calf's sweetbread
- green peas
- turtle eggs, removed from shell
- butter
- butter sauce
- 3 egg yolks
- lemon juice
- truffles, sliced in leaves

## Directions

1. Prepare the turtle as previously described
2. Cut the turtle into small pieces
3. Add crayfish claws, 1 boiled calf's sweetbread, green peas, and the removed turtle eggs; mix everything well together
4. Place in the center of a serving dish and garnish with croquettes, marrow dumplings, etc.
5. Make a butter sauce from the turtle broth
6. Bind the sauce with 3 egg yolks beaten with some lemon juice
7. Mix the sauce with sliced truffels as desired
8. Pour part of this sauce over the ragout and serve the remaining sauce in a sauce boat

title: Schildkröten-Suppe (Turtle Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 turtles, head, feet and tail
- finely chopped green parsley
- butter
- flour
- pea broth
- pepper
- nutmeg flower
- cream
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Cut off the head, feet and tail from 3 or 4 turtles, scald and remove them, cut into fine pieces
2. Braise them with finely chopped green parsley and a small piece of butter in a pot
3. Dust with a little flour and pour pea broth over
4. At the end add a little pepper, nutmeg flower and 2 spoonfuls of cream
5. Let it simmer well
6. Serve over toasted bread
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schildkrötenpäſtete (Turtle Pastry)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough, two knife-backs thick
- egg white
- butter sauce
- asparagus, boiled
- turtle, boiled and divided into pieces
- mushrooms
- crayfish tails
- liver, baked

## Directions

1. Make a bowl pastry from butter dough two knife-backs thick
2. Bake brushed with egg white
3. Make a small butter sauce and braise boiled asparagus, cleaned turtle cooked in salted water and divided into pieces, mushrooms, shelled crayfish tails, and baked liver in it
4. Once the pastry is baked, carefully remove the lid
5. Fill the mixed dish neatly inside
6. Put the lid back on and serve quickly
7. The pastry must be placed on a dish before it is filled
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schill warm mit Maſtre⸗d'hötel Kartoffeln (Whitefish Warm with Maître d'Hôtel Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whitefish weighing 1-1.2 kilograms
- 1/2 kilogram new potatoes
- 2 lemons
- 1/4 liter vinegar
- 8 spoonfuls salt
- root vegetables
- 1 onion
- 5 peppercorns
- 2 bay leaves
- thyme
- hot butter
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Scale and wash the whitefish, then tie at the opening
2. Tie very firmly at the head and tail, loosely in the middle (so the fish cooks through at the middle)
3. In the fish poacher, bring to a boil 1/4 liter vinegar, 8 spoonfuls salt, root vegetables, 1 onion, 5 peppercorns, 2 bay leaves, and thyme for 30 minutes
4. Lower the fish in, pull the pan back (the fish must not boil or it will fall apart) and let the fish poach without boiling for 8-10 minutes
5. Remove the fish and untie it
6. Quickly place on the fish platter, drizzle with hot butter, scatter with chopped parsley
7. Garnish with the potatoes
8. Serve hot butter or Hollandaise sauce separately (see Appetizers: Cauliflower with Hollandaise)
9. The potatoes are sliced and arranged on the edge of the platter

title: Schinken (Ham on the Spit)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- White wine
- Breadcrumbs, fine
- Green parsley
- Paper for wrapping

## Directions

1. Remove the rind from the ham and clean it well below
2. Soak it in lukewarm water
3. Pour one measure of white wine over it and let it soak for ten to twelve hours, or overnight until roasting
4. Thread it onto a spit and wrap it with paper
5. Pour the wine it was soaked in into the roasting pan and baste it frequently during roasting
6. When it is almost tender enough, remove the paper
7. Sprinkle it with fine breadcrumbs and green parsley and let it brown nicely
8. Remove it from the spit, let it cool, arrange it on a white napkin placed over the dish, and garnish with green parsley
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schinken auf portugiesische Art (Ham in Portuguese Style)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Water
- Beef broth, good quality
- Red wine
- Onions
- Yellow turnips
- Parsley roots
- Peppercorns
- Cloves
- Cinnamon, whole
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Remove the rind and anything inedible, and soak overnight in water
2. Place the ham in a casserole
3. Pour half a measure of good beef broth and the same amount of red wine over it
4. Add many onions, yellow turnips, parsley roots, peppercorns, cloves, and whole cinnamon
5. Cover well and cook on weak fire for four to five hours until tender
6. Let it cool in the broth
7. Remove it from the broth and skim all fat from the sediment
8. Clarify with a little egg white
9. Place a hand-breadth border of hard dough on the serving dish
10. Bake it in the oven
11. Place the ham in the middle and fill the space with the clarified sediment as it begins to gel
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schinken in Brotteig gebacken (Ham Baked in Bread Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large ham
- 5 crushed juniper berries
- 1 bay leaf
- sprigs of thyme
- cold water
- finger-thick, black bread dough

## Directions

1. Place the large ham in a casserole with 5 crushed juniper berries, 1 bay leaf, and several sprigs of thyme
2. Fill the casserole with cold water and simmer the ham slowly for about 1 hour
3. Remove the ham, dry it with a cloth, and wrap it the next day in finger-thick, black bread dough
4. Make several cuts so the dough does not burst during baking
5. Bake the ham for 3 hours in a baker's oven or baking oven
6. Allow the ham to cool in the bread dough and it can be stored in it for a longer time
7. Note: The cooking time before wrapping in bread dough depends on the size of the ham - a large ham requires 1 hour, a smaller ham only 1/2 hour, and a very small ham is wrapped in bread dough and baked without precooking

title: Schinken mit gebackenen Semmelſpalten (Ham with Baked Bread Rolls)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- ham, cut small and well soaked
- broth
- white wine
- green parsley
- chives

## Directions

1. Place small pieces of ham that have been well soaked in a pan or casserole, pour a measure of broth and a glass of good white wine over it, then add some finely chopped green parsley and chives
2. Let all this boil until it becomes soft and only very little sauce remains; note that the ham must be well soaked of salt
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schinken mit Madeira (Ham with Madeira)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- softly cooked ham
- 1/4 pint Madeira wine
- equal amount of broth
- Madeira sauce
- truffles (optional)
- paper or silver foil

## Directions

1. Cut the skin carefully from the softly cooked ham
2. Remove excess fat from the ham
3. Place the ham in a suitable casserole with 1/4 pint Madeira wine and an equal amount of broth
4. Braise for about 1/4 hour
5. Cut the ham into leafy pieces and arrange them in a bowl so it looks whole
6. Serve with Madeira sauce and truffles if desired
7. Attach a paper or silver foil to the wine

title: Schinken Strudel (Ham Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 ham
- 8 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter sour cream
- 8 egg whites
- 1/2 liter flour
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Finely chop 1/2 ham with a knife
2. Gradually beat in 8 egg yolks and 1/4 liter sour cream
3. Lightly fold in the snow of 8 egg whites
4. Prepare strudel dough from 1/2 liter flour as for baking and let rest 1/2 hour
5. Cover a table with a clean cloth, sprinkle with flour, roll and pull the dough as much as possible
6. Brush the well-pulled strudel dough with melted butter using a brush
7. Distribute the ham filling evenly over it
8. Turn up the dough handbreadth, add the rest of the filling on top, then roll up the dough over the cloth (Lungen-Strudel Recipe 75)
9. Place the strudel in a saucepan lined with butter in a spiral shape
10. Brush with melted butter and bake in a very hot oven for 1/2 to 3/4 hour
11. Cut into desired pieces, arrange on a plate with a slotted spoon and serve

title: Schinken's Hasteten mit Ecsinfetts-Fülle (Ham Pastry Cases with Ham Filling)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg yolks
- 16 deca finely cut ham
- 3 egg yolks
- 3/4 liter sour cream or sweet cream
- Salt
- 3 egg whites
- Butter
- Flour
- Butter dough, knife-back thickness

## Directions

1. Beat 2 egg yolks, cut them into small squares.
2. Mix finely cut ham with 3 egg yolks, mix well with 3/4 liter sour cream or sweet cream, the cut egg yolks, and some salt, then fold in the snow from 3 egg whites.
3. Line small sandal molds buttered and dusted with flour with knife-back thick squares of butter dough.
4. Fill to a finger-wide edge with the ham mixture given above, fold the 4 corners slightly over each other in the center, brush with beaten eggs, but leave all edges knife-back width dry.
5. Place the molds on a baking sheet in the oven and bake the pastries at moderate heat for about 1/2 hour.
6. After this time, lift them out of the molds with a fork, arrange on a platter, and serve as a main course, flour dish, or for supper or tea.

title: Schinken-Knödel (Ham Quenelles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 Loffel fresh butter
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg
- 32 Loffel very finely cut lean ham
- 1/2 liter roll crumbs

## Directions

1. Stir 20 Loffel fresh butter until fluffy
2. Gradually add 3 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks
3. Add salt and nutmeg
4. Finally add 32 Loffel very finely cut lean ham and 1/2 liter roll crumbs and stir well together
5. Make a small knödel as a test
6. If it falls apart during cooking, add more crumbs
7. Shortly before serving, cook them 10 minutes slowly and covered in salted water and lift them out with a skimming spoon into the soup

title: Schinken-Rissoles (Ham-Filled Pastry Crescents)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 ounces flour
- less than 1/8 liter cold milk
- 32 ounces flour
- 1 pinch salt
- cloth for covering
- 32 ounces finely chopped ham
- 3 tablespoons cream
- 2 egg yolks
- finely chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper
- flour for dusting
- eggs for coating
- very hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Beat 8 ounces butter until fluffy in a bowl
2. Gradually stir in 4 egg yolks
3. Dissolve 2 ounces flour in less than 1/8 liter of cold milk
4. Add this mixture along with 32 ounces flour and 1 pinch of salt to the butter
5. Stir well and beat the dough until it comes away from the spoon
6. Cover the bowl with a cloth and let rest beside the stove in a warm place until the dough doubles in height
7. Meanwhile prepare the ham filling: mix 32 ounces finely chopped ham with 3 tablespoons cream, 2 egg yolks, finely chopped parsley, salt, and pepper
8. Roll the dough on a floured board to the thickness of two knife-backs
9. Proceed as described in the recipe for Rissoles mit Farce, using the ham mixture as filling
10. Place on a floured board, cover with a cloth, and let rest in a warm place
11. When risen, fry in very hot fat on both sides until golden

title: Schinkenfleckerl (Ham Noodle Squares)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dkg flour
- 3 eggs
- salt
- 8 dkg butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 dkg cream
- ham, chopped

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from 30 dkg flour, 3 eggs, and salt
2. Cut into small squares
3. Place in boiling salted water and cook for 5 minutes
4. Drain and pour cold water over them
5. To the creamed 8 dkg butter, stir in 3 egg yolks and salt
6. Add 1 dkg cream, the chopped ham, the squares, and 3 egg whites beaten stiff
7. Fill the mixture into a well-buttered baking pan
8. Bake until light brown over higher heat
9. Remove with a wooden spoon and serve on a round platter

title: Schinkennudeln oder Erdäpfelflecke (Ham Noodles or Potato Patches)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- salt water
- finely chopped onion
- parsley
- butter
- 1 pound cooked ham
- 6 egg yolks
- 3 Seidel (about 1.5 liters) sour cream
- nutmeg (very little)
- butter for the form
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Make a regular noodle dough from 4 eggs, roll it out, and cut it into square patches
2. Boil them in salt water and strain through a colander
3. Braise finely chopped onion and parsley in butter
4. Cut 1 pound of cooked ham very finely
5. In a bowl, stir together 6 egg yolks with 3 Seidel of sour cream and nutmeg (but very little)
6. Add the braised mixture and ham to this
7. Carefully stir the patches into the mixture
8. Coat a form well with butter, dust it with grated bread
9. Fill the noodles into it and let it bake for about an hour
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schinkenpaſtete (Ham Pastry)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- flour for pasta dough
- salt
- butter
- ham
- milk cream

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from 2 eggs
2. Roll it into thin sheets and cut each sheet into about six pieces
3. Cook well in salted water
4. Drain in a sieve and rinse with cold water
5. Let dry on a cloth
6. Make a short pastry and line a buttered casserole with it
7. Lay a layer of pasta pieces on top
8. Beat a piece of butter with 3 eggs and 8 spoonfuls of milk cream together well
9. Pour some of this egg and cream mixture onto the pasta layer
10. Sprinkle three-quarters of a pound of boiled, finely cut ham
11. Alternate with another layer of pasta pieces until the casserole is full
12. Cover with a sheet of short pastry and bake brushed with egg white for one hour in the oven
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schinkenstrudel (Ham Strudel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough
- butter
- lean ham
- 2 whole eggs
- half seidel milk cream (Milchrahm)

## Directions

1. Make an ordinary strudel dough with some butter added to make it tender
2. Let it rest a while, covered with a warm cloth, after it is well worked
3. Meanwhile, cut a piece of lean ham into small pieces
4. Draw out the dough finely, brush it with melted butter
5. Beat 2 whole eggs in half seidel milk cream well together, pour this over the dough
6. Spread it evenly on all sides, sprinkle the ham on it
7. Roll the strudel loosely together, place it in a butter-brushed ring mold, brush it with melted butter, and bake it beautifully
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schlehenwein (Sloe Wine)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 strong Viennese Metzen of hawthorn sloes
- 1 bucket of strong French wine
- optional: 1-several pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Take 1 strong Viennese Metzen of hawthorn sloes per bucket of wine and crush them somewhat with a wooden stamper. The best sloes are those that have already shriveled from several night frosts. If using dried sloes, these are also good and you can use somewhat less, crushing them likewise a little.
2. Roll the wine with the sloes in the cellar daily once or several times for several months so that all the strength and color of the sloes are drawn out.
3. Then bring the barrel to storage and allow the wine to clarify completely.
4. You now have a wine that is exceedingly pleasant to eye and taste, very beneficial to health, and particularly good for the stomach and intestines.
5. If you wish to add one or several pounds of sugar to the sloes, the wine will certainly become even more noble.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schlesische Knödel von Gerstenmehl (Silesian Barley Flour Dumplings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Barley flour
- Salt
- Cold water
- Browned lard or cracklings
- Lard or onion

## Directions

1. Make a soft dough from barley flour, salt, and cold water, as for rolls.
2. Roll out the dough to the thickness of a finger and sprinkle it generously with browned lard or cracklings.
3. Roll it up like a strudel and cut it into pieces 3 fingers wide.
4. Cook the pieces in salted water.
5. Sprinkle with fried, finely diced lard or, if desired, with fried onion.
6. Serve with boiled meat or sauerkraut.

title: Schlickkrapfen (Fried Noodle Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg flour
- 1 egg
- some lukewarm water
- salt
- 30 dkg ground beef (hachee)

## Directions

1. Make a firm strudel dough from the flour, egg, lukewarm water and salt
2. Let it rest for ½ hour under a warmed casserole
3. Roll it out thin and cut out shapes with a cutter
4. Place hachee in the middle, brush edges with egg white, and press together over the filling
5. Cook in strained, boiling beef broth in a casserole

title: Schliess Krapfen (Schloss Ravioli)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 deca flour
- approximately 3 whole eggs
- salt
- 8 deca butter or fat
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley
- 4 deca white bread crumbs
- 10 deca finely chopped cooked or roasted meat
- salt and spices to taste
- 1 whole egg
- 1 egg yolk
- 2 liters beef broth or salt water

## Directions

1. Mix 30 deca flour with approximately 3 whole eggs and some salt using a spoon, then work it with both hands or a wide knife on a board
2. Cut the whole dough into pieces and form small balls from it, rolling them out very finely
3. While working on rolling out one piece, the others must remain covered
4. In 8 deca butter or fat, roast 1 teaspoon of finely chopped green parsley and add 4 deca white bread crumbs
5. Mix 10 deca finely chopped cooked or roasted meat with salt and spices to taste, and stir well with the above, then add 1 whole egg and 1 egg yolk
6. On the very finely rolled out dough, place a row of hazelnut-sized balls of this filling, at a distance of 2 finger-widths from each other
7. Brush them around with beaten egg and fold over the empty dough so that the filling is covered
8. Using an upside-down pastry cutter, press the dough around the filling tightly and round, then scallop it or cut it out with a pastry cutter in a half-moon shape
9. Add the Schloss ravioli to boiling beef broth or salt water, let them simmer covered for 8-10 minutes, remove them with a wooden spoon, and serve them in the beef broth

title: Schliess Krapfen mit Hirn (Schloss Ravioli with Brain)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 skinned calf brain
- 4 deca butter
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley
- 4 deca trimmed, finely chopped, in milk soaked and well wrung out bread rolls
- 1 whole egg
- 1 pinch of nutmeg
- same amount of white pepper
- dough and other ingredients as in Schliess Krapfen recipe

## Directions

1. Roast the skinned calf brain in 4 deca butter with 1 teaspoon of finely chopped green parsley
2. Add to a mixing bowl 4 deca trimmed bread rolls that have been finely chopped, soaked in milk, and well wrung out, then add 1 whole egg, 1 pinch of nutmeg, and the same amount of white pepper
3. Mix everything well together
4. Continue as with the Schliess Krapfen

title: Schliess Krapfen mit Schinken-Fuelle (Schloss Ravioli with Ham Filling)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- dough as in previous recipe
- finely chopped ham instead of ordinary meat
- other ingredients as in previous recipe

## Directions

1. Prepare as described in the Schliess Krapfen recipe, using finely chopped ham instead of ordinary meat for the filling

title: Schliess Krapfen von Karpfen Milch (Schloss Ravioli with Carp Roe)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- carp roe
- other ingredients as in brain ravioli recipe

## Directions

1. For fast days, use carp roe instead of calf brain, which is roasted and passed through a sieve and treated as with the Schliess Krapfen with brain

title: Schloſſerbuben (Breaded Plums with Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- 1 egg
- salt
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 10 dkg flour
- 1 tablespoon rum
- plums
- water
- ½ swelled almond per plum
- hot lard
- grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat together the milk, 1 egg, salt, 2 tablespoons sugar, 10 dkg flour, and 1 tablespoon rum until smooth.
2. Wash the plums and place them in boiling water.
3. Cook for 8 minutes, then drain and let cool.
4. Remove the pits from the plums and insert ½ swelled almond in place of each pit.
5. Dip the plums in the batter.
6. Fry in plenty of hot lard until light brown.
7. Drain and while still hot, roll them in grated chocolate.
8. Serve warm.

title: Schluck (Drink)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter red whipped cream
- 1/2 liter Rhine wine
- 1/4 kilogram sifted sugar
- lemon sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. In a porcelain dish, add 1/2 liter red whipped cream, 1/2 liter Rhine wine, 1/4 kilogram sifted sugar and lemon sugar to taste
2. Place on ice, but must be well covered
3. After approximately 1 hour, add the mixture to a whisk bowl in which it is beaten with the whisk until it becomes foamy

title: Schmackhafte Klöße von Erdäpfeln (Flavorful Potato Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Not fully cooked potatoes of arbitrary amount
- Mehl (one-sixteenth the weight of the potatoes)
- Salt
- Spice herbs
- Pepper
- Ground pickled beef or pickling
- Fried bread cubes

## Directions

1. Take an arbitrary amount of not fully cooked potatoes and grate them finely.
2. Mix in one-sixteenth of their weight in flour, as well as salt, spice herbs, and a little pepper, also grated pickled beef or pickling, and add some fried bread cubes (which should be grated or crushed finely).
3. Form the dumplings from this mixture.
4. Cook them until they swim on top of the water.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schmackhafte Klöße von Erdäpfeln (Tasty Potato Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Not quite fully cooked potatoes, quantity as desired
- Flour, one-sixteenth the weight of the potatoes
- Salt
- Herb seasonings
- Pepper
- Grated pickled beef or pickling
- Fried bread cubes, grated or pounded fine

## Directions

1. Grate the potatoes finely and mix with flour equal to one-sixteenth their weight, salt, herb seasonings, and a little pepper
2. Add grated pickled beef or pickling and some fried bread cubes, grated or pounded fine
3. Form dumplings from this mixture
4. Cook them in water until they float on the surface
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schmarftzerl-Pudding (Choux Pastry Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk used to prepare Schmarftzerln (recipe 1345)
- 5 egg yolks
- 8 ounces sugar
- 5 egg whites, beaten to snow

## Directions

1. Prepare Schmarftzerln from 1/2 liter milk
2. Cook them in 1/2 liter boiling milk and stir over heat until they are well cooked through
3. Set aside and let cool
4. Beat 5 egg yolks with 8 ounces sugar for 1 hour and 15 minutes
5. Slowly combine with the cooled Schmarftzerln
6. Gently fold in the snow from 5 egg whites
7. Line a buttered mold with flour, lay the cut Schmarftzerln, fill with the mixture above
8. Cover and steam for 1 hour and 15 minutes

title: Schmelzteig (Melting Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter fine flour
- 1/4 liter white wine
- 1 tablespoon oil
- Salt
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Put 1/4 liter of fine flour into a deep bowl, pour 1/4 liter of white wine gradually while stirring constantly along with 1 tablespoon of oil which has been previously made hot and boiling on the plate, stir with a knife-point of salt into a smooth dough.
2. Lightly fold in the whipped egg white from 4 eggs and use the dough for various purposes.

title: Schnecken à la poulette (Snails à la Poulette)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- snails
- salted water
- liaison sauce

## Directions

1. Place snails in boiling salted water and cook for 20 minutes
2. Meanwhile, prepare a liaison sauce
3. Add the extracted snails to the sauce
4. Serve in a dish

title: Schnecken-Suppe (Snail Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- snails
- salt
- water
- hard-boiled eggs
- onions
- green parsley
- bread soaked in milk
- flour
- pea broth
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the snails in salted water until the lids loosen
2. Remove them from their shells with a fork
3. Clean them by removing the tail, peeling off the brown skin, cutting off the front point and removing the stinger
4. Lay them in a bowl, rub with salt to remove the slime, and rinse with fresh water
5. Chop them finely together with some hard-boiled eggs, onions, green parsley and bread soaked in milk
6. Make a roux from one spoonful of flour in a pot
7. Add the chopped mixture
8. Pour in not-too-thick pea broth
9. Season with pepper and salt
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schneckensalat (Snail Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Snails
- Water
- Anchovies
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Onions
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the snails soft in water
2. Clean them nicely and cut them into small oblong pieces
3. Also cut anchovies, hard-boiled eggs and onions into small pieces
4. Mix everything together
5. Dress with vinegar and oil, salt and pepper and serve at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schnee Omelette (Snow Omelette)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 6 egg whites
- 1 heaping tablespoon flour
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Stir 6 egg yolks with 4 tablespoons sugar for 1 hour
2. Lightly fold in the firmly beaten snow from 6 egg whites with 1 heaping tablespoon flour
3. Put 1 tablespoon hot fat on a very hot omelette pan and then evenly put in 2-3 tablespoons of the above-mentioned mixture
4. Bake on the lower side only until it forms a light brown skin, then strike the omelettes from both sides together and place them side by side in a bowl
5. Put the bowl briefly in the oven and let the omelettes bake through, then sprinkle with preserved fruit as desired and serve

title: Schneeballen (Snowballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg flour
- 5 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 dkg sugar
- 1 spoon cream
- 2 spoons wine or rum
- hot lard
- sugar
- raspberry juice

## Directions

1. Make a dough on the board from the flour, butter, egg yolks, sugar, cream, and wine or rum.
2. Divide it into equally large pieces, roll them into thin sheets, and cut finger-wide strips lengthwise without going over the edge.
3. Cover them with a cloth and let them rest for one hour.
4. Thread the sheets onto a cooking spoon handle, alternating one strip on top and one on the bottom.
5. Hold them in hot lard, bake them until light brown, and serve them sprinkled with sugar, with or without raspberry juice.

title: Schneeberge mit Creme (Snow Mountains with Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half milk and half water
- whites of 15 eggs

## Directions

1. Place a casserole with half milk and half water on fire
2. Beat the whites of 15 eggs to stiff snow
3. As soon as it boils, insert a spoon of snow at a time, let boil once, lay on a sieve or napkin to drain, and continue until all the snow is used
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schneeberge mit Creme (Snow Mountains with Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 egg whites
- half milk and half water
- 2 loth finely ground bitter almonds
- 1 seidel cream
- some orange blossom water
- one quarter pound sugar
- egg yolks
- sliced almonds
- colored sugar

## Directions

1. Set a casserole with half milk and half water on the fire
2. Beat the whites of 15 eggs into stiff snow
3. Once it boils, spoon in one serving spoon full of snow at a time, let it boil once, lay it on a sieve or napkin to cool, and continue until all the snow is used
4. Stir the egg yolks with 2 loth finely ground bitter almonds, 1 seidel cream, some orange blossom water, and one quarter pound sugar
5. Layer the cooked snow on a serving dish, arrange cream over it, then snow again, then cream again, until the snow is piled up like a mountain
6. Cut some almonds lengthwise and stick them in the snow, arrange the cream completely over it, and sprinkle with colored sugar
7. Serve the snow mountains cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schneebällen (Fried Snowballs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deka flour
- 20 deka butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- salt to taste
- 1/16 liter milk
- wine or a few tablespoons rum

## Directions

1. Put 32 deka flour and 20 deka butter on a board, crumble these together with both hands
2. Make a hollow in the middle and add 3 egg yolks, 1 teaspoon sugar, some salt, 1/16 liter milk, and wine or rum
3. Work this into a dough like for strudel, which must have the same consistency, otherwise the snowballs will not rise properly
4. When the dough is sufficiently worked so that blisters become visible, let it rest in a cool place
5. Then form small balls from it, roll these out knife-back thick, and either cut round discs with a large round cutter or make them with a needle
6. Cut 5 lines through these at equal distance, finger-width apart, but so that the edge remains whole
7. Then grasp every other strip with a spoon handle, push the dough together on the stick, and draw the first grasped strip over the others, so that it now lies under the stick at the end, whereby a kind of loaf is formed
8. Hold this in a saucepan with very hot fat and pull out the spoon handle only when the dough remains together in the intended form
9. When the snowballs are baked light brown under frequent shaking of the saucepan on a baking sheet, turn them and bake them until done
10. Put them on baking paper and from there coat them in cinnamon and sugar or as desired
11. When serving, any desired marmalade can be served with it

title: Schneebällen ohne Butter in der Form gebacken (Fried Snowballs without Butter, Baked in Mold)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter flour
- 6 eggs
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put 1/4 liter flour, 6 eggs, 1 tablespoon butter, 1 teaspoon sugar, and some salt on a board
2. Make a kind of strudel dough from these, form small balls from it, which are rolled out knife-back thick
3. Make 5 cuts in these pieces at little-finger-width distance with a knife or needle
4. Grasp the dough by lightly pressing it together with the fingertips, put it in the mold already heated in very hot fat, cover it with the lid, and bake the snowballs in very hot fat under frequent shaking
5. When they are baked light brown, let them roll out of the mold, sprinkle them immediately with sugar, put the next already prepared snowball immediately back in the same mold, and continue until all the dough is used

title: Schneegebäckerei Von Quitten-Marmelade (Snow Confection from Quince Marmalade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 16 Deca finely sifted sugar
- same amount of quince marmalade

## Directions

1. Beat 2 egg whites until stiff.
2. Mix thoroughly with 16 Deca finely sifted sugar and an equal amount of quince marmalade.
3. Drop large spoonfuls onto a baking sheet lined with wafers.
4. Sprinkle with finely powdered sugar and let dry in an airy location.

title: Schneehuhn (Ptarmigan)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Ptarmigan
- Preparations as for hazel hens

## Directions

1. Prepare as hazel hens

title: Schnellräucherung ohne Feuer und Rauch (Quick-Smoking Without Fire and Smoke)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- All meat must be completely fresh
- For veal: saltpeter, cooking salt, crushed juniper berries, chopped garlic, rose geranium leaves, lemon peel with white pith, bay leaves
- Rain water or boiled well water
- For the smoking process: 2 pounds gloss soot (chimney soot) from a chimney where only wood is burned, 6 measures (18 apothecary pounds) rain or boiled well water, 8 pounds cooking salt
- For finishing: pure distilled wood vinegar

## Directions

1. Ensure all meat is completely fresh; for veal, remove from hide immediately after slaughtering to prevent unpleasant odors
2. Wash the veal meat clean and rub it with some saltpeter, then let it lie for four hours in a cool place in an appropriate oak or stone vessel without pressure
3. Dissolve cooking salt in warm rain water or boiled well water
4. Rub the meat firmly with crushed juniper berries, sprinkle with finely chopped garlic and some rose geranium leaves, lemon peels with white pith, and bay leaves
5. Ensure the meat forms no hollow spaces in its layers; fill any gaps with small pieces of the same meat to enable even pressure
6. Cover the meat with the cooled salt brine and let it stand for three days in a dry cellar, covered only with a cloth to protect from dirt, without any pressure
7. On the fourth day, load the meat with stones and increase pressure in winter until the twelfth day, then remove the stones; after two days, apply the previous pressure again for four days
8. For veal, the proper time in brine is eight to twelve days in summer, fourteen to eighteen days in winter
9. For beef and pork, use 14 pounds salt and 2 pounds saltpeter per 25 pounds meat; let it lie 24 days in winter or 14-18 days in summer
10. For poultry and fish, rub salt in the cavity (and some pepper may be used) but do not apply pressure
11. Approximately 60 hours before removing the meat from the salt brine, prepare 2 pounds of clean gloss soot by grinding it to fine powder
12. Pour 6 measures (18 apothecary pounds) of rain or boiled well water over the soot, cover the vessel, and stir the prepared brine frequently
13. Before the last two hours pass, add eight pounds of cooking salt to this soot brine and stir well
14. Remove the meat from the salt brine, let it drain, place it in another clean vessel, and cover it with the soot brine
15. If the brine is insufficient, mix the remaining sediment with the required amount of boiled water and add it
16. Let the treated meat lie for 5 to 6 hours depending on its diameter, tilting the vessel frequently
17. Remove the meat from the soot brine and hang it in an airy place, avoiding contact with walls, to dry completely
18. Finally, brush it with a brush using pure distilled, clarified wood vinegar (though it can be used immediately without this step)
19. Note that the meat must not remain too long in the gloss soot brine, as it would otherwise acquire an unpleasant pitch odor
20. For beef and pork, prepare the gloss soot brine from 6 pounds cooking salt, 1 pound pure gloss soot, and 5 measures water; weight these meats with stones and let them lie 8 to 9 hours, frequently turning the pieces and stirring the brine well
21. Poultry and fish remain in the veal's gloss soot brine for 1 to 2 hours depending on their size
22. All meat pieces must have loops attached before salting for later hanging and drying
23. All types of sausages made from fresh meat and fat of completely healthy animals, without excessive thinning of the sausage mass by meat broth or other liquids promoting souring, can be smoked without prior salting by lying 3 hours in the described gloss soot brine in a manner completely harmless to health; they should hang in the air at least 48 hours
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schnetteig (Schnee Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter fine flour
- 1/4 liter white wine
- 1 tablespoon oil
- Salt to taste
- Egg whites from 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Put 1/4 liter fine flour into a deep bowl.
2. Pour 1/4 liter white wine gradually while stirring constantly, along with 1 tablespoon of oil which has been previously heated until boiling on the plate.
3. Stir in salt with a knife point until smooth.
4. Lightly fold in the whipped egg white from 4 eggs.
5. Use the dough for various purposes.

title: Schnitten von Germteig zum Kaffee (Yeast Cake Slices for Coffee)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 Deca butter
- 3 egg yolks and 1 whole egg
- 2 Deca fresh yeast dissolved in 4 tablespoons lukewarm milk
- 16 Deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter, then gradually stir in the egg yolks and whole egg
2. Add the yeast dissolved in milk and flour, mix well
3. Beat until the dough pulls away from the spoon
4. Spread the dough on a buttered baking sheet to a finger-thickness
5. Let rise in a warm place until doubled in height
6. Brush with beaten egg, sprinkle with coarse sugar mixed with finely chopped almonds
7. Bake slowly in a moderately hot oven
8. Cut diagonally into pieces 2 fingers wide and finger-long
9. Serve warm or cold with tea, coffee, chocolate, or wine as desired

title: Schnittlauch's Sauce (Chive Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons butter
- Equal amount of flour
- 3 tablespoons finely chopped chives
- 1/4 liter soup
- Equal amount of sour cream

## Directions

1. Make a light roux from 8 tablespoons butter and equal amount of flour
2. Add 3 tablespoons finely chopped chives, let them roast a bit
3. Pour this with 1/4 liter soup and equal amount of sour cream and let it simmer well

title: Schnittlauch-Sauce (Chives Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca bread crusts, soaked in water and pressed out
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- vinegar
- water
- 6 tablespoons oil
- pepper
- 2 tablespoons finely cut chives
- 1 coffee spoon sugar

## Directions

1. Pound bread soaked in water and pressed out with hard-boiled egg yolks in a mortar
2. Pass through a sieve
3. Stir with vinegar and water
4. Mix with 6 tablespoons oil, pepper, 2 tablespoons finely cut chives, and 1 coffee spoon sugar
5. Mix everything well together

title: Schnitze von einem Euter, grillirt, auf einer Kräuterſauce (Grilled Udder Slices with Herb Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole udder
- salt
- melted butter
- bread crumbs
- herb sauce

## Directions

1. Cook one whole udder until very soft
2. Slice it into pieces the thickness of a knife back
3. Sprinkle the slices with salt
4. Turn each slice in melted butter and bread crumbs
5. Grill or fry them on both sides on the grate
6. Meanwhile prepare a herb sauce, arrange it on the platter, place the udder on top, and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schnitzel Soup (Soupe maigre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo cleaned schnitzel or pike meat
- 10 Deca butter
- 8 Deca rice
- 2.5 liters broth
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks

## Directions

1. Braise the meat
2. Place the braised meat in a casserole
3. Add 10 Deca butter, 8 Deca rice, and 2.5 liters broth
4. Add 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
5. Simmer together
6. Mix well and pass through a sieve
7. Season with salt and spices
8. Place the casserole in hot water and stir the soup frequently with a spoon

title: Schnürkrapfen (Cord-Tied Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 blanched almonds
- 1 egg yolk
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 16 tablespoons sugar of choice
- 1 tablespoon starch
- 2 tablespoons milk cream
- 32 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Mix the blanched almonds with 1 egg yolk to a very fine dough.
2. Beat 8 tablespoons butter until fluffy, stir in the ground almonds and then 16 tablespoons sugar of choice, 1 tablespoon starch, 2 tablespoons milk cream and 32 tablespoons flour.
3. Let this dough stand for some time in a cool place after it is well mixed, then place it on a board dusted with flour, roll it out to the thickness of a knife back, and cut strips that are finger-length and 3 fingers wide.
4. Tie a cord of approximately 1 foot length around the barrel of the mold and pull the cord through it as well.
5. After heating the mold very hot in lard, place a cut dough strip on it, hold the cord lightly on the dough with your index finger and wrap it with the cord, not too tightly, then lead the end of the cord back to the handle and hold it there.
6. Bake the dough wrapped in this way, whose ends should point upward, until light brown in very hot lard.
7. Remove the mold from the lard, hold it over a plate, carefully loosen the cord and carefully remove the baked, arch-shaped fritter from the mold, where the cuts from the cord should run at equal distances.
8. Place the fritter on a dish and sprinkle with sugar.
9. Continue until all the dough is used, then serve the fritters with dissolved marmalade, any warm cream or Chaudeau, or whipped cream or chocolate.

title: Schnürskraflein (Twine Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deka blanched almonds
- 1 egg white
- 8 deka butter
- 16 deka sugar of any kind
- 6 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons sour cream
- 32 deka flour

## Directions

1. Pound 8 deka blanched almonds with 1 egg white into a very fine paste
2. Whip 8 deka butter until fluffy, stir in the ground almonds
3. After these add 16 deka sugar of any kind, 6 egg yolks, 2 tablespoons sour cream, and 32 deka flour
4. Let this dough stand for a while in a cool place after it is well mixed together, then put it on a board dusted with flour, roll it out knife-back thick, and cut from it finger-long and 3-finger-wide strips
5. Then tie a piece of twine or string approximately 1.5 meters long, quite thick, tightly at the beginning of the mold and pull the twine through it

title: Schokoladeauflauf (Chocolate Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 dkg butter
- 8 dkg sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 bread rolls
- milk
- 3 egg whites
- 8 dkg grated chocolate
- butter for form
- sugar for form

## Directions

1. Cream 8 dkg butter and stir in 8 dkg sugar and 4 egg yolks
2. Soak 2 bread rolls (with crusts removed) in milk, squeeze out well, and add
3. Fold in 3 stiffly beaten egg whites together with 8 dkg grated chocolate
4. Fill into a well-buttered and sugared soufflé form
5. Bake at lower heat for 20-30 minutes
6. Sprinkle with sugar and serve in the form
7. This amount serves 4 persons; for 6 persons add 2 more eggs and proportionally more of the other ingredients

title: Schokoladebrot (Chocolate Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 11 dkg sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 14 dkg raw grated almonds
- 6 dkg grated chocolate
- 2 egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
- 7 dkg flour

## Directions

1. Beat 11 dkg sugar with 4 egg yolks for 15 minutes in a wide mixing basin
2. Mix in 14 dkg raw grated almonds, 6 dkg grated chocolate, 2 egg whites beaten to stiff peaks, and 7 dkg flour
3. Spread in an oblong, not too wide shape on a buttered baking sheet
4. Bake in a moderately hot oven
5. Cut into slices only the next day

title: Schokoladebuſſerl Type 1 (Chocolate Hussar)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg whites
- 10 dkg sugar
- 10 dkg raw grated almonds
- 4 dkg grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Blend 2 egg whites with 10 dkg sugar, 10 dkg raw grated almonds, and 4 dkg grated chocolate in a wide mixing basin
2. Using two small spoons, form little mounds on a buttered baking sheet
3. Bake in a moderately hot oven until brown

title: Schokoladebuſſerl Type 2 (Chocolate Hussar)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 1 egg white
- 7 dkg grated chocolate
- sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dkg sugar with 1 egg white and 7 dkg grated chocolate in a wide mixing basin until thick
2. Roll out on a sugar-dusted board
3. Cut out rings
4. Place on buttered baking sheet and dry in the oven more than bake

title: Schokoladecréme (Chocolate Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg cube sugar
- 1/4 liter water
- 12 dkg chocolate
- 1 dkg white gelatin
- 1/2 liter whipped cream

## Directions

1. In a small pot, spin 14 dkg cube sugar with 1/4 liter water
2. Soften 12 dkg chocolate in a double boiler and gradually mix with the spun sugar, stirring until smooth, then set aside to cool
3. Dissolve the gelatin and whip the cream
4. Fold the chocolate mixture into the cream, then slowly pour the melted gelatin through a tea sieve while stirring continuously
5. Fill the mixture into a pudding mold rinsed with water
6. Bury in ice for 2-3 hours until firm, then loosen, hold briefly in hot water, turn out onto a glass dish, and optionally decorate with 1/2 liter whipped cream
7. In winter, it is sufficient to place the cream in cold water

title: Schokoladeis (Chocolate Glaze)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 drams of finely sifted sugar
- 4 tablets of chocolate
- 1/2 liter of boiling water

## Directions

1. Put 28 drams of finely sifted sugar into a deep bowl.
2. Warm 4 tablets of chocolate on a sheet in the oven until the chocolate is so soft that the finger can break through it.
3. Put it in a saucepan, pour 1/2 liter of boiling water over it and stir on the stove until the chocolate is completely stirred and smooth.
4. Add it hot to the sugar and stir this together for about 1/2 hour and then use it.
5. It must flow thickly.
6. If the mass is too thick, add some warm water; if it is too thin, add some sugar; in both cases it must still be stirred for 1/2 hour.

title: Schokoladeis anderer Art (Chocolate Glaze, Another Kind)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 drams of fine chocolate
- 1/4 liter of water
- 24 drams of sugar
- 1/8 liter of water

## Directions

1. Put 24 drams of fine chocolate in a saucepan, pour 1/4 liter of water into it and let it dissolve on the fire, stir the mass finely and then add 24 drams of sugar and 1/8 liter of water and mix this well.
2. Let this mass boil until a thin thread pulls when you dampen thumb and forefinger with it.
3. Take it from the fire and stir it in the same saucepan for about 1/2 hour, then pour the glaze over the cake and let it dry.
4. This glaze should be brown, shiny and mirror-smooth.
5. If double-roasted chocolate is used, the glaze will be black.
6. You can also add 3 drops of lemon juice, after which the glaze must not boil any longer.

title: Schokoladeis mit Butter (Chocolate Glaze with Butter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- same as Chocolate Glaze (No. 1556)
- 4 drams of butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the same as the 'Chocolate Glaze' (No. 1556), with the difference that about 4 drams of butter are allowed to boil with it.
2. This glaze is only suitable for use if the cake is eaten the same day, because over time it becomes dull from the butter.

title: Schokoladekoch (Chocolate Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maß cream
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/2 pound curd cheese
- 1/4 pound ground almonds
- 6 whole eggs
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 pound sugar
- 6 loth chocolate

## Directions

1. Pour 1/2 maß cream into a pot
2. Beat in 2 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks
3. Beat well and set over heat until it becomes a curd cheese
4. Bind in a cloth and hang overnight to let the water run out
5. Beat 1/4 pound butter in a bowl
6. Pound 1/2 pound curd cheese and 1/4 pound ground almonds together well
7. Put into the beaten butter
8. Beat 6 whole eggs and 6 egg yolks in a bowl
9. Beat egg whites from the eggs to peaks
10. Alternately stir in a pair of eggs and a little peak until it takes half an hour total
11. Add 1 pound sugar and 6 loth chocolate
12. Grease the ring mold with butter
13. Fill the beaten mixture in and bake slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schokoladekuchen (Chocolate Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dkg butter
- 12 dkg sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 12 dkg softened or grated chocolate
- 4 egg whites
- 12 dkg flour
- butter and flour for the pan
- jam or sweetened whipped cream

## Directions

1. Cream together 12 dkg butter
2. Gradually mix in 12 dkg sugar with 4 egg yolks
3. Mix in 12 dkg softened or grated chocolate
4. Fold in 4 stiffly beaten egg whites together with 12 dkg flour
5. Spread finger-thick on a buttered and floured baking sheet
6. Bake at lower heat
7. While still warm, cut into rectangles
8. Cool completely, then sandwich 2 and 2 together with jam or sweetened whipped cream

title: Schokoladen-Kochli (Chocolate Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka butter
- 16 Deka sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 16 Deka crusts removed, bread softened in milk and well pressed out
- 8 egg whites
- 16 Deka grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat butter until fluffy
2. Stir in sugar, egg yolks for ¼ hour
3. Add bread with crusts removed, softened in milk, and well pressed out
4. Lightly mix in the snow from 8 egg whites simultaneously with 16 Deka grated chocolate
5. Fill a suitable butter-brushed, flour-dusted mold with this mixture
6. Cook covered for ½ to ¾ hour in steam or bake in the oven

title: Schokolades Suppe (Chocolate Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca chocolate
- 2 liters boiling milk
- 6 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons cold milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- baked bread or bread slices

## Directions

1. Lay 24 deca chocolate on a baking sheet and let soften in the oven, put it in a casserole and gradually pour 2 liters boiling milk into it while stirring constantly
2. Must be very careful so it does not become crumbly. The chocolate can also be grated into the milk
3. The cooked chocolate is then beaten with 6 egg yolks, 3 tablespoons cold milk, sugar as needed and, if desired, some cinnamon
4. Serve with baked bread or bread slices

title: Schokoladesalami (Chocolate Salami)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dkg sugar
- 14 dkg raw almonds, half grated and half cut into thin flakes
- 6 dkg grated chocolate
- 1½ egg whites
- sugar for dusting
- icing if desired

## Directions

1. Blend 14 dkg sugar, 14 dkg raw almonds (half grated, half cut into thin flakes), and 6 dkg grated chocolate with 1½ egg whites in a wide mixing basin
2. Place on a sugar-dusted board and knead together
3. Form into a sausage, wrap in paper, and let lie for 1-2 days
4. Slice diagonally, or alternatively roll out into a square, let dry, ice white or red, and cut into narrow slices

title: Schokoladesauce (Chocolate Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 deciliters water
- 12 dekagrams chocolate, broken up
- 12 dekagrams sugar
- 2 spoons potato flour

## Directions

1. Cook the water with the chocolate, sugar and potato flour under frequent boiling until thickened and cool it.

title: Schokoladesauce (Chocolate Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dl water
