- 12 dag chopped chocolate
- 12 dag sugar
- 2 spoons potato starch

## Directions

1. Cook the water with the chocolate, sugar, and potato starch, stirring frequently, until thickened
2. Let cool

title: Schokoladescreme (Chocolate Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablets chocolate
- 0.5 liters water
- 12 Deca sugar
- 2 Deca fine gelatin
- 0.5 liters water
- 0.5 liters heavy cream

## Directions

1. Melt 6 tablets chocolate in a warm place, then transfer to a saucepan and pour in 0.5 liters water in which 12 Deca sugar has been dissolved.
2. Stir well until the chocolate is smooth and shiny.
3. Add 2 Deca fine gelatin, which has been dissolved in 0.5 liters water.
4. Fold in 0.5 liters whipped heavy cream.
5. Proceed as directed for Rice with Vanilla Cream (No. 1689).

title: Schokoladestrudel (Chocolate Strudel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough
- 3 slices chocolate (Zelten)
- quarter pound blanched almonds, finely cut
- melted butter
- 5 spoonfuls good cream (Obers)

## Directions

1. Make a strudel dough, draw it out finely, and let it dry
2. Grate 3 slices chocolate, finely cut quarter pound blanched almonds
3. Pour some spoonfuls of melted butter over the dough, sprinkle it with the chocolate and almonds
4. Pour 5 spoonfuls of good cream over it, roll it together
5. Pour a good inch of cream into a ring mold, place the strudel in it, apply heat above and below, and let it bake beautifully crisp
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schokoladetorte (Chocolate Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dkg butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 14 dkg sugar
- 14 dkg softened chocolate
- 6 egg whites
- 8 dkg flour
- 7 dkg sugar for filling
- 10 dkg grated chocolate for filling
- 1 tablespoon water for filling
- 3 egg yolks for filling
- ½ l whipped cream for filling
- optional: jam and glaze

## Directions

1. Beat 7 dkg butter and gradually add 6 egg yolks, 14 dkg sugar, and 14 dkg softened chocolate
2. Soften the chocolate on a small pan briefly in the oven
3. Fold in 6 stiff egg whites together with 8 dkg flour
4. Fill the mixture into the form and bake at weaker heat
5. For the filling: beat 7 dkg sugar, 10 dkg grated chocolate, 1 tablespoon water, and 3 egg yolks in a double boiler until thick
6. Cool and fold in ½ l whipped cream
7. Fill the cake inside and brush outside, or brush inside and on top with jam and glaze white or brown

title: Schokoladetorte, sehr fein (Chocolate Cake, Very Fine)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg butter
- 15 dkg sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- 15 dkg softened chocolate
- 5 stiff egg whites
- 15 dkg raw grated almonds
- 6 dkg rum-moistened breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Beat together butter and sugar gradually, then egg yolks and softened chocolate
2. Fold in stiff egg whites together with raw grated almonds and rum-moistened breadcrumbs
3. Fill mixture into cake form
4. Bake at higher heat
5. Fill with jam when cooled
6. Glaze brown on top

title: Schokoladetorte, sehr fein (Fine Chocolate Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 dkg butter
- 15 dkg sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- 15 dkg softened chocolate
- 5 egg whites
- 15 dkg raw grated almonds
- 6 dkg rum-moistened breadcrumbs
- jam
- brown glaze

## Directions

1. Beat 15 dkg butter and gradually add 15 dkg sugar, 5 egg yolks, and 15 dkg softened chocolate
2. Fold in 5 stiff egg whites together with 15 dkg raw grated almonds and 6 dkg rum-moistened breadcrumbs
3. Fill the mixture into the form
4. Bake at higher heat
5. Fill with jam and ice brown on top

title: Schokoladetortelettes (Chocolate Tartlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 19 dkg flour
- 14 dkg butter
- 1 egg yolk
- 7 dkg sugar
- 10 dkg grated chocolate
- 1/2 milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon flour
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 7 dkg grated chocolate

## Directions

1. On the board, make a dough from flour, butter, egg yolk, sugar, and grated chocolate
2. Roll out the dough and cut rounds
3. Bake on a lightly buttered sheet at moderate heat
4. For the filling: Over steam, beat 1/2 milk, 2 egg yolks, 1 teaspoon flour, 2 tablespoons sugar, and 7 dkg grated chocolate until thick
5. Fill the baked tartlets
6. Ice with white or red icing or sprinkle with sugar if desired

title: Schokoladetortelettes (Chocolate Tartlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 19 dkg flour
- 14 dkg butter
- 1 egg yolk
- 7 dkg sugar
- 10 dkg grated chocolate
- milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 spoonful flour
- 2 spoonfuls sugar
- 7 dkg grated chocolate for filling

## Directions

1. Make a dough of 19 dkg flour, 14 dkg butter, 1 egg yolk, 7 dkg sugar, and 10 dkg grated chocolate on the board
2. Roll out and cut into rounds
3. Bake on a lightly buttered baking sheet in a moderately hot oven
4. For the filling: beat ½ milk, 2 egg yolks, 1 spoonful flour, 2 spoonfuls sugar, and 7 dkg grated chocolate over steam in a snow basin until thick
5. Fill the baked tartlets and optionally ice or sprinkle with sugar

title: Schwache Schildkröten-Suppe (Potage Fausse Tortue à la Française - Mock Turtle Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned and brushed calf's head with tongue
- broth or salt water
- 3/4 liter beef broth
- 1 1/4 liters Madeira wine
- 8 decagrams finely sliced truffles (optional)
- 14 decagrams butter
- equal amount of flour
- 1 finely sliced champignon
- green parsley
- 2 liters beef broth
- cooked pear
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Simmer the cleaned calf's head with tongue in broth or salt water until tender
2. Remove from broth and dry with a cloth
3. Cut round pieces with a cutter or cut into desired pieces
4. Place these pieces in a casserole with 3/4 liter of beef broth and 1 1/4 liters of Madeira wine, and optionally 8 decagrams of finely sliced truffles
5. Cook covered for approximately 1 1/2 hours
6. Meanwhile, make a brown roux from 14 decagrams of butter and equal amount of flour
7. Roast a finely sliced champignon and some green parsley in it
8. Pour on 2 liters of beef broth in which the calf's head was cooked and let it cook well
9. Cut the skinned, salt-water-cooked pear into pieces and add to the soup
10. Pass the brown sauce into the casserole with the calf's head, broth, and wine
11. Add salt and pepper if desired and bring to a boil
12. Serve with pasta, crayfish quenelles, or roasted bread slices

title: Schwammerl-Strudeln (Mushroom Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 plate of mushrooms, approximately 1 kilo (Pilsling, Bratling, yellow mushrooms, morels or champignons)
- 12 Deka butter or lard
- chopped green parsley
- 8 Deka bread crumbs
- 1/2 liter flour for dough
- 1/4 liter milk cream
- 4 whole eggs
- white pepper (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean and wash mushrooms, dry with cloth, chop finely
2. Braise in 12 Deka butter or lard with parsley
3. When half braised, add 8 Deka bread crumbs and braise together
4. When done, spread filling on strudel dough made from 1/2 liter flour
5. Whip 1/4 liter milk cream with 4 whole eggs and pour over in spoonfuls
6. Roll dough lightly and treat as lung strudel
7. Add white pepper if desired

title: Schwammerl-Suppe (Mushroom Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- nail mushrooms (Nägerlschwämme)
- pea broth
- butter
- green parsley
- flour
- pepper
- nutmeg flower
- cream
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Wash nail mushrooms in warm water several times
2. Let them cook in pea broth
3. Pour the broth into another pot
4. Braise the mushrooms in some butter and green parsley
5. Dust with one spoonful of flour
6. Pour the above pea broth over
7. Salt and season with ground pepper and nutmeg flower
8. Add one spoonful of good cream
9. Serve over toasted bread slices
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schwarzbeeren-Strudel (Blackberry Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Strudel dough
- black currants, picked
- raisins
- currants
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Fill with black currants picked from the berries instead of apples
2. Preparation is otherwise the same as for Apple Strudel (No. 1238)

title: Schwarzbrot Suppe mit Ei (Black Bread Soup with Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- black rye bread
- beef broth, about 2 liters
- eggs
- salt and pepper to taste
- braised bratwurst or cooked frankfurter sausages (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of black rye bread into thin slices and toast it on a baking sheet in the oven.
2. Place the toasted bread in a casserole, add approximately 2 liters of beef broth, and let it simmer slowly for about 1/4 hour.
3. Pour it into the soup pot, stir in lard, and season with pepper to taste.
4. If desired, add braised bratwurst or cooked frankfurter sausages cut into pieces.
5. Place 3 finger-widths of risen beef broth in a shallow casserole; gradually beat in as many eggs as there are servings into the simmering soup so that when the egg white has set in the simmering soup, fold it together using a slotted spoon so that the egg yolk is completely covered.
6. After 4-5 minutes, add the eggs very carefully into a bowl and pour the simmering soup to the side of it.
7. Serve the eggs with the broth soup. The eggs do not always need to be served separately; they can also be added to the soup pot.

title: Schwarzbrot-Suppe mit Ei (Black Bread Soup with Egg)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of black house bread
- approximately 2 liters beef broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Slice black house bread thinly and roast on a baking sheet in the oven
2. Place the roasted bread in a casserole
3. Add approximately 2 liters beef broth and let cook slowly for approximately 1/4 hour
4. Pour into the soup pot and mix

title: Schwarzwurzeln mit Butterfauce (Black Salsify with Butter Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- black salsify (scorzonera)
- cold water
- flour
- vinegar
- bouillon
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Once the black salsify is cleaned, cut it into finger-length pieces
2. Immediately throw them into cold water with a little flour and some drops of vinegar
3. Set a casserole with bouillon and some butter on coal fire
4. Lay the dry roots in it
5. Add pepper and salt
6. Let them cook until they are soft
7. Then make a butter sauce with lemon juice over them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schwedischer Rostbraten (Swedish-Style Beef Roast)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- flattened and salted beef roast pieces
- flour
- hot fat or lard
- fat or bacon
- 1 piece onion, sliced leafy
- finely chopped anchovy fillets
- parsley
- 1/2 liter cream
- 1/4 liter broth

## Directions

1. Coat the flattened and salted beef roast pieces in flour
2. Quickly brown them on both sides in hot fat or lard
3. Meanwhile, add fat or bacon to a casserole
4. Add 1 piece of leafy sliced onion, finely chopped anchovy fillets, and parsley
5. Place the roast pieces (6 pieces) on top
6. Pour 1/2 liter cream and 1/4 liter broth over them
7. Braise covered until tender
8. Transfer to a serving dish
9. Thin the sauce if necessary, which can also be strained
10. Pour the sauce over the roast

title: Schweinefleisch mit Paprika (Pork with Paprika)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork neck, shoulders, or breast pieces, cut into thumb-width cubes
- Diced bacon
- 1 onion, finely minced
- 1/2 teaspoon caraway seeds
- Salt
- Paprika
- Broth
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the diced bacon in a casserole dish.
2. Add the cut pork pieces and finely minced onion.
3. Mix in 1/2 teaspoon of caraway seeds and allow to braise covered.
4. When the onion has become slightly roasted, add enough broth and vinegar so the liquid does not completely cover the meat.
5. Season with salt and a knife-tip of paprika.
6. Allow the meat to braise covered until tender.
7. Arrange the meat on a platter with the defatted braising liquid and serve with noodles, turnips, or similar accompaniments.
8. Optionally, peeled potatoes cut into pieces can be added at the same time and braised together with the meat.

title: Schweinsbraten (Roast Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- 120-140 dkg pork loin or young pork rib
- caraway seeds
- salt
- fat
- water or soup

## Directions

1. Wash the meat
2. Rub both sides with caraway seeds and salt
3. Place in a roasting pan with the good side down
4. Add a piece of fat and some water on top
5. Roast in the oven for 2-2½ hours, basting frequently with hot water or soup
6. Turn when brown
7. Remove, slice, then chop through
8. Serve on a long platter with the degreased pan juices

title: Schweinslungen-Wurst (Pork Lung Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork lung or offal, as much as needed
- Calf offal and mesentery, optional for multiple sausages
- Fatty pork or fat
- Water for cooking
- Four bread rolls (Semmeln) per two offal pieces and a couple pounds of meat
- Three to four onions
- A piece of garlic
- One lot powdered marjoram
- One lot pepper
- Salt
- Pork intestines for casing

## Directions

1. Precook the pork lung or offal in water until not quite soft
2. Remove all glands and cartilage carefully
3. Cut everything into small pieces and chop together to desired fineness
4. Cut four bread rolls into pieces and place them in the broth in which the meat was cooked
5. Once the bread rolls are soft, add them to the mixture along with three to four finely cut onions, a piece of finely cut garlic, one lot powdered marjoram, one lot pepper, and salt well
6. Mix thoroughly and add one measure of the aforementioned broth to thin
7. Mix again and fill into cleaned pork intestines
8. Do not fill too full, tie closed, place in boiling water
9. Leave in the water until slightly set but not fully cooked, approximately a quarter hour
10. Remove and store for future use
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Schwäbische Brotsuppe (Swabian Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-70 dkg Beef (shoulder cut)
- 1 pair Sausages
- 6 dkg Hot fat
- 1 Onion (finely chopped)
- Bread (cut into strips)
- Beef broth
- Salt

## Directions

1. 20 minutes before serving, roast finely chopped onion and small pieces of noodle-cut bread in hot fat in a casserole
2. Pour strained beef broth over the mixture
3. Add sausages (washed and cut into strips)
4. Cook together for a few minutes
5. Optionally, stir in 1 egg, but the soup must not boil after this

title: Schwäbische Filets (Swabian Fillets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cutlets à 25 dkg
- 4 dkg bacon
- 2 anchovies
- 4 tablespoons capers
- 1 pickled gherkin
- some broth or water
- cream
- salt
- pepper
- fat for searing

## Directions

1. Wipe the cutlets, remove the skin, pound them, salt and pepper them, lard them, make a small cut at the edge, quickly sear them on both sides in hot fat and place on a plate
2. Pour part of the remaining fat into a casserole
3. Finely chop 1 onion, the gherkin, the capers, the boned anchovies, 1 bay leaf and the bacon scraps and roast them in the hot fat in the casserole
4. Add the fillets, pour in some hot water and cover
5. Braise them under frequent basting for 2-2½ hours
6. Remove the fillets, dust the sauce, pass it through a sieve, season with vinegar, add cream and cook well with the meat
7. Serve on a long platter with the sauce

title: Schöberl (Cabbage Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head cabbage
- 2 bread rolls
- 0.5 deciliter milk
- 4 dekagrams butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and cut the cabbage into noodle-like strips.
2. Braise it in 4 dekagrams of butter over frequent stirring for 25-30 minutes until soft and cool it.
3. Make a roux of 4 dekagrams butter and stir in 2 egg yolks.
4. Soak 2 bread rolls in milk, squeeze them well and add them to the roux.
5. Add the cooked cabbage, salt, pepper and 2 beaten egg whites.
6. Fill the mixture into a schöberl pan and bake at moderate heat.
7. Loosen the schöberl, turn it out, cut it and serve warm.

title: Schöberl (Liver Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ white rolls
- 12 dkg beef liver
- 2 white rolls
- a small piece of finely chopped onion
- parsley
- 6 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Soak and squeeze out 1½ white rolls
2. Scrape the liver and pass through with the squeezed rolls, onion, and parsley
3. Mix with creamed butter, egg yolks, salt, and pepper
4. Lightly fold in stiffly beaten egg whites from 2 eggs
5. Fill into a dumplings pan and bake over low heat
6. Loosen, turn out, and cut into pieces

title: Schöpfenfleisch mit Petersilie (Stewed Mutton with Parsley)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kilos mutton meat
- 3/4 liter water
- 1/2 tablespoon salt
- 1 teaspoon caraway
- 1 head of onion (optional)
- Finely cut green parsley
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton meat into pieces of any size.
2. Place in a casserole and pour over 3/4 liter water and 1/2 tablespoon salt.
3. Add 1 teaspoon caraway and optionally 1 head of onion.
4. Cover and cook gently for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
5. Arrange on a platter, sprinkle with finely cut green parsley.
6. Serve with boiled potatoes as a snack, instead of roast meat, or at tea.

title: Schüſſel-Paſtete mit Hecht-Ragout (Platter Pastry with Pike Ragout)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- butter
- lemon peel
- nutmeg
- artichokes
- morels
- crayfish tails
- bread crumbs
- broth
- hard baked dough
- eggs
- butter pastry
- egg yolks
- vinegar
- flour
- onion
- parsley water

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the pike, cut it into small pieces, salt them lightly and dry them thoroughly
2. Heat butter on charcoal, add lemon peel and nutmeg, sauté the pike in it as if making a fricassee
3. Cut artichokes into small pieces and add them with morels (previously soaked and washed) and crayfish tails to the pike
4. Let them sweat a little, sprinkle bread crumbs in, pour a couple spoonfuls of broth and let everything cook a little
5. Butter a tin platter, arrange the ragout neatly in it, pour the little broth over it
6. Add a little nutmeg and finely cut lemon peel
7. Make a sheet from hard baked dough, cover the platter with it as far as the pike lies, puff it up a little so it becomes round
8. Brush it with eggs, draw a good butter pastry over it, form and cut the pastry as well as you can and let it bake in the oven
9. When half baked, cut a small hole so it does not burst
10. Meanwhile make the following broth: whisk three to four egg yolks in a casserole, add a few drops of vinegar, a little fine flour, stir it, add a piece of butter and a whole onion, pour in broth or parsley water and stir it over the coals until it begins to thicken
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Scorbutbruehe (Scurvy Broth)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Two handfuls each of spoonwort, watercress, figwort, and celery
- One quench bitter orange peel
- One loth wild turnip seed
- Two sliced calf hearts
- Tails and claws of one dozen crayfish
- Water

## Directions

1. Cook all ingredients together in a sufficient quantity of water
2. Use in two portions
3. This broth disperses phlegm, drives out urine, and purifies the blood
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Scrambled Eggs with Bacon (Oeufs brouilles an lard)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ounces bacon, diced
- 12 eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chives, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Cut 12 ounces of bacon into fine dice and place in a sauté pan or egg pan, roasting until it becomes translucent.
2. Meanwhile, beat 12 eggs well, season to taste with salt and pepper, and pour over the roasted bacon.
3. Cook over gentle heat, stirring and scraping, until the eggs begin to set.
4. Serve as desired, either firm or more liquid, with a serving spoon in a warm bowl, sprinkled with finely chopped chives. It can also be served in the egg pan, which is placed on a hot plate.

title: Scrambled Eggs with Ragout (Oeufs au ragout mele)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces truffles, very finely chopped
- 12 ounces fish or other meats, diced small
- Butter
- Cream or soup
- 2 tablespoons milk
- 5 eggs
- 2 yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bread rolls, baked in butter

## Directions

1. Cut 8 ounces of truffles very finely, and cut 12 ounces of fish or other meats into small dice.
2. Braise these in butter and add a few spoonfuls of cream or soup.
3. Beat 2 tablespoons milk, 5 eggs, and 2 yolks together, season with salt and pepper.
4. Heat 8 ounces of butter until hot, stir in the beaten egg mixture.
5. When it begins to thicken, fold in the chopped truffles and meat mixture.
6. Serve with bread rolls baked in butter.

title: Seelachs mit Sardellen (Pollack with Anchovies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 80 dkg pollack fish
- 4 anchovies
- 1/4 liter cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as oyster fish, but baste it with cream while frying
2. Transfer it to a platter and pour hot anchovy butter over it

title: Sehlingen (Gudgeon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Gudgeon
- Water
- Salt
- Potatoes
- Hot butter
- Sauce à la Hollandaise or other sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the fins from all around, pull off the skin from the gills toward the head on both sides, cut off the head with the entrails
2. Wash the fish and salt it
3. Place the cleaned fish in boiling salted water, bring to a boil once, then arrange in a bowl
4. Garnish with potatoes and serve with hot butter, Sauce à la Hollandaise or any desired sauce

title: Sehr feiner französischer Essig (Very Fine French Vinegar)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Strongly frozen wine vinegar
- Leaves of dragon root
- Many rocambole
- Laurel leaves
- Basil
- Very little peppermint leaves
- Some shallots with the rocambole
- Leek
- Angelica roots
- Some calamus roots
- Some anchovies

## Directions

1. Observe the proportions well according to preference, but do not add too much
2. Chop everything coarsely
3. Digest it in a wide-mouthed, capacious bottle closed with a bladder, so that it can be shaken from time to time
4. Make a hole in the bladder by means of a needle prick
5. Digestion in the sun is better than at a warm oven
6. If you love such vinegar or need it often, it is good to prepare a quantity of it at once
7. When it is filled into bottles and well stoppered and placed in the right location, it will keep longer than one lives
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sehr vorzügliches Bier (Very Excellent Beer)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- good lager beer brewed in winter
- strong wine spirit
- sugar
- strong sweet wine (2 to 3 bottles per 40 Wiener Maß optional)

## Directions

1. Use a good lager beer that has aged in the cellar until it is free of yeast sediment and is malt-rich and strong enough
2. Fill it into the strongest glass bottles, more slowly than wine and so carefully that foaming is avoided as much as possible
3. Do not fill the bottles completely at once, but leave one fifth space in them
4. When finished with the last bottle, fill the first, then the second and so forth just until three inches of space remains, then stopper them
5. Shortly before filling, rinse the bottles thoroughly with strong wine spirit (pouring the spirit from the first bottle into the second, from the second into the third, etc.) and immediately stopper them firmly
6. Now fill them, but put a large piece of sugar in each bottle
7. The beer becomes even more excellent if you add a strong sweet wine to it - about 2 to 3 bottles per 40 Wiener Maß
8. The stoppers or corks must be particularly good and somewhat larger than the mouth of the bottle
9. Before use, you can tap them moderately with a wooden mallet like coopers use, which makes them soft and allows them to be driven in more easily and deeply
10. Fasten the stoppers around the neck of the bottle with binding twine crossed over if not with wire as with champagne wine
11. Then, so that no air can enter, dip them deep and strongly into a mixture of melted pitch, tallow and rosin
12. Set the bottles in a good cellar in sand on their heads
13. In summer, water the sand daily
14. After some time, fermentation begins in the bottles and when the beer begins to mousse, it can be drunk
15. It will foam and prickle like champagne
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Selchfleisch mit Sauerkraut (Smoked Meat with Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- 80 dkg to 1 kg shoulder roast or rib
- 1/2 kg sauerkraut
- vinegar
- caraway
- salt
- water
- 6 dkg fat

## Directions

1. Wash the meat
2. Place in cold water without salt
3. Cook for 1½ to 2 hours
4. Remove and cut, then chop through
5. Serve on a long platter
6. Wash the sauerkraut and cut 2-3 times
7. Cook with vinegar, caraway, salt, and enough water to cover for 1½ to 2 hours
8. Add a light onion roux made from 6 dkg fat
9. Season with salt and sour to taste
10. Cook together for 20 minutes
11. Optionally add roasting juices

title: Selleriesalat (Celery Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- celery, sliced
- rapunzel
- lactuke or other greens
- wine vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Slice the celery into pieces
2. It can be eaten mixed with rapunzel, lactuke, and other greens, or alone raw, blanched with boiling water, or also cooked
3. For an excellent flavorful preparation: stew the slices in good wine vinegar, well covered
4. When soft enough, remove from heat and let cool in its gelatinous sauce
5. Add the necessary oil without pouring additional fresh vinegar over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Selleriesuppa (Celery Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley
- 4 deca butter
- 1/2 liter pea water
- 2 heads celery, peeled and cut into dice
- 12 deca butter
- flour
- 2 liters pea water
- salt
- spices
- 1/4 liter cream

## Directions

1. Roast 1 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley in 4 deca butter
2. Pour in 1/2 liter pea water, strain well, and add
3. Add 2 heads of celery cut into dice and braise gently
4. Make a pale yellow roux with 12 deca butter and flour
5. Pour in 2 liters pea water as for a thickened soup, stirring well
6. Add the braised celery, salt, spices and 1/4 liter cream
7. Let simmer well

title: Selleriesuppe (Celery Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- flour
- cream
- sliced celery
- roasted bread slices
- a piece of butter
- pea broth

## Directions

1. Stir eggs mixed with a bit of flour in cream into the sliced celery, roasted bread slices and a piece of butter in pea broth
2. Pour the boiling soup over and season to taste
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Selleriesuppe (Celery Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Flour
- Cream
- Celery, sliced
- Roasted bread rolls
- Butter
- Pea broth

## Directions

1. Pour eggs stirred with flour in cream over sliced celery, roasted bread rolls, and a piece of butter in boiling pea broth
2. Season to taste
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Semmel Pudding (Bread Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter milk or cream
- 16 teaspoons trimmed, finely sliced rolls

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, add 3/4 liter of milk or cream, 16 teaspoons of trimmed, finely sliced rolls and stir after they have softened completely

title: Semmel-Knödel (Roll Quenelles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Loffel butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 20 Loffel finely diced rolls mixed with butter milk
- roasted parsley in butter
- salt
- 3 tablespoons flour
- approximately 3 tablespoons roll crumbs

## Directions

1. Beat 12 Loffel butter until fluffy
2. Stir in 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
3. Mix 20 Loffel finely diced rolls with butter milk, add roasted parsley in butter
4. Add salt, 3 tablespoons flour and approximately 3 tablespoons roll crumbs and stir well together
5. Form knödel of desired size

title: Semmel-Pudding (Bread Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter milk or cream
- 16 deca finely sliced rolls
- 14 deca butter, beaten
- 14 deca vanilla sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 8 deca plump raisins
- 8 deca currants
- 8 deca finely diced citron
- 8 deca blanched, cut into sticks almonds

## Directions

1. Pour 1/4 liter milk or cream into a saucepan
2. Add 16 deca finely sliced rolls and stir over heat until completely softened and very thick
3. Set aside and let cool
4. Beat 14 deca butter with 14 deca vanilla sugar and 8 egg yolks gradually until well mixed
5. Add the cooled rolls, mix well
6. Lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites together with 8 deca plump raisins, 8 deca currants, 8 deca finely diced citron, and 8 deca blanched almonds cut into sticks
7. Fill a buttered and floured mold with this mixture
8. Steam covered for 1/2 hour
9. Serve with desired cream

title: Semmel-Suppe (Bread Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 crusts removed bread rolls, diced
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- approximately 2 liters boiling beef broth

## Directions

1. Place 6 diced bread rolls (crusts removed) in a casserole
2. Add 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks, stir well together
3. Pour approximately 2 liters boiling beef broth over it without stirring much
4. It must boil only from one side and is served after 3/4 hour
5. Be very careful that no lumps form during cooking

title: Semmelknödel (Bread Dumpling)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread rolls, cut into cubes
- 6 dkg fat
- 1/4 finely chopped onion
- Parsley
- 2 dl milk
- 1 egg
- Salt
- 3-5 tablespoons flour
- Bread crumbs roasted in fat

## Directions

1. Cut bread rolls into cubes and place in a bowl
2. Toast the finely chopped onion and parsley in 6 dkg hot fat
3. Pour over the bread and let stand a while
4. Beat together 2 dl milk, 1 egg, and salt
5. Pour over the bread
6. Mix lightly with 3-5 tablespoons flour
7. Form dumplings
8. Place in a casserole with boiling salt water
9. Cook slowly for 8-10 minutes
10. Transfer to a round platter
11. Pour roasted bread crumbs in fat over them

title: Semmelknödel ohne Ei (Breadcrumb Dumplings Without Egg)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pieces of rolls (32 deca)
- 16 deca lard or pork fat
- 1 teaspoon of finely chopped parsley
- 1 liter flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 liter cold water

## Directions

1. Cut the rolls into cubes and roast them in the lard or pork fat with a teaspoon of finely chopped parsley until they turn golden brown quickly.
2. In a deep bowl, combine 1 liter of flour and 1 teaspoon of salt.
3. Stir the flour with 1/2 liter of cold water into a thin dough.
4. Add the roasted rolls and stir everything together well.
5. Instead of water, milk can also be used for these dumplings.
6. Follow all further steps as directed in the recipe for ordinary bread dumplings.

title: Semmelknödeln (Bread Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 loth butter
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg
- finely chopped parsley
- 12 loth finely grated bread

## Directions

1. Stir 12 loth butter to foam
2. Gradually add 3 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks with salt, nutmeg, and finely chopped parsley
3. Fold in 12 loth finely grated bread
4. Form small dumplings
5. Cook slowly in bouillon for a quarter hour
6. Serve under young chickens, pigeons, veal, and lamb, or with crayfish sauce
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Semmelkoch (Bread Roll Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small bread roll
- 1 seidel cream
- 1/2 quarter-pound butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 10 egg yolks
- sugar to taste
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Pour 1 seidel cream over 1 small bread roll
2. Let it boil and stir over heat until fine
3. Let cool
4. Beat 1/2 quarter-pound butter until high and fluffy
5. Add 2 whole eggs and 10 egg yolks, one after another
6. Add sugar to taste and lemon zest
7. Make a ring mold of dough
8. Bake until done
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Semmelkren (Bread Horseradish Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a little flour
- a piece of butter or skimmed fat
- one handful breadcrumbs
- broth as needed
- grated horseradish
- sugar
- two spoonfuls milk cream
- a little saffron

## Directions

1. Let the flour in a piece of butter or skimmed fat run slightly brown
2. Toast it with one handful of breadcrumbs for a few moments
3. Pour in as much broth as necessary to achieve proper consistency
4. Finally add grated horseradish, some sugar, two spoonfuls of milk cream, and a little saffron
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Semmelkren (Bread Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread rolls
- Light broth
- Salt
- Horseradish

## Directions

1. Remove crusts from bread rolls and cut into flakes
2. Cook well in a pot with a ladle of light broth
3. Season with salt
4. Just before serving, mix in cleaned grated horseradish
5. Serve warm

title: Semmelschmarren (Bread Fritter Pancake)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread rolls, sliced into leaves
- milk
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- salt
- 6 dkg butter or fat
- 5 dkg raisins
- sugar for sprinkling
- compote or raspberry juice for serving

## Directions

1. Slice the bread rolls into leaves and place in a bowl
2. Whisk together milk, 2 eggs, 2 tablespoons sugar, and salt
3. Pour the milk mixture over the bread and let stand for 30 minutes
4. Heat 6 dkg butter or fat in a casserole
5. Add the soaked bread and 5 dkg raisins
6. Let them stew first on the stovetop, then in the oven, stirring frequently, until they are grainy yet soft
7. Sprinkle with sugar and serve with compote or raspberry juice

title: Semmelschöberl (Bread Roll Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deca butter
- 16 Deca peeled, diced bread rolls soaked in milk
- 8 egg yolks
- Salt
- A knife-point of finely chopped parsley
- Snow from 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat 12 Deca butter until fluffy
2. Stir in 16 Deca peeled, diced bread rolls soaked in milk along with 8 egg yolks, some salt, and a knife-point of finely chopped parsley
3. Mix in the snow from 6 eggs
4. Fill and bake as with Kaiserschöberl

title: Semmelstreit (Bread Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons peeled, finely shredded rolls
- 1/2 liter white stock
- 2 tablespoons grated horseradish
- 1/2 teaspoon finely cut nutmeg or garlic
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons cream

## Directions

1. 8 tablespoons peeled, finely shredded rolls are poured with 1/2 liter white stock and cooked with 2 tablespoons grated horseradish, 1/2 teaspoon finely cut nutmeg or garlic, a little salt, and nutmeg
2. 2 egg yolks are whisked with 2 tablespoons cream and mixed well with the bread sauce
3. Eggs and cream can also be omitted

title: Semolina in Milk (Gries in der Milch)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 0.125 liter semolina
- 8 tablespoons sugar
- 1 pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Pour 1 liter milk into a saucepan with 8 tablespoons sugar and 1 pinch of salt if desired
2. Stir in 0.125 liter semolina under constant stirring and let it cook thick
3. Arrange in a serving dish and serve dusted with finely ground sugar and optionally with cinnamon
4. Optionally cook with 4 tablespoons butter or before serving, pour with 4 tablespoons very brown butter

title: Semolina Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 dkg semolina
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Place 8 dkg semolina in a pouring vessel and let it run into boiling strained beef broth while stirring
2. Cook for 5 minutes

title: Senf-Sauce (Remoulade Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 handful tarragon leaves
- equal amount sorrel
- some parsley
- some burnet
- 4 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 5 tablespoons oil
- 2 tablespoons French mustard
- salt
- spice
- 1/8 liter oil
- 3 tablespoons tarragon vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil tarragon leaves, sorrel, parsley, and burnet in salted water until tender
2. Pound with 4 hard-boiled egg yolks in a mortar
3. Dilute with 5 tablespoons oil
4. Add 2 tablespoons French mustard, necessary salt, and some spice
5. Pass through a fine sieve into a deep bowl
6. Place on ice
7. Gradually stir in 1/8 liter oil and 3 tablespoons tarragon vinegar, or juice from a whole lemon

title: Senf-Sauce mit Estragon (Mustard Sauce with Tarragon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pieces cleaned anchovies
- 1 handful tarragon
- 1 handful sorrel
- 1 handful chives
- 3 pieces peeled shallots
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons French mustard
- 4 tablespoons oil
- pinch of pepper

## Directions

1. Cut finely together 8 pieces cleaned anchovies, 1 handful tarragon, sorrel, chives, and 3 pieces peeled shallots
2. Pound together in a mortar with 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
3. Pass into a bowl with 4 tablespoons French mustard
4. Stir in well with 4 tablespoons oil and a pinch of pepper

title: Senfs Sauce (Mustard Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter sauce
- 1 strong tablespoon mustard
- Some roasted parsley

## Directions

1. Add 1 strong tablespoon mustard with some roasted parsley to a well-simmered butter sauce
2. Let it simmer well and pass through a sieve
3. If desired, add some gherkin

title: Senfsauce (Mustard Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Anchovies
- 4 hard egg yolks
- Onions
- Parsley
- Mustard
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Sugar or salt

## Directions

1. Finely chop anchovies, hard egg yolks, onions, and parsley.
2. Mix everything on a plate or in a shallow bowl with mustard, vinegar, oil, and a little finely ground sugar (or salt instead of sugar).
3. Usually served with cold fish or roasts.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Senfschweinsbraten (Mustard Pork Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60-80 dekagrams pork shoulder
- 5 dekagrams bacon
- 1/2 liter cream
- 4 dekagrams fat
- 1 onion
- salt
- paprika
- parsley
- 2 tablespoons French mustard
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, salt it, lard it with bacon rolled in paprika and finely chopped parsley
2. In a roasting pan, brown a finely chopped onion in 4 dekagrams hot fat until light golden
3. Add the meat, pour in broth or water and roast for 1½-2 hours under frequent basting with its own juice and additional liquid
4. Remove the meat, defat the juice, dust it, strain it, mix with 1/2 liter cream and 2 tablespoons French mustard, bring to a boil and serve with the meat

title: Serbian Rice Veal Ragout (Ragout de veau au riz à la serbe)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- broth
- washed rice

## Directions

1. Prepare as the veal goulash, but when the meat is partially braised, add washed rice.
2. Pour broth over so that it covers the rice, and finish braising.

title: Serbisches Reisfleisch (Serbian Rice Veal)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal
- 1/2 liter washed rice
- Broth

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal as described for Veal Goulash
2. When the meat is half-tender from braising, add 1/2 liter of washed rice
3. Pour enough broth over it so that the broth covers the rice
4. Allow it to finish braising

title: Serviettenknödel (Napkin Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 bread rolls
- 1/2 liter milk
- 3 eggs
- salt
- 6 dekagrams melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut the rolls into small cubes and place in a bowl
2. Pour over with milk mixed with 3 eggs, salt and 6 dekagrams melted butter
3. Let stand for 1 hour
4. Place on a well-buttered napkin, form into a roll, bind tightly in the napkin, wrap with string
5. Place in a pan with plenty of boiling salted water and cook for 1 hour
6. Unwrap the roll, cut into slices and arrange in steps on both sides of the meat

title: Sfarmitschken (Sfarmitschken)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca finely pounded sugar
- 3 whole eggs
- 10 deca flour
- wax
- 1/2 tablespoon batter
- 1/4 liter whipped cream
- 16 deca sugar
- 1 coffee cup fresh strawberries or 12 deca vanilla sugar, or any marmalade, or 2 bars grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Stir 14 deca finely pounded sugar and 3 whole eggs for 1 hour and after this time mix well with 10 deca flour.
2. On a baking sheet coated with wax and completely cooled, place 1/2 tablespoon of the above batter at 2 hand-widths distance apart, which you stretch with the spoon back-of-knife thickness lengthwise. Place the sheet in the oven and let it bake at moderate heat until the batter releases from the sheet; then remove the baking sheet from the oven, place it on the warm stove where you hold one end of the baked batter strip at the pointed end of the Sfarmitsche form, turn the batter obliquely over the form so that it forms a cone and then place it on a strainer.
3. Repeat this procedure with a second Sfarmitsche form, which is then also placed on the sheet, whereupon you remove the first twisted Sfarmitsche from the form, place it on a sieve and let it cool. Continue in this way until the batter is used up.
4. Then beat 1/4 liter whipped cream to thick foam, mix lightly with 16 deca sugar and 1 coffee cup fresh strawberries or 12 deca vanilla sugar, or any marmalade, or also with 2 bars grated chocolate and fill the cold Sfarmitschen with this. The Sfarmitschen should be served at table as soon as they are filled as they become soft after 10 minutes.

title: Simple Fish Broth (Bouillon au poisson)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 decagrams butter
- onion cut in thin slices
- 1 kg cleaned, opened, rinsed fish
- ¾ liter cold water
- 2 liters clear pea water

## Directions

1. Place butter and thinly sliced onion in a casserole, add the cleaned fish cut in pieces with the head divided in 4 parts
2. For those not adhering strictly to fasting time, add 16 decagrams thinly sliced beef or calf liver to the onion and roast covered until brown
3. When brown, pour ¾ liter cold water over and braise covered carefully so it does not scorch
4. Pour the brown mixture with 2 liters clear pea water and simmer slowly about 2 hours
5. Remove fat, strain soup through fine sieve and use as desired

title: Sirop d'Orgeade (Almond Syrup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 3 loths bitter almonds
- 1 glass of water
- 1 pound fine sugar
- 1 seidel water

## Directions

1. Carefully clean 1 pound sweet and 3 loths bitter almonds
2. Wash them
3. Pound them very finely with a drinking glass full of water so they do not become oily
4. Cook 1 pound fine sugar with 1 seidel water, stirring frequently, until when you draw a little from the spoon with your second finger, or pull it apart with the help of your thumb, it draws a small thread that breaks immediately and leaves a drop on your thumb
5. Add the pounded almonds gradually to the gentle fire and stir
6. Press through a linen cloth while still warm
7. Fill this syrup into bottles, where it keeps very well for a long time
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Skandinavischen Gesundheitsmilch (Scandinavian Health Milk / Taette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh milk
- Contents of one bottle of 'Vindobona' Taette ferment

## Directions

1. Proceed similarly to the preparation of kefir, using 'Taette' ferment instead of 'Kefir' ferment
2. Let the bottles remain at a temperature of approximately 25°C for 12 to 14 hours instead of 8 to 10 hours

title: Slices of Udder, Grilled, on an Herb Sauce (Schnitze von einem Euter, grillirt, auf einer Kraͤuterſauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- whole udder
- salt
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook a whole udder very tender, then cut it into slices the thickness of a knife back, sprinkle them with salt, turn each slice in melted butter and in breadcrumbs, grill or fry them on both sides on the grate.
2. Meanwhile, an herb sauce is prepared.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Small Pastry for Vienna Torte (kleine Bäckerei zur Vienna Torte)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Vienna Torte dough (Recipe 1502)
- Butter
- Flour
- Icing
- Spanish almond paste

## Directions

1. Fill buttered and floured torte form or flat saucepan with dough
2. Bake at moderate heat until pale golden
3. Turn baked cake onto board
4. Cut out decorative figures with desired cutters
5. Cover with icing
6. When dry, decorate as desired with Spanish almond paste

title: Small Patties of Crayfish (Pastechen von Krebsen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Butter or some veal broth
- White flour
- Egg yolk
- Lemon juice
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook the crayfish and remove the fleshy tail pieces, setting them aside
2. Pound the rest, bring it with butter or some veal broth to the fire
3. Pass it finally through a sieve and bind the passed-through mixture with some white flour stirred in, one egg yolk, a little lemon juice
4. Season gently with salt and let the prepared crayfish tail pieces become warm in this sauce
5. Then fill them into the patties
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Small Patties of Lobster and Crayfish (Pastechen von Seekrebsen und Hummern)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster or crayfish
- One shallot
- Fine herbs
- Meat of one sardine
- Strong vinegar, several spoonfuls
- A little oil or butter
- Lemon juice
- White meat broth

## Directions

1. Boil the lobster or crayfish until soft
2. Remove the head innards and pound them with a shallot, fine herbs, the meat of a sardine
3. Stir over gentle heat with several spoonfuls of strong vinegar and a little oil or butter, almost to boiling
4. Dilute it with some lemon juice and white meat broth and pass it through a clean sieve
5. Meanwhile, cut the meat from the tails and claws of the lobster or crayfish into dice
6. Place this into the strained sauce, add salt and a little spice, and let it become hot together
7. Then fill it into the patties
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Small Peas English Style (Petits pois à l'anglaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine green peas
- Salt water

## Directions

1. Boil fine green peas uncovered in salt water until tender; drain the water well and arrange the peas on a plate.

title: Small Soufflé of Calf Brains (Petit soufflé de cervelle de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Two calf brains, freed of skin and cooked in salted water
- 1.5 tablespoons butter
- 6 tablespoons butter
- 6 tablespoons flour
- 1/8 liter milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 12 tablespoons finely diced ham
- Salt and spices as needed
- 5 egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks
- Puff pastry for lining the mold

## Directions

1. Roast the two prepared brains in 1.5 tablespoons butter for about 3/4 hour
2. Place 6 tablespoons butter, 6 tablespoons flour, and 1/8 liter milk in a saucepan and cook over steady heat, stirring constantly until the mixture loosens from the spoon
3. Pour into a deep bowl and when completely cooled, add 5 egg yolks
4. Mix together with the roasted brains, 12 tablespoons finely diced ham, necessary salt, and spices if desired
5. Lightly fold in the beaten snow of 5 egg whites
6. Line a large, flour-dusted mold (or small shell molds) with puff pastry drawn with a knife back
7. Fill with the mixture leaving a finger's breadth of empty space
8. Brush the edge of the dough with beaten eggs, fold lightly over and bake slowly
9. Serve instead of roasted meat or as an entrée

title: Smothered Goose Breast (Foie-gras braise au madeira)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 beautiful, large goose liver
- bacon slices
- cold milk
- salt
- pepper
- truffles, cut into thin slices
- 1/8 liter Madeira wine or white wine
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove the bile from the goose liver and discard any green spots
2. Wash the goose liver and soak it in cold milk for 1/4 hour so that the milk covers it
3. Remove the liver, place it on cloth, dry it, and dust with salt
4. Using a knife, make 3-4 cuts across the upper side of the liver, about thumb-width apart, and insert thinly sliced truffles into these cuts
5. Line a casserole with bacon slices, place the liver on top, and cover with one sliced bacon slice
6. Place this casserole in a second casserole containing about 3 fingers depth of slowly simmering water
7. Let it stand covered for approximately 1.5 hours, adding 1/8 liter of Madeira or white wine after 1/3 hour and finely sliced truffles as desired
8. Remove the goose liver to a serving dish and garnish with truffle ragout, salmis, puff pastry, oyster dumplings, steamed rice, macaroni or similar
9. Serve the cooking liquid or a strong brown Madeira-wine sauce on the side
10. Alternatively, the liver can be steamed covered in the oven, but then add 3/4 liter of Madeira or white wine at the beginning and pay special attention during steaming as the liver burns easily at too high heat

title: Snail Soup (Soupe aux limaces)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 cooked snails
- butter
- parsley
- semolina breadcrumbs
- 2 liters pea water or water
- cream
- spices (optional)
- toasted semolina slices

## Directions

1. Finely chop the cooked snails
2. Toast them in butter with parsley and semolina breadcrumbs
3. Add 2 liters of pea water or water and cream
4. Simmer
5. Add spices if desired
6. Pour over toasted semolina slices

title: Solls Hirslflaecrmittel Suppe (Hirsute Noodle Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 whole eggs
- approximately 12 deca white flour
- 1 tablespoon full of sweet milk
- 2 liters beef broth

## Directions

1. Mix 4 whole eggs with approximately 12 deca white flour and 1 tablespoon of sweet milk, stirring well so that the dough is liquid
2. In 2 liters of boiling beef broth, pour the dough through a foam ladle or funnel and simmer for 10-15 minutes

title: Solstzig zum Tee (Salty Yeast Pastry for Tea)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deca flour
- 4 Deca butter
- 1 heaping tablespoon salt
- Cold milk
- Beaten egg
- Salt
- Caraway seeds
- Anise

## Directions

1. Place flour on a board and make a well in the center
2. Add butter, salt, and enough cold milk to form a smooth, fine dough
3. Shape into a thick disc, cover, and let rest for 1 hour
4. Cut off a 3-finger-wide piece and roll out knife-back-thick on a floured board
5. Cut out crescent shapes and place on a buttered baking sheet at finger-width intervals
6. Cut another piece from the dough, roll out knife-back-thick, cut out leaf shapes, and place on the sheet
7. Continue until all dough is used, forming sticks, pretzels, and similar shapes as desired
8. Brush everything with beaten egg, sprinkle with very finely crushed salt mixed with caraway seeds and anise
9. Bake in a moderately hot oven with careful attention to prevent burning

title: Sorbet aux Cerises au Vin de Bordeaux (Cherry Sorbet with Bordeaux Wine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Firmly frozen, well-worked Cherry-Frozen
- Bordeaux wine (amount as per the Champagne recipe)

## Directions

1. All sorbets from any desired fruits are prepared in the same way as the Sorbet from Pineapple with Champagne, using white wine for light frozen desserts and red wine for dark ones.

title: Sorbet d'Ananas au Vin de Champagne (Pineapple Sorbet with Champagne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Firmly frozen, well-worked Pineapple-Frozen
- 1/2 liter Champagne wine

## Directions

1. To a firmly frozen, well-worked Pineapple-Frozen, stir in 1/2 liter Champagne wine shortly before serving.
2. Serve it in glasses.

title: Souffle with Fruit (Soufflé aux fruits)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 drams butter
- 12 drams flour
- 12 drams vanilla sugar
- 1/2 liter hot milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites, beaten stiff
- butter for the mold
- flour for dusting
- puff pastry
- marmalade
- pitted and peeled firm apples

## Directions

1. Place butter, flour, and vanilla sugar in a casserole, stir finely, and pour in 1/2 liter boiling milk while stirring constantly.
2. Stir over heat until the dough is dry and releases from the spoon.
3. Pour the dough into a deep bowl and let cool completely.
4. Gradually mix in the 8 egg yolks, then lightly fold in the snow from 6 egg whites.
5. Line a buttered mold, dusted with flour, with butter puff pastry.
6. Fill with half the dough mixture.
7. Fill peeled apples (freed from the core house) with marmalade and place in the filled mold.
8. Add the second half of the dough mixture and cover with a butter pastry sheet, pressing the edges together.
9. Bake in the oven at first at strong heat, later at reduced heat, for a total of 1 1/3 hours.
10. Turn the mold into a dish, dust the soufflé with sugar, and serve.

title: Soufflé in Paper Cases (Soufflé en petites caisses)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 24 drams vanilla sugar
- 9 egg whites, beaten stiff
- 2 drams flour
- thick whipped cream

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks with 24 drams vanilla sugar for 1/2 hour.
2. Lightly fold in the snow from 9 egg whites mixed with 2 drams flour.
3. Lightly blend this with a few spoonfuls of thick whipped cream.
4. Fill paper cups halfway with the mixture.
5. Place on a paper-covered baking sheet.
6. Bake in the preheated oven for 1/4 hour.

title: Soufflé à la Française (French Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg whites (6)
- Sugar, finely crushed (2 tablespoons)
- Flour (2 tablespoons)
- Egg yolks (6)
- Butter (hot)
- Marmalade

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites until stiff
2. Lightly mix with finely crushed sugar, flour and egg yolks
3. Heat a soufflé pan and coat with 1 tablespoon hot butter
4. Pour in half the mixture and bake at fairly high heat in the oven
5. When done, slide the soufflé into a dish using a wooden spoon, brush with dissolved marmalade and place in a warm spot
6. Coat the pan again with 1 tablespoon hot butter and bake the second half of the mixture
7. When done, slide onto the marmalade-coated first soufflé so that one covers the other exactly
8. Sprinkle with sugar and serve
9. Alternatively, both soufflés can be baked on the stove: when the bottom of the first is golden, cover with a second hot buttered pan so the soufflé is in contact with both pans without pressure, turn it so the uncooked side now faces down and bake until golden

title: Soup of Sparrows and Small Birds (Soupe de petits oiseaux)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- sparrows or other birds
- butter
- onion
- 2 liters water
- 16 decagrams black bread cut in thin slices
- salt
- 3 beef marrow
- ¼ liter wine

## Directions

1. Pluck and flambe the birds, then pluck again and let cool
2. Fry them quickly with fat and thinly sliced onion in a saucepan
3. Pound them with the onion in a mortar and place this mixture in a casserole with 2 liters water and 16 decagrams thinly sliced black bread
4. Simmer about 1 hour with salt and the fat in which the birds were fried
5. Strain and serve the soup with diced bread rolls
6. Can add 3 beef marrow spoonfuls sprinkled with ¼ liter wine

title: Soup with Fried Potato Dumplings (Suppe mit gebackenen Kartoffelnockerln)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dkg butter
- 1 egg
- salt
- 15 dkg cooked, passed, and cooled potatoes
- 4-6 dkg flour
- lard for frying

## Directions

1. Cream the butter, stir in the egg, salt, cooled potatoes, and flour
2. Form small dumplings with a spoon
3. Drop into hot lard and fry until light brown
4. Drain and serve warm separately
5. The same mixture can be formed into larger dumplings (Knöderl) and prepared the same way

title: Soup with Semolina Dumplings (Suppe mit Grießnockerln)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dkg butter
- salt
- 1 egg
- 4-5 dkg semolina
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Cream the butter with a pinch of salt
2. Stir in the egg and semolina, let stand for a while
3. Using a spoon dipped in hot broth, form small dumplings and drop into simmering beef broth
4. Let them cook slowly for 5 minutes
5. If the broth boils too vigorously, add a ladle of cold water to reduce the heat
6. Alternatively, use 2 eggs: add the yolks to the creamed butter and fold in the beaten whites together with the semolina; proceed as before

title: Soup with Tyrolean Dumplings
body:
## Ingredients

- 50–70 dkg beef shoulder and tail
- 6–8 rolls
- 1/2 milk
- 12 dkg cured meat

## Directions

1. Cut the rolls into cubes and place in a bowl
2. In 5 dkg hot fat, let a small piece of finely chopped onion and parsley brown, pour over the rolls, and let stand for a while
3. Beat the milk with 1 egg and salt, pour over, mix without stirring much
4. Mix in 2–3 spoons of flour, form dumplings, press flat on a floured board
5. Place chopped ham in the center, close the dough over it, and form dumplings
6. Place in boiling strained beef broth for 8 minutes and cook slowly
7. Alternatively, mix the chopped ham into the dough and proceed as above

title: Sour Cherry Pudding (Weichselkoch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- dried sour cherries
- wine
- butter
- eggs
- egg white
- breadcrumbs or crushed biscuits
- sifted sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon peel, finely cut

## Directions

1. Wash dried sour cherries clean and cook them in wine
2. Press them through a sieve and let them cool
3. Beat butter fluffy
4. Stir in eggs and egg white beaten to foam
5. Take breadcrumbs or finely crushed biscuits, sifted sugar, and cinnamon to taste
6. Add the cooled cherries and finely cut lemon peel
7. Bake it slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sour Cherry Vinegar (Weiclöel-Essig)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh sour cherries
- Cinnamon
- Cloves
- Wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Use fresh sour cherries, which after being removed from their stems are crushed and put into a bottle, filling only the third part
2. Add some cinnamon and a few cloves
3. Fill the bottle completely with genuine wine vinegar, cork it well and place it in the sun
4. When the cherries have settled to the bottom after some time, strain the vinegar through a felt sack, fill it into bottles, cork and seal these, and store them in a cool place

title: Sour Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg turnips
- caraway seeds
- salt
- cold water
- 4 dkg fat
- onion

## Directions

1. Lightly wash the turnips
2. Place them in a pot with caraway seeds, salt, and enough cold water to cover
3. Cook for 1½ hours
4. Add a light onion roux made with 4 dkg fat
5. Cook for 30 minutes more

title: Spanferkel am Spieße gebrätelt (Suckling Pig Roasted on a Spit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- small spits
- twine
- speck or pork fat
- beer

## Directions

1. Prepare the suckling pig as in the previous recipe with the difference that instead of roasting it in the oven, place it on a spit
2. Secure it with small spits to the large spit as well as possible
3. In the absence of small spits, tie the snout and tail with twine so the suckling pig does not rub itself raw from turning
4. Roast it under frequent basting with speck at moderate heat for 1-1 1/2 hours
5. Wipe away any juice emerging from inside the pig with a clean cloth; if neglected, blisters form from which burn spots result, giving the roast a less favorable appearance
6. Once it has a beautiful yellow and crispy crust, remove it from the spit and treat it further as described in the previous recipe

title: Spanferkel auf deutsche Art (German-Style Suckling Pig)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig
- Pig liver
- Blanched bacon
- Truffles
- Mushrooms
- Capers
- Anchovy, soaked
- Garlic, 2 cloves
- Fine herbs
- Provençal oil

## Directions

1. Scald the suckling pig well and clean it
2. Finely chop the liver and cook it in a casserole with blanched bacon, truffles, mushrooms, capers, soaked anchovy, garlic cloves, and fine herbs
3. Season the mixture
4. Stuff the cavity of the suckling pig with this filling
5. Bind it closed
6. Roast it on a spit
7. Baste it with Provençal oil during roasting
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Spanferkel auf Ziegenkitzart (Suckling Pig Prepared as Venison)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- plucked suckling pig
- crushed juniper berries
- vinegar
- red wine
- stock
- Bearnaise sauce or lemon sauce or vinegar and oil

## Directions

1. Let a plucked suckling pig rest for about 2 hours
2. Rub it with crushed juniper berries and burn off the hair with a glowing iron (this should be done by a blacksmith)
3. Cut open the belly and remove the entrails
4. Wash the suckling pig thoroughly, let it rest in water for a time, then remove it and dry it with a cloth
5. Cut it into desired pieces and cook it like venison with root vegetables in a vinegar and red wine mixture
6. Once the meat is sufficiently soft, remove it with a slotted spoon to a platter, pour some stock over it, and serve with Bearnaise sauce, lemon sauce, or vinegar and oil
7. Take great care so the meat does not become overcooked

title: Spanferkel in der Röhre gebraten (Roasted Suckling Pig in the Oven)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- powdered white pitch (colophonium)
- boiling water
- salt
- caraway
- marjoram (optional)
- writing paper
- speck or pork fat
- beer
- peeled almonds
- butter
- butcher's needle
- twine

## Directions

1. Hold the suckling pig so it cannot move, then push a long, narrow, pointed knife through the ribs just below the neck downward so it penetrates the heart
2. Hold the suckling pig with its head downward after removing the knife so the blood runs out well
3. Rub the suckling pig with powdered white pitch and scald with boiling water
4. Strip off the bristles and clean in cold water
5. Cut open the belly and remove the entrails
6. Place in cold water and clean carefully, then place in fresh water and let sit for 5 minutes
7. Remove, dry with a cloth, and rub inside and outside with salt, inside also with caraway and marjoram if desired
8. Insert several sheets of thin writing paper into the opening made in the belly and sew it closed
9. Using a trussing needle and twine, truss the front legs backward and the hind legs forward so the suckling pig rests on its feet
10. Insert a spit or, if unavailable, a piece of wood from the anus to the snout and cut the neck of the suckling pig so the speck is well scored
11. Remove the eyes and replace with peeled almonds
12. Wrap the ears and tail with paper brushed with butter and bind with twine
13. Place the suckling pig on cooking spoons or wooden pieces in a pan with speck or pork fat
14. Roast under frequent basting with speck at moderate heat for 1-1 1/2 hours in the oven
15. If drops of juice emerge from inside the suckling pig during roasting, wipe them away each time with a clean cloth to prevent blistering
16. Once the suckling pig takes on a yellow color, dip a piece of speck on a long fork in beer, brush the pig with it, and repeat often
17. Once the pig has a beautiful yellow and crispy crust, remove the paper from the ears and tail, carefully pull out the twine without injuring the meat, remove the paper in the belly, arrange the suckling pig neatly on a platter, and insert a roasted apple or 1/2 lemon in the snout as the spit or wood is removed

title: Spanferkel mit Öl (Suckling Pig with Oil)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Tarragon (finely chopped)
- Aspic (chopped)
- Mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the suckling pig until tender as with boiled meat
2. When it is in the broth, arrange it on a platter
3. Pour vinegar and oil over it
4. Sprinkle with finely chopped tarragon
5. Garnish with chopped aspic
6. Serve with mustard sauce

title: Spanische Bittorte mit Mandeln (Spanish Almond Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 48 deca peeled, dried, and finely cut almonds
- 8 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 32 deca vanilla sugar
- powdered sugar
- writing paper
- unsalted bacon

## Directions

1. Prepare as with the preceding recipe, with the difference that 48 deca peeled, well-dried, and thereafter finely cut almonds are mixed into the snow mixture.
2. The mixture is then piped onto the paper strips with the aid of a small spoon.

title: Spanische Creme (Spanish Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 spoonfuls rice flour
- the yolk of 5 eggs
- 3 spoonfuls water
- 2 spoonfuls orange blossom water
- 1 seidel good cream

## Directions

1. Stir together 3 spoonfuls rice flour, the yolk of 5 eggs, 3 spoonfuls water, and 2 spoonfuls orange blossom water well
2. Boil 1 seidel good cream and pour it in
3. Let this mixture come together over charcoal fire under constant stirring until it is as thick as required for cream
4. Serve in small cream cups
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Spanische Vögerl (Spanish Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40-50 dkg beef roulade slices
- 2 anchovies
- farce
- 5 dkg hot fat
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- water or broth
- salt
- pepper
- rice, noodles or potato pancakes

## Directions

1. Wipe the meat, trim the skin, tap lightly, salt, pepper, coat with farce, roll up and tie.
2. In a roasting pan, in 5 dkg hot fat, let 1 finely chopped onion turn golden, add the meat.
3. Pour in some water or broth, cover and braise 1 1/2 hours, adding water frequently.
4. Remove the meat, untie it, strain the sauce, optionally pass it through a sieve, serve with the meat.
5. Side dishes: rice, noodles, potato pancakes.

title: Spanischer Kalbsbraten (Spanish Veal Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 70-80 dag boned veal shoulder or leg
- 6 dag root vegetables
- 1 pickled cucumber
- 5 dag bacon
- 5 dag tongue
- ½ liter cream (Rahm)
- 5 dag hot fat
- soup or water

## Directions

1. Wash and salt the meat, then lard it with cucumber, tongue, and bacon; roll and bind if necessary
2. In a roasting pan, brown the prepared, sliced root vegetables and a sliced onion in hot fat
3. Add the meat and pour in soup or water
4. Roast in the oven for 1½-2 hours, basting frequently with its own juices and adding liquid as needed
5. Remove and keep warm
6. Dust the pan juices with flour, strain, mix with cream, bring to a boil again, and serve separately
7. Slice the meat, arrange on a long platter, and garnish with pommes duchesses

title: Spanischer Krapfen von wienerischem Blüdt (Spanish Fritters with Viennese Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg whites
- 32 deca fine pounded sugar
- 18 deca flour

## Directions

1. Whip stiffly the whites of 8 eggs and lightly fold in 32 deca fine pounded sugar
2. Using a paper cornet, pipe in arbitrarily large pointed mounds on a paper-lined baking sheet
3. Sprinkle with fine pounded sugar
4. Dry in a cool oven
5. Prepare a biscuit with 18 deca flour baked knife-back thickness
6. After cooling completely, cut into round finger-thick slices using a pastry cutter
7. From the completely dried and cooled Spanish dough, cut out the center of the bottom and hollow out almost to the point with careful care

title: Spargels Salat (Asparagus Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Spargel (asparagus)
- Butter oder Semmelbröseln
- Butter-Sauce
- Essig
- Öl
- Mayonnaise (optional)

## Directions

1. Der Spargel-Salat wird so zubereitet, wie es bei Dir. 291 angegeben ist, dann ausgeführt, nur wird statt Butter mit Semmelbröseln oder Butter-Sauce, Essig und Öl beigegeben. Kann auch mit beliebiger Mayonnaise serviert werden.

title: Spargels-Erbsen (Pois d'asperges)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- cold water
- salt water
- butter sauce
- green parsley
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and cut asparagus into pea-sized pieces
2. Place in cold water
3. Cook in salt water until tender
4. Drain and place in cold water
5. In a butter sauce, roast some green parsley and add sugar to taste
6. Add the drained asparagus peas, bring to a boil and serve

title: Spargelſalat (Asparagus Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Salted water
- Fresh water
- Beautiful yellow head lettuce
- Wine vinegar
- Provençal oil
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean the asparagus as usual and tie it in bundles
2. Cook it until tender and drain the water off completely
3. Arrange it on a serving dish so that the heads point inward and the asparagus becomes completely cold
4. Take beautiful yellow head lettuce, wash it clean
5. Arrange it star-shaped in the dish with the asparagus
6. Salt it and pour over it a mixture of good wine vinegar with Provençal oil about a quarter hour before serving at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Speckeier (Bacon and Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 dkg bacon
- 6 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into thin slices, line an omelet pan with them.
2. Lightly roast the bacon and break the eggs into the pan next to each other.
3. Salt, pepper, and sprinkle with a little vinegar.
4. When the eggs are set, either turn them with a spatula or let them brown a bit more, then lift onto a plate and serve immediately.

title: Speckeier (Bacon and Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 dkg Speck (bacon)
- 6 Eier (eggs)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into thin slices and line an omelet pan with it.
2. Lightly brown the bacon and break the eggs into the pan side by side.
3. Season with salt and pepper and spray with a bit of vinegar.
4. When the eggs are set, either turn them with a spatula or let them fry a bit more on the bottom, then lift onto a plate and serve immediately.

title: Specklinſen (Lentils with Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 l lentils
- cold water, hand-high amount
- 12 dkg bacon
- salt

## Directions

1. Sort and wash the lentils.
2. Cover them with cold water, hand-high amount.
3. Cover and braise for about 1 hour, braising more than boiling.
4. Do not stir much so the lentils do not fall apart.
5. Strain them and arrange on a round platter.
6. Salt them.
7. Cut the bacon into small cubes, roast it until light yellow, strain it and pour over the lentils.
8. Place the cracklings in the middle.

title: Speisen von Zuckerteia (Preparations of Sugar Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled, sifted egg yolks
- 14 deka butter

## Directions

1. Finely pound 6 hard-boiled, sifted egg yolks with 14 deka butter
2. Place on a board and mix with 14 deka sugar syrup

title: Spickgans (Larded Goose)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One well-nourished, fat goose
- Salt
- Wheat bran
- Twine

## Directions

1. After slaughter, pluck the goose very carefully so as not to damage the skin
2. Singe it at a straw fire
3. Wash well with water and bran
4. Cut wings and neck close to the body
5. Rub with salt and lay in a barrel until after three days the salt has penetrated
6. Roll the salted geese in wheat bran
7. Hang by the legs with twine in smoke for eight days
8. Store like any other smoked meat
9. Can be eaten raw or cooked
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Spider Crab (Meersspinnen)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Spider crab
- Salt water
- Salt
- Green parsley (optional)
- Breadcrumbs
- Lemon juice
- Cold oil

## Directions

1. Cook the spider crabs like crayfish in salt water until the water begins to foam
2. Remove them and take off their legs
3. Remove all meat from the shell and upper limbs
4. Discard the eggs, paired organs and anything black as unusable
5. Fill the shell, sprinkle with salt, optionally with some green parsley and breadcrumbs
6. Drip some lemon juice and cold oil on top
7. Place the shells on the grill, put in the oven for 5 minutes and arrange to serve
8. Note that spider crabs are especially fatty at full moon

title: Spiegel-Eier (Fried Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter or lard
- Eggs
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Put a hazelnut-sized piece of butter or lard in each indentation of a muffin mold.
2. When hot, crack an egg into each indentation, dust the eggs with salt and pepper and cook until the egg white is completely set.
3. Remove the eggs with 2 metal spoons and serve on puree, vegetables or sauces.

title: Spinach (Epinards)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 handfuls fresh, cleaned spinach
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 8 drams butter
- 8 drams flour
- Small, finely cut rolls
- Garlic, crushed (if desired)
- ½ liter broth
- Salt
- Pepper (if desired)

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned spinach in boiling salt water; cook it uncovered so it retains its green color; drain it well, place it in cold water, press it thoroughly, and chop it finely.
2. Make a roux from 8 drams butter and 8 drams flour; stir in some finely cut rolls and, if desired, a little crushed garlic.
3. Add the spinach, pour ½ liter broth over it; season to taste, stir, and let it cook until it boils.

title: Spinach (Spinat)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg spinach
- salt
- 5 dkg butter
- spinach water or broth
- pepper

## Directions

1. Remove bad leaves and stems from spinach, wash thoroughly
2. Place in boiling salted water and cook uncovered for 20 minutes
3. Drain and pass through a sieve
4. Make a light roux with 5 dkg butter and moisten with spinach water or broth
5. Add the spinach, season with salt and pepper, cook well
6. Alternative: moisten the spinach with milk instead of broth or water

title: Spinach Butter (Beurre à l'Epinard)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 handfuls washed spinach
- butter

## Directions

1. Pound the washed spinach very finely in a mortar
2. Strain the pounded mixture
3. Pour the strained juice into a small saucepan and leave it on the fire until it begins to reduce and becomes thick
4. Pour the whole mixture onto a sieve so the liquid drains off quickly
5. The remaining thick mass serves as the coloring agent for butter, Mayonnaise, soup, sauces, vegetables, and the like

title: Spinach Noodles (Spinatnüdeln)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/3 liter of flour
- 8 egg yolks
- 1/2 liter of cream
- 30 tablespoons of cooked, lean, finely sliced ham
- 1/2 teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from 1/3 liter of flour
2. Cut the thinly rolled sheets into arbitrarily large squares
3. Add to boiling salted water and cook covered until they rise to the surface of the water
4. Drain in a colander and rinse with cold water and let stand
5. Place 8 egg yolks in a deep bowl
6. Add 1/2 liter of cream gradually
7. Add 30 tablespoons of cooked, lean, finely sliced ham
8. Add the drained noodles and 1/2 teaspoon of salt one after another
9. Gently fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 8 eggs
10. Fill a buttered, floured mold with this mixture

title: Spinach with Cream (Epinards à la crème)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 8 drams hot butter
- Cream
- Salt
- Pepper (if desired)
- Bread rolls

## Directions

1. Boil the same amount of spinach as above in salt water, then pour cold water over it; pass it through a sieve once the water has drained sufficiently.
2. Place the strained spinach in a casserole with 8 drams hot butter, the necessary cream, some salt, and if desired, a pinch of pepper; let it boil well while stirring frequently.
3. Cut bread rolls into desired pieces or hollow them out, toast them in butter, and serve alongside.

title: Spinach à la Française (Epinards à la française)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper (if desired)
- Semolina biscuits or bread

## Directions

1. Boil the spinach as described above, pass it through a sieve, and bring it to a boil with 4 drams butter, the necessary salt, and if desired, pepper, stirring constantly.
2. Serve as a side dish or on a plate with toasted bread slices (Croutons).

title: Spinat mit Butter (Spinach with Butter)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- water
- butter
- salt
- nutmeg
- cream

## Directions

1. When the spinach is carefully sorted and washed, blanch it in water uncovered, otherwise it becomes yellow
2. Then pour off with cold water and press out well
3. Pass it in good butter with salt and some nutmeg
4. Finally pour in a cup of good cream
5. Arrange it very hot. Spinach can also be used for various dishes, for egg dishes, rissoles, tarts and more
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Spitzbuben (Cream-Filled Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 ounces butter
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 30 ounces flour
- 8 whole eggs
- eggs for brushing
- flour for dusting
- Vanilla chocolate or cream for filling

## Directions

1. Add salt to the butter and flour, stirring constantly over heat for 30 minutes until the dough is dry and separates from the pan
2. Empty the dough into a deep bowl and after complete cooling, stir in the 8 eggs gradually
3. Using a tablespoon, remove dough pieces of desired size and spread them on a buttered and floured baking sheet at 3-finger width intervals
4. Brush with beaten eggs and bake in a fairly hot oven until golden
5. Remove with a thin knife, cut them open at the side to the middle, and fill as desired with vanilla chocolate or cream
6. Arrange elevated on a dish and serve warm or cold
7. Alternatively, Spitzbuben can be made in very hot lard instead of on the sheet: place dough pieces with a tablespoon into hot lard, but not too many at once as they double in size during frying
8. When brown on both sides with occasional shaking of the pan, place briefly on blotting paper, arrange elevated on a dish, sprinkle with sugar, and serve with cream of choice

title: Spitzbuben (Jam Sandwich Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 17 dkg flour
- 14 dkg butter
- 7 dkg sugar
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make a crumbly dough from flour, butter, sugar, and egg yolks
2. Let it rest
3. Roll it out and cut out rounds
4. Cut out eyes from half of the rounds with a small round cutter
5. Place all rounds on an unbuttered baking sheet
6. Bake at higher heat until light brown
7. Spread jam on a whole round, place a round with eyes on top, and sprinkle with sugar

title: Spritz Krapfen (Piped Choux Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter milk
- Lard for frying
- Powdered sugar for serving
- Cream or warm marmalade for serving

## Directions

1. Use choux pastry made from 1/4 liter milk, fill it into a metal piping syringe with a star-shaped opening.
2. Using the wooden pestle, pipe the dough from the syringe in cross or feather shapes into the hot lard.
3. When the fritters are browned on one side, turn them with a skimming spoon and fry them equally on the second side, shaking the pan frequently during this time.
4. When the fritters have puffed up to twice their height and have a brown, crispy crust, remove them with a skimming spoon, place on paper towels for a few moments, then arrange elevated in a dish, sprinkle with finely powdered sugar and serve hot.
5. Cream or warm marmalade can be served with them as desired.

title: Spritzkrapfen (Fried Choux Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- choux pastry made from: 1/2 l milk, 5 dkg butter, salt, 15 dkg flour, 2-3 small eggs
- hot lard for frying
- sugar
- Chaudeau or raspberry juice
