## Directions

1. Prepare choux pastry from milk, butter, salt, flour and eggs (see Teige section).
2. The dough must be quite stiff.
3. Fill the pastry bag with the dough and pipe various shapes like sticks, rings, and spirals into hot lard.
4. Fry until light brown.
5. Drain and sprinkle with sugar.
6. Make a test puff first - if the dough is too loose, add more flour and stir well.
7. Serve warm with Chaudeau or raspberry juice.

title: Sprouts
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Sprouts
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 8 drams butter
- 8 drams flour
- Broth
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut away as much of the white stem as needed so the sprout does not fall apart; boil it in salt water and place it in cold water.
2. Make a butter sauce from 8 drams butter and 8 drams flour; pour it with broth over the drained sprouts; add the necessary salt and let it boil a bit longer.

title: Sprouts Another Way
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Sprouts
- Salt water
- Hot butter
- Toasted bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the sprouts as above in salt water; place them in 8 drams hot butter, stir them well together, and use as a garnish.
2. May also be topped with toasted bread crumbs.

title: Sproſſen-Kohl (Brussels Sprouts)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Small sprouts
- Water, lightly salted
- 12 Loth butter
- Salt
- White pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the small sprouts of their coarser leaves
2. Cook them soft in plenty of lightly salted water, such that only the outer leaves are easily crushed; the interior of the small sprout must remain somewhat firm
3. Drain the cooked sprouts well
4. Season one measure of the sprouts in 12 Loth butter with salt and a little white pepper
5. Stew over strong heat for a quarter hour, such that the accumulated juice evaporates
6. Serve with an accompaniment of bratwurst, pickled tongues, or mutton cutlets, etc. as a vegetable
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stachelbeeren-Sauce (Gooseberry Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 deca butter
- equal amount flour
- 3/4 liter ripe gooseberries, mashed
- necessary broth
- some white wine

## Directions

1. Make a roux from butter and flour
2. Add the mashed ripe gooseberries
3. Pour in the necessary broth and some white wine
4. Season to taste and let it cook well
5. This sauce can be served strained or unstrained

title: Stachelbeeren-Sauce (Gooseberry Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablespoons butter
- 6 tablespoons flour
- 3/4 liter ripe crushed gooseberries
- necessary broth
- some white wine

## Directions

1. Make a roux of 6 tablespoons butter with equal amount flour
2. Add 3/4 liter very ripe crushed gooseberries
3. Pour in with the necessary broth and some white wine
4. Season to taste and let it boil thoroughly
5. This sauce can be passed or served unpasseed

title: Staerkende Suppe fur Genesende (Strengthening Soup for Convalescents)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One egg
- Wine or sugar and cinnamon, or meat broth
- Nutmeg
- Yellow-toasted bread rolls or white bread slices

## Directions

1. Boil one egg until somewhat hard and press it through
2. Mix either with wine, or sugar and cinnamon, or with meat broth into which a little nutmeg has been added
3. Prepare this broth over yellow-toasted bread roll or white bread slices
4. Give the patient this soup to eat every morning
5. Alternatively, omit the egg and spices, soak the bread slice in some wine or lemon juice, and pour thin meat broth over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Staffierte gebackene Erdäpfels-Scheiben à la Duchesse (Duchess Potatoes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams fine butter
- 3 egg yolks
- approximately 1/2 kilogram mashed potatoes
- salt
- nutmeg
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix 8 decagrams fine butter with 3 egg yolks thoroughly
2. Stir in approximately 1/2 kilogram mashed potatoes, along with salt and nutmeg to make a dough-like mass
3. Roll the mixture finger-thick on a board dusted with flour
4. Cut out shapes with a cutter dipped in flour
5. Place in very hot lard and let brown on both sides to a light golden color
6. The lard should be only finger-high in the pan so the potatoes do not fall apart

title: Stark eröffnende Tisane (Strongly Opening Tisane)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds each of quitch grass and grass roots and the roots of Pfaffenröhrlein
- two Maß of water
- six Quentchen of polychrest salt
- one half pound of oxymel

## Directions

1. Take quitch grass and grass roots and the roots of Pfaffenröhrlein, one and a half pounds each
2. Cook them for two hours in two Maß of water until one Maß remains
3. Press strongly through a cloth
4. Add six Quentchen of polychrest salt and one half pound of oxymel
5. Take half a tea cup full every two hours for fourteen days or three to four weeks in succession
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Starker, haltbarer Essig (Strong, Long-Lasting Vinegar)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Vinegar
- Wine

## Directions

1. Let the vinegar freeze together with wine
2. Pour the unfrozen portion into bottles
3. This vinegar will keep for a long time
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Steamed Cucumbers with Pork Cutlets (Gedünstete Umurken mit Schweins-Coteletten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- Salt
- Beer vinegar
- Flour
- Butter
- Sugar
- Pepper
- Nutmeg flower
- Basil
- Pork cutlets or other roasted meat

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, cut them into slices, and pickle them with salt for a couple of hours; then strain off the much extracted water, and put them with good beer vinegar over the fire.
2. When they are almost cooked enough, roast a couple of spoonfuls of flour in butter in a casserole, stir it smooth with the vinegar from the cooked cucumbers, break the sourness with sugar, season with pepper, nutmeg flower and basil, add the cucumbers, and let them braise.
3. On meat days you can serve them with pork cutlets or other roasted meat; on fast days with omelets and other egg dishes.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Steamed Mutton Leg (Gedämpfter Hammelſchlägel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fat mutton leg
- Coarse bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Dried herbs, finely ground
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Bay leaf
- Parsley
- Potatoes, peeled
- Bacon strips
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Beat a fat mutton leg properly
2. Loosen the end bones and trim the longer parts
3. Cut coarse bacon
4. Turn it repeatedly in salt, pepper, spices, and finely ground dried herbs, and pierce it with this mixture
5. Place it in a long casserole with some yellow carrots and whole onions, cloves, a bay leaf, a bunch of parsley, some peeled potatoes, salt, pepper, and bacon strips
6. Set the casserole on charcoal fire with all its contents
7. Pour in a couple of spoonfuls of bouillon
8. Cover it then
9. In this way the leg must cook for nearly two hours and be turned often
10. When serving, dress it on the platter with the vegetables around it
11. Skim off the fat
12. Reduce the sauce briefly and pour it over the mutton leg
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Steamed Pheasant with Sauerkraut (Faisan braise à la choucroute)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant, dressed and trussed
- Bacon lardons
- Ham slices
- Onion, sliced in leaves
- 1 bay leaf
- Bacon slices
- 1/2 liter white wine
- Stock
- Sauerkraut, steamed

## Directions

1. Line a casserole with bacon slices, ham slices, thinly sliced onion, and 1 bay leaf
2. Place the dressed and trussed pheasant wrapped with bacon slices into the casserole and braise covered
3. After approximately 1.5 hours, pour in 1/2 liter white wine and some stock and continue braising covered until the meat is tender
4. Remove the pheasant carefully after it has cooled somewhat in its own juices
5. Remove the bacon, cut the trussing string, and carve the pheasant
6. Reassemble on a platter and serve with defatted juices and steamed sauerkraut

title: Steamed Rutabagas with Smoked Meat (Gedämpfte Steckrüben mit geräuchertem Fleisch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Rutabagas
- Melted beef fat or butter
- Anis
- Sugar
- Butter
- Meat broth
- Flour
- Smoked meat

## Directions

1. Place the scraped and lengthwise-cut rutabagas in a pan with melted beef fat or butter, and roast them until they turn brownish.
2. Then place a layer of them in an earthenware vessel, sprinkle anis and sugar over it, lay butter pieces over it, then rutabagas again, and so on until the rutabagas are arranged alternately in the vessel.
3. Pour meat broth over it, let the rutabagas simmer slowly, and finally sprinkle a little flour over it to thicken the broth.
4. Then arrange and garnish it with pieces of smoked meat.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Steamed Veal Breast (Tendrons de veau à l'étuveé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal breast
- veal forcemeat (about 1/2 pound)
- boiled ham or tongue, cut into pencil-thick pieces
- lard, cut similarly
- truffles
- 1 small goose liver, roasted and cut into pieces
- lard strips
- broth
- white wine

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from a veal breast and cut out the cartilage.
2. Pound the meat with the flat of a cleaver and season with salt and pepper.
3. Spread veal forcemeat on the inner side from which the bones were removed.
4. Arrange pencil-thick, finger-long pieces of cooked ham or tongue, similarly cut lard, truffles, and small roasted goose liver pieces on top.
5. Roll the meat very tightly and bind securely with kitchen twine.
6. Place in a casserole lined with lard, cover the top with lard strips, and roast for about 1.5 hours.
7. Remove the meat, untie the string, cut into thick slices forming round pieces, and serve with defatted sauce.

title: Steckrüben mit Kalbskaldaunen (Rutabaga with Calf Offal)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- rutabagas
- beef broth
- flour
- rendered fat or butter
- sugar
- salt
- calf offal (tripe)
- wine vinegar
- water
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the rutabagas as usual and cook them in beef broth.
2. In rendered fat or butter, fry a little flour, dissolve it with some of the broth from the rutabagas, add a little sugar and salt, and let them cook down so that only a small amount of sauce remains.
3. Meanwhile, cut the calf offal into small pieces, remove all the fatty tissue, rub with salt thoroughly, and wash in water until all slime is gone.
4. Make a light roux with a few drops of wine vinegar in a pan, add the offal, cook it in water, skim it properly, and let it become very tender.
5. Drain it well on a colander, add it to the rutabagas, and toss together.
6. Alternatively: turn the cooked offal in butter and breadcrumbs, fry it in paper capsules on a grate, and use it to garnish the arranged rutabagas.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stefanie Kartoffeln (Stefanie Potatoes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca blanched almonds
- 4 egg yolks
- 14 deca sifted sugar
- 8 egg whites
- 14 deca sugar
- 4 egg whites for cream filling
- 14 deca sugar for cream
- Chocolate, coffee, vanilla or other flavorings for cream

## Directions

1. Pound the 14 deca blanched almonds very finely with 4 egg yolks
2. Stir in 14 deca sifted sugar
3. Beat 8 egg whites to stiff peaks and mix with 14 deca sugar
4. Fold the pounded almonds into the egg white mixture
5. Fill a pastry bag with the mixture and pipe small, equal-sized puffs onto a paper-lined baking sheet
6. Sprinkle with sugar and dry in a hot oven for 1/4 hour
7. While still warm, remove from the paper
8. Prepare the cream filling: beat 4 egg whites to stiff peaks and mix with 14 deca sugar, adding desired flavoring such as chocolate, coffee, or vanilla
9. Once completely cooled, fill the puffs with cream, pair two together, brush with thin warm marmalade, and glaze
10. Alternatively, fill with whipped cream and do not glaze

title: Steinbutte (Turbot)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Turbot
- Salt water
- Sauce Hollandaise

## Directions

1. The turbot, the largest of the flatfish, has a very appetizing, though somewhat firm flesh
2. The flesh on the underside, which is covered with white skin, is finer than that on the upper side, from which the scales must be removed and the entrails near the head removed
3. Boil the turbot in salt water 10 minutes longer than freshwater fish
4. Serve with Sauce Hollandaise

title: Ster-Krapfen (Star Fritters)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deka flour
- 12 deka butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/8 liter sour cream
- 2 deka sugar
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put 32 deka flour on a strudel board, cut 12 deka butter into it, and work this with the flour
2. Make a hollow, crack 3 egg yolks into it, add 1/8 liter sour cream, 2 deka sugar, and some salt
3. Work this into a dough until blisters become visible, then let it rest for an hour in a cool place
4. Roll it out knife-back thick, cut from it finger-long and finger-wide strips, whose both ends are cut at an angle
5. Six such strips, of which the two lowest are the longest, the two middle ones somewhat shorter, and the two topmost the shortest, are now laid cross-wise over each other so that the resulting figure forms a star
6. Each dough strip is dotted in the middle with beaten eggs before the next one is laid on it, which makes one strip hold the other; on the last, topmost strip, press strongly in the middle with the finger so that a hollow forms
7. These star fritters are baked in hot fat until pale yellow, shaking the saucepan somewhat; then they are sprinkled with vanilla or desired spice sugar, and in each hollow something put in the middle of each groove
8. Some marmalade is put and served this way. You can also serve sauce with it

title: Sterz von Furutzmehl (Buckwheat Polenta)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter boiling water
- 1/2 liter buckwheat flour

## Directions

1. Put 1 liter of boiling water in a fairly large pot
2. Add 1/2 liter buckwheat flour
3. Cook covered for 15 minutes
4. Turn the flour mass with a wooden spoon so the bottom comes to the top
5. Cook covered again for 15 minutes
6. Continue with the treatment described in the recipe for 'Sterz von Haidenmehl'

title: Sterz von Haidenmehl (Millet Polenta)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter water
- 1/2 liter millet flour
- 12 decagrams hot lard or rendered fat

## Directions

1. Put 1 liter water in a fairly large pot and when it boils, add 1/2 liter millet flour
2. Cook covered for 10 to 15 minutes
3. Turn the flour mass with a wooden spoon so the lower side comes to the top
4. Cook covered again for 10 to 15 minutes, stirring through the flour mass with a wooden spoon
5. Pour off the water that has not evaporated
6. Stir the flour mass well together
7. Put it in a flat casserole, pour 12 decagrams hot lard or rendered fat over it
8. Let it stand covered over low heat for 1/4 hour
9. Divide the Sterz into smaller pieces with a broad spoon
10. Arrange raised on a dish, pour hot lard or fat cracklings over it
11. Serve if desired with beef broth, mushroom broth or boiled milk, or serve with tea

title: Sterz von Hirsebreі (Millet Porridge)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter millet
- water
- 12 decagrams hot butter
- onion
- lard cracklings

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 liter washed millet in a casserole
2. Pour so much cold water over it that it comes finger-high above the millet
3. Cook covered until soft and thick
4. When no liquid remains, pour 12 decagrams hot butter over it, in which onion has been roasted if desired
5. Let it stand covered over low heat
6. Put the Sterz on a dish, sprinkle with lard cracklings and serve hot

title: Stettins Sauce (Horseradish Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- About 15 small carrots
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 8 tablespoons butter
- Equal amount of flour
- Finely chopped green parsley or tarragon
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- 1/4 liter sour cream
- Vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Peel about 15 small carrots, cut them finely into thin slices
2. Cook in salt water, then place in cold water
3. Make a light roux from 8 tablespoons butter and equal amount of flour
4. Add finely chopped green parsley or tarragon, let roast a bit, stir in with 1/2 liter soup
5. Season with salt and nutmeg and let simmer well
6. Add the strained carrots with 1/4 liter sour cream and cook once more
7. As desired, one can season this sauce with vinegar or lemon juice

title: Stewed Pike or Ragout (Brochet en ragout)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deca butter
- 1 piece onion
- several leaves parsley root
- 2 pieces finely sliced mushrooms
- 1 cleaned and split fish collar or broken pike head
- 3/4 liter pea water or clear water
- 5 bunches combined parsley sprigs
- 12 deca butter
- equal amount flour
- juice of 1 lemon
- salt
- pike cut into pieces
- salt water for cooking pike
- optional: cooked and stewed mushrooms
- optional: crayfish tails
- optional: green peas
- optional: 1/4 liter white wine
- optional: 1/8 liter Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Put butter and diced onion, parsley root leaves, mushroom pieces, and cleaned and split fish collar or broken pike head into a casserole
2. Let brown covered
3. Pour on 3/4 liter pea water or clear water and add 5 bunches of tied parsley sprigs
4. Let cook slowly covered for 1/2 hour
5. Make a very light roux from 12 deca butter and equal amount flour, pour it onto the stock and let cook well while stirring constantly
6. Strain the sauce, let it come to a boil again, skim off fat, and add juice of 1 lemon and necessary salt
7. Arrange pike pieces cut into pieces and cooked in salt water elevated on a platter and garnish with breaded, pea, or harlequin dumplings
8. Stir the sauce and pour over the meat and dumplings
9. Serve remaining sauce in a sauceboat
10. Optional additions: cooked and stewed mushrooms, crayfish tails, and green peas may be added with the prepared sauce
11. Optional: 1/4 liter white wine may be added with the pea water and let cook together, or sauce may be mixed shortly before serving with 1/8 liter Madeira wine

title: Stewed Pike with Anchovies (Gedünsteten Hecht mit Sardellen)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onions
- shallots
- lemon peel
- anchovies
- parsley
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the pike as before
2. Cut it whole or into pieces
3. Place in a pot with salt and pepper
4. Add a piece of butter, finely cut onions, shallots and lemon peel
5. Stew it well
6. Before it is completely cooked, add some finely cut anchovies with parsley and a little lemon juice
7. Arrange the pike with this sauce
8. It can also be cut into pieces, fried with butter, anchovies and lemon peel
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stewed Plums
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh pitted plums
- water
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Place fresh pitted plums in a casserole with some water
2. Add sugar and cinnamon to taste
3. Braise covered until soft
4. Add several spoonfuls of butter sauce
5. Let it come to the boil
6. If desired, add some white or red wine or crushed spices

title: Stick-Cut Almond Pudding (Geſtifteltes Mandelkoch)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound sugar
- fourteen loths ground almonds
- eight whole eggs
- lemon peel
- fourteen loths stick-cut almonds
- butter

## Directions

1. Take three-quarters pound sugar, cook it nicely thick, and let it cool
2. Take fourteen loths ground almonds into the sugar and stir well
3. Beat in eight whole eggs, one after the other
4. Add lemon peel and finally fourteen loths stick-cut almonds
5. Stir everything well together
6. Coat a mold with butter and bake it slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stirred Eggs with Asparagus and Crayfish Tails (Eingerührte Eyer mit Spargel und Krebsschweiferln)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a bundle of asparagus, boiled in salted water and cut into oblong pieces
- 20 crayfish tails, removed and each cut in half
- twelve eggs
- salt
- pepper, pounded
- a piece of crayfish butter

## Directions

1. Take a bundle of asparagus that has been boiled in salted water and cut into oblong pieces, and from 20 crayfish remove the tails and cut each in half.
2. Beat twelve eggs in a pot, salt them and stir them together, add the asparagus and crayfish tails to the eggs, add some pounded pepper, and stir everything well together.
3. Let a piece of crayfish butter become warm, add the whole mixture into it, stir it often, and break it into small pieces.
4. When serving at table, arrange it on a small round dish; pile it high in the middle, and arrange crayfish tails around the edge.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stirred Eggs with Asparagus and Crayfish Tails (Eingerührte Eyer mit Spargel und Krebſchweiferln)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus boiled in salt water, cut into small pieces
- crayfish tails from 20 crayfish, halved

## Directions

1. Take a bundle of asparagus boiled in salt water and cut into small pieces
2. Remove the tails from 20 crayfish and halve each one
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stirred Eggs with Breadcrumbs
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- breadcrumb pieces
- milk
- salt
- sugar
- chopped lemon zest
- fresh egg yolks
- egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Soak breadcrumb pieces in milk for about 2 hours until very soft
2. Pass them through a fine sieve
3. Add a little salt, sugar, and chopped lemon zest
4. Beat in as many fresh egg yolks as needed
5. Mix with the breadcrumbs
6. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in
7. Coat a casserole with butter
8. Place the mixture inside
9. Bake in the oven or under a cake cover with heat from below and above
10. Turn out onto a serving dish
11. Sprinkle with sugar
12. Glaze with a hot shovel
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stockfischpudding (Dried Cod Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds stockfish
- 1/2 pound butter, beaten to a foam
- 10 eggs
- finely chopped onion
- salt
- nutmeg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch 5 pounds stockfish, which should only draw, not boil, lay it out when cooked so it drains well, free it from bones, and chop it finely.
2. Add it to 1/2 pound butter beaten to a foam, along with 10 eggs, some finely chopped onion, salt, nutmeg, and a piece of sugar, and stir everything well together.
3. Cook this mass for one hour in a pot of simmering salt water according to the method above.
4. Serve with a butter sauce poured over it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stockfischschauce (Stockfish Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Fresh mushrooms
- Fine onions
- Garlic (optional)
- Finely chopped parsley
- Flour
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Sauté fresh mushrooms with fine onions in butter, and if desired, with a small piece of garlic until soft.
2. Mix in some finely chopped parsley.
3. Stir in approximately one spoonful of flour.
4. Fill the casserole with bouillon.
5. Let it reduce to half.
6. Before serving over cooked stockfish, pass this sauce through a sieve.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stockfish in Bouillon (Morue Bouillie)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 prepared stockfish
- Salted water

## Directions

1. After the skin is removed and the fish has soaked one hour in fresh water, place it in enough boiling salted water so that the water covers it completely.
2. Bring the stockfish to a boil once covered; then place it on the side of the stove and let it stand covered in its broth for 1.5 hours.
3. After this time, pour off the water, lay the stockfish on a cloth so the water drips off.
4. If desired, either serve the fish in one piece or break it into smaller pieces with a wooden spoon, place these in a bowl and cover with anchovy butter or breadcrumbs.
5. Finely sliced onion, if desired, is browned until golden in hot butter and then poured over the arranged dish.

title: Stockfish with Anchovy Butter (Stocffifd mit Sardellenbutter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Stockfish
- Anchovy butter
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Take the cooked and arranged stockfish (see recipe 968), sprinkle with breadcrumbs and instead of fried onion, pour anchovy butter over it.

title: Stockfish with Oil and Anchovy (Stocffifd mit Öl und Anchovis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo cooked stockfish
- Browned onion
- Green parsley, finely chopped
- 2 cloves crushed garlic
- 6 tablespoons oil

## Directions

1. Remove the cooked stockfish from the bones and place the meat, still warm from the broth, in a casserole.
2. Add browned onion, finely chopped green parsley and 2 cloves of crushed garlic.
3. Pour 6 tablespoons of oil over it and shake the casserole over the fire until the fish inside is very hot.

title: Stockfish with Potatoes and Sour Cream (Morue à la crème aigre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo prepared stockfish
- Anchovy butter
- Potatoes
- 1 liter cream
- 2 egg yolks
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Boil the stockfish and then cut it into neat pieces.
2. Spread a bowl with anchovy butter, line it with blanched, finely sliced potatoes.
3. Add a layer of the cooked stockfish on top and sprinkle with blanched, finely diced anchovies.
4. Whisk 1 liter of cream with 2 egg yolks and pour some of this mixture over the stockfish.
5. Repeat this process until all stockfish is used.
6. On the final layer, add again boiled, finely sliced potatoes, cover with some cream, sprinkle with breadcrumbs and cover with anchovy butter.
7. Place the arranged bowl in the oven and let it sit for approximately 2 to 3 hours so that the dish turns golden and then serve.

title: Stockfiſch (Stockfish)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Stockfish
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Toasted breadcrumbs
- Brown onions

## Directions

1. Soak the stockfish thoroughly in cold water
2. Place on heat and cook until soft as an egg
3. Remove the skin and lay on a napkin
4. Arrange on a platter
5. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
6. Pour over clear butter and toasted breadcrumbs as well as brown onions
7. Keep warm until serving
8. Alternatively, after boiling and salting, it can be breaded in eggs, flour and breadcrumbs and fried, being only lightly golden and not heavily fried
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Strasbourg Pâté (Strasburger Pâté in Terrrine)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 duck or goose livers
- 1/2 kilo diced bacon
- 1 handful of finely cut yellow roasted onions
- 2 tablespoons rum
- pâté seasoning
- 1 duck liver
- clarified lard with ham

## Directions

1. Cut out the hearts, veins, and bloody fibers from 4 duck livers.
2. Round them by cutting the edges, place the livers in cold water, remove after 10 minutes, and lay on a cloth to dry.
3. Make 3 cuts on the curved side of each liver lengthwise.
4. Rub with pâté seasoning and place finely cut truffles into the cuts.
5. Pound the scraps from the livers with 1/2 kilo diced bacon in a mortar and pass with 1 handful of finely cut yellow roasted onions through a sieve into a bowl.
6. Mix well with 2 tablespoons rum and pâté seasoning.
7. Line a pâté terrine with 1 duck liver, add a spoonful of the prepared forcemeat, alternate livers and forcemeat until the terrine is full.
8. Cover with a fitting lid and seal.
9. Cook in a bain-marie for 1 hour for smaller terrines or up to 3.5 hours for larger ones.
10. Remove from heat, take off the lid, and let the pâté cool in the bain-marie.
11. Press the cooked mass firmly, turn out the terrine so all liquid drains well, and smooth it.
12. Pour cold clarified lard with ham over the pâté in the terrine high enough so the fat reaches to the outer edge.
13. When set, seal with plaster or tin foil for storage.

title: Strawberry Frozen Dessert (Glace aux fraises)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberry puree, passed through a fine sieve
- 1/4 liter cold sugar syrup
- Juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Mix the cold sugar syrup with the juice of 2 lemons into the strawberries
2. Pass the entire mixture through a fine sieve again and stir well together
3. Weigh the prepared strawberry juice with a saccharometer until it shows 22 degrees sugar content
4. If the juice does not reach this degree, add the necessary sugar dissolved in some water; if it exceeds this, add some water and lemon juice
5. Pour the well-stirred mixture into the freezing container and proceed as explained at the beginning for frozen desserts (Recipe 1746), but for fresh fruit frozen desserts, bury the container in ice 1 hour earlier and sprinkle with a larger quantity of salt or, as with à la minute frozen desserts, also with a mixture of coarsely crushed saltpeter and sal ammoniac

title: Strawberry Frozen Dessert with Whipped Cream (Glace aux fraises, mit geschlagsenem Obers)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Finished strawberry frozen dessert (Recipe 1746)
- 1/4 liter whipped cream

## Directions

1. As desired, fold 1/4 liter whipped cream into the finished strawberry frozen dessert (Recipe 1746)
2. Keep it in the container until serving, for about seven days
3. The mixing of whipped cream can be done with any other desired fruit frozen dessert in the same manner as with strawberry frozen dessert

title: Strawberry Frozen Dessert with Wine (Glace de fraises au vin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter passed strawberry puree
- 56 deca grams sugar
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 1/2 liter good white wine

## Directions

1. Stir 56 deca grams sugar into the 1/2 liter strawberry puree
2. Mix in the juice of 1 lemon and 1/2 liter white wine
3. Fill this liquid into the freezing container and proceed as with the 'Family Frozen Dessert' (Recipe 1720)
4. Serve in goblets

title: Strawberry Ice (Gefrornes von Erdbeeren)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 pounds strawberries
- 5 loth red currants (ribes)
- 1 pound sugar

## Directions

1. Pass 2.5 pounds strawberries and 5 loth red currants through a fine sieve
2. Clarify 1 pound sugar
3. After cooling, stir it in until it is time to pour everything into the freezing container
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Strettfleisch (Shredded Pork)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of young cabbage
- dark and light yellow turnips
- celery
- parsley
- vinegar
- water
- suckling pig

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage, turnips, celery, and parsley into thin noodle-like strips
2. Place in a casserole, pour in one part vinegar and one part water, and bring to a boil
3. Cut the suckling pig into desired pieces and add to the casserole
4. Cook until the meat is tender
5. Remove the meat with a slotted spoon and arrange in a round shape on a plate
6. Garnish with the noodle-like vegetables and place grated horseradish in the center of the plate

title: Striekel (Braided Bread)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 liter flour
- 3 deca compressed yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter lukewarm milk
- 1 whole egg
- 3/8 liter milk
- 9 to 12 deca melted butter
- 6 deca sugar
- 1 teaspoon full of salt
- raisins (optional, 1/8 deca)

## Directions

1. Put 1 1/2 liter flour in a bowl, make a well in the middle, pour in 3 deca compressed yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter lukewarm milk
2. Stir it a little, dust with some flour and let it rise covered in a warm place
3. When this so-called sponge has risen, add 1 whole egg, 3/8 liter milk with 9 to 12 deca melted butter, 6 deca sugar and 1 teaspoon of salt
4. Stir it into a dough and knead it with the hands until it pulls away from them
5. Cover it in the bowl with a cloth and place in a warm location
6. After the dough has risen to double height, shape it into a striekel by placing the dough on a flour-dusted board and dividing it into 4 unequal pieces
7. Cut the largest piece into 4 pieces, shape these into 2 finger-thick ropes, which are then braided into a four-strand braid and gently stretched lengthwise
8. From the two next smaller pieces, by cutting each into 3 parts, make two 3-strand braids in the same way and stretch them slightly
9. Twist the remaining fourth piece into two ropes and twist them together lightly
10. Line a baking sheet with white writing paper, arrange the 4 braids in correct order (the four-strand at the bottom, the two-strand at the top) into a striekel shape and cover it with a cloth, let it rise 1/2 hour in a warm place
11. Brush the striekel with beaten egg and bake slowly for 1 hour in the oven
12. Alternatively, the dough can be shaped into a loaf placed in a floured breadmaking basket covered and placed in a warm location until it has risen to double height, then placed on a buttered baking sheet and treated as with the striekel
13. If desired, 1/8 deca raisins can be added when combining the dough for either the striekel or the loaf

title: Stufato (Stufato oder Husarenbraten)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-70 dkg black beef or oxtail
- 6 dkg root vegetables
- 6 dkg bacon
- 2 tomatoes
- ½ l red wine
- salt
- pepper
- 6 dkg hot fat
- 1 onion ring
- 1 bay leaf
- thyme

## Directions

1. Beat the meat, wash it, salt and pepper it, and lard it.
2. In 6 dkg hot fat, roast the cleaned, finely cut root vegetables, 1 onion ring, 1 bay leaf, and some thyme until dark brown.
3. Add the meat, pour in a little water and the wine, cover and braise for 2-2¼ hours, pouring in liquid occasionally.
4. During braising, add tomatoes or tomato paste.

title: Stuffed Boiled Eggs (Oeufs Farcis Froids)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled, cooled eggs
- capers
- crayfish tail meat
- fish
- capers
- cold, good butter
- salt
- pepper
- cold mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Cut hard-boiled cooled eggs lengthwise in half and remove the yolks without damaging the whites.
2. Finely mince the yolks with capers (1 caper per yolk), crayfish tail meat, some arbitrary fish, and capers, then add about 1 tablespoon cold good butter, some salt and pepper to taste, and mix with enough cold mustard sauce to form a thick ragout.
3. Heap this filling into the whites of the halved eggs and arrange them on a dish, raised on a platter, which can also be garnished with aspic and olives, and sprinkle some capers over them.
4. Serve cold mustard sauce or cold Fricassee sauce separately in a sauceboat.

title: Stuffed Eggs (Œufs Farcis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 hard-boiled eggs
- 8 ounces breadcrumbs, deveined and softened in milk
- 6 peeled capers
- egg yolks from the hard-boiled eggs
- 8 ounces butter, beaten fluffy
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon milk cream
- salt
- pepper
- 1 strong pinch finely chopped green parsley
- bread crumbs
- milk cream for pouring

## Directions

1. Cut hard-boiled eggs in half lengthwise and carefully remove the yolks.
2. Press the deveined breadcrumbs softened in milk through a sieve into a deep dish.
3. Gradually stir 1 whole egg and 2 egg yolks into 8 ounces of fluffy beaten butter, then mix well with the sieved breadcrumb mixture, the boiled egg yolks, 1 tablespoon milk cream, salt, pepper, and 1 pinch of finely chopped green parsley.
4. Heap this filling generously on each egg half so it regains the form of a whole egg.
5. Place filled eggs in a casserole, pour milk cream over them, sprinkle with bread crumbs, and bake for 1/4 hour in the oven.
6. Serve the eggs with the milk cream in a dish, or remove them from the casserole if desired, arrange in a dish, and garnish with finely diced capers in a lattice pattern, serving the cream sauce separately.

title: Stuffed Morels (Morilles Farcies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- breadcrumbs soaked in milk
- green parsley
- egg yolks
- cream
- morels
- cold water
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mix 8 ounces of butter with 4 ounces of breadcrumbs soaked in milk, some finely chopped green parsley, 3 egg yolks and 1 tablespoon of cream
2. Remove the morels from their stems and place them in cold water
3. After 1/4 hour, remove them and place them on a strainer
4. When the water has drained, fill them with the above mixture
5. Butter a deep casserole, pour some cream into it, place the filled morels in it, pour some more cream over them, sprinkle with breadcrumbs and braise uncovered

title: Stuffed Mushrooms (Champignons Farcis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms of arbitrary size
- mushroom stems
- green parsley
- onion
- butter
- salt
- white pepper
- breadcrumbs
- cream

## Directions

1. Break off the stems from mushrooms and peel them
2. Place them in cold water and then in a strainer
3. Finely chop the mushroom stems, remaining trimmings, green parsley, some onion and one whole mushroom
4. Roast this mixture in butter, then stir in the necessary salt and some white pepper to make the stuffing
5. Arrange the hollowed out mushrooms in a well-buttered casserole with the stuffing facing upward
6. Fill these with the paste, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, pour cream over them and braise in the oven for 10 minutes
7. Serve with any meat dishes

title: Stuffed Pheasant (Faisan farci)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 well-hung and cleaned pheasant
- 8 deca bacon, finely chopped
- 1 goose liver
- 12 deca truffles, diced
- Second half of goose liver, diced
- Stock
- Red wine
- Soup
- Root vegetables
- Juniper berries
- 6 deca black bread, sliced in leaves
- Bacon slices
- Fat

## Directions

1. Cut the skin on the pheasant's back and carefully remove the skeleton without damaging the skin
2. Remove the meat from the skeleton and mince it with 8 deca bacon and 1 goose liver
3. Fry in a mortar and pass through a sieve
4. Mix the passed mixture with 12 deca diced truffles and diced second half of goose liver
5. Spread the pheasant skin on a board and fill with this forcemeat
6. Sew together in the natural pheasant shape, tuck the feet into the cavity, wrap with bacon slices
7. Place in a pan and roast in the oven at moderate heat, basting frequently with stock and fat
8. The removed bones are finely pounded and placed in a casserole with fat, root vegetables, juniper berries, and 6 deca sliced black bread
9. Toast slightly, add 1/4 liter red wine and equal amount of soup, reduce well, pass, and strain
10. Serve with the arranged pheasant

title: Stuffed Pike (Brochet farci)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- pike flesh
- breadcrumbs soaked in milk

## Directions

1. Choose any desired size pike, scale it, cut open the belly, remove the entrails and rinse the fish quickly
2. Dry it well with a clean cloth, sew the belly which was opened with a strong thread (unbleached twine)
3. Trim the swimming fins and lay the fish on its belly on a water-dampened board
4. Cut the skin on the back from the tail toward the head and separate it 2 fingers to the side of the head
5. Do the same on the other side of the fish
6. Carefully pull the skin from the flesh and spread it completely on the board
7. Using a small knife, remove all the flesh from the skeleton, but do not injure the head and skin
8. Leave the fish lying with the spread skin sprinkled with some salt and cover with a cloth dipped in cold water
9. Meanwhile make the following forcemeat: 0.5 deca pike flesh, 10 deca rind-removed breadcrumbs, soaked in milk and well pressed out

title: Stuffed Pork Leg (Cuissot de porc farci)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- pork leg
- salt
- pepper
- capers
- shallots
- parsley
- egg
- fat
- root vegetables
- onions
- broth
- vinegar
- glaze or butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Bone the pork leg, remove the thick skin (rind) and cut out the meat from the inside where it is crossed by sinews
2. Free the cut meat of sinews, chop it finely together with capers, capers and shallots using a chopping knife, beat in a whole egg, mix well with necessary salt and pepper
3. Spread this mixture on the inside of the prepared leg, roll it up tightly and tie it with twine
4. Meanwhile, place some fat, thin-sliced root vegetables and onions in a casserole, add the tied meat and let it braise uncovered until tender
5. After 1½ hours, pour in some broth and vinegar and let it boil well
6. Remove the meat, untie the twine, brush it with dissolved glaze or coat with butter, sprinkle with breadcrumbs if desired and let it roast in the oven until it takes on a golden color
7. Cut the meat into desired pieces, arrange on a platter, garnish with braised rice, turnips or chestnut purée etc. and serve with caramel sauce or the meat's own juices

title: Stuffed Potatoes (Gefüllte Erdäpfel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Large potatoes (not floury)
- Butter
- Eggs
- Lemon zest
- Nutmeg flower
- Salt
- Cream (Schmette)
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Take large but not floury potatoes, scrape them clean, rinse them out, cut off a top slice, and hollow them out.
2. Beat a piece of butter with a couple of eggs, lemon zest, nutmeg flower, salt, a little cream, and the hollowed-out parts, and add as many bread crumbs as are necessary to make the forcemeat somewhat thick; then fill the hollowed-out potatoes [remainder of recipe cut off]
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stuffed Quails (Gefüllerte Wachteln)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 cleaned, singed quails (not gutted)
- Hen breast
- Bacon (6 teaspoons)
- Semolina bread (6 teaspoons, soaked in water and well pressed out)
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg yolks
- Salt and spices
- Bacon slices
- Butter
- Root vegetables
- Celery
- Cleaned, thinly cut mushrooms
- Soup
- White wine
- Madeira wine
- Truffles (optional)

## Directions

1. Carefully separate the breast and leg bones from 12 quails without gutting them
2. Cut the hen breast with bacon, place in a mortar and pound with soaked pressed bread, 1 whole egg, 2 egg yolks, salt and spices until fine
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Fill the quails with this stuffing and sew closed
5. Wrap with bacon slices
6. Place in a casserole with butter, root vegetables, celery, and mushrooms
7. Cover and roast together
8. Pour some soup and white wine over it
9. Let simmer gently covered until tender
10. Remove the quails and cut off the string
11. Arrange the quails in a circle on a plate
12. Pour prepared salmis sauce over them
13. Place artichoke hearts or truffles in the center of the plate

title: Stuffed Romaine Lettuce (Gefüllter Hanseltatng)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Romaine lettuce heads
- Ground meat of choice
- Crayfish
- Bread crumbs
- Butter
- Onions
- Herbs
- Fresh parsley
- Seasoning
- Salt
- Twine
- Meat broth

## Directions

1. Leaf through selected beautiful heads after they have been well washed, and fill them with a forcemeat made of ground meat of choice, crayfish, bread crumbs, butter, onions, herbs, fresh parsley, seasoning and salt.
2. Bind them with twine and place them with a piece of butter in a casserole, into which you also pour meat broth, and cook the lettuce in it.
3. Finally make a sauce from meat broth, crayfish, crayfish butter, marrow, onions and herbs, cook the lettuce in it thoroughly and serve it to table.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stuffed Snails (Gefüllte Schnecken)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- large snails
- salted water
- salt
- fresh parsley
- anchovies, washed and cleaned
- lemon peel
- lemon juice
- butter
- fine bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook large snails, remove them from their shells, salt them, and let them lie in salted water for a while.
2. Wash the shells clean and clean the snails of their slime, and cut off the tails.
3. Take fresh parsley, some washed and cleaned anchovies, some lemon peel, and some of the snails, chop everything fine together.
4. Beat a piece of butter until light, add the chopped mixture, some lemon juice and a small handful of fine bread crumbs, and stir everything well.
5. Fill part of this mixture into a snail shell, add half a snail, then fill again with the mixture until the shell is full.
6. Place them on a metal dish with heat above and below, and they are ready.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Stänzeltli (Almond Batter Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 24 ounces powdered sugar
- 24 ounces flour
- beaten eggs for brushing
- coarsely chopped almonds
- sugar mixed with almonds

## Directions

1. Stir 8 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs with 24 ounces powdered sugar for 1 hour
2. Lightly mix in 24 ounces flour
3. Spread this mass finger-thick on a buttered sheet
4. Brush with beaten eggs, sprinkle with sugar mixed with coarsely chopped almonds, and bake in a moderate oven until golden
5. Cut into desired size pieces immediately while warm

title: Suckling Pig, Stuffed (Spanferkel, faschirt)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig legs
- Fine veal forcemeat
- Chopped truffles
- Herbs
- Bacon
- Braise
- Sour jelly

## Directions

1. Remove all the bones from the legs and stuff them with fine veal forcemeat, chopped truffles, and herbs, well seasoned.
2. Shape them nicely and cook thoroughly in a good braise.
3. Cover with bacon.
4. Allow to cool in its cooking liquid.
5. Serve with sour jelly.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Sulz von Citronen (Lemon Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 decagrams sugar
- 1/2 liter water
- 1/4 liter milk
- Juice of 3 lemons
- 7 decagrams isinglass, still hot

## Directions

1. Clarify 40 decagrams sugar with 1/2 liter water and let it cool covered
2. Add to 1/4 liter milk the juice of 3 lemons
3. Place this on the fire, whereby it becomes curdled and strain through cloth
4. Mix the strained whey with 7 decagrams of still hot isinglass

title: Sulz von frischen Kirschen (Fresh Cherry Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilo black ripe cherries, stems removed
- 1/4 kilo red currants
- 40 decagrams refined sugar
- 1 lemon peel
- 7 decagrams isinglass
- 1/2 liter of the strained juice

## Directions

1. Remove stems from 1/2 kilo ripe black cherries and 1/4 kilo red currants, crush them all, cover in an earthen vessel and let stand overnight.
2. Press the juice through cloth.
3. Add 40 decagrams of refined sugar heated with lemon peel cooked in it to 7 decagrams of hot dissolved isinglass.
4. Cook this together for 10 minutes, strain through fine, filtered linen cloth, add 1/2 liter of the pressed juice and proceed as with Orange Jelly (No. 1660).

title: Sulz von Orangenblueten (Orange Blossom Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 decagrams fresh picked orange blossoms
- 40 decagrams boiling, clarified sugar
- 7 decagrams isinglass, hot and dissolved
- Clear juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same way as Violet Sulz (see recipe 1677)
2. Use 4 decagrams fresh picked orange blossoms instead of violet flowers
3. Instead of lemon and orange juice, use other juice

title: Sulz zum Brunnenkresse (Watercress Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 decagrams isinglass
- 1 liter water
- 40 decagrams sugar
- 1/2 liter water for clarifying sugar
- 12 decagrams selected watercress
- Juice of 3 lemons
- 1/4 liter milk
- 1/2 liter whey, in warm state

## Directions

1. Boil 7 decagrams isinglass in 1 liter water and reduce to 1/2 liter
2. Clarify 40 decagrams sugar in 1/2 liter water
3. Pound 12 decagrams selected watercress and press through cloth
4. Add this juice and the juice of 3 lemons to 1/4 liter milk
5. Place this liquid on heat until it becomes curdled
6. Strain this through fine cloth
7. Pour the hot, clarified sugar to the boiling isinglass, and also mix in the strained whey (1/2 liter) in warm state
8. Stir everything, let run through fine linen cloth and proceed further as for Orange Sulz (see recipe 1660)

title: Suppe für Kranke (Soup for the Sick)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Kilo mageres, klein geschnittenes Rindfleisch
- 1,5 Liter kaltes Wasser
- 1 gereinigtes Huhn
- 2 Eierklar
- 1,5 Liter kaltes Wasser

## Directions

1. 1 Kilo mageres, klein geschnittenes Rindfleisch in einen Topf geben.
2. 1,5 Liter kaltes Wasser darauf geben, decken und 1 Stunde langsam kochen lassen.
3. Inzwischen 1 gereinigtes Huhn, das man in einem Mörser gestoßen hat, mit 2 Eierklar und 1,5 Liter kaltem Wasser gut gemischt in den Topf zu dem Fleische geben.
4. Abermals 1 Stunde ungedeckt kochen lassen.
5. Das vorhandene Fett gut abnehmen und die Suppe durch ein reines Tuch seihen.
6. Die Suppe wird erst dann gesalzen, wenn man sie verbraucht.

title: Suppe Julienne (Julienne Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- strong beef broth
- well-plucked and cleaned partridge or woodcock
- egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make a particularly strong broth and cook it well, then clean it of all fat.
2. Strain it through a cloth, add necessary salt, and keep it hot.
3. Take a well-plucked and cleaned partridge or woodcock and pound it very finely raw in a mortar, removing the entrails from the partridge but not from the woodcock.
4. Add one serving spoon of broth and 4 egg yolks to it.
5. Pound everything together well, then strain it through a hair cloth.
6. If the strained mixture is too thick, add some cooled broth.
7. When the soup is hot and seasoned with salt and grated nutmeg to taste, pour it into the soup pot and add the strained mixture while constantly stirring.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Biskuitschöberl (Soup with Biscuit Shoerl)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg whites
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- 4 dkg flour

## Directions

1. Fold 3 egg yolks and a pinch of salt gently into the stiff snow of 3 egg whites
2. Mix in 4 dkg flour
3. Fill the mixture into a schöberl pan and bake at moderate heat until light brown
4. Loosen the schöberl, turn it out, cut it, and serve warm

title: Suppe mit Blumenkohl (Cauliflower Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Several well-cleaned cauliflower florets
- Good bouillon
- 6 lothes cauliflower
- 2 pounds butter
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Toasted bread roll slices, fine dumplings, or similar

## Directions

1. Blanch well-cleaned cauliflower florets in hot water and then simmer in good bouillon until completely soft. For 6 lothes of cauliflower, take 2 pounds butter and let it sweat in the butter after removing from bouillon. Place in soup pot (terrine). Draw off broth with several egg yolks, season with salt and nutmeg, and pour over cauliflower. May add toasted bread roll slices, fine dumplings, or similar. The soup can be further improved by adding minced chicken or veal breast, crayfish tails and similar, with some crayfish butter added.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Butternockerln (Soup with Butter Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50-70 dkg shoulder and tail beef
- 5 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- 2 egg whites
- 6 dkg flour

## Directions

1. To the creamed butter, stir in 2 egg yolks and salt
2. Mix in the stiff snow of 2 egg whites together with 6 dkg flour
3. Let this stand for a while
4. Form small dumplings with a spoon dipped in hot soup
5. Place them in the strained boiling beef soup and let them cook slowly for 5 minutes
6. If the dough is too loose, add more flour as needed

title: Suppe mit einem Pudding von mehreren Farben (Soup with a Many-Colored Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bread roll, crust removed
- milk
- 1/4 pound butter or good beef lard
- 6 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- marrow, finely diced
- crab butter
- spinach juice, clarified
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Soak a bread roll with crust removed in milk
2. Beat 1/4 pound butter or good beef lard until fluffy
3. Add the well-squeezed bread roll
4. Beat in 6 eggs and 2 egg yolks, one after another
5. Add finely diced marrow
6. Make two types of mixture: one red from crab butter and one green from clarified spinach juice
7. Cut each type into large cubes and add to the mixture
8. Stir gently in so as not to break apart the cubes
9. Dust with as much flour as can be held between five fingers
10. Coat a cloth with butter
11. Place the mixture on it
12. Tie tightly with string so it cannot rise
13. Place in a pot of simmering, slightly salted water
14. Let it cook for a whole hour
15. Remove and untie the cloth
16. Place the pudding in a serving dish
17. Pour meat broth or consommé over it
18. Serve at table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Eingebundenem (Soup with Bound Forcemeat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Braised or fried veal
- Fried capon or guinea fowl breast
- Bread roll, softened in milk
- 3 eggs
- Fresh parsley
- 4 lothes butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bread crumbs
- 2 spoonfuls good cream

## Directions

1. Cut braised or fried veal and fried capon or guinea fowl breast small. Soak bread roll in milk and press out. Make a mixture from 3 eggs and chop fresh parsley finely. Beat 4 lothes butter fluffy, stir in the chopped ingredients, and add 3 eggs one after another. Mix salt, pepper, some bread crumbs, and 2 spoonfuls of good cream well together and add to the mixture. Butter a cloth, place the mixture on it, tie together and simmer in boiling salt water for one hour. After removing, place in soup pot and pour brown soup over it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Griesfangel (Soup with Semolina Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 loth lard or butter
- egg
- 2 egg yolks
- milk
- salt
- semolina
- beef soup or pea broth

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of 4 loth lard or butter beaten very fluffy, a whole egg, 2 egg yolks, a couple spoonfuls of milk, the necessary salt, and as much semolina as needed
2. Let a piece of lard in a pan become hot, put the mixture in, let it brown on top and bottom from the heat, and then simmer it in beef soup (on fast days in pea broth)
3. When serving sprinkle it with nutmeg flower
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Leberschöberl (Soup with Liver Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose liver
- 4 loth butter or beef lard
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 bread rolls
- milk
- green parsley, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper, ground
- fine bread crumbs
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Scrape the skin from a goose liver and chop it finely
2. Beat 4 loth butter or beef lard until fluffy
3. Beat in 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks, one after another
4. Scrape the crust from 2 bread rolls, soak them in milk
5. Add the soaked bread with finely chopped green parsley, salt, ground pepper, and fine bread crumbs
6. Stir everything well together
7. Fold in the snow from 2 egg whites
8. Coat a casserole with butter
9. Sprinkle with fine bread crumbs
10. Fill in the mixture
11. Bake in the oven or on coals
12. Cut into pieces
13. Pour pressed liver soup over it and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Lungenstrudeln (Soup with Lung Strudel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's lung with heart
- butter
- green parsley, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper, ground
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- strudel dough
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook a calf's lung with heart until soft
2. Remove the esophagus and chop the rest finely
3. Warm a piece of butter
4. Add finely chopped green parsley and let it warm through
5. Add the chopped lung with salt and ground pepper
6. Let it brown a little
7. Beat 2 whole eggs and 1 egg yolk over it
8. Stir it well together
9. Let it cool
10. Make ordinary strudel dough and stretch it thin
11. Fill the lung mixture into it, leaving an empty space of about one and a half spans in the middle
12. Cut the empty space in the middle
13. Roll the dough from both sides together so the empty space wraps around the filled part several times
14. Press with a flour-dusted cooking spoon handle one small finger length through
15. Separate these parts with a knife
16. Place them in a light brown soup and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Markknöderln (Soup with Marrow Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dkg beef brisket
- 5 dkg marrow
- 1 bread roll
- 1 egg
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- 3 dkg bread crumbs
- Beef broth

## Directions

1. Melt the finely cut marrow, strain through a sieve into a bowl and let solidify
2. Beat until fluffy
3. Mix with 1 egg, 1 bread roll that has been trimmed, soaked in water, and well squeezed
4. Add salt, pepper, finely chopped parsley, and 3 dkg bread crumbs
5. Let stand a while
6. Form small dumplings
7. Place in strained, boiling beef broth
8. Cook slowly for 5 minutes

title: Suppe mit Ragout-Knödeln (Soup with Ragout Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of calf's sweetbreads
- 1 udder
- butter
- small fish finely chopped
- 3 egg yolks
- milk
- nutmeg
- salt
- square wafers
- egg white
- eggs, beaten
- fine bread crumbs
- lard
- white pressed soup

## Directions

1. Boil a couple of calf's sweetbreads and an udder briefly and chop finely into cubes
2. Warm a piece of butter
3. Add the sweetbreads and finely chopped fish and braise
4. Beat 3 egg yolks in a little milk
5. Whisk it well
6. Pour it over and stir in with nutmeg and salt
7. Coat square wafers with egg white
8. Put 3 spoonfuls of the ragout on each
9. Shape it nicely round like a dumpling
10. Roll in beaten eggs
11. Then roll in fine bread crumbs
12. Fry hot in lard
13. Place in the soup pot
14. Pour white pressed soup over it and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Reis (Rice Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 spoonfuls cleaned rice
- approximately 1.5 liters white broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the rice in warm water to remove any staleness
2. Heat the rice with approximately 1.5 liters white broth and let it cook covered evenly
3. Before serving, thin with as much strong broth as needed
4. Alternatively, cook 16 spoonfuls rice in 0.5 liter white broth with salt, covered, slowly. The rice will absorb the broth, remain white and whole
5. Add approximately 1.5 liters white or brown broth to the pot with the cooked rice

title: Suppe mit Reis (Soup with Rice)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a measure good meat soup
- 4 pounds rice
- salt
- nutmeg
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Pour half a measure of good meat soup over 4 pounds rice for 6 persons, which should first be washed with cold water, then lukewarm, and finally hot water until nothing white comes off anymore (because it often takes on a stale smell from lying in barrels)
2. Let it cook salted, without stirring, until tender if you want to serve it with brown soup; you can also stir it often once you have fricasseed it with eggs
3. Since the poured soup will be drawn into the rice, put it in a dish and pour a measure of beef soup colored with consommé over it, salt it and serve it to the table, and you may also present grated Parmesan cheese on a separate plate
4. If you do not want the soup this way, stir 3 egg yolks whisked with a spoonful of water into the beef soup to be added until it becomes thick, then serve it with the rice, and sprinkle both this and the brown soup with nutmeg flower
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Reisknödeln (Soup with Rice Dumplings)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound washed rice
- meat broth
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- 4 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Stir 1 pound washed rice cooked in meat broth, but not too soft, together well with breadcrumbs fried brown in butter and 4 whole eggs
2. Cut out dumplings and cook them in the soup
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Sago Jus (Sago Soup with Jus)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds sago
- Fresh water
- Good jus (Schü)
- Parsnip
- Yellow from lettuce head
- Chervil
- Sorrel, finely chopped
- Toasted bread roll slices

## Directions

1. Wash sago twice with fresh water. Blanch for 4 hours. Lay on hair sieve and cool with fresh water. In a casserole, fill with sago and good jus, adding parsnip, yellow from lettuce head, chervil, and finely chopped sorrel. After cooking for half an hour, serve with toasted bread roll slices.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Schlickkrapfen von Geschling (Soup with Dumplings of Organ Meat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked organ meat
- onion
- butter
- egg yolks
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- egg
- water
- nutmeg
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. When the organ meat is cooked and finely chopped with an onion, braise it in a pan with butter
2. Add a couple of chopped egg yolks, finely chopped parsley, salt and pepper, and let it cool
3. Take flour on a board, salt it a little, break a whole egg into it, add some butter, make the dough with lukewarm water, and work it by hand until it detaches itself from the board
4. Roll it out with a rolling pin and help with the hand until it is as fine and thin as possible
5. Brush the rolled out dough with a beaten egg, place the organ meat mixture here and there with a spoon as much as you need dough for one dumpling, then cut them out with a dumpling wheel, and fold the empty dough over them
6. Set water on the fire in a casserole, when it boils put the dumplings in, let them come to a boil a couple of times, remove them with a slotted spoon, and let the water drain well
7. Arrange them on a dish, carefully pour the soup over them with a spoon so none tear, sprinkle them with finely chopped green parsley, pepper and grated nutmeg, and finally with breadcrumbs browned in butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe mit Ulmer Gerste (Soup with Ulm Barley)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- barley
- meat broth
- egg yolks
- nutmeg flower
- chives
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cook selected and well-washed, blanched barley in meat broth in a pot
2. Stir often during cooking to prevent scorching, and do not place too close to the fire
3. When serving, whisk 3 to 4 egg yolks into the well-strained soup set aside for serving
4. Pour it over the barley, stir it, sprinkle with some nutmeg flower, and garnish the soup with some finely chopped chives
5. Serve halved lemons on a separate plate for expressing juice into the soup
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe Tortue (Turtle Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- strong beef broth
- celery heads
- parsnip or parsley root
- yellow turnip roots
- thyme
- marjoram
- beef cut into 2-inch thick slices
- veal knuckles
- 2-pound carp
- 1-pound pike
- 1/2 pound veal meat
- 2 hard-cooked eggs
- butter
- salt
- flour
- 1 egg yolk
- cooked veal foot
- pepper
- Burgundy wine

## Directions

1. Make a good broth and add cleaned celery heads, parsnip or parsley root, yellow turnip roots with some thyme and marjoram.
2. Fill with broth and cook until the beef sliced 2 inches thick and the veal knuckles are half-cooked.
3. Take a 2-pound carp, wash it clean, cut it into pieces, and add it to the broth.
4. When the meat is completely tender, strain the soup through a fine hair sieve and keep it hot.
5. Make a force from 1 pound of pike and separately from 1/2 pound of veal meat.
6. Take the yolk from 2 hard-cooked eggs, stir it smooth in a casserole with a proportional piece of butter, add some salt, a little flour, and 1 egg yolk.
7. From a cooked veal foot, remove all bones and cartilage and cut each piece into fine thin strips, placing them immediately into the soup.
8. Alternately form small round dumplings with a spoon from the pike force and the veal meat force, and poach these along with the egg yolk force in boiling broth.
9. Make the soup very hot, season with pepper, and add 1 quarter liter of Burgundy wine.
10. Let it stand at the fire to become hot again, but do not let it come to a boil; serve when properly warmed in the soup pot.
11. Drain all the various dumplings in a sieve and add them to the soup.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe von gerostelem Grieß (Soup of Roasted Semolina)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 spoonfuls butter
- 16 spoonfuls semolina
- approximately 2 liters boiling beef broth
- 3 spoonfuls sour cream
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Roast 16 spoonfuls semolina in 8 spoonfuls butter until yellow
2. Pour approximately 2 liters boiling beef broth over it
3. Let cook for 1/2 hour, skimming frequently
4. Beat 3 spoonfuls sour cream with 3 egg yolks
5. Add to the soup pot, stir slowly, salt, and serve at table

title: Suppe von grünen Erbsen mit jungen Hühnern (Soup of Green Peas with Young Chickens)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- young peas
- butter
- sugar
- flour
- good bouillon
- parsley
- pepper herb
- yellow lettuce
- 4 pounds butter
- yellow turnips
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare a couple of young chickens and cook them in consommé until half done
2. Take two seidel of young peas and sweat the finest ones with a piece of butter in a casserole, to which you may add some sugar if desired
3. Add one tablespoon of flour, stir this with half a measure of good bouillon until clear
4. Add a bunch of parsley and pepper herb, as well as the young chickens, and let everything cook until tender
5. Sweat the larger peas with pepper herb, yellow lettuce, 4 pounds butter and some flour
6. Fill with the necessary bouillon and cook slowly for an hour
7. Add some yellow turnips and finally strain through a hair sieve
8. When the young chickens are tender, place them in the soup pot and arrange the green peas and the other soup over them, adding a little salt and nutmeg
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe von Habergruetze mit Fleischbruehe (Oat Groats Soup with Meat Broth)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One handful oat groats
- Water
- A good piece of butter
- Thin meat broth
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- One egg yolk

## Directions

1. Set one handful oat groats in water over the fire and let it cook fairly thick
2. When thick enough, stir in a good piece of butter
3. Pour on sufficient thin meat broth
4. Add a little salt and some nutmeg
5. Beat the yolk of one egg and add it just before serving
6. This soup is very beneficial for consumptive and dry natures
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe von Spargel, faschiertem Salat und Maurachen (Asparagus, Minced Lettuce and Char Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus heads
- Endive or escarole lettuce
- Char fish
- Butter
- Mace
- Parmesan lettuce head
- Baked bread roll

## Directions

1. Cut asparagus heads to the length of one segment and clean endive or escarole lettuce. Blanch both together, strain and place in fresh water. Chop one lettuce head with several well-washed char underneath. Strain asparagus well on sieve. Put butter in a casserole, place the ingredients in it and steam slightly, season with mace and set aside. When the jus has reduced, strain it and let it simmer well with the remaining minced lettuce. A quarter hour before serving, add baked bread roll and salt. The celery soup, minced cabbage and other herb soups are made the same way.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Suppe à la Colbert
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Vegetables and broth (prepared as for French root vegetable soup)
- bouched chicken liver per person
- braised chicken meat pieces

## Directions

1. Prepare the soup somewhat stronger than French root vegetable soup, adding one bouched chicken liver per person.
2. Add braised chicken meat pieces to the soup pot.

title: Suppen-Profitéroles (Soup Profiteroles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 rolls, crust removed and cut into finger-thick slices
- milk
- 4 whole eggs
- breadcrumbs
- hot lard or butter for frying
- roasted brain for filling
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the rolls into finger-thick slices and lightly dip them in milk.
2. Place a filling of roasted brain between each two pieces.
3. Dip these profiteroles into 4 beaten eggs, coat in breadcrumbs, fry in hot lard, and place on blotting paper.
4. Serve them on a serviette with the soup.
5. Instead of breadcrumbs, they can also be coated in flour.
6. The profiteroles can be served as a garnish with vegetables.

title: Sweet Noodle Croquettes (Süße Nudel-Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 whole eggs
- flour equal to the volume of the eggs
- 1 liter boiling milk or cream
- 12 tablespoons sugar
- 1 pinch salt
- 6 egg yolks
- 2 tablets grated chocolate or 3 tablespoons strong black coffee (optional)
- flour for coating
- eggs for dipping
- breadcrumbs for coating
- hot lard for frying
- zest of oranges or lemons (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare a noodle dough from 2 whole eggs and as much flour as they will hold
2. Treat it as described in the noodle soup recipe
3. Cut the dough into knife-back thick, equally wide, and half-finger long noodles
4. Add them to 1 liter of boiling milk or cream and cook into a thick mush, stirring frequently
5. Add 12 tablespoons sugar and 1 pinch salt during cooking
6. Pour the cooked mass into a deep bowl
7. After it has cooled somewhat, gradually stir in 6 egg yolks and optionally 2 tablets of grated chocolate or 3 tablespoons of strong black coffee
8. Continue treatment as described for Rice-Croquettes (No. 1136)
9. Optionally, rub the required sugar with orange or lemon zest (using only the outer yellow skin) to flavor the dish

title: Sweet Potato Croquettes (Süße Kartoffel-Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo potatoes
- 12 tablespoons butter
- 16 tablespoons vanilla sugar
- salt
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter cream or milk
- flour for coating
- eggs for dipping
- breadcrumbs for coating
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Bake 1 kilo potatoes in the oven or in hot ashes, peel them
2. Pass through a ricer into a saucepan
3. Mix with 12 tablespoons butter, 16 tablespoons vanilla sugar, and some salt
4. Stir in 6 egg yolks one after another
5. Place the saucepan on the heat, pour in 1/4 liter of cream or milk, and bring to a boil while stirring constantly
6. Pour into a bowl and cool
7. After completely cooling, prepare as Rice-Croquettes (No. 1136)

title: Sweet Rice Croquettes (Süße Reis-Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk or cream
- 32 tablespoons rice
- 16 tablespoons sugar
- 12 tablespoons butter
- 1 pinch salt
- 16 tablespoons blanched almonds
- some milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- flour for coating
- breadcrumbs for coating
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Pour 1 liter milk or cream into a saucepan and add 32 tablespoons riced rice, letting it cook covered for a long time
2. During cooking, add 16 tablespoons sugar, 12 tablespoons butter, and 1 pinch of salt
3. Meanwhile, grind 16 tablespoons of blanched almonds with some milk into a dough and add to the rice mass, stir well and bring to a boil once
4. Pour into a deep bowl and cover with buttered paper
5. After completely cooling, stir in 3 egg yolks and 1 whole egg
6. Place the rice mass on a flour-dusted board and form thick sticks of equal size
7. Dip in beaten egg and roll in breadcrumbs
8. Fry in very hot lard with frequent shaking of the pan until evenly golden
9. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on blotting paper, then arrange in a bowl covered with a napkin

title: Sweet Rice Croquettes with Coffee Flavor (Süße Reis-Croquettes mit Kaffeegeschmack)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rice croquette mass from recipe 1136
- 3 tablespoons strong black coffee

## Directions

1. Prepare the mixture exactly as for the Rice-Croquettes (No. 1136)
2. When the rice is almost soft, add 3 tablespoons of strong black coffee and let it cook together with the mixture

title: Sweet Timbale (Timbale anderer Art)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff dough
- Butter
- Flour
- Dried peas, beans, or bran
- Beaten eggs
- Fruits or whipped cream
- Strawberries, roasted chestnuts, or custard creams

## Directions

1. Follow the same preparation as the preceding Timbale
2. After the timbale is completely cooled, instead of filling with meat, fill with desired fruits or whipped cream mixed with strawberries, roasted chestnuts, or desired cream sauces

title: Sweetbread Ragout (Beuſchel)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ calf or 1 pork sweetbread, approximately ½-¾ kg
- 6 dkg root vegetables
- ⅛ liter cream
- 1 scallion
- salt and vinegar
- 5 peppercorns and spice berries
- 2 bay leaves
- 5 dkg fat for roux
- lemon juice
- semolina strudel or dumplings

## Directions

1. Wash the sweetbread twice with plenty of water
2. Cook with cleaned, washed, thinly sliced root vegetables, 1 scallion, several tablespoons salt and vinegar, 5 peppercorns and spice berries, 2 bay leaves for 1-1¼ hours
3. Lift out, cool and cut finely into noodle-like pieces
4. Make a light brown roux with 5 dkg fat
5. Pour sweetbread cooking water over it, add the sweetbread
6. Season with lemon juice, cook well together
7. Add the cream and bring to a boil again
8. Serve with semolina strudel or dumplings

title: Swiss Brezel (Swiss Pretzel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deka worked butter
- 6 deka sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 16 deka flour
- beaten egg white for brushing
- crystal sugar mixed with anise

## Directions

1. Stir 8 deka worked butter gradually with 6 deka sugar, 3 egg yolks, and 16 deka flour well together, let this mass rest covered in a cold place for some time
2. After about half an hour, form arbitrarily large pretzels from this dough mass, place them on a butter-brushed baking sheet with the help of a knife
3. Brush with beaten egg white, sprinkle with crystal sugar mixed with anise, and bake at low heat in the oven until golden yellow

title: Syrisches Kraut (Syrian-Style Sauerkraut)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- sauerkraut
- boiling salt water
- diced bacon
- finely cut onion
- caraway seeds
- flour
- beef broth
- vinegar
- milk cream

## Directions

1. Cut the sauerkraut as for sauerkraut salad, place in a deep bowl, pour boiling salt water over it and let it stand for a while
2. In a casserole, place diced bacon and finely cut onion, add the expressed sauerkraut, salt it, add a little caraway seed, stew it for some time
3. Dust it with the necessary flour, pour some beef broth, a little vinegar and milk cream to it, season it, let it stew for some time more and serve on a platter

title: Szegediner Gulyas (Hungarian Szeged Goulash)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 dkg Schweinsschulter (pork shoulder)
- 25 dkg jungschweinernes Bauchfleisch (young pork belly)
- ½ kg Sauerkraut (sauerkraut)
- ¼ l Rahm (sour cream)
- 7 dkg Speck (bacon)
- Zwiebel (onion)
- 4 dkg Fett (fat)
- Paprika (paprika)
- Kümmel (caraway)
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, cut into cubes and salt it.
2. Cut the bacon into small cubes and lightly brown it with finely chopped onion in 4 dkg hot fat.
3. Add the meat, then add the lightly washed and cut sauerkraut, paprika, caraway and some water.
4. Let it braise covered for 1-1½ hours.
5. Add the sour cream and braise for another 20 minutes.
6. Serve the meat on a round platter.

title: Süße Maccaroni (Sweet Macaroons)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound sweet almonds
- Egg whites
- 2 pounds finest pounded sugar
- Zest of 2 lemons
- 12 to 18 eggs typically required

## Directions

1. Blanch 1 pound sweet almonds in boiling water, then plunge into cold water for a few minutes
2. Remove the skins and wash them, then let them dry for 24 hours under a napkin
3. Take a handful of almonds, pound them in a mortar or on a grater until fine but not too fine so they do not become oily
4. Pour the white of one egg onto the crushed mass, stir and mix well until it becomes a fine dough
5. Empty the mortar and proceed with the remaining almonds in the same way, using additional egg whites as needed
6. Once all the almond mass is processed, return it to the mortar and add 2 pounds of finest pounded sugar and the zest of 2 lemons
7. Stir all well together and add enough egg white so the dough achieves the necessary consistency
8. Take a two-finger-wide wooden ruler with a handle, place some dough on it, hold the instrument with your left hand, and use a clean knife to set oblong-round heaps about finger-thick on a sheet of paper, spacing them one and a half inches apart
9. Push the macaroons on a sheet into a moderately hot oven and bake them slowly for three quarters of an hour
10. Ensure the almonds are very dry before putting them in the mortar
11. Be careful when beating the eggs so that no yolk mixes with the white, as this would ruin the entire dough mass and the macaroons would collapse in the oven instead of rising
12. Keep the oven only moderately heated as almonds and sugar burn very easily
13. To be completely safe, put a couple into the oven as a test and bake for three quarters of an hour; if they are not burned and have a beautiful yellow color, the oven has the right temperature
14. If they are black underneath, it shows the lower heat is stronger than the upper, and in this case lay 2 or 3 sheets of paper on the baking sheet to moderate the effect
15. Only after testing the oven heat in this way should the macaroon dough be placed on paper, and only when completely cooled should the macaroons be removed from it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Süße Milchrahmsuppe (Sweet Milk Cream Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- a few drops of cold water
- 2 measures of sweet cream heated with sugar

## Directions

1. Put 6 egg yolks with a few drops of cold water in a pot
2. Pour 2 measures of sweet cream heated with sugar into it while constantly stirring
3. Serve over toasted bread slices
4. If desired, you can also cook some peach leaves or bitter almonds with it for flavor
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Süße Rosetten (Sweet Filled Bread Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rolls of any size
- jam of choice
