- cold milk or sweetened white wine or red wine
- flour
- beaten eggs
- bread crumbs
- fat for frying
- vanilla sugar or sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Trim the rolls and halve them
2. Cut each half deeply and fill with jam
3. Soak in cold milk, sweetened white wine, or red wine
4. Dust with flour
5. Dip in beaten eggs
6. Sprinkle with bread crumbs
7. Fry in hot fat
8. Sprinkle with vanilla sugar or sugar and cinnamon when serving

title: Süßer Grieß-Strudel (Sweet Semolina Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 deca butter
- 4 egg yolks
- ¼ liter semolina
- 16 deca vanilla sugar
- ½ liter milk cream
- 4 egg whites
- ½ liter flour
- 16 deca raisins
- ½ liter milk

## Directions

1. Gradually mix 12 deca butter with 4 egg yolks
2. Blend in ¼ liter semolina, 16 deca vanilla sugar, and ½ liter milk cream well
3. Let this mixture rest for ½ hour so the semolina can swell
4. Lightly fold in the snow (whipped egg whites) from 4 egg whites
5. Prepare strudel dough from ½ liter flour and stretch it finely as described in the Young Strudel recipe
6. Spread with the semolina filling above
7. Sprinkle with 16 deca raisins
8. Roll together lightly and treat as described in the Ham Strudel recipe
9. When the strudel is half-baked, pour ½ liter mixed milk over it
10. Finish baking and cut into desired pieces, arrange on a serving dish, and serve

title: Süßes Torte mit Mandeln (Sweet Almond Cake; Tourte à la Crème aux Amandes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg whites
- 32 decagrams sugar
- 32 decagrams finely ground and sifted almonds
- 1 1 decagrams finely diced citron
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites until stiff and fold in 32 decagrams sugar, 32 decagrams finely ground and sifted almonds, and 1 1 decagrams finely diced citron, mixing lightly.
2. Fill a buttered cake form dusted with flour with this mixture and bake at very low heat for 1 1/2 hours in the oven.

title: Süßkraut (Sweet Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- 8 dkg fat
- 1 onion ring
- 5-8 pieces sugar cubes
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Caraway
- Water

## Directions

1. Remove bad leaves and stalks from cabbage
2. Cut into quarters, wash, and cut into fine noodle-like strips
3. Heat 8 dkg fat in a casserole
4. Toast 1 onion ring until dark yellow
5. Brown 5-8 sugar cubes in the fat
6. Add the cabbage
7. Season with salt, vinegar, and caraway
8. Add water until cabbage is covered
9. Braise covered for 2-2.5 hours, adding water occasionally
10. Season to taste with salt, sugar, and vinegar

title: Süßländische Dampfnudeln (Southern-style Steamed Dumplings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 Deka flour
- 4 Deka sugar
- 5 grams salt
- 2 Deka yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter uncooked milk
- 4 Stercks butter
- 1 whole egg
- 1/8 liter uncooked milk
- 10 Deka melted butter or 8 Deka lard
- Lukewarm milk for cooking
- Melted butter for brushing

## Directions

1. Mix together in a deep bowl 60 Deka flour, 4 Deka sugar and 5 grams salt
2. Dissolve 2 Deka yeast in 1/4 liter uncooked milk with constant stirring and keep it on the warm stove until the milk becomes lukewarm
3. Beat 4 Stercks butter and 1 whole egg together with 1/8 liter uncooked milk and pour this at the same time with the dissolved yeast into the middle of the flour, stir into a dough and beat finely until it detaches from the spoon
4. Again stir in 10 Deka melted butter or 8 Deka lard until the dough is combined with the butter
5. Let the dough rise covered beside the stove in a warm place until it has risen about halfway
6. In a butter-brushed casserole, pour in enough lukewarm milk to cover the bottom
7. Using a spoon, place large pieces of the dough next to each other in the casserole, each time brushing them with melted butter
8. When the casserole is filled, let it stand covered in a warm place until the dough has risen somewhat
9. Drizzle it once more with melted butter and bake for 3/4 hour in a fairly hot oven
10. After this time, turn out the casserole, separate the dumplings from one another, arrange them on a platter and serve at table with custard or cream

title: Süßstückchen feinster Art (Fine Sweet Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams beaten butter
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 5 egg yolks
- 4 decagrams pressed yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
- 56 decagrams flour
- melted butter for brushing
- filling of choice

## Directions

1. Mix the beaten butter with sugar, salt, and gradually stir in the egg yolks
2. Add the pressed yeast dissolved in lukewarm milk and the flour, stirring well together
3. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon, then cover and let it rise in a warm place until doubled in height
4. Using a spoon, place pieces of dough on a flour-dusted board and press them apart with floured fingers
5. Fill the center of each piece with 1 teaspoon of filling, and press the dough together over it with 4 fingers
6. Place in a butter-greased casserole, brush the sides where rolls touch with melted butter, and cover with a cloth
7. Let rise in a warm place until the dough has risen again
8. Brush with melted butter and bake for 1 hour
9. Turn out, separate the rolls from each other, arrange on a plate, sprinkle with sugar, and serve

title: Tafel-Bouillon, oder trockene Fleischbruhe (Table Bouillon, or Dried Meat Broth)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- about 12 pounds of juicy and strong beef
- marrow from some bones
- flesh from a couple of old hens together with their bones
- one calf's foot
- some mace
- some ginger and pepper

## Directions

1. Let all of this cook for 12 hours in an earthenware pot over charcoal fire, covered, stirring occasionally and skimming
2. Strain it through a hair sieve, skim off the fat and let it cool
3. Let the gelée slowly thicken over gentle charcoal fire, then allow it to solidify in a tin dish, cut it when cooled into pieces three fingers long and wide
4. Dry it in an earthenware dish in a baking oven when the bread has been removed, and keep it dry
5. For unexpected visits, on travels, etc., make a meat soup by cooking chervil, spinach, etc. in water with salt and now dissolving one lot of these tablets over charcoal fire in two soup plates of broth
6. Add some seasoning if desired
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Tamarind Soufflé
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Deka semolina
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter cold milk
- 16 Deka powdered sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- 5 egg whites
- butter for coating the bowl

## Directions

1. Put 8 Deka semolina, 4 egg yolks, and 1/4 liter cold milk in a snow bowl and beat with a whisk at low heat until it forms a thick mass.
2. Stir in 16 Deka butter well, pour it into a deep bowl and let it cool.
3. Beat 16 Deka powdered sugar and 5 egg yolks for 1/2 hour, then stir into the cooled semolina mass.
4. Lightly fold in the snow of 5 egg whites.
5. Line a bowl brushed with butter with the mixture and bake for 3/4 hour at low heat in the oven.
6. Remove the bowl from the oven, turn it out onto a second bowl, sprinkle the cake with finely ground sugar, and serve immediately.

title: Tauben auf Wildbretart (Pigeons en salmis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons, cleaned, dressed, and larded
- Speck (bacon), sliced in thin strips
- Root vegetables
- Onion
- Whole spices
- 5 crushed juniper berries
- Broth
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Black bread, sliced thin
- Sour cream (optional)
- Lemon slices
- Puff pastry or butter paste for garnish

## Directions

1. Line a casserole with speck, thinly sliced root vegetables, onion, whole spices, and crushed juniper berries.
2. Place the prepared, dressed, and larded pigeons in the casserole and let them braise covered for approximately 1/4 hour.
3. After about 1/4 hour, pour some broth, wine, and vinegar over them and let the pigeons braise covered and softly with thinly sliced black bread.
4. Remove the pigeons and cut them lengthwise down the middle.
5. Arrange on a platter and pour the skimmed and strained sauce over them.
6. If desired, add some spoonfuls of sour cream to the strained sauce and bring to a boil again.
7. Pigeons prepared this way can also be filled with liver forcemeat.
8. Arrange lemon slices on the pigeons and garnish with fallen or puff pastry.

title: Tauben in Papilloten (Squabs in Paper Cases)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Four squabs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine ground herbs
- Butter
- Scraped bacon
- Mushrooms
- Parsley
- Shallots
- Seasonings
- White thick paper
- Oil

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the four squabs, remove the claws, tuck in the feet, and cut through the back
2. Flatten them with a knife and sprinkle with salt, pepper, and finely ground herbs
3. Pass a piece of butter and scraped bacon with the squabs and fine herbs over charcoal fire until it has drawn together, then remove to a plate
4. Throw finely chopped mushrooms, parsley, shallots, and some seasonings into the same butter, let it sweat for a while, and pour everything over the squabs
5. Set the prepared squabs aside to cool
6. Make capsules from white thick paper, each holding half a squab, grease the capsules inside with oil, and place the squab inside
7. At the moment of serving, place them on a grate, grill them very slowly, and serve clear sauce underneath
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Tauben mit feinen Kräutern (Pigeons aux fines herbes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons
- Butter
- Chervil, finely cut
- Green parsley, finely cut
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- White wine
- Broth

## Directions

1. After removing the entrails and cleaning the pigeons in water, cut them apart, remove the breast and back bones, and beat the meat with the flat side of a meat cleaver.
2. Heavily butter a shallow casserole, lay the meat in it, sprinkle with finely cut chervil, green parsley, salt, and pepper, and quickly brown on both sides.
3. Remove the pigeons and arrange on a platter.
4. Dust the butter remaining in the casserole with flour, roast it, pour in white wine and some broth, stir well, and let it simmer.
5. Pour this sauce over the arranged pigeons.

title: Tauben, gebraten (Roasted Pigeons)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- young fleshy pigeons
- butter
- semmel (bread roll), one and a half
- milk
- parsley, finely cut
- saffron
- salt
- two eggs and one yolk

## Directions

1. Blow the pigeons up with a feather quill, pluck and clean them well, salt them, and let them lie for half an hour
2. For the filling for two pigeons: beat a piece of butter until fluffy
3. Rub the crust from one and a half semmels, soak the crumb in milk, press it out well, add finely cut green parsley, a little saffron and salt, stir well together
4. Beat in two eggs and one yolk, stir for a while
5. Fill the pigeons with this mixture through a funnel, tie them up, thread them on a thin spit
6. Roast them for half an hour, frequently basting with butter during roasting
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Tee Souffle (Tea Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping teaspoon Holland's tea
- 0.5 liter heavy cream, boiling
- 16 hectograms flour
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 14 hectograms butter
- 16 hectograms sugar
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Place 1 heaping teaspoon Holland's tea in a casserole, pour 0.5 liter boiling heavy cream over it and let stand covered until cold
2. Strain through a sieve
3. Place 16 hectograms flour in a casserole, gradually stir smooth with the strained tea
4. Add 4 egg yolks, 2 whole eggs, 14 hectograms butter and 16 hectograms sugar and stir over the fire until the mixture separates from the spoon
5. Set aside, and after completely cooling, add 6 egg yolks one by one, stirring
6. Finally fold in the snow from 6 egg whites lightly
7. Fill this mixture into a form buttered and floured, and bake at low heat for approximately 1 hour

title: Teig zu flachen (französischen) Kuchen (Dough for Flat French Cakes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 Deka flour
- 32 Deka butter
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 16 Deka finely sifted sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped lemon zest
- 5 egg yolks
- approximately 1/8 liter cream

## Directions

1. Place flour on a board
2. Cut in 32 Deka butter and rub fine with hands
3. Make a well in the center
4. Add 1/2 teaspoon salt, 16 Deka finely sifted sugar, 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped lemon zest, 5 egg yolks and approximately 1/8 liter cream into the well
5. Mix well with a knife and work into a fairly firm, smooth dough like strudel dough
6. Place on a flour-dusted board, cover with a cloth and let rest one hour in a cool place
7. After this time, roll out strongly knife-back thick on a flour-dusted board and use as desired

title: Teig zu flachen Obstkuchen (Dough for Flat Fruit Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca flour
- 22 deca firm butter
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 whole egg
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 6 deca finely pounded sugar
- 1 teaspoon finely cut lemon peel
- approximately 1/4 liter cream

## Directions

1. Put 28 deca flour on a board
2. Cut 22 deca firm butter into small pieces and work it fine with the flour using both hands
3. Make a depression in the middle, add 1 egg yolk, 1 whole egg, 1 teaspoon salt, 6 deca finely pounded sugar, 1 teaspoon finely cut lemon peel, and approximately 1/4 liter cream
4. Mix everything well with a knife and work it as quickly as possible into a smooth dough until no cracks are visible
5. Put the dough on a board dusted with flour and let it rest 1/2 hour in a cold place
6. After this time, roll out the dough on a flour-dusted board, fold it together, and let it rest another 1/2 hour
7. Then roll out the dough thick as a small finger and use as desired

title: Teig zu Flaumkuchen (Dough for Flaumkuchen Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 deca flour
- 32 deca butter
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 16 deca fine sifted sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped

## Directions

1. Place flour on a board
2. Cut 32 deca butter into it
3. Rub finely with both hands
4. Make a well in the middle
5. Add 1/2 teaspoon salt, 16 deca finely sifted sugar, and 1/2 teaspoon finely chopped ingredients into the well

title: Tendron de Veau à la Broche (Stuffed Veal Breast Roasted on the Spit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal breast
- 6 Deca beaten butter
- 2 whole eggs
- ½ teaspoon finely cut green parsley
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- 8 Deca bread, rind removed, softened in milk, well pressed out
- paper brushed with butter
- twine

## Directions

1. From the lower broad side up toward the neck, open the meat with a knife from the ribs, insert the hand, and separate it as widely as possible, being careful not to create another opening
2. Beat the butter and stir in 2 whole eggs, ½ teaspoon finely cut green parsley, salt, and season with nutmeg
3. Add 8 Deca softened and well-pressed bread and stir well together
4. Fill the veal breast with this mixture by spreading it between flesh and ribs and distributing it evenly, closing the opening while pressing the hand on the upper skin and pushing the filling in
5. Sew the opening shut with a trussing needle using twine
6. Press the breast flat, salt it on both sides, bind it with 2 paper towels brushed with butter, and roast it on the spit for approximately 1 hour over strong fire
7. After this time, remove the paper and continue roasting until it has a brown color
8. Remove the breast from the spit, cut it into pieces of desired size, and serve it with the defatted drippings and any desired salad or vegetables

title: Tendrons de Veau (Braised Veal Breast)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal breast with bones removed
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the veal breast by pushing back the meat, turning the bone, and pulling it out
2. Salt and pepper the breast
3. Roll it together and bind it with twine
4. Treat it like the naturally roasted veal breast

title: Tendrons de Veau Braisés (Braised Veal Breast)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal breast
- boiling salted water
- white butter sauce as needed

## Directions

1. Cook the veal breast in boiling salted water until it has a white color
2. To maintain this color, remove the veal breast and place it in cold water for a few minutes
3. Cut the meat into pieces of desired size
4. Place it in a baking pan and cover it with as much white butter sauce as needed

title: Tendrons de Veau Farcis (Stuffed Veal Breast)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal breast
- salt
- pepper
- veal forcemeat, approximately ½ kilo
- pencil-thick, finger-long pieces of boiled ham
- cleaned sardelles
- cooked tongue
- truffles, finely sliced, alternatively
- butter for roasting
- warm butter for basting

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the breast by carefully pushing back the meat, turning the bone, and pulling it out
2. Carefully remove the cartilage from the breast
3. Beat the breast with the flat side of a meat cleaver
4. Salt and pepper it
5. Brush it finger-thick with veal forcemeat (approximately ½ kilo)
6. Lay pencil-thick, finger-long pieces of boiled ham, cleaned sardelles, and cooked tongue on this, or use finely sliced truffles instead of the sardelles
7. Roll it together: turn the thick part inward and roll the meat toward the thin part
8. Bind it tightly with twine and place it in a roasting pan so that the end where the meat closes lies on top
9. Cover the meat with warm butter and roast it for 1½ hours, basting frequently with the drippings that form
10. Remove the meat, loosen the twine, cut it into pieces of desired size, arrange these on a serving dish, and pour the defatted drippings alongside

title: Tendrons de Veau Roulés (Veal Breast Rolled with Bratwurst)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kilo bratwurst
- 4 pieces of mushrooms, finely sliced
- finely cut green parsley
- butter
- 2 whole eggs
- salt
- pepper
- veal breast

## Directions

1. Remove the filling from ½ kilo of bratwurst
2. Roast 4 pieces of finely sliced mushrooms with finely cut green parsley in butter
3. Mix this roasted mixture with 2 whole eggs
4. Stir well together, add salt and pepper as desired
5. Spread this mixture on the spread out, boiled veal breast
6. Roll it together, bind it tightly with twine, and roast it like the previously described veal breast, or braise it with soup and wine
7. When serving, garnish it with any desired puree or braised mushrooms or braised cucumbers and pour the defatted drippings over the meat

title: Tete de Porc à l'Autrichienne (Pig's Head Austrian Style)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's head, cleaned
- Water
- Vinegar
- Onion
- Root vegetables
- Parsnip, small amount
- Salt
- Pepper
- Garlic, crushed (optional)
- Rocambole or shallots, finely chopped (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil the cleaned pig's head gently in 1 1/2 liters water, 1/4 liter vinegar with onion, root vegetables and some parsnip
2. Remove the meat from the broth, cut it into finger-length pieces, and arrange on a platter
3. Remove the fat from the broth and add the necessary salt and pepper
4. The flavor must be pleasantly sour
5. If desired, add some crushed garlic, or finely chopped rocambole and shallots, mix well together
6. Pour over the cut pig's head and let it stand in a cool place until completely stiff
7. Serve either with the platter, or cut into pieces and serve with vinegar and oil
8. Pig's feet can also be used instead of pig's head

title: Tete de Porc à la Vinaigrette (Pig's Head with Vinaigrette)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's head, cleaned
- Onion, cut into pieces
- Root vegetables
- Parsnip, small amount
- Vinegar
- Water
- Salt
- Seasonings
- Brains

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from a cleaned pig's head into pieces
2. Boil it with a piece of cut onion, some root vegetables and a little parsnip in vinegar and water so that the liquid covers the meat (3/4 liter water and 1/2 liter vinegar)
3. Add salt and seasonings when the meat is sufficiently tender
4. Remove the meat with a wooden spoon and arrange it on a platter in a circle
5. Place grated brains in the middle
6. Serve with vinegar and oil

title: Thee (Tea)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Weak black tea
- Hot water
- Boiling water
- Cold cream
- Sugar
- Rum or cognac
- Lemons

## Directions

1. Fill the teapot with hot water to warm it, then pour out the water after a few minutes.
2. Add tea (for 1 person use 1 teaspoon full; for several persons use somewhat less per individual).
3. Pour boiling water over it, but fill the pot only half full.
4. After 5 minutes, add the remaining water, also boiling, and after the same length of time serve the tea.
5. Always provide boiling hot water, either in a pot or kept boiling hot in the teapot by a spirit lamp.
6. Simultaneously serve unboiled cream, 1 bottle of rum or cognac, and lemons so that the tea can be drunk as desired.
7. Pour the tea through a strainer into cups, but do not fill the cups completely with tea, always leave about 1 finger's width empty.

title: Thee-Gefrornees (Tea Frozen Dessert)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deca tea
- 1 liter boiling cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as the Orange Gefrornees (No. 1728), with the difference that instead of orange peel, throw 4 deca tea into the hot cream

title: Thees Staaten (Tea Slices)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deca butter, beaten
- 16 Deca powdered sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1 teaspoon rum
- 16 Deca flour
- 1 egg white, beaten
- finely sifted sugar
- finely chopped citron
- raisins
- currants
- blanched almonds, cut into sticks
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Mix 16 Deca beaten butter gradually with 16 Deca powdered sugar, 4 egg yolks and 1 whole egg
2. Stir for 1 hour and then mix well with 1 teaspoon rum and 16 Deca flour
3. Spread knife-back width thick on a butter-brushed baking sheet
4. Cover with the beaten egg white from 1 egg
5. Sprinkle with finely sifted sugar
6. Distribute finely chopped citron, raisins, currants, blanched almonds cut into sticks and pistachios over the top

title: Thees Statuten (Thees Biscuits)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deka beaten butter
- 16 deka (umid) sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1 teaspoon rum
- 16 deka flour
- 1 egg white, beaten
- fine sifted sugar
- finely chopped citron
- raisins
- blanched, finely cut almonds
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Beat 16 deka beaten butter with 16 deka sugar, 4 egg yolks, and 1 whole egg gradually until well combined
2. Stir for 1 hour, then mix in 1 teaspoon rum and 16 deka flour well together
3. Spread this mixture knife-blade-thick on a butter-greased baking sheet
4. Cover with beaten egg white from 1 egg
5. Sprinkle with finely sifted sugar
6. Scatter finely chopped citron, raisins, blanched finely cut almonds, and pistachios over it
7. Bake in a hot oven, then cut diagonally into 2 finger-wide and finger-long pieces
8. Serve with tea

title: Timbale (Timbale)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff dough
- Butter
- Flour
- Dried peas, beans, or bran (for filling)
- Beaten eggs
- Salmi of game or other meat filling (optional)
- Sautéed vegetables, rice, macaroni, or sauce (for filling)

## Directions

1. Brush a casserole or mold with butter and dust with flour
2. Cut a square of finger-thick puff dough to fit the size of the casserole
3. Fold the four corners lightly together in the middle and place in the casserole
4. Spread the four corners apart over the sides of the casserole
5. Distribute the dough in the casserole so it forms no wrinkles and trim any excess hanging over the edge
6. Fill the inner empty space with dried uncooked peas, beans, or bran and cover with a finger-thick dough disc fitted to the size of the casserole
7. Make several cuts in the dough cover with a knife
8. Place in the oven and bake at fairly strong heat for approximately 1 hour until the dough is golden yellow
9. Remove the casserole and loosen the dough cover around the edges
10. Place the dough cover on a plate, cover the casserole with a spoon, and holding both together firmly, invert the casserole quickly
11. Let stand for a few moments, then carefully remove the casserole and let the timbale cool slightly
12. Carefully place in a bowl, remove any remaining peas or beans with a spoon or fork
13. Fill as desired: brush with hot game salmi and garnish with sautéed game or other meat, or fill with sautéed cabbage mixed with meat, sautéed rice, macaroni, or hare or partridge salmi
14. Cover again with the lid and serve hot at the table

title: Timbale von Repphühnern (Timbale of Partridges)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 prepared partridges
- Butter
- Salt
- Fine herbs
- Pepper
- Spices
- 1 glass of wine
- Shortcrust dough
- Bacon sheet
- Eggs
- Spanish or piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the breasts and legs from the prepared partridges
2. Brown them in butter with salt, fine herbs, pepper, and spices
3. Add a glass of wine and cook everything until tender, then let cool
4. Roll out shortcrust dough to finger thickness
5. Line a buttered casserole symmetrically with the dough, pressing firmly so it does not fold
6. Cover the bottom and sides with the forcemeat
7. Arrange the partridges in the casserole
8. Cover them with the remaining forcemeat and the herbs in which they were cooked
9. Top with a thin bacon sheet
10. Make a proper lid from the remaining dough, brush the protruding pastry edge with eggs, place the lid on top and seal well
11. Make a small hole in the center of the lid
12. Bake slowly for approximately 2 hours
13. Turn out onto a plate, carefully remove the lid so it does not break, and make small openings in the forcemeat with a knife
14. When serving, gradually pour Spanish or piquant sauce into the timbale
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Tirapfeu Hon (Chocolate Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 22 decagrams butter
- 1 tablet grated chocolate
- juice of 1 lemon
- snow from 1 egg white
- finely cut almonds
- fine pounded sugar
- wafers

## Directions

1. Stir together butter, grated chocolate, and lemon juice in a deep bowl
2. Mix lightly with the snow from 1 egg white
3. Using a coffee spoon, take out equal-sized pieces and release them with the handle of a second spoon in small heaps at finger-width distance on a baking sheet lined with wafers
4. Sprinkle with finely cut almonds and fine pounded sugar
5. Dry for approximately 2 hours in the oven at low heat
6. When completely dry, remove from oven, cut each piece off individually while keeping the wafer attached

title: Tiroler Knödel (Tyrolean Dumplings)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 deca finely diced lard
- 32 deca finely diced rolls
- 1 liter flour
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/2 liter milk
- Optional: finely diced, fried boiled meat or braised vegetables such as sauerkraut or cabbage

## Directions

1. Roast 30 deca of finely diced lard until it looks glassy.
2. Add 32 deca of finely diced rolls, stir both together over the fire until the fat has been absorbed into the rolls.
3. Place them in a deep bowl and let them cool.
4. Half an hour before cooking, add 1 liter of flour to the rolls.
5. Beat 2 whole eggs and 1 egg yolk with 1/2 liter of milk well together, pour it over the rolls, stir everything well together, and let it rest covered until cooking time so that the roasted rolls soften.
6. Form dumplings of any size, place them in simmering salted water, and let them cook covered for 1.5 hours.
7. If desired, a spoonful of finely diced, fried boiled meat or similarly braised vegetables such as sauerkraut or cabbage can be placed in the center of each dumpling.

title: Tiroler Strudel (Tyrolean Strudel)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- half pound almonds
- half pound sugar
- zest of an orange or lemon, finely cut
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 loth citron (Citronat), finely cut
- egg white

## Directions

1. Make a butter dough, roll it out more in length than width, like a noodle dough
2. Blanch and pound half pound almonds finely, then equally much sugar finely pounded, cut the zest of an orange or lemon finely
3. Put all this in a mixing bowl, add 2 whole eggs and 4 egg yolks, stir for half an hour so it becomes quite fluffy
4. Spread it on the butter dough, spread it with a knife, sprinkle it with 2 loth finely cut citron
5. Roll the strudel loosely together, place it on a butter-brushed baking sheet, brush it with egg white, and bake it with sustained heat
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Tiroler Strudel von Germteig (Tyrolean Yeast Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 5 egg yolks
- 2 deca pressed yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter milk
- 30 deca flour
- almond filling as specified in Almond Strudel of Butter Dough (No. 1090)
- raisins
- currants
- finely chopped citron

## Directions

1. Beat butter fluffy, gradually add sugar, salt, and egg yolks
2. Mix in the yeast dissolved in milk and flour, mix well
3. Beat the dough until it lifts from the spoon
4. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in height
5. Roll out dough on a flour-dusted board to the thickness of a small finger
6. Brush with almond filling, sprinkle with raisins, currants, and citron
7. Roll up the dough and place on a butter-greased baking sheet
8. Cover and let rise in a warm place for 1/2 hour
9. Brush with melted butter and bake at moderate heat for 3/4 hour
10. Serve warm or cold at table with cream or custard milk

title: Tokay from Country Wine (Tokayer aus Landwein)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one barrel (half ohm) of good and strong French white wine
- 30 pounds yellow cane sugar
- 16 pounds large raisins, freed from stems and torn by hand

## Directions

1. Fill the barrel not quite full with good and strong French white wine
2. Add the yellow cane sugar and the large raisins (which must be of superior quality; small raisins give a particularly good Hungarian flavor)
3. Once the sugar has dissolved and everything is well shaken together, in summer bring the wine to the ground where the hottest days are most suitable, but in winter place it in a heated room of 13-14 degrees Reaumur
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Tongues in Hetschepetsch Sauce (Zungen in der Hetſchepetſch-Sauee)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- tongue
- salt water
- 4 to 5 spoonfuls of preserved hetschepetsch
- sugar
- white wine

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue in salt water until tender and cut it into thin slices.
2. Thin 4 to 5 spoonfuls of preserved hetschepetsch with some sugar and white wine to make a sauce, pour it on the bottom of the dish, and arrange the sliced tongue in two rows on the sauce, but in such a way that you leave an empty space in the middle of the dish so that the sauce is visible.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Tongues in Polish Sauce (Zungen in der pohlniſchen Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- tongue
- salt water
- bacon sheets
- one large onion
- one yellow carrot
- whole allspice
- veal bones
- flour
- red wine
- broth
- 3 to 4 loth of gooseberries
- 3 loth of blanched and julienned almonds

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue in salt water until tender, remove the skin, and place it in a casserole; add bacon sheets, a large onion, a yellow carrot cut into slices, some whole allspice and some veal bones; let all of this braise on a moderate fire until it becomes nicely brown.
2. Dust a good spoonful of flour over it, let it brown, fill it halfway with red wine and halfway with broth, and let it cook well; strain the sauce through a sieve into another casserole, add 3 to 4 loth of cleaned gooseberries and 3 loth of blanched and julienned almonds, place the tongue in it, and let it come to a good boil.
3. Before serving, lard the tongue throughout with julienned almonds, arrange the sauce on the dish, and place the tongue on top.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Tongues on a Spit Roasted, with Anchovy Sauce (Zungen am Spieße gebraten, mit Sardellensauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one fresh, large tongue
- bacon
- anchovies
- butter
- half a seidel of cream
- breadcrumbs
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cook a fresh, large tongue until tender, then remove the skin; it should not be taken from the pot until it is ready to be placed on the spit; it must also be served from the spit to the table.
2. Once the skin is removed, lard it beautifully and neatly with two parts bacon and one part anchovies, place it on a thin spit, let a piece of butter melt, add half a seidel of cream to it, and baste it three to four times during roasting, sprinkle it with breadcrumbs, squeeze lemon juice over it, and apply the sauce once more.
3. The tongue must be roasted quickly; then remove it from the spit, place it on a dish, and pour the sauce over it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Topfen Filling (Cheese Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 dkg butter
- 1 egg
- 5 dkg sugar
- topfen (cheese)
- cream
- lemon zest, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Beat 5 dkg butter until creamy
2. Stir in 1 egg, 5 dkg sugar, the strained topfen, a few spoons of cream, and finely chopped lemon zest

title: Topfen Fülle (Quark Filling)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 Deka strained quark
- sugar to taste
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped lemon zest
- finely chopped and blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Pass 20 Deka of quark through a sieve
2. Sweeten to taste
3. Mix in 1 teaspoon finely chopped lemon zest and finely chopped, blanched almonds

title: Topfen or Powidl Pockets (Cheese or Plum Pockets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg round potatoes
- 5 dkg butter
- 2 whole eggs
- salt
- 2 spoons cream
- flour
- 1/2 kg topfen or powidl (plum butter)
- 12 dkg butter or fat for roasting
- breadcrumbs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a potato dough from the potatoes, 5 dkg butter, 2 eggs, salt, 2 spoons cream, and flour
2. Roll out thinly and cut into rounds
3. Place filling in the center of each round
4. Brush the edges with egg white and fold together, pressing to seal
5. Place the pockets in a pot of boiling salted water and cook slowly for 5–8 minutes
6. Remove and drain
7. Toss in a large pot with breadcrumbs roasted in 12 dkg hot butter or fat
8. Transfer to a round platter, dust with sugar, and serve warm

title: Topfen Torte (Curd Cheese Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 21 dkg sugar
- 21 dkg sifted hard curd cheese
- 6 stiff egg whites
- 21 dkg swelled grated almonds
- 7 dkg breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cream together 21 dkg butter
2. Stir in 6 egg yolks and 21 dkg sugar
3. Mix in 21 dkg sifted hard curd cheese, then fold in 6 stiff egg whites together with 21 dkg swelled grated almonds and 7 dkg breadcrumbs
4. Fill the batter into a buttered, floured torte form and bake at higher heat until light brown

title: Topfen-Kuchen (Curd Cheese Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough as for cherry cake
- 12 Deka butter
- 12 Deka finely sifted sugar
- 1 whole egg
- 32 Deka pressed curd cheese (Topfen)
- 8 Deka raisins
- 1/4 liter cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as described for cherry cake
2. Instead of cherries, cover the dough with the following curd filling:
3. Beat 12 Deka butter fluffy
4. Mix in 12 Deka finely sifted sugar
5. Stir in 1 whole egg
6. Mix in 32 Deka pressed curd cheese, 8 Deka raisins and 1/4 liter cream, one after another
7. Bake as for cherry cake

title: Topfenfülle (Curd Cheese Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dkg butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 2-3 tablespoons vanilla sugar
- lemon peel, finely chopped
- topfen, pressed through a sieve
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cream the 6 dkg butter
2. Stir in 3 egg yolks
3. Add 2-3 tablespoons vanilla sugar and finely chopped lemon peel
4. Mix in the pressed topfen
5. Fold in 3 stiffly beaten egg whites

title: Topfenfülle (Quark Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dkg butter
- 1 egg
- 2 spoons sugar
- finely chopped lemon peel
- 2 spoons cream
- quark (curd cheese), passed through a sieve
- 3 dkg currants

## Directions

1. To the mixture of 4 dkg butter, add 1 egg, 2 spoons sugar, finely chopped lemon peel, 2 spoons cream, the passed quark, and 3 dkg currants.

title: Topfenhaluschka (Curd Cheese Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 dkg flour
- 2 eggs
- salt
- water
- 20 dkg curd cheese
- 12-15 dkg bacon

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough and cut into larger fleckerln
2. Cook in boiling salted water for 5 minutes, drain and shock
3. Cut the bacon into small dice and roast until light yellow
4. Strain the fat into a large casserole
5. Add the fleckerln, salt them and warm in the oven
6. Arrange on a round platter, sprinkle with strained curd cheese and top with bacon cracklings

title: Topfenknödel (Curd Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 dkg butter
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- 1/4 kg curd
- 3 egg whites
- 12-14 dkg breadcrumbs
- salt water
- butter for breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cream 8 dkg butter and stir in 3 egg yolks and salt
2. Add the strained curd
3. Fold in 3 stiffly beaten egg whites together with 12-14 dkg breadcrumbs
4. Let stand for a while
5. If the curd is stiff, add a few spoonfuls of cream
6. Form dumplings
7. Place in a casserole in boiling salted water
8. Cook slowly for 5-8 minutes
9. Lift onto a round platter
10. Pour over with breadcrumbs roasted in butter

title: Topfenknödel mit Ei (Curd Cheese Dumplings with Egg)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 deca butter
- 5 whole eggs
- 60 deca strained curd cheese
- Salt
- 1/2 liter flour
- 12 deca white breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Beat 10 deca of butter until fluffy.
2. Add 5 whole eggs, 60 deca of strained curd cheese, some salt, 1/2 liter of flour, and 12 deca of white breadcrumbs, one after another, and stir well.
3. Form medium-sized dumplings from this dough and treat them as directed in the recipe for curd cheese dumplings with semolina.

title: Topfenkoch (Curd Cheese Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 maß good milk
- 6 whole eggs
- juice of 1 lemon
- bread crumbs from 1 small roll
- 12 loth butter
- 6 whole eggs
- 6 egg yolks
- zest of 1 lemon, finely diced
- 3 loth citron, finely cut
- 1 loth pistachio nuts, finely cut
- 4 loth almonds, finely ground

## Directions

1. Put 1 maß good milk in a pot with 6 whole eggs and juice of 1 lemon
2. Beat everything together well
3. Strain through a sieve into a ramekin
4. Set over heat and stir constantly until it becomes a not too firm curd cheese
5. Strain through a sieve and let cool
6. Pound it in a mortar with bread crumbs from 1 small roll
7. Beat 12 loth butter in a bowl until fluffy
8. Beat in 6 whole eggs and 6 egg yolks, each well beaten, then the curd cheese
9. Add finely diced lemon zest, finely cut citron, finely cut pistachio nuts, and finely ground almonds
10. Stir everything constantly until baking time
11. Butter a metal dish and ring mold well
12. Fill the mixture in and bake in a cake pan
13. Sprinkle with sugar
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Topfenkuchen (Quark Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dkg flour
- 21 dkg butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon rum
- 2 tablespoons cream
- 2 dkg sugar
- 5 dkg butter for filling
- 2 egg yolks for filling
- 2 tablespoons vanilla sugar
- finely chopped lemon zest
- ½ kg quark (Topfen)
- 3 dkg raisins
- 1 egg for brushing
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make a crumbly dough from 28 dkg flour, 21 dkg butter, 2 egg yolks, 1 tablespoon rum, 2 tablespoons cream, and 2 dkg sugar
2. After resting, divide into 2 parts and roll each to baking sheet size
3. Place one part on an unbuttered baking sheet and spread the filling on it
4. Sprinkle 3 dkg raisins on top
5. Place the second dough part on top and press edges together
6. Brush with beaten egg and bake at higher heat until light brown
7. Cut into pieces, sprinkle with powdered sugar, and serve cooled
8. For the filling: Beat 5 dkg butter until creamy, mix in 2 egg yolks, 2 tablespoons vanilla sugar, finely chopped lemon zest, and strained quark

title: Topfennudeln (Curd Cheese Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- curd cheese, pressed
- 1 egg
- salt
- 4 dkg butter
- 8 dkg flour
- 12 dkg hot butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Mix the pressed curd cheese with 1 egg, salt, 4 dkg butter, and 8 dkg flour on a board to form a dough
2. Roll into a log and cut into equal-sized pieces
3. Form thick noodles
4. Place in a casserole with boiling salted water
5. Cook very slowly for 3-4 minutes
6. Toast breadcrumbs in 12 dkg hot butter until light brown
7. Drain the noodles and toss in the browned breadcrumbs
8. Serve on a round platter

title: Topfenschmarren (Curd Cheese Fritter Pancake)
body:
## Ingredients

- curd cheese, pressed
- 3 egg yolks
- cream
- salt
- lemon zest
- 4 dkg raisins
- 3 egg whites, beaten stiff
- 14 dkg flour
- 6 dkg hot butter or fat

## Directions

1. Mix the pressed curd cheese with 3 egg yolks, cream, salt, lemon zest, 4 dkg raisins, and 3 beaten egg whites
2. Fold in 14 dkg flour
3. Pour into a pan with 6 dkg hot butter or fat
4. Prepare and serve like Semmelschmarren

title: Topfenstrudel (Curd Cheese Strudel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 dkg Flour
- 1 Egg
- Salt
- Butter (walnut-sized)
- Almost 1 dl lukewarm water
- 6 dkg Butter (for filling)
- 3 Egg yolks
- 8 dkg Sugar
- 40 dkg sieved curd cheese
- ¼ l Cream
- Lemon zest (finely chopped)
- 3 Egg whites

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough from flour, egg, salt, butter, and lukewarm water
2. After resting, stretch thinly on floured cloth
3. Prepare filling by creaming butter, add egg yolks and sugar, mix in sieved curd cheese, cream, and lemon zest, fold in beaten egg whites
4. Spread filling on dough, roll up, and place in buttered baking pan
5. Bake until light brown, basting frequently with hot milk
6. Cut into pieces, sprinkle with sugar, and serve warm

title: Topfentorte (Curd Cheese Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 21 dkg sugar
- 21 dkg strained hard curd cheese
- 6 egg whites
- 21 dkg blanched grated almonds
- 7 dkg breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Stir 6 egg yolks, 21 dkg sugar, 21 dkg sieved hard curd cheese, 6 egg whites together with 21 dkg blanched grated almonds and 7 dkg breadcrumbs into the beaten 21 dkg butter.
2. Fill the mass into the buttered, floured cake form.
3. Bake at higher heat until light brown.
4. Remove from form when cooled and serve dusted with powdered sugar as a sweet dish.

title: Topinambours (Erdbirnen) in der Butterfauce (Topinambours in Butter Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- topinambours (Jerusalem artichokes)
- butter
- optional: veal or mutton

## Directions

1. Boil the topinambours or Jerusalem artichokes properly and peel them.
2. Cut the large ones into several pieces and leave the small ones whole.
3. Make a butter sauce and boil them in it.
4. If desired, they can be used as a side dish and boiled together with veal or mutton.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Torte au fecule de pommes de terre (Potato Starch Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 deca lemon sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- juice of 1 whole lemon
- 12 egg whites
- 12 deca potato starch
- 12 deca finest almond flour
- butter
- 8-10 deca small raisins (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat 24 deca lemon sugar and 12 egg yolks with the juice of 1 whole lemon for 1 hour.
2. Lightly fold in the snow (beaten egg whites) from 12 egg whites alternately with 12 deca potato starch and 12 deca finest almond flour.
3. Fill a buttered cake form lined with arrowroot with this mixture.
4. Bake at low heat for approximately 1 hour.
5. Alternatively, omit the almond flour and use only 24 deca potato starch; in this case, beat the juice of 2 lemons with the eggs, lightly fold in half the flour, and then lightly fold in the beaten egg whites simultaneously with the remaining half of the flour by spoonfuls.
6. Small raisins (8-10 deca) can be mixed into the dough if baked in a Gugelhupf form.
7. This cake can also be covered with chocolate glaze.

title: Torte in Naturbutter Wittemaße (Cake in Natural Butter Spanish Style)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- writing paper
- 8 sheets of writing paper
- unsalted bacon
- 8 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 32 deca vanilla sugar
- powdered sugar
- desired fruit foam or frozen dessert

## Directions

1. Cut approximately 8 round discs from white writing paper.
2. In the middle of each, cut out circular openings so that each disc forms a ring; each successive ring becomes somewhat narrower, with the eighth ring being approximately one finger-width broad.
3. Line all paper strips with unsalted bacon and place them side by side on a baking sheet with the lined side facing up.
4. Beat 8 egg whites stiffly and lightly mix with 32 deca vanilla sugar.
5. Using a paper cornet or syringe, pipe this mixture onto the paper rings without interruption and at even height (approximately finger-high).
6. Sprinkle with finely pounded sugar.
7. Additionally, cut a round disc the same size as the broadest ring, brush it with unsalted bacon, and likewise cover it finger-high with the snow mixture and sprinkle with finely pounded sugar.
8. Dry all of these in the oven at high heat. As these rings take up considerable space on the baking sheet and it is usually not possible to dry them all simultaneously, they can be made in batches, though the snow mixture should always be prepared only shortly before piping and placing in the oven.
9. Once completely dry, carefully remove the rings from the paper with a sharp, thin knife.
10. Place the large round disc on a cake plate, arrange the broadest ring on top and secure with egg glaze or white marmalade; arrange all remaining rings, each progressively smaller, as well as the bottom ring on top.
11. Fill the middle empty space with desired fruit foam or frozen dessert and serve the cake immediately on an appropriate platter.

title: Torte Stärke (Potato Starch Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 Deka lemon sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- juice of 1 whole lemon
- whites of 12 eggs
- 12 Deka potato starch
- 12 Deka finest arrowroot flour

## Directions

1. Stir together 24 Deka lemon sugar and 12 egg yolks with the juice of 1 whole lemon for 1 minute
2. Lightly fold in the whites of 12 eggs with 12 Deka potato starch and 12 Deka finest arrowroot flour

title: Torte von Maidenelf (Almond Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 teaspoons butter
- 32 teaspoons finely pounded sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 tablespoons almond flour
- 32 teaspoons flour

## Directions

1. Mix 32 teaspoons butter with 32 teaspoons finely pounded sugar, add 8 egg yolks one after the other, and after each yolk stir in 1 tablespoon almond flour and the remaining flour, of which 32 teaspoons total by weight.
2. Fill this mass into a cake form brushed with butter and dusted with flour and bake approximately 1 hour in the oven.

title: Touched Linzer Torte (Tourte à la Linz)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka blanched almonds
- 1 egg white for grinding almonds
- 32 Deka beaten butter
- 24 Deka caster sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 32 Deka flour

## Directions

1. Grind blanched almonds very finely with egg white
2. Mix well with beaten butter
3. Add caster sugar and egg yolks one by one
4. Stir together for 1/2 hour
5. Mix in flour
6. Fill into a butter-greased and flour-dusted torte form
7. Bake for 3/4 to 1 hour

title: Touched Linzer Torte with Snow (Tourte à la Linz)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 Deka blanched almonds
- juice of 1 lemon
- 32 Deka butter
- 32 Deka caster sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 1.5 knife tips finely chopped lemon zest
- snow from 8 egg whites
- 32 Deka powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Grind blanched almonds very finely with lemon juice
2. Mix well with butter and caster sugar
3. Add egg yolks and lemon zest gradually
4. Stir for 1/4 hour
5. Fold in the snow of egg whites mixed with powdered sugar
6. Proceed as in the 'touched Linzer torte' (no. 1483)

title: Tourte aux noix (Walnut Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 teaspoons finely pounded walnuts
- 24 teaspoons finely pounded sugar
- 24 teaspoons peeled and passed almonds pounded with shell
- 1 pinch grated coffee
- 4 teaspoons white bread crumbs
- 1 1/6 liter boiling water
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 whole eggs
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix together 24 teaspoons finely pounded walnuts, 24 teaspoons finely pounded sugar, 24 teaspoons peeled and passed almonds pounded with shell and 1 pinch grated coffee.
2. Take 6 teaspoons of this mass, place it with 4 teaspoons white bread crumbs in a casserole, pour with 1 1/6 liter boiling water and let it boil for several minutes with constant stirring, then set aside and let cool.
3. In the meantime, mix the remaining mass, which is placed in a deep pan, gradually with 6 egg yolks and 4 whole eggs, and lastly lightly fold in the snow of 6 egg whites.
4. Divide this mass into two equal parts, which are filled one by one into cake forms brushed with butter and dusted with flour and baked at low heat for 1 2 hours in the oven.
5. After this time, place the cakes on a sieve and allow to cool, then spread one cake with the aforementioned cooked but cold filling, cover with the other cake, and frost or sprinkle with sugar as desired.
6. This baked cake mass may be cut out with any desired forms or cut into pieces the width and length of a finger.

title: Tourte aux noix mit Butter (Walnut Cake with Butter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 teaspoons butter
- 28 teaspoons finely pounded sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 24 teaspoons walnuts pounded very finely with 1 egg white
- 28 teaspoons flour

## Directions

1. Beat 28 teaspoons butter with 28 teaspoons finely pounded sugar and 6 egg yolks for 1 4 hours, add 24 teaspoons walnuts pounded very finely with 1 egg white, stir again for 3/4 hour and finally mix in 28 teaspoons flour.
2. Fill into a cake form brushed with butter and dusted with flour and bake for 1 hour in a well-heated oven.
3. After this time, remove the form's edge, place the cake on a board and cover with desired glaze once completely cooled.

title: Tourte aux noix mit Cœurs und spanischem Wind
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- mass as for No. 1495
- whipped vanilla cream
- Spanish wind mass for decoration

## Directions

1. Prepare the same mass as described for the Walnut Cake (No. 1495), fill it into a ring form brushed with butter and dusted with flour and bake at low heat for 3/4 to 1 hour in the oven.
2. Turn the cake onto a sieve and allow to cool.
3. Arrange the cooled cake on a platter, fill the middle empty space with whipped vanilla cream and finish with the following decoration.

title: Tourte aux noix mit Obere und spanischem Wind (Walnut Cake with Cream and Spanish Wind Mass)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- mass as for No. 1495
- cream filling
- Spanish wind mass for decoration

## Directions

1. Prepare the same mass as described for the Walnut Cake (No. 1495), fill it into a ring form brushed with butter and dusted with flour and bake at low heat for 3/4 to 1 hour in the oven.
2. After this time, turn the cake onto a sieve and allow to cool.
3. Once this is done, arrange the cake on a platter, fill the empty middle space with whipped vanilla cream and finish as follows.

title: Tourte aux noix ohne Butter (Walnut Cake without Butter)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 teaspoons egg whites
- 12 egg yolks
- 8-10 teaspoons pounded and passed hazelnuts
- 16 teaspoons pounded walnuts
- 1 pinch grated coffee
- 1 tablet grated chocolate
- 8 egg whites
- powdered sugar and pounded nuts or Spanish wind mass for filling and decoration

## Directions

1. Beat 28 teaspoons of egg whites with 12 egg yolks for 1 2 hours, add 8-10 teaspoons pounded and passed hazelnuts, 16 teaspoons pounded walnuts, 1 pinch grated coffee and 1 tablet grated chocolate, stir well together and lastly lightly fold in the snow of 8 egg whites.
2. Divide this mass into two equal parts, which are filled into cake forms brushed with butter and dusted with flour and baked for 3/4 to 1 hour in a well-heated oven, then cool on a sieve.
3. When this is done, trim the edges from both cakes that were on the upper side during baking, brush with desired filling, place one on the other and cover with desired glaze or decorate with Spanish wind mass, in which case allow it to dry a few minutes in the open oven.
4. As desired, instead of filling with pastry cream, the stiffly beaten snow of 2 egg whites may be lightly mixed with 8 teaspoons sugar, 1 tablet grated chocolate and 1 tablespoon pounded nuts and used to fill the cake.

title: Tourte Sableuse (Sand Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 teaspoons butter
- 28 teaspoons powdered sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1 pinch finely cut lemon peel
- 8 egg whites
- 28 teaspoons flour, or semolina, potato flour as desired

## Directions

1. Beat 28 teaspoons butter with 28 teaspoons powdered sugar and 8 egg yolks gradually and mix.
2. Stir for 1/2 hour with the juice of 1/2 lemon and 1 pinch finely cut lemon peel.
3. Lightly fold in the stiff snow of 8 egg whites with 28 teaspoons regular flour, or semolina or potato flour as desired.
4. Fill this mass into a cake form brushed with butter and dusted with flour and bake for 1 hour in a well-heated oven.
5. Once the form's edge has been removed and the cake cooled on a sieve, cover it with egg glaze, sprinkle with peeled finely cut almonds and sugar and serve on a flat plate once dry.

title: Tourte à la Bavaroise (Bavarian Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 teaspoons butter
- 16 teaspoons peeled almonds
- 1 egg white
- juice of 1 lemon
- 16 teaspoons powdered sugar
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks, passed
- 36 teaspoons flour

## Directions

1. Beat 32 teaspoons of butter with 16 teaspoons of peeled almonds pounded very finely with 1 egg white, the juice of 1 lemon and 16 teaspoons powdered sugar, mix thoroughly.
2. Mix this with 6 hard-boiled, passed egg yolks and 36 teaspoons flour.
3. Treat the dough as described for the 'Sugar Dough Cake in French Style' (No. 1481).

title: Tourte à la Hollandaise (Dutch Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 teaspoons butter
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 12 teaspoons powdered sugar
- 1 pinch cinnamon
- ground nutmeg
- ground cloves
- 40 teaspoons flour
- eggs
- marmalade
- glaze

## Directions

1. Beat 30 teaspoons of butter until fluffy, add the juice of 1/2 lemon with 12 teaspoons powdered sugar, stir well together and mix with 1 pinch cinnamon, equal amount of grated nutmeg, crushed cloves and 40 teaspoons flour, which is stirred in spoonful by spoonful.
2. Work the dough on a flour-dusted board into a round loaf, roll it out and cut into three equal round slices.
3. Place these on a sheet lined with buttered paper, brush with beaten eggs and bake at moderate heat in the oven.
4. Remove from paper, place on a sieve and allow to cool completely, then layer all three slices together with marmalade spread between them.
5. Cover the whole with glaze.

title: Tourte à la Milanaise (Milanese Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teaspoons orange blossoms
- 6 tablespoons cold water
- 30 teaspoons peeled almonds
- 28 teaspoons butter
- 1 teaspoon lemon zest
- pinch of cinnamon
- egg yolks
- 30 teaspoons flour

## Directions

1. Soak the orange blossoms in cold water for approximately 2 hours, then pour through a sieve and pound very finely with the peeled almonds.
2. Mix the butter with the lemon zest, the aforementioned almonds, a pinch of cinnamon and egg yolks, adding one after the other.
3. Stir for 1 to 2 hours and gradually mix in 30 teaspoons of flour.
4. Continue as described for the Viennese Cake (No. 1486).

title: Traganth Drops in Peppermint (Pfefferminzdrops aus Traganthmasse)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deka gum tragacanth
- 1.3 liters water
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 kilos and some peppermint oil
- various desired shapes

## Directions

1. Dissolve 7 deka gum tragacanth in 1.3 liters water. The dissolution takes 12 hours.
2. Pass the gum tragacanth and slowly stir with 4 egg yolks on a marble slab.
3. Gradually add 2 kilos and some peppermint oil while stirring.
4. Cut out desired shapes and dry them slowly.
5. For tragacanth dough for figures and various decorations such as leaves and flowers, make the same way, only with the difference that only 2 egg yolks, 1/8 kilo sugar, and 1/2 kilo powdered flour are added.

title: Traunkirchner-Torte (Traunkirchen Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 dkg flour
- 21 dkg butter
- 7 dkg sugar
- 7 dkg finely sieved sugar
- 9 dkg apricot or raspberry marmalade
- 2 stiff egg whites

## Directions

1. Make a crumb dough from 25 dkg flour, 21 dkg butter, and 7 dkg sugar.
2. After resting, divide it into four parts.
3. Roll each part out to cake form bottom size and place on unbuttered sheet.
4. Bake at higher heat until light brown.
5. After cooling, brush the layers, stack them together, and dust with powdered sugar.
6. For the filling: stir 7 dkg finely sieved sugar with 9 dkg apricot or raspberry marmalade and fold in 2 stiff egg whites.
7. The filling is better if made 2-3 days before use.

title: Tremierte Spanferkel (Suckling Pig à la Gelée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 calf feet and 2 pork feet
- Wine vinegar
- Water
- 1 onion
- Spice cloves
- 1 yellow turnip
- Celery
- 5 pieces each of spices
- 5 sprigs of parsley
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 shallot
- Salt
- White wine
- Egg yolk
- Snow water

## Directions

1. Chop the calf feet and pork feet and place them in a braising pan
2. Add wine vinegar and water and cook together well
3. Remove the fat and scum carefully during cooking
4. Add 1 piece of onion pierced with 1 spice clove, 1 yellow turnip, 1 small piece of celery, 5 pieces each of spices, 5 sprigs of parsley, 1 bay leaf, 1 piece of shallot, and some salt
5. Let everything cook together for one hour
6. Add the suckling pig cut into desired pieces and cover with white wine
7. Cook until the meat is sufficiently tender
8. Remove the meat with a slotted spoon, lay it on a platter and cover with a cloth soaked in white wine
9. Pour the broth through a sieve and then through a cloth into an earthen vessel and remove the fat carefully
10. In a braising pan, place the egg yolk from 3 eggs and whisk it slightly with a whisk
11. Add the suckling pig broth and whisk over the fire until it begins to boil
12. Pull the braising pan back, cover it with a fitting lid, place it on the same coals
13. The protein on top will shrink together so that the liquid obtains a clear color
14. Pour the broth through a cloth as with aspic and set aside in an earthen vessel
15. Place a mold in ice, pour in strongly finger-deep of the prepared broth and let it cool
16. Lay the suckling pig pieces with the skin side down on the cooled gelatin so that they join together to form the whole suckling pig
17. Cover with enough broth to fill the mold
18. When the liquid is completely cooled, remove the mold, wipe it off, dip it for a moment in hot water, dry it with a cloth, turn it out onto a fitting platter, carefully remove the mold and garnish with the above chopped aspic

title: Trommelsuppe (Drum Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- dough from the Bohemian Maultaschen recipe
- 1/4 liter poppy seed filling or nut and almond filling
- 2 deca finely pounded nuts
- 20 pieces shelled nuts pounded with sugar
- 20 pieces pounded, sieved hazelnuts or almonds
- 1 tablespoon breadcrumbs
- 1/8 liter boiling milk or cream
- optionally 1 pinch grated coffee or finely cut lemon peel

## Directions

1. Prepare dough as for Bohemian Maultaschen
2. After dough has risen one-third higher, roll out knife-back thickness on flour-dusted board
3. Cut out round discs with dough cutter
4. Cover one disc with poppy seed filling and brush edges with beaten egg
5. Cover with a second dough disc and top with filling
6. Continue layering 6 dough discs total, each covered with different filling (vanilla, apricots or jam)
7. Place on butter-brushed baking sheet using a knife
8. Sprinkle with grated nuts
9. Brush with melted butter or cream
10. Bake slowly in moderate heat oven
11. Serve warm
12. Alternatively, can be baked on a butter-brushed tart pan to form a large cake-shaped pastry

title: Trout in Aspic (Forellen mit Aspic)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- small trout
- aspic, very clear

## Directions

1. Choose a small variety of trout that has been boiled blue and cooled in its broth
2. Chill an oblong smooth mold in ice ½ hour before use
3. Pour 2 fingers high of very clear, cooled aspic into the mold
4. When set, arrange the trout so they rest with their backs on the aspic
5. Position them so the head of one trout lies next to the tail of another
6. Ensure they do not touch the edge of the mold and appear to swim freely
7. Use chopped aspic to secure them in place
8. Add some green parsley between the trout and in their mouths
9. Carefully pour 2 more fingers high of aspic over them
10. Break up the remaining trout into unequal pieces
11. Lay these skin-side down on the set aspic
12. Fill the mold completely with cooled liquid aspic
13. Use a tall mold, about twice hand-breadth high
14. Once the aspic is completely set and covered, remove from ice
15. Plunge briefly into hot water
16. Wipe dry and quickly turn out onto a flat platter or porcelain torte plate
17. Decorate with very finely chopped yellow and red aspic and some green parsley
18. Scatter capers here and there over it
19. Serve with vinegar and oil

title: Truffelfarce (Truffle Forcemeat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh, peeled and finely chopped black truffles
- butter

## Directions

1. Sauté the fresh, peeled and finely chopped black truffles in butter
2. Pound them very finely in a mortar
3. Press them through a hair sieve
4. Mix them into the desired forcemeats
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Truffles in Napkin with Champagne Wine (Truffles en Serviette au Vin de Champagne)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- large truffles
- Champagne or ordinary wine
- good broth
- soup glaze

## Directions

1. Clean large truffles as described above and place them unpeeled in a casserole
2. Add Champagne or ordinary wine with good broth, using 2 parts wine and only 1 part broth, but add only enough liquid so that it barely covers the truffles
3. Let them cook covered for approximately 1/4 hour
4. Remove them with a serving spoon and serve them still hot in a napkin-lined bowl or in a dish with a lid, keeping their black skin intact
5. To the remaining cooking liquid, add some soup glaze and serve this in a sauce boat with the truffles
6. Serve as an intermediate course

title: Trüffel-Sauce (Truffle Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces truffles, cut into leaves or noodle shapes
- strained Butter-Sauce
- Madeira wine
- dissolved soup glace

## Directions

1. Add truffles cut into leaves or noodle shapes to the strained Butter-Sauce
2. Cover and braise with several spoonfuls of Madeira wine and, if available, some dissolved soup glace

title: Trüffelfarce (Truffle Forcemeat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh, peeled and finely chopped black truffles
- Butter

## Directions

1. Sweat the truffles in butter
2. Pound them finely in a mortar
3. Press through a hair sieve
4. Mix into any desired forcemeat
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Trüffelsauce (Truffle Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsley
- Shallots
- Butter
- Truffles
- Salt
- Pepper
- 1 good glass white wine
- Several spoonfuls espagnole or brown coulis
- Lemon juice
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Finely chop parsley and shallots and sweat in butter.
2. Peel and chop the truffles and add them.
3. Add salt, pepper, 1 good glass of white wine, and several spoonfuls of espagnole or brown coulis.
4. Let everything reduce slowly together.
5. Remove scum and fat.
6. When serving, add lemon juice and nutmeg.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Turbot (Surbot)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot
- lemon juice
- salt
- water
- milk
- lemon slices
- onions
- bay leaves
- hollandaise sauce or clear butter

## Directions

1. Scale the turbot cleanly
2. Gut it on the side
3. Clean it white with lemon juice and salt
4. Make a crosscut on its back
5. In a copper round dish put water, milk, some salt, lemon slices, onions, and some bay leaves
6. Let this boil
7. Remove from fire and place the fish in it
8. Set it on a gentle coal fire and let it poach slowly until cooked through
9. Serve with hollandaise sauce or clear butter in a sauceboat
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Turkish Tomato Soup (Soupe à la turque)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 large tomatoes
- 3 sprigs parsley
- One small bunch white onion
- 1.5 liters white broth or water
- 8 Deca butter
- 8 Deca flour
- Salt and pepper
- Optional: 0.25 liters white wine or 0.125 liters Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Cut each tomato in half and place in a casserole
2. Add the parsley sprigs and white onion bunch
3. Simmer with 1.5 liters white broth or water for 0.5 hour slowly
4. In another pan, blend 8 Deca butter and 8 Deca flour together
5. Pour the above soup into this mixture while stirring constantly
6. Simmer for another 1 hour
7. Add salt and pepper as needed
8. Pass through a sieve
9. Optional: add white wine or Madeira
10. Serve with braised meat and green peas, or other desired garnishes
11. If fresh tomatoes are unavailable, use 0.25 liters tomato marmalade and 3.5 liters broth, preparing as above

title: Tutti-Frutti Frozen Dessert (Glace à la tutti-frutti)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Firm, finely worked pineapple, orange, or lemon frozen dessert
- Small diced, preserved fruits such as pineapple, figs, grapes, apricots, peaches, quinces, pears, and similar

## Directions

1. Into firm, finely worked pineapple, orange, or lemon frozen dessert, slowly mix small diced preserved fruits such as pineapple, figs, grapes, apricots, peaches, quinces, pears, and the like
2. Serve this heaped on cups or in glasses

title: Tutti-frutti-Creme (Tutti-Frutti Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Deca gelatin
- 0.25 liters water
- 0.5 liters Maraschino, orange water, or fruit liqueur
- green almonds, black walnuts, Angelica, whole raisins, and pineapple pieces, 12 Deca total
- 16 Deca sugar
- 0.5 liters heavy cream

## Directions

1. Dissolve 2 Deca gelatin with 0.25 liters water and add 0.5 liters Maraschino, orange water, or fruit liqueur of choice.
2. Cut various finely prepared fruits (green almonds, black walnuts, Angelica, whole raisins, and pineapple pieces), 12 Deca total, into small pieces.
3. Heat the fruit liqueur with 16 Deca sugar and add the cut fruits to the dissolved gelatin.
4. The liquid should not exceed 0.5 liters total.
5. Transfer to a deep bowl and stir until it cools.
6. Fold in 0.5 liters whipped cream.
7. Transfer to a mold brushed with oil, which has been buried in ice 0.5 hour earlier.
8. From this mass, as from any other cream mass, one can prepare Cabinet Pudding as follows: Line a mold or cake plate with thin sliced baked biscuit or fine baked dough, cover thickly with cream, then alternate layers of biscuit and cream until the mold is full, cover and bury in ice for 2 hours, turn out onto a platter, and garnish as desired with sugar, fruit, marmalade, or whipped cream.

title: Tête d'Agneau (Lamb's Head)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb head and feet
- salt water
- fat
- root vegetables
- onion
- broth
- heart and tongue
- liver

## Directions

1. Clean the lamb head and feet as specified for calf's head, cook them gently in salt water and clean again.
2. In a casserole, add fat, root vegetables and onion, place the head and feet, let them brown uncovered and pour the necessary broth over.
3. After some time, about 1/2 hour, add the heart and tongue, and 10 minutes before the meat is completely tender, add the liver.
4. When everything is sufficiently tender, remove it, cut it into desired pieces and arrange on a platter.
5. Make a flavorful, piquant sauce with the broth, pour it over the arranged meat and border the platter with cut-out butter pastry.

title: Türkische Chokolade-Creme (Turkish Chocolate Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound finely grated chocolate of the best kind
- 1 seidel cream
- 20 egg yolks
- some lukewarm milk

## Directions

1. Cook half pound finely grated chocolate of the best kind with 1 seidel cream
2. Beat 20 egg yolks in a pot and stir them clear with some lukewarm milk
3. Pour the boiling chocolate to the eggs while constantly whisking
4. It becomes a thick foam, which you pour onto a plate and serve cold
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Umgestürzte Creme a la 12 (Inverted Cream a la 12)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a Maß good cream
- A piece of vanilla
- One quarter pound sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- Butter
- Lemon
- 8 yolks
- Almonds
- Butter

## Directions

1. Take half a Maß good cream, a piece of vanilla, and one quarter pound sugar, set the cream on coal fire, let it boil a few times, and then cool
2. Beat 12 egg yolks and 3 whole eggs well in a casserole, stir in the cold cream, pass everything a couple of times through a fine sieve
3. Brush the mold very lightly with butter and pour the cream into it
4. Set a casserole with water on coal fire, when it is hot place the cups or molds into it, and take care that they do not begin to boil; cover with a lid with light coals
5. As soon as the cream begins to set, turn this onto a plate
6. Continue stirring the remaining cream on the coal fire until it adheres to the cooking spoon
7. Set aside a little, this can also be poured over and served
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ummerzels (Röstels) Salat (Corn Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Ummerzel (corn salad)
- Essig
- Öl
- Salz

## Directions

1. Nachdem man etwa von den Stengeln abgeschnitten und die ersten zwei Blätter entfernt hat, wäscht man ihn in kaltem Wasser, gibt ihn in eine Schüssel, salzt ihn etwa und gießt Essig und Öl darüber. Man gibt ihn gewöhnlich als Garnierung zu anderen Salaten.

title: Une chartreuse (A Chartreuse)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- various dark, light and yellow vegetables
- butter
- sugar
- broth
- white vegetables
- salt water
- eggs
- veal or fish forcemeat
- sausage

## Directions

1. Punch various dark, light and yellow vegetables with desired forms and braise soft with butter, sugar and the necessary broth.
2. With a vegetable cutter knife, cut white vegetables into beautiful forms, cook them in salt water and roast in butter and sugar.
3. Similarly, cook sprouts, green beans, green peas, asparagus tips and artichokes in salt water, each cooked separately without covering, then drain the water.
4. Butter a mold well, place a peeled, halved potato in the center so the flat side lies on the bottom; the remaining bottom space and the side walls are lined according to taste with the various vegetables.
5. Cover the layer finger-thick with veal, pâté or fish forcemeat and fill the remaining space with the rest of the mixed vegetables.
6. Brush with forcemeat, layer with thinly sliced sweetbread, tongue or sausage, add more forcemeat, press well together, place the mold in a steam bath and let it stand 1 hour covered.

title: Ungarische Salami von Mandeln (Hungarian Almond Salami)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 decagrams fine sifted sugar
- 1 egg white
- 16 decagrams peeled almonds
- 1 egg white
- 2 decagrams tragacanth gum
- 1/2 liter lukewarm water
- 1 tablespoon almond milk
- fine pounded sugar
- 8 decagrams coarsely cut almonds
- 8 decagrams cubed citron
- 8 decagrams finely cut brunelles
- 1 pinch cinnamon
- nutmeg
- crushed spice cloves
- grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Put 16 decagrams fine sifted sugar, 1 egg white, peeled almonds pounded very finely with 1 egg white, and tragacanth gum dissolved in 1/2 liter lukewarm water (strained through cloth) in a bowl
2. Stir over the fire until the thick boiled mass releases from the bowl
3. Mix with 1 tablespoon almond milk
4. Spread on a board dusted with fine pounded sugar
5. Work in coarsely cut almonds, cubed citron, finely cut brunelles, cinnamon, nutmeg, and crushed spice cloves into a dough
6. Form the dough into sausage shapes
7. Roll in fine sifted sugar mixed with grated chocolate
8. Dry for 6 to 8 hours in a very cool oven

title: Ungarisches (Hungarian Beef)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- A well-marbled piece of beef from the rib piece
- Kernel fat, finely chopped
- Onion, sliced
- Salt
- Turkish pepper (paprika)
- Flour

## Directions

1. Skin a well-marbled piece of beef from the rib piece and cut it into pieces somewhat longer than one finger joint and broad
2. Place very little finely chopped kernel fat in a casserole, as well as a couple of sliced onions
3. Add the cut meat and let it braise in its own broth
4. Salt it properly, season it only with Turkish pepper (paprika), but proceed moderately as it is very strong, and highly peppered meat might not appeal to a German as much as to a Hungarian
5. Finally, dust it with a little flour
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ungarisches Goulash (Goulash à la Hongroise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Potatoes
- Caraway seeds
- Paprika
- Salt
- Onion
- Fat

## Directions

1. The preparation is as above, only with the difference that raw, cube-cut potatoes are added to the half-tender meat and braised tender with the meat, after which it is first salted.

title: Ungarisches Rebhuhn (Hungarian Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 veal feet
- 2 pork feet
- 15 dkg lean pork meat
- 6 l prepared and washed root vegetables, cut into leaves
- 1 scallion
- ¼ l vinegar
- 2 spoons salt
- 1½ l water

## Directions

1. Boil all the prepared and washed ingredients in a pot like aspic for 2-3 hours.
2. When the meat is tender, remove it (leaving the rest to continue cooking), cut it into small pieces and put it in a bowl or mold.
3. Strain the finished cooking broth over it, place the bowl in a cold place until the aspic is set.
4. Unmold it and serve with vinegar, oil and also with onion rings.

title: Ungarisches Rée (Hungarian Beef)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- well-marbled beef from the rib section
- very little finely chopped suet
- a pair of onions, sliced
- salt
- Turkish pepper (paprika)
- a little flour

## Directions

1. Skin a well-marbled beef from the rib section and cut it into pieces somewhat longer and wider than one finger joint
2. Put very little finely chopped suet in a casserole, along with a pair of onions sliced into rounds
3. Add the cut meat and let it braise in its own broth
4. Salt it properly and season it only with Turkish pepper (paprika), but do so moderately as it gives a strong flavor and heavily peppered meat may not please Germans as much as Hungarians
5. Finally dust it with a little flour to thicken the sauce and do not let it cook too soft
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ungarisches Sauertraut (Hungarian-Style Sauerkraut)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cellar full of sauerkraut
- 1 piece smoked pork meat
- 10 tablespoons fat
- 1 small head of onion, cut
- 10 tablespoons flour
- 1/4 liter sour broth and sauerkraut broth
- 1 to 2 teaspoons paprika
- 1/4 liter cream

## Directions

1. Cook 1 cellar full of sauerkraut together with 1 piece of smoked pork meat gently
2. Make a light brown roux from 10 tablespoons fat, 1 finely cut small head of onion and 10 tablespoons flour, stirring carefully so no lumps form
3. Pour it with 1/4 liter sour broth and sauerkraut broth
4. Place the meat on a platter, pour some sauerkraut broth over it, cover and set aside
5. Stir the strained sauerkraut into the roux, add 1 to 2 teaspoons paprika and 1/4 liter cream, stir well together
6. If the sauerkraut is too thick, add some sauerkraut broth and let it cook well
7. At serving time, slice the meat into desired pieces and serve it with the sauerkraut in a bowl

title: Unreines Strudel von Germnudeln (Walnut Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Deka shelled walnuts
- 8 Deka blanched, roasted hazelnuts
- 16 Deka finely sifted sugar
- 1 pinch grated coffee
- Dough as per Mohn-Strudel Nr. 1256(b)

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as specified in Mohn-Strudel Nr. 1256(b)
2. Very finely chop 8 Deka of walnuts and mix well with 8 Deka of blanched, roasted hazelnuts and 16 Deka of finely sifted sugar and 1 pinch of grated coffee
3. Apply this filling to the strudel dough in the same manner as the poppy seed filling
4. Roll the dough completely together, place it on a buttered baking sheet and allow it to rise covered in a warm place
5. Once risen, brush it thoroughly with beaten eggs and bake in the oven

title: Vanilla Butter (Beurre à la Vanille)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams powdered vanilla sugar
- 16 decagrams beaten butter

## Directions

1. Stir the powdered vanilla sugar into the beaten butter
2. Let it stand on ice for 1 hour
3. If the butter is stiff, place it in a decorative form on a plate
4. Garnish with butter bread cut into strips or serve with whipped cream or strawberries

title: Vanilla Cream (Vanillecreme)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- 1 spoonful flour
- 2-3 egg yolks
- 1 piece vanilla
- 2 spoonfuls sugar

## Directions

1. In a snow kettle, stir some milk with 1 spoonful of flour.
2. Add ½ l milk, 2-3 egg yolks, 1 piece of vanilla, and 2 spoonfuls of sugar.
3. Whip everything with a whisk over steam until light cream forms.

title: Vanilla Cream Ice (Rahm-Gefrornes mit Vanille)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 loth vanilla
- 1.5 measures sweet cream
- 18 egg yolks
- 0.75 pound sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the vanilla into small pieces
2. Cook the sweet cream with the vanilla
3. Beat the cream with 18 egg yolks
4. Clarify three-quarters pound of sugar and mix it into the cream
5. Beat everything well so it binds together
6. Pass through a fine sieve
7. Stir until cooled so no skin forms
8. Pour into the freezing container
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Vanilla Soufflé (Soufflé à la Vanille)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 tablespoons flour
- 0.5 liter milk
- 12 tablespoons sugar
