- 9 egg yolks
- 9 egg whites
- butter
- flour
- strawberry jam or other jam

## Directions

1. Slowly stir 0.5 liter of milk into 16 tablespoons of flour and stir over the fire until it forms a thick dough that separates from the spoon
2. Transfer to a deep bowl and let cool
3. Meanwhile, beat 12 tablespoons of sugar with 9 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
4. Slowly mix this into the cooled mixture
5. Lightly fold in the snow of 9 egg whites
6. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour with this mixture
7. Cover and cook 3/4 hours in a water bath or bake in the oven at moderate heat
8. Turn out onto a serving dish and pour dissolved strawberry jam or other jam over it

title: Vanille-Biscuit (Vanilla Biscuit)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound sugar
- One stick vanilla
- 6 egg yolks
- Whites of the eggs
- 4 Loth fine flour

## Directions

1. Pound and finely sieve half a pound sugar and one stick vanilla together, stir with 6 egg yolks to a foam
2. Beat the egg whites to snow and fold in slowly together with 4 Loth fine flour
3. Otherwise bake for a quarter hour at gentle oven heat
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Vanille-Bögen (Vanilla Bows)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarter pound sugar
- Vanilla pounded with sugar
- Snow from 2 egg whites
- A little lemon juice
- 2 loths flour

## Directions

1. Place the sugar, pounded vanilla, and snow from 2 egg whites into a pot
2. Add a little lemon juice
3. Stir until it becomes thick
4. Mix in 2 loths of flour
5. Spread it on broad wafers
6. Bake until beautiful
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Vanille-Bögen (Vanilla Bows)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarter pound sugar
- Vanilla pounded with sugar
- Snow from 2 egg whites
- A little lemon juice
- 2 Loth flour
- Oblaten

## Directions

1. Put one quarter pound sugar, some vanilla pounded with sugar, and the snow from 2 egg whites in a pot
2. Add a little lemon juice and stir until it becomes thick
3. Mix in 2 Loth flour, spread it on wide oblaten, and bake them nicely
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Vanille-Liqueur (Vanilla Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Maß brandy
- 1 Maß sweet cream
- 1 Loth cochineal
- 1 Loth vanilla, finely cut
- 1.5 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Place in a bottle at a warm stove for 14 days
3. Filter through filter paper
4. The liqueur is ready
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Vanille-Mandeln (Vanilla Almonds)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough as for almond pastries
- 8 Deka butter
- 8 Deka granulated sugar
- Pinch of salt
- 4 egg yolks
- 3/4 liter cream
- Snow from 4 egg whites
- 8 Deka flour
- Candied pineapple (4 pieces per almond)

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough and treat as described for almond pastries (No. 1116)
2. Instead of almond filling, fill with the following mixture:
3. Beat 8 Deka butter fluffy
4. Add 8 Deka granulated sugar, pinch of salt, 4 egg yolks and 3/4 liter cream, one after another
5. Finally lightly fold in the snow from 4 egg whites simultaneously with 8 Deka flour
6. Stick 4 pieces of candied pineapple in each almond
7. Bake as described for almond pastries

title: Vanille-Reis mit Erdbeeren (Vanilla Rice with Strawberries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 Deka rice, washed multiple times with hot water
- 8 Deka vanilla extract
- 1 liter boiling cream
- 1/4 liter strained strawberries
- 16 Deka sugar
- 1/4 liter whipped cream

## Directions

1. Add 24 Deka rice, which has been washed multiple times with hot water, with 8 Deka vanilla extract to 1 liter boiling cream, cover and let it cook
2. Fill the rice into a mold lined with milk and set in a bowl
3. Put 1/4 liter strained strawberries into a freezing container, mixed with 16 Deka sugar and 1/2 liter whipped cream, place the mold in ice and stir the mass for 1/2 hour, after which arrange it decoratively in the center of the already prepared rice and serve
4. Instead of strawberries, raspberries, currants, apricots, peaches, etc. can be used

title: Vanille-Sulz (Vanilla Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 decagrams sugar, clarified (see recipe 1542)
- 1 vanilla bean cut into pieces
- 7 decagrams isinglass, hot and dissolved
- Juice of 2 lemons
- Cherry spirit or maraschino, as much as needed
- Total liquid of 1 liter with dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Clarify 40 decagrams sugar (see recipe 1542) and add the vanilla bean pieces at the same time
2. After straining the clarified sugar through cloth, add it hot to 7 decagrams boiling isinglass
3. Mix both, strain the mass through fine, thin linen cloth
4. Add the clear juice of 2 lemons and as much cherry spirit or maraschino so that the entire mass with the dissolved isinglass does not exceed 1 liter
5. Proceed further as for Orange Sulz (see recipe 1660)

title: Vanillebutter (Vanilla Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg yolks
- 10-12 dkg vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 2 egg yolks with 10-12 dkg vanilla sugar until thick and smooth

title: Vanilleeis (Vanilla Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l milk
- 3 dl milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1 piece vanilla
- 14 dkg sugar

## Directions

1. In a snow kettle, whisk 3 dl milk with 3 egg yolks, 1 whole egg, 1 piece vanilla and 14 dkg sugar over gentle heat until slightly thickened.
2. Strain the cream, cool it, fill it into a snow kettle or freezing container and freeze it.
3. If making the ice in a snow kettle, place it in a tub with well-salted ice and let the mass become stiff under continuous turning of the snow kettle.
4. When frozen material begins to form on the wall of the kettle, it must be loosened with a cooking spoon or spatula.
5. If making the ice in a machine, the container must first be inserted, then surrounded with salted ice, after which the mass is filled in and frozen under continuous turning.

title: Vanilleeis (Vanilla Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ liter milk
- 3 dl milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 1 piece vanilla
- 14 dkg sugar

## Directions

1. In the mixing bowl, beat 3 dl milk with 3 egg yolks, 1 whole egg, 1 piece vanilla, and 14 dkg sugar over gentle heat until weakly thick.
2. Strain the cream through a sieve, cool it down, fill it into a mixing bowl or freezing container, and freeze it.
3. If the ice is made in the mixing bowl, place it in a container with well-salted ice and let the mass stiffen under continuous turning of the mixing bowl.

title: Vanillekipferl (Vanilla Crescent Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg flour
- 16 dkg butter
- 10 dkg raw grated almonds
- 5 dkg sugar
- vanilla sugar for coating

## Directions

1. Make a dough on the board from flour, butter, raw grated almonds, and sugar
2. Form it into a roll
3. Cut off equal-sized pieces
4. Form each into a crescent
5. Place on an unbuttered baking sheet
6. Bake at higher heat until light brown
7. While still warm, roll in vanilla sugar

title: Vanilles Brezel (Vanilla Pretzel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 deka butter cut into pieces
- 24 deka flour
- 16 deka vanilla sugar
- 1 whole egg
- beaten egg white for brushing
- crystal sugar mixed with coarsely cut almonds

## Directions

1. Rub 16 deka butter cut into pieces finely with 24 deka flour, work together with 16 deka vanilla sugar and 1 whole egg into a dough
2. After it rests covered in a cold place for 12 hours, cut into nut-large pieces
3. Roll each individual piece pencil-thick noodle-fashion, form a pretzel, place it on a butter-brushed baking sheet
4. Brush with beaten egg white and sprinkle as desired with crystal sugar mixed with coarsely cut almonds, bake at low heat in the oven until pale yellow

title: Vanilles Zuckerei (Vanilla Confection)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 16 deka vanilla sugar
- paper cups or piping bags

## Directions

1. After stirring 6 egg yolks with 16 deka vanilla sugar for 1 hour, fill this mass into paper cups which are placed on a baking sheet in a cool oven and let the sugar mass dry
2. Instead of in paper cups, one can also fill this mass into piping bags, which are then placed in bran or sand on a baking sheet and similarly dried in the cool oven
3. This dried sugar mass can be served in the cups or bags or one can dissolve it from the paper and serve it mixed with other baked goods

title: Veal Breast Galantine (Tendrons de veau en galantines)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- veal forcemeat
- ham
- lard
- truffles
- goose liver
- blanched pistachios
- onion
- root vegetables
- bay leaf
- thyme
- pepper
- broth
- white wine
- aspic
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal breast as described for steamed veal breast, filling it with forcemeat, ham, lard, truffles, goose liver, and pistachios.
2. Roll tightly and bind securely with kitchen twine.
3. Line a casserole with thinly sliced lard, place a piece of onion, thinly sliced root vegetables, a bay leaf, and some thyme.
4. Place the bound meat on top, cover with lard strips or pour fat over, cover with double-folded paper, fit with a suitable lid, and braise for about 0.5 hour.
5. Add broth and some white wine and braise covered for another hour.
6. Allow to cool completely, then remove the meat, discard the top lard, and untie the string.
7. The meat may be wrapped in cloth and stored in a cool place for several days if not used immediately.
8. Before serving, cut into half-finger-thick slices, arrange neatly on a platter, garnish with chopped aspic, and serve with any mayonnaise.

title: Veal Breast with Asparagus Tips (Tendrons de veau aux pointes d'asperge)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- asparagus tips
- salted water

## Directions

1. Prepare the meat as described for braised veal breast.
2. Cook asparagus tips in salted water until tender.
3. Drain the asparagus, place in a casserole, pour the thickened sauce over, stir well, and serve with the veal breast.

title: Veal Breast with Paprika (Tendrons de veau au paprica)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- lard, diced
- root vegetables, thinly sliced
- 2 mushroom pieces, diced
- 1 onion piece
- white broth
- bread or roll slices
- milk cream
- paprika

## Directions

1. Place diced lard and thinly sliced root vegetables, mushroom pieces, and onion piece in a casserole.
2. Place the veal breast on top and braise covered.
3. When the liquid has evaporated, remove the bones from the meat.
4. Pour white broth over, add bread or roll slices, and braise until the meat is tender.
5. Add milk cream, let it cook well through.
6. Remove the meat, cut into neat pieces, and arrange heaped on a platter.
7. Season the defatted sauce with paprika, bring to a boil once, and pour through a sieve over the meat.
8. The sauce should be yellowish in color.
9. Serve with noodles, braised peas, or potatoes as desired.

title: Veal Breast with White Sauce à la Toulouse (Tendrons de veau à la Toulouse)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- 8 pieces smallest mushroom variety
- 8 pieces ham comb
- veal sweetbreads cut into neat pieces
- truffle pieces, formed
- beef glace or brown stock (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare the meat as described for braised veal breast.
2. Add the mushroom pieces, ham comb pieces, and prepared sweetbreads to the thickened sauce.
3. Stir well, bring to a boil once, and serve with the arranged veal breast.
4. If desired, give the sauce a brown color by adding dissolved beef glace or brown stock.
5. Formed truffle pieces may also be added as desired.

title: Veal Carbonades (Cutlets) in Paper (Côtelettes de veau en papillotes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal carbonades
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Bacon
- Beef marrow
- Shallots
- Onion
- Parsley
- Bread
- Mushrooms
- Eggs
- Oil or butter
- Bouillon glace or Tartar sauce

## Directions

1. Veal carbonades, after the meat has been scraped from the backbone, are beaten flat, sprinkled with salt and pepper, and placed in a shallow casserole with a little very hot butter, to lightly fry them on each side for 1 to 2 minutes so they take on a white color.
2. Meanwhile, cut finely together 6 decagrams bacon, 6 decagrams beef marrow if desired, 1 piece shallot, onion, green parsley, 4 decagrams bread soaked in water and well pressed, and 2 mushrooms. Salt and season this mixture, add 2 egg yolks and stir well together.
3. The carbonades, after they are removed from the butter and cooled, are spread on both sides very thinly with the prepared forcemeat and filled on both sides with thin bacon slices.
4. A doubly folded paper is cut around as wide as a thumb larger than a cutlet is long, so that when the paper is unfolded it forms a heart shape. This paper is soaked in oil or spread thickly with butter, the prepared cutlet is placed on one side, the other half is folded over it, and beginning from above, namely from the opposite side of the rib bone, the paper is folded at the edges so that one fold holds down the other.
5. When all cutlets are wrapped in this way, they are placed on a sheet of paper soaked in oil or butter which lies on a roasting pan, allowed to fry slowly on both sides, then removed from the paper and arranged in a circle on a serving dish. In the center place dissolved bouillon glace or Tartar sauce.

title: Veal Cutlets in Paper, Another Method (Côtelettes de veau en papillotes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Salt
- Pepper
- Mushrooms
- Parsley
- Chervil
- Tarragon
- Shallots
- Butter
- Bacon
- Oil or butter
- Fine herbs
- Bouillon glace or Tartar sauce

## Directions

1. When the meat has been scraped from the rib, the cutlets are beaten flat and salted.
2. Mushrooms, green parsley, chervil, tarragon if desired and shallots are cut very finely and fried with the cutlets in hot butter for 3-5 minutes.
3. They are then set aside and, when completely cooled, are spread on both sides with the fine herbs and covered on both sides with thin bacon slices, into which small incisions are made with a knife.
4. The wrapping of the cutlets with paper and the further handling is done in the same way as described for the preceding cutlets.

title: Veal Cutlets, Natural (Côtelettes de veau naturelles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets (carbonades)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Fat

## Directions

1. After the cutlets (carbonades) have been cut, the meat is held away from each individual bone, the rib bone is scraped and the meat pushed back.
2. The cutlets are beaten flat with a meat mallet or knife handle without damaging the meat.
3. They are salted, and if desired, sprinkled with a little pepper, lightly dipped in flour on both sides and fried on both sides until golden yellow in very hot fat.
4. Each rib bone is then covered with a papillote.

title: Veal Fillets, Stuffed (Filets de veau farcis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal forcemeat
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon peel
- Flour
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Fat

## Directions

1. The veal forcemeat, as specified in the recipe, is salted, seasoned, then passed and carefully mixed with finely cut lemon peel added with a knife point.
2. On a board sprinkled with flour, this forcemeat is rolled out finger-thick with a rolling pin which is also lightly sprinkled with flour, and cutlets are cut out with a cutter form and laid on a flour-sprinkled board.
3. They are then spread with beaten eggs, sprinkled with bread crumbs, and by turning them with the help of a knife or pastry server, this is repeated on the other side.
4. Shortly before use, the fillets are placed in a casserole in finger-thick, very hot fat, fried light yellow on both sides and removed with a pastry server.
5. In the same way, these fillets (cutlets) of fish forcemeat can be prepared for fast days. Ribs for the cutlets are formed from a piece of parsley or cinnamon bark and covered with paper papillotes. They are served with fine vegetables, fine ragout or a piquant sauce.

title: Veal Leftovers in Piquant Sauce (Veau sauce piquante)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter sauce
- Milk cream
- Lemon juice
- Capers
- Nutmeg
- Sliced ham (optional)
- Leftover roasted veal

## Directions

1. To a butter sauce add milk cream, lemon juice, capers, nutmeg, and finely sliced ham if desired.
2. Let simmer together well.
3. Meanwhile, slice the leftover roasted veal into pieces of any size.
4. Add the pieces to the sauce and let boil.

title: Veal Meat with Paprika Goulash Another Way (Goulyas de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces lard, diced
- root vegetables, diced
- onion
- mushrooms, thinly sliced
- veal breast, cut into neat pieces
- broth
- milk cream
- paprika
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Place diced lard, diced root vegetables, onion, and thinly sliced mushrooms in a casserole.
2. When these have roasted together slightly, add the veal breast pieces.
3. When they have roasted a little, pour broth over and braise the meat until tender.
4. Add milk cream, salt, and season with paprika to taste.
5. Let it cook together slightly and arrange in a platter.
6. Alternatively, one can use 1 kilogram of meat from the shoulder, neck, or leg.

title: Veal Purée Soup (Purée de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 mushroom
- a little parsley
- 1 shallot
- 6 ounces butter
- approximately 1 liter finely minced veal meat remains
- 8 ounces rice
- 2.5 liters beef soup
- salt
- spices

## Directions

1. Roast 1 mushroom, a little parsley and 1 shallot in 6 ounces butter
2. Add approximately 1 liter of finely minced veal meat remains and 8 ounces rice
3. Pour in 2.5 liters of beef soup and let it cook until the rice is completely soft
4. Pass through a fine sieve
5. Boil briefly, add salt and spices if desired
6. Serve with braised bread pieces

title: Veal Schnitzel (Kalbsschnitzel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40-50 dkg veal leg
- salt
- flour
- egg
- lard for frying
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as pork schnitzel

title: Veal Sweetbread Escalopes (Escalopes de ris de veau)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal sweetbreads
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Bread crumbs
- Pepper (optional)
- Paper
- Aspic glaze

## Directions

1. Soak veal sweetbreads in lukewarm water for one hour, then boil for 10 minutes in salt water.
2. Clean with cold water and cook completely tender in less broth.
3. When removed and cooled, cut diagonally following the shape of the board into slices the thickness of a knife back.
4. Lay them side by side in a casserole heavily lined with butter, sprinkle with bread crumbs and, if desired, some pepper, cover with paper brushed with butter, and set aside in a cool place.
5. About one quarter hour before serving, fry the sweetbreads at medium heat for 2-3 minutes.
6. Turn the slices over and fry equally on the second side.
7. To the sauce from which the fat has been removed earlier, pour in dissolved aspic glaze and let it come to a boil once.
8. Arrange the sliced sweetbreads alternately with bread slices roasted in butter on a plate and pour the sauce over.

title: Veal Sweetbreads in Butter Sauce (Ris de veau sauce au beurre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal sweetbreads
- Salt water
- Butter sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the sweetbreads tender in salt water.
2. Cut into desired pieces and mix gently with a well-made butter sauce.

title: Veal Sweetbreads in Fricassee Sauce (Ris de veau en fricassée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal sweetbreads
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Onions
- Mushrooms
- Spices (optional)
- Broth
- Flour
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice or wine

## Directions

1. Place the veal sweetbreads in lukewarm water and keep at moderate heat on the stove for approximately half an hour to maintain this temperature.
2. Remove them from the water, release from the membrane, and boil the sweetbreads in salt water.
3. When tender, remove and place in cold water, then lay on a board, cover with another board, and weight it down.
4. In the meantime, place some butter in a casserole with a little onion, mushroom, and if desired a little spice, roast these.
5. Pour in good broth and let the meat from the sweetbreads that has been loosened braise covered until tender.
6. Strain the sauce and cut the meat and sweetbreads into desired pieces.
7. Make a roux from flour and butter in the proportion of 2 tablespoons each, pour it with the strained sauce in which the meat was cooked, and dilute as needed with broth.
8. Mix 2 egg yolks and the juice of one-fifth of a lemon or some wine, combine with the sauce, and stir together over heat until the eggs are incorporated.
9. Strain through a sieve and serve with the sweetbreads and meat on a plate.

title: Veal Sweetbreads in Paper (Ris de veau en papillottes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal sweetbreads
- Broth
- Mushrooms
- Paper
- Fine herbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal sweetbreads as indicated for Escalopes, cooking tender in white broth with mushrooms.
2. After completely cooling, cut into half-finger-thick slices.
3. Wrap these like Veal Cutlets with fine herbs in paper and grill them like Cutlets over a grate.

title: Veal Sweetbreads Larded and Glazed (Ris de veau piqué et glacé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal sweetbreads
- Water
- Salt
- Bacon
- Butter
- Broth
- Aspic glaze
- Mushroom purée
- Braised lettuce
- Sorrel purée

## Directions

1. Let the veal sweetbreads lie in lukewarm water for one hour, then remove and boil for a few minutes in salt water until it slightly yields to finger pressure.
2. Place in cold water, remove, and lay on a board, cover with another board, and weight it down.
3. When completely dry, lard it with thin, half-finger-long strips of bacon.
4. Place the sweetbreads in a casserole lined with butter, moisten with some broth, and braise quickly.
5. When completely tender, cut into pieces, arrange circularly on a plate, and brush with dissolved aspic glaze.
6. In the center place mushroom purée, braised lettuce (as a vegetable) or sorrel purée.

title: Veal Sweetbreads with Fricassee Sauce (Ris de veau à la sauce fricassée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal sweetbreads
- Broth
- Fricassee sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the expressed sweetbreads tender in broth as indicated for Escalopes and serve with fricassee sauce.

title: Veal Sweetbreads with Truffles (Ris de veau aux truffes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal sweetbreads
- Truffles

## Directions

1. Prepare as indicated for Escalopes.
2. Arrange the leaf-cut sweetbreads alternately with leaf-cut truffles in a circle on a plate.

title: Veal Tails in White Sauce (Queues de veau à la poulette)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 veal tails
- Salt water
- Butter
- White sauce
- Egg yolks
- Green parsley
- Gum arabic marmalade (optional)
- Wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the veal tails into finger-length pieces and boil for approximately 15 minutes in salt water.
2. Remove, dry with a cloth, and braise covered in 15 ounces of butter for approximately one quarter hour.
3. Moisten with as much white sauce as to cover the pieces one finger-height.
4. When the sauce is well cooked, strain it through a sieve, thicken with 4 egg yolks beaten separately, combine with some finely cut green parsley, and pour over the pieces arranged on a plate.
5. Alternatively, add 3 tablespoons of gum arabic marmalade to the cooked sauce, wine as needed, and let come to a boil again.

title: Veal Tails with Rice (Queues de veau au riz)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces butter
- 3 veal tails
- Water
- Rice
- Parsley
- Mushroom
- Onion
- Clove

## Directions

1. Put 8 ounces of butter with 3 veal tails cut into finger-length pieces in half a liter of water and let simmer.
2. When they begin to become tender, add one quarter liter of rice, some sprigs of bound parsley, one piece of cleaned mushroom, and a small piece of onion skewered with a clove.
3. Braise until the rice is tender.
4. Before serving, remove the onion and parsley and serve the rice with the pieces on a plate.

title: Veal with Unripe Gooseberries (Kalbfleisch mit unreifen Stachelbeeren)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal
- Warm water
- Salt
- Unripe gooseberries
- A couple of lightly baked bread slices
- Meat broth
- A little vinegar
- Ginger
- Pepper
- Grated nutmeg
- Butter
- Cream (Schmette)

## Directions

1. Set the meat with warm water over the fire, salt it, skim it clean, and let it simmer gently until tender.
2. Meanwhile, put unripe gooseberries with a couple of lightly baked bread slices in a small pot, pour meat broth and a little vinegar over them, and let them simmer until quite soft.
3. Press them through a strainer, pour meat broth over them again, season with ginger, pepper, salt and grated nutmeg.
4. Add more gooseberries and several spoonfuls of cream, and let them simmer until soft.
5. Add butter and serve over the meat.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Veau à l'Italienne (Veal in Italian Style)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo veal from the knuckle
- salt
- twine
- 1 piece yellow onion, thinly sliced
- 4 bay leaves
- pepper
- 1/2 liter water
- 1/4 kilo olive oil or fine table oil
- 4 pieces finely cut anchovies

## Directions

1. Remove the sinews from the veal, salt it, and tie it with twine
2. In a casserole, place thinly sliced onion, 4 bay leaves, and pepper, lay the meat on top, add 1/2 liter water, and braise gently covered for 1/4 hour
3. Remove the meat, pour 1/4 kilo olive oil into a second casserole, and when very hot, add 4 finely cut anchovies
4. Lay the veal on top, pour the sauce over it, and braise gently covered for 3/4 hour with frequent basting, keeping the meat white without browning
5. The sauce should reduce until only the oil remains
6. Remove the twine, cut the meat into desired pieces, arrange in a platter with oil poured over, and serve with risotto or macaroni
7. Beef can also be prepared this way

title: Veilchen-Creme (Violet Cream)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka sugar
- 18 Deka violet flowers with all green parts removed
- 3 tablespoons Maraschino
- some dissolved indigo
- 2 Deka dissolved gelatin (gelatin solution No. 1687)
- 1/2 liter whipped cream

## Directions

1. Heat 16 Deka sugar and add to it while still hot 18 Deka violet flowers from which all green parts have been removed, cover the sugar and let it sit for 7 hours over the violet flowers so that it takes on their scent
2. The flowers must be picked before sunrise
3. When the sugar is still lukewarm, stir in 3 tablespoons Maraschino and some dissolved indigo, mix this all into 2 Deka dissolved, still hot gelatin (gelatin solution No. 1687), strain it through cloth, stir the strained mixture while still warm to 1/2 liter whipped cream and proceed further as with Coffee Cream with Gelatin (No. 1690)

title: Veilchen-Sulz (Violet Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 decagrams fresh picked violet flowers
- 40 decagrams boiling, clarified sugar (see recipe 1542)
- 1 teaspoon cochineal dye or 1 drop alkermes juice
- 7 decagrams isinglass, hot and dissolved
- Clear juice of 2 lemons
- Cherry water or Champagne, as much as needed
- Total liquid of 1 liter

## Directions

1. Place 6 decagrams fresh picked violet flowers in a porcelain bowl
2. Pour 40 decagrams boiling, clarified sugar over it
3. Add 1 teaspoon cochineal dye or 1 drop alkermes juice
4. Leave covered overnight
5. The next day, heat it again, strain through cloth
6. Pour hot to 7 decagrams of hot dissolved isinglass
7. Strain this jelly mass through fine, thin linen cloth into a deep bowl
8. Add the clear juice of 2 lemons and as much cherry water or Champagne (preferred) so that the whole makes 1 liter of liquid
9. Stir somewhat and proceed as for Orange Sulz (see recipe 1660)

title: Venison Saddle, Roasted (Hirschziemer, gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- venison saddle
- brine/marinade
- bacon
- butter
- capers

## Directions

1. Wash the venison saddle and remove the skin
2. Pound it until it becomes tender, salt it well, and let it lie in brine for two or three days
3. Roast it in a roasting pan in the oven, cover it with a paper brushed with fat so the bacon does not burn, and pour brine over it frequently
4. When well roasted, place it on a dish and pour caper sauce over it
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Verlorene Eier (Poached Eggs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Eggs
- Pepper
- Herbs

## Directions

1. Put water, some vinegar and a little salt into a shallow casserole.
2. When boiling, crack the desired number of eggs into it and stir gently for 2-3 minutes until the egg white is completely set.
3. Remove the eggs with a slotted spoon and serve on a plate on puree, pache, vegetables or sauce.
4. The eggs can then be sprinkled with a little pepper and finely cut herbs as desired.

title: Verlorene Eyer in Champignonsoße (Poached Eggs in Mushroom Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, a small piece
- Flour
- Parsley water or weak pea broth
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Mushrooms (Champignons), finely cut
- Asparagus, optional

## Directions

1. Melt a small piece of butter in a saucepan and roast a little flour in it so it barely takes on a yellow color.
2. Pour parsley water or weak pea broth on it, salt it, season with nutmeg, add finely cut mushrooms, and let it simmer.
3. Follow the procedure for poached eggs, pour the prepared sauce into a dish, place the eggs in it, and let them simmer once more.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Vermischter Saint Jacot Gemüsen (Macedoine Salad)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 große, gekochte, fein blätterig geschnittene Erdäpfel
- 1/4 Stück ebenso geschnittene Essig-Gurken
- eine rote Rübe, in der Röhre gebraten, gepult und fein blätterig geschnitten
- eine in Salzwasser gekochte, in kleine Röschen geteilte Kariol-Rose
- 20 Stück in der Mitte voneinander gebrochene, gekochte, grüne Pistolen
- 10 in halbfingerlange Stücke gebrochene, in Salzwasser gekochte Spargel
- einige fein geschnittene Blätter gekochte Sellerie
- einige blätterig geschnittene gelbe Rüben
- Essig

## Directions

1. Dieses alles gibt man in eine tiefe Schüssel, begießt es mit etwa Essig und läßt es zugedeckt zehn Minuten stehen.

title: Verschiedene eingekochte Früchte in Dunst (Various Preserved Fruits in Steam)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- medlars (mispeln)
- pineapples
- strawberries with stems
- mulberries
- blackberries
- cloudberries
- service berries (dirnelbeeren, kirschen or cornel cherries)
- gooseberries
- sloes
- lingonberries
- 80 decilitres sugar
- sugar water

## Directions

1. All these fruits are treated in the same way
2. Always choose a beautiful, large variety and place them, but dry and with completely undamaged skin, in the preserving jars
3. Add so much sugar water of 80 decilitres (as with the Muscatel pears Nr. 1842) that it covers the fruit
4. Proceed as with the Muscatel pears and let them cook 3-4 hours in steam
5. Before placing medlars in the jars, cut off the protruding sepals with a knife
6. Gooseberries are left whole and, if desired, the seeds are removed

title: Verschiedene Getränke (Various Beverages)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5-1 liter washed rice, roasted barley, oat gruel, or sago
- 0.5 liter water or unsalted broth
- Salt
- Red wine or lemon juice
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 0.5-1 liter of washed rice, roasted barley, oat gruel, or sago in 0.5 liter more water or unsalted broth.
2. If made with broth, add some salt after straining.
3. If made with water, mix it with some red wine or lemon juice and sugar.

title: Vervain Vinegar (Bertram-Essig)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Leaves from 5 bunches of vervain
- 5 liters of good vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the plucked leaves from 5 bunches of vervain into a bottle
2. Pour 5 liters of good vinegar over them, but a space three fingers wide must remain empty in the bottle
3. Cork the bottle and bind it with linen cloth

title: Vespertro-Liqueur (Vespertro Liqueur)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Maß good brandy
- 2 drams angelica roots
- 1 ounce coriander
- anise and fennel, as much as can be held between four fingers
- yellow peel from 2 lemons
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1.5 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Crush all roots and seeds roughly together
2. Place in brandy
3. Carefully cut the yellow peel from 2 lemons so no white part is included
4. Add the lemon peel and juice to the brandy
5. Add 1.5 pounds sugar
6. Place everything in a preserving glass
7. Let stand for 6 days, stirring twice daily
8. On the seventh day, filter through filter paper with a funnel
9. Fill into bottles, cork well, and store in a cool place
10. The older it becomes, the better it is
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Viennese Semolina (Panade) Soup
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart grated semolina breadcrumbs
- 24 drams butter
- 2-4 liters beef or meat broth or water
- salt
- nutmeg
- 4 egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. Toast the grated semolina breadcrumbs slowly in butter until light brown
2. Pour in 2-4 liters of beef or meat broth or water
3. Simmer slowly for about 1/2 hour
4. Season with salt and a little nutmeg
5. Strain well and serve
6. If desired, the soup can be thickened with 4 egg yolks

title: Viennese Torte (Tourte à la Viennoise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 Deka beaten butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 whole eggs
- 24 Deka sugar
- 1 knife tip finely chopped lemon zest
- 28 Deka flour
- jam for assembly
- white glaze

## Directions

1. Add egg yolks, whole eggs, sugar, and lemon zest gradually to beaten butter
2. Stir for 1/2 hour
3. Mix in flour well
4. Cut round or square sheets from paper and brush with butter
5. Spread the mixture finger-high on them
6. Place them next to each other on a baking sheet
7. Bake at moderate heat in the oven
8. Remove from oven and once completely cooled, remove from paper
9. Brush the side that was not covered with paper with dissolved jam
10. Stack the sheets so that a spread side is covered with a dry side
11. Do not spread the top sheet
12. Cut the edges evenly with a fine, sharp knife
13. Cover with white glaze and let dry

title: Vin Brilé (Flaming Wine)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 measures good Rhenish or Frankish wine
- cinnamon
- cloves
- lemon peel
- 0.5 pound sugar

## Directions

1. Place 3 measures of good Rhenish or Frankish wine with some cinnamon, cloves and lemon peel in a soup pot
2. Sweeten with 0.5 pound sugar
3. Heat on hot ashes until boiling hot
4. Ignite with burning paper
5. Let it burn until it extinguishes itself
6. Strain through a napkin
7. Serve warm
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Vin Brulé (Burnt Wine)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 measures good Rhine or Franconian wine
- Cinnamon
- Cloves
- Lemon peels
- 1/4 pound sugar

## Directions

1. Take 3 measures of good Rhine or Franconian wine, never Champagne, which is not suitable for this.
2. Put it in a soup pot with some cinnamon, cloves and lemon peels.
3. Sweeten it with 1/4 pound sugar.
4. Make it boiling hot on hot ashes and ignite it.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Vinegar Horseradish (Vinegar Horseradish)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces grated rolls soaked in water and well pressed out
- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- some vinegar
- 2 tablespoons oil
- 1 pinch white pepper
- 4 tablespoons broth
- some salt
- ½ teaspoon sugar
- finely cut chives (optional)

## Directions

1. Pass 8 ounces of grated rolls soaked in water and well pressed out together with 3 hard-boiled egg yolks through a sieve into a bowl
2. Stir together with some vinegar, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 pinch white pepper, 4 tablespoons broth, some salt and ½ teaspoon sugar into a thick sauce
3. You can also add finely cut chives on top

title: Vinegar Horseradish Another Way (Vinegar Horseradish Another Way)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- 2 tablespoons mustard
- 2 tablespoons oil
- 1 teaspoon grated horseradish
- salt
- pepper
- raspberry vinegar

## Directions

1. Pass 6 hard-boiled eggs through a sieve
2. Stir in 2 tablespoons mustard and the same amount of oil, then add 1 teaspoon of grated horseradish, some salt and pepper and stir well with the necessary raspberry vinegar

title: Vinegar Soup (Soupe au vinaigre)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Deca butter
- Finely chopped onion
- Flour
- 1.5 liters water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. In 10 Deca butter, add 1 teaspoon of finely chopped onion
2. Let it roast somewhat
3. Add flour as needed, about 3-4 tablespoons, and roast together
4. Pour in 1.5 liters water and as much vinegar as required
5. Cook well
6. Pass through a fine sieve
7. Add salt and nutmeg
8. Turnips, noodles or similar can be cooked in the soup

title: Vol au vent (Puff Pastry Cases)
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dkg flour
- 18 dkg butter
- 1 egg
- salt
- 1 spoon vinegar
- some water
- egg white for brushing
- lingonberries for filling

## Directions

1. Make a butter dough from the flour, butter, egg, salt, vinegar and water (see doughs section), roll out not too thin
2. Cut out 2 and 2 leaves
3. Place one leaf on an ungreased baking sheet and brush the edge with egg white
4. Cut out the second leaf with a smaller cutter and place the ring on the first leaf
5. Just before serving, brush the pastries on top with beaten egg, bake until light brown over very high heat
6. Press in the middle and fill with lingonberries

title: Vol au vent (Puff Pastry Shell)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough, approximately 1/2 kilogram
- eggs

## Directions

1. A chilled butter dough of approximately 1/2 kilogram is rolled out 2 fingers thick and a round plate is cut out.
2. Butter-brushed paper is placed on a baking sheet, the round dough plate is placed on it, brushed with beaten eggs so far that a strongly knife-back-width margin remains, then the dough is cut with a knife a finger's breadth deep in the rounding so that a 2-finger-wide margin is obtained all around.
3. The pâté is then placed in the oven and baked at strong heat for 3/4 to 1 hour.
4. To prevent the pâté from becoming too dark, it is covered with paper once it is yellow.
5. The pâté, now twice as high, is then removed from the oven, the cut plate is carefully loosened with a knife, removed, and the soft and fatty dough inside is carefully removed with a spoon without injuring the crust at the bottom.
6. If this is done as quickly as possible, place the pâté in a bowl, fill it shortly before serving with any desired hot ragout, cover it again with the lid, and serve the pâté to the table.

title: Vol au vent Pastries (Small Pâtés) with Filling
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare butter dough and roll it out, after it has rested for 1 hour, on a flour-dusted board 2 knife-back-widths thick.
2. From one half of the dough, round pieces are cut out with a pastry cutter, which are placed at finger-width distance on a water-moistened baking sheet, brushed with beaten eggs so far that a strongly knife-back-width margin remains all around, which must never be brushed with eggs so that the dough of the pâté becomes flaky.
3. From the other half of the rolled-out dough, somewhat smaller pieces are cut out, which are then, to form rings, cut out once more in the middle.
4. These are now placed on the round plates so that the side which lay on the board does not lie on the brushed plates, but comes to lie upward.
5. These rings are then brushed in the middle with beaten eggs so far that both outside and inside a knife-back-width margin remains.
6. They are then placed in the oven and baked yellow at fairly strong heat.
7. Then remove them with a knife from the sheet, fill the middle empty space with any desired farce, and arrange them elevated in a bowl covered with a napkin.
8. It is particularly noteworthy that the margin of the pâté must never be brushed or dripped with eggs or water, as in this case the butter dough could not puff up.

title: Vollkranzen (Third Variety of Butter Pastry)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter pastry of finest type, made from 28 deca flour
- Eggs, beaten
- Whipped cream or cold cream for filling

## Directions

1. Roll the dough strongly knife-back-thick on a flour-dusted board
2. Cut into strips 2 fingers wide and 2 fingers long
3. Spirally wind each strip individually on 3-finger-thick round wooden sticks or round lead forms
4. Brush with beaten eggs
5. Place 3 fingers apart on a water-dampened baking sheet and bake slowly at gentle heat in the oven
6. Once done, carefully remove the sticks and fill the Moulades after complete cooling, by means of a paper piping bag or a coffee spoon, with whipped cream or cold cream

title: Wachholder-Branntwein (Juniper Brandy)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Juniper berries
- Purified brandy in proportion to the berries

## Directions

1. Crush a desired quantity of juniper berries without crushing the inner kernels, which give a harsh and unpleasant taste
2. Pour purified brandy over them in proportion
3. Allow it to stand and digest in the sun or at the oven
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Waiths Märcherei (Almond Confection)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg whites, firmly beaten
- 24 decagrams powdered sugar
- 28 decagrams peeled almonds
- 1 egg white
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- grated chocolate (optional)

## Directions

1. Stir the beaten egg whites and powdered sugar over the fire until the mass is hot
2. Add peeled almonds that have been very finely pounded with 1 egg white or pounded with the shell and passed through a sieve
3. Continue stirring over the fire until hot and thick throughout
4. Remove from fire and stir in a pinch of nutmeg and cinnamon until cool
5. Pipe the mass into desired figures on a baking sheet coated with wax using a piping bag or paper cone
6. Bake at low heat in the oven
7. If desired, add 1 tablet of grated chocolate to the mass before piping

title: Wald-Huhn (Wood Grouse)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Old or young wood grouse
- Preparations as for Aueraahn

## Directions

1. Prepare old and young wood grouse the same way as Aueraahn

title: Waldhuhn-Salmis (Wood Grouse Salmis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Wood grouse
- Ingredients as for partridge salmis

## Directions

1. Prepare as for partridge salmis, Nr. 749

title: Waldmeister (Woodruff)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 20 sprigs fresh woodruff
- 30 deciliters sugar in pieces
- 1 peeled orange cut in half
- 1 liter good white wine

## Directions

1. In a porcelain dish, add approximately 20 sprigs freshly picked woodruff, 30 deciliters sugar broken into pieces, 1 peeled orange cut in halves and 1 liter good white wine
2. Let it stand covered 1/2 hour
3. Strain and serve immediately
4. Shortly before serving, 1/4 liter ice-cold water can be added

title: Wandeln, Croustaden, Corsettchen mit frischem Obst (Puff Pastry Tartlets with Fresh Fruit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 ounces flour
- 12 ounces butter
- 1.5 pinches salt
- 1 whole egg
- 3 tablespoons sour cream or sweet custard
- 3 tablespoons wine
- flour for dusting
- butter for greasing the molds
- 5 fresh prunes for filling
- optional: other fresh fruit

## Directions

1. Place 16 ounces flour on a board and cut in 12 ounces butter
2. Rub flour and butter together with hands
3. Make a well in the center and add 1.5 pinches salt, 1 whole egg, 3 tablespoons sour cream or sweet custard, and 3 tablespoons wine
4. Mix well with a knife and work everything into a smooth dough
5. Place on a floured board, cover with a cloth, and let rest in a cool place for 1 hour
6. Roll dough to the thickness of a knife-back
7. Cut into squares to fit the tartlet molds
8. Line the buttered molds dusted with flour
9. Line the sides and bottom with 5 fresh prunes cut in half
10. Cut away overhanging dough
11. Place on a floured board, cover with a cloth, and let rise in a warm place
12. Bake at medium heat for 1.5 hours
13. Carefully remove from the molds with a spatula, arrange elevated on a platter, and dust with sugar
14. Alternative fruits may be used instead of prunes

title: Warme Limonade (Hot Lemonade)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter boiling water
- juice of 3 lemons
- 28 deciliters sugar

## Directions

1. In 1 liter boiling water, add strained juice of 3 lemons and 28 deciliters sugar

title: Warme Mayonnaise (Warm Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 1 spoonful vinegar
- 1 spoonful cold water
- 1 spoonful lemon juice
- mustard
- salt
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. In a double boiler, whisk 4 egg yolks with 1 spoonful vinegar, 1 spoonful cold water, and 1 spoonful lemon juice over heat until thick
2. Cool and mix with mustard, salt, vinegar, and oil
3. If the mayonnaise curdles, slowly add 1 spoonful of cold water at the edge while stirring constantly

title: Warme Pasteteufarce (Warm Pastry Filling)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds veal from the leg
- 1 pound bacon
- 1 pound ham
- 1 pound kidney fat
- 4 large onions
- mushrooms or truffles
- dried herbs
- 1 glass good wine
- white bread soaked in milk or water, rubbed and squeezed out
- 6 whole eggs
- salt
- pepper
- spices

## Directions

1. Remove all sinews from 4 pounds veal from the leg
2. Cut the veal into slices, then into cubes, along with 1 pound bacon, 1 pound ham, and 1 pound kidney fat
3. Sweat all of this with 4 large onions, mushrooms or truffles, and some dried herbs over gentle charcoal fire for half an hour
4. Pour in 1 glass of good wine
5. Finely chop the meat and bacon together with the above mixture and white bread that has been soaked in milk or water, rubbed, and squeezed out
6. Let the sauce that results from this reduce briefly
7. Rub everything together finely in a mortar with 6 whole eggs
8. Season with salt, pepper, and spices as needed
9. This filling can be used for all warm pastries
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Warme Sardellenſauce (Warm Anchovy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 anchovies
- nutmeg-sized butter
- soup
- 4 dkg fat
- salt

## Directions

1. Bone the anchovies
2. Knead together with nutmeg-sized butter on a board
3. Cook with some soup in a small pot for 5 minutes
4. Pass through a sieve
5. Add to a roux made from 4 dkg fat
6. Salt and cook for 15 minutes

title: Warmen Hopfensalat (Warm Hop Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Hop salad
- Salt water
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the hop salad thoroughly and rinse
2. Boil it in salted water
3. Place it in a pot with a piece of butter, salt, pepper and a little vinegar
4. Cook it until tender
5. Before serving, fricassee it with a couple of egg yolks
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Warmen Kohlsalat (Warm Cabbage Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- head cabbage
- salt
- butter
- vinegar
- wheat flour
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the head cabbage very finely or shave it finely
2. Sprinkle it with salt
3. Set a pan over charcoal fire and let butter melt in it
4. Sweat the cabbage in the butter
5. After a quarter hour, pour in the vinegar
6. Finally, to have a thickish broth, add some stirred wheat flour and pepper
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Warmen Speckſalat (Warm Bacon Salad)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Salad (endive or Bologna lettuce preferred)
- One quarter pound bacon, diced
- Wine vinegar
- Salt
- Butter (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the salad thoroughly
2. Cut the bacon into small dice
3. Let it turn golden in a pan
4. Pour wine vinegar and salt over it
5. Let it come to a boil with the bacon
6. Pour the boiling mixture over the salad
7. The vinegar can be strained and brought to a boil again if desired
8. Alternatively, the salad can be made warm with butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Warmer Krautſalat (Warm Sauerkraut Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large head of cabbage
- salt
- caraway seeds
- water
- 10 dkg bacon
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove bad leaves and cores from the cabbage, wash it, and cut finely into noodle-like strips
2. Sprinkle with salt and caraway seeds in a colander, pour boiling water over it, let it stand covered for 15 minutes, drain the water, and repeat the process
3. Cut the bacon into small cubes, roast until light yellow in a saucepan, and strain
4. When the fat has cooled, add vinegar, bring to a boil, and pour over the drained cabbage
5. Arrange the salad on a round platter and place the warmed bacon pieces in the center

title: Wasserbrandteis (Wasserbrandteis, Éclairs, Schwindeln)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/16 liter of cold water
- 3 1/6 liter of milk
- 21 drams of butter
- 1 knife point of salt
- 30 drams of flour
- 8 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Put 3/16 liter of cold water, 3 1/6 liter of milk, 21 drams of butter and 1 knife point of salt in a saucepan, and when this boils, gradually stir in 30 drams of flour while stirring constantly, then stir it on the stove until the dough is dry and separates from the saucepan.
2. The dough is now poured into a deep bowl and after it has cooled completely, 8 whole eggs are gradually stirred in.
3. Using a spoon, remove pieces of dough of any size, which are piped at a distance of 3 fingers on a baking sheet brushed with butter and sprinkled with flour, brushed with beaten egg and baked golden in the fairly hot oven.
4. When this is done, take them from the baking sheet with a very thin knife and cut them open on the side to the middle.

title: Wassernöckerl (Water Noodles)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 liter cold water

## Directions

1. In a deep bowl, combine 1/2 liter flour and 1/2 teaspoon salt
2. Gradually pour in approximately 1/4 liter cold water while stirring constantly
3. Beat the dough until it falls from the spoon
4. Place dough on a board and proceed as indicated in the Holzbackerzöferln recipe

title: Waſſer von Erdbeeren, Himbeeren, ſauren Kirſchen oder Johannisbeeren (Water of Strawberries, Raspberries, Sour Cherries, or Currants)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fresh berries
- one Seidel of water
- one quarter pound of sugar
- juice of one lemon (for strawberry water only)

## Directions

1. Take one pound of fresh berries for one Seidel of water
2. Press them in the water
3. Add one quarter pound of sugar
4. Stir everything together well
5. When the sugar is completely dissolved, let the water run through a filter cloth
6. Keep it in a cool place
7. For strawberry water, press in the juice of one lemon (this is not necessary for the other fruits)
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Weiche Eier in Sauce (Soft-Boiled Eggs in Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauce (sorrel sauce, tarragon sauce, caper sauce, lentil or any puree)
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Put some desired sauce into a shallow casserole, such as sorrel sauce, tarragon sauce, caper sauce, lentil or any puree.
2. Once it boils, crack the eggs into it and let them cook until the egg white is set.
3. Remove the eggs with 2 metal spoons and place in a bowl containing the same sauce in which they were cooked.

title: Weiche Eyer (Soft-Boiled Eggs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Cold water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bread, finely cut
- Butter

## Directions

1. Place eggs with cold water over the fire, and remove them when the water begins to boil.
2. Serve with finely cut bread, salt, pepper, and butter (in some regions, butter is served separately to melt in them).
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Weichsel-, Himbeer-, Bertram- und Maulbeer-Essig (Sour Cherry, Raspberry, Pellitory, and Mulberry Vinegar)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cherries, raspberries, pellitory, and mulberries as needed
- good wine vinegar to fill the bottle
- pellitory leaves, 1 strong handful per measure

## Directions

1. Put as much of these ingredients as needed into a bottle
2. Fill the bottle with good wine vinegar
3. Let it stand in the sun for 8 to 14 days
4. After this time, strain the vinegar through filter paper
5. Stopper the bottles well
6. Let it stand in the cellar, where it keeps well
7. Note: of pellitory leaves take only 1 strong handful per measure; of the other ingredients, too much will not hurt
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Weichsel-Kochli (Cherry Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deka cherry sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- 18 Deka white bread crumbs
- Butter for the mold
- Flour for dusting
- Stewed cherries

## Directions

1. Stir cherry sugar and 8 egg yolks together
2. Lightly mix this with the snow from 8 egg whites simultaneously with 18 Deka white bread crumbs
3. Fill into a butter-brushed, flour-dusted cake mold
4. Top with stewed cherries, which then slowly sink in
5. Bake for ¾ hour in the oven

title: Weichsel-Sauce (Sour Cherry Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 deca butter
- equal amount flour
- 3/4 liter ripe gooseberries
- necessary broth
- red wine
- sour cherries

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as the gooseberry sauce, with the difference that the stones are removed

title: Weichsel-Sulz (Sour Cherry Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- stewed sour cherry juice
- refined sugar
- isinglass

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as Orange Jelly (No. 1660), using juice from stewed sour cherries.

title: Weichselwein (Sour Cherry Wine)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Maß (or one quarter Eimer) wine
- one Büttl of sour cherries
- 2 Büttl of small black cherries with their pits
- one and a half or 2 pounds sugar
- 1 loth cinnamon
- 1 loth cloves
- 1 loth nutmeg blossom
- one stone

## Directions

1. Crush the sour cherries and black cherries in a mortar
2. With a second mortar, add one and a half or 2 pounds sugar
3. Place all spices in a sachet and fasten a stone at the end of it so the spices sink to the bottom
4. Make the crushed cherries and cherries into a juice and press them well
5. Pour the juice into the small barrel, add the wine on top
6. Stir it and let it stand for eight days
7. When taking from it, you can always pour fresh wine in for a while
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wein aus faulem und anbrüchigem Obste (Wine from Rotten and Damaged Fruit)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- rotten or damaged fruit
- water, as much as needed
- one third hopfen (hops)

## Directions

1. Cut the fruit into pieces
2. Put it with water and one third hopfen into the kettle
3. Boil it for one and a half hours
4. Strain it
5. Let it ferment
6. Draw it off into bottles
7. You will have a beer that is wine-like in character
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wein-Creme (Wine Cream)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- one small bottle of good aged wine
- one small stick of vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks in a pot, pour one small bottle of good aged wine to it, place a small stick of vanilla in it
2. Whisk wine and eggs over gentle charcoal fire until it is about to boil once
3. Immediately pour it onto a plate, remove the vanilla, and serve warm
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wein-Kochli (Blue Wine Souffle)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ liter wine
- 8 Deka sugar
- 1 clove
- 1 piece cinnamon
- 12 Deka bread crusts removed, cut into thin slices

## Directions

1. Put wine with sugar, clove, and cinnamon in a saucepan
2. Allow to simmer well and pour through a sieve over bread with crusts removed, cut into thin slices
3. Stir on the fire until this mass has cooked together, then set aside and let cool

title: Wein-Soufflé (Wine Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter wine
- 8 Deca sugar
- 1 clove
- 1 piece cinnamon
- 12 Deca sliced rolls (Semmeln), cut in flakes
- 1 Deca butter, worked
- 16 Deca finely pounded sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites (snow)

## Directions

1. Pour 1/4 liter wine with 8 Deca sugar, 1 clove and 1 piece cinnamon into a saucepan, bring to a good boil and pour through a sieve over 12 Deca sliced rolls, cut in flakes
2. Stir over the fire until it has cooked well, then set it aside and let it cool
3. Meanwhile work 1 Deca butter with 16 Deca finely pounded sugar well together, stir in 8 egg yolks one after another, then mix this well with the cooled mass and finally fold in lightly the snow (whipped egg whites) from 8 egg whites
4. Fill a buttered form dusted with flour with this mass, cover and cook for 3/4 hours in steam or bake in the oven

title: Wein-Sulz (Wine Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 decagrams sugar, clarified
- 7 decagrams isinglass, hot and dissolved
- Wine of choice, instead of wine and fruit juices

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Orange Sulz (see recipe 1660)
2. Instead of orange and lemon juice, use wine of choice

title: Wein-Suppe (Wine Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- good white wine, half a measure
- water, 1 coffee cup full
- sugar
- whole cinnamon
- lemon peel
- cloves
- egg yolks, 4
- sweet cream, 2 spoonfuls
- toasted bread slices
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Cook half a measure of good white wine with one coffee cup of water, a piece of sugar, some whole cinnamon, lemon peel and some cloves for half an hour
2. Remove the spices
3. Beat 4 egg yolks in a pot with 2 spoonfuls of sweet cream
4. Pour the hot wine onto the egg mixture while stirring vigorously
5. Pour into the soup pot
6. Top with neatly cut toasted bread slices dusted thickly with fine sugar and glazed with a hot shovel
7. Serve quickly to the table so the bread does not soften
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wein-Suppe (Wine Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 strong tablespoon flour
- 6 tablespoons egg
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 liter boiling wine
- some cinnamon
- 24 deca sugar

## Directions

1. Stir 1 strong tablespoon flour with 6 tablespoons egg, beat 8 egg yolks in
2. Beat this with 1 liter boiling wine together with some cinnamon and 24 deca sugar on the fire with a whisk in a snow bowl or pass through a sieve into the soup pot as soon as it begins to boil
3. The soup must not boil and, if desired, the flour and cinnamon can be omitted
4. If the soup is not sweet enough, add sugar as needed

title: Weinhuͤppchen (Wine Strips)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 eggs
- 1 pound sugar
- zest of one lemon
- three-quarters pound fine flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Whip the whites of 14 eggs to foam
2. Pound 1 pound sugar, over which lemon zest has been rubbed
3. Gradually stir in three-quarters pound fine flour
4. Spread this mass in 2 finger-widths strips on a butter-rubbed sheet
5. Bake quickly for a quarter hour
6. Cut and while still warm wind over a finger-thick round stick
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Weinkoch (Wine Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg-sized portion of good lard
- fine bread crumbs, finely grated
- two parts wine and one part water
- one-quarter pound butter
- five whole eggs and five yolks
- raisins, cleaned and washed
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Let one egg-sized portion of good lard become hot in a cake pan
2. Roast finely grated bread crumbs golden yellow in it
3. Let two parts wine and one part water simmer together and pour it over the bread crumbs
4. Stir it like a child's pudding and allow it to cool well
5. Sweeten it well with sugar
6. Beat one-quarter pound butter until fluffy
7. Stir in five whole eggs and five yolks, each stirred well, and the cooled pudding
8. Add cleaned and washed raisins and some ground cinnamon
9. Stir everything for half an hour
10. Spread a sheet pan and rim or a cake pan with butter
11. Pour the pudding into it and bake it slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Weinkoch (Wine Cake)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg-sized portion of good lard
- finely grated bread crumbs
- 2 parts wine
- 1 part water
- sugar
- 1/4 pound butter
- 5 whole eggs
- 5 egg yolks
- cleaned and washed currants
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Heat the lard in a pan until hot
2. Toast the finely grated bread crumbs in it until golden yellow
3. Let 2 parts wine and 1 part water boil together and pour into the bread crumbs
4. Stir it like a milk pudding and let it cool completely
5. Sugar it well
6. Beat 1/4 pound butter until fluffy
7. Stir in 5 whole eggs and 5 egg yolks, each well beaten, one by one
8. Stir in the cooled cake mixture
9. Add some cleaned and washed currants and some ground cinnamon
10. Stir everything for half an hour
11. Butter a metal dish and ring mold or ramekin
12. Pour the mixture in and bake slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Weintrauben frisch zu bewahren (Preserving Grapes Fresh)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- grapes
- wax or pitch
- chaff or millet
- wheat bran
- twine

## Directions

1. Cut grapes on dry, clear days at midday
2. Dip the stem ends in melted wax or pitch
3. Remove any false or damaged berries with scissors
4. Hang grapes on an airy floor to dry completely
5. Layer them in well-sealed boxes between chaff, millet, or completely dry wheat bran so that no grape touches another
6. If desired, cut whole branches with multiple grape bunches and hang them; they will keep longer
7. Alternatively, for fleshier varieties cut before ripeness with stems attached, dip stem ends in melted pitch (better than wax), and hang in cool rooms by wrapping the stems with twine from a cord
8. Avoid creating dust in such rooms
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Weintrauben-Kuchen (Grape Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter
- 16 deca caster sugar
- 8 deca peeled almonds
- 2 egg whites
- 8 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 10 deca semmel crumbs
- grapes

## Directions

1. In a deep bowl, beat 14 deca butter until fluffy
2. Add 16 deca caster sugar, 8 deca peeled almonds finely ground with 2 egg whites, and 8 egg yolks
3. Stir together for 1/4 hour
4. Lightly mix in the stiff peaks of 6 egg whites with 10 deca semmel crumbs
5. Fill into a baking pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour
6. Allow plucked grapes, 3 and 3 one upon the other, to sink into the dough
7. Further treatment is as with the Brown Cherry or Morello Cherry Cake (No. 1460)

title: Weintrauben-Marmelade (Grape Jam)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Blue grapes
- 1 kilo grapes with 1/4 kilo powdered sugar
- Ground cinnamon and clove nuts (optional)

## Directions

1. Blue grapes are stripped from the bunch
2. 1 kilo of them with 1/4 kilo powdered sugar are boiled for approximately 15 minutes in a saucepan
3. Then like the apricot jam filled hot into jars
4. One can cook ground cinnamon and clove nuts with it

title: Weintrauben-Sauce (Grape Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- white or red wine
- white or blue grapes
- cinnamon as desired

## Directions

1. Prepare like the quince sauce, pouring white wine with white grapes and red wine with blue grapes
2. If desired, add 1 strong pinch of cinnamon

title: Weintraubenkuchen (Wine Grape Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dkg flour
- 16 dkg butter
- 2 tablespoons milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 5 dkg sugar
- salt
- 3 egg whites
- 16 dkg sugar
- 7 dkg grated raw almonds
- 1 kg black grapes

## Directions

1. Prepare a crumbly dough (see Doughs) from the flour, butter, milk, egg yolks, sugar, and salt
2. After resting, roll out the dough to sheet size and place on an unbuttered baking sheet
3. Bake at higher heat until light brown
4. Whip 3 egg whites into stiff peaks and stir in 16 dkg sugar, 7 dkg grated raw almonds, and the grapes which have been washed and removed from their stems
5. Spread the mixture on the baked cake and allow it to bake in the oven until light brown
6. Cut the cake and serve cooled

title: Weinöheits-Punch mit Gerstenmaiser (Health Punch with Barley)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sugar in small pieces
- juice of 1 lemon
- juice of 4 oranges
- 1/4 liter rum
- 1/8 liter barley grains
- 1 liter water

## Directions

1. Prepare like the Gesundheits-Punch mit Reis
2. Instead of rice, boil 1/8 liter rinsed barley grains in 1 liter water for 1/2 hour
3. Strain and add to the punch components

title: Weisser Kirschenkuchen (White Cherry Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca butter
- 24 deca caster sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 30 deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat 28 deca butter with 24 deca caster sugar and 6 egg yolks for 1/2 hour
2. Lightly mix in the stiff peaks of 6 egg whites with 30 deca flour
3. Fill into a baking pan brushed with butter and dusted with flour, 2 to 3 fingers high
4. Further treatment is as with the Brown Cherry or Morello Cherry Cake (No. 1460)

title: Weiß eingemachter Aal (Eel in White Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- salt water

## Directions

1. After preparing the eel, cut it into 2-finger wide pieces and poach gently in salt water. Then lay the eel on a napkin so that all fat drains off, arrange it then in a platter and serve with any desired sauce: sugar, mustard, Hollandaise sauce or the like.

title: Weiß gefülltes Kraut (German-Style Stuffed Cabbage)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage leaves
- boiling water
- 12 tablespoons butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 30 tablespoons finely cut bacon
- 1/6 teaspoon finely cut parsley
- 1 knife-point pepper
- salt, if necessary
- 1 1/4 liters milk cream
- 4 tablespoons soaked rolls without crust, well pressed
- 1/4 liter broth
- milk cream
- 1/4 liter butter sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves from the cabbage, dip them in boiling water, lay them on a cloth so each leaf lies separately
2. Make a filling: 12 tablespoons butter are beaten fluffy, 2 whole eggs, 30 tablespoons finely cut bacon, 1/6 teaspoon finely cut parsley, 1 knife-point pepper, if necessary some salt, 1 1/4 liters milk cream, 4 tablespoons soaked rolls without crust, well pressed, all mixed together
3. Spread this on the leaves, roll them together, place in a casserole lined with butter, pour 1/4 liter broth and some milk cream over them and stew for approximately 3/4 hours
4. After this time, pour 1/4 liter butter sauce over the steamed dish and cook once more
5. Then remove the fat cleanly, lift out the cabbage with a serving spoon, place on a platter and serve the sauce with it

title: Weiß-Strudeln (White Rice Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk
- 20 Deka cooked rice
- 10 Deka butter
- 4 whole eggs
- salt
- green parsley
- 1/3 liter flour for dough

## Directions

1. Cook 20 Deka rice in 1/2 liter milk until soft
2. Place in a bowl and let cool
3. Whip 10 Deka butter until fluffy
4. Beat in 4 whole eggs one by one
5. Add cooled rice, salt, and green parsley
6. Mix well
7. Spread on strudel dough made from 1/3 liter flour
8. Treat as lung strudel

title: Weiße Boudins (White Boudins)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds pork fat, finely worked in a mortar or grindstone
- onions, softened in butter
- white bread, soaked in milk and squeezed out
- poultry meat, finely chopped and ground in a grindstone, equal amount to the fat
- three-quarter measure sweet cream
- six to seven egg yolks
- salt
- spices

## Directions

1. Soften the onions in butter as described above
2. Finely work the 2 pounds pork fat in a mortar or grindstone
3. Mix the worked fat with the milk-soaked and squeezed white bread, and combine with the softened onions
4. Take as much onion as fat was used
5. Stir in the equal amount of finely chopped and ground poultry meat and three-quarter measure sweet cream
6. Mix in six to seven egg yolks, salt, and spices until well combined
7. Fill into thoroughly cleaned and washed pork intestines, tying both ends securely
8. Ensure no air remains in the intestines, otherwise the boudins will burst
9. Cook in water and milk using the aforementioned method
10. After cooling, place them on paper brushed with butter or fine oil
11. Roast them very slowly on a grill
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Weiße Bröfelnknödel (White Bread Knödel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 deca beaten butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- some salt
- 20 deca white bread crumbs
- 4 tablespoons milk

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until creamy. Stir in the egg yolks, whole egg, and salt.
2. Add the white bread crumbs that have been soaked in 4 tablespoons milk and mix well together.
3. Let stand covered for 1/4 hour.
4. Form small knödel from the mixture.
5. Cook them covered for approximately 10 minutes in boiling salted water or in the soup.
6. They can be added to soup or to preserved dishes.

title: Weiße Coulis (White Coulis)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound raw lean ham
- beautiful veal meat in equal amount to ham
- onions
- celery
- parsley roots
- 2 pounds butter
- extraction flour (wheat flour)
- white bouillon

## Directions

1. Cut the pound of raw lean ham and equal amount of beautiful veal meat into thin slices, as well as onions, celery, and parsley roots, adjusting quantities as desired for larger or smaller amounts
2. Melt 2 pounds of butter, then stir in extraction flour with a wooden spoon so that it becomes neither stiff nor too thin, but can be stirred comfortably
3. Stir this on gentle heat until it begins to foam white
4. Stir in the aforementioned ham, veal meat and vegetables
5. Add first some white bouillon and stir it smooth (if too much bouillon is added at once to the sweated flour, it becomes lumpy)
6. Then pour in as much white bouillon as needed for the quantity of coulis desired
7. Cook on gentle heat from the side until it reaches proper thickness
8. During cooking, remove all rising fat and foam with a spoon
9. Pass through a sieve without pressing through with a spoon, otherwise it will take on a blackish appearance from the cooked meat and vegetables
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Weiße Hühnerconsommé (White Chicken Consommé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 alte Hühner
- 2 Liter kaltes Wasser
- 1 kleines Bouquet grüne Petersilie
- 1 kleines Stück gebratene Zwiebel

## Directions

1. 2 alte Hühner werden, nachdem sie abgeflammiert und gewaschen sind, jedes in 4 Teile zerschnitten.
2. In einer Casserolle mit 2 Liter kaltem Wasser aufgestellt und zum Kochen gebracht.
3. Nachdem es gut abgeschäumt ist, gibt man ein kleines Bouquet grüne Petersilie und ein kleines Stück gebratene Zwiebel hinein.
4. Decken und 3 Stunden langsam kochen lassen, so daß 1,5 Liter geschmackvolle, weiße Suppe erzeugt wird.
5. Das verkochtе Hühnerflеisch kann zur Bereitung von Pâté oder Consommé verwendet werden.

title: Weiße Spargels-Fißolen (Haricots d'asperges)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus kidney beans
- butter
- bread crumbs
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Prepare as the green kidney beans
2. Serve warm with butter and bread crumbs, or if served cold, with vinegar and oil

title: Weißer Marzipan (White Marzipan)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Marzipan dough
- Sugar
- Orange blossom water
- Fine, narrow, thread-like cut candied orange peel
- Diamond-shaped citron leaves
- Colored, sugar-preserved fruits (walnuts, cherries, mirabelles, apricots)
- Nonpareille

## Directions

1. Work the marzipan dough well with sugar.
2. Stretch it to the thickness of a knife back and cut various figures with a pastry wheel, such as hearts, stars, rosettes, etc.
3. Surround these figures with a finger-width marzipan border and crimp it with a crimping iron so it appears frilly.
4. Let the marzipan dry well for 8 days.
5. Bake in a gently heated oven until light yellow.
6. Cook some sugar in orange blossom water to the large pearl stage and brush the borders of the marzipan with it.
7. Cover the base of the marzipan with the icing described above.
8. Have ready fine, narrow, thread-like cut candied orange peels and diamond-shaped citron leaves.
9. Arrange the orange peels branch- or stem-like on the still moist marzipan base.
10. Add the citron as leaves.
11. Finally arrange various colored, sugar-preserved fruits on top, such as walnuts, cherries, mirabelles, apricots, etc.
12. Let the icing dry and become glossy in the oven.
13. Brush the upper side of the borders with any sugar juice and sprinkle with nonpareille.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Weißes Eis (White Icing)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 deka finely sifted sugar
- white of 1 small egg
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Put 32 deka finely sifted sugar into a deep bowl
2. Stir this using a wooden spoon meant only for this work, with the white of a small egg and the juice of 1 lemon for approximately 1 hour until well beaten
3. If the mixture is too thick, add some water; if too thin, add more sugar, and stir for another 1/4 hour
4. The icing must form a brilliantly white and glossy mass that flows thickly from the spoon
5. This icing, which contains egg white, must be used on the same day, as it becomes hard if it sits longer

title: Weißgebuttelte Kohlrüben (White-braised Kohlrabi)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 kohlrabi
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 6 ounces butter
- 1 tablespoon parsley
- White soup
- Butter sauce
- Meat for garnish

## Directions

1. Peel the kohlrabi and cut them into pieces of your choice
2. Cook them tender in salt water
3. Place the drained kohlrabi in a casserole with 6 ounces of butter and 1 tablespoon of parsley
4. Pour enough cold water over them so they are covered and braise covered until tender
5. If the liquid evaporates and the kohlrabi are not yet tender, add the necessary white soup
6. Pour a thin butter sauce over them and bring to a boil again
7. Cook the fine greens from the kohlrabi in salt water, cut finely, and mix with butter sauce into the kohlrabi
8. Garnish with fried or braised meat

title: Weißgebuttelte weiße Rüben (White-braised White Turnips)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- White turnips
- Butter
- Milk or soup
- Parsley
- Meat for garnish

## Directions

1. Peel the white turnips and treat them the same way as kohlrabi
2. Note: For the sauce, soup can be used instead of milk

title: Weißgedünstete Kohlrabi (White-Braised Kohlrabi)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Young kohlrabi, peeled and cut into knife-back-thick slices
- Green parsley, finely cut
- Skimmed fat
- Broth
- 2 or 3 cleaned chickens, cut into pieces
- Flour
- Nutmeg
- Milk cream

## Directions

1. Cut round discs from knife-back-thick slices of young peeled kohlrabi
2. Braise them with finely cut green parsley and skimmed fat, adding broth occasionally so they become soft and remain white
3. Braise 2 or 3 cleaned, cut-up chickens in skimmed fat
4. Dust with flour and when they have browned somewhat, add enough broth to achieve the proper consistency
5. Cook with a little nutmeg until the chickens are soft
6. Add the kohlrabi with a spoonful of good milk cream
7. When all is softly braised, arrange the kohlrabi in the center of the platter and the chickens neatly around the edge
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wellington-Filet (Fillet in Pastry Crust)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 to 1.20 kg tenderloin
- 10 dkg bacon
- 3 mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- hot fat
- 15 dkg flour
- 15 dkg butter
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- water
- fines herbes
- egg white

## Directions

1. Prepare and roast the meat as for English Fillet, but it must be finished 1 hour before serving and allowed to cool completely.
2. Make a butter dough from 15 dkg flour, 15 dkg butter, 1 egg, salt, 2 tablespoons vinegar, and water.
3. Roll out the dough into a rectangle, not too thin.
4. Place the cooled meat in the center and brush with fines herbes.
5. Brush the dough with egg white and wrap it around the meat from all sides.
6. Place seam side down on a baking sheet and brush the top with beaten egg.
7. Cut decorative shapes from remaining thin dough, brush with egg, and arrange on top of the meat.
8. Bake at high heat until light brown.
9. Slice thinly and arrange on a long platter.
10. Serve with mushroom sauce on the side.

title: Wermuth- und Kräuterweine (Wormwood and Herbal Wines)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 handfuls common wormwood
- 3 handfuls Roman wormwood
- 3 handfuls Kardobenedikten (blessed thistle) herb
- 2 handfuls liverwort
- 2 handfuls rosemary
- 2 handfuls lemon balm
- 2 handfuls hyssop
- must (newly pressed wine)

## Directions

1. Cut the herbs finely
2. Fill them into a small sachet
3. Hang the sachet in the small barrel with the must for 12 to 14 days
4. After this time, the wine is already drinkable
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Whist
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 loths fine tea
- 1 measure water
- 2 pounds sugar
- 12 lemons
- 6 bottles good Medoc

## Directions

1. Cook 2 loths of fine tea in 1 measure of water.
2. Pour through a napkin onto 2 pounds sugar.
3. Press the juice of 12 lemons into the mixture.
4. Add 6 bottles of good Medoc.
5. Make it boiling hot on a charcoal fire.
6. Serve in glasses.
7. This beverage must absolutely not boil, as it will otherwise lose its pleasant strength.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: White Butter (Beurre Blanc)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 decagrams butter
- salt
- white pepper, finely ground
- 3 tablespoons fine oil

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the butter with salt and finely ground white pepper
2. If the butter is hard, pound it in a mortar until creamy and gradually mix in 3 tablespoons of fine oil
