3. If the butter is soft, stir it in a bowl with a wooden spoon and place it on ice

title: White Marzipan (Weißer Marzipan)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- marzipan dough
- sugar

## Directions

1. Work the marzipan dough well with sugar
2. Stretch it to the thickness of a knife back
3. Cut various figures from it with the pastry wheel: hearts, stars, rosettes, etc.
4. Surround these figures with a finger-width marzipan border and pinch it with a crimping iron so it looks curly
5. Let the marzipan dry well for 8 days
6. Then bake in a weakly heated oven until light yellow
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: White Turnips Braised
body:
## Ingredients

- 3–4 white turnips
- salt
- water
- 4 dkg fat
- onion
- beef broth or water
- sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel the turnips and cut them into small cubes
2. Cook in salted water for 30–40 minutes
3. Make a light onion roux with 4 dkg fat, deglaze with beef broth or water
4. Add the drained turnips
5. Season with salt, sugar, and vinegar to taste
6. Cook for 30 minutes more

title: White Turnips Glazed
body:
## Ingredients

- white turnips
- 5 dkg butter
- 6 pieces sugar cubes
- salt
- water or broth

## Directions

1. Peel the turnips and cut them into thin slices
2. In 5 dkg hot butter, brown 6 sugar cubes
3. Add the turnips and salt
4. Deglaze with a little water or broth and cover
5. Braise for 1/2–1 hour without stirring much
6. Dust lightly before serving

title: White Turnips with Bratwurst (Weiße Ruben mit Bratwürsten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- White turnips
- Water
- Flour
- Butter
- Beef broth
- Bread
- Pepper
- Bratwurst or veal, lamb and pork cutlets
- Anis
- Sugar
- Caraway
- Salt

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the turnips, let them boil up in hot water, then place them on a strainer, let them drain, put them in a casserole, brown flour in butter, fill with beef broth, and let the turnips cook down with it. Since young turnips are commonly bitter, place a slice of bread in which draws out the bitterness.
2. Alternatively: place the cut turnips in fresh water, let butter become hot, add the turnips after they have drained, and let them sweat well in it, pour them into a pot, pour boiling meat broth over them, add grated bread or roux and plenty of pepper, and let it simmer until it becomes a thick broth. However, they are better with the bread than with the roux. You can garnish them with bratwurst or cutlets of veal, lamb, and pork.
3. Or alternatively: when the turnips are peeled and cut, steam them brown in butter, then place one layer in a dish, sprinkle anis and sugar over it, and cover with butter slices; then place another layer of turnips, and so on until all are arranged. Finally pour meat broth over them and let the turnips steam slowly and well covered.
4. Or alternatively: cut a couple of medium onions into dice, add a fair handful of fresh parsley, steam both with the cut turnips in a fair amount of butter, after an hour add a spoonful of flour, a little caraway, water and salt, gradually pour meat broth over them so they have some but not much broth, stir them frequently, and let them become tender.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Whitened Mayonnaise (Beschneite Mayonnaise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter clear, hot vinegar
- heavy cream
- salt to taste
- white pepper to taste
- nutmeg to taste
- lemon juice to taste
- tarragon vinegar to taste

## Directions

1. Pour 1/4 liter of clear, hot vinegar into a sauce pot and whip it with a whisk on ice until it forms stiff foam
2. When this is the case, gradually pour in 1/4 liter heavy cream while constantly stirring with the whisk
3. Flavor to taste with lemon juice and tarragon vinegar
4. Add horseradish, white pepper, and nutmeg as needed
5. Transfer to a clean dish and keep on ice until serving
6. Serve with fish, lobster, and langoustine

title: Whole Udder Stuffed (Das ganze Euter faſchirt)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- udder
- fine forcemeat
- egg yolks
- sour cream
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. This udder must consist of one or two large whole pieces. After it has been boiled soft and the skin removed, cut it lengthwise in slices, but not through to the bottom; then make a good, very fine forcemeat, mix it with a couple of egg yolks, and fill this forcemeat between each such cut.
2. Then press the udder back together into shape, pour over it a seidel of sour cream stirred with a couple of egg yolks, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, and bake it in the oven a little until it takes on a nice color.
3. Then serve it with the sauce that has formed.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wiener Biscuit-Torte (Viennese Biscuit Torte)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound very fine sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- Zest of one lemon
- Juice of half a lemon
- Whites of 14 eggs
- 28 Loth fine flour
- Butter

## Directions

1. In an earthen dish, stir one pound very fine sugar with 16 egg yolks and the zest of one lemon for a good hour
2. Press the juice of half a lemon and stir it for another quarter hour
3. Beat the whites of 14 eggs into stiff snow and fold slowly into the mixture
4. Finally stir in 28 Loth fine flour, brush a regular torte pan lightly with butter, and fill in the mixture
5. The pan should only be half full and must be baked very slowly
6. When the torte rises two finger-widths above the pan and has some cracks on top, it is done
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wiener Pudding (Viennese Pudding)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter, beaten
- 12 deca vanilla sugar
- 7 egg yolks
- 18 deca blanched, finely pounded almonds
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 14 deca butter with 12 deca vanilla sugar until mixed
2. Gradually add 7 egg yolks and 18 deca blanched, finely pounded almonds, one after the other
3. Beat this mixture for 1/2 hour
4. Lightly fold in the snow from 4 egg whites
5. Fill a buttered and floured mold with this mixture
6. Steam covered for 3/4 hour
7. Turn out onto a platter and serve with Chaudeau

title: Wienerauflauf von Maschanzgeräpfeln (Viennese Crab Apple Soufflé)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 beautiful crab apples
- Three-quarters pound hard roasted zwieback
- 3 measures milk
- Lemon juice
- Three-quarters pound sweet finely pounded almonds
- Half pound clear sugar
- Lemon zest
- Half pound butter
- 12 egg yolks
- 6 whole eggs
- Egg whites from 8 eggs
- Butter puff pastry

## Directions

1. Peel 24 beautiful crab apples and grate them on a grater.
2. Pound three-quarters pound hard roasted zwieback finely and sift it.
3. Make cheese from 3 measures milk with a little lemon juice, press it dry, and mix with three-quarters pound sweet finely pounded almonds, half pound clear sugar and grated lemon zest along with the zwieback flour and grated apples.
4. Beat half pound butter with 12 egg yolks and 6 whole eggs to foam, fold in both masses gradually, and stir for 3 quarters of an hour so that the mass becomes very light.
5. Finally add the snow from 8 eggs and bake the soufflé in a form lined with butter puff pastry.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wild Ducks, Larded and Roasted (Wildenten, gespickt und gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- wild ducks
- brine/marinade
- bacon
- cream

## Directions

1. Clean the wild ducks well and place them in brine for several days
2. Salt them and lard the breast neatly in four or six rows with finely cut bacon
3. Roast them on a spit, basting frequently with cream and brine
4. Serve with the strained sauce poured over them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wilde Schwein-Filets, gebraten (Roasted Wild Boar Fillets)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Black wild boar, cleaned
- Bacon
- Truffles
- Vinegar
- Wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onions
- Parsley
- Dry herbs
- Butter
- Sauce pikante

## Directions

1. Cut beautiful fillets from cleaned black game as desired, lard them with bacon and truffles, and place them in a marinade of vinegar, wine, salt, pepper, many onions and parsley, along with dry herbs
2. Add a piece of butter and let it come to a boil slightly in a casserole over gentle charcoal fire so that the fillets take on the taste of the marinade
3. Stick them on the spit, bind them with paper, and roast them slowly, basting them with butter and marinade
4. Make a sauce pikante from the marinade, arrange the fillets nicely on the plate, glaze them, and serve the sauce over them
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wilde Schweinskeule (Wild Boar Leg)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild boar leg
- Bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Dried herbs, finely crushed
- Onions, sliced
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Parsley
- Wine
- Water
- Yellow turnips
- Cloves
- Breadcrumbs, finely grated
- Glaze

## Directions

1. Singe the wild boar leg well, wash it, and brush it clean
2. Dry it thoroughly, remove the bones, and lard it with bacon
3. Rub it with salt, pepper, spices, and finely crushed dried herbs
4. Bind it together
5. Marinate it for several days with sliced onions, bay leaves, thyme, salt, parsley, and pepper
6. Place it in a stone vessel
7. Fill completely with two measures of wine and water
8. Add yellow turnips, cloves, whole onions, and the above marinade
9. Cover carefully and cook for six to seven hours until tender
10. Skim off the fat and let the leg rest in the braise for some time
11. Brush with glaze and sprinkle with finely grated breadcrumbs
12. Place briefly in the oven and serve in a beautiful presentation
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wildes Nierenbraten (Roasted Veal Loin)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin
- Salt
- Fat
- Broth

## Directions

1. The loin from the hipbone to the second rib is called the veal loin
2. Hack the rib bones in the middle; either hack the bone or remove it, though removing it causes loss of juices during roasting
3. When the bones are hacked, wipe the meat with a cloth and salt it, then let it rest
4. Place the salted veal loin in a casserole containing fat, cover with hot fat, and roast in the oven with frequent basting
5. For a larger piece, roasting time is approximately 2 hours; for a smaller one, 1-1.5 hours
6. When the meat has a light brown crust, remove it and cut into pieces
7. Remove the fat from the juices, pour a few spoonfuls of broth into the drippings, reduce slightly and serve with the meat
8. The marrow can be removed, trimmed of fat, cut into pieces and added to the roast, or carve the roast with the marrow inside
9. Note that the juices should not be poured over the roast but served separately

title: Wildfenten mit Oliven-Sauce (Wild Duck with Olive Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- wild duck, prepared as for common wild duck
- Madeira wine (4/5 liter)
- olives (12 pieces), pitted

## Directions

1. Prepare the duck as for common wild duck.
2. The only difference is that to the prepared butter sauce, which is poured over the broth, add 4/5 liter of Madeira wine, bring to a boil and about 1 minute before serving add 12 pitted olives to the sauce.
3. Pour this over the carved duck arranged on the platter and garnish with some additional sliced olives.

title: Wildkirschen-Saft (Wild Cherry Syrup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild morello cherries
- Stones crushed with the cherries

## Directions

1. One takes wild morello cherries, which after being freed from the stems are crushed with the stones and left in an earthen or porcelain bowl for 2 days in a cool place

title: Wildpretsoße (Game Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lemons
- Sugar
- Shallots
- 4 hard egg yolks
- Onions
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 20 crushed juniper berries
- Vinegar
- Oil

## Directions

1. Grate the zest of 2 lemons onto a piece of sugar.
2. Grind it finely and mix with finely chopped shallots, 4 hard egg yolks, and onions.
3. Add the juice of 2 lemons, 20 crushed juniper berries, vinegar and oil until it reaches sauce consistency.
4. Commonly served with dark game.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wildschweinkopf (Wild Boar Head)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild boar head
- Good truffle forcemeat
- Wild boar meat
- String
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Veal feet
- Bacon
- Onions
- Dry herbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Seasonings
- Gelée

## Directions

1. Clean the wild boar head, remove the bones completely, line it with good truffle forcemeat, and fill it with wild boar meat
2. Put the head together, sew it with string as if it were whole, and cook it in a strong braise of wine, vinegar, veal feet, bacon, onions, and dry herbs until it is tender
3. Do not forget salt, pepper, and seasonings
4. The next day it can be served sliced with gelée
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wildsfels Sauce (another method)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter water
- 1/4 liter red wine
- 6 deca hot butter
- equal amount flour
- 1/2 liter crushed juniper berries
- 1 nutmeg
- 3 pieces crushed juniper berries
- 1 tablespoon finely cut lemon peel
- lemon juice as desired

## Directions

1. Heat water and red wine together
2. Make a roux from hot butter and flour, let it roast a little
3. Pour the cooked wine on it
4. Let it cook for 1/2 hour
5. Add the crushed juniper berries, nutmeg, and crushed juniper berries
6. Season to taste
7. Add finely cut lemon peel
8. Let it cook once more
9. Add lemon juice as desired
10. This sauce can be strained or served with game

title: Wildsfels Sauce (Venison Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 deca butter
- equal amount flour
- 3/4 liter ripe gooseberries, mashed
- red wine
- necessary broth

## Directions

1. Prepare like the gooseberry sauce, with the difference that red wine is used instead of white wine for the venison sauce

title: Wilted Endive (Chicorée)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Endive (Chicorée)
- Salt water
- Preparation ingredients as above

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as the above recipe.

title: Windbäckerei (Meringue Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg whites
- 5 dkg fine vanilla sugar
- 10 dkg sugar
- butter for sheet
- optional: a few drops of food coloring, coffee, or finely sifted chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat 3 egg whites very stiff
2. Mix in 5 dkg fine vanilla sugar
3. Beat both together for 10 minutes
4. Stir in 10 dkg sugar
5. Fill the mixture into a piping bag with a star tip
6. Pipe rings and small puffs onto a buttered baking sheet
7. Sprinkle heavily with sugar
8. Place in the oven to dry
9. Only glowing coals should be in the oven
10. For colored pastries, add a few drops of food coloring, coffee, or finely sifted chocolate to the sugared meringue

title: Wirtschaft's Torte (Economy Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 deca beaten butter
- 12 deca vanilla sugar
- 36 deca flour
- marmalade
- finely pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 30 deca beaten butter with 12 deca vanilla sugar and 36 deca flour well together.
2. Roll out in finger-thick sheets and bake in cake pans.
3. After 1/2 hour remove from the oven and after completely cooling, spread with marmalade of choice.
4. Layer them together and sprinkle the top with finely pounded sugar.

title: Wirtschaftstorte (Economy Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 stiff egg whites
- 15 dkg sugar
- 15 dkg grated hazelnuts
- 3 dkg grated chocolate
- marmalade
- brown icing or powdered sugar

## Directions

1. With a wooden spoon, gradually stir 15 dkg sugar, 15 dkg grated hazelnuts, and 3 dkg grated chocolate into 6 stiff egg whites.
2. Fill the mass into the cake form and bake weakly.
3. The next day, cut it straight, brush the top with marmalade, and brown ice or dust with powdered sugar.
4. Make this cake only when you have egg whites available.

title: Wohlfeiler und leicht gemachter Essig (Cheap and Easy-Made Vinegar)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- One measure of wine
- Almost the same amount of water
- Six loth of freshly baked rye bread

## Directions

1. Mix one measure of wine and almost the same amount of water
2. Throw six loth of freshly baked rye bread into it
3. In summer, this vinegar placed in the sun can be good for use in three days
4. In winter, at oven warmth, it can be good for use in eight days
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wohlfeiler und leicht gemachter Essig (Inexpensive and Easy-Made Vinegar)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure of wine
- approximately the same amount of water
- 6 loth of freshly baked rye bread

## Directions

1. Mix 1 measure of wine and approximately the same amount of water
2. Throw 6 loth of freshly baked rye bread into the mixture
3. In summer, place the vinegar in the sun; it will be ready for use in 3 days
4. In winter, place it near oven heat; it will be ready for use in 8 days
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wolfrett-Strudel (Poppy Seed Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ liter flour
- 12 deca butter
- 16 deca vanilla sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 48 deca passed poppy seeds
- ⅓ teaspoon salt
- ¼ liter milk cream
- 6 egg whites
- raisins
- Canary milk (as described in Cream Strudel recipe)

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough from ½ liter flour and let it rest for ¾ hour
2. Mix 12 deca butter with 16 deca vanilla sugar
3. Gradually stir in 6 egg yolks
4. Blend in 48 deca passed poppy seeds, ⅓ teaspoon salt, and ¼ liter milk cream gradually
5. Lightly fold in the snow (whipped egg whites) from 6 egg whites last
6. Stretch the rested strudel dough finely as described in the Young Strudel recipe
7. Spread with the poppy seed filling
8. Sprinkle with raisins
9. Roll up lightly and treat as described in the Baked Ham Strudel recipe
10. Cut into desired pieces, arrange on a serving dish, and serve with Canary milk as described in the Cream Strudel recipe

title: Woodcock Salmis (Schnepfensalmis)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock innards
- Liver
- Piece of poultry, chicken, or veal liver, finely cut
- Mushroom piece
- Small piece of soaked black bread, well pressed out
- Sprigs of parsley
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- 2 crushed juniper berries
- Thick semolina slices

## Directions

1. Remove innards from woodcock and set aside the liver
2. Cut the liver and other liver pieces very finely together with mushroom, black bread, and parsley
3. Roast this mixture in butter
4. Add the finely cut liver with necessary salt, pepper, and crushed juniper berries
5. Roast briefly
6. Pass through a sieve and stir well with an egg yolk
7. Brush thick semolina slices with this mixture
8. Place on a sheet in the oven and let dry slightly
9. Garnish the woodcock with these semolina slices

title: Woodcocks, Roasted (Schnepfen, gebraten)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks
- entrails (intestines and liver)
- onion
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- lard

## Directions

1. Clean the woodcocks by plucking and singeing them
2. Remove the intestines and liver, salt them, and dress them well
3. Bend the feet and wings, pass the beak through the right wing
4. Roast them on a spit
5. Finely chop the intestines and liver together with some onion, toast some breadcrumbs in butter, add the chopped mixture, and cook it briefly
6. Fry some thin slices of bread in lard, place them on a dish, spread the woodcock paste on top, and serve with the roasted woodcocks on the table
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Wurstsuppe für Fasttage (Sausage Soup for Fast Days)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp roe
- Parsley
- Celery
- Salt
- Water
- Flour, roasted dark red
- Carp spleen, crushed
- The black matter between spleen and roe
- Marjoram
- Garlic
- Carp blood
- Rye bread, cut lengthwise and roasted

## Directions

1. Cook the roe of a carp with parsley and celery in salted water until well cooked
2. Make a roux with dark red roasted flour
3. Add the crushed spleen and black matter with some marjoram and garlic
4. Finally pour in the carp blood and let the soup come to a boil once more
5. Serve over lengthwise cut, roasted rye bread
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Würste auf Reisen mitzunehmen (Travel Sausages)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- two Pfund finely cut marrow
- two Pfund pure pork lard
- two Loth cloves
- two nutmegs
- 2 Pfund finely grated Parmesan cheese
- two Loth cinnamon
- pepper
- four Loth salt
- clean casings

## Directions

1. Mix two Pfund finely cut marrow, two Pfund pure pork lard, two Loth cloves, two nutmegs (both finely ground), 2 Pfund finely grated Parmesan cheese, two Loth cinnamon, and pepper together well with four Loth salt
2. Fill into clean casings and make each sausage half a pound
3. When traveling in a place where good soup is not available, take a pot of boiling water of two to three Maß, add one such sliced sausage to it, let it only simmer, and serve it on toasted bread for the best soup
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Würste von Gänselebern (Goose Liver Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- chopped onions
- butter
- bread, soaked in milk and squeezed out
- truffles, finely chopped
- fresh fat
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- herb seasonings (thyme, basil, marjoram)
- egg yolks
- one cup cream
- pork intestines

## Directions

1. Cook the goose liver and grate it on a grater
2. Soften chopped onions in butter
3. Add milk-soaked and squeezed bread to the onions
4. Finely grind small chopped truffles with fresh fat in a grindstone and add to the mixture
5. Add salt, pepper, spices, and finely pounded sausage herbs (thyme, basil, marjoram) in moderate amounts
6. Beat several egg yolks as for a farce and add them
7. Pour in one cup of cream and stir everything together well
8. Fill into thoroughly cleaned pork intestines
9. Cook the sausages in milk
10. Place in paper casings brushed with butter
11. Roast on a grill and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Würste von Hecht (Pike Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- finely chopped onions
- shallots
- butter
- basil
- thyme
- marjoram
- milk
- semmels (rolls)
- 8 to 10 eggs
- salt
- spices
- melted butter
- fatty pork intestines

## Directions

1. Scale, clean, and wash the pike; cut out the flesh and remove the bones
2. Soften finely chopped onions and shallots in butter
3. Cook basil, thyme, and marjoram in milk along with some semmels soaked in the milk
4. Pass the pike flesh with the onions and shallots in butter
5. Chop it finely with them, salt and season properly
6. Beat 8 to 10 eggs and stir everything together well in a bowl
7. Pour in as much melted butter as needed, and gradually add the herb milk until the mixture is not too stiff
8. Fill into fatty pork intestines
9. Cook the sausages in milk
10. When serving, braise them in paper casings with butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Würste von Kaninchen (Rabbit Sausages)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked rabbit meat
- soft side parts of rabbit
- a pair of bread rolls
- milk
- one pound pork fat
- onions
- sausage herbs
- salt
- spices
- sweet cream
- six egg yolks

## Directions

1. From cooked rabbit, scrape the fine meat from the nerves and chop finely together with the soft side parts
2. Soak bread rolls further in milk, then press them out
3. Cut one pound pork fat into cubes
4. Sauté a handful of finely chopped onions in the fat
5. Stir in the rabbit mixture and add the bread rolls, sausage herbs, salt, and spices
6. Stir everything together with sweet cream and six egg yolks
7. Proceed as with white boudin sausages
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Würste von Karpfen (Carp Sausage)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- lemon zest
- mace
- cloves, all finely pounded
- 5 eggs
- mutton intestines
- brown butter

## Directions

1. Clean and scale the carp, wash it, cut out the white flesh
2. Remove all bones from the flesh
3. Add butter, salt, pepper, parsley, lemon zest, mace, and cloves, all finely pounded
4. Mix in a binding made from 5 eggs with the flesh
5. Braise the mixture in a casserole
6. Fill into mutton intestines
7. Roast on a grill
8. Serve with brown butter
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Yellow Butter (Beurre Jaune)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- saffron

## Directions

1. Rub some saffron threads and stir them in about 2 tablespoons of cold water
2. Let them stand in a warm place until they dissolve well
3. Press them through a cloth and obtain the mixed color
4. Proceed as indicated for green butter

title: Yellow Turnips Purée Soup (Purée de carottes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 pieces yellow turnips, cleaned and washed
- 2 Spanish onions
- 1 large parsley root
- 30 ounces butter
- 3 liters soup
- sugar

## Directions

1. Slice 2 Spanish onions and 1 large parsley root thinly
2. Place in 30 ounces butter with 16 pieces of cleaned and washed yellow turnips, cover and braise very slowly until soft
3. Gradually pour in 3 liters of soup and let it cook for 1/2 hour
4. Pass through a fine sieve, place on the fire and slowly bring to a boil so the rising fat and scum can be cleanly removed
5. If the turnips are not sweet enough, adjust with sugar

title: Zampone di Modena (Modena Stuffed Pork Leg)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Skin from the foreleg or front foot of a pig
- Meat and cartilage from the bones
- Finely chopped tender meat
- Kidney fat
- Salt and spices to taste

## Directions

1. Draw the skin from the foreleg or front foot of the pig without tearing or otherwise damaging it
2. Loosen the meat and cartilage from the bones
3. Coarsely chop the cartilaginous parts and mix with finely chopped tender meat and some kidney fat
4. Season and salt sufficiently according to taste
5. Stuff the mixture into the inside fatty skin of the foreleg, but not too tightly
6. Bind the skin at both ends as strongly as possible
7. Place it in salt for as long as the skin requires to become durable
8. Hang it in smoke, and later until use in a dry place
9. Before cooking, soak and rinse it
10. Simmer it for only about half as long as a ham
11. Serve warm or cooled, as an appetizer or as a side to vegetables
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zimmel-Nuss Auflauf (Cinnamon-Nut Soufflé)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter boiling milk
- 20 Deca cinnamon-nut powder
- 12 Deca butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 12 Deca vanilla sugar
- 6 egg whites (snow)

## Directions

1. Cook 20 Deca cinnamon-nut powder with constant stirring into 1/2 liter boiling milk and let cook, then add 12 Deca butter, stir well together and set aside
2. Meanwhile stir 6 egg yolks and 12 Deca vanilla sugar for 1/2 hour, add the cooled mass, stir well together and finally fold in lightly the snow from 6 egg whites
3. Fill half of this mass into a buttered form dusted with flour

title: Zimmermannsche Erbsen
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- dried peas
- 8 ounces fat
- 8 ounces flour

## Directions

1. Cook these peas the same way as the Böhmische Erbsen
2. Make a roux from 8 ounces of fat and 8 ounces of flour and add to the peas

title: Zimmt-Confect (Cinnamon Confection)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound marzipan dough
- egg yolks, as needed
- 1 loth finely ground cinnamon
- wafer sheets

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 pound marzipan dough with as many egg yolks as required to make a mass that can be easily stretched with a knife.
2. Add 1 loth finely ground cinnamon and stir everything well together.
3. Cut various shapes from wafer sheets.
4. Spread the mass knife-back high onto the shapes and bake in moderate heat.
5. To give this confection an even more beautiful appearance, it can be glazed.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zimmtkoch (Cinnamon Pudding)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound rice
- milk
- 20 eggs (only half the whites)
- half pound sugar
- 3 loth cinnamon, finely pounded

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the rice with milk
2. Stir it with 20 eggs (using only half the whites) in a mixing bowl (Weidling) without butter
3. Mix this with half pound sugar and 3 loth finely pounded cinnamon
4. Stir everything together thoroughly
5. Place the rice on a tin plate, pour the stirred mixture into it, and bake it slowly
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zimteis-Eintrag (Cinnamon Glaze)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg whites
- 28 drams of fine sugar
- juice of a lemon
- 3 tablespoons of finely strained strawberry juice

## Directions

1. Whisk 2 egg whites to stiff snow, add 28 drams of fine sugar, juice of a lemon and 3 tablespoons of finely strained strawberry juice and stir this for 3/4 to 1 hour.
2. Then put this mass on the cake, spread it with a knife dipped in hot water, sprinkle it with finely sifted sugar and let the cake dry in the open oven for about 10 minutes.
3. This can be made from any fruit desired.

title: Zimtnnudeln (Cinnamon Noodles)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Vierding (about 4 oz) butter
- 5 whole eggs
- 5 egg yolks
- 1/2 Seidel (about 1/4 liter) lukewarm cream
- 4 spoonfuls beer yeast
- 1 pound fine flour
- 1 Loth (about 0.5 oz) cinnamon
- 1 Vierding (about 4 oz) sugar
- butter for the pan

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy, then stir in the 5 whole eggs and 5 egg yolks
2. Pour in the lukewarm cream and 4 spoonfuls of beer yeast
3. Add 1 pound of fine flour
4. Beat the dough well, take it out onto a floured board, and roll it finger-thick
5. Cut the dough out with a spoon or biscuit mold
6. Pound 1 Loth cinnamon and 1 Vierding sugar finely sifted together
7. Coat a pan with butter and layer the noodles in it, filling it up
8. Sprinkle each layer of noodles strongly with the cinnamon-sugar mixture
9. Do not allow them to rise too much and bake them slowly and carefully
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zitonen-Beuschel (Veal Lung with Lemon)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lamb or 1 veal lung
- Salt water
- 16 deca butter
- Flour
- Parsley
- Onion
- 1/2 liter broth
- 1/4 liter vinegar

## Directions

1. Poach 2 lamb lungs or 1 veal lung in salt water until tender
2. Remove and when cooled, cut into noodle-like pieces
3. Roast equal amounts of flour in butter, add finely cut parsley and onion if desired
4. When brown, pour in 1/2 liter broth and 1/4 liter vinegar and stir well together

title: Zitronat-Strudel (Citron Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 34 deca fine citron peel, cut into thin strips
- 8 deca pistachios
- 28 deca blanched almonds, cut into thin sticks
- 32 deca finely ground sugar
- finely cut lemon peel
- juice of 3 lemons
- Strudel dough
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Place the citron peel, pistachios, blanched almonds cut into thin sticks, finely ground sugar and finely cut lemon peel in a deep bowl
2. Mix well with the juice of 3 lemons and let stand for some time
3. Brush the stretched strudel dough with melted butter
4. Sprinkle with the mixture in the bowl
5. Roll lightly together
6. Treat further as for Ham Strudel (No. 1229)

title: Zitronen-Sauce (Lemon Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peeled lemon
- juice of 1 lemon
- some finely cut lemon peel
- some sugar
- salt
- nutmeg
- butter sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the flesh of 1 peeled lemon and cut it into dice
2. Add it with the juice of 1 lemon and some finely cut lemon peel, some sugar, salt, and nutmeg to a butter sauce
3. Boil thoroughly and pass it

title: Zitronen-Sulz (Lemon Jelly)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lemons
- white wine
- same quantity of lemon peel as orange peel in the orange jelly
- refined sugar
- isinglass

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as Orange Jelly (No. 1660), using juice from 6 lemons and only white wine.
2. Cook the same amount of lemon peel in the sugar as would be used for orange jelly.

title: Zitronenauflauf (Lemon Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 7 dkg sugar
- juice and zest of ½ lemon
- 4 egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. 30 minutes before serving, beat the 4 egg yolks with 7 dkg sugar until very foamy.
2. Stir in the juice and zest of ½ lemon and the 4 beaten egg whites.
3. Bake the same way as the chocolate soufflé.
4. This mixture is sufficient for 4 persons. For 6 persons, one must take 2 more eggs and correspondingly more of the other ingredients.

title: Zitronenauflauf (Lemon Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 7 dkg sugar
- 1/2 lemon
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. 30 minutes before serving, beat 4 egg yolks with 7 dkg sugar until very foamy
2. Stir in the juice and zest of 1/2 lemon and 4 stiffly beaten egg whites
3. Bake the same way as the chocolate soufflé
4. This amount serves 4 persons; for 6 persons add 2 more eggs and proportionally more of the other ingredients

title: Zitroneneis (Lemon Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 lemons
- 2 dl spun sugar (approximately 18 dkg cube sugar cooked with 1 dl water for 15 minutes)
- cold water

## Directions

1. Squeeze the lemons and mix with 2 dl spun sugar and cold water until the immersed scale (see Strawberry Ice Cream) reads 19°.
2. Peel 2 lemons very thinly, place the peels in the solution, let stand for 45 minutes, strain through a hair sieve and freeze (see Vanilla Ice Cream).

title: Zitronensaft (Lemon Juice)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Lemons
- Lemon juice
- Citric acid dissolved in water

## Directions

1. To preserve the natural color of peeled apples, pears and other peeled fruit that one wishes to cook in steam, use lemons, lemon juice, or dissolved citric acid with which the fruits are rubbed as explained below, and then thrown into cold water mixed with lemon juice
2. A more economical alternative to this is dissolved citric acid

title: Zu Tüll zum Guittèn-Marmelns (Quince Jelly Candies)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Deca finely sifted sugar
- 16 Deca quince marmalade
- 1 tablespoon very finely chopped citron
- some nutmeg
- more sugar as needed

## Directions

1. Place 8 Deca of finely sifted sugar and 16 Deca of quince marmalade in a bowl and stir together over heat until the mixture separates from the bowl.
2. Pour onto a board sprinkled with finely sifted sugar and work together with 1 tablespoon very finely chopped citron, some nutmeg, and additional sugar to form a dough.
3. Roll out to finger-thickness and cut into desired pieces.
4. Lay these pieces on white paper and let them dry in an airy location.

title: Zucchetti (Kürbisse)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- reife, geschälte Zucchetti
- Öl
- Salzwasser
- Butter-Sauce

## Directions

1. von den reifen, geschälten Zucchetti werden die Kerne entfernt
2. das Fleisch wird in beliebige Stücke geschnitten und nebeneinander in eine Casserole gelegt
3. sie werden mit Öl begossen und zugedeckt in der Röhre gebraten
4. oder die geschnittenen Zucchetti werden in Salzwasser gekocht
5. dieses wird abgeseiht und sie werden mit Butter-Sauce serviert
6. oder sie werden wie die gebratenen Kürbisse oder wie sonstige Gemüse behandelt

title: Zucker für schwereingekochtes Obst (Syrup for Heavily Preserved Fruit)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ litre cold water
- 80 decilitres finest sugar

## Directions

1. Put ¾ litre cold water and 80 decilitres finest sugar into a very clean copper pot
2. Boil it for 10 minutes, removing the foam that forms
3. Pour the syrup into an earthen vessel and use it in a lukewarm state

title: Zucker-Krapfen (Sugar Puffs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks, passed through a sieve
- 14 deca butter
- 14 deca powdered sugar
- 10 deca flour

## Directions

1. Pass the 6 hard-boiled egg yolks through a sieve and mix finely with 14 deca butter
2. Place this mixture on a board and work it with 14 deca powdered sugar and 10 deca flour into a dough
3. Cover the dough and let it rest in a cold place
4. Once the dough has become stiff, cut it into nut-sized pieces
5. Treat further as specified in the Vanilla Puffs recipe

title: Zuckerbretzeln (Sugar Pretzels)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- Half a pound sugar
- One and a half pounds flour
- 9 eggs
- 1 Quäntchen cinnamon
- Zest of one lemon
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Stir the butter to a foam, gradually beat in the eggs, add the spices, and finally the flour
2. Make small pretzels from it, brush them with egg white, and bake not too hot
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zuckerhutwurzel (Salsify)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Salsify roots
- Flour
- Water
- Vinegar
- Warm water

## Directions

1. This root must be cleaned very quickly, as it loses color through prolonged contact with the hand and turns red
2. To prevent this, put some flour with water in a bowl, stir it well, add some vinegar and warm water, and as soon as a root is cleaned, place it immediately in this mixture

title: Zuckerstrauben (Sugar Strauben)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- six tablespoons fine flour
- six egg yolks
- one seidel cream
- four tablespoons ground sugar
- hot lard
- sugar and cinnamon for dusting

## Directions

1. Make a batter from six tablespoons fine flour, six egg yolks, one seidel cream, and four tablespoons ground sugar
2. Stir everything well
3. Drop it through the strauben spoon into hot lard
4. Fry the strauben until beautifully golden yellow
5. Dust them with sugar and cinnamon and serve
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zuckerstrudel Brügel (Spit Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 64 Deka butter
- 64 Deka lemon sugar
- 20 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- blanched spice cloves
- cinnamon
- white of 15 eggs
- 64 Deka flour
- butter for coating

## Directions

1. Cream 64 Deka butter until fluffy
2. Add 64 Deka lemon sugar and 20 egg yolks one after another
3. Stir in 1 teaspoon nutmeg, an equal amount of blanched spice cloves and cinnamon
4. Lightly mix in the whites of 15 eggs with 64 Deka flour
5. Stretch the hollow brügel (a rod that is wide at one end and tapers at the other) onto a spit and secure well with small wooden pegs
6. When the brügel is warmed through, brush it with paper coated with butter and secure this with twine, which is fastened at both ends of the wood at the ends
7. Make a bed of charcoal in the base of the spit
8. Place a long pan under the spit to catch dripping batter
9. Turn the spit over the fire until the paper is yellow, then drizzle with clarified butter
10. When it begins to foam, pour the dough mixture with a ladle while rotating the spit evenly until the form is evenly covered
11. Continue rotating the spit evenly so the batter forms in a point
12. Let the poured batter turn yellow, then pour the same amount of batter again
13. Repeat this process until all batter is used
14. When done, cut the cake evenly at both ends, loosen the twine, remove the spit, carefully remove the cake, place on a plate
15. After one or two days, sprinkle with various icing, garnish with small sticks and decorate the top with flowers

title: Zuckerstränge (Sugar Strands)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 tablespoons fine flour
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 5 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons wine
- 4 egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Put 5 tablespoons fine flour and 3 tablespoons sugar into a saucepan
2. Add gradually 5 tablespoons butter and 2 tablespoons wine
3. Stir this very finely and gently fold in the stiff beaten egg whites from 4 eggs
4. Drop this batter through a funnel or pastry bag into very hot fat
5. When the strands are baked, remove them with a pastry spoon, bend them over the back of an oblong rounded form, and arrange them sprinkled with sugar
6. Serve with any desired chaudeau sauce

title: Zuckerstäuben (Sugar Puffs)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound flour
- 6 loths pounded sugar
- White of ten eggs
- A little wine

## Directions

1. Place flour and pounded sugar into an earthenware dish
2. Gradually stir in the white of ten eggs and a little wine, taking care not to stir too vigorously so it does not foam
3. Let this mass run through a funnel with a narrow opening into hot lard, forming round puffs
4. When they are done, lay them over a rolling pin so they become curved
5. Sprinkle them with sugar and cinnamon
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zuckerteigskatfeln (Sugar Dough Puffs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 deca softened butter
- 12 deca lemon sugar
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 10 hard-boiled egg yolks, passed through a sieve
- 16 deca blanched almonds, pounded very finely with 1 egg white
- 30 deca flour

## Directions

1. Beat 28 deca butter until creamy
2. Gradually mix in 12 deca lemon sugar, juice of 1 lemon, and 10 hard-boiled egg yolks passed through a sieve
3. Mix in 16 deca blanched almonds that have been pounded very finely with 1 egg white
4. Mix in 30 deca flour and work into a smooth dough
5. Cover and let rest in a cold place
6. Once stiff, roll out the dough finger-thick on a flour-dusted board
7. Cut out round discs with a puff cutter and place on a butter-brushed baking sheet
8. Brush with beaten eggs and bake until pale yellow in a moderate oven
9. Once cool, remove from the sheet with a thin knife, place on a sieve or board, sprinkle with finely pounded sugar, and let dry in an oven

title: Zunge mit Kräutern (Beef Tongue with Fine Herbs)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled and tender cooked beef tongue
- 8 tablespoons butter
- 1 strong tablespoon parsley
- 5 sprigs tarragon
- equal amount chervil
- some sorrel
- juice of 1 lemon
- glaze in 1/8 liter soup
- parsley sprigs

## Directions

1. Cut the peeled and tender cooked tongue into finger-thick slices
2. In a shallow saucepan, put 8 tablespoons butter with 1 tablespoon parsley, 5 sprigs tarragon, equal amount chervil, and some sorrel, all finely cut, and stir in the melted butter
3. Lay the cooked tongue slices in beside each other and cover with a butter-brushed piece of paper
4. 10 minutes before serving, place the saucepan on the fire, remove the paper, let the tongue slices brown for 2 minutes on each side
5. Arrange them nicely in the center of a deep round bowl
6. To the herbs remaining in the saucepan, add juice of 1 lemon and some glaze dissolved in 1/8 liter soup, bring to a boil and pour this sauce over the tongue
7. Border the bowl with finely plucked parsley sprigs

title: Zunge mit saurem Sauce (Beef Tongue with Caper Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- 3/4 liter sour cream
- 1 tablespoon capers

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue as described above
2. Prepare the Madeira sauce as above, but with the difference that instead of Madeira wine, add 3/4 liter sour cream and 1 tablespoon capers and heat through
3. Pour over the sliced tongue
4. Alternative: use 1/8 liter vinegar and 3/8 liter soup, or 1/4 liter ordinary white wine instead of the sour cream

title: Zungen am Spieße gebraten mit Sardellensauce (Tongues Roasted on a Spit with Anchovy Sauce)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh large tongue
- bacon
- sardelles
- butter
- cream

## Directions

1. Cook a fresh large tongue until tender and remove the skin; it should not be taken from the pot before being put on the spit, and must be served from the spit.
2. When the skin is removed, lard it beautifully and neatly with two parts bacon and one part sardelles.
3. Put it on a thin spit, let a piece of butter melt, add half a seidel of cream to it, and baste it three to four times during roasting, sprinkle it...
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zungen in Fricandeau (Tongues Braised)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- tongues
- parsley
- chives
- yellow turnips
- salt
- bacon
- onion
- ham
- brown broth
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Put the tongues in a pot with a bundle of parsley, chives, some yellow turnips, and salt, let cook until the skin can be removed.
2. Then lard them with finely cut bacon in three or four rows, one after another.
3. Arrange bacon, onion, yellow turnips, and ham all cut in thin slices in a casserole, place the tongues in it.
4. Fill with brown broth, cover the casserole, and let the tongues braise on gentle heat until tender enough.
5. Then remove them, strain the sauce, add two spoonfuls of good vinegar, and let the sauce cook until it becomes glace.
6. Then put the tongues back in, turn them on all sides until done. You can also lay a wreath of small braised onions around the edge of the dish.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zusammengesetzte Brot-Tifane (Compound Bread Tisane)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- half a measure simple bread tisane
- citron juice
- 3 loth cinnamon water
- 1 loth Rhine wine
- 1 quarter measure crushed sugar
- yolk of one egg (optional)

## Directions

1. Take half a measure of simple bread tisane
2. Add citron juice, 3 loth cinnamon water, 1 loth Rhine wine, and crushed sugar to taste until pleasantly sweet
3. Mix all together
4. If desired and there is no high fever, you may add the yolk of one egg
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zuſammengeſetzter Aepfeltrank (Compound Apple Drink)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty Loth of peeled apples (Maschanzger or Renetten variety)
- one Seidel of water
- one Quentchen of crushed nutmeg
- two Loth of grated bread
- four Loth of Rhine wine
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Take twenty Loth of peeled apples (Maschanzger or Renetten variety)
2. Cook them in one Seidel of water in a covered pot for one hour
3. Press them through a cloth
4. Add one Quentchen of crushed nutmeg
5. Add two Loth of grated bread
6. Add four Loth of Rhine wine
7. Add as much sugar as needed for pleasant taste
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zweierlei-Kuchen (Two-Kind Cake)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 deca butter
- 4 deca lemon sugar
- 1 knife point salt
- 3 deca fresh yeast
- 1/8 liter warm cream
- 40 deca flour
- 3 egg whites
- butter for dusting

## Directions

1. Mix 14 deca butter with 4 deca lemon sugar and 1 knife point salt
2. Add 3 deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter warm cream and 40 deca flour
3. Stir well and beat the dough from the spoon until it releases from the spoon
4. Lightly mix in the stiff peaks of 3 egg whites
5. Roll out this dough on a flour-dusted board to finger thickness and cut out round discs the size of an ordinary plate
6. Press the dough in the middle with your fingers so that it forms a finger-wide and slightly raised rim
7. Cover these cakes and let them rise at a warm place until the dough has risen somewhat
8. Meanwhile prepare the following fillings: Cheese filling: 20 deca sifted curd is sweetened to taste and mixed well with 1/2 knife point finely cut lemon peel and finely cut toasted almonds
9. Poppy seed filling: 1/8 liter finely ground poppy seed and 1/2 deca vanilla sugar are placed in a saucepan, 1/8 liter boiling milk is poured in and stirred over the fire until it thickens, then mixed well with 1/2 knife point finely cut lemon peel as desired and 1 toasted spoonful

title: Zwetschgen-Kroquette (Plum Croquettes)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 decagrams butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 kilo dried plums, half-soft cooked and finely cut
- 16 decagrams blanched, finely ground almonds
- 8 decagrams sugar
- 8 decagrams crusts, soaked in water and well pressed out rolls
- finely cut lemon zest
- wafer cookies
- water
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Beat 8 decagrams of butter until fluffy
2. Gradually stir in 4 egg yolks
3. Mix in 1/4 kilo of dried, half-soft cooked, finely cut plums
4. Add 16 decagrams of blanched, finely ground almonds
5. Add 8 decagrams sugar
6. Add 8 decagrams of crusts that have been soaked in water and well pressed out
7. Add finely cut lemon zest
8. Mix everything well together
9. Form finger-thick strips on a flour-dusted board
10. Wrap these in wafers that have been moistened with water
11. Dip in beaten eggs
12. Roll in bread crumbs
13. Fry in very hot fat

title: Zwetschgen-Marmelade (Plum Preserves)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram powdered sugar
- 2 kilograms very ripe fresh plums

## Directions

1. Put 1 kilogram of powdered sugar in a saucepan.
2. Quickly peel 2 kilograms of very ripe fresh plums, remove the kernels, and immediately place each plum into the sugar.
3. Place over heat and cook while stirring constantly and rapidly into a thick preserves, being careful that the plums do not lose their beautiful color, which resembles that of apricot preserves.
4. After cooking for 10 to 15 minutes, fill hot into jars like the Apricot Preserves.
5. If desired, you can add 1 teaspoon of vanilla, cinnamon, or clove oil, or a mixture of these, while cooking the preserves.

title: Zwetschgen-Strudel (Plum Strudel)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Strudel dough made from 1/2 liter flour
- fresh plums, pitted

## Directions

1. Prepare according to Apple Strudel (No. 1238)
2. Fill with fresh pitted plums instead of apples
3. Treat further as indicated

title: Zwetschgen-Tascherlein (Plum Pastries)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- noodle dough
- flour
- plums
- salted water
- toasted bread crumbs
- nuts
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Hopfen Tascherlein, using plums instead of hop filling
2. Alternatively, instead of coating with toasted bread crumbs, sprinkle with finely ground nuts mixed with cinnamon and bread crumbs
3. Mix well and serve

title: Zwetschken (Plums)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- stems cut short
- lemon juice
- 60 decilitres sugar
- 1 litre water

## Directions

1. Cut the stems of the plums somewhat short
2. Peel the plums and rub with lemon juice
3. Treat as the preceding fruit
4. Only let them cook a moment
5. Use 60 decilitres sugar in 1 litre water for the sugar water

title: Zwetschkenknödel von Germteig (Plum Dumplings with Yeast Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 4 ounces fine sugar
- 5 grams salt
- 1/2 liter flour
- 3 ounces yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter cold milk
- fresh plums

## Directions

1. Beat 6 ounces of butter until light
2. Gradually beat in 2 egg yolks, 1 whole egg, 4 ounces fine sugar, and 5 grams salt
3. Stir in 1/2 liter flour and 3 ounces yeast dissolved in 1/8 liter cold milk until the dough releases from the spoon
4. Cover and let rest in a warm place until it rises by one-third
5. With an egg spoon, place portions on a flour-dusted board, separate with floured fingers
6. Top with wiped raw plums, distribute dough evenly over them
7. Place dumplings on a flour-dusted board, cover with cloth, let rise in a warm place until somewhat larger
8. Proceed as in Zwetschkenknödel von Strudelteig (Recipe 1223)
9. Any ripe fruit such as small apricots or greengage plums can be used instead of plums

title: Zwetschkenknödel von Strudelteig (Plum Dumplings with Strudel Dough)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter flour
- milk
- butter
- plums

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough made with milk and let it rest for 3/4 hour
2. On a flour-dusted board, roll out the dough very thin
3. Cut into squares corresponding in size to plums
4. Wipe fresh plums with a cloth
5. Place one plum on each square of dough
6. Distribute the dough evenly over it
7. Place the dumplings on a flour-dusted board
8. 1/4 hour before serving, put the dumplings in boiling salted water and cook covered
9. Meanwhile, in a saucepan, toast 8 ounces of breadcrumbs in 10 ounces of hot butter
10. Add the drained plum dumplings, shake lightly, arrange on a plate and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Zwetschkenkuchen (Plum Cake with Yeast Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 dkg flour
- Salt
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 7 dkg butter
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 whole egg
- 1.5 dkg yeast
- 1 dl lukewarm milk
- Plums
- Sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Warm 25 dkg flour with salt and 2 tablespoons sugar in a bowl
2. Rub in 7 dkg butter
3. Add 1 egg yolk, 1 whole egg, and the risen leaven from 1.5 dkg yeast
4. Add nearly 1 dl lukewarm milk
5. Beat the dough until it leaves the spoon and comes away from the sides
6. Place near the stove to rise
7. When sufficiently risen, place on a well-greased baking sheet
8. Stretch out with floured fingers
9. Cover with plums as with the previous cake
10. Bake at lower heat
11. Divide into equal pieces
12. Sprinkle with sugar
13. Serve cooled

title: Zwetschkenröster (Plum Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg dried or ½ kg fresh plums
- 10-12 pieces of cube sugar
- Water
- Butter (walnut-sized)
- 1 tablespoon Cinnamon
- Flour

## Directions

1. Wash plums, remove pits
2. Cook with cube sugar and enough water to cover, covered, stirring occasionally, for 30-45 minutes
3. In a pot, mix melted butter with cinnamon and flour until smooth
4. Add to plums and cook for 10 minutes
5. Serve warm

title: Zwieback (Gräzer) (Twice-Baked Bread)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Deca flour
- 15 Deca finely sifted sugar
- 1 whole egg
- 1 egg yolk
- blanched, finely cut almonds
- butter for coating the baking sheet

## Directions

1. On a board, work together 16 Deca flour, 15 Deca finely sifted sugar, 1 whole egg, and 1 egg yolk to form a dough.
2. Shape this dough into a long roll and place it on a buttered baking sheet.
3. Brush with crushed egg white and sprinkle with blanched, finely cut almonds if desired.
4. Bake in a moderate oven for 1/2 to 3/4 hour.
5. Remove the roll, cut it into pieces as thick as the back of a knife, and dry these pieces in a moderately warm oven.

title: Zwiebel mit Glaceierung (Oignons glacés)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- Haselnussgroße Zwiebeln
- Fett
- Zucker
- weißen Wein oder Suppe

## Directions

1. Haselnussgroße Zwiebeln werden geschält
2. unterdessen gibt man in heißes Fett etwas Zucker
3. dieser wird braun
4. dann werden die Zwiebeln hinein gegeben
5. sie werden etwas geröstet
6. es wird, daß es knapp über die Zwiebeln zusammengeht, weißen Wein oder Suppe darauf gegossen
7. es läßt sich zugedeckt dünsten

title: Zwiebel-Puree (Purée à la soubise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Stück geschälte, blätterig geschnittene, spanische, weiße Zwiebel
- 10 Deca Butter
- 1 Liter Béchamel-Sauce
- Salz
- weißer Pfeffer
- 1 Eßlöffel aufgelöste Suppen Glace

## Directions

1. 10 Stück geschälte, blätterig geschnittene, spanische, weiße Zwiebel werden in 10 Deca Butter zugedeckt
2. weiß und weich gedünstet
3. mit 1 Liter Béchamel-Sauce aufgegossen
4. gut untereinander gerührt
5. durch ein feines Sieb gestrichen
6. mit Salz und weißem Pfeffer gewürzt
7. wenn man hat und will, 1 Eßlöffel aufgelöste Suppen Glace beigegeben

title: Zwiebel-Puree anderer Art (Purée à la soubise)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- rohe Zwiebel
- einige Scheiben rohen Karoinen
- 1 Eßlöffel Mehl
- 1 1/4 Liter Suppe
- 1/4 Liter Ober

## Directions

1. Béchamel kann ersetzt werden, indem man einige Scheiben rohen Karoinen zur rohen Zwiebel gibt
2. wenn der sich bildende Saft vollkommen verdünstet ist, bestäubt man es mit 1 Eßlöffel Mehl
3. 1 1/4 Liter Suppe wird hinzugegossen
4. alles wird gut untereinander gerührt
5. läßt es bei starkem Feuer gut verkochen
6. man verdünnt es dann mit 1/4 Liter Ober und läßt es abermals verkochen
7. man streicht es hierauf durch ein Sieb und verdünnt es nach Bedarf mit Ober

title: Zwiebel-Sauce (Onion Sauce)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons hot butter or lard
- 2 tablespoons finely ground sugar
- 1 head peeled onion, finely sliced
- 8 tablespoons flour
- 3/4 liter soup
- Some vinegar
- Salt

## Directions

1. In 8 tablespoons hot butter or lard, add 2 tablespoons finely ground sugar and let it become dark brown
2. Brown 1 head peeled onion, finely sliced, with roasting
3. Stir 8 tablespoons flour to it, let it roast a bit more
4. Pour it with 3/4 liter soup and some vinegar
5. Salt it and simmer, let it boil up and pass through a sieve
6. The sauce must be pleasantly sweet-sour

title: Zwiebelsuppe (Onion Soup)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound of butter
- peeled onions, each cut in two parts
- bouillon or water
- salt and pepper
- bread slices, diced and browned

## Directions

1. Sauté the peeled and halved onions in butter in a casserole over charcoal fire until they turn brownish
2. Fill with bouillon or water and cook for half an hour
3. Finally salt and pepper
4. Serve over diced and browned bread slices
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Zwiebärdt (Twisted Braid Bread)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Deca butter
- 8 Deca finely sifted sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 3 Deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk
- 24 Deca flour
- unsalted bacon fat for coating

## Directions

1. Beat 12 Deca butter until creamy and add 8 Deca finely sifted sugar and 4 egg yolks one by one.
2. Stir in 3 Deca fresh yeast dissolved in 1/4 liter lukewarm milk along with 24 Deca flour and mix well.
3. Beat the dough until it separates from the spoon.
4. Place on a flour-dusted board, divide into five parts, and form two of these parts into finger-length, finger-thick rolls.
5. Place on a buttered baking sheet, cover, and let rise once more in a warm place.
6. Bake on the sheet in the oven at fairly strong heat until golden.
7. Remove from the sheet with a knife and cool on a board or sieve.
8. The next day, cut each roll lengthwise into two equal parts, place cut-side up on a dry sheet, brush with beaten egg, sprinkle with chopped pistachios mixed with coarsely cut almonds, and bake until light brown at low heat.

title: À la Braise (Braised Meat)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds lean ham, cut into pieces
- 1 pound good kidney fat, cut into pieces
- 1 measure fatty bouillon
- one bay leaf
- some cloves
- pepper
- mace
- several large onions
- celery
- yellow turnips
- one bundle parsley
- 2 pounds bacon

## Directions

1. Place the ham, kidney fat, bouillon, bay leaf, cloves, pepper, mace, onions, celery, yellow turnips, and parsley bundle on the fire
2. Cut the bacon into broad slices to cover the piece of meat or poultry to be prepared
3. Use a casserole with a very tight-fitting lid for the braise
4. Place glowing coals on top of the lid and beneath it so that it cooks as little as possible rapidly
5. Even if it only bubbles occasionally, the meat, which should not be too soft anyway, will certainly become much more tender than if it cooked rapidly
6. Note that the piece of meat or whatever it may be that is to provide this braise must fill the casserole exactly, which must not be too large
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: À la Daube (Braised Meat in Aspic)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- a pair of veal feet
- a piece of ham
- cloves
- pepper
- mace
- root vegetables and herbs uncut
- one bundle parsley
- a glass of white wine
- some vinegar

## Directions

1. This is almost the same as what one understands by braise, except that for a daube, if it is to become a strong aspic which is given to cold pastries and similar dishes, one also adds a pair of veal feet, a piece of ham, cloves, pepper, mace, root vegetables and a bundle of parsley (all uncut), a glass of white wine, and some vinegar
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Äpfel auf Pommersche Marzipan-Art (Apples in Pomeranian Marzipan Style)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Good portion of black bread
- Finely crushed sugar
- Cinnamon
- Grated lemon peel
- One quarter pound butter
- Apple marmalade
- Large raisins
- Apricot marmalade
- Finely cut candied citron

## Directions

1. Grate a good portion of black bread and mix it with much finely crushed sugar, cinnamon and grated lemon peel.
2. Let one quarter pound butter melt in a round dish and sprinkle in as many bread crumbs that the cake is as high as a small finger.
3. Then a thick layer of apple marmalade is laid on top, into which large raisins, apricot marmalade, and finely cut candied citron are mixed, and afterwards this layer is covered equally thickly with the grated bread mentioned above.
4. Finally sprinkle it everywhere finely with melted butter and bake it three quarters of an hour slowly in the oven.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Äpfel in Reiß (Apples in Rice)
author: Anna Dorn
body:
## Ingredients

- Several apples
- Sugar
- Water
- Lemon juice
- Butter
- Rice
- Milk
- Preserves
- Candied citron or orange peel
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Peel some apples, leave them whole, and hollow out only the core.
2. Cook a thin syrup with lemon juice, sugar and water, and then place the apples in it.
3. When you see that they become soft, remove them and lay them on a hair sieve to drain.
4. Make a marmalade especially from some apples.
5. Butter a dish and cover the bottom with rice cooked in milk, as for rice Chaudeaux.
6. Arrange the apples on top, fill them in the middle where the core was with preserves, and cover them completely with rice.
7. Finely cut candied citron or orange peel, together with fine powdered sugar, are still sprinkled on top.
8. Let these apples in rice take on a beautiful glazed color in the oven.
source_title: Allgemeines österreichisches oder neuestes Wiener Kochbuch

title: Ölsardellensuppe (Sardine Soup)
author: Louise Seleskowitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 deca butter
- flour
- 2 liters water
- some parsley root
- 10 deca cleaned finely chopped sardines
- some celery
- garlic
- finely chopped lemon peel
- 1.5 liters cream
- salt
- ginger

## Directions

1. Make a light roux with 10 deca butter and flour
2. Add 2 liters water and some parsley root to 10 deca cleaned finely chopped sardines, some celery and garlic, and finely chopped lemon peel
3. Let simmer well and pour the roux with this and 1.5 liters cream over, add salt and ginger
4. Let simmer well
5. Can be served passed or unpassed
